12/20/20, 5:45 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: ProjectSave Devotional
Sunday,
December 20th, 2020.

*THE POWER OF SHAMELESS AUDACITY IN PRAYER*


Luke11:8 I tell you, even though he will not get up and give you the bread because of friendship, yet because of your shameless audacity he will surely get up and give you as much as you need.  NIV



Jesus was teaching about prayer in Luke 11. He told the story of a man who kept asking his friend for bread to feed his guest at an odd hour. Jesus said this man would grant his friend's request not because of friendship but because of his persistent request. NIV bible calls that act of persistence "shameless audacity". Jesus taught that persistence in the place of prayer is needed to get answers to our prayers. In a generation of instant answer and results, we must be ready to tarry in God's presence till result comes.







One of my sons knows how to pester me to buy him whatever he want. I remember telling him to pray about something he needed. Earlier, I told him we don't have the money to purchase that thing. After he prayed, he kept asking me to go and buy what he requested for in prayers. I told him God hadn't provided the money, he told me to go and buy it because he believed in his prayers. His audacity while requesting was shameless and all he cared about was what he wanted regardless of whether I can buy it or not. As far as he was concerned, I was in the best position to supply what he wanted.



Don't be tired in the place of prayer. In my few years of walking with God, I have waited for answer to certain things for years and I've seen those who waited on God for donkey years for just one thing. Delayed answer to prayer doesn't mean God has ignored you.
You may have been asking God for one thing over the years and it may seem God is ignoring your request. Don't stop praying, your shameless audacity in the place of prayer will get the job done. Keep asking, keep seeking and keep knocking on the door of heaven, very soon, you'll get a response.


*PRAYER*: Father, I receive the grace to be persistent in the place of prayer in Jesus name.

Monday,
December 21st, 2020.

*CONSIDER THIS BEFORE MARRIAGE*

Proverb 22:24 Do not make friends with a hot-tempered person, do not associate with one easily angered,

Years back, an incident made the news. It was the story of a man who stabbed his banker wife to death. He professes to be a Christian. After the judge passed the verdict on him, I saw him speaking in tongues in chains while walking towards the prison vehicle. I watched this on t.v.This brother was her fellowship president when they were in school. Coroner's report showed that he stabbed his wife 72 times. He was angry to the point where he took the life of his wife. From the story, he had the habit of physically abusing his wife until the day he finally killed his wife. Another man( a Pastor)  in the U.S. killed his wife as promised. Anger caused it.


The book of Proverbs is full of practical wisdom for daily living. The truth revealed today in the opening text is that we should never make friend with a hot-tempered person. If scriptures forbid friendship with such persons, it is obvious God won't support any of his children to marry such a person. Any brother or sister that cannot control his temper is not worth marrying. Don't consider him/her. I have heard of people who will destroy the t.v., home theatre and other valuable items at home when they're angry. Some may attempt to kill. From such persons, run away. *Ability to control ones temper is a sign of maturity.*



There are certain things that need no prayer of inquisition. Don't disturb God for direction on what he has clearly revealed in his word. Do you know what the scriptures call an angry man? *Do not be quickly provoked in your spirit, for anger resides in the lap of fools*,Ecc7:9. A hot-tempered person is a fool. Now that you're still in courtship, you can assess his temper to be sure you won't be a victim of physical abuse in the future. If he has started slapping and punching you before marriage,  you don't need a prophet to tell you to run for your  dear life. To whom brain is given, sense is expected.

*PRAYER*: Father, I receive the grace and wisdom to choose wisely in Jesus name.

DEVOTIONAL 0003 | Source start line: 380

12/22/20, 4:12 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: ProjectSave Devotional
Tuesday
December 22nd, 2020.

*DADDY, WHEN ARE WE GOING TO HEAVEN*?

1Cor 15:19 If only for this life we have hope in Christ, we are of all people most to be pitied.NIV


During a conversation with my son, he asked: "Daddy, when are we going to heaven"? The question was unexpected though I'm getting used to his unusual questions. I explained the concept of rapture to him in simple and clear terms. I told him a day is coming that no man knows except the father. On that day, the angel will blow the trumpet and we'll disappear. He was fascinated about my description of disappearance. He has seen something similar in cartoons but he didn't know it can happen in reality.



That incident reminded me of the scriptures that says: *"He has made everything beautiful in its time. He has also set eternity in the human heart; yet no one can fathom what God has done from beginning to end*", Eccl 3:11. Dearest, we need to look forward to that day when the saints shall be caught up to meet with the Lord in glory. It should be our dream and desire. Apostle Paul said, *"while we look forward with hope to that wonderful day when the glory of our great God and Savior, Jesus Christ, will be revealed*", Titus2:13. If Paul and the early believers looked forward with hope to the appearing of Christ, ours shouldn't be different.


What are you longing for? The riches of this world is fleeting. Citizens of third world nations look forward to travelling overseas with eager anticipation. Some do desperate things to become citizens of world power countries. Wherever we live on earth, you must know that we're not yet home. We should long to return home and prepare for our return adequately. *The moment your focus is on your eternal home, it'll affect the choices and decisions you make here on earth.* Men of yore had a different perspective about life and that affected their pursuit greatly. It was said of Abraham that, *"For he was looking forward to the city with foundations, whose architect and builder is God*", Heb11:10.


*PRAYER*: Father, I long to be with you. Help me.

DEVOTIONAL 0004 | Source start line: 506
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
12/22/20, 11:19 PM - Lanre Oyeleke: PROJECTSAVE DEVOTIONAL
wednesday,
December 23rd,  2020.

*HOW TO LIVE A MEANINGFUL LIFE*

John 18:37 Pilate therefore said unto him, Art thou a king then? Jesus answered, Thou sayest that I am a king. To this end was I born, and for this cause came I into the world, that I should bear witness unto the truth. Every one that is of the truth heareth my voice.

The issue of finding purpose for living seems to me to be one of the most important discoveries to be made after salvation. Everything we are seeking for in life is tied to our purpose. I preached in a singles meeting recently and I told them that purpose discovery precedes spouse discovery. Knowing God's purpose for your life will aid the discovery of your spouse. You know most singles love to hear about how to choose a life partner which is good. We love to learn  how to make money and live a good life, that's also good. However,  it is purpose that gives meaning and value to marriage, wealth and life. When Jesus stood trial before Pontius Pilate, he declared to him why he came into the world- to bear witness unto the truth. His life was meaningful when he was on earth because he did all we read about him on purpose. After his death and resurrection, the impact of his purpose on earth when he came is what we are still enjoying till date. Walking in God's plan and purpose for your life has benefit for now and for posterity.

When Paul described David's sojourn on earth, he said, *"Now when David had served God’s purpose in his own generation, he fell asleep; he was buried with his ancestors and his body decayed*", Acts 13:36 NIV. The life of David was meaningful because he lived it on purpose. He played his part, fulfilled his divine assignment and died. That's a good report. No man should die until he has fulfilled why he came. We rarely can preach a balanced doctrinal message without quoting Paul. This kingdom enigma is still preaching on our pulpits simply because he discovered purpose. Towards the end of his life when he had finished his assignment, he said *"I have fought a good fight, I have finished my course, I have kept the faith: Henceforth there is laid up for me a crown of righteousness, which the Lord, the righteous judge, shall give me at that day: and not to me only, but unto all them also that love his appearing*",2Tim4:7-8.

*Purpose is what gives meaning to life.*  Just like any product in the market, it is the manufacturer that defines the purpose of the product. You wouldn't know the purpose of a product except it is defined by the manufacturer. God is our maker. He holds the blue print for our lives. *Purpose cannot be sought for with human method, it can only be revealed by God*. You need to ask him: *"Call to me and I will answer you and tell you great and unsearchable things you do not know*",Jer33:3.  Degrees are good but it only brings fulfillment when it aligns with God's purpose for your life. Why  many people are not fulfilled in their endeavour and pursuit is because they're on the wrong path. It is impossible to be on track and not enjoy what you're doing. Walking outside God's plan and purpose keeps you in the proverbial rat race. "You start living the day you discover your life's purpose and start pursuing it.*



*PRAYER*: Father, reveal your purpose for my life to me so that I can live a meaningful life in Jesus name.

DEVOTIONAL 0005 | Source start line: 607
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
12/24/20, 7:33 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: PROJECTSAVE DEVOTIONAL
Thursday,
December 24th, 2020.

*HE WAS CONCERNED ABOUT POSTERITY*

Judges 2:10 After that whole generation had been gathered to their ancestors, another generation grew up who knew neither the Lord nor what he had done for Israel.

In one of our outreaches, I was privileged to preach to an old man. I won't be wrong to say he was close to death because he was frail and had lost his sight. This man listened to the gospel with rapt attention. He was willing to give his life to Christ but he asked if accepting Christ will permit him to continue in ancestral worship. We told him it is impossible. He told us that the idol in his custody was handed down to him by his parents and that he must hand same over to his son. Ancestral worship was a legacy of some sort in his family.

He doesn't want idol worship to become extinct in his family. His concern was  about posterity. Even though I wasn't happy about his refusal to accept Jesus, I picked a lesson from that encounter. We can pass on a legacy of faith in Christ to the next generation. Talking about Timothy, Paul said, *"I am reminded of your sincere faith, which first lived in your grandmother Lois and in your mother Eunice and, I am persuaded, now lives in you also*", 2Tim1:5. We are not saying salvation can be passed down to children like a property. What we mean is that we  can train them in the way of Lord.

Europe has become a post Christian continent. A generation was on  fire for God to the extent that they sent missionaries to almost all the countries of the world. However, they failed to pass on the legacy of faith to the next generation. Their big cathedrals are now empty, they've become another relics of history. If an idol worshipper is futuristic in thinking so that his idol worship can continue, how much more those of us that profess the true faith.


*PRAYER*: Father, I will not fail in this regard in Jesus name.

DEVOTIONAL 0006 | Source start line: 662

12/24/20, 11:16 PM - Lanre Oyeleke: PROJECTSAVE DEVOTIONAL,
Friday,
December 25th, 2020.

*THE PURPOSE OF HIS BIRTH*

Matthew1:21 And she will have a son, and you are to name him Jesus, for he will save his people from their sins.” NLT

Every December 25th, we celebrate the birth of Jesus Christ. Some orthodox churches celebrate theirs on January 7th. As an aside, the issue of Christ's birth and the date has been highly debated among scholars. Also, the pagan root and history behind Christmas season has divided opinions. On the two sides of the divide, there are good Christians with their convictions.

I don't intend to take sides with anyone. Rather, I believe we should examine the purpose of his birth and seek to make it a reality in our lives and in others regardless of when we celebrate his birth. *God has not called us to win arguments but to win souls.*  The angel of God told Mary that Christ will save people from their sins. This is the primary purpose of his coming. Whenever we remember Christ's birth in incarnation, we should be driven to pray for lost souls and to seek ways to win them over to the Lord.

The celebration is only of value if channeled towards the purpose of his birth. Till now, God doesn't want people to perish in their sins. That's the good news. We will do well to tell that good news to those that need to hear it. The wages of sin is still death but the gift of God is eternal life, ROM 6:23. Jesus has come to give eternal life. To those of us that have Christ in our lives, it is a thing of Joy to know that we have eternal life and we're heaven-bound. This new life that God's son brought to us must be given due publicity. Tell someone about Jesus today.


*PRAYER*: Father, I receive the grace to preach the good news to the lost in Jesus name.

DEVOTIONAL 0007 | Source start line: 891
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
12/25/20, 11:56 PM - Lanre Oyeleke: ProjectSave Devotional
Saturday,
December 26th, 2020.

*PASS IT ON*

1 Cor15:3 I passed on to you what was most important and what had also been passed on to me. Christ died for our sins, just as the Scriptures said.  NLT

I wasn't privileged to run in a relay race while in secondary school but I've watched several relay races where batons were passed from one sprinter to the other. Relay race is not complete until the last sprinter receives the baton. In every relay race, the most important thing is the baton. If a sprinter run to the finish line without the baton, such race is null despite coming first. Apostle Paul talked about a message that was passed to him which he passed to others. In fact, he said what he passed to others was most important. That is the message of Christ's death.


The realities of redemption were activated when Christ died and resurrected. In 1Cor15:7, Paul said, *"And if Christ has not been raised, then your faith is a delusion and you are still lost in your sins*" GNT. The death of Christ brought us eternal salvation and now we can confidently say we belong to Christ and we have the hope of reigning with Christ in heaven. As you go round preaching to the lost, tell the world about the purpose of Christ's death. *There wouldn't have been salvation or full forgiveness if Christ hadn't died. The shedding of his blood brought forgiveness to us.*


Eph1:17 says, *"In him we have redemption through his blood, the forgiveness of sins, in accordance with the riches of God's grace*", Hallelujah! The world is seeking God's forgiveness through rituals and religious piety. Hey! That will amount to nothing. Sometimes ago, I watched the video of a young man that went to the Ganges river to bathe so that his sins might be forgiven. All religious rituals done to earn God's forgiveness and favour will end the moment they understand the benefit of his death.The message about the wonders of his death is what you should pass on like a baton. The benefit of Christ's death is part of the good news that we must preach to the lost. Pass it on.

*CONFESSION:* Father, I thank you because my salvation was made possible because of Christ's death. I thank you because I'm an heir of the father and joint heirs with the son. I receive grace to pass this message on those that need to hear it in Jesus name. Amen



N:B We desire to give field missionaries cash gifts for Christmas. As you plan for Christmas, don't forget the missionaries on the field.


info@projectsaveng.org

DEVOTIONAL 0008 | Source start line: 966

Sunday,
December 27th, 2020.


*ANAGKAZO*


Luke 14:23 “Then the master told his servant, ‘Go out to the roads and country lanes and compel them to come in, so that my house will be full. NIV


Jesus told the story about a man who prepared a feast. He sent invitation to several guests but they refused to come. This man told his servant to go into the street to compel people to come to the banquet. The Greek word "Anagkazo" was used in vs 23 as compel. Anagkazo mean to compel, constrain or to use entreaties. In matters of soul winning, God is expecting us to "Anagkazo" souls into the kingdom. That we failed at it yesterday doesn't mean we won't succeed today.





A young man who once fixed cable t.v. for me practiced "Anagkazo" on me and my wife.  Any time he comes around to work for me, he'll find a way to tell me about his prophet. He has a strong faith in this prophet and his ministry to the point where he'll spend productive hours talking about him. On some occasions, he told me to watch the live telecast of his prophet. He didn't have the message of God's grace to tell the world, he doesn't emphasize Christ and his redemptive work on the cross yet he has a way of compelling people into error pointing them to a mere mortal. How much more those of us that have the true message of the kingdom.


This is not the time to keep silent. Get on the streets, go to the villages, and tell the world about the redeeming power of this Jesus Christ who died and resurrected. In 2Kings 7:9, those lepers said, *"Then they said to each other, "What we're doing is not right. This is a day of good news and we are keeping it to ourselves. If we wait until daylight, punishment will overtake us. Let's go at once and report this to the royal palace*". Brother, today is the day of good news, don't keep it. Jesus is coming soon and we must win as much as we can to the Lord before he comes. Let's " Anagkazo" them into the kingdom.


*PRAYER*: Father, I receive the grace to devote myself to the great commission like never before in Jesus name.

DEVOTIONAL 0009 | Source start line: 1144
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
12/27/20, 11:17 PM - Lanre Oyeleke: PROJECTSAVE DEVOTIONAL
Monday,
December 28th, 2020.

*BEWARE OF SIMONY*

Acts8:20 Peter answered: "May your money perish with you, because you thought you could buy the gift of God with money! NIV

Simony is the term that comes from Simon Magus, the magician who tried to buy the power to bestow miracles from the Apostles (Acts 8:18). Simony is the practice of buying or selling of something spiritual or closely connected with the spiritual. Simon Magus thought God's power could be bought with money but he was totally wrong. Thank God Peter was quick to let him know God's power and anointing can't be bought with paper currency. If Peter was covetous, he could have told Simon Magus to sow into the anointing to attract the anointing as some do today.


I see alot of people deceived into Simony today. Telling someone to sow into the anointing to attract the anointing is not different from what Simon Magus attempted to do. Thank God Peter put things straight as a record for this deceived generation that is constantly told to sow into the anointing to attract the anointing. *Spiritual things are priceless and can't be bought with money. The moment you understand this, you'll be free from manipulations.* I have read several cases of people who were deceived to sow a seed to attract a particular anointing. They sold their properties or borrowed. When they didn't get the promised result, they felt deceived while some demanded a refund. *If anointing can be bought with money, only the rich would be anointed.*




There are those who will tell unfounded stories about how someone sowed into their lives and got miracle money. Most of these are gimmicks to empty people's pocket. I am not saying God doesn't bless people miraculously but principally, money is made by principle which is why unbelievers can make money without fraud. Our blessing in Christ is spiritual and the blood of Christ purchased it for us, *"Praise be to the God and Father of our Lord Jesus Christ, who has blessed us in the heavenly realms with every spiritual blessing in Christ, *"Ephesians 1:3. Beware of merchants on the pulpit. If you have to pay a certain amount to attract a kind of blessing, just know it is not from the throne of grace. *The price for anointing is paid with personal consecration not with human currency.*

*PRAYER*: I refuse to be deceived in Jesus name. Amen

DEVOTIONAL 0010 | Source start line: 1201

12/29/20, 3:34 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: ProjectSave Devotional
Tuesday
December 29th, 2020.

*RECOVERY IS POSSIBLE*

1Sam30:18 David recovered everything the Amalekites had taken, including his two wives.

This year has been topsy-turvy for so many  folks. Even in developed nations with strong economies, there has been an outcry of pains, woes and death. Watching the t.v.during the heat of the pandemic, I saw despair and frustration written over the faces of people. In my part of the world, hunger and financial loss is the cry of many. So many lost their jobs, businesses crumbled and things fell apart in many homes.

A woman told me she lost millions due to the lock down. So many are beginning to look at next year for a fresh start. They have lost hope about the possibility of attaining their goals this year. I'm reminded of how the Amalekites plundered the camp of David. Valiant men lost host hope and were driven to the point of tears: *"So David and his men wept aloud until they had no strength left to weep*", 1 Samuel 30:4 NIV. Your case may be like that of David and his company who lost everything temporarily except their lives.


I am here as a merchant of hope today to tell you that recovery is possible. *"For there is hope of a tree, if it be cut down, that it will sprout again, and that the tender branch thereof will not cease*", Job14:7. What the enemy has stolen, plundered and forcefully taken from you shall be restored to you. The remaining days of this year shall be days of recovery. That's what I hear in my spirit. *"Instead of your shame you will receive a double portion, and instead of disgrace you will rejoice in your inheritance. And so you will inherit a double portion in your land, and everlasting joy will be yours,*" Isaiah 61:7. This is the word of the Lord.



*PRAYER*: Father, I recover all the enemy has stolen from me since the year began and I receive double recovery for all my losses in Jesus name.

RECOVERY STRATEGY: DIVINE DIRECTION

1Sam30:8 and David inquired of the Lord , “Shall I pursue this raiding party? Will I overtake them?” “Pursue them,” he answered. “You will certainly overtake them and succeed in the rescue.” NIV

A preacher in the U.S. was instructed by the Lord to buy a piece of land. He was reluctant about it but he bought the land eventually. Not long after, oil  was discovered on that land. The implication is that he'll get paid heavily because of the crude oil found there. He made a huge fortune from that land.  If he had been struggling financially for years, obedience to a divine instruction terminated that struggle in his life. David was mighty in strength yet the enemy raided his tent and that of his company.

What brought about recovery for himself and his company was divine direction. He inquired from God to know what ought to be done. Your pursuit will make sense if you're led by God. A lot will run the race of recovery as they have always done but will fail. The strategies of recovery is from God and not man. You need to be plugged to the flow of heaven's instruction per time.

Psalm32:8 *I will instruct you and teach you in the way you should go; I will counsel you with my loving eye on you*. This is the promise of God to you. In the kingdom of God, we don't think somethings out, we hear them out. Strategies of spiritual warfare are not the same all the time but the principles are unchanging. In the battle for recovery,  God may lead you to do something 'insignificant' from the human point of view. As you pray, ask the Lord for your own recovery strategy. He'll speak to you.


*PRAYER* : Father, reveal my recovery strategies to me in Jesus name.

DEVOTIONAL 0012 | Source start line: 1372
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
12/30/20, 11:02 PM - Lanre Oyeleke: PROJECTSAVE DEVOTIONAL
Thursday,
December 31st, 2020.

*BEFORE 11:59PM*

Eccl7:8 Better is the end of a thing than the beginning thereof: and the patient in spirit is better than the proud in spirit.

Pastor E.A.Adeboye told the story of how some people received new cars some minutes to the end of a particular year. In the cross over service of that particular year, he made an altar call for those that believe they could get a car before the end of the year. Few persons walked forward believing they could get a new car before entering into the new year. Right there in that service,  car keys were given to those that came out for the altar call. They got a surprise package for the end of the year.

This year hasn't ended until the clock reads 12am. At 11:59pm, great miracles can happen in your life. There are surprises, blessings, miracles and favours that are packaged for the end. Heaven doesn't  roll over what belongs to us. It is our faithlessness that postpones our miracles. I see a lot of people giving up on the year. They have pushed their expectations into the new year. So many have lost hope. Listen to the word of the Lord, *"At least there is hope for a tree: If it is cut down, it will sprout again, and its new shoots will not fail*",Job 14:7 NIV. As long as you're alive, there's hope. In few hours, great things can happen.

If you can't believe God for a surprise package before the year runs out, it may be hard believing him in the new year. I have decided not to look down on God's power. In man's calendar, things may look late but with God, this is the right time. There's no lateness with God. As you step out, i want you to believe God for an arrears of all that is due you since the beginning of the year. The fulfilment of Joseph's dream was delayed for 17years but in less than 24 hours, he was enthroned. 24 hours is too much for God to turn your life around. God is still in the business of giving out surprise packages at the end of the year, take yours by faith.


*PRAYER*: Father, I take all that belongs to me(...specify) this year today. I am not carrying my good expectations over into the next year. I claim the blessings due me this year.  No carry over as I cross over in Jesus name

DEVOTIONAL 0013 | Source start line: 1510
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
1/1/21, 12:20 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: PROJECTSAVE DEVOTIONAL,
Friday,
January 1st, 2021

*SHAPING YOUR YEAR* Pt.1

Rev 22:13 I am the Alpha and the Omega, the First and the Last, the Beginning and the End.” NLT



I welcome you to a brand new year in the name of the Lord. It shall be a year of supernatural happenings in your favour in Jesus name. Like every other year, this year has 365 days, 8760 hours, 525600 minutes and 3153600 seconds except in a leap year. Every year present us with the value of time. Contrary to popular beliefs and opinion, the determinant of what happens in any calendar year is not the year but humans who pass through the year. Each year only present to us the value of time.



It is up to you to determine what you'll do with the time you have. That's why I don't believe time is going. Time remains because it has been before you were born. It is humans that are growing old and going to the grave not time. No man has been buried with time. Time will continue to exist till it becomes unquantifiable in eternity. Time remains an ageless tool. God has given you the power to shape your year and to make it count by doing the right thing with time.

The very first thing I want you to do with time onward is to find time for God daily and never leave him out of the affairs of your life. *"Seek the Lord while you can find him. Call on him now while he is near*", Isaiah 55:6 NLT. Don't sail without Jesus in your boat. Peter toiled all night trying to catch fish but he caught nothing. The moment Jesus stepped in, he had a net-breaking catch, Luke5.  You can't afford to spend time on things that has no value like you've done in previous years. You need to re order your priorities to create that time of fellowship with God. This is a vital key that can shape your year.

*PRAYER*: Father, this year, I receive the grace to give you quality time in the secret place in Jesus name.

DEVOTIONAL 0014 | Source start line: 1604
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
1/2/21, 6:16 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: ProjectSave Devotional
Saturday,
January 2nd, 2021.

*SHAPING YOUR YEAR* Pt.2

Heb10:25 Not forsaking the assembling of ourselves together, as the manner of some is; but exhorting one another: and so much the more, as ye see the day approaching. KJV

The importance of a place of worship cannot be overemphasized. In shaping your year, you need to be part of a local church where you can draw spiritual nourishment. We live in the days of digital church. There's a  high proliferation of online services and Christian programs on the internet. In a sense, this is good because it helps us to spread the gospel to people we may not be able to reach physically.


However, it has certain limitations which points to why meeting physically is important. For example, the issue of distraction while listening or watching an online service is a major set back. In physical meetings, there seems to be some control put in place to ensure maximum concentration. That doesn't mean you cannot discipline yourself to focus but more often than not, it is easier said than done. Also, the issue of network connection may pose a great challenge to us. There are villages without strong network for online streaming of services. We cannot exempt people in such places from worship because of modernization.

Dearest, you cannot grow fully into fullness of Christ if you're not properly fed with the word in a local assembly. If all you do is to hop from place to place on sundays or hook on to any online gathering in the name of fellowship, your spiritual growth will be stunted. You need to be under a true shepherd who can care, nurture and provide the necessary support for your spiritual well-being. Let truancy in God's house end with last year. Turn a new leaf this year.

*PRAYER*: Father, I receive the grace to identify and participate with a local body of believers for spiritual nourishment in Jesus name.

*SHAPING YOUR YEAR* Pt.3

This year, I want you to live daily with the consciousness that Christ can come any time.



*PRAYER*: Father, I receive the grace to live ready for your coming in Jesus name.

*SHAPING YOUR YEAR* Pt.4

Mark16:15 And he said unto them, Go ye into all the world, and preach the gospel to every creature.

One morning while in the university, the Spirit of God led me to preach in the front of a hostel. I told my room mate to join me. It was very early in the morning when the skies were dark. I preached in the front of a block of hostel while my room mate preached in another location within the same hostel. At day break, we heard the voices and footsteps of angry boys walking towards  our room. They chided us for preaching in front of their hostel. At the time they entered my room, my room mate was ironing his clothes. As one of them wanted to inflict injury on him with the hot iron, I spoke out. I told them to face me and not him because I was the one that preached in front of their block.

Their leader began to tell me how his sister married a bishop and his involvement in church. He justified himself with those things.  My conclusion was that he was either a backslider or he was never saved.  Those guys were known for their notoriety in the school. I wouldn't be wrong to call them cultists because they were rumoured to be in a confraternity. Not long after, this young man died mysteriously. I was told he got burnt alone in a house. I wasn't happy he died because I knew he was the reason why the Holy Spirit sent me to preach that morning. The words that flowed out of my mouth were words of warning and call to repentance.

Do not joke with evangelism this year. *God may be sending you to rescue someone who is about crossing into Christless eternity.* Failure to deliver the message would make God require the blood of that person from your hands. God said, *"When I say unto the wicked, O wicked man, thou shalt surely die; if thou dost not speak to warn the wicked from his way, that wicked man shall die in his iniquity; but his blood will I require at thine hand*", Ezekiel 33:8 KJV. *The command is "Go ye" not "Stay ye" or "look ye".* Talk to people about Jesus any where and everywhere. Do not be at ease seeing souls on their way to hell: *"Woe to them that are at ease in Zion*", Amos6:1.

*PRAYER*: Father, I receive the grace to preach Jesus to the lost. Give me a bountiful harvest of souls this year in Jesus name.

*SHAPING YOUR YEAR* Pt.5

Matt29:19 Go ye therefore, and teach all nations, baptizing them in the name of the Father, and of the Son, and of the Holy Ghost:KJV

Dr Morris Cerullo of blessed memory shared a story. He traveled to a country not far from the U.S. He became thirsty so he felt should take a drink. As he walked the streets, he saw the advert of Coca- Cola in front of a shop. He bought a bottle of coke. As he was about drinking it, he asked the vendor through his interpreter if he knew about Jesus. The vendor asked  if Jesus was another brand of drink like Coca-Cola. Dr Cerullo said he couldn't drink his coke again because he was shocked that a man living in a country not far from United States of America ( supposedly a Christian country) haven't heard about Jesus.

This year, God want you to be involved in his global mission of taking the gospel to places  where little or nothing is known about Christ. People from such places are called unreached people groups. It is not their choice to be unreached neither is it because they rejected the gospel message. They are largely unreached because no one has taken the gospel message to them.  There are thousands of such places today on earth. In those places, people will live and die if no one takes the gospel message there. That's why you and I should be involved in missions more than ever.

There is a wide open door of going to the field as a missionary. God can call you to reach the unreached peoples as a missionary, do not hesitate to obey. *The only begotten son of God was sent to the world as a missionary for the salvation of many. It won't be a misnomer if other adopted sons of God like you me spread the message among those that haven't heard.* You can also send missionaries to such places through your giving. Consider your money as God's resources entrusted to you. When God makes a demand for it, just release it. You're meant to be a pipe through which resources flow out for kingdom purposes. You shouldn't be a storage tank. Jesus said, *"Lay not up for yourselves treasures upon earth, where moth and rust doth corrupt, and where thieves break through and steal:   But lay up for yourselves treasures in heaven, where neither moth nor rust doth corrupt, and where thieves do not break through nor steal:*", Matthew 6:19-20 KJV. Let what is in your hand flow out for God's missions, that's one of the ways to store treasure in heaven.

*PRAYER*: Father, I receive the grace to be fully involved in missions this year in Jesus name.

*SHAPING YOUR YEAR* Pt. 6

Proverbs 13:20 Walk with the wise and become wise; associate with fools and get in trouble.NLT

A wise man said, *"If you walk with 5 wise men, you're likely to be the 6th. If you walk with 5 millionaires, you're likely to be the 6th*". What this wise man is trying to say is that we are a reflection of the company we keep. As the saying goes, *"Your association determines your transformation*".  The bible says, *"Don’t be fooled by those who say such things, for “bad company corrupts good character*”, 1Cor 15:33 NLT.

The counsel Amnon received from his friend was his undoing. He was surrounded by a shrewd friend named Jonadab who gave him a counsel that led to his death, 2Sam13:3. The Psalmist knew the power of association. In one of his writings he said, *"I have not sat with vain persons, neither will I go in with dissemblers*", Psalms26:4. Whether we like to admit or not, we all are products of our association and we operate under the influence of someone or the association we belong to.


You can shape your year correctly by doing an audit of your company. You have the power to determine those you'll walk with and cut-off from those that aren't leading you towards the actualization of your goals and vision. Friendship is not by force, it should be by choice. You can be successful by association and can fail because of wrong association. Start associating with men and women going in your direction. Walk with those who can help you attain your desired goal. Don't waste time staying in the company of fools lest you become one.

*PRAYER*: Father, I sever from unprofitable association in Jesus name

*SHAPING YOUR YEAR* Pt.7

Proverbs1:5 A wise man will hear, and will increase learning; and a man of understanding shall attain unto wise counsels: KJV

When I wanted to enrol in the seminary, I needed a senior Pastor in my church to sign for me as a refree. When I presented the form to him, he asked to know if I needed to enrol in the seminary. To him, he felt I don't need it probably because he had heard me preach and teach. Also, i have preached in several places within and without with the manifestation of God's power. All these may be the reason he asked to know if I needed a formal training in scriptures.  However, deep within me, there was a hunger to learn and know God's word more. I enrolled and I became better for it. The things I thought I knew back then were put in the right perspective for me and my eyes opened to a lot of things I didn't know. One of the ways to shape your year is through continuous learning. Wise people commit themselves to learning and not just that but continuous learning whether formal or informal. A wise man said, *"The more you learn the more you'll discover you don't know*".

In my personal opinion, one of the things that prevent people from learning is pride. A proud man will never submit himself to learn especially from those he thinks are below him in status whereas most teachers do not always have the "status" yet they know what they know and can impart the knowledge to their learners to attain "status". Do you notice that the world celebrates star footballers more than coaches? Yet, we know that no matter how talented a player is, it takes the help of a coach for him to attain stardom. The day he refuse to listen to the tactics and game plan of the coach, that's the day he'll start having problem.*You may have height but you need men of depth to teach you and help you solidify your rising.*


Do not waste your year by operating on the same level of knowledge you operated on last year. Go and learn. There are books you can read that'll make you better and increase your productivity on the job, ministry and business. As the saying goes, *"The day you stop learning you'll start dying*". You can never get to the point in your life where learning will stop except at death. It has been discovered that top billionaires in the world are readers and they're given to continuous learning. Also, excellent leaders have also been discovered to be avid readers. When one of the former president of U.S.died, the last book he read was found under his pillow. Little wonder why that nation is great. Jesus learnt through study. He said, *"Then said I, Lo, I come (in the volume of the book it is written of me,) to do thy will, O God*", Heb10:7. How did he know what was written about him if he hadn't read? One of the reasons Apostle Paul was exceptional was because he was given to continuous learning. Talking to Timothy, he said, *"The cloke that I left at Troas with Carpus, when thou comest, bring with thee, and the books, but especially the parchments*", 2Tim4:13. There'll always be a difference between a man that is committed to learning and the man that is not. Keep learning, get trained.

*PRAYER*: Father, I receive the grace for continuous learning in Jesus name.

*SHAPING YOUR YEAR* Pt.8

Prov27:8 As a bird that wandereth from her nest, so is a man that wandereth from his place. KJV


William Wilberforce was a human right activist. He wanted to go into pastoral ministry. He informed John Newton, a former slave dealer, who after his conversion to Christ composed the popular hymn, 'Amazing Grace'. He advised him against going into pastoral ministry. *"You need to spend your life doing one thing, the thing God has called you to do and that you feel, in your heart is necessary*", John Newton said.  With this counsel, William Wilberforce dedicated his life for the abolition of slavery. That was his undying passion.



Just fifty years after, in 1833, while he was on his death bed, a messenger whispered to him, "Sir, the abolition has been approved!" He heaved a sigh of relief for a few moments before he died, he remarked, *"I finished well*". David Brainerd lived his entire life as a missionary- intercessor for the Red Indians. He lived his life majorly on intercession and died at the age of 29. Before he died, he said, *"Oh that I had a thousand lives to give in a cause so glorious*". These men lived for one thing all their lives and they left their footprint in the sand of times.


Decide to put your all into one thing that is in line with your divine design. It is called purpose. You have a purpose for existence. You're not an accident of creation. *You are not an after thought in God's mind.* He shot you into the world like an arrow to hit a target. That target could be the people he has sent you to and the things he want you to do. There are so many displaced humans on the face of the earth that are not walking on the path God intended for them. They move with the generality thereby walking out of their path in life. To shape this year and years to come, pay the price to know God's purpose for your life and start fulfilling it. Remember the counsel of John Newton.

*PRAYER*: Holy Spirit, reveal to me why I am on earth and help me to fulfill my assignment in Jesus name.

*SHAPING YOUR YEAR* Pt.9

*SHAPING YOUR YEAR* Pt.10


Isaiah 54:13 And all thy children shall be taught of the LORD; and great shall be the peace of thy children.




In a movie, there was a girl who was lured into a female cult group. She wanted to inform her father about her struggles but he was so busy and didn't have her time at all. Obviously, the mother was also busy. Eventually, this lady was initiated into the cult group and her life was ruined. Though a movie, these things happen in reality.




To shape your year, you must have time for your family. You must be intentional about it. Abraham was rich in cattles and in the material things of his days, this presupposes that he was a business man yet God was proud about how his availability to teach his family: *"For I know him, that he will command his children and his household after him, and they shall keep the way of the Lord , to do justice and judgment; that the Lord may bring upon Abraham that which he hath spoken of him*", Gen 18:19 KJV. This year, don't be an absentee father or mother. Aside teaching your children the ways of the Lord, when you are around for your children, it affects their psyche positively. They
don't feel neglected or forgotten. More so, as they see you live the christian life, they'll begin to imitate you. Children learn more from what we do than what we say. When you teach them, you must be on ground to model what you teach. That's complete parenting. No matter how busy you are, find quality time for your children. This will help this year and in years to come.


*PRAYER*: Father, I receive the grace to pay attention to my family in Jesus name.

*SHAPING YOUR YEAR* Pt.11

Ecclesiastes9:10 Whatever your hand finds to do, do it with all your might, for in the realm of the dead, where you are going, there is neither working nor planning nor knowledge nor wisdom.

Two men went to a diviner. They visited him to check their fortunes. The first was told that he'll be very rich. He was told that he would swim in wealth and become a sought after. The second friend was told that he'll be poor. In fact, the diviner said his poverty will make history. These two men left the diviner with different feelings. The first man was extremely happy while the second was sad. Very sad. When the first friend got home, he told his wife and family. They were happy. He decided to throw a party. He abandoned work and he began to live like a king expecting miracle money.


The other man thought to himself that he'll fight poverty with his last breath. He relocated to another village and he began to farm with all his might. After sometime, he got a breakthrough in farming. He became so wealthy to the extent that he could afford to feed the whole village. However, his friend's life had deteriorated in poverty. He became beggarly and couldn't afford to feed. He lived on the goodwill of his rich friend till he died.


This year, don't joke with work. Even in the garden of Eden where all things were made ready and supplied for Adam, God gave him a work to do. Man was designed to work even before the fall. Work was not invented after the fall. This year, don't be lazy. God's promise is to bless the work of your hands not your loitering around. I'm yet to see money come down from heaven like rain. Show great commitment to your business and if you're under a boss, be diligent in your work. Stop going  late to work with the excuse of "I went to a vigil". Learn to strike a balance.

*PRAYER*: Father, I receive grace to be diligent in Jesus name.

*SHAPING YOUR YEAR* Pt.12

Ecclesiastes9:10 Whatever your hand finds to do, do it with all your might, for in the realm of the dead, where you are going, there is neither working nor planning nor knowledge nor wisdom.

When I started using a vehicle, my experience with automobile technicians was not palatable. I've been lied to and exploited several times. After writing my final exams in the university, I waited for seven months for my NYSC call-up letter. If I had known, I would have acquired a skill in automobile repairs during those waiting  periods. I discovered that the guys who repaired my vehicle were almost always busy repairing a vehicle and making money. What they make in a week is more than what some salary earners are making in a month save that most of them do not have a saving culture.


After that experience, my eyes opened to the need for skill acquisition. One of the things you may need to do to shape your year going forward may be to acquire a skill you can monetize or trade with. We live in an economy where white collar jobs can't go round considering the number of graduates our tertiary institutions churn out every year. Even in advanced nations, government do not provide jobs for everyone except that they provide an enabling environment for businesses to thrive.


Regardless of the nature of  your job, you can learn a skill so that you'll have an additional stream of income. A wise man said everyone must have at least four sources of income. Even if you work as a salary earner, you can offer certain services after work or during weekends that will put extra cash in your pocket using the skill you have acquired. There is nothing to be ashamed of if the services you're offering is legitimate even if it is Uber driving as long as your bills are paid. You owe no man any explanation. Acquire a skill, offer a service to people  that will open up another stream of income for you. That way, you'll be shaping your finances for this year and years to come.

*PRAYER*: I receive wisdom for my financial life in Jesus name.

*SHAPING YOUR YEAR* Pt.14

Proverbs 13:22: “A good man leaves an inheritance to his children's children.” (NKJV)

One of the extremes my uncle told me about was that they were told not to have more than N3500 in their account because their money would waste when Christ comes. *SHAPING YOUR YEAR* Pt.15

Prov1:20 Wisdom shouts in the streets. She cries out in the public square. NLT

Reading through the bible, I have seen that the lessons in it are applicable to all areas of our lives, finance inclusive. Jesus talked about "counting the cost". In finance, they call it planning and budgeting. He said, *"For which of you, intending to build a tower, sitteth not down first, and counteth the cost, whether he have sufficient to finish it?*", Luke 14:28. We can  infer that Jesus was an advocate of proper planning and budgeting in financial matters.



One of the things you need do to shape your finances this year is to plan and budget properly for your income and expenses. He that fails to plan has planned fail. Avoid buying on impulse no matter how good it seems. Sometimes,  one of the reasons for lack isn't lack of good income but improper planning and lack of discipline on spending. One of the ways to know a man that lacks financial discipline is impulsive buying. Marketers are trained to compel you to buy what you don't need. They can create an artificial hunger or thirst in you for their products only for you to realize later that you've wasted your money.

Further, the trap of buy now pay later puts people under financial slavery. Avoid it. Some salary earners buy clothes, shoes and other items on credit. When salary is paid, the sales representative is on ground to collect his/her share. In some cases, nothing is left to cater for the family.  *"The rich ruleth over the poor, and the borrower is servant to the lender*", Prov 22:7. Do not enter into a financial commitment that is beyond your financial capacity.  Live in a house you can afford, put your children in a school you can afford, buy the car you can maintain and wear what you can afford. Trying to keep up with the Joneses makes one to be broke. Be wise.


*PRAYER*: Father, I receive the grace to be wise in my finances in Jesus name.

*SHAPING YOUR YEAR* Pt.16


3John2 Beloved, I wish above all things that thou mayest prosper and be in health, even as thy soul prospereth. KJV


*Wealth is useless without good health*. There was a young preacher that was mightily used by God in Europe over a century ago. At a very young age, he had shaken his world. However, at 29, he died. On his death bed, he said, *"God gave me a message and a horse. I destroyed the horse so i couldn't deliver the message.*" The horse was his body while the message was the gospel he preached. He couldn't fulfill his evangelistic assignment to the fullest because he didn't take care of his health.

Another way you can shape your year is to take good care of your health. Most believers hide under "faith" to disregard simple health warnings and tips. I have seen folks who talk faith and boast in faith yet they took Ill. Could it be that God doesn't honour faith? I'm 100% sure he does. However, when faith is mixed with foolishness, carelessness, irresponsibility and ignorance, God stays away from such.  *There's nothing God has done or will do for mankind without leaving room for man to play his own part.* God is ever faithful to his promises but man is prone to failing in his own duties.

Do not joke with your health this year. Without good health, you have no ministry. Without good health, forget wealth. Whatever your goals are and those lofty dreams of yours, the absence of good health will make them a pipe dream. Whenever you need to rest, please rest and stop jumping around. Eat right, drink clean water( not soda) and exercise your body. Don't wait till you're sick before seeing the medics. You can do a comprehensive medical check up once in a year. That's not a sign of faithlessness but wisdom. As much as you can, learn about healthy living through study.

*PRAYER*: Father, I receive grace to live healthy in Jesus name

*SHAPING YOUR YEAR* Pt.17


Gen13:14 And the Lord said unto Abram, after that Lot was separated from him, Lift up now thine eyes, and look from the place where thou art northward, and southward, and eastward, and westward: KJV



A wealthy man decided to give $1,000, 000 to a worthy cause in the kingdom.  He approached his Pastor and he asked to know what he would do if the church receives a $1,000,000 donation. His pastor replied by saying, "I'll fix the toilets". The rich man was curious to know if that was all the Pastor had in mind to do with $1,000,000. The Pastor couldn't find a meaningful use for that huge donation. This rich man asked to know what the budget of fixing the toilet was. He handed the token needed for fixing toilet to the Pastor.

This rich man kept his $1,000,000. He was driving round town one day, he over heard another Pastor talking about their plans to organize a city-wide crusade that will harvest souls and to build another church auditorium with a larger capacity to contain both old and incoming members. The rich man saw that as a worthy cause worth supporting. He approached the Pastor of that church and handed over the $1,000,000 to the church for the crusade and new church auditorium. This is no fiction.



I picked a few lessons from that story. The size of your vision determines the size of resources that will flow towards you. The man that had a toilet-size vision got the resources for toilet repairs. The man that had a city-wide vision got the resources for his vision. The quality of your vision determines the quality of resources that will flow towards it. This year, expand your vision. You can shape your year by re accessing your vision, the scope and coverage, and what is needed to actualize it. Per adventure you're done with a phase in your vision, feel free to catch another vision and run with it. However, don't forget to cast a vision that only God can help you fulfill because he's the source of true vision. If it is not greater than you and your ability, it is not from God.


*PRAYER*: Father, enlarge my vision in Jesus name.

*BE VULNERABLE IN HIS PRESENCE*

Matthew 26: 39 And he went a little farther, and fell on his face, and prayed, saying, O my Father, if it be possible, let this cup pass from me: nevertheless not as I will, but as thou wilt.

I love Jesus. He is our perfect example. He is the messiah. In his incarnation, he expressed how he felt to God without shame. Despite the fact that he is God, he didn't hesitate to tell his father the thoughts running through his mind when it was time to be crucified. He was destined to die a shameful death for the salvation of mankind. The bible called him the lamb that was slain before the foundation of the world, Rev 13:8. He knew about this but when he was faced with the reality of what was written about him in the books, he said, *"...O my Father, if it be possible, let this cup pass from me: nevertheless not as I will, but as thou wilt.*"

In fact, he said to his disciples: *"...My soul is exceeding sorrowful, even unto death: tarry ye here, and watch with me.*" Matthew 26:38 KJV. Hearing this statement from the mouth of Jesus,  not many faith preachers of our day will call him a man of faith. We often misunderstand faith to mean not owning up about our weaknesses, pains and our feelings.  Most times, we come into God's presence with a mask over our feelings because someone told us not to acknowledge those feelings as a sign of faith. We try to sing and dance like everyone, but something is eating us deep within.


Dearest, you cannot be faith-filled more than Jesus, yet, he told his father in heaven how he felt when he was praying in the garden of Gethsemane. He wanted the cup of suffering to pass over him because he already envisaged the pain. Sometimes ago, I passed through a tough challenge that made me cry twice. I poured my heart to God without shame. When you come into the presence of your heavenly father, forget your title, remove your mask and be real. Tell him how you feel just the way it is and ask for grace. *"For we have not an high priest which cannot be touched with the feeling of our infirmities; but was in all points tempted like as we are, yet without sin.*" Hebrews 4:15 KJV

*ACTION POINT*: Pour your heart to him in prayer and tell him exactly how you feel.

*BREAK FREE FROM TRADITIONS* Pt.1


Mark7:13 Thus you nullify the word of God by your tradition that you have handed down. And you do many things like that.” NIV


The Pharisees and the scribes asked Jesus why his disciples did not wash their hands before eating *according to the tradition of the elders.* Jesus responded by saying, *"For laying aside the commandment of God, ye hold the tradition of men, as the washing of pots and cups: and many other such like things ye do,* Mark 7:8.  There's something I want us to examine in that text. They( Pharisees & scribes) laid aside God's commandments to fulfill the tradition of the elders thereby nullifying the word of God.

I was privileged to talk to a man who told me it is normal for a man to cheat on his wife. However, he forbade such for his wife. Later, I discovered that his opinion is popular among several men in the society. Cheating is seen as normal or as an adventure which is an exclusive reserve of men. I noticed that when a woman is caught in adultery,  it would be announced from the roof top by men who themselves are guilty of the same but if a man is caught in the act, tradition of men  may exonerate him on the spot saying it is a man's world yet God doesn't excuse such sin for any reason regardless of gender.



Nothing is wrong with the tradition of men as long as it doesn't conflict with the word of God but where there's a conflict, God frowns at it. The tradition of men is the tradition of compromise, fleshly gratification and lust. The believe is that as long as it doesn't affect anyone, it is all well and good. It is a tradition of "the end justifies the means" even if the means is ungodly. Beware of societal norms that are acceptable but stand in opposition to what God's word says. Apostle Paul said,  *"And be not conformed to this world[tradition of men]: but be ye transformed by the renewing of your mind, that ye may prove what is that good, and acceptable, and perfect, will of God*", Romans 12:2 ( Emphasis mine).


*PRAYER*: Father, I receive the grace to break free from negative tradition of men in Jesus name

*BREAK FREE FROM TRADITIONS* Pt.2

Mark7:13 Thus you nullify the word of God by your tradition that you have handed down. And you do many things like that.” NIV

For those that aren't from Africa or third world countries where tribal differences isn't a major concern, they may not understand the weight of this matter.

They inherited the hatred and dysfunctional world view of their progenitors. The Jews were like that in the days of Jesus. They do not want to have anything doing with Gentiles yet salvation is for both Jews and Gentiles.  Break free from tribal limitations, it can limit you in destiny.


*PRAYER*: Father, deliver me from the traditions of men in Jesus name.

*THE MEETING IN ACTS 13:1-3*

Acts 13:2 One day as these men were worshiping the Lord and fasting, the Holy Spirit said, “Appoint Barnabas and Saul for the special work to which I have called them.” NLT

In Acts 13, we were told about certain prophets and teachers who were ministering to the Lord. In the process, the Holy Spirit said: *“Appoint Barnabas and Saul for the special work to which I have called them*”. I want you to note something in this text. The verb 'call' was in past tense- called. This implied that  Paul and Barnabas had received the call into ministry before that very meeting. The prophetic utterance in that gathering was only a confirmation of what Paul and Barnabas knew before the meeting.

There are believers who took  major decisions because they heard "thus saith the Lord" from a prophet or another believer. Often, such decisions are major decisions involving life and their future, yet,  they failed to check the veracity of such prophetic utterances/ revelations with the Holy Spirit. That's a departure from the new testament practice.

As a new testament believer, your guide is the Holy Spirit not a prophet. Prophets shouldn't play the role of the Holy Spirit in your life. Their duty is to confirm what the Spirit has told you. That's why you must have deep intimacy with the Holy Spirit. Even if a prophet/ believer is bringing a new revelation  to you, learn to ask the Holy Spirit to confirm it to you. Never take a major step in life and ministry on the basis of what you were told until you've heard God yourself.


*PRAYER*: Father, I thank you for giving me your Spirit. I receive the grace to hear from you and not be misled in Jesus name.

*PAY THE PRICE FOR SELF DEVELOPMENT*


Prov 23:23 Buy the truth and do not sell it-- wisdom, instruction and insight as well.


I made an enquiry about certain things from someone I thought should know. Due to his limited knowledge about the subject of my enquiry, he referred me to an expert in the field. I called the man on phone and I told him I was referred to him by "Rev XXX". He told me that he sells information and that I'll have to pay for the little information he wanted to give me. I needed that information badly because it will help me to make informed decision. I paid him a token for consultation after he spoke to me and shared the truth about my subject of enquiry.


While that may sound odd to some persons, that goes to show that getting quality information comes at a cost. Even in spiritual things, getting to know certain spiritual truths comes at a price. Paul paid the price for the depth of revelation he wrote in his epistles. He told us that he spent three solid years in  Arabia: *"To reveal his Son in me, that I might preach him among the heathen; immediately I conferred not with flesh and blood:  Neither went I up to Jerusalem to them which were apostles before me; but I went into Arabia, and returned again unto Damascus, Then after three years I went up to Jerusalem to see Peter, and abode with him fifteen days*", Gal 1:16‭-18. What do you think he was doing in Arabia for three years? In my opinion, I think that was where he paid the price for his deep insights into the mysteries of grace.


Dearest, true knowledge comes at a cost. It is either you pay the price of your money or the price of your time. Some persons desire to improve in certain fields or sphere of life but they are unwilling to pay the price. It is painful when believers shun buying of good books, CDs or trainings yet they want to grow. *I'm yet to see a high flyer in any field of endeavour who didn't commit time and money to self development.* When we were young believers, they told us to empty our pocket into our head and some of us did. Today, I am reaping the harvest. *Never let the price/cost of self development becloud the worth of value you'll add.* That's the secret of growth and self development.


*PRAYER*: Father, I receive the grace to pay the price for self development in Jesus name.

*OVERCOMING MASTURBATION* Pt.1


Romans 1:29-30 Being filled with all unrighteousness, fornication, wickedness, covetousness, maliciousness; full of envy, murder, debate, deceit, malignity; whisperers,  Backbiters, haters of God, despiteful, proud, boasters, inventors of evil things, disobedient to parents, KJV

*MY EXPERIENCE*

I masturbated four times. At the time I masturbated, I was already a preacher and teacher of the gospel. Though that was many years ago as a single brother. It was a  very hard battle that I confronted because I felt bad and guilty after each episode of masturbation. The same happens to most children of God who are neck-deep into masturbation. Praise God! I won the battle over masturbation. In this series,  I'll be sharing bible-based principles on how you can overcome masturbation and other addictions.



*What is masturbation*? According to WebMD, masturbation is the self-stimulation of the genitals to achieve sexual arousal and pleasure, usually to the point of orgasm (sexual climax). Many know the act but do not know the name.  In one national study, 95% of males and 89% of females reported that they have masturbated. It is a common vice that has received the endorsement of some medical practitioners. Another study revealed that 90% of men masturbate while 40% of women are involved in the act. I don't know how true it is but my concern is about you becoming victorious over masturbation.


In the opening text, we were told about a people who are perverse, filled with fornication and were inventors of evil. Masturbation is a perversion of God's order for sexual fulfilment. While many folks still contend with the idea of whether masturbation is sinful or not, I want you to know that *lust is the fuel for masturbation.* What does the bible say about lust? *For all that is in the world, the lust of the flesh, and the lust of the eyes, and the pride of life, is not of the Father, but is of the world*, 1 John 2:16. Masturbation is an evil invention that saps spiritual strength and vitality. For you to overcome,  you must realize that it is not normal neither is it God's will for your life. As long as you're still in doubt about whether it is sinful/ not, victory will elude you. Confront and conquer.

*PRAYER*: Father, I receive the grace to overcome masturbation in Jesus name.

*OVERCOMING MASTURBATION* Pt.2

1 John2:16 For all that is in the world, the lust of the flesh, and the lust of the eyes, and the pride of life, is not of the Father, but is of the world. KJV

*IS MASTURBATION MENTIONED IN THE BIBLE AS SINFUL*?

This question is such that has generated controversy even among brethren. I was in a group where the issue of masturbation was discussed years back. A brother asserted that masturbation isn't a sin. Quite a number of people may also believe that masturbation isn't a sin because it wasn't mentioned in the bible. Truly,  all sins are not mentioned explicitly in the bible including masturbation but all sins are categorized under: Lust of the eyes, Lust of the flesh and pride of life. Lust is the engine of all sins- masturbation inclusive. Reading the bible further, offenses against God are put under three major headings and they are: sin- this is simply missing the mark, transgression- breaking of laws, iniquity- perversion.

Encyclopedia Encarta defines perversion as:
(1) A sexual practice regarded as abnormal
(2) A change of something good, true or correct into something bad or wrong.
Masturbation is an abnormal sexual practice, it is not just a sin but an iniquity- a perversion.  *Every act of perversion glorifies the devil because it is a form of worship to the devil.* In ancient Egypt, Atum, was the main deity of the Egyptian pantheon, who existed by himself. They believe that his ejaculation produced the first pair of twin gods, Shu (air god) and Tefnut (moisture goddess). Atum's semen was the origin of the world, and masturbation the act of creation. Egyptians worship Atum by masturbating along river Nile so that their semen will flow with the river. Every act of perversion is rooted in demon worship. That's why the narrative of Paul in Romans 1:23-31 linked idolatry with sexual perversion. Those involved may not know but that's exactly what they are doing as the scriptures says.

I have no way to pamper the truth about this subject matter. The truth about it may be unpopular but I take sides with scriptures. *Masturbation is a sin that is capable of wrecking  destinies and ultimately lead to hell.* I want this truth to settle in your heart. No matter how bad your case is,  you can overcome masturbation and be free. God is still in the business of delivering his children from addictions. An encounter with biblical truth on how to be free and the readiness on your part to apply yourself to it is the key to lasting freedom. In Christ, there's deliverance: *If the Son therefore shall make you free, ye shall be free indeed*, John8:36.


*PRAYER*: Father, I receive the grace to overcome masturbation in Jesus name.

*OVERCOMING MASTURBATION* Pt.6

1 John2:16 For all that is in the world, the lust of the flesh, and the lust of the eyes, and the pride of life, is not of the Father, but is of the world. KJV

*WHY PEOPLE MASTURBATE*

Have you ever wondered why people masturbate? Topmost on the list is heightened sexual tension. Those that masturbate engage in the act to quickly ease sexual tension that is bottled up within. Some persons are burning within. The inferno of lustful thoughts in them can burn a whole building if converted into physical flames. Read about a man sometimes  ago, he ejaculated on the bum of a lady while on a queue in the public. Some persons may excuse themselves from the crowd to a lonely place where they can practice the act. Why? Sexual tension in them is heightened. Sexual tension is built when the mind is filled with the filth of sexual images. Sexual tension is not innate, it is built over time by exposing the mind to the wrong thing. The battle ground is the mind.  King Solomon said, *"Above all else, guard your heart, for everything you do flows from it*", Prov 4:23.

Further, some persons masturbate because they don't want to be physically involved in an affair with someone. This is common to those who do not want to be involved in/ caught "fornicating" with another person. They see it as a "play safe" method without harassment from anyone. On the outside, they may appear spiritual and pious but deep within, they are struggling to quench the flames of sexual tension. Some hinge their reason for masturbation on science saying it is a way to prevent prostate cancer.

None of the reasons given for masturbation is valid in light of scriptures. Excuses build in us a mental stronghold that keeps us on the same spot. There'll always be a reason for everything including masturbation. The deeply rooted mental stronghold is the factory that produces the alibis for this evil act. Paul said: *"The weapons we fight with are not the weapons of the world. On the contrary, they have divine power to demolish strongholds. We demolish arguments and every pretension that sets itself up against the knowledge of God, and we take captive every thought to make it obedient to Christ*", 2 Corinthians 10:4-5 NIV. Now you're learning the way of sexual purity from scriptures. As you open your mind to scriptures, you'll be demolishing the mental strong holds holding you down to this perversion. Flush your mind with the word.

*PRAYER*: I demolish the mental strongholds keeping me in this perversion in Jesus name.

*OVERCOMING MASTURBATION* Pt.11

Romans 12:2 Do not conform to the pattern of this world, but be transformed by the renewing of your mind. Then you will be able to test and approve what God’s will is—his good, pleasing and perfect will. NIV

As you meditate on the word daily, an internal transformation occurs: *"Oh, how I love your law! I meditate on it all day long*", Psalm119:97.

The primary source of where you can find noble things to think about is the word of God. Make a decision to study your bible daily( several times in a day), meditate and concentrate on it. Let your heart be fixed on it. Make notes where necessary, memorize, and pray the lessons you've learnt into your life.  Just as you've taken time to put junks into your mind and concentrate on them, just replace the process with the word. The word of God has purifying power: *"How can a young person stay on the path of purity? By living according to your word"*, Psalm119:9.

*ACTION POINT*: Make the study of God's word a daily habit and stop feeding your mind with whatever fuels lust.

The big question is, how do I starve lust to death?

With the daily intake of God's word for mind renewal as a constant, the vital step to starving lust to death is to discover the trigger for masturbation that  your flesh craves and avoid it. For example, the times I masturbated, I discovered that I had exposed myself to explicit erotic content on the internet. The flesh was comfortable watching those nudes and I fed my mind on it heavily. Masturbation was what resulted. If you're masturbating, you have yours. Be sincere with yourself and point out that very thing that has been feeding lust in your life. You may need to delete those porn videos on your phone and laptop. That t.v.program that arouses lust must be avoided at all cost. If you do this, you'll be starving lust till it dies. Never pamper the flesh with little indulgence here and there. It sums up to strengthen the flesh and make it grow. One may ask, "what about  memories of past exposure to erotic contents that triggers masturbation"? This still happens to very spiritual people. Sometimes, memories of past tries to trigger evil passion in us. In this case, you can deal with this by binding the devil at that very hour. Speak out against that demon and get your mind busy with something productive. It could be the word or an anointed music that connects you with the Spirit.

*ACTION POINT*: Discover the trigger of lust and starve the flesh to death.by avoiding what feeds it.

*MARTHA SYNDROME*

Luke 10:38-39 Now it came to pass, as they went, that he entered into a certain village: and a certain woman named Martha received him into her house.  And she had a sister called Mary, which also sat at Jesus' feet, and heard his word. KJV

The story of Mary and Martha has alot of lessons for us whom the end of the age has come. When Jesus visited, Mary sat at the feet of Jesus  learning. She paid attention to the things Jesus taught but Martha was encumbered with other things that doesn't really matter.  Jesus said: *"...Martha, Martha, thou art careful and troubled about many things:   But one thing is needful: and Mary hath chosen that good part, which shall not be taken away from her*", Luke 10:41-42.


Several times, I've had some persons tell me they don't understand the bible when they read. Some read the bible as a daily tradition but they are not profiting from their study. Most times, Martha syndrome is their problem. Martha paid attention to the things that weren't needful, she was full of activities but not productive spiritually. There are brethren who are active in other seemingly spiritual things but not making progress in their walk with God. *Activity is not the same as productivity*. Mary sat at the feet of Jesus to learn and it paid off for her.

The key to overcoming Martha syndrome is to obey the injunction in the book of Proverbs 4:20-21: *"My son, attend to my words; incline thine ear unto my sayings.  Let them not depart from thine eyes; keep them in the midst of thine heart*". Leave activities and pay attention to God's word. Don't be in a hurry to exit the place of study and prayer. While in church, bring your mind to focus on what the preacher is saying and make notes. If you do these, this is what will happen: *"For they are life unto those that find them, and health to all their flesh*", Proverbs 4:22 KJV.

*PRAYER*: Father, I receive the grace to pay attention to God's word and to learn at the feet of Jesus. I overcome Martha syndrome in Jesus name.

*CONSUMED BY THE PRAISES OF MEN*

Acts 12:21-22 And upon a set day Herod, arrayed in royal apparel, sat upon his throne, and made an oration unto them.  And the people gave a shout, saying, It is the voice of a god, and not of a man.

Every child of God need to learn from the story of Herod Agrippa 1. He had been quarreling with the people of Tyre and Sidon; they now joined together and sought an audience with him through the support of his personal assistant, Blastus. They asked for peace, because they depended on his country for their food supply. On the appointed day, Herod Agrippa wearing his royal robes, sat on his throne and delivered a public address to the people. They shouted, ‘this is the voice of a god, not of a man.’

Instead of him to give God the glory, he allowed the praises of men to get into his head. The bible said, *"And immediately the angel of the Lord smote him, because he gave not God the glory: and he was eaten of worms, and gave up the ghost*",Acts 12:23. As God's children, we need to be careful about the praises of men. One of the ways the devil present to us the opportunity to be proud is when we allow the praises of men get the best of us. They said Herod's speech was the voice of a god not a man. *The moment we fail to realize that our exploits are products of God's grace and not ourselves, we set ourselves up for a life of shame.*

It is dangerous to live for the praises of men. It is even more dangerous to do ministry for the praises of men. Are those facebook and social media praise comments the motivation behind your constant posts? When you sang the last time, what were you expecting the people to say after your ministration? Are you beginning to preach/ teach to impress? The praises of men consumes, learn from Herod. *God can share his power but not his glory.* Please, don't touch the glory that belongs to him even when praise singers are pushing you to do so. Beware of praise singers.


*PRAYER*: Father, I receive the grace to stay humble and never allow the praises of men get the best of me in Jesus name.
