*PRACTICAL KEY TO SUSTAINING LOVE*

Prov 17:9 He that covereth a transgression seeketh love; but he that repeateth a matter separateth very friends. KJV

I would have loved to teach this topic in series but after studying that text, I noticed that the counsel is all encompassing. Any other key given to sustaining love is embedded in the key I'll be sharing in this devotional piece. So what is the key to sustaining love in any relationship be it family, marriage, friendship and other forms of relationship? *The vital key is to cover(forgive) transgressions and not revisit it again.*  To simplify this vital key further, the opening text mean that faults should be forgiven and the matter should never be repeated again.

*One thing that is almost impossible is to relate without offending one another. As long as we are humans, our individual differences, temperaments, perspective on issues and approach to life may cause a strain in our relationship with people, the home is not exempted.* Those that are not skilled in handling such differences end up exploding in anger, bitterness and resentment towards others. Gradually, love gives way and hatred comes in. If you want to sustain love in any relationship, be quick to forgive offences. *As you keep record of wrongs, you'll experience love leakage*.


*Forgiving doesn't necessarily mean forgetting as some have opined. God didn't create our minds to forget things so quickly except we want to deny the workings of our minds. However, we are recreated in Christ( with a divine ability) to overlook  offenses, let go and continue brotherly love*. If love will prosper and be sustained in any relationship,  you must ensure  you don't repeat a matter again. Repeating the wrongs of people releases the gall of bitterness into you and it shows you haven't let go. You need to build capacity to overlook offenses and forgive promptly. *Forgiving others is not in our default mode, it is what we must do intentionally even when it doesn't make sense.*   How do I forgive? Don't dwell on offenses, let it go.

*PRAYER*: Father, I receive the grace to do as I've been taught in today's devotional in Jesus name.

*TAKE IT TO THE LORD IN PRAYERS*

1 Pet 5:7 Cast all your anxiety on him because he cares for you.

One thing we must learn to do in moments of anxiety is to speak to the Lord about it. Anxiety is an improper use of mental energy and imagination. Sometimes, the devil fires the arrow of worries into our hearts so that we can lose sight of God's power to solve our challenges. It is a diversionary tactic. Have you noticed that in moments of worries, to pray becomes a problem? Instead of fellowshiping with God, the enemy makes us fellowship with our problems.

You and I must master handling anxiety the bible way. The first thing is to take all your worries to the Lord in prayer. Tell God the exact thing the devil is telling you, describe to God those negative imaginations and graphic images the enemy has painted to you. Do not hide the details of your worries from the Lord. When you do that, tell him to take charge and stop the enemy in his tracks. You can do this as though you're talking to someone close to you. *The beauty of true fellowship is that GOD want it to be conversational.*

When you begin to pray this way, burdens will be unrolled from your heart. In moments of anxiety, do not cease to talk scriptures. Speak out the word till it becomes your reality. Get good music that ministers to you the most and hear it all day long. A word filled mind will be free from anxiety. Stay away from anything or anyone that stirs anxiety in you. Listen to good news and faith boosting testimonies. Don't waste productive energy dwelling on things that the enemy is suggesting to your heart.

*CONFESSION*: Father, I thank you for the privilege of having access to your presence. I receive the grace to unburden in your presence and to overcome anxiety in Jesus name.

DEVOTIONAL 0056 | Source start line: 7150
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
2/12/21, 11:27 PM - Lanre Oyeleke: ProjectSave Devotional
Saturday,
February 13th, 2021.

*WHEN THE LORD MAKES A DEMAND FOR WHAT YOU TREASURE*

1Kings 17:7 Some time later the brook dried up because there had been no rain in the land. 8 Then the word of the Lord came to him: 9 “Go at once to Zarephath in the region of Sidon and stay there. I have directed a widow there to supply you with food.” NIV

A devout Christian woman shared her experience with me on the price she paid because of disobedience when God demanded for her money.  In the late 80s, she had an acquaintance whom God instructed to tell her to be part of their church building project. Instead of her to pray and ask God if truly he gave the message, she rejected the idea outrightly and went ahead with her building project in the village. Herself and her husband had the money but they felt their building project should come first.

As she told me, it took them 33 years to complete their house in the village. It was a great struggle. In fact, part of the building collapsed in the course of construction. She told me that if they had known, they would have given the money to support that church building project back in the 80s. *Refusal to give that little can hinder much*.  There are times God makes demand for that very thing you hold dear. Not because he wants to deprive you but such demands are tests from God to know if we have become attached to the things of this world or whether we still trust him to supply our needs even if he decides to take all. *The moment you find it hard to release what is in your hands to God, it shows your heart is affixed on worldly things*.

It wasn't easy on the part of the widow of Zarephath to give her last meal to the prophet of God during famine. If Elijah were to be a prophet in our time, he'll have gotten bad press. The news headline may read thus: "Prophets That Exploit Widows".  However, the ways of God are totally different from the ways of man. That singular act of obedience opened her up for continuous supply of provision during famine: *"15 She went away and did as Elijah had told her. So there was food every day for Elijah and for the woman and her family. 16 For the jar of flour was not used up and the jug of oil did not run dry, in keeping with the word of the Lord spoken by Elijah*".  A wise man said, *"Giving is the proof that you've conquered greed*".  When God is asking for that little in your hands, it is because greater things are coming. Obedience in matters of giving open doors.


*PRAYER*: Father, I receive the grace to be a sacrificial giver in Jesus name

*BEFORE YOU CLAIM PHILIPPIANS 4:19*


Phil 4:19 But my God shall supply all your need according to his riches in glory by Christ Jesus. KJV


One of the most popular verses in the new testament is Philippians 4:19. Whenever we are in need and we're trusting God for supplies, that is the scripture that readily comes to mind. Paul prayed that prayer for the Philippian church because of what they did. It was not a regular prayer, their action necessitated that prayer. For God to answer that prayer in our lives, we must do what the brethren in Phillipi did.


So you may ask, what did they do that provoked such a wonderful prayer from Paul? The answer is very simple. They gave their substance to support Paul and his ministry:  *"Now ye Philippians know also, that in the beginning of the gospel, when I departed from Macedonia, no church communicated with me as concerning giving and receiving, but ye only.  For even in Thessalonica ye sent once and again unto my necessity*", Philippians 4:15-16 KJV. The Philippian church gave Paul the needed financial and material support to propagate the gospel. Their act of giving provoked the popular prayers in verse 19.



If you want to walk in the realities of supernatural supplies as prayed by Paul, you must do what the Philippian church did. *Support a ministry and a minister that is running with a vision of kingdom expansion.* Nobody gives to genuine kingdom course and suffer shame of poverty. In as much as we don't preach to people to give because of what they'll get, yet, there's a standing principle that whatsoever a man sow, he'll reap. Sowers are bound to reap. *Heaven's resources will be released to those who will use it to further heaven's agenda on earth*. Don't look on while the opportunity to be a blessing to genuine kingdom cause and servant pass you by. Take the right action, it is until after you do that you can boldly claim Phillppian 4:19.



*PRAYER*: Father, I receive the grace to act on the message in this devotional in Jesus name.

DEVOTIONAL 0058 | Source start line: 7399

2/16/21, 3:53 PM - Lanre Oyeleke: ProjectSave Devotional
Tuesday
February 16th, 2021.

*STOP LIVING ON EXPIRED INSTRUCTIONS*

Genesis22:1 Some time later God tested Abraham. He said to him, “Abraham!” “Here I am,” he replied. 2 Then God said, “Take your son, your only son, whom you love—Isaac—and go to the region of Moriah. Sacrifice him there as a burnt offering on a mountain I will show you.”

There is something for us all to learn in the story of Abraham and his attempt to sacrifice Isaac. What we commonly accept as the major lesson is that God tested Abraham and he passed the test. Some have also taken that scenario to preach on sacrificial giving.  All that said, there's another vital lesson we all must draw from that story.

It was God that told Abraham to take his son to mount Moriah for sacrifice. I'm sure Abraham heard God clearly on that because the bible said so. However, that instruction had a time lag. While he was about sacrificing his son, the instruction changed, God told him to stop. The old instruction to sacrifice  his son expired the moment God gave a counter instruction even though the intention of the former instruction was to test his heart.  If Abraham was insensitive to God, he would have killed his son citing the first instruction whereas God has moved on. *Walking with God demands that we are current with him.*


There are believers who live on stale or expired  instruction. God has moved but they remained on the previous spot. The instruction to move from Egypt to wilderness was meant to be a temporary one, Canaan was the goal yet most of those who left Egypt couldn't make it to Canaan. *You must be sensitive to differentiate between the journey and the destination.* There are certain instructions that are but for a moment. If you build a monument around it, you'll be walking outside God's program for your life. If you're still running a conference or program that was meant for a season, you'll be feeding people with stale food. Some programs have outlived its usefulness. Take time to know God's mind for the now so that you won't sacrifice your Isaac out of insensitivity.

*PRAYER*: Father, I receive grace to be current with you in Jesus name.

DEVOTIONAL 0059 | Source start line: 7477
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
2/17/21, 4:25 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: PROJECTSAVE DEVOTIONAL
wednesday,
February 17th, 2021.

*THE SEDUCTION OF SUCCESS*

Luke 12: 16 And he told them this parable: “The ground of a certain rich man yielded an abundant harvest. 17 He thought to himself, ‘What shall I do? I have no place to store my crops.’

The parable of the rich fool explains what I mean by 'seduction of success'. The ground of this man yielded so much harvest to a point where he contemplated  where to store the harvest.  That's normal for any man that experiences a boom. However, the disposition of his heart is where he missed it. In verses 18&19, he said: *"And he said, This will I do: I will pull down my barns, and build greater; and there will I bestow all my fruits and my goods. And I will say to my soul, Soul, thou hast much goods laid up for many years; take thine ease, eat, drink, and be merry*".

His life's security was in material prosperity alone not in God. In verse 20, God said: *"But God said unto him, Thou fool, this night thy soul shall be required of thee: then whose shall those things be, which thou hast provided*"? How did he get to this point where his success in farming made a fool of him? When he was toiling and praying to be successful, why didn't God call him a fool? In my opinion, he was seduced by the traps that beset successful people. He felt he had arrived and that was his undoing.

Some persons stopped praying, fasting, studying the bible and their devotional life went on the downward spiral after they achieved little success. They stopped doing the very things they did that got them to the top. On getting to the top, they just couldn't manage success. For some ministers of the gospel, they fell into the trap of over compensation. They over compensate themselves with exhorbitant material things under the guise of having suffered for the ministry in the past without being seen. We all need to learn how to manage success so that we won't end like the rich fool. The time to be more humble, more prayerful and closer to God is when our ground yields a bountiful harvest. Beware of the seductions that come with success.

*PRAYER*: Father, I receive the grace not to fall for the seductions that comes with success in Jesus name.

*DON'T MISINTERPRET DIVINE INSTRUCTIONS*

Jer 33:3 Call to me and I will answer you and tell you great and unsearchable things you do not know.' NIV

My mentor in ministry told  the story of a Pastor. God told this man that he'll give him more wives yet the scriptures is anti polygamy. Instead of asking God to know what he meant, he went ahead to marry more wives. In the process of time, he died. During the burial ceremony, the Spirit of God came upon a woman who prophesied saying, *"thus saith the Lord, truly I told my servant that I'll give him more wives but he failed to ask further questions on what I meant. The church is my bride(wife) and what I intended was that I'll give him more branches to oversee*"( paraphrase).




This Pastor misinterpreted God's instruction totally. He forgot that the bride of Christ is the church. We often act like that Pastor. Truly, we may have heard or sensed something about God's intention for our lives but due to hasty interpretation of what God said, we mess things up. *There are many believers running with a program that is against God's will even though they heard God say something to them.*  Another error people make is to give thesame interpretation to similar revelations every time. For example, may be God showed you eggs in a revelation in January and told you that it meant progress, seeing thesame type and numbers of eggs in another revelation in June does not mean it will have thesame meaning like the previous.


*We miss God often because we lack the patience to clarify instructions with him.* We want to hear and act immediately whereas, God is always expecting us to ask for clarity. That reminds me the story of a sister who said God told her she'll marry someone working in the oil&gas sector. Her fiancé was a school teacher at that time, she called it quit with him because of that revelation. Shortly after, the brother he abandoned got a job with a leading oil&gas firm. *When you hear from God, don't be in a hurry to act especially if the instruction received is figurative or parabolic in nature.* Don't depend on dream interpretation books/prophets to clarify divine instructions for you. Often, there are revelations with ready interpretation from scriptures but we ignore them because of the desire for the esoteric. The God who spoke to you is capable of clarifying what he told you. Ask him.

*PRAYER*: Father, may I not miss you. I receive the grace to interpret revelations correctly  in Jesus name.

*STAY WHERE YOU ARE PLANTED AND ROOTED*


Psalm92:13 Those that be planted in the house of the LORD shall flourish in the courts of our God.


The story of a young man was told in a conference meeting I attended. This young man left the church where he was fed with undiluted God's word in search of what wasn't lost. Probably, he was caught up with the attractive adverts of miracles and "It is happening here" syndrome common to some churches of our age. This is the age where some Pastors boast that nobody comes to their church and remain poor yet there are. The emphasis on Christ and the things that matter to him is less. This man, aftter going round several churches for three years, he returned to his former Church and told the Pastor, *"I left this church 'Nicodemusly' after you gave me money. I've gone round for three years but now I'm back*". Thank God he found his way back to the place where he received fresh spiritual nourishment.


Years back, a young man( my barber) pestered me to come see his prophet. My wife and I nicknamed him "prophetic barber". If I was unstable, I may have gone seeking for what is not lost and forsake the place of my spiritual nourishment. *One of the reasons why some believers have not grown spiritually is because they have not been consistently fed with quality spiritual food from a stable source. The instability may not be from the source but they themselves have been unstable and truant in the place God planted them*. They jump from programs to programs, prayer  meetings to crusade programs. After going round, they may come in contact with diluted teachings and practices that are anti bible. They sow huge seeds they can't give to their churches in those programs. Feed other pastors fat and starve the pastor labouring over them. Some left their God- assigned pastor for another pastor whom they felt was more anointed and popular. *Some missed it because they wanted to identify with a popular church in town at the expense of their spiritual growth while some are just seeking miracles instead of Jesus.*



Some have followed after empty boasts from some pulpits. They heard statements like: "Once you're in my church, you're covered". Dearest, no man or church can provide whatever type of covering you need. God didn't give any pastor a 'covering license'. Our covering from being defeated, shame and despair is in Christ not in a particular church. *Do not seek from men what only God has the prerogative to give*. Stay where God has planted you. Make yourself available to be pastored by that humble servant of God. He may not have the glitz, glamour and popularity of the man next door, as long as God's word is with him, stay there.
If you stay under that pastoral leadership that God has ordained for you, your miracle will reach you there. Keep loving God and seek him genuinely.


*PRAYER*: Father, I receive the grace to stay where you have planted me in Jesus name.

*UPGRADE YOUR PRAYER THEOLOGY*

Matthew 8:29 And, behold, they cried out, saying, What have we to do with thee, Jesus, thou Son of God? art thou come hither to torment us before the time?

Years back, I was in a group with some brethren. We prayed and fasted often. There was a brother among us who was fond of telling us to blow the devil when he led prayers. It was such a physical and body wearying type of prayer. We exerted ourselves. We all forgot what Paul said saying,  *"For we wrestle not against flesh and blood, but against principalities, against powers, against the rulers of the darkness of this world, against spiritual wickedness in high places*",Eph 6:12. One of the reasons why the prayer of some believers remain unanswered is because they're praying outside the word.

Another place where some miss it in their prayers is on the  issue of demons. Some have this believe that demons can die and hence they focus on "killing" them with prayers. That's very unscriptural. I understand and agree with the figurative expression of that prayer but when it is intended to mean what it is literally, it is anti scriptures. No where in scriptures did we see Jesus or the Apostles pray a demon to death. In fact, when demons saw Jesus, they were intelligent enough to remind him that their time of torment had not come as cited in our opening text. Jesus had to cast them out but he didn't command them to die.

What about casting devil into the abyss? The answer can be found in Rev20:1-2, *"And I saw an angel come down from heaven, having the key of the bottomless pit and a great chain in his hand.  And he laid hold on the dragon, that old serpent, which is the Devil, and Satan, and bound him a thousand years,*". The time for the  devil to be cast into the abyss( bottomless pit) will only be after the tribulation before the millennium. No matter how much you pray about that fervently now, as long as   the great tribulation hasn't ended, devil won't be thrown into the abyss.  Do you want answers to your prayers? I'm sure you do. Start praying the will of God  as revealed in scriptures. Those that are lazy in studying God's word may end up praying the wrong prayers. Upgrade your prayer theolgy through the word especially the new testament and you'll get result.


*PRAYER*: Father help me. Henceforth, I receive the grace not to pray amiss in Jesus name.

*WHAT DO YOU PRAY ABOUT OFTEN*?


Col 3:1-2 If ye then be risen with Christ, seek those things which are above, where Christ sitteth on the right hand of God.  Set your affection on things above, not on things on the earth.


In Jewish liturgy, their prayer is incomplete until they pray about the kingdom of God. Why? Their heart is fixed and focused on the promised kingdom. To them, that was the most important thing. When Jesus taught his disciples to pray, you'll notice that there was a bold prayer about the kingdom: *"thy kingdom come, thy will be done on earth even as it in heaven*", Matthew 6:10. Other content of the prayer are kingdom focused in a way.
It was only in one line he asked for bread ( provision).


What do we have today? We have turned our prayers upside down. We ask for daily bread more than we pray kingdom focused prayers. We are full of 'give me give me bless me bless me' prayers but hardly will you find a prayer meeting dedicated to praying about the things that matters to God and his kingdom. If we call for a prayer meeting and give it a very sensational theme like: "Pray yourself into victory over witches" or "Prayer to conquer household Enemies", I'm sure the attendance will double that of a prayer meeting with a theme that is kingdom focused. *A generation prayed to see God's glory, and they saw it through awekenings and revivals. Another generation  is praying that men may see their own glory and they lost the fire of revival*.

I want you to have a biblical focus about your life on earth. I have not in any way said it is wrong to ask God for provisions and others things. However, your prayer should centre around the things that matters to God. In Matthew 6:33, Jesus said, *"Seek the Kingdom of God above all else, and live righteously, and he will give you everything you need*". Pray about your walk with God and always ask him to overhaul your life so that you can be presented blameless before God(Col1:10). Ask the Lord for harvest of souls around the world and to open door of ministry unto the saints(Psalm2:8, Matthew9:36-38). Pray for revival like men of yore did. These and many more are very important to God.


*PRAYER*: Father, I receive grace to pray meaningful prayers henceforth in Jesus name.

2/21/21, 11:39 PM - Lanre Oyeleke: PROJECTSAVE DEVOTIONAL
Monday,
February 22nd, 2021.

*SHE'S BETTER THAN A MAD PERSON*

Prov12:15 Fools think their own way is right, but the wise listen to others.NLT


A learned man was driving his car on third mainland bridge in Lagos-Nigeria on a fateful day. One of his tyres deflated and he parked his vehicle to change it. Due to his carelessness while trying to fix the tyre, all the four bolts entered into the lagoon through the expansion joint.  He didn't know what to do because leaving the vehicle on the bridge could spell danger because the place was robbery prone. He waited several hours trying to figure out what he should do. No one offered to help him because doing such was risky on that high way.

When it was almost evening time, a mad man walked up to him. This raving mad man made a mockery of him knowing that the simple challenge he had was with getting bolts to replace the missing ones. The mad man told him there was a way out. He counseled him to remove one bolt from each of the three tyres to fix the extra. That counsel from a mad man saved him hours of fruitless waiting. Though he was an educated man, it was the counsel of a mad man that saved his day. Any lesson learnt? So much to learn from that story but I'll tailor my counsel towards marriage.

*Husband, listen to your wife's counsel. She's better than a mad man*. There are men who have a bias against the counsel of their wives. They hold a belief that women can't offer wise counsel and that the best of their counsel is to suit their selfish purposes but that's not always true. They forgot that wisdom is not gender specific. *Success and progress has eluded some men because they prefer the counsel of  their beer guzzling friends whilst neglecting the wise counsel from the wife of their youth.* When Abraham's family was distressed, in Gen 21:12, God said: *"...Do not be upset over the boy and your servant. Do whatever Sarah tells you, for Isaac is the son through whom your descendants will be counted*".  If the wise counsel of a mad man saved the day for a learned man, the wise counsel of your wife will save you years of wastage and backwardness. *She's better than a mad person, listen to her.*


*PRAYER*: Father, I thank you for the wife you've given to me. I receive grace to break free from the evil cultural mould that makes men shun wise counsel from their wives in Jesus name.

*ANTI MARITAL SUCCESS MENTALITY* Pt.1

Romans8:14 For all who are led by the Spirit of God are children of God. NLT

*YOU DON'T NEED TO BE LED BY GOD BEFORE CHOOSING A SPOUSE*

I have come across Christian singles and preachers who down play the place of being led by the Spirit of God in the choice of whom to marry. They hinge their conclusion on the fact that since the time of Adam, God stopped interfering in marital affairs because Adam blamed him for giving him Eve who led to his fall. Those who hold this view often say that God changed his method to: *“Whoso findeth a wife findeth a good thing, and obtaineth favour of the LORD*” (Proverbs 18:22). Their emphasis is on "finding" your spouse and presenting your choice to God for ratification.  What a weak and spineless argument. They misinterpreted that scripture totally.

This mentality removes God ( the founder of the marriage institution) from the decision making process as though he is irrelevant. They easily forget where Jesus said, *"I am the vine, ye are the branches: He that abideth in me, and I in him, the same bringeth forth much fruit: FOR WITHOUT ME YE CAN DO NOTHING*". The same book of Proverbs that talks about "finding" a wife also said(3:5-6): *"Trust in the LORD with all thine heart; and lean not unto thine own understanding*". That is, we should quit trying to use human understanding for life's issues( including marriage). It further says, *"In all thy ways( marriage inclusive) acknowledge him, and he shall direct thy paths*". God is still in the business of leading his children on any matter IF they're willing to be led.

Don't subscribe to worldly arguments and assertions that have crept into the church. Nothing beats being led by God on the choice of whom to marry. The world has become so dangerous. There are phony people out there and even in church who have mastered the art of deception. What about agents of darkness whose mission is to destroy the lives of God's precious sons&daughters through marital entanglement? No matter how wise, learned or smart you are, there's no way you can know these people except God is involved. *To get it right on whom to marry, you must be led*. Only the Spirit of God knows who is right for you. Hinge your prayers on this scripture: *"I will instruct thee and teach thee in the way which thou shalt go: I will guide thee with mine eye*", Psalms 32:8.


*PRAYER*: LORD, I surrender to you. I quit using human wisdom. I'm willing to be led by your Spirit on marriage issues.

*ANTI MARITAL SUCCESS MENTALITY* Pt.2

Jeremiah 29:11 For I know the plans I have for you,” says the LORD. “They are plans for good and not for disaster, to give you a future and a hope. NLT

*GOD MAY GIVE ME WHO I DON'T LIKE*

One of the reasons why some singles loath the idea of being led by God on the choice of whom to marry is because they have the fear in them that God may give them a spouse they don't like. I have heard this complaint severally. For example, a brother may think that God will tell him to marry a sister he is not attracted to physically. That's not true as some persons has made us believe. Those with this mentality do not trust God's judgement on the matter of their marriage so they relegate God in their decision making to satisfy their carnal desires only.

Some are yet to fully trust in God's love. They see God as a distant task master who do not care but our opening text proves them wrong. In Matthew 7:9-11, Jesus said, *"You parents—if your children ask for a loaf of bread, do you give them a stone instead?Or if they ask for a fish, do you give them a snake? Of course not!So if you sinful people know how to give good gifts to your children, how much more will your heavenly Father give good gifts to those who ask him*". Hallelujah!!! Jesus has cleared your doubts and erased your fears.


If our earthly  fathers can strive to give us the best, how much more our heavenly father. *When you ask God to lead you on the choice of a spouse, his leading is perfect and he'll give you what suits your life( spiritual, physical, material, etc) and destiny so that you can fulfill his purpose for your life here on earth. It is a total package.* God gives us what we need and not necessarily what we want. What he gives is the best. Trust God's judgement on the matter of your marriage that he'll give you the best. If you can't trust God to lead you aright on marital issues, how can you trust him with your life?

*PRAYER*: Father, forgive me for not trusting you enough to give me the best. Your best is good for me and I submit myself to be led by you in Jesus name.

*ANTI MARITAL SUCCESS MENTALITY* Pt.3

2Cor 6:14 Don’t team up with those who are unbelievers. How can righteousness be a partner with wickedness? How can light live with darkness? NLT

*FAITH DOESN'T MATTER LOVE IS EVERYTHING*


Years back, I met a Christian lady who was in a relationship with an unbeliever. She didn't see anything wrong with what she was doing. I knew the guy he was hooked with and I knew their relationship was doomed for crisis because their value systems differ. While the Christian lady doesn't believe in polygamy, the faith of this young man permitted polygamy. In fact, he was already showing the tendencies because he was double dating. Do not be deceived that love is enough. I'm privileged to have met devout Christian women married to unbelievers who spoke with regret about their marriage. They wished they could turn back the hands of time so that they can right their wrongs.

Every relationship start on the note of "I love you" but that love is not always enough. So many things are attached to love that can either kill or strengthen love. That time, the people of other culture were pagans. They weren't under the covenant of Yaweh. They went awhoring after other gods. God knew that there was a high tendency for his children to give their worship to other gods if they marry from those pagan people.

Besides Pharaoh’s daughter, he married women from Moab, Ammon, Edom, Sidon, and from among the Hittites. The Lord had clearly instructed the people of Israel, “You must not marry them, because they will turn your hearts to their gods.” Yet Solomon insisted on loving them anyway. He had 700 wives of royal birth and 300 concubines. AND IN FACT, THEY DID TURN HIS HEART AWAY FROM THE LORD*", NLT. Don't be deceived with seemingly good marriages of people who married from other faith. You're not living with them to know their struggles and pains. Don't let the devil deceive you with subtle lies like: "I will convert him/ her after marriage". You're not a converter. That brother/ sister may start following you to church because he want to marry you yet his heart may be very far from God. Don't be yoked with an unbeliever in marriage.


*PRAYER*: Father, I receive the grace to marry from within the kingdom in Jesus name.

*ANTI MARITAL SUCCESS MENTALITY* Pt. 4

Galatians3: 28 There is neither Jew nor Gentile, neither slave nor free, nor is there male and female, for you are all one in Christ Jesus.

*I MUST MARRY FROM MY TRIBE*

A sister was ripe for marriage. She was successful in her business and she loved the Lord but she was conscious about her tribe. When she wanted to marry, she told a minister that her husband must come from her place. She was told to pray but she insisted. Eventually, she got what she wanted but she suffered physical and verbal abuse till she died.


In a multi ethnic country like Nigeria and some African countries, tribal factors are considered by many whenever they want to marry. No thanks to some parents who will insist that their children must marry from their tribe and never marry outside. Unfortunately, those with such stand exist in the church and even among the Pastors. They view people from earthly standpoint and associate certain characters with people from a particular tribe. *"So we have stopped evaluating others from a human point of view. At one time we thought of Christ merely from a human point of view. How differently we know him now!*", 2Cor5:16.


*Being good or bad has nothing to do with tribe.* There are good and bad people in all tribes. You need to look beyond tribal affiliation when you're set to marry.  *People are who they are because of their decisions and who is leading them.* If a man lives by the flesh, he will live according to the dictates of the flesh. This is something that can happen to anyone from any tribe. The fear of evil custom and tradition is another factor. My belief is that no true child of God will hold on to evil traditions.  Don't box the Holy Spirit in the choice of whom to marry. He can lead you to any of God's children regardless of tribe. Open your heart to his leading.



*PRAYER*: Father, I refuse to become a tribal Christian in Jesus name.

*ANTI MARITAL SUCCESS MENTALITY* Pt. 5

Galatians3: 28 There is neither Jew nor Gentile, neither slave nor free, nor is there male and female, for you are all one in Christ Jesus.

*ALL MEN ARE CHEATS AND WOMEN ARE HOES*

I watched a video where a lady stressed that all men are cheats and nothing can be done about it. I have read several times on blogs where men wrote that women are hoes and are not loyal. This is a demonic mentality that makes singles settle for anything and distort the trust between couples. There are singles who stayed in a relationship where their mate is cheating on them. Instead of calling it quits,  they'll rationalize their stay in that relationship with this evil mentality of "All men are cheats and women are hoes". This mindset is not healthy if you will have a successful relationship and marriage. *Not all men cheat and all ladies aren't hoes*. You need to change your mindset about this if you want to have a peaceful marriage. The mindset with which you approach marriage matters a lot. Also, do not use the experience of others in marriage to form your conclusion.


The major challenge this evil mindset poses is that you'll live to suspect your spouse forever except you change your mindset. *One of the problems some marriages are facing today is lack of trust. Not because the parties involved aren't faithful but because someone has a mind wired not to trust*. This mindset is the reason why some wives try to eavesdrop on every conversation their husbands makes and some husbands try to read all SMS on their wives phone just to prove this point. Some women have developed high blood pressure due to lack of trust. Some men have grounded their wives and prevented them from living a productive life because they are always suspicious of infidelity. No marriage can  succeed this way. If you ask some men who made their wives to become sit-at-home wives, some of them will tell you that they can't trust a working class wife forgetting that there are full  house wives who are cheating fully.


Have I said infidelity doesn't exist or happen in some marriages? Of course not. However, as a single, do not ruin your future marital joy with that mentality so that you won't live all your marital life in suspicion. *As a general rule of thumb, do not marry a man or woman you can't trust*. Simple! The moment you notice traces of infidelity in your would-be spouse, you are at liberty to call it quit. Don't justify immorality with the common mindset people have in town. Some are managing a relationship that is doomed to fail. Whenever they seek counsel from their friends, they'll tell them cheating is normal. Dearest, cheating or any form of sexual immorality is not normal. It is a sin. Do not accept it as the norm. *Give honor to marriage, and remain faithful to one another in marriage. God will surely judge people who are immoral and those who commit adultery*, Hebrews 13:4 NLT.

*PRAYER*: Father, I rid my mind of every negative mentality in  Jesus name

*ANTI MARITAL SUCCESS MENTALITY* Pt. 6

1Cor 6:18 Flee fornication. Every sin that a man doeth is without the body; but he that committeth fornication sinneth against his own body.



*SEX IS A SIGN THAT YOU'RE IN LOVE*.


This is another big lie from the pit of hell that has gained wide acceptance all over the world. The moment love is mentioned, what comes to the mind of some is sex, especially the male folks. Many ladies have had to succumb to the pressure to have sex just to prove a point to their partner that they're truly in love.  A sister once shared her experience with us in a gathering. She was in a relationship with a church brother who had sex with her. She did that to express her love to him.  He deflowered her and called it quit thereafter.


*If sex is the true sign of love, prostitutes would be the most loved set of people on earth.* However, you'll agree with me that sex with prostitutes is purely transactional. It has nothing to do with love. A man can sleep with a prostitute and a brutal fight may ensue due to the man's failure to pay for services rendered. Where is love in that? A man can sleep with a lady countless times yet doesn't love her and vice versa. Stop laying your temple down on the altar of sexual immorality. 1Cor6:19-20 says, *"Or do you not know that your body is a temple of the Holy Spirit within you, whom you have from God? You are not your own, for you were bought with a price. So glorify God in your body*”.


God's standard for courtship is sexual purity within and without: *"For this is the will of God, even your sanctification, that ye should abstain from fornication*", 1 Thessalonians 4:3 KJV. Do not be deceived. What the world calls fun(pre marital sex) is a sin before the Lord. Another deceit akin to the one being discussed is the deceit to test for sexual compatibility. How many ladies will you sleep with before you find the exact one you're compatible with sexually? Sisters, don't allow yourself to be used for sexual compatibility testing. God is raising Joseph's who when confronted with the sin of sexual immorality would say, *"How could I do such a wicked thing? It would be a great sin against God*”, Gen39:9.


*PRAYER*: Father, purge me of hellish mentality in Jesus name.

*ANTI MARITAL SUCCESS MENTALITY* Pt. 7

Eccl10:10 If the iron be blunt, and he do not whet the edge, then must he put to more strength: but wisdom is profitable to direct.

*I PITY HIM...I WILL MARRY HIM*

Jane and John courted for two years. Both families met for the traditional introduction as their marital future became closer. Their relationship started when John sought the counsel of Jane on how to handle his addiction to Cannabis.  Jane is a trained counselor and life coach. John's family knew about his addiction and how he decided to get counsel from a professional counselor. They were happy to know that their son was engaged to Jane, a counselor. John's parents felt she was the right person for John.


Jane accepted John's proposal for marriage out of pity. She felt she shouldn't leave John to his fate and that she could be useful at some point in case there was a relapse. Truthfully, John didn't overcome his addiction to cannabis but he became more discrete about it. Jane  caught him smoking cannabis severally but John would beg her and even engage his parents to appeal to her not to leave him alone. Out of pity for his despicable condition, Jane stayed in that relationship till  they tied the knots. Six months into the marriage, John became uncontrollable and that caused a separation.

*Starting a relationship out of pity shows  lack of love.* True love is born of out of rationality not out of a wave of emotions. Only genuine love sustains a marriage. Pity has nothing to do with a healthy marriage. Love comes from within and cannot be spearheaded by pity. This means marriages sealed out of pity never last. *If you start a relationship out of pity, you'll end up being pitied.*

*PRAYER*: I receive the grace to act right in Jesus name

DEVOTIONAL 0072 | Source start line: 8806

3/2/21, 6:39 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: ProjectSave Devotional
Tuesday
March 2nd, 2021.

*TWO VITAL LESSONS FROM THE LIFE OF JOHN THE BAPTIST* Pt .1

Luke 1:80 As John grew up, God's Spirit gave him great power. John lived in the desert until the time he was sent to the people of Israel. CEV

The opening text is about John the baptist. He was anointed and given a special assignment for his generation. The prophets prophesied about his coming as the fore runner of the messiah. An angel appeared to his father while doing his priestly duty to announce his coming. Everything that surrounded his birth was supernatural and there was a pre knowledge that a great child was coming. However, I learnt two things from the life of John the baptist in that text.

*First, he stayed in the wilderness for God to deal with and prepare him for the journey ahead*. He didn't just rush out saying, "I'm the promised child and the anointed one for this generation. Everybody, come and listen to me". Impatience with God's dealing is a common syndrome today among God's children. Truly, they may have sensed the hand of God upon their lives for certain assignment, instead of waiting in the training school of God so that they can be strengthened and equipped, they rush out. It wasn't so with John the baptist. He patiently waited  under God in the University of Wilderness to take the pre requisite courses.

Moses knew he was called to be a deliverer but he stepped out ahead of God's timing when he had not been made by God. Ex 2:14 *"The man said, "Who made you ruler and judge over us? Are you thinking of killing me as you killed the Egyptian?" Then Moses was afraid and thought, "What I did must have become known*". When you have not fulfilled the requirement for manifesting in destiny, your world will ask the same question from you: " Who made you..." Every good product must have a label of the maker because it ascertains standard and quality. When a product is without a label, people may doubt its authenticity. The world will  ask for your product label: "Who made you". No matter how strong the passion is, no matter how clear the vision is, do not despise the making process of God. *God doesn't launch unmade men to undertake the task of making men*.


*PRAYER*: Father, I receive the grace to stay in your making room in Jesus name.

DEVOTIONAL 0073 | Source start line: 8888

3/3/21, 5:49 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: PROJECTSAVE DEVOTIONAL
wednesday,
March 3rd, 2021.

*TWO VITAL LESSONS FROM THE LIFE OF JOHN THE BAPTIST* Pt .2


Luke 1:80 As John grew up, God's Spirit gave him great power. John lived in the desert until the time he was sent to the people of Israel. CEV


The second lesson we can learn from the life of John the Baptist is to wait on God till the right time before launching out. John didn't leave the desert until it was time for him to appear to the nation of Israel. It is one thing for God to prepare you for an assignment and another crucial thing is for you to know the right time to start in the right place. Both are equally important in fulfilling God's plan and purposes. The preparation for destiny sometimes is like military training. After the training, soldiers would be deployed to stations designated by the military commander. A soldier who get trained but goes to the wrong barrack may be disciplined. Also, a soldier who abscond from training will never be deployed or enlisted in the army.  At the set time, John graduated from the University of Wilderness and he was sent to the people of Israel. Not Egypt or Assyria. Israel was the place of his primary assignment. Some are doing the right thing in the wrong place. It was not so with John the Baptist.


Even though Moses knew what God had in plan for him, he launched out at the wrong time. It seemed awkward to the human mind that at 40, God saw Moses unfit to lead. In our time, we would have called Moses a fool because we believe a fool at 40 is a fool forever. In God's wisdom, his assignment was to start at 80 years of age. That was the right time. You'll notice that when his years of preparation in the wilderness ended, it was God that encountered him to announce his deployment to his field of work. *You don't leave a phase of God's training and dealings until God says it is over". Never be in a hurry to give yourself compulsory exit from God's training. Timing is crucial. In John 7:3, the brothers of Jesus told him to leave Galilee to Judea so that he can be popular: *"and Jesus’ brothers said to him, “Leave here and go to Judea, where your followers can see your miracles!*". They were trying to project Jesus to the world even though they didn't believe in him. Yes, the world should know Jesus and see his miracles so that they can believe in the one who sent him. Their intention was good but the timing was wrong.


In verse 6 Jesus replied by saying: *"Now is not the right time for me to go, but you can go anytime*". What a wonderful Jesus. He walked with a sense of divine timing. Brethren, if our Lord and master didn't rush into doing certain things because it wasn't time, if John the Baptist, the greatest in the old testament and other men in the bible walked with a sense of divine timing, ours shouldn't be different. All the revelations you've had about a glorious ministry or assignment that God is commiting into your hands has a set time. If you launch out before your time, you will fizzle out in no time. As you walk with God, be detailed. God can show you a vision of the next twenty years and it may seem as if he wants you to start now. More often than not, the fulfilment of divine vision takes time. As I read in a post, Kenneth E.Hagin didn't start his popular camp meeting program until he was 57 years of age. He is gone to be with the Lord now but camp meetings continue.  Joseph had a dream at the age of 17 but the fulfilment took 13 years. The bible said, *"Until the TIME came to fulfill his dreams, the Lord tested Joseph’s character*", Psalms 105:19 NLT.

*PRAYER*: Father, I receive the grace to wait for the right time in Jesus name.

3/4/21, 12:21 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: PROJECTSAVE DEVOTIONAL
Thursday,
March 4th, 2021.


*DON'T MISS THE SUPERNATURAL BY SEEKING THE SPECTACULAR*


2 Kings 5:10 "I thought that he would surely come out to me and stand and call upon the name of the LORD his God, and wave his hand over the place and cure the leper." Naaman had ranted.


From the wall of one of my lecturers, I read the story of a woman who traveled for seven hours  to see a powerful man of God she had heard so much about to find solution to her problem. This minister agreed to see the woman even though there was no prior appointment. There was no mobile network those days, and land phones were not that reliable. After telling her problems to this man of God, he simply held her hands and prayed with her for a few minutes and said to her, " madam it is settled." "Is that all?" She exclaimed. The flustered woman couldn't imagine she came on a seven-hour journey, just for a simple prayer. Not after all she had been told about this man of God. She had expected something more spectacular. It is God's prerogative to move the way he wants not our own way.


*God does not operate in the realm of the spectacular. He operates in the realm of faith.* Naaman expected prophet Elisha to do special 'prophetic work' on him. He was disappointed and furious, when Elisha, the man of God, sent a messenger to tell him to go and bathe in River Jordan to be cleansed of his leprosy. Most times, we are like Naaman. The moment you don't believe in simple prayers prayed in Jesus name, you may not receive answers. The reason some are jumping from mountains to prayer houses till they get into the wrongs hands is because they sought for the spectacular. Who said you must fall under anointing before you can be healed/ receive your miracles? After praying for some persons, they'll ask, "Did you see anything"? Must something be seen? A very young secondary school girl told me she left her dad's church for an occultic because they see visions in the later.  The expectation of the spectacular has made some ministers to become stage performers for their members. They come up with theatrics, yell and do stuffs that'll make people say, "I feel God here." Dear, you can't feel God because he is Spirit, you can only sense him in your spirit even if there's nothing physical about it. *Sensationalism is not the way of the Spirit. We don't feel our way into spiritual things. Feelings are fickle*.


*The spectacular is not a precursor to the supernatural, faith is*. God want us to put our faith in him even if he chooses to move in a simple way with no airs of the spectacular. If a preacher is always seeking the spectacular before believing God moved in his ministrations, he may end up becoming a performer or demons will perform for him. Not all those people falling down or screaming are doing so by the Spirit. I've walked with God for a while to know that. Demons can distract a preacher with what seems like "manifestation" so that he'll leave preaching/ teaching of the word to give them attention.  There is a longing in our hearts for the supernatural, even some unbelievers want to experience it. However, God has chosen to manifest himself in the realm of simple faith not in 'powerful prayers'. What some call 'powerful prayers' is just prayers with raised voice and theatrics. If someone prays for them with a low tone, they'll conclude the prayer is not powerful. *You don't need a 'powerful prayer'. What you need is to offer your prayers( in faith) to a powerful God. That's how to get result*.

*PRAYER*: Father, I receive the grace to believe you and express simple faith in your word for a flow of the supernatural in Jesus name. Amen

DEVOTIONAL 0075 | Source start line: 9320

3/5/21, 7:17 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: PROJECTSAVE DEVOTIONAL
Friday,
March 5th, 2021.


*STAY ON TRACK*


Romans12:6 In his grace, God has given us different gifts for doing certain things well... NLT


James Audubon was a lover of art. He was gifted in drawing and painting but early in life, he didn't pay much attention to his innate gifting. He worked in several places but he wasn't fulfilled. He resigned from those jobs to focus on his passion. He decided to draw and paint birds in America. Much later, his popular paintings named "Birds of America" sold for over a million dollars.


One reason why people struggle and fail in life is because they fail to stay in the area of their God-given ability. Apostle Paul made it clear that we all have been given certain gifts to do certain things well. Trying to do what you're not gifted to do will surely lead to frustration. This applies to all areas of life including ministry. Do not try to function as a Pastor if your calling is purely evangelistic. The former is an aquarium keeper while the latter is a fisher of men.


In the race of life, we all have a lane. In track events, it is wrong to cross from one lane to another. Even if you cross the finish the line before others, as long as you're on the wrong lane, you will be penalized by the umpire. The key to overcoming your struggles, frustration and persistent lack of fulfilment is to discover what you can do well and start doing it. Proverbs27:8 *"As a bird that wandereth from her nest, so is a man that wandereth from his place*". God has not sent anyone into the world as a nonentity. *Nonentities are not born, they're made*. Take your time to discover the very thing you're gifted to do and build your life around it.


*PRAYER*: Father, I receive grace to discover and walk on my path in life in Jesus name.

DEVOTIONAL 0076 | Source start line: 9642

3/5/21, 11:00 PM - Lanre Oyeleke: ProjectSave Devotional
Saturday,
March 6th, 2021.

*COME DOWN TO THEIR LEVEL*

Genesis18:19 For I know him, that he will command his children and his household after him, and they shall keep the way of the LORD, to do justice and judgment; that the LORD may bring upon Abraham that which he hath spoken of him.

I've been trying to explain the concept of rapture to my eldest son. He must have heard me say that word several times but he didn't understand. I was clueless on how to explain the whole idea behind it to him without sounding too theological. From one of his favourite t.v.series in one of the channels for kids, I saw people disappearing into thin air and they reappeared in another location. It is an action movie he loved so much. Coincidentally, I watched that same t.v.series as a kid. From what happened in that scene, I taught him rapture and the things that surround the event.

His understanding became fruitful on the subject. Weeks later, I asked him if he still remembered rapture. He answered affirmatively and he described to me what will happen when the arch angel blows the trumpet and how God's children will disappear like his favorite actor in that t.v.series. In fact, he's longing for it already. If you want your children to understand the scriptures, use illustrations they can easily relate with. Speak the language they understand best to communicate scriptures. *Your goal should not just be to mark daily register in your family devotion but to impart the knowledge of God's word and sound bible doctrine to your children so that their understanding can be fruitful*.


Paul said, *"And that from a child thou hast known the holy scriptures, which are able to make thee wise unto salvation through faith which is in Christ Jesus*", 2 Timothy 3:15 KJV. The word "known" in that verse of scripture is from the Greek word "oida". It means to understand perfectly. In essence, what Paul was saying was that Timothy understood the scriptures perfectly from childhood. Little wonder why he became one of the youngest Bishops in early church history. Who taught Timothy? *When I call to remembrance the unfeigned faith that is in thee, which dwelt first in thy grandmother Lois, and thy mother Eunice; and I am persuaded that in thee also*, 2Tim1:5. The mother and grandmother of Timothy taught Timothy the scriptures not religiously but intentionally in such a way  that his understanding can be fruitful. This should be our goal as parents. Start trusting God for ideas in this regard.


*PRAYER*: Father, I receive the grace to teach my children the scriptures in ways they'll understand in Jesus name.

DEVOTIONAL 0077 | Source start line: 9800
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
3/7/21, 5:19 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: ProjectSave Devotional
Sunday,
March 7th, 2021.


*RICH BUT POOR*


Rev 3:17 You say, ‘I am rich; I have acquired wealth and do not need a thing.’ But you do not realize that you are wretched, pitiful, poor, blind and naked. NIV

The story of the conversation between Pope Innocent and Saint Thomas Aquinas is a reference point to illustrate this topic. Seeing the mighty edifice of the old Catholic Church and how beautiful this physical church was decorated, Pope Innocent said, *"the church can no longer say 'silver and Gold we have none'*". He said that to refute the claims of Peter and John in Acts 3 who told the cripple man at the beautiful gate that "Silver and gold we have none". He must have felt it was a statement made out of poverty by the leaders of the early church but seeing how rich the church was in his own time, he issued a counter statement.


Saint Thomas Aquinas heard him clearly and must have seen the pride and lust for material things in his heart. He replied the Pope by saying, *"neither can the church say 'rise up&walk'*". That's thoughtful of St.Thomas Aquinas. Probably, the Pope thought silver and gold, wealth and riches can replace the place of God's power. His own generation had money and what money can buy yet they do not carry God's presence and power. The story is almost the same as ours today. Glitz and glamour has been added to ministry. In an interview,  I heard a preacher say that glitz and glamour is good for the ministry. Even his interviewer expressed dismay.


Gone are the days where a minister is known by his life of consecration to the Lord and the presence of God he carries. Today, we size up a man of God by the number of his congregation, network of branches, wealth, material things and cathedral. The concern is about "the church with largest church auditorium or largest number of worshippers in a service" not the church with the largest number of revived souls prepared ready for the coming kingdom. Just as Jesus said to the Laodicean church that they are wretched, poor, pitiful,  blind and naked, same can be said to some of the churches in our generation. What is the way out? *"I counsel thee to buy of me gold tried in the fire, that thou mayest be rich; and white raiment, that thou mayest be clothed, and that the shame of thy nakedness do not appear; and anoint thine eyes with eyesalve, that thou mayest see.   As many as I love, I rebuke and chasten: be zealous therefore, and repent.   Behold, I stand at the door, and knock: if any man hear my voice, and open the door, I will come in to him, and will sup with him, and he with me*".
Revelation 3:18‭-‬20 KJV



*PRAYER*: Father, revive me in Jesus name.

3/8/21, 5:05 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: PROJECTSAVE DEVOTIONAL
Monday,
March 8th, 2021.

*MENTORING YOUNG VESSELS*

Acts 9:27 But Barnabas took him, and brought him to the apostles, and declared unto them how he had seen the Lord in the way, and that he had spoken to him, and how he had preached boldly at Damascus in the name of Jesus. KJV




So many preachers talk about Paul and make reference to his epistles but when we teach about spiritual fatherhood or mentorship, we rarely talk about Barnabas. Unfortunately, the most common story in our preaching and teaching about the duo is their call into the Apostolic(Acts13) & their conflict in Acts 15. Beyond all that, we can learn a few things from Barnabas. We won't be wrong to call Barnabas the spiritual father/ mentor of Paul.

After Paul's conversion, he took Paul around, introduced him to the brethren so that they can be confident about his changed life. From afar, I perceive Barnabas had a good heart and he was a mentor who doesn't give up on his mentee. In their first missionary journey, they took John Mark along but the young man disembarked at Pamphylia. When they decided to revisit the places where they had preached the gospel, Barnabas suggested to Paul that John Mark should go with them but Paul objected and that led to a sharp disagreement that separated them(Acts15:37-41).



Much later, I was surprised at what Paul said about the same John Mark in 2Tim4:11: *"Only Luke is with me. Bring Mark with you when you come, for he will be helpful to me in my ministry*". The man he rejected in Acts 15 later became helpful to him in ministry. Barnabas saw the potential in John Mark and he knew he was a useful vessel. *When mentoring young people, there's a tendency for us to be tired about their inconsistencies, please don't give up*. Paul saw Mark's inconsistencies in Acts 15 but Barnabas saw a man that would be useful later in ministry. *If you're privileged to have young people around you, do not use their present uninspiring life to judge their glorious future*. Keep investing in them, never cease to pray and instruct them. They may act as though they're not paying attention but God is working on their heart to help them overcome their struggles.

*PRAYER*: Father, I receive the grace to mentor young vessels patiently in Jesus name.

DEVOTIONAL 0079 | Source start line: 10011

3/9/21, 5:31 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: ProjectSave Devotional
Tuesday
March 9th,  2021.

*I ASSUMED WRONGLY*

Prov18:2 A fool takes no pleasure in understanding, but only in expressing his opinion.


I was in a relationship with a sister while in the university. That relationship lasted for only four months before we parted ways. Why did I enter into that relationship in the first place? I assumed I was spiritual enough to handle a relationship. I saw others who were in a relationship and I felt I could handle one. One of my trusted friends and my room mates contributed to my hasty decision because they had eaten "cooler ministration" from the sister and they felt she was an opportunity I shouldn't miss.




One of my friends, a spiritually sensitive sister warned me not to enter into that relationship. I dismissed her counsel because I felt she was jealous being single and unengaged. Lo! I missed it. For the short period spent in that relationship, I felt dry, empty and lonely even amongst a crowd of people. I was tormented within. That doesn't mean something is wrong with the sister but she wasn't meant for me. When someone isn't meant for you, you may find yourself struggling to keep up with the person in that relationship. That doesn't mean the person is bad. It'll look like trying to fix a square peg in a round hole.  I learnt several lessons from that experience. *You don't enter into a relationship with the opposite sex on assumption. Assumption leads to hasty decisions and hasty decisions are bad decisions.*

I assumed I was ripe enough to enter into a relationship since others were in one. When it comes to marriage focused relationship, it is not what you enter because you think time is not on your side or because others are into one. Culture or tradition shouldn't determine that for you. Do you know it wasn't Adam that demanded for a wife? Genesis 2:18 *"Then the LORD God said, “It is not good for the man to be alone. I will make a helper who is just right for him*”. The timing for Adam's marriage was God's idea not his. If he had assumed that time was not on his side and rushed into marriage, he may have married one of the animals but he was busy with the assignment God gave to him. Find God's purpose for your life as a single. Meet heaven's requirements in your single phase of life before proceeding to the next phase.

*PRAYER*: Father, I receive the grace to wait on you for direction in Jesus name.

3/10/21, 5:09 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: PROJECTSAVE DEVOTIONAL
wednesday,
March 10th, 2021.

*DON'T STRUGGLE TO KEEP IT*

Prov18:2 A fool takes no pleasure in understanding, but only in expressing his opinion.

I had no peace about my relationship back in the university. I struggled to keep that relationship even when it was obvious it was heading no where. Why was i struggling to keep it? Shame. That's the word. I felt it would be shameful for a "spiritual" brother( whom some are looking up to) like me to call it quit. I imagined the several persons(  friends&families) who would feel disappointed after making them believe God led me into that relationship.

One day, I visited school during I.T.period. I was in the hostel room of a brother when I heard a voice that said, "If you don't dissolve that relationship, it will land you in trouble". *Dear single, don't struggle to keep a relationship out of sentiments*. As long as you know God is not involved or may be you're seeing red flags, please quit honourably. You don't need to fight, just close the door without banging it. *Don't marry out of shame and fear*. This syndrome is common to church folks. They're afraid to quit their relationship because of what Pastor would say.

*There are several persons trying to manage a relationship that won't work. They've seen the red flags of uncontrollable anger, infidelity, battery,  lack of focus, laziness and others yet they keep managing*. They're considering what people (friends, families&brethren) would say. Some are thinking time is no longer on their side so they just want to get married anyhow. They think they've gone too far in the journey of courtship to disembark. As the saying goes, "a broken courtship is better than a broken marriage". *It is not God's will for marriages to break but there's nothing wrong in breaking a courtship ( on genuine grounds) that may lead into a marriage that will eventually break*. Be wise.

*PRAYER*: Father, I receive the grace to wait on you for direction in Jesus name.

DEVOTIONAL 0081 | Source start line: 10162

3/10/21, 11:15 PM - Lanre Oyeleke: PROJECTSAVE DEVOTIONAL
Thursday,
March 11th, 2021.

*WHAT THE LORD TOLD ME*


Isaiah 55:8-9 8 “For my thoughts are not your thoughts,neither are your ways my ways,” declares the Lord. “As the heavens are higher than the earth, so are my ways higher than your ways and my thoughts than your thoughts.


I struggled to quit that relationship God warned me against. Shame, fear coupled with spiritual pride hindered me from acting promptly.   While talking to God, I told him that she was what I wanted and that he said in his word that he'll grant me the desires of my heart( Psalms37:4). I quoted other portions of scriptures where we were told to ask for what we want. Those faith scriptures came alive in me and they flowed out of me like a river.



Then, the Lord said, *"Do not use scriptures to your own destruction*". That struck me hard. If I had insisted on my ways justifying disobedience with scriptures, God would have allowed me have my way but I'll be out of God's will for my life. I discovered that we can justify disobedience with scriptures. *Disobedience will never change status if backed by scriptures. Under any condition, disobedience remains what it is- disobedience*.


When God promised to grant our heart desires, that scripture only applies in context of those desires that are in line with his will for our lives not our carnal wishes.  If God has ministered to your heart to call it quit, it could be a job, friendship, relationship or whatever form of engagement you may be involved in, stop quoting scriptures to the author. Simply obey. *The longer you stay in disobedience, the farther you'll be from where God planned for your life*.

*PRAYER*: Father, I receive the grace to obey you promptly in Jesus name.

DEVOTIONAL 0082 | Source start line: 10249
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
3/12/21, 4:52 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: PROJECTSAVE DEVOTIONAL
Friday,
March 12th, 2021.


*DON'T CHEAT THE PROCESS*


Psalms105:19 Until the time that his word came: the word of the LORD tried him.



When God revealed the future to Joseph, he was happy and he may have thought he would have a smooth sail to his destination. Little did he know that a process was involved before entering into destiny. *The suffering phase was the making phase in preparation for his glorious future so it is for every man of great destiny*. He had the opportunity to cheat the process and exit the suffering. If he had slept with Potiphar's wife, he may have become a pampered servant whose pain and suffering would be relieved momentarily by his "sugar mummy" but he would have remained on that spot for a long time without smelling the palace.


Another possibility of exit from the process was for him to return to his father's house. I think it was possible for Joseph to run away. Even if he doesn't know the way home, if he had asked a few persons, they'll lead him there yet he stayed back in Egypt where his vision training school was. *When God gives a vision, there are processes involved before the fulfilment of that vision*. You can't cheat the process and expect to get to your destination. *No matter how anointed a woman is, if she gets pregnant, the process of fertilization, implantation and foetal development before delivery takes 38-40 weeks. She can't cheat the process*. A baby delivered before term would require incubation to survive. That shows that process can't be cheated.


*God is not in a hurry. He doesn't neglect the means to arrive at an end in a hurry*. To him, the means is as important as the end. *He is concerned about processes involved in the making of a man who will ultimately fulfill his vision*. Jesus is God, in his incarnation, he had to go through the process. He would have started preaching from the day of his birth yet he waited for 30 solid years before launching out. God took him through the required process before launching him out. Only processed products have market value.  Stop trying to cut corners to fulfil destiny. Short cut will cut you short.

*PRAYER*: Father, I submit myself to your process in Jesus name.

DEVOTIONAL 0083 | Source start line: 10317
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
3/13/21, 5:46 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: ProjectSave Devotional
Saturday,
March 13th, 2021.

*WHERE DID YOU PITCH YOUR TENT*?

Genesis13:13 Abram lived in the land of Canaan, while Lot lived among the cities of the plain and pitched his tents near Sodom. NIV

After Lot and Abram separated, Lot relocated to one of the cities of the plain and pitched his tents near Sodom. In the opening chapter, the bible didn't say he lived in Sodom. His house was beside Sodom or along the road leading to Sodom. Why the emphasis about Sodom? The city of Sodom&Gomorrah were known for their iniquity: *"So the Lord told Abraham, “I have heard a great outcry from Sodom and Gomorrah, because their sin is so flagrant*", Genesis 18:20(NLT). In Sodom, homosexual sin was common. That's where the term "Sodomy"( Anal sex between men) was coined.

In Genesis 13, Lot was living near Sodom but in Genesis 19, we saw that he had moved into Sodom.  Sodom pulled him in. *His initial intention of living near Sodom may be harmless but his placement was wrong. He moved gradually until he found himself in Sodom. That's the danger of staying around sin for a long time*. Much later, we were told that righteous Lot slept with his daughters( under the influence of alcohol by his daughters) because he was tormented with the lawlessness he saw&heard in Sodom (2Pet2:7-8). His daughters used the wisdom of Sodom to get pregnant. You had the mind not to fornicate but you're living in the same flat with a sister that is not your wife. Your intention was to accommodate her and provide shelter but your mind is under sexual torture night and day. You've pitched your tent wrongly, it is just a matter time, you'll fall into sexual sin head long if you don't take the right action.


In 1Thess5:22, Apostle Paul said: *"Stay away from every kind of evil*"(NLT). You cannot afford to pitch your tent near sin, it is magnetic and alluring. Sometimes, we don't have the intention to do certain things but we do them because we place ourselves in an environment where that thing thrives. Whether we believe it or not, our immediate environment ( physical & spiritual) play a great role in our lives. There are relationships you need to break before they break you. Don't be a friend to a thief or sexually immoral person if you don't want to do same. *Any man that want to please God must be very far from the things that displeases Him*.


*PRAYER*: Father, I receive the grace to pitch my tent rightly in Jesus name.

DEVOTIONAL 0084 | Source start line: 10358

Sunday,
March 14th, 2021.


*WHEN THE TIME OF GOD'S FAVOUR COMES*



Esther 6:1 On that night the king could not sleep; and he commanded to bring the book of records of the chronicles, and they were read before the king.


I know a young girl whose mother is a widow. She's been struggling to take care of her three kids and life hasn't been good since the demise of her husband. One day, a wealthy lawyer called introducing himself as the man whose children were taught in private coaching in high school by her late husband who was a science teacher. He said God woke him up one night with an instruction to ask how she was faring since her husband died. Also, he was instructed by God to take responsibility for the education of her eldest child. This wealthy lawyer told his wife and children( most of whom have graduated from the university and living abroad) the instruction he received from the Lord.


I am close to this family to some extent. This man decided to call me on phone and he told me what the Holy Spirit instructed him and his wife to do. During that conversation, he instructed i put the phone on speaker so that this young girl can hear our conversation. He asked the girl the course she would love to study In the university and she replied. He said, "I will sponsor you to study your choice course in any university that offer the course in the world". Since then till date, he's been living up to his promise. Its been happening like a dream ever since that great favour came the way of that widow.

When your time of favour comes, one of the things that will happen is that God will put a very heavy sense of compulsion in the hearts of men to help you. It may happen that they'll lose their sleep just because of you. Not only that, those who should help you will remember and locate you so that they can be of help to you. I'm glad to tell you that your time is now. Psalms 102:13 says, *"Thou shalt arise, and have mercy upon Zion: for the time to favour her, yea, the set time, is come*". We all need help. I told some brethren that my life need God's help. I need God's favour upon my life and ministry. What about you? What you're struggling to achieve or to do can be gotten on the platter of God's favour. If you can pray, this dimension can become your new normal.

*PRAYER*: Father, let my whole life experience your favour in Jesus name.

DEVOTIONAL 0085 | Source start line: 10457

3/15/21, 5:21 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: PROJECTSAVE DEVOTIONAL
Monday,
March 15th, 2021.

*STAYING VICTORIOUS OVER DEMONS* Pt.1

Luke 4:18 The Spirit of the Lord is upon me, because he hath anointed me to preach the gospel to the poor; he hath sent me to heal the brokenhearted, to preach deliverance to the captives, and recovering of sight to the blind, to set at liberty them that are bruised, KJV


*WHAT JESUS SAID*

Someone asked me a question about deliverance. She inquired to know why demons keep  manifesting through the shame set people during deliverance sessions all the time. She had observed that same set of brethren always need deliverance or are the ones that roll on the floor and scream during every deliverance sessions. I have also seen the same thing happen over the years. I have seen people who had momentary relief from demonic oppression and later had a relapse. If you've been around for a while, you might have seen this too. Why is it so? Whose fault is it?

In Matthew 12:44-45, Jesus gave the answer: *"When the unclean spirit is gone out of a man, he walketh through dry places, seeking rest, and findeth none.   Then he saith, I will return into my house from whence I came out; and when he is come, he findeth it empty, swept, and garnished.   Then goeth he, and taketh with himself seven other spirits more wicked than himself, and they enter in and dwell there: and the last state of that man is worse than the first. Even so shall it be also unto this wicked generation*". This portion of scriptures is an answer to the question raised.

That you cast demons out yesterday doesn't mean they won't attempt to return at other times. If you open the door of your life, they'll enter. That's the point Jesus established in the scripture we read.  God's power is potent to cast out demons and dislodge them from their hidden places. When the same set of people keep manifesting during deliverance sessions all the time, God is not at fault. There are certain things they must do and keep doing.   The person being ministered to needs scriptural knowledge on how stay victorious over demons by exercising his believers authority. *Knowledge (of the word&your new creation status) sustains your victory over demons more than any other thing*.  That's what will keep you from becoming a candidate of "deliverance meetings". God's intention for us is to become victorious over demons not to dread them.

*CONFESSION*: Father I thank you for the victory on the cross. I reign and rule over the demons in Jesus name.
