*STAYING VICTORIOUS OVER DEMONS* Pt.2

Luke 10:19 Look, I have given you authority over all the power of the enemy, and you can walk among snakes and scorpions and crush them. Nothing will injure you. NLT


*TAKING AUTHORITY IN JESUS NAME*

*Redemption is not reduction*. I was in a conversation with someone sometimes ago. The demon that spoke through the person challenged me asking "who are you"? Note, I didn't say the person asked me that question but the demon spirit in her.  In that confession, the demon spirit said so many things that got me upset from within my spirit. It all started like a harmless conversation between me and the person under demonic influence in the presence of three other persons. We didn't take the whole thing serious at first not until we realized that a demon was speaking through her. The moment I realized I had wasted too much time listening to the boasts and rants of another Goliath, I switched on my new creation mentality and commanded the spirit to leave.

She hit the floor without me touching her. The power of God filled the room and the atmosphere changed. After taking authority in the name of Jesus, it was as if she came to her senses and she began to talk normally. One of the keys to staying victorious over demons is to take authority over them using the name of Jesus. The moment you notice you've come under demonic influence, use the authority you have. Never treat demons with kids gloves. Stop going around looking for who will help you chase demons out. Jesus said, *"These miraculous signs will accompany those who believe: They will cast out demons in my name, and they will speak in new languages*", Mark16:17 NLT.

You have the redemptive right to send demons packing. *Don't be soldier acting like a boy scout neither should you be a Lion living like a pussy cat*. The power in you and authority you have is far greater than what is on the other side. *“Little children, you are from God and have overcome them, for he who is in you is greater than he who is in the world*",1 John 4:4, ESV. Hallelujah!!! Jesus dealt with demons on the cross and gave you the victory: *"In this way, he disarmed the spiritual rulers and authorities. He shamed them publicly by his victory over them on the cross*", Col 2:15 NLT. Your duty is to enforce the victory of Christ by taking authority over the hordes of hell. It is shameful to see God's children running and cringing in fear of demons. God is not glorified when demons press you, have sex with you  and even feed you in your sleep. Take authority now.

*CONFESSION*: I take authority over demons troubling my life and I send you parking in Jesus name.

*STAYING VICTORIOUS OVER DEMONS* Pt.3

Luke 10:19 Look, I have given you authority over all the power of the enemy, and you can walk among snakes and scorpions and crush them. Nothing will injure you. NLT

*KNOW YOUR NEW CREATION STATUS(A)*

*Knowledge is very important in this warfare*. I heard the story of some brethren who engaged in a deliverance session. They struggled to cast the demon out but nothing happened. The demon asked them, "where are you seated"? None of them could answer. A brother who heard all that was happening moved closer to the scene and he gave a scriptural reply, "I am seated with Christ in heavenly places, in Jesus name come out of her". That was how the demon left. *In this warfare, the knowledge of your identity in Christ is vital*. Demons won't fear you because of your title neither will they tremble because of your physical height.

In Proverbs 11:9, the bible says, *"An hypocrite with his mouth destroyeth his neighbour: but through knowledge shall the just be delivered*". The deliverance of the righteous is not in long hours of gymnastics kind of prayer. *Some persons go physical when casting out demons using physical energy without scriptural knowledge energy*. The best way to conduct deliverance is to impart knowledge of God's word into the person in need of deliverance before and after casting out demons. That's why Jesus said in Luke 4:18, *"Luke 4:18 The Spirit of the Lord is upon me, because he hath anointed me to... PREACH(impart knowledge) DELIVERANCE to the captives...(Emphasis mine)*",KJV. Hallelujah!!!.

*According to Jesus, deliverance is preached before it is prayed*. Some are freaks of "deliverance prayers" but fail woefully in seeking the knowledge that will sustain their deliverance and put them above. That's why the same demon keep tormenting them over and over again. You'll see such persons manifest the demon in them in all deliverance meetings. A believer who doesn't know his redemptive rights and privileges will always be in need of "deliverance prayers". When you know who you are in Christ, your kingdom authority, your placement and the status of your sonship, you will put demons where they belong always.


*CONFESSION*: I am the righteousness of God in Christ Jesus. I have the nature of God in me. I've been translated from the kingdom of darkness into the kingdom of his dear son. I live in light and I'm full of light. Nothing shall by any means hurt me. I take authority over demons troubling my life and I send you parking in Jesus name.

*STAYING VICTORIOUS OVER DEMONS* Pt.4



Luke 10:19 Look, I have given you authority over all the power of the enemy, and you can walk among snakes and scorpions and crush them. Nothing will injure you. NLT



*KNOW YOUR NEW CREATION STATUS(B)*



A man decided to travel by sea to another country. He paid for the ticket of a ship that was sailing to the country he wanted to visit. The journey lasted for weeks. He traveled with snacks and bread he felt could sustain him through the journey. After a while, his bread became stale and he began to starve. He saw other passengers feasting and merrying in the ship. When he could no longer bear the starvation, he walked up to one of those who sat at the table to eat and begged for some food. The other man looked at him with amazement. He asked, "Did you pay for this trip"? He replied with a 'yes'. The man requested to see his ticket. He showed this co passenger his ticket. The man showed him where it was written that the payment for the trip covered feeding, medicals and other royalty treatment.  For once, this man felt good but mixed with regret. He felt he had suffered ignorantly. The ticket was an all expense paid trip but he struggled to survive almost all through the journey.


That's the case with many believers today. They do not know the benefits that accrue to redemption. They have a new status that is celestial but they prefer to maintain their terrestrial status where devil and demons hold sway. In Col1:13,  Apostle said, *"For he has rescued us from the kingdom of darkness and transferred us into the Kingdom of his dear Son*", NLT. Hallelujah!!! Did you read that? Redemption puts us in a new world. Formerly, we were with the devil, he was our king and master but the moment we gave our lives to Jesus, power changed hands and we moved to the kingdom of God's dear son. That's a superior kingdom. Hallelujah!!!

God didn't stop there. In Eph2:6, Paul said, *"For he raised us from the dead along with Christ and seated us with him in the heavenly realms because we are united with Christ Jesus*",NLT. You are seated above while demons are beneath. Where you are is far from where they are. Regardless of the attacks you may have faced, this is the word of God and nothing can change it. If you start thinking in line with God's word and you approach demonic onslaughts with this word-based mentality, you'll surely be victorious. We are dealing with a defeated foe that is trying to recover from the wounds of the battle he lost.


*CONFESSION*: I am the righteousness of God in Christ Jesus. I have the nature of God in me. I've been translated from the kingdom of darkness into the kingdom of his dear son. I live in light and I'm full of light. Nothing shall by any means hurt me. I take authority over demons troubling my life and I send you parking in Jesus name.

DEVOTIONAL 0089 | Source start line: 10714

3/19/21, 6:16 AM - Adu D: PROJECTSAVE DEVOTIONAL

*STAYING VICTORIOUS OVER DEMONS* Pt.5



Luke 10:19 Look, I have given you authority over all the power of the enemy, and you can walk among snakes and scorpions and crush them. Nothing will injure you. NLT



*THE FORCE OF FAITH*

Permit me to illustrate this point using a football match. If your country or favourite club has played an opposing team but you had no time to watch it, however, maybe a friend told you the match ended 3-0 in favour of your team, you wouldn't be anxious if you're to watch a replay of that football match. Even while watching the replay, if your opponent is pressing towards the goal, because of the information at your disposal, you just know the opposing team cannot score. Your faith is rocksolid because you're watching a match that had been decided in your favour. That's faith in expression.

*Spiritual warfare is faith warfare*. We fight from the standpoint of what we believe Christ has done for us on the cross not in what the arms of flesh has done or can do. A lot of Christians want to fight the devil. To them, spiritual warfare involves a lot on their part but they fail to realize that the real battle has been fought and won on the cross: *"And having disarmed the powers and authorities, he made a public spectacle of them, triumphing over them by the cross*", Col 2:15 NIV. Hallelujah!!! The devil is a defeated foe. *Christ is not about to defeat the devil, he has been defeated already*.

No matter how hard you pray and fast, you cannot outdo what Christ has done on the cross. You'll be scripturally wrong to think of spiritual warfare in terms of trying to do something outside what Christ has done. The major challenge for most believers is how to experience that victory that Christ won on the cross. The victories and benefits of salvation that we read about in the bible seem to some as fairy tale. They often ask why they are still under demonic oppression and caged by the enemy. They become uneasy the moment they hear demons or Satan mentioned. What they hear about the power of God is opposite their daily experience. The answer to this quagmire is in the bible: *"For every child of God defeats this evil world, and we achieve this victory through our faith*", 1 John 5:4 NLT. Faith( in what Christ has done) is the key to experiencing victory. That faith is what gives us the assurance that the battle is won even before we start praying. *When we engage in faith warfare, we are not fighting for victory rather, we are fighting from victory*. It is like watching the replay of a match that had been won. You don't watch the replay of a football match with fear because it has been decided already.

*CONFESSION*: I am the righteousness of God in Christ Jesus. I have the nature of God in me. I've been translated from the kingdom of darkness into the kingdom of his dear son. I live in light and I'm full of light. Nothing shall by any means hurt me. I take authority over demons troubling my life and I send you parking in Jesus name.

3/20/21, 10:46 PM - Lanre Oyeleke: ProjectSave Devotional
Saturday,
March 20th, 2021.

*STAYING VICTORIOUS OVER DEMONS* Pt.6

Luke 10:19 Look, I have given you authority over all the power of the enemy, and you can walk among snakes and scorpions and crush them. Nothing will injure you. NLT

*PURITY FACTOR*

I have asked to know why some never cease to be tormented, oppressed and influenced by demons. They're always in need of "deliverance". If there's a power service/impartation meeting, they manifest almost always. They wriggle like snake or start screaming. I know some of you have seen things like this and asked why. Aside ignorance and other factors, I realized that the absence of purity causes power leakage.

Proverbs16:7 says, *"When people’s lives please the Lord , even their enemies are at peace with them*". This is a general truth from scriptures that applies to us in this age.


We cannot talk about spiritual warfare without talking about holy living. There's a connection between the authority you wield over demons and the life you're living. Anyone living in sin is a friend to the devil and such a fellow cannot be victorious over the kingdom he represent. Sin opens the door widely for demons to come take permanent residence in lives. *Sin breaks the edge for the serpent(Satan and his demons) to bite*. Stop playing with sin if you want to be free from demonic influence over your life.

*Holy living  is a defense*. The deliverance some folks need is the deliverance from carnality and lust for worldliness. Jumping from deliverance meetings to another may not help you if you don't realize quickly that you're the culprit in this matter.  Ask the Lord for grace to live a life that is pleasing unto him and he'll take care of your enemies.

*CONFESSION*: I am the righteousness of God in Christ Jesus. I have the nature of God in me, so therefore, I walk in purity. I've been translated from the kingdom of darkness into the kingdom of his dear son. I live in light and I'm full of light. Nothing shall by any means hurt me. I take authority over demons troubling my life and I send you parking in Jesus name.

*STAYING VICTORIOUS OVER DEMONS* Pt.7



Eph6:18 And pray in the Spirit on all occasions with all kinds of prayers and requests. With this in mind, be alert and always keep on praying for all the Lord’s people.




*A VIRILE LIFE OF PRAYER*


*A virile life of prayer sets you on fire and makes you untouchable for demons*. Sometimes, you may wonder why some believers come under persistent demonic oppression and have become victims of satanic influence. Most times, their prayer life is weak or non existent.


*Consistency in the place of prayer sets us on fire and makes us flaming hot for the devil to touch*. There's a dimension in God where your name will cause ripples in the waters of satanic kingdom. A man in contemporary church history called A.A.Allen operated in that dimension. He'll tell demon possessed folks saying, "I am A.A.Allen" and the demon in them will scream. Consistency in the place of prayer launched him there.

Jesus and Paul  were known by demons having tormented them severally: *"But one time when they tried it, the evil spirit replied, “I know Jesus, and I know Paul, but who are you*?” Acts 19:15 NLT. Jesus' appearance made demons to feel uneasy (Matthew 8:28-34; Mark 5:1-20). They didn't get there by accident or by mere wishing. *Their humanity was subsumed in divinity in the place of prayer. They walked in the power of heaven and tormented hell till it manifested physically.* Until we take our prayer lives serious, demons will continue to have a field day in the affairs of our lives.

*PRAYER*: Father, set my prayer altar on fire in Jesus name

DEVOTIONAL 0093 | Source start line: 10868

3/21/21, 11:59 PM - Lanre Oyeleke: PROJECTSAVE DEVOTIONAL
Monday,
March 22nd, 2021.

*REDEFINING SUCCESS* Part 1.

Proverbs 28: 22 Greedy people try to get rich quick but don’t realize they’re headed for poverty. NLT

*WEALTH MENTALITY*

I was elated when i linked up with a teenager whom I knew when he was a young boy in primary school. He started a conversation with me on facebook. Not having seen him for years, i asked to know how he was faring and all. I began preaching the gospel to him. During our conversation, I noticed he stressed so much on making money. This young teenager was obsessed with how to make money. I asked if he has children and wife he was responsible for. He told me he had none since he was still in S.S.S.3. When I heard that, I told him he doesn't need money because his parents should take care of him. He said I can't understand. I knew he had been exposed to  wrong people who fanned the embers of greed into flames in him.

Later, it occurred to me that his definition of success in life is having plenty money. *Your definition of success will determine your pursuit*. What people pursue after daily is strongly connected to what they perceive as being successful. How God sees "success" is totally different from how we see it from the human standpoint. The culture of the world tend to give recognition to men and women who have made money. We put them in a class and even come up with rankings. That's why Forbes list exist. Unfortunately, some preachers are driven by wealth not souls. They pursue wealth and preach the gospel of materialism.

Jesus redefined success for us in Luke 12:15 saying, *"...“Beware! Guard against every kind of greed. Life is not measured by how much you own*", NLT. This is plain and simple to understand. *As far as God is concerned, your wealth and abundance is not his parameter for measuring success*. Far from it. People kill, defraud and lie to make money just because all they see as being successful is to be rich.  *Until you see success through God's eyes and get what it truly mean from God's perspective, your pursuit would be wrong*. Apostle Paul warned us: *For the love of money is the root of all kinds of evil. And some people, craving money, have wandered from the true faith and pierced themselves with many sorrows. But you, Timothy, are a man of God; so run from all these evil things. Pursue righteousness and a godly life, along with faith, love, perseverance, and gentleness*, 1 Timothy 6:10-11 NLT.

*PRAYER*: Father, I receive the grace to stop the wrong pursuit and follow after you in Jesus name

DEVOTIONAL 0094 | Source start line: 11066

3/23/21, 4:07 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: ProjectSave Devotional
Tuesday
March 23rd,  2021.

*REDEFINING SUCCESS* Part 2


John3:30 He must increase [in prominence], but I must decrease. AMP

*THE DESIRE TO BE FAMOUS*

Years back, I listened to a preacher on t.v.where he taught ministers in a meeting. He spoke about the revelation he had about the score card of churches in his country. According to him, in that revelation, God showed him churches in his country who were doing his will and running ministry according to pattern. He didn't know those churches because they weren't popular. He was sincere enough to tell us that his church was not among the churches God commended. He was surprised that the churches that were popular with men and had wide network of branches weren't popular in heaven for being at the centre of God's will.  In all, I picked a lesson from that story. *Popularity with men is not equal to success in God's sight*. With the pursuit of wealth comes the desire for fame as well.




Some even pray for fame and they sweat it out just to be known by men. To them, success is when you become a household name. Just think about the things that people do to be famous with men. A talented gospel singer may feel he doesn't have the publicity he desires so he may just switch to singing songs that aren't glorifying to God, increase fan base and attract endorsement. Those that want fame are always putting themselves on the spot where they can be noticed. If a preacher is consumed by the lust for fame, his church and programme publicity will point men to him and not Christ. That inordinate desire for fame is the reason why some call themselves by bogus titles and appellations alien to Christ. John(3:30) said, *"...This is the assigned moment for him to move into the center, while I slip off to the sidelines*", MSG. While some are concerned about souls of men, they're concerned about their "fan base" and the number of likes and followers on social media. They live to please their fans and perform to maintain the fame while neglecting the God. Their concern is not about what God says.

I think the Laodicean church had some measure of fame in their time but God's score card on them showed they failed him: *"You say, ‘I am rich. I have everything I want. I don’t need a thing!’ And you don’t realize that you are wretched and miserable and poor and blind and naked*", Rev 3:17 NLT. What a shame! Now that the world knows you and they deem you successful because you've become a household name, what is God saying about you? *True success begins the day your pursuit is channeled towards fulfilling God's will for your life*.

*PRAYER*: Father, I receive the grace to fine tune my pursuit towards eternal things in Jesus name. Amen

DEVOTIONAL 0095 | Source start line: 11173

3/24/21, 5:30 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: PROJECTSAVE DEVOTIONAL
wednesday,
March 24th, 2021.

*REDEFINING SUCCESS* Part 3

John3:30 He must increase [in prominence], but I must decrease. AMP

*THE QUEST FOR POSITIONAL POWER*

Another erroneous perspective some have about success is 'power'. They believe that being powerful or attaining to a powerful position is the height of success. The tussle for power is real. Those whose definition of success is tied to power will do all that is within their reach to attain to the position of power. This is the reason behind bitter politics around the globe today. Unfortunately, this inordinate desire to attain a powerful position exist among God's children and has brought a hellish dimension of politics, power tussle into the body of Christ. This is sad to say but it is real. I heard the story of two Pastors vying for an office in their denomination. The tussle degenerated into bitter fight between the duo and they exchanged words. Unfortunately, one of them lost four of his children the same day. When the hierarchy of the church saw what happened, they decided not to give either of them that office. There's no smoke without fire. Trying to establish a positional status is the reason why some take on bogus titles. My mentor told me how some ministers now use title like: 'AG' meaning assistant God.


We need to learn from Jesus. He was a focused man. His eyes was on the goal and the purpose for which he came not on titles. Even though he occupied all the five fold offices, he led a simple life. No special regalia like the Pharisees and Sadducees. When the Jews wanted to make him a king by force, he avoided it. JOHN 6:15  *"Then Jesus, knowing that they were about to come and take Him by force to make Him king, withdrew again to the mountain by Himself alone*". With the influence that Jesus had, he could have decided to contest for emperorship. The Jews would be glad to have their own as the ruler of the Roman empire after years of colonialism. However, the concern of Jesus was on doing the will of the father:  *"Jesus saith unto them, My meat is to do the WILL of him that sent me, and to finish his work*",  John4:34. Positional power meant nothing to Jesus. He was a man of purpose. If he hasn't fulfilled his purpose of salvation for man's redemption, heaven will adjudge him a failure even if he was an emperor or king. *The moment people start seeking positional power wrongly, they abandon their assignment in life*. That's the beginning of failure.


Tied to the lust for power is the pride of life. In 1John2:15-17,  John said, *"Do not love the world or the things in the world. If anyone loves the world, love for the Father is not in him. For all that is in the world, the lust of the flesh and the lust of the eyes and the pride of life, is not of the Father but is of the world. And the world passes away, and the lust of it; BUT HE WHO DOES THE WILL OF GOD ABIDES FOREVER*". Being in God's will has always been God's definition of success. Any other pursuit outside the will of God for our lives is the wrong pursuit regardless of achievements. That is why Paul said in Eph5:17, *"Wherefore be ye not unwise, but understanding what the will of the Lord is*".  There's a generic will of God written for us in scriptures and there's a specific will of God that has to be revealed to us by the Spirit of God. In both, we must please God. For example, If it is God's will for a man to live in a village doing ministry among an unreached tribe but he decides to pitch his tent in a city where he built large auditorium with numerous membership. Regardless of his prominence and how the media celebrate him, in God's score card, he is unsuccessful. Pitiably, we celebrate so many unsuccessful men today and discourage those ones heaven has given a pass mark. Who is a successful man or woman according to God's standard? The man at the centre of God's will.


*PRAYER*: Father, I receive the grace to know and do your will in all areas of my life in Jesus name.

*SAVED BY GRACE( WITHOUT WORKS) FOR GOOD WORKS* Pt.1


Eph2:8-9 God saved you by his grace when you believed. And you can’t take credit for this; it is a gift from God. Salvation is not a reward for the good things we have done, so none of us can boast about it. NLT

*UNDERSTANDING SALVATION*


I was teaching a group of believers sometime ago. I asked them to tell me the special good work(s) they did that got them saved. None could point to any. *God did not design salvation to be earned with good works. It is a gift from God that we didn't work for.* The nature of salvation is such that there is nothing a man can do or would have done to be saved other than to believe in Jesus. Yes, that's just it. Acts16:31: *"They replied, “Believe in the Lord Jesus and you will be saved, along with everyone in your household*”.




Religion teaches how to earn salvation. Religion tells you to do certain things, engage in rituals and ensure you follow certain codes of conduct before you can be saved. Man is not known to meet up with religious standards because it is full of do's and dont's. Even under Moses, they couldn't fulfill all of the 613 laws because of man's imperfections:
*"For no one can ever be made right with God by doing what the law commands. The law simply shows us how sinful we are*", Romans 3:20 NLT. Some are still trying to obey the laws of Moses so that they can earn status with God. If they succeed in obeying one today, they'll fail in others tomorrow. This what Paul say about such people: *"But those who depend on the law to make them right with God are under his curse, for the Scriptures say, “Cursed is everyone who does not observe and obey all the commands that are written in God’s Book of the Law*", Gal3:10.






The weight and burden of the law was too heavy for mankind to bear because failure to meet up with the standard of the law attracted punishment: *"For the law always brings punishment on those who try to obey it. (The only way to avoid breaking the law is to have no law to break!)*", Romans 4:15 NLT.  Thanks be to God who sent his son Jesus Christ to purchase redemption for us with his blood so that we won't need to do any good work, sacrifice or pay to saved. *Beware of any gospel that present to you the things you must do to be saved aside believing in the finished work of Christ on the cross*.


*PRAYER*: Lord, I thank you because my salvation is full and free. Help me to preach this gospel of your grace around the world in Jesus name. Amen

3/25/21, 11:16 PM - Lanre Oyeleke: PROJECTSAVE DEVOTIONAL

*SAVED BY GRACE( WITHOUT WORKS) FOR GOOD WORKS* Pt.2






Romans5:1-2 Therefore, since we have been justified through faith, we have peace with God through our Lord Jesus Christ, through whom we have gained access by faith into this grace in which we now stand. And we boast in the hope of the glory of God. NIV


*JUSTIFIED THROUGH FAITH*


A criminal was tried in a civil court and convicted of several crimes that should result in death sentence or life imprisonment. When he got to the court, the numerous allegations against him were proven true as there were witnesses to corroborate them. However, on the day of final hearing, the judge decided to declare him innocent despite the evidences. He hinged his judgement on the fact that this criminal through his lawyer deferred to the legal system instituted by the most righteous man in their country who sacrificed his life for all criminals who are ready to follow his path. As long as they follow him, they'll be declared innocent automatically. On this wise, this notorious criminal was declared innocent and made righteous in the order of the most righteous man in their country. This is the story of our justification as believers. The word justified means “pronounced or treated as righteous.” For a Christian, justification is the act of God not only forgiving the believer’s sins but imputing to him the righteousness of Christ. The moment you gave your life to Christ, you were made righteous. The very righteousness of Christ was imputed to you. *It is God's gift to every believer who comes to him through Christ*. *"God made him who had no sin to be sin for us, so that in him we might become the righteousness of God*",2 Corinthians 5:21 NIV. The Christian, being declared righteous, is thus freed from the guilt of sin.


Hallelujah!!! You have been made righteous not because of works or any special thing that you did. The court of heaven has declared you not guilty of your past because of the blood. God is no longer holding your past sins again you. You may not believe this because you've been told God will revisit your old sins. Here is what the bible says: *"For God was in Christ, reconciling the world to himself, NO LONGER COUNTING PEOPLE'S SINS AGAINST THEM. And he gave us this wonderful message of reconciliation*", 2 Corinthians 5:19 NLT. Now that you're born again, you can walk into God's presence without fear of being rejected or with shame because when God sees you, he doesn't see your dirty past any longer but a justified being: *"Therefore if any man be in Christ, he is a new creature: old things are passed away; behold, all things are become new*", 2 Corinthians 5:17 KJV. Hallelujah!!! Under the law, this kind of provision( justification) doesn't exist. Even if there was, they could not attain it through the law. Why? *"THE LAW OF MOSES WAS UNABLE TO SAVE US because of the weakness of our sinful nature. So God did what the law could not do. He sent his own Son in a body like the bodies we sinners have. And in that body God declared an end to sin’s control over us by giving his Son as a sacrifice for our sins*", Roman 8:3 NLT.


A writer said,  *"The teaching of justification by faith is what separates biblical Christianity from all other belief systems. In every religion, and in some branches of what is called “Christianity,” man is working his way to God. Only in true, biblical Christianity is man saved as a result of grace through faith. Only when we get back to the Bible do we see that justification is by faith, apart from works*". How can a man be declared righteous despite a rough past? Any price to pay to be justified? There is no ritual, no sacrament, no deed that can make us worthy of the righteousness of Christ. It is only by His grace, in response to our faith( in his finished work on the cross), that God has credited to us the holiness of His Son. This is what religion tries to offer with rigid codes but failed woefully. No matter how hard a man tries, salvation and the benefits cannot be earned through works of the law. The grace of God is deep, unfathomable and immense. The sinner just need to believe that the sacrifice of Christ on the cross is enough to cleanse his sin and make him justified. That's all. Hallelujah!!! I am justified by grace.




*PRAYER*: Lord, I thank you because my salvation is full and free. Thank you because I'm made righteous and declared innocent.  Help me to preach this gospel of your grace around the world in Jesus name. Amen

DEVOTIONAL 0098 | Source start line: 11480

3/27/21, 4:19 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: ProjectSave Devotional
Saturday,
March 27th, 2021.

*SAVED BY GRACE( WITHOUT WORKS) FOR GOOD WORKS* Pt.3

Eph1:7 He is so rich in kindness and grace that he purchased our freedom with the blood of his Son and forgave our sins. NLT


*FORGIVEN FREELY WITHOUT WORKS*


I watched the video of a Hindu festival titled "Khumbh Mela". The video is a documentary of the largest religious festival in the world that attracts about 80 million worshippers in a single week. People come from all over India to a river called "Ganges" for cleansing. In that festival, in the video i watched, there were about 23,000 officiating priests who poured water on people. The ritual involve pouring ashes on the head and washing in the river. Why are they doing all these? They're seeking for forgiveness. Even after doing all these things, they are not sure if they're forgiven. They keep coming every year because they are guilt ridden and never sure they're forgiven.


Hallelujah!!! Our case is different. We have received of the Lord full pardon for our sins: *"Blessed are those whose transgressions are forgiven, whose sins are covered*",Romans4:7. You don't need to beat yourself because of sins that GOD has forgiven and put out of his record. Isaiah 43:25 says: *"I, even I, am he who blots out your transgressions, for my own sake, and remembers your sins no more*". Hallelujah!!! He won't remember my sins any more for his own sake. The day you came to Christ, your slate(of sin) was wiped clean: *"Therefore if any man be in Christ, he is a new creature: old things are passed away; behold, all things are become new*", 2 Cor 5:17 KJV.


Forgiveness is the work of grace lavished on us because of the sacrifice of Christ on the cross of calvary not because of your effort. Fanny Crosby wrote in her popular hymn 'To God Be The Glory' saying, *"O perfect redemption, the purchase of blood, To every believer the promise of God; The vilest offender who truly believes,That moment from Jesus a pardon receives*".


When you preach to sinners, your duty is to point them to the atoning work of Christ on the cross and encourage them to believe in it. Righting all wrongs before salvation is not the way to earn salvation. If we embark on that, we'll not meet up. *When a man comes to the cross just as he is, with a single and believing heart focused on Jesus, he'll not return until he receives full pardon and the gift of salvation that comes by faith*. Salvation comes  by believing in Jesus: *“For this is how God loved the world: He gave his one and only Son, so that EVERYONE WHO BELIEVES IN HIM WILL NOT PERISH but have eternal life.  “There is no judgment against anyone who believes in him. But anyone who does not believe in him has already been judged for not believing in God’s one and only Son*", John 3:16, 18 NLT.

Anytime you try to do "things" aside believing in what Christ has done on the cross, you're clearly in the wrong. It doesn't matter how pious what people do to earn forgiveness may sound, as long as it is opposed to faith in the atoning work of Christ on the cross, it is not acceptable by God.

*CONFESSION*: Father, I thank you for lavishing your grace upon me. I am forgiven not because of works but by your grace. I am redeemed and free from guilt of wrongs in my past. My slate is wiped clean and I'm a new man. Hallelujah!!!

info@projectsaveng.org

3/28/21, 6:34 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: ProjectSave Devotional

*SAVED BY GRACE( WITHOUT WORKS) FOR GOOD WORKS* Pt.4



Eph 2:10 For we are his workmanship, created in Christ Jesus unto good works, which God hath before ordained that we should walk in them. KJV



*SAVED WITHOUT WORKS FOR GOOD WORKS*



Our redemption was paid for fully by Christ. We didn't work for it because we don't have to. If we try to work for it, we won't be able to pay for it. The price is just too high for any human to pay because: *"For all have sinned, and come short of the glory of God;*", Rom 3:23 KJV. We lack the spiritual capacity to purchase our own redemption because of the inherent sin nature that was passed to us through Adam: *"Wherefore, as by one man sin entered into the world, and death by sin; and so death passed upon all men, for that all have sinned:*", Rom5:12. The state of mankind without a saviour was pitiable and doomed but for Christ's rescue mission: *"For the wages of sin is death; but the gift of God is eternal life through Jesus Christ our Lord*", Rom6:23.


Though (good)works cannot earn salvation and couldn't have given us the gift of eternal life, yet we are saved to produce good works( Godly living) that is pleasing unto God. Babies don't come to the world on their own volition. Some persons paid the price for their coming into the world. The action of two people ( husband and wife) brought them into the world. Nothing is expected of a foetus in the womb but the moment they come into the world, the journey of responsibility begins. A child is expected to grow from dependence to independence, from childishness to maturity. This illustration points to what is expected of us after salvation. Now that you're in God's family, good works( Godly living) is expected of you. It should be a life style. In the opening text, that is why Paul said God hath ordained us to walk( present continuous tense in Greek) in them. Our daily walk is expected to be in godliness. That's the essence of your salvation.


That Christ-like character is what God expect from you now that you're saved. That is the good works you're expected to walk in. *You can bask in the euphoria of being saved by grace( which is true) but God is calling you and me to a place of responsibility as sons or else we'll slip into libertinism*. Your faith in the finished work of Christ that brought you into salvation must produce good deeds: *"So you see, faith by itself isn’t enough. Unless it produces good deeds, it is dead and useless*", James2:17 NLT.


*PRAYER*: Father, I receive the grace to abound in good work in Jesus name.

DEVOTIONAL 0100 | Source start line: 11683
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
3/29/21, 6:38 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: PROJECTSAVE DEVOTIONAL
Monday,
March 29th, 2021.

*SAVED BY GRACE( WITHOUT WORKS) FOR GOOD WORKS* Pt.5

Revelation 2:19, KJV: "I know thy works, and charity, and service, and faith, and thy patience, and thy works; and the last to be more than the first."


*I KNOW THY WORKS*

There are those in the body of Christ who believe we shouldn't be talking about works because we are not saved by works. It is absolutely true that we are not saved by works but we are saved to show works. Further, they misunderstand work to be rituals and Mosaic  practices. However, in context of relevant new testament scriptures, "works" does not always mean adherence to the law. In the opening text, Jesus was talking to the church in Thyatira, a new testament church that was saved by grace and washed in the blood of the lamb. He said to them, *"I know thy works*". If you read that verse and others contextually, you'll see that works implied good deeds or Christian character. Let's look at another version of the bible: *"I know all the things you do. I have seen your love, your faith, your service, and your patient endurance. And I can see your constant improvement in all these things*", Rev 2:19 NLT. The NIV used the phrase *"good deeds*" in place of "works".

In verse 19, they were commended by the Lord for good works but in the verses after, they were rebuked and a pronouncement of judgement was made upon them if they refuse to repent: *“But I have this complaint against you. You are permitting that woman—that Jezebel who calls herself a prophet—to lead my servants astray. She teaches them to commit sexual sin and to eat food offered to idols.  “Therefore, I will throw her on a bed of suffering, and those who commit adultery with her will suffer greatly unless they repent and turn away from her evil deeds*", Rev 2:20, 22 NLT. I believe you read that. Those are the very words of Jesus himself to a new testament church. I didn't frame them up.  It is unfortunate that some fail to recognize that repentance is necessary if found in the wrong. The believe that our sins are automatically forgiven without genuine repentance and asking for God's forgiveness is a huge deceit. Some believe post dated sins are already forgiven automatically. If it is true, why was Jesus telling the church in Thyatira to repent? Can't you see the opening sentence in verse 20? It says, *"Nevertheless, I have this against you:*". If Jesus had something against them, it showed that there was a persistent sin amidst them that needed repentance. Instead of saying that sins( present&future) have been forgiven automatically, I'll rather have you say the provision for forgiveness of sins has been made available awaiting appropriation. *Forgiveness is for those that appropriate the provision made for it in redemption*.

Who says our works( what we do after salvation) are not important? I'll be glad you take out time to read Revelation 2&3. You'll see how Jesus stressed so much on "works" to new testament believers like you and me. What we do doesn't go unnoticed by God. Quit thinking God doesn't see what you do or to think he's unconcerned about it. He is very concerned. Imagine Jesus saying, "I know thy works". If they're good enough,  he would not need to say, " I have this against you". Our reward will be based on our work: *"And, behold, I come quickly; and my reward is with me, to give EVERY MAN( Believers&Unbelievers) according as his work shall be*", Rev22:12. How is your work? When your work is no different from that of worldly people, you'll receive their kind of reward- eternal damnation. Think about the kind of life you're living and your walk with the Lord, what will Jesus say about your works?


*PRAYER*: Father, I receive the grace to produce the fruit of salvation in true righteousness and holiness in Jesus name.

DEVOTIONAL 0101 | Source start line: 11732
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
3/30/21, 6:52 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: ProjectSave Devotional
Tuesday
March 30th,  2021.

*SAVED BY GRACE( WITHOUT WORKS) FOR GOOD WORKS* Pt.6
Romans5:17 For if, by the trespass of the one man, death reigned through that one man, how much more will those who receive God’s abundant provision of grace and of the gift of righteousness reign in life through the one man, Jesus Christ! NIV


*TWO DIMENSIONS OF RIGHTEOUSNESS*

There are two major dimensions of righteousness for the believer: (a) Gift of righteousness, (b) Fruit(works) of righteousness. I won't dwell much on the former because it has been treated in previous teachings. However, I need to restate that the gift of righteousness was given to all believers when we came to Christ. We didn't work for it neither did we do anything to merit it. The very righteousness of Christ was imputed to us at salvation so that we can stand before God without feeling guilty and ashamed of our past. *"God made him who had no sin to be sin for us, so that in him we might become the righteousness of God*", 2 Cor 5:21 NIV. Hallelujah!!! We are the righteousness of God inChrist.


On the flip side, there's another dimension of righteousness Apostle Paul called, "Fruit(work)of righteousness". This is the practical expression of doing the right things as expected of us as believers: *"And this is my prayer: that your love may abound more and more in knowledge and depth of insight, so that you may be able to discern what is best and may be pure and blameless for the day of Christ, FILLED WITH THE FRUIT OF RIGHTEOUSNESS that comes through Jesus Christ—to the glory and praise of God*", Philippians 1:9-11 NIV. In the NLT translation of the bible, it was called, *"Fruit of salvation*". This fruit of righteousness is expected of the believer so that he can be pure and blameless for the day of Christ(Rapture of the saints).


You cannot dwell on having the gift of righteousness only. There must be a corresponding fruit to show for it. You cannot be living in sin and shouting " I'm the righteousness of God in Christ Jesus" when the fruit of your salvation is not evident. Just imagine a man with a traveling passport but doesn't have a visa. No matter how he screams at the airport, he won't be allowed to board. God is calling everyone of us to the place of responsibility. Babies are spoon fed but sons are responsible. Christ must be seen in us through the fruit(works) that we produce: *"Let your light shine before men in such a way that they may see your good deeds and moral excellence, and [recognize and honor and] glorify your Father who is in heaven*", Matthew 5:16 AMP.


*PRAYER*: Father, fill me with the fruit of righteousness so that I can stand pure and blameless before you on the day of Christ in Jesus name.

DEVOTIONAL 0102 | Source start line: 11800
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
3/31/21, 7:12 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: PROJECTSAVE DEVOTIONAL
wednesday,
March 31st, 2021.


*SAVED BY GRACE( WITHOUT WORKS) FOR GOOD WORKS* Pt.7

Galatians 5:22-23 But the fruit of the Spirit is love, joy, peace, forbearance, kindness, goodness, faithfulness, gentleness and self-control. Against such things there is no law. NIV

*PORTRAIT OF FRUIT OF RIGHTEOUSNESS*

A portrait is a painting, drawing, photograph, or engraving of a person, especially one depicting only the face or head and shoulders. In essence, a portrait depicts something. To understand the fruit of righteousness the believer is expected to produce, you need to examine what Paul called the fruit of the Spirit. They're one and thesame. *The fruit of the Spirit depict christian conduct and character that is expected of believers. Any believer whose life manifest the fruit of the Spirit is pleasing to the Lord and is living like Christ.* The totality of Christ likeness is depicted by the fruit of the Spirit.


As new testament believers, our lives should not be patterned after Mosaic laws. We're meant to live Spirit led lives: *"But when you are directed by the Spirit, you are not under obligation to the law of Moses*", Gal 5:18 NLT. When you live a life that is led by the Spirit then you'll be walking in true righteousness and holiness that is expected of you. Some are still obsessed with the ceremonies and rituals of the law. Truly, they desire to please God but their reference(Mosaic codes) is wrong. They think they can succeed where the Jews failed. In the long run, they end up fulfilling the laws that are convenient for them and they leave out others. Paul said such persons are under a curse: *"But those who depend on the law to make them right with God are under his curse, for the Scriptures say, “Cursed is everyone who does not observe and obey all the commands that are written in God’s Book of the Law*”, Gal 3:10 NLT.

Why put yourself under a curse? Our concern should be how to produce the fruit that is expected of us as believers. The only way to produce the fruit is to abide in Christ: *"Abide in me, and I in you. As the branch cannot bear fruit of itself, except it abide in the vine; no more can ye, except ye abide in me.   I am the vine, ye are the branches: He that abideth in me, and I in him, the same bringeth forth much fruit: for without me ye can do nothing*", John 15:4-5 KJV. As you stay in Christ and remain in the place of fellowship with him, your life will bear fruit.


*PRAYER*: Father, I receive the grace to abide in you so that my life can produce the fruit of the Spirit in Jesus name.

4/1/21, 5:12 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: PROJECTSAVE DEVOTIONAL
Thursday,
April 1st, 2021.


*SAVED BY GRACE( WITHOUT WORKS) FOR GOOD WORKS* Pt.8


2 Pet1:5-7 In view of all this, make every effort to respond to God’s promises. Supplement your faith with a generous provision of moral excellence, and moral excellence with knowledge, and godliness with brotherly affection, and brotherly affection with love for everyone. NLT

*ADD THESE SUPPLEMENTS TO YOUR FAITH*


A supplement is a substance you add to something. Sometimes, dieticians recommend food supplements so that deficient nutrients can be adequately supplied to our bodies. In 2 Pet1:5-7, apostle Peter enjoined that we add certain supplements to our faith in Christ. In preceding verses(3&4), he spoke about the divine nature and precious promises we have as believers. All that sounds good. It is sweet to hear about our imputed righteousness and all the benefits that accrue to the new creation.


However, Peter said we still have a role to play in keeping that precious salvation: *"So, dear brothers and sisters, work hard to prove that you really are among those God has called and chosen. DO THESE THINGS, and you will never fall away*" 2 Pet1:10 NLT. What are the things he said we should do? You'll find the list in the opening text. In verse 10, he made it clear that if we add those Christian virtues to our lives, we won't fall away. From what? From God's eternal kingdom: *"Do these things, and you will never fall away...Then God will give you a grand entrance into the eternal Kingdom of our Lord and Savior Jesus Christ*", 2 Peter 1:10-11 NLT.


Hey! From the scripture above,  it shows there's a possibility for someone who has enjoyed God's free gift of righteousness and benefited from grace to be denied entry into God's eternal kingdom if he doesn't do the things listed in vs 5-7. Dearest, you have a role to play. Don't hide behind God's grace and gift of righteousness to live a life that gratify fleshly desires. Grace is not a licence to sin but an empowerment to produce the fruits of righteousness: *"For the grace of God that bringeth salvation hath appeared to all men,  Teaching us that, denying ungodliness and worldly lusts, we should live soberly, righteously, and godly, in this present world*"; Titus 2:11,12 KJV


*PRAYER*: Father, I receive the grace to produce the fruit of righteousness in Jesus name.

DEVOTIONAL 0104 | Source start line: 11917
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
4/2/21, 7:26 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: PROJECTSAVE DEVOTIONAL
Friday,
April 2nd, 2021.


*SAVED BY GRACE( WITHOUT WORKS) FOR GOOD WORKS* Pt.9

Acts2:21 And it shall come to pass, that whosoever shall call on the name of the Lord shall be saved.

*SALVATION: PAST, PRESENT&FUTURE*

Reading through the bible, the word "save/saved" was used in three senses:
( a) We have been saved( Justification)
(b) We are being saved(Sanctification)
(c) We shall be saved( Glorification)

When you came to Christ, you were saved: *"For whosoever shall call upon the name of the Lord shall be saved*",
Romans 10:13 KJV. That very day you confessed Jesus as your Lord and personal saviour, you received the priceless gift of salvation. You became a new creation and your record of wrongs were wiped clean. You were justified of all your wrongs and declared righteous, 2Cor 5:17, 21.For those that are already in Christ,this happened in the past. You have it already. You didn't do anything to merit this, no work, simply grace. However, salvation doesn't stop at this phase.


1 Corinthians1:18 open to us another dimension or phase of salvation: *“For the message of the cross is foolishness to those who are perishing, but to us who are BEING SAVED it is the power of God*".The capitalized phrase refers to an ongoing process. More like a present continuous event. The salvation of the believer has a present dimension to it and this is where work begins. At this phase, by the help of the Holy Spirit, you're expected to produce fruit of righteousness and guard your salvation jealously:  *“Therefore, my brothers and sisters, MAKE EVERY EFFORT to confirm your calling and election. For if you do these things, you will never stumble*", 2Pet1:10. You don't go to sleep at this phase, by the help of the Holy Spirit, you work out salvation with fear and trembling by abstaining from the works of the flesh and living a Spirit controlled life: *"Wherefore, my beloved, as ye have always obeyed, not as in my presence only, but now much more in my absence, work out your own salvation with fear and trembling.  For it is God which worketh in you both to will and to do of his good pleasure*", Philippians 2:12-13 KJV. If salvation is on auto drive after we prayed the sinners prayer, Paul wouldn't have enjoined that we "work out" our salvation. *What has been worked into us ( justification, Sanctification) in Christ when we came to Christ must be worked out (expressed through daily Christian living) by us through the help of the Holy Spirit. It is our decision to do this not God's. Failure to do this, we risk losing the next phase of salvation which is futuristic*.



The last phase in salvation as seen in the bible is the salvation we are waiting for:  *“And do this, understanding the present time: The hour has already come for you to wake up from your slumber, because our salvation is nearer now than when we first believed*", Romans13:11. The salvation that is nearer than when we first believed speaks of the future salvation of believers when we shall meet with Christ in glory( heaven). It is referred to as the "blessed hope": *"Looking for that blessed hope, and the glorious appearing of the great God and our Saviour Jesus Christ*", Titus2:13. Because of this future salvation, James said:, *“Therefore, get rid of all moral filth and the evil that is so prevalent and humbly accept the word planted in you, which can save you*", James1:21. Paul also expressed the same thought about the salvation to come when he said,*"Watch your life and doctrine closely. Persevere in them, because if you do, you will save both yourself and your hearers*”,James1:21. Dearest, the salvation ahead of us( glorification of the saints) should not be missed by anyone who truly loves the Lord and want to see him just as he is. This should drive us into a life of purity: *"Dear friends, now we are children of God, and what we will be has not yet been made known. But we know that WHEN CHRIST APPEARS, we shall be like him, for we shall see him as he is. All who have this hope( of his appearing) in him purify themselves, just as he is pure*", 1John3:2-3,NIV. Emphasis mine.






*PRAYER*: Father, I receive the grace to stand firm in you till the end in Jesus name.

DEVOTIONAL 0105 | Source start line: 12067

4/3/21, 6:02 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: ProjectSave Devotional
Saturday,
April 3rd, 2021.

*TETELESTAI: BILL PAID IN FULL*

John 19:30 When Jesus therefore had received the vinegar, he said, It is finished: and he bowed his head, and gave up the ghost. KJV

Most people believe in the importance of the last words of a man before death.  The last words of Christ on the cross is "It is finished." That sentence is pregnant with a deep meaning than what we may understand it to mean in English language. Found only in the Gospel of John, the Greek word translated “it is finished” is tetelestai, an accounting term that means “paid in full.” The word "tetelestai" was also written on business documents or receipts in New Testament times indicating that a bill had been paid in full.


What transaction did Jesus pay for? Who owed the debt? In Romans 3:23, Paul said, *"For all have sinned, and come short of the glory of God;*". The debt that Jesus paid for was the debt of sin.  Mankind was on a downward spiral into Christless eternity. God saw it fit to send his son in the likeness of man to come and pay the debt we owe. Hallelujah!!! My sins have been paid for in full. I'm not trying to sacrifice to get them paid, someone's sacrifice paid for it once and for all: *"Christ suffered for our sins once for all time. He never sinned, but he died for sinners to bring you safely home to God. He suffered physical death, but he was raised to life in the Spirit*", 1Pet3:18.


To expound further, whatever sin has brought upon your life is part of the "Tetelestai" transaction. He had paid for it all. Don't let the devil tell you that you have to partake in the generational curse operating in your family neither should you accept sickness and other dirty dozens of his as normal. When Christ breathe his last, he saw into the future and all the devil may want to bring upon your life and he said: "It is finished: I have paid the bill in full". I want you to stand on the word of God and reject the bill of sickness, frustration, despair, depression and a life of failure that the enemy is writing with your name attached to it. Tell the devil you no longer owe him a dime because someone paid your bill in full so that you can free. Hallelujah!!!



*PRAYER*: Thank you for paying my debt. Hallelujah!!! My sins are fully paid for. I am free from condemnation.  Devil, take your nonsense(...mention name) out of my life and family in Jesus name.

*THE RESURRECTION POWER*


Phil 3:10 That I may know him, and the power of his resurrection, and the fellowship of his sufferings, being made conformable unto his death; KJV


There's something about the resurrection of Jesus that makes it a unique event. There are several testimonies about people who died and came back to life after prayer yet that cannot be compared to the resurrection of Jesus Christ. *The difference between the resurrection of Jesus Christ and those who came back to life after they were prayed for is that the later will die again but Jesus rose never to die again. He now possess the power of an indestructible life. Mortality has been shed off and now he has put on immortality. Hallelujah!!!*


The worst thing the devil ever did to Jesus was to have him killed. That's the height of what he could do. If he had any power at all, the power he used to get the son of God crucified was the highest display of his power but that was his greatest undoing. Paul said, *"No, the wisdom we speak of is the mystery of God —his plan that was previously hidden, even though he made it for our ultimate glory before the world began. But the rulers of this world have not understood it; if they had, they would not have crucified our glorious Lord*", 1 Corinthians 2:7-8 NLT. The devil has done his worst and he cannot do more than that. The power that raised Christ from the dead is limitless and eternal. Hallelujah!!!


We are partakers of that resurrection power. That power is already at work in believers. Romans8:11 says, *"And if the Spirit of him who raised Jesus from the dead is living in you, he who raised Christ from the dead will also give life to your mortal bodies because of his Spirit who lives in you*", Rom8:11. Hallelujah!!!  The resurrection power is available for use. No power of hell can stand the unleashing of God's resurrection power. Hell has done their worst to our master and he triumphed. That's why I know that your challenge is solvable. That sickness, backwardness, pain, shame, frustration, barrenness, depression, demonic oppression and all the fiery darts of the enemy can't stand the resurrection power of Christ. You will triumph.


*CONFESSION*: I unleash the resurrection power of Christ on (...mention name of your challenge). I reject it and send it back to hell. It is not mine. The power of God is at work in my mortal body to quicken me and make me fit spirit, soul and body in Jesus name.

4/5/21, 3:43 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: PROJECTSAVE DEVOTIONAL
Monday,
APRIL 5th, 2021.

*DEBUNKING LIES: HE AROSE*

1 Cor 15:14 And if Christ has not been raised, then all our preaching is useless, and your faith is useless.

Over the years, there has been several attempts to debunk the bodily resurrection of Jesus Christ.  It is a deliberate attempt to rubbish our faith because the culmination of Christ's purpose on earth was fulfilled in his resurrection not even in his birth. Virtually all religion believe that he was born but they're confused about his resurrection. If Christ had not risen, we wouldn't have been saved. One of such demonic theories is the swoon theory. This theory believed that Jesus fainted from exhaustion and was revived in the coolness of the tomb, arose and departed. They forgot that his executioners were professionals who knew how to crucify a criminal to the point of death. He was confirmed dead by his executioners. Another theory is "Hallucination theory". They believe that all of Christ's post resurrection appearances were not real, people who claimed they saw him only had mere hallucinations. This is laughable, isn't it? *He was buried, and he was raised from the dead on the third day, just as the Scriptures said. He was seen by Peter and then by the Twelve. After that, he was seen by more than 500 of his followers at one time, most of whom are still alive, though some have died*, 1 Cor 15:4-6 NLT. Can 500 persons hallucinate all at once?

The devil didn't stop there. Through his minions, he came up with another theory called "Impersonation theory". This is the view that the appearances were not really Christ at all, but someone impersonating Him. This, the opponents say, is evident because in some cases they did not recognize Him at first (or at all). They fail to realizes that his disciples saw his glorified body( John20:17). Permit me to share three more theories. Spiritual resurrection: "This is the view that Christ’s resurrection was not a real physical resurrection". Proponents of this theory assert that Christ’s body remained in the grave and His real resurrection was spiritual in nature. It was only told this way to illustrate the truth of spiritual resurrection. Big lie! Have they forgotten that the grave became empty as it was recorded in John 20:1-31? Theft theory is popular among the Jews. They believe his disciples stole his body and claimed that He rose from the dead. This theory was propounded by the religious leaders of his day who paid soldiers to spread the news that his body was stolen, Matthew28:13. Lastly, some believed his tomb is unknown. How can that be? Didn't they read where the bible says that Joseph of Arimathea requested for Jesus' body to be buried in an unused tomb? Read John19:38-42.

The devil knows that if he can make you believe these lies, your faith in Christ is useless. There wouldn't have been a hope of resurrection for the dead in Christ and that could also mean that there wouldn't be hope of heaven for anyone.  Hallelujah!!! Christ arose. He arose  a victor from the domain of darkness because death could not hold him down, the power of resurrection and the glory was too much for the grave to handle. The earth practically vomited him: *"But God raised him from the dead, freeing him from the agony of death, because it was impossible for death to keep its hold on him*", Acts2:24. No other religious leader rose from the dead. They're dead, buried and rotten in the grave. *Christ is not a religious leader, he's a life giver. He carries the power of an indestructible life*. When he arose, he came out triumphant.  All of the powers of hell and Satan their master were disarmed: *"And having disarmed the powers and authorities, he made a public spectacle of them, triumphing over them by the cross*", Col 2:15 NIV. Whatever you're facing must bow to the risen Christ.  The power of resurrection is available, tap into it.


*CONFESSION*: I unleash the resurrection power of Christ on (...mention name of your challenge). I reject it and send it back to hell. It is not mine. I refuse to bow to the whims and caprices of the enemy. I no longer belong to the kingdom of darkness. The power of God is at work in my mortal body to quicken me and make me fit spirit, soul and body in Jesus name.









N:B You can get our free tracts for evangelism at Bible Wonderland Bookshop( opposite pedestrian bridge) at Ketu bus stop, Ikorodu road, Lagos.

info@projectsaveng.org

*RETURN TO THE MASTER*

Joel2:13 And rend your heart and not your garments.”
Now return to the Lord your God, For He is gracious and compassionate, Slow to anger, abounding in lovingkindness And relenting of evil.


During my final days in the university, I met a man who told me about his glorious past. He told me how he was used mightily by the Lord to do great exploits in the kingdom. One phrase that caught my attention during our discussion was "when I was on fire". He said that  repeatedly and knowing that his "firefull" days were in the past got me. In fact, that night, he was hanging out with his girlfriend and it was obvious that their relationship was unholy.


In my few years of walking with the Lord, I discovered that it is very easy to deviate and wander away from the Lord without obvious notice. Backsliding doesn't occur all at once, it is a gradual thing. It starts with little negligence and apathy towards spiritual things in the secret before it becomes an open or visible thing.

"Little sins" is another strategy of the enemy to draw men away from the master. *When the things we once abhorred and detested now becomes a norm, we need to return to the Lord. We need to find the path of consecration once again and walk in it*. The church in Ephesus also wandered off the path of consecration and Jesus said to them, *"Remember therefore from whence thou art fallen, and repent, and do the first works; or else I will come unto thee quickly, and will remove thy candlestick out of his place, except thou repent*", Rev 2:5 KJV.


Most times, the deception of the enemy is that he allow us to engage in physical activities in the church as a cover up for a weak fellowship with the master.  A preacher living in sin may pray and prophesy with signs and wonders following. It is possible to have a very weak prayer life and zero word study life and still be speaking in tongues. Never let activities replace your personal consecration to the Lord. There's a need for us to return to the master. The beginning of true revival starts with individual and corporate repentance. If we want to push back the evil in our world and establish God's kingdom in vital spheres of life, we cannot do it without walking in the footsteps of our master. The master of the church is saying, *“If you return to the Almighty, you will be restored;If you remove unrighteousness far from your tent*", Job22:23.


*PRAYER*: Father, I return to you. Cure me of my backsliding in Jesus name.

*RETURN TO THE MESSAGE*

Joel2:13 And rend your heart and not your garments.” Now return to the Lord your God, For He is gracious and compassionate, Slow to anger, abounding in lovingkindness And relenting of evil.

Years back, myself and some brethren attended a thanksgiving service with the state governor in attendance. The celebrant was a top functionary in his cabinet.  The priest who preached in that service left the message in the bible and decided to campaign for the governor's second term ambition from the pulpit. He abandoned the bible message to speak about the good works of the governor and what he had done. Maybe he expected the governor to donate a large sum to the building project of the church but when the governor gave his remark, he seem not to even be in tune with the man's ego massage. It was a shameful episode right in the eyes of every one who knew what ought to be. I wouldn't be wrong to say 'He preached to the gallery but the gallery failed him'. Pardon my grammar.


Men who should hear the message of repentance were pampered and rubbed on the head just because of pecuniary gains. God is calling us all to return to the message of the bible. Right in the bible, we saw Christ, the prophets and the apostles preach repentance. On the day of Pentecost, after the 120 received the baptism of the Holy Spirit, Peter preached saying: *"...Repent, and be baptized every one of you in the name of Jesus Christ for the remission of sins, and ye shall receive the gift of the Holy Ghost*",  Acts 2:38 KJV. Through that message 3,000 were added to the church. Don't tell me the message of repentance is outdated.

We need to stop pampering people with sweet words and philosophy of men when they need an encounter with the truth of God's word. If the apostles had preached the messages some preachers are preaching today, they may not have been persecuted. When the gospel message of the apostles filled Jerusalem, the religious council tried to stop them but Peter and other apostles said, *"We must obey God rather than human beings!*", Acts5:29. Paul preached Christ and him crucified till he was beheaded. He didn't tell us about his latest trip to America and how he built a big cathedral in Ephesus.  If we want to see true biblical transformation in the lives of men, we need to preach Christ. We owe God the duty to return to the true message of the bible- Christ.


*PRAYER*: Father, I receive the grace to return to the message of the bible in Jesus name.

*RETURN TO GOD'S MISSION*


Joel2:13 And rend your heart and not your garments.” Now return to the Lord your God, For He is gracious and compassionate, Slow to anger, abounding in lovingkindness And relenting of evil.





Have you ever asked what God's mission is? Sometimes, we misunderstand God and that's why it is hard to walk in his perfect will. One may think God's mission is to make us wealthy. Leaning on this wall, we are leaning on the wrong wall. God's ultimate goal for mankind is that he want men from all tribes to bring him worship: *"After this I beheld, and, lo, a great multitude, which no man could number, of all nations, and kindreds, and people, and tongues, stood before the throne, and before the Lamb, clothed with white robes, and palms in their hands;  And cried with a loud voice, saying, Salvation to our God which sitteth upon the throne, and unto the Lamb*", Rev 7:9-10 KJV.


Worship is the ultimate goal of God for mankind. What God want from all men is also what the devil craves from us- worship. To buttress this point, in the temptation of Jesus, Satan demanded worship from Jesus by asking him to bow down. It shows how important worship is to God and Satan. How will men from all tribes, tongues, race, nations and kindred bring their worship to a God they don't know? This can only be possible if we return to God's mission which is to spread the gospel to all men across different tribes and tongues. *It is only men that have come to God through Christ that will give him true worship*.



The early church were involved in God's mission of taking the gospel round and spreading it to those that are unreached in their days:  *"And daily in the temple, and in every house, they ceased not to teach and preach Jesus Christ*", Acts 5:42 KJV. Their commitment to preaching from house to and in the temple yielded greatly: *"And this continued by the space of two years; so that all they which dwelt in Asia heard the word of the Lord Jesus, both Jews and Greeks*",Acts 19:10 KJV. Wow! In two years of rigorous evangelism, they covered Asia. Our generation can do more if we plunge into the field of souls with our hearts and with our resources.

Over 70,000 people die daily without hearing about Jesus Christ. So sad! There are millions of people around the world that haven't heard about Jesus Christ and we're at ease in our air conditioned and paneled churches. We now have Evangelists who are known for how well they can lead "Fire fall" prayers but no souls in their account. The command is "Go Ye" not "Stay ye". *The believer that is not winning souls is weak and the church that is not involved in missions is dead*. We are saved to be involved in the salvation of others. Don't look on while souls are perishing. Be a Christian that is mission minded.




*PRAYER*: Father, I receive the grace to return to your mission of reaching the unreached in Jesus name.

*RETURN TO THE SPIRIT'S MOMENTUM*




Joel2:13 And rend your heart and not your garments.” Now return to the Lord your God, For He is gracious and compassionate, Slow to anger, abounding in lovingkindness And relenting of evil.





Momentum is a term in mechanics that refers to mass in motion. In lay man's understanding, it is synonymous with energy, drive or force. In the early Church, we saw a people that were driven by the power, zeal and energy of the Holy Spirit. After the Holy Spirit came upon them on the day of Pentecost, they began to operate in the supernatural. In Acts 5:15-16 we read, *“People brought the sick into the streets and laid them on beds and mats so that at least Peter’s shadow might fall on some of them as he passed by. Crowds gathered also from the towns around Jerusalem, bringing their sick and those tormented by evil spirits, and all of them were healed*”.


They( early believers) had a church where the gifts of the Spirit flowed without restrictions. They didn't fake the manifestations of the Spirit because they were vessels through whom the Spirit flowed without hindrance. The power of God was so thick amidst them to the extent that lying had immediate consequence: *"Whiles it remained, was it not thine own? and after it was sold, was it not in thine own power? why hast thou conceived this thing in thine heart? thou hast not lied unto men, but unto God.  And Ananias hearing these words fell down, and gave up the ghost: and great fear came on all them that heard these things*", Acts 5:4-5 KJV. In a church like this, who would dare God by living a double life?


*We need to return to the Spirit's momentum. Enough of powerless and sloppy Christianity that cannot prove the Lordship of Jesus Christ to people*. There's so much noise and display of physical energy on the altar without a corresponding life changing result. Who says the world wouldn't hear the gospel if it is backed by power? Speaking about Jesus, Luke 4:14 says, *"Jesus returned to Galilee in the power of the Spirit, and news about him spread through the whole countryside*". Men began to follow Jesus everywhere after he launched out with power of the Spirit. You can gather the Spirit's momentum only in the place of fellowship. As you stay in God's presence, He will fill you with himself afresh.






*PRAYER*: Father, fill me afresh in Jesus name.

DEVOTIONAL 0112 | Source start line: 12616

4/10/21, 6:25 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: ProjectSave Devotional
Saturday,
April 10th, 2021.


*TUNE IN*

Eph6:18 Pray in the Spirit at all times and on every occasion. Stay alert and be persistent in your prayers for all believers everywhere. NLT


Sometimes ago, while I driving to a building site to check the progress of work done by the carpenters, i was prompted by the Spirit to pray and I began to pray in tongues. It was as though God was preparing me for something. When I got to the building site, we decided to climb on the wood they prepared for decking. While climbing the ladder, I asked the bricklayer, "what if we see a snake on the decking"? The carpenter was silent as we climbed. When we got to the decking table, not up to 2 minutes, I heard someone banging the decking floor.


When we looked back, the carpenter was trying to kill a long green Mamba snake. It was an unusual place to find a snake because they had work on the building for days and one would expect that reptiles should have left. After hitting the snake, the carpenter said he was silent deliberately when I asked the bricklayer about the possibility of seeing a snake on the decking floor while climbing because my question was senseless but he agreed I made sense after the snake showed up. I paraphrased what he said.  It was after the whole episode I knew what the Spirit was preparing me for in the place of prayer while driving to the building site. If I had not prayed, something evil may have happened to any of us.


Being in the Spirit and tuning to his frequency is to our advantage at all times. God doesn't want things to catch us by surprise.  You must be sensitive at all times to know when the Spirit of God want you to take care of life's issues in the place of prayer ahead of time. The man that pray in tongues consistently shouldn't find it hard tuning to the frequency of the Spirit. Just like a transistor radio, if you want to get clear radio waves signal, you must learn to tune to the right frequency. It is possible to sense what is ahead spiritually and take care of whatever the enemy may be planning in the place of prayer. Just tune to the frequency of the Spirit always by praying in tongues.


*ACTION POINT*: Pray for a minimum of 30 minutes in tongues every day.

*CHILDLIKE FAITH*


Matthew 17:20 He replied, "Because you have so little faith. Truly I tell you, if you have faith as small as a mustard seed, you can say to this mountain, 'Move from here to there,' and it will move. Nothing will be impossible for you.


One of the teachers in my son's school was sick sometimes ago. They are very close because after school hours, he normally stay with her before going home. When he got to know that she was sick, he told her, "Come to our house, the power of God is in our room". What he was trying to tell her was that she'll receive her healing if she could come to our house. He was sure about what he said because his faith knew no impossibility. Even myself, I  haven't given it a thought to bring someone to our guest room to be healed but the faith of that young boy knew no impossibility. When I was a little boy, whenever we needed power restored, my younger sister and I would go on our knees to pray for power to be restored and we got answers.


If you want to know the faith level of children, tell them to pray. Some of their requests are so big. They make requests without restrictions in their minds. As an adult, if you're to pray the same prayer, you may be double minded about it because you have encountered so many obstacles in life and that has punctured your faith thereby making you to place certain limitations on God: *"But when you ask, you must believe and not doubt, because the one who doubts is like a wave of the sea, blown and tossed by the wind*", James1:6. The faith that works is the faith that knows no impossibility and doesn't place a limitation on God.


*'Childlike faith' as a term is not in the bible but it is a coinage that refers to the impossibility mindset that children have*. Your faith level will determine the kind of result your life will produce. So many desire to see God at work but they have hindered the move of God in their lives because of their double mindedness. You'll get to know this from their words. One moment, they're praying and asking God for great things but the next moment, they're repudiating their prayers through negative confessions. Until you start believing God for the impossible, possibilities will be far from you. In John 11:40,  *"Then Jesus said, "Did I not tell you that if you believe, you will see the glory of God?*". Faith is the key to living a life of glory.


*CONFESSION*: My faith is working, it is not weak and unhindered. My life will produce result and I'll walk in glory and power of God. Impossibilities aren't permitted around me and I'm on track for a life of excellence in Jesus name
