*DON'T TAMPER WITH THE WORD*

Mark 7:13 And so you cancel the word of God in order to hand down your own tradition. And this is only one example among many others.”

I was talking to a young man in a village sometime ago. He professed to be a christian but he told me that whenever they want to celebrate their local deity, everyone in their family must be present even if they are pastors. Himself told me he attended a bible school and I expected that he should have divorced himself from the tradition of men based on knowledge of scriptures. There are several human traditions that stand against what the scripture teaches.

The religious leaders in the time of Jesus were traditionalists. They placed the customs and traditions of their fathers above the word. Some believers are guilty of the same thing. When matters arise, they'll say things like, "let's leave the bible aside". The moment you hear that statement, someone is about to impose the tradition of men and tamper with the word of God. *Never leave the bible aside in the affairs of your life. The principles in the bible should be your guiding light on matters pertaining to life not the traditions of men*.


The tradition of men cuts across all spheres of life, ministry inclusive.  The moment you follow the traditions of men, your life will produce the result men produced. The word of God is incomparable with the traditions of men. God's word is supra culture. Psalms 119:89 says, *"For ever, O LORD, thy word is settled in heaven*". God's word is heavenly, stick to it.


*PRAYER*: I refuse to tamper with the word of God because of human tradition and I receive the grace to stick to God's word in Jesus name.

*INGREDIENTS OF FAITH* Pt.1

Matthew24:35 Heaven and earth will pass away, but my words will never pass away.NIV

*THE WORD*

Over the years, I've heard faith preached, taught and expounded from scriptures. Some persons take faith to mean mere absolute trust in God on an issue but without a basis. What some folks call 'faith' has failed them and they're discouraged. The reason for this is because they fail to realize that when we put our faith in God for  result, certain spiritual ingredients must be included in our faith for it to work. You can grind pepper in the market but until you cook it with the necessary ingredients, it won't be edible.


One vital ingredient of result producing faith is the word of God. Whenever you claim to have faith, it must rest on a particular verse(s) of scriptures that promise you what you desire. In Jeremiah 1:11-12,  *"The word of the Lord came to me: “What do you see, Jeremiah?” “I see the branch of an almond tree,” I replied.  The Lord said to me, “You have seen correctly, for I am watching to see that my word is fulfilled*”. Jeremiah 1:11-12 NIV. God's commitment is to his word not your wish. The bible says, *"Not at all! Let God be true, and every human being a liar...*", Romans3:4. God's integrity is tied to his word. He doesn't want men to call him a liar.

*So shall my word be that goeth forth out of my mouth: it shall not return unto me void, but it shall accomplish that which I please, and it shall prosper in the thing whereto I sent it*, Isaiah 55:11. Whenever we come to him in prayers and present his word to him, he is bound to fulfill it in our lives. Faith won't work until we add the ingredient of relevant scriptures that promise us what we desire from God and stand on it. *Any faith that is not resting on specific scriptures that promises our desire is just a mere wish. God doesn't respond to wishes but faith backed by his word*.
*ACTION POINT*: Search for relevant scriptures that promise you what you desire and place your faith in those scriptures.

*INGREDIENTS OF FAITH* Pt.2

I went with a young man whose mum told me his friend insinuated he may not return home because he told them he sensed he may not return home. Immediately I heard that, I knew I had to step up my faith game. Faith won't work until you confess God's word that you believe.


I told the devil that the blessedness of going out is in returning home so I refused evil imaginations, thoughts and revelations. At a time, my companion's condition grew worse and I heard a voice saying, "There's a battle in the heavenlies". Thank God the host of heaven won the battle over the onslaught of the enemy. However, word-based confession was in my mouth. *There's a difference between positive confession and word-based confession. The former exist in the mental realm and even unbelievers engage it but the later is spiritual and it involves speaking the word of God as found in the bible and enforcing it on life's ugly situations till a change occurs*.

Even motivational speakers encourage people to speak positively. While it is good to do so, it doesn't carry much power as when you confess God's word. God may not be able to do beyond what you say about your life: *"So tell them, ‘As surely as I live, declares the Lord , I WILL DO TO YOU THE VERY THING I HEARD YOU SAY:*", Numbers14:28. What are you saying?

*CONFESSION*: I am the head and not beneath. I'm not struggling to get by, I'm not a hustler, I'm blessed. My going out and coming in is blessed. The desire of my enemy shall not come to pass over my life in Jesus name. I am triumphant.

*INGREDIENTS OF FAITH* Pt.3




Mark 9:24 Immediately the boy's father exclaimed, "I do believe; help me overcome my unbelief!"

*FAITH SUPPORT SYSTEM*


After we tested positive to corona virus, it was as if our world was crashing. I tested positive thrice. Oct 7th,2020 was a sad day back in Ghana because we were meant to travel the next day. When our result was sent, the atmosphere changed and it became hot. The phone conversation between us and my companion's mother from Nigeria was heated. If she had wings, she'll fly down to pick her son and carry him to Nigeria. No thanks to Nigeria High Commission in Ghana who ignored us after they promised to get back to us. At a time, my faith would go up and at other times, it came down. I cried twice on one occasion. The experience wasn't a palatable one because the life of another person was involved. His state of illness gave me a lot of concern.

However, I learnt something. The man of God we stayed with consciously decided to control the atmosphere in and around his house by preaching&teaching us faith. After we had a heated conversation with my companion's mother, he told us openly that he didn't like what we did because that conversation changed the atmosphere in his house so he led us in worship. Calm was restored. After my personal morning prayers which often lasted for about 4 hours or more sometimes, he'll start another round of faith boosting teachings and we also shared together from scriptures. This could last another 2 hours. He consciously killed every aura of doubt and fear around us and monitored us as if we're just learning to live a life of faith. Now, that's what I call "faith support system".



If your faith would work, you need faith support system. It could be someone/ something who will constantly help refocus your attention on the word and not the circumstance. Some persons love to be pitied. They surround themselves with people who will pity them while some focus on bad news and stay in environments that'll prepare their hearts for the worst. I liken faith to a balloon that can deflate. If you stay around the wrong people and news for a long time, it will deflate. The moment your faith begins to shake, find the right person who can help you to keep it in focus and stay in the right environment. Run from faith killers. No matter how spiritual we are, when devil begins to throw fiery darts of hell at us and it seems our world is crashing,  we'll need faith support system(s).



*ACTION POINT*: Consciously stay away from anyone/thing that runs your faith down. Stay with people who can boost your faith.

*INGREDIENTS OF FAITH* Pt.4

Romans4:19-20 And being not weak in faith, he considered not his own body now dead, when he was about an hundred years old, neither yet the deadness of Sara's womb:  He staggered not at the promise of God through unbelief; but was strong in faith, giving glory to God; KJV



*CHANGE YOUR FOCUS AND GIVE THANKS*


At the onset of symptoms after I tested positive, I read so many online articles on COVID-19. It was as though all I saw in the news was COVID. I was following the reports on the daily number of infections, discharge and deaths in Ghana. That was the period Donald Trump, Cristiano Ronaldo and other public figures had COVID. I read almost all scholarly articles about COVID and I asked plenty questions from the medics. After my bible reading, COVID information was the next and I did that more than I read my bible. How wrong! I also noticed that the many information I bombarded my mind with made me cold and withdrawn. I stopped writing daily devotionals and I avoided relating with people because the devil made me feel I have a problem that needed 100% attention.

I assigned someone to post old devotionals on our social media platforms. One day, in our morning devotion, the man of God we stayed with said that one of the ways to fight the fight of faith and win is never to give the devil too much attention by focusing on our problems. He further said that if there was anything we were doing that was blessing lives(e.g writing of daily devotional), we shouldn't stop. I knew  God was talking to me through him. Immediately, I told the brother who was posting old devotionals on our social media platforms that I'll resume duty. When he heard that, he was happy. From the day I shifted my attention from feeding my mind with daily COVID-19 update, my faith grew stronger.


*Faith becomes weak when you focus on your problems than on the word*. Some persons went into hiding from friends, families and the brethren because of their challenges. It could be because of barrenness, joblessness or singleness. They listen to news and  people who speak more about their problems. The worst is when they stop being a blessing to others because they feel they need to focus on their problems. That's the devil at work. In the opening text, we were told that Abraham didn't consider his aged body and deadness of Sarah's womb but remained strong in the faith. If he had given attention to what the gynaecologists said about Sarah, his faith would be weak. *Stop reading about or listening to whatever feed your fears and weaken your faith*. Thank God father Abraham didn't give the devil attention rather he gave thanks. *Don't allow the devil draw your attention away from God's promises. Maintain your word-based confessions, live a life of thanksgiving and go about your daily duties as if nothing is happening*.


*CONFESSION*: God's word is at work in me. It won't fail. I am triumphant in Jesus name.

*INGREDIENTS OF FAITH* Pt.5

2Cor10:4-5 (For the weapons of our warfare are not carnal, but mighty through God to the pulling down of strong holds;)  Casting down imaginations, and every high thing that exalteth itself against the knowledge of God, and bringing into captivity every thought to the obedience of Christ; KJV

The devil told me I'll go to jail. He specifically told me I'll spend two years in jail on arrival in Nigeria. Why? The life of a young man hung on my neck. This day will the Lord deliver thee into mine hand; and I will smite thee, and take thine head from thee; and I will give the carcases of the host of the Philistines this day unto the fowls of the air, and to the wild beasts of the earth; that all the earth may know that there is a God in Israel.  And all this assembly shall know that the Lord saveth not with sword and spear: for the battle is the Lord's , and he will give you into our hands*",1 Samuel 17:45-47 KJV.

What reply did you give to the devil the last time he spoke to you?When you notice your imagination is beginning to  run wild over that challenge, talk back. Don't sink into your chair or retire to bed crying, talk back. Walk around your house or room replying the devil and rebuking him. Be bold to tell him he's a liar. He doesn't have a track record of fulfilled promises. He may tell you that you'll die poor, wretched, beggarly without a child. Don't let those imaginations and words of his go like that, talk back. Goliath won't stop talking, but the only way to silence him is to talk back.


*ACTION POINT*: Talk back

*INGREDIENTS OF FAITH* Pt.6

Hebrews6:12 That ye be not slothful, but followers of them who through faith and patience inherit the promises. KJV



*PATIENT WAITING*




While we were waiting for a negative covid test result, God taught us patience. After praying and ensuring our faith was intact, we got confirmation that all would be well through one of my mentors and my companion's mentor in Nigeria. Despite all the confirmations, we tested positive still. At a time, my companion felt bad and confused but I knew that it takes patience to walk with God. *The absence of result doesn't mean God has reneged on his promises.* My result came out negative first.  I left my companion in Ghana and i returned to Nigeria on October 29th, 2021. Though it was a risky decision but we needed to take that decision to cut cost. Finally, he joined me in Nigeria on November 11th after testing negative. The timeline between when we prayed and got confirmations that all would be well was a bit far from the time of manifestation.


*God is not a magician*. We always want things done instantly. Our era has witnessed fast food, speed train, supersonic airplanes and fast internet speed. Humans want quick result. Impatience seems to be a common gene we all possess.  *We tend to bring God into the sense realm where we want things done quickly but we fail to realize that God doesn't boycott process.* God can make a waiting mother get pregnant in one day, mature the foetus and make her deliver her baby immediately. His power is unlimited yet God wouldn't do such because there's a divinely appointed time for gestation. No amount of prayer and fasting can make a woman give birth in less than nine months except a she want a pre term. I listened to the testimony of a woman who waited on God for the fruit of the womb. She told us how she prayed for several years. On a certain day, God told her he has heard her prayers. After that confirmation from God, she waited for several years before giving birth to her first child.


*The faith that wins is a patient faith*. Abraham waited for 25 solid years before obtaining the promise. Noah had faith that rain would fall but he waited patiently for 120 years. Everyone must have called him a fool. The dream of Joseph didn't come to pass until after 13 years of patient waiting. These men made the faith's hall of fame as faith heroes. You'll make a mess of your faith and prayers if you don't know how to wait patiently. Beware of those who make faith look like God's instant miracle tool. There's no doubt that in some cases, certain miracles happen instantly and that's God's prerogative not ours. *Lack of evidence or manifestation doesn't necessarily mean the absence of faith.* The man under God's processing may look faithless when result hasn't come. Wait for your time, be patient with God, he is working out something behind the scene.


*PRAYER*: I receive the grace to wait patiently in Jesus name.

4/19/21, 5:30 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: PROJECTSAVE DEVOTIONAL

*INGREDIENTS OF FAITH* Pt.7

James2:14 What good is it, dear brothers and sisters, if you say you have faith but don’t show it by your actions? Can that kind of faith save anyone? NLT

*TAKE THE RIGHT ACTION(S) WHILE WAITING*

When my result came out negative and that of my companion remained positive, it came with mixed feelings. I wasn't too happy about his result even though I was thankful for mine. I wished it happened the other way so that he could return to Nigeria while I wait in Ghana for a negative result. He felt bad upon seeing his result because we both agreed in prayers before going for the test that came out negative for me. When we were talking, I pointed to him a few things I felt he didn't do well while waiting for a change. He had a lackadaisical attitude towards taking the natural spices and prescriptions recommended for boosting our immune system. I felt if he had taken it as i did, his recovery may be quicker because younger people are known to have quick recoveries because of their immune system. All through my stay, I took those things as a mini diet sort of but he felt since we've prayed, there wasn't any need for those things. He realized he didn't act well in that regard and he changed for the better. Kindly note that the source of our healing was God not the natural spices and prescriptions. However, he may choose to work through any means, that's purely his prerogative.

Jesus didn't need John's baptism to validate his ministry because he came directly from heaven as God in human flesh yet Jesus still submitted to John's baptism so that all righteousness might be fulfilled. *There are actions you may need to take that may be crucial to your faith as long as they're not anti scriptures*. There's a gross misunderstanding in the body of Christ that faith in God automatically translate to not taking appropriate actions. For example, I've heard about several people who lost their lives because they felt taking medication was anti faith. They just loathe anything that makes their faith look as though they're 'assisting' God. That wrong misrepresentation of faith has been for long and has led many into the grave untimely. Let's look at this scripture: *"Are any of you sick? You should call for the elders of the church to come and pray over you, anointing you with oil in the name of the Lord. Such a prayer offered in faith will heal the sick, and the Lord will make you well. And if you have committed any sins, you will be forgiven*",James 5:14-15 NLT. The word "anointing" in that text is  "Aleipho" in the Greek. It implies medicinal oil. Apostle James recommended medicine for the sick but he didn't stop there. He said that what will heal the sick eventually is the prayer of faith not the medicinal oil. However, the use of medicine is the appropriate action to be taken in instances where it is inevitable or required.

In our walk of faith, there's a God-ward side and Man-ward side to making our faith work. *Don't try to act on a level of faith you've not built or attained. The testimony of faith you heard from that brother or sister is only reflective of his/ her level of faith personally. It is not a template for all.*  Faith is personal. You can build your faith to a point where taking certain actions like using of medications may be unnecessary but pending when you'll get there, take appropriate actions. It doesn't nullify your faith. *God deals with us at our level of faith not the faith of another person because it has always been 'Be it nto you according to YOUR FAITH and so shall it be*. Don't sit at home trusting God for a job without sending out applications and connecting with people who could be of assistance. Imagine waiting couples that aren't getting intimate regularly, how will they achieve conception? *God can do everything because he's omnipotent but he has chosen not to do all things because he didn't create a robot who can't take initiative*.  There are so many answers to prayers and faith confessions still hanging because the owner hasn't taken appropriate action.  Do a review of your life and look out for areas where you've not taken the right action. You may be one right action away from your desired miracle.

*PRAYER*: Father, open my eyes to the appropriate actions I need to take for my faith to produce the desired result in Jesus name.

DEVOTIONAL 0122 | Source start line: 13254

4/20/21, 6:34 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: ProjectSave Devotional
Tuesday
April 20th,  2021.

*ENFORCING PROPHECIES* Pt.1

1 Tim1:18 This charge I commit unto thee, son Timothy, according to the prophecies which went before on thee, that thou by them mightest war a good warfare; KJV

*INTENSE PRAYER*


We need to understand the nature of certain predictive prophecies so that we can be properly guided. In the book of Genesis15:13, God told Abram that his descendants will spend 400 years in captivity. I want to believe this is specific enough. However, in Ex12:40-41, it was stated that their sojourn in Egypt took 430 years. Why this disparity? Has God turned a liar? Of course not. I know there are a lot of theological opinions as regards this but I choose to think differently about it.

*Not all prophecies would be fulfilled automatically. Some are conditioned on certain things that God expect man to do*. The prophetic prediction that declared 400 years was validly from God but it was conditioned on their willingness to pray their nation out of slavery. Failure to raise a cry for help on time probably prolonged their stay in Egypt for another extra 30 years.  *"And it came to pass in process of time, that the king of Egypt died: and the children of Israel sighed by reason of the bondage, and they cried, and their cry came up unto God by reason of the bondage*", Ex2:23 KJV. God knew they were suffering but he couldn't do anything about it until they cried to him. If they had expressed satisfaction with slavery, God wouldn't be able to help them despite the prophecy of freedom that has been prophesied. *Sometimes, God is handicapped because man failed to pray out his plans and purposes*.

There are certain prophecies that require your prayer input before they come to pass. You may have received a word from God( with several confirmations) that is yet to be fulfilled. That doesn't mean God is not powerful enough to make good his word but you may be failing to play your part in the place of prayer in making that prophecy come to pass. *We can lay hold of unseen realities by faith in the place of prayer and birth the fulfilment of prophecy*. Some of us think the moment God speaks, all we need do is to go to bed and sleep whereas in most cases, it is a call to intense prayer warfare. There are oppositions against the fulfilment of prophecies, you'll only conquer them and enter into the fulfilment of those prophecies if you pray intensely.

*PRAYER*: Father, I receive the grace to pray out your will for my life in Jesus name.

DEVOTIONAL 0123 | Source start line: 13306
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
4/21/21, 7:18 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: PROJECTSAVE DEVOTIONAL
wednesday,
April 21st, 2021.

*ENFORCING PROPHECIES* Pt.2

1 Tim1:18 This charge I commit unto thee, son Timothy, according to the prophecies which went before on thee, that thou by them mightest war a good warfare; KJV


*THE FORCE OF HARDWORK*

Two friends visited the fortuneteller to check their star. One was told that he was destined to be poor and miserable in life. The second man was told he'll be wealthy and famous. When they left the fortuneteller, the first man was bitterly sad while the second man went home rejoicing. In fact, when he got home, he threw a party and he began to celebrate his future fortunes with friends and families. He married more wives telling them how wealthy he'll become in the future. He stopped working thinking wealth will fall from heaven. The first man decided to relocate to another town. When he got there, he bought cutlasses and hoes for farming. He decided that he'll work on the farm until his fortune changes. Years later, the man that was told would become rich became poor and beggarly while the first man had become a rich farmer in the next town.  He ended up feeding and helping his friend who was told would become rich. That's the power of hard work. Proverbs 14:23 says, *"All hard work brings a profit, but mere talk leads only to poverty*", NIV.


Some have great prophecies hanging over them. Some brethren are so loud about what God told them but we're not seeing anything around them that points to the fulfillment of what God has said. Some even have special names and aliases that depicts the prophecy that went ahead of them. You may hear names like " covenant child" and so on. Failure to work hard may put you on the same spot like a man who never had a glorious future. The prophecies that went ahead of Jesus Christ were numerous. If Christ were to be like some of us, he wouldn't lift a finger and he'll just say 'Que' Sera sera', whatever will be will be.  In John 5:17, Jesus said, *"...My Father is always working, and so am I*". He applied himself to the principle of hardwork despite the numerous prophecies that went ahead of him. If he hadn't worked as expected of him, he may not fulfill those Messianic prophecies. Apostle Paul was the greatest among all the apostles in the new testament judging from his ministry coverage, and depth of revelations in his epistolary writings. He was a scholar and devoted apostle yet he didn't achieve his prophetic mandate without applying the force of hardwork. In 1Corinthians 15:10, he said, *"But by the grace of God I am what I am, and his grace to me was not without effect. No, I worked harder than all of them--yet not I, but the grace of God that was with me*".  He took advantage of grace to work hard not to sleep all night and play all day.


*Que sera sera is the language of lazy and visionless folks. You have a part to play in what will be. You don't surrender your glorious future to the forces on earth driving the lives and destinies of men off track.*  Some prophecies don't just happen, they're worked out diligently. If God has told you that you'll be a global preacher, labour in the place of prayer and study. Improve daily on your preaching skill, take courses and trainings that will enhance your prophetic future. Don't be idle. The idea that God gives glory to the lazy is anti bible. That's a common belief in my part of the world yet I haven't seen a lazy man who amounted to anything meaningful. You need to build capacity to be able to handle your prophetic destiny. Even if the prophecy comes to pass automatically and its fulfillment meets you unprepared, you'll make a mess of the whole thing. *Hard work doesn't destroy, it only refines and helps us to build capacity required to handle greatness.*


*PRAYER*: Father, I receive the grace to work hard in Jesus name.

*ENFORCING PROPHECIES* Pt.3




1 Tim1:18 This charge I commit unto thee, son Timothy, according to the prophecies which went before on thee, that thou by them mightest war a good warfare; KJV


*THE POWER OF RELATIONSHIP*


David was anointed to take over from Saul. His prophetic destiny was to replace Saul and reign in his stead. However, when you look at the journey of David to the throne, Jonathan played the crucial role of preserving his life from Saul at one time or the other, 1 Sam 20. What about Joseph the dreamer? The butler was his link to the palace of Pharaoh. Though he forgot him for sometime but the relationship paid off at a later time. No matter how fervent Joseph prayed about his prophetic destiny, he couldn't reach his goal without the butler.


*God can do all things without man but he has chosen not to do certain things without men*. For every prophecy that has gone ahead of you, there are God-ordained men that are meant to act as catalyst and midwives before you can birth the promise of God. Most times, people in this category are people around us that we have met in time past or may be around us presently. Knowing how to relate with them properly is the key that will unlock the doors of fulfillment of prophecy.



*Stop taking people for granted*. There's always an interplay of human and divine partnership in God's program for our lives. You may be gifted like Joseph with the prophecy of rulership hanging over your head but you'll need someone who knows the king to recommend you for the throne. You never can tell who will announce your expertise where it is needed. Treasure relationships and never look down on people. I always thank those who give a token to support our ministry as though they give millions. Sometimes, they wonder why? Over the years I've seen those who started partnering with us by giving little token and had become big givers today. If I had discarded them in their days of little giving and didn't treasure our relationship, I'll have lost the privilege of sharing in their breakthrough. *Keep that relationship intact, it may be the last ladder you need to climb before entering into the fullness of God's plan for your life*.


*PRAYER*: Father, I thank you for sending your word to me. I receive grace to treasure and keep relationships in Jesus name. Amen.

*BORN ONCE DIE TWICE, BORN TWICE DIE ONCE*.

Hebrews9:27 And as it is appointed unto men once to die, but after this the judgment: KJV

The issue of eternity is on our hearts constantly or often. Almost everyone is concerned about the life after this life we're living on earth. That's why some persons pray forgiveness and sacrifice for the dead but forgetting that once a man crosses into eternity, that's the end. No amount of prayer, offering or sacrifice can change the fate of a man that is dead. *Repentance and forgiveness only exist on this side of eternity before crossing to the other side*. Once you breathe your last, you'll be faced with the consequences of your actions and inactions here on earth.


In life, there's a time to born and a time to die, Eccl3:2. Every man is destined to experience physical birth and death once. However, it doesn't end there. There's another type of  birth and death that is entirely spiritual. You have a choice in the second birth. Jesus said, *"Jesus answered and said unto him, Verily, verily, I say unto thee, EXCEPT A MAN BE BORN AGAIN, HE CANNOT SEE THE KINGDOM OF GOD*", John 3:3 KJV. Being born again is the second birth that will make you and I escape the second death( in the lake of fire) that is reserved for sinners and the ungodly: *"AND DEATH AND HELL WERE CAST INTO THE LAKE OF FIRE. THIS IS THE SECOND DEATH.  And whosoever was not found written in the book of life was cast into the lake of fire*", Rev 20:14-15 KJV.

This rebirth is the guarantee that you will enter heaven and be with God forever. What does it mean to be born again? It simply means to accept Jesus Christ as your Lord and personal saviour and to live for him all the days of your life. Now that you've been born physically, you need to be born again(spiritually) so that you can avoid that horrible second death which is the wrath of God on sinners&the ungodly. I escaped this coming wrath( second death) years back when I gave my life to Jesus Christ and you can also. The choice is yours. Believing that Jesus is able to save you and confessing his Lordship is all it takes to be born again. *If you're born once, you will die twice but if you're born twice, you'll die once*. If you don't want to die twice, kindly make this confession:

*Jesus, come into my heart. I know I'm a sinner. I've been born once but I want rebirth so that I won't die twice. Forgive me of my sins and wash me with your precious blood. I confess you as my Lord and personal saviour. Write my name in the book of life and accept me in Jesus name. Amen*




N:B Now that you're saved, you need to attend a bible believing church where you can be discipled.


info@projectsaveng.org

*THE SORRY STATE OF MAN WITHOUT CHRIST*

Sin separated man from God, we inherited spiritual death( eternal separation from God) and we fell short of God's glory. This is the sorry state of man without Jesus.

For it is with your heart that you believe and are justified, and it is with your mouth that you profess your faith and are saved*", Romans 10:9-10 NIV. To exit this sorry state, make the confession below:


*Jesus, come into my heart. I know I'm a sinner. I've been born once but I want rebirth so that I won't die twice. Forgive me of my sins and wash me with your precious blood. I confess you as my Lord and personal saviour. Write my name in the book of life and accept me in Jesus name. Amen*

*WHY YOU'RE NOT LED BY THE SPIRIT* Pt.1

Matthew15:8 ‘These people honor me with their lips, but their hearts are far from me. NLT

*PHYSICALLY PRESENT BUT FAR FROM GOD*

I have been concerned about why some believers don't hear from God. Some prefer to consult prophets and preachers like diviners. We live in an era where singles take pictures of suitors to prophets to check whom they should marry. Some put up fleeces to decipher God's will in matters that pertains to their progress. I still wonder if a loving father would prefer to speak to his children through a second or third party or through methods that seems like throwing dice. It baffles me. Can God be so distant from his children? Speaking about the Holy Spirit in John 16:13,  Jesus said, *"When the Spirit of truth comes, he will guide you into all truth. He will not speak on his own but will tell you what he has heard. He will tell you about the future*".



Hearing from the Holy Spirit is not an exclusive right of some, he was sent for us all. Jesus made it clear He will speak to us directly and guide us. Why is this not a reality in the lives of many? The first reason some folks don't hear from God is because they're far from Him. You can only hear from someone you're close to in fellowship. *When fellowship with God is weak, hearing from him will be a rarity*. It is in the place of personal fellowship our hearts align with God. *The secrets of God are revealed in the secret place only to those that are present in the secret place*. Some are present in all church meetings and programs( though good) just to please their pastor or parents but their hearts aren't in alignment with God because they're far from God in the place of fellowship.

Another problem associated with weak fellowship is inability to recognize his voice or leading. My dad and his brothers have similar voices. Some persons may find it hard to distinguish who is speaking but for me, I don't have problem with that. I have lived with him for decades and related with his siblings to know who is who. The first line of James4:8 says, *"Draw nigh to God, and he will draw nigh to you*". When you come close to God in fellowship, you'll recognize his voice. Don't be a stranger to your father.

*PRAYER*: Father, I receive the grace to draw closer to you in the place of fellowship in Jesus name.

DEVOTIONAL 0129 | Source start line: 13669

4/27/21, 6:01 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: ProjectSave Devotional

April 27th,  2021.

**WHY YOU'RE NOT LED BY THE SPIRIT* Pt.2

Psalms25:14 The secret of the LORD is with those who fear Him, And He will show them His covenant. NKJV

*THE ABSENCE OF THE FEAR OF THE LORD*

Several years ago,  I listened to a preacher who said that we are not to fear God but to love him. He preached as though he had a special revelation on the subject. However, I realized that he misunderstood the biblical meaning of the phrase "fear of the Lord". He thought it meant to be afraid of God.  They're not the same. The former is constructive while the latter is destructive. The contextual meaning of the phrase "Fear of the Lord" in several portions of scriptures refers to having reverence for the Lord. Apostle Paul, in 2 Corinthians 7:1 said, *"Having therefore these promises, dearly beloved, let us cleanse ourselves from all filthiness of the flesh and spirit, perfecting holiness in the fear of God*". The fear of the Lord is connected to purity of heart.

Biblically, the fear of the Lord is to have reverential fear for him. To honour and esteem him. It is not a destructive kind of fear. Having cleared the air on that, the Lord has a soft spot for those that fear him. He doesn't hide secrets from such people. In Genesis18:11, speaking about Abraham, God said, *"And the LORD said, Shall I hide from Abraham that thing which I do;*". Abraham was a man that feared and honoured the Lord. It was impossible for God to hide secrets from him.


*Where the fear of God is constant in the life of a believer, divine leading and guidance is assured*. Another reason some believers don't hear clearly from God is because the fear of God is absent. The fear of God is the guarantee of a ceaseless flow of divine leading by the Spirit of God. Joseph had insight about his future and into deep mysteries because he feared God. Genesis 42:18 confirms this: *"Then Joseph said to them the third day, “Do this and live, for I fear God:*". It may sound unpopular and old-fashioned but having  reverential fear for the Lord makes it easy for the Lord to reveal deep secrets to you.

*PRAYER*: Father, I receive the grace to walk in fear of you in Jesus name.

*WHY YOU'RE NOT LED BY THE SPIRIT* Pt.3


Proverbs3:5-5 Trust in the Lord with all your heart; do not depend on your own understanding. Seek his will in all you do, and he will show you which path to take. NLT

*FULL OF SELF*

I have interacted with several people(singles inclusive) who believe God doesn't lead people in matters that pertains to marriage and other areas of life. Most people with this mentality believe that instead of God to lead, they're to find what they want and present it to God for ratification. They don't trust God's judgement to give them what he desires, they want to impose their will on God. Unfortunately, those in this category have scriptural backing( in a twisted form) for their assertions. They're full of themselves and their ways and do not have a submissive heart to God's will. They prefer to impose their will on God than to be led by him.


*The man that is full of his own ways will never be led by God*. The opening text tells us to acknowledge God in ALL( no exception) our ways,then he will direct our path. That's a vital criteria for receiving divine direction. One reason believers allow self in decision making is because they don't have faith in God's leading. The devil deceives them to believe God's will may not be the best. They always fail to see God as a loving father who will not give them poison. Speaking about the loving nature of God, Jesus said, *"You parents—if your children ask for a loaf of bread, do you give them a stone instead?  Or if they ask for a fish, do you give them a snake? Of course not!  So if you sinful people know how to give good gifts to your children, how much more will your heavenly Father give good gifts to those who ask him*", Matthew 7:9-11 NLT.

*God is a loving father who will not give you what will damage your life. No he won't*. In Jeremiah 29:11, he said, *"For I know the plans I have for you,” says the Lord . “They are plans for good and not for disaster, to give you a future and a hope*", Jeremiah 29:11 NLT. Whatever makes you think God's best is inferior is from the evil one. To be led by God, you must play the fool. Act as though you know nothing and have no other person to lead except him. If truly you want to hear from God, empty yourself of your own ways and acknowledge him in all you, then he'll direct your path.

*PRAYER*: Father, I surrender to you.

*WHY YOU'RE NOT LED BY THE SPIRIT* Pt.4


Ezekiel14:4 Therefore speak unto them, and say unto them, Thus saith the Lord GOD; Every man of the house of Israel that setteth up his idols in his heart, and putteth the stumblingblock of his iniquity before his face, and cometh to the prophet; I the LORD will answer him that cometh according to the multitude of his idols;


*THE SHRINE IN YOUR HEART*


I was discussing marriage with a woman who has grown up daughters eligible for marriage. I spoke to her about allowing God's will to be done in matters of marriage. She told me that whatever God will do, he should only lead her children to people of same tribe. In her heart, she believe that you can't find genuine believers from other tribes except hers. Her stance is so rigid and she believe God won't go against her will. I have met some believers with this incorrect mentality.






*The moment you get to the point where you hold on to an opinion or stance that God cannot change, you already have an idol in your heart.* Some folks already have an unbiblical preconceived idea on how God can lead and how he cannot. A young minister about starting ministry may have heard that you have to start ministry in certain cities before you breakthrough in ministry. Hence, when he prays, he'll hear himself and pack his loads to a city he's not sent.  God may need him in suburb or rural area but when he thinks about the suffering and what he heard from some persons about how success in ministry is only achievable in fixed locations, he'll lead himself.


When you have an idol in your heart, you'll always be led according to the idol in your heart. *The man that want to walk in God's will must demolish the shrines in his heart*. What are the idols you've set up in your heart against God's will? Sometimes, we suffer needlessly because we want things done our own way and when the suffering comes, we'll run to God thinking he can perform a magic. *It is only what God ordains he will maintain. It is only what he commission he makes provisions for*. Demolish the shrines and break all the idols in your heart today.



*PRAYER*: Father, I surrender all to you. I break the idols and demolish the shrines I've set up in my heart in Jesus name.

*WHY YOU'RE NOT LED BY THE SPIRIT* Pt.5


Matthew15:8 ‘These people honor me with their lips, but their hearts are far from me. NLT


*ATTACHMENT TO MEN'S OPINION*


Another reason some believers are not led by the Spirit of God is because they are fond of seeking men's opinion instead of asking God for direction. Even when they enquire from God, they have "trusted" friends whose counsel they hold in high esteem than the counsel of God. We can liken those in this category to the young prophet who heard God clearly but surrendered divine counsel to the misleading opinion of the old prophet. *You need to consciously dissociate from old prophets who are not in tune with God*.


Some persons, out of respect coupled with the fear of men have chosen to follow the counsel of their mentors or spiritual fathers even when it is clearly opposed to what God is saying or has said. When you esteem men's counsel more, the Holy Spirit won't lead you. *God always look at the sincerity of heart before speaking to us*. He leads those that are willing to follow. Some mentors/ spiritual fathers are possessive and domineering. They manipulate their mentees. If you find yourself under or around a man who doesn't believe God's counsel is superior to his opinion, please dissociate with wisdom.

God may use your mentors to confirm whatever he tells you if they're in tune with the Spirit but they're not to take his place. Trust him to lead you aright. Do not surrender to popular opinion of men if you want to enjoy divine direction.

*PRAYER*: Father, I receive the grace to trust you totally for direction in Jesus name.

5/1/21, 1:21 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: ProjectSave Devotional

*HOW TO LIVE IN PLENTY DURING SCARCITY* Pt.1


Genesis 26:1-2 A severe famine now struck the land, as had happened before in Abraham’s time. So Isaac moved to Gerar, where Abimelech, king of the Philistines, lived. The Lord appeared to Isaac and said, “Do not go down to Egypt, but do as I tell you. NLT

*SEEK RELEVANT INSTRUCTION FOR THE HOUR*

I was discussing with a man in his fifties sometimes ago. He shared with me how he spent 12 solid years in U.S.and Canada combined. In all the 12 years spent, they were years of misery, sorrow, confusion and distress. He traveled with the mentality of seeking the proverbial green pastures but it all turned out awry for him. After so many years of fruitlessness, he returned to Nigeria. Some persons thought he was under the influence of some diabolical powers but it was based on divine arrangement. On getting to Nigeria, good things began to happen to him in succession and he's fulfilled. The very country where people are running from is now his Canaan.


The world is not new to economic recession, scarcity and cash crunch. In the time of Isaac, there was scarcity and people were migrating to Egypt. It is plausible that there was surplus in Egypt and they had the proverbial greener pasture. Probably, he contemplated joining the bandwagon of migrants but God spoke to him expressly not to go. He obeyed that instruction which may have sounded foolish then considering the harsh economic realities of that day. What was the result? The bible says, *"When Isaac planted his crops that year, he harvested a hundred times more grain than he planted, for the Lord blessed him. HE BECAME A VERY RICH MAN, AND HIS WEALTH CONTINUED TO GROW*", Gen 26:12‭-‬13 NLT. The very land people were running from became the land where he enjoyed super abundance.

The first key to living in plenty during scarcity is to find the relevant instruction for the hour. You need to enquire from God whether to stay or to go. For some, going could be a displacement from God's will while for others, it may be a movement into destiny. However, you cannot tell what it is for you exactly until you're divinely instructed. *It is only a life lived by divine instruction that will end in distinction*. The natural instinct of man would want to exit any area where things seems not to be working to places where it is perceived things are better. However, whatever God says is very crucial to avoid regret. If you will live in plenty during scarcity, find relevant instruction for the hour.


*PRAYER*: Father, should I go or stay where I am? I'm open to your leading, Lord speak to me in Jesus name.

DEVOTIONAL 0134 | Source start line: 14160
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
5/2/21, 5:42 PM - Lanre Oyeleke: ProjectSave Devotional
Sunday,
May 2nd, 2021.


*HOW TO LIVE IN PLENTY DURING SCARCITY* Pt.2

1 King 17:3-4 “Go to the east and hide by Kerith Brook, near where it enters the Jordan River. Drink from the brook and eat what the ravens bring you, for I have commanded them to bring you food.” NLT



*THE MYSTERY OF BEING IN THE RIGHT PLACE*


The nation of Israel experienced famine and drought in the days of Elijah after he decreed that rain or dew won't fall, 1Kings17:1. When drought happens, the resultant effect is famine. Vegetations will cease to grow or may become stunted. Farmers groan when rain do not fall in season. Obviously, the nation will experience food crisis and shortage. God didn't want his servant to suffer so he instructed Elijah to go to brook Cherith. Elijah obeyed God and relocated to brook Cherith where he drank from the river and was fed by ravens: *"So Elijah did as the Lord told him and camped beside Kerith Brook, east of the Jordan. The ravens brought him bread and meat each morning and evening, and he drank from the brook*", 1 Kings 17:5-6 NLT. That's supernatural provision in a strange place because a raven is a bird not a human. If that happens in today's world, some would doubt it.

Further, It was observed that Elijah moved from brook Cherith to Zarephath after the river dried. God instructed him to relocate to Zarephath: *"Go and live in the village of Zarephath, near the city of Sidon. I have instructed a widow there to feed you*", 1 Kings 17:9 NLT. Elijah ate from this widow's table till the famine ended. We can see clearly how God instructed him to move from one place to the other on two occasions. *In times of famine, relocation is not bad if led by the Spirit of God*. Similar to what was shared previously about Isaac, Elijah received a clear instruction on where to go for supernatural provision in time of famine. *The same God that instructed Isaac to stay back and not go to Egypt is the same God that instructed Elijah to relocate to where he'll get abundance of supply during famine. What matters is hearing clearly from God on where to be per time*.


More often than not, your location determines your allocation. *Egypt will never yield for you what has been deposited in Canaan no matter how hard you try*. Quit using Canaan principles in Egypt, it will end slavery. Just relocate. God brought Jacob and his descendants to Egypt so that their needs could be met but after sometime, their time expired there and they became slaves for overstaying their welcome. Your location has a lot to do with the quality and quantity of supplies you'll get. Don't be dogmatic about staying where you are presently, your time may be up there. 2 Kings 7:3 told us about four lepers, *'And there were four leprous men at the entering in of the gate: and they said one to another, Why sit we here until we die?*". The lepers at the gate of Samaria were sensitive to know that sometimes, you need to move on to where there is abundance of supplies. They plundered Samaria and even returned to Israel to inform their kinsmen about where they could get abundance of supplies. You may be flourishing in a location yet God want you to move on. Don't be deceived by little success or comfort. Sometimes, famine could also be a sign or God's way of drawing your attention for a change of location. Whether to stay or to relocate, what matters is that you hear clearly from God. *A spiritually sensitive man will never partake in famine*.

*PRAYER*: Father, I receive the grace to be sensitive at all times in Jesus name.


====================================================================================================
DEVOTIONAL 0135 | Source start line: 14243
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
5/3/21, 8:09 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: PROJECTSAVE DEVOTIONAL
Monday,
May 3rd, 2021.

*HOW TO LIVE IN PLENTY DURING SCARCITY* Pt.3


Proverbs 11:25 The generous will prosper; those who refresh others will themselves be refreshed. NLT

*GIVING OUT OF OBEDIENCE*

When COVID-19 lockdown was declared, what I had on me was little. My income was meagre because of the abrupt closure. I wrote a cheque of a substantial amount 3 months before the lockdown. It was a seed to a woman of God I revere. I expected the cheque to be cleared on time but when it was just some days to lockdown, the huge amount was debited from my account.  I couldn't wrap my head around the fact that everything would go on standstill. My children began to eat more than I expected. May be idleness caused it. One of the days at the onset of lockdown, my wife went to grind pepper. After grinding, the girl who operated the grinding machine mistakenly poured everything on the floor. She returned with nothing. It was a serious issue. From the little we had, my wife and i began to give people in distress. Though it wasn't easy but we did it anyway.


Later, I was prompted to raise money for brethren and missionaries in need. In obedience, we started a fund raising for COVID-19 palliative and we raked in almost a million naira but I didn't take a dime from it though I needed money to feed my family as well.   I sold one of my books for a period of time to raise money for palliative and I faithfully gave all proceeds to those in need and I sent evidence of disbursement to almost everyone who supported the cause. In all of this, we had great needs in the family. Those who supported the palliative initiative didn't even bother to ask for my account details and I became worried because it was as if i was forgotten.  I didn't know it was a test. If they ask about my welfare, I said fine. I just didn't want to  take advantage of their benevolence at that time and divert funds to myself. Not long after, someone requested for my account details. l received a "dangerous" alert. Quickly I paid my tithe and i supported brethren in need from the gift. I thought I'll manage the remaining till the end of lock down. Surprisingly, the bank alert of that miraculous supply I received became a monthly thing up until some months after the lockdown.






At the beginning of every new month, before the end of the first five days, dangerous supplies poured in. Miraculously, God paid my school fees( in the seminary) for two straight sessions during the lockdown. Sacrificial giving is a time tested method of opening the heavens for supernatural supply during scarcity. *Those that don't know how to give are not ready to live in plenty during scarcity*. Stop waiting for when you'll have plenty before giving. Giving when you don't have plenty is the way to know if truly you're a giver. *Give freely and become more wealthy; be stingy and lose everything*, Proverbs 11:24 NLT



*PRAYER*: Father, I receive the grace to excel in giving in Jesus name.

DEVOTIONAL 0136 | Source start line: 14619
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
5/4/21, 6:46 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: ProjectSave Devotional
Tuesday
May 4th,  2021.

*HOW TO LIVE IN PLENTY DURING SCARCITY* Pt.4


Psalms 126:5-6Those who plant in tears will harvest with shouts of joy. They weep as they go to plant their seed, but they sing as they return with the harvest.NLT

*SACRIFICE*

The widow of Zarephath is a good example of someone who gave sacrificially. We preach about sacrificial giving but often ignore her. She gave her last meal. I can imagine how poor she was with only one son. God sent Elijah the prophet to be fed by her during famine. That may sound foolish to you. How can God send a prophet to be fed in the house of a poor widow, doesn't he know her condition? When Elijah requested for food, she said, *“I swear by the Lord your God that I don’t have a single piece of bread in the house. And I have only a handful of flour left in the jar and a little cooking oil in the bottom of the jug. I was just gathering a few sticks to cook this last meal, and then my son and I will die*”, 1 Kings 17:12 NLT.


One good thing about her was that she obeyed the prophet of God and cooked her last meal. That's sacrifice. I don't think she did that dancing and singing in the kitchen while cooking. It was said about her that *"...she did as Elijah said, and she and Elijah and her family continued to eat for many days. There was always enough flour and olive oil left in the containers, just as the Lord had promised through Elijah*",1 Kings 17:15-16 NLT. She enjoyed abundance of supernatural supply during famine. Hallelujah!!!. What will you do when God come asking for that thing you treasure most? I'm very sure Elijah would have made news headlines if he were to live in our day. He may be called a greedy or hungry prophet who manipulated a widow. I know a couple who struggled to build their house for 34 years. The wife told me that back in the 80s, God spoke through a genuine servant of God that they should support the church building project. God's servant told them God told her that they won't give the money because God revealed that to her. Truly, they  refused. The money they ought to have given the church was spent on their building yet they couldn't finish building an ordinary bungalow for 34 years.


*Readiness to sacrifice is an indication that you're willing to experience a radical change. The price you'll pay for a rise will always cost you something- sacrifice*.  Sacrifice often comes with pain. It is a type of giving that cost you something.  There are seeds you sow while dancing but there are others that you'll have to sow in tears: *"They that sow in tears shall reap in joy.  He that goeth forth and weepeth, bearing precious seed, shall doubtless come again with rejoicing, bringing his sheaves with him*", Psalm 126:5-6 KJV. When God want to launch you into a new dimension of supplies especially during scarcity, he may demand from you what will cost you something.  Your obedience to sacrificial giving is a door opener during scarcity. The opportunity to give sacrificially may be towards kingdom projects, missions, a genuine servant of God and even the brethren. At all times, be sensitive to know when God is demanding from you what you treasure.

*PRAYER*:  Father, I receive the grace to excel in giving in Jesus name.

DEVOTIONAL 0137 | Source start line: 14842
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
5/5/21, 12:29 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: PROJECTSAVE DEVOTIONAL
wednesday,
May 5th, 2021.

*HOW TO LIVE IN PLENTY DURING SCARCITY* Pt.5

2 Cor 4:7 But we have this treasure in earthen vessels, that the excellency of the power may be of God, and not of us. KJV

*UNLEASH YOUR POTENTIAL*

Disney productions was launched in 1929 just before the great depression days in America. That period goes down in the history of America as one of the worst economic down times. In 1928, brothers Walt and Roy Disney introduced the world to Mickey Mouse via their short-animated film. These brothers damned the troubling times, they knew that America needed a smile more than ever. They made money by making people  facing economic crisis smile. They made money from those who do not have money. Isn't that a paradox? They navigated the depression days and grew their business right inside a national crisis. In 2019, Disney productions was worth $69.6 billion dollars in revenue.

*Sometimes, scarcity is an opportunity to unleash our creativity and ingenuity*. Who could have thought that making people smile during a national crisis would rake in money? That was what Walt and Roy Disney did. During scarcity, God will not fail to leave us with something we can leverage on to live in plenty. *We often ignore the great ideas locked within when in our comfort zones. Hunger is a trigger for creativity sometimes*.  If you ask successful entrepreneurs, quite a number of them will tell you that crisis fueled their foray into business.

Some years back, I know a woman running a big confectionery in Lagos. Before she started her business, life became hard for her family.  Their landlord sent them packing. She and her family moved to an uncompleted apartment. From there, she started making chin-chin. She had great patronage. Her M.Sc holding husband quitted his white collar job to join his wife in chin-chin making business.  They ended up building a great company with plenty staff and wide distributorship of their products across Nigeria. Opportunities abound in every scarcity. There's something you can do in exchange for money which you've ignored. There's a passion you have that hasn't been pursued. Do something about it now.


*PRAYER*:  Father, open my eyes to see the opportunities in scarcity and help me to launch out in Jesus name

DEVOTIONAL 0138 | Source start line: 14920
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
5/6/21, 6:32 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: PROJECTSAVE DEVOTIONAL
Thursday,
May 6th, 2021.


*THEY NEED YOU*


Proverbs22:6  Train up a child in the way he should go: and when he is old, he will not depart from it.  KJV


A woman walked out of her marriage for reasons that held no water. The elders appealed to her to reconsider her decision and her husband begged her but she refused. It was glaring to everyone that her excuses were flimsy because her life was not threatened. She just wanted to be free after four kids. She left her husband with a five month old baby.  All her vituperations were seen by the older kids and they knew something was wrong between dad and mom. Her husband, though a busy man had to raise 4 kids( 3 girls and a boy) all  alone. This woman didn't give room for reconciliation.


Later, she remarried but her first husband remained single. He provided all they needed but didn't have their time.  Their only son became bitter towards her mother. In the process of time, this young boy had become wild and stubborn. On one occasion, he was found with Indian hemp while one of her sister had become sexually immoral. When their father caught wind of what was happening, he felt bad. He emphasized on how he provided all they needed. Unfortunately, providing for his children wasn't enough. Those children lacked motherly care and fatherly attention.


If we want to raise wholesome children, they need our time and attention.  While some couples are still together, they don't have time for their children. They're caught in the web of this ever-busy world pursuing mundane things.They want to build great career but not godly children. They monitor their children with phones and electronic gadgets. *Digital parenting cannot replace your physical presence*.   *It is not enough to provide all that money can buy for your children, they need you*. Your presence around them is priceless. I know a woman in her forties who told us her mother wasn't there for her when she needed her most. She abandoned her and siblings and eloped with another man. Ministry engagements isn't an excuse to starve your family of attention. Your home is your first ministry. If you fail there, you've failed woefully in life even if you're successful in other areas. The increase in juvenile delinquency is a pointer to deficient parenting. Seeing children and teenagers living a wayward life shows the home is under siege by the enemy.  You need to re order your priorities and make your family number one.


*PRAYER*: Father, may I not fail as a parent in Jesus name.

DEVOTIONAL 0139 | Source start line: 14984
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
5/7/21, 6:08 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: PROJECTSAVE DEVOTIONAL
Friday,
May 7th, 2021.

*MODELING BIBLICAL MARRIAGE TO YOUR CHILDREN*

Proverbs22:6  Train up a child in the way he should go: and when he is old, he will not depart from it.  KJV


When my parents celebrated their 40th wedding anniversary, I posted on my facebook wall that their marriage was the first marriage seminar I attended. The youth wing of a church invited me to come explain what I meant by that post. The first marriage seminar I attended was not a formal meeting where singles gathered to hear a marriage counselor teach. The casual observation of how my parents lived together and raised us formed the base line knowledge I have about marriage. Consciously or unconsciously, everyone attended their first marriage seminar under the roof of their parents/ guardian. Sadly, despite the increase in knowledge about marriage in this age, marriages are still failing including that of marriage counselors because a faulty foundation.  *Until God deals with unbiblical marriage model and mentality that some people learnt from their parents, knowledge( from books&seminars) won't be helpful.*


*From my personal observation, modeling a godly life and christian marriage imparts children more than mere oral teaching*. Children learn quickly through observation and imitation.  The foundation upon which your children will build their marriage is what you're laying now. Have you wondered why wife battery continues in our society despite the awareness? Research reveals that most of the men who beat their wives saw their dad do same to their mothers. In homes where fathers abuse their wives physically, sons grow up to think it is the right thing to do when their wives offend them.  *There are irresponsible and uncaring husbands today because most of them were raised by irresponsible fathers*. They didn't have the opportunity of learning biblical fatherhood under a good father.



There are wives who picked the habit of verbal abuse from their mother. They were raised by a mother that was never submissive, always nagging and ready to pick a quarrel.  Divorce, polygamy, single parenting ( "baby mama syndrome") and infidelity is not an exemption in this matter. Most divorcée come from broken homes where their parents think the best way to resolve differences is to part ways. Polygamous& unfaithful parents breeds children that may likely do same. The cycle continues. Unfortunately, we live in a society where we quickly conclude that these evil cycle is a generational curse whereas it is the product of irresponsibility taught and transferred from one generation to another.  What kind of marriage do you desire for your children? *You need to make your marriage a model that your children can imitate and build on. Be intentional about doing the right thing for them to see so that they can learn*. God the father exemplifies modeling correct fatherhood and Jesus confirms it by saying: *"...I tell you the truth, the Son can do nothing by himself. He does only what he sees the Father doing. Whatever the Father does, the Son also does*", John 5:19 NLT. This is how it should be.


*PRAYER*: Father, I receive the grace to model the right thing for my children. I'll raise a godly and sanctified generation in Jesus name.

5/8/21, 6:58 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: ProjectSave Devotional
Saturday,
May 8th, 2021.

*MODELING GODLY LIVING TO THE NEXT GENERATION*

Proverbs22:6  Train up a child in the way he should go: and when he is old, he will not depart from it.  KJV


Sometimes ago, i visited a family. When I got there, we heard a knock on the door, they inquired to know who was at the door. When the man replied, the children quickly cleared the house of all  that could show their dad was around. No single trace found. I wondered what was happening so I  asked. I was told that their dad already gave an instruction that when that particular man comes, they should tell him he wasn't around. Eventually, when they let the man in, that was what they told him. I saw their dad when he returned from work and I knew he was inside. The man taught them to lie. The little I know about them, the children and their mother were fond of lying to the man as well. There's no openness in that family.


Some mothers mentored their daughters in the school of ungodliness. They raised their daughters never to be truthful to their husbands especially on financial matters. They make them believe all men are the same and do not deserve to be told the truth. Such daughters grow up to lie and use under handed methods in dealing with their husbands. Some fathers modeled to their sons never to be truthful to their wives about their finances. Some modeled irresponsible living. A man once told his wife he can't stay faithful to her only because no one does in his family. His father ran after anything in  skirt even after marrying two wives. This man flaunted his immoral lifestyle.This led to a big trouble in their marriage that almost made his wife stab him. Whatever happens, I'm sure their children are watching. Only the future will tell.


Though godly living is not genetically transferred but it can be modeled for the next generation to imitate. Speaking about Timothy, Paul said, *"I remember your sincere and unqualified faith [the surrendering of your entire self to God in Christ with confident trust in His power, wisdom and goodness, a faith] which first lived in [the heart of] your grandmother Lois and your mother Eunice, and I am confident that it is in you as well*", 2Tim1:5, AMP. Children learn primarily by imitation. They can adhere to instructions for fear of discipline but what will eventually become their predominant character, perspective and world view in later years is what they see their parents do. We need to be intentional about parenting. They're watching. We need to consciously pass on the baton of godliness to the next generation.

*PRAYER*: Father, I receive the grace to be a godly model to the next generation in Jesus name

DEVOTIONAL 0141 | Source start line: 15200

5/9/21, 6:08 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: ProjectSave Devotional
Sunday,
May 9th, 2021.


*MODEL CHRISTIAN DEVOTION TO THEM*


Luke 4:16 And he came to Nazareth, where he had been brought up: and, as his custom was, he went into the synagogue on the sabbath day, and stood up for to read. KJV


On the day of my convocation, as my parents were about leaving the convocation ground, they prayed together in the car thanking God for the day and they committed the journey into God's hands. One of my room mates who looked on as they prayed told me that in over 20 years of existence, his family had never prayed together.  *Every parent must consciously model christian devotion to their children. Christian devotion in context mean all that pertains to Christian living: prayer, fasting, study of the bible, regular attendance in church, evangelism, giving and supporting God's work and so on*.


Severally, I have seen church folks who complain about their children not loving the things of the Lord. Some parents complain that their children do not pray, study the bible neither do they come to church. A cursory look at some of these complaints, I noticed that some of these children were not initiated into these things by way of practical engagement and modeling. *Children can learn the art of christian devotion if their parents lead the way in that regard.* In Luke 2, the parents of Jesus took him to the temple at Jerusalem for the feast at a tender age. In Luke 4, when Jesus became a full grown man, we were told it was customary for Jesus to go to the temple. Idol worshippers and people of other faith are intentional about these things and so should we. The first time I visited Sierra Leone for a mission outreach, I was told that parents initiate children into age grade cults. That's how idol worship is sustained all over the world.


Let your children be part of your christian devotion. Let them see you praying and make them pray as well. When you're studying the bible, don't do it in hiding. Let them know that your source of sustenance is drawn from heaven through these things and that you expect them to do same. To be sincere, they may not imbibe these values early in life or they may become cold about it in their teenage years but as you keep praying for them, the Holy Spirit will ignite the fire of the seed you've sown in their hearts. At a time in my life, I loathed all the Christian training my parents gave me especially in my teenage years but a time came in my life, I discovered there was a seed in me fighting to germinate and eventually it did. Don't be discouraged about not getting results, keep doing it anyway. They're watching. The seed will grow some day.


*PRAYER*: Father, I receive the grace to model Christian devotion to my children in Jesus name

5/10/21, 5:58 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: PROJECTSAVE DEVOTIONAL
Monday,
May 10th,  2021.

*WHAT WE EXPECT FROM YOU* Pt.1

Hebrews 5:12 In fact, though by this time you ought to be teachers, you need someone to teach you the elementary truths of God's word all over again. You need milk, not solid food! NIV

*DEDICATION TO SPIRITUAL GROWTH*

Sometimes ago, a woman brought her son to me for prayers. The boy ought to have been walking but was still crawling. The mother was bothered about his condition. After praying for the boy, I told her that he'll walk. Months after that prayer, she sent me a message that her son has started walking. That's the joy of every mother, to see steady growth in their children. Mothers become bothered about children that eat and do not grow. Some mothers would visit the hospital to consult the doctor on why their child isn't growing. It is painful to keep feeding a child without a marked growth. It betrays the expectation of every mother.





Same applies to every pastor or minister that God has placed over you. One of our expectations is that you grow spiritually. *My expectation is that all readers of this devotional attain unto maturity in Christ.* That's why I never cease to teach on daily basis. *If you eat spiritual food and do not grow spiritually, you'll be breaking the heart of your pastor or whoever minister to you constantly*. The writer of Hebrew express such concern in the opening text. Just as milk is for babies and solid food is for the mature, his expectation was that the believers under his care would grow up spiritually to the point where they'll start eating solid (spiritual) meat and teaching others.




Carnality is one of the tell tale signs that shows you're not growing spiritually: *"And I, brethren, could not speak unto you as unto spiritual, but as unto carnal, even as unto babes in Christ*", 1 Cor 3:1-2 KJV. Further, in verse 3, Paul told us the traits of carnality: *"For ye are yet carnal: for whereas there is among you envying, and strife, and divisions, are ye not carnal, and walk as men?*". When the works of the flesh is still pleasurable to you, you're carnal and haven't grown spiritually. The way out of this is to take heed to the word of God and the teachings you've heard from your pastor. Make conscious effort to practice what your pastor is teaching you and pray for grace to abide.


*PRAYER*: Father,I receive the grace to be dedicated to spiritual growth in Jesus name.

5/11/21, 6:36 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: ProjectSave Devotional
Tuesday
May 11th,  2021.

*WHAT WE EXPECT FROM YOU* Pt.2

Romans12:11 Not slothful in business; fervent in spirit; serving the Lord

*DEDICATION TO KINGDOM SERVICE*

There's a brother I love so much. He is very committed and dedicated to the work of the Lord. Anytime I interact with his leaders in church, they always have good commendations about him because he is someone they can count on. In the ministry of apostle Paul, he commended some brothers for their dedication to kingdom service. He called them co-workers: *"Epaphras, my fellow prisoner in Christ Jesus, sends you his greetings. So do Mark, Aristarchus, Demas, and Luke, my co-workers*", Philemon 1:23-24 NLT. Apostle Paul wouldn't have called them his co-workers if they were not dedicated to kingdom service. Your skill, training and gifts are tools given by God for kingdom service: *“See, I have chosen Bezalel son of Uri, the son of Hur, of the tribe of Judah, 3 and I have filled him with the Spirit of God, with wisdom, with understanding, with knowledge and with all kinds of skills— 4 to make artistic designs for work in gold, silver and bronze, ...Moreover, I have appointed Oholiab son of Ahisamak, of the tribe of Dan, to help him. Also I have given ability to all the skilled workers to make everything I have commanded you:*", Ex31:2-6.

Every pastor is looking for someone that is committed to his vision not an opposition. Every pastor is praying for people who will serve wholeheartedly, not for price or reward. There are people who give half hearted service. They're only motivated by a pay cheque. Some want to be seen and do not want to do the seemingly small tasks in the church. They want to share the pulpit in the church with pastor because they feel they can preach probably because they were fellowship president or executive on campus. If such persons are assigned to take Sunday school or do other things, they deem it downgrading.  Some hop in and out of church, they only serve in their unit whenever they feel like. If all these describes you, I want to tell you that your pastor is not happy with you. Every set man is praying for people he can entrust with tasks while he gives attention to other things. The worst I've seen is when brethren warm the benches in church not doing a thing or lift their finger in contribution to the progress of the church. Sometimes, I ask if it is possible to be genuinely saved and not want to do anything in God's house? David, in all his royalty, he said, *"Better is one day in your courts than a thousand elsewhere; I would rather be a doorkeeper in the house of my God than dwell in the tents of the wicked*",Psalms84:10. He was so in love with God and His house to the extent that he doesn't mind serving as an usher. Imagine the king/ governor of your state serving as an usher in your church.

You cannot out grow kingdom service regardless of your degree or societal status. Bear in mind that you're not serving the pastor but God under the leadership of that pastor. No kingdom labour will go unrewarded: *"For God is not unjust. He will not forget how hard you have worked for him and how you have shown your love to him by caring for other believers, as you still do*", Hebrew6:10. Volunteer for productive kingdom service and be the answer to the prayer of that set man that is labouring to fulfill God's vision.


*PRAYER*: Father, I receive the grace to serve faithfully and wholeheartedly in Jesus name

wednesday,
May 12th, 2021.

*WHAT WE EXPECT FROM YOU* Pt.3

1 Thess5:25   Dear brothers and sisters, pray for us.

*DEDICATION TO INTERCESSION*

In my few years of ministry, I've observed that church folks want their pastors to  pray for them always. They put so much prayer burden on their pastors and leaders but they rarely pray for their pastor(s), his family and ministry. Some pastors spend much of their prayer time praying for people who don't know they're being prayed for, they fast and take up prayer burdens while members are sleeping. Personally, I can't count the number of times some persons have asked to know if I'm praying for them. In fact, some have turned their pastors and other genuine ministers to prayer consultants. They may not come to church regularly or be dedicated to the progress of the church but the moment they're in trouble, they'll consult their pastor for prayers.


What we expect from you in this regard is that as we pray for you, you should be praying for us dedicatedly. The time spent gossiping about your pastor, his family and ministry should be converted to prayer time for him. *When preachers run into trouble, sometimes they can be very lonely. From afar, people think they are super men who do not have human feelings but deep within, something is eating them up yet they don't have our prayer support as expected*. Nothing delights the devil more than seeing God's people pity/gossip about their leaders instead of praying for them.

*If you see a preacher flourishing in ministry, there's a strong prayer support base somewhere*. Let your pastor, that missionary or christian leader who has impacted your life so much be your priority in the place of prayer. Pray that God should uphold him and make him fulfill his ministry. Pray for his wife to be strengthened to be a suitable help meet and that his children will be a godly model unto others. Pray for the all-round growth of his ministry, his finances and health. No matter how great, prayerful and spiritual your pastor is, he needs your prayers. *If apostle Paul could solicit to be prayed for, no minister should be exempted from this holy service of the saints. We should be humble enough to ask for prayers. It is not a sign of weakness.*

*ACTION POINT*: Pray for me( Oyeleke Olanrewaju Benjamin) as you're led by the Spirit of God.
