*WHAT WE EXPECT FROM YOU* Pt.4



Acts5:42 And daily in the temple, and in every house, they ceased not to teach and preach Jesus Christ. KJV


*DEDICATION TO EVANGELISM&CHURCH GROWTH*


I read a book about a heretical movement that knock on 900,000 doors on weekly basis all over the world. They are committed to spreading their heresy to anyone who care to listen. You'll find them at bus stops sharing their booklets, tracts and publications. They're working assiduously to bring souls into their fold. Both old and young among them are committed to this. Though they peddle heresy, their commitment is noteworthy and their church is growing. The adherents of that sect didn't leave the work of soul winning for their pastor. Everyone is involved.


Every pastor is happy when his sheep is committed to soul winning and church growth. In the early church, the work of soul winning was not left for the apostles. All the members of the early church were actively involved in bringing men to the saving knowledge of the risen Christ and ensuring that they are part of the church. Their brand of Christianity didn't create an evangelism unit/ department. Everyone was involved.


Since you gave your life to Christ, how many souls have you won to the Lord? How many people have you brought to the church where you worship?  Your pastor cannot grow that church alone. He needs you to be part of the process just as the disciples of Christ found one another: *"One of the two which heard John speak, and followed him, was Andrew, Simon Peter's brother.  He first findeth his own brother Simon, and saith unto him, We have found the Messias, which is, being interpreted, the Christ*",
John 1:40-41 KJV. Reading downwards, Philip did the same thing: *"Philip findeth Nathanael, and saith unto him, We have found him, of whom Moses in the law, and the prophets, did write, Jesus of Nazareth, the son of Joseph*", John 1:45 KJV. Go find the unchurched and bring them to church. Ensure those empty chairs are filled, invite someone to church next Sunday.

*PRAYER*: I receive grace to win souls and bring them into the church in Jesus name.

5/14/21, 12:31 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: PROJECTSAVE DEVOTIONAL

*WHAT WE EXPECT FROM YOU* Pt.5



Philippians 4:3 Yes, I also ask you, my true partner, to help these women. They have worked hard with me to advance the gospel, along with Clement and the rest of my fellow workers, whose names are in the Book of Life.

*DEDICATION TO THE VISION&LOYALTY*

Sometimes, I still wonder why Paul decided not to take John Mark along with him after Barnabas suggested he joined them in their apostolic journey. From my perspective, it could be that John Mark was not dedicated to the apostolic vision of Paul and  Barnabas. Hence, he saw no need to take him along in their second missionary journey. John Mark left them at a point in their first missionary journey and I want to believe that the way and manner he left must have informed Paul's decision to drop him when their second missionary journey was about to start. Read Acts 15:36-41.


*No pastor will take serious a disloyal member who is not committed to the vision God gave him*. There are so many disloyal members in the body of Christ today. Their loyalty lies in churches where there are pecuniary gains like job, contract, palliative and marriage opportunities. Such people jump from church to church, programs to programs. Anywhere they hear it is happening, they jump ship immediately. They can tag their friends the advert of a program by another minister that is happening in town but not their pastor's program. They divert the offerings that should have helped the progress of their church elsewhere yet needs abound where they're fed with God's word. The day their pastor's sermon is wonderful, they feel alright with his ministry but the day they feel he didn't dish out "rhema" or may be anointing didn't flow, they'll start comparing him with another preacher out there. *You should pitch your tent in a church/ ministry under a pastor by revelation and conviction. This way, you won't be a victim of ministry/ church prostitution*.


There are associate ministers who can't take pain to build and run with the vision of their senior pastor. They're always on the look out for a church that will pay more or a church/ ministry that is well established where they won't have to stress themselves building from the scratch. They despise days of little beginnings. As a setman, feel free to let go a disloyal associate that is not committed to your vision. *Whoever is not ready to help you build according to the pattern God gave you is not meant to be around you*. Apostle John had such disloyal associate around him, Diotrephes by name, 3 John1-9-10. Don't be that disloyal member or ministry associate. Nothing you do will go unrewarded.

*PRAYER*: Father, I receive the grace to be loyal and dedicated to the ministry vision of my leader in Jesus name.

DEVOTIONAL 0147 | Source start line: 15730

5/15/21, 7:20 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: ProjectSave Devotional
Saturday,
May 15th, 2021.

*WHAT WE EXPECT FROM YOU* Pt.6

Galatians 6:6 Those who are taught the word of God should provide for their teachers, sharing all good things with them. NLT

*DEDICATION TO FINANCIAL SUPPORT*

Sometimes ago, I was discussing the issue of finance with a minister of the gospel. I told him how some church folks use their monies to support preachers with sweet mouth who promise instant breakthroughs while their church and pastor is in great need. The same thing applies to missionaries. A number of missionaries and evangelists with genuine ministries have been forgotten by the people who should have supported them. Most times, the issue is not lack of money but lack of dedication to giving to the right cause on the part of God's people.



*If the only time you give to support your church, your pastor or that ministry is when they sweet-talk, coerce and make spurious promises of sudden breakthroughs and miracles, you're an emotional giver not someone who gives out of conviction and dedication to a genuine cause.* It has always been my style to cast a vision and get people to commit themselves to it financially not using "evangelistic lies" to gather money. *I'll rather have 1000 ministry partners who understand my vision and what a soul is worth than 10,000 who only want to give because of what they can get.* Some folks are looking out for a preacher with debt canceling anointing or sudden breakthrough anointing. All of these are gimmicks to make you part with your money. *The blessing attached to giving comes only when you give out of conviction not out of coercion*.


I heard of folks who demanded a preacher to return their money because what he promised would happen didn't happen after they gave. Primarily, your giving should be to worship God and to meet a need. Your financial commitment in the kingdom should be in two fold: (a) to support kingdom advancement programs & projects (b) to support genuine ministers of the gospel. It is scriptural: *"In the same way, the Lord ordered that those who preach the Good News should be supported by those who benefit from it*", 1 Corinthians 9:14 NLT. Stop waiting for your pastor or that minister to announce a need before you give. In any ministry that is productive ( winning souls, planting churches,  discipling believers, supporting missionaries and the needy) there would always be a need for money. A genuine pastor/ minister of the gospel may not tell you he has needs. Give to him as an obligation whether he has needs or not. Where you draw spiritual nourishment is a good ground to sow your seed, don't forget those who labour to make you grow spiritually.


*PRAYER*: Father, as from today, I receive the grace to give out of conviction to a genuine cause not out of coercion in Jesus name

*WHAT WE EXPECT FROM YOU* Pt.7

1 Tim4:8 For while bodily training is of some value, godliness is of value in every way, as it holds promise for the present life and also for the life to come. ESV


*DEDICATION TO GODLY LIVING*


Every child of God is God's ambassador on earth. We represent God. That is why there's so much noise in the media when a Christian or pastor misbehave. News about the misdeeds of someone from another faith may not trend on social media but you can be sure that if a christian or prominent face in the church is involved, the world will hear.
Further, every believer is an ambassador of his/ her church. People out there tend to judge our character and conclude that everyone in our church is the same. Sometimes, some persons have made statements like, "If the church of Mr 'X' or Mrs 'X' is the only church on earth, I won't attend any church". People make statements like this because of the ungodly attitude of some believers.


Why people out there link ungodly behaviour of believers with their church remains unclear to me. Probably they think ungodliness is learnt and taught in the church. The crux of the matter is that in one way or the other, we all represent our churches and pastors. Every pastor expects good representation( expressed through godly living) from his members out there. Godliness can attract people to  church. A genuine pastor would be happy hearing that his members are representing Christ and the church outside the four walls of the church. In Matthew 5:26, Jesus said, *"In the same way, let your light shine before others, that they may see your good deeds and glorify your Father in heaven*".


What will bring glory to God in your life is your good deeds not your big house or exotic car. No pastor will be happy hearing that his member is a street fighter, sexually immoral or is guilty of embezzlement and financial scandal. You're meant to be a proof of your Pastor's ministry, please represent him well: *"Even if others think I am not an apostle, I certainly am to you. You yourselves are proof that I am the Lord's apostle*", 1 For 9:2, NLT. Take those teachings of his serious and pray for grace to internalize them. Don't just hear him preach holiness, practice it.

*PRAYER* Father, I receive the grace to live godly in Jesus name. Amen

5/17/21, 7:57 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: PROJECTSAVE DEVOTIONAL
Monday,
May 17th,  2021.

*THE POWER TO CHOOSE*

Deuteronomy 30:19 I call heaven and earth to witness against you today, that I have set before you life and death, blessing and curse. Therefore choose life, that you and your offspring may live, ESV

Some persons have asked to know who tempted Satan in heaven before God cast him down to the earth. They wanted to know if there was another Satan that tempted Satan. Well, there was no other Satan that tempted the Satan that God cast down from heaven. Lucifer made a choice to rebel against God. Speaking about Lucifer in Ezekiel 28:15, we were told that iniquity was found in him. No one put it there, it was all his decision: *"You were perfect in your ways from the day you were created, Till iniquity was found in you*". He exercised his will power by deciding what he wanted to do.


One of the greatest power God gave mankind is the ability to choose. Man is a free moral agent not a robot that needs to be teleguided. From the old testament, we saw Joshua telling the people to choose whom they will serve: *"And if it is evil in your eyes to serve the Lord, choose this day whom you will serve, whether the gods your fathers served in the region beyond the River, or the gods of the Amorites in whose land you dwell. But as for me and my house, we will serve the Lord*", Joshua24:15. From time immemorial, no external force is strong enough to make man do what he doesn't want to do. Every action has always been a product of man's choice. Adam and Eve fell because they ate the forbidden fruit willingly. The serpent didn't force it on them. Though they were tempted, but the choice to yield to temptation was all theirs. The devil will always suggest evil things to us but it is our decision to accept his suggestions or not.


In Romans 6:13, Paul confirmed this by imploring us not to yield our members to temptation: *"Neither yield ye your members as instruments of unrighteousness unto sin: but yield yourselves unto God, as those that are alive from the dead, and your members as instruments of righteousness unto God*". I want you to note the word "yield". To yield is our choice not the devil's.  If you're tempted to fornicate and you are told the person you want to fornicate with has COVID-19/ HIV, I'm very sure the desire will die immediately. It shows you have control over your actions. God want us to take responsibility for our actions and not to always put the blame on the devil.

*PRAYER*: Father, I receive the grace to act right at all times in Jesus name.

DEVOTIONAL 0150 | Source start line: 15948

5/18/21, 7:14 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: ProjectSave Devotional
Tuesday
May 18th,  2021.

*UNDERSTANDING GOD'S REWARD SYSTEM*

1Cor 3:8 The one who plants and the one who waters work together with the same purpose. And both will be rewarded for their own hard work. NLT

There's a misconception about how God reward his people that most believers have. It has made some persons to pursue the wrong things and ignore the things that are germane. In the Corinthian church, some took sides with Paul while others took sides with Apollos for reasons best known to them. Paul told them his assignment was akin to that of a planter while Apollos watered what he planted. Further, he said that God will reward each man based on their work ( assignment, purpose). There's something I want you us to examine there.

*God doesn't reward just any work. His reward is for the work he has assigned to us not for what we aren't sent to do*. Paul, a planter knew his reward would be based on the effectiveness of his planting work, he didn't cross from his lane into the watering ministry of Apollos. Some persons ignore the "little" assignment God gave them because it is unpopular. They see the beauty in the assignment of others because of how it is presented in the media but  failed to check the eternal impact. Some see themselves as disadvantaged because of their location and have been thinking of relocating to a city where people will see their works or to intensify on media hype of their exploits. Another class of people abandoned their real assignment for what they perceive will put money into their pocket, yet they may claim to be working for God. I almost fell for this early in ministry. I almost abandoned field ministry to go start a church when God hasn't instructed because I felt having a standing congregation will better my lot.

*Dearest, what God reward is faithfulness, dedication and commitment to the very assignment he has committed into our hands even if it seems little( though there are no little assignments) or unpopular*. Your duty is to seek God to know His mind on the exact thing he want you to do. I love Paul the apostle. He knew the assignment that will earn him his reward and he was faithful to it. In 1Cor 1:17, he said, *"FOR CHRIST DIDN'T SEND ME TO BAPTIZE, BUT TO PREACH the Good News—and not with clever speech, for fear that the cross of Christ would lose its power*". Wow! That's profound. Is it bad to baptize people? Of course, it is not bad yet Paul said God didn't send him to baptize people. He knew his reward is attached to his assignment of preaching and establishing a local body of believers where none existed. Your reward is attached to your purpose in life, please fulfill it.

*PRAYER*: Father, I receive the grace to find my assignment, fulfill it faithfully till you come for me in Jesus name. Amen

wednesday,
May 19th, 2021.

*GOD CAN CHANGE YOUR STORY*

1 Chronicles 4: 9Jabez was more honorable than his brothers. His mother had named him Jabez, c saying, “I gave birth to him in pain.”


I know someone who had it rough in his growing years. He sold newspaper in traffic while his mother lived from hand to mouth. Today, he's highly placed and his past life of sorrow had gone into oblivion. His present life doesn't reflect the sorrows and pains of the past. Another man that is known to me was once a motor boy in Lagos, Nigeria. He did all manners of hard labour while living with his elder brother just to make ends meet. Today, the story is no longer the same. We are familiar with the story of Jabez. He was a child of sorrow just as his name implies. The bible didn't tell us much about him but we can infer from the few verses written about him that his life was very rough and full of pains.



God can lift a  poor man and make him dine with princes: *"He lifts the poor from the dust and the needy from the garbage dump. He sets them among princes, even the princes of his own people!*",Psalms 113:7-8 NLT. Hallelujah!!! You may be having it rough and hard. Life may be dealing you heavy blows of backwardness, stagnancy, joblessness, barrenness, and frustration, I'm glad to inform you that God can change your story. If God can change the story of Jabez, He will change your story.

Before Jabez's story changed, he did something. In verse 10 we were told that he cried to God: *"Jabez cried out to the God of Israel, “Oh, that you would bless me and enlarge my territory! Let your hand be with me, and keep me from harm so that I will be free from pain.” And God granted his request*". To experience a change, you need to cry for it. You must crave and cry for it desperately. The prayer of a man that needs a radical change from bad to good must be intense and consistent. Jabez didn't complain about the economy neither did he shift blames on anyone. He asked for what he wanted and God granted his request.

*PRAYER*:  Father, change my story(...insert the story you want God to change) and give me a testimony in Jesus name.

*OVERCOMING UNBIBLICAL STEREOTYPES* Pt.1


Romans12:2 Don’t copy the behavior and customs of this world, but let God transform you into a new person by changing the way you think. Then you will learn to know God’s will for you, which is good and pleasing and perfect. NLT




*HEAVEN HELP THOSE WHO HELP THEMSELF*


I have heard on several occasions where people said, " heaven help those who help themself". They often allude this statement to scriptures. Ever since I've been studying my bible, I haven't found this statement in the bible. Not even a semblance of it. Often, the context in which most people make this statement is at the point where compromise seems to be the immediate answer to a lingering problem.


For example, a student who has failed examination severally and has not compromised his faith by engaging in malpractice or a female student that failed by refusing to yield to the sexual advances of her lecturer may be advised by friends to compromise. The statement that  comes with such evil counsel may be, "Heaven help those who help themself". They're trying to use 'heaven'( the throne of God) to support compromise. This is one of the unbiblical stereotypes that has bedevilled our world and some believers have fallen for it.
Heaven's help is for those who cannot help themselves.



*The voice of compromise comes to us in the form of 'wise' counsel but heeding to such will lead to eternal doom*. The same voice of deception was heard in the garden of Eden and Eve fell for it. That voice is reverberating in the hearts and ears of many today all over the world. The way out is to stick to the revealed truth in the word of God stubbornly even if it means death. The three Hebrews were compelled to bow to the golden statue but were resolute not to bow even if God chooses not to rescue them. They didn't help themselves out by bowing to the statue. The golden statue is always before us waiting for us to bow in compromise.  *Compromise will come wearing the garb of "heaven help those who help themself " or "It doesn't matter, everyone is doing it"*. You need to come to that point in your walk where nothing but the truth in God's word is what you'll stick to.



*PRAYER*: Father, I receive the grace to overcome unbiblical stereotypes. I refuse to compromise and I receive grace to stand in Jesus name.

*OVERCOMING UNBIBLICAL STEREOTYPES* Pt.2


Romans12:2 Don’t copy the behavior and customs of this world, but let God transform you into a new person by changing the way you think. Then you will learn to know God’s will for you, which is good and pleasing and perfect. NLT

*MEN ARE POLYGAMOUS BY NATURE- IT IS NORMAL TO CHEAT*.

A man walked into my office years back. He had separated from his wife. As a lover of peace, I tried to mend the broken relationship but the man told me I shouldn't bother because he had made up his mind to move on. The wife accused him of battery, gross irresponsibility and infidelity. He told me that there's no big deal about cheating because it is normal for men to do that. I could remember vividly that while he was proud about cheating, he was bitterly suspicious of his wife's fidelity. In my heart, I felt he shouldn't be pained if his wife pay him back in his own coin. Whoever & whatever makes it "normal" for men to cheat on their wives shouldn't outlaw cheating for wives too. After all, all cheating men do so with a woman( either single/ married). Cheating is incomplete until both sexes are involved.


The society has alot of sinful androcentric stereotypes that has damaged the lives of men, ruined their destinies and scattered their homes. The devil has sold a free lie to men that they cannot be faithful to a woman all their life because they aren't wired for one woman. Big lie! What men should be shameful about has become a thing of pride with a feeling of conquest. In Proverbs6:32, king Solomon, a professor in women affairs said: *"But the man who commits adultery is an utter fool, for he destroys himself*". No one knows the negative impact of adultery better than Solomon. He said every adulterer is a fool heading for destruction.


If animals like Beavers, Gibbons,Parakeet,
Yellow Crested Penguin, Swan, French Angelfish, Owl and a host of others are faithful to their mates in a monogamous relationship till death , how much more humans especially believers who live under the grace of God that has taught us to say 'No' to ungodliness, Titus2:11-12. *God didn't create men to be polygamous by nature. It is just a carnal mindset most men accept as norm over time*. Talking about marital fidelity, in Proverbs 5:15-17, the bible says, *"Drink water from your own well— share your love only with your wife. Why spill the water of your springs in the streets, having sex with just anyone? You should reserve it for yourselves. Never share it with strangers*". The bible describes that man/woman you're having an affair with as a stranger. Run from strangers.

*PRAYER*: Father, I receive the grace to stay faithful to my spouse in Jesus name. Amen

*OVERCOMING UNBIBLICAL STEREOTYPES* Pt.3

Romans12:2 Don’t copy the behavior and customs of this world, but let God transform you into a new person by changing the way you think. Then you will learn to know God’s will for you, which is good and pleasing and perfect. NLT

A man was counseling a single over the phone sometimes ago. Talking about sexual immorality, the writer of Hebrews liken sexually immoral people to Esau: *"See that no one is sexually immoral, or is godless like Esau, who for a single meal sold his inheritance rights as the oldest son*", Heb12:16. DEVOTIONAL 0155 | Source start line: 16593
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
5/23/21, 5:52 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: ProjectSave Devotional
Sunday,
May 23rd, 2021.


*OVERCOMING UNBIBLICAL STEREOTYPES* Pt.4


Romans3:7 “But,” someone might still argue, “how can God condemn me as a sinner if my dishonesty highlights his truthfulness and brings him more glory?”



*THE END JUSTIFY THE MEANS*

This quote is very popular. It has been attributed to Niccolò Machiavelli, the famous Italian political philosopher. It is a sly way of saying that what matters is result even if the means/ process of getting that result is bad. This idea has several names: "Situation Ethics," "Moral Relativity," "practicality," and other terms. One man called it "using the devil's tools to do God's work." Those who imbibe this unbiblical stereotype aren't after the purity/ rightness of process, the concern is just about getting the desired result. *Our God is not a God of "the end justifies the mean". The means is very important to Him just as the end is*. There are plethora of scriptures that proves that the way and manner( means) we go about a thing matters to God. They don't go unnoticed.


In 1 Sam15:3, 10-23, God instructed Saul to destroy all the Amalekites and their flocks, but Saul kept the best animals to offer sacrifice to God. Offering sacrifice to God was "a good work," but it did not justify disregarding God's command. In verse 11, God declared that Saul did not obey His commandments. "To obey is better than sacrifice" (v22). God love sacrifice but not at the expense of the right means- obedience to His commands. The end did not justify a different means from what God had specified. What about Nadab and Abihu? Their goal was to offer incense to worship God but they used a strange fire different from what God specified. Their action resulted in death. Lev10:1-2. They learned too late that the end did not justify using a different means. Further, in 1 Chronicles 13:6,7,9,10 - David and Israel thought it was "good" to bring the ark of the covenant back among them (vv 2,4). The end or goal was "good." But while transporting it by ox cart, Uzzah touched it to keep it from falling. God killed him. Why? In 1 Chronicles 15:2,12-15 - God had appointed that the ark should be carried on poles by priests and Levites. No one else could touch it [Num. 4:15-20]. God killed Uzzah because they "did not consult Him about the proper order." Moving the ark was good, but God had specified the "proper order" to use. The end did not justify using a means different from what God said.


Jesus did not mince words in  Matthew 7:21-23 when he said that many will come with great testimonies of their good exploits( healing, deliverance, miracles, etc) in ministry on that day but He will say, *"depart from me, ye workers of iniquity*". Their goals ( to operate in the miraculous & do good works) are good but God is concerned about the purity life of the person behind those manifestations and the rightness of the means used in achieving those goals. The life a believer should not be hinged on the end justifying the means, God is concerned with every step and process involved. Don't think that paying tithe of stolen money will make God change His mind about His hatred for stealing. That testimony of academic success, promotion or employment gotten through sexual immorality may motivate people in the church to pray more and expect a miracle but it doesn't move God a bit because you got the right thing the wrong way. God's standard won't because of anyone.  Paul says, *"Nevertheless, the foundation of God standeth sure, having this seal, The Lord knoweth them that are his. And, Let every one that nameth the name of Christ depart from iniquity*", 2Tim2:19.


*PRAYER*: Father, I receive the grace to do things right and to pursue my goals rightly in Jesus name.

DEVOTIONAL 0156 | Source start line: 16727

5/24/21, 6:33 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: PROJECTSAVE DEVOTIONAL
Monday,
May 24th,  2021.

*OVERCOMING UNBIBLICAL STEREOTYPES* Pt.5

Romans12:2 Don’t copy the behavior and customs of this world, but let God transform you into a new person by changing the way you think. Then you will learn to know God’s will for you, which is good and pleasing and perfect. NLT



*IF YOU CAN'T BEAT THEM JOIN THEM*

The statement above means that if someone is too strong for you, just team up with them and be on their side. In our day-to- day conversation and interaction with the outside world, this statement has come to connote "compromise". It is suggestive of aligning with  unbiblical norms and practices in our society so as to have an edge. For example, a student that has failed several times may join the bandwagon of those involved in examination malpractice just to escape the shame of yearly failure. In some offices, some cut corners to make extra  cash. If you find yourself in that environment, you may discover that your colleagues at work are living fine while you go broke before month end. The devil may  tempt you to join them if you can't beat them.The conformity to  the sinful standard in the world is anti bible regardless of the circumstance or situation in which we find ourselves.


Right from under the old covenant, God warned against unholy alliance and joining multitude to do evil:*"Thou shalt not follow a multitude to do evil;...*", Ex.23:2.  A practice may be popular and en vogue but may not be of God. Whenever we are in the crowd for too long, an irresistible  influence is exerted upon us. We should never look at what people are doing as our standard for daily living. Our calling is to be separated unto the Lord: *"Therefore, "Come out from them and be separate, says the Lord. Touch no unclean thing, and I will receive you*", 2Cor 6:17. Every day, the devil is inventing new ways of getting us in the mix. He'll present compromise to us as harmless with a sense of "it doesn't matter, so many people are doing it". Dearest, anytime the devil tells you that a thing doesn't matter, just know that it matters much.

Sometimes ago, someone told me he can help find a way to adjust my prepaid meter to reduce the billing. He presented it to me as though it is what everyone is doing and it doesn't matter. As harmless as it is, it is a way of "joining them" to do what is not right. Majority may be doing it but that doesn't make it right. Don't join them. They may be lying about their age to secure a job, inflate contracts and demand kick backs as a means of survival in this harsh economy. Don't join them. The way to eternal life has always been narrow with only few persons walking in it: *"Because strait is the gate, and narrow is the way, which leadeth unto life, and few there be that find it*", Matthew7:14. Daily, the road is getting more narrow because only the few who have refused to" join them" remains on the path. Today, God is telling you to separate from them if you can't beat them.

*PRAYER*: Father, I receive the grace to separate from them instead of joining them in Jesus name. Amen.

DEVOTIONAL 0157 | Source start line: 16821

5/25/21, 6:32 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: ProjectSave Devotional
Tuesday
May 25th,  2021.

*GOD SPEAKS THROUGH UNEXPECTED VESSELS*

Psalms 32:8 I will instruct thee and teach thee in the way which thou shalt go: I will guide thee with mine eye.


When I started writing daily devotional, I was using a blackberry bold 1 phone. It came as a gift given to me by someone. Initially, when I started using the phone, I was using it for calls, social media chats and browsing only. I didn't see it as a tool for kingdom advancement. One day, during a conversation with someone who drinks, party and is into women, he told me that I can use my phone to spread the gospel. I didn't expect such  counsel from him but I was sensitive to know God spoke through him that day. Since then till now, I've been writing daily devotionals that God has used to transform lives across the globe.

The point I'm trying to make here is that God can speak to us through unexpected vessels. Sometimes, we may be praying for divine direction on certain things, we may have fasted and done all the spiritual exercises needed, yet, He didn't speak to us the way we expected.  *God may speak to us through a child or through someone we least expect. What matters is that we should be spiritually alert&sensitive to God's Spirit at all times so that whenever He's talking to us, we'll pick  it*. To hear God speak to us even if He chooses to speak to us through a mad man we need to position ourselves like John the beloved: *"I was in the Spirit on the Lord's day, and heard behind me a great voice, as of a trumpet*", Revelation 1:10 KJV.



John heard those mysteries in the book of revelation because he was in the Spirit. *Often, we miss God  because we are in the flesh and have a preconceived notion about how we want Him to speak to us*. I've been there several times. There were days I expected God to speak audibly or with a bold inscription on the wall. Unfortunately, God didn't show up that way. Just like Elijah In 1 Kings19:11-13, when he sought to hear God, we were told that there was a wind, an earthquake and fire but God was not there. Finally, he heard God through the still small voice. That divine direction you've been praying for may come through your little child, house help and from people you least expect. Just stay alert and be sensitive so that you can recognize God when He's speaking.

*PRAYER*: Father, I receive the grace to be sensitive at all times in Jesus name.

DEVOTIONAL 0158 | Source start line: 16987

5/26/21, 5:45 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: PROJECTSAVE DEVOTIONAL
wednesday,
May 26th, 2021.

*COST OF FOLLOWING JESUS* Pt.1

Luke 9:23 Then he said to the crowd, “If any of you wants to be my follower, you must give up your own way, take up your cross daily, and follow me. NLT

*GIVING UP YOUR OWN WAY*

I read about one of the oriental religions where adherents were demanded to abstain from sex except for procreation. Couples only meet for sex when they want to make babies. They also have dietary regulations, they are vegetarians. Male devotees shave their heads, leaving only a small tuft of hair called a sikha, a sign of surrender to their teacher. Each morning male and female believers mark their foreheads with clay as a reminder that their bodies are temples of their deity. Followership in that  religion takes from them the right to decide and follow their own way. They just have to behave as demanded not as desired.

When it comes to following Jesus, it requires giving up your own way. It could be your way of thinking, behaviour and conduct. *Walking with Jesus should be as demanded not as desired*. I see a generation that want to walk with Jesus on their own terms and not on His. True disciples of Jesus are men and women who have surrendered totally to His Lordship and are willing to do his bidding, not theirs.  So many have come to know Jesus as saviour but not as a Lord. They're fascinated about the wonders of the cross and new creation realities but when new creation lifestyle is demanded, they're less concerned.

The moment you quit doing things your own way, you will not have struggle obeying God. At that level, it can be said of us that we are truly sold out to God. Our struggle with obedience is simply because there's a conflict within between God's ways and ours. The bible says, *"The sinful nature wants to do evil, which is just the opposite of what the Spirit wants. And the Spirit gives us desires that are the opposite of what the sinful nature desires. These two forces are constantly fighting each other, so you are not free to carry out your good intentions*", Galatians 5:17 NLT. Accepting Jesus is free but following Him comes at a cost. The price you and I must pay is the price up giving up our own way to follow His own. Total surrender is required.

*PRAYER*:  Lord Jesus, I give up on my ways in Jesus name



info@projectsaveng. org

DEVOTIONAL 0159 | Source start line: 17101
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
5/27/21, 6:08 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: PROJECTSAVE DEVOTIONAL
Thursday,
May 27th, 2021.



*COST OF FOLLOWING JESUS* Pt.2



Luke 9:23 Then he said to the crowd, “If any of you wants to be my follower, you must give up your own way, take up your cross daily, and follow me. NLT



*TAKE UP YOUR CROSS DAILY*




Historically, from about the 6th century BC until the 4th century AD, the cross was an instrument of execution that resulted in death by the most torturous and painful of ways. Execution by the guillotine came much later as advancement increases but the use of cross to execute criminals was an age-long Roman practice. When you mention the word "cross" to someone who lived in that era, it meant death and nothing more. It meant tortuous death. The Romans forced convicted criminals to carry their own crosses to the place of crucifixion. Bearing a cross meant carrying their own execution device while facing ridicule along the way to death.



What did Jesus mean when He said, "Take up your cross and follow Me"? It means willingness to die in order to follow Jesus. This is called “dying to self.” It’s a call to absolute surrender. In context, we know Jesus implied "death" because the proximate verse(24&25) reads: *"For whoever wants to save his life will lose it, but whoever loses his life for me will save it. What good is it for a man to gain the whole world, and yet lose or forfeit his very self?*” (Luke 9:24-25). Death to self is a state where we are not controlled by self. It is a kind of death that occurs within while we are still alive. Just as a dead man is not conscious of anything happening around him because he has no pull or attraction to the things of this world, the man that is dead to self is not conscious of the allures of this world. Paul said,  *“I have been crucified with Christ. It is no longer I who live, but Christ who lives in me. And the life I now live in the flesh I live by faith in the Son of God, who loved me and gave himself for me*” (Galatians 2:20).


*Of a truth, the man that want to follow Jesus must be ready to die. It is a tough call*. Death to self first and willingness to suffer for the cause of the kingdom even if it means losing ones life physically. This is not sweet to the ears especially in our days when we loathe "suffering" for the kingdom. If the apostles were to come back to listen to our messages, I'm sure Paul and others will cry. From inception, suffering( for Christ's sake) and death is part of this walk. In Philippians 1:29, Paul said, *"For unto you it is given in the behalf of Christ, not only to believe on him, but also to suffer for his sake*". If God sends you on an errand deemed dangerous by many, will you go? Can God tamper with your comfort to advance His kingdom knowing fully that you will suffer? Do you truly mean to follow Jesus even if it means death? Count the cost to know where you stand. *Self denial and willingness to take up ones cross on daily basis is the  identifying mark of true disciples*.




*PRAYER*: Father, I need grace in this regard. Lord help me in Jesus name.

Friday,
May 28th, 2021.

*LEARNING OBEDIENCE THROUGH SUFFERING*

Hebrews5:8 Son though he was, he learned obedience from what he suffered. NIV

One of the scriptures that I find amazing is the opening text which talks about Jesus learning obedience from what he suffered. *As the son of God in His divinity, He didn't have to learn obedience because He is God. However, in His incarnation(as a human), it was necessary for Him to learn obedience, not because He disobeyed or had rebelliousness to bring under control, but as a requirement for priesthood so that He can understand fully what humans go through*. It is only through this experience He will be able to bring comfort and help to those passing through suffering which he had experienced: *"Since he himself has gone through suffering and testing, he is able to help us when we are being tested*", Hebrews2:18.


So, I asked myself, what were the things He suffered while on earth? We are familiar with the suffering on the cross which is the ultimate but beyond that Jesus suffered from other things before the cross. It was through those things He learned obedience.  Though He is God, Jesus obeyed His parents (Luke 2:51). He could have told His earthly father to shut up because He was anointed yet He took the lowly position of a child who had to obey His father and mother(like every other child) even though He is God. Sincerely, I may find it hard to obey any human if I was in the position of Jesus because I created them and I'm from heaven while they're from beneath. *For God to come to a sinful earth is debasing and demeaning,  yet Jesus lived like humans and was still without sin*. That's not easy. Even as an adult, Jesus obeyed the law entirely (Matthew 5:17). When men accused Jesus wrongly and spoke ill about Him, He had the power to call legions of angels to deal with them yet He controlled Himself. Sincerely, I doubt if I won't prove a point if I was in His shoes.

All these experiences weren't palatable for Jesus but He learned to obey God's will through each of them. *Sometimes, God will allow us have the Jesus treatment so that we can learn obedience. If you call the shots all the time, you can't tell if you're humble and obedient not until we bring you under someone who is lesser than you in every ramification but made a leader over you*. When I was on campus, there were students who didn't attend a student fellowship because they couldn't stand a fellow student leading them. It is painful to see someone who have lesser credentials leading and ordering you around. If you're there, it is a learning phase. It is also a test. Please, don't fail. *God won't use "divine" experiences to prune us and make us. He'll always give us the Jesus treatment by bringing us to face day-to-day earthly realities through which we can learn obedience*. We pray and desire to be like Jesus but often we fail to realize that Jesus(in his incarnation) was made perfect through suffering. Anytime you pray that God should make and break you, you're praying for suffering. Whatever you're going through presently, I want you to find comfort in this: *"For we do not have a High Priest who cannot sympathize with our weaknesses, but was in all points tempted as we are, yet without sin*", Hebrew 4:15.


*PRAYER*: Father, I receive the grace to learn through suffering so that I can be made perfect in Jesus name.

Saturday,
May 29th, 2021.


*HIS SILENCE DOESN'T MEAN CONSENT*


Ecclesiastes8:11 When a crime is not punished quickly, people feel it is safe to do wrong. NLT


There's a popular mantra that says, "silence means consent". While it may be true in some cases but it is not always true when God chooses to remain silent over the sinful acts of men. Solomon is a good case study. In 1Kings3:1, it was said of him that: *"Solomon made an alliance with Pharaoh, the king of Egypt, and married one of his daughters. He brought her to live in the City of David until he could finish building his palace and the Temple of the Lord and the wall around the city*", NLT. He made an alliance with an idolatrous king and married one of his daughters in violation of God's laid down principle for marriage. In chapter 11:1-2, it was said that: *"Now King Solomon loved many foreign women. Besides Pharaoh’s daughter, he married women from Moab, Ammon, Edom, Sidon, and from among the Hittites. The Lord had clearly instructed the people of Israel, “You must not marry them, because they will turn your hearts to their gods*”.



Let's look at the outcome in verse 3-5: *"Yet Solomon insisted on loving them anyway. He had 700 wives of royal birth and 300 concubines. And in fact, they did turn his heart away from the Lord . In Solomon’s old age, they turned his heart to worship other gods instead of being completely faithful to the Lord his God, as his father, David, had been. Solomon worshiped Ashtoreth, the goddess of the Sidonians, and Molech, the detestable god of the Ammonites*". There was a wide gap between 1Kings 3 and chapter 11. So many notable and divine events took place. After his marriage to a strange woman, he asked for wisdom and God gave it to him, he built the temple and brought the ark into the temple, built a palace and had a divine encounter in his dream.



What surprised me was that while he was doing all these exploits for the Lord, he was marrying strange women and having concubines( as against God's will) yet God was silent. In chapter 9, I thought God will point out this sin specifically but God spoke to him on other issues. He may have thought that the silence of God meant consent but he was wrong. The outcome was disastrous, vs 3-5.  *There are instructions God may not repeat twice. The moment He sends warning a couple of times without seeing repentance,  He may not repeat it again*. A man may be living in sin yet doing exploits for the Lord. *The deceit of seeing miraculous manifestations while living in sin is the reason some believers trivialize sin*. A polygamous and adulterous prophet told some persons that If God was angry with His polygamy, why would he still be operating in accurate prophetic gifts? The operations of gifts deceived him to thinking that God was in support of his polygamous lifestyle. Dearest, before God becomes silent, it is consistent with  His nature to send prior warning. When warning is not heeded, He may become silent. However, His silence doesn't mean consent.




*PRAYER*: Father search my heart to see if there be any evil way. Do not be silent over my life. Point my failings to me and help me by the power of your Spirit to amend my ways in Jesus name.

5/30/21, 12:14 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: ProjectSave Devotional
Sunday,
May 30th, 2021.

*GENESIS 28:16 SYNDROME*


Genesis28:16 Then Jacob awoke from his sleep and said, “Surely the LORD is in this place, and I wasn’t even aware of it!”  NLT

There's a story about Jacob's dream in Bethel( formerly called Luz) in Genesis 28. He saw a stairway leading to heaven with angels of God ascending and descending. God arranged that encounter and He made trans generational promises to him. That encounter was a meeting with destiny. Unfortunately, Jacob was not sensitive to know that God had encountered him to institute a generational blessing. How did I know that Jacob didn't know he met God? In the latter part of the opening text, he said, *"Surely the LORD is in this place, and I wasn't even aware of it*". That's something worth considering. You may think what he said was a humble statement but it is not. He meant exactly what he said.


Jacob didn't know/ understand the weight of what God had promised him in verses 13-15. If he had understood the details of that encounter, he wouldn't have made these statements in verses 20-12: *"Then Jacob made this vow: “If God will indeed be with me and protect me on this journey, and if he will provide me with food and clothing, and if I return safely to my father’s home, then the Lord will certainly be my God*". How can he say such after all that God told him?  He said, *"If God will indeed be with me*", whereas God already told him in verse 15, *"What’s more, I am with you, and I will protect you wherever you go. One day I will bring you back to this land. I will not leave you until I have finished giving you everything I have promised you*". All these shows that Jacob missed out on something in that encounter. Painfully, He didn't know God was where he was. Tacitly, Jacob was begging God for provision whereas God promised him a nation. Will God promise to give a man a nation without making plans on how to feed him?



*It is a terrible thing to be in a place where God is and not be sensitive to know God is there*. The resultant effect will  always be like that of Jacob's. God will speak, make the future clear and give promises yet the man God is speaking to may not understand. *Insensitivity to God's presence and move is the major reason why people miss God*. Jacob thought Luz was just a destination in his sojourn. The place may not have any aura of the supernatural yet that was God's appointed place of destiny encounter for him. This often happens in church circle. I have seen how people miss God because they feel God will speak to them through a "great servant of God" whereas, He has ordained an encounter for them in their present church whose Pastor may not be deemed "great" by men.  Some may even change their church to the happening church in town thinking they'll find Him in popular places.  *Sometimes, when God is coming for you, He may come unannounced and casually. The place and the person bringing His word to you may not fit into your ideal image of a carrier of God's word*.  At all times and wherever we find ourselves, we must stay sensitive to be able to recognize God.


*PRAYER*: Father, may I not miss you in Jesus name.

5/31/21, 6:33 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: PROJECTSAVE DEVOTIONAL

May 31st,  2021.

*MISSING OUT ON THE MIRACULOUS*

Mark6:4 Jesus said to them, "A prophet is not without honor except in his own town, among his relatives and in his own home."

*THE TRAP OF FAMILIARITY*

A man of God told the story of a man who wanted to see him but was told he was busy. This visitor visited his office on a day he fixed for long prayer. However, the visitor insisted on waiting till he was done praying. After he ended his prayers, this visitor was privileged to meet him. He asked to know what he wanted. The visitor said all he wanted was just a handskake. The man of God was surprised that someone who waited several hours to see him wanted handshake and nothing more. He shook hands with him and he left. His faith connected to the flow of God's anointing on this man of God for the miraculous. This preacher said shaking hands with his associates is a daily thing but they didn't take it serious like the visitor did because of familiarity.

I've observed that those who are close to ministers of the gospel( friends, neighbours, families& old timers in church) and even associates in ministry  rarely receive miracles through the ministrations of the minister unlike new converts and new members. This is simply because of familiarity. They see the minister as their colleague, son or brother not as a man sent from God. This is the same mistake the Jews made when Jesus came the first time. In Mark6:3, they said, *"Isn't this the carpenter? Isn't this Mary's son and the brother of James, Joseph, Judas and Simon? Aren't his sisters here with us?" And they took offense at him*". They were offended because of how well they knew him. They saw him as their "son or brother" instead of seeing him as a man sent from God. In verse 6, it was reported that, *"And because of their unbelief, he couldn’t do any miracles among them except to place his hands on a few sick people and heal them*".

Do you truly want to experience the miraculous? Stop seeing that minister from the natural standpoint of who he is or has been to you. The Jews in Jesus' hometown didn't honour Jesus as God sent thereby missing out on the miraculous. You may even know the imperfections of that minister, that should not cause you to look away from God's ability through him to reach out to you in areas of need. Often, the miracles we need can be ministered to us through those closest to us but we often don't receive them because of familiarity.


*ACTION POINT*:  If that servant of God is your friend or acquaintance, start seeing him differently. That's how the grace of God for the miraculous upon his life can flow to you.

*IT IS NOT WORTH IT*

2 Cor6: 14-15 *Be ye not unequally yoked together with unbelievers: for what fellowship hath righteousness with unrighteousness? and what communion hath light with darkness?  And what concord hath Christ with Belial? or what part hath he that believeth with an infidel*?


After teaching singles about marriage in a youth conference years back, a lady asked to know if she can marry an unbeliever who is caring and loving. She told me that the christian brother she loved was not forthcoming as regards marriage proposal but another guy who is not born again was pestering her for marriage and he appears caring and loving. I told her to perish the idea because what she was contemplating is against the laid down principles of the scriptures.


There are so many singles in the body of Christ today who doesn't care about the faith their suitor practices. I'm seeing a growing trend of "love is all that matters". Well, as far as God is concerned as regards marriage, salvation precedes marital "love". Besides, no unbeliever can love you with the love of Christ: *"Husbands, love your wives, even as Christ also loved the church, and gave himself for it;  That he might sanctify and cleanse it with the washing of water by the word*", Ephesians 5:25-26 KJV. Get this clear, no unbeliever have the capacity to love you as Christ loved the church. That love is a cleansing and sanctifying love.

Right from under the old covenant, God forbade His children from forming a marriage alliance with the ungodly and those that weren't under the covenant. Solomon tried it and his love for God became cold, 1 Kings11:1-6. Don't let the devil tell you that you'll convert and marry him. You are not a converter, that's the work of the Holy Spirit. If an unbeliever promises to convert because of marriage, it is not because he is convinced about marriage but because of what he want. Don't be deceived about what looks like "love" that is not real love. The devil may point your attention to couples of mixed faith that are doing fine. What you see outwardly may not be a true reflection of what is happening in their homes. There may be silent regrets and pains unknown to you. Don't be deceived.

*PRAYER*: Father, I will not fall into the marital trap of the enemy in Jesus name.

6/2/21, 6:18 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: PROJECTSAVE DEVOTIONAL
wednesday,
June 2nd, 2021.

*DON'T JUMP THE GUN*

Ecclesiastes3:1 For everything there is a season, a time for every activity under heaven. NLT


God told a man about His assignment in one of the African countries. He was excited about what God told him so he packed his bags and left. He laboured fruitlessly for several years. He returned home discouraged. He asked God why all his efforts were fruitless. God told him he went before time. Later, this man returned to the same country at the right time. His ministry flourished and later, he established one of the most reputable bible schools in that country.

To jump the gun is to take action before the appropriate timing. The expression was borrowed from track event. Before sprinters run, they must hear the gun before running. Anyone who runs before the gun is shot is said to jump the gun. When that is done repeatedly by one athlete, he/she may be disqualified from the race. *God works with time to fulfill His purposes and plans on earth*. Sometimes, He gives us an idea or clue about what lies ahead but that doesn't necessarily mean it is time to act. God will tell you certain things ahead of time so that you can prepare but not necessarily for action. *Good dreams often die on the altar of running ahead of time*.

As important as the salvation of mankind is, God didn't send Christ before time, He was sent to come and die at the right time: *"But when the right time came, God sent his Son, born of a woman, subject to the law*", Galatians 4:4,NLT. If Christ had come before the appropriate time, we may not be here today. In God's programme, timing is crucial. All you read in the bible happened to time. He has a clock. God doesn't act before time, He acts right on time. *Whenever you pray and God speaks to you about His plans, learn to ask further about the issue of timing for execution of His plans*.


*PRAYER*:  I receive the grace to wait for the appropriate timing in all I do in Jesus name.

*IT IS NOT A BLACK MARKET*

Romans8:14 For all who are led by the Spirit of God are children of God. NLT

One unbiblical stereotypes about marriage I've heard is when some persons refer to making marital choice as buying a commodity from a "black market". A market where the quality of products are unpredictable and uncertain. Those with this mentality believe a good spouse is gotten by "luck" not by principles or by the Spirit.  I have heard Christians and ministers of the gospel make this unbiblical statement. The reason why they say this is because of the several bitter experiences some persons have had especially those who experienced a sudden change in the character of whom they married.


*In the scheme of God's dealings with man on marriage, luck does not have a place. Marriage is a choice we have to make prayerfully and watchfully based on principles*. When people complain that their spouses turn from good to worse after marriage and begin to exhibit funny characters that were unknown to them in courtship, I always disagree with that. The reason is because it is either they didn't observe or watchout for shades of character and habits that may be inimical to the health of their marriage while in courtship or they saw it but choose to turn a blind eye to it assuming that a change will occur in marriage. *God will never lead us into marriage with poisonous spouse. Who we marry is a product of our choice whether good/bad. That's why it is good to make a choice based on information not by emotion*. For example, a control freak, disrespectful and domineering person cannot pretend for long and will never make a good spouse. You can know this by watching how he/she treats others. *A single brother/sister who doesn't respect elders and parents will not cherish and respect his/her spouse in marriage even if married to an angel*.


You don't need a prophet to tell you that a hot-tempered man is a potential wife beater. While he may not do this in courtship because of "love", but by merely observing how he relates with others, you will know. How people relate with others starting from their family, to their co workers in the office and neighbours tells a lot about them.  A man that cannot wait for sex before marriage is a potential candidate for extra marital affairs in marriage because he lacks self control. Even after marriage, he'll need that self control to keep himself because sexual temptation never stops. Now, a sister may be seeing a brother like that and may assume he'll change once they start having sex in marriage. It doesn't work that way. *Failure to discern the core values and life principles by which a person operates is what leads to shocking surprises later on*. My counsel to you is that you should make your choice by the leading of the Spirit and by principle not by emotion. Don't pamper or ignore habits, attitudes, character or pattern of thinking in a person that may pose threat to your marital joy in the future.


*PRAYER*: Father, I refuse to fall into the marital trap the enemy set for me in Jesus name.

*THE RIGHT PRAYER POSTURE*. Pt.1


Luke 18:14 I tell you, this sinner, not the Pharisee, returned home justified before God. For those who exalt themselves will be humbled, and those who humble themselves will be exalted.” NLT

*THE POSTURE OF HUMILITY*


In Luke 18 from verse 10, Jesus told the story of a Pharisee and a tax collector who went to the temple to pray. The tax collector went into prayer with a penitent heart. He knew he was unworthy by human credentials and even by his deeds. However, he pleaded for mercy: *"But the tax collector stood at a distance and dared not even lift his eyes to heaven as he prayed. Instead, he beat his chest in sorrow, saying, ‘O God, be merciful to me, for I am a sinner*", vs 13. Unlike the Pharisee whom everyone knew to be a religious leader and custodian of the law of Moses. The Pharisee went into prayer with a heart full of spiritual pride. Read the content of his prayer: *"...‘I thank you, God, that I am not like other people—cheaters, sinners, adulterers. I’m certainly not like that tax collector!*",vs 11.


Jesus said that it was the tax collector that went home justified. Obviously, if he had other requests, God answered as well because the posture of his heart in prayer was right. *One mistake some believers make in the place of prayer is that they take on the wrong posture in their heart*. They pray with this mentality of "I'm better than brother 'X' who doesn't pay tithe and sow seed, Sister "B" doesn't evangelize, Lord, why should you bless her and not bless me"? A sister who married as a virgin may tell God 101 reasons why she should conceive immediately after marriage. There are ladies who married as virgins but are still trusting God for the fruit of the womb yet there are serial abortionists who made babies like pigs after marriage. That's not to say we're supporting immorality but I want you to know that God's mercy makes the difference in life.


*In the place of prayer, you don't brag about your credentials to God as the basis for which He must answer you. Can your sacrifice be greater than that of Christ? We receive answer to prayers because of the righteousness of Christ not ours*. Unfortunately, I see a trend in the body of Christ where people engage in service for answer to their prayers or for a turn around. Those with this mentality work in the house of God, pay tithe and evangelize so that they can have a basis for pride in the place of prayer. However, when their expectations aren't met, they become bitter about God. *The best posture for kingdom service is the posture of love*. I do all I'm doing in God's vineyard because I love God not because of what I want to get from Him. *When next you go to God in prayers, let your focus be on the finished work of Christ not in your ability or righteousness*. That's how to get answers to prayers.


*ACTION POINT*: Adjust the posture of your heart.

*THE RIGHT PRAYER POSTURE*. Pt.2


Mark 11: 25 But when you are praying, first forgive anyone you are holding a grudge against, so that your Father in heaven will forgive your sins, too. ” NLT

*THE POSTURE OF FORGIVENESS*


A man of God had a vision. In that vision, what he saw troubled him. Top leaders in his church were pregnant in the vision. He was disturbed because men don't get pregnant while some of the women had past child bearing age. God told him that the pregnancy was symbolic of unforgiveness. Most of them had grouse with one another and they have failed to forgive. I can't imagine how they have been praying with heavy hearts against one another.


What some believers don't know is that unforgiveness can hinder our prayers. *Praying whilst holding grudges makes prayer exercise a waste*. The devil knows that your prayer is powerful because the bible said so. He tries everything within his power to make your prayer of no effect. He makes you bitter about those that may have offended you because he wants you to take on the posture of unforgiveness. Bitterness produces unforgiveness, that's the root. That's why the bible says: *"Look after each other so that none of you fails to receive the grace of God. Watch out that no poisonous root of bitterness grows up to trouble you, corrupting many*", Hebrews 12:15,NLT.



*The poison bitterness spills into our spiritual bloodstream is unforgiveness*. God didn't promise us that we won't be offended. Almost on daily basis, there'll be something or someone that will offend you wanting you to slip into bitterness and ultimately into a posture of unforgiveness. Jesus confirmed this by saying: *"Woe to the world because of offenses! For offenses must come, but woe to that man by whom the offense comes!*", Matthew18:7. However, because God's love over us didn't hold our offenses against us, he expects us to extend the same unto others even when they don't deserve it. *Instead, be kind to each other, tenderhearted, forgiving one another, just as God through Christ has forgiven you*, Ephesians 4:32 NLT.


*PRAYER*: Father, I forgive(...mention name) in Jesus name.

*THE RIGHT PRAYER POSTURE*. Pt.3


Mark 11: 24 I tell you, you can pray for anything, and if you believe that you’ve received it, it will be yours.


*THE POSTURE OF CHILD-LIKE FAITH*


When I was growing up, myself and my younger sister had a unique faith that made us believe God will answer our
prayer if we ask for anything. Often, we exercised our faith any time the power authority interrupted power supply. Both of us would go on our knees to pray until power was restored. I can't remember if our faith failed at anytime, everyone in the house counted on us because our faith worked. We care less to know if the interruption in power was due to human inefficiency, systemic rot or bad power supply equipment. All our faith wanted was 'power' so that we can watch t.v. It was when we reached adulthood we discovered that the problem of epileptic power supply wasn't demonic but purely human. However, God honoured our faith.

DEVOTIONAL 0170 | Source start line: 17793

6/7/21, 6:34 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: PROJECTSAVE DEVOTIONAL
Monday,
June 7th,  2021.

*TWO IMPORTANT C's* Pt.1

Psalms78:72 So he fed them according to the integrity of his heart; and guided them by the skilfulness of his hands.

*CHARACTER*

Any time I study the bible, especially the life of Joseph and Samson, I'm forced to always compare these two bible characters. The divine mandate both of them had was earthshaking. Both of them had leadership entrusted to them divinely. While one received his mandate in a vision or dream, the angel of God declared the future of the latter before he was born. The future was declared but destiny was not assured until character was proven. In the course of time, one built strength of character and was able to reach the top and sustained it while the other made a mess of God's assignment for his life because of bad character. Both of them were faced with the same type of temptation, one succeeded while the other failed.

*There's a popular saying that skill can take a man to the top but it takes character to sustain him there*. In life, two C's are crucial in achieving greatness and sustaining it. The first is character. A man may be anointed like Samson, gifted like Bezaleel, powerful in speech like Winston Churchill, rugged like Paul and skillful like Diego Maradona, if he lacks strength of character, he'll make a mess of greatness. Ever wonder why some men fail when they get to the top?  Character deficiency is it. *What brought some men down when they got to the top journeyed with them to the top*.


*A man defined character as who you are when no one is watching*. You need to pay attention to your character. In your quest for greatness, spot those things that shouldn't go with you to the top. What about anti marriage characters? Spot them now and trust the Holy Spirit to help you do away with them before they destroy your marriage. The writer of Hebrews says, *"Therefore, since we are surrounded by such a huge crowd of witnesses to the life of faith, let us strip off every weight that slows us down, especially the sin that so easily trips us up. And let us run with endurance the race God has set before us*",Hebrews 12:1 NLT. If you don't want to be a casualty of moral deficiency, start talking to God about those sins that easily beset you. As you fellowship with Him daily and surrender to His Lordship, you'll be refined. *The future maybe declared divinely but landing safely in destiny and sustaining it is not assured until character is proven*.

*PRAYER*: Father, I receive the grace to develop strength of character in Jesus name

DEVOTIONAL 0171 | Source start line: 17869

6/8/21, 6:57 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: ProjectSave Devotional
Tuesday
June 8th,  2021.

*TWO IMPORTANT C's* Pt.2

Psalms78:72 So he fed them according to the integrity of his heart; and guided them by the skilfulness of his hands.

*COMPETENCE*

When I wanted to enrol in the seminary, I needed a refree to sign my form. I decided to approach a senior Pastor  to stand for me as my refree. When he saw my form, he was surprised that I wanted to enrol in the seminary. He asked if i needed to enrol since I've been preaching and doing ministry well. To him, I was a good preacher.  I can't remember my response exactly but within me, I knew I needed to build competence&capacity through learning to make full proof of my calling. In addition to developing character, building competence is very crucial.

*Competence is built through continuous learning*. Apostle Paul, though he was anointed yet he never stopped building ministerial competence through study:  *"When you come, be sure to bring the coat I left with Carpus at Troas. Also bring my books, and especially my papers*",2Tim4:13, NLT. Hardly can we preach without referring to one Pauline epistle. The way and manner some preachers mention "bro Paul" made me realize he was no push over.  Paul is still preaching on our pulpits today though long dead. In his days, he preached to the stoics&Epicureans philosophers( Acts18:17). Stoicism was a philosophical school of thought in his day founded by Zeno of Citium. It is only a scholar, a well read man that could have done that because the Stoics&Epicureans were one of the intelligentsias of that time. A careful study of new testament Greek shows that Paul's usage of the language was different from others because of his scholarship, little wonder why he stood before great men in his days.  If he had remained a mediocre, he would have placed a limit on the spread of his ministry.

*As you build character please build competence through continuous learning. It is a door opener*. Yours may not be ministry, your destiny alignment may be in the field of academics, medicine, engineering, music, commerce and so on. Do not be satisfied with that level of competence you've attained, you can build more. God hates mediocrity. *Always aim to be a better version of yourself daily*. You may be anointed to the overflowing but there are doors, places and platforms that requires competence to stand on. Don't be lazy, keep building competence in your chosen field.

*PRAYER*: Father, I receive the grace to build competence in Jesus name.

6/9/21, 6:23 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: PROJECTSAVE DEVOTIONAL
wednesday,
June 9th, 2021.

*HOW TO KILL LOVE IN MARRIAGE*

Proverbs 17:9 Whoever would foster love covers over an offense, but whoever repeats the matter separates close friends.

A couple had a very stormy marriage. The wife always found fault with her husband and nothing satisfied her about him. Both of them decided to visit a counselor to resolve their issues. The counselor told them to make a box for keeping records of offenses. Anytime each of them offended, the counselor instructed them to write the nature of offense, time and date on a paper and drop it inside the box. This continued for a specified period of time. Later, the box was opened. The wife's box was full of complaints while the box of the man contained only one piece of paper saying, "I love you". The man chose not to dwell on her nagging, faults and fault finding attitude but the wife was a trophy winning fault finder. This singular action made her realize that love only prospers when couples don't dwell on each other's faults.

*I have discovered how to kill love in marriage. The easiest way to sap the joy, strength, love  and kill the love in marriage is to always dwell on offenses, pile them up and never forgive*. As you focus on the imperfections of your spouse daily, it magnifies till it injects hatred into the bloodstream of your marriage. *We often fail to realize that we're not married to angels. It would be very unrealistic to think that someone with flesh and blood won't offend us*. Love only prospers when we dwell more on the good side of our spouse. Stop trying to change what the Holy Spirit alone can change. If you do, you'll be frustrated always because you'll fail. Commit what you can't change to the ultimate changer- the Holy Spirit.


Look at the strength of your spouse, those things that makes him/her standout. If you look for fault always, you'll find one. Fault finding is the reason some concluded "all women/ men are the same". Fault finding and failure to forgive is what often leads to the phrase "irreconcilable differences". Some marriages would have survived their storm if the couples had followed this biblical counsel. In fact, if you want to help your spouse out of his/her love killing habits, focus on his/ her area of strength, give all your energy to it and create an environment where virtues and strength thrive, unconsciously, those things that get you upset may find the exit door. Constant fault finding changes no one. Wise husbands&wives who don't want to die before their time must learn to look away from certain things. Don't die before your time by constant fault finding and piling up offenses.  *"Hatred stirs up strife but LOVE COVERS ALL SINS*" (Proverbs 10:12).

*PRAYER*: Father, I receive grace to stay in love and never to kill it in Jesus name.

*CONSIDER THIS CHURCH*


Revelations 3:1-2 *“Write this letter to the angel of the church in Sardis. This is the message from the one who has the sevenfold Spirit of God and the seven stars: “I know all the things you do, and that you have a reputation for being alive—but you are dead.  Wake up! Strengthen what little remains, for even what is left is almost dead. I find that your actions do not meet the requirements of my God*.



As I studied my bible one night, God opened my eyes to the ills of the church in Sardis which is similar to the present day church. I have been reading this chapter for years but the meaning became clearer as God ministered to me from that portion of scriptures personally. The church( referring to the believers) in Sardis was one of the seven churches in Asia minor. This location of this church can be found in present day Turkey, village of Sart, near Salihli in  Manisa province. It is not a fictitious church as some may think. However, what was it that Jesus said about this church that is worth considering?


Jesus said, *"they had a reputation for being alive but there were dead*". That struck me. In the eyes of the people living in that day, that church was reputed to be alive probably due to the various activities and programs they saw them do. They had a branded social image that made people point to them as "living church" or say "church on fire" yet Jesus said they were dead. If it were to be in our day, we may adjudge that kind of church as a living church because we see their heavy presence on social media showing us their 'exploits' in miracles, programs, powerful short instagram videos, outreaches, large following on twitter and other activities. Their public appeal through branding may also be another reason we quickly assume they're alive but in all this, the stench of their decay reaches to the heavens.


It is possible to be full of 'church/ ministry activities' and yet be adjudged as "dead". Why? Jesus gave the answer in verse 2 saying, *"I find that your actions do not meet the requirements of my God*". When the actions/ lifestyle of a believer doesn't matchup to the standard of God, even if he's busy with the Lord's work jumping here and there, Jesus is seeing a dead man. *The deception of this age is creating a public spiritual reputation either through social media or other means when quality walk with God is long dead*. So many carcasses are preaching, pastoring, singing & holding the microphone today but covering it with good suit, shirt, tie, good grammar but within, there's a stench in their secret life. This is the counsel of Jesus: *"Go back to what you heard and believed at first; hold to it firmly. Repent and turn to me again...*" vs 3.


*PRAYER*: Father, I receive life and vitality in my walk with you in Jesus name.

6/11/21, 7:10 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: PROJECTSAVE DEVOTIONAL
Friday,
June 11th, 2021.

*SUBTLE BACKSLIDING: BEWARE*

Revelation 2:4 “But I have this complaint against you. You don’t love me or each other as you did at first! NLT


One day, I was preparing to take my bath when the Spirit of God ministered to me about the subject of 'backsliding'. The Spirit of God made me realize that we often conclude a believer has backslide when we observe actions like: living in sin openly, erratic church attendance, and so on. However, those are the terminal signs of backsliding. He made me realize that there's a subtle sign of backsliding that we often don't notice in our lives even as preachers and believers that are going to church regularly, speaking in tongues and full of church activities. That subtle sign of backsliding is when our priorities change from spiritual to self. When we prioritize the things that please us above what please God, that is a subtle but dangerous sign of backsliding. Certain things that are pleasing to us may not be outrightly sinful but they may not be in God's will either.


It is possible to be committed in church and punctual in all services because you want to be in the good books of the pastor. You don't want the visitation team to come checking on you because of your absence. You no longer attend church because you want to fellowship with God and the brethren. Before, you evangelize on your own without telling anyone but now, you prefer to pray or give money( though not bad) to support those preaching while you close your mouth without telling sinners about Jesus. You assume your money and prayers will get the job done for you forgetting that the great commission is a task for everyone. You changed your priority from preaching the gospel to giving and praying for those who are preaching the gospel whereas you are suppose to be preaching the gospel while not neglecting other things( prayer&support). That's subtle backsliding.


Before you became a pastor, you had time for sound fellowship with God but now, irrelevant meetings, invitations, counseling and "church office work" is your priority. You no longer study the bible for your own spiritual growth but you do because you're looking for what to preach. You are committed to preaching nice sermons of homiletical exactitude but no commitment to personal spiritual growth. This is a case of being committed to the ministry more than the one who called us into the ministry. Very dangerous. It is just a matter of time, you'll burn out spiritually and stand in front of God's people as a performer. When believers backslide this way, it is not noticeable because they're full of activities and performance but less of life. It is even worse when pastors mandate members to evangelize whereas they just sit back in church office attending to other things.  Check your priorities once again. The moment we don't prioritize the very things that matter to God and we place them at the lower rung of the ladder for what pleases self, it is a sure sign of backsliding. Jesus is saying to you and me: *"Look how far you have fallen! Turn back to me and do the works you did at first...*"Revelation 2:5 NLT.


*PRAYER*: Father, restore me.

6/12/21, 9:22 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: ProjectSave Devotional
