June 12th, 2021.

*GRACE TEACHES*

Titus 2:11-12 For the grace of God that bringeth salvation hath appeared to all men,  Teaching us that, denying ungodliness and worldly lusts, we should live soberly, righteously, and godly, in this present world;

Our understanding about the subject of grace is central to the quality of our walk with God. Without grace, we cannot be saved: *"For by grace are ye saved through faith; and that not of yourselves: it is the gift of God*",  Ephesians 2:8 KJV. We didn't do anything to be saved other than to believe in the
sacrifice of Christ and the work of His vicarious substitution. Following rules and living by the Mosaic laws couldn't save man. All of man's effort to be saved cannot earn him salvation so God in His wisdom decided to offer eternal salvation to mankind through grace.



From time immemorial, some have taken the position of "grace cover anything I do even if it is displeases God". They believe that a believer doesn't have to strive to live a life pleasing to God. Apostle Paul knew this and he said: *"What shall we say then? Shall we continue in sin, that grace may abound*"?Romans 6:1 KJV. He asked a question and this is his response in verse 2: *"God forbid. How shall we, that are dead to sin, live any longer therein*"? When the subject of grace is misunderstood, it may lead to wantonness and wayward lifestyle: *"For there are certain men crept in unawares, who were before of old ordained to this condemnation, ungodly men, turning the grace of our God into lasciviousness, and denying the only Lord God, and our Lord Jesus Christ*", Jude 1:4 KJV.




So, after a believer have been saved by grace, what will grace do thereafter? Titus 2:11-12 says that grace will teach us to deny ungodliness,worldly lusts, sobriety, righteous living and godliness in this present world. The grace of God is a teaching grace and we can be sure that it will teach us to live like Christ. *The grace that saves that doesn't teach the saved godly living is not the grace of God*.


*PRAYER*: Father, I receive the grace to be taught your ways so that my life can be pleasing to you in Jesus name. Amen

*PREACH NOW*


2 Tim4:2 Preach the Word; be prepared in season and out of season; correct, rebuke and encourage--with great patience and careful instruction.


In my 300L in the university, I was led to preach in the front of one of the blocks in my hostel. I told my room mate who was an executive in the fellowship to preach in the frontage of another block in thesame hostel. I preached under a special anointing that morning warning my hearers about the coming judgement and appealing to them to give their lives to Christ. It was still dark in the morning and one may think no one heard but I didn't know some guys were hearing me loud and clear.  When it was day break, I was lying on my bed while my room mate was pressing his clothes, we heard footsteps and voice of angry boys coming towards our hostel. They entered our room demanding to know who preached in front of their hostel.

They almost injured my room mate with the hot iron he was using if not for my intervention. I told the irate boy that I was the one who preached in front of their hostel. Their ring leader sat me down talking angrily. He told he was a brother to the wife of a Bishop and how was formerly involved in church activities and so on. So need to preach to him. He warned me not to preach in their frontage again. I perceived two things from our conversation: (1) He was the reason God stirred me to preach that morning,(2) The message touched him as it was descriptive of his condition which I knew nothing about prior to the time of my preaching.  Of course, they were cultists. Some of their sympathizers also told me and my room mate to soft pedal on our evangelistic activities. After four months, that man died. I was told that he was trapped in a burning building and he lost his life. I felt sad. Very sad.


It dawned on me that my message to him four months before was one of the last warning from God to him to amend his ways and give his life to Christ. Unfortunately, from the little I gathered around, he died without Christ. What if I didn't preach to him? I may live with the guilt of my inaction for years. Do not be ashamed/afraid to preach the gospel especially when there's a burden on your heart. Your listener may have one more day to live. If you fail to preach the truth to them, you may be sending the person to eternity without Christ. Even if your listeners won't accept Christ, let it be on record that you played your part in sharing the love of Christ with them. When they stand before God, it will be on record that they heard the gospel for a witness, Mt24:14. Preach now.


*PRAYER*: Father, I receive the grace to preach the word in season and out of season in Jesus name.

*DON'T DESPISE HYMNS- STORIES OF POPULAR HYMNS* Pt.1


Eph5:19 speaking to one another in psalms and hymns and spiritual songs, singing and making melody with your heart to the Lord;

*WHEREVER HE LEADS, I'LL GO*.

R.S. Jones was a missionary to Brazil. He came home on furlough and he attended a Sunday school conference in Alabama,  in the year 1936. During lunch with a hymn writer, B.B. McKinney, at the conference, he told the hymn writer about his health challenges that may prevent him from returning to Brazil, the country he had grown to love. The hymn writer asked, “What will you do?” And through tears, the missionary, R.S. Jones, told the hymn writer, B.B. McKinney, “Wherever He leads, I’ll go.” B.B. McKinney was so moved that he penned the classic hymn that afternoon and sang it that night after Jones had preached, recounts Terry C. Terry, a musicologist who wrote his doctoral dissertation about McKinney. This hymn has been one of the popular hymns rendered on different occasions.




There are are lessons to learn from the stories that birth most of the hymns we sing today. Spiritual men wrote most of these hymns out of a life that is devoted to God. Our generation often despise hymns because we do not know the power in singing hymns especially the hymns that are bible-based, cross centred and devotional. We often see hymns as outdated and unnecessary in our liturgical proceedings. This is an aspect of orthodoxy and apostolic tradition that we must not jettison. Even Jesus Christ sang a hymn with His disciples in preparation for crucifixion: *"When they had sung a hymn, they went out to the Mount of Olives*", Mt26:30. It is an apostolic tradition to admonish one another with the lyrics of hymns: *'Let the word of Christ richly dwell within you, with all wisdom teaching and admonishing one another with psalms and hymns and spiritual songs, singing with thankfulness in your hearts to God*", Col 3:16. We can praise God with hymns like Paul and Silas did: *"But about midnight Paul and Silas were praying and singing hymns of praise to God, and the prisoners were listening to them*",Acts 16:25.



*PRAYER*: Father, I'll follow you till I see you in glory in Jesus name.

DEVOTIONAL 0178 | Source start line: 18319

6/15/21, 5:33 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: ProjectSave Devotional
Tuesday
June 15th,  2021.

*DON'T DESPISE HYMNS- STORIES OF POPULAR HYMNS* Pt.2


John 15: 13-14 There is no greater love than to lay down one’s life for one’s friends.  You are my friends if you do what I command.

*WHAT A FRIEND WE HAVE IN JESUS*

The writer of this popular hymn, Joseph Scriven, was set to marry his fiancée in 1844 after completing his college education.  As he was traveling to meet her on the day before the planned wedding, he came upon a horrible scene—his beautiful fiancée tragically lying under the water in a creek bed after falling off her horse.Later, Scriven relocated to Canada and eventually fell in love again. However, his fiancée died just weeks before their marriage. For the second time, this humble Christian felt the loss of the woman he loved. The following year, he wrote a poem to his mother in Ireland that described the deep friendship with Jesus he had cultivated in prayer through the hardships of his life.

The first publication of the poem had the title, “Pray Without Ceasing.” In 1868, attorney Charles Converse set the text to a tune and renamed it “What a Friend We Have in Jesus.” This humble believer didn't curse God neither did he ask Him foolish questions. In pains and hardship, he discovered the friendship of God through prayer. The song is reflective of the depth of fellowship with God in prayers during the time of hardship.

God didn't promise us a life without challenges. In John 16:33: *"I have told you all this so that you may have peace in me. Here on earth you will have many trials and sorrows. But take heart, because I have overcome the world*.” John 16:33 NLT. Whatever you might be going through, I want you to see Jesus as a friend that can be trusted, a friend you can share with and lay your burdens on. He is not someone to fight or run from. Those challenges should make you launch into depths and dimensions of fellowship with God in the place of prayer. The reality of Christ friendship would dawn on you if you stay in fellowship in times of pains. Don't forget, trouble days don't last always.

*PRAYER*: Jesus, thank you for your friendship. This is what I'm going through right now( ...mention the case), heal my hurting heart and show me the way out of this trouble and confusion in Jesus name.

DEVOTIONAL 0179 | Source start line: 18372
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
6/16/21, 6:20 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: PROJECTSAVE DEVOTIONAL
wednesday,
June 16th, 2021.

*DON'T DESPISE HYMNS- STORIES OF POPULAR HYMNS* Pt.3

2Cor 4:17 For our present troubles are small and won’t last very long. Yet they produce for us a glory that vastly outweighs them and will last forever! NLT

*IT IS WELL WITH MY SOUL*

Horatio Spafford was a successful attorney. He had a wonderful family of four daughters. Tragically,  most of his business were destroyed in 1871 Chicago fire. Two years later, his wife and daughters were aboard the ocean liner that had an accident. All four daughters drowned but his wife survived and nine days later was able to contact her husband by telegraph with this question: “Saved alone—what shall I do?” Spafford took the next available ship to join his wife. During the trip,  the captain of the ship notified Spafford they were crossing the place where the ship his wife and daughters had sunk.


After those moments of reflection and over the course of the rest of the journey, Spafford penned the words of this beloved hymn. May God teach us that “whatever our lot” we can still say, “It is well.” Mike Harland says,  *"God’s grace enables his child to trust the heart of the Father. Life will confront us too. The songs we sing in the darkest of midnight will be the very songs that show the world the unwavering faithfulness of our Father who loves us so much.The darker it gets, the more we should sing.*"


If you were in Horatio Spafford's shoes, will you write a song or curse God? *The strength of our faith in God can only be known in times of trials not in good times.* Don't let the devil steal your song. Be encouraged by the second stanza of this loved hymn: *"Though Satan should buffet, though trials should come, Let this blest assurance control, That Christ hath regarded my helpless estate, And hath shed His own blood for my soul.*"


*PRAYER*: God of all comfort, comfort me on all sides, heal my hurting heart and help me to sing the songs of heaven in Jesus name

*MUSINGS FROM PSALM103:10*


Psalm103:10 he does not treat us as our sins deserve or repay us according to our iniquities. NIV


Recently, I was in a gathering where we all read Psalm 103 in worship. This is one of the Psalms that talks about the wonderous love and mercy of God. As we were reading this Psalm in worship, verse 10 struck my heart and I pondered on it. I see from that verse the depth of God's mercy over us as believers and mankind by extension. It reads: *"he does not treat us as our sins deserve or repay us according to our iniquities*".


This verse of the scripture helps bring a balance to certain misconceptions. Some are of the opinion that every misfortune or challenge a believer encounter is because of sin. Then I ask, if life's challenges comes because of our sins, what was the sin of Job&Jesus? People who hold this unbalanced view are never soothing in their comments towards those passing through challenges and life's trials. *It is wrong to tell those that are hurting that their challenges is because of their sins*. I've heard this kind of harsh remarks from the brethren often. To be sincere, if God has to repay us according to our iniquities, we all would be dead without exception. The Psalmist(130:3) says: *"If you, LORD, kept a record of sins, Lord, who could stand?*" No one can stand God's retribution. However, God has chosen not to tow that path. This is not a license to sin, rather I'm showing us a side of God that the devil often blinds us from seeing to keep us guilty and condemned even after we're forgiven.



When the Psalmist realized this depth of God's love, I believe that was what prompted him to write that line. One of the mind attacks the devil brings to us during times of trials is to make us think we're suffering because of our sins. He makes us feel unforgiven and guilty. When that happens, just know the enemy of your soul has started his tricks. Apostle Paul says, *"that God was reconciling the world to himself in Christ, not counting people’s sins against them. And he has committed to us the message of reconciliation*", 2Cor 5:19 NIV. If God is not counting sins against the new creation, why burden yourself with that load of guilt? Just as the Psalmist worshipped God realizing the depth of God's love, do same on daily basis.


*PRAYER*: Father, I thank you for your love.

6/18/21, 7:04 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: PROJECTSAVE DEVOTIONAL
Friday,
June 18th, 2021.

*YIELD*


Romans 6:16 Know ye not, that to whom ye yield yourselves servants to obey, his servants ye are to whom ye obey; whether of sin unto death, or of obedience unto righteousness? KJV


In a bible study class on the book of Acts of Apostles, the lecturer asked us to know our views on why the manifestation of the supernatural isn't common as it was in the days of the apostles. Several views were shared but the view that stood out and ministered to me was about yielding to the Spirit. A careful study of those great supernatural manifestations in the book of Acts happened because the vessels God used yielded fully to the Spirit. They became a conduit for the power of God to flow because their lives were yielded fully to the Spirit.


Peter didn't go to the beautiful gate with the aim of making the lame beggar walk, he was just going for prayers like every other person and he encountered a lame beggar then the miracle happened, Acts 3. He may not be fasting on that very day as some may think but one thing was constant in his life- he was living a yielded life. He didn't intend to make his shadows heal the sick, it was the people that discovered his shadow carried the supernatural. I doubt if he ever prayed that God should anoint his shadows. It was all the prerogative of the Spirit not his. However, that was possible in His case because he yielded to the Spirit.


We are trying to turn the principle around by seeking to walk in the supernatural, demanding to operate in unique miraculous dimensions and manifesting power through several spiritual exercises without living a yielded life. That's why some have fasted and prayed for power for years but without result. *The Holy Spirit is willing to manifest the power and glory of God through us more than we are willing. He's only looking for yielded vessels who will manifest the power of the world to come here on earth*. To yield is to obey the promptings of the Spirit, even in little things that we think doesn't count. That's how to position oneself for the outflow of God's power and glory.


*PRAYER*: Father, I receive the grace to yield to God's Spirit in Jesus name.

DEVOTIONAL 0182 | Source start line: 18589
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
6/19/21, 9:44 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: ProjectSave Devotional
Saturday,
June 19th, 2021.

*SPEAK THEIR LANGUAGE*

Matthew13:34 Jesus always used stories and illustrations like these when speaking to the crowds. In fact, he never spoke to them without using such parables.


Cameron Townsend was a third era pioneer missionary. He was a scholar and a contemporary of Ralph Winter. In his effort to spread the gospel of Christ, he decided to travel to Guatemala. When he got there, he began sharing Spanish bibles. He gave the bible to an Indian man who asked him, "If your God is so smart, why can't He speak my language"? That unsettled Cameron Townsend. Because of this encounter, he ventured into bible translation. Later, he founded Wycliffe Bible Translators. He saw the importance of presenting the gospel to people in a language they understand.


The lesson I want to draw from this story is that as we preach, teach or communicate the gospel, we should aim at connecting with our audience. We should speak the language they understand and come down to their level. When you go to preach the gospel to sinners, don't speak Greek or Hebrew, don't try to impress with high sounding biblical words. All they need to hear is the gospel in the language they understand. Most times, when I preach on the mission field, i try my best to present the gospel in a way my audience can easily understand. That's why I use a lot of audio- visuals to pass the message across. A wise man said, *"When you speak above the intelligence of your audience, you attack them. When you speak below the intelligence of your audience, you insult them*".


I learnt this principle from my kids as well. Whenever I'm teaching them scriptures, I try to present age-appropriate knowledge of the scriptures with them. Sometimes ago, I tried to explain the concept of rapture to them. One day, as we watched one of their favourite cartoon series together, I saw the actor varnish into thin air. It occurred to me immediately that on the strength of that scene, I can use that to illustrate the concept of rapture. Jesus used parables that were relevant to his host culture to preach the gospel to his audience. *Anywhere you stand to preach the gospel, don't let your audience see God as a man that is not smart enough to communicate to them*.


*PRAYER*: I receive grace to preach the gospel without ambiguity in Jesus name.

6/20/21, 11:57 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: ProjectSave Devotional

June 20th, 2021.

*HE CAN COME NOW-BE READY*

Matthew24:36  “However, no one knows the day or hour when these things will happen, not even the angels in heaven or the Son himself. Only the Father knows. NLT


I came across two wonderful sermon illustrations about rapture. The first is about a mother who explained to her little girl, the death of her father. The mother said: “God has sent for your father and will send for us, but I do not know just when.” Finally, the little girl said: *“If we do not know just when God is going to send for us, do you not think we had better pack up and get ready to go? God might send when we are not ready*.”  F.B. Meyer once asked D.L. Moody, “What is the secret of your success?”  Moody replied, “For many years I have never given an address without the consciousness that the Lord may come before I have finished.”

These stories illustrates what it mean to be ready. In the history of the early church, tradition has it that they lived under a very strong conviction that Christ would come in their time. Little wonder why they took the work of soul-winning serious. A wise man said we should stop getting ready for the coming of the Lord but we should live ready for his coming. The process of getting ready is not the same as being in a state of readiness. No true believer will feel at home here because *"...our citizenship is in heaven, from which we also eagerly wait for the Savior, the Lord Jesus Christ*",Phil 3:20.



Jesus is coming soon. We must live with the consciousness of His soon return every day. The signs are there for us to see. The message of His coming is becoming rare on pulpits today. Men live as though there's no where after here forgetting that the life after this life is eternal and never ending. Our goals and plans must factor in eternity. *We are not designed to live here forever, there's a home after here*. Knowing that Christ is coming will help us stay pure in readiness for His coming: *"And everyone who has this hope in Him purifies himself, just as He is pure*",1John3:3.


*PRAYER*: Father, I receive grace to live ready on daily basis in Jesus name.

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
6/21/21, 4:58 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: PROJECTSAVE DEVOTIONAL
Monday,
June 21st,  2021.

*DEALING WITH "HIGH THINGS*"

2Corinthians 10:5 casting down imaginations, and every high thing that exalteth itself against the knowledge of God, and bringing into captivity every thought to the obedience of Christ; KJV

The phrase "high thing" is from the Greek word 'hupsoma'. The simple literal meaning refers to something elevated above the earth. E.g., Mountains, hills,etc.  This definition is quite easy but Paul meant something deeper which his audience understood clearly.  The term "hupsoma" is an astrological term denoting a location above the earth associated with supernatural events and beings. This is where horoscopes are set forth. All the zodiac readings you read about are believed to come from the space above the earth. It is safe to conclude that when Paul talked about "high things" in context of the opening scripture, he was referring to demonic powers operating above the earth that work on people's minds. They seek to manipulate men by making them believe the wrong things.



The  greatest warfare starts in the mind. There are believers who still believe in zodiac signs. They read what astrologers received from "high places" to determine the course of their lives. I'm appalled when I see believers claiming they have a particular trait based on zodiac readings. The devil doesn't stop there, when he wants to put people in demonic bondage, he starts by preaching the wrong sermon to them. The moment they believe those things, an unbiblical mental stronghold is built in their minds. From there, the devil begins to torment them.

Paul called those wrong beliefs "High thing that exalteth itself against the knowledge of God". You need to rebel against anything you believe that is not in full agreement with the knowledge of God. What an astrologer says about you is not valid when compared with what God says about you in His word. How do you win this battle? Paul said, *" casting down imaginations....bringing into captivity every thought to the obedience of Christ*". You can achieve these two by feeding your mind with God's word, renouncing contrary beliefs,  confessing God's word and dealing with cosmic powers seeking to manipulate your mind.


*PRAYER*: Father, I receive freedom from wrong mental stronghold the enemy is using against my life in Jesus name.

DEVOTIONAL 0185 | Source start line: 18726
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
6/22/21, 7:13 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: ProjectSave Devotional
Tuesday
June 22nd,  2021.

*YOUR COMFORT OR HIS PURPOSE*?


Isaiah6:8 Then I heard the voice of the Lord saying, “Whom shall I send? And who will go for us?” And I said, “Here am I. Send me!”


We are familiar with the story of Jonah. God sent him to Nineveh but he boarded a ship going to Tarshish. He left his assignment for what he felt was convenient. We all know how the story ended. Another biblical example is the early church. In Acts 1:8, Jesus told them that they would be witnesses in Jerusalem, Judea, Samaria and to the ends of the earth but they stayed in Jerusalem, their comfort zone,  instead of spreading out to witness the gospel in other places. For God's purpose to be achieved, He allowed persecution: *"... A great wave of persecution began that day, sweeping over the church in Jerusalem; and all the believers except the apostles were scattered through the regions of Judea and Samaria*."Acts8:1 NLT

It was when they were forced out of their comfort zone that they spread out and preached the message every where. In my opinion, the persecution that forced them out of Jerusalem may not have been God's perfect method for making them spread the gospel to other places. However, God permitted it for His will to be done.  *One of the major challenges that prevent us from entering into the plans and purpose of God for our lives is because we cherish our convenience more than the assignment.* There are many believers with a clear call into ministry but when they weigh their assignment against their convenience/ comfort, they delay or pend their obedience. Almost every now and then, I meet people with unique and wonderful assignments. Their conviction is so strong but they develop cold feet because they don't want to leave their comfort zone.

One of the dangers of delayed obedience is that it can open you up for the onslaught of the enemy. Delayed obedience is akin to breaking the edge of protection around your life. The bible says, *"... and whoso breaketh an hedge, a serpent shall bite him*." Eccl10:8. When God send you on an errand, His assignment is the priority not your convenience. It is high time our generation had known that. *God's priority is not your convenience when His work is left undone.* The latter should come first before the former. Jesus left His comfort zone( Heaven) to fulfill God's purpose on earth. What do you think would have happened to us if he hadn't come? The destinies of multitude is tied to your obedience. Failure to act, you'll be destroying the lives of many. In what areas have you considered your comfort above His assignment/ purpose?






*PRAYER*: Father, I'm willing to obey. Help me in Jesus name.

DEVOTIONAL 0186 | Source start line: 18774

6/23/21, 7:41 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: PROJECTSAVE DEVOTIONAL
wednesday,
June 23rd, 2021.

*PURPOSE AND PATTERN*

Exodus25:40 “Be sure that you make everything according to the pattern I have shown you here on the mountain.

During the voice of healing days in America, most evangelists had a tent for program. It was the norm.  That season experienced a massive display of the miraculous because of the revival that broke out on Azusa street. This revival spread far within United States. The evangelists of that era had large tents for their healing programmes. One of the young ministers who witnessed that move of God was Kenneth E.Hagin of blessed memory. On several occasions, he was told by fellow ministers to get a large tent to start his own outdoor healing and miracle meetings but he declined with wisdom. The reason he gave for that was because God didn't instruct him to do that. It wasn't part of his ministerial job description.

*There's something that is distinctly unique about God's purpose for your life. There's a unique pattern that is peculiar to the nature of your assignment which may not be exactly the same with another person doing something similar*. God's purpose for your life has its unique pattern and you must discover yours. God told Moses to build according to the pattern shown to him on the mountain. It is possible to build and not follow the architectural pattern or plan. When the building process finish, people may celebrate and rejoice with the builder for completion but the architect knows that something is wrong somewhere.

When you receive an assignment from God, you need to press further to know the "how", " where", "who" and "what". That is how you can get the details of the pattern for that assignment. *God is never haphazard, He is a God of precision and accuracy*. Stop copying what you see everyone doing, discover the uniqueness of God's purpose for your life and stay on it. *Beware of mixing trends with pattern. It corrupts the very essence of God's purpose for your life*. Your divine pattern may not be popular but stick to it to achieve the intended purpose. Moses received the pattern on the mountain denoting the place of fellowship with God. There's no other place where you can receive yours except in the place of fellowship.


*PRAYER*: Father, I receive the grace to follow the  pattern of your purpose for my life in Jesus name.

DEVOTIONAL 0187 | Source start line: 18826

6/24/21, 6:44 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: PROJECTSAVE DEVOTIONAL
Thursday,
June 24th, 2021.


*TRAINED TO FIT FOR PURPOSE*


1Chronicles 25:7 Their number who were trained in singing to the Lord, with their relatives, all who were skillful, was 288.


In the old testament temple worship, the Levites were chosen by God to do the work of ministry in that dispensation. During the reign of David, they began to assign priestly duties by lots and when they wanted to assign the work of music, they chose the descendants of Asaph. What caught my attention in the opening text is the phrase *"Their number who were TRAINED in singing to the Lord.*" You may not know why it caught my attention but I'll explain. The Levites had only one purpose- priestly duties which included every aspect of temple service. It was their divine calling and ordination. Why were they  trained to execute a divine assignment entrusted into their hands?  Can't they rely on anointing alone to do the work?




There's a lesson there for us all. The discovery of your God ordained purpose does not make training less important. *The discovery of your purpose in life is not an automatic license for you to fulfill it except you're trained.* God is not haphazard in His doings neither is He in a hurry to launch you out; He doesn't launch men out for an assignment without giving room for preparation. Moses spent the first 80 years of his life undergoing serious divine training. Joshua trained under Moses, Paul trained under Gamaliel in his pre conversion days and after his conversion, trained under Barnabas and spent 3 years in Arabia in preparation for his assignment. The disciples didn't launch out in ministry not until after they were certified fit by Jesus, their trainer.



*It is one thing to know what God want you to do in life and it is another thing to be adequately trained to do that which God has called you to do.* What will take an untrained fellow to achieve in 10 years may take a trained man 2 years. An untrained man may have quantity of result but quality will be lacking. It takes thorough training to be fit for purpose. An untrained preacher may think preaching is all about calling, elocution and being able to put rhyming words together but a trained preacher knows that the art of preaching is to expound scriptures accurately and grow the man in Christ to become mature, transformed and fit for the master's use.   I'm yet to see a star athlete that didn't train his way into stardom. Don't make a mess of that assignment that God has given you even if it looks so 'little' though there's no little assignment in God. Get quality training.


*PRAYER*: Father, I receive the grace to submit to quality training in the name of Jesus Christ

6/25/21, 6:54 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: PROJECTSAVE DEVOTIONAL
Friday,
June 25th, 2021.

*GOD DOES NOT NEED TO MAKE "SENSE" JUST OBEY*


Isaiah 55:8-9 “For my thoughts are not your thoughts, neither are your ways my ways,” declares the Lord. “As the heavens are higher than the earth, so are my ways higher than your ways and my thoughts than your thoughts. NIV


The instruction that came to Abram to leave his father's house to an unknown place doesn't make human sense, Gen12:1. When God instructed Noah to build an ark because of the coming deluge, i am sure it didn't make much sense to him because up until the time the flood came, rain hadn't fallen as we were told by most bible scholars. The rain that caused the flood was the first rain on earth yet he obeyed and preached the message of repentance for 120 years. In fact, I perceive the reason his message was spurned by the people of his day may be because they couldn't imagine water pouring from the heavens upon the earth and drowning them, Gen6:9-22. I don't think it made sense for Elijah to go to brook Cherith to be fed by a bird during famine and later move to the house of a poor widow for food. It wouldn't have made any sense for the widow to give Elijah her last meal. Absolutely senseless.1 Kings 17:7-16.



How much of sense does it make to go to war singing and dancing as against the normal way of unleashing military artillery against the enemy? Yet, Jehoshaphat obeyed God, 2Chronicles 20.  Do you think it made sense for David to go to war against a veteran like Goliath with five stones and a sling?Absolutely, it made no military sense, 1Sam17. I wouldn't try that against an enemy that is not even as skilled as Goliath. The immaculate conception doesn't make any biological sense. How can a woman be pregnant without knowing a man? That's a mystery that's hard for some folks to understand till date. In the bible, there are several examples of divine instructions that doesn't make sense. I asked why it is like that often? The answer is simply because God doesn't live in the realm of human sense that's why His instructions won't make sense oftentimes.



It is natural for every human to try make sense out of every divine instruction because we live in the sense realm. We believe the authenticity of what we can see, touch, taste, smell and hear. However, the God we are dealing with doesn't operate in our realm, He is transcendent. He lives in a world of His own and does as He pleases: *"Our God is in heaven; he does whatever pleases him*", Psalms115:3. Walking with God requires trust and obedience. You need to trust  the integrity of who is leading you except you don't know Him. That's why the knowledge of God is important. The moment the issue of trust is settled, simply obey. The men we read about in scriptures became kingdom icons because they trusted and obeyed God even when His instruction made no sense. *Stop trying to question every instruction from God, simply obey*.



*PRAYER*: Father, I receive the grace to obey you in Jesus name

6/26/21, 9:57 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: ProjectSave Devotional
Saturday,
June 26th, 2021.

*GOD IS NOT TIRED OF YOU*

Luke18:1 One day Jesus told his disciples a story to show that they should always pray and never give up. NLT

Some years back, I was home alone with my first son when he was around three years old. He called me daddy more than 10 times in 2minutes trying to draw my attention to something. Sometimes I acted as if I understand for peace to reign. I became weary because if I don't answer him, he won't stop calling me.  I wondered how he developed his own vocabulary.  Almost every insect or animal was called 'kukuku'.  My wife said, "can you see what I go through with him when you're not around"? She understood almost all his vocabularies and gestures perfectly except a few. He developed his own vocabularies for objects.


That experience made me appreciate mothers more especially what they go through with children in their formative years. My son is much older now and has blended but I picked a lesson from that encounter with him: *Don't stop calling on God, He is never tired of you.* God is our father, He wants us to come to Him like a child. Though God is our father, He is a father with motherly traits and characteristics. The bible reveals God to us as "El Shaddai". That word is suggestive of someone that is multi breasted. God nurses and nourishes like a mother does.  Good mothers are never tired of their children.


Also, I discovered that my son developed his own vocabularies most of which his mother understand. Same applies to prayer. We often miss it in prayer because we try to be "formal" and perfect in language whereas, the bible says we have an infirmity- we don't know how to pray. That's why we engage the Holy Spirit in prayer,Rom8:26. There are things men cannot understand especially how you feel in times of trials.He understand your vocabulary. *Praying to God goes beyond kneeling and speaking choice words*.  He's waiting to hear those vocabularies you've developed that only Him understands. Don't stop calling on Him, he never sleeps he never slumbers, rest in His love.

*PRAYER*: God, I thank you for your fatherhood over my life. Lord, I ask (...insert request) in Jesus name.

DEVOTIONAL 0190 | Source start line: 19004

6/27/21, 6:25 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: ProjectSave Devotional
Sunday,
June 27th, 2021.

*THE GROOM AND THE UNPREPARED BRIDE*

Luke 12:37 It will be good for those servants whose master finds them watching when he comes. Truly I tell you, he will dress himself to serve, will have them recline at the table and will come and wait on them. NIV


I read a very touching story by the homiletics class of West Coast Baptist College.  In the story, a young man, who was to return home and be married, came sooner than was expected. He did not notify his bride-to-be of the exact time. He desired to please her with a surprise. He loved her devotedly and had worked hard and saved his earnings in order to purchase a home for her. It was about midnight when he arrived, but, with a happy heart, he felt he must go by her home. However, when he came near he saw the house was all lighted up. As he came nearer he heard music. Still nearer he saw dancing. Finally looking through the window he saw his bride-to-be in the embrace of another man. His heart sank within him and he went away never to return.

*If Jesus comes today, where will He find His professed bride*? We are the bride of Christ; Satan and the world is the fake groom making us feel our lover will not return. But from His words, He has assured us that He'll return. Are you ready? When His disciples asked about his return, he said, *"But of that day and hour knoweth no man, no, not the angels of heaven, but my Father only*", Matthew24:36. When an important guest or visitor refuses to give details about when he would come, the wisest thing to do is to be fully prepared as though he may come the present moment. The wisdom for living that you and me must adopt should be that of a soldier that is ever ready. Living a life that is worthy of Him is the only way He'll find us rapturable. We must be watchful and abstain from distractions.



I love the fourth stanza and the chorus of the popular hymn "When Jesus Comes To Reward His Servants". It goes thus: "Blessed are those whom the Lord finds watching,
In His glory they shall share;
If He shall come at the dawn or midnight,
Will He find us watching there?"

Chorus: O can we say we are ready, brother?
Ready for the soul’s bright home?
Say, will He find you and me still watching,
Waiting, watching when the Lord shall come?



*PRAYER*: Father, I receive grace to be ready when you come for me. Lord, excise from my life those carnal entanglements and weights and make me rapturable in Jesus name.

DEVOTIONAL 0191 | Source start line: 19109

6/28/21, 7:09 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: PROJECTSAVE DEVOTIONAL
Monday,
June 28th,  2021.

*ROMANS 8:28*

Romans8:28 And we know that God causes everything to work together for the good of those who love God and are called according to his purpose for them.  NLT

I read about an evangelist that went to preach in a town. As he was about preaching, the clouds formed and the skies poured its content heavily. He prayed that rain won't fall but he felt so bad that rain fell and his crusade couldn't hold. He left that village frustrated and was bitter against God. Years later, he met a man from that town where he had a failed crusade. The man confessed to him that he was a witch doctor who had a plan to harm him on the night rain fell during his botched crusade. The man confessed to him that it was the rain that destroyed the potency of his charm and that led to his conversion. The evangelist was awestruck. He repented quickly and gave thanks to God for using a seemingly bad occurrence to spare his life.


My first time in Ghana, I experienced something similar as we were about starting our evening crusade in Agou-Fie village among the Challa people. There was a very heavy downpour. I felt so bad about it because it looked like a hindrance to me. After the rain stopped, the village nurse told me that the people would say it was our presence that brought rain because they've been praying for rain to fall for months because of their farms. Eventually, when we started the program, so many villagers came to hear God's word because they were happy about what happened. We might have looked like "gods" to some of them. To me, the rain was a distraction but God used it to announce His presence to the villagers. Even the paramount chief, an idolater was present in the program. *In God's grand plan for our lives, we can never be disadvantaged no matter how bad the situation is. We tend to focus on the ugliness of our situation but we often fail to see the beauty God is working out through it.*


We need to have what a preacher call "Gen 50-20" vision. What is that all about? In Genesis 50:20, Joseph said, *"But as for you, ye thought evil against me; but God meant it unto good, to bring to pass, as it is this day, to save much people alive.*" That's the way God works. Stop accusing God for that delay, He is in your situation working things out to fit into His great grand plan for your life. That setback is a set up for a step up. Joni Erickson Tada says, *“God allows what he hates to accomplish what he loves.*” When the devil struck Jesus, he thought he had won but he failed to realize that killing Jesus was his greatest undoing: *"But the rulers of this world have not understood it; if they had, they would not have crucified our glorious Lord.*"(1Cor2:8). When he resurrected, He arose triumphant saying: *“...All authority in heaven and on earth has been given to me.*",Matt28:18. God is turning your pain to gain. He is working behind the scene to make you laugh last. Trust in Him.

*CONFESSION*: Father I thank you because you're turning my ugly situation around to bring out your beauty and glory in me. All things are working for my good in the name of Jesus Christ. I am triumphant.


N:B July 23rd-25th, we shall be in Lemo for crusade and Awado people shall also benefit from our medical&welfare outreach. Partner with us in prayers&finances to execute this program effectively. If you have good clothes&gift items for the villagers, kindly contact Bro Monday to pick them up: 08069526360.


info@projectsaveng.org

6/29/21, 7:06 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: ProjectSave Devotional
Tuesday
June 29th,  2021.



*HOW CHRIST IS FORMED IN US*.


Galatians 4:19 My little children, of whom I travail in birth again until Christ be formed in you,




Oftentimes, we pray to be more like Christ and to manifest His glory. We ask God to break and make us to become more like Christ.   However, we fail to realize that the answer to this prayer is something that stands opposed to what the flesh want. The dealings of God that brings out the beauty of the Christ life in a believer is not a roller coaster neither is it a walk in the park. God has designed His own method of dealings for individual who desire the life of Christ to manifest in them. Philippians 2:13 says, *"For it is God which worketh in you both to will and to do of his good pleasure.*"





How does God work in us to work out Christlike attributes through us? What tool does God use to shape us and mould us into the image of Christ? I'll love to share the thoughts of Paul Rapoza on this: *"It is on the mountain top that we meet Christ but it is in the valleys and struggles of life on earth itself that He is formed in us. Those who boast of the mountain top yet avoid the overcoming required in the valleys make shallow believers "having a form of godliness but denying the power to overcome the world." It's in the valleys that the testimony of Christ is lived out and declared.Trials in our lives are like the crucible that the Spirit of God uses to form Christ in us. Those are the times when the Spirit of God seems far away yet He is doing His greatest work in us during those times.*"


The trials and tests of life is the tool God uses to form Christ in us. When we go through challenges, God is using it to work out something glorious in us. Paul says: *"For our present troubles are small and won’t last very long. Yet they produce for us a glory that vastly outweighs them and will last forever!*" 2 Cor 4:17. You cannot develop a virtue like patience if you haven't experienced delay, you cannot tell if you have self control until something tries to push you off the limit. Your attitude to life's challenges must change henceforth. Apostle James said, *"Dear brothers and sisters, when troubles of any kind come your way, consider it an opportunity for great joy. For you know that when your faith is tested, your endurance has a chance to grow*", James 1:2-3 NLT. This is the path to perfection. I love the next verse: *"So let it grow, for when your endurance is fully developed, you will be perfect and complete, needing nothing*", James 1:4 NLT.




*CONFESSION*: Father I thank you because trouble days won't last long. I know you're working out something great in me and through me. I will not skip lessons and classes till I become like Christ in Jesus name.






N:B You can get our free tracts for evangelism at Bible Wonderland Bookshop( opposite pedestrian bridge) at Ketu bus stop, Ikorodu road, Lagos.

info@projectsaveng.org

DEVOTIONAL 0193 | Source start line: 19352

6/30/21, 6:46 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: PROJECTSAVE DEVOTIONAL
wednesday,
June 30th, 2021.

*WORK OUT WHAT IS INSIDE*

Phil2: 12 Therefore, my dear friends, as you have always obeyed—not only in my presence, but now much more in my absence—continue to work out your salvation with fear and trembling, NIV


The opening text is very crucial to our understanding of the common salvation we share. It started with "Therefore". As a rule of thumb, whenever you see that word, you need to know what was there before. Certain line of thoughts and admonition had gone before getting to that point so contextual reading will help us to interpret properly. From Philipian 1:27, Paul began to give admonition about how to live the christian life: *"Whatever happens, conduct yourselves in a manner worthy of the gospel of Christ. Then, whether I come and see you or only hear about you in my absence, I will know that you stand firm in the one Spirit, striving together as one for the faith of the gospel*". If you read downward into chapter 2, Paul stressed on living a Christ like life( godliness), unity and imitating Christ's humility. That's the intention of the writer, Paul.


So when Paul enjoined that they  work out their salvation with fear and trembling, he was trying to tell them to produce the fruit of salvation( godliness, humility and unity) in the way and manner they live. Whenever this scripture is quoted, some persons are quick to misinterpret our intention to mean, "they are promoting salvation by works." No we are not. The import of what Paul meant was that we(saved believers) should "work out" what has been "worked into" us. It is not the same as saying "do these to be saved." It would not have been because he was talking to saved persons.  A believer is someone who have the life of God and imbued with the capacity for godliness: *"By his divine power, GOD HAS GIVEN US EVERYTHING WE NEED FOR LIVING A GODLY LIFE. We have received all of this by coming to know him, the one who called us to himself by means of his marvelous glory and excellence.*"(2 Peter 1:3 NLT).


God's deposit for godly living is in you already. You are a carrier of a different kind of life, eternal life. This is a life that is not patterned after the flesh and mundane affection. Paul is saying God has worked this life into you, just work it out. The Christian life is not on auto pilot. God has played His part, it is your turn to play your own part. God didn't create man to be a robot that has to be controlled electronically. There's a "doing" of righteousness expected of believers. The grace "to do" has been released already, take advantage of it: *"For the grace of God that bringeth salvation hath appeared to all men, TEACHING us that, DENYING UNGODLINESS and worldly lusts, we should live soberly, righteously, and godly, in this present world;*", Titus2:11-12.


*PRAYER*: Father, I receive the grace to work out what has been worked into me in Jesus name

DEVOTIONAL 0194 | Source start line: 19490

7/1/21, 6:46 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: PROJECTSAVE DEVOTIONAL
Thursday,
July 1st, 2021.


*ARE YOU LIKE JOB'S FRIENDS*?


Job17:17 A friend loveth at all times, And a brother is born for adversity. KJV

I read the book of Job for years before I discovered the errors of Job's friends.  We all know the calamities that befell him. Owing to this, his friends who came around to mourn with him ended up making  unsavoury remarks about his integrity and purity. His friends couldn't comprehend why such calamity befell him  because their belief is that righteous people do not face challenges of life. Very wrong mentality. Those that were meant to bring him word of comfort added to his pains. They insinuated that his sins caused what befell him, Job4:3-8, 8:20. Surprisingly, this is a man whom the bible said, *"There was a man in the land of Uz, whose name was Job; and that man was perfect and upright, and one that feared God, and eschewed evil*". KJV





The words of Job's friends didn't only grief Job, it griefed God as well. In Job 42:7-8, the Lord saw their words as a display of  foolishness: *"After the Lord had spoken these words to Job, the Lord said to Eliphaz the Temanite: *“My anger burns against you and against your two friends, for you have not spoken of me what is right, as my servant Job has. 8 Now therefore take seven bulls and seven rams and go to my servant Job and offer up a burnt offering for yourselves. And my servant Job shall pray for you, for I will accept his prayer not to deal with you according to your folly. For you have not spoken of me what is right, as my servant Job has.*” God is not happy when you don't speak the right words to those in pains. You misrepresent God to the hurting when you speak as though God is paying them back for their evil.  It is not everyone facing the vicissitudes of life that is reaping their evil and wickedness.


*If you can't help the hurting to heal and find comfort, do not talk to them*. Sometimes, those who break your heart the most during life challenges are believers. The moment some brethren see a child of God facing life's challenges, they switch over to the attitude of Job's friends. They easily forget that no one is exempted from facing life's challenges. Pastor Paul Ilo said, *"You're either on your way into a trial, in it or on your way out of it*".  That hurting brother/ sister needs succour not an accuser. We often step into the ministry of Satan against our fellow brothers. We aid the condemnatory feelings and low self esteem people have during their trials instead of helping them out. *If you want to be a good friend, help people to find inner healing for their pains and employ yourself as a personal intercessor*.


*PRAYER*: Father, I thank you for loving me. I receive grace to be a good friend in Jesus name.

7/2/21, 12:26 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: PROJECTSAVE DEVOTIONAL
Friday,
July 2nd, 2021.

*DON'T FEEL TOO BIG TO ASK FOR HELP*

Psalm 34:17 When the righteous cry for help, the Lord hears, and rescues them from all their troubles.

Years back, I was traveling to Benin city in the southern part of Nigeria. While in the bus, a fashionista young lady of senior secondary school age( SSS 3) was pressed but she refused to tell the driver to stop so that she can ease herself. She felt too big to ask for such in the public.  Her mother saw that she was uncomfortable, she sought her permission to tell the driver to stop beside the bush for her but she blatantly refused. We all saw that she was uncomfortable but she held on till we got to the police check point where we were held in a mild traffic. She defecated on her body badly to the extent that we saw faeces on her body. Kind co travellers in the bus gave her satchet water to clean up and her mother removed her head gear to tie around her waist till we got to our destination because her trouser was meesed up. She blessed us with a bad stinking odour till we got to our destination. What a journey it was.



My assessment of the scenario was that she felt too big to ask for help. She was forming "big girl" as we say in this part of the world.  I noticed the same trend even among God's children especially church leaders. In times and moments of struggles, because we don't want to appear as weaklings or as though we don't
have faith, we often hide behind the common mantra "It is well" when all is not well. The most common lies on the lips of some believers is "It is well" when all is not well. One would think they're expressing faith but deep within, they need help but they feel too big to ask for it. I love Paul. When he needed intercessory help, he said, *"Brethren, pray for us*",1 Thessalonians 5:25. This apostle was not a spiritual babe, arguably the greatest Christian in church history yet he asked for help.



Why keep mum when you can easily access help? I'm yet to find a man that is all-sufficient. *God created us to be dependent in some areas. In all your spirituality, you'll need help at one point or the other*. As anointed as Moses was, when he needed financial help for the ministry in the wilderness, he asked for it and he got it. Unfortunately, some don't even ask God for help. I love Peter. In Matthew 14:30,  when he began to sank, he cried to Jesus for help:  *"But when he saw the strong wind and the waves, he was terrified and began to sink. “Save me, Lord!” he shouted*". The help you need may just be wise counsel or a single instruction that will turn your life around. Drop the toga of pride and speak out when necessary. Don't die in silence.



*PRAYER*: Father, help me.

DEVOTIONAL 0196 | Source start line: 19578

7/3/21, 10:27 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: ProjectSave Devotional
Saturday,
July 3rd, 2021.

*GALATIANS 6:7 PRINCIPLE*


Galatians 6:7 Don’t be misled—you cannot mock the justice of God. You will always harvest what you plant. NLT

During COVID-19 lockdown, certain truths i know were reinforced and I engaged them to live above the stress of that time. Dr Mike Murdock said, *"what you make happen for others, God will make happen for you*". On the strength of that biblical wisdom, I took upon myself the burden of helping others despite having my own needs. Naturally, i should have focused on myself and family because that period, the consumption rate of my children increased but the truth I know didn't allow me to rest. Starting from the little we have as a family, myself and my wife decided to give out to those in need. When we saw that more people needed help, we involved the brethren. At the beginning, the idea looked foolish because there was no end in sight for the lockdown but we kept at it.


The miraculous provisions that came for us was amazing. God paid all my bills, no debt no stress whatsoever.  *Be involved positively in the progress and well being of others and you'll see God take care of you*. That's something we all must learn. *Some are praying for God's help yet they have not helped others. Some are praying for provision yet they have never met the needs of others in their own little way*. Some have great visions for life and ministry and are praying about it but they haven't helped others to achieve their vision through positive contribution. Paul said, *"Don’t be concerned for your own good but for the good of others*", 1Cor10:24. He is not saying you shouldn't take good care of yourself but it is another way of saying show concern for others. I see how people sweat in prayers on issues that require practical engagement of biblical principle. *You have no moral right to ask God for what you have not given or done to others*.


In Genesis 8:22, the bible says, *"While the earth remaineth, seedtime and harvest,...shall not cease.*" What you sow( this is not restricted to money only) is what you'll reap. This is not about how long you can pray, it is a biblical principle. Start looking out for the good of others. Life shouldn't be all about you. As you're building a great life, help others build theirs. In the pursuit of your vision, be instrumental in helping others to fulfill theirs. Never compete with others neither should you pull them down. The answer to your prayer requests of years lies in you being the answer to the prayer of another person. There are needs you have today, you may need to meet the same or similar needs for others for yours to be met.  Don't forget Dr Mike Murdock's truism: *"what you make happen for others, God will make happen for you*".

*PRAYER*: Father, I receive the grace to be positively involved in the life of others in Jesus name.

DEVOTIONAL 0197 | Source start line: 19597

7/4/21, 12:20 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: ProjectSave Devotional
Sunday,
July 4th, 2021.

*DO THE WORK BEFORE NIGHT TIME*


John 9:4 We must quickly carry out the tasks assigned us by the one who sent us. The night is coming, and then no one can work. NLT


A wonderful story of Dietrich Bonhoeffer was told in "Our Daily Bread" daily devotional, November 10 1991. At the height of World War II Dietrich Bonhoeffer was imprisoned for taking a stand against Hitler. Yet he continued to urge fellow believers to resist Nazi tyranny. A group of Christians, believing that Hitler was the Antichrist, asked Bonhoeffer, *“Why do you expose yourself to all this danger? Jesus will return any day, and all your work and suffering will be for nothing.” Bonhoeffer replied, “If Jesus returns tomorrow, then tomorrow I’ll rest from my labor. But today I have work to do. I must continue the struggle until it’s finished.*”


There are a number of God's children with a heavenly mandate for their generation. Attached to their names are souls of men awaiting when they'll step out to rescue them. However, some persons in this category are foot-dragging and dilly-dallying. They have one excuse or the other why they are yet start out on what God has called them to do. There is another group of persons who are doing the work God has called them to do but they aren't passionate about it. They are lazy, they see kingdom service as a second option or side job. The zeal for the work is lacking. They think they have all the time to do the work of God forgetting that every kingdom assignment is always within a specific time frame. God intend to do great things through people in these categories but their attitude to kingdom has limited them.



We have a role model in Jesus and the apostles. In Corinthians 9:16, Paul said, *"Woe to me if I do not preach the gospel!*" Paul threw himself into the work and he stood out from among others.  Dearest, do not be lazy about kingdom assignments. Don't use ministry as your retirement plan if God has called you. The day time is work time. There'll always be a night time when no man work. Don't do at 60 what you ought to do at 30. Time is going,
very soon, our time will be up and we'll all return to give account to our master. We'll rest from our labours someday if we labour.  Apostle Paul enjoined us in Romans 12:11 saying, *"Not slothful in business; fervent in spirit; serving the Lord;*".




*PRAYER*: Father, I receive grace for fruitful labour in the kingdom in Jesus name.

7/5/21, 6:26 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: PROJECTSAVE DEVOTIONAL
Monday,
July 5th,  2021.

*HANDLING CONFUSION AND DIRECTION* Pt.1

Isaiah 30:21  *And thine ears shall hear a word behind thee, saying, This is the way, walk ye in it, when ye turn to the right hand, and when ye turn to the left.*

*GOD IS NOT THE AUTHOR OF CONFUSION*

In the course of counseling and teaching the word, I have met people who claimed to be confused on what God is saying to them either on marriage or ministry. Most times, I'm surprised when some persons in this category claim to have heard God but are still confused. It is often common to hear singles talk about being confused on marital matters. Ministers or those intending to start ministry are not excluded from this. What could be the reason for confusion after hearing God? The very first truth that we must settle in our hearts is that God is not the author of confusion: *"For God is not the author of confusion, but of peace, as in all churches of the saints.*"(1Cor14:33). It is not possible for confusion and divine direction to coexist if truly we have heard God.



The purpose of God's leading is not to bring confusion but to bring clarity to grey areas of our lives. If God is not the author of confusion then we know the author is the devil. The nature of God's goodness forbids the coexistence of confusion and direction. God's promise of leading you and me is comprehensive: *"I will INSTRUCT thee and TEACH thee in the way which thou shalt go: I will GUIDE thee with mine eye.*"(Psalm 32:8 KJV). In this promise that you just read, God didn't include confusion in the package. It is a promise to instruct, teach and guide. This is the nature of God's leading, confusion is not in the package.



When Christ told us about the guiding work of the Spirit, He said, *"When the Spirit of truth comes, he will guide you into all truth. He will not speak on his own but will tell you what he has heard. He will tell you about the future.*"(John16:13). Hallelujah!!! The Holy Spirit is not a confuser. So where is confusion coming from? The moment you realize that confusion is an attack from hell to derail you from making the right choice(s), you'll stand against it. Don't side with the devil by agreeing that God's leading brings confusion. Henceforth, anytime you're confused, rebuke the demon of confusion and stay action on whatever you intend doing until you find a clear leading through inward witness of the Spirit.

*CONFESSION*: I refuse to be confused. I am not a candidate of confusion. Arrow of confusion fired into my life is hereby destroyed in Jesus name. I'll walk in the exactness of God's plan for my life in all areas and I'll get it right in Jesus name.

If you have good clothes&gift items for the villagers, kindly contact Bro Monday to pick them up: 08069526360.


info@projectsaveng.org

DEVOTIONAL 0199 | Source start line: 19743
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
7/6/21, 4:49 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: ProjectSave Devotional
Tuesday
July 6th,  2021.

*HANDLING CONFUSION AND DIRECTION* Pt.2

Romans 8:16 The Spirit itself beareth witness with our spirit, that we are the children of God: KJV


*TUNE IN PROPERLY TO THE SPIRIT'S FREQUENCY*

I love to use the old transistor radio to illustrate hearing from God and receiving clear direction. When we were growing up, most people in my age bracket and those that are older grew up in an environment where analog radio is common unlike today's children who listen to digital radio with a scan button. The analog transistor radio has an antenna and a tuning knob. If you want to listen to a program on a particular radio station clearly, you'll have to tune the radio to the right frequency using the knob. Most times, when you're close to the frequency of the desired radio station, there could be interference from another radio station whose frequency is close to the one you desire. At that moment, you'll be hearing two different programs almost at the same time. It creates a confusion because of the cacophony of voices. The solution at that time is to slowly tune in to the right frequency using the knob until there's a clear signal.

Sometimes, the inner confusion we experience at the point of making decision is similar to the illustration given above. There are times we get to that point where we are disturbed within because of the cacophony of voices. Sometimes, it is as though two to three voices are speaking all at once and imaginations are running wild already. I have been there severally and I know you may have experienced this as well. Anyone in this state or condition is superbly confused. It is not advisable to take any action under this condition. So, how can you tune your spirit man to the frequency of the Spirit where confusion doesn't exist and where direction is clear? The simple thing to do is to worship God with spirit lifting music and pray in the Holy Ghost.

*Creating an atmosphere of worship makes your spirit man tender and  receptive while praying in tongues tunes your spirit man to the frequency of the Spirit properly*. Paul said, *"He that speaketh in an unknown tongue edifieth himself;...*",1 Corinthians 14:4. That self edification is what you need to dispel confusion and receive clarity of instruction from God. This is not something you do once and stop. You have to keep doing this until you find it easy to tune in to the frequency of the Holy Spirit. Don't let the devil keep you in a state of confusion one more day. You have an advantage of the Spirit's baptism on your side. On daily basis, set your heart on that issue and pray in tongues extensively about it. The day/ moment will come when a light will beam out of your spirit man on the issue. You'll just recognize what God is saying accurately and confusion will disappear.


*CONFESSION*: I refuse to be confused. I am not a candidate of wrong decision in Jesus name.









N:B You can get our free tracts for evangelism at Bible Wonderland Bookshop( opposite pedestrian bridge) at Ketu bus stop, Ikorodu road, Lagos.

info@projectsaveng.org

7/7/21, 5:20 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: PROJECTSAVE DEVOTIONAL
wednesday,
July 7th, 2021.

*HANDLING CONFUSION AND DIRECTION* Pt.3

Isaiah 30:21  *And thine ears shall hear a word behind thee, saying, This is the way, walk ye in it, when ye turn to the right hand, and when ye turn to the left.*

*DEAL WITH CONFLICT OF INTEREST*


I'll retell a story about myself to illustrate this point. Years back, I became tired of field ministry because of poor welfare. I thought about what the way out could be and I concluded that if I start a church, my lot would be better. Those days, I saw young men like me sitting as senior pastors over churches and I told myself that I could do same. In fact, a man told me young men like me were starting churches around town and I could do same. I began to nurse the idea of starting a church. However, I had serious trouble within me almost immediately after concluding on that plan. I became confused and that confusion lingered for long.  I called one of my mentors  and told him what I was planning. He asked me a simple question, "what did God tell you in the beginning"? That question struck me hard and gave me a reset.

From the onset when I started to plan my exit from field ministry, I knew God didn't tell me to start a church but because I was interested in another path of ministry I felt was rewarding, I became confused. The confusion was caused by the clash between divine instruction received earlier and my personal interest which was not in line with God's will for my life. *It is possible to have heard from God truly and later become confused due to conflicting self-glorifying personal interest*. More often than not, this happens to most believers. Look at it this way, a single brother/ sister who prayed and heard clearly from God about whom to marry may experience confusion because God's leading may not tilt towards personal  interest. If a brother with tribal sentiment receives the leading to marry a sister from another tribe when he is already eyeing a sister from his own tribe, he may become confused because of the clash between his personal interest and what God is saying.


*The presence of confusion is not always the absence of divine direction/ a clear leading from God. Sometimes, it could result from a clash of interest. The only way to handle this is to surrender to God's will and be obedient to Him*. Sometimes, we go into the place of prayer asking God for direction whereas we  have made up our minds on what we want. When God speaks, instead of accepting His leading, we enter into a phase of internal turbulence. I have not condemned having a personal desire but you need not be rigid about it. Empty yourself of your ways and you'll know peace. Proverbs 3:5-6 says, *"Trust in the LORD with all your heart and lean not on your own understanding; 6 in all your ways submit to him, and he will make your paths straight.*"

*PRAYER*: Father, I receive grace to obey you and to empty myself of my ways in Jesus name.

*HANDLING CONFUSION AND DIRECTION* Pt.4


2 Tim 1:7 For God has not given us a spirit of fear and timidity, but of power, love, and self-discipline.

*DEAL WITH FEAR OF THE UNKNOWN*


After I settled in my heart that ministry is the way to go, the next thing that hit me was a high dose of confusion. This type of confusion was not a doubt about God's leading but a confusion that is rooted in fear of the unknown. The fear of what the future holds seeing others face their careers and forging ahead with life. I was gripped by the fear of the future because I wanted to secure it by my strength. Instead of setting out to fulfill my assignment the way I was sent and where I was asked to stay, I decided to modify it by relocating out of the country. I applied to a school in Europe and got admission but couldn't secure a visa. I submitted several international applications that were unsuccessful.








Surprisingly, a minister in the U.S.who knew nothing about my exit plans spoke to me saying, "The Lord said I should tell you,  stay where you are". That struck me. I withdrew from all plans and I rolled my life over to the one who created it. *Fear can make a man distort divine direction*. For example, God may call a man into ministry to a rural or suburb setting but the fear of what to eat, drink and wear may cause him to relocate the ministry to the city centre where he is not sent thinking he may secure his future there.  He may still be in ministry but at the wrong location due to fear of the unknown.


*Fear is an enemy of divine instruction*. Fear can make a man doubt God's voice and become deaf to the leading of the Spirit. *Fear comes because we try to process every divine instruction with our five senses*. In the sense realm,  that's where our minds analyse the 'what' and 'how' trying to know how certain things work. However, the end result of trying to grasp spiritual instructions with the mind without the Holy Spirit produces a crippling fear. Refuse to allow the fear of unknown cripple you. What the Lord expect from you and me is obedience to His instruction, He knows how to take good care us. Fear not.


*PRAYER*: Father, help me.

7/9/21, 7:25 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: PROJECTSAVE DEVOTIONAL

*HANDLING CONFUSION AND DIRECTION* Pt.5

Psalm 32:8 The Lord says, “I will guide you along the best pathway for your life. I will advise you and watch over you. NLT


*BEWARE OF CONFLICTING COUNSEL*


The young prophet received a clear instruction from God to curse the idolatrous altar of Jeroboam at Bethel. In addition, He was instructed not to eat or drink but this young prophet met an old prophet that gave a contrary instruction: *"But the old prophet answered, “I am a prophet, too, just as you are. And an angel gave me this command from the Lord : ‘Bring him home with you so he can have something to eat and drink.’” But the old prophet was lying.*" 1 Kings13:18. Eventually, that young prophet lost his life because he heeded the counsel of the old prophet. I am sure the young prophet was confused before he heeded the counsel of the old prophet. He heard God truly but his confusion set in when the old prophet claimed to have also heard from the same God. Out of "respect", he obeyed man's leading and he died.


*Another thing that brings confusion after hearing from God is wrong counsel especially from spiritual leaders.*  I am an advocate of mentorship and active spiritual fatherhood. I strongly believe that every child of God should be under a pastor. However, I've also discovered that there are times God's children experience confusion because the counsel received from mentors/fathers is contrary to what the Holy Spirit has told them. This happens because we often follow our leaders sheepishly without checking with God to see if they're leading us aright. It is a big problem in the part of the world where I come from. *People deify their spiritual leaders, "paparize" them and jettison the leadership of "papa" God*. For instance, there are situations where Bro "A" received a leading to marry sister "B" but because his mentor has a special interest in his marriage, he decided to talk down sister "B"( probably because she doesn't belong to his church or she's not a "yes" person) and fix him up with sister "J" contrary to God's will. So many have missed God's will for marriage because they abandoned God's leading for the wrong counsel of spiritual leaders. Know this: *"No human spiritual leader is sensitive and accurate at all times in every situation*".


*As a spiritual leader myself, I've come to know I'm a human with knowledge limitations, the best I can do in the counseling room is to counsel biblically but I'll not say"thus saith the Lord" when the Lord hasn't spoken neither will I over rule what God has told His children*.  When you hear from God, the duty of your spiritual leader or mentor is to confirm and counsel you but not to overrule the voice/ leading of the Spirit. *The counsel of man is not superior to the leading of the Spirit*. So many church leaders try to play the role of the Holy Spirit in the lives of their people, this is wrong.  It confuses God's children because two voices are saying different things. Great visions have died in the body of Christ because some insensitive spiritual leaders stood against it. I remember the story of Mount Zion Film Ministry. At the onset, most of the church leaders of the founder didn't believe and supported him, thank God he forged ahead without looking back.  Dear reader, don't misconstrue my teaching as a reason to neglect mentors. What I'm simply saying is that the moment you recognize the leading of God on a matter clearly and spiritual authorities are saying otherwise, obey God rather than men.  Don't waste your life like the young prophet. The best of man is still a man and God will always remain as God. *God is not happy when spiritual leaders stand on His way by preventing His children from entering into His will*. If you're a pastor, teach your members to hear God, don't play God in their lives.


*ACTION POINT*: Reflect on this teaching and do a self appraisal.

DEVOTIONAL 0203 | Source start line: 20074
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
7/10/21, 4:09 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: ProjectSave Devotional
Saturday,
July 10th, 2021.

*HANDLING CONFUSION AND DIRECTION* Pt.6

Romans 8:16 The Spirit itself beareth witness with our spirit, that we are the children of God: KJV


*AQUAINT YOURSELF WITH THE WORKINGS OF INWARD WITNESS*


Anytime I teach on the leading of the Spirit, I always try my best to explain what inward witness is using the traffic light colors. On the highway, when the traffic light signals "green", we know that's an unspoken command to go. All motorists will move without fear of being apprehended by traffic enforcement officers. When the traffic light signals "red", we know it means stop. If anyone moves, the danger it portends could be fatal because there's likelihood of collision with oncoming vehicles. The amber colour signals "get ready". The traffic light leads drivers without words yet drivers regard it as a valid means of traffic control and direction. That's how the inward witness of the Spirit works.


One primary way by which God leads all His children is through the inward witness. Whether we recognize it or not, that's another issue entirely. That 'knowing' we have from within us about an issue is known as inward witness. It is not a voice but a knowing within. Has it ever happened to you that you know something about a situation supernaturally without hearing a voice or seeing visions? I've been there severally.  When you are about taking a step, that sense of inner peace and tranquility that gives you a "go ahead" from within is inward witness of the Spirit saying "go ahead" but that turbulence and lack of peace within that you can't explain even when all things seem right in the physical is inward witness saying "stop". Now, there's no believer that hasn't experienced this at one time or the other. Some would say, "when I wanted to board the bus, 'something' pulled me back". That 'something' is the inward witness of the Spirit at work.

Confusion persists because most believers are not acquainted with the workings of the inward witness of the Spirit. They have experienced it but they haven't come to terms with the fact that it is the way by which God will lead them primarily on almost all issues. They're expecting a "supernatural" leading like open vision and trance, whereas, God is leading them through inward witness almost on daily basis. I've been there severally but now I know better. There were days I wished a supernatural hand writes on the wall for me to read or an audible voice speaks to me in clear terms. Several times, I've desired to see Jesus appear to give me a word of direction. It is not in God's nature to always lead us spectacularly. If you can train your spirit man with the word of God and spend time in the place of prayer (especially praying in tongues), you'll recognize the leading of the Spirit through inward witness.


*ACTION POINT*: Spend 30 minutes daily praying in tongues. It will sharpen your sensitivity and enhance your ability to recognize the workings of the inward witness.

7/11/21, 6:12 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: ProjectSave Devotional
