8/5/21, 12:13 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: PROJECTSAVE DEVOTIONAL
Thursday,
August 5th, 2021.


*CAPACITY-BUILDING THROUGH LEARNING* Pt.2


Proverbs 19:2 It is not good to have zeal without knowledge, nor to be hasty and miss the way.


*YOU NEED STRUCTURED LEARNING*


One of the things I was introduced to early as a believer was to build a strong library and read good Christian literatures as one of the ways of building spiritual capacity. Those days, we also had a mobile tape player(walkman) to listen to messages. The books I read and the tapes I listened to helped me to grow and they influenced my preaching and teaching. It was not uncommon to see brethren in my category talk about what a man of God said in his books or message because primarily, we learnt most of what we knew through those books. It was our pride. One can easily distinguish a brother who has read through his messages and quotes. However, in the process of time, I discovered that if I am to be grounded in the faith I profess and to handle the word correctly, I need to learn scriptures in a structured system where I can relate with sound teachers of the word. That doesn't mean I won't read books again but my approach to scriptures will no longer be through what I read in the books only but through accurate interpretation of scriptures having learnt the "how" and the "what". That was how I became a seminarian. To me, that's one of the greatest ( if not the greatest) capacity any preacher&teacher of the word should build.


One of the senior pastors in my church asked to know what I needed a seminary education for since I've been preaching well in church and even outside the shores of my country. *For me, it is no longer about preaching well but preaching&teaching right*. I needed to build capacity for that through structured learning and will continue as God help. *One of the ways you can build capacity is to be taught in a structured system. If you're in ministry, you don't deserve to teach if you have not been taught rightly.* No one in his/ her right senses goes to a doctor who claims to have read medical books only but haven't passed through the medical school and certified fit to practice medicine. We love ourselves so much that we wouldn't take that risk yet we don't accord our spiritual lives and that of others with such a great importance. That's why our generation is filled with half-baked, unbaked and immature believers because those who should rightly divide the word of truth haven't learnt in a structured system so they can't give what they don't have. Show me a lawyer who became an advocate just because he can talk/argue without going to the law school.  Everyone can talk or argue but to do same in the court, you'll need to learn the ropes through lecturers in a structured environment from the  university and law school. That's capacity-building.

*The deception of seeing results and being "big" often prevents people from submitting themselves for learning but it is possible for the biggest to be the wrongest.*  You must conquer that syndrome of "I am big" if you want to build capacity for true greatness in life and ministry. Can you swallow your pride for once and admit you don't know as you ought? My reference to ministry is simply because it is my primary constituency. In your area of divine purpose and calling, develop your capacity to do it better and right by learning. Almost all doctors can perform basic surgeries but when confronted with a complex case in need of surgery, a consultant surgeon will be called upon to perform the surgery. Why? The consultant has increased his capacity for such task through structured learning. Refusal to build your capacity for more through learning is stupidity. Proverbs1:7 says,  *"To have knowledge, you must first have reverence for the LORD. Stupid people have no respect for wisdom and refuse to learn.*" ( GNT).


*ACTION POINT*: Rebellion against mediocrity in your field is a legal battle you must fight at all times to develop capacity. Fight well.

8/6/21, 2:42 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: PROJECTSAVE DEVOTIONAL

August 6th, 2021.

*CAPACITY-BUILDING THROUGH LEARNING* Pt.3


Proverbs 19:2 It is not good to have zeal without knowledge, nor to be hasty and miss the way.

*STUDY AND MENTORSHIP*


Anthony Robbins is one of America's topmost performance coach. His parents divorced when he was just 7 years. His mom broke four relationships after she left his dad. At 17, he had an altercation with his mom that threw him out of the house. He couldn't go to college so he worked as a janitor earning $40 per week. He decided to succeed through self development. He attended Jim Rohn's seminar at the cost of $35, that was almost all his weekly wages. Rohn mentored Robbins to develop a coaching style that made him famous by his mid-20s. Having fallen in love with performance coaching, he developed capacity to consult for company excutives, scholars in academia and professionals( most of whom were university graduates and professors), In seven years, Anthony Robbins read over 700 books. In his mid 20s, he became a millionaire. This is the story of a man without a college degree who developed the capacity for his passion through mentorship and reading.


Aside failure to submit to structured training/ learning, another reason for low and poor performance is failure to develop capacity through mentorship and study. *Learning through mentorship and study makes one to be fresh and green. The strength of your library and your submission to mentorship will reflect in the way you go about your God-given purpose*.  Looking at the life and ministry of Jesus, it is evident that he was studious. On several occasions, in conversations, Jesus referred to what was "written". When the religious leaders of his day confronted him with a question on marriage&divorce, he said, *"Haven’t you read,” he replied,...*"( Matthew19:4). Even during temptation, in two of his responses, he said, "It is written". He wouldn't have known what was written if He hadn't read. It is evident that Jesus was informed and mentally sound.


Another good example of a man who built capacity for his life's purpose was apostle Paul. He was a scholar who schooled under Gamaliel, the grandson of Hillel. He was mentored by Barnabas. In bible scholarship, Paul's usage of the Greek language was unique among other new testament writers and his epistles are hotly debated among scholars till date. He was well read even to the point where he quoted Gentile poets in his day, Acts17:28. If he was not a reader, how would he have known what a poet wrote? He stood as a witness for Christ among the stoics&epicureans in his day, those were the scholars of that time who had knowledge of science&philosophy(Acts17:28). You need to study about the calibre of men who were in the Areopagus council in Athens that Paul addressed in his day( Acts17). They were the thinkers of that time. As anointed as other apostles were, none had the kind of audience Paul had. He developed an uncommon capacity to confront great minds in his day so that he can be a witness for Christ in circles where the ordinary people couldn't reach. He was given to thorough study. He told Timothy, *"When you come, be sure to bring the coat I left with Carpus at Troas. ALSO BRING MY BOOKS, and especially my papers*" (2 Tim4:13). A wise man said, *"study so that you won't be stupid*". Commit yourself to lifelong capacity building in your God-given purpose through study. It will pay off some day.


*ACTION POINT*: Rebellion against mediocrity in your life's purpose is a legal battle you must fight at all times to develop capacity. Fight it well.

DEVOTIONAL 0231 | Source start line: 22212

8/7/21, 3:58 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: ProjectSave Devotional
Saturday,
August 7th, 2021.

*CAPACITY-BUILDING THROUGH LEARNING* Pt.4

Proverbs 19:2 It is not good to have zeal without knowledge, nor to be hasty and miss the way.

*CULTIVATE THE RIGHT ATTITUDE TO LEARNING*

On a certain day in the seminary, I was in a bible language class when i saw a man dressed in white entering the classroom to greet my lecturer. He wore the traditional white cap of royal fathers and I saw beads on his neck and hands typical of chiefs and kings. My lecturer greeted him calling him "Kabiyesi"( title for kings in Yoruba land in South west, Nigeria). The man stretched his hand for a handshake. He was a lively person.  Myself and other colleagues looked on at the interaction between him and our erudite lecturer. It was purely academic related. Not long after, my lecturer introduced him to us as the king of a town in South west of Nigeria. We all greeted him and were happy to meet a king. Right there, our lecturer told us he was a student in the school as well. A very dedicated student who organized free tutorials on bible languages for his younger classmates. That was a shocker. His relationship in the school with students and staff was devoid of kingly protocols. He was a student just like every other person. I saw humility all over this man. We decided to take pictures with him but that encounter didn't leave me the same. I knew God got my attention there.


I saw pride in me that day. The kind of pride I saw was not the boastful type but more of an attitudinal type that was averse to learning especially from certain places and people. Prior to the time I enrolled in the seminary, I have always questioned what people spend years learning there. I just felt it was a waste of time because I know alot of ministers who are doing well in ministry but had no seminary education. Little did I know that such attitude smirks of pride and arrogance. Unfortunately, it is common to preachers. Through out that day and for sometime, I didn't get over what I saw. What got my attention was not the fact that I shook hands with a king but I saw a man who had the right attitude to learning. *God taught me that one of the ways to know a humble man is not that gentle and reserved mien that we commonly call humility but submitting oneself to the rigour of capacity building through learning regardless of status and height attained is a great sign of humility*.


*A humble man will always learn.* If you truly mean to develop and increase your capacity for quality output and greatness in your God-given purpose, you must cultivate the right attitude to learning- humility. As a pioneer pastor, don't say "I can't sit in a leadership seminar organized by an 'ordinary' assembly pastor' or someone that is not pastoring. Can you even listen to him first before you conclude he has nothing to offer? Bro Gbile Akanni is just a 'brother' by title but he is a discipler of pastors, bishops and ministers yet he doesn't oversee a church. Do you have any explanation for that? There are folks in ministry today who believe the only person that can  teach them is a man that operates in a 'higher class' e.g., a man with thousands of congregants. This is a very wrong and carnal attitude that prevents people from learning especially those that measure success by human parameters. If Moses had that mentality, he wouldn't have learnt from Jethro. Aside the fact that Jethro was his father-in-law, he was no match for Moses' ministerial achievements but that meek man, Moses, humbled himself and learnt from a man whose congregation was unknown. It  paid off. *In humility we learn, in continous learning we build capacity for qualitative greatness*.


*PRAYER*: Father, deal with that pride and arrogance in me that is preventing me from learning in Jesus name.

8/8/21, 3:18 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: ProjectSave Devotional
Sunday,
August 8th, 2021.


*ATMOSPHERE MATTERS*


1Sam9:24 He stripped off his garments, and he too prophesied in Samuel’s presence. He lay naked all that day and all that night. This is why people say, “Is Saul also among the prophets?” NIV


In 1 Samuel 19, Saul hunted for the life of David. He ran to Samuel at Ramah. When he told Samuel what Saul did to take his life, they moved to Naioth. News reached Saul that David had taken refuge in Naioth. Saul sent men to capture David but when they got there, they met a band of prophets prophesying with Samuel standing in their midst, the Spirit of God came upon them and they prophesied with the prophets. When Saul heard what happened, he sent the second batch of valiant men to capture David but the same thing happened to them even to the third batch. The Holy Spirit came upon them and they prophesied. He decided to go to Naioth by himself. On getting there, he met the same atmosphere, a band of prophets who were prophesying with Samuel standing in their midst. In verse 24, we were told that he stripped off his garments and prophesied lying naked on the floor all night. When the people saw what happened, they said, "Is Saul also among the prophets"? Of course, he had no prophetic calling but he prophesied because he found himself in an atmosphere where the Spirit of prophecy pervaded. Surprisingly, he was backslidden at that time.



There's a spiritual truth about that incident and it applies to you and me today. *The truth is that you become your atmosphere. Our spirit is wired to tap into whatever is in the spiritual atmosphere where we find ourselves*. This has both good and bad sides to it. The good side of it is that if you find yourself in an atmosphere where the Spirit of God is moving, you can easily contact whatever the Spirit of God is doing at that time. Also, if you stay in an atmosphere where the devil is at work, you become whatever the enemy is doing at that moment. Sin thrives in an atmosphere and in environment where it pervades. Lot became immoral because the atmosphere of Sodom was immoral.  *No atmosphere leaves us thesame*. The bible calls the devil " the prince of the power of the air"(Eph2:2) because he operates using the instrumentality of the atmosphere.  The Greek word translated "air" in that text literally means atmosphere. Many years ago as an undergraduate, my next door neighbour in the hostel played lewd songs often and the volume of his CD player sometimes shakes the building. Do you know that those songs kept playing in my mind even after his CD player has been switched off. It was so bad that the songs played out in my mind when I want to pray. I fought hard to bring my mind back to sanity. I was soaked in that atmosphere where lewd songs filled the air.



I have interacted with graduates who attended campus fellowships in their days on campus. Almost everyone of them kept telling me that the spiritual atmosphere under which they were in school was reviving and fire-filled but that can't be said of their churches at home. Inability to create thesame atmosphere has made some to backslid. So many churches have lost their useful youths to this syndrome because of dull atmosphere. The few pastors or churches who understand this secret are attracting some of these youths. Today onward, I want you to take advantage of the power of the right atmosphere for your spiritual life. If your prayer life is weak, simply go to a place where they are praying, in no time, you'll be soaked with the Spirit of prayer operating in that atmosphere. I noticed that I easily contact a fresh unction to pray the moment I find myself in an atmosphere of prayer. That's why I go to prayer mountain or camps. I may not pray along when they give their prayer points but that atmosphere help me to pray anyway.  If you're down and depressed, simply find an atmosphere of worship and stay there until heaviness leaves you. *As a minister, create the kind of atmosphere that makes room for the Spirit of God to flow in your meetings. Bad music can kill something in the atmosphere before your ministration. A carnal atmosphere can prevent the flow of the Spirit.*


*ACTION POINT*: Be intentional about being in the right atmosphere where the Spirit of God can reach you with the largesse of the kingdom. You can create the atmosphere you desire.

*DON'T DWELL ON NEGATIVE NEWS*

Isaiah 8:12 Say ye not, A confederacy, to all them to whom this people shall say, A confederacy; neither fear ye their fear, nor be afraid.

Recently, I told my wife that I am tired of listening to news and other programs where they discuss the state of the nation. All we see and hear on television is not encouraging. Kidnap and other woes are the major themes. One can easily predict the content. The worst aspect of the news we hear these days is the part where they give economic forecast. They may say things like, "XXX number of persons will be poor in 2030 and famine will ravage some part of the nation". They prophesy evil. It is impossible to listen to these kind of news constantly and not have one's faith punctured. My first son heard me discussing with my wife about the myriads of bad news on t.v.and why I won't bother listening any longer. The young boy said I should be watching cartoon. That's hilarious.  No bad news in cartoon.



We live in a world where the devil want to puncture our faith by making us dwell on the bad things happening around the world. In the book of Numbers 13, twelve spies were sent to spy the land. Ten saw the giants, their focus was on the obstacles surrounding their conquest. Just as it is today, some persons have concluded in their hearts that nothing good can happen to them because of the present state of the economy. In their minds, they've closed the door against miracles. Blessed be God, Caleb and Joshua saw differently. The bible says, *"Then Caleb quieted the people before Moses, and said, “Let us go up at once and take possession, for we are well able to overcome it*”(Num13:30).


Don't listen to the seminar of fear being peddled around. There are 101 reasons the devil will give you for not putting your trust in God. *The miraculous acts of God are not economy dependent*. If it were, Elijah and the widow of Zarephath wouldn't have experienced supernatural supplies during famine.  God wrought great miracles during hard times. I'm a living witness. During the lockdown, it was surprising to see people who embarked on building projects. While some were down and depressed because of what to eat and drink, others were buying cars, building houses and living as though nothing was happening. *Don't make your faith a victim of the current circumstances*. Tell the devil you will live above the earthly realities of the times because your reality is what God's word says not what economic experts say.

*CONFESSION*: My reality is what God's word say about my life. I refuse to dwell on negative news. I live and walk in kingdom possibilities.




N:B  Lemo outreach was glorious. Thank you for your partnership.  *Tepona&Kelewu* is next. Partner with us in prayers&finances to execute this program effectively.

info@projectsaveng.org

DEVOTIONAL 0234 | Source start line: 22419

8/10/21, 7:51 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: ProjectSave Devotional
Tuesday
August 10th,  2021.

*LIKE BULUS*

2Cor4:7 But we have this treasure in jars of clay to show that this all-surpassing power is from God and not from us.


In one of the revivals in  Northern Nigeria that happened in the 60s, it was recorded that a young man named Bulus volunteered to be part of a evangelical team for four weeks. He doesn't look like someone who should go for such a task so the leaders in charge rejected him. They said, "there is no where we can send you, they said." He pleaded by saying, 'Isn't there any place where no one else wants to go?' The leaders responded by telling him that behind the hills where the gospel had not yet gone was the only place no one had gone. May be they thought that would discourage him, this young man accepted the assignment. He trekked for two days before getting there. He preached in that place for four weeks with great suffering. The people refused to give him food so he fed on grasses and leaves but he never ceased to tell them about Christ.

One night, as he declared to them, 'I am the servant of the living God. I have come to tell you the truth from heaven', they brought a dying girl lying on a stretcher. They said, 'if you are the servant of God, then you must believe your God can heal. We want you to make this girl well.' He was stunned but he prayed by faith. The little girl sat up and asked for food. Everyone was amazed at the miracle. At the end of four weeks, 200 souls had turned to Christ through the ministry of this humble soul that was despised by his spiritual leaders. Bulus was a testament to the fact that there's no one God cannot use if they surrender to Him totally.


Dearest, maybe God is speaking to you to undertake an assignment but you have been despised by those who should encourage and support you. You may not have the qualification(educational) and even the outlook of a man that God will use. Maybe you're smallish in stature and you don't even have pulpit voice, you don't need to worry. Eliab had the stature but David carried the mantle and mandate. God doesn't look at those things before using us. They may have assigned you to seemingly impossible task just to discourage you like they did to Bulus, God requires just one thing from you, total surrender. There's no limit to how God can use a yielded vessel. *The moment you get to that point in your heart where you're willing to go anywhere he leads and do whatever he bids you, you'll begin to operate in unusual dimensions of power and glory*. A lot of people try to use fasting and prayer to command exploits in the kingdom,  that's fine but without total surrender to God, there's a limit.

*PRAYER*: Father, I surrender my life to you totally, use me.

DEVOTIONAL 0235 | Source start line: 22462

8/11/21, 5:39 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: PROJECTSAVE DEVOTIONAL
wednesday,
August 11th, 2021.

*THE RIVER IS FLOWING ALREADY*

Eph 5:18 Don’t be drunk with wine, because that will ruin your life. Instead, be filled with the Holy Spirit, NLT

In the 70s, Ademu Idama was a key figure in the revival that took place in Ilorin, a city in North Central Nigeria. He had an invitation to preach in a student gathering about the Holy Spirit. Up until that time, he hadn't experienced the baptism of the Spirit with the evidence of speaking in tongues. After his message, a misty cloud descended on the classroom where they were having fellowship. One student began to pray, and began to speak in tongues,  others joined. Some clapped their hands, some cried while others confessed their sin. He was surprised at the holy "commotion" because he hadn't seen anything like it before. It continued until midnight.  More so, he hadn't experienced 'pentecost' but he was a good preacher and Christian. The news about what happened spread throughout Ilorin township.

The following day, when he returned home from work, he peeled some yam tubers and put them on stove to boil. He went to his bedroom to pray. As he knelt down, the Holy Spirit fell on him and he burst out speaking in tongues for hours. The burning pot of yam was what attracted his neighbours who notified him about it. He prayed for several hours in tongues with his hands raised to the sky. He was so overwhelmed that he  prayed in tongues for seven consecutive days both at home and in the office. Hallelujah!!! The Holy Spirit doesn't know human order. The moment He comes into a place, He establishes His own type of "order" that may seem disorderly to us. We often lock Him out of our lives because we want "orderliness". We don't want to be seen as weird.


All over the world, the Spirit of God is moving seeking whom to fill afresh. God is still in the business of baptizing His people with the Holy Spirit and power. Don't put Him in a box. If you're hungry and thirsty for Him, He'll fill you. John recorded what Jesus said in John 7:37-38, *"On the last and greatest day of the festival, Jesus stood and said in a loud voice, “Let anyone who is thirsty come to me and drink.Whoever believes in me, as Scripture has said, rivers of living water will flow from within them*”. The river of the Spirit is flowing now. Don't allow unsubstantiated theological arguments prevent you from plunging into the flow, don't be tuned off by the " disorderliness" you've seen at the manifestation of His coming. *He comes in ways and manners that seems best to him. He could be gentle and soft in His manifestation and at other times, He comes with a roar*. The early apostles were called names after He came upon them but the experience paid off. That enduement of power from on high made the difference in their walk with the Lord. We wouldn't have had Acts of the Apostles without that encounter. God want to use you to continue the exploits we read in Acts of Apostles but not without His Spirit. *The river is flowing already, don't stay by the riverside to watch how others are getting soaked and wet, plunge into it*.

*ACTION POINT*: Form a habit of praying in tongues for at least 30 minutes on daily basis. It will pay off.

DEVOTIONAL 0236 | Source start line: 22504
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
8/12/21, 5:21 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: PROJECTSAVE DEVOTIONAL
Thursday,
August 12th, 2021.


*THE KIND OF OFFERING GOD ACCEPT*


Ex 25:2 “Speak to the children of Israel, that they bring Me an offering. FROM EVERYONE WHO GIVES IT WILLINGLY WITH HIS HEART YOU SHALL TAKE MY OFFERING. NKJV


An elder shared his experience with me. He attended an evangelistic meeting where the evangelist told everyone of them in the meeting to empty their pocket. He was a young believer then, he took the last dime in his pocket and gave it as an offering under "evangelistic pressure". After the service, he trekked home because he had no transport fare on him again. Some of you have been there before. You have parted with gifts or monies that didn't come from your heart because someone put you under pressure instead of giving you biblical reasons why you should give and allowing the Holy Spirit do the work of conviction. Even Abraham's attempt to offer Isaac as a sacrifice was willing, God saw it in Him and he was commended for his willingness.


*God doesn't accept any offering that is not given willingly*. From the old testament to the new, the nature of God as regards this matter is unchanging. In Paul's  epistle to the Corinthians in 2Corinthians9:7,  Paul said, *"So let each one give as he purposes in his heart, not grudgingly or of necessity; for God loves a cheerful giver*". You may need to read that again patiently. The moment giving( any form of giving at all) comes with threats, curses, arm twisting and spurious promises of "special things" God will do when you give your money or substance, all of these are against the standard of God. He doesn't accept unwilling offerings given under pressure. *There's no gift you want to give to God that is greater than what God gave- Christ.  Any other thing you give ranks 2nd. If giving under Moses(law) was to be done willingly, how much more under the dispensation of Christ(Grace).*




As a preacher of the gospel, simply tell your congregants the purpose their giving will serve and teach them to love God, His kingdom and stewardship. If they understand these concepts, they will give willingly. *When believers grow in the knowledge of the word, giving becomes a life style because there's an understanding.  Coercion during giving  is not God's way*. It is not on record that the apostles in the early church asked their listeners to empty their pocket or bank account. Giving was done willingly in the early church. They must have told them why they should give and the purpose it will serve. In Acts 4:33-34, the bible says, *"With great power the apostles continued to testify to the resurrection of the Lord Jesus. And God’s grace was so powerfully at work in them all- that there were no needy persons among them. For from time to time those who owned land or houses sold them, brought the money from the sales*".  When you approach the offering plate, check your heart to be sure there's a willingness to give.


*ACTION POINT*: Don't give against your will, God won't accept it.

DEVOTIONAL 0237 | Source start line: 22528
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
8/13/21, 6:43 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: PROJECTSAVE DEVOTIONAL
Friday,
August 13th, 2021.

*BEWARE OF OPPORTUNITIES*

Proverbs 14:12 There is a way that appears to be right, but in the end it leads to death.

Years back, I listened to a preacher who has become a household name in Nigeria. He told the story of how he started in ministry with some brethren. They started their ministry on campus with some brethren. One of them left the ministry and traveled out of the country because he had the opportunity. Others stayed back and laboured till the ministry became renowned. Several years after, he returned to the country looking for his old friends they started the ministry together. Eventually, he found the leader and it was a great reunion but not without regrets. This man told the leader, "I've been hearing your name". There was a tone of regret in his voice because other lieutenants had become big with international recognition. He knew he missed something years after he left them.


In the pursuit of your God-given purpose, there are several opportunities that will come your way. The first type of opportunities that beckons are mostly the opportunities that will derail you from your assignment. They look good, pleasant and appealing. Those opportunities come in time of trouble, lack and discomfort. They always appear as the solution to a problem but in the long run, they are not. Accepting those opportunities will make you live a good life but not a fulfilled life. In third world nations where people suffer due to poor economic conditions, the desire of the average man is to live a good life. After settling for a good life, then the search for fulfilment begins instead of doing the latter first. *Don't discard your convictions for opportunities that promises good life instead of fulfilment*.


The second category of opportunities that come are those that establish you in your God-given assignment. They often look like responsibility and work in the outlook. They're not attractive but they're loaded with the benefits. They don't come early, mostly they come when you get busy doing what God has sent you to do. From the day God sends you on the errand of His purpose for your life, opportunities will come, you need to be sensitive to know which is from God for your lifting. You need to always pray God to show you opportunities that are not in line with His will and those that are in His will for your life.  In Philippians 3:13, Paul said, *"Brothers and sisters, I do not consider myself yet to have taken hold of it. But one thing I do: Forgetting what is behind and straining toward what is ahead*". Some opportunities can be a distraction from your assignment, beware.


*PRAYER*: Father, open my eyes to know those opportunities that are for me and those that aren't for me in Jesus name.

DEVOTIONAL 0238 | Source start line: 22739

8/14/21, 12:35 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: ProjectSave Devotional
Saturday,
August 14th, 2021.

*CONFRONT DEMONIC DISTURBANCES*

Colossians 2:15 In this way, he disarmed the spiritual rulers and authorities. He shamed them publicly by his victory over them on the cross. NLT

In 2010, I joined Nigeria Christian youth corpers in a village outreach dubbed rural rugged evangelism. One of the zones invited me to be part of their outreach. When we got to the venue, there was a heavy downpour that made us stay indoor. We waited for the rain to stop but nothing happened. In that venue, we had a tent and our chairs were arranged on the field. I entered the rain to have a feel of the heavy downpour but to my chagrin,  I discovered that the rain was falling on the area where we had our tent and on the chairs only. Just behind the chairs in the open field, there was no single drop. I called the prayer coordinator to come see the wonder. When she stepped out of the area where we had our chairs, there was no single drop of rain but coming to where our chairs were, there was rain.


Immediately, we knew the enemy was up to something evil. I told the brethren that we'll all come under the rain and defy it. By faith, we began to sing under the rain. If i can remember vividly, some brethren were praying. We were resolute about defying the rain knowing it was a demonic ploy to stop what God was about to do.  After a short time, the rain stopped. Hallelujah!!! We had our crusade that night and it was glorious. The devil knows a good thing when he sees one. He thought he could stop God's work and prevent souls from coming to the Lord but he failed woefully. When you're cooking greatness in the pot, he may not know the content but he can smell the aroma. He's all out to frustrate every good plan God has for you.


He's the reason you have had several near success attempt, he's the architect of your several  frustrations.  He is your greatest opposition. In 1Thes 2:18, Paul said, *"We wanted very much to come to you, and I, Paul, tried again and again, but Satan prevented us.*" Stand against him in faith. We don't run from the devil, we confront him with the weaponry of God's word and the name of Jesus. James 4:7 says, *"Submit yourselves therefore to God. Resist the devil, and he will flee from you.*" If you don't resist him, you won't conquer. Faith in  God's word remains a potent tool of victory when confronting satan: *"For whatsoever is born of God overcometh the world: and this is the victory that overcometh the world, even our faith*"(1John5:4). Be ready to unleash your faith against the onslaught of the enemy using the name of Jesus.


*PRAYER*: Father, I confront every demonic battle and gang up against me causing ( ...insert case), I paralyze them over my life in Jesus name.

8/15/21, 7:05 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: ProjectSave Devotional
Sunday,
August 15th, 2021.



*LOVED BY GOD HATED AMONG MEN*

Matthew5:10 Blessed are those who are persecuted for righteousness’ sake, For theirs is the kingdom of heaven.


Jeremiah was an international prophet in his days. He spoke the truth, lived for the truth and was passionate about the things of God. Bible scholars made us realize that he had no single convert or follower because no one believed in him. He was hated and despised by men but loved by God. In 1 Kings 22, Micaiah son of Imlah was hated by Ahab and probably by those in power. Ahab hated him because he spoke the mind of God without adding or removing from it. Ahab wanted to fight Ramoth Gilead and he told Jehoshaphat to join forces with him. They enquired from the prophets of Ahab whether they should go to battle or not. All Ahab's prophet told him what he wanted to hear. He was told to go ahead with his plans assuring him of victory. Jehoshaphat wasn't convinced about the authenticity of the message those prophets gave so he demanded they hear from another prophet: *"The king of Israel answered Jehoshaphat, “There is still one prophet through whom we can inquire of the Lord, but I hate him because he never prophesies anything good about me, but always bad. He is Micaiah son of Imlah.”*(1 Kings22:8).


I am writing this piece to someone who has been hated by men for speaking and standing for the truth. Sometimes, you seem to be lonely because your opinion doesn't flow with the generality of what people are saying simply because theirs is tainted with corruption and lies. Sometimes, you may be surprised seeing fellow believers toeing the path of popularism instead of the path of righteousness and you feel lonely and despised. I am  here to tell you that you are loved and not forgotten. You are not the first to feel isolated because you think, talk and act differently. There were other prophets in the time of Jeremiah who "prophelied", they made Jeremiah look odd because he stood for the truth. Despite all this, he didn't waver neither did he turn back from the path of righteousness. *Don't feel bad when the world around you hate you for not compromising*.


The life of a believer is not meant to conform to the normal day-to-day life on earth: *"If you belonged to the world, it would love you as its own. As it is, you do not belong to the world, but I have chosen you out of the world. That is why the world hates you*" (John15:19). That's why Jesus said the path we walk is a narrow one. The narrow path is a lonely path. Only a few walk in it.*"But small is the gate and narrow the road that leads to life, and only a few find it*" (Mt7:14). Keep walking on that path, Jesus is seeing all you're going through. This journey will soon come to an end and we'll meet our master to give us our reward. Your goal is to keep walking on that path of righteousness despite the hatred of men. You may have been hated by men and isolated but know this, you're loved by God.


*PRAYER*: Father, thank you for loving me. I receive the grace to stand for the truth in Jesus name.

8/16/21, 6:52 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: PROJECTSAVE DEVOTIONAL

August 16th,  2021.

*DO NOT BE A VESSEL OF TEMPTATION*

Luke 17:1 One day Jesus said to his disciples, “There will always be temptations to sin, but what sorrow awaits the person who does the tempting! NLT

My pastor told us about an evangelist back in the days. This evangelist saw a lady dressed immodestly while walking. He looked on not knowing an electric pole was in his front, he was carried away.  After hitting his head on the pole, he came back to his senses. It is hilarious but true. The flames of lust was fanned in the heart of that man because of what he saw. In the opening text, Jesus made it clear that there will always be temptations to sin. In this depraved world, we cannot put an end to temptation but we can run from it. We can guard our hearts against falling into temptation and run from all appearance of evil. In matters such as described above, all we need is to exercise self control. You don't need to be lost in lust while looking around carelessly.


That said, I'll like to address the issue of the tempter. There are times some believers surrender themselves to the devil to be used as agents of temptation. Years back,  I belonged to a gathering of believers where indecent dressing was the norm from the pulpit to the pew. There were days we got to the fellowship seeing cleavages of those ministering on the altar and turning right and left, no one looked different among the sisters. One of the brothers through whom I attended the fellowship told me he stirred at a lady's butt throughout one of the fellowship meetings. He promised to tell her how much he admired what he saw whenever they meet. I called myself to order and I left that fellowship. I was there to serve God but my mind wandered often because of what I saw. In verse two of the opening text, talking about the tempter, Jesus said, *"It would be better to be thrown into the sea with a millstone hung around your neck than to cause one of these little ones to fall into sin.*"


As a believer, do not be a stumbling block to another person. The way you hang around that sister, hug and call her pet names may be doing more damage than good.  How you dress matter a lot. Don't tell me it doesn't matter neither should you say "I don't care what people think or say about me." There's something the bible call the attire of an harlot( Prov7:10). The moment a princess put it on, what everyone begins to see is an harlot even though she's a princess. One of the reasons why some ladies attract irresponsible guys is because of their dressing even though they're responsible. Stop dressing to slay, you're not a slayer as a child of God. You're meant to be a builder. Brothers are not exempted in this. Don't let the people around you mistake you for the mad man at Gadarene.  One of the things the bible said him after legions of demons left him was: *"When they came to Jesus, they saw the man who had been possessed by the legion of demons, sitting there, DRESSED AND IN HIS RIGHT MIND; and they were afraid.*"( Mk5:15). Your indecent dressing is affecting someone negatively and may push them to hell. Always remember that there's a "woe" on anyone who makes others fall. Jesus Himself declared it: *"Woe to the world because of the things that cause people to stumble! Such things must come, BUT WOE TO THE PERSON THROUGH WHOM THEY COME!"*. Mt18:7.

*PRAYER*: Father, I will not cause any of your children to fall. I receive grace in Jesus name.

*DO NOT BLOCK THE PATHWAY*

Psalms25:14 The secret of the LORD is with them that fear him; and he will shew them his covenant.

Several years ago, I woke up to pray at night. After saying some words, I slept off. The Lord showed me a dream. In that dream, i saw the death of a female elder in my church. The night I had that dream was a Saturday night preceding Sunday. When I woke up from that dream, instead of praying against it, I slept. When we got to church in the morning, news came that the female elder God revealed to me at night died. I felt bad because God gave me the opportunity to avert her death in prayers but I failed in my responsibility as an intercessor. Whenever her husband (who is late now) stands to talk in church about how he missed his wife, I always feel a sharp emotional pain as though I killed her. I missed an opportunity to save a life.



God doesn't reveal things to us for fun. It is simply because He wants us to take action. The moment you fail to take action on divine revelation, God will look for another vessel who is yielded and responsible. God do replace people. Some have blocked the pathway of receiving revelations from God because on several occasions, God trusted them with information for immediate kingdom action but they neglected it. *If God no longer show you deep secrets like before, check how you handled the previous secrets He shared with you*.



*There are things God will share with you for your knowledge only but for you to take private personal action. At other times, God expect you to share what He reveals to the brethren for corporate action*. In all, you must be sensitive to know what ought to be done per time. God needs men who will pray out His will and establish His order in the scheme of things on earth. *The moment you stand in the place of an intercessor for men, nations and institutions, God will open a pathway of receiving revelations for you so that you can pray out His will*. When He does, do not block that  pathway through laziness in the place of prayer and negligence.


*PRAYER*: Father, I receive the grace to take prompt action in the name of Jesus Christ.

wednesday,

*PRIESTLY PRAYER*

Rev1:6 and has made us to be a kingdom and priests to serve his God and Father—to him be glory and power for ever and ever! Amen. NIV


Years back, the Lord woke me up around 3 am to pray. The prompting in my spirit was to pray for the nation of Afghanistan. I prayed for 3 hours not knowing why I did. I didn't bother about my personal requests though I had them, Afghanistan was my focus. Recent happenings in that country refreshed my memory about what I did  years back under the leading of the Spirit. If I had known, I would have continually prayed for that country and even raise a prayer altar for that nation among the brethren. *There's a dimension of prayer I call the priestly prayer. It is an intercessory kind of prayer whose focus is not about self but others. It is a dimension in God where you ask God what He wants you to pray about not you telling God what you want.* In this dimension of prayer, you start receiving insight about heaven's agenda that require prayers.


Our generation do not understand this dimension of prayer because we often go to God to pray about ourselves, we see prayer as a means to an end. We are quick to pray from prayer books instead of asking God "what do you want me to pray about." It will surprise you to know that God also has "prayer requests." Not that He is in need if anything, no He doesn't need anything. However, He has  programs for the earth and mankind that requires the input of man. He would have enforced His will on earth automatically but He wouldn't do that because Adam sold out his right as the emperor of the earth in the garden of Eden and the devil became the  god of this world.  Kenneth E.Hagin would say, "he(Adam) doesn't have the moral right to do so but he does have the legal right". Since the devil became the god of this world, he also came up with his agenda for the earth and for humanity(2Cor4:4). *The devil being the god of this world doesn't mean he is the owner of the earth. The earth is the LORD's and the fullness thereof(Psalms 24:1) but the rulership of the earth that God gave to man(Psalms115:16) was handed over to Satan*.


That's why there are wars, insurgencies, natural disasters, multiplication of iniquity, and other negative mishaps on earth. God is not happy seeing  all these.  However, He has program for the earth and for mankind but He cannot do much except man get Him involved through intercessory prayers. God is looking for men and women who will step into the priestly role of intercessors, a go-between, someone to stand in the gap. *"And I sought for a man among them, that should make up the hedge, and stand in the gap before me for the land, that I should not destroy it: but I found none.*" (Ezekiel 22:30). Men who will enter into the place of  prayer to ask, "Lord, what item on your agenda should I pray about"? What do you want to do in India, Pakistan, Belize that requires prayers? Men who will also stop the agenda of the enemy on their knees. Men who will not be comfortable seeing devastations, religious crisis and terrorism without praying about it. Men who will have needs but will make the kingdom agenda their priority in prayer. Men who will pray tirelessly for souls, missionaries and those on the field. These are the men God is looking for. *Our calling as priests is not a thing of status, rather it places on us an intercessory responsibility.*


*PRAYER*: Father, I will not fail in my priestly role in Jesus name. Lord, baptize me with the zeal to pray priestly prayers henceforth in Jesus name

DEVOTIONAL 0243 | Source start line: 23055

8/19/21, 6:34 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: PROJECTSAVE DEVOTIONAL
Thursday,
August 19th, 2021.


*WHEN THE HEART AND THE SPIRIT PRAYS*


Romans 8:26-27 And the Holy Spirit helps us in our weakness. For example, we don’t know what God wants us to pray for. But the Holy Spirit prays for us with groanings that cannot be expressed in words. And the Father who knows all hearts knows what the Spirit is saying, for the Spirit pleads for us believers in harmony with God’s own will.


Quite a number of believers want to have a sound prayer life. They have a mental picture of what a very great prayer life is and some even desire to be prayerful like a brother or sister they see as a model in that regard.  However, when it is time to pray, they feel cold or weak about it. At other times, we get to the place of prayer but we do not know what we ought to pray about. The bible calls it a weakness or infirmity. This prayer "sickness" is common to all but God has  provided a way out- the Holy Spirit. When you engage the Holy Spirit, He will help you to pray the perfect will of God by giving you utterances beyond intelligible words.



More often than not, we enter into prayer believing we have perfect words to present to God but we fail to realize our limitations. When you are lost for words in prayer, allow the Spirit give you utterances in another language: *"And the Father who knows all hearts knows what the Spirit is saying, for the Spirit pleads for us believers in harmony with God’s own will.*" (vs 27). That's one of the essence of baptism with the Holy Spirit. *You can pray in a known language and pray amiss but you cannot pray in tongues and pray amiss*. That's my summary of verse 27. Stop struggling to pray, just surrender to the Holy Spirit. You are limited but He is not. You may have certain heart desires but may not know how to present them to God perfectly. Some start repeating words thinking God will hear them instead of praying in tongues. That's an unbiblical way to pray. Allow the Spirit help you in prayer.



Another important thing in prayer is that you can engage your heart. It is my believe that there are times we can pray with unspoken words. Prayer doesn't necessarily have to be verbalized or spoken loud. The best verse to indicate the validity of unspoken prayers is 1 Thessalonians 5:17: “Pray without ceasing.” To pray unceasingly obviously cannot mean we are praying out loud all of the time. Rather, it means we are to be in a constant state of God-consciousness. This is very important. *There are times when communion with God has to be silent and contemplative. This art is lost today among the Charismatics because we don't believe we have prayed until we shout*. Sometimes, we make so much noise and end up saying nothing. We must learn to focus our heart on God and stay that way. There are times words aren't flowing because the Spirit may want us to be still in God's presence.  Unceasing prayer will include prayers that are spoken, whispered, shouted, sung, and silent as we direct our thoughts of praise, petition, supplication, and thanksgiving to God. *True prayer is not about words that are spoken or unspoken, it is about the heart that is in communion with God  and focused on Him alone.*



*PRAYER*: Father, I receive the grace to have an effective and fruitful prayer life in Jesus name

8/20/21, 5:14 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: PROJECTSAVE DEVOTIONAL
Friday,

*STIRRING THE POWER OF HIS PRESENCE*

2 Kings 3:15 But now bring me a harpist.” While the harpist was playing, the hand of the LORD came on Elisha


Three kings( Israel, Judah &Edom) set out to engage the king of Moab in battle. After a roundabout march of seven days, the army had no more water for themselves or for the animals with them so Jehoshaphat insisted they inquire from the Lord through a prophet. Elisha was suggested and they went to him. Reading from that text, it was obvious Elisha was angry with the king of Israel because he clung to other god apart from Yahweh. Also, he had his own prophets who weren't of God but because of king Jehoshaphat, Elisha obliged their request to inquire from God. However,  In that state, it was impossible to hear God clearly so he demanded for a minstrel. As the minstrel played, the hand of God came upon Elisha and he prophesied. He stirred the Spirit of God.



That's a big lesson there for us all especially ministers of the Gospel. You can stir God's power, you can provoke prophecies. You can stir the Spirit to move.  Smith Wigglesworth of blessed memory said, "If the Spirit doesn't move me, I'll move the Spirit." *It doesn't mean we can force the Spirit of God to perform but in context of this teaching, what we are saying is that we can make room and create the type of atmosphere needed for the Spirit of God to move.* There's a kind of atmosphere where the Spirit of God moves. In Acts 13:2, they created that atmosphere as they worshipped and ministered unto the Lord then the Spirit of God spoke: *"While they were WORSHIPING THE LORD AND FASTING, the Holy Spirit said, “Set apart for me Barnabas and Saul for the work to which I have called them.”*


*You can operate in the supernatural and flow with the Holy Spirit if you understand how. The Holy Spirit is ever available for us but He loves to move in an atmosphere of worship*. He is everywhere but His manifest presence isn't everywhere unless we unlock it. Some persons wonder why miracles and healings of great dimensions occur in programs where Jesus is lifted and worshipped yet doesn't occur on the streets where there are blind beggars, cripple and weaklings. What we have discussed may be the reason except in cases where God's sovereignty play out. *When we make room for the Holy Spirit and the heart of the people is connected by faith, there'll be a manifestation of the supernatural*.


*ACTION POINT*: Around yourself in the house, office, car and even in the toilet, make room for the Holy Ghost.

DEVOTIONAL 0245 | Source start line: 23183

8/21/21, 6:15 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: ProjectSave Devotional
Saturday,
August 21st, 2021.

*TWO EXTREMES IN WARFARE* Pt.1

1 Pet 5:8 Stay alert! Watch out for your great enemy, the devil. He prowls around like a roaring lion, looking for someone to devour. NLT

*UNDERRATING THE ENEMY*

When I was a little boy of 6 years, I lived in my village with my dad,siblings and cousins. We relocated from Lagos to be with dad because he was pastoring in our home town at that time. As a 'Lagos boy', I felt different in that environment and I carried myself that way. One of my cousins who lived with us was fondly called 'Lagos boy'. On a certain day after we returned from school, we stayed with our neighbour temporarily waiting to gain entrance into our house. A young stunted looking boy who hawked fufu ( a local solid food made from cassava) came around to sell. He was dark and unassuming. My cousins told me I can beat him. Mind you, he didn't look for my trouble but they just wanted me to prove myself because I was boastful. I told them I'll finish him. I relied on my knowledge of wrestling and I underrated the boy because he looked like someone who will pass out after one blow.

The young lad understood that they were setting us up for a fight. He put his fufu tray down and he decided to fight. He gave me three powerful head butt, that was all he did, I found myself lying on the floor crying. It was a total knockout. Immediately, I demanded for cassava flakes (garri) because the defeat triggered serious hunger,   It was as if my head hit a rock. I drank garri in tears. Everyone there laughed at the 'Lagos boy' who boasted he could beat the village boy. Till I left the village, I didn't pick a fight with anyone, I avoided the type of humiliation I suffered at all cost. I learnt a big lesson from that incident: "In warfare, do not underrate the enemy".


Some have an underrated view of the enemy we are dealing with while some also overrate him. These are two extremes in warfare we must avoid. Some persons just have this "unhealthy" view that satan cannot do anything anymore. Their misguided belief is that salvation is an exclusion from the attacks of the enemy. Such folks loose guard spiritually and often suffer serious defeat. In some cases, people in this category may become philosophical with their challenges by assuming it is natural instead of rebuking the devil and standing against him vehemently. *If truly Satan can no longer launch an attack, the injunction in 1Pet5:8 and Eph6:10-18 would not be necessary*. You need to be sensitive at all times to know when the devil is at work. In your life, family and ministry, you need to draw the boundary line for him and tell him never to cross it. He'll always try to cross but you must take your stand in the victory of the cross. *The life of faith is not an assumption that the devil has gone on holiday, no it is not. Rather, it is a life that requires being on guard at all times by standing in faith on the victory of the cross.* 1 John5:4 says: *"For every child of God defeats this evil world, and we achieve this victory through our faith*", NLT.


*PRAYER*: Father, I thank you for the victory on the cross. I receive grace to stand in faith and never loose guard in the name of Jesus Christ.

DEVOTIONAL 0246 | Source start line: 23258

8/22/21, 6:01 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: ProjectSave Devotional

*TWO EXTREMES IN WARFARE* Pt.2


1 Pet 5:8 Stay alert! Watch out for your great enemy, the devil. He prowls around like a roaring lion, looking for someone to devour. NLT


*OVERRATING THE ENEMY*


I heard the story of a brother who invited another brother to come pray for him because he felt he was under attack seeing frogs around his house. The brother that was invited got to his house having received the invitation to come for deliverance prayers. When he got there, the brother who claimed he was under attack narrated to him his experience about the invasion of frogs. Before this brother prayed, he walked around the house just to check the surroundings. He found a pond that bred frogs and he told his friend that the frog attack was not spiritual but because of the pond. Now, that describes how so many believers are demon conscious even when the fault is all theirs or something they can fix with the application of common sense.


*When you overrate the enemy, you will live in constant fear*. Fear in itself is of the devil and not of God. *"For God hath not given us the spirit of fear; but of power, and of love, and of a sound mind.*"(2Tim1:7). There are believers whose surroundings are not clean but when they see cockroaches, they'll scream "blood of Jesus" thinking a demon has visited. Such folks have an overrated view of the enemy. Those who operate that way rarely think straight neither do they have clear minds on issues because they are enemy conscious. I have seen believers who live in the consciousness of demons and enemy. Hardly will they pray without mentioning something about the enemy that is after them. *The worst that happens when believers overrate the enemy is when they begin to ascribe mightiness to Satan above God*. Here in Africa, some still believe you should pay homage to demons where necessary and perform rites when demanded. Such persons have not divorced from superstitions and certain traditional beliefs for fear of repercussions. That's why some pregnant woman attach pin to the edge of their gowns to prevent giving birth to a demonic child.



*Until you know who you are in Christ and what He did to Satan during his death, burial and resurrection, you'll never be free from fear of the enemy.* This is what Ephesians2:6 has to say about us as believers: *"And God raised us up with Christ and seated us with him in the heavenly realms in Christ Jesus,*". Hallelujah!!! Your position in Christ is far above where demons, curses, household witches, wizards can reach. Further, in Colossians 3:3, Paul said, *"For you died, and your life is now hidden with Christ in God.*". You need not run from your village because of that witch. Your life is hidden with Christ IN GOD. Hallelujah!!! Anyone after your life must get Jesus first and then get God before getting you. That's the implication. The devil is a defeated foe; in Colossians 2:15, it was said that: *"Having disarmed principalities and powers, He made a public spectacle of them, triumphing over them in it.*". After resurrection, in Matthew 28:18, Jesus Himself said, *"...All authority in heaven and on earth has been given to me.*" Stop making Satan and His cohorts your subject of prayers at all times, the attention you're giving Him is unnecessary. He likes it. Just put him where he belongs using the name of Jesus Christ and never stop looking unto Him. That's how to fight and win in this warfare.



*CONFESSION*: I am born of God. I overcome the world. I am washed by the blood and redeemed to reign and rule over the enemy. I refuse to bow in shame and fear if the enemy. I rise as a son to take my place in redemption in the name of Jesus Christ.


Song of the day: https://youtu.be/P8ajtJtNXF4

@lanreoyeleke

8/23/21, 7:18 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: PROJECTSAVE DEVOTIONAL
Monday,
August 23rd,  2021.

*THERE'S HOPE FOR THAT CHILD*

1 Thessalonians 5:17 Never stop praying. NLT


A devout Christian mother had a very wayward son who refused to yield to the godly training he was given. He was recalcitrant and rebellious. This young boy was a thorn in her mother's flesh but she kept praying for him. He grew to become a man but didn't change his ways. At old age, his mother kept praying for him until she died. On a certain day, this young man had an accident and he became unconscious. He had an out of body experience and saw himself walking towards hell. He was afraid because he felt the pains and anguish in that atmosphere. As he was about to be thrown into hell, a voice stopped the demons. God opened his eyes to see how his late mother prayed for his salvation while on earth. On the strength of her prayers while on earth, God showed him mercy and he came back to life. Without struggle, he  gave his life to Christ and became devoted to the Lord.




The prayer of that Christian mother was stored up for the right time. She left time and transited into eternity but her prayer  didn't. That goes to show the efficacy of persistent prayer. Dear Christian parent, don't give up on that child who seems wayward and unyielding. I know the shame, pain and regrets that comes with being a devout Christian parent who follow all that is taught and laid down in healthy parenting yet without result. If you have done all you know to do without positive result, never stop praying and showing love. Prayer is still a very potent weapon to turn things around. It may not happen immediately but be rest assured that it will surely happen.  There are reasons best known to God on why certain expectations linger.


Do not give up on that child. Do not curse him/her. There's hope for that child: *"At least there is hope for a tree: If it is cut down, it will sprout again, and its new shoots will not fail*" (Job14:7). I know how shameful it is to have such a child especially if other siblings are godly. You may begin to think about what you did that made you succeed on others and why you failed on this particular one. The more you think and subject yourself to reasoning ( though not bad) you'll always end in one form of guilt and condemnation. Why not make heavy prayer investment into the life of that child? At some point in my life as a Pastor's child, I did unprintable things not because I don't know the truth but because I was under the control of powers stronger than me. However, there was an invisible hand that directed the course of my life and made me who I am today. Only prayer could have done that. In parenting, prayer is what binds all others together. You can chart the course of godliness for that child on your knees. You can break unholy alliances and relationships on your knees and realign your children on the right path.


*PRAYER*: Father, I pray for (...insert name of Children), may his/ her heart be captured by the Gospel. May he/she be convicted about sin by the power of the Spirit and may he be restored completely in Jesus name.

DEVOTIONAL 0248 | Source start line: 23501

8/24/21, 6:46 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: ProjectSave Devotional
Tuesday
August 24th,  2021.

*GIVE THIS TEACHING A TITLE*

1 Tim6:10 For the love of money is a root of all kinds of evil. Some people, eager for money, have wandered from the faith and pierced themselves with many griefs.

Reading about the Charismatic revival in Nigeria in the 60s and 70s, it was recorded that one of the things that extinguished the fire of revival was the love of money and materialism that crept into the body of Christ. The pursuit of God changed to the pursuit of what God can give. Men began to preach for money, live for money and money became a master instead of being a servant. Matthew 6:24, *"No man can serve two masters: for either he will hate the one, and love the other; or else he will hold to the one, and despise the other. Ye cannot serve God and mammon.*" Mammon is a Syriac deity that symbolizes wealth and materialism. While some persons may not be worshipping Mammon in the shrine, they worship him in their hearts right inside the church using bible in a twisted form to support their worship.


*Till today, one demonic ploy of the enemy to water down the truth of the Gospel is when God is presented as a means to an end*. Anywhere instant breakthroughs and miracle money is promised may end up with the tag "fastest growing church". *People now use the Gospel to preach American dream. Oh! Sorry, the Gospel is not a message about American dream but the life of God*.    In the days of Jesus Christ on earth, none could put him on the list of richest pastors in the world in their time not because he was poor but simply because His emphasis wasn't on materialism. He wasn't in contest with anyone. His message was simply about the kingdom yet he lacked nothing. Contest and competition is real in today's world because someone wants to be regarded as "the largest", "the biggest" and so on but the desire to be like Christ and to preach Him only is absent. Paul said, *"For I determined not to know anything among you except Jesus Christ and Him crucified.*"(1 Cor2:2).

If you attend a church service and you leave with the motivation to pursue wealth and material things only, you have attended a business class not a church. The aftermath of quality sermon in a living church should make you desire Christ more and to be like Him. Paul said, *"That I may know Him...*" but our generation is saying, *"That we may use Him*". Jesus warned in Luke12:15, *“...Beware! Guard against every kind of greed. Life is not measured by how much you own.”*  How much of wealth and material things you have is not a good measurement of the quality of your life. Stop living for material things and start living for the kingdom. *Money is a good servant but a bad master*. I always ask, " If believers are chasing what unbelievers have without having God, what difference will the Gospel that we have accepted make in our lives"? Except we want to deny that there are no unbelievers that do not have these things. Our pursuit should be God and His kingdom, other things shall be added. *Your salvation is so precious, worth more than gold and silver. Guard it jealously and work it out daily with fear and trembling*.

*PRAYER*: Father, I refuse to be a slave of money in Jesus name.

DEVOTIONAL 0249 | Source start line: 23590
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
8/25/21, 12:33 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: PROJECTSAVE DEVOTIONAL
wednesday,
August 25th, 2021.

*GOD AGAINST THE WORLD SYSTEM* Pt.1

1John2: 15 Love not the world, neither the things that are in the world. If any man love the world, the love of the Father is not in him.

*LOVE NOT THE WORLD*

I read a fictional story on the internet about a man who just bought a brand-new BMW car. As he opened the door to get out of the car, another car sped by and tore off the door of the car. He got out and he was starring at his car. And a bystander came and said, 'Sir, don't look at your car. Look at your arm.' He asks, 'What's wrong with my arm?' 'It's torn off!' And the man then looks at his arm on the floor and says, 'Oh no, my Rolex watch!'.  From the same online source, I read that when a man falls down with an iPhone 6 in his pocket and he hears a 'crack' sound, he will pray, 'Lord, let it be my leg.' (laughter). This story is a testament to the fact that humans can love the 'things' of the world so much at the expense of their well being because the world has a lure for everyone of us even Christians.

When we talk about the world, we are not referring to the people in the world because God so loved the world ( referring to humans in John3:16) to the extent of sending His only begotten son to die  neither are we referring to the physical planet earth. *'The world' in context of the opening text refers to the system that governs the affairs of this world.* It is the system of practices associated with the standards of the society without reference to any demands or requirements of God. It also refers to the influence the system of the world exerts on us. *The world is a world of seductive material things& an ungodly system or pattern of living.* When God warned us not to love the world through Apostle John, He wasn't saying having a Ferrari or iPhone 7 is bad rather he was referring to the influence the devil exerts on us through those things. Often, I tell people that material things aren't really the problem but how those things influence us and take us away from God. The world uses the things in it to seduce us and take us away from God. *The greatest threat to our walk with God is not persecution from the world, but seduction by the world( material things&ungodly pattern of living).*


It is impossible to love the world and love God at the same time. Apostle James corroborated this in James 4:4 saying, *"You adulterers! Don’t you realize that friendship with the world makes you an enemy of God? I say it again: If you want to be a friend of the world, you make yourself an enemy of God.*" Satan knew that the world has a strong alluring and seductive power. When he tempted Jesus the third time, he presented the kingdom of the world and its glories to Him on a platter of gold but Christ refused it. *'The   world' Jesus rejected is what some are praying and doing vigils to have. They go to prayer meetings to ask for 'the world'*. I remember I was once caught in that trap after school, pre NYSC. I went to camp to pray about getting a car in less than 3 months into NYSC because I wanted to enjoy life my own way. Then, i told God how i'll drive around doing evangelism but was that really my motive? I rolled on the floor praying instead of asking God to place me right at the centre of His will for my NYSC. In His mercies, He put me where He wanted me to be - full time ministry. Surprisingly, what i desired came months after i left service when i didn't even pray or fast for it. When the heart of a believer is captured by the world, he/she will find scriptures and reasons to back it up. *Your love for God is not certain and sure until the throne of 'the world' is removed from your heart. Do you love God or the world?*


*PRAYER*: Father, purge from my heart all the traces and deposits of worldliness in Jesus name.

DEVOTIONAL 0250 | Source start line: 23654
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
8/26/21, 12:15 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: PROJECTSAVE DEVOTIONAL
Thursday,
August 26th, 2021.


*GOD AGAINST THE WORLD SYSTEM* Pt.2


1John2:16 For all that is in the world, the lust of the flesh, and the lust of the eyes, and the pride of life, is not of the Father, but is of the world.


*LUST OF THE FLESH*

In the world, there are three major things that drive those that are trapped and they are: the lust
of the flesh, lust of the eyes and pride of life. All sins are  categorized under these three drivers. We shall examine the lust of the flesh first. What is it? What does it really mean? We need to understand what lust is first. *Lust is simply inordinate desire. Often, we associate it with sex but it is more than that. Inordinate desires are not limited to sex though it is an integral part of it*. One can lust after material things. What is "flesh"? Flesh refers to human nature that is corrupted by sin. When we talk about " flesh" in Christian circles, I do see some persons touching their bodies referring to it as flesh. No, that's not what the bible is saying. Flesh is just a sinful nature, it refers to all of human tendencies to misbehave and go against God's will.  *Therefore, the lust of the flesh refers to the inordinate desires of the human nature that are in contrast to the will of God*.


*The world is engineered to stir in us fleshly passions and lust. It is a very dangerous thing to still have ties with the world after salvation because the allures of the world ignites sinful flames in the flesh.* Sometimes, have you ever wondered why you fall into a sin you thought you have conquered? The simple reason that is happening is because you undermine the power of the flesh and gave room for its expression. Apostle Paul knew this to be true that is why he said:  *"But put on the Lord Jesus Christ, and make no provision for the flesh, to fulfill its lusts*" ( Rom13:14). Abstinence from anything that can trigger fleshly lust is the first key to victory. In 1 Peter 2:11, apostle Peter said: *"Dearly beloved, I beseech you as strangers and pilgrims, abstain from fleshly lusts, which war against the soul;"*. Don't stay around anything or anyone that triggers inordinate desires in you. *If you struggle to keep your sanity anytime you shake hands or have body contacts with the opposite sex, simply abstain*.




Apostle Paul also told us to "mortify" the flesh. Romans 8:13 says,  *"For if ye live after the flesh, ye shall die: but if ye through the Spirit do mortify the deeds of the body, ye shall live."* The word mortify means “to kill or subdue.” In a biblical context, to mortify is to subdue the flesh. We get our English words mortuary and mortician from the same Latin root that gives us mortify. Therefore, mortification of sin / the flesh is the “killing” of sin and the flesh through discipline and self denial. This requires a level of consciousness by saying "no" to whatever the flesh wants. The flesh loves pleasure, sex( illicit fantasies and adventures), food( gluttony), sleep( in excess), social activities( that aren't edifying and glorifying God). You can mortify the flesh through deprivation. Lastly, we can overcome the lust of the flesh by crucifying it:  *"I am crucified with Christ: nevertheless I live; yet not I, but Christ liveth in"*( Gal2:20). This connotes living like a disciple of Christ- a follower, an imitator. As long as we are alive, these are the things we have to continually do to live in victory over the flesh. Victory yesterday may not sustain you today, we must deal with the flesh daily.

*PRAYER*: I receive grace to live in victory over the flesh.

DEVOTIONAL 0251 | Source start line: 23692

8/27/21, 6:32 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: PROJECTSAVE DEVOTIONAL
Friday,

*GOD AGAINST THE WORLD SYSTEM* Pt.3


1John2:16 For all that is in the world, the lust of the flesh, and the lust of the eyes, and the pride of life, is not of the Father, but is of the world.


*LUST OF THE EYES*

Recently, I told the story of a man who looked at a lady that was seductively dressed while walking on the road. He looked on not knowing there was an electric pole ahead. He didn't stop looking until he collided with the pole. That's a simple example of how lust of the eyes operates. We have said in previous teaching that lust is inordinate desire. Uncontrolled looking can stir lust in the heart.  *Lust of the eyes is the inordinate desire to possess what we see or to have those things which have visual appeal.* We live in a world of visual appeal. The adverts you see on t.v., trending fashion and designs, are designed to stir lust in you so that you can run after those things and make them your prime pursuit.

*The first sin lust of the eyes trigger in us is covetousness. The devil makes us to focus on the world and the things in it so that we can covet them. The moment covetousness sets in, desperation to possess follows.* The foolishness of covetousness is that it desires things that doesn't last, things that fade off and become irrelevant with time. Some of  those who lived in the 60s&70s used the Volkswagen beetles car. They were the big boys of that time. Some stole money and defrauded people to buy that car. Today, if anyone takes pride in owning that brand and model of Volkswagen, they'll see him/her as backward because there are modern cars with better functions. Unfortunately, what you call "modern, new model and trendy" today shall fade off and become unappealing tomorrow yet that is what the devil is making many to labour, steal and kill for.


In the decalogue, God addressed the lust of the eyes in its prohibition against coveting. Exodus 20:17 commands, *“You shall not covet your neighbor’s house; you shall not covet your neighbor’s wife, or his male servant, or his female servant, or his ox, or his donkey, or anything that is your neighbor’s.”* Coveting can include a desire to have people, possessions, or status. The tactic of Satan is to use a visual image to trap us. He did it to Eve and she fell. Eve looked at the forbidden fruit and saw that it was “pleasing to the eye” (Genesis 3:6). He tried the same tactic on Jesus, he “showed him all the kingdoms of the world and their splendor” (Matthew 4:8). He then promised to give them to Jesus—for a price. Satan won't stop using this trick to get believers. Those whose hearts are not set on things above will always be trapped, they'll find themselves in endless pursuit of whatever they see. The antidote is to shift focus: *"Set your minds on things above, not on earthly things."* ( Col3:2).


*ACTION POINT*: Fix your gaze on Jesus and you'll never be trapped: *"Looking unto Jesus the author and finisher of our faith; who for the joy that was set before him endured the cross, despising the shame, and is set down at the right hand of the throne of God.*"( Heb12:2).
