DEVOTIONAL 0252 | Source start line: 23729
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
8/28/21, 4:00 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: ProjectSave Devotional
Saturday,
August 28th, 2021.

*GOD AGAINST THE WORLD SYSTEM* Pt.4

1John2:16 For all that is in the world, the lust of the flesh, and the lust of the eyes, and the pride of life, is not of the Father, but is of the world.

*PRIDE OF LIFE*

I read the story of Muhammad Ali on the internet about what happened between him and an air hostess. Once he was on an airplane and he often said, “I'm the greatest.” The air hostess looked at him and saw he didn't have his seatbelt fastened. She said to him, “Mr. Ali, you'll have to fasten your seatbelt.” Ali responded, “Superman don't need seatbelt.” The hostess then replied, “Superman don't need airplane either.” A short study on the phrase "pride of life" in the Greek shows that Apostle John was referring to the pride that results from having acquired possession, property and livelihood. This is the outcome of "lust of the eyes". A man that has lived to satisfy the lust of his eyes by pursuing and acquiring earthly things will end up being proud of his "achievements".


The Greek "Bios" is the word translated life and it implies the biological life. Whereas, this is what Jesus said about this "life": *"And he said unto them, Take heed and beware of all covetousness; for a man's life consists not in the abundance of the things which he possesses.*" KJV. The man that is possessed by the pride of life will only be living for this "life" and not eternal life. Unfortunately, there are many believers that are trapped by this "life" and nothing more. Some ministers aren't excluded. The acquisition of earthly possessions and properties mean so much to them. It is a thing of "joy" to them as this reflects often in their testimony.  In ministry, the moment we begin to speak comparatively in an emphatic manner about being the "biggest", "largest" repeatedly without restraint, it shows we're trapped in the rat race of this "life" to outdo others.  Having achieve the goal, pride enters and we blow our trumpet. Isn't it happening?


*The triumvirate evil apostle John told us in the opening text is a tactic that has been working from time immemorial and will continue to work because the devil has no new trick. He only modifies it.* The temptations of Jesus Christ is an example of each. When the devil told Jesus to jump from the top of the building so that angels can catch him IF truly He was the son of God, the devil was tempting to see if pride of life was in Jesus. How? He wanted him to prove a point about his sonship. *Anytime you're tempted to prove a point to people about your status and what you have achieved, you're already close to being trapped by the pride of life*. We all need to submit the whole of our life to God in its entirety so that these triune evil( lust of the flesh,  lust of the eyes and pride of life) won't find expression in us.

*PRAYER*: Lord, search my heart and rid me of these evil in Jesus name. I refuse to submit to the dictates and principles of these triune forces in Jesus name. Amen


====================================================================================================
DEVOTIONAL 0253 | Source start line: 23771
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
8/29/21, 12:02 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: ProjectSave Devotional
Sunday,
August 29th, 2021.


*GOD AGAINST THE WORLD SYSTEM* Pt.5


1 John 2:17 And this world is fading away, along with everything that people crave. But anyone who does what pleases God will live forever. NLT


*PLEASING GOD IN A FADING WORLD*

Sister Judith applied for a number of jobs after school. She wrote several aptitude tests to get her dream job but one of those interviews looked more like she was closer to achieving her dreams. When she got to the panel, the head of the panel was impressed with her résumé and performance in the written interview. The panel examined her credentials and discussed with her about the prospect of landing a job in their coy. Total monthly income was $2000. To her, it looked like an answer to prayer after waiting for eight years doing petty jobs and receiving meagre salary. Days after the interview just before she was told to pick up her appointment letter, the executive director of the company called her on phone asking for a date with her in one of the big hotels in town. The man told her point blank that picking up her appointment letter was dependent on her acceding to his request. She thought about how she had suffered while waiting to get a dream job but the Spirit of God asked her this simple question: "Will you please God and reign with Him eternally or please man and lose your soul"? She got the message and declined the offer.


Now we know the world is driven by principles and systems that are anti God. The triune forces of lust of the flesh, lust of the eyes and pride of life rules the world. Apostle John further told us the world is fading already. *Right from after the fall of man, the world has entered into a fading mode that is why trends don't last*. The fashions and designs of this world are always changing from time to time. Even the physical heaven and earth will fade away. At the end of time, God will melt the elements of heaven and this world with fervent heat because this world wasn't created to last forever: *"But the day of the Lord will come like a thief. The heavens will disappear with a roar; the elements will be destroyed by fire, and the earth and everything done in it will be laid bare*", 2Pet 3:10.


The words of Jesus confirms this truism: *"Heaven and earth will pass away, but my words will never pass away*"( Matthew24:35). Why crave for something that will fade away as though it is eternal? Apostle John made it clear that only those that please God will live forever. Living forever after doing the will of God connotes reigning with Christ. *Our primary pursuit should be to please God in all we do*. We haven't said that we won't need the things of this world for our daily sustenance but when they become our prime pursuit and we become desperate about them in a manner that is anti God, then we have been taken over by the spirit that runs the world. *Every waking moment, our prayer and desire should be "father, help me to live a life that is pleasing to you in this fading world."* God honours such request highly. Set your heart on pleasing your heavenly father in this fading world and He will reward you with a royal welcome in His eternal rest.



*PRAYER*: Father, I receive the grace to please you in this fading world in Jesus name.

DEVOTIONAL 0254 | Source start line: 23805
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
8/30/21, 7:22 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: PROJECTSAVE DEVOTIONAL
Monday,
August 30th,  2021.

*RISKY DELEGATION*

Romans8:14 For those who are led by the Spirit of God are the children of God.

Several years back, a woman told me about how a young banker was interested in marrying her daughter but the relationship crumbled. She desired their relationship end on the altar but she felt sad that everything crumbled. When she came to me, she wanted me to pray for the restoration of their courtship but I noticed some moves she had made weren't consistent with my theology. She printed this guy's picture from the internet and distributed it to several prophets who told her that this man was meant for her daughter. She brought the same picture to me so that I can have it in my custody for prayers but I declined with wisdom. I simply told her that I'll pray for God's will to be done. I called her daughter and prayed with her. However, I noticed that what gave her the boldness to come for prayers was because she had consulted wide(probably diviners) and they told her they were meant for each other. She wanted me to hear God for her to validate what others have said and to pray for restoration of their relationship.




*One among several other things you should never delegate is hearing from God. Never try to delegate hearing from God about your life to people no matter how anointed and gifted.* One of the things we often teach leaders and those that have responsibilities to handle is delegation of duty. This is something that all and sundry must know how to do to avoid collapsing under the weight of heavy workload. However, I discovered that there are certain things that are risky to delegate especially spiritual things that makes for growth and depth in God. *One of the things spiritual babes do is to delegate hearing from God to prophets.* They have delegated their duty of hearing from God to prophets. They consult prophets like the heathen do consult diviners. Unfortunately, they put those prophets under the pressure to perform so they manufacture "thus saith the Lord" from the blues. *God may send a prophet/ any of his children to give you a message, but there must be a confirmation in your spirit about what is said.* I don't act on instructions until I'm convinced personally that God is involved.



*Don't take the risk of delegating hearing from God to prophets. They are not to be consulted like diviners.*  God is your father and He can speak to you as well. In life, one of the causes of regret after making a wrong decision is to discover that you'll d have made a better decision without the influence of what a prophet said. Some are biting their fingers in marriage now because their marriage was based on prophetic match-making. Ministry is another dangerous terrain to tread on this matter. *As a minister, you can delegate many things to ease your workload, you can even delegate preaching as it is not even advisable you preach all the time but you should never delegate people to hear from God for you as touching the direction the church/ ministry should go.* The devil sometimes uses lying prophets to derail people from His plans and purposes. Don't be lazy in the place of fellowship with God, He can speak to you.

*PRAYER*: I receive the grace to stay in the secret place to tap into divine secrets in Jesus name.


====================================================================================================
DEVOTIONAL 0255 | Source start line: 23901
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
8/31/21, 6:23 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: ProjectSave Devotional
Tuesday
August 31st,  2021.

*WHO WANTS TO BE A MILLIONAIRE*?

Proverbs 11:30 The fruit of the righteous is a tree of life; and he that winneth souls is wise. KJV

Years back, a t.v. program was on air weekly. The theme was "Who Wants To Be A Millionaire"? It was one of the most educative programs at that time. Participants were asked questions on facts, history, science and other things. Questions answered correctly attracted monetary rewards ranging from several thousands to millions of naira which is the ultimate price. Many young folks and even old adults applied to this program because they wanted to be legitimate millionaires.

*In the kingdom, there's an opportunity for everyone to be a millionaire not in cash or in the currency of this world but in souls. We can be millionaires in the number of souls won to the Lord*. Many are striving to hit millions; several years back, it was common to hear motivational speakers talk about how they made their first million at a certain age especially in their early twenties. Most young folks get motivated to make millions the moment they hear about a young millionaire but not many in the kingdom have the vision to bring in as many souls into the kingdom. The devil is winning souls to hell in numbers. The bible says hell is enlarged to accommodate the numerous souls the devil is winning:  *"Therefore hell hath enlarged herself, and opened her mouth without measure:* and their glory, and
their multitude, and their pomp, and he that rejoiceth, shall descend into it". ( Isa5:14). He's using all tricks and tactics at his disposal to get men to his side.



*If God were to convert the number of souls you've won into cash, how much will it be?* You need to prioritize soul-winning. You can never be too old, too educated, too sophisticated to win souls. The "evangelists of hell" are not ashamed to lure people into perdition through music, fashion, cultism and outright preaching of the philosophies of men, why should you be ashamed to bring people into eternal bliss with God through the preaching of the Gospel? Jesus want us to be vigorous about bringing men into the kingdom. In one of His parables that typified evangelism and souls winning,  he used the word 'compel' to describe how to bring in people( Luke14:23). In the Greek, that word is "Anagkazo". It means to constrain, to use force and entreaties. It is a very strong word. We have to " Anagkazo" men into the kingdom. The opportunity to be a millionaire in souls abound for every believer. The economy of heaven favours it. While you may not be privileged to win millions of souls by head count, you can win souls who in turn will win millions of souls to the Lord. In multilevel marketing, that's a plus to you. Don't neglect the call for soul winning, you are saved to arise for the salvation of others.

*PRAYER*: Father, I receive the grace to be a millionaire in souls in Jesus name.

9/1/21, 7:12 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: PROJECTSAVE DEVOTIONAL
wednesday,

*YOU ARE THE WATCHMAN POSTED BY GOD TO YOUR NATION*

Isaiah 62:6 O Jerusalem, I have posted watchmen on your walls; they will pray day and night, continually. Take no rest, all you who pray to the Lord. NLT

When i studied the current happenings in our nation at the time of writing this devotional, I observed that the devil has made so many believers to be weary about interceding for the nation. Quite of a number of those who were eagerly praying for the nation have lost hope and have joined those who speak negatively about the nation. *When intercessors begin to say what everyone is saying, they repudiate their prayers of yesteryears.* The daily bad news that filters through national t.v.and  social media is the enemy's plan to make us feel God is not interested in our nation any more and to weaken our faith. *The devil will make the worst happen in our dear nation if we stop praying*.

*In every nation, God has posted watchmen there for the purpose of praying night and day. Today, God's children are the watchmen posted to salvage their nation from the claws of wickedness in the place of prayer*. While I agree with the school of thought that doing the right thing at all strata of leadership is what we need to build a great nation, we shouldn't deny the place of continuous intercession for our dear nation. Everything has its place. We often compare ourselves with nations that aren't as prayerful as we are yet they are prospering. We fail to realize that what they are enjoying is the prayer labour of their fore fathers who spent hours in prayer just to see a better nation. Don't also forget their rich history of revivals, awakenings and Reformation. The impact of these events has lasted centuries and outlived their forebears. Unlike nations with idolatrous roots and demonic covenants.

In Psalm122:6, the bible says, *"Pray for the peace of Jerusalem: they shall prosper that love thee.*" Our Jerusalem is our own dear country in this case and we must arise and pray. At this point in our national history, prayer for the nation should be relentless. The word of the Lord says, *'Give the Lord no rest until he completes his work, until he makes Jerusalem the pride of the earth"*.(Isaiah 62:7 NLT). Until our dear nation becomes the pride of the earth where everyone would like to visit or relocate to, we shouldn't stop praying. You are the watchman posted by God to pray for your nation. Intercessors will not come from another planet, God's children must make it a point of duty to pray for their nation even when it seems all is getting worse.

*PRAYER*: Father, I receive the grace to pray for my nation at all times in Jesus name.

9/2/21, 7:13 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: PROJECTSAVE DEVOTIONAL
Thursday,
September 2nd, 2021.


*UPPER ROOM OR SUPPER ROOM CHRISTIAN*?. Pt.1

Acts 1:13 And when they were come in, they went up into an upper room, where abode both Peter, and James, and John, and Andrew, Philip, and Thomas, Bartholomew, and Matthew, James the son of Alphæus, and Simon Zelotes, and Judas the brother of James.

*THE PLACE OF COMMUNION*


In Jewish culture, it is their practice to build a room at the top of the house for prayer and communion with God. Even poor Jews endeavour to build that small room so that they can have deep fellowship with God.  An example of the upper room being used as a place of prayer and worship occurs in the story of Daniel, where he retreats to his upper room to pray, as it was his custom, even when a decree is put out to kill those who worship anyone besides the king, Daniel 6:10. Another  example of the use of the upper room in entreating God’s power can be clearly seen in the miracles performed by Elijah and Elisha in raising the widow and Shunammite’s sons respectively, 1 Kings17:17-24, 2 Kings 4:18-37 . Therefore, it is clear that an upper room was common among the people of Israel, and that it was considered as a place of retreat and prayer, as well as a place where God’s power was shown.



*Today, we may not be privileged to have a physical upper room but figuratively, our hearts can become the upper room that burns for God and yearns for more of Him*. In the early church, after the ascension of Christ, the disciples retreated to the upper room. Why didn't they go to the synagogue? The answer is not far-fetched. God was about to birth the new testament church but not without men who do not have communion with Him in the place of prayer. God needed men with "upper room hearts". *We can't do great things for God if we are not upper room Christians*. The early church began in the upper room but sadly, some churches have become a "supper room" where they are raising men with supper room mentality. It is all about raising millionaires not disciples. It is all about fighting household enemies and imprecatory prayers no sound teaching of the word.


Supper room believers are moved by the perks, the acts of God but not the ways of God. *"He made known His ways to Moses, His acts to the children of Israel.*"( Psl103:7). The acts of God are miracles and those things He does for our benefits but His ways are the secrets behind His acts. They're His principles. The moment supper Christians hear "It is happening here", they switch camp immediately. Always looking for  someone who will declare powerful words on them and tell them "thus saith the Lord" but will never sit in the presence of the same Lord they desire to hear from. Why has revival eluded us in this generation and become history? It is because the upper room has been turned to supper and many that should have a upper room heart now have supper room mentality. *God does nothing until He finds men with deep communion and those with a burning heart that yearns to see His glory.*


*PRAYER*: Father, I receive the grace to have a upper room heart in Jesus name.

9/3/21, 7:24 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: PROJECTSAVE DEVOTIONAL

*UPPER ROOM OR SUPPER ROOM CHRISTIAN*?. Pt.2

Acts 1:14 They all met together and were constantly united in prayer, along with Mary the mother of Jesus, several other women, and the brothers of Jesus. NLT

*A PLACE OF UNITED PRAYER*

I read about the Moravian believers who prayed for 100 years non stop. They had a 24 hours prayer chain that spanned 100 years. I don't know of any church or gathering of believers that has achieved the same feat. Through that continuous united prayer, doors of the Gospel opened around Europe to the Moravians. They had the spirit of believers in the upper room. The bible said the early apostles  were united in prayer, Mary the mother of Jesus, other women and the siblings of Jesus were there.  Their prayer focus was the same and their hearts were knitted together in the place of prayer. I'm sure they weren't praying to Mary neither were they pleading to her to act as an intermediary between them and God.


In other portions of Acts of apostles, these set of believers shook hell with their united prayer and it reverberated here on earth: *"After this prayer, the meeting place shook, and they were all filled with the Holy Spirit. Then they preached the word of God with boldness*" (Acts4:31). You need to read Acts 4 to know the content of what they prayed about unitedly. It was devoid of selfish request, they were praying kingdom focused prayers. The type of prayers that makes God glad. The type of prayer that advances God's kingdom here on earth. There's a reverse today among supper room believers and in supper room churches. The prayer is always imprecatory and it is about self and nothing more. They use prayer as a tool of assassination thereby making God look like hired assassin.



*The day the church returns to bible-based united prayer, we'll take kingdoms for Christ, shake hell, set the captives free and establish God's throne in the hearts of men*. That united prayer was the reason for the angelic deliverance of Peter in Acts 12. The prayer of the upper room was not about cars, houses, neither was it about millions, it was about the kingdom of God. Stop running around seeking for "supper" in prayers. I have not said you shouldn't ask for provisions, please do but it shouldn't be your priority in prayer. *"Seek the Kingdom of God above all else, and live righteously, and he will give you everything you need.*" Matthew 6:33 NLT. As a church leader, use the opportunity you have to lead the people to pray kingdom relevant prayers. Imagine what will happen if all believers all over the world are praying the same prayer point every time they gather, what do you think will happen? If we want to change our country and communities, we need to be united in prayer. That's how to achieve kingdom result as a church.


*PRAYER*: Father, I refuse to be a supper room Christian. My heart is set on kingdom matters and I'll pray about them henceforth in Jesus name.

9/4/21, 6:55 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: ProjectSave Devotional

*UPPER ROOM OR SUPPER ROOM CHRISTIAN*? Pt.3

Acts 2:1-2 And when the day of Pentecost was fully come, they were all with one accord in one place. And suddenly there came a sound from heaven as of a rushing mighty wind, and it filled all the house where they were sitting. KJV

*A PLACE OF ENCOUNTER*

One thing that characterized the upper room experience was the encounter the early believers had with the Holy Ghost. The upper room was where the Holy Spirit came fully upon the church, it was a definite experience that changed the course of church history. They stayed in that room till the promise of the father came: *"On one occasion, while he was eating with them, he gave them this command: “Do not leave Jerusalem, but wait for the gift my Father promised, which you have heard me speak about.*"(Acts1:4). In expectation of what God promised them, they waited patiently until there was an outpouring.


The bible says, *"And there appeared unto them cloven tongues like as of fire, and it sat upon each of them. And they were all filled with the Holy Ghost, and began to speak with other tongues, as the Spirit gave them utterance*"(Acts 2:3-4). While we may not have the physical upper room in our various homes, one thing we must have is the upper room heart. That's the heart that is drawn to God's presence. *The early apostles experienced the outpouring of the Spirit not only because it was promised but because their hearts were positioned for that encounter. There are things God has promised but will only deliver to those with a upper room heart- those whose hearts and attitudes are positioned for an encounter.*


Our generation can experience greater dimensions of the Spirit than the people of yore if only we have the heart and attitude of upper room Christians. The Azusa revival was born out of upper room hearts, men and women who prayed all night continuously sometimes putting their heads in crates of egg all because they wanted to see God's glory. Supper room believers are always asking for their own glory to be seen instead of asking for God's glory to manifest in the lives of men. *I'm yet to see a supper room Christian who had a definite encounter with God.* They prey on encounters of others for personal benefit. They are fond of what "papa" said than what God said because they're not close to Him. Quit living your spiritual life on another man's encounter. God is ready to reveal Himself to you. The key to that spiritual encounter with God is to have a upper room heart.


*PRAYER*: Father, I refuse to be a supper room Christian. My heart is set on kingdom matters and I'll pray about them henceforth in Jesus name.

DEVOTIONAL 0260 | Source start line: 24385
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
9/5/21, 7:22 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: ProjectSave Devotional
Sunday,
September 5th, 2021.


*UPPER ROOM OR SUPPER ROOM CHRISTIAN*? Pt.4


Acts2:42 All the believers devoted themselves to the apostles’ teaching, and to fellowship, and to sharing in meals (including the Lord’s Supper), and to prayer.


*COMMITMENT TO APOSTOLIC TEACHING*


After the upper room experience, there was a spirit of devotion and commitment among the brethren in the early church. Their experience wasn't just a one off thing, it had a resultant effect. The early believers had a four fold commiment: commitment to the teaching of the apostle, to fellowship, to sharing meals( Lord Supper inclusive) and to prayer. We need not stress the later but we shall look at the first three. *Apostles' Teaching*, the foundation of the church was laid by the teaching of the apostles, most of what we call basic bible doctrines ( not dogma) are products of what the apostles taught. The upper room believers were devoted to this divine apostolic teachings little wonder why their life radiated Christ: *'When he found him, he brought him back to Antioch. Both of them stayed there with the church for a full year, teaching large crowds of people. (It was at Antioch that the believers were first called Christians.)*" (Acts11:26 NLT).


*A believer grows through teaching not through preaching. We use the foolishness of preaching to proclaim Christ to unbelievers but it is through the wisdom of teaching we explain Christ to the believer*. A believer cannot grow until you expose him to continuous teaching and constant relational discipleship. I noticed that in the early church, they did more of teaching meetings than jamboree meetings. Supper room churches and believers care less about teaching and explaining Christ to the people. Every meeting is either a prayer meeting where we tell people to come and take their blessings. It is a "God bless me" meeting. Even contemporary church in the early days of Christianity placed a great premium on teaching to the extent that a Sunday school movement was birthed by Robert Raikes. Today, that practice is lost by modern church. They see it as archaic. In most churches, even weekly meetings for teachings are week and only a few attend because a lot of people are busy at work. The opportunity to gather people on Sunday into small groups to explain Christ is a precious and rare opportunity we must not neglect.


Some of us grew immensely from the Sunday school classes we attended and till date, what we learnt is still in us. Believers who have been faithfully attending teaching classes( sunday school inclusive) are always sound. Why should our generation shift this major landmark? *An upper room believer will not miss Sunday school neither will he be comfortable with celebration service only, he will not be comfortable with a church where Christ is not taught deeply, clearly and soundly. An upper room pastor will spend time in study, first for his own life and to transmit sound doctrine to the church*. This is the pattern of the bible especially the new testament church. Today, if we organize a praise jamz, crowds of believers will come but the moment we advertise a teaching meeting where new testament doctrines would be taught, supper room Christians will go into hiding. *One of the signs that shows how committed you are to personal spiritual growth is your commitment to bible teachings.*


*PRAYER*: Father, I refuse to be a supper room Christian. I receive grace to be committed to the accurate teaching of God's word in Jesus name.

DEVOTIONAL 0261 | Source start line: 24674

9/6/21, 6:35 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: PROJECTSAVE DEVOTIONAL
Monday,
September 6th,  2021.

*UPPER ROOM OR SUPPER ROOM CHRISTIAN*? Pt.5

Acts2:42 All the believers devoted themselves to the apostles’ teaching, and to fellowship, and to sharing in meals (including the Lord’s Supper), and to prayer.

*COMMITMENT TO FELLOWSHIP*

In a Christian drama i watched years back, someone once explained fellowship as being in the ship with your fellows. Though he said it derogatorily but he made some sense. Fellowship is an association with people of similar interest. One of the commitments of believers in the early church was to the fellowship of believers. The apostles and their members were committed to that gathering together wherever the door opened. Early church history revealed that they met in homes for fellowship. To them, gathering together does not have to be in a big building that looks like cathedral, the place does not need air condition, rug, tiles and marble walls. These upper room believers cared less about aesthetics, stage lights and the location of their meeting place in the city, all they cared about was the ever abiding presence of God in their midst.


That gathering in small groups was what they regarded as their church service. It wasn't just a Sunday thing, it was almost daily if not daily. It was a place where they drew strength from one  another, it was a gathering where the gifts of the Spirit manifested not only by the apostles but by anyone that is Spirit-filled, Christ was taught, explained and exalted in their fellowship not a place where the preacher is exalted and celebrated. No room for celebrity preachers, no not at all. There were no special adverts about 'the great man of God'. It was a gathering where people had something to contribute to the spiritual growth of the brethren. How did I know? Paul said, *"What then shall we say, brothers and sisters? When you come together, each of you has a hymn, or a word of instruction, a revelation, a tongue or an interpretation. Everything must be done so that the church may be built up.*"(1 Cor14:26). If you understand that text, you'll see that he was trying to describe how the gathering of the early church was, it was a gathering of spirituals where people come to contribute something edifying though he was trying to bring orderliness to that but it wasn't a gathering where some are performers.

Supper room believers do not value the gathering of the saints neither are they committed to it. They attend church gatherings ceremonially. They hop from one place to the other. They change church almost every now and then always looking for where it is happening. They prefer to identify with a 'popular church' yet they're not edified. The moment they hear about the emergence of another "big name", they change ship. Supper room believers may not jump from place to place but they will not submit to the training structures of the church for their growth. They only service their membership on Sundays, they may even come late. Hebrews 10:25 is God's counsel to supper room believers: *"And let us not neglect our meeting together, as some people do, but encourage one another, especially now that the day of his return is drawing near.*" You begin to lose value the moment you don't value being in the gathering of believers.


*PRAYER*: Father, I refuse to be a supper room Christian. I receive grace to be committed to the accurate teaching of God's word in Jesus name.

DEVOTIONAL 0262 | Source start line: 24805

9/7/21, 6:58 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: ProjectSave Devotional
Tuesday
September 7th,2021.

*UPPER ROOM OR SUPPER ROOM CHRISTIAN*? Pt.6

Acts 2:45 They sold their property and possessions and shared the money with those in need. NLT

*HELPING NEEDY BRETHREN*

In the early church, a number of believers lost their jobs and means of livelihood because they abandoned Judaism for Christianity. In that era, Judaism was interwoven into the fabric of their society and national life. It was impossible to be in business, career, military and other profession without being a Judaizer. The moment anyone renounces Judaism, he/she will be persona non grata within the society, among families and friends. It would be practically impossible to get a job let alone practice craft with other craftsmen. Also, business patronage will be gone. This affected many of the early believers but they found themselves in a community of men and women who sacrificed so much to help them ameliorate their condition. It was said about the brethren in the early church that: *"There were no needy people among them, because those who owned land or houses would sell them and bring the money to the apostles to give to those in need.*"(Acts4:34-35 NLT). That's a huge sacrifice.

Today, can the same be said about us and the church? We are not talking about pampering lazy people who want to hide under the umbrella of the church to milk us dry but here my concern is about those in genuine need. We have them around us but often we fail to identify them because we've lost the heart of compassion the early church had towards those in need. *Having an upper room heart isn't about prayer and speaking in tongues only, it must show in our disposition towards those in need among the brethren*. Apostle Paul said, *"When God’s people are in need, be ready to help them. Always be eager to practice hospitality.*"(Romans 12:13 NLT). Supper room believers think about themselves only and do not care for the brethren a bit.

Jesus takes our help to the brethren personally. He sees it as something done to Him directly. *"For I was hungry, and you fed me. I was thirsty, and you gave me a drink. I was a stranger, and you invited me into your home. I was naked, and you gave me clothing. I was sick, and you cared for me. I was in prison, and you visited me.’ “And the King will say, ‘I tell you the truth, when you did it to one of the least of these my brothers and sisters, you were doing it to me!’*" (Matthew25:35-36, 40 NLT). The early church did not have a class of haves and have nots. Everyone appeared as though they were in the same class. No special seat for a honourable because everyone bought with the blood of Christ  were regarded as honourable. The Lord need to restore the lost art of compassion towards needy brethren back to the church. Let me leave you with the admonition of Paul: *"Therefore, whenever we have the opportunity, we should do good to everyone—especially to those in the family of faith.*"(Gal6:10).


*PRAYER*: Father, give me a upper room heart and help me to live it out in Jesus name.

*UPPER ROOM OR SUPPER ROOM CHRISTIAN*? Pt.7

Acts 5:42 And every day, in the Temple and from house to house, they continued to teach and preach this message: “Jesus is the Messiah.” NLT

*COMMITMENT TO SOUL WINNING*

Another important lesson we can draw from the early church is their commitment to soul winning. *Evangelism was not a department in their church as we have in modern churches rather evangelism was the task of everyone.* After the Holy Spirit came on all the believers in the upper room, they became mobile evangelists. Lay men in that era preached the gospel everywhere they went, they were happy to share their new found faith with the people they met. After persecution scattered the church in Jerusalem, the brethren still preached the gospel wherever they went: *"Meanwhile, the believers who had been scattered during the persecution after Stephen’s death traveled as far as Phoenicia, Cyprus, and Antioch of Syria. They preached the word of God, but only to Jews.*"(Acts11:19 NLT)


The dispersal was only a vehicle/ means through which God allowed the message of His love to spread. These early believers didn't care about their comfort, uppermost in their hearts were lost souls and how to reach them with the message of the kingdom. In less than two years, the early church conquered Asia Minor for Christ: *"This went on for the next two years, so that people throughout the province of Asia—both Jews and Greeks—heard the word of the Lord.*"( Acts19:10 NLT).  *Upper room Christians have a burning heart to see souls come to the Lord and discipled. Upper room believers see every sinner as a potential believer if preached to. They are not comfortable seeing people walk the broadway leading to eternal damnation*. Upper room believer doesn't just give or support evangelistic causes with money but is actively involved in preaching in   buses, on the street, sharing tracts in public places and everywhere door opens.


*The upper room believer doesn't separate his career from this holy assignment of "Go Ye". Career or business platform is an opportunity to share Christ's love with people.*  The upper room Pastor will teach his flock how to win souls,  lead them into action and grow the church by bringing in the lost not by sheep stealing. Supper room Christians have the lost as best of friends yet they haven''t deemed it fit to share their faith with them. They don't care about their eternity. God expects a radical change from you as regards winning the lost. We must be deliberate and intentional about it because the lost is uppermost in God's heart. It should not be something we do only when motivated by a church leader. *The baptism of the Spirit is not just for you to speak capital letter tongues, that empowerment is for you to be a witness for Jesus Christ, Acts1:8*. If we receive the same Holy Spirit the early church received, we'll do what they did- evangelism. *If you speak in tongues and you're not speaking to men about the love of Christ and eternal salvation, you'll leave us in doubt about the source of your tongues*.

*PRAYER*: Father, give me a burning desire to see souls come to you in Jesus name.

DEVOTIONAL 0264 | Source start line: 25014
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
9/9/21, 7:45 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: PROJECTSAVE DEVOTIONAL
Thursday,
September 9th, 2021.


*STOP MAKING CARNAL PROVISIONS*


Romans 13:14 But put ye on the Lord Jesus Christ, and make not provision for the flesh, to fulfil the lusts thereof. KJV


Several years ago, I lived in a student environment off campus. The occupants of the house where I resided and our neighbourhood was full of hard guys on campus. Sexual immorality, heavy alcohol consumption, partying and other vices weren't a big deal. I struggled in that environment as a very young Christian. There was a church brother in our flat who should have been a model of Christian living but one thing he did that raised doubts in the hearts of everyone was that he cohabited with his fiancée. They wake up praying in tongues, go to school and even attend fellowship meetings together just for that but of cohabitation. On one occasion, I got talking with one of the guys in the house who said, "these people can't tell me they are not sleeping with each other". Truly, it would be hard for that brother to convince anyone in that neighbourhood that he wasn't steeped in sexual immorality because he made provision for the flesh to manifest.



*You cannot desire to conquer the flesh and still give flesh a room for expression*. It does not work that way. Take the sin of sexual immorality as an example, the moment you don't set healthy boundaries between yourself and the opposite sex, falling may become inevitable. No one falls into sexual immorality by accident. *There's nothing like "I had sex by mistake". Every act of sexual immorality is carefully planned and prepared for through deliberate actions and inactions*. Do you think you can cohabit with the lady you profess to love as a single and not be tempted to explore? Absolutely impossible. May be you have strong attraction towards women with certain types of body features and you now employed a secretary or house help that fits that spec, you're on your way down. You cannot be praying to overcome alcoholism and still have a bar in your house or buy wines with very minute percentage of alcohol saying it means nothing. *Whatever you want to conquer, you don't pamper.*


*In dealing with the flesh, holy brutality is allowed*. Paul said, *"For if ye live after the flesh, ye shall die: but if ye through the Spirit do mortify the deeds of the body, ye shall live.*" (Rom8:13). The key word in that text is "Mortify". It is the root word from which we got mortician and mortuary. *Paul enjoined that we mortify( kill) the flesh. How can we do this? By denying the flesh what it craves for*. The moment you provide for the flesh, it finds expression fully. Think about those things you call "weakness" or the things you're struggling with, you'll notice that they have lingered this long because you pampered it by making daily or occasional provision for it. Identify those things that trigger fleshly expressions and cut off.


*PRAYER*: Father, I receive the grace to mortify the flesh in Jesus name.


====================================================================================================
DEVOTIONAL 0265 | Source start line: 25068
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
9/10/21, 7:47 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: PROJECTSAVE DEVOTIONAL
Friday,
September 10th, 2021.

*THE PRINCIPLE OF BECOMING*


2 Cor 3:18 But we all, with open face beholding as in a glass the glory of the Lord, are changed into the same image from glory to glory, even as by the Spirit of the Lord.


I heard about the experience of a brother who listened to a preacher. As he listened, he was sexually aroused. It was as though he began to have sex with an imaginary being. While that was happening, he came to his senses and the Holy Spirit told him that the preacher he was listening to was sexually immoral. So what he experienced was an impartation or transmission of the spirit the man carried into his life because he opened his heart to listen to him. Though the man was preaching the bible, something else was being transmitted.
There is a deep spiritual principle on how people become whatever they are. To become, for example, sexually immoral, a faith filled person, healed or angry, there are ways this things are imparted from the spirit realm into our lives.


*Spiritual things(good or bad) are imparted by visuals(what we see) and audio( what we hear). In physics, we can say that light and sound waves are the ways through which things are transmitted*. Though the physicists restricted the working of waves to physical things but it is just an adaptation of how spiritual things are transmitted. *When the devil want people to become whatever he desires especially in character & behaviour, he programme them for it through what they constantly hear and watch*. In the bible, Lot was called a righteous man but he committed incest. How did it happen? Through what he saw and heard in Sodom&Gomorrah. One would think it was because of alcohol but that's just a trigger, immorality has filled his heart long before he was drunk, alcohol only gave it expression: *"and delivered just Lot, vexed with the filthy conversation of the wicked: (for that righteous man dwelling among them, IN SEEING AND HEARING, vexed his righteous soul from day to day with their unlawful deeds;)*", 2 Peter 2:7-8 KJV. When the devil want to demonize people and make them follow his ways, he exposes them to bad television and radio programs or books. As they watch, listen or read, a demon enters their life. *Guard your eye and ear gate jealously*.


When the Holy Spirit want to bring about a change in the life of any man, the same vehicle of hearing&seeing is used. Our opening text says it all: *"But we all, with open face BEHOLDING (that is to see or look) as in a glass the glory of the Lord, are changed into the same image from glory to glory, even as by the Spirit of the Lord.*"(2 Cor3:18). Apostle John confirms this truth in 1John3:2: *"Beloved, now are we the sons of God, and it doth not yet appear what we shall be: but we know that, when he shall appear, we shall be like him; for we shall SEE him as he is.*" When we expose ourselves to seeing and hearing things about Christ, we become like Christ. When we listen to faith messages, faith is imparted because faith cometh by hearing, Rom10:17. To become, be mindful of what light and sound waves is imparting into your spirit. Do not sit with television programs where immorality is celebrated. In no time, you'll become immoral.


*ACTION POINT*: Do a review of your daily exposure, stop whatever is capable of making you become what you watch/listen and give attention to those things that can make you become God's desire for your life.

9/11/21, 7:57 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: ProjectSave Devotional
Saturday,
September 11th, 2021.

*WHEN FATHERS FAIL*

1 Samuel 3:12-13 *I am going to carry out all my threats against Eli and his family, from beginning to end. I have warned him that judgment is coming upon his family forever, because his sons are blaspheming God and he hasn’t disciplined them.

Eli the priest of God failed as a father. His sons, Hophni and Phineas were scoundrels, their sins went up to heaven and God decided to kill them both in one day and take the priesthood from their lineage. I believe you know their story from Sunday school class. Looking at their lives deeply, one would see that these boys were not properly trained by their father. The element of godly instruction and discipline in their training was lacking. God wasn't happy with their father because he failed in this regard: *"I have warned him that judgment is coming upon his family forever, because his sons are blaspheming God and HE HASN'T DISCIPLINED THEM*",1 Samuel 3:13 NLT. Eli failed here and his generation forfeited the priesthood.


In our world, androcentric stereotypes made us belief that godly training for the children is the duty of women only. Culture and tradition exclude men from being active in the training of their children except for making provision. We have proverbs to back up this unbiblical mentality. For example, the elders will say "a bad child is for the mother but a good one is for the father". Why is it like that? The intent is to let the world know that bad child is the product of poor training by the mother while the good is for the father but trained by the mother. So the father takes the glory when mothers train their children successfully. This is totally unbiblical. You'll notice that when God saw the atrocities of Eli's children, he didn't confront their mother, God sent a message to the father- Eli. Why is it so? *Fathers are the principal trainers and instructors in the home, mothers are only helpers*. Fathers determine to a large extent how their children will fulfill destiny that's why in the OT, the patriarchs prophetically spoke about the destinies of their children. Fathers are prophets in their home.


*When fathers fail in their leadership, priestly and prophetic role, generations may forfeit a glorious destiny like Eli's generation did*. If you're still in doubt about the role of fathers as Chief trainer in the home, hear what God said about Abraham, *"FOR I KNOW HIM, THAT HE WILL COMMAND HIS CHILDREN and his household after him, and they shall keep the way of the LORD, to do justice and judgment; that the LORD may bring upon Abraham that which he hath spoken of him.*"( Genesis 18:19 KJV). God never said Sarah will command her children to walk in the ways of the Lord neither is there a place in the bible where God held women responsible for any failure in the home. Even though Eve lured Adam to sin yet when God called them out from hiding, Adam was the person God questioned and held accountable. We're not saying women shouldn't train their children but biblically, God expects men to take the lead while the women support. The reversal is what we have today and it is affecting children negatively. *Area fathers produce area sons, negligent fathers produce delinquent sons&daughters*. The only thing some men are good at is sex. They're studs but do not know how to play fatherly role. Fathers, your duty is not just to provide money for the home, you're to provide godly leadership, train the children in the way of the Lord and ensure they follow the path of righteousness. You will not fail in Jesus name.

*PRAYER*: Father, may I not fail in my role as a father in Jesus name.

9/12/21, 6:16 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: ProjectSave Devotional
Sunday,
September 12th, 2021.


*SEPTEMBER 12, 2020*


Psalm34:1 I will bless the Lord at all times: his praise shall continually be in my mouth.


When we were in elementary school, often, our teachers
told us to write about memorable days and events in our lives. For me, September 12th 2020 stands as one of the memorable days in my diary. Though It was not the most memorable day because there were other days in between that year that stood out because of several events but if I hadn't taken the step to travel on September 12, 2020, what happened on other days in that year may not have happened. It was the beginning of a turbulent journey that shook my faith and that of those around me. As an itinerant missionary evangelist and bible teacher, I traveled out of my earthly country to one of the neighbouring West African states, (Ghana to be precise on this day)for  evangelistic outreaches to four unreached tribes. I took a young man along with me for media coverage, he has never been part of my team but he was recommended by my media assistant who couldn't make the journey. Getting to the airport by 3pm, we were meant to fly to Accra by 8:45pm that day but we had a delay till the next day. We didn't fly until after 10am the next day. We were discouraged about the turn of events but I kept praying in the airport.


A number of persons decided they will return home because they were tired of the failed promises from the airliners. For me, there was no going back because I've said good bye to friends and families. When the airline staff told us the likelihood of canceling the flight, I walked to one of them telling her that a king sent me on errand. I told her that if I don't go, many souls will die and go to hell. I told her I was on a rescue mission. I looked strange to those airline staff. I paced the floor of the airport in the midnight praying when almost everyone had slept. When it was daybreak, they told us to get ready because an aeroplane was coming from Accra to pick us. God answered my prayer and the young man with me was excited. When we got to Ghana, we had wonderful meetings. Souls responded to the call for salvation and there were numerous miracles such that hasn't happened before in our meetings. A lady on crutches was healed miraculously, swellings disappeared, pains were healed instantly and in all Jesus was glorified.  After the whole program, the first thing that happened was that our return flight was canceled and we had to reschedule. In between, we tested positive to COVID-19. That was a huge blow on our faith. I felt like Elijah who killed prophets of Baal but ran from Jezebel.


We spent weeks in Ghana more than scheduled to the point where we couldn't pay hotel bills not because we don't have money but because if we had continued, we wouldn't have anything left on us for our return. The young man had very bad symptoms, he could barely walk. On one of the Sundays, I called for an ambulance to come pick him because we had an emergency. If you weren't there, you cannot picture exactly what I'm trying to describe. In a country where you don't have families and relatives, where you cannot easily access funds. His mother became very disturbed emotionally. The devil kept telling me the young man will die and I will spend two years in jail when I return to Nigeria. If that young man had died, my ministry may have ended or tainted with a bad image forever because a number of persons may have concluded that I took him out of the country for rituals. My intention is not to tell stories today but to thank the father of all mercies who kept me and saved me from shame. It is good to thank God always and not forget His benefits. Whatever God has done in the past(big/small), please never forget it. Our amnesia about God's greatness and deliverances shows that we do not value His interventions but for me, I value this and several other things the Lord has done for me: *"Bless the LORD, O my soul: And all that is within me, bless his holy name.*" Psalm 103:1 KJV


Praise the Lord with us!


Song of the day: https://youtu.be/Hb4JBNDWhOA

IG: @Projectsave_ministries
@lanreoyeleke

Twitter: @Lanrezion01

Email: info@projectsaveng.org

9/13/21, 6:14 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: PROJECTSAVE DEVOTIONAL
Monday,
September 13th,  2021.

*UPHOLDING KINGDOM VALUES* Pt.1

Matthew 6:33 Seek the Kingdom of God above all else, and live righteously, and he will give you everything you need. NLT

*KINGDOM PURSUIT*

There are several definitions of the word "value". Value  refers to worth, what someone holds dear. The kingdom we are referring to in this context has nothing to do with the kingdoms of this world ruled by an earthly king. The kingdom in context refers to the reign and rule of God in the hearts of men. The kingdom of God is not restricted to a church or a fellowship gathering, it refers to the totality of God's reign in the lives of a believer. *"One day the Pharisees asked Jesus, “When will the Kingdom of God come?” Jesus replied, “The Kingdom of God can’t be detected by visible signs. You won’t be able to say, ‘Here it is!’ or ‘It’s over there!’ For the Kingdom of God is already among you*". Luke 17:20-21 NLT. Hallelujah!!! The best way to define kingdom values is this: *'they are principles or standards of behaviour that God holds dear; they refer to those things that are important to God*". If our walk with God will be fruitful, we must uphold what He values and seek to please Him always.

In most organizations, there are core values. The ethos of the organization, their policy, products&services revolves round those core values. If they recruit anyone into their employ, they'll indoctrinate them with their core values. There are several things people pursue today. Mundane pursuit is the reason why we have the proverbial rat race. In rat race, there's no winner and most times, as we are about getting to the finish line, the track is extended. Rat race leads to a cycle of pursuit that is unending till death comes. It renders men useless and makes them to labour for that which perish. Jesus said: *"But don’t be so concerned about perishable things like food. Spend your energy seeking the eternal life that the Son of Man can give you. For God the Father has given me the seal of his approval*”. John 6:27 NLT

God values kingdom pursuit above all else. Embedded in kingdom pursuit are other things that God values. A man that pursues the kingdom is concerned first about things that are heavenly. His affection is set on things above not beneath: *"Since you have been raised to new life with Christ, set your sights on the realities of heaven, where Christ sits in the place of honor at God’s right hand. Think about the things of heaven, not the things of earth*", Colossians 3:1-2 NLT. When God sees those who pursue all that His kingdom represent, the whole of heaven holds them in high esteem.

*PRAYER*: Father, I receive the grace to pursue the kingdom of God in Jesus name.


====================================================================================================
DEVOTIONAL 0269 | Source start line: 25442
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
9/14/21, 5:52 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: ProjectSave Devotional
Tuesday
September 14th,2021.

*UPHOLDING KINGDOM VALUES* Pt.2

Heb 12:14 Work at living in peace with everyone, and work at living a holy life, for those who are not holy will not see the Lord. NLT

*HOLY LIVING*

I once listened to a preacher who questioned the opening text. He insinuated that the verse didn't really mean what it says. At other times, I've also come in contact with articles or writings suggesting the verse didn't mean what it says literally. Those in the latter category believe that we have been made holy so there's no need for a believer to pursue or strive to be holy. I understand their drift from the standpoint of scriptures like: *"Some of you were once like that. But you were cleansed; YOU WERE MADE HOLY; you were made right with God by calling on the name of the Lord Jesus Christ and by the Spirit of our God.*"(1Cor6:11). You'll find similar phrasal expressions in Hebrews 10:8-10,14. This is what we call positional holiness. *Our salvation in Christ has made us holy not because of what we did but because of Christ's finished work on the cross.*

Is that all there is to holiness? When reading scriptures, try to get all scriptures talking about a word or phrase to know the sense in which it was used and interpret contextually. The scriptures cited earlier used the phrase "made holy" denoting a state, no addition no subtraction. A fait accompli. However "holiness" didn't end there. Some other scriptures enjoined that "we become" holy. They suggested we make "holiness" an ongoing action or a lifestyle: *"BUT NOW YOU MUST BE HOLY IN EVERYTHING YOU DO, just as God who chose you is holy.*" 1 Peter 1:15 NLT. This is different from other scriptures that presents holiness as an accomplished state. In Greek, this is called an imperative statement.  *“Therefore, having these promises, beloved, let us cleanse ourselves from all filthiness of the flesh and spirit, perfecting holiness in the fear of God*” (2 Corinthians 7:1). We have a role to play in matters of holiness.

*"Holiness is not just about what we have been made to be at the the cross but also about what we do because of what we have been made to be*. God  expect us to be active in the "doing" aspect of holiness. A man can be 'made  to be' something great and not live up to his full potential. *Our holiness should not only be positional but progressive or an ongoing action depicted through a life that is pure and set apart for God.* This is a foremost kingdom value you and me must hold dear if we want to see Jesus. It may seem awkward to the world that we're not compromising our faith. Even among some believers, holy living may sound outdated yet that is what God demands. God will not compromise on this kingdom value neither should we.

*PRAYER*: Father, I receive the grace to live holy in Jesus name.

DEVOTIONAL 0270 | Source start line: 25515

9/15/21, 6:20 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: PROJECTSAVE DEVOTIONAL
Wednesday,
September 15th, 2021.

UPHOLDING KINGDOM VALUES* Pt.3

Proverbs 10:9. 9 Whoever walks in integrity walks securely, ...

*THE VALUE OF INTEGRITY*

In our daily bread of April 15,1998, Henry G. Bosch told a story about integrity and it struck me: "As a schoolboy, I worked with my father during the summer months. Each morning we stopped to pick up the early edition of the newspaper at a small grocery store. One morning when we got to work, my father found that by mistake he had taken two newspapers instead of one. He first thought of paying the man the extra price the next morning, but then after a moment’s consideration he said, “I had better go back with this paper. I don’t want the man at the store to think I’m dishonest.” He got in his car, drove back to the store, and returned the paper. About a week later, someone stole money from the grocery store. When police pinpointed the time it occurred, the grocer remembered only two people being in the store at the time—and one was my father. The grocer immediately dismissed my father as a suspect, saying, “That man is really honest. He came all the way back here just to return a newspaper he took by mistake.” The police then focused their investigation on the other man, who soon made a full confession. My father’s honesty made a big impression on that non-Christian storeowner, and on me."


*What is integrity? It is the quality of being honest and having strong moral principles. It is doing the right thing behind closed doors when no one sees you.* This value is priceless and golden. Today, the world, church and the society is in dire need of men with integrity. I heard from fathers in the faith that in time past,  company owners advertise  vacancies with preference for Christians because they believed that most Christians can be trusted with money. Today, the story is not the same. There are Christians who steal and embezzle church funds right in the church. What do you think they will do with public funds? Your guess is as good as mine. Unfortunately, I have interacted with unbelievers who are honest and straightforward. Their word is their bond but so many believers are found wanting here.


*No matter how much we claim to be the righteousness of God in Christ, speak in tongues and be committed to the work of the Lord, if integrity is lacking, our Christianity is worthless to the world around us*.  Why should we use an unbeliever to replace a believer because the latter lacked integrity? It shouldn't be. I have listened to several believers who told me they prefer to deal with unbelievers in business because a number of church folks lack integrity. That should not be said about you, come out and don't be counted among people with such record. Talking about David's leadership, Psalm78:72 says, *"And David shepherded them with integrity of heart; with skillful hands he led them.*" If you're privileged to lead, do not abuse the privilege of office to amass wealth unjustly. Be clean in your leadership. Don't use the power of office to deal with ladies. You know what I mean? Uphold the value of integrity. *"The integrity of the upright guides them, but the unfaithful are destroyed by their duplicity.*"( Prov11:3).

*PRAYER*: Father, I receive the grace to walk in integrity in Jesus name.

9/16/21, 5:57 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: PROJECTSAVE DEVOTIONAL

*UPHOLDING KINGDOM VALUES* Pt.4

Luke 9:23 “Then he said to them all: “Whoever wants to be my disciple must deny themselves and take up their cross daily and follow me.”

*THE VALUE OF DISCIPLESHIP*


The term "disciple" is from the Koine Greek word mathētḗs. It simply means "one who engages in learning through instruction from another, pupil, apprentice". When Jesus called the twelve to be His disciples, they understood Him perfectly because the Jewish concept of discipleship is akin to apprenticeship. It involves followership through learning and imitation. That is why the twelve abandoned all they were doing to follow Jesus and to learn from Him daily. Central to our walk with Jesus is discipleship. *A common calling that we all have is the call to discipleship- a follower of Jesus Christ.*


In Matthew 28:19, Jesus said, *"Therefore, go and make disciples of all the nations, baptizing them in the name of the Father and the Son and the Holy Spirit.*" As we follow Christ daily, our aim should be to make others a follower of Christ. *Discipleship is not a tea party, it is not an event neither is it a class, it is a life style that is committed to following one person(Christ), to become like Him*. Today, some are beginning to turn this concept around to mean what it is not. Their understanding of discipleship is that which is centred around man instead of Christ. Some preachers are proud to call those around them "my disciples" instead of disciples of Christ. That's similar to what the Pharisees were doing in the days of Jesus, they took the place of God in the hearts of men that was why Jesus said, *"And do not call anyone on earth ‘father,’ for you have one Father, and he is in heaven.*"(Matthew 23:9 NIV). They had large followership to the point where people no longer see God as their father but the Pharisees. They were hypocrites and they made others children of hell like they were. That's why Jesus was at loggerheads with them. They misrepresented the whole concept of discipleship.






*In Christian discipleship, we follow Christ not man. God can use man to teach us Christ but we should bear in mind that the person we are following ultimately is Christ not that man.* This is a kimgdom value we must not jettison for anything. The faith of some believers becomes weak when their human model fails and falls. It shouldn't be. The writer of Hebrews says, *"Looking unto Jesus the author and finisher of our faith; who for the joy that was set before him endured the cross, despising the shame, and is set down at the right hand of the throne of God.*"(Heb12:2). Henceforth, you must make Christ your object of focus, learn of Him and walk in His footsteps.



*PRAYER*: Father, I receive grace to walk in the footsteps of Christ and to be His lifelong disciple in Jesus name.

*UPHOLDING KINGDOM VALUES* Pt.5

Matthew 11:29 Take My yoke upon you and learn from Me, for I am gentle and lowly in heart, and you will find rest for your souls. NKJV


*THE VALUE OF TEACHING AND LEARNING*


*One thing that stands out in discipleship is learning. The call to Christian discipleship is the call to learning*. When Jesus told those that are heavy laden to come unto Him and to bear His yoke, He made a vital statement saying "learn from me". Some versions of the bible used the phrase "I will teach you". We learn through teaching, they are related. The believer in Christ cannot grow into the image and stature of Christ without being taught. *True disciples are lifelong learners not just mere followers. In fact you cannot follow well if you haven't learnt well.* It took Jesus three and half years to teach and groom His disciples on how to be good followers of Him. Jesus Himself was their bible study teacher, they attended His theology school.


I want you to observe how vital "  teaching/ learning" is in the great commission verses: *"Therefore, go and make disciples of all the nations, baptizing them in the name of the Father and the Son and the Holy Spirit. TEACH these new disciples to obey all the commands I have given you. And be sure of this: I am with you always, even to the end of the age.”* (Matthew 28:19-20 NLT). The disciples are to be taught to obey. There's a nexus between "learning" and "obedience". You cannot obey a command you don't know about. You cannot be a good disciple if you're not willing to be educated about Christ and His kingdom. In the kingdom, learning is of great value that is why the scriptures is full of teachings. If God places premium on learning we have no excuse. You must grow to love learning.


Do not be like some who love jamboree meetings and sensationalism but ignore avenues to learn about Christ. As a Bible teacher, I have noticed a sad trend among some Christians, in places where deep teachings of the kingdom are taught, often responses are low but when people hear themes on "relationship, marriage, prosperity & miracles" or may be you call for a "prayer meeting" with funny themes like: "this witch must die" and the likes, you'll likely have a full house. Some people want the branch but not the root whereas scriptures emphasized that our rootedness in Christ is a function of learning the word: *"Let your roots grow down into him, and let your lives be built on him. THEN YOUR FAITH WILL GROW STRONG IN THE TRUTH YOU WERE TAUGHT, and you will overflow with thankfulness.*"(Col 2:7NLT). Did you notice the word "taught"? When last did you attend bible study meeting/Sunday school in your church to be taught scriptures? When you hear bible teachers, do you despise them by calling them boring? If you have a negative attitude towards learning, you need to repent quickly. I've always said, the believer can only grow in Christ through  teaching. If you place a great value on your spiritual life, you will value learning.


*PRAYER*: Father, I receive the grace to learn of Christ and His ways in Jesus name.

DEVOTIONAL 0273 | Source start line: 25729

9/18/21, 5:46 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: ProjectSave Devotional
Saturday,
September 18th, 2021.


*UPHOLDING KINGDOM VALUES* Pt.6

1 Samuel 15:22-23 “Has the LORD as great delight in burnt offerings and sacrifices, as in obeying the voice of the LORD? Behold, to obey is better than sacrifice, and to listen than the fat of rams. For rebellion is as the sin of divination, and presumption is as iniquity and idolatry. Because you have rejected the word of the LORD, he has also rejected you from being king.”

*THE VALUE OF OBEDIENCE*

On the internet, i read the story of a boy who went trekking on the mountain. He reached a great height when he realized that it was getting dark and he had to reach the bottom soon. He began climbing down. It was getting darker and darker, so dark that he could barely see. He was somehow managing to get down the mountain supporting with his hands and feet. Suddenly, his feet slipped. He fell and caught hold of the branch of the tree. He was holding the branch tight enough since that was his only support. He began shouting, “Is there anybody to help me out.” No reply! He kept yelling and yelling. Finally, he heard a soft still voice. “Yes, I am there to help you.” Greatly excited, he shouted, “Thank you, May I know who you are?” Quick came the reply, “I am God. I will help you, but you will have to remove your hands off the branch.


Not willing to do so, the boy kept hanging all through the night and finally died. The next morning, the villagers saw the dead boy and found out that the branch on which he was hanging was just two feet above the ground. Had he listened to the voice of God, his life would have been saved. *Failure to obey God puts us in danger and positions us to be attacked by the enemy.* Obedience is of great value to God and in our walk with God. A man can know all the scriptures with hermeneutical exactitude but once obedience is missing, his knowledge is meaningless to God. Haven't you seen people who knows the scriptures but do not live the life of the scriptures?  That's becoming a common phenomenon in this generation where Knowledge is accessible but hasn't transformed lives. *God is not impressed about how much you know but how ready you're willing to obey.*


*The attestation of a disciple of Christ is life of obedience to God*. When we claim to love God and we don't obey Him, our love is just a mere lip service. There are many songs we sing in church about loving God. Sometimes, those songs stir emotions when accompanied with skillful playing of instruments. Singing those songs can bring tears to the eyes sometimes but God isn't moved except we prove our love to Him by living a life of absolute obedience: *“If you love me, obey my commandments.*"(John14:15). That's the ultimate proof of love- obedience. God's instruction to us may not be easy for the flesh. Whoever says it is always easy is lying. The bible only told us how Abraham obeyed God when he was instructed to sacrifice Isaac but we don't know the struggles he had in his mind before obeying. Sometimes, it is not easy to let go what you've struggled to get for almost a life time. That's why giving is hard for some persons. For every instruction God gives us,  grace for obedience is always available if only we can ask for it.

*PRAYER*: Father, I receive grace to live a life of obedience to your will in Jesus name.

DEVOTIONAL 0274 | Source start line: 25838

9/19/21, 9:14 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: ProjectSave Devotional
Sunday,
September 19th, 2021.


*UPHOLDING KINGDOM VALUES* Pt.7


1 Cor 4:2 Moreover it is required in stewards that one be found faithful.


*THE VALUE OF STEWARDSHIP*


The house of John Wesley got burnt. Fire razed it down and the ruins were visible. A man told John Wesley that his house was on fire. John Wesley replied by saying, "No. The Lord's house has burned to the ground. That means one less responsibility for me." John Wesley understood the principle of stewardship. He understood that he was just a steward, manager of God's resources not the owner of it.  In the New Testament books, the word “steward” is rooted in the Greek word “oikonomos,” which means “the manager of a household." *A Christian owns nothing, we are only stewards of the resources that God has put in our care including money*. Stewardship is the acceptance of our managerial responsibility from God that we are not owners of anything in this world - including our lives. Life is a trust given to us and we shall account for it.



*Kingdom minded people do not see anything as theirs that's why they don't boast about ownership of material things neither do they live for a show. They don't go on social media to display wealth and tell the world about their newly acquired machine( cars).  They don't personalize ownership because they know God owns it all*. This is kingdom attitude towards money and material things or else we'll be consumed by vain pursuit. *Stewards do not find it hard to give because they know the owner can demand for what belongs to him anything*. Your struggle in giving is because you have a possessive attitude instead of an attitude of a steward. We live in an era where false teachers are teaching people to "eat" their money and resources instead of using it to further the cause of the kingdom. I know that the crusade against giving to support kingdom cause arose because of phony preachers who milk God's people dry because of their pocket and turn the offering plate to a money vending machine. They commercialize the Gospel and merchandise the grace of God. I know all of these has happened and is still  happening.



However, this shouldn't make you turn a blind eye against genuine kingdom causes because not all preachers are greedy. More so, if you're led by the Spirit,  you'll know where, what and how to give what God entrusted in your care. My mentor told a story of the honorarium (N200,000) he received after a ministration. Shortly after he left, his host called him that God instructed him to make it up to a million naira. He kept that money for two weeks asking the Holy Spirit what he should do with the money. He was instructed to purchase a vehicle for the Pastor that joined him in holy matrimony. He added a few thousands to the money and he acted as instructed. Now, that's a man who understands stewardship. He knew the money wasn't his so he enquired from the real owner how he should disburse it. The early church understood this, that was why they gave all to the Lord when he demanded for it including their lives. They were ready to go anywhere for the Lord even in the face of stiff oppositions and persecution. They were sold out to God. Know that what you're holding back( your life inclusive) doesn't belong to you, give it to the owner.


*PRAYER*: Father, I receive grace to be a faithful steward in Jesus name.

DEVOTIONAL 0275 | Source start line: 25916
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
9/20/21, 12:00 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: PROJECTSAVE DEVOTIONAL
Monday,
September 20th,  2021.

*UPHOLDING KINGDOM VALUES* Pt.8

Proverbs 3:5-6 Trust in the LORD with all thine heart; And lean not unto thine own understanding. In all thy ways acknowledge him, And he shall direct thy paths.KJV


*THE VALUE OF SEEKING DIVINE DIRECTION*


We live in the age of false knowledge, self help and human permutations. This is the age where some preachers are beginning to tell people they don't need God's direction always on life's issues always especially marriage. Times without number, I have heard some believers( preachers inclusive) say that God is not involved in the choice of a life partner. They hold the view that the Spirit of God need not lead anyone on the choice of whom to marry since we have liberty to choose whoever we like based on our parameters and take it to God for endorsement. I see a people who tacitly draw lines for the Holy Spirit in their lives and are sense ruled instead of being Spirit led. God loves and value when His children come asking for direction on how to go about life's issues. Old timers in the faith have a culture of "seeking God's face"( as they say) before taking major steps. They don't gamble with their lives like many are doing today.


If divine direction is of no value, God wouldn't have promised to lead us as He did in Psalms 32:8 saying,  *"I will instruct thee and teach thee in the way which thou shalt go: I will guide thee with mine eye.*" When Jesus was about leaving the earth, in His last days, He promised the apostles( and believers by extension) that the Holy Spirit will come to lead and guide us into all truths( no aspect of our lives is excluded): *"When the Spirit of truth comes, he will guide you into all truth. He will not speak on his own but will tell you what he has heard. He will tell you about the future.*"(John16:13). The scriptures cited so far underscore the importance of being led by the Spirit. God is interested in every area of our lives and He is more than willing to direct us and clear our confusion. How can we call God a loving father yet will be uninterested in helping us out in making right decisions about life? That doesn't describe the God I've read about severally in my bible. Under the old covenant, he said to the people: *"Call unto me, and I will answer thee, and shew thee great and mighty things, which thou knowest not.*"Jeremiah 33:3 KJV. If God can give this promise to those under the old covenant, those who weren't partaker of the grace of Christ, how much more you and me that are washed and cleansed by the precious blood of Christ?


Being led by the Spirit is one of the proofs of our sonship: *"For as many as are led by the Spirit of God, they are the sons of God.*"( Romans 8:14 KJV). The man that will receive clear instructions and directions from God must be a man who is seeking for it and is willing to obey God. The key to hearing God clearly is to empty yourself of human ideas and ways. *God will not direct you until He sees that your heart is totally empty of self devised schemes.* As disciples of Christ, we value taking orders from above before marching forward. There's nothing that beats being in the will of God in all areas of your life. Make it a lifestyle to always ask God on which way to go and never take steps until you've heard from him. *In life, it is better to be slow and right than to be fast in the wrong direction. Speed is only meaningful in the right direction but meaningless in the wrong direction*. The way to getting it right is to seek God's direction always. God's leading is always available for those that are willing to be led not those that trust in the wisdom and schemes of man.


*PRAYER*: Father, I empty myself of human schemes and devices, Holy Spirit, lead me on( ...  Mention areas in need of direction). I dispel confusion, I rebuke the spirit of hasty decision in Jesus name.
