DEVOTIONAL 0324 | Source start line: 30104
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
11/8/21, 3:54 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: PROJECTSAVE DEVOTIONAL
Monday,
November 8th,  2021.

*LESSONS FROM THE LIFE OF JOB? Pt.1

Job1: 22 In spite of everything, Job did not sin or accuse God of doing wrong.  CEV

The story of Job is known to almost all believers who have spent some time reading scriptures and hearing preachers preach about the suffering of Job. Quite a number of theories have been put forth about Job's suffering. Some believed Job was the cause of his suffering because of what he said in Job 3:25 *"What I feared has come upon me; what I dreaded has happened to me*". They concluded that fear was the reason behind his calamities. Similar to that school of thought, some persons believe that it was his failure to tithe that brought calamity upon him. Is it true in light of scriptures? Abraham was the first person who tithed, Genesis14:20. He tithed as a personal decision he made not because he was taught or commanded by anyone. Tithing became a binding law/ command centuries  after Abraham tithed not before his existence. So anyone who didn't tithe before Mosaic law didn't commit any sin. Historically, Job lived before Abraham( the first man who tithed); so his failure to tithe wouldn't have been  the reason for his calamity. He knew nothing about it and God didn't rebuke him for that because the law of tithing hadn't been given in his time. That the book of Job was placed after several events starting from Genesis to Esther doesn't mean the books were arranged by order of event. For example, Mark was written before Matthew but the latter was placed before the former.


If we'll be truthful in our exposition about Job's ordeal, we shouldn't neglect the conversation between God and Satan, that's where it all started: *"Then the Lord said to Satan, “Have you considered my servant Job?There is no one on earth like him; he is blameless and upright, a man who fears God and shuns evil*”(Job1:8). God started the conversation that led to his ordeal. When Satan complained about the edge of protection around Job, God said: *"All right, you may test him,” the Lord said to Satan. “Do whatever you want with everything he possesses, but don’t harm him physically.” So Satan left the Lord’s presence*".(Job 1:12 NLT). Whether Job had fears about the future or not, God gave Satan an express permission to go ahead with his dirty dozens and there was nothing and no one that would have stopped Satan except God. Sometimes, when I think about this, I humbly ask God never to leave me to the desires of my enemies. He shouldn't look on while the enemy tries to run rampant in my life. Take a few seconds to pray that prayer. Now, all I've said so far is to lay a foundation for this teaching, that's not my major concern but the life of Job.


The first lesson I picked from the life of Job was his testimony of uprightness and integrity. The introductory part of Job chapter 1(NLT) says, *"There once was a man named Job who lived in the land of Uz. HE WAS BLAMELESS—A MAN OF COMPLETE INTEGRITY. HE FEARED GOD AND STAYED AWAY FROM EVIL.*" What a wonderful commendation. Not many of Job's critics have this report. This is what God saw that made God to be proud of this humble patriarch of faith. Can God say the same about you? Can God boast about you in front of the hordes of hell? I've tried my best to peep into Job's life though we are millenniums apart, all I saw was a man who had nothing but the fear of God in his heart. What will God say about you? What mattered to God was not his wealth but his walk with him little wonder the bible introduced Job the upright and blameless man to us before we were told about his wealth. Don't try to reverse it, your Christian testimony about the correctness of your life and how it aligns with the will of God matter more to God than whatever you've acquired. Stop the wrong pursuit.


*PRAYER*: Father, I thank you for your word that has come to me, I receive the grace to live a life that is pleasing unto you. I want to be that man you will be proud of, help me father, in Jesus name.

*LESSONS FROM THE LIFE OF JOB? Pt.2*

Job1: 22 In spite of everything, Job did not sin or accuse God of doing wrong.  CEV


*HIS ATTITUDE OF PRAISE*

There's so much to learn from the life of Job rather than criticizing him for his actions and inactions. Whenever you study bible characters especially men & women with good report such as this, it is wise to pay attention to little details about their life and imitate where possible. The writer of Hebrew says: *"We do not want you to become lazy, but to imitate those who through faith and patience inherit what has been promised*", Hebrews6:12. The bible is God's wisdom in written form for this age. Whatever we read about in scriptures is included by God as examples for us, the life of Job is not an exception: *"For everything that was written in the past was written to teach us, so that through the endurance taught in the Scriptures and the encouragement they provide we might have hope*", Romans15:4. This is the word of the Lord.




I saw something that amazed me about Job. When the messenger told him about his loses, scripture says: He said, “I came naked from my mother’s womb, and I will be naked when I leave. The Lord gave me what I had, and the Lord has taken it away. PRAISE THE NAME OF THE LORD!*”, Job 1:21 NLT. Will you praise God in times of trouble? How can a man say "praise the name of the LORD" after losing all he had? Does it make sense? Just think about it.  I can't be sure I'll say the same thing he said if I were in his shoes. Maybe I will whine a little, complain and do plenty of crying. The heaviness in my heart will reflect all over me. If i remember to pray at all, i'll start binding and casting demons and raining fire and brimstone on them. I'm just being truthful. Despite Job's troubles, he maintained an attitude of praise. That's a lesson for us there.


*If the devil can steal your song he will steal your praise*. As a child of God, you're meant to have an attitude of praise and joy regardless of happenings. *Happiness is dependent on happenings but Joy is one of  the fruit of the Spirit that is not dependent on any event*. The only time some brethren will dance and sing is when a miracle has happened. You can tell when some persons have a testimony to share through their looks. *In our faith walk, we praise God on our way to testimonies not until we have one*. Faith for change works well when we have an attitude of  praise. Stop looking&dressing your troubles. Do not let people discern your challenges from your looks. Dress well, wear a good perfume, witness the Gospel to someone joyfully, share with the brethren and stop disturbing people around for prayers. Sometimes, faithlessness is the reason why people are always looking for who will pray for them. Disgrace the enemy with your praise. Be of good cheer, Christ has overcome the world. Hallelujah!!!


*PRAYER*: Father I thank you because my challenges and troubles are momentary. They won't last forever. I wear the garment of praise, I refuse the spirit of heaviness, sadness and gloom. I triumph by faith through the finished work of the son of God in Jesus name.

*LESSONS FROM THE LIFE OF JOB? Pt.3*

Job1: 22 In spite of everything, Job did not sin or accuse God of doing wrong.  CEV

*HIS ATTITUDE TOWARDS GOD*.

One of the fuel for atheism and agnosticism is pain. Atheists says there's no God, agnostics have repeatedly ask if there's God. They keep asking this question because they see how those who serve God go through hard times, suffering and even those who claim to have faith in God yet they suffer pain. They see how the earth is ravaged by calamities, wars, famine, natural disasters yet God didn't do anything to stop it. The atheist concluded that there's no God because there wouldn't have been a God and evil will be in the world. The agnostics say,"If there's God, then He must be a wicked and uncaring God" but they aren't sure there's God. Unfortunately, there are Christians who are beginning to have this attitude because of life's challenges.



There's a side of God we may not understand fully on this side of eternity until we enter glory. This  is what I'm trying to say; we have seen situations similar to that of Job where calamity befell a righteous man or someone who prayed  and it was as though God didn't answer. I know you've seen situations like this before happening to anointed men. It is in situations like this we become humble in thoughts and we soberly submit to God but some have seized such occasions to talk to God in a manner that is not glorifying. Some even turned their backs on God totally but Job was different. The bible says, *"In spite of everything, Job did not sin or accuse God of doing wrong*" ( Job1:22). Just a little pain, some have made themselves a judge and they passed their verdict on God as unfaithful and uncaring. They may not say it by word of the mouth but their action says it all. They may stop serving in church or stop going to church outrightly. That's the danger of serving God for gain instead of love. Those who serve God for gain at some point may be disappointed.



Despite all that happened to Job, he still adjudged God as faithful neither did he accuse God and pass on Him a damning verdict. Seeing the enormity of Job's calamity, his wife told him to curse God and die but Job said, *“...You are talking like a foolish woman. Shall we accept good from God, and not trouble?” In all this, JOB DID NOT SIN IN WHAT HE SAID*", Job 2:10. What do you say about God in times of trouble and trials? *Dearest, you must come to a point where you need to be convinced that God remains faithful, loving and caring even in unfavourable times and situations*. Happenings around you should not change this conviction. You must learn to see God's purpose in everything even when it seems the enemy is having a field day. *"And we know that ALL THINGS(Good, bad&ugly) work together for good to them that love God, to them who are the called according to his purpose*". ( Rom8:28, Emphasis mine). His sovereignty permits certain unfavourable things we can't explain to happen. Our theology or personal philosophy can't explain the reason for this but we shall understand better bye and bye. In all, at all times and in every situation, adjudge Him faithful. This should be your attitude towards God in trying times.


*PRAYER*: Father, you're faithful at all times even in times of  pain. I receive the grace to have the right attitude towards you at all times in Jesus name.

*LESSONS FROM THE LIFE OF JOB? Pt.4*


Job1: 22 In spite of everything, Job did not sin or accuse God of doing wrong.  CEV



*HIS ATTITUDE IN WAITING*.


One of the hardest thing to do in our walk with God is to wait for the fulfilment of His promises. Waiting is not easy especially when there are short cuts and alternatives. At 75, God spoke to Abram about him being the father of nations and led him out from among his people to a promised land. The pictures God painted to him seemed like something that will happen quickly.  He waited but in the process of waiting, he found an alternative in Hagar and gave birth to trouble- Ishmael. God told him Ishmael wasn't the promised child. The generation of Ishmael are the troublers of Israel till date. *When you step out of God's waiting line into alternative line, you attract trouble that may last a life time or even outlive you*. Abraham waited more till he was 100 years before the promised child came. Sincerely, that was not easy.  *What makes waiting hard are the ungodly alternatives that present itself to us as the easy way out*.



Job could have sought for ungodly alternatives during his ordeal, he could have cursed God and take sides with the devil but he said:  *"If a man die, shall he live again? ALL THE DAYS OF MY APPOINTED TIME WILL I WAIT, till my change come*", Job14:14. This is profound. Job knew there was an appointed time for his change, he had a sense of timing so he concluded that he would wait. Your troubles days have an appointed time for expiry. Are you truly waiting or wandering? Some have misconstrued the faith message to mean getting instant result. That you don't get instant result doesn't mean you lack faith, if it were so, then Abraham lacked faith but we know that it is not true. In fact, in all the years of his  waiting, he waited in faith. Faith and patience in waiting work together: *"that ye be not slothful, but followers of those who through faith and patience inherit the promises*",(Heb6:12). True faith will wait for the promise.



*While waiting, learn the lessons God want you to learn. Your waiting time should not be a wasted time*. You must possess the attitude of a learner during your waiting period. The crucible of suffering is a tool for preparing us for greater task and glory but only those who learn during suffering will partake in the glory that will come after. As you wait, don't be bitter with those who hurt you or those who possess your desired promise or testimony. For example, if you're waiting on God for the fruit of the womb, be happy with every woman who deliver of a baby, buy them gifts and be part of the ceremony. Don't stop attending weddings because you're not married. The devil often uses such attitude to make singles bitter through the back door. *Bitterness will prolong your waiting days in the wilderness of suffering. Shake it off*.



*PRAYER*: Father I thank you for teaching me to have the right attitude in and through life's challenges. I receive grace to wait for my appointed time of change in Jesus name.

*LESSONS FROM THE LIFE OF JOB? Pt.5*

Job1: 22 In spite of everything, Job did not sin or accuse God of doing wrong.  CEV



*FORGIVENESS*.


In my walk of faith, I've learnt how brethren can be hurting and unkind in words to those passing through life's challenges. I know this by experience. Several years ago, a brother and a sister were trusting God for the fruit of the womb. God blessed them with the good things of life but they had a no child for several years. Another brother whom I thought would be reasonable in reasoning told me he knew their wealth was impure and that may be the reason for their delay. In fact, he attributed their delay to the dark secret behind their wealth. This is someone who is related to the brother by blood. I cringed with fear because I never expected such from him. I made a personal decision to avoid any discussion that touched on that brother and sister with him neither did I inform the expectant family what I heard. I trusted God with that family and to God's glory, they have a child now. Hallelujah!!! A word of caution, be careful what you say to those passing through life's challenges so that you won't be seen as their enemy when their testimony comes.



Job's friends were unkind in their remarks towards Him. If you take your time to study Job, you will notice that some of his friends attributed his ordeal to the sins of his youth or to some evil he did. This is a man whom the bible described as perfect, a man that was pleasing to God but men are saying he was suffering for his sins. I'm sure those words hurt Job deep into his marrows but he did something worthy of emulation- he forgave his friends. *"After Job had prayed for his friends, the LORD restored his fortunes and gave him twice as much as he had before*", Job42:10. Your restoration is hinged upon forgiving those who hurt you in time of trouble, those who spoke unkind words to you attributing your challenges to your sin, prayerlessness, unkind attitude and so on. Please forgive them, pray for them and let go.




Sometimes, you really can't tell what people have in mind about you until you find yourself in the murky waters of life's trouble. I am sure that some of those who benefited from the ministry of Jesus were among those who shouted "Crucify Him". When Jesus hung on the cross looking helpless, he was mocked by His own people but listen to what He said in Luke 23:34, *"Jesus said, “Father, forgive them, for they do not know what they are doing.” And they divided up his clothes by casting lots*". When life deal a deadly blow on you and men turn their backs on you speaking hurtful words instead of encouraging you, don't let it bother you. It is the devil's ploy to prolong your stay in the wilderness of life if you allow bitterness and unforgiveness set in. Emulate Job and Jesus in this. I see God turning your life around soon in Jesus name.



*CONFESSION*: My change is coming. The dark days will soon be over and I shall walk into my testimony in Jesus name. I refuse to bear grudge, I forgive every unkind word spoken to me and about my challenges. I walk in love in Jesus name.

*LESSONS FROM THE LIFE OF JOB? Pt.6*

Job 42:12 So the Lord blessed Job in the second half of his life even more than in the beginning. For now he had 14,000 sheep, 6,000 camels, 1,000 teams of oxen, and 1,000 female donkeys. NLT

*THE SECOND HALF SHALL BE BETTER*

I love the account of Job's life. It gives hope and assures that if a child of God pass through fire and waters of trouble, the latter end shall be better. The latter part of Job's life was better than the beginning. I love the way New Living Translation puts it, *"So the Lord blessed Job in the second half of his life even more than in the beginning...*". There's a second half of victory, breakthrough, revival, testimony, answered prayers and success.
Life is in phases. This present moment may be your own trouble phase, waiting phase, prayer & fasting phase, mockery phase. Don't bother yourself too much about this phase because it shall pass away. Apostle Paul call this phase "momentary", that is it won't last long: *"For our light and momentary troubles are achieving for us an eternal glory that far outweighs them all*", 2Cor4:17.


When a thing is momentary, it won't last long. Abraham's barrenness was momentary, eventually, he became a father and Sarah carried her baby. That will be your testimony. You cannot die like this, no you can't. There's a power that transforms and lift a man out of the dungeon of trouble and settle him in the place of peace. That power is the power of God. It is working in my life already. I have seen it change the story of those whom the world never thought will amount anything. I've seen this power break the yoke of delay in marriage and settle people in their homes. Irrespective of what your challenge is, I decree, that power will bring about a change in your life in Jesus name.


Review all you've learnt in the life of Job and practice them. Live a life of praise, forgive those that mock you and walk worthy of God. Don't waste this precious insight that you have learnt. I see you testifying soon in Jesus name. There's a second half of testimony, victory, testimony, breakthrough, success and triumph. Now is your appointed time, you're in that season already. Enter into the reality of it Jesus name.


*PRAYER*: Father, change my story in Jesus name.

DEVOTIONAL 0330 | Source start line: 30583

11/14/21, 7:35 PM - Lanre Oyeleke: ProjectSave Devotional
Sunday,
November 15th, 2021.


*THE NATURE OF REVELATION* Pt.1

Ecclesiastes 3:1 For everything there is a season, a time for every activity under heaven. NLT


*DISCERNING REVELATION FOR THE FUTURE*


I read an online article about the camp meeting of Kenneth E. Hagin of blessed memory. He started that camp meeting program at the age of 57 according to the writer. Till date, the camp meeting he started is still running well and fine. However, there was something striking about the article that caught my attention. The writer said the camp meeting came as an instruction from God to him probably in the early days of his ministry but he didn't start until he clocked 57. Now, that may sound like starting too late but as far as God was concerned, that was the right time for him to start. What if he had started earlier stating that God spoke to him? He may have made a mess of it.



You need to understand that God can reveal His plan to you about something He wants you to do in the future. When God informs His children about a task programmed for the future, the purpose is to get us prepared for what will eventually happen. Certain things require training, prolong prayer, mentorship and adequate preparation. For example, God may have told you that you'll pastor a mega church but that may be an insight into the future. It may not be for immediate action. Some have rushed into ministry prematurely and they're biting their fingers. While waiting for time, you need to equip yourself in preparation for the fulfillment of what God has said. At other times, the preparation for what God has revealed may demand that you understudy someone who is already in ministry. There are many people who want to be pioneers but do not want to learn under those that have pioneered. This is one of the causes of failure in any endeavour.



Jesus came into the world knowing fully why He came and what He came for but he didn't launch out until He was 30 years of age. All He did in three and half years were known prior but he didn't launch out because he understood the nature of divine revelation. *It is not everything God reveals to you that requires immediate action*. Certain things revealed to you are not for immediate action but for the future. Learn to ask God questions whenever He speaks to you about a task to be done so that you don't take off before time. *There's right timing for every of God's purpose for your life. Don't be in haste.*


*PRAYER*: Father, I receive the grace to discern the timing of your purpose for my life in Jesus name.

DEVOTIONAL 0331 | Source start line: 30617
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
11/15/21, 4:45 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: PROJECTSAVE DEVOTIONAL
Monday,
November 16th,  2021.

*THE NATURE OF REVELATION* Pt.2

Psalms 5:8 Lead me in the right path, O LORD, or my enemies will conquer me. Make your way plain for me to follow.


*WHEN HE SPEAKS FIGURATIVELY*



My mentor told the story of a man of God who married many wives. He claimed God spoke to him that he should marry more wives. This surprised everyone that an evangelical preacher would subscribe to polygamy. He must have preached and taught against it but his sudden change shocked everyone. Eventually he died. At his funeral, the Spirit of God came upon someone in the congregation saying "Truly, I told my servant that I will give him more wives but what I meant was that I'll give my church more branches because the church is my bride"(paraphrase). Did God speak to him truly? Yes, He did. However, God spoke to him figuratively but he misunderstood and misinterpreted what God meant.



Sometimes, the nature of revelation is not always direct. God speaks figuratively, sometimes using types, shadows and illustrations to communicate to his children but misinterpretation is often the problem. In the body of Christ and among individual believers, there are several cases of misinterpretation of revelation. This has led many into untold error. Often, I tell people not to patronize dream interpreters and their books for obvious reasons. For example, God can show me egg in my dream as a symbol of something known to Him with the expectation that I'll ask Him what it meant. If I consult a dream interpretation book, I may get the wrong answer because God may mean differently at that point in time trying to communicate a peculiar message to me. That two persons see egg in their dream doesn't mean God is saying the same thing to both of them. God may use the egg as an illustrative symbol of the different messages He's trying to pass across.


Often, we are always quick to consult asking for meaning from humans without asking the Holy Spirit. Often, they may interpret that revelation on the basis of private meaning. That is, a meaning that was specific to their situation years before when God gave them a similar revelation. That may not be the general meaning at all times for all ages. That's where I have issues with dream interpretation books. Don't act on a revelation you don't understand fully. Learn to press deep into the mind of God for clarity after hearing or seeing into the plans and programs of God for your life so that you won't run the risk of wrong interpretation. If God can speak to you figuratively, He can interpret it for you. After Jesus spoke in parables, the disciples often asked Him for interpretation and He never failed to tell them. You are a son, ask your father for the true meaning of that revelation.


*PRAYER*: Father, I thank you for giving me the Spirit, the revealer of all truths. Lord, explain plainly to me the meaning of every unclear revelation in Jesus name.

DEVOTIONAL 0332 | Source start line: 30715

11/16/21, 5:01 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: ProjectSave Devotional
Tuesday
November 16th, 2021.

*THE NATURE OF REVELATION* Pt.3

Psalms 62:11 God has spoken plainly, and I have heard it many times: Power, O God, belongs to you; NLT


*WHEN GOD SEND A REMINDER*

In 2009 after I finished my final exams in the university, I stayed back in school awaiting project defense. During that period, I engaged in  a personal retreat. During that period, God revealed certain things to me about my life and ministry. It was clear and direct with confirmation. More so, the instructions were specific. Years down the line, I've had several reminders from God. On one occasion during my service year, I entered the bank to transact on behalf of the fellowship when we wanted to purchase our bus. When I entered the bank, in my mind, I began to fantasize about working in the bank after National youth service. It was as though something came over me and I just loved all I saw. That moment, ministry was not considered but banking. When I got to the counter where I wanted to transact, I signed the paper and did all that was required. The man looked at me and said, "when you finish your NYSC, don't consider working in a bank, face your ministry". Wow! That was direct. He didn't know what was on my mind when I entered the banking hall but God spoke through him to remind me about my ministry.


There was another occasion where a man of God who lived across the seas called to remind me about some of the specific instruction I received in 2009. I didn't discuss that vital aspect with him but God told him to remind me. Why does God send us reminder? There are several reasons for that. As humans, we have the tendencies to deviate from the specifics of God's instruction for our lives and be carried away. God, in His omniscience knowing our tendencies to deviate and be carried away by things that aren't necessary, He will send a reminder.




Another reason for divine reminders is to alert you about what ought to be done before time. There are instructions that requires certain preparation. When God sees that we are complacent about the preparatory phase, He may send us a reminder to wake us up so that we can prepare for the task ahead. If you don't want to risk forgetfulness, deviation and complacency about God's instruction to you, journal your dealings with God. You must have a book where instructions&revelations given by God are written and properly documented. Keep the book close to where you can have access to it from time to time. This is what helped Daniel to pray for Israel's independence(Daniel 9) from Babylonian captivity when it was almost time. He read from the book of prophet Jeremiah(25:11-13) that the time of their captivity was 70 years. Daniel prayed because Jeremiah's revelations were written. You can't pray effectively about certain instructions except they are well documented for reference purposes.


*ACTION POINT*: Why do you think God is constantly reminding you about that specific instruction? If you don't have a journal of your dealings with God, get one and start documenting every instruction received from God.


====================================================================================================
DEVOTIONAL 0333 | Source start line: 30791
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
11/17/21, 4:07 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: PROJECTSAVE DEVOTIONAL
Wednesday,
November 17th, 2021.

*THE NATURE OF REVELATION* Pt.4

Psalms 62:11 God has spoken plainly, and I have heard it many times: Power, O God, belongs to you; NLT

*THE NEED TO BE CURRENT WITH GOD*
When it comes to matters of divine instruction and revelation, we need to be current with God. All believers must understand the dynamics of divine revelation and how it works. I'll explain what I mean by "being current with God" using the story of Abraham and Isaac. In Genesis 22:2, God instructed Abraham to sacrifice his son, Isaac. It was God that spoke not devil. In obedience to that instruction, Abraham set out for mount Moriah the next day with the aim of sacrificing Isaac that very day all because God instructed him. Reading that chapter downward to verse 12, God gave a counter instruction saying, *"Do not lay a hand on the boy,” he said. “Do not do anything to him. Now I know that you fear God, because you have not withheld from me your son, your only son*".


Do you know that if Abraham wasn't sensitive to hear the latter instruction, he would have sacrificed Isaac in obedience to the first instruction not knowing that God was only testing him to know his heart. Abraham was current with God to know the first  and latter instruction. *Some believers are acting on stale instructions whose lifespan is over. Truly, they heard God about what they're doing presently but the time set for it is over. God is expecting them to hear the next instruction and move on*. Many are not current with God in matters of leading & instruction. Sometimes, we stay put on an instruction that is to last for a time before moving to the next phase. Certain programs have outlived its usefulness but we're still doing it because of the popularity it has gained. Sometimes, we fail to realize that God wants to be progressive with us. This is the problem with most believers.



Bishop David Oyedepo told the story of how God led him to plant a church in Kaduna. After sometime, he moved to Lagos and from there to Ota in Ogun state by the leading of the Spirit. Same God leading him to different places. If he had stayed in his first station without progressing with God to the next destination, he may have placed a limit on his ministry. I've heard about ministry set men who resigned from the ministry God put them over as the set man because of another assignment God has for them. Some would have stayed with the church as if it is their property claiming they received from God to start the church whereas God has moved on but they're left behind in the scheme of things. *It is dangerous for you stay on instructions that has expired or run out its course*. You may think you're making progress but you're just in one phase. Move with God and be current with Him.

*PRAYER*: Father, I receive the grace to be current with you in Jesus name.

11/18/21, 5:20 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: PROJECTSAVE DEVOTIONAL

November 18th,  2021.


*RIGHT ATTITUDE IN GIVING*


2Cor 9:7 Each one must give as he has decided in his heart, not reluctantly or under compulsion, for God loves a cheerful giver. ESV



Sometime ago, someone abroad reached out to me on social media having seen the pictures of our outreaches. She offered to partner with us. That made me glad because we need more hands to partner with us in spreading the Gospel. I told her I'll like her to be part of the social media group we created for partners so that she can get regular updates about programs & events. She accepted to join and she did but she exited the group same day. When I enquired to know why she did that, she said "I thought you were struggling". I asked to know what she meant. She told me that she thought we were struggling financially. It sounded rude to me. I replied by telling her "God is our provider". Knowing she's coming with foreign currency, I could have have resorted to begging and admitting that we are struggling financially so that she can send plenty money but I knew in my spirit that her attitude was not right.



I refused to enlist our ministry in the camp of strugglers even though we needed money and would continue to need money but I don't want anyone to give to us out of pity and sympathy. I could have told her about our projects on and off the field, the several missionaries we want to support, the churches to be planted on the mission fields and so on but I didn't do that simply because she wanted to give to a "struggling ministry" not to the kingdom vision we are running with. *No man can lift a ministry out of struggle, only God can because He is the owner of the ministry not us*. When you want to give to a ministry or minister, you need to do so out of conviction about the genuineness of the value the ministry/ minister is adding to kingdom expansion. *Your giving should support a worthy kingdom vision regardless of whether they have plenty or little. That's the attitude you must have when giving*. Those who want to help a "struggling ministry" may stop giving the day they perceive there's plenty money in the ministry coffers instead of supporting the vision. Besides, no genuine ministry can have enough to finish the great commission so why would someone think that his/her giving is so big to lift a ministry out of the struggle zone? *Ministers, beware of those who may want to use money to Lord over your life and ministry. You're not in ministry for the highest/ biggest donor. No man should take God's glory in your life and ministry*.



*If you stop giving to that ministry/ minister because they don't look like they're struggling anymore, your prior giving wasn't done with the right attitude*. Some may say, "I can't give any pastor riding a car or jet my money". That's a misguided statement. I noticed that some church folks love to see their pastors and his family look beggarly, tattered and hungry. That's their conviction for giving. The moment he rides a car and his children attend a good school, their giving will stop. They'll say "he's well fed". That's wrong. The command to support ministries and ministers has nothing to do with how much they have or do not have.  *Dearest, you need to have the right heart when extending your finances to a ministry or minister, check the vision of the ministry*. Are they involved in the great commission or they're already in great omission? Is their vision a kingdom vision or a self serving vision? Are they accountable to God and ministry partners? *Also, give as an extension of your worship to God and love for His kingdom. That's the attitude you must put on.*




*PRAYER*: Father, I receive the grace to put on the right attitude in matters of giving in Jesus name.

11/19/21, 5:20 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: PROJECTSAVE DEVOTIONAL
Friday,
November 19th, 2021.


*GIVING DOUBLE HONOUR TO YOUR MINISTER* Pt.1


1Tim5:17 The elders who direct the affairs of the church well are worthy of double honor, especially those whose work is preaching and teaching. For Scripture says, “Do not muzzle an ox while it is treading out the grain,” and “The worker deserves his wages.” NIV


*WHAT IS DOUBLE HONOUR*?



For several years, I read this portion of scriptures but I didn't know the exact meaning. My understanding was limited to the way it was rendered in English language. I thought double honour meant special reverence or respect for ministers of the gospel but studies made me realize that Paul meant differently. The phrase "double honour" in the Greek is "diplous time". It means to pay someone with the value of money. In context, Paul was advocating for proper care of ministers of the Gospel through payment and good welfare. Yes, that's what he meant and nothing more. This injunction of Paul to believers is what so many are attacking vehemently today. Some persons who are privileged to be chosen into the church board/ council will stop at nothing to oppose an improvement in their Pastor's welfare. If you're like this, stop it.





Since the "revolution" against certain scriptural practices started, I noticed that some church folks disregarded scriptural injunctions that talked about giving to their pastors because someone has told them all preachers are in ministry for their pocket. Hey! Not all are in ministry for their pocket and you can easily know this by careful observation. Such persons enjoy messages on other themes of scriptures but the moment we touch on issues like this, they may say, "That preacher has become greedy". When he was preaching holiness, sanctification, et al, he was pure in their eyes but on this, he has become a money monger. Whether you like it or not, this is part of the whole counsel of God we must teach even though we don't major on it. Same apostle Paul said, *"For I have not shunned to declare to you the whole counsel of God*", Acts 20:27. If we exclude this and preach other themes, we would not be delivering the total counsel of God.



Dearest, God's word to you and me is that we should consider those who labour over us in preaching and teaching worthy of double honour. This include field missionaries and those from whom you draw spiritual nourishment either through their books, writings ( online/ offline) and counsel.  Sometimes, when I look at those who forsook all to go to the mission field in extreme place where nothing is happening economically, I see men who deserve our constant support. I know so many missionaries working on the field of souls without pay. Not because they don't want to receive but their sending agencies cannot afford it. They are front line soldiers at the battle field, they don't have time for civilian affairs ( business) because of the nature of their calling yet some believers see them as "misguided overzealous lots". Listen to Paul: *"Who serves as a soldier at his own expense?...*", 1Cor9:7. The answer is "NO". There's no soldier who goes to war at his own expense. Let's become bible Christians who follow what is commanded in scriptures. There will always be abuses in matters like this but that shouldn't take our eyes off those who are doing it right. *Don't try to be socially correct and be scripturally wrong*. I call you blessed.



*ACTION POINT*: Since the year began, what have you done to support your Pastor and others who nourish you spiritually? Have you supported a field missionary?

DEVOTIONAL 0336 | Source start line: 30949

11/20/21, 6:39 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: ProjectSave Devotional
Saturday,
November 20th, 2021.

*GIVING DOUBLE HONOUR TO YOUR MINISTER* Pt.2


1Tim5:17 The elders who direct the affairs of the church well are worthy of double honor, especially those whose work is preaching and teaching. For Scripture says, “Do not muzzle an ox while it is treading out the grain,” and “The worker deserves his wages.” NIV

*DOUBLE HONOUR- A COMMAND BY GOD*

On several occasions, I've heard some believers deride being in ministry full time. There are several opinions about the issue of being in ministry full time and I know the arguments for and against but that's not the crux of our teaching. The unspoken  idea most of those who ridicule being in ministry fully have is that they're not comfortable when they see or hear that a minister is earning pay. As far as they're concerned, all those called into the five-fold ministry should be bivocational and they should work as volunteers not as one paid. That's the unspoken ridiculous mindset those who argue against being in ministry fully have. In their heart, they hate the prosperity of God's servants whereas, scriptures says: *"Let them shout for joy, and be glad, that favour my righteous cause: yea, let them say continually, Let the LORD be magnified, which hath pleasure in the prosperity of his servant*", Psalms 35:27.


What does God say about this issue? Apostle Paul, speaking by the Spirit says, *"In the same way, THE LORD HAS COMMANDED THAT THOSE WHO PREACH THE GOSPEL SHOULD RECEIVE THEIR LIVING FROM THE GOSPEL*", 1 Cor9:14, NIV. This is God's word, not mine. Who ordered that preachers be supported? God. A common arguments against supporting ministers is that Paul worked with his hands and did not receive support from the Corinthian church (1Cor 9:12) but in 2 Cor 11:8, we saw that Paul took wages from other churches: *"I robbed other churches, taking WAGES of them, to do you service*". His arrangement in Corinth was the exception, not the rule. To expound more on scriptures, Apostle Paul supported the "double honour" citing the arrangement God had with the temple workers in the old testament: *"Don’t you realize that those who work in the temple get their meals from the offerings brought to the temple? And those who serve at the altar get a share of the sacrificial offerings*", 1Cor 9:13. Another scripture I love to share on this subject is the epistle to the Galatians in chapter 6:6: *"Those who are taught the word of God should provide for their teachers, sharing all good things with them*", NLT. This is profound.


Dearest, the scriptures has spoken to us. All texts cited were not written by me but by inspiration of God through His holy Apostle.  *Never allow popular opinion determine your action. I teach this with all sense of moderation not as someone after filthy lucre. I'm voicing the mind of God to you pointing you to your holy responsibility to GENUINE servants of God not for those who are using ministry as a means to an end*. Don't ignore your pastor's welfare. Those who teach you God's word ( online/offline) should  receive support from you. On several occasions, I've sent my seed to preachers I've not met but whose teachings has blessed me tremendously. A brother once told us that whenever a popular Christian drama ministry produces a new film, he sends a token to the ministry. That's the spirit. Right from my days in the university, as a young teenager in love with Jesus and His kingdom, I adopted a missionary for support from my meagre allowance and I allowed a village evangelist sleep in my room whenever he comes to preach in the villages around my campus. Today, it is a culture in our ministry to support genuine missionaries. *There's nothing that is too much to be done for those who are genuinely working for the Lord so that they can focus on their assignment*. If people see nothing wrong in giving a wealthy music star their money how much more God's holy servants. *Giving is the proof that you've conquered greed*.


*ACTION POINT*: Since the year began, what have you done to support your Pastor and others who nourish you spiritually? Have you supported a field missionary? If you haven't, do something before the year is over.

*A BIKE MAN TAUGHT ME ABOUT GOD*


Psalms 63:1 O God, you are my God, earnestly I seek you; my soul thirsts for you, my body longs for you, in a dry and weary land where there is no water. I have seen you in the sanctuary and beheld your power and your glory.



Sometime ago, I wanted to board a bike in front of an old man's house. This old man told me to wait for another bike because the very bike I wanted to board had a bad brake. I thanked him for letting me in on that. Later, an old bike man rode to where I was. I thanked the old man who cautioned me about the other bike. As I was about boarding the new bike, the bike man also demanded I say "thank you" to him. I was startled. I told him politely that he doesn't deserve my "thank you" till we get to our destination. Then, he picked me up on that and throughout the short journey, he taught me certain things about God I haven't paid attention to before then. I paid attention to this man's sermon as though I haven't heard a preacher/ teacher of the word all my life. He practically adjusted and help realign my "theology".



When I alighted from the bike, I didn't need anyone to tell me to say 'thank you' to the man. He added huge value to me. In fact, as I was about departing from him, he gave me a word of knowledge and I knew the man was God-sent for that short time to lift my spirit and edify me. My spirit agreed with his totally. When I returned home that day, I told my wife about the encounter. I thank God I didn't let pride get in my way that day. If I had shut my mind against the bike man because of who he was, I may have missed that lovely encounter.  What point am I trying to make here? God can arrange encounters for you in places you least expect. God can send the lowliest men to teach and school you properly about Him. The Jews missed their time of encounter because they despised the son of a carpenter and Jesus wept for them:  *"and level you, and your children within you, to the ground; and they will not leave in you one stone upon another, because you did not know the time of your visitation*”, Luke19:44.




I found something interesting in Luke3:1-2 about John the Baptist. The bible told us about men of timber and calibre in the city including the high priest of the temple yet God's word was with a man whose ministry headquarters was in the wilderness: *"In the fifteenth year of the reign of Tiberius Caesar—when Pontius Pilate was governor of Judea, Herod tetrarch of Galilee, his brother Philip tetrarch of Iturea and Traconitis, and Lysanias tetrarch of Abilene— during the high-priesthood of Annas and Caiaphas, THE WORD OF GOD CAME TO JOHN SON OF ZECHARIAH IN THE WILDERNESS*". God can give an answer to the question of your heart through men and women you least expect. There are places you never would think you'll find God but He is there. In this age of high level social media publicity and hype of events, program and persons, we tend to attach value to class instead of substance.  John the baptist was not a preacher with class but he had substance. I've had people ask me if I listen to a particular preacher or teacher. Sometimes, I've listened but I can't really find edification and the spiritual value I need. I ask if I'm hearing well. Whereas, there are men unknown to the public whose message or teaching in a few minutes will cut through my heart and get me praying. For me, that's true encounter with God. *What your spiritual life need may not be in popular places and with popular preachers*. The grocer across the street may be a carrier of the word you need. That church without heavy social media presence with unpopular pastor may be the person you need. Let your heart be positioned rightly. *Stop hunting  after class, go for substance*.



*PRAYER*: Father, I thank you for your word today. Help me in Jesus name.

*YOU ARE IMPORTANT TO GOD*

Psalms 8:4 What are mere mortals that you should think about them, human beings that you should care for them? NLT


I have battled condemnation and guilt in the past. It was so bad I slipped into depression. One of the several things the devil did was to make me feel unimportant to God. Those days, anytime I'm in church and a word of knowledge comes, I always expected the preacher to mention my case to prove that God knows my name and hasn't forgotten me. Have you been there? On several occasions, during a prophetic ministration, the preacher may point at someone and call the person out. Deep in my heart, I wondered why God would call out one person in the crowd and neglect me. These things gave me serious concern many years ago. For me, i attached so much importance to such dramatic encounters as proofs of being loved by God. The devil knew I lacked the understanding of how important I was to God and he played on my emotions and feelings. On several occasions, I felt i was neglected by God.


*To start with, physical happenings like names&cases being mentioned in a prophetic ministration doesn't validate God's love to us. How important we are to God is not  also dependent on how we feel*. As a matter of fact, you can't 'feel' God's love you're expected to sense it because feelings are fickle and could be triggered by several internal and external factors. Moreso, God doesn't exist in sense realm of touch and feelings, He is Spirit, John 4:24. You need faith to connect with Him. You need to settle this truth in your heart that you're very important to God regardless of how you feel. The Psalmist saw how important mankind is to God, he said: *"what are mere mortals that you should think about them, human beings that you should care for them? YET YOU MADE THEM ONLY A LITTLE LOWER THAN GOD AND CROWNED THEM WITH GLORY AND HONOR*", Psalms 8:4-5 NLT. Wow! After the Godhead, man is next in rank not even angels. You are greater than angels.



*The day you start seeing yourself the way God sees you as written in His word, you will be free from self-pity, guilt and condemnation*. You're so important to God to the point where He gave up His only begotten son to die for your redemption, John 3:16. If you were the only one on earth, that won't stop God from sending Jesus to die for you. Hallelujah!!! Quit self-pity. Prophet Isaiah underscore your importance to God by revealing God's mind about you: “Never! Can a mother forget her nursing child? Can she feel no love for the child she has borne? But even if that were possible, I would not forget you!See, I have written your name on the palms of my hands. Always in my mind is a picture of Jerusalem’s walls in ruins*", Isaiah49:15-16,NLT. Though He was talking to Israel but it applies to every believer in Christ because we now share in the common wealth of Israel through redemption. Glory! The devil will attack us from the sense realm through our thoughts because he want to hinder our fellowship with the father and block us from receiving what belongs to us by faith. *The moment you start doubting your importance to God, you block answers to your prayers*. The devil will always try to make you feel unimportant, if you stay with him in the realm of reasoning, he'll deal with you but if you stick to what God has said about you in His word, you'll triumph.


*CONFESSION*: Father I thank you for loving me. I now know I'm important because your word say so. I reject guilt and condemnation.

*OVERCOMING THE POWERS OF THIS AGE*. Pt.1

1Tim4:1 Now the Spirit speaketh expressly, that in the latter times some shall depart from the faith, giving heed to seducing spirits, and doctrines of devils;

*SPIRIT OF SEDUCTION*

On 5th of August 2007, it was reported in an online news blog that five University students in Nigeria embarked on an apocalyptic journey seeking for rapture in the forest. They were members of a fellowship that taught extremes and deceptive ideas. From oral report, some of the students who belonged to this group were taught unscriptural esoteric practices and they delved into certain dimensions of spiritism. Some were taught that they could be in two places at a time. Some of them intentionally missed tests&exams because they believed they could be in the hostel and they'll appear in class to write exams at the same time. They had food recipes for hearing into the spiritual realm. Surprisingly, a chunk of their devotees were drawn from various campus fellowships, people who were fed by the word of God but were fascinated by extra biblical knowledge. They opened up to the spirit of seduction and they were deceived. The spirit of seduction is one demonic spirit you and I will have to combat in this age if we'll remain on track in our walk with God. What some disdained yesterday has become the norm today because of deception. It  always start as a harmless idea till it snowballs into what cannot be handled easily.



The word seduction is from the Greek word "Planaho". The word that best describe it is 'deception'. It denotes causing someone to wander and be led astray. There are uncountable numbers of seducing spirits that has been released into the world today, deceiving people and making them believe lies. Unfortunately, they are already operating in the church among believers. Many years ago, when LGBTQ was uncommon and detested by many believers, a sister sent me a video about how it will be accepted through subtle deception. It was predicted then that what the church( believers) was against ( LGBTQ), she will embrace through the workings of this demon(Paraphrase). Today, that prediction has come to pass. There are believers who stood against LGBTQ years back but now are drumming support for its permissiveness in the society and church. They're telling us that God made some persons that way and we must accept them. This is not ordinary brethren. This age is the most dangerous to live in because on daily basis, the enemy is trying to make us belief lies and accept it as truth.


How can you prevent this spirit from gaining a foothold in your life? The answer is in Proverbs 4:23, *"GUARD YOUR HEART above all else, for it determines the course of your life*", NLT. Seducing spirits captures the heart of people through what they hear and see continually. The moment you expose yourself to the wrong stuff, you'll be seduced. Be careful; people can seduce you without your realizing what is happening to you. *Sometimes, another factor that makes seduction to take root in people's heart is undue craving for power, the esoteric and extra biblical knowledge*. There are folks always trying to seek some special knowledge and supernatural experience. It is just a matter of time, they'll be deceived. I once met a guy who was once a member of a Holiness preaching church, a conservative one at that. When I inquired to know why he changed to Islam, he replied by saying the search for the truth led him into Islam. Same happened to a pastor who decided to carry out a research on African Traditional Religion, he ended up as a fetish priest( 'babalawo' in Yoruba). That's seduction at work. The moment a man's heart is not in alignment with  simple truths of scriptures, this spirit will take over. From today, don't listen or watch just anything on your radio and t.v. All magazines are not for your consumption. Social media is awash with intentional propaganda to deceive many. Guard your heart!

*ACTION POINT*: Check those strange ideas, books, discussion, relationship, t.v.&radio programmes you're exposed to. Weigh them on the scale of scriptures. If they're found wanting, discard them. If there's any idea or concept you don't understand that sounds strange, prayerfully ask a brother/ sister who can shed more light on it.

11/24/21, 5:34 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: PROJECTSAVE DEVOTIONAL

*OVERCOMING THE POWERS OF THIS AGE*. Pt.2

Leviticus 19:31 Regard not them that have familiar spirits, neither seek after wizards, to be defiled by them: I am the LORD your God.

*FAMILIAR SPIRITS*
Years back, we had an outreach in a village in Akoko-Edo, Edo state of Nigeria. When the outreach was on, we heard sound of shootings that reverberated through the village. It continued repeatedly. At a time, i became afraid because I took team members to a place far from home but I noticed that the villagers were unperturbed. I asked to know what was happening. I was told that the necromancers were talking to someone who died to know the cause of his/ her death and to know if the person had involvement with witchcraft while alive. That's their custom in that village and the people believe in necromancy so much. People from different parts of the country go to this village to consult necromancers to help them consult their departed loved ones for vital information. I asked to know if the dead person appear truly, they answered in the affirmative. I've met someone who consulted a necromancer when his father died and he told me he saw his dad.






Necromancy is not the focus of my teaching but to unravel the spirit behind their art. Necromancers use familiar spirits to operate. What are familiar spirits? These are spirits that are familiar with you and your history. These spirits study specific bloodlines of a family, they have details of that family and can perpetuate evil in the blood line. *When necromancers consult the dead on behalf of people, it is not the dead person that appears or speak to them, it is a familiar spirit( a demon that knows the dead person) that takes the form of the person and speak like the person*. The truth is that these spirits can speak accurately like the person would if he/she were alive because they know the person and have details about him/ her. This is a high level demonic manipulation. God doesn't send the dead to speak to the living because *"...it is appointed unto men once to die, but after this the judgment:*", Heb 9:27. However, do familiar spirits operate in people's lives while still alive? The answer is yes. In that case, what do they do? They perpetuate evil in the blood line. They ensure negative family history is repeated in a lineage.



They monitor the repetition of evil patterns from one generation to the other in a family because they are familiar with the family history. In my few years of ministry and standing in the gap with the saints, I've seen evil patterns perpetuated in families. I've seen families where no one stay in marriage. Even if they are virtuous in character, that demon will find a way to perpetuate the evil in their family. They can influence their spouse( if he/she is not standing solidly in Christ) to maltreat them or frustrate them out if the marriage. I once ministered to a lady who told me about the untimely death in her mom's family. At that time, her mother who was the last&only surviving child of her family was sick. Later, she died within the age bracket her other siblings died and of the same sickness. Death wiped off all the children of a family in a similar fashion. Those that lack spiritual intelligence may call it coincidence or call the sickness genetic( which may be true scientifically) but forgetting that there's a power behind it. I'm here to tell you that you can be free from any form of evil pattern in your family history. The very first thing you need is an understanding of your redemptive rights and privileges. You now belong to God's family where the blood of deity ( Christ) runs, 1 Pet1:17-21. It is a blood without negative family pattern/ history. Using the name of Jesus coupled with this understanding, you can demand your freedom from the bondage of negative family pattern perpetuated by familiar spirits.


*CONFESSION*: I belong to the family of God, I'm birth by the blood of Christ. In my ancestry, evil is not permitted. I'm redeemed from evil family pattern. I decree and declare, I break free from the yoke of negative family pattern perpetuated by demons in Jesus name. I refuse the evil in my earthly ancestry. In Christ, I am triumphant in Jesus name.

DEVOTIONAL 0341 | Source start line: 31206

11/25/21, 4:16 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: PROJECTSAVE DEVOTIONAL
Thursday,
November 25th,  2021.


*OVERCOMING THE POWERS OF THIS AGE*. Pt.3


1Kings 22:22 And the LORD said unto him, Wherewith? And he said, I will go forth, and I will be a lying spirit in the mouth of all his prophets. And he said, Thou shalt persuade him, and prevail also: go forth, and do so. KJV


*LYING SPIRIT*

The opening text tells the story of prophet Micaiah the son of Imlah when he was before Ahab and Jehoshaphat partly. To get the whole story, I'll advice you read the whole chapter. Ahab wanted to go to war against Ramoth Gilead, he informed Jehoshaphat who visited him. The latter requested they enquire from God to know whether they should go or not. Ahab's prophets prophesied that he should go to war. They all said the same thing. Jehoshaphat perceived something was wrong so he asked if they could consult another prophet. Ahab told him there was another prophet, Micaiah. His remark about him was that he never prophesied something good to and about him. Truly, when he came on the scene, he spoke the mind of God about the war which was opposed to what the prophets of Ahab said. He affirmed that the prophets of Ahab were speaking by the spirit of lies. A demon spirit of deception. He was jailed for speaking the truth but his word came to pass.




*Another spirit of this age is the spirit of lies. Often, this spirit present lies as the truth through men and women who are under its influence*. There are people whose words cannot be trusted because they lie at all times. We have heard of people who lied about certain things in their past before entering into marriage. Some built their marriage on lies. When I was single, there were sisters who lied to me that God spoke to them that I'll be their husband. Very 'spiritual' and pious looking sisters. One of them involved her pastor to convince me and she reported me to my predecessor. Her insistence on marrying me was so strong all because she was told by a diviner that I was meant for her. A reliable source told me she took my name and some brothers to a diviner for checking.  I knew God was not involved at all. *It is more dangerous when the spirit of lies speak through prophets and those in the ministry*. In our generation, there are several cases of people who were lied to by a prophet. Some prophets lie because they want to impress while others are under pressure to say "thus saith the Lord" because of the pressure to perform.  A preacher can quickly manufacture something  to please the curiosity of the people when God hasn't spoken.  *I'm always careful around those who always have a word for almost everyone but not themselves*. We should be careful about those who always want to prophesy during and after every service. They always have something to say.  It is not uncommon in our generation to hear prophets predict lies at the beginning of the year and during election seasons. In recent and distant past,  i've heard and read so many 'political prophecies' like that of Ahab's prophets. Unfortunately, many elects are deceived into believing these lying prophets even when their prophetic prediction fails. The spirit of lies is at work massively in this generation.





When you position yourself to always want to hear something that suits you ( aside the word of God revealed in scriptures) under the guise of "thus saith the Lord", you will be deceived by the spirit of lies. *A cursory look at the story of Ahab showed that he loved a lie. Why did I say so? This is because he knew there was another prophet whose prediction would be different from that of his personal prophets but he wasn't interested in hearing what he has to say.  Deep within, he knew his prophets were lying but he couldn't refute their prediction because what they were saying suited his fancy. This is the most dangerous state for anyone. A state where you'll surround yourself with those who will say what you want to hear instead of what you need to hear. Very dangerous*. It is an express license given to the spirit of lies to minister always. Take a stand for the truth and never shift grounds. Stick to the word of God. Let it be what you want to hear at all times. This way, you will not position yourself to be influenced by the spirit of lies.



*PRAYER*: Father in the name of Jesus Christ, I refuse to come under the influence of the spirit of lies in Jesus name.

*OVERCOMING THE POWERS OF THIS AGE*. Pt.4


Acts 16:16 And it came to pass, as we went to prayer, a certain damsel possessed with a spirit of divination met us, which brought her masters much gain by soothsaying:  KJV



*SPIRIT OF DIVINATION*


One of our ministry associates told me about a prophet who visited his former residence. The man had clients who were into internet fraud in that area. This man was their personal prophet; in one of his visits, he told him certain things about his life of which they were accurate. Later, this prophet told him certain things about his future. However, he was wise to know that this prophet was not operating by the Spirit of God. In contrast to lying spirit, there's another demon that can predict accurately and speak truthfully about hidden things. This spirit is called the spirit of divination. This is the spirit behind the prophetic accuracy of some prophets. It is the same spirit that work with fetish priests&diviners. They'll tell you your phone number, what you ate, your address, etc just to prove to you they're seeing correctly. Before you talk, they may tell you your problems. Have you seen this before?




The slave girl in Acts 16 operated by this demon. What she said about Paul and Silas was true: *"...These men are the servants of the most high God, which shew unto us the way of salvation*",Acts 16:17 KJV. She predicted accurately. If it were today, some preachers may ordain her as a minister in church. The next they'll tell her is, "do you know you have a call into ministry"? Apostle Paul, being a man of the Spirit knew something was out of place. He rebuked the demon speaking through that girl: *"And this did she many days. But Paul, being grieved, turned and said to the spirit, I command thee in the name of Jesus Christ to come out of her. And he came out the same hour*",  Acts 16:18 KJV. Hallelujah!!! We need to be discerning like Paul. There are many of such persons around today who need the kind of ministration Paul did on the slave girl.




*Dearest, don't let accuracy of prediction freak you. Accuracy of prophetic prediction is not the proof of a genuine call into ministry*. The hearts of many have been captured by this but often they end in error. When those diviners see things supernaturally, the prescribed solution is often against  scriptures. That's why we have people going to the river to bath, buying prayer soap and water, and engaging in other extra biblical practices all because a prophet saw accurately and they feel they owe him obedience to instructions. We are not saying there are no genuine predictions by the Holy Spirit, there are but we need not seek after predictions but after Jesus through prayer and the word of God. *Those that truthfully seek after Jesus will never fall into wrong hands*. Most times, people are trapped in error because they're seeking the miraculous, someone to predict the future and tell them hidden things. *When your pursuit of God is pure and with the right heart, you'll not fall into error*.



*PRAYER*: Father, I refuse to be trapped by the spirit of divination in Jesus name.

*OVERCOMING THE POWERS OF THIS AGE*. Pt.5


Hosea 5:4 They will not frame their doings to turn unto their God: for the spirit of whoredoms is in the midst of them, and they have not known the LORD.

I've been privileged to preach to prostitutes and I've had very close discussion with some of them. A cursory look into the rise of sexual immorality in our world, one cannot but conclude that what we are seeing is not ordinary. There are demons of sexual immorality the enemy has released into our world to wreck lives and destinies without a recourse for age, gender and status. When a man comes under the influence of this demon, he will find it hard to control himself. Some ladies under the influence of this demon cannot go a day without having sex. While science calls them nymphomaniac, I know from the little  spiritual intelligence I have that such is the outworking of a demonic spirit. I once watched the video of a young girl teenager who said that any day without sex will make her feel abnormal. She has become addicted to sex. This demon work with other demons to bring men&women of great destinies down. It hunt for star studded destinies. Any child of God that is not careful can come under its influence.  There are many polluted minds walking around today. They carry in their bodies a strong sexual urge that needs quick discharge. That's why we have a lot of sexual abuses going on in our world. It is inconceivable that a man will defile a young girl of 18 months. Isn't that strange?



How do people come under the influence of this demon? The simple answer is exposure. When people expose their minds to sexual pollutants, this demon is given a foothold. The enemy is smart. Almost everything we see in our media today has a form of sex appeal ranging from the advert of soap to cars. The music, comedy and movies aren't helping matter. We live in the days of explicit sexual content. Sometimes while working on your internet enabled PC, phones and device, a sexually suggestive video/ post can pop up. As people expose their minds to contents sponsored by this demon, they come under its influence. The Psalmist(119:9) says, *"How can a young person stay pure? By obeying your word*". The word of God has a life transforming power, Rom12:2. While taking these practical steps daily,  if you know you're under the influence of this demon, you need to cast it out.


*PRAYER*: Fire of the Holy Spirit, purge my heart. Every demon of sexual immorality lurking within seeking to control my mind and action, take your leave right now in Jesus name.

*OVERCOMING THE POWERS OF THIS AGE*. Pt.6


Luke 13:11 And, behold, there was a woman which had a spirit of infirmity eighteen years, and was bowed together, and could in no wise lift up herself. KJV


*SPIRIT OF INFIRMITY*

During the lockdown of 2020, we had time to watch television especially global news. The daily updates about mortality was scary. During our family devotion, we confessed Psalm91 often. I knew in my spirit that the pandemic wasn't ordinary because it grinded almost everything to a halt. However, I didn't confront it for what it is not until I tested positive to it. Before I tested positive to COVID-19 in Ghana, I had strange symptoms but I bought anti malaria& anti biotics thinking what I had was malaria or bacterial infection. After I finished the medications, nothing change. My muscle stiffened, i lost my sense of smell totally & taste to some extent. It was after I tested positive that I confronted the spirit behind it.



There are demons of infirmity the devil has released into the world. Presently, the world is under the siege of these demons. The monies that some families and individuals ought to be using for kingdom expansion and personal welfare is being pumped into purchase of drugs. The world of medical science research has received so much grants not because of any other thing but to find cure for demonic ailments. When the spirit of infirmity chooses to deal with people, they may spend all their life savings on finding cure. What a wicked demon! Unfortunately, so many do not see sickness especially the incurable ones from this perspective. I understand that sicknesses can be self inflicted through poor hygiene, lack of exercise and improper diet. Hey! What about COVID-19, HIV AIDS, Cancer, autoimmune diseases, genetic disorders and several other dangerous diseases? Till date, science cannot tell us the exact causes of these diseases, just speculations. I know the direct causative agent of these diseases- spirit of infirmity.



Don't accept sickness as the norm. The death of Christ on the was a payment for your healing so you have no reason to be down one more day: *"who his own self bare our sins in his own body on the tree, that we, being dead to sins, should live unto righteousness: by whose stripes ye were healed*", 1 Peter 2:24 KJV. All the diseases were laid on Jesus, who is laying it on you again? That wouldn't have been God, it is the devil. God cannot use sickness to teach you a lesson. That's a relegation of the ministry of the Spirit. *You can be free from infirmity if you desire to be free*. The name of Jesus and your faith in the finished work of Christ on the cross is the answer to your healing and health.




*CONFESSION*: I refuse infirmity, Christ has paid for my healing. I refuse to be bedridden, I refuse being a regular caller at the hospital. What cannot be found in Christ's body should not be found in my body. I rebuke the spirit of (...mention the name of the sickness) in the name of Jesus. The blood of Christ has made me whole.

*OVERCOMING THE POWERS OF THIS AGE*. Pt.7

Isaiah61:3 To appoint unto them that mourn in Zion, to give unto them abeauty for ashes, the oil of bjoy for mourning, the garment of praise for the spirit of heaviness; that they might be called ctrees of drighteousness, the eplanting of the Lord, that he might be glorified.


*SPIRIT OF HEAVINESS*


What is the meaning of heaviness? The word 'heaviness' is from the Hebrew word "Keheh" meaning dull, dim or faint. It speaks of a situation where someone is sorrowful. This sorrow is not necessarily as a result of negative happenings. It is caused by a demon called the spirit of heaviness. Most bible scholars agree that this demon is behind depression. *Some are depressed not because of a sad event but simply because they are under the influence of a demonic spirit*. In fact, some go as far as commiting suicide despite having medical care by experts.  A vivid example  is the case of Matthew Warren, son of Rick Warren. When he committed suicide, Kristin Cole, the spokeswoman said, “Despite the best health care available, this was an illness that was never fully controlled and the emotional pain resulted in his decision to take his life,”. The issue wasn't about medical care because he had the best medical care. A demonic spirit was involved.



Depression is a common mental disorder. According to World Health Organization, globally, it is estimated that 5.0% of adults suffer from depression. Depression is a leading cause of disability worldwide and is a major contributor to the overall global burden of disease. The rise in the cases  of suicide is becoming alarming. I visited an area some day and I sensed deep sorrow in the atmosphere. My host told me that one of their neighbours committed suicide and when such happens, traditionalists will have to do a spiritual cleansing of that environment. They had the spiritual intelligence to know there's something spiritual about what happened though they applied the wrong solution. The point I'm trying to bring out here is that what we commonly call depression( a mental disorder) also has a spiritual side to it. Several years ago, I was  depressed for months.  Mine came with feelings of guilt and loss of self worth. Sometimes, I felt something heavy in my chest region. I battled this for several months. When i realized it was demonic, I confronted it with the weapons in my arsenal. I felt something lifted from my chest region. Hallelujah!!! I've been free ever since.



You can be free from depression. You need to realize that your challenge is principally spiritual. Deliverance is one of the ministries of Jesus, Luke 4:18. I appreciate medical scientists. They have been helpful in many ways especially in this aspect. *However, pills and injection cannot solve a spiritual problem, it can only take care of physical symptoms*. Learn to take authority over this demon whenever you sense it putting heaviness on your heart. Be conscious of your position of authority over demons always and do not cease to use the name of Jesus to send this demon of heaviness packing: *"Wherefore God also hath highly exalted him, and given him a name which is above every name: that at the name of Jesus every knee should bow, of things in heaven, and things in earth, and things under the earth;*" Philippians 2:9‭-‬10 KJV. Another helpful thing to do is to live a praise-filled life. When your life is full of thanksgiving and praises, the spirit of heaviness will be far from you because that demon doesn't stay where people are praising God. Also, it doesn't stay in the life of someone that is full of praises.


*PRAYER*: Father in the name of Jesus, I take authority over every demon of heaviness causing depression in my life,. You demon of depression, i send you out of my life never to return in Jesus name.
