*OVERCOMING THE POWERS OF THIS AGE*. Pt.8

Matthew 6:24 “No man can serve two masters: for either he will hate the one, and love the other; or else he will hold to the one, and despise the other. Ye cannot serve God and mammon,”

*SPIRIT OF MAMMON-RA*

Another end time demon that Jesus warned against was mammon. The word mammon is from the Greek word mammonas which is often associated with “money, wealth, and material possessions.” According to Pat Holliday's research, "Mammon-Ra was the name of the ancient Syrian god of riches and wealth. It was the Babylonian god of money and forces. Mammon-Ra, symbolized by a double headed eagle, having octopus-like tentacles, is the Egyptian god Pharaoh went down to the river each morning to worship. Mammon is a water demon. The spirit of Mammon is a powerful demon, worshipped with great pomp by pagan Jewish Cabalist hungry and greedy for wealth and power. It’s also the spirit that the world worships". Without any shadow of doubt, this is the demon that makes people pursue wealth unjustly and live for mundane things alone. Think about the rise in internet fraud, kidnapping, selling human parts for money and other vices, this is the chief demon behind it.




When Jesus spoke about this demon, he brought in the issue of service/ worship. Why? This demon demand worship from people. Jesus said, "Ye cannot serve(worship) God and mammon". Emphasis mine. This demon is always competing with God in the hearts of men. It tries to take the place of God in the lives of people. If it weren't so, Jesus would have spoken about it the way He did saying "Ye cannot serve two masters". *Mammon is a bad master*. No true child of God can serve mammon and God at the same time. *Today, most people do not bow down to this god in the physical because his temple isn't around anymore but in their hearts, they bow to him daily- his spirit lives on*. They neglect God and His kingdom and run after what mammon can give. This is the reason behind all forms of greed and avarice we see today. Some politicians who are wealthy will steal government money their 10th generation cannot exhaust because they're led by mammon. Mammon is a dangerous demon.



Unfortunately, the demon of mammon controls the actions&preaching of some preachers& ministers of the Gospel. *The Gospel that present God as one who needs your money before He can bless you and perform miracles in your life is a mammon motivated Gospel*. Isn't Mammon the reason behind some "dangerous" seed sowing demand that is almost tantamount to merchandising God's free grace and gifts? I've preached a mammon motivated message before unknowingly because that's what I heard those before me preach. I heard it from senior preachers and read it in their books.  *When mammon rules the heart of a preacher/ minister, he will not preach,teach or sing without a fee*. Listen to what Jesus has to say in Luke12:15(KJV): *"And He said unto them, “Take heed and beware of covetousness, for a man’s life consisteth not in the abundance of the things which he possesseth*”. This has been a caution for me anytime my heart is drifting towards mammon worship. The key to freedom is to dethrone the altar of mammon in your heart through prayers&accurate understanding of the word. As long as you understand your bible from Mammon's perspective, you'll never be free. *The theology of Mammon is strong and it has so many scholars promulgating it*. As long as you listen to Mammon motivated preachers and attend Mammon preaching churches,  you won't be free.  The hold of Mammon is strong, Mammon's culture is a global culture manifesting in different ways. Stand against it.


*PRAYER*: Father, I receive total deliverance from the control of Mammon. I break the altar of Mammon in my life and I refuse to bow any more to this evil god controlling the hearts of men in Jesus name. Amen

*UNHEALTHY SURPRISE*


Jer 3:15 And I will give you shepherds after my own heart, who will guide you with knowledge and understanding. NLT



In my few years of counseling and praying with singles trusting God for a life partner, I've seen and experienced what I call "unhealthy surprise". This is what I mean; some singles are fond of asking their pastors for prayers when they're trusting God for a life partner but the moment a suitor comes knocking or may be they see a sister, they break off from their pastors and spiritual mentors for a while, keep them in the dark about their decision, only to resurface for pre marital counseling when marriage plans have been concluded. This comes as a surprise to most pastors especially those that
laboured in prayers with these singles.  An extreme form of this secrecy is when singles go as far concluding on wedding plans in another location without informing their pastor. They may return to the church as 'Mr&Mrs' but their pastor wasn't carried along in the decision making process.   Most pastors have experienced this at one point or the other in the course of ministry.



Often, I noticed that when singles are shielding their relationship from pastors&mentors, there's something they're hiding and it is almost certain they are on the path of marital error. *As a single trusting God to be married, one of the greatest disservice you can do to your marital future is to keep your pastor or mentor in the dark about the person you have decided to marry*. I heard about a sister who accepted to marry an unbeliever, she knew the kind of questions the pastor may likely ask him so she taught the brother how he should answer questions thrown to him by the Pastor. Their union ended in premium tears.  Some may object this teaching but that doesn't change the fact that your pastor is meant to be part of your decision-making process especially those crucial areas because he will give account someday:  *"Obey them that have the rule over you, and SUBMIT YOURSELVES: FOR THEY WATCH OVER YOUR SOULS, as they that must give account, that they may do it with joy, and not with grief: for that is unprofitable for you*", Hebrews 13:7 KJV. The scriptures is telling us to obey and submit to our spiritual leaders. *If you have 101 reasons for not submitting to your pastor as your spiritual leader, it is either you don't understand his role in your life or you may likely be under the wrong spiritual leadership. If that's the case, why stay under a leader you cannot submit to?*


The fear of submitting your relationship for scrutiny raises 'legitimate fears' about the quality of your marital choice. *Singles, beware of persons who are shielding you from your pastor or those that God has placed over your life*. Before accepting that marriage proposal carry your pastor along, allow him ask questions as led by the Spirit. Before proposing to that sister, let your spiritual leader know your convictions and allow him ask probing questions. *Don't marry someone who doesn't have a verifiable pastoral headship over him/her. Loose canons are dangerous.* The generation that shun counsel and disregard the place of spiritual leaders may live in bitter regret till death. I am not saying your pastor should take the place of the Holy Spirit in your life by imposing a spouse/ rejecting your choice sternly, rather he is meant to act as an unbiased guide and counselor. Most times, those that enter into deceitful marriages bypassed quality pastoral counsel and scrutiny. Somehow, a Shepherd that is worth his salt will discern when something is wrong with the sheep. Don't surprise your pastor with your relationship.



*ACTION POINT*: Stop shielding your relationship from your pastor, be accountable to him. Allow him give appropriate counsel.

*WILL YOU ALLOW US PASTOR YOU*?


Jer 3:15 And I will give you shepherds after my own heart, who will guide you with knowledge and understanding. NLT



In 2011, I visited a church in Lagos as a first timer. As common to most churches, first timers were asked to stand up and I did. I was given a warm welcome and I filled the form given to me. In the form, they asked a striking question that didn't leave me on time: "Will you allow us to Pastor you"? That was the first time I heard such. Later, I realized that the question was deeper than I thought. On the surface, it looked like they were asking if i wanted to be a member of their church but I think it is much deeper. It is a question with the intent of wanting to know if I wanted to submit to the leadership and training structures of the church. *Some believers are in churches as attendees but they are not under the pastor.  They are not in any way submitted to the pastoral leadership& training structure of the local church*. They prefer to "follow" an itinerant preacher's ministry, his teachings and ideologies yet they have refused to stay directly under his leadership. Those in this category always create problems for their pastors because of conflicting ministerial ideologies.




This in my opinion is one of the reasons why so many believers haven't benefited from the ministry of the local church. What do you think will happen to a sheep that refuses to identify with and follow the leadership of the shepherd? We thank God for online preachers and teachers, there's a rise of gifted teachers, preachers and ministers in the body of Christ. This is one of the signs of revival that we've been praying for. However, in as much as those in these categories have deep truths for the body of Christ, their ministries cannot and should never replace the ministry of the pastor of a local church. *Ministerially, it is wrong to assume the role of a pastor over a people you're remotely connected to. Until you stay with them and allow them have constant access to you over a long period of time, you cannot call yourself their pastor*.  One of the beauty of proper pastoring is physical access. To access your pastor for relational discipleship is a lot easier than those t.v.radio&internet preachers.



*Pastoring cannot be forced on anyone. However, the moment you decide to identify with a local church having prayed and sought the face of God, you must submit yourself to the leadership and training structure of the church*. Listen to the writer of Hebrews:  *"Obey them that have the rule over you, and SUBMIT YOURSELVES: FOR THEY WATCH OVER YOUR SOULS, as they that must give account, that they may do it with joy, and not with grief: for that is unprofitable for you*", Hebrews 13:7 KJV. A sheep that refuses to submit to the shepherd will be lost.  *A local church is not a place you attend out of sentiment, your decision to be a member of that church must be intentional knowing it is the right place for fellowship and your spiritual growth.* If you want to enjoy God and be nourished spiritually in that local church, submit to the training& leadership structures of that church.



*ACTION POINT*: Am I being pastored or I'm just a number in the multitude?  Have I submitted to the leadership and training structures of the church I attend? If you know that your place of worship is the right place for you to be, submit yourself for proper pastoring.

*DON'T BE LOST IN THE CROWD*


Jer 3:15 And I will give you shepherds after my own heart, who will guide you with knowledge and understanding. NLT


I was invited to preach in the youth meeting of a church years back. After  my message, the senior pastor addressed them on the need to be fully involved in the local church. He cited examples of some youths who left the church for other churches with large attendance but whenever they need pastoral help, they'll come to him. No one knows them in their church because they are lost in the crowd. It is even worse if the church does not have a structure in place for reaching people in smaller unit.  It was easy for me to relate with what that Pastor said because I've seen it happen over the years. I've seen situations where some believers will go begging their former pastor to join them in marriage or to christen their child. Then I ask, what's wrong with the current pastor? Often, the case is that such people are lost in the crowd of the new church. They don't have access to pastoral care. Very bad!



*It doesn't make spiritual sense to abandon the place where you're reached and cared for spiritually. Also, going back to your former pastor to play the role of a pastor in your life when you have a new pastor shows that you lack understanding about spiritual authority and how it works*. Some left the place where they are genuinely cared for and monitored for growth all because they want to identify with a popular church or popular names.  This is one funny reason why some change church often. They just want to identify with "big names" but when you check their growth, it is becoming stunted. The idea of worshiping in a cathedral is what fascinate some persons. The moment they hear that a church is dedicating a large auditorium, they start looking at their "small" sized meeting place as unbefitting for their status. *If you value your spiritual growth, how popular or big a church is will not be your consideration in deciding where to worship.*




I don't have problem with popular churches or crowded churches. No not at all. What I have problem with is when people are lost in the crowd without being cared for spiritually. It accounts for why we have many unbaked believers today. No one has sat them down to explain scriptures to them personally. Relational discipleship is missing. *Brethren, stay where you find the word in truth. Stay where you can be reached and monitored. Stay where pastoral care can reach you. Stay where there's true fellowship*. All these and many more are very important if you'll grow spiritually. Don't despise that caring pastor in your heart because his ministry is not popular or big. *Impact in ministry is not measured by size but by input and transformation into Christlikeness*.


*PRAYER*: Father, I refuse to be lost in the crowd in Jesus name.

*IN MARRIAGE WITH A LEARNER'S PERMIT*

Proverbs 18:15 An intelligent heart acquires knowledge, and the ear of the wise seeks knowledge. ESV


Before entering into marriage, I read so many books on marriage and I listened to many preachers and teachers who preached on the subject. I felt I knew all I needed to know. The confidence was high because of what I knew. If my memory won't fail me, I counseled couples as singles. Truthfully, the knowledge from books, sermons and other sources were helpful. However, I saw the need to keep learning to make my marriage work. I discovered from practical experience that a marriage that will work requires that you both learn about how to make it work continuously regardless of previous knowledge. *Situations and circumstances will always reveal the area where you are deficient in knowledge. The institution of marriage is an institution for life long learning. The certificate you received on your wedding day is not a graduation certificate, rather it is a learner's permit*.


If the knowledge people have before entering into marriage is enough to make their marriages work, there wouldn't be cases of divorce and separation even among marriage counselors. *In life, you need to learn, unlearn and relearn*. The knowledge you have before marriage may be helpful to a certain extent but in marriage, you need to keep learning because certain situations and circumstances may be peculiar to your marriage which may not be found in the books. As a matter of fact, no  two preachers, teachers or marriage counselors teach on marriage the same way simply because they are products of different experiences and perspectives. That's why I believe what should be taught are biblical principles of marriage not methods. Principles are universal but methods of applying the principles may vary. For instance, I once listened to a preacher on radio who insisted that wives must kneel for their husbands. As far as I'm concerned,  he has only promoted or preached his culture which is not applicable to every one. Rather, emphasis should have been on the principle of respect and submission while Individuals should decide on methods of expressing respect and submission.



*The true measure of learning in marriage is when you begin to apply what works for your marriage and not what is conventional*. What separate some couples is what makes others stronger. Learning to understand each other is what makes the difference. Don't approach marriage with the mentality of "Mr Know all". Be humble enough to learn. Sometimes, you need to learn your spouse and what makes him/her peculiar and different from others. It is in marriage you'll discover that relating with your sister/brother is not the same with that of your wife/husband. They are different Individuals with their own peculiarities. The writer of proverbs(1:5) says, *“A wise man will hear, and will increase learning: and a man of understanding shall attain unto wise counsels*”. Learn!

*ACTION POINT*: The challenge (s) you are having with your spouse may be a pointer to knowledge deficiency. Prayerfully, learn how to handle him/her and stick to what works for your marriage as long as it is not against what the bible teaches.

DEVOTIONAL 0351 | Source start line: 32132
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
12/5/21, 4:57 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: ProjectSave Devotional
Sunday,
December 5th, 2021.


*TOO FORMAL IN HIS PRESENCE*?


2 Sam6:16 But as the Ark of the Lord entered the City of David, Michal, the daughter of Saul, looked down from her window. When she saw King David leaping and dancing before the Lord, she was filled with contempt for him. NLT



The story of Michal and David bring to fore an important issue in our corporate and private worship that must be addressed. King David danced in a manner that seemed "Unkingly" even to the point of going half naked. He danced and praised God when the ark of the Lord was returning to Jerusalem.  Second Samuel 6:16 says that David danced with all his might before the Lord and that his wife “despised him in her heart.”  It was recorded that: *“Michal daughter of Saul came out to meet him and said, ‘How the king of Israel has distinguished himself today, going around half-naked in full view of the slave girls of his servants as any vulgar fellow would!’”* (2 Samuel 6:20). David gave her a good response: *"David retorted to Michal, “I was dancing before the Lord, who chose me above your father and all his family! He appointed me as the leader of Israel, the people of the Lord, so I CELEBRATE BEFORE THE LORD*", 2 Samuel 6:21 NLT.



Princess Michal was a formal lady who worship with protocol and maintains her "dignity&royalty" in God's presence. There are people like Michal today. When they come into God's presence, they don't loosen up. They are rigid, always want to maintain their "dignity" in God's presence. When they see those that are lost in worship, shedding tears, clapping, rolling on the floor or even screaming because of God's awesomeness, they despise such people in their heart. They are careful about the neatness of their clothes and shoes. The look on as people throw themselves on God in wreckless abandon in worship but their heart antagonizes such. Unfortunately, such people do not get anything tangible from the Lord.



*Don't be conscious about your earthly status before God. It means nothing to Him. You don't come into God's presence thinking about your degrees and earthly attainment as a reason for being "courteous" in worship*. When you come around people of lower earthly status in God's house,  God's grace  put you on the same level with them. Don't be conscious about your suit, blouse, shoes, hair and skin in His presence. Be a David that will put off all forms of royal dignity in God's presence and worship in wreckless abandon. Don't despise the way people choose to express their worship to God. Michal did but she didn't turn out well. We were told that she died barren. You lose something when you're too formal in His presence. Loosen up!


*PRAYER*: Father, I receive the grace to worship you wholeheartedly in Jesus name.

12/6/21, 5:37 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: PROJECTSAVE DEVOTIONAL
Monday,
December 6th,  2021.

*YOU CAN RISE AGAIN*. Pt.1

Micah 7:8 Do not gloat over me, my enemy! Though I have fallen, I will rise. Though I sit in darkness, the LORD will be my light. NIV


The story of Jim Baker is a lesson for everyone who thinks God can no longer use him/her after messing up. This man was high up there in ministry with global recognition and impact. However, due to carelessness, he fell from a lofty height that seemed to mark the end of his life and ministry. He was incarcerated for years spending time with inmates. At that period of his life, it was as though his sun has set without hope of rising again. American press didn't help either. Blessed be God, Jim Baker is restored and back in ministry to deal a deadly blow on satan and his cohorts. In his book titled "I Was Wrong", one of the lessons I was able to draw from it was that no matter how bad your experience is especially when it seems you have failed God and messed things up, you can still rise and move on to the things God has for you.




*We are often bitter against those we once knew as giants and strong in the faith but fell. In these days of social media, fellow believers will run such people down on social media, adding to their pain and misery*. I've been a church boy from my boyhood and I've seen this thing happen over and over. Often, I try to see into the mind of those who fell or messed up. So many are always discouraged and trying hard to do what they once did. More so, the problem of acceptance is there. Some even prefer to remain in their sins because the devil sold a free lie to them that they can't do anything about their weakness. That's why  I'm an advocate of sound pastoral care, It helps. The purpose of discipline is not punitive but corrective and restorative. Once discipline falls short of the latter, it loses value. We all must have the heart of that shepherd who sought for one lost sheep while securing the remaining 99.


Today, I have a message for that fallen and backslidden minister and child of God. You can rise again and move on in destiny. That fall is not the end of your life, ministry or marriage. Even when the brethren doesn't give you a chance, God is a God of another chance.  The book of Isaiah says, *"Forget the former things; do not dwell on the past*", Isaiah43:18. Dwelling on your past errors will keep you on the ground. When God is saying, "rise up and move on",  the devil will  want to point you to the past and make you feel unforgiven. At this point, you need to show him God's word which says, *"For I will forgive their wickedness and will remember their sins no more*”, Hebrew 8:12. Hallelujah! You don't need to battle with guilt and condemnation from your past any more. The vision God gave you can be achieved despite all that has happened. One of the things we often do not emphasize in the story of Samson's fall is that he killed more Philistines ( enemy of Israel) in death than when he was alive: *"he prayed, “Let me die with the Philistines.” And the temple crashed down on the Philistine rulers and all the people. So he killed more people when he died than he had during his entire lifetime*",Judges16:30. He fulfilled his assignment, albeit it cost him his life. As long as you're still alive and breathing, God is presenting another opportunity to you for repentance and rising.


*PRAYER*: Father, I repent of my actions. I know you love and still want me to fulfill my assignment. Forgive and restore me. I shall rise again never to fall in Jesus name.

DEVOTIONAL 0353 | Source start line: 32270
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
12/7/21, 5:08 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: ProjectSave Devotional
Tuesday
December 7th, 2021.

*YOU CAN RISE AGAIN*. Pt.2

Rev 2:5 Consider how far you have fallen! Repent and do the things you did at first. If you do not repent, I will come to you and remove your lampstand from its place. NIV

*REPENT*


Repentance is the number one key to rising again. It is the starting point for anyone who has fallen and want to rise again. The subject of repentance is grossly misunderstood by some and we need to examine what it really is. *The Greek word "Metanoia" is translated as 'repent'. It means to change one's attitude and thoughts completely thereby resulting in a change of life*. True repentance starts from within. It is a total overhauling of the mindset, habit, attitude and thinking pattern that controls and drives our actions. *It is a misnomer trying to change actions without changing the ideology behind the action. True repentance will aim at correcting the faulty thinking pattern that fuels wrong action*. That's why the greatest battles aren't the physical ones but the ones we fight within. If you can conquer within, you will conquer without.


Apostle Paul, knowing this truth enjoined that we renew our minds(with the word of God), Romans12:2. Whatever is not in your mind cannot be in your life. *Before falling flat into sin thereby leading to backsliding, there must have been a wrong content in the mind that led to the very action that brought you down*. A fall is not accidental. Often we talk about Samson's fall as though it was the day he met Delilah his weakness for women began. Prior to meeting Delilah, Samson's mind was full of lust and he's had cases of sexual misconduct with women. The devil saw this and arranged Delilah for him. He failed to change the content of his mind which led to the negative action that made him fall. Repentance is not genuine until there's a change of mind. You can rise again as a giant that God has made you to be, your vision can come alive again, you can shake your world for Christ and be on fire for the Lord once again if you repent.




Often, we tend to equate remorse with repentance. Never misconstrue repentance for remorse. Both are not the same. One can be remorseful without repenting. There are cases of criminals who became remorseful when they were caught because they were close to death. The moment they were released, they returned to their vomit. *Your tears is not what God requires at this moment, not even that pious religious look of penitence but a change of mind that will result in a change of action*. Repentance also entails doing what you use to do that kept you on the right track with God: *"...Repent and do the things you did at first...*" Rev 2:5. Return to your acts of personal consecration, the very things that kept you on track. You will rise again.


*ACTION*: Think deeply about the negative mindset driving your negative action and change them. Further, return to the very things you were doing before that strengthened and kept you on track with God.





N:B Download our audio and video messages on telegram. Connect by clicking the link below:
https://t.me/projectsaveministries


info@projectsaveng.org

Dec 8th, 2021.

*YOU CAN RISE AGAIN*. Pt.3

James 5:16 Confess your sins to each other and pray for each other so that you may be healed. The earnest prayer of a righteous person has great power and produces wonderful results.NLT


*BREAK THE STRENGTH*

Deacon James was deeply involved in extra marital affairs with his secretary. He was once vibrant and useful for the Lord but his heart became cold towards the things of God and his kingdom. However, God in his mercies encountered him and he repented. His wife has always been a devoted believer whose walk with God is consistent. All through the time Mr James was cold spiritually, she employed herself as his private intercessor but she didn't know exactly what her husband was into. Seven months after, this secretary began to show interest in deacon James and she seduced him till he fell again. While praying and and asking the Lord for forgiveness, the Lord told him, "break the strength of sin by confession". It was very hard on deacon James.  He had his fears and he felt it would be shameful to do so.


Months rolled on, he struggled to keep himself but he had a desire to be free totally. He fasted and prayed for 3 days but at the end, he fell into the hands of this seductive secretary again. Weeks after, he became  paralyzed. While in the hospital, an angel appeared to him in a vision telling him his time was up. The angel further told him that his life would have been preserved and elongated if he had confessed his sin earlier either to his wife or pastor but the enemy had poisoned his body system through the secretary who happens to be a demonic agent from the marine world. After the vision, he saw his wife and pastor praying beside him in the hospital. Immediately, he opened up to them and they pleaded for God's mercy to preserve and elongate his life. God showed mercy and after 6 months, deacon James left the hospital partially restored. He didn't regain the full use of his limbs. When he resumed to his office, he found a note on his table saying, "mission accomplished". The secretary had left.



Dearest, if you will rise again never to fall into sin, you need to confess to someone who will hold you accountable. *The strength of sin is in secrecy. The more you keep it as secret, the stronger its hold over your life*. Even though we know God is merciful and loving, after He has forgiven us, He desires that we confess and confide in someone. *When the devil want to destroy a man's life completely, he makes him shut his mouth in the presence of those that can help him*. Sometimes, pride makes believers keep sealed lips about their struggles. The fear of being exposed shamefully is another factor that makes people withdraw. However, you have nothing to fear if you can prayerfully ask God to lead you to someone that is mature and will be a catalyst for your restoration. God still has faithful sons. Don't be a victim of secrecy, break the strength of that besetting sin now through confession and be restored.



*ACTION POINT*: The moment you're trapped in sin, the devil is out for a kill. Prayerfully ask God to direct you on whom to confide in so that you can be held accountable. You can't handle it alone.

12/9/21, 5:01 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: PROJECTSAVE DEVOTIONAL
Thursday,
December 9th,  2021.


*DECEMBER 9TH, 2011*


Jeremiah 1:5 “Before I formed you in the womb I knew you, before you were born I set you apart; I appointed you as a prophet to the nations.” NIV



*I STARTED FOLLOWING GOD TO AN UNKNOWN DESTINATION*


I use to be very ambitious. I use to have my own goals and my personally coined vision for life which makes my ambitious drive to be on the high. No thanks to motivational speakers whom I listened to.  I wanted to be on top of my own game that was why i studied very hard in the university to make a first class and i did by God's grace yet I knew God had a different path for my life, an uncommon one. Though I sensed a strong call into ministry and I didn't object to it yet I wanted to do ministry my own way because I didn't want my personal ambitions to be tampered with but I came to the place of surrender. It was then God interrupted my plans and self ambition for His glory to be made manifest in my life. He led me on a different journey and I began to follow him to a destination which I don't know but known to him alone. Till now, i'm still following Him daily on that journey but is becoming clearer each passing day. Dec 9th, 2011, I stepped out into field ministry in obedience to His command.



Today, the journey is 10 years and we're still counting. Just like yesterday. *For ambitious men who carry God's mandate to reach the nations, there will be a struggle between following God's plan for your life and the pursuit of self ambition. The latter seems good and most times it is for our ego but the former serves an eternal purpose that will strike a chord in eternity*. The path of self ambition will kill and destroy every good plan of God for your life no matter how attractive it is. *If you truly mean to follow God and His plans for your life( which is the best), you must rid yourself of personal ambitions and surrender to Him to be used of Him just the way He desires*. You may look stupid and foolish to many who think you're whiling away your productive years but if you're employed in the service of the most high, you're investing time in the most profitable service. *If God is calling you into the ministry, don't hesitate to obey. The deliverance of many is tied to your obedience*.



Every man under God's employ is like Abraham who obeyed God to come out of self ambition to follow on a journey with a destination fully known to divinity. After Abraham obeyed God, in a vision God appeared to him: *"After this, the word of the LORD came to Abram in a vision: “Do not be afraid, Abram. I AM YOUR SHIELD, YOUR VERY GREAT REWARD*", Genesis 15:1. Today, I speak not as a man that has reached the peak of his calling. Just like Paul, I've chosen to forget the pains and struggles of the past and I'm pressing towards the mark of my high calling in Jesus. The journey ahead is still far, farther than where we started. I'm still committed to following Him to that unknown destination that is fully known to Him. Until we win all the lost earmarked for us and build the saints God has prepared for us, we will keep pressing on. We're still far from the finish line.



*ACTION POINT*: Today is our 10th anniversary, we adjudge Him faithful. Thank God for our ministry, the impact among the unreached and unsaved around the world. Pray that we shall not be distracted on this journey and that we shall fulfill our mandate.

DEVOTIONAL 0356 | Source start line: 32490

12/9/21, 11:13 PM - Lanre Oyeleke: PROJECTSAVE DEVOTIONAL
Friday,
December 10th, 2021.


*YOU CAN RISE AGAIN*. Pt.4


Psalms 1:1 Blessed is the man that walketh not in the counsel of the ungodly, nor standeth in the way of sinners, Nor sitteth in the seat of the scornful. KJV


*CHANGE COMPANY*


The story of an eaglet born in an eyrie close to a poultry illustrate today's point better. The eaglet fell from the nest where he was born and he found himself in a poultry. He grew among chickens eating their food and drinking their water. He saw their flight capacity and he did same as well but not at the capacity of an eagle. As a matter of fact, he thought he was a different breed of chicken. One day, while playing around, he saw someone soaring up high, the appearance of the person flying looked exactly like his. He decided to step out from that company, when he looked around up on the trees, he discovered eyries where birds of the same stature were. Not that only, he saw how they took off from their nest into the heavens. He flapped his wings with all his strength and soared up high to meet with other eagles. That was how that eaglet was restored.



*That you have fallen and found yourself in the company of fallen men doesn't men you belong there*. One of the secrets of rising from a fall is to change company and relate with men that are standing in their rightful place. *You need to be around revived men in an atmosphere of revival to be revived. Lukewarmness and backsliding are contagious*. If you stay where sin reigns and rules, the spirit of sin in that atmosphere may seduce and subdue you. The story of lot is a vivid example here. In Genesis 13:12 we were told that Lot pitched his tent near Sodom:, *"Abram lived in the land of Canaan, while Lot lived among the cities of the plain and pitched his tents near Sodom*". Reading further in Genesis 19, we were told that Lot lived in Sodom. While living near Sodom, he gradually moved into Sodom probably because he was attracted. Little wonder why in 2Pet2:7, we were told that his righteous soul was vexed. His mind became corrupted. Company matter a lot.



*You cannot be in the company of Jezebels and turn out to be an Abigail. Ahab can't be your best friend and you turn out to be a David*. We all are products of influence ( positively/ negatively). *From whatever height you may have fallen, never stay in the company of fallen men if you want to rise again*. Just act like that eaglet who stepped out from the company of chickens so as to attain his full potential. The environment and people that makes sin thrive in your life should be totally avoided. Places and people that sap your spiritual strength should be far from you. You will rise again.



*ACTION POINT*: Any company that doesn't support your rising spiritually should be avoided.

12/11/21, 6:31 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: ProjectSave Devotional
Saturday,
December 11th, 2021.

*YOU CAN RISE AGAIN*. Pt.5

Psl119:11 I have hidden your word in my heart that I might not sin against you.

*EAT RIGHT*

There was a conversation between mother eagle and the eaglet. The eaglet asked her mother, "where do you get the strength to soar from despite your age"? The mother eagle replied by saying, "I eat the food of eagles and i stay in their company. This is the source of my strength. As long as I keep eating the food of eagles and I stay among other eagles whose aim is to soar to unimaginable heights daily, I'll remain strong".


If you want to rise and never fall, you need to feed on the food of the spirit where you can derive strength daily. That food is the word of God. Just as it is in the physical as regards diet, so it is in the spiritual. A believer can be under fed and malnourished spiritually. Just as there are consequences for poor nutrition in the physical so it is in the spiritual.  The simple formula taught in song in our early days in the faith still works: "Read your bible pray every day, if you want to grow". The place of consistency in prayer and the word cannot be overemphasized if you want to rise from your fallen state and stay up without falling.


*When the devil want to bring a believer down, the first thing he attacks is the person's prayer and word study life*. He gets the person so busy and weak to pray, fast and study. At other times, it may not be weakness but he makes them esteem prayer and the word less. This trap works well for some ministers especially those that have either gone to a bible school or have spent years in the faith. Whenever they open their bibles, they don't see any new thing there. When they study, it is because they want to preach, not  for personal growth. If you have fallen, the strength to rise on your feet is in the word and grace never to fall can be released in prayer. Go back to the place of fellowship, that's where your strength lies.


*ACTION POINT*: Make it a point of duty to study and pray on daily basis not neglecting the place of fellowship with the brethren and obedience to God's word.

DEVOTIONAL 0358 | Source start line: 32566
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
12/12/21, 4:28 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: ProjectSave Devotional
Sunday,
December 12th, 2021.


*OUR CONVERSATION IN THE VEHICLE*


Romans 8:29 For those whom he foreknew he also predestined to be conformed to the image of his Son, in order that he might be the firstborn among many brothers.


Sometimes, I pray and share from scriptures with my children while driving regardless of whatever time spent in family devotion at home. One morning as I was driving my children to the school, I shared God's word with them as usual and I asked them to tell me what they wanted God to do for them. The younger boy told me he wanted a bicycle. I told him God can buy him one and that he should trust him. In my sermon that morning, I already told them God can do anything for them if they believe. I waited for the older brother to tell us his prayer request. Then he said, "I want to be more like God". That struck me. I wasn't expecting something that profound from a six year old.




In my heart, I knew he had asked for the most important thing- to be like Christ. To be conformed to the image of the begotten son of God. That may seem like a meaningless prayer point to the younger brother who wanted a bicycle but I knew his request that morning was deep even though his understanding about it may be shallow. Quite a number of believers are like the younger boy, always asking for things that satisfies their cravings but with no eternal value. I have attended several prayer meetings and I've taken my time to analyze most of the prayer points we pray in most of our churches today, quite a number of them are reflective of our desire to possess the earth but no desire for eternity. If I raise prayer points on "household enemies" and the likes, the thunderous response may be deafening but when we switch to kingdom oriented prayers, I may be seen as a "dry" prayer leader yet that's what the word of God want us to pray about.



What we pray about often reflects our dominant thoughts. In Colossians3:1-2, Paul said, *"Since you have been raised to new life with Christ, set your sights on the realities of heaven, where Christ sits in the place of honor at God’s right hand. Think about the things of heaven, not the things of earth*", NLT. What kind of prayers do you think the early Apostles prayed? If they had prayed our kind of prayer, would they have taken their world for Christ and subdued kingdoms? Look at this Pauline desire: *"My little children, of whom I travail in birth again until Christ be formed in you*", Gal 4:19 KJV. I'm sure his major prayer for those believers was that Christ be formed in their hearts. What kind of prayers dominate your prayer time? *Only those whose minds are set on kingdom matters pray kingdom prayers*. If you are privileged to meet Jesus in person today and He gives you the opportunity to make a single request, what will it be? *Your walk with God may never experience a radical change and your spiritual life may not grow until you start praying right*.


*ACTION POINT*: Do a review of your constant prayers and ask if they align with God's desire for your conformity to the image of Christ.

DEVOTIONAL 0359 | Source start line: 32728
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
12/13/21, 5:35 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: PROJECTSAVE DEVOTIONAL
Monday,
December 13th,  2021.

*THE VISION FOR YONDER*

Hebrew11:10 Abraham was confidently looking forward to a city with eternal foundations, a city designed and built by God.


When my paternal grandmother transited to glory, it was pertinent we bury her in the village. I have been there severally and even spent considerable time there. However, my wife and children hadn't visited my hometown before the burial. All I made to them were promises of "some day, we shall visit". The opportunity of burial came and they anticipated the journey greatly. My children spoke about the journey almost daily because their hearts has already moved from Lagos, all I was seeing was their bodies. The same applies to anyone who wants to travel to a destination where he hasn't been to, there's always an eagerness and anticipation. The thinking will shift from the present environment to the next destination. Sometimes, children may refuse to eat on the day of a much anticipated journey because their hearts has moved, food becomes irrelevant and secondary at that moment.


As believers, our mind must be occupied with the thoughts of our real home. We must long for it daily. The opening text says, *"Abraham was confidently looking forward to a city with eternal foundations, a city designed and built by God*". His mind was not fixated on the cities of the earth because his vision was for yonder. Often, we downplay our heavenly home when we make statements like, "don't be heavenly conscious and irrelevant on earth". Hear this, it is impossible to be heavenly conscious and irrelevant on earth if the person understand scriptures. It is our vision for yonder that will stir us to be relevant on earth so that we can manifest kingdom wisdom, power and glory, and bring others along with us to eternity with Jesus. *The vision for yonder is a catalyst for holy living, evangelism&missions, compassion for the brethren and care for humanity*.


I once read in an article that after the generation of apostle John, the writer of Revelation, the church had a practice of reading the book of revelation every service because they don't want to lose the vision for yonder. Apostle Paul said, *"Since you have been raised to new life with Christ, set your sights on the realities of heaven, where Christ sits in the place of honor at God’s right hand. Think about the things of heaven, not the things of earth*", Col 3:1-2 NLT. Don't wait till you attend a funeral before thinking about heaven. The only time some desire yonder is when they sing heaven related hymns. You must find a way to sustain the vision daily as it will help you in your daily conduct in preparation for your flight there.

*PRAYER*: Father, help me to sustain the vision for yonder and let it burn in my heart daily in Jesus name.

DEVOTIONAL 0360 | Source start line: 32800
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
12/14/21, 6:34 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: ProjectSave Devotional
Tuesday
December 14th, 2021.

*MY CONVERSATION WITH A POLYGAMIST*


Gal 5:22-23 But the Holy Spirit produces this kind of fruit in our lives: love, joy, peace, patience, kindness, goodness, faithfulness, gentleness, and self-control. There is no law against these things! NLT


Recently, I had a conversation with a polygamist on the issue of adultery. Though we started our discussion on a different issue,  gradually it progressed into marriage. We discussed on marriage and what the standard of God is on the issue of monogamy & polygamy.  He said that it is better to have many wives than to have secret affair. He opined that polygamy is the cure for adultery. When he said that, I asked why some polygamist don't stop at two wives since the second came in as the solution for adultery? Further, I asked if he knows that some polygamists still have secret affair outside marriage despite having several options under their roof. He affirmed that to be true. Then I told him that having multiple wives isn't the cure for sexual weakness. At that moment, he was calm and he gave me listening ears.


Brethren, have you ever wondered what God is trying to teach us when he demanded us to be faithful to our spouse and to keep the marriage bed undefiled? The very discipline God want us to imbibe is self control which is one of the vital fruit of the spirit. The writer of proverbs(25:28) says, *"A person without self-control is like a city with broken-down walls*". Of a truth, we all come under temptation to cheat and be unfaithful at one point or the other. You'll be untruthful to say that you've never admired another person since you got married even if it is once. That very carnal desire is what God is teaching us to control. He could have killed that inordinate desire in us with His power but He didn't because He want us to take responsibility for our actions. Most polygamists felt the way to control themselves was to bring their object of lust home instead of stealing glances from afar but only for them to realize that they still lust after other women. The same applies to singles who think marriage is the cure for fornication.


*If you can't control your sexual urge before marriage, entering into marriage may worsen your problem unless you deal with it*. How will you cope if your spouse travel or is indisposed? I know some brothers think they'll have sex everyday in marriage but I don't know of any hardworking man/woman who is available every night.  Some persons have made marriage to look like an escape valve for sexual indiscipline. From experience, it is not. *The discipline and self control you lack before marriage will not be injected into you automatically in marriage. Marriage is not a rehabilitation program*. To develop self control, a sound relationship with Jesus through prayer, word and obedience is the starting point. Further, avoiding whatever may trigger you from losing control should be avoided. You may not be able to control yourself if you're always around the opposite sex.  You need to start living as demanded not as desired.That's discipline.


*PRAYER*: Father, I receive the grace to control self in Jesus name.

DEVOTIONAL 0361 | Source start line: 32901
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
12/15/21, 6:05 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: PROJECTSAVE DEVOTIONAL
Wednesday,
Dec 15th, 2021.

*DADDY, VISITORS ARE NOT COMING AGAIN*

Proverbs7:4 Say to wisdom, “You are my sister,” and to insight, “You are my relative.”




On a certain day, we were in the living room when Zuriel( my second son) said, "Daddy, visitors are no longer coming to our house". For me, it meant nothing to me that visitors were no longer coming. I tried so hard to make sense out of his outburst but I just couldn't. However, my wife knew the point he was trying to make though he didn't say it. My wife told me the reason he was worried about visitors who weren't coming again was because they no longer have money to keep in their piggy bank. I wondered what the correlation was. She further explained that most times, what goes into their piggy bank are monies given to them whenever a visitor come around but since they stopped coming, cash flow into their piggy bank reduced. I didn't know his concern was about their piggy bank. I laughed but I learnt a lesson from that scenario. My wife already developed the skill of hearing his inaudible complaints.




If you want to parent excellently, you need to develop the skill of listening to the inaudible voice of your children. There are certain things, situations, happenings and challenges they might be going through  but they may not open up in full either because they lack the right words to express it or they're afraid of what your reaction might be. It is very common to abused children to make incomplete statements that requires further probe or sometimes they may not speak at all. Several times, I've misconstrued simple statements from my children to mean what it is not and at other times, I've disregarded weighty statements that sounded childish yet requires my full attention. In these days of increase in juvenile delinquency, our children may want to explain or report what they might be going through with their peers. Most times, these complaints may not be presented formally to catch our attention but we must learn to pay attention.


In a drama I watched several years ago, a young girl was under pressure to join an occultic group in school. Anytime she wanted to complain to her dad, he'll say "I'm busy, let's talk later". This happened severally. Later, she  joined and got her fingers burnt. Looking at how she pestered her dad, a sensitive father will know that there's something bothering her mind. Don't shut your children up whenever they want to talk to you. Make room for their "childish" expressions and complaints, you can pick the inaudible voice of their mind from there and help them out where needed.


*PRAYER*: Father, I receive the grace to develop the skill of listening to the inaudible in Jesus name.

12/16/21, 2:27 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: PROJECTSAVE DEVOTIONAL
Thursday,
December 16th,  2021.


*GENERATING POWER FOR YOUR CONFESSIONS*


Jude 1:20 But you, dear friends, must build each other up in your most holy faith, pray in the power of the Holy Spirit, NLT


There's so much emphasis about making positive or word based confession in the body of Christ in the past decades. There are numerous faith confession books and materials on the shelf with so many people using them religiously. Some do not even pray as led by the Spirit, they rely so much on faith confession materials. However, one need to ask why some have made confession a daily art but without result. Let's imagine a car without a battery, a remote without a battery or a lamp holder with a bulb that is not connected to a power source. What do you think will happen to a driver trying to start a car without a battery, or someone trying to use a remote control that doesn't have batteries or  switching on a light that is not connected to the electric power source? I can imagine the kind of frustration that will result from that fruitless action.


That's what happen when we make confessions and our confessions aren't backed by the power that will make it happen. If the president of your country says, "all police men should set up a security check point for the next one week" and you an ordinary citizen comes to give a counter order saying, "all police men should stay in their stations for the next one week", please who do you think the police men will obey? It is the president. The president made a declaration like you did but his orders will be obeyed because the power vested on him drives whatever he says for prompt obedience. Yours is just a mere wish because it lacked power.  Anyone can make any good confession, a number of persons make confession on daily basis but only a few are getting result.  Have you ever asked why a simple line of prayer or confession is so powerful in the mouth of some persons but the long hours of confession seems ineffective in the mouth of others? *The secret behind making your confession active and potent is to generate the power for its fulfilment by praying in the Holy Ghost*. When you pray in the Spirit, you're taking the power within and bringing it upon for use.



*One of the things you should not fail to do after praying in tongues for a long period of time is to make confessions. Why? You have generated the power to drive the fulfilment of whatever you say afterwards. Until you plug into the power source, your confessions may remain a wish*. The power of the Holy Spirit is non negotiable in the life of a believer. Confession books are good but generating the power to make them come to pass in the place of prayer is much better. There was a generation that didn't have the luxury of topical confession books yet wrought terrible things in righteousness and got what they wanted from heaven because they understood that it takes power to drive home desires. As a new testament believer that is filled and baptized in the Spirit, empower your words by praying in the Spirit daily. That's where to generate the power that'll drive your confessions.



*ACTION POINT*: Spend a minimum of 30 minutes daily praying in the Spirit.

Friday,
December 17th, 2021.


*WORTH MORE THAN LIFE*


Colossians 1:27 “God has chosen to make known among the Gentiles the glorious riches of this mystery, which is Christ in you, the hope of glory.”


I studied a little about world religion and i discovered that religious people are committed to their faith because of the value they place on it. Terrorists treasure their faith all because of the promises made to them about a paradise of sensual pleasure if they can kill infidels and even take  their lives in the process.  They're devoted and committed to taking their lives and that of others because this life is worth nothing to them. The focus is an utopian paradise. *For the believer in Christ, what we have is also worth more than life itself yet most believers are pursuing all that life offers.  What we carry, who we carry and the exceeding great and precious promises we have is worth more than gold and silver*.




In Colossians 1:26, Paul is writing to believers in Jesus and he addressed us as “the Lord’s people”. If you know what it is to be associated with divinity, you'll jump and shout daily. After Jesus ascended to heaven, He sent the Holy Spirit to live within us, never to leave (John 14:16–17; 16:7). Jesus told His disciples, *“On that day you will realize that I am in my Father . . . and I am in you*” (John 14:20). Paul further said that the Holy Spirit seals us for the day of redemption (Ephesians 4:30). In other words, the Spirit’s presence in our hearts guarantees our ultimate salvation. Though we are in this world, we are not of it (John 17:16). Hallelujah!!!
Because of all these and many more, that's what Paul meant by saying, “Christ in you, the hope of glory.” The J. B. Phillips translation of Colossians 1:27 puts it this way: *“The secret is simply this: Christ in you! Yes, Christ in you bringing with him the hope of all glorious things to come*”. This is the hope that we shall see Jesus someday having been conformed to His image (Romans 8:29; 1 John 3:2). Inclusive in this hope of glory is our resurrection: *“If the Spirit of him who raised Jesus from the dead is living in you, he who raised Christ from the dead will also give life to your mortal bodies because of his Spirit who lives in you*” (Romans 8:11).It includes a heavenly inheritance: *“In his great mercy he has given us new birth into a living hope through the resurrection of Jesus Christ from the dead, and into an inheritance that can never perish, spoil or fade. This inheritance is kept in heaven for you*” (1 Peter 1:3–4). All these wonderful promises and many more are worth more than this life itself. That's why Paul said, *"However, I consider my life worth nothing to me; my only aim is to finish the race and complete the task the Lord Jesus has given me--the task of testifying to the good news of God's grace*", Acts20:24. Stop living for the now, treasure the things that are greater than this life.


*PRAYER*: Father, I thank you for the salvation of my soul. It is worth more than life itself. Thank you for your precious Holy Spirit. Lord I'm grateful for eternal life.

*MAKING PROGRESS WHILE WAITING*

Proverbs 2:11 Discretion will protect you, and understanding will guard you.


I once lived with a farmer as a teenager. Whenever he goes to farm in another state/town, he stayed several months before returning. Then, i was curious about what he was doing but now I know better. I'm yet to see a farmer who goes to bed after planting. After planting, a wise farmer will keep weeding his farm to preserve the harvest. If he goes to bed waiting for the day of harvest without doing anything prior to the harvest, he may end up harvesting nothing. The period between planting and harvest is a crucial period for any farmer. I have observed over the years that waiting on God for a miracle, job, spouse or a breakthrough has been misconstrued by some to mean doing nothing while waiting. People in this category think their expectations will fall on them like ripe cherries in their state of idleness. God doesn't work that way. When you wait on God and you stay idle, it means you're wasting away.



I've observed an extreme form of wrong waiting among some singles, especially sisters. There are some sisters who halted their progress and didn't move up the ladder either in business or career all because they're waiting for a husband. A number of them think if they make progress in their career or business, men will be afraid to approach them. I've seen single ladies who didn't accept leadership roles all because they had their hopes on marriage. They thought accepting such will hinder their marital settlement.  Unfortunately, the society(especially African society) fuel this wrong mindset and attitude. I have spoken to some persons who believe everything about a woman should have limit because men will run from them if they're successful. This mentality has made so many single sisters to live only for marriage and nothing else. They halt everything about their progress just to achieve marriage. That's a vivid example of what it is to waste away while waiting. Those who view marriage as the ultimate achievement will be trapped in this evil web.


*It is unwise to put your life on hold because you're waiting for the man that will  marry you*. What if he doesn't come in 5-10 years? You don't know how long you'll have to wait before he comes. There are things you can easily achieve now as a single than in marriage. A wise man will appreciate you more if he meets you doing something meaningful about your progress than to sit idle doing nothing because he'll think of the value you'll add to his life. Afterall, Idleness adds no value.     *If you think your progress is a threat to your marital settlement, you may attract a man who will keep you perpetually down and backward anytime you want to rise. That's not the will of God for your life. Any brother who feels threatened because of your progress is not meant for you. He is insecure and such people prevent the rising of their wives*. There are ladies for such men  but not you O' daughter of Zion. Run from dream killers.  You are God's investment that must bring profit to Him. There's a man who will appreciate you as you make progress while waiting.    As a single lady, pursue your dreams and God's purpose for your life while you're waiting for your king.


*ACTION POINT*: What am I doing while waiting? While waiting for that miracle job, don't stay idle. Let your miracle meet you while doing something meaningful with your life.

*GOD DOESN'T HAVE DOUBLE STANDARDS*


2 Tim2:19 Nevertheless, God’s solid foundation stands firm, sealed with this inscription: “The Lord knows those who are his,” and, “Everyone who confesses the name of the Lord must turn away from wickedness.” NIV


I was teaching a group of bible students sometimes ago, a doctrinal issue came up in the class and everyone began to air their views. During that discussion, some persons compared what obtains in a particular church with theirs. They affirmed their belief to be right and biblical but gave an exception to what obtains in  another church. One of my students said, "Maybe that's God's instruction to their senior Pastor". Now, I find that disturbing because I don't know how God, the perfect one, will have two opinions or standard on the same issue.  How possible is it for God to say something different from what is written in His word? Does God exempt some of His children from certain commands? Does location/ country change the interpretation of certain scriptures? How come certain dogmas are enforced in certain places but when the same preachers move across the sea, they change the standard?





If you're a curious christian like me who desire to get things right, you should be asking these questions. Can God have two standard or opinion on an issue?
One of the bane of modern day Christianity is the common cliché "God spoke to me". In itself, it is not bad because fathers must speak to sons. A relationship without that "speaking" is null and void. However, when what is heard is not in line with accurate scriptural interpretation, it is not God. *Some have built doctrines on encounters that cannot be found in scriptures while others built strong church praxis on misinterpreted scriptures*. The most unfortunate thing is when results is used as a yardstick for measuring the rightness of a thing. In the middle east, shamans also perform miracles and get results.



*Dearest, God doesn't have double standards. The mischief of man is the reason God is presented as a man with double standards.* Whatever the scripture does not mean to Paul and the early church cannot take on a different meaning in oir generation. That will be a distortion. The Psalmist says, *"Your word, O LORD, is everlasting; IT IS FIRMLY FIXED in the heavens*", Psalms 119:89(BSB). His word doesn't change with location, circumstances, culture and customs. We need to resist voices( humans&spirits) that tells us things are not firmly fixed in the heavens. There's an end time hellish agenda to relegate scriptures and the truth it teaches for lies of demons under the guise of "supernatural encounters". Don't surrender to opinions and teachings that are not in line with accurate understanding of scriptures. God hates to be misquoted and misunderstood just as we hate it to. Treasure the word more than any other thing and seek to know the truth it teaches accurately. God has no double standard.



*PRAYER*: Father, I thank you for giving me your word. I receive the grace to study and understand it in Jesus name.

IG: @Projectsave_ministries
@lanreoyeleke

Twitter: @Lanrezion01

Email: info@projectsaveng.org

12/20/21, 5:21 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: PROJECTSAVE DEVOTIONAL
Monday,
December 20th,  2021.

*KILL THAT PYTHON NOW*

Rev 20:2 He seized the dragon, that ancient serpent, who is the devil, or Satan, and bound him for a thousand years. NIV

My pastor told me about a man who kept a small python as a pet. He fed that baby python and showed Iove to a python. Often, he'll leave his child with the python when going to work. The python will come around to play with his child. The python will measure his height with the man's child by lying on the child. Gradually, the python began to measure up to the height of this child. The owner of this python kept feeding this beast. One day, when the python measured its height by standing near the man's child, the python was already taller than the child. He quickly constricted the child and swallowed him. The owner returned home to find his only child in the mouth of his beloved pet.


*There are things you don't feed. If you do, they'll consume you eventually*. The flesh is a friend of that old serpent known as Satan. Those who keep their flesh as a pet will be consumed by it someday. The bible told us to mortify the deeds of the flesh by the Spirit, it didn't say we should feed it neither did it say we should ignore it: *"For if ye live after the flesh, ye shall die: but if ye through the Spirit do mortify the deeds of the body, ye shall live*", Romans8:13. The word "mortify" is akin to mortuary. The flesh can die, that's the possibility shown to us from scriptures. The very things that brings men down grows with them to the top. Samson fed lust until his lust for women consumed him on the laps of Delilah. As he saw the beautiful curvy ladies of his days, he fed his eyes with their appearance. Lust grew in him waiting for the day of final consumption and that was what happened. Solomon fed his flesh with the sight of all the damsels in his days till he had 700 wives and 300 concubines. Please, how did cope?



Later, the python he fed drew his heart away from the Lord and he became an idol worshipper: *"As Solomon grew old, his wives turned his heart after other gods, and his heart was not fully devoted to the Lord his God, as the heart of David his father had been*", 1Kings11:4. What are you doing to the flesh? Feeding or killing it? What you don't kill today will kill you tomorrow even if you're the most anointed person on earth. *The flesh doesn't respect your pulpit power or skill neither does it respect your theological knowledge*.  Judas attended the best theological school ever. He learnt directly from the master yet his lust for gains was his undoing. You need to   observe those things that excites your flesh, stay away from them and pray for grace to overcome them. Kill that python now.


*PRAYER*: Father, I receive the grace to mortify the deeds of the flesh in Jesus name.

12/21/21, 5:53 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: ProjectSave Devotional
Tuesday
December 21st, 2021.

*SOLVING PROBLEMS RIGHTLY*

Proverbs 2:11 Discretion will protect you, and understanding will guard you.



When I was a teenager, I had serious issues with bed wetting. It was so embarrassing because I could hardly travel for holidays. All my younger ones had stopped bed wetting some as far back as when they were in primary school. I tried my best but nothing worked. One of my uncles resolved to caning me whenever I wet the bed but it didn't work. Some persons told me to urinate in the local stove at night so that I'll feel a burning sensation whenever I want to bed wet at night. All these didn't work. My mum thought it was a spiritual problem. She organized deliverance for me. She engaged a pastor who was fond of going to the prayer mountain to pray. Probably the mountain anointing will chase the demon of bed wetting away. Alas! The vigil we held to fight this demon became a sleeping vigil. We slept from around after 12 midnight and woke up around after 4am thanking God for answered prayers.


My bed wetting took a different dimension after that prayer session. Those days, I sometimes wet the bed during the short sleep after vigil. My dad observed that my major challenge was that I sleep so deep to the point where I don't have the consciousness of waking at night to ease myself when pressed.  He withdrew me from my room, got me a mat and I began to sleep on the floor of his room. He sacrificed sweet sleep and was waking me up at intervals to urinate.  The purpose of proximity was to instill discipline in me. After several weeks of staying with him in his room, i stopped bed wetting. I became accustomed to  waking at night to urinate. Till date, my body is use to waking up at certain times of the night. *God answer prayers but sometimes, He requires we take some steps and do certain things to get the desired solution*. As believers, prayer is our default mode, I'm not downplaying prayer.



However, God expects we take practical step to solve certain problems after prayer. I've been part of a prayer meeting where we were praying for the healing of a brother who had painful hernia. Instead of us to take him to the hospital, we prayed till he died. Foolishness or wisdom?  I always tell those around me that if prayer alone can change our nation, we should be the best nation on earth. Alas! We are worse off than atheistic societies. Obviously, there's something we're not doing right. Look at what the bible says in Proverbs 14:34: “RIGHTEOUSNESS( doing the right thing at all levels) exalts a nation, but sin is a reproach to any people*”. God will not come down to do "righteousness" for us. It is ours to do. The problem some persons are fasting and praying about may just require a change of habit and attitude. A single lady who hang around a brother that is not committed to her in a marital relationship may give the wrong impression to brothers who might be interested in her for marriage. If such a sister isn't careful, she may spend donkey years praying to be married. The indecent dressing of some ladies is making them attract indecent men yet they think it is a spiritual problem while some sisters dress shabbily like mothers of 3 children, all they attract are aged men/ widowers who want to take them in as second wife. As funny as it may sound, some attribute all these problems to demons. *When you're passing through a challenge, God expect you to do a sincere analysis of the problem to be sure you're not defaulting on your part*. God is faithful and He will never fail in His own part but we often fail in ours always pushing all on Him. *Don't try to solve a problem through fasting&prayer what application of common sense or discipline can solve*.

*PRAYER*: I receive grace to know and to apply the right solution to my challenges in Jesus name.

*HONOURING THE FATHERS*

Proverbs 30: [11] There is a generation that curseth their father, and doth not bless their mother. ... [17] The eye that mocketh at his father, and despiseth to obey his mother, the ravens of the valley shall pick it out, and the young eagles shall eat it.
Recently, I read the post of a lady who called out the ministry of an old minister of the Gospel on her wall. She bad mouthed the man's ministry for not understanding grace and being Mosaic in doctrines. She also had others who supported her act of dishonour. Most of her friends gave responses that were in the same line of thought as hers. This man didn't do anything that was untoward neither did he cross moral lines but because these set of people do not like his stand against sin and unrighteousness, they displayed folly with their comments. I wanted to give a counter response but I refrained because they won't understand since they've chosen to be blinded by their delusions.


*Dearest, it is not a sign of being woke to call out and attack a genuine and preachers online because his teachings doesn't align with yours in certain areas. We must learn to disagree without being disagreeable. While I agree that we can refute teachings that are inimical to the body of Christ and correct doctrinal deviations or errors, but we should not pull down the person. No preacher is 100% perfect in doctrine. *Much of what i see online is no longer correction of errors but attack against persons, expression of bile, and an attempt to pull preachers down*. Some think God will use them to lead another reformation that will birth a new order. That's the most delusional thought expressed by some. They want to wake up to hear that a church has packed up and ceases to exist because a new movement has come.  They easily forget that a perfect church doesn't exist on earth. Our perfection will be fully realized when we see Him. The bible says in Colossians3:13 "Make allowance for each other's faults,...".


*It is a sign of dishonour to always hunt for errors and mistakes of the fathers*. While they may not have the depth of scriptural knowledge you have, they still have something working for them- depth of relationship with God. And that's basic. Don't join them to speak about the errors of God's servant. God has not assigned anyone to police his church. Be wise! I am in no way saying you should swallow every teaching hook line and sinker. That's not what I'm saying. Feel free to follow whatever God's word says if it doesn't align with the teachings of a church leader/ father. This should be done respectfully and    honourably without pointing the attention of others to their faults. *The way some go about hunting for errors is taking the shine off their life because they abandoned their real assignment*. Shem and Japheth were blessed for covering their father's nakedness but the dishonourable action of  Ham who made a mockery of his father's naked attracted a curse, Gen9:21-27. Don't join the gang of blind critics and mockers. They don't always turn out well.

*PRAYER*: Father, help me.

12/23/21, 5:28 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: PROJECTSAVE DEVOTIONAL
Thursday,
December 23rd,  2021.


*DEVELOP THE CULTURE OF GRATITUDE*


Luke 17:17 Jesus asked, “Were not all ten cleansed? Where are the other nine?


While growing up as a young boy, my mum taught me to say "thank you" for almost everything done for us. Whenever she and dad pay our fees, buy clothes and the things we need, she demanded us to thank them. Though it is their duty to do those things and they never slacked in their responsibility towards us yet she taught us to be grateful at all times for every little thing.   In fact, I cannot easily forget those days when she'll beat us for our wrongs and thereafter tell us to say "thank you" while tears was still in our eyes. Her philosophy is that discipline is a sign of love and it is a tool for moulding our lives. Hence, we should be grateful for the discipline and corrections received.  Though it was painful to say "thank you" with tears and stinging pains from beatings but we did anyway. Thank God for where we are today. If we had not been raised in the way of the Lord, considering the environment where we grew, we may have become street urchins.



*We must develop a culture of gratitude to God first. Inclusive in our gratitude to Him is to give Him all the glory knowing that He alone does wonders that no man can do*. Often, we take lightly the daily blessings we receive because we think it is a right. Well, have you asked why others you know were denied of thesame "right"?  Sleeping and waking alone is something worth thanking God for daily. I didn't appreciate the miracle of going and coming not until I had delays in Ghana in one of our outreaches due to health challenges. I was used to fixing a time for our trips and returning at the stipulated time. I didn't appreciate that not until the time things didn't work as planned. In our ministry, we now have a yearly culture of thanking God not only with our mouth but with our substance after we're done with all our programs for the year. Never underestimate the little things you enjoy daily, they are worth thanking God for. The Psalmist says, *"Bless the LORD, O my soul, And forget not all his benefits:*", Psalm 103:2 KJV.



*Be grateful to men who helped you in the course of the year. Show gratitude in words, cash and kind to that preacher who has consistently blessed your life through teaching and prayers*. A sound preacher/ teacher of the word will not demand gratitude but don't take advantage of his simplicity to make it look as if he hasn't contributed positively to your life. Even if it is ordinary SMS saying "thank you for all you do" would go a long way. *Nobody want to labour and not be acknowledged. It dampens the heart and kills motivation*.  How can someone labour over you in prayer, fasting and constant biblical counsel yet you act as if he's done nothing? The year is ending, in what ways have you appreciated your pastor? Those that have blessed my life have always received a "thank you" token from me even if it is little. I once gave a token to a bible teacher whose weekly teachings has blessed my life over time. I belong to his WhatsApp and telegram group where he post his weekly teachings. He reside in another state. I seized the opportunity of an occasion to see him. When I presented my little gift to him, hearing I came all the way from Lagos, he was touched. He told me he had wanted to stop his weekly teachings but I'm one of those motivating him to continue because he didn't know that the impact of his discipleship classes are far reaching. He didn't need my gift to survive because he's blessed by God but the atmosphere of gratitude around the little gift I presented went a long way telling him not to stop the good work.    *It is not a sin to be grateful to men as long as we're not giving them the glory that is due God*.



*ACTION POINT*: .Consider your journey this year and make a list of those deserving your heartfelt gratitude. Go do something for them to show how grateful you are. Encourage them and let their heart bless you. Stop "using" people, we didn't learn of Christ that way.
