*THE DRIVER ASKED A SINCERE QUESTION*



Acts 4:12 Salvation is found in no one else, for there is no other name under heaven given to mankind by which we must be saved.”




I was in a vehicle with a driver I've known for sometime. We were discussing several issues relating to salvation then he asked, "what about good and nice unbelievers, won't they make it heaven"?  I responded by telling him that heaven is reserved for only those that are saved and washed by the blood of the lamb because that is what the bible says. He was mute for sometime after I gave that answer. I perceived that was a very hard truth for him to accept because he knew nice unbelievers with good character. Unfortunately, being nice is not enough.



Jesus said. *"Jesus answered, “I am the way and the truth and the life. No one comes to the Father except through me.*" John14:6. I've studied several religion and religious leaders, none ever claimed to be the way to heaven. *Christ is the only way to God*. There's no other way that I know. A man may be full of good works with nice reputation before men. As long as he doesn't have Christ in his life as his Lord and personal saviour, he has no standing with God. Religion teaches men to be full of good works and to ensure that good work outweighs evil to achieve salvation. While that sounds like what agrees with common sense, it doesn't agree with "God sense". *"For my thoughts are not your thoughts, neither are your ways my ways," declares the LORD. "As the heavens are higher than the earth, so are my ways higher than your ways and my thoughts than your thoughts*', Isaiah55:8.



Every man inherited sin from Adam: *"When Adam sinned, sin entered the world. Adam’s sin brought death, so death spread to everyone, for everyone sinned*", Romans5:12. Because of this sin that we all inherited, Paul says, *"for all have sinned and fall short of the glory of God,*" Rom3:23. What is the remedy for sin and eternal death? Is it good works? This is what the bible says, *"For the wages of sin is death, BUT THE FREE GIFT OF GOD IS ETERNAL LIFE THROUGH CHRIST JESUS OUR LORD*", Romans6:23. That free gift is salvation. It is not what human effort through good works can achieve: *"For it is by grace you have been saved, through faith—and this is not from yourselves, it is the gift of God*", Eph 2:8. It is after accepting Christ into your life that your good works will make sense to God. If you're yet to accept Jesus as your Lord and personal saviour, say the words of the prayer below:


*"Father, I know I'm a sinner. Forgive me of my sins, write my name in the book of life. I accept Jesus as my Lord and personal saviour. I believe he died and resurrected for my sake. Thank you for saving me eternally, in Jesus name. Amen*".


Compliment of the season!

DEVOTIONAL 0371 | Source start line: 33666
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
12/25/21, 6:36 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: ProjectSave Devotional
Saturday,
December 25th, 2021.


*UNDERSTANDING CHRISTMAS*


Matthew 1:21 And she will have a son, and you are to name him Jesus, for he will save his people from their sins.” NLT


Today is Christmas. In recent times, the celebration attracted criticism from within the circle of believers because of its origin which stems from paganism. It is a polemical issue. Truly, the early church didn't celebrate Christmas because the season between Dec 17-25th was used by pagans in Rome to celebrate Saturnalia. When Christianity became a state religion, the Roman Catholic Bishops decided that December 25th should be dedicated to the celebration of Christ's birth not because Christ was born on 25th of December. They weren't dogmatic about that date. After all, some days like fathers day, mothers day were fixed not necessarily because all fathers were born on that day but just to mark the existence of fathers and celebrate fatherhood.  The decision to celebrate Christ specially on a day like this was made to take the attention of the people away from the pagan worship of that time and to help them focus on Christ. Hence, "Christ mass" meaning Christ's celebration.

If I have the power and influence the old Roman Catholic Bishops had, I can also declare other pagan worship days to celebrate the Holy Spirit. After all, Sunday which has become the official worship day of Christians all over the world(except in Arab world) was formally a day dedicated to worship the sun god yet we now regard it as a holy day. In fact, almost all weeks had their names from the pagan worship of that time. Listen to what Paul said, *"In the same way, some think one day is more holy than another day, while others think every day is alike. You should each be fully convinced that whichever day you choose is acceptable. THOSE WHO WORSHIP THE LORD ON A SPECIAL DAY DO IT TO HONOR HIM*", Romans 14:5-6 NLT. This scripture should settle all debates and get everyone to decide on what they want based on their conviction.


The essence of the celebration and the relationship people have with the person we are celebrating is my major concern not the debate. *Unfortunately, many celebrate Christmas without Christ in their lives*. The purpose of his coming is often overlooked and some persons use a day like this to indulge in revelry and immorality. The purpose of Christ's birth is to save mankind from sin. Are you saved? Are you born again? Is your name written in the Lamb's book of life? If Jesus comes now, will you rapture with him? If Christ is not in your life as you celebrate Christmas, you have no basis for celebration. You are not different from the pagans of that time. *God became man to save man so that man will not die eternally. This is the message of Christmas that we must preach*. It should be a season of soul harvest not partying. If you're yet to accept Jesus as your Lord and personal saviour, say the words of the prayer below:


*"Father, I know I'm a sinner. Forgive me of my sins, write my name in the book of life. I accept Jesus as my Lord and personal saviour. I believe he died and resurrected for my sake. Thank you for saving me eternally, in Jesus name. Amen*".


*Compliment of the season*!

DEVOTIONAL 0372 | Source start line: 33749

12/26/21, 2:23 PM - Lanre Oyeleke: ProjectSave Devotional
Sunday,
December 26th, 2021.


*THE DAY I FINALLY REPENTED OF  SPIRITUAL PRIDE*


John17:21 that all of them may be one, Father, just as you are in me and I am in you. May they also be in us so that the world may believe that you have sent me. NIV


Years back, I received an invitation to preach in a white garment church. All my life, I've been a pentecostal with a bias orientation against non-pentecostals and white garment churches. I cannot count the number of times my pastors openly spoke against those churches and even the churches we call 'orthodox' in Nigeria. I grew with suspicion for those from that circle and pride of being better was in me. The very first time God dealt with me was in 2003 when I met a sister from one of the orthodox churches. Her life and doctrine changed my orientation about her church but there's still some work to be done on me because God knew my ministry will cut across denominational lines.



When the invitation to preach in a white garment church came, I prepared a wonderful sermon not knowing that God was sending me there to be sermonized. When I got there,  one of their youth executives gave a charge. In that charge, I saw a revived soul teaching the word of God with power and accuracy. I couldn't reconcile the message he preached with what I heard about them.  When one of their leaders stepped up to preach against sexual immorality, it surprised me because one of the things they told us was that they don't care about sexual purity. The man is a monogamist not polygamous like some. When I stepped up to preach, I confessed my sin to the people before preaching then I continued my message with a more relaxed mind. Before and after the message, they showed me great love. God made me realize He has sons and daughters in places people least expect. From that day, I don't generalize any longer. While some are bad, there are a reserved few that hasn't bowed their knees to Baal.



If God will use you across board, God must break that spiritual pride that makes you feel you're better than others. Spiritual pride is real and it exist among the brethren. People are proud of their denominational root to the point where they don't want to accept people from other denominations. *There can be no true revival until there's unity in the body of Christ. There's a limit to what your denomination alone can achieve in God's endtime agenda. We need to unite so as to get the job done. The devil knows this that's why he's doing all he knows to pitch us against one another*. A strand of broom is not enough to sweep the house clean, we need a bunch to get the job done. God permitted denominations for his children to be reached effectively in small numbers for discipleship purposes. It wasn't designed to separate us. While we may not all have the same understanding of scriptures, the fundamentals are enough to bind us as one to confront our common enemy and subdue kingdoms.  Don't allow spiritual pride and the demon of denominationalism take hold of you. There's power in unity.



*PRAYER*: Father, I rebuke spiritual pride in my life. I receive the grace to accept the brethren and to see the body of Christ the way you see it in Jesus name.

12/27/21, 7:45 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: PROJECTSAVE DEVOTIONAL
Monday,
December 27th,  2021.

*A SURE WORD OF PROPHECY*

2Pet1: 19 We have also a more sure word of prophecy; whereunto ye do well that ye take heed, as unto a light that shineth in a dark place, until the day dawn, and the day star arise in your hearts:


It is almost a yearly practice by some prophets to reel out new year prophecies. The stock-in-trade of some ministers is to reel out fearful predictions for the new year towards end of the year or at the turn of the new year. In my home country, the publicity these prophecies have is unprecedented. Some attend special services at the turn of the year just to hear what the prophet has to say about the new year. Now, I don't have issues with prophecies as long as it is from God. However, one common factor about most of these prophecies is how it programs the mind of people to live in fear because from A-Z, it has no word of comfort. To discerning minds, some(not all) of these end of the year prophecies are permutations from current happenings. For example, you don't need a prophet to tell you that there will be bloodshed in a country where there are insurgents and terrorists. Telling me there'll be economic crisis is not a prophetic prediction because we have been in economic crisis for a long time. Inflation is at an all time high. Can you tell me something else?


Unfortunately, some believers accept these negative predictions till it becomes their reality. When the twelve spies returned from their espionage, ten gave a negative report about the obstacles ahead and how hard it would be to conquer the land. They saw giants who could trample them under feet but they never saw victory ahead. They failed to acknowledge the power of God. Their report brought fear into the hearts of others but two men who understood how God works spoke the mind of God about the possibility of taking the land. *"But Caleb tried to quiet the people as they stood before Moses. “Let’s go at once to take the land,” he said. “We can certainly conquer it!*”, Numbers13:30. Hallelujah!!! I refuse to live by negative predictions. At this time, many are jittery because of the things they've heard about the new year. I refuse to be jittery. Isaiah8:12 says, *"Say ye not, A confederacy, to all them to whom this people shall say, A confederacy; neither fear ye their fear, nor be afraid.*"


I am not against prophetic predictions as long as it is of God and not mere permutations.  I believe in the more sure word of prophecy which is what is written about me in scriptures. I've seen countless spoken predictions fail but I'm yet to see God's word fail: *"Heaven and earth shall pass away, but my words shall not pass away*"(Matthew24:35). I'll stick to the word even when it seems things aren't working until what is written becomes my reality. My reality is not what anyone predict about the economy and security of the nation. My reality is in what God's word says. Have you found your reality in the written word of God? Start searching for it now. The moment you find it, start confessing it even in the face of contrary situations. That's how we triumph in our walk of faith.

*CONFESSION*: I refuse to be rule by fear. My reality is in what God's word says not in new year predictions. I am exempted from the evil to come by the power in the blood of the lamb in Jesus name.


N:B We can help your church write sound biblical sunday school outlines. If you're interested, kindly contact : +2347080100893.


info@projectsaveng.org

DEVOTIONAL 0374 | Source start line: 33818

12/28/21, 5:13 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: ProjectSave Devotional
Tuesday
December 28th, 2021.

*THAT MIRACLE CAN STILL HAPPEN*.

Jeremiah 32:27  “Behold, I am the LORD, the God of all flesh. Is anything too hard for me? ESV

Have you considered Joseph's story? He had good dreams and his expectations about life was positive. Imagine if Joseph were to live in our generation, he would have set goals around his dreams. Unfortunately, his immediate circle, his brothers ganged up against his dream and sold him into slavery. I'm sure the young man must have doubted his dreams severally considering his ordeal but he kept believing God. Now, this is the point I'm trying to make by telling this familiar bible story: Joseph's elevation from prison to palace happened in less than 24 hours having waited for the fulfillment of his dreams for years. The miracle happened at a time he least expected.


At this time of the year, most people will be evaluating the year to see if they've achieved their goals for the year. Some will check what the Lord told them earlier  to be sure it has come to pass. Some have pushed certain expectations to next  year believing  God will not do it  this year. In their heart, they've given up on the year and they're beginning to think about a fresh start.


Dearest, don't give up on God for the remaining days this year. God can still turn your life around. The life of Joseph received a divine transformation in less than 24 hours. The number of days left in the year is too much for God to change your story. If God can make the longing of Joseph to be fulfilled in less than 24 hours, He can do same for you. It doesn't cost God days, months and years to deliver your long awaited testimonies. Don't push it till next year, this year is still loaded with blessings. Refuse to postpone the blessings God has for you this year to next year. Stay on the faith lane and demand what is yours.

*PRAYER*: Father, I believe you can perform this miracle in my life (.... Mention name of expected miracle). I refuse to give up on you. Let me end this year with great testimonies in Jesus name.

DEVOTIONAL 0375 | Source start line: 33857
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
12/29/21, 10:20 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: PROJECTSAVE DEVOTIONAL
Wednesday,
Dec 29th, 2021.

*FATHER, OPEN MY EYES*


2 Kings 6:17 *"And Elisha prayed, and said, Lord, I pray thee, open his eyes, that he may see. And the Lord opened the eyes of the young man; and he saw: and, behold, the mountain was full of horses and chariots of fire round about Elisha.*".


Fanny J. Crosby was the writer of the famous hymn titled, "Blessed Assurance". She was physically blind. Crosby was visiting her friend Phoebe Knapp as the Knapp home was having a large pipe organ installed. The organ was incomplete, so Mrs. Knapp, using the piano, played a new melody she had just composed. When Knapp asked Crosby, "What do you think the tune says?", Crosby replied, "Blessed assurance; Jesus is mine." In the second stanza of the hymn, Crosby wrote saying: *"Perfect submission, perfect delight,VISIONS OF RAPTURE NOW BURST ON MY SIGHT, Angels descending bring from above, Echoes of mercy, whispers of love*". How did a woman without physical sight see the visions of rapture bursting on her sight? There must be something more than meets the eye.


Aside the physical sight that make up the five senses of humans, there's an inner sight that we possess that can see into the supernatural realm. Some are aware of this sight while others are not. Spiritualists&occultic people are fully aware of this spiritual sight and they take advantage of it. Most of us are too conscious of what the physical eyes can see not miniding our inner eyes.  *There's so much to see in the spirit realm that we're yet to see. What we see spiritually  determines to a large extent the strength of our faith in God*. The story of Elisha's servant is the basis for my assertion. In 2 Kings 6, the king of Syria commanded his soldiers to surround Dothan where Elisha was. The servant of Elisha woke up in the morning to see their city surrounded with chariots. He said, *"...Alas! My master, how shall we do*"? He was afraid because he was living in the sense realm of physical sight. Elisha replied(Vs 16) saying, *"...FEAR NOT: for they that be with us are more than they that be with them*". What Elisha saw with his inner eyes gave him so much confidence amidst trouble. That's the power of seeing beyond the ordinary.



In verse 17:  *"And Elisha prayed, and said, Lord, I pray thee, open his eyes, that he may see. And the Lord opened the eyes of the young man; and he saw: and, behold, the mountain was full of horses and chariots of fire round about Elisha.*". Hallelujah!!! The moment he saw that they were surrounded by a far greater power, his mind was at rest. From today, God need to open your inner eyes to see so that your heart can be at rest. If all you see is what your physical eyes sees, you're not seeing enough. I don't know of any man of faith who saw only with the physical eyes. *To a large extent, your rootedness in the faith is a function of what you see with your inner eyes*. Paul called that eyes the eyes of your heart.  Paul knew the importance of seeing beyond the ordinary, he prayed thus for Ephesian believers: *"I pray that the eyes of your heart may be enlightened in order that you may know the hope to which he has called you, the riches of his glorious inheritance in his holy people,*". Eph1:18. You can always pray the same prayer for yourself.


*PRAYER*: Father, open the eyes of my heart so that I can see your glory.

12/30/21, 1:14 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: PROJECTSAVE DEVOTIONAL
Thursday,
December 30th,  2021.


*TIME FOR SELF APRRAISAL*


2 Corinthians 13:5 Examine yourselves as to whether you are in the faith. Test yourselves. Do you not know yourselves, that Jesus Christ is in you?—unless indeed you are disqualified.



In the secular world particularly in large corporations, at the end of each year especially the financial year, they engage in self appraisal exercises to ascertain if their goals for the year were achieved. The process of self appraisal involves asking probing questions and fact finding. At the end of every self appraisal exercise, the areas of weaknesses and strength will be exposed and the board will decide on the way forward which may include training, re training, redeployment, increasing staff strength and retrenchment in extreme cases. The overriding purpose of every self appraisal exercise is to ensure that the vision is preserved and the goals aimed at achieving the vision are met.



Looking at our opening text vividly, apostle enjoined that we do a self appraisal of our lives. It is not a bad idea to conduct a self appraisal exercise for ourselves. *The first aspect of our lives that requires a self appraisal is our spiritual lives*. There are questions we need to ask ourselves before crossing into the new year. *Apostle Paul said we should examine ourselves whether we are still in the faith. If it is impossible to abandon the faith and backslide, Paul wouldn't have made such a statement. That's a scripture to consider for those who say a believer cannot depart from the faith*. Are you still on the narrow path where you started your Christian journey or you're drifting between the alley of broad and narrow way? How regular is your prayer communion? Are you studying your bible regularly or you read it to mark register? What about church attendance? Is Sunday your only day in church during week? Do you still cherish mid week services or you think they're for over zealous brethren? Do you still fast or you wait till when your pastor declare a fast? What about evangelism? Do you still preach the Gospel to sinners and support missions in prayer and finances? The list goes on and on.




Socrates said, *"The unexamined life is not worth living*". Don't cross into the new year without examining your life in the current year thoroughly. *It is a waste of time and energy shouting "happy new" only to continue with a routine that has made your life stagnant in the current year. If you make progress in your finances and career at the expense of your spiritual life, you've not made the progress heaven required of you*. You must sincerely answer the questions raised in this devotional and other questions the Holy Spirit may lay in your heart before saying goodbye to this year. Write out your answers and score yourself. Anywhere you fail, ask God to forgive you and ask for grace to do better next year. Also, draw an action plan to remedy those areas where you failed this year. Take responsibility for your life and don't give excuses. That's the way to grow.



*ACTION POINT*: Find a quiet place today. Get a pen and jotter. Answer all the questions raised in today's devotional and ensure you write out your action plan on how to avoid repeating the errors and mistakes you made this year.

12/31/21, 6:23 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: PROJECTSAVE DEVOTIONAL
Friday,

*SOMETHING TO BE GRATEFUL FOR*


Psalms 103:1 Bless the LORD, O my soul: And all that is within me, bless his holy name. KJV


The popular hymn titled "Count Your Blessings" was written by, a Methodist lay preacher named Johnson Oatman. He was a succesful business man. He worked as an administrator in an insurance company. He wrote more than 5,000 hymn text. On a day like this, it is good to reflect on the lines of the hymn he wrote. Many people may look at the year and shake their heads in regret because of certain unpleasant occurrences, unmet targets and unfulfilled dreams. The lines of Mr Oatman's hymn did not teach us to deny our challenges but despite those challenges, if we take time to count our blessings, we'll see that what God has done far outweighs the things that are yet undone. *The enemy robs us of so many blessings because we fix our gaze on the things yet undone.*



Today is not a day to count losses because in God, we don't lose. Even if you've had a rough year, just know that it is for a purpose. The book of Romans 8:28 says, *"And we know that in all things God works for the good of those who love him, who have been called according to his purpose*". Look beyond what you didn't achieve in the last 364 days and give God quality praise today. Being alive is worth dancing and shouting for. The bible says in Eccl 9:4, *"For to him that is joined to all the living there is hope: for a living dog is better than a dead lion*". As  long as there's life in you, you have hope. If you're reading this devotional on the hospital bed or prison, burst forth into thanksgiving. Your deliverance is tied to thanksgiving.



If you take your pen to write the things God has done for you, you will see that God is faithful. There are unseen battles God fights for us. If only God can open our eyes to see them, you'll acknowledge the awesomeness of God. Don't end the year with complaints and a long face. With an attitude of praise and thanksgiving, your testimony can still land at your doorstep before the turn of the new year. Create an atmosphere of thanksgiving around you today and don't allow the enemy rob you of last minute miracle. Count your blessings!


*ACTION POINT*: Create around you the atmosphere of praise and thanksgiving. Don't murmur.

1/1/22, 8:53 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: ProjectSave Devotional
Saturday,
1st January, 2022

*START THE YEAR ON THE RIGHT FOOT* Pt.1


Luke 2:43 After the festival was over, while his parents were returning home, the boy Jesus stayed behind in Jerusalem, but they were unaware of it.


*START WITH GOD*


The story of Jesus and his parents will serve as a  figurative illustration for this teaching. They went to Jerusalem for feast of passover. After the whole feast, they returned to base but they were surprised that Jesus was not with them having journeyed for three days. They returned to Jerusalem to look for Jesus and they found him conversing with teachers of Mosaic law. The opening text renders the situation as though it was Jesus that intentionally stayed back in Jerusalem. It wasn't really so. According to Ellicot, a bible scholar, he opined that it was the neglect of Jesus' parents that made him stay back in Jerusalem because It was customary for children of his age to learn from teachers of the law for the period of seven days of the feast. Other parents would have sought for their children after the feast was over but Jesus was left behind by his parents. It took them three days of journey before they discovered he wasn't with them.


*Don't start the journey of this new year without Jesus*. The year is fresh and loaded with opportunities. It is an opportunity for a fresh start. Just like every other year, this year has 8760 hours, 525600 minutes, 31536000 seconds, 31536000000 milliseconds, 12 months and 365 days. You cannot afford to journey this year like you did in previous years without a solid relationship with your maker. God must be first in all you do. *Don't run the race of the year and later remember you left God behind*. In previous years, you sought his face only in times of trouble having taken the wrong step and walked your own path.  There's a reason we call him alpha and omega. No man regret involving Jesus in the journey of their life.


You will take a lot of life changing steps this year some of which may include: marriage, ministry,change of Job, traveling and engaging in business transactions. Don't do any of these without enquiring from your maker. He knows the right path to follow in any of  these. Proverbs 3:5-6 says, *"Trust in the LORD with all thine heart; and lean not unto thine own understanding. In all thy ways acknowledge him, and he shall direct thy paths*". For those that are saved, that's a vital way to engage God in the journey of your life this year. Do nothing without him. If you don't have Jesus in your life, you can invite him to be part of your journey this year and forever. He is ready to journey with you if you invite him. Invite him into your life by saying praying below:

*"Father, I know I'm a sinner. Forgive me of my sins. Write my name in the book of life. I'm inviting Jesus to be the Lord of my life. I believe he died for me and resurrected for my sake. I confess him as my Lord and personal saviour. In Jesus name.*"


*Happy New Year*!!!

N:B Start the journey of 2022 with God as we  fast and pray from January 1st-Feb 9th(40 days). Let's launch the year by waiting on God. You can download our prayer bulletin via this link:

https://bit.ly/2022prayerbulletin

DEVOTIONAL 0379 | Source start line: 34279
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
1/2/22, 7:04 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: ProjectSave Devotional
Sunday,
2nd January, 2022.


*START THE YEAR ON THE RIGHT FOOT* Pt.2


1 John 1:3 what we have seen and heard we proclaim to you also, so that you too may have fellowship with us; and indeed our fellowship is with the Father, and with His Son Jesus Christ.


*REGULAR FELLOWSHIP WITH GOD*.


Those days when we were in children department in the church, there was a song we sang those days which says: "Read your bible pray every day if you want to grow". Then it was a sweet song sung in our children classes but we didn't take the lessons to heart. Then, they were teaching us the basics of  daily fellowship with God. The synonym of the word fellowship is communion. It is from the Greek word "Koinonia". It denotes participatory intimacy. For example, the kind of relationship that exist between a husband and wife that are in love with each other is a type of fellowship. When you see some couples after years of marriage, they resemble each other. I know you've seen this before. The wife can predict her husband to a certain extent in certain circumstances and situations and vice versa. That's the proof of fellowship.



You must make it your goal to fellowship with God in the place of prayer and the word on daily basis. *The secret of becoming like Jesus lies in fellowshipping with the Spirit of Jesus daily via prayer and the word of God*. Just as husbands and wives in a  loving relationship fellowship with each other to the point of resemblance, same is expected of us as God's children. In 2Cor 13:14, Apostle Paul knowing the spiritual benefit of fellowship prayed thus: *"May the grace of the Lord Jesus Christ, the love of God, and the fellowship of the Holy Spirit be with you all*".


Another vital aspect of fellowship that is lost today is solitude. We live in a busy world that is full of noise. Our minds are busy because we've not been able to still the cacophony of voices in our minds through external influence. Staying in a noiseless  environment where your spirit man can focus on God is a vital element of fellowship. That way, God will be able to speak to you. *To start your year on the right foot, you must make up your mind to go deeper with God through regular fellowship with Him in the word and prayer. The years of spiritual laxity are gone. Start revived and stay revived*. Your desire to be spiritual will remain a farce if you don't take this vital step. You have to be intentional about spending quality time with God in the place of prayer and the word. You don't need to put yourself under undue pressure. A chapter daily and 30 minutes in prayer especially praying in the Holy Ghost is a good way to start. Gradually, your capacity for more will increase.



*PRAYER*: Father, take me deeper in you this year in Jesus name.

DEVOTIONAL 0380 | Source start line: 34397
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
1/3/22, 6:55 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: PROJECTSAVE DEVOTIONAL
Monday,
3rd January,  2022.

*START THE YEAR ON THE RIGHT FOOT* Pt.3

Hebrews 10:25 And let us not neglect our meeting together, as some people do, but encourage one another, especially now that the day of his return is drawing near. NLT

*REGULAR FELLOWSHIP WITH THE BRETHREN*



Sometime ago, a retired Colonel(as he claimed) in the Nigerian Army reached out to me on phone claiming that he gave his life to Christ after reading our tract. He shared some of his challenges with me and I told him that the nature of his challenges required he submit to pastoral leadership where he can be discipled and nourished with God's word. His response showed that he wasn't ready to identify with any bible believing church in town. He insisted I step in but I told him I wouldn't cross the line in ministry. I know what God has called me to do, I will not step into pastoral leadership except God tells me to do so. There are many people in our world today who are not attending a church neither are they willing to submit to pastoral leadership.


Several times, I've heard people say, "Church is in my heart". They say this to excuse themselves from gathering with the brethren to fellowship with them. Wrong use of technology has also fuelled this act of irresponsibility. There are people who were committed to attending physical services in their local churches before but now they dissociated totally from a regular church to stream services online. They prefer to watch their favourite preacher online while sitting at home. Technology is good and not demonic as some think but we must not allow technological advancement replace real fellowship. *True christian discipleship cannot be achieved remotely*. That preacher you watch on t.v. may not be there for you in time of need. There's a limit to the quality of counsel you can get remotely. Sometimes, you need someone you can access to ask questions, fellowship with people of like minds and an environment where your spiritual needs can be met. This can't be achieved by streaming services online.




This new year, let it be topmost on your agenda to be part of a bible believing church where pastoral care and leadership can reach you. Jesus Christ, our Lord and saviour was regular in synagogue  meetings in his incarnation. *"When he came to the village of Nazareth, his boyhood home, HE WENT AS USUAL TO THE SYNAGOGUE ON THE SABBATH and stood up to read the Scriptures*", Luke 4:16 NLT. He didn't see himself as 'too big' and important to fellowship with those He created. You cannot outgrow fellowship with the brethren. Aostle Paul, a great preacher and teacher of the word in his days went to the synagogue: *"As was his custom, Paul went into the synagogue, and on three Sabbath days he reasoned with them from the Scriptures*", Acts17:2. Though he went there to preach on this particular occasion, yet he had a custom of going to the synagogue on Sabbath days. He wouldn't have been the preacher all the time he went to the synagogue. There were days he must have listened to others as well. Don't say "the nature of my ministry doesn't permit me to identify with a church". No matter the kind of ministry God gives you to the body of Christ, you must  identify with a church and be under pastoral leadership. Don't be an irresponsible minister who hop from church to church under the pretext of invitation. *Brethren, beware of loose canons wearing the garb of minister*. They cause more harm. If Jesus, Paul, and contemporary fathers in the faith still attend a church either to minister or be ministered to, you have no excuse to sit at home watching t.v.or streaming a service online when others are physically present in church. Turn a new leaf this year.


*ACTION POINT*: Prayerfully identify with a bible believing church, be active and regular there. If you have a church you attend, don't be a bench warmer there. Find your ministry within that church and serve in a department. It pays to serve the Lord.

DEVOTIONAL 0381 | Source start line: 34522
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
1/3/22, 11:05 PM - Lanre Oyeleke: ProjectSave Devotional
Tuesday
4th January, 2022.

*START THE YEAR ON THE RIGHT FOOT* Pt.4

Titus 2:12-13 *And we are instructed to turn from godless living and sinful pleasures. We should live in this evil world with wisdom, righteousness, and devotion to God, while we look forward with hope to that wonderful day when the glory of our great God and Savior, Jesus Christ, will be revealed*. NLT




*LIVE PREPARED FOR ETERNITY*


I have asked singles several times if they want Jesus to come. Most of them would normally give a negative answer. A number of them want to taste marriage especially the sisters. They dream wearing the white gown and throwing the  flower to spinsters ready for marriage. They dream of the dance and the glamour surrounding wedding. Single sisters, do you want Jesus to come now? For some brothers, they just don't want Jesus to come now because they want to marry and have sex. It sound funny right? I've just spoken the mind of many Christian singles. Some that are married do not want Jesus to come because they desire to be grandparents. Some men want to give out their daughters in marriage while the women desire to be grand moms who will go round taking care of their grandchildren.  These and many more are  the reasons some want the coming of Jesus to be delayed.


Whenever I hear such from people, I know the days of Noah that Jesus described are upon us again: *“When the Son of Man returns, it will be like it was in Noah’s day. In those days before the flood, the people were enjoying banquets and parties and weddings right up to the time Noah entered his boat*", Mt24:37-38. Just as it was in the days of Noah when men were given to frivolities and mundane concerns, so it is in our days. A number of persons live as though eternity is not real. It is not in their consciousness at all. It reflect in their daily living and their perspective of life. All they set as goals are things that revolve round this world. Unfortunately, there are believers that are trapped in this forgetfulness about their eternal home. They live for here and now.



This year, I want you to live ready and conscious of eternity. Jesus may come this year. I know this type of message is rare on some pulpits and some of you may not hear it again all through the year. Living with the consciousness of His coming will shape your life and conduct because the bible say so: *"Dear friends, now we are children of God, and what we will be has not yet been made known. But we know that when Christ appears, we shall be like him, for we shall see him as he is.And all who have this eager expectation( Of His coming) will keep themselves pure, just as he is pure*",1 John3:2&3(Emphasis mine, NIV&NLT). Those that have the hope of his coming and are eager for it will live pure lives. When people live impure lives, it reflects the very opposite of this scripture. It shows they are not expecting His return. There are two doors that leads to eternity: death and rapture. While we don't like to hear about the former, yet it happens even to devoted believers including preachers. When we're  done here, we'll go home. On the other hand, the rapture can take place at anytime, we should live ready as though the trumpet will sound the next moment. It may happen this year as we are seeing the fulfilment of end time prophecies daily right before our eyes. Live ready and be prepared.

*PRAYER*: Father, help me to live ready for your coming in Jesus name.

1/5/22, 6:57 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: PROJECTSAVE DEVOTIONAL

*START THE YEAR ON THE RIGHT FOOT* Pt.5

Matthew 28:19 Go ye therefore, and teach all nations, baptizing them in the name of the Father, and of the Son, and of the Holy Ghost: KJV


*EVANGELIZE*

I have interacted with people who told me that they will never go against the wish of their late parents especially the instructions they gave on their dying bed. I remember vividly that my late paternal grandmother instructed they must not put her dead body in ice as she always say. Almost everyone I know takes to heart the last wishes of their departed loved ones. Some are superstitious about it. Do you know that Jesus also gave a last command before ascending to heaven? Yes He did. In Mark 16:15, He said, *"And he said unto them, Go ye into all the world, and preach the gospel to every creature*", Mark 16:15.


*His last command should be our first priority if we truly care about the words of Jesus*. His last command was not for us to organize praise programs even though it is good, He didn't instruct us to  build cathedrals and edifices for Him because He doesn't dwell there. Heaven is His abode. Those things are not bad because they are for our convenience but it is unfortunate that it is not God's priority. *God's priority is world evangelization, for lost souls to come to the saving knowledge of the risen Christ so that they won't die eternally.* 2 Pet 3:9 says, *"The Lord is not slow in keeping his promise, as some understand slowness. Instead he is patient with you, not wanting anyone to perish, but everyone to come to repentance.*" Three major level of obedience is required of every believer, obedience to "Come unto the Lord" ( salvation), obedience to "Follow Jesus"( discipleship) and obedience to "Go into the world"( evangelism). Everyone is called to do the three.



In this new year, decide to be a soul winner. Preach the gospel of God's redemption to sinners around you. *Every sinner is a potential risk and hazard to the righteous if they remain in their sin*. The bad boy you fail to win to the Lord may become the armed robber that will threaten your peace and security. Anytime I hear about bandits, insurgents and terrorists, it reminds me of our failure and folly as a church. If we had done the needful by winning those people early enough, they may likely be carrying bibles instead of AK-47. The writer of Proverbs say in 11:30, *"The fruit of the righteous is a tree of life; and he that winneth souls is wise*". You don't need to have theological training before you can preach. Sharing of tracts and simple statements like "God loves you, give him your heart" can melt the hardest heart if backed by the Spirit.


*PRAYER*: Father, I receive the grace to obey your last command in Jesus name.

1/6/22, 4:51 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: PROJECTSAVE DEVOTIONAL
Thursday,
6th January,  2022.


*START THE YEAR ON THE RIGHT FOOT* Pt.6


Matthew 28:19-20 *Go therefore and make disciples of all nations, baptizing them in the name of the Father and of the Son and of the Holy Spirit, teaching them to observe all that I have commanded you. And behold, I am with you always, to the end of the age*.


*BE MISSION MINDED (A)*

The word mission is from the Latin "missio" which means "to send". It has the same meaning with the Greek word " Apostolos". That's the root of the English word 'Apostle'. Christian missions then will involve sending believers out to preach the gospel across cultures in unreached places. The popular great commission scripture, Matthew 28:19-20 defines properly where the mission of the believer is: among the nations. The word "nations" is from the Greek "ethnos", that's the root of the English word "ethnic" or "people group". So when Jesus commissioned us as believers to go out on missions to nations, He sent us to the different ethnic groups, tribes and races on earth where the Gospel has not reached. This is true Christian missions.




The population of the world according to the latest worldometer report of 2022 says we have 7.9 billion people living on the surface of the earth. According to Christian mission researchers, we have a total of 17,432 ethnic groups in the world with  7,402 ethnic groups yet unreached with the Gospel (i.e. they have less than 2% evangelical Christians). That represents a total of 3.28 billion people on the surface of the earth who may never hear the Gospel till they die not because they rejected it but because they have never had the option to accept or reject it simply because no one has ever told them about the love of God through Christ. *Over 70,000 people die daily without hearing about Christ*. Also, we have another group of people we refer to as "unevangelized peoples".  In their tribes( about 2,960), they have greater than 2% Evangelical Christian but still great numbers are unsaved. That's about 819 million people. Unfortunately, there are a few Christian missionaries/ workers labouring for the souls of these people. These facts should stir in you a sense of urgency to be actively involved in reaching out with the Gospel. So much yet undone, many territories yet to be taken so many lands yet to be conquered for our master.



*To be mission minded is to be aware of these sobering facts about billions who have never heard the Gospel adequately and to take practical steps in ensuring that they are reached. You cannot be genuinely saved and not show concern for the unreached and unevangelized*. That some have not heard the Gospel and may never hear not because they don't want to but because no one has ever taken it to them should not leave you the same whenever you hear such. *God's mission to the unreached and unevangelized is not for a few but for all His children. It is like a big company with several departments where people have their roles and job descriptions*. This year, you need to find your role in God's end time missions. There is something you can do to be actively involved in this mission to the unreached and unevangelized. God is waiting for you to be enlisted in His army for this task.



*PRAYER*: Father, I receive the grace to be mission minded and to be actively involved in Jesus name.

DEVOTIONAL 0384 | Source start line: 34745

1/7/22, 11:34 PM - Lanre Oyeleke: ProjectSave Devotional
Saturday,
8th, January, 2022

*START THE YEAR ON THE RIGHT FOOT* Pt.8


Mt 9:38 Pray ye therefore the Lord of the harvest, that he will send forth labourers into his harvest.


*BE MISSION MINDED (C)*


*Have you heard about the prayer meeting that lasted for 24 hours, 7 days a week for 100 years?* The Moravian Community of Herrnhut in Saxony, in 1727, commenced a round-the-clock “prayer watch” that continued nonstop for over a hundred years. 65 years after commencement of that prayer program, the small Moravian community had sent 300 missionaries to the ends of the earth. Their prayers opened the doors of nations that were locked against the Gospel. Through consistent fervent prayer, they took territories from hell. Zinzendorf, the Moravian community leader later recalled: “The whole place represented truly a visible habitation of God among men.” That's what fervent prayers can do when we focus our prayer effort rightly.


As I said previously, think about missions as a company with several departments. In God's mission to the unreached, there's a prayer department where fervent intercession is offered to God for souls and those God sent to the field of harvest. *Fervent intercession is a basic component in world evangelization. Missions without prayer is akin to pouring water in an empty basket*. Some are confused on what to do about missions but I often tell people that our failure to do just one thing that is significant for and in missions is a sign of spiritual irresponsibility and lukewarmness towards unreached peoples. Imagine that all Christian families and churches world wide are praying daily for souls of unreached peoples and open doors of ministry for missionaries, the task of reaching the unreached may have gotten to a significant point. If you can't go to the unreached because of distance, job and family life, you can pray.




James 5:16b says, *"The effectual fervent prayer of a righteous man availeth much*".This is a new year, offer prayers and intercession for the salvation of souls among the unreached in your prayer time. This won't cost you a dime. Pray for that missionary and mission agency that is into missions. As a pastor, your church can be a mission minded church by leading your congregation to pray for Christian missionaries. You can imbue in your members the necessity of fervent prayers in world evangelization.  Your prayer time shouldn't be all about you. *Someone prayed you into salvation and Christian maturity, who are you praying the same  for*? Oh how I wish God can restore to the church the prayer zeal of the Moravians. They weren't concerned about "house hold battles", the thrust of their  prayer was to take the unreached lands of their time for Jesus. What are you taking with your prayer? Cars and houses? God said He will give us unreached nations if we ask of him: *"Ask of me, and I shall give thee the heathen for thine inheritance, and the uttermost parts of the earth for thy possession*", Ps2:8. Pray to possess the nations.


*PRAYER*: Father, i receive the grace to pray for the furtherance of  missions and your agenda for the unreached in Jesus name.

*START THE YEAR ON THE RIGHT FOOT* Pt.9


Luke 8:2-3 along with some women who had been cured of evil spirits and diseases. Among them were Mary Magdalene, from whom he had cast out seven demons; Joanna, the wife of Chuza, Herod’s business manager; Susanna; and many others who were contributing from their own resources to support Jesus and his disciples.



*BE MISSION MINDED (D)*


Every company has a finance department. Imagine God's end time missions is also a company with several departments, there'll surely be a finance department that is open and available for God's children to function in. According to mission researchers,  the annual Income of all Church members at the time they conducted their research was $51 trillion. Total income of Evangelical Christians annually is approximately $6.72 trillion. According to their research, it was reported that In 2001, only 1% of giving to "Missions" went to unreached - if that trend holds true today it would be $490 million.  The estimated $490 million going toward unreached people group is only .001% of the $51 trillion Income of Christians.  For every $100,000 that Christians make, they give $1 to the unreached. *Evangelical Christians could provide all of the funds needed to plant a church in each of the 7,400 unreached people groups with only 0.03% of their income*.


Though these facts were drawn from the American church but I'm sure the case is not so different all over the world. *We cannot claim to be mission minded and soul loving if our finances is not involved*. I don't know of any true missionary adventure that runs without adequate finances. Over the years, I've observed how Satan expands his kingdom and do his own dirty mission. He raises financiers who are ready to give their last until their goal is achieved. For example, terrorism is Satan's end time mission and it is a fully funded programme in trillion of dollars. How come terrorists have so much money to buy bombs, guns and even hold the government of nations to ransom yet some of our missionaries are barely struggling to take a village for Christ? Some people are funding that satanic audacity. What are you funding? If we truly love missions&evangelism, we'll put our money into it. I'm yet to see a man in love who doesn't spend except his love is fake. Do you love God's mission enough to give your finances?




*Dearest, engage your finances in missions this year*. Stop thinking field missionaries are jobless and lazy  people who should find something else doing with their time. More often than not, such mentality comes from a heart that lacks understanding of the nature and terrain of missions especially in certain areas. No military man can succeed trying to live like a civilian at the same time. Paul said, *"Who serves as a soldier at his own expense?...*". The key to effectiveness is doing the work with full concentration while the church( you and me) give our support. Mission thought leaders said, *"The Church has roughly 3,000 times the financial resources and 9,000 times the manpower needed to finish the Great Commission. If every evangelical gave 10% of their income to missions we could easily support 2 million new missionaries*". If everyone can commit little here and there, we'll achieve much.


*ACTION POINT*: This year, make up your mind to join the finance department of missions. A little given consistently will go a long way to further our father's kingdom among unreached people.

*START THE YEAR ON THE RIGHT FOOT* Pt.10

Proverbs4:20 My son, pay attention to what I say; turn your ear to my words.


*MAKE GOOD USE OF KNOWLEDGE*


Someone told me about a doctor who sent his wife packing from their marriage because she gave birth to females. As the custom is in most African states, he remarried so that he can have a male child. When she told me, I was surprised because a doctor is someone who has spent years in the medical school learning medical science. At one point or the other, he must have been taught that females have  XX chromosome while men have XY chromosome which is a determinant of gender based whichever one produced by the man. Even O'level biology students know this truth. I'm sure that he must have counseled couples having similar crisis at some point based on knowledge of science. This doctor is fully aware of the fact that he's a determinant of the gender which is the same for other men but he ignored this knowledge to satisfy unhealthy tradition of men.

Over the years, I've seen people acquire knowledge but their lives doesn't reflect the knowledge they've acquired. There are believers who attend major conferences, bible seminars while some enrol in a seminary to upgrade their knowledge of scriptures and ministry yet no significant change in their lives. The days when they told us to empty our pocket into our brain witnessed many brethren buying books, listening to tapes and CDs of anointed preachers and teachers yet no significant growth. This is the era sound teachers on marriage and relationship. I doubt if there were family life ministries in the 50s, 60s and 70s like we have today. Our generation have more relationship and family life teachers or coaches yet the number of divorce keeps increasing and most  singles are still making the very mistake those teachings emphasized they should avoid. Whose fault?


The problem is not with knowledge especially if it is the right knowledge, the problem often is always with those acquiring knowledge. They refuse to internalise what is taught. They have head knowledge but not heart knowledge. It is possible to be under a preacher/ pastor who teaches correctly yet no positive change because it is only an internalise knowledge that makes for fruitfulness and productivity in life. The showers of knowledge fall on many almost daily yet they are not soaked. The writer of proverbs( 4:20-22) says: *"My child, pay attention to what I say. Listen carefully to my words. Don’t lose sight of them. LET THEM PENETRATE DEEP INTO YOUR HEART, for they bring life to those who find them, and healing to their whole body*"( NLT).  For knowledge to be fruitful, it must penetrate deep into our hearts. This year, let the knowledge of God's word and other useful knowledge that you will acquire penetrate deeply into your heart. Don't just be a reader of this daily devotional, receive the teachings with an open heart and make good use of them.


*ACTION POINT*: Internalise the knowledge of God's word and other relevant knowledge and make good use of them.

1/11/22, 6:40 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: ProjectSave Devotional
Tuesday
11th January, 2022.

*START THE YEAR ON THE RIGHT FOOT* Pt.11

Proverbs14:23 All hard work brings a profit, but mere talk leads only to poverty.


*SHUN LAZINESS*

Two friends went to a fortuneteller to check their fortunes. The first was told that he was destined to be poor in life. The second friend was told that he was destined to be wealthy. He was told that his wealth would supersede that of the king. Both of them left the diviner with different moods. The first was sad while the second was joyful. When the first man got home, he became sad and looked forlorn. He made a decision to relocate to another village where he would farm on a large expanse of land. He bought new farming tools and he resolved to change his fortune through hardwork. The second friend stopped working. He married more wives and threw parties to celebrate his  fortune.


After several years, there was great famine in the village where these two friends hailed. Even the king was affected. Surprisingly, the man that was told would be wealthy had become poor and sickly. His wives left him and he was all alone. Most people heard about abundance of supply in another village. They decided to go there to buy foodstuffs. This sick friend also took a trip to the next village to buy food. On getting there, he saw a big farm owned by one man with many workers selling raw food. From afar, he sighted someone that looked like his old friend issuing orders with people obeying his orders but he was confused because he knew what the fortuneteller said. When he moved closer, he saw clearly and he discovered that his friend had become prosperous in his farming business. The same farming he abandoned for purposeless living made his friend wealthy. The bible says there's dignity in labour.


This year, don't joke with work. Stop living in the utopia of what someone predicted about you having a good life. None of such prophecies will happen without hard work.  Work doesn't kill rather it refines us. God created man to work even before the fall. God gave Adam work to do before he fell. Some think we work because of the fall. No it is not true. There are many idle people waiting for big job opportunities while some feel there are certain jobs that do not befit their status. The reason many graduates are jobless in our country is because most of them want to work in an air conditioned office. Some despise certain jobs because they see it as demeaning. The language of money is value. If you add value to others by deploying your skills and offering services, money is what you'll get in return. If unbelievers do this and do it well, they will make money. It has nothing to do with spirituality, this is just a natural law set in motion by God. Making money has nothing to do with your course of study or the prestige attached to the type of job you do. It is all about the value you add. Don't call any work "small". Great profit lies in doing "small" jobs well.

*ACTION POINT*: *Whatever your hand finds to do, do it with all your might, for in the realm of the dead, where you are going, there is neither working nor planning nor knowledge nor wisdom*, Eccl9:10.

1/12/22, 5:07 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: PROJECTSAVE DEVOTIONAL
Wednesday,

*START THE YEAR ON THE RIGHT FOOT* Pt.12

Phil 3:13 Brothers and sisters, I do not consider myself yet to have taken hold of it. But one thing I do: Forgetting what is behind and straining toward what is ahead,


*DEVELOP A CONSISTENT SUCCESS ROUTINE*


I read about a successful basketball player sometimes ago. He had a daily training regime of making 500 throws. While playing, he can find the net from almost anywhere on the basket ball pitch because he had mastered it in training. What he did daily in private training translated to success in public games. I heard from a preacher that Smith Wigglesworth had a daily habit of reading the bible every 45 minutes. Little wonder why raising the dead and raw manifestation of God's power was common in his ministry.  E.W. Kenyon had an open bible in different parts of the house. He read a few verses of scripture anytime he entered any of the rooms in his house. That was the secret behind the great scriptural insight he had. I read in a book that the late Arch Bishop Benson Idahosa of blessed memory doesn't step out of his house until he prays for four hours. That was his daily routine.


You may have come across the name 'Demosthenes' in books or in writings. He was an orator in his days. He was, recognized as the greatest of ancient Greek orators, who roused Athens to oppose Philip of Macedon and, later, his son Alexander the Great. His speeches provide valuable information on the political, social, and economic life of 4th-century Athens. Demosthenes trained himself as an orator. He also studied legal rhetoric. However, he was born with speech defect and a bad body stature which prevented him from getting gymnastic education. Demosthenes built an underground study where he exercised his voice, shaving one half of his head so that he could not go out in public. Plutarch says that Demosthenes had a speech defect, “an inarticulate and stammering pronunciation” that he overcame by speaking with pebbles in his mouth and by reciting verses when running or out of breath. He also practiced speaking before a large mirror. That was his routine. Most people only know him as a great orator, only few pay attention to the fact that in secret, he trained with pebbles in his mouth.




*Success in any endeavour is predictable. What you do daily is a pointer to the result your life will produce. Results aren't produced in a day, result is a product of daily habit cultivated over time*. When you see any man that is outstanding in his field of endeavour, check his daily routine. We'll only achieve success in our goals for the year if we succeed in developing daily habits that will contribute to the overall goal. Success in anything is not sudden. Don't just admire success, study the daily habits and routine of men and women who have succeeded, that's where their secret lies. This year, cultivate healthy success habits or routines and be consistent with them.


*ACTION POINT*:  Take a look at the area of your life where you want to be outstanding and develop a consistent daily routine that will make your desired result a reality.

*START THE YEAR ON THE RIGHT FOOT* Pt.13


Proverbs 13:20 He that walketh with wise men shall be wise: But a companion of fools shall be destroyed. KJV


*CHOOSE YOUR COMPANY WISELY*


I once found myself in a circle of guys that drink and chase after girls several years ago. I can vividly remember that most of our discussions centred around girls, sexual exploits and booze. That period of time in my life was short but I did everything those guys did. On one occasion, I got drunk on gin. One of the guys in my circle of friends was a motivator of all the evil I did. Whenever I wanted to visit the red light district, he'll follow me but will never enter with me. It was a thing of pride then because the next day when we meet, we'll discuss what happened the previous day and score ourselves. I never knew I was mortgaging my destiny. Graciously, the grace of God that brings salvation to all men found me and now I'm a preacher of the gospel.



When I switched to the camp of those that are heaven bound, tongue talking and holy living, it rubbed off on me. I've been in this company ever since. No going back. If you will fulfill all that God has for you this year and in the coming years, the company you keep matters. *You cannot be in the company of chickens and expect to fly like an eagle. You cannot be in the company of dogs and roar like a lion*. The quality of life you want to live this year onwards and the height you want to reach will be determined partly by your association. A wise man said, "your association determines your transformation". Our opening text confirms this. *Every man is a product of influence. You are either influenced positively or negatively by the company you keep. You can't be neutral*.



The disciples of Jesus were around Jesus for a long time, they were around him until his divinity influenced their humanity. The leaders of their time saw and heard them and they concluded that they had been with Jesus: *"When they saw the courage of Peter and John and realized that they were unschooled, ordinary men, they were astonished and they took note that these men had been with Jesus*", Acts 4:13. What should determine my circle of friends? It is simply your vision. *Your vision should make you selective in your relationship*. There are relationships that will kill your vision and ruin all that God has invested in you. We all must have relationships like that of Mary and Elizabeth. When Mary visited Elizabeth, the baby in her womb leaped for joy and she was filled with the Holy Spirit(Luke1:41). That's symbolic of vision. When you find yourself in the company of like minds and great visionaries, something must leap for joy from within. If you are always around those who don't see what you are seeing and aren't going where you are going, it will kill something in you. Divorce from that company. This year shouldn't be like last year.



*ACTION POINT*: Audit your company. Know those to stay with and those that shouldn't be in your life.

DEVOTIONAL 0390 | Source start line: 35356

1/14/22, 5:30 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: PROJECTSAVE DEVOTIONAL
Friday,
14th January, 2022.


*START THE YEAR ON THE RIGHT FOOT* Pt.14


Prov 22:7: “The rich rules over the poor, and the borrower is slave of the lender.”


*PLAN TO OWN ASSETS*


My dad got born again in the early 70s. The church he attended with some of his brothers was one of the holiness churches of that time. They were extremely conservative in their approach to life because heaven was the only thing they heard in all their messages. For me, that's good because that message has been lost in most churches. However, their approach about life then was not balanced. My uncle told me that they were taught not to have more than N3000 in their account because having more than that meant you've become worldly and that Jesus can come anytime. When he comes, having more than that will mean wastage.  My dad also told me that some of the elders didn't encourage them to build their own houses because it was deemed worldly. A number of them in that generation were satisfied as tenants though a few wise ones built secretly.




Later in life, most of them began to have problems with their landlords most of whom are unbelievers. Some were persecuted and hated for their faith in the houses they rented. Prayer& house fellowship gathering became a problem while some had diabolical landlords. As a matter of fact, I witnessed the evils and troubles we faced with our landlord while growing up. Most of those elders and Christians who adhered to that teaching regretted bitterly for not building their house when they had the means. Whenever I'm talking with my dad, he'll always tell me that if he had known that things will become what it is in Lagos, he would have built about three houses because they were begging them to buy land In the 70s. In some places, they don't measure land for buyers as I was told. All you need do is to throw a stone, wherever it lands from where you are standing is the size of your land. There was allowance for payment by instalment but wrong teaching blindfolded their eyes.



Most of the landlords of that era are long dead but their children are enjoying that investment today because it has appreciated in value. Dearest, one of the steps you need to take this year is to invest in assets. *It is better to own than to rent*.  An asset is something that add value of gain to you while a liability takes money from you. As children of God, owning solid assets makes us stake holders and deciders in the scheme of things wherever we find ourselves. Hence the term "Landlords". The lords of the land are key figures anywhere. Why buy a car worth millions and live like a king in a rented apartment when the same money can make you a landlord? A lecturer told us how he desired to live in two bedroom apartment as a young man. He raised the money( N65,000) but before paying, someone told him to come buy a plot of land with the same amount. He refused. After some years, the very area where he was told to buy land at N65,000 has appreciated to N2.5M per plot. He was pained when he spoke to us. Ladies, why buy human hair that can comfortably make you a landowner in some places? This year, don't be foolish with your finances.  *Start thinking posterity, think assets. Don't suspend common sense for trends and fashion. Don't eat your tomorrow today*.



*ACTION POINT*: Take steps. A little here a little there will get you there. Consult those who know about these things and prayerfully ask God to help you.

DEVOTIONAL 0391 | Source start line: 35420

1/15/22, 6:21 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: ProjectSave Devotional
Saturday,
15th, January, 2022

*START THE YEAR ON THE RIGHT FOOT* Pt.15

Ecclesiastes 7:11-12. Wisdom is as good as an inheritance. Yes, it is more excellent for those who see the sun. For wisdom is a defense, even as money is a defense; but the excellency of knowledge is that wisdom preserves the life of him who has it.

*ACQUIRE FINANCIAL EDUCATION*( A)


I've been privileged to relate with church folks and ministers of the Gospel on money matters over the years, not as an expert on financial matters but as a Christian counselor who is expected to give godly counsel on issues. For me, it has helped me personally because I've learnt from several experiences and I've grown too. One major challenge I see as regards finance is the lack of financial education on the part of so many believers. Some know so much about many things that are spiritual but know so little about money yet they never stop praying and asking God for breakthrough. The wisdom they lack could have been gotten through simple financial education. In Luke 16:8, Jesus said, *"...for the children of this world are in their generation wiser than the children of light*". ASV


Why are the children of this world wiser in certain things than the children of light? It is simply because the children of light have refused to acquire the knowledge that'll translate into wisdom in crucial areas of their lives. I define financial education as learning about the three M's of money which are: Making money legally, Managing money& Multiplying money legally. Any one who knows how to do these three things is deemed to be financially educated and intelligent. All you'll ever learn about money is summed up in these three M's. No matter how spiritual you are, if you don't know about these three M's, you'll be praying always for an utopian breakthrough. Most believers pray for money as though it will fall from heaven whereas God doesn't produce money up there. What God gives is money making ideas and He expects us to manage it as stewards and multiply it through profitable ventures so that we can use it to expand His kingdom.


This year, you cannot afford to be ignorant about the subject of money like you did in previous years. Don't pretend as if this subject is not important in your life as some do. When I saw a lot of Christians engage in money doubling scheme years back especially MMM, then it occurred to me that so many believers do not know the difference between investment and money doubling which is no investment at all. A number of persons lost their monies due to ignorance and greed.  Till date, I'm still learning about the subject of money from men of integrity with proven track record. To think all prosperous persons are cheats and fraudsters shows you're deficient in knowledge on financial matters. The era of ignorance is over.   Admit you don't know about money as you should and get some education on the subject.


*ACTION POINT*: This year, be deliberate about getting financial education. It will help you in the long run.

*START THE YEAR ON THE RIGHT FOOT* Pt.16


Ecclesiastes 7:11-12. Wisdom is as good as an inheritance. Yes, it is more excellent for those who see the sun. For wisdom is a defense, even as money is a defense; but the excellency of knowledge is that wisdom preserves the life of him who has it.

*ACQUIRE FINANCIAL EDUCATION*(B)


In proper financial education, there are three M's of money you'll learn: Make, Manage& Multiply. Let's talk about the first 'M' which is to make money legally. I wouldn't have touched on this crucial topic on a normal day if not for the fact that the Holy Spirit impressed it upon my heart to do so. More so, many of God's children are caught in the web of unnecessary money problems that are avoidable. As I said in previous teaching, I'm not teaching this as a finance expert rather as a Christian counselor using bible sense to proffer solution to common problems.


In your quest for financial freedom, God is not against making money as long as the means is legal. Time tested ways and methods that God has set in motion for making money is work. It is either you're gainfully employed or self employed offering services to people. God hates idleness: *"Go to the ant, you sluggard; consider its ways and be wise!...How long will you lie there, you sluggard? When will you get up from your sleep? A little sleep, a little slumber, a little folding of the hands to rest.11 and poverty will come on you like a thief and scarcity like an armed man*", (Pro6:6,9-11). Some believers are fond of waiting for big jobs/businesses under the guise of faith. Whereas, great opportunities lies in doing small jobs well. The making of money is not tied to any other thing than work. Beware of "sudden breakthrough" promises. Go get a job and stop lazying around. Work doesn't kill, it refines. As believers, we must have healthy mindset on the issue of money or else we'll go the way of the world.



I've seen brethren involved involved in money doubling and betting/ gambling. That's a sign of ignorance and lack of proper financial education. I've never seen men or women who built lasting wealth through gambling or money doubling. True wealth is built gradually and it takes time. It is not sudden. Greed and covetousness wants it quick. Jesus said, *"And he said unto them, Take heed, and beware of covetousness: for a man's life consisteth not in the abundance of the things which he possesseth*", Luke12:15. Run from anything that promises money without diligent work this year. To make money, you have to work.


*ACTION POINT*: This year, manna will not fall from heaven neither will those prayers be answered except God find you doing something profitable.  Stop sleeping at home doing nothing. Go out there and find something doing.

*START THE YEAR ON THE RIGHT FOOT* Pt.17


Ecclesiastes 7:11-12. Wisdom is as good as an inheritance. Yes, it is more excellent for those who see the sun. For wisdom is a defense, even as money is a defense; but the excellency of knowledge is that wisdom preserves the life of him who has it.

*ACQUIRE FINANCIAL EDUCATION*(C)


Years back when I was a bachelor, I needed an apartment. I decided to settle for what we commonly call room and parlour self contain in Nigeria. That's a living room with a single bedroom. After making enquiries, that was what I could afford then. I couldn't even afford to rent in my immediate environs because of the exorbitant cost. I decided to move out of my area to rent what I could afford. I remember telling a sister in the Lord that I needed a room and parlour self contain. She tried to dissuade me from renting a room and parlour because she felt what befitted my 'status' was a 2 bedroom flat in a posh area. I ignored her counsel because I knew what I could afford then without taking loan. I wasn't living for 'status',  If I had rented a two bedroom flat as she suggested, I'll be paying rent with a loan. That's not a sign of financial wisdom and intelligence.



The second 'M' in the school of money is "Management". You need to learn how to manage money effectively. *Money management is the judicious use of money. In normal day-to-day expression, it is living within your means*. Some believers under the guise of "faith", live an unrealistic life. They live in houses they cannot afford, send their children to school they can't afford, buy clothes they can't afford conveniently, and are in debt because they are borrowing money to service liabilities. That's lack of money management in display. Don't live in houses you can't afford conveniently. If you have to take loan to pay your rent always, you're not applying wisdom of money management in your finances. If your children are always sent out of school termly, you may need to check again if they are in the school your pocket can pay for easily. *Stop pushing hard to live a life you can't afford, it is not a sign of faith*. There's a time to shop for clothes in second hand market, there's another time to buy from classic boutiques. If you wear what you can afford with style and swagger, no one will ask where you bought it. Besides, it is not the cloth that  gives you value, you are the one that brings value on it. I give value to what I wear and not the other way round.


God cannot increase your finances if you're not faithful in managing the little he has given you. We are stewards of money not the owner: *“Whoever can be trusted with very little can also be trusted with much, and whoever is dishonest with very little will also be dishonest with much*", Lk16:10. God is watching how you handle money, He is the umpire in the game. If you live in a rented apartment and you're using phones whose value  can buy a plot of land conveniently yet you have none, you lack financial intelligence. *Stop carrying a plot of land on your head in the name of human hair and wigs. Don't mortgage your future because of trends and fashion. Don't eat your future today*. This year, you need head reset. Money management is key and crucial to survival this year. This is not the time to be wasteful and profligate. God has managed the earth properly and he still does by making resources available to all regardless of race. As kingdom people, we need to apply sanctified common sense in our daily living so that the world won't make a mockery of us.

*ACTION POINT*: Observe areas of your life where you're not managing money properly and adjust properly.

1/18/22, 5:48 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: ProjectSave Devotional
