*START THE YEAR ON THE RIGHT FOOT* Pt.18

Ecclesiastes 7:11-12. Wisdom is as good as an inheritance. Yes, it is more excellent for those who see the sun. For wisdom is a defense, even as money is a defense; but the excellency of knowledge is that wisdom preserves the life of him who has it.

*ACQUIRE FINANCIAL EDUCATION*(D)

The parable of the talent best illustrates today's point. Jesus told a parable about a master who gave his servants talents. One was given five talents, another was given two talents while the last one was given a single talent. Talent was the currency of that time just as naira, dollar, pounds and euros is to our generation. The servant with five talents traded with his talents and got five more, the one with two talents traded with his and got two more while the last one buried his. There's a lesson for us all in that parable. All the three servants were given money but only two multiplied it. Another principle of financial intelligence is revealed to us in that parable- money multiplication. The last 'M' in the the school of money is 'Multiply'. After making and managing money, you need to find ways to multiply it. This year, you need to acquire  the skill of money multiplication in a legal way. I'm not talking about money doubling or get-rich-quick schemes but gradual and intentional multiplication of money. This takes time. It is not sudden.



*One of the ways to multiply money is through investments and profitable ventures*. Entrepreneurship is God's method of multiplying what He has given to us. *When money is made and managed but not multiplied, financially stagnation is inevitable. Money not multiplied loses value with time because of inflation*. I'm of the opinion that no man/ woman should have a single means of income including ministers. In Joshua 21:1-2, we saw that the priests & Levites had cattles. It was a means of passive income for them. They were partially self sufficient and not totally dependent on tithes&offerings.  Woe unto that preacher whose total reliance is on tithes, offerings and honorarium. If  not careful, such minister may trade the truth for money because his meal ticket is tied to the proceeds from the altar. That's why we have many centres today where the Gospel is watered down to attract more crowd who in turn will give more. I'm not insinuating that preachers shouldn't be paid but their reliance shouldn't be on ministry proceeds only. It is not appropriate financially to have income from a single means. Financial experts advise that we all need a minimum of four means of income either actively or passively.



To multiply money, start praying about what you need to do with that money you're making and managing. You don't need to quit your job to do this, there are services you can render to people in exchange for money right from the comfort of your home/ office. Technology affords our generation the opportunity to market products cheaply. You may need to acquire some skills that you can deploy at your free times in exchange for money. You can multiply money using what you love to do. *Legal money multiplication revolves around solving problems for people in exchange for money*. This is why i hate money doubling schemes. It is not solving any problem. They just promise you that you'll get a double. This year, don't be ashamed to plug into legal opportunities that will fetch you more. Degree or certificated jobs may not launch you into the realm of financial multiplication. It may earn you stability but not surplus. Farming business that many elites neglected years back is what almost everyone is going into again including professors.  Colonel Harland Sanders built KFC by frying chicken for sales because that's what he loved doing. At 65, he launched a business that became a global franchise. *He who has no shame will multiply money easily*.


*ACTION POINT*: What do you love to do? What solution can you provide in exchange for money? Start praying and thinking of ways you can multiply what you've made and managed.

*START THE YEAR ON THE RIGHT FOOT* Pt.19

Proverbs 19:21 Many are the plans in a man's heart, but it is the Lord's purpose that prevails

*FIND GOD'S PURPOSE FOR YOUR LIFE*.

When I was on campus as an undergraduate, one of the messages that struck me and kept me on my knees were  the messages and teachings on purpose. The books( Plans, Purpose& Pursuit; Following God's Plan For Your Life; I Believe in Vision) of Kenneth E. Hagin of blessed memory on how he spent the first few years of ministry out of God's will challenged me to pray more to know God's mind for my life. Dr Myles' books on the subject of purpose taught me that the greatest pursuit in life is the pursuit of one's purpose. I am not in ministry by accident, I could have done other things that many deemed lucrative but those things aren't in the plan of God for my life. I remember a cousin who told me where my mates were, he was trying to dissuade me from pursuing the call of God. In his zeal to get me to see his point, he said "If you become a preacher, you'll be popular. We'll see you on t.v. but you won't have money". That didn't bother me because money isn't my pursuit, I just want to fulfill why I came to the world. *I learnt early that God won't reward us for doing just anything but for what He has sent and ordained us to do*.




Dearest, life makes no meaning if you're out of God's original plan and purpose for your life. God had an intention of sending you to the world. He didn't send you here to make up the numbers. *You came like an arrow shot to hit a target. God was intentional about sending you here. He determined your family, country, state and local government. All these didn't happen by accident. It is on purpose*. Unfortunately, many don't realize this, they wished they were born in a different country and into a different family.  Many have come and gone yet they didn't fulfill why they came. Everything you need in life is tied to you finding God's purpose for your life. *The day you discover your purpose in life is the day you start living.* Living according to God's plan and purpose makes your life different, unique and uncommon. Your life becomes unfamiliar but relevant.


Dr Myles Munroe of blessed memory said, "when the purpose of a thing is not known, abuse is inevitable ". Imagine using a shovel to eat or using a spoon to pack sand. That's an abuse of purpose. The shovel isn't meant for eating but for packing sand while the spoon isn't meant for packing sand but for eating. When used for anything different from the purposes they were made for, you're abusing those items. That's exactly how the lives of some persons are, they're living an abusive life. This year, make up your mind to press deep into the mind of God to know why He sent you to this world. *You cannot design your life's purpose by yourself because you didn't create yourself. God's purpose for your life is bigger than you.  God's purpose for your life existed in His mind long before you came on the scene, you only need to discover it by the help of the Spirit*. Ask Him, He will reveal it to you.



*PRAYER*: Father, I thank you because I'm sent to this world for a purpose. Holy Spirit, reveal the mind of the father to me on why I'm here and enable me to fulfill it in Jesus name.

*START THE YEAR ON THE RIGHT FOOT* Pt.20

Proverbs 22: 6 Train up a child in the way he should go: and when he is old, he will not depart from it.


*CREATE TIME FOR THE FAMILY*



The nature of my ministry takes me round. When I married newly, not long afterwards, I traveled to the mission field leaving my wife behind in Lagos. I went up north. When children started coming, business continued as usual. One day, my first son( less than five years old then) had an outburst of emotion and he accused me of traveling and neglecting him and his younger brother. It touched me because I knew the young boy needed me around. It wasn't easy for me to take them round because of school but I got the message he was passing across to me and I knew God was speaking to me through him. Since then, I adjusted. Occasionally, we go out together to have holy fun. Still longing for when we'll start having vacation out of the country.  Even when i had prolonged delay in Ghana due to COVID-19, we were communicating on phone though they longed for me but for health reasons. The younger boy said, "I should come down from the aeroplane". He thought Ghana was in the air. He missed my presence around the house but he couldn't express himself properly.




This is not the time to neglect your family and leave them alone. *The devil is looking for children of absentee fathers and mothers  because they are vulnerable*.  Years back, as an undergraduate, I read the book titled "God's Generals". I read the story of mighty men who wrought terrible things in righteousness but a number of them had children and wives who were bitter towards them because of neglect. Some wives of these great evangelists died in pain and some of their children weren't properly cared for. This account for the waywardness of some preachers children. They're always out there, no time for their family. Some preachers practically live on prayer mountains without their wives and children. Their children are attention starved and wives are love starved. I've seen this several times.  *Listen to this, you have no public ministry unless your home is intact because your home is your first and most important ministry. Any ministry you do that makes you neglect your wife and children should be checked.* This counsel is not limited to those in ministry. I started from there because that's my bend.




There are career couples who neglect their children because of their jobs. They leave home early while it is dark when the children are still on the bed leaving them in care of house helps and nannies. They return while it is dark when the children are fast asleep. This is how many couples lost their children to the devil. Nothing is worth robbing you of having quality time with your family. *God's design for the family is that parents ( husband and wife) will live together and have quality time with and for their children. Anything outside this is from the evil one*. It doesn't mean you'll be with them everywhere and do devotion with them all day but your presence around the house and what you do is the loudest teaching and training for children because they learn from what you do not necessarily what you say. It is not enough to call them over the phone to pray and teach them the word. Show them practical Christian living by being around them. *This Pharoahic system of the world want us to leave our children behind in Egypt like he suggested to Moses but we must refuse this*. It is an aberration to leave your children in care of someone while you both are far away. They are God's heritage not yours and you'll give account to God someday on how you handled His heritage.




*ACTION POINT*: This year, hold a meeting with your spouse and reach a compromise on how those children will receive proper attention and training especially if your jobs takes your time.

*START THE YEAR ON THE RIGHT FOOT* Pt.21

Proverbs 25:27 It is not good to eat too much honey, nor is it honorable to search out matters that are too deep.



*PAY ATTENTION TO YOUR HEALTH*


In contemporary church history, there was a man mightily used by God in the early revivals. He was a very young man who preached from place to place with great manifestations of God's power. At the age of 29, he became gravely sick. On his death bed, he said "God gave me a message and a horse, i couldn't deliver the message because I damaged the horse"( paraphrase). He referred to his body as horse and his ministry as the message. He died untimely because he didn't pay attention to his health. There are several cases of ministers who died as a result of carelessness as regards the issue of their health. Some were caught in the trap of too much work without rest, some ate the wrong stuff and weren't health conscious at all.



Some believers are careless about their health. We often push everything about our health to "faith". Some brethren took the issue of health and wellness to the extreme by refusing to see doctors. In some circles, it is forbidden to use medications. Some teachings have made medical science look like the wisdom of hell. In fact, some believers 'demonize' doctors as though they're working in partnership with the devil. When you listen to the testimony of some while reporting what the medics say, they'll present it as though the doctor is a mouth piece and partner with the devil in their health crisis but God came to their rescue. Really, I don't know how Christian doctors feel when they hear things like this. We need wisdom on health matters. I was part of a prayer team who prayed for a brother who had hernia many years ago, he was writhing in pain. We spoke all manners of tongues on this brother till he died. So painful. Would it have been wrong if someone had taken the initiative to take him to the hospital?



In the part of the world where I come from, it is common to see pregnant women give birth in prayer houses. So many maternal&paediatric casualties have happened in such places due to quackery. I've not said it is wrong to pray but wisdom is the principal thing not prayer. *On health matters, it is wise to take advantage of God's wisdom available through science*. No matter how anointed you are, if you don't pay attention to your health, you may cut short your life. Our bodies haven't become immortal yet, it is subject to corruption and decay. *You may have good dreams and visions for life but without good health, those things remain as mere wishful thinking*. This year, don't play with your health. Eat right, rest well and exercise your body. Avoid living a sedentary lifestyle. There are simple exercises you can engage in if you can't afford a gym. Brisk walking, skipping and other simpler forms of exercises can be done within your house. As advised, don't wait till you're sick before doing a medical check on your body. *It is better to live in prevention mode than to live in attention mode where you'll always need the attention of medics*. Every good thing you've conceived in your heart for the year rises and fall on good health. Take care of your health.


*ACTION POINT*: Educate yourself on simple health tips by reading books on how to stay healthy and get quality counsel from experts about healthy living. Above all, ask God to give you good health.

*START THE YEAR ON THE RIGHT FOOT* Pt.22

Mal 2:16 "For I Hate Divorce," says the Lord:

*KEEP YOUR MARRIAGE INTACT*


I ministered on the assembly of a secondary one morning. I do that often when I'm not on the rural mission field. On this particular morning, I was led by the Spirit of God to follow the SSS1 students into their classroom after assembly. When I got there, I led the song, "This little light of mine, I'm gonna let it shine". After we all sang, I was led to pray. In my prayers, I found myself praying against the spirit of hopelessness and depression arising from issues at home. Unknown to me, one of the girls in the class was badly depressed because her parents were divorced. After the prayer, I saw this girl crying. She opened up to me what was happening in her family. I counselled and prayed with her.


That incident made me realize the damage caused by divorce. In my heart, I had compassion on the girl more than I would on the father or mother if they were visibly standing in front of me. That scenario looked to me like the girl already had mental torture affecting everything about her life and future except grace intervenes. A research shows that most people from divorced homes are likely to experience same in their own marriages because it has been ingrained in their sub conscious that one can walk out of marriage at will. Same happens to children who are exposed to physical abuse. If a boy sees his father beating his mother or a girl sees her mom abusing her father verbally,  while that scenario may disgust them as little children, it registers in their sub conscious and they may find themselves doing same in their future home. A sociological research showed that most inmates in a particular prison were products of broken homes.


The devil is working round the clock to tamper with God's instituted order for marriage. Many are leaving the marriage institution faster than people are entering into it. This year, make up your mind to make your marriage work. The scriptural formula has always been *“Wives, submit yourselves unto your own husbands, as unto the Lord”... Husbands, love your wives, just as Christ loved the church and gave himself up for her*" (Eph5:22,25). The husband's work  to keep the marriage intact is to love his wife without reservations&condition. Love her enough to overlook her flaws and to lovingly correct the ones you can correct. The wife's duty is to submit to her husband as unto the Lord. You may be higher in rank and career but you have to be under the leadership of your husband.  Don't subscribe to foolish philosophies of men that is wrecking homes today. Your marriage shouldn't make your children unfortunate and suffer unnecessary mental torture. Divorce makes it hard to raise godly children.  Break that evil cycle. Your home shouldn't add to the  rising statistics of broken marriages. You and your spouse should prayerfully decide to keep your home intact.


*ACTION POINT*: This year, be intentional about making your marriage work. If you're on the verge of a divorce, pray and seek wise counsel with your spouse.

1/23/22, 7:50 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: ProjectSave Devotional

23rd, January, 2022.


*START THE YEAR ON THE RIGHT FOOT* Pt.23


Hosea 4:6 my people are destroyed from lack of knowledge. “Because you have rejected knowledge, I also reject you as my priests; because you have ignored the law of your God, I also will ignore your children. NIV


*INVEST IN KNOWLEDGE ACQUISITION*


Christopher Columbus was a voyager in his days. He went on several voyages with his team. History has it that on a particular voyage, they ran out of food and had nothing to eat. While on the sea, they sighted an Island and they decided to berth their ship to source for food on the Island. They begged the people for food but they refused to give them food. All entreaties failed and they felt hopeless. Columbus remembered that he read in a book about eclipse of the sun that will happen. On the strength of that information, he told those on the Island that he will cause darkness to cover the land if they don't give him food. They refused. After some days, darkness covered everywhere and they were afraid. They rushed to Columbus with food begging him to accept their peace offering. Probably they thought he was a god. Knowledge acquired through study put food on Columbus table.



*You cannot grow in life beyond your private study*. There is power in reading and acquiring relevant information. The world has become a world of readers and research. When you devote yourself to personal development through study, you make yourself an asset. You become resourceful. Often, our problem is not resources but resourcefulness. Most developed countries in the world today run on what I call knowledge economy. Japan doesn't have resources like most African states do yet they have a stronger economy than most African states. The key is knowledge. It is as though the continent with resources is plundered by those with knowledge.


This year, don't joke with knowledge acquisition. Whatever it will take you to develop yourself and be on top of your game wouldn't be bad as long as it is legal. In your profession, business and trade, don't be stagnant. Acquire knowledge. If you're a preacher, don't say "the only book I read is bible and nothing more". Some preachers are stale. They tell more of stories and expound less of scriptures that's why their members aren't growing or leaving the church for a better place.  The way some preachers handle scriptures shows they need thorough bible training. Many biblical illiterates are on the pulpits today feeding souls with junks. Don't be irked when you're confronted with your ignorance, go get knowledge. Only humble people learn.  *Study so that you won't be stupid. This is the year to learn, unlearn and relearn*. Buy books and read them. Don't have a library decorated with books. Let it be functional. Attend relevant seminars and trainings.  Don't be stagnant in knowledge.



*ACTION POINT*: Discern areas of your life where you're deficient in knowledge and invest in upgrading yourself.

DEVOTIONAL 0400 | Source start line: 36779

1/24/22, 5:06 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: PROJECTSAVE DEVOTIONAL
Monday,
24th January,  2022.

*POSTURES FOR DIVINE DIRECTION* Pt.1

Deut 29:29 “The secret things belong to the Lord our God, but the things revealed belong to us and to our children forever, that we may follow all the words of this law.”

*HEART POSTURE*( A)

According to definition of words from Oxford languages, posture can be defined as the position in which someone holds their body when standing or sitting. Also, it refers to a particular approach or attitude. The later is relevant to our  discussion. Many believers wonder why they don't hear from God, some are perplexed about their inability to receive a clear direction from God on issues they need clarity on despite spending time in prayer and fasting. What many fail to realize is that there are postures suitable for receiving clearly from God. In the physical, the posture for receiving an object or item is almost the same all over the world. It involves stretching of hands and opening of palms. There's also an acceptable posture for receiving clear direction from God.

To receive clear directions from God on issues, the first posture of your heart must align with the fear of the Lord. In Psalms 25:14,  the bible says, *"The secret of the LORD is with them that fear him; and he will shew them his covenant*". What does it mean to fear the Lord? It simply means to reverence God. Some persons say a believer shouldn't fear the Lord because fear is of the devil. They insinuated that the phrase "fear of the Lord" is an old testament idea because the new testament command is to love the Lord. I think this is a case of misinterpretation of scriptural words. The type of fear implied in context of the scripture cited is not the destructive fear that is demon inspired. Rather, it is a reverential fear. More so, apostle Paul, a new testament teacher, recommended that we perfect holiness in the fear of God: *"Having therefore these promises, dearly beloved, let us cleanse ourselves from all filthiness of the flesh and spirit, PERFECTING HOLINESS IN THE FEAR OF GOD*", 2Cor 7:1. That's a new testament scripture for you not old testament.



Those who don't reverence God cannot hear from God clearly. *The fear of the Lord is expressed through proper Christian conduct and right living*. There are those who live the way they want, they gratify the desires of the flesh yet they want to hear from the Spirit. It is impossible. You have to live in the Spirit to hear from the Spirit. There are no two ways about it. *You cannot be sold out to the flesh and expect attract a spiritual benefit such as divine direction*.  God reveals His secrets to sons that are in fellowship with Him not to those that run to Him only when they need Him. *When the fear of God is constant in a man's life, divine direction flow unhindered*. Everything in the kingdom has its place. Prayer has its place, fasting does as well. However, when you neglect one at the expense of the other, you may not get the desired result. *No matter how long you spend on the mountain fasting and prayer, if the fear of the Lord is not a constant, it is either you receive demonic direction or you lead yourself as prompted by the flesh*.


*PRAYER*: Father, I receive the grace to reverence you in Jesus name

DEVOTIONAL 0401 | Source start line: 36918
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
1/25/22, 6:29 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: ProjectSave Devotional

25th January, 2022.

*POSTURES FOR DIVINE DIRECTION* Pt.2

Ezekiel 14:4 Therefore speak unto them, and say unto them, Thus saith the Lord GOD; Every man of the house of Israel that setteth up his idols in his heart, and putteth the stumblingblock of his iniquity before his face, and cometh to the prophet; I the LORD will answer him that cometh according to the multitude of his idols;


*HEART POSTURE*( B)

I heard the story of a seminarian who was asking God to know His will for life and ministry after leaving the seminary. While praying, someone heard what he was saying. The person hid himself somewhere and spoke in response to his request saying "My son, go to Africa". This seminarian quickly rebuked the devil saying "I rebuke you devil in Jesus name. I will not go to Africa". Though he was praying to know the will of God for ministry, it was obvious that Africa was a no go area for him. In his heart, he had made up his mind already on where not to to go yet he was asking the Lord to send him out on ministry. The posture of his heart already had preference(s) even before going to God in prayer. That's what it means to set up idols in ones heart.


The reason some do not hear clearly from God is because they have set up idols in their heart. They have rigid preferences that God cannot change. *When you set up idols in your heart, it is either you don't hear God speak at all or you hear yourself based on your preference*. I have seen this work out in matters like marriage and other aspect of life. I once spoke to a woman who stood vehemently against inter tribal marriage. She said emphatically that God cannot tell her children to marry from another tribe and that she will tell God what she want. Her belief holds that she has the power to dictate to God whatever she want and then God will grant her request. Often, this assertion is backed by some scriptures wrongly misunderstood. Does it mean we should not have a desire as believers? Of course not. We are not robots. However, God want us to be pliable and not rigid.


*If the posture of your heart is not in total surrender to God's absolute will for your life, you block the flow of divine direction*. It is not how long you spend on the mountain that matters but the posture of your heart while praying. God can lead you in no time if your heart is ready. *Sometimes, I perceive  the reason we don't get clear direction from God quickly is because He is weighing our hearts and working on us till we are ready to receive what He has to say*. There's so much going on within that requires adjustment. Hearts that are unwilling to follow God's will and obey Him will never enjoy the benefit of divine direction: *“If ye be willing and obedient, ye shall eat the good of the land: “But if ye refuse and rebel, ye shall be devoured with the sword: for the mouth of the Lord hath spoken it*” (Isa. 1:19–20). Do you really want to hear from God on that matter bothering your heart? Re tune your heart and let it be made ready and open to receiving whatever God has to say.


*ACTION POINT*: Search your heart and evict the idols preventing you from hearing clearly from heaven.

*POSTURES FOR DIVINE DIRECTION* Pt.3

Jer33:3 Call to me and I will answer you and tell you great and unsearchable things you do not know.’

*MOUTH POSTURE*

The first posture you must get right in matters of receiving divine direction from God is your heart posture after which you can engage your mouth. A request for divine direction is not enough until the posture of the heart is right. The posture of the mouth here refers to making a request about the issue in need of clarity. Desire is not enough. So many desire to hear clearly from God but they haven't tabled a request before God.



An angel appeared to a man who had a desire. He told the angel what he desired in their discussion.  After talking on several issues, the angel reminded him to ask the father about his desire. A desire to be led is not enough, you need to ask. You must pay the price in the place of prayer if you want to be led. To unravel divine secrets, you must press deep in the place of prayer. Often, we fail to get clear direction from God because of laziness in prayer. Prayer position us to hear what God is saying.


Through prayer, Daniel and his companion had access to the secrets of heaven when the king had a vision that confused him: *"He urged them to plead for mercy from the God of heaven concerning this mystery, so that he and his friends might not be executed with the rest of the wise men of Babylon.During the night the mystery was revealed to Daniel in a vision. Then Daniel praised the God of heaven*", Daniel2:18-19. We have a promissory note from God for divine direction: *I will instruct you and teach you in the way you should go; I will counsel you with my loving eye on you*, Psalms 32:8 NIV. We have the right to ask for divine secrets to be unveiled because God promised us in His word. Don't leave your desire to be led by God in the realm of desire. Tarry in prayer until you get a clear signal from God.


*PRAYER*: I receive the grace to tarry in prayer till I get a clear signal from heaven in Jesus name

DEVOTIONAL 0403 | Source start line: 37052

27th January,  2022.


*POSTURES FOR DIVINE DIRECTION* Pt.4

Psalns62:11 God hath spoken once; twice have I heard this; that power belongeth unto God.


*EAR POSTURE*


Before my service year, I prayed extensively to know God's will for my life and ministry. Towards the end of my 100 level till I finished the university, that was topmost on my prayer requests. Though I sensed I'll be in ministry but certain details weren't clear to me. I had solid career plans that I wanted to pursue alongside ministry but I knew banking job was out of it because I wanted a job that will give me time for ministry and kingdom service. I don't want to be a sunday Christian. On one of the days during NYSC, myself and the fellowship Secretary entered the bank to transact for the fellowship. As we entered that banking hall, I thought within that banking job wouldn't be bad idea after NYSC. That day, the banking hall was scanty so I assumed banking job wasn't as stressful like those I see in Lagos where people troop in en masse and the few workers are worn out. Note that this thought was within, i didn't verbalize it. As I got to the counter, after a short while, the man at the counter looked at me and said, "After your NYSC, don't bother working in the bank, go and face your ministry". Wow! He spoke prophetically. I knew God spoke through him that day. Quietly, I stepped out of the bank after we finished our transaction.



*Sometimes, our challenge in hearing or receiving from God is not because God is not speaking but our inability to pick His voice or catch divine signals from heaven. Lack of sensitivity to divine direction is a huge limitation in hearing God's voice*.  I heard the story of a brother whom the Lord spoke to through a mad man at the bus stop that he should obey the call into ministry. You may wonder why God will go to the extent of speaking through a mad man. Don't forget that the same God spoke through Balaam's donkey. If He could speak through an animal, how much more a human being that is mentally unstable. However, one would wonder why God would go to the extent of speaking through a mad man. It is not in His habit to bypass men and women in need of direction but sometimes, when His children are dull to hearing, insensitive or disobedient, He may adopt a method of communication that will pass home the message clearly. You don't need to get to that level where God will seek an "unusual" means to get your attention if your ear posture is right.


*The posture of your inner ears should be that of a man whose ears are placed on the chest of God to know His heart/ mind per time. It is a state of spiritual sensitivity*. The moment your sensitivity to God's leading is dull, even if God uses a loud speaker to speak, it may be hard for you to hear Him. Jacob had a visitation in a vision yet he didn't take advantage of that rare privilege because he was not sensitive. When he woke up, he said: "... *Surely the LORD is in this place, and I wasn’t even aware of it!*”, Gen26:16. That's not a good testimony. As you put your heart and mouth in the right posture, your ears must also align properly in the posture of sensitivity to pick spiritual  signals.



*ACTION POINT*: Pray in tongues for at least 30 minutes daily. It aids spiritual sensitivity.

*POSTURES FOR DIVINE DIRECTION* Pt.5


Proverbs 20:27- “The spirit of man is the candle of the LORD, searching all the inward parts of the belly.”


*SHARPENING YOUR SENSITIVITY*(A)


Back in the days, we used transistor radio device before digital radio emerge. For those who used the transistor radio, they tuned the radio with a knob till it aligned with the desired frequency for clarity. Sometimes, it requires a very careful setting/tuning because two radio frequencies can interfere as one may be hearing two stations on one single frequency. Often, skilled users of the transistor radio will painstakingly set the radio till reception becomes sharp and clear. That's akin to the way our spirit man works. *The immaterial part of us known as spirit is what receives the leading of the Spirit. It requires some tuning or sharpening so that it can pick clearly divine signals and for it to operate at the frequency of heaven*.


You can tune your spirit man to receive clearly from God. Attaining spiritual sensitivity is one of the major challenges most children of God have. They hear a cacophony of voices from within and are confused almost always. While the hand is a tool for setting the transistor radio till it attains the right frequency, the first tool for attaining spiritual sensitivity is the word of God. Often, many read/ study the word of God from a religious standpoint. They don't want to be caught( by friends and mentors) missing their private bible study in a day. That's fine but that shouldn't be the purpose of studying your bible. You should approach your daily study of the bible as a spiritual necessity for the survival of your spirit man.  *What food is to the body is what the word of God is to the spirit man*.



*The word of God has the power to tune our spirit man to operate at the frequency of heaven if we allow the word permeate our being*. Thorough meditation and deliberate practice of the word attune us to hear clearly from God. *Those that have not heard God from the pages of the scripture may never hear God when He speaks to their hearts*. There's a nexus between the two. If you want your spiritual sensitivity to be sharp, don't give your flesh all the treatment it deserves at the expense of your spirit. You can't feed your flesh 3 times a day with good food for seven days in a week and feed your spirit man once a week and be sensitive. It is impossible. Bodily, some are like sumo wrestlers but in the spirit, they're Lilliputians. Start working towards building a strong spirit that can pick spiritual signals by paying attention to God's word daily.



*PRAYER*: Father, I receive the grace to pay attention to your word daily in Jesus name. Amen

1/29/22, 8:40 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: ProjectSave Devotional

*POSTURES FOR DIVINE DIRECTION* Pt.6


Proverbs 20:27- “The spirit of man is the candle of the LORD, searching all the inward parts of the belly.”


*SHARPENING YOUR SENSITIVITY*(B)

Spiritual sensitivity can be likened to the way we tune the old television antenna till it gives a clear picture and sound. When I was growing up, we didnt have a decoder like most people do today. Those days, only the rich had what we called "satellite dish".  Our television stations operated on UHF and VHF frequencies. Those that used what we call black and white television will remember this very well. Most times, before the picture and sound quality is clear, someone was always outside to set the antenna manually while the person inside gives feedback on clarity. Thanks to technological advancement. Later, antenna with a booster device and remote came into the market. Tuning was a big headache not until digitalization became the order of the day. Oldies like me won't forget that era.


Your spiritual sensitivity can be tuned to operate at the frequency of heaven. The idea of radio and television frequencies first existed in the realm of the spirit before it was discovered by scientists. Man's spirit can be tuned to catch heavenly signals and pick information. Prayer is another tool for achieving sensitivity especially praying in tongues. 1Cor 14:14 *"For if I pray in tongues, my spirit is praying,...*". So if the spirit is what prays  when we pray in tongues, it also means that praying in tongues is a spiritual exercise that attunes us to the spirit realm. It makes us keen and receptive to the things of the Spirit. *We enter into the supernatural when we pray in the Holy Ghost extensively*. Hearing God is a spiritual communication  from spirit to spirit. John 4:24 say, *"God is spirit, and his worshipers must worship in the Spirit and in truth”. God doesn't have a bodily form, we only use anthropomorphic descriptions to describe him. If any man will hear from Him, his spirit must be alive.

Dearest, don't joke with your prayer life. Prayer positions your spirit to pick divine signals. While it was easy to tune a transistor radio and analog t.v.to their respective frequencies, it is not easy to tune the spirit quickly. This is because we are accustomed to the sense realm. It takes conscious and consistent deliberate effort to tune the spirit and make it sensitive. For years, I've formed the habit of praying in tongues on daily basis. It is a bit easier for me now to tune my spirit than it was 10 years ago. The more I pray in the Holy Ghost, the better I become in matters of sensitivity. Develop that spiritual discipline of praying in the Holy Ghost on daily basis. Focus on making your spirit man sharp,  receptive and sensitive to spiritual things.


*ACTION POINT*: Pray in the Spirit for a minimum of 30 minutes on daily basis.

*POSTURES FOR DIVINE DIRECTION* Pt.7


2Kings 3:15-16 Now bring me someone who can play the harp.” While the harp was being played, the power of the Lord came upon Elisha, and he said, “This is what the Lord says: This dry valley will be filled with pools of water! NLT


*SHARPENING YOUR SENSITIVITY*(C)
Some of us are familiar with the story in 2Kings 3. Joram succeeded his father, Ahab. Meesha the king of Moab normally pay tributes to Ahab in his life time but when his son ascended the throne, he rebelled. Joram decided to wage war against Moab. King Jehoshaphat of Judah and the king of Edom allied with Joram to fight king Meesha of Moab. They moved in the wilderness for about 7 days and they got to a point where there was no water for the soldiers and animals. The situation seemed hopeless but one of Joram's soldiers suggested they consulted Elisha so that they can know the mind of God. When they got to Elisha, it was obvious that Elisha wasn't happy with Joram because his father wasn't fully committed to the God of Israel.


I perceive that Elisha was not so in tune with the Spirit at the time they requested to know God's mind. He requested for a minstrel to come play music. The bible says, *But now bring me a minstrel. And it came to pass, when the minstrel played, that the hand of the LORD came upon him. And he said, Thus saith the LORD, Make this valley full of ditches*", vs 15. He couldnt say "thus saith the Lord" not until an anointed minstrel played. He took advantage of anointed music and minstrel to connect with heaven for a word of knowledge. Unfortunately, many in the music ministry in churches today are either glorified entertainers or business merchant who want to merchandise God's grace.  Do you know that anointed music can aid sensitivity? *When you stay in an atmosphere where worship fills the air, your heart becomes tender and you'll easily connect to the Spirit*. What many don't know is that even those in the occult also take advantage of demon inspired music to connect with the spirit realm if they want to commune with demons.



*Strive to be in an atmosphere where Jesus is lifted and worshipped. It opens the pathway for God to speak*. Stop listening to junk music if you want to connect to the frequency of heaven. From experience as a preacher, a dry and uninspired worship session in church may affect the flow of the preacher while a Spirit-filled worship session can make preaching/teaching easy for a preacher. Saul knew that his relief from demonic oppression lies in anointed music. That was why he engaged David in playing the harp so that he could be relieved. In your vehicle, house and office, use anointed music to create an atmosphere where the Spirit of God can easily reachout to you and speak to your spirit man. *Anointed music aids spiritual sensitivity*.


*PRAYER POINT*: Father, I receive the grace to connect to the Spirit through anointed music in Jesus name.

DEVOTIONAL 0408 | Source start line: 37420

2/1/22, 5:48 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: ProjectSave Devotional
Tuesday
1st February, 2022.

*KINGDOM FOCUSED PRAYER*

Col 3:1-2 If ye then be risen with Christ, seek those things which are above, where Christ sitteth on the right hand of God.  Set your affection on things above, not on things on the earth.

In Jewish liturgy, their prayer is incomplete until they pray about the kingdom of God. Why? Their heart is fixed and focused on the promised kingdom. To them, that was the most important thing. When Jesus taught his disciples to pray, you'll notice that there was a bold prayer about the kingdom: *"thy kingdom come, thy will be done on earth even as it in heaven*", Matthew 6:10. Other content of the prayer are kingdom focused in a way. It was only in one line he asked for bread ( provision).

What do we have today? We have turned our prayers upside down. We ask for daily bread more than we pray kingdom focused prayers. We are full of 'give me give me bless me bless me' prayers but hardly will you find a prayer meeting dedicated to praying about the things that matters to God and his kingdom. If we call for a prayer meeting and give it a very sensational theme like: "Pray yourself into victory over witches" or "Prayer to conquer household Enemies", I'm sure the attendance will double that of a prayer meeting with a theme that is kingdom focused. *A generation prayed to see God's glory, and they saw it through awekenings and revivals. Another generation  is praying that men may see their own glory and they lost the fire of revival*.

I want you to have a biblical focus about your life on earth. I have not in any way said it is wrong to ask God for provisions and others things. However, your prayer should centre around the things that matters to God. In Matthew 6:33, Jesus said, *"Seek the Kingdom of God above all else, and live righteously, and he will give you everything you need*". Pray about your walk with God and always ask him to overhaul your life so that you can be presented blameless before God(Col1:10). Ask the Lord for harvest of souls around the world and to open door of ministry unto the saints(Psalm2:8,Matthew9:36-38). Pray for revival like men of yore did. These and many more are very important to God.

*PRAYER*: Father, I receive grace to pray meaningful prayers henceforth in Jesus name.

2/2/22, 6:31 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: PROJECTSAVE DEVOTIONAL

2nd February, 2022.

*WATCH THE CENTRE*

Psalms 62: 10 Don’t make your living by extortion or put your hope in stealing. And if your wealth increases, don’t make it the center of your life. NLT

The story of the rich fool in the bible illustrates better the meaning of our topic and buttresses our opening text. The man saw that he had a huge harvest from his farm and he said, *"And I’ll say to myself, “You have plenty of grain laid up for many years. Take life easy; eat, drink and be merry*"’, Luke 12:19. His harvest or we say wealth became the centre of his life and God pronounced a verdict on him in verse 20: *“But God said to him, ‘You fool! This very night your life will be demanded from you. Then who will get what you have prepared for yourself?*". This rich fool made his wealth the centre of his life and he paid dearly with his life.

What does it mean when something is said to be at the centre of ones life? When something occupies a pole position or is taken to be highly important than other things, we'll say that very thing is at the centre of a man's life. When wealth making is at the centre of a man's life, he will do anything to make money. That's exactly what we are experiencing today. Unfortunately, some preachers also have wealth as the centre of their ministry and message. They can do just anything for money and manipulate people into giving with spurious promises of breakthrough. That's "spiritual yahoo yahoo". We're having young men who doesn't want to work and use their hands to make profit. They want to make it "big". Ritual killing and internet fraud is at an all time high. The bible says: *"Tainted wealth has no lasting value, but right living can save your life*", Proverbs 10:2 NLT.


Why lose your peace of mind because of ill-gotten wealth? Godliness with contentment will always produce great gain. Beware of money centred gospel. God won't give you wealth at the expense of your soul. The bible warns against the love of money: *"For the love of money is the root of all kinds of evil. And some people, craving money, have wandered from the true faith and pierced themselves with many sorrows*", 1 Tim 6:10 NLT. The moment your life is skewed towards acquiring "things", you'll end like the rich fool. You need to ask God to help diagnose your life in case the lust for material things lurk within. God and His kingdom should be at the centre of our lives not money. *"Then he said, “Beware! Guard against every kind of greed. Life is not measured by how much you own*”. Luke 12:15 NLT


*PRAYER*: Father, I receive the grace to put you at the centre of my life in Jesus name. Amen

*DADDY, MY ANOINTING IS WORKING*


Eph 3:20 Now unto him that is able to do exceeding abundantly above all that we ask or think, according to the power that worketh in us, Ephesians 3:20 KJV


On a glorious day, I returned home, entered the room and was about spending some time with God, my second son rushed in and said, "Daddy, my anointing is working". I inquired further to know to know what he meant. He said, " I commanded a lizard to go and he ran. And I commanded the hanger to fall and it fell*. It was hilarious but  I decided not to discourage him because i don't know how much of the anointing he desired and how he's been praying about it in his own little way. Obviously, he's been desirous of demonstrating God's power and that occasion meant a lot to him. Though I believe you don't need anointing to make a lizard run because lizards don't wait at the sight of humans especially at a close range. I can't explain the fallen hanger but I don't know how anointing will make hanger fall. If he had healed the sick or raised the dead, I wouldn't have raised doubts about the quality of his working anointing.



However, I learnt something that day. The young boy was conscious of God's power. He knew there is a super human power from above that can make things work beyond human effort. Paul told the Ephesian believers about this power and he brought them to its consciousness: *"Now unto him that is able to do exceeding abundantly above all that we ask or think, according to the power that worketh in us,*" Ephesians 3:20. He told them there's a power( Dunamis) at work in them already. It is also at work in us. Hallelujah!!! That power is the reason God will do exceedingly abundantly above all that we ask or imagine. That phrase "Exceeding abundantly" is from the Greek "Uperekperissou" meaning extreme. *The power of God in us can do extreme things beyond our imagination. Hallelujah!!!*




Reading further, the power in that text is known as "Dunamis". It is a self starting power. Inherent super human ability. That's the power Jesus promised we'll have when the Holy Spirit comes on us. Hey! If you're baptized in the Holy Spirit, you have that power already. Hallelujah!!! *To operate in the various dimensions that power can afford, you need to be conscious of it*. Stop thinking like a powerless and defeated fellow. Be conscious of 'Dunamis', that inherent ability to operate in the miraculous and make such happen for others. Don't be afraid to pray for the sick, be bold to tell the devil to come out and don't ever beg demons. *The power that operates in you puts you on a plane of " exceeding abundantly " where your wildest imagination cannot capture*. Learn to spend time praying in the Holy Ghost to stir this power. That's one of the secret of operating in the length and breadth of "Dunamis".


*CONFESSION*: Father, I thank you for your power that is at work in me. It can do exceeding abundantly above all that we can ever ask or imagine. Henceforth, I walk in the consciousness of that power. I refuse to beg demons and to be fearful. I walk in authority and power in Jesus name.



*ACTION POINT*: Spend considerable time praying in the Holy Ghost. Jude 1:20

DEVOTIONAL 0411 | Source start line: 37647

2/4/22, 5:15 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: PROJECTSAVE DEVOTIONAL
Friday,
4th February, 2022.


*WHEN GOOD MOTIVES CLASH WITH GODLY STANDARDS*


2Tim2:19 Nevertheless, God’s solid foundation stands firm, sealed with this inscription: “The Lord knows those who are his,” and, “Everyone who confesses the name of the Lord must turn away from wickedness.” NIV


Pastor Temi Ola once shared a story about an evangelist who was into prostitute evangelism. This preacher told him that their method of reaching out to prostitutes entailed taking them out and getting romantically involved with them even to the point of having unholy body contact with them( paraphrasing). While we know that evangelism is the heartbeat of God and the motive of reaching out is to win them unto the Lord, the man's method is totally in opposition to godly standard. Do you know Uzzah? That was the man who tried to steady the ark of covenant from falling when the oxen pulling it stumbled. His motive was to prevent the ark from falling yet God struck him dead instantly, 2 Sam6:1-7. That seemed too harsh right? Truly he had good intention but his method didn't match with God's standard.


In Numbers 4:15, God gave a clear instruction about the holy articles( ark inclusive): *"After Aaron and his sons have finished covering the holy furnishings and all the holy articles, and when the camp is ready to move, the Kohathites are to come to do the carrying. BUT THEY MUST NOT TOUCH THE HOLY THINGS OR THEY WILL DIE. The Kohathites are to carry those things that are in the Tent of Meeting*”. No matter how innocently it was done coupled with good motive as we've seen in the case of Uzzah, touching the ark was in direct violation of God’s law and was to result in death.  Also, the process involved in carrying the ark was too casual. David took some men to carry the ark rather than allowing the Levites to bring it to him. That was a great mistake, since it ought never to have been put upon a cart, old or new. It was to be borne upon men’s shoulders, and carried by Levites only, and those of the family of Kohath (Exodus 25:12-14; Numbers 7:9), using the poles prescribed. God was just in His judgement because from the starting point to the time when he struck Uzzah, there was a breach of standard even though they had good motives.


*Good motives aren't enough. When walking with God, adherence to His principles and standards comes first*. Good motives must not clash with godly standard. There's so much talk about having good intentions in today's world even from the pulpit, we hear such messages often. Such messages are backed with a popular mantra, "Once it is good for humanity, it is good for God". That maxim is not necessarily true in light of God's word. As a student writing exam, it may seem good to man to help a colleague in the exam hall with answers but it is totally against God's standard. *Masturbation, sexual involvement with a widow/ widower or someone whose spouse is far away may seem good to humanity as a way of relieving sexual tension but it is totally against God's standard*. Some persons believe helping a woman whose husband is impotent to achieve pregnancy is a form of help while some traditions also believe that it isn't a bad idea for a man to have children outside wedlock if the wife at home isn't fertile. All these scenarios are borne out of good motives to help but they clash with the standards of heaven. *When godly standards are involved, what is good for humans may not be good for God.*



*ACTION POINT*: For every good intention you have, check if it aligns with the standard of God. Drop motives that clash with godly standards.

DEVOTIONAL 0412 | Source start line: 37703
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
2/5/22, 7:38 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: ProjectSave Devotional
Saturday,
5th, February, 2022.

*HOW TO LEARN FROM MENTORS* Pt.1

Prov 17:16 It is senseless to pay to educate a fool, since he has no heart for learning.NLT


Years back, one of the women in my local assembly told me about a young brother who needed mentoring. She spoke glowingly about this young man, his zeal and his desire to grow in the things of the Spirit. She wanted me to undertake the task of relational discipleship with him of which I agreed to. When this mentoring relationship started, I noticed that this young man wasn't willing to learn because of his attitude of always trying to teach me whenever we're discussing. I expected him to listen and ask questions but he never gave me the chance to share God's word and wise counsel with him. He did almost all the talking while I was the major listener.  Actually, I felt it was something I could change so I became patient with him seeing he was younger. In all my trials, I failed. When I saw that he wasn't ready to learn, I called it quit silently.


Some of us need to learn how to how to learn from mentors. When my trainers are talking, the very first thing I do is to listen. *Your ability to listen to those God has placed over you for mentorship is the first step to growth*. One of the counsels in the book of proverbs from the supposed father to son says: *"Listen, my sons, to a father's instruction; pay attention and gain understanding*", Prov 4:1. You cannot benefit from mentoring relationships if you're not a listener. I don't have many mentors but I listen to the few I have whenever they're talking. *Mentorship will do you no good if you're not ready to listen attentively to your mentors*. I haven't said listening is akin to imposition of ideas but the honour you'll give a mentor is to open your ears to whatever he has to say then it is left for you to decide whether you'll obey or not. Talk less around mentors.


*Stop brandishing your knowledge and achievements whenever you're around those who carry the spirit of fathering over your life*. They know and can see what you're doing. You may have reached a very great height but you don't have their depth. *Their duty is to draw you deeper not just to take you higher*. When you are around men of depth, be wise to listen. *What I noticed about men who carry the grace to mentor and disciple people is that they aren't pursuing great height but they seek great depth*. You'll be wasting your time telling them about your "exploits", just listen to them. *Your meeting time with your mentor is not the time to show your exegetical prowess. There are men who don't know exegesis but they know God by reason of encounter*. They have depth of relationship with Him. Start cultivating this attitude of listening, it helps.

*ACTION POINT*: Listen.

DEVOTIONAL 0413 | Source start line: 37742
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
2/6/22, 6:00 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: ProjectSave Devotional
Sunday,
6th, February, 2022.


*HOW TO LEARN FROM MENTORS* Pt.2


Proverbs 4:1 My children, listen when your father corrects you. Pay attention and learn good judgment, NLT


There are several benefits attached to having godly mentors. Those who stand alone without the support of mentors rarely go far in life and destiny. Such persons are prone to errors and their mistakes could be fatal. There's a wisdom you'll get from relating with those that have gone ahead of you in life. In life, we must learn to stand upon the shoulders of those that have gone ahead of us. *It should be noted that mentors are not meant to play role of the Holy Spirit in our lives neither are they to lord over us but they're in our lives to give wise counsel and to guide in certain areas of life*.


Another way to learn from mentors is to pay attention to what they say. This connotes considering the things they prescribe. It entails giving thoughtful consideration to their counsel. It is not enough to listen, you must give their counsel thoughtful consideration.  Severally, I've noticed that some of the people who come around for counseling or who submits for mentorship already have a preconceived idea about what they intend doing. Most time, they're seeking approval and validation from mentors. I'm careful around those who are seeking approval for their ideas and decisions especially when their intention is selfish and unbiblical. The moment I notice that, I'm careful with what I say. Such people set traps with questions waiting for what you'll say. The moment you dance to their tune, they'll let the world know you're behind their decision.


*If you want to benefit from the wisdom of a mentor, don't seek validation for your ideas*. Go as a learner that is ready to do and apply counsel. *Don't make yourself hard to teach. Be humble in learning*. Loyal mentees aren't just listeners, they are doers. You cannot tell me that you're a mentee who listen to what the mentor say without doing the things said. *Until you start 'doing' or acting upon wise counsel, we can't say you're being mentored*. The fruit of biblical mentoring must be seen in the way you go about the things you do. Don't be static. Make progress with counsel. The only exception to this is if the Holy Spirit expressly instruct against the counsel received. However, all things being equal


*ACTION POINT*: As long as the Spirit is not saying otherwise, act on the counsel received from your mentor.

2/7/22, 5:18 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: PROJECTSAVE DEVOTIONAL
Monday,
7th February, 2022.

*HOW TO LEARN FROM MENTORS* Pt.3

Proverbs 4:1 My children, listen when your father corrects you. Pay attention and learn good judgment, NLT


There is a Yoruba adage that says, "The man that ask for the way will not miss the way". In mentoring relationships, one of the ways to learn is to ask questions. I love the disciples of Jesus for that. They were fond of asking relevant questions: *"The disciples asked him, “Why then do the teachers of the law say that Elijah must come first?*", Mt17:10. In several portions of the gospels, the disciples asked questions to draw wise counsel from Jesus. They weren't satisfied with their level of knowledge. In fact, they took their relationahip to another level by asking Christ to teach them the "How" of prayer: *"One day Jesus was praying in a certain place. When he finished, one of his disciples said to him, “Lord, teach us to pray, just as John taught his disciples*", Luke 11:1. They were all out to learn from the master and their method was either to learn by asking questions or asking Jesus to teach them on key issues outrightly.


One of the reasons people don't benefit from mentors is because they don't ask relevant questions that will draw wise counsel. A true child of God who is privileged to be around someone with depth should ask questions that will draw out biblical counsel. The goal should be to learn Christ and improve in other areas of life. Unfortunately, when some come around their mentors, they do more of "adoration". By that, I mean they express false humility and covet "grace" without wanting to learn the things that made the mentor who he is. Some are even under the wrong mentors because they chose who will mentor them based on fame, popularity and material gain not because of depth. Some just want to be associated with a popular name not because they want to learn anything that will benefit their lives.

You can learn from a mentor if you ask questions or by asking him/her to teach you outrightly. I cannot mention to you the number of times I've benefited from mentoring relationships because I've asked questions. It is not a sin to ask to learn what you don't know. You should be around a mentor because there's something to learn. The disciples of Jesus were well taught. It was evident in the life of Peter and John when they stood trial. The council observed that and said, *"When they saw the courage of Peter and John and realized that they were unschooled, ordinary men, they were astonished and they took note that these men had been with Jesus*", Acts4:13. They discovered that they had no formal training in scriptures but their learning was evident because they had been with Jesus. Let it be said of you that your life has changed because of proper mentoring. Never allow the glitz and glamour you see around your mentor be the focal point of your relationship with him. Learn by asking sincere questions. He has something to teach you.


*ACTION POINT*: Learn by asking questions from your mentor.

DEVOTIONAL 0415 | Source start line: 37899

2/8/22, 5:44 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: ProjectSave Devotional
Tuesday
8th February, 2022.

*GOD'S SILENT AUDITION*

Proverbs 21:2 A person may think their own ways are right, but the LORD weighs the heart.

I watched the audition for a music competition recently. Contestants were chosen  and allowed to proceed to the next round based on the quality of their performance and presentation. In movie auditions, those intending to act would be given an impromptu role to play. If they perform well, they'll be given a role to play. The purpose of any audition program is to check if the contestants are fit for the intended role. Do you know God conducts audition program for his children unknown to us? I was in a ministers meeting years back. A word of knowledge came openly for a pastor who was frustrated with the poor growth of his church despite his labours. The Lord said that he was passing through a test in ministry and that he had passed. He passed through a rough phase not knowing it was an audition phase that will determine his next level.


From the story of David and the events that led to his choice as the king of Israel, l concluded that God conduct silent audition program for his children and he gives a score card. When prophet Samuel received the instruction to go anoint a king in the house of Jesse, the brothers of David came forward to be anointed but the Lord rejected them for kingship: *"But the LORD said to Samuel, “Do not consider his appearance or his height, for I have rejected him. The LORD does not look at the things people look at. People look at the outward appearance, but the LORD looks at the heart*”, 1Sam16:7. I want you to note the tense of the word "rejected". It is rendered in past tense. The implication of that is clear, God already rejected the brothers of David for kingship even before the day of physical audition. Unknown to them, God had accessed them, weighed their hearts and reached a conclusion on how far they can go even before Samuel came. Only God knows what He saw in their lives that made them all to be rejected. Surprisingly, David, the shepherd boy passed his audition tests in the wilderness while tending sheep's and fighting wild animals. God saw how he led animals well before allowing him to lead humans.

Dearest, God is conducting His audition unknown to us. A day will come when a man is needed to occupy a crucial place in the grand plan of God and men with great CV would be rejected because they failed God's silent audition. *As we live our lives daily, we should be conscious and mindful of the fact that God is watching our actions and inactions*. He is weighing our thoughts and checking our attitudes before assigning task to us in destiny. Sometimes, when we see certain persons doing certain things for the Lord and the extent to which God's hand is upon them, we often think it is because they can pray and fast. While prayer and fasting is good, there other things God may have used as a criteria before allowing the weight of His glory rest on them in an unusual dimension. *When God is conducting his audition, it is often done unannounced but the result is evident to all*. He watches for the things we deem as "little" or "trivial" to reach a conclusion. I read in an article about a preacher that was given the healing anointing because he obeyed the instruction to help his wife in the kitchen. Live in the consciousness of the fact that God is watching the little things you do to audition you for roles to play in His agenda.

*PRAYER*: Father, I receive the grace to pass your silent auditions in Jesus name.

*DON'T PUT YOUR LIFE IN REVERSE GEAR*.

Proverbs 19:21 Many are the plans in a man's heart, but it is the Lord's purpose that prevails.

In my final year in the university, there was anxiety about where to serve in Nigeria because of how corps members were killed in several parts of North. It was a sad tale those days because everyone feared to be posted to the north. Often, people use their influence to work their posting back to the south. During our final year brethren( FYB) program, on the very last day, God spoke through the invited guest( Rev Biodun Akintayo) that someone among the final year brethren shouldn't influence his NYSC posting. I knew that word of knowledge was for me because prior to that time, God had dealt with me on that issue. Before we received our NYSC call up letter, one of my room mates told me to join him in Port Harcourt promising that I'll get a good job before NYSC but I declined because if it would entail influencing my NYSC posting. He got a job in an oil servicing company even before we did our project defense. I looked foolish but I knew I was flowing with the Spirit.


I spent seven months at home preparing myself for NYSC. I fasted and prayed like no man's business. I worked in a boarding school that period. One of the parents who knew I was waiting for my call up told me he could help with my NYSC posting due to the killings in the north. He was sincere about his offer based on the bitter realities in Nigeria  then but I declined. Unfortunately, it has not gotten any better at the time of this writing. I prayed extensively about being at the centre of God's will for my life and by divine providence, God posted me to where I met destiny. Whenever I look back at the journey of my life, I always thank God because I didn't accept the offer to influence my NYSC posting because what I am doing today in ministry is traceable to the encounters and trainings I received that period. Some of our ministry board members and partners are products of my NYSC sojourn. If I had gone elsewhere, I would have reversed my life 10 years backward. I'll struggle to meet up. Lest i forget, I met my lovely wife in NYSC, we served together in Corpers fellowship and we're still serving together in ministry today. The fellowship continues. You know what i mean. Hallelujah!!!


*Some persons are 10 years behind in God's program for their lives because they used human effort borne out of fear of tomorrow and anxiety to work themselves out of God's will for their lives.* Those who have a sense of purpose don't jump at every offer or opportunity. God has a programme and he has people He want to feature in His program provided they flow with Him. *The plan and purpose of God is unchanging but unfortunately, the people who are meant to feature in that plan may be changed or replaced*. Whoever walks away from God's will be replaced. *Beware of trying to always influence things to work in your favour with human connections. You may influence your life out of God's program for your life*. Certain things are destined to happen in certain places but if you walk elsewhere, it may take you a longer time before achieving those things including marriage. Some walked away from a certain location to where they heard "things" are happening but nothing is happening in their lives. Isaac would have missed God if he had joined the bandwagon to Egypt, Gen26. You need to trust God enough believing that He has a good plan for you. Jer29:11 says,  *"For I know the plans I have for you,” declares the LORD, “plans to prosper you and not to harm you, plans to give you hope and a future*". God's plan for you is good enough. Find it, discern it and never use human effort to put your life in reverse gear. *A wind is blowing, it is the wind of God's plan and purpose. Let it blow you to wherever God has destined for you. It is to your benefit.*


*PRAYER*: Father, let your wind blow me into your plan and purpose for my life. I don't want to miss it Lord.Help me.

2/10/22, 5:50 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: PROJECTSAVE DEVOTIONAL

*BEWARE OF ALTERNATIVES*


Hebrews 6:12 We do not want you to become lazy, but to imitate those who through faith and patience inherit what has been promised.



In 2020 when I had COVID-19 in Ghana after our mission outreaches, myself and my companion stayed in a town located beside the Atlantic ocean. From the balcony of our house, I saw big ships, passenger boats and other vessels. The scenery was beautiful. Anytime I saw those boats, I was tempted to quit isolation and escape through the water ways. I was told the
there was a station where one could buy ticket to Nigeria. I was told the journey would last four days on the sea. I was homesick because I felt confined and stuck to routines. The expectation of health officials and airline operators was that one must test negative before embarking on any trip outside the country. At a time, I became very impatient with the process of waiting so l was ready to explore illegal exit opportunities and escape routes. I knew it may affect my passport because there would be no immigration officer to stamp out my passport but I got to the point where I almost didn't care about what the consequences may be. Thank God I didn't.



The second option I had was to go to the border and walk across on foot, then take bike from Togo to Nigeria. There'll be no one to stamp out my passport because it was an illegal route. I may  escape the pain of waiting to test negative but i would not escape the problems of not stamping out my Int'l passport may create. I was tempted to exit illegally. In all, I thank God I waited patiently till I tested negative. There's a lesson in that story for us all. *When God is taking us through a process especially the ones that involve waiting patiently, ungodly exit options will present itself and those around you may suggest it. That's when you have to be extra careful so that you won't miss God's appointed time and perfect solution*. If I had escaped isolation through the sea or through illegal borders, it won't cure or change my condition. I'll only experience a change of environment but the problem would have persisted at least for sometime. Not all exit options or alternatives are godly. For example, it is easy to compromise on sexual purity to satisfy a man who is saying "If you don't sleep with me, I won't marry you". Some compromised and got married through that route but ask what has become of their marriage today. *The devil is skilled at showing to us how juicy it is to explore his alternatives but he won't show us the aftermath pain and sorrow that comes with it.*




*Whenever God permits us to have wilderness experience and it seems the exit road is getting longer daily, He is using time to process and make us better*. God could have taken the children of Israel through a shorter route to the promised land but the bible says: *"When Pharaoh let the people go, God did not lead them on the road through the Philistine country, though that was shorter. For God said, “If they face war, they might change their minds and return to Egypt*”, Ex13:17. God's intention was to take them through the wilderness for 40 days so as to prepare them militarily for the conquest of Canaan. If they had gone to Canaan through the 3 days route, they'll get to Canaan but not as a conqueror. They'll be subservient to the Canaanites again after their servitude in Egypt. It would be from slavery to slavery. *When the devil present to us quick exit options in our waiting times, we'll miss out on divine lessons and equipping if we yield*. Job knew this truth that's why he said: *"If a man die, shall he live again? all the days of my appointed time will I wait, till my change come*", Job14:14.


*PRAYER POINT*: I receive the grace to wait and shun ungodly alternatives in Jesus name.

*AVOID TRIGGERS OF UNGODLY ALTERNATIVES*


Hebrews 6:12 We do not want you to become lazy, but to imitate those who through faith and patience inherit what has been promised.



I remember my Eba and egusi joint at Pram junction in Greater Accra, Ghana. When i became home sick during my COVID-19 ordeal, I asked around for a place where I could get Nigerian made meals. The type of rice I ate in the community where I resided stirred serious longing for home-made meals. I remember vividly that I was frequent at the spot where we bought pap in nylon for 50 pesewas. I rarely take pap as breakfast but there was nothing I could do under the condition I found myself. All that stirred my desire for better meals. Though I was infected,  I was saddled with the responsibility of caring for my travel assistant who could barely help himself. Unfortunately, our request for isolation in the government hospital wasn't granted. I fellowshipped in a church where there were Nigerians. After one of the Sunday services, I asked where Nigerian meals were sold. Someone gave me a good description. I didn't know it was a familiar place along the route to church. While in that restaurant, I got talking with the Nigerian lady who managed the place. In our conversation, she spoke about traveling through some illegal border routes to Nigeria. She told me some persons were using the route without hassles. That sank deeply. Another brother in church said something similar. That was where my eagerness to explore illegal alternatives started from before it graduated into seeking for an escape route via the water ways. That was my last resort.



I heard a transport company was helping people cross the border. I called the front desk officer of the transport company. He said they have links at every border post. He promised to carry us to the border between Ghana and Togo then some bike men will pick us from there to the border between Togo and Benin republic and from there to Nigeria. It sounded good but I had a check deep in my spirit. I stayed with a wonderful brother who calmed my anxiety with his soothing words. His teachings during our devotion and perspective on issues were my inspiration to stay on the right track. Thank God my patience paid off and God proved Himself as our provider. Any lesson in this concise experience I shared? *When you relate too long with people who see nothing wrong with exploring ungodly alternatives, you may be influenced*. I almost came under the influence of those whom I heard talking about using illegal routes.


*While waiting on the Lord during your trying times, do not associate with men who point you to or offer quick fixes. If you stay long with or around them, you'll come under their influence*. Every man on the face of the earth is a product of influence positively or negatively. Even Abraham, the friend of God was wrongly influenced by his wife, Gen16:2. His wife told him to go in to Hagar so that he could have a son and he did. We didn't know the number of times Sarah pointed Abraham to that alternative but I'm sure that repeated exposure was what triggered Abraham to do what he did. Today, the outcome of Abraham's action is part of the problems facing the world. Abraham didn't live to see the havoc the descendants of Ishmael have wrecked in the world. *Today's ungodly quick fixes to our challenges may solve some problems temporarily but may create a long standing problems for the next generation*. This is what Abraham didn't realise when he went in to Hagar. If he had known, he may not have explored that option. There's only one way to obtaining the promise: Faith&Patience. What you'll get with ungodly alternatives are things that looks like the promise but not the promise itself. Wait on God patiently, your time is coming.


*PRAYER*: Father, I receive the grace to wait on you patiently. Separate me from men and women who may seek to influence me wrongly. Help me Lord.

2/12/22, 7:17 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: ProjectSave Devotional
