*REJOICING IN PERSECUTION*

Mt 5: 11-12 “God blesses you when people mock you and persecute you and lie about you and say all sorts of evil things against you because you are my followers. Be happy about it! Be very glad! For a great reward awaits you in heaven. And remember, the ancient prophets were persecuted in the same way. NLT


In A.D.64, Nero the Roman emperor burnt a part of Rome. He was a wicked leader who desired that nothing would be left after he was gone. He wanted to be the last ruler of that modern city so he arranged with some of his men to set a larger part of Rome on fire. In history we were told the fire raged for 7 days while other sources said 9 days. This infuriated the citizens of Rome and they questioned him. In a bid to cover up his heinous act, he lied on the Christians saying they were behind the burning of Rome. Many innocent Christians were arrested and killed for an offence they knew nothing about. At that time, many people saw Christianity as "Religio Illicita" ( illegal religion) so it meant nothing if believers were killed. They fed them to hungry lions.



The message that pitched the believers against the rulers of that time was the message about the coming kingdom of God and rulership of the Messiah. They felt threatened that they wanted to take over. That is the Gospel the early church preached that earned them persecution. They didn't preach a feel good Gospel. What are we preaching today? Oral tradition says that the Christians killed by Nero sang and rejoiced in the face of death. That made them look weird to the people watching them. Nero was surprised seeing people singing when faced with death.  Long after they died,  Nero was angry seeing their laughing skull and he wondered the kind of breed they were. He kicked their skulls. The same Nero killed Paul and Peter. He was a brutal leader whom some thought was the anti Christ.


I've asked myself if I can laugh and sing hymns in the face of death like those men did. They saw the glory of God while on this side of eternity so death meant nothing to them. The admonition of Christ in the sermon he preached on the mount is that we should rejoice when we are persecuted on his behalf. That's not the time to curse God and ask questions. Your faith in Christ will be opposed and mocked: *"In fact, everyone who wants to live a godly life in Christ Jesus will be persecuted*", 2 Tim3:12. Godliness attracts persecution. Ours may not be a brutal persecution like men of old but it will come in another dimension. The time will come when you'll be derided and deprived on account of your uncompromising stand, the bible says you should rejoice.



*ACTION POINT*: Pray for grace to rejoice in the face of persecution. Also, pray that God should send help to believers in persecuted regions of the world.

2/13/22, 11:17 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: ProjectSave Devotional
Sunday,
13th, February, 2022.

*WE LIED*

Psalms101:7 He that worketh deceit shall not dwell within my house: he that telleth lies shall not tarry in my sight.


In 2011, I was in a group of fellowship leaders who went for a retreat in Jos. It is safe to say all of were pastors. Each of us served in different stations across the country. After our meeting in Jos. We wanted to get a direct bus to our various stations but our timing to the park was late. We decided to break our journey. We boarded a vehicle in Jos that was meant to go to Markurdi but we wanted to go to Enugu. When we told the driver that our intention was to get to Enugu that day so that we can connect to our various destinations. He told us that he would lie at the gate of the park that he was taking us to Markurdi because Enugu was not his route and that we should nod or respond affirmatively. We all agreed silently.



Eventually, when he drove out of the gate, one of the motor park managers at the gate asked where he was going, he said Makurdi and the rest of us acquiesced. Just after we drove past Otukpo, a town after Makurdi, we had a flat tyre. We couldn't go further because the road was bad. We slept over in Otukpo that night. The next day, as we moved, we had another flat tyre. We changed it and moved on. After some hours, we had two flat tyres again. The driver changed it. As were were approaching Enugu, the gear stopped working. It dawned on all of us that we laid the foundation of our journey on lies and that was why it was a tumultuous journey.  The trouble started after we left Markurdi which was the place we lied we were going. Getting to Enugu became a problem. We all repented and moved on.



When you lay the foundation of anything on lies, it will crumble. Lies makes the journey of life slow and boring. The lies we told in that journey gave the devil a foothold to delay our our journey. Same applies to the journey of life. A liar can't go far with God, never. If you lay the foundation of your marriage with lies, it may not last. *If you have lied consistently to sustain a thing, go make your way right or else, you'll tell more lies. Albeit, do it as led by the Spirit, prayerfully and with quality counsel*. Stop giving the devil a foothold in your life neither should you give him what he can use to accuse you. Jesus said, *"I will not speak with you much longer, for the ruler of the world (Satan) is coming. And he has no claim on Me [no power over Me nor anything that he can use against Me];*", John14:30.


*PRAYER*: Father, I receive the grace to be truthful in my walk and dealings in Jesus name

DEVOTIONAL 0421 | Source start line: 38582

2/14/22, 5:32 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: PROJECTSAVE DEVOTIONAL
Monday,
14th February, 2022.

*HE IS THE OWNER OF THE BAKERY*

Mt6:11 Give us today our daily bread.

In recent times, a perspective is being popularized by some persons about the aspect of the Lord's prayer where Jesus prayed, "Give us this day our daily bread". Some have questioned that prayer and are saying, "Why ask for daily bread when you can own a bakery"? At first, I agreed to that but later the Holy Spirit cautioned me because that idea corrupt the very meaning of what Jesus intended in that prayer. It is like saying, "Jesus, you didn't pray well. You should have prayed that aspect this way". Could Jesus be wrong to have taught his disciples to pray asking for daily bread? Of course He wasn't. While I agree that the Lord's prayer is not cast-in-stone for believers, however, it reveals to  us a pattern or a model we can adopt in our prayer lives. It should be noted that "Daily Bread" is a  figurative phrase denoting daily provisions and divine supplies.


Why did Jesus teach his disciples to ask for daily bread and not to ask for bakery?  The answer is simple. *God want to be our source of sustenance and provision. He want to own the bakery while we receive the bread. The day we own the bakery that supplies our bread, we'll stop trusting Him for divine supplies. That doesn't mean we won't work but our jobs, businesses and career expressions are meant to be means of supply but not the source*. Man has tendency to think he is sufficient to meet his needs. The pride of "I can do it on my own" can easily set in and we'll think we don't need God to meet our needs. It is possible to be working hard and still not have your needs met. *The moment we get to the point of self sufficiency, we'll miss out on God's largesse for our lives because we'll be full of ourselves and we'll be disconnected from His abundant supply*.  He is the abundant source that never runs dry. The Psalmist says in Psalm145:15-16, *"The eyes of all look to You, And You give them their food in due time. You open Your hand And satisfy the desire of every living thing*". Hallelujah!!!




The owner of our bakery( God) is never tired of supplying our daily bread. *The day you start baking your own bread with your effort, you'll run out of flour and your hands will become weak.*  Our bakery owner is rich in all and can supply generously without being tired. I learnt this by experience during the lockdown of 2020. I've never seen God in that dimension before.  Paul knew this truth about God that was why he said: *"But my God shall supply all your need according to his riches in glory by Christ Jesus*", Philippians 4:19 KJV. Hallelujah!!! He is not going to supply some but all our needs. He is committed to doing that if we trust Him. God is my supplier, I'm the receiver. I don't need to give myself headache about trying to produce my bread. Why go through the stress of baking bread when I can just ask for it and receive it on time? Trust God for your needs and quit using human effort. Trust is part of what God is teaching us.


*PRAYER*: Father, I trust you for my daily bread. I don't need a bakery because I can't maintain it. Just do the baking and I'll receive the supply. Thank you for answering my prayer, in Jesus name.


====================================================================================================
DEVOTIONAL 0422 | Source start line: 38720
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
2/15/22, 5:46 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: ProjectSave Devotional
Tuesday
15th February, 2022.

*A LIFE SPONSORED BY GOD* Pt.1

Psalms34:5 They looked unto him, and were lightened: and their faces were not ashamed.


The most recent experience that I will never forget in my adult  life is the 2020 lockdown. For me, it was a season of test and trials especially as regards provision. At the onset of that lockdown, I gave out almost all I had on me. There was nothing substantial I could fall back on. If my concern was about me, I wouldn't be bothered but my wife and three children were there to feed. While I had little, I gave from the little and I was prompted to raise monetary support for missionaries, ministers and brethren who were badly hit by the lockdown. In addition, I sold one of my books to raise fund for the  palliative program yet I had little or nothing substantial on me. It was easy for me to take from the monies received because I was in need but I didn't. Some persons asked how I was putting up, all I said was "fine". If I had told them how I needed money badly, they may send something to me but all I said was "Fine". Faithfully, I disbursed all donations to those in need. I never knew I was writing a test.


After a while, God sent surprises to me. It became a monthly thing. Sometimes during lockdown, those supernatural provisions landed at intervals of weeks. Sometimes, my children ate several times in a day. There was a day my boys counted for their mom saying, "Mummy, this is section 7". It was the 7th time they ate on that day. I was practically sponsored by God. Other miracles of provisions followed that period that are numerous to mention. Since then, I knew what it meant to trust God totally without looking up to man. I make bold to say that I was sponsored by God and still under God's sponsorship. Sometimes, I've had people ask how we get funds to run our outreaches. The answer is simple, our ministry is sponsored by God not man. Men will surely get tired to our consistent asking but God never will. When I started out in ministry, I had fears. One of the things i desired then was that I will be the sole or major financier of the ministry so I had schemes on how to get that done. Little did I know that a divine vision can't be sponsored by man. *The best of human effort is like a basket under the rain, it will never get filled.*


*I do not know many secrets on how to get God sponsor a man but the very first I'll share among others is to be right at the centre of God's will for ones life*. That is the starting point. When you embark on the journey to where God has sent you doing what He has called you to do, you have signed up for God's sponsorship. There's something peculiar about being at the centre of God's plan and purpose for your life. Certain things will begin to happen on their own accord. They'll practically walk into your life without stress. This may not happen immediately but with time, this will be your experience. Saying "No" to the will of God for your life is a call to a life of self sponsorship. That is a hard life. *God want to sponsor your life but not without your cooperation*. You cooperate with God by obeying His will. God is saying to you and me, *"If ye be willing and obedient, ye shall eat the good of the land:*" Isaiah 1:19 KJV.


*ACTION POINT*: What is God telling you to do that you've not been doing? Prayerfully seek the face of God to know His will for your life per time.

DEVOTIONAL 0423 | Source start line: 38832

2/16/22, 5:34 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: PROJECTSAVE DEVOTIONAL
Wednesday,
16th February, 2022.

*A LIFE SPONSORED BY GOD* Pt.2

Gen 26:2 And the LORD appeared unto him, and said, Go not down into Egypt; dwell in the land which I shall tell thee of:  KJV


*LEARN FROM ISAAC'S STORY*



Our opening text speaks about God's instruction to Isaac. In his time, there was famine. That's similar to what we call economic recession today. Economic crunch is not new to the world, its been from bible days. In the days of Isaac, almost everyone desired to seek greener pastures in Egypt. Egypt was the economic giant of that time. Isaac wanted to join the bandwagon but the Lord instructed him not to go and he obeyed. *You set yourself up for destruction when you fail to obey instruction*.  God is speaking to us this season from this short story. The bible says: *"For everything that was written in the past was written to teach us, so that through the endurance taught in the Scriptures and the encouragement they provide we might have hope*", Rom 15:4.


The first lesson I picked in that story is God knows the right location where we should be per time. Where people deem as "Greener Pastures" may not be God's will for your life. This is a problem of people from third world country. The craze to travel abroad to secure one's future is common in almost all third world nations. This is due to economic hardship and suffering. Often, most believers fail to ask God if they should go or not. In fact, in most churches in Africa, you can predict that someone is praying to travel abroad. I've been to prayer meetings and gatherings where people came with their passports. Their minds are made up already, they didn't come to seek direction. It is not bad to relocate provided it is God's plan for your life. Many are not asking God if relocation is in line with His will. *God's sponsorship is only available to those who are in the right location.* There are blessings with your name and address tagged on it but delivery is only possible if you're rightly located.


*Some preachers often talk about Isaac's seed but I've only heard few messages on Isaac's obedience to God's instruction*. His obedience to God's instruction( not to go to Egypt) was the reason he reaped a hundredfold not : *"Then Isaac sowed in that land, and received in the same year an hundredfold: and the LORD blessed him. And the man waxed great, and went forward, and grew until he became very great*", Genesis 26:12-13 KJV. If Isaac had left Gerar for Egypt, he would have sown seed in his new location but it won't yield a hundredfold there because the soil is not favourable for such. *It is possible to labour rightly in the wrong location. In such instance, good result will be far from such a person*. When you're in the wrong location, you'll  pay your bills. God want to sponsor you and pay your bills provided you're in the right address.


*PRAYER*: Father, open my eyes and ears to receive clear instruction on where I should be per time. Henceforth, I receive divine instruction unhindered and I receive the grace to obey in Jesus name.

2/17/22, 5:39 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: PROJECTSAVE DEVOTIONAL

*A LIFE SPONSORED BY GOD* Pt.3

1Kings 17:2-4 Then the Lord said to Elijah, “Go to the east and hide by Kerith Brook, near where it enters the Jordan River. Drink from the brook and eat what the ravens bring you, for I have commanded them to bring you food.” NLT


*ANOTHER LESSON FROM ELIJAH'S STORY*


To avoid being one sided, we need to consider Elijah's story as much as we examined Isaac's. God instructed Isaac not to go to Egypt during famine and he obeyed. In 1 Kings 17:2-4, the same God instructed Elijah to relocate during famine:  *"Then the Lord said to Elijah, “Go to the east and hide by Kerith Brook, near where it enters the Jordan River. Drink from the brook and eat what the ravens bring you, for I have commanded them to bring you food*”. If we consider Isaac's story without considering Elijah's story, we may think that God's strategy for sponsorship is to remain in the location where there's famine at all times. God is not static, He's  progressive. *It is wrong to decide on what to do in a situation using previous experience or popular opinion. It doesn't work that way. Sometimes, there are fresh instructions for every season and that's why we must be sensitive to know the mind of God*.




If Elijah had assumed God's strategy for sponsorship is to expect a miracle in a dry land, he may have died of hunger. *Sometimes, relocation is God's plan for His children to receive their allocation*. Does it mean God made the mistake of putting them where they were before telling them to relocate? Of course not. The idea behind telling His children to relocate could be because their purpose for being there has been fulfilled and they have another assignment elsewhere. At other times, it could be for protective reasons as it was in the case of baby Jesus, Mt2:13-23.  In the story of Elijah, he didn't relocate once. After brook Cherith dried, he was instructed to relocate to the house of a widow in Zarephath: *"But after a while the brook dried up, for there was no rainfall anywhere in the land. “Go and live in the village of Zarephath, near the city of Sidon. I have instructed a widow there to feed you*", 1 Kings 17:7, 9 NLT. Can you see that? That was the second instruction for relocation.



I know a man who was barely surviving here in Nigeria, God instructed him to relocate to one of the west African countries as a missionary, he obeyed without delay not knowing anyone in the country where he was sent. He is serving God's purpose in a very remote part of that country but God's sponsorship is reaching him there. He married, built a house and even started a thriving business there with a flourishing ministry. He has planted over 10 churches among the unreached peoples. As a child of God, what matters is to know the mind of God per time. If Isaac had done the popular thing(relocation) everyone did in his time, He would have missed out on God's sponsorship. On the other hand, if Elijah had stayed back in a land that was ravaged with famine, He would have missed out on supernatural provision. *The land flowing with milk and honey may not supply its richness to some because they're not meant to be there yet another land that people call dry may be the Canaan of some*. Don't let  times and opinion determine your action, just allow the Holy Spirit lead you.


*PRAYER*: Holy Spirit, I surrender to you. Lead me to where God's sponsorship is available for me in Jesus name.

2/18/22, 8:27 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: PROJECTSAVE DEVOTIONAL

*A LIFE SPONSORED BY GOD* Pt.4


1Kings17: 11 As she was going to get it, he called, “And bring me, please, a piece of bread.” 12 “As surely as the Lord your God lives,” she replied, “I don’t have any bread—only a handful of flour in a jar and a little olive oil(D) in a jug. I am gathering a few sticks to take home and make a meal for myself and my son, that we may eat it—and die.”

*HOW GOD SPONSORED THE WIDOW*



The story of how God sponsored the widow of Zarephath during famine remains an unusual example about divine sponsorship. After the brooks dried, Elijah was instructed to go to the house of a widow. Obviously, God led him there to be provided for by the widow. This was during a severe famine that ravaged the land. Sometimes, I wonder why God didn't lead him to an influential or rich family. It sounded unfair to me that the only person God could think of was a struggling and poor widow who was about to eat her last meal and die. When Elijah demanded for food, listen to what she said: *"As surely as the Lord your God lives,” she replied, “I don’t have any bread—only a handful of flour in a jar and a little olive oil(D) in a jug. I am gathering a few sticks to take home and make a meal for myself and my son, that we may eat it—and die*”. If Elijah was a prophet in our time, I'm sure he'll have bad press. The next headline you'll see is "Greedy Prophets Taking Advantage of Poor Widows". All internet news outlet will make this incident trend for weeks.



However, I perceive the woman was sensitive to know that it was God that spoke through the servant of the Lord, the bible says, *"She went away and did as Elijah had told her. So there was food every day for Elijah and for the woman and her family. 16 FOR THE JAR OF FLOUR WAS NOT USED UP AND THE JUG OF OIL DID NOT RUN DRY, IN KEEPING WITH THE WORD OF THE LORD SPOKEN BY ELIJAH*". This is the foolishness of God that is wiser than the wisdom of men at work. How can God demand for the last meal of a poor widow to give her continuous supply of food during famine? It doesn't make sense humanly but that's where God's wisdom supersede ours.



*Sometimes, when God want to sponsor you, He may demand for something you hold dear not because He needs it but to test your heart to be sure you haven't become a slave to that thing*. God hates when things become our master and Lord. He want to be our only Lord.  There are people who have become slaves to money and material things. One can easily know this when they refuse to give it. You can't be praying to God for sponsorship when there are things you can't let go. It doesn't work that way. Most times, the Spirit of the Lord may be impressing on your heart to sacrifice a thing for the Lord but you're struggling to let go because you're thinking of how and where the next one will come. The good thing about this type of instruction is that the Holy Spirit will minister to you personally on this. Even if a demand comes through a servant of God, you will have a witness deep within about it. He who desires heaven's sponsorship must be ready to obey God's instruction ( not manipulation of men) to give sacrificially.



*PRAYER*: Father, I receive the grace to obey in Jesus name

DEVOTIONAL 0426 | Source start line: 39273

2/19/22, 8:41 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: ProjectSave Devotional
Saturday,
19th, February, 2022.

*YOUR PROFESSION- GOD'S PLATFORM*.

MARK 16:15 "And he said unto them, Go ye into all the world, and preach the gospel to every creature." KJV


Anytime I read the salvation story of Bishop David Oyedepo, it reinforces my stand on the fact that end time harvest will not only be achieved by evangelists and those we call "clergy"  but by God's children who have contact with their world through professional platforms. Bishop David Oyedepo gave his life to Christ on February 19th 1969. He was led to Christ by Betty Lasher, his school teacher. At the end of that year, his school end of session report by his Principal read ‘Spiritually Robust’. That harvest has impacted lives globally and is still doing exploits. Imagine this generation doesn't have a man like him? He is not a product of a crusade meeting. A school teacher brought him into the faith and he has been burning ever since.


One would think a man of that stature got born again on the crusade ground. It is common to hear stories of how people got born again in special meetings conducted by a great evangelist or how they saw Jesus Christ in a vision. That's fine but not everyone will have that experience. Betty Lasher is a model believer who understand that the work of soul winning is not limited to the crusade ground. She saw her job as a platform for spreading the Gospel. I'm sure the woman must have led others to Christ from the classroom most of whom we don't know. She'll be rewarded like every other person who led people to Christ from the altar. Those days in UNILAG, we had a driver who sang evangelistic songs anytime students board his cab. The moment you board his cab, you cannot but listen to the Gospel through his songs.


*The end time harvest is not the duty of professional preachers alone but for every child of God. Our career expressions are divine platforms to reach out to the lost and dying world with the message of eternal salvation*. God didn't give you that job all because of money. He saw ahead and decided to place you in that office because He want you to reach certain people who may never come to church or a crusade meeting. *The average church folk in business or working in an office makes contact with people from different walks of life most of whom a full time pastor or evangelist may not meet in his life time*. Imagine the number of students( harvest) that will pass through a university lecturer or school teacher who served for 35 years?  What if believers working in those environment come up with an evangelistic strategy that best suit that environment without breaking ethical codes? When Jesus told us to "Go" into the world, He didn't say everyone should "Go" as a professional preacher. We are to obey the  "Go ye" as we are. Doctors, lawyers, school teachers, artisans, technicians, students, etc are to "Go ye" taking advantage of their profession as a platform.  Let God's kingdom profit from your career expression.


*ACTION POINT*: Device strategies to reach out to the lost using your career platform. Do this in such a way that the ethics of your profession won't be breached.

2/20/22, 1:33 PM - Lanre Oyeleke: ProjectSave Devotional
Sunday,
20th, February, 2022.

*YOU HAVE A DIVINE TOOL FOR THE TASK*


Ex 4:2-3 *Then the Lord said to him, “What is that in your hand?” “A staff,” he replied. The Lord said, “Throw it on the ground.” Moses threw it on the ground and it became a snake, and he ran from it*. NIV


I love the story of Moses. When God called him to deliver the children of Israel from slavery in Egypt, he asked the Lord, *"...“What if they do not believe me or listen to me and say, ‘The Lord did not appear to you’?*” Exodus 4:1. He heard God clearly about an assignment that seemed so great. It is not easy to be at the forefront of a liberation task. He felt he was not equipped and trained for the job. That's how some of us felt when God called us. God saw his heart and the thoughts running through his mind, then the Lord said, *"What is that in your hand?” “A staff,” he replied. The Lord said, “Throw it on the ground.” Moses threw it on the ground and it became a snake, and he ran from it*". Wow! Moses' staff was a major tool in his liberation task but he didn't know not until God revealed to him. Now, you must realize that his staff was a mere staff but when the call came, the power of God made an ordinary staff a supernatural tool for his divine assignment.


You may have sensed the call of God upon your life to do a thing. God may have impressed upon your heart certain things He want you to do for Him but you feel inadequate. Sometimes, you may feel there are better people God could have sent on that assignment yet heaven is pointing at you to undertake that assignment. *God will not send anyone on any assignment without equipping the person. The training process sometimes may not necessarily be formal. In fact, it is often not a formal training. Moses didn't know that the wilderness was part of his training. Also, he didn't know that his shepherd's staff was a vital equipment in his liberation assignment*. When the children of God got to the red sea and there was nowhere to turn, God told Moses, *"And the Lord said to Moses, “Why do you cry to Me? Tell the children of Israel to go forward. 16 BUT LIFT UP YOUR ROD, and stretch out your hand over the sea and divide it. And the children of Israel shall go on dry ground through the midst of the sea*", Ex14:15-16. The same rod that turned snake was the same rod God used in parting the red sea.


*When God is training you for a task, he will give you a tool for the task. Often, we look down on those tools God has given us for the assignment yet we are not sensitive enough to know that those things are part of our equipping for the task*. Your writing skill may just be what God want to use to change lives. No one knew David's sling was a tool that will make him a national champion and from there be enthroned. Unlike Moses, he was sensitive to know that the sling in his hand had potentials to kill a giant. He didn't go to battle wearing the armour of Saul. Just a sling and five smooth stones. Dearest, you have the tool for the task. Once the hand of God rest on that tool, He will perform wonders with it. You need to be sensitive to know the tool for that assignment. Whenever God reminds you about that task or assignment, simply ask Him to reveal the tool He has given to you.


*PRAYER*: Father, I receive the grace to discern the tool you've given me for my life's assignment in Jesus name

DEVOTIONAL 0428 | Source start line: 39366

2/21/22, 5:26 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: PROJECTSAVE DEVOTIONAL
Monday,
21st, February, 2022.

*UNDERSTANDING BIBLICAL REVIVAL* Pt.1

Acts 2:37 *Now when they heard this, they were pricked in their heart, and said unto Peter and to the rest of the apostles, Men and brethren, what shall we do. 38 Then Peter said unto them, Repent, and be baptized every one of you in the name of Jesus Christ for the remission of sins, and ye shall receive the gift of the Holy Ghost*”.

*WHAT IS REVIVAL?*

In my few years of walking with the Lord, one of the few misunderstood terminologies I grew up hearing in the church is "Revival". Another one is the word "Apostolic/Apostle". I see things like " Apostolic invasion/ covering". Let's save this for another time.  As touching revival, while growing up in the faith, what we were made to understand is that our week long prayer program was revival. The posters and banners reads, "Revival Revival Revival !". The evangelists featured mostly in those programs. Little did I know that true revival is not a program but an inner working of the Spirit in the lives of men that produces kingdom fruit. *Revival need no advertisement or media publicity because it announces itself and people( believers&unbelievers) will see it. The flames of revival cannot be hidden because all it produces are burning men*.  From the simple English meaning of the word 'Revival', it means to strengthen something that is weak. The thing you want to revive once had a form of vibrancy and liveliness but now has become weak. True revival is an awakening of those that are slumbering, weak and weary.



Robert Coleman defined Christian revival as  "the awakening or quickening of God's people to their true nature and purpose." On the issue of revival, Charles Finney says it is the return of the Church from her backslidings, and the conversion of sinners. True revival will set the backsliders on fire once again and will bring about massive conversion of the lost because the revived believers will go all out to the harvest field to win souls for the Lord. That's why I always say that one of the marks of a revived life is the willingness to engage in evangelism. *You cannot be truly revived and not go all out for Jesus or not partake in missions in one way or the other. If they're still begging you to evangelize and be part of  God's end time missions, you need  spiritual revival*. When we call for praise Jamz/concert, you will see a mammoth crowd in attendance but when we tell the same people to present themselves for evangelism, one may not see up to 10%. When the early church caught the fire of revival, they went from house-to-house preaching the Gospel: *"And every day, in the Temple and from house to house, they continued to teach and preach this message: “Jesus is the Messiah*”, Acts 5:42 NLT


The church is what it is today because we lost the fire of revival. In church history, when revival broke out among believers, the nation was sanitized because the light of believers shined so much that darkness was dispelled. One of the outcomes of the revival in Pennsylvania at the turn of the century was that the cells and prisons became empty because there were no criminals. A corrupt and decadent nation is a mirror of a weak church.  You and me need true revival, the church need true revival. Our souls need to be hot and burning for Christ in true righteousness and holiness. The love of many are waxing cold already, negative endtime prophecies are being fulfilled right in our very eyes. I hear Jesus saying: *"Be watchful, and strengthen the things, which remain, that are ready to die: for I have not found thy works perfect before God*", Rev3:2.

*PRAYER*: Father, revive me. Let your church experience true revival in Jesus name.

DEVOTIONAL 0429 | Source start line: 39421
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
2/22/22, 5:34 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: ProjectSave Devotional
Tuesday
22nd February, 2022.

*UNDERSTANDING BIBLICAL REVIVAL* Pt.2

Acts 2:37 *Now when they heard this, they were pricked in their heart, and said unto Peter and to the rest of the apostles, Men and brethren, what shall we do. 38 Then Peter said unto them, Repent, and be baptized every one of you in the name of Jesus Christ for the remission of sins, and ye shall receive the gift of the Holy Ghost*”.

*IT STARTS WITH REPENTANCE*

In American revival history, there was a woman named Maria Woodworth Etter. She was mightily used by God during the revival of her era. I read that whenever she was preaching, the first thing that happened to people is a strong conviction about their sinfulness after which the people will repent in deep groanings. At other times, some fell into trances and had visions of eternity. This revival swept her city clean of sinners to the point where police said they had no criminal cases to handle any longer. This was sustained for years. I desire this type of revival. What about Charles G.Finney? This man's ministry was characterized by large scale repentance in cities and towns. It was as though he had the anointing to make a whole city repent. I hear of city taking anointing by preachers of today yet what is implied is not large scale repentance like Finney's but gathering of crowd into human empires.



What do you think is the first fruit of revival? Often people think true revival is all about  the miraculous while some think it is about gathering crowd. Have you forgotten so soon that the Jews who left Egypt( in large numbers) saw raw miracles yet their hearts were hardened and still they longed to return to Egypt at a time in their journey. That goes to show that crowd and miracles aren't the perfect yardstick for measuring revival though miracles happen in revivals.  *Revival begins with genuine repentance and turning to the Lord in sincerity*. It starts with the realization of how far man is from God and the willingness to make things right with Him. In the book of Acts 2:37, when the people listened to Peter's sermon, they said, *"...Men and brethren, what shall we do*".? Peter didn't make altar call, they were the one who asked for the way to the altar. In response( vs38), Peter said, *"...Repent, and be baptized every one of you in the name of Jesus Christ for the remission of sins, and ye shall receive the gift of the Holy Ghost*”.


*True revival will not happen until we repent genuinely and see same happen in the life of others*. True revival reveals the holiness of God through men. That's the beauty of revival. God will not send His raw presence and power on a people who are conveniently living in sin rather He will sweep their hearts clean first and then make available His raw presence. *True revival starts with a burning heart that hates sin and loves God*. Falling under anointing is not revival, anyone can fall under anointing and still rise up into a life of sin. The media hype and stage lightening is not a sign of revival. *One can have fame and not have flame*. Anywhere there's revival, the first fruit we see is true repentance. As you pray for revival ( personal & Corporate), the first thing the Holy Spirit will prompt in you is the desire to make things right with God. That's the foundation upon which other things will be built.


*PRAYER*: Father, let my life and the church experience true revival in Jesus name

DEVOTIONAL 0430 | Source start line: 39469
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
2/23/22, 5:59 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: PROJECTSAVE DEVOTIONAL
Wednesday,
23rd February, 2022.

*UNDERSTANDING BIBLICAL REVIVAL* Pt.3

Psalms85:6 Will you not revive us again, that your people may rejoice in you?

*BIRTHING REVIVAL*


The famous Oke Oye revival in Ilesha in 1930 is one of the most notable revival in sub Saharan Africa. In 1918, some men who saw the lukewarmness of the brethren and the pitiable state of the church in Nigeria especially South West began to pray. They formed a prayer group in Ijebu Ode. Most of them were members of the Anglican communion. They prayed& fasted to see true revival. Unfortunately, they were opposed by the authority of the church because of their belief in the gifts of the Spirit and they were seen as over zealous people who weren't in sync with the church that had become cold and rutualistic. Sometimes, the first opposition against revival comes from within. In 1928, God began to stir a young man in the person of Joseph Ayo Babalola. His prayer life caught fire and he was moved by God to preach the message of repentance in his hometown warning them against idolatry. God prepared him in the place of prayer for about 2 years until 1930 when the revival broke out. 1930 revival was the answer to the cry of the prayers that started in 1918.

*Revival is incomplete without prayer. The prayer of saints birth revival and the prayer of revived men sustain revival*. In history of revivals all over the world and wherever it may have happened, I've not heard of a prayer less revival. Some persons think God will burst out on His people at will without some persons having laboured in prayers. It doesn't work that way. Even if God want to work in the midst of His people to revive them, one of the things He look for are praying men. *God will do no such thing as revival unless He finds praying men. Revival is birth through prayer and it is sustained through prayer*. The most famous revival in church history is the Azusa street revival. This revival was a revival of righteousness, it swept souls off hell and brought them into the kingdom en masse yet it was not without the prayer of the saints. Some persons spent nights praying with their heads in crates asking God for revival. Thereafter, America saw God.


If you desire true revival in your life and church, you must be ready to pay the price of prayer. *The good thing about revival is that it also produces praying men*. However, you will not pray well for revival until you see the need for it. It takes God to make you see the need for revival. Those who prayed for revival have some things in common. They are men and women who saw how the church had deviated from the teachings of Christ and the Apostles and are eager to see the church return to the place of purity and power. Can you see the lukewarmness, coldness and backsliding state of the church? If you look at yourself and consider  how you started your walk with the Lord, can you adjudge yourself as "still on fire"? Do you still have passion for souls like before? How far with your prayer life? Do you hate sin like Jesus did or now you're pampering sin and making excuses for it? What about your love for the brethren?/The moment you realize how far you've deviated from the holy path you once trod, you need to ask God for revival. I'm convinced that the church of today need a strong revival. Shall we pray.


*PRAYER*: Father, revive me. Lord revive your church in Jesus name.

DEVOTIONAL 0431 | Source start line: 39591
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
2/24/22, 5:37 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: PROJECTSAVE DEVOTIONAL
Thursday,
24th February,  2022.


*UNDERSTANDING BIBLICAL REVIVAL* Pt.4


Psalms85:6 Will you not revive us again, that your people may rejoice in you?


*REVIVAL STIRS PASSION FOR EVANGELISM& MISSIONS*


During the revival that broke out on Nigerian campuses in the 70s and early 80s, it was recorded that young men and women who were part of that revival developed an unusual passion for evangelism. The mission movement in Nigeria is a product of that revival. Some of those we call fathers in missions were products of that revival. Though most of them are old today but the fire of missions is still in their bones. That era witnessed sending out of young men and women to the mission fields of Africa and pioneering of mission agencies that are still with us till date. The prophecy of Pa S.G. Elton as regards missions and evangelism came to pass when young Christian corpers in different states gathered to pray and evangelize their states unknown to most of them. The fire of revival they brought from various campuses ignited a movement that is devoted to rural evangelism till date. That generation is still asking why there is no revival in our generation especially on our campuses.


*We can't say "Revival is here" when the passion to reach the lost is absent*. One of the fruits of revival is evangelism and missions. The revival of the early church stirred men to evangelize. Acts 5:46 says,  *"Day after day, in the temple courts and from house to house, they never stopped teaching and proclaiming the good news that Jesus is the Messiah*". In fact, when persecution arose and the church scattered abroad, they didn't stop preaching the Gospel: *"Those who had been scattered preached the word wherever they went*", Acts 8:4. It wasn't convenient to preach in time of persecution and displacement yet that revived did. What excuses are we giving today for not wanting to go all out for Christ? *One can easily tell the spiritual state of a Christian/ church by checking their love and passion for soul-winning*". No true revived church or believer will neglect evangelism and missions or relegate it to mere talks without a corresponding action. Do not confuse this evangelism for preaching church or inviting people to attend church services.


In some circles, the definition of evangelism is when they distribute church fliers inviting people to special services. That's good but that's not evangelism. Bible evangelism entails preaching Christ and eternal salvation to the lost and making them members of a living church. *It is possible to invite someone to Church and the person will not be in Christ but it is impossible for someone to be in Christ and not be in church*. The revived early church and Apostles evangelized Asia minor in two years: *"This went on for two years, so that all the Jews and Greeks who lived in the province of Asia heard the word of the Lord*", Acts 19:10. When true revival comes, we'll cover the whole of Africa in less than 10 years. The church have the resources and spiritual man power but not enough of revived men to undertake the task. We are responsible for the multitudes going to hell in droves especially in unreached areas where people haven't heard about Christ. Oh! That the Lord will revive the church for the plentiful end time harvest.




*PRAYER*: Father, revive me. Father revive your church in Jesus name.

*UNDERSTANDING BIBLICAL REVIVAL* Pt.5


Psalms85:6 Will you not revive us again, that your people may rejoice in you?


*DEVOTION TO THE WORD*


Till the time of this writing, churches in China can't piblicly declare their faith because the state has declared the Christian faith "Religio Illicita" ( Illegal religion). Publicly claiming to be a Christian will attract persecution even unto death. However, people keep coming to the Lord in large numbers through strategic evangelism and mission work going on there. The churches also operate secretly in unknown destinations hence we call the China church "Underground Church". The Christians in China are one of the most revived believers on the surface of the earth presently. They tick almost all the boxes for the attributes of a revived church. I heard the story of an American preacher who was invited to preach in one of the underground churches in China.
When he got there, he preached for about 3 hours. After his message, his listeners requested he continue his message because they were used to listening to teachings that lasted up to 12 hours. This American preacher was surprised at their devotion to the word of God. A number of these Chinese Christians don't have bibles because of the laws against it but now, they met someone from Christian America who should know all of the bible yet wasn't willing to teach it extensively. The testimony is the same in all Chinese churches. When revival comes, the people of God will be devoted to private study of the word and shall not be tired of listening to accurate teachings of God's word. These are days where accurate scriptural teaching is becoming uncommon. In some circles, bible study is weak and Sunday services are done in a rush to accommodate the next service.



When revival broke out in the early church, Acts 2:42 says, *"THEY DEVOTED THEMSELVES TO THE APOSTLES’ TEACHING and to fellowship, to the breaking of bread and to prayer*". That was the reality of the early church. *A revived church is a teaching church not a place for nice cool talks. No time for jamboree*. Early church history says that in the time of the Apostles and sub Apostolic era, teachings lasted. 3-4 hours. These are doctrinal teachings aimed at maturing the believer. *Do you still love deep teachings or you love piece meal sermons and churches where you'll get spiritual fast food? What about your private study of God's word*?
The revived early believers had strong private bible study culture such that they had time to check for the accuracy of whatever they were taught: *"Now the Berean Jews were of more noble character than those in Thessalonica, for they received the message with great eagerness and examined the Scriptures every day to see if what Paul said was true*", Acts17:11. How often do you check the teachings and preachings you hear for their accuracy or you just take everything in hook line and sinker? *God is raising a generation like the Berean believers who will be diligent in study of God's word and not rely on what 'papa' said but the truth of God's word. They'll ask questions and search for the truth. They'll be inclined towards deep teachings of scriptures and not piece meal.  It is happening already but I pray it spreads like wildfire all over.  You need revival, I need revival and the church need revival.


*PRAYER*: Father revive me, stir in me a hunger for the word in Jesus name. Lord, revive your church.

*UNDERSTANDING BIBLICAL REVIVAL* Pt.6

Psalms85:6 Will you not revive us again, that your people may rejoice in you?

*REVIVAL BREEDS UNITY*


I have spent time reading books on revival and listening to those who were part of the revival of yore. A recurring story in most revivals is the unity of brethren. The revival of the 70s& early 80s in Nigeria knew no denomination. Brethren saw one another as brethren not as members of a particular church. Being a Christian and having a solid relationship with the Lord was more important than the name or denomination of church.  To understand revival and the gains that come with it, we need to study the early church and revival history. *Whenever God brings revival to His people especially on a corporate level, the wall of division is broken and Christ takes the centre*. Christ still desires to take the centre in the body of Christ not any church founder or denomination.


Take a look at the early church:
*ALL THE BELIEVERS WERE UNITED IN HEART AND MIND. And they felt that what they owned was not their own, so they shared everything they had*. Acts 4:32 NLT. You may not understand this scripture when compared with what obtains today but that's the reality of revival. Today, one of the challenges we have in the body of Christ is lack of unity. We are fighting one another instead of fighting our common enemy- Satan. The enemy has infiltrated our rank and file and churches are now competing instead of forming a united front to win souls. I have seen believers fight each other mentioning the names of their General overseers and bragging over the "grace" they carry. I can't imagine believers in the early church fighting each another and saying, "The God of my Pastor, Peter the rock, the man that spits fire will deal with you". It sounds weird. We can't plunder hell with that attitude.



Stop seeing your church as better than every other church. Some persons do not believe other pastors are genuine and truly called into the ministry except their Pastor. This is the  attitude of a lukewarm believer. The disciples of Jesus saw a man casting out devils and they felt threatened because their expectation was that such should have happened from their camp: *“Master,” said John, “we saw someone driving out demons in your name and we tried to stop him, because he is not one of us*”( Lk9:49). Listen to what Jesus said, *"Do not stop him,” Jesus said, “for whoever is not against you is for you*”(vs 50). *A united devil will do more exploit than a divided Jesus*. I hope you understand what I'm saying here? Christ himself can't be divided but when His followers are divided, Satan will overrun them and get his evil agenda fulfilled. Ask the Lord to give you a heart that loves the brethren and His kingdom. Ask for revival.


*PRAYER*:Father, revive us and restore your church to the place of genuine unity in Jesus name

*UNDERSTANDING BIBLICAL REVIVAL* Pt.7


Psalms85:6 Will you not revive us again, that your people may rejoice in you?


*THE MIRACULOUS*


Revival is not complete until we see the miraculous power of God at work. Here, we're talking about real, genuine and confirmable miracles not an arranged or assumed miracle. The early church was a church of signs and wonders even though that was not their message neither did they advertise miracles as a bait to bring people yet miracles happened because true miracles are produced by the divine power of God to establish the Lordship of Christ in the hearts of men. The bible says, *"Fellow Israelites, listen to this: Jesus of Nazareth was a man accredited by God to you by miracles, wonders and signs, which God did among you through him, as you yourselves know*", Acts 2:42. Hallelujah!!!. The miracles that happen when revival breaks out will establish and enthrone the Lordship of Jesus Christ in the hearts of men.
The miraculous is not something to advertise a man, no it is not. When revival comes, the miracles will point men to Jesus for salvation. That's why there were records of more people getting saved than there were those getting miracles because salvation of soul was the focus: *"Yet more and more people believed and were brought to the Lord—crowds of both men and women. As a result of the apostles’ work, sick people were brought out into the streets on beds and mats so that Peter’s shadow might fall across some of them as he went by*", Acts5:14-15. From the scripture above, after people saw and heard what happened to Ananias&Sapphira, they believed. That is, they gave their lives to Jesus. The next verse told us how miracles happened through Peter's shadow.



Apostle Peter didn't start "Shadow Healing Services". He knew all was just an act of the Holy Spirit. Their commitment was to the Gospel and bible doctrines. Until we have a biblical perspective to miracles, we may not experience the outbreak of the raw manifestation of God's power the way it should. Oh! How we need that kind of Apostolic revival today! The world doesn't believe in the living Jesus, one of the ways multitudes can be convinced about His Lordship when they see His acts in clear and undoubted ways. A new season is upon us, we need revival. A fresh outpouring.  Ask the Lord of revival to send revival.


*PRAYER*: Father, let there be a revival in my life and in the church in Jesus name.

*RAISE THEM FOR THEIR FUTURE HOMES*

Isaiah54:13 All your children will be taught by the LORD, and great will be their peace.


I read the post of a young man on facebook who beat his wife. His complaint was that his wife reported him to some concerned authorities and he felt bad about it. What caught my attention in his post was when he said his father use to beat his mother and she doesn't report to anyone. He saw his wife's action as lack of endurance and he compared her to his mother who doesn't complain about his father's constant battery. Apparently, from my judgement, he didn't see anything wrong with beating his wife. Unconsciously, he picked up that habit or act of beating a woman from his dad. I'm sure his dad didn't coach him to beat his wife while growing but he picked up that negative stuff from his dad through observation. He walked in the footsteps of his dad because that's what his father modeled to him.




Research shows that what most people do in their marriage is a result of what they saw their parents doing while growing. *A man that beats his wife is unconsciously teaching his sons that women are meant to be dealt with physically when they misbehave. A girl child may think nagging and verbal abuse is normal because that's what mum does to dad*.  Unfortunately, one would think such anomaly doesn't occur among Christians but some believers and even pastors are exhibiting in their marriages the negatives they picked in their parent's marriages while growing up. Some of those in this category may be nice to outsiders but they're beasts at home.  *We have untrained parents trying to train their own children*. Bad marriage was modeled to them and they're modeling same to their children. *In the nearest future, the world may experience chains of turbulent and broken marriages more than we're seeing today because the present generation of parents aren't modeling to their children what a good home should be*.

You are not reading this devotional by accident. God doesn't want you to be part of rot. *Your son, that little boy will be someone's husband tomorrow, raise him well*. As a father, he is learning fatherhood and family leadership from you first hand. No other marriage seminar or book can be greater than the marriage you model to your children. If you beat your wife and starve her of love, care and attention, he may likely do same to his future wife except God intervenes. *Your daughter, that little girl will be someone's wife tomorrow, raise her well*. If all you do is nag and tongue lash your husband at the slightest provocation, you've taught her to do same. If you don't model submission and motherhood to her, she may not learn it anywhere not even in books. She needs a good model. *Sometimes, bad husbands & wives are products of bad/ defective parenting. They take after their parents. They become what their parents model to them*. The potential husband or wife of someone lives under your roof right now, raise them for their future home by being a good and godly model they can take after.


*PRAYER*: Father, I won't fail in my duty as a parent in Jesus name.

*BE CONSISTENT IN YOUR PRAYER DEVOTION*.

Rom12:12 rejoicing in hope, being patient in tribulation, being constant in prayer, BLB


The world once saw the exploits of a man who shook hell and enthroned righteousness. He was at the forefront of a revival that made alcohol not to be sold in a city. 100 years after he left, liquor wasn't sold. His name is John Wesley. John Wesley (the father of Methodism) was a man whose life of faith and devotion drew many to the Lord. His prayer life was exemplary and worth emulating. *Wesley spent two hours every morning in prayers in his closet off the bedroom. That small room was known as the 'The Powerhouse of Methodism'*. That man shook his world for Christ.

Many have read and heard about John Wesley and they desire to do the exploits of John Wesley. However, they are not consistent in the place of prayer like John Wesley. He was consistent in his prayer life. Some persons enter a season of fervent prayer but when they don't see the manifestation of the supernatural, they relent. *To be used mightily by God, your consistency in prayer must be established. To be much for God, you have to be much with God.* You cannot do greater works until you pay the price for greater works in the place of consistent prayer. Jesus was a man of consistent prayer and he spent time alone praying for hours( Luke5:16). Greater works is meant for prayer addicts and not those that lazy around. I'm yet to see a man that shook his world for Christ who is not a prayer addict because there is no other place where they can draw strength from except in the place of prayer. The psalmist says, *"Seven times a day do I praise you because of your righteous judgments*", Psl119:164. This is a strong prayer culture worth emulating.


Sometimes ago, I read about the late Yongi Choo. Before one of his services, he was to join his friends at the table for lunch at 2 O'clock but he went to pray in tongues for four hours. He told them that he needed four hours with God in the place of prayer before evening service. How long do you pray before ministering in your evening or mid week services?  *Until you are consistent in the place of prayer, greater works is not in view*. His exploits in ministry was traceable to his consistency in the place of prayer. *There's a generation that is not praying but they're criticizing those that are praying. They give more attention to the glitz and glamour of ministry, love stage light but have no light on their prayer altar. Little wonder why there's no revival*. Sometimes, before God will launch us into certain dimensions, he may need to see decades of consistency. Most of the exploits of Kathryn Khulman happened in the last ten years of her life. She was consistent for years before she launched into the divine. In the school of prayer, we don't go on holiday.

PRAYER: Father, baptize me with the Spirit of ceaseless prayer In Jesus name.

*LOOK TO HIM FOR HELP NOT EGYPT*

Isaiah 31:1 What sorrow awaits those who look to Egypt for help, trusting their horses, chariots, and charioteers and depending on the strength of human armies instead of looking to the Lord, the Holy One of Israel. NLT


I heard the story of a preacher in a locality who spoke against occultism. He was known to be fiery in His preaching against the occult. He took Ill and all they did proved abortive. Someone suggested they should seek the help of occultic people in the area so he agreed. When he got there, the occult master was shocked to see their greatest critic coming for help. He agreed to help on the condition that he would recant all he ever said against them. That was a tough one but he did. Immediately he acceded to that request, he died. That's how the devil mess up those who go to Egypt for help.


Egypt is symbolic of the world. It is an anti God system created by Satan but run by men loyal to him. They have their principles and strategies of getting things done. Most times, their help seems like a better option but it leads to destruction. On several occasions when I board public transport, I see posters of Indian temples telling people to come for love charm, contract charm and other types of charms that will make life better for them. These people have a high patronage even from among those that claim to be children of God. In time of challenges, where do you seek help from? Any help you receive outside the principles and power of the living God is a help from Egypt. *"Woe to them that go down to Egypt for help; and stay on horses, and trust in chariots, because they are many; and in horsemen, because they are very strong;...*", Isa31:1. Sometimes, seeking help from Egypt may not necessarily entail diabolism. It could be a compromise of one's faith to get something done.

Some young ladies have been told to lower their standard of purity to get a husband. Some actually got their husbands that way but one can tell the kind of husband they got- a man without godly principles. There are counsels of Egypt today. You cannot look up to God and be put to shame neither is there any woe or sorrow for those whose gaze is fixed on the holy one of Israel. Psalm34:5 says, *"Those who look to him for help will be radiant with joy; no shadow of shame will darken their faces*", Hallelujah!!! The help you need is not with any man neither can it be found in Egypt but in God. All you need do is to focus on him alone. Don't subscribe to options. I love the illustration God gave to Bishop David Oyedepo. God  instructed him to look up with one eye and look down with the other. He couldn't do it. That shows the impossibility of looking up to God and looking elsewhere for help at the same time. Let your motto be "If it is not from God, it is not good for me". Our help comes from above.


*PRAYER*: Father, help me. I subscribe to your help. I refuse the help of Egypt in Jesus name.

DEVOTIONAL 0438 | Source start line: 40050

3/3/22, 3:12 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: PROJECTSAVE DEVOTIONAL
Thursday,
3rd March,  2022.


*FOR LOVE OR FOR EXTRINSIC REWARD*?


1Cor 2:9 However, as it is written: “What no eye has seen, what no ear has heard, and what no human mind has conceived”— the things God has prepared for those who love him— NIV


Psychologists believe that there are two motivating factors that determine why people do what they do. The first motivation is what they call "Intrinsic Motivation" while the second is called "Extrinsic Motivation". The former involves doing things or engaging in certain activities out of love and pleasure. Love and pleasure is seen as the reward itself. The latter involves doing things because of factors outside oneself. Here, it could be because of money, job, promotion or approval. In life, we all do what we do for either of these two factors including preaching/ ministry. Should that surprise you? Paul the Apostle says, *"It is true that some preach Christ out of envy and rivalry, but others out of goodwill. THE LATTER DO SO OUT OF LOVE( Intrinsic Motivation), knowing that I am put here for the defense of the gospel*", Phil 1:15-16 NIV. There's a semblance of intrinsic and extrinsic motivation in the verse above. Paul affirmed that his preaching was borne out of love.


Another scripture that buttresses this point further can be found in 2Cor2:17: *"YOU SEE, WE ARE NOT LIKE THE MANY HUCKSTERS WHO PREACH FOR PERSONAL PROFIT( Extrinsic Motivation). We preach the word of God with sincerity and with Christ’s authority, knowing that God is watching us*", NLT. This is the one million dollar question I have for you today, "Should we serve God for the Intrinsic Motivation or the Extrinsic Motivation"? Should our service to God, giving( tithe&offering), support for missions, commitment, church attendance, prayer, fasting and devotion be tied to the physical reward that satisfies our earthly longing? There are messages that encourages people to serve God and His kingdom for extrinsic gratification. Such messages present kingdom service, giving and other forms of commitment to God as an exchange platform to meet human needs. Most people in this category becomes weary when their needs aren't met. Some even backslide. Haven't you heard of people who turned their backs on God because they didn't get the result they wanted after they served, gave and committed their time to God? If they had done those out of love, I'm sure they'll be standing regardless of result. We often forget that the goodness of God can't be bought but mostly an act of God's grace. Some of the characters in the faith hall of fame were so much in love with God to the point where they died even without seeing the promise fulfilled.



Hey! What about being sold out to God and serving Him wholeheartedly out of a loving devotion even if no physical reward is attached? *Spiritual babes( regardless of the number of years spent in the faith) serve the Lord for the perks not for love*. What if God chooses not to answer that prayer, give you a spouse, a child, etc, will you still serve Him wholeheartedly from a loving heart? *Our service, giving and devotion may not be acceptable to God until love for God becomes the motivation and driving force*. While in Paul's day, some were preaching for extrinsic gratification, Paul made it clear that he was in it for love whether he gets honorarium or not. The persecutions, beatings and imprisonments of Paul did not deter him from pursuing his ministry because he was motivated by love not the extrinsic gratification of comfort and what money can buy. *When you mature in spiritual things, you'll begin to do things in the kingdom because of the love you have for God even when no one says "thank you" or gives a physical reward*.


*PRAYER*: Father, I receive the grace to do the things I do for you out of love in Jesus name.

DEVOTIONAL 0439 | Source start line: 40123

3/4/22, 6:16 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: PROJECTSAVE DEVOTIONAL
Friday,
4th March, 2022.


*HIGHER DIMENSION OF LOVE AND SERVANTHOOD*


Romans 1:1 Paul, a servant of Christ Jesus, called to be an apostle and set apart for the gospel of God NIV


Paul called himself a servant of Christ. The Greek word "Doulos" was translated as "Servant" in the opening text though it is an imperfect translation. Merely reading that text, one may not get the full import of the servanthood Paul meant. If we are to interpret "Doulos" properly, it should be rendered as "Bondservant". Now, there's a world of difference between 'Servants' and 'bondservants'. The Jews know the difference and Paul does as well. In Exodus 21:1-7, God told the Israelites that if they buy a Hebrew slave, the slave should not serve more than six years. On the seventh year he/she must be freed. However, if the slave refuses to go because he love his master, he must must be presented before God and his ear must be pierced with an awl at the doorpost. After that, such a slave becomes a bondservant. That is, he will serve his master till death, no freedom. He beomes a property of his master including his wife, children and all he owns.



It was from this background of understanding that Paul called himself a servant, that is, a bondservant. Rarely will you find that expression in other non Pauline epistles except in 2Pet1:1. Only Paul and Peter called themselves "Bondservants". They are saying, "we love the Lord, we don't want to be free from his yoke. We become his property with all we have till death". We often read the old testament at the surface not trying to find out what the real meaning of what we read is in the new testament. *The physical bondservanthood in the old testament was just a type and shadow of the spiritual bondservanthood God expects of His children under the new covenant. It is a life of being sold out totally to GOD*. However, I discovered that the drive to submit for bondservanthood is " LOVE". Only servants who love their master stay as bondservants.



*The test of your love for God will reflect in your submission to serve and let go those personal cravings, ambitions and desires that doesn't align with His perfect will for your life*. The fact that you dictate for God at all times and insist on what you want without considering His will is a proof that you're not His bondservant. *Bondservants don't negotiate instructions with their master*. We often claim to love God especially in our songs. Some even shed tears in worship when singing "love songs" to God but when God makes a demand for their money, time, skill and intellect, they give excuses or give them out grudgingly or partially. While it appears some let go certain things for the Lord willingly, it could just be because of the expectation for extrinsic reward not necessarily because of love. *Bondservants don't serve for perks and benefits. They own nothing, their master owns everything*. I am not preaching or teaching as a man that is there, I'm on the journey with you. God is calling us to a genuine love walk that submits for bondservanthood on the love lane.



*PRAYER*: Father, I receive the grace to love you. You own me, take my life and let it be consecrated to your will totally in Jesus name.

*UNSCHOOLED BUT SUPERNATURAL*

Acts4:13 *Now when they saw the boldness of Peter and John, and perceived that they were unlearned and ignorant men, they marvelled; and they took knowledge of them, that they had been with Jesus*.


Sometimes ago, I met a prophet of God. He is the eight child of his mother and the only son in a polygamous family of seven wives. He was in his mother's womb for 3 years and was born with some teeth. He grew up in a battle ground of polygamous tussle between wives. That family problem impeded his schooling so he stopped schooling in primary three. He couldn't read and write because of his academic background. When he came to Christ, he loved the Lord and the Holy Spirit taught him how to read and write. Today, the man can speak good English, read and write. He is preaching everywhere doing exploit for Jesus.



Peter and John stood before the council after they performed a great miracle. The council members most of whom were the legal scholars of that time were surprised when they heard them speak because they were unschooled but they were supernatural. Then they concluded that they had been with Jesus because there was no other way they could have been outstanding. The secret of exploit in the kingdom is not the number of degree you acquire. How I wish the wise and intelligentsias can explain the things of the Spirit with scientific wisdom.

However, nothing beats staying with Christ and learning under Him. *Degrees can't unlock the supernatural*. If any man any where in the world can stay with Jesus like Peter and John, regardless of tribe, background and education, they will do exploit. Education is good but still cannot take the place of being with Jesus Christ. The life of Christ in us is priceless. The difference maker is Christ. *As we fellowship with Christ in His word and in prayer, a transformation would occur in us that would make the world marvel. Christ is the secret behind kingdom exploit*.


PRAYER: Jesus, I want to know you more, to be with you and walk with you till your divinity swallow my humanity in Jesus name.

3/5/22, 11:05 PM - Lanre Oyeleke: ProjectSave Devotional

*SIGHT WITHOUT INSIGHT*


1Corinthians 15:52 In a moment, in the twinkling of an eye, at the last trump: for the trumpet shall sound, and the dead shall be raised incorruptible, and we shall be changed.



Several years back, I read the story of a blind lyricist who composed thousands of hymns. Fanny Jane van Alstyne (nee Crosby) was an American poet, lyricist and composer. She wrote over 8,000 hymns and gospel songs with over 100 million copies printed despite being blind shortly after birth. She was known as the 'Queen of Gospel Song Writers'. Despite the fact that she lived almost all her life in a blind state, her music ministry was impactful and her heart longed for eternity.



Some of Crosby's best known songs include 'Pass Me Not, O Gentle Saviour'! Blessed Assurance', 'To God Be the Glory' and many others. One of the lines of 'Blessed Assurance' reads, "Visions of rapture now burst on my sight". "One of the worst thing in life is to have sight without vision*. There are two eye sights God gave to every man. However, we are conversant with the physical eye sight that make up our five senses. *While it is not good to be physically blind but it is much worse to be blind spiritually and not see the visions of God. Vision in context implies insight*. It is God's desire for you and me to have insight into spiritual realities.


*The reason for our weak and impassionate pursuit on spiritual matters is largely because we do not have insight about those things*. For example, a man that has insight about heaven will long for it, a man that has insight about prayer and the word will hardly miss prayer&word time because he'll live as though his total being depend on those things. Truly, it does. Apostle Paul prayed in Ephesians 1:17 saying, *"asking God, the glorious Father of our Lord Jesus Christ, to give you spiritual wisdom and INSIGHT so that you might grow in your knowledge of God*".  Nothing beats having deep insight about spiritual things. Without physical sight, Fanny  saw the visions of rapture with angels descending. She had insight about a place where people with physical eye sight couldn't see. This is a call to deeper fellowship with God. The closer you are to God, the deeper your insight( vision) will be.


*PRAYER*: Father, give me insight into spiritual realities in Jesus name.

*DEAL WITH CONDEMNATION AND GUILT*

Heb8:12 And I will forgive their wickedness, and I will never again remember their sins.” NLT


Several years ago, I opened my heart to some materials that affected me negatively. I felt condemned after reading those materials because they were filled with legalistic ideas nothing about God's grace and mercy. My past was brought to fore and I saw how guilty and helpless I was. If there's any battle I fought sternly in my life, it is the battle against condemnation and guilt. I almost ran mad because I kept hearing accusing voices and I felt condemned and unworthy of God totally. I lost the conviction about the cleansing power in the blood of Jesus and my thoughts about God's forgiving power came under intense attack of doubt and fear. I was carrying too heavy a load around. I was weakened spiritually.



Thank God for His word. My liberation came through the word of God. I read the opening text above and I took it personal. I confessed it severally till it sank into my spirit. Is the devil condemning you and taking you on a guilt trip based on your past? Simply let him know that you have been forgiven completely because God's promise is that He would forgive your shortcomings completely as long as genuine repentance is constant. I've read and heard some extreme messages about forgiveness. Some persons believe forgiveness is not easy to obtain so you have to do somethings to obtain it. I've heard those who prescribe fasting and prayer as a requirement to obtain forgiveness. While fasting and prayer is good as a spiritual exercise, I didn't see anywhere it was recommended under the new covenant as a prerequisite for forgiveness.
Proverbs28:13 says, *"He that covereth his sins shall not prosper: but whoso CONFESSETH and FORSAKETH them shall have mercy*". Confession and forsaking of sins gets the job done. No need for special sacrifice. Another scripture you should not forget is 1John1:9, *"But if we CONFESS our sins to him, he is faithful and just to forgive us our sins and to cleanse us from all wickedness*". When we don't believe in God's word enough, the devil will make us doubt scriptures like these and use our emotional feelings to get us. Believing God's word about your forgiveness and righteous status doesn't depend on how you feel but about His word. No matter how bad your past was, a drop of Christ's blood has a strong cleansing power. Throw the feelings of guilt away and stick to your faith in the scripture above.

CONFESSING: I am the righteousness of God in Christ Jesus, I rise above condemnation, shame and guilt. I am in God's love camp and I refuse perpetual guilt and shame In Jesus name. Amen

3/8/22, 5:28 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: ProjectSave Devotional
Tuesday
8th March, 2022.

*LOOK, THINK& ACT RIGHT*

Prov 2:11 Discretion shall preserve thee, understanding shall keep thee:

Sometimes ago, I took my wife and children out to have a nice time somewhere away from home. We visit the place once in a while because my children love the place. In that facility, there's a shopping mall, recreation facilities for adults and children, cinema and other facilities.  When we got to the place, I saw a section where they wrote "Restaurant &Bar". Actually, the restaurant aspect caught my eye and I didn't bother to interpret or ponder on the word "Bar" especially what could happen in such a place  in the public. More so, that's not my first time in that public facility just that up until that time, I haven't entered into that section where they wrote "Restaurant &, Bar". As we entered, the atmosphere was different from other sections in that mall.

We managed to get a seat. We tried  placing our order but we couldn't because the calibre of people we saw there didn't look like us. The ladies looked like prostitutes, the guys look weird, more like internet fraudsters and the place was rowdy and loud with heavy music, the type I don't listen to. The straw that broke the camel's back was when a young lady smoked from a long pipe. I have never seen that all my life. Some guys did as well. Later, I was told that's what they call shisha. That very moment it dawned on me that I was in the wrong place with my family, myself and wifey carried our children and went to our usual eatery. When we got there,  the difference was clear but where did I miss it aside not hearing from the Holy Spirit? I didn't look well and think well. I saw the inscription "Restaurant & Bar" but I didn't take my time to think about what could happen in a public bar. I assumed it was just a mere advert. The moment I saw "Bar", I should have given it a thought but I assumed it meant nothing until I entered.


*Sometimes, we miss it because we assume alot and throw common sense to the bin*. As they say, assumption is the lowest form of knowledge. Often, people take decisions as circumstances appear without taking time to think on what the outcome might be. *Sometimes, we solve a temporary problem but create a permanent long standing problem because we fail to look, think and act right. Do you need a prophet to tell you that a man who is slapping you in courtship will turn you to a punching bag in marriage? Are you still assuming that you'll be able to cope with his/ her corrosive temper tantrums in marriage? Look at that decision you're about to make critically, think about what the outcome might be and act right. If you do this often, there are relationships you won't enter while you'll break some. There are places you will not go let alone enter. *God didn't create our thinking faculties for decoration or design. He puts them there to make wise decisions*. Think so that you will not stink. Besides, don't forget that you have the mind of Christ, make good use of it at all times, it helps.



*CONFESSION*: I have the mind of Christ, I refuse to take the wrong decision. I will think with the mind of Christ, examine issues with the lens of the Spirit and act right in Jesus name.



info@projectsaveng.org
