*THE SPIRIT REALM*. Pt.1

2 Cor 12:2-3 I was caught up to the third heaven fourteen years ago. Whether I was in my body or out of my body, I don’t know—only God knows. that I was caught up to paradise and heard things so astounding that they cannot be expressed in words, things no human is allowed to tell.



Years back after an outreach, I was privileged to join other brethren to pull down a shrine in a remote village in Ghana. One of the items surrendered was an iron arrow and a carved snake. I was told that when these evil people want to harm their enemies, they'll shoot that iron arrow or speak to the carved snake to go harm their victims. Believe me, it works. We've heard of people who will cry from their sleep all of a sudden because of a sharp pain. They do  not understand where it is coming from and how they came about it. Spiritualist and those in the occult understand these things and how it works. Spiritists, the occults and those in the dark world understand this fully. That's why they can tell you certain mysteries and secrets that the ordinary person doesn't know. Haven't you seen those who will predict accurately what will happen tomorrow yet not by the Spirit of God?  The slave girl in Acts16 comes to mind.



A realm is an area of interest or activity. We are on earth living our daily lives and carrying out various activities. This space can be regarded as 'earthly realm'. For now, this is where our physical body operates. However, there is another realm that is more real and superior in organization than this earthly realm. This realm controls the earthly realm where we live and only those that understand the operations of this realm will be masters over the earthly realm. We call this realm the spirit realm. Often, we have heard the spirit realm controls the physical. That's a truism that is incontrovertible. Most of the things we see today have been concluded in the spirit realm before they manifest in the physical. The sicknesses, wars, volcano, air mishaps, road accidents and all the negatives we see have been settled in a realm where most of us aren't conscious about. If you've ever had a descriptive dream that came to pass the way you see it, you've been privileged to see into that realm.




*The realm of the spirit is one not two*.  It is a realm that is real with structures and organization. Satan and God operate in that realm. The holy angels of God and satanic angels and demons also operate in that realm but on different sides and with different goals.  *We cannot fully take charge over our lives here on earth unless we understand spiritual principles and secrets that unlock the spirit realm*. Apostle Paul enjoined that we live in the spirit: *"So I say, walk by the Spirit, and you will not gratify the desires of the flesh*", Gal5:16. Our walk should be a walk in the Spirit and by the Spirit. We have the advantage of the Holy Spirit to navigate the spirit realm and live in victory.


*CONFESSION*: I refuse to walk in the flesh. I operate from above to control my daily affairs in Jesus name. I reject the manifestation of evil works concluded over my life in the spirit realm in Jesus name.

*THE SPIRIT REALM*. Pt.4

There are things God will do without your prayers and mine but there so many other things He won't do until we pray. The good thing about the spirit realm is that we can influence the operations in that realm through prayer&intercession.


*CONFESSION*: I refuse to be a casualty of this warfare. I have been translated from darkness into the kingdom of light. I refuse to be defeated, greater is He that is in me than he that is in the world. I reign and rule. I am triumphant in Christ in Jesus name.

*THE SPIRIT REALM*. Pt.5


Rev 12:7 *Then there was war in heaven. Michael and his angels fought against the dragon and his angels*. NLT


*OPERATING SYSTEM*


In computer science, an operating system is a program that manages the functions of software and hardware. There's no computer that will function as desired without an operating system. *The operating system in the realm of the spirit that makes everything work is submission to authority*. In that realm, there's a high regard for superiors and hierarchy is recognized. When Lucifer wanted to breach this order by exalting his throne above God, he was deposed and thrown out of heaven:  *"For you said to yourself, ‘I will ascend to heaven and set my throne above God’s stars. I will preside on the mountain of the gods far away in the north. I will climb to the highest heavens and be like the Most High.’ Instead, you will be brought down to the place of the dead, down to its lowest depths*",
Isaiah 14:13-15 NLT.



In that realm, insubordination, pride and lack of submission is frowned against. It earned Lucifer a deposition. Another example is the story told in Jude talking about Satan disputing  the body of Moses with Michael:  *"But even Michael, one of the mightiest of the angels, did not dare accuse the devil of blasphemy, but simply said, “The Lord rebuke you!” (This took place when Michael was arguing with the devil about Moses’ body.)*" Jude 1:9 NLT. Did you notice the bible said Michael didn't accuse Satan of blasphemy? Yes he didn't. Why? Satan was an archangel in heaven likewise Michael. They were of the same order, rank and authority so it would be an act of disrespect for Michael to accuse Lucifer even though they weren't in the same kingdom any longer. However, to overcome Lucifer, Michael followed the operating principle in that realm by saying "The Lord rebuke you". Why? The LORD, was superior to them all and no one is strong enough to oppose Him. Lucifer had no option than to bow to the name of the Lord and allow Michael perform his duty. Hallelujah!!! That's another example in submission.



Demons know their superior and they submit. Don't forget that realm has rank and order. Everyone knows his limit.  *It is dangerous for a believer to be rebellious and insubordinate to authorities. It reduces spiritual authority and deposes one in the realm of the spirit. Demons know this and the moment they sight a rebellious believer, they deal accordingly*. Coming to the new testament, Paul gave us an insight on how to deal with the various  hierarchies in that realm to get what we desire: *"Wherefore God also hath HIGHLY EXALTED HIM, AND GIVEN HIM A NAME WHICH IS ABOVE EVERY NAME: that at the name of Jesus every knee should bow, of things IN HEAVEN, and things IN EARTH, and things under the earth; and that every tongue should confess that Jesus Christ is Lord, to the glory of God the Father*",(Philip2:9-11 KJV). That's deep. In that realm, they respect Jesus. On both side of the divide, He rank higher than all officers( demons& angels). That's why it is wrong to pray in the name of angels or any arch angels. Even Lucifer cannot stand His authority. You have that edge and advantage over demons because of the name of Jesus Christ.

*CONFESSION*: I run into the name of Jesus. I am on the winning side and I refuse to be defeated in Jesus name.

*THE SPIRIT REALM*. Pt.6

Psalms33:6 By the word of the LORD were the heavens made; and all the host of them by the breath of his mouth.

*LAWS OF OPERATION*( A)

I watched a Christian drama where a young man struggled to make a headway in life. He was jobless and indebted though a Christian. Having met with disappointment several times, he prayed wholeheartedly and the Lord revealed a demonic personality that has been taking record of all negative words he spoke and the ones spoken to him. Those words were used against him so that he'll remain stagnant in life. It was in the heat of our ordeal his mother told me what he told his friend. It almost happened as declared if not for God's mercy that prevailed. Is there anyone who still doubt the power of the spoken word?


In the realm of the spirit, spoken word is one of the tools for getting results. God had the picture of a beautiful heaven and earth in mind but these weren't created until He spoke them into existence. That's why creation account has phrases like "God said". Paul further shared with us in Rom4:17 how God made something out of nothing through the spoken word: *"as it is written: “I have made thee a father of many nations”), in the presence of Him whom he believed, even God, who quickeneth the dead and calleth those things which are not, as though they were*". In the spirit realm, as far as getting results is concerned, the spoken word is the major trading currency. Those that practice animism do same. They'll make incantations to send demons on errand. Without the spoken words, demons won't take instruction.


The Psalmist says, *"Praise the LORD, you his angels, you mighty ones who do his bidding, who obey his word*", Psalm103:20. Did you notice that angels act on God's word of command? Yes they do. God's angels will not work in your favour if your words oppose what they stand for except you speak words that are in line with God's word: *"Are not all angels ministering spirits sent to serve those who will inherit salvation?*", ( Heb1:14). Just as God's angels act on your words so do demons. What are you saying about your life. Your mouth should be filled with what God's word say about your life not what you feel. In that realm, words are trading currency. They're waiting for the next word you'll say to determine the line of action. Don't speak careless and vain words. To show how important words are, Jesus said in Mt12:36 *"But I tell you that everyone will have to give account on the day of judgment for every empty word they have spoken*". What are you saying?


*CONFESSION*: I speak life into my body, marriage, business and ministry. I cancel the effects if negative words spoken into my life in Jesus name

*THE SPIRIT REALM*. Pt.7


John11:40 Then Jesus said, “Did I not tell you that if you believe, you will see the glory of God?”


*LAWS OF OPERATION*(B)


In the ministry of Jesus Christ during his time on earth, he taught and demonstrated the power of God's kingdom using the laws of operation in the spirit realm. One vital law of operation He taught and demonstrated was the law of faith. There's a nexus between the first law I shared and this second operational law. The potency of your word is linked to what you believe. In Mark 11:23, Jesus said, *"For verily I say unto you, That whosoever shall say unto this mountain, Be thou removed, and be thou cast into the sea; and shall not doubt in his heart, but shall BELIEVE that those things which he saith shall come to pass; he shall have whatsoever he saith*".


What makes confession work is your believe/ faith. In Matthew 17:20, Jesus said, *"...for verily I say unto you, IF YE HAVE FAITH as a grain of mustard seed, ye shall say unto this mountain, Remove hence to yonder place; and it shall remove; and nothing shall be impossible unto you*". On some occasions, when people came to Jesus to be healed or for miracle, he often said "Be it unto you according to your faith". If you want to navigate your way to victory in the realm of the spirit, your faith must be intact. Now, you may ask, faith in what and who? It is important to know the wall on which our faith must lean on because some have faith in the wrong stuff and person.

Hallelujah!!! If you believe in His victory over demons and Satan, you cannot be defeated. In addition to believing in His finished work, your faith must also rest on His word: *"Heaven and earth will pass away, but my words will never pass away*", Mt24:35. You can take God's word to the bank. It will surely come to pass and not fail. Satan hates to hear "It is written". That was exactly what Jesus used against him during the temptation. Let your words or confession reflect what Christ has done for you on the cross and let your faith be rooted in God's word. That's how to win in that realm.

*THE SPIRIT REALM*. Pt.8


Prov 16:7 *When people’s lives please the LORD, even their enemies are at peace with them*".


*LAWS OF OPERATION* (C)


The realm of the spirit has system of operation that is unique and peculiar. One may think that an unseen realm is without laws and principles but it will surprise you to know that they are more organized and principled. If only God can give you insight into the realities of that realm, then you'll agree with me that their organization is second to none. Previously, we talked about the power of the spoken word and faith as some of the laws of operation in that realm. I want us to examine another law that is vital and protective yet ignored by some or say people do not see as important in spiritual warfare.


*The law of purity* is a vital operational law in the realm of the spirit. The opening text says, *"When people’s lives please the LORD, even their enemies are at peace with them*". That scripture is deep. It expresses a general truth that we must not ignore. By reason of how we live our lives, Satan knows those that are threading on dangerous grounds. He knows there's a boundary line between the kingdoms of light and darkness but there are people who cross the line through careless living. They take sides with the devil in character and conduct thereby opening the door for demonic attacks. I know a man who was blessed and flourishing years back. The moment he began philandering, he entered into a serious crisis that put his wife and children in trouble. One of the ladies he messed around with confessed in a deliverance session that the he will find it hard to breakthrough in life. Ever since, its been a life of struggle for him. He broke the law of purity.



Holiness is protective. When God says we shouldn't do a thing, it is not because of Him, it is for our good. Aside going to hell, the commandments of God are for our benefit and profiting on earth. God knew before hand that the enemy will want to lay claims on his right to control the affairs of people and afflict them on account of how they live their lives, that's why He warned us ahead through His word. When you live a life that is pleasing to God, Satan knows there's a hedge of protection around you. He testified to that in the case of Job saying, *"Then Satan answered the LORD, and said, Doth Job fear God for nought? HAST NOT THOU MADE AN HEDGE ABOUT him and about his house, and about all that he hath on every side? thou hast blessed the work of his hands, and his substance is increased in the land*", Job 1:9-10 KJV. Job would have remained unscathed save that God gave Satan the permission. Don't play around anyhow. How you live your life is a crucial weapon of warfare. There's power in purity.



*PRAYER*: Father, I receive grace to please you in Jesus name.

3/17/22, 5:39 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: PROJECTSAVE DEVOTIONAL
Thursday,
17th March,  2022.

*GIVING AUTHORITY TO LIES*

Jer 23:36 But stop using this phrase, ‘prophecy from the Lord.’ For people are using it to give authority to their own ideas, turning upside down the words of our God, the living God, the Lord of Heaven’s Armies. NLT


A man of God told me how God dealt with him to go correct himself on the altar after his ministration. While ministering, he gave a pseudo word of knowledge saying "Thus saith the Lord". Later, the Holy Spirit told him go tell the congregation that the word of knowledge he gave was his idea not from him. He obeyed that instruction fully. That takes some courage. Doing that, I know he earned the respect of his listeners. While on campus as a fellowship executive, I remember a prayer meeting we had where executive members were asked to give their counsel on a burning issue. A number of persons who spoke told us what the Holy Spirit told them and those things were conflicting. Immediately, I knew something was wrong because God's Spirit can't contradict Himself. Those days it was common to hear the brethren ask "What's the Holy Spirit saying" and from there several persons will say what the "Holy Spirit" said. Quite a number of times, those ideas were conflicting.


It is common in Christian circle today for people who want their opinion to hold sway to give authority to it by saying "The Holy Spirit said". People now give authority to lies and human ideas by using " Thus saith the Lord". The abuse of that phrase in our generation is rife and common in the Charismatic circles probably because someone want to appear to others as "Spiritual". Some brethren even use it as a mechanism to control others in church. If they give an idea or suggestion, they give it a ring of authority for others to tilt towards them. It is even worse when some brothers want to propose to a sister. The way and manner they present it to the sister will make it look like "If you don't say 'Yes', you'll be disobeying God not me". The scripture reveals to us what will happen to anyone who uses "Thus saith the Lord" falsely: *“Son of man, prophesy against the false prophets of Israel who are inventing their own prophecies. Say to them, ‘Listen to the word of the Lord. This is what the Sovereign Lord says: WHAT SORROW awaits the false prophets who are following their own imaginations and have seen nothing at all!*", Ezekiel 13:2-3 NLT.


*Stop trying to give authority to your ideas by adding "Thus saith the Lord" to it. If you have a good idea, you can present it without fear of rejection*. If you love a sister and you're at peace with her, don't tell her "Thus saith the Lord" when the Lord has not spoken. Just tell her plainly why you want her to be your woman and how you're at peace with the idea in your mind. Wives, stop using "Thus saith the Lord" to manipulate your husbands. It is common to some church women to bring their husbands under control because they appear more spiritual. Most gullible husbands also fall for that trick often and they stand by whatever their wives tell them because she's a "seer". What a shame! *God frowns at using His name to endorse lies*. In the old testament, it led to the death of some prophets especially during the days of Jeremiah. In our time, don't think that God is happy with the numerous lies coated with His name. The last time I checked, this is what the bible says about liars: *"But the cowardly, the unbelieving,... and ALL LIARS —they will be consigned to the fiery lake of burning sulfur. This is the second death*”, Rev 21:8. NLT


*ACTION POINT*: Stop saying " Thus saith the Lord" when the Lord has not spoken. Simply say, "I have an idea" or "What I said is my personal opinion".

3/18/22, 4:51 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: PROJECTSAVE DEVOTIONAL
Friday,

*THE BELIEVER'S JUSTIFICATION*. Pt.1


Romans5:1 *Therefore, since we have been JUSTIFIED through faith, we have peace with God through our Lord Jesus Christ*.


A notorious criminal was apprehended after commiting several crimes. He was known for crimes. Often, he's always in and out of the police cell.  The straw that broke the Camel's back was when he murdered a popular philanthropist in cold blood when he wanted to dispossessed him of his belongings. He met his waterloo while trying to run away. Police on patrol caught him and charged him to court. According to the law of the land, everyone knew he would be killed. They were only waiting for the day his judgement will be pronounced. The judgment day came and the whole town was waiting to hear the judge's verdict. Surprisingly, the judge declared him innocent of all charges and justified him on the grounds that someone offered to pay the penalty for his crimes even unto death. . This came as a surprise to everyone. This notorious criminal was released and declared innocent. That's an illustration  of what Justification is about.



*Justification is an act of God whereby He pronounces a sinner to be righteous because of that sinner’s faith in Christ*. Simply put, to justify is to declare righteous. In justification, God declares the sinner who believes in the atoning work of Christ as righteous. *The righteousness of Christ is imputed on him as though he has never sinned. In fact, he is declared not guilty before God regardless of his past*.  When we all came to Christ, we all were declared to be righteous. Now, we can be called the righteousness of God in Christ:  *"God made him who had no sin to be sin for us, so that in him we might become the righteousness of God*",
2 Corinthians 5:21 NIV
. Hallelujah!!!


When you came to Christ, you were justified. God declared you innocent of your past sins. As far as God is concerned, those past sins do not exist in his record anymore. If this is true, why are you so bothered about your past thinking God hasn't forgiven you? The righteousness of the new creation in Christ is not a filthy rag before God *(Isa64:6)* because the  righteousness of Christ has been imputed to us: *"For if by the transgression of the one, death reigned through the one, much more those who receive the abundance of grace and of the gift of righteousness will reign in life through the One, Jesus Christ*', Rom5:17. Don't let the devil preach the sermon of guilt and condemnation to you. Just like that criminal, God has declared you innocent through the death of Christ.



*CONFESSION*: I am justified by the blood of Jesus Christ. My past is gone, I'm a new creation.



info@projectsaveng.org

3/19/22, 7:23 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: ProjectSave Devotional

*THE BELIEVER'S JUSTIFICATION*. Pt.2

Romans5:1 *Therefore, since we have been JUSTIFIED through faith, we have peace with God through our Lord Jesus Christ*.

*TRUTHS ABOUT JUSTIFICATION* (A)


Justification is a new testament doctrine that places the believer in a different class from those in the old testament. It is one of the works of grace to the man who accept Jesus as his Lord&saviour. The first truth you need to know is that justification cannot be achieved by obeying the laws of Moses neither can we earn justification through rule-keeping or our own good works:
*"Therefore by the deeds of the law there shall no flesh be justified in his sight: for by the law is the knowledge of sin*", Romans 3:20 KJV. There was no way those in the time of Moses could have been justified because the law was not designed to justify anyone.


In Galatians 2:16, Paul say, *"KNOWING THAT A MAN IS NOT JUSTIFIED BY THE WORKS OF THE LAW, but by the faith of Jesus Christ, even we have believed in Jesus Christ, that we might be justified by the faith of Christ, and not by the works of the law: for by the works of the law shall no flesh be justified*". The Mosaic code was 613 in number and no one I mean no one could have kept those laws in totality. The legal code was weak and couldn't justify any man. It could not make anyone righteous. Speaking about the law, the writer of Hebrews(7:18) says, *"The former regulation is set aside because it was weak and useless*". To think that you can be justified by obeying set of rules is to disregard the finished work of Christ on the cross. Your justification is not because of anything you've done but because of what He(Christ) has done. Hallelujah!!!



I'm glad to tell you that your sins are forgiven and the record does not exist anymore: *"For I will forgive their wickedness and will remember their sins no more*", Hebrews 8: 12. You have been declared innocent of your past sins and freed from the prison of guilt that comes with it.  Christ took up your punishment on the cross in His death and you now have His righteousness imputed to you: *"For he hath made him to be sin for us, who knew no sin; that we might be made the righteousness of God in him*",2 Corinthians 5:21 KJV. Religion does not understand how a sinful man can be made innocent just because of his faith in Christ. Unfortunately, some church folks still do not understand this because they've been taught wrongly and they see it too cheap. Yes it is cheap to us but someone paid the price. Whatever anyone believes, the word of God stands. *The believer is no longer guilty of any sin in his past as long as he has received Christ. The new man is justified and made righteous*. You now have access to God's throne room without fear and guilt of the past. Don't allow anyone lock you in the prison of your past.


*CONFESSION*: I am free by the blood. Made righteous by the grace of God and justified because of the finished work of Christ. I refuse to live in guilt and condemnation. Now I have a clean slate because Christ washed me. Thank you father.

*THE BELIEVER'S JUSTIFICATION*. Pt.3


Romans5:1 *Therefore, since we have been JUSTIFIED through faith, we have peace with God through our Lord Jesus Christ*.


*TRUTHS ABOUT JUSTIFICATION* (B)



I watched a video titled "Kumbh Mela" years back. It is a video about the largest religious gathering in the world, a Hindu festival. In one week, they gather people numbering 80million from different parts of India. They normally converge at the "Ganges", a river where Hindu priests numbering about 23,000 will serve them ashes and to wash their bodies in the river so that they can obtain forgiveness. I see it as a gathering of guilt ridden people seeking for justification in the wrong place. Unfortunately, they keep doing this yearly because they never had assurance of forgiveness. We have something better in Christ. Hallelujah!!!


The justification of the believer sounds too good to be true. This is a teaching or subject religion has fought against for centuries. Some persons still find it hard to accept this truth plainly even some preachers. *Jesus did not come to add to the guilt burden of the sinner that repents, His assignment is to take his sins& wash the record away totally*. That a sinner comes to the Lord, confesses or say the sinners prayer and accept a Jesus he didn't see but believes in and then come out saying "my sins are forgiven and I'm innocent of all charges" is incomprehensible to the carnal man, that's just too cheap and easy. How can a man be declared innocent, righteous and justified freely without paying a price of money or effort?


One may ask, "is justification free?" This is a question begging for answer.  *Justification is made possible in the sacrificial death of Christ; it is based on the shed blood of Christ*. Apostle Paul says,  *"Since we have now been justified by his blood, how much more shall we be saved from God’s wrath through him"!*(Romans 5:9 NIV). The receiver of justification ( the believer) receives it freely but the giver( Christ) paid for it. Hallelujah!!! You don't need to pay or apply effort to be justified. Just believe in the justifier( Christ). This is what the enemy is preventing many from believing because it sounds too cheap and too good to be true but that's the Gospel, the goodnews. That once you're in Christ, the record of your wrongs are erased and that you have a new life. In the court of man, you may be guilty because men keep record of wrongs but in heaven, the record no longer exist because of the blood. This is the message believers of old heard that made them thank God for their salvation always.


*CONFESSION*: I am saved, washed by the blood and justified. I am redeemed from eternal punishment. Jesus paid for my justification in full not in part. I reject guilt, condemnation and the accusing voice of the enemy in Jesus name.
I am saved. Hallelujah!!!

Twitter: @Lanrezion01

*THE BELIEVER'S JUSTIFICATION*. Pt.4

Romans5:1 *Therefore, since we have been JUSTIFIED through faith, we have peace with God through our Lord Jesus Christ*.

*WHAT HAPPENS TO THE JUSTIFIED BELIEVER*(A)


Human race inherited sin from Adam, our federal head: *"Therefore, just as sin entered the world through one man, and death through sin, and in this way death came to all people, because all sinned*"
(Romans 5:12 NIV).
In Adam, all of sinned. We were in his loins when we sinned not because we acted wrongly but because we inherited a sinful nature from him:*"for all have sinned and fall short of the glory of God*",Romans 3:23 NIV. The unborn child is a sinner by nature.  To make matters worse, everyone was conceived and born in sin: *"Surely I was sinful at birth, sinful from the time my mother conceived me*", (Psalms 51:5 NIV). The unregenerate man is inherently sinful. No man is born born-again. Everyone born of a woman inherited that nature of sin. In our generation, some persons have claimed to be saved from their mother's womb but that's against the truth of scriptures. Not even children of preachers are born again from birth. Because of this sinful nature, we all attracted a penalty- death. Scriptures says, *"For the wages of sin is DEATH, but the gift of God is eternal life in Christ Jesus our Lord*",
(Romans 6:23 NIV). The death implied here is spiritual death ( separation from God) and eternal death ( eternal punishment in the lake of fire). That's the penalty for our sinful nature.



One of the several things justification does to the believer is the remission of the penalty of sin, which was spiritual & eternal death: *"He was delivered over to death for our sins and was raised to life for our justification*"
(Romans 4:25 NIV). Check Romans 3:24-26.4. In justification, Christ took the penalty for our sins and gave us His righteousness. Hallelujah!!! Paul confirmed this truth and preached it in his first missionary journey: *"Therefore, my friends, I want you to know that through Jesus the forgiveness of sins is proclaimed to you. Through him everyone who believes is set free from every sin, a justification you were not able to obtain under the law of Moses*"(
Acts 13:38‭-‬39 NIV). Your justification has freed you from partaking in the penalty of the sinful nature- death.



As a child of God, you carry a new life. The eternal life of God is already in you: *"I write these things to you who believe in the name of the Son of God so that you may know that you have eternal life*"( 1Jn5:13). Hallelujah!!! You're no longer subjected to the penalty of death( spiritual & eternal) because you now carry the very life of God. Yes, we are prone to dying physically but the believer who dies or sleep in the Lord has the hope of resurrecting into eternal bliss. The benefits of the believer's justification is immeasurable. Money cannot buy it neither can religion grasp its length and breadth. Every day when you wake up, the first thing you should thank God for is the salvation of your soul. You're not just justified only to be forgiven but to be free from the penalty of the sinful nature. *The way of salvation always sound too good to be true that's why the devil invented religious rites for men so that they can pay for what Christ has made free*. Let it sink deep down into your heart, as long as you've given your life to Christ, you have no recorded past in Christ( 2Cor5:17).


*CONFESSION*: Jesus I thank you for your vicarious substitution. I refuse to take your finished work for granted.  Henceforth, I walk in the reality of all that you've done for me. I declare to the hordes of hell that I'm justified, free from all accusations and guilt. Christ took my place in punishment and now I'm free. Hallelujah!!!

*THE BELIEVER'S JUSTIFICATION*. Pt.5

Romans5:1 *Therefore, since we have been JUSTIFIED through faith, we have peace with God through our Lord Jesus Christ*.


*WHAT HAPPENS TO THE JUSTIFIED BELIEVER*(B)



God is a family man. How does that sound in your ears? Right from creation, God's intention for mankind is to  relate with man like a father. Religion assent to the fact that their is God( though religion doesn't know Him) but it doesn't understand the fatherhood of God. When Jesus taught His disciples to pray, he brought a new dimension to the issue of prayer that was alien to the legalistic Jews. He started by saying: *"OUR FATHER*". His intention was to emphasize to them the fatherhood of God and teach them to relate with him as one. *One of the greatest revelation of God you can have is the revelation of His fatherhood*.



Sin broke family relationship between God and man right from the garden of Eden. In fact, God sent our father ( Adam) and mother( Eve) packing from the garden but God sought to find a way to restore that family relationship. He achieved it in Christ. *Through justification, the believer is fully accepted by God into His family*. Hallelujah!!!  1 John 3:1 says: *"Behold, what manner of alove the Father hath bestowed upon us, that we should be called the SONS OF GOD: therefore the world knoweth us not, because it knew him not*". Read this text again. God is my father because I'm His son. In every family, inheritance is a right of sons. Look at what Paul says in Romans8:17, *"And if children, then heirs; heirs of God, and joint-heirs with Christ; if so be that we suffer with him, that we may be also glorified together*".



Aside being in God's family, God made provision for friendship through Christ: *"Henceforth I call you not servants; for the servant knoweth not what his lord doeth: but I have called you friends; for all things that I have heard of my Father I have made known unto you*"( John15:15). Now that you are saved, you are God's friend and a bonafide member of His family. You're not a bastard. You can walk into his presence without fear, guilt or shame and relate with Him as your father. *Those who run from God and see Him as a distant being are those who do not understand His fatherhood.* In warfare, you can brag on God in the presence of demons&Satan that you stand on the authority of your  father to confront them. Through justification, you've become a son and a friend of God.



*CONFESSION*: Hallelujah! I am a friend of God. Jesus is my big brother and friend. I belong to God's family. I'm a heir of God and joint heir with Christ. The right of sonship has been conferred on me. I triumph over all demoniacal forces, over the vicissitudes of life and the dirty dozens of the enemy. I am triumphant.  Hallelujah!!!

*THE BELIEVER'S JUSTIFICATION*. Pt.6


Romans5:1 *Therefore, since we have been JUSTIFIED through faith, we have peace with God through our Lord Jesus Christ*.

*WHAT HAPPENS TO THE JUSTIFIED BELIEVER*(C)


One of the major challenges some Christians have with guilt, condemnation and accusations of the enemy is because they lack knowledge about what Christ wrought for them in His sacrificial death. They think salvation to be something partial while they barely get along in their relationship with God. Some have been in the faith for years but know little or nothing about their justification partly because they were not taught and also because they don't believe it whenever they read about it from scriptures. We emphasize and re emphasize this truth because of the unchanging nature of biblical truth. We are not cheapening grace, rather we uphold it and make all of God's children take advantage of their rights and privileges in Christ. While religion is battling to obtain justification by works, someone obtained it for us once and for all and gave it to us freely. Hallelujah!!!



Another miracle of justification is that the very righteousness of Christ is imputed to us. We are declared righteous legally: *"Because one person disobeyed God, many became sinners. But because one other person obeyed God, many will be made righteous*"( Romans 5:19 NLT). Sin was imputed to us through Adam but righteousness was imputed to us through Christ. In a simpler language, we inherited sin through Adam but now that we are in Christ, we have inherited his righteousness. He took our sin and gave us His righteousness: *"God made him who had no sin to be sin for us, so that in him we might become the righteousness of God*" (2 Corinthians 5:21 NIV). It this imputed righteousness that gives us access to the father without fear, guilt or condemnation. This righteousness is not like a filthy rag because it is the very righteousness of Christ. It is our self righteousness that is like a filthy rag before God. A believer is not expected to be self righteous, rather we should stand in the righteousness of Christ. Never you call the righteousness of Christ imputed to you a filthy rag because you want to sound humble. Religion thrives on false humility but faith in Christ thrives on accurate biblical knowledge. You can't have this understanding and still be moody because of your past. I got my freedom when I understood this truth after decades of being in the Lord.


*Another purpose of the imputed righteousness is to make you live above sin*. Knowing that you have been made righteous is an all- important key to victory over  sin: *"For the sin of this one man, Adam, caused death to rule over many. But even greater is God’s wonderful grace and his gift of righteousness, FOR ALL WHO RECEIVE IT WILL LIVE IN TRIUMPH OVER SIN AND DEATH through this one man, Jesus Christ*" (Romans 5:17 NLT). This is profound, read it again. If a believer wallow in sin, it is not because sin is so powerful that he cannot overcome it, it is a function of lack of knowledge about this righteous status that he possesses and inability to subdue the flesh. Sin has been conquered on the cross. *The imputed  righteousness is the edge we have above sin*. Apostle Paul says, *"FOR SIN SHALL NO LONGER BE YOUR MASTER, because you are not under the law, but under grace*"
(Romans 6:14 NIV). This is what religion does not have. Religion seeks to overcome sin through do's and dont's but in Christ, we have the power and enablement to live right without struggling because it is our new normal. That's why I stand opposed to teachings of grace that gives men liberty to indulge in sin. *Grace will not teach you to indulge in what he( Christ) has come to solve- the problem of sin*. Now that you're the righteousness of God in Christ, live like it and let men see it in your daily life and conduct.


*CONFESSION*: I am the righteousness of God in Christ Jesus. No condemnation for me, no guilt or shame. I have access to the very presence oJesus because of the imputed righteousness. The righteousness of Christ is not like a filthy rag. I live in victory over sin and death in Jesus name. Hallelujah!!!

*THE BELIEVER'S JUSTIFICATION*. Pt.7


Romans5:1 *Therefore, since we have been JUSTIFIED through faith, we have peace with God through our Lord Jesus Christ*.


*WHAT HAPPENS TO THE JUSTIFIED BELIEVER*(D)


I watched a Christian drama that centred on the state of mind of a wealthy man. He was so wealthy. He had houses in major cities of the world. He had big businesses and the things money could buy. He was blessed with a good family but he lacked something- peace. In search for peace of mind, he did several things but his situation remained the same not until he met a preacher who showed him the secret behind his problem. He was rich but not saved, the devil was his master. He had all money could buy but there was something money couldn't buy that he lacked- peace with God.


Often, when i tell people that you don't need to be a Christian to be wealthy, they tend to look at me somehow especially those that have been taught that salvation is the doorway to wealth. This is one of the heresies of "Prosperity Gospel".  Often I point at the richest guys on Forbes list and I ask if those guys were wealthy because they were saved. Then, some persons will twist their answer to suit their argument but the unchanging truth remains. Often, there's something the unsaved wealthy, intelligentsias and religious people lack. They lack peace with God. There are people you can't tell to accept Jesus for breakthrough. They and their 4th generation have crossed the poverty line. There must be something more than money that they need. In their hearts, there's a void, an emptiness and a longing to make peace with God. Religion makes attempt to fill that emptiness and make peace with God but without result. *One may have all that money can buy and still lack peace with God*.



Your justification as a believer is what brings peace between you and God: *"Therefore, since we have been JUSTIFIED through faith, WE HAVE PEACE WITH GOD through our Lord Jesus Christ*", ( Romans 5:1). This peace is made possible simply because Jesus Christ reconciled you to God: *"For God was in Christ, reconciling the world to himself, NO LONGER COUNTING PEOPLE'S SINS AGAINST THEM. And he gave us this wonderful message of reconciliation*" (2 Cor 5:19 NLT). The reconciliation of Christ is what brought peace. Hallelujah!!! Peace with God is what you have now that you're saved.  This is something you should be thankful for daily. If God had put a monetary price tag on this, you may not be able to purchase it. That's why salvation is worth more than gold&silver. Wealth is good but cannot buy the treasures of salvation. Knowing that I have peace with God is the reason I won't listen to the enemy's accusations about my past and so should you.


*CONFESSION*: I have peace with God, I am saved. I have peace with God, I am justified. I have peace with God, Christ has reconciled me to God. The father is not holding my past against me because Christ paid for it. His blood washed me now I'm saved. I refuse to reason with the devil, I refuse to listen to my feelings, I have peace with God, I am saved. Hallelujah!!!

3/25/22, 5:37 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: PROJECTSAVE DEVOTIONAL

*THE BELIEVER'S JUSTIFICATION*. Pt.8


Romans5:1 *Therefore, since we have been JUSTIFIED through faith, we have peace with God through our Lord Jesus Christ*.


*WHAT HAPPENS TO THE JUSTIFIED BELIEVER*(E)


Recently, my mind has been flooded with the wonders of our precious salvation. Everyday, I place more value on it than I did before. The more I see people struggling to "earn" what we have freely, I thank God. In some faith, they don't believe God can forgive completely. They may have to make forgiveness chant 100s of time before they get some temporal relief from guilt. Some believers are also like that. Whenever guilt overcomes them, they'll give their life to Christ over again. They're not always sure they're saved despite their years of  being in Church. I once heard a pastor say that every night, he always give his life to Christ again. I don't know how long he has to do that but it is a sign that something is faulty with his knowledge of salvation and the things that Christ made available for us in His finished work. Some of us were like that too. Those days, I responded to every altar calls because I was not sure if the salvation, forgiveness of yesterday would suffice. Some of us were like that. I didn't read in my bible where it was commanded that I must give my life to Christ everyday by praying the sinners prayer. If you know the scripture where it is written, kindly show me.



For me, it became worse when I get into circle of those who always tell us that we should ask God for forgiveness of all our sins from birth. It became a repeated prayer point in some meetings. Then, I asked, "how many times do I have to ask for forgiveness for the sins of my childhood"? My liberation came when I understood fully that once sin is confessed and forgiven by God, I don't need to ask for forgiveness for the same sin again: *"And I will forgive their wickedness, and I will never again remember their sins*”(Hebrews 8:12 NLT). Then i found another scripture even in the old testament:
*“I—yes, I alone—will blot out your sins for my own sake and will never think of them again*" (
Isaiah 43:25 NLT). When these scriptures and others registered in my mind, I stopped asking for forgiveness of forgiven sins. Once God forgives a sin, it is forgiven already. The burden of guilt is the reason why some believers keep asking for forgiveness of the sin God has already forgiven in decades past. The devil loves when we do that because he takes our eyes off the provision of the cross and points us to our inadequacies. Some think it to be a sign of piety to do that all the time. They're never sure their sins are forgiven and forgotten. If that's the case with Christianity, then it is no different from other religions because none of them promises complete forgiveness neither do they have provision for it like we do in Christ.



In the sight of God, our filthy garment is stripped off us like it happened to Joshua the high priest: *"So the angel said to the others standing there, “Take off his filthy clothes.” And turning to Jeshua he said, “See, I have taken away your sins, and now I am giving you these fine new clothes.*”
(Zechariah 3:4 NLT). Through justification, you're now clothed with the robe of righteousness. What happened when you came to the Lord is akin to what the father of the prodigal son did to him when he returned: *“But his father said to the servants, ‘Quick! Bring the finest robe in the house and put it on him. Get a ring for his finger and sandals for his feet*" (
Luke 15:22 NLT). This is the message of the Gospel, God's love story. That hymn writer says, "Wonderful story of love, tell it to me again". We shouldn't be tired of hearing it over and over till we are full of what Christ wrought for us in His finished work. *Also, this is the message we should preach to sinners persuading them that if they accept Christ, they'll be justified. They've tried religion but now they need to key into eternal life. Don't promise them wealth and riches, it is not written in Scriptures that salvation is the key or passport to wealth. Some of them have it already. Eternal life is the promise we have. That's what they don't have. Inclusive in our walk is suffering and persecution but eternal life is assured*.  Don't let the enemy preach a different gospel to you neither should you preach his gospel.



*CONFESSION*: I am saved. I am washed in the blood of the lamb. My past is over in Christ. I am a new creation and God is my father. He is not holding my past forgiven sins against me. I refuse to believe the lies of the enemy. I am justified by the blood. Hallelujah!!!

3/26/22, 7:52 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: ProjectSave Devotional
Saturday,

*THE BELIEVER'S JUSTIFICATION*. Pt.9


Romans5:1 *Therefore, since we have been JUSTIFIED through faith, we have peace with God through our Lord Jesus Christ*.

*THE AUTHOR OF JUSTIFICATION*


Every invention has an inventor. Whatever idea or concept that is created has a creator. Likewise the subject of justification. We are discussing justification today because someone authored it. The believer's justification didn't fall from the skies. It is not an afterthought neither is it my invention. I didn't just wake up one morning shouting "Justification". It is not the doctrinal concept of any church or pastor. GOD IS THE AUTHOR OF JUSTIFICATION. How did I know this? I found it in my bible: *"Who will bring any charge against those whom God has chosen? It is God who justifies*"( Romans 8:33 NIV). Hallelujah!!! It is God who justifies.


*The grace of God is amazing. When God planned salvation, He made plans for something full and free for the vilest sinner who believes*. He foresaw that nothing else could help man out of the enemy's accusations so He made provision for justification. Picture a court room with a criminal standing trial. Everyone is expecting the judge to sentence to him to life imprisonment or death but all of a sudden, he pronounced him innocent and righteous despite evidences confirming the alleged crimes. This is the picture of justification and  what happens to the sinner that comes to Christ. He is declared innocent of charges and imputed with the righteousness of Christ. In the natural, that's a travesty of justice but in God's judicial prerogative, He chose to help the sinner this way as long as he put his faith in  Christ: *"However, to the one who does not work but trusts God who justifies the ungodly, their faith is credited as righteousness*"(Romans 4:5 NIV).


*Justifcation may look arbitrary and unjust to you but it is not because someone paid the price*. If Christ had not paid the price for our justification, it would be unjust and unrighteous for God to declare a sinner innocent and righteous. Since Christ paid for it, God was bound to honour the sacrifice of Christ  by justifying the sinner who comes through Christ: *"he did it to demonstrate his righteousness at the present time, so as to be just and the one who justifies those who have faith in Jesus*"(Romans 3:26 NIV). Now that you're saved, know that your justification is from God himself and not any man. Stay away from guilt and toxic environments that puts you in remembrance of your unholy past. Even if you were a thief, prostitute or murderer before coming to Christ, now you're in Christ, you are Justified.



*CONFESSION*: I have peace with God, I am saved. I have peace with God, I am justified. I have peace with God, Christ has reconciled me to God. The father is not holding my past against me because Christ paid for it. His blood washed me now I'm saved. I refuse to reason with the devil, I refuse to listen to my feelings, I have peace with God, I am saved. Hallelujah!!!

3/27/22, 7:23 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: ProjectSave Devotional

*THE BELIEVER'S JUSTIFICATION*. Pt.10


Romans5:1 *Therefore, since we have been JUSTIFIED through faith, we have peace with God through our Lord Jesus Christ*.


*THE PRINCIPLE OF JUSTIFICATION*


God is a principled God. Behind every act of God, there is a principle. Justification is not exempted in this. One might ask what the principle behind justification is. The answer is summarized in one word "Grace". The principle of justification is grace. This means that we are justified not because of our good works or behaviour. It is purely an act of grace. We did nothing to deserve it. Apostle Paul confirmed this in Romans 3:24 saying, *"and all are justified freely by his grace through the redemption that came by Christ Jesus*". Hallelujah!!! The enemy has tried to pervert this over the years by introducing do's and dont's yet they do not suffice in making man justified before God.



GRACE, a word misunderstood by many yet so powerful and deep. A word some hate to preach on yet without it salvation is impossible. A word some also use to indulge in sin not knowing in it lies the power to overcome sin(Titus2:11-12).  If justification involves works, that is doing things, following a set of rules and rituals and legal codes, it is no longer grace but works. Our salvation wouldn't have been possible if not for grace. Paul said, *"For it is by grace you have been saved, through faith—and this is not from yourselves, it is the gift of God*" (Ephesians 2:8 NIV). A graceless salvation is a useless salvation.


Religion is trying to make up rules and legal codes for men to be justified but Christ has brought justification to us freely by His grace: *"so that, having been justified by his grace, we might become heirs having the hope of eternal life*"(Titus 3:7 NIV). Hallelujah!!! When next you meet a sinner who is burdened with the load of sin, talk to him/ her about the grace of God that justifies. Sometimes, we don't win souls because we preach the wrong stuff to them. An average unbeliever who is religious is in bondage to rituals of religion because he's seeking salvation and justification. A simple explanation on how Christ justifies by grace can release him from the bondage of religion and bring him into the glorious  liberty of sons.



*CONFESSION*: I am justified freely by grace, I am saved. I have peace with God, I am justified. I have peace with God, Christ has reconciled me to God. The father is not holding my past against me because Christ paid for it. His blood washed me now I'm saved. I refuse to reason with the devil, I refuse to listen to my feelings, I have peace with God, I am saved. Hallelujah!!!

*THE BELIEVER'S JUSTIFICATION*. Pt.11

Romans5:1 *Therefore, since we have been JUSTIFIED through faith, we have peace with God through our Lord Jesus Christ*.


*THE GROUND OF JUSTIFICATION*


God is sovereign but not arbitrary. For all He does, He has purpose(s) in mind. Also, there are principles that govern His acts. Even though we sometimes think of God as someone that decides what He wishes but from scriptures, we see clearly that God is not arbitrary. Justification, as good as it is, there is a ground for it which without it, God wouldn't  justify anyone. God did not just declare the sinner justified. There's a ground for it and the ground for justification is the blood of Jesus: *"Since we have now been justified by his blood, how much more shall we be saved from God’s wrath through him!*"(
Romans 5:9 NIV).


There is power in the blood of Jesus. Without it, your redemption and mine wouldn't be possible: *"But now in Christ Jesus you who once were far off have been brought near by the blood of Christ*"(Eph2:13). Justification is one of the benefits of redemption. Old timers know the power in the blood of Jesus that's why they sing and talk about it. Anything that can cleanse sin is powerful enough to provide justification: *"Whom God put forward as a propitiation by his blood, to be received by faith. This was to show God’s righteousness, because in his divine forbearance he had passed over former sins*"( Rom3:25). The blood is heaven's detergent for cleansing: *"In him we have redemption through his blood, the forgiveness of our trespasses, according to the riches of his grace*"(Eph1:7). Hallelujah!!!


When the devil comes with his barrage of accusations, guilt and condemnation, show him the blood. Remind him about the cleansing power of the blood. If you don't know how powerful the blood of Jesus is, read this scripture: *"But if we walk in the light, as he is in the light, we have fellowship with one another, and the blood of Jesus his Son cleanses us from all sin*"(1John1:7).  Even under the old covenant, the blood was a vital part of their ritual: *"Indeed, under the law almost everything is purified with blood, and without the shedding of blood there is no forgiveness of sin*"(Heb9:22). The devil hates when we talk about the blood of Jesus. That's why he uses all manners of tricks to prevent us from partaking in the communion. Never underestimate the power of the blood.


*CONFESSION*: I bring all accusations of Satan under the blood. I am justified freely by grace, I am saved. I have peace with God, I am justified. I have peace with God, Christ has reconciled me to God. The father is not holding my past against me because Christ paid for it. His blood washed me now I'm saved. I refuse to reason with the devil, I refuse to listen to my feelings, I have peace with God, I am saved. Hallelujah!!!

3/29/22, 5:18 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: ProjectSave Devotional

*THE BELIEVER'S JUSTIFICATION*. Pt.12

Romans5:1 *Therefore, since we have been JUSTIFIED through FAITH, we have peace with God through our Lord Jesus Christ*.


*MANWARD CONDITION FOR JUSTIFICATION*.


Man is a free moral agent. That is, God created man with the ability to make choices and exercise the power of will to make decisions. Man is not a robot controlled with programming and buttons. The power to say 'yes' or 'No', act rightly or wrongly, believe or not believe in the vicarious substitution of Christ, lies within the will of man. God could have made all men to accept Christ by using His sovereign power but He didn't because man is created to make choices by himself. He is not a robot. In God's scheme for justification, there's a role that man must play or we say a condition man must fulfill to be justified.  *FAITH is the CONDITION for Justification*. Apostle Paul says, *"knowing that a man is not justified by the works of the law, but by the FAITH of Jesus Christ, even we have believed in Jesus Christ, that we might be JUSTIFIED BY THE FAITH OF CHRIST, and not by the works of the law: for by the works of the law shall no flesh be justified*"(Galatians 2:16 KJV). Hallelujah!!!


Faith in Christ is the only man-ward condition for being justified. If you can believe on the Lord Jesus Christ, you will be justified. Your sins will be forgiven and you'll be declared innocent. Romans 4:5 (NLT) says, *"But people are counted as righteous, not because of their work, but because of their faith in God who forgives sinners*". Your duty in justification is to believe in the sufficiency of Christ's atoning sacrifice on the cross. You must look up to the man of Calvary and believe in the sufficiency of the work He wrought on the cross to be justified. The finished work of Christ on the cross is enough for me and you to be justified. All God expects from us is FAITH in what Christ has done for us. Religion will always prevent man from believing in the sufficiency of Christ work on the cross. The cross is so important to Paul and he said: *"May I never boast except in the cross of our Lord Jesus Christ, through which the world has been crucified to me, and I to the world*"(Galatians 6:14).


To be justified, just believe in what Christ did on the cross- He died for your sins. That death is enough. The chorus of a hymn says, *"I need no other argument, I need no other plea;It is enough that Jesus died, And that He died for me*". The death of Jesus is enough to justify you just believe. If you're burdened with guilt often, and the enemy never ceases to accuse you of the wrongs in your past, all you need do is press your faith button and put your trust in the sufficiency and efficacy of Christ's sacrifice on the cross. Apostle Paul says, *"WE ARE MADE RIGHT WITH GOD BY PLACING OUR FAITH IN JESUS CHRIST. And this is true for everyone who believes, no matter who we are*"( Romans3:22).


*CONFESSION*: I believe in the atoning sacrifice of Christ. I have faith in God and in the finished work of Christ. I am justified freely by grace, I am saved. I have peace with God, I am justified. I have peace with God, Christ has reconciled me to God. The father is not holding my past against me because Christ paid for it. His blood washed me now I'm saved. I refuse to reason with the devil, I refuse to listen to my feelings, I have peace with God, I am saved. Hallelujah!!!

3/30/22, 6:30 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: PROJECTSAVE DEVOTIONAL

*THE BELIEVER'S JUSTIFICATION*. Pt.13

Romans5:1 *Therefore, since we have been JUSTIFIED through faith, we have peace with God through our Lord Jesus Christ*.


*THE GUARANTEE OF JUSTIFICATION*


There are different schools of thought/ theories on the resurrection of Jesus. Ranging from the “stolen body theory” proposed by the Jewish religious leaders in Matthew’s gospel to the “swoon theory” advanced by the 19th-century critic Friedrich Schleiermacher. Bible critics over the years have stopped at nothing to disproof the resurrection of Christ. They have advanced funny theories and the most laughable of them is the "hallucination theory". Here, they're claiming that the whole resurrection thing was a product of the disciples' hallucinations. Accepting these evil theories is dangerous and inimical to the faith because *resurrection is the guarantee of justification*.



In Romans4:25(NIV), Apostle Paul says, *"He was delivered over to death for our sins and was raised to life for our justification*". Hallelujah!!! Resurrection of Christ is the visible proof that Christ had done the needful and that He had fought and won the battle against Satan and the hordes of hell. It is also a sign that He had finished the work of our redemption. If God was not satisfied with the work of Christ in the grave, He wouldn't have raised him from the dead but Christ met all requirements and God raised him from the dead: *"But God raised him from the dead, freeing him from the agony of death, because it was impossible for death to keep its hold on him*"(Acts2:24). If Christ had not risen from the dead, justification would have been impossible but blessed be God, Christ arose from the dead. Hallelujah!!!



*Ressurection is the guarantee of justification*. We need to celebrate the resurrection of Christ always not only during Easter. If Christ had not risen, there would be no Christianity. What gives us the boldness to preach and witness around is the reality of Christ's resurrection. That resurrection power is the force behind the supernatural we experience in our meetings. Apostle Paul says, *"And if Christ has not been raised, then all our preaching is useless, and your faith is useless*"(1Cor15:14). When next the devil comes around making you doubt your justification, let him know that your justification is not a matter of "maybe or maybe not", it is guaranteed and settled because Christ resurrected. Hallelujah!!!


*CONFESSION*: I believe in the bodily resurrection of Christ, my justification is guaranteed. I have faith in God and in the finished work of Christ. I am justified freely by grace, I am saved. I have peace with God, I am justified. I have peace with God, Christ has reconciled me to God. The father is not holding my past against me because Christ paid for it. His blood washed me now I'm saved. I refuse to reason with the devil, I refuse to listen to my feelings, I have peace with God, I am saved. Hallelujah!!!

DEVOTIONAL 0467 | Source start line: 42329

3/31/22, 10:03 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: PROJECTSAVE DEVOTIONAL
Thursday,
31st March,  2022.


*THE BELIEVER'S JUSTIFICATION*. Pt.14


Romans5:1 *Therefore, since we have been JUSTIFIED through faith, we have peace with God through our Lord Jesus Christ*.


*EVIDENCE OF JUSTIFICATION*


There's a misconception among those who have a one-sided view on the subject of salvation and justification about the word "Works" and how it relates to our imputed righteousness. The confusion stems from the writing of James in chapter 2:24: *"You see that a person is considered righteous( justified) by what they do and not by faith alone*"( Emphasis mine). This scripture seems to contradict what Paul said in Romans 3:20 *"Therefore no one will be declared righteous in God’s sight by the works of the law; rather, through the law we become conscious of our sin*". It seems one is supporting "works" as a criteria for righteousness/ justification while the other seemed not to support "works". Even this controversy came up during canonization process. Martin Luther called the book of James "Right-strawy" epistle because he felt the book was opposed to salvation by grace which became one of  the major themes of reformation.


However, in simple bible interpretation rule, we know there's harmony of scriptures and doctrine. Biblical doctrines aren't contradictory. Even the old testament which is a legalistic regime is a type and shadow of the new testament. It is impossible for Paul to be opposed to James on the subject of salvation. So what is Apostle James saying? Let's read James2:20: *"You foolish person, do you want EVIDENCE that faith without deeds is useless?*"(NIV). Apostle James was not saying "Works" is the criteria for salvation rather he was saying that the evidence of salvation/ faith in Christ is good works- works of righteousness. Apostle James expresses the same truism in James 2:18: *"But someone will say, “You have faith; I have deeds.” Show me your faith without deeds, and I will show you my faith by my deeds*"(James 2:18 NIV).



Paul confirmed the same truth in Ephesians 2:10 saying, *"For we are his workmanship, created in Christ Jesus unto good works, which God hath before ordained that we should walk in them*".
WORKS is the EVIDENCE of Justification. Works here refers to the fruit of the Spirit or fruit of righteousness. *You can't be saved, justified and declared righteous without visible evidence or fruit to show for it*. You can't be making declarations saying "I'm the righteousness of God in Christ" when the works of righteousness are not evident in your life. As a justified sinner, knowing what Christ has done for you on the cross, you will not live in sin under the pretext of grace: *"What shall we say then? Shall we continue in sin, that grace may abound? God forbid. How shall we, that are dead to sin, live any longer therein?*"(Romans 6:1-2 KJV).




*CONFESSION*: I am saved by grace for good works. I have peace with God, I am justified. I have peace with God, Christ has reconciled me to God. The father is not holding my past against me because Christ paid for it. His blood washed me now I'm saved. I refuse to reason with the devil, I refuse to listen to my feelings, I have peace with God, I am saved. Hallelujah!!!

info@projectsaveng.org

4/1/22, 6:29 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: PROJECTSAVE DEVOTIONAL
Friday,
April 1st, 2022.


*DO THESE TO YOUR MINISTER OFTEN* Pt.1


2Tim2:14: *"May the Lord show special kindness to Onesiphorus and all his family because HE OFTEN VISITED AND ENCOURAGED ME. He was never ashamed of me because I was in chains*"


One day, a ministry partner ( medical doctor) checked on me. I presume she saw my ministry activities via social media. The way I was jumping around for the Lord. She greeted and asked about my health. People rarely ask such questions. In that conversation, she was concerned about my health and how I was doing. She was insistent on knowing if I often check my blood pressure, eat right and if I rest well. Sincerely, i defaulted in many ways medically. My sleeping time in 24 hours mostly is less or not more than 6 hours at night often. No nap during the day. Honestly, I wasn't expecting such questions from her. It was unusual because people rarely ask such from me. Often, more of what I see from people is: "Are you praying for me"? Sometimes, it could be: "Pastor, pray for me. I have so and so problem". At other times, it could be " Pastor, I need help. I haven't eaten for days". My phone and social media is flooded with such requests.



That day, I felt someone really care about me too. That conversation was soothing and refreshing. You know it is not easy to be a preacher especially a traveling preacher. Most times, we tend to care for people, ask about their welfare,visit, pray and counsel people yet we get little of these in return. In fact, I've had people who got angry with me because I didn't ask after them. Some persons have outrightly accused me of neglecting them. I apologized and I did what a normal minister of God would do- pray. Year back, I was on admission in the hospital. While on the hospital bed, someone still called for quick prayers. She didn't ask about my well being.  Unfortunately, people forget we are humans who need the same treatment. We need care, love and due appreciation (not praises/worship). Not only us, our wives and children need to hear your voices too. Often, pastor's wives and children are isolated. They see daddy praying long hours for people or counseling them yet the persons aren't even greeting them. *How church folks treat pastors can make children of preachers love or hate ministry. Stop looking out for their errors*.



As you love your pastor, love his wife and children. It is not easy to be a preacher's wife or child. Apostle Paul specially prayed for Onesiphorus because he visited and encouraged him. That was enough for Paul at that time in his life and he said: *"May the Lord show special kindness to Onesiphorus and all his family because HE OFTEN VISITED AND ENCOURAGED ME. He was never ashamed of me because I was in chains*" (2Tim2:14). Please, be our Onesiphorus. Visit us where possible and encourage us. Don't hear us preach/ teach or write devotionals without due appreciation for imparting you. Saying "Thank you for that sermon" is not flattery. Pray for us and ask about our welfare. *Don't let your relationship with us be all about your satisfaction. We are not spiritual vendors or merchants of spiritual things neither are we contractors*. Please, change.



*ACTION POINT*: Today, call your Pastor, that missionary or that minister who has been a blessing to you. Pray, encourage and check on them to be sure they're in health and safety.

*DO THESE TO YOUR MINISTER OFTEN* Pt.2


1 Thess5:25 Dear brothers and sisters, pray for us.


*PRAY FOR US*


One afternoon, I received a call from a brother in the Lord in far away Abuja. I was in the salon when the call came. He said, "I just finished praying for you and the Lord said..." I was glad that someone somewhere remembered me in prayers even to the point where God gave him a word for me. That afternoon was special because the call and what was said didn't leave me on time. That was a radical departure from the usual calls I often receive from people to pray for them. At other times, I've met brethren who ask me, "Are you praying for me"? Some throw such questions at ministers as though they employed them as a prayer contractor.


Apostle Paul, the greatest Apostle ( in my judgement), as spiritual, anointed and insightful as he was, he solicited for prayers saying, *"Dear brothers and sisters, pray for us*" ( 1Thess5:25). He wasn't proud to ask for prayers. He didn't see himself as superhuman, he understood what it is to be a man before the phrase "Of God" comes in. Every 'Man of God' is first a "man". He is subject to temptations and trials of life. If he doesn't receive adequate prayer support and mercy from the Lord, what consumes men may consume him. In your sojourn on earth, you must have seen preachers with challenges( broken homes, illness, wayward children, dwindling finances, threat to life,etc) that are not uncommon to their church members and even unbelievers.


We face spiritual battles as you do, including our wives and children. I once ministered to a missionary's wife who was attacked spiritually. She couldn't walk. If the church had raised corporate and individual prayer altar in homes for that pastor's wife, maybe the enemy wouldn't have succeeded. Unfortunately, there are church folks who desire the death of their Pastor's wife so that they can step into her shoes. I pity such ladies. Instead of criticizing preachers or your Pastor on things you know nothing about, why not employ yourself as his personal intercessor? *The fact that you're cool with gossips about the scandals of preachers shows that you're not praying enough for them. You won't gossip about those you're praying for. I'm yet to see a successful minister of the Gospel who doesn't have prayer support from those he minister to*. They may not announce themselves as prayer partners ( prayer labour is a secret labour any way) but their impact is felt visibly. I desire to have thousands who will lift up my family and ministry in prayers on daily basis. I desire family altars where ministers are prayed for. We need your prayer support just we pray for you without ceasing.


*ACTION POINT*: Lift up your pastor and those you know in ministry in prayers. Pray for them as the Spirit leads.

DEVOTIONAL 0470 | Source start line: 42553

4/3/22, 5:10 PM - Lanre Oyeleke: ProjectSave Devotional
Sunday,
3rd April 2022.


*DO THESE TO YOUR MINISTER OFTEN* Pt.3


1Cor 4:2 Moreover it is required in stewards, that a man be found faithful. KJV


*BE LOYAL*


Years back, I know an associate pastor who left his senior pastor for another Pastor because he heard the other pastor was giving cars to associates from what I heard. What he failed to tell people was that his former pastor once gave him an international platform by sending him to go preach on his behalf in the U.S. That's a rare privilege. It seemed to me that when the going was good in terms of benefits, he remained but when the tide turned, he jumped ship. Unfortunately for him, his former pastor became bigger and better after. From the onset, he wasn't loyal to him. It only showed when the opportunity to be given a car showed up. There are many disloyal church folks today. They "use" and "dump" their pastors only to jump ship whenever they hear it is happening elsewhere or whenever they perceive benefits will accrue. Some are only loyal because their present church fits their current "status". *The test of loyalty comes when there's a change in status from middle class to upper class. Will that Pastor still remain your pastor if you become the Governor or President?* Will you still be faithful in your duties to God in that church?



*The stress of ministry is too much to be surrounded by disloyal associates and members*. We need unwavering loyalty from you in every respect as long as it is in the will of God. By the way, what is loyalty? According to Oxford Advanced Learner's Dictionary, the word 'loyal' is defined as giving' or showing firm and constant support or allegiance to a person or institution". *Firm and constant support or allegiance is the thrust of loyalty*. Other synonymous words are: faithful, trustworthy, devoted, etc. *Every preacher/ Pastor is in need of loyal people not heart-breakers*. Those whose loyalty to his cause and vision is unwavering. There are persons who deserted a pastor/ ministry because of unmet personal interest. They jump ship to another preacher's church for trivial reasons. What some pastors termed "growth" today, it is just a gathering/ convergence of disloyal members and associates from different churches. Such folks create more problems than they help solve existing ones.



Apostle Paul was blessed with loyal people: *"Yes, and I ask you, my true companion, help these women since they have contended at my side in the cause of the gospel, along with Clement and the rest of my co-workers, whose names are in the book of life*"(Philippians 4:3 NIV). What a joy it is to have true companions not rebels. If you are no longer in tune with the practices, teachings and doings in that church, it is honourable to leave peacefully instead of breaking the church into factions. *Stop trying to "unpreach" or "unteach" your pastor's messages to prove you have knowledge. It is possible for him to be wrong just like any other preacher out there. If error/ heresy persist, prayerfully take a decision that is good for your sanity. Don't break that church into two with your "revelation"*. Don't forget that he laboured over you to grow. Don't be the reason behind the breaking or factions in a church. You are better elsewhere among a group of like minds that are in tune with your ideological leanings than where you'll always be at loggerheads with leadership. If that church/ ministry is where God want you, your loyalty should be unshaking. Be loyal, be supportive, be trustworthy, don't talk down the vision of your pastor.



*PRAYER*: I receive grace to be loyal in Jesus name. Amen

*DO THESE TO YOUR MINISTER OFTEN* Pt.4

Hebrews13:17 Have confidence in your leaders and submit to their authority, because they keep watch over you as those who must give an account. Do this so that their work will be a joy, not a burden, for that would be of no benefit to you. NIV


*SUBMIT FOR DISCIPLESHIP*

Those days when I was unsure of what God wanted me to do in ministry, I thought I would pastor a church like every other person so I visited a church a senior pastor told me to check. The purpose of the visit was to check them out in case I would love to pick up a pastoral role with them. I attended that service but nothing clicked in my spirit. Deep down, I knew I was in the wrong place but I learnt something from the first timers card they gave me. In the card, they asked "Will you like we pastor you"? Ever since I gave my life to Christ, no one has ever asked that question from me. That church wanted to be sure their first timers are willing to submit to discipleship and their training  structures because that's the best way to grow and mature them in the Lord. Sometimes, people leave churches for several reasons but more often than not, under the pretext of "I'm not being fed properly in that church" or "I'm not growing spiritually there". One question we need to ask is: "Did you submit yourself for discipleship and the training structures of that church before reaching that conclusion"?


Often, most people who complain about their churches have not even explored all the avenues for growth in that church before taking their exit. As a church member, move from the level of membership to discipleship. *Your pastor's ministry can't be fruitful and beneficial to you if you don't allow him to pastor you by way of discipleship*. Failure to submit to laid down training structures and discipleship in that church puts you at the level of a passerby. Some persons feel cool hanging around pastors or ministers of the Gospel. They're cool with being seen around spiritual people especially those in ministry  but they are unwilling to submit to their leadership. Some love to be part of a popular church yet they've not submitted to discipleship. Unfortunately, some pastors have members who attend their churches and have become church workers but their allegiance is somewhere else. They are being discipled from afar by another preacher whom they don't even have access to but they fail to honour and receive the ministry of their pastor. You can't be taking instruction from several places and be loyal. If you're like that, please change. The child that eats around doesn't grow. Our mothers know that.


It is better to be under a pastor whose discipleship&training structures you'll submit to than to identify with a church whose training structures you reject. For a long time, I've been very selective in mentoring people because some persons love to identify with me  but they're not ready to submit for thorough training. Some persons just delight in pointing to Pastor "XXX" as mentor and trainer but they're not submissive to his leadership. Stop hanging around preachers or spiritual people to boost your ego. Your pastor will give account over make his job easy: *"Have confidence in your leaders and submit to their authority, because they keep watch over you as those who must give an account. Do this so that their work will be a joy, not a burden, for that would be of no benefit to you*" (Heb13:17 NIV).



*ACTION POINT*: Submit to the training structures of your local church and let your pastor disciple you.
