*DO THESE TO YOUR MINISTER OFTEN* Pt.5

Mark6:4 Then Jesus told them, “A prophet is honored everywhere except in his own hometown and among his relatives and his own family.” NLT

*AVOID FAMILIARITY*

During the earthly ministry of Jesus, one of the reasons the Jews especially his kinsfolk didn't benefit from his anointing was because they despised him in their hearts due to familiarity. They knew his root, his father and mother, his siblings and what they do. They couldn't get their healing and miracles through his ministry because they despised him: *"Then they scoffed, “He’s just a carpenter, the son of Mary and the brother of James, Joseph, Judas, and Simon. And his sisters live right here among us.” They were deeply offended and refused to believe in him. And because of their unbelief, he couldn’t do any miracles among them except to place his hands on a few sick people and heal them*"( Mark 6:3, 5 NLT). This is very pathetic. A whole Messiah couldn't help his people not because He wasn't powerful enough but simply because His own people despised him. There's a lesson there for us.


Did you notice that those closest to men of God rarely benefit from them spiritually? Outsiders and new converts have more testimonies than old timers in the ministry. Surprisingly, the same pastor may receive an invitation to preach elsewhere and the whole place will be on fire not because the fire isn't there but his people quenched it in their hearts. *If you want to benefit from the grace of God upon the life of your Pastor or that minister, don't relate with him/her from the standpoint of familiarity*. The English people say, "Familiarity breeds contempt". *Be careful of treating the anointing and the anointed with contempt. I wish I can say this a thousand times to people in this generation who regard servants of God less and want to rub shoulders with them*. We often despise the call of God upon our  Pastors and those  God placed over us because we know them too well. Sometimes, church folks miss God's blessings because they're privy to the weaknesses of their leaders and whenever they stand to minister, they shut their minds against whatever God is set to do through him. They magnify his weaknesses in their hearts and miss God. It should be noted that I'm not supporting waywardness for ministers. I'm only trying to let you know that the fallibility of your Pastor or that leader is no reason enough to despise him in your heart. Do you remember what David called Saul in his backslidden state? He called him "The LORD'S ANOINTED": *"He said to his men, “The LORD forbid that I should do such a thing to my master, the LORD’s anointed, or lay my hand on him; for he is the anointed of the LORD*”.


*I beg you in the name of the Lord God of heaven, don't despise a man that is truly called of God for whatever reason. Even if you're older in age or more educated than your Pastor, he is your father in the Lord*. It is possible that your younger sibling in the family is a Pastor( referring to any of the five fold office), accord him the honour of office. Be wise to relate with him as a servant of God and as a younger sibling without contempt. This generation is fast losing it here but we won't fail to declare the total counsel of God on this matter. Don't take access given to you for granted and don't abuse access because you have classified information that others don't know. That way, you shut yourself out of God's grace and blessings that flows from the life of that preacher. Be careful.

*ACTION POINT*:  Don't despise that preacher. Avoid being too familiar with him.

*DO THESE TO YOUR MINISTER OFTEN* Pt.6

Romans 16:3 Greet Prisca and Aquila, my fellow workers in Christ Jesus,

*LABOUR WITH HIM*

Every genuine Pastor (including others in the five-fold ministry) have a vision from God. They have an assignment from God to expand His kingdom. However, the work is always bigger than what one person can handle. Take for example, in a church setting, there are several departments. The Pastor alone cannot be in all departments. He needs faithful and able men who will labour with him to ensure the job is done. He needs co labourers who will be devoted to ensuring the fulfilment of his vision. From the opening text, apostle Paul mentioned the name of two co labourers in his apostolic ministry, Priscilla & Aquila.

As anointed as Paul was, he couldn't do the work alone, he had faithful workers who laboured with him. When you labour with your Pastor to grow the church or with that evangelist to win souls, you encourage him: *"and also Jesus who is called JUSTUS; these are the only FELLOW WORKERS for the kingdom of God who are from the circumcision, AND THEY HAVE PROVED TO BE AN ENCOURAGEMENT TO ME*"( Col4:11). The success of Paul in ministry was not his effort alone, there were men who devoted their time, energy, skill, knowledge, wisdom and all they had to see that Paul fulfilled God's purpose for his life.Another faithful co labourer Paul had were Timothy: *"Timothy my fellow worker greets you, and so do Lucius and Jason and Sosipater, my kinsmen*"(Romans16:21).


You can volunteer to be the support your pastor need to fulfill his vision. In that church, don't be a bench warmer. Your commitment should not only be on sundays as a church attendant. Move up to being a worker. There's something you can do in God's house. You encourage your pastor when you support his vision. No preacher want to be lonely in the work. The stress that comes with ministry kills. Most preachers do pray for the gift of men. Be the answer to that prayer. That para church/ evangelistic ministry whose outreaches you love need you to volunteer for the openings in their ministry. Don't sit back and say "Well done" when others are working, join the workforce. Don't forget that you're not labouring for your pastor or that preacher, you're labouring with God for kingdom expansion under the vision God has given that pastor: *"For we are God’s fellow workers; you are God’s field, God’s building*"(1Cor3:9). In that case, God is your rewarder not the pastor: *"For God is not unrighteous to forget your work and labour of love, which ye have shewed toward his name, in that ye have ministered to the saints, and do minister*"(Heb6:10).

*ACTION POINT*: Stop being a bench warmer. Labour with God under the vision God has given your Pastor or preacher.

*DO THESE TO YOUR MINISTER OFTEN* Pt.7


Gal6:6 Those who are taught the word of God should provide for their teachers, sharing all good things with them.NLT


*SUPPORT THEIR WELFARE*


One of the most controversial topics conservative preachers are shying away from in this age is the issue of minister's welfare. The reasons are obvious. No genuine minister want to be seen as hungry for money or gain. Also, the extremes of "prosperity gospel" and the materialistic teachings of some ministers has tainted the image of most ministers in  public view. The general public can easily misconstrue a message on minister's welfare as one of those money-driven messages. In fact, some may see this teaching as "one of those teachings". However, regardless of whatever the world says, we should just stick to the bible on the subject to avoid extremes. Also, if we believe in preaching and teaching the total counsel of God, then we must emphasize this aspect of the Gospel in a balanced manner.


Does the bible teach us to support the welfare( financially & materially) of those that minister to us? The answer is  YES. Let's examine the opening text: *"Those who are taught the word of God should provide for their teachers, sharing all good things with them*" (Galatians 6:6 NLT).In Greek language, this text is in the imperative mood. That is a command that must be obeyed. It is not a suggestion neither is it an advice. Apostle Paul said that if you're taught the scriptures by a teacher of God's word as I teach daily through our devotionals,  it is your duty to give back to your teacher.   Another scripture that is akin to this is 1 Tim5:17: *"The elders who direct the affairs of the church well are worthy of double honor, especially those whose work is preaching and teaching*". The phrase "double honour" is from the Greek "Diplous Timé". It speaks of giving monetary value to a thing. In clear terms, Paul was commanding that preachers&teachers be paid handsomely. Note the word "double". They should be paid well.



If you draw spiritual nourishment from a preacher or teacher of the Gospel and your spiritual life has taken a turn for the positive, without being told, giving to such a preacher is your duty. *A trained minister of the Gospel won't put up his account details asking that you sow a seed into his life neither will he be suggestive but that doesn't mean he doesn't have needs*. You must be sensitive enough to know how to come in to be of support to that preacher/ teacher that has blessed you. "Thank you" doesn't pay his bills, do something. I was raised by a Pastor and I am also in ministry now. I can tell you authoritatively that most pastors are responsible for the welfare of some members and people in one way or the other. They deprive themselves of certain comfort just to make others happy. If you give that preacher money, you may be surprised that what he'll spend on himself&family may not be up to 50% because he'll share with others. Neglect in areas of giving is what turned some preachers to using gimmicks& coercion to get money from their members though it is wrong. Know this as your duty and do it willingly without being coerced and you'll be blessed. Don't wait till they ask before you give. It should be habitual.



*ACTION POINT*: As from today, be a blessing to that preacher, teacher that has been a blessing to you.   It doesn't have to be plenty but that token given consistently will go a long way.

*DO THESE TO YOUR MINISTER OFTEN* Pt.8


Gal6:6 Those who are taught the word of God should provide for their teachers, sharing all good things with them.NLT


*PAULINE QUESTION*.


One of the works of the enemy this endtime is to make God's children turn their backs against God's instituted order and to attack God's servant who are labouring faithfully. This attack can be on their health, ministry, family or finances. In recent times, the latter has happened much more than ever and some of God's children are the tools used by Satan to achieve this evil. No thanks to social media critics. In a bid to correct the ills and excesses of some ministers, they ended up lumping all ministers together as the same thereby making most people look away from playing their biblical roles of giving and supporting their local church and pastor. Often times, I've seen people who have vowed never to give a penny to any preacher or church. They reached this conclusion in response to an evil propaganda against ministers. They failed to realize that not all preachers are bad eggs. Some boast about not tithing in years and how they diverted what should have gone to the Lord and His servants elsewhere. The devil won't get away with this.



I perceive something similar happened in the time of Paul and he asked a question: *"If we sowed spiritual things to you, is it too much if we reap in material things from you*"?( 1 Cor 9:14). This is Paul, a spiritual man asking Corinthian believers a very simple but profound question. Today, on behalf on *faithful ministers* of the Gospel who have laboured to make you grow spiritually, I'm asking *"is it too much if they reap in material things from you*"? Find an answer to that. Sometimes, I try to peep into the minds of some church folks and what I see is hatred for the well-being of God's servants whereas bible said: *"Let them shout for joy, and be glad, that favour my righteous cause: yea, let them say continually, Let the LORD be magnified, which hath pleasure in the prosperity (well-being) of his servant*" (Psalms 35:27, Emphasis mine). Don't hate on genuine God's servant when God blesses them. You must desire the well-being of your Pastor or that minister whom God is using for you. That has been the order from the old testament.



*Brethren, don't let the devil preach another Gospel to you. An impoverished preacher may not concentrate fully on the work because his attention will be divided*. From my findings, suicide bombers and terrorists who advance Satanic ideologies get adequate support and funding from those who believe in their cause, why should we treat God's footsoldiers less? Sometimes, my heart bleeds when i see how faithful missionaries who are labouring to take the Gospel to where it has not been heard are neglected by church folks. We need a change of attitude in this.  I want you to prayerfully consider this teaching and the previous as sound and balanced. On your own, study it and check all scriptures and allow the Spirit of God minister to you. I call you blessed.



*ACTION POINT*: The answer to the Pauline question raised today is that it is never too much to be a blessing to genuine God's servants. If you've not been doing it, make it a culture from today.

*DO THESE TO YOUR MINISTER OFTEN* Pt.9

Gal6:6 Those who are taught the word of God should provide for their teachers, sharing all good things with them.NLT

*THE LORD COMMANDED IT*

Whenever we preach or teach about taking care of our ministers and those that labour in doctrine, it is not something we make up to draw sympathy neither are we emphasizing it for self gain. Actually, it is one of the several commands or instructions given by God. In 1 Corinthians9:14, apostle Paul made a profound statement worth considering. He said: *"In the same way, THE LORD ORDERED that those who preach the Good News should be supported by those who benefit from it*". It is an order( command) from the Lord not any preacher. The Lord commanded that those who benefit from the ministry of a minister should support him. This scripture is in the heart of the new testament.


Right from the old testament, God made it clear to the children of Israel that the Levites were to take certain sacred portions of offerings brought into the temple. It was God's ordained means of taking care of their needs so they could focus on the work of the ministry. If God had so great a plan for ministers of death, how much more the ministers of the new covenant?(2Cor3:6-7). We cannot claim to be obedient to the total counsel of God and neglect this vital aspect. The world and some Christians are trying to be philosophical about issues such as this but no matter how hard they try, the scriptures cannot be broken.



You cannot be selective in your obedience. If the Lord says it, you must do it. Listen to Paul again, *"Don’t you realize that those who work in the temple get their meals from the offerings brought to the temple? And those who serve at the altar get a share of the sacrificial offerings*"(1 Corinthians 9:13 NLT). Why is it so? It is simple- the Lord commanded it. If you're privileged to sit in your church council, whenever ministers welfare is being discussed, don't join the bandwagon of those who love to use lack and poverty to cut their pastors to size. Such persons don't end well. The Psalmist says, *"Let them shout for joy, and be glad, that favour my righteous cause: yea, let them say continually, Let the LORD be magnified, which hath pleasure in the prosperity (well-being) of his servant*" (Psalms 35:27, Emphasis mine).


*ACTION POINT*: If you've not been supporting the welfare of ministers, please turn a new leaf. Knowing that the Lord commanded it.

*TWO THINGS SENSIBLE PEOPLE DO*


Proverbs19:11 Sensible people control their temper; they earn respect by overlooking wrongs. NLT


Recently, I was in discussion with a group of believers. We were talking about physical abuse in marriage and how some women have lost their lives to brute beasts called 'husbands'. Also, our discussion touched on several reasons why people stay in abusive marriages. The topmost reason in that discussion was wrong counsel from ministers who tell women facing such to stay and endure. Unfortunately, when it results in death, these preachers/ counselors will only shed crocodile tears and walk away leaving the children motherless. In that discussion, a brother said that it is possible that most victims ( women) of physical abuse did something to trigger it. While that may be true in some cases, there's no justification for physical battery for any reason.  It is an "anathema" to do such in marriage.



*Sensible men don't beat their wives for any reason*. According to the opening text, there are two things that sensible people do:
(1) They control their temper
(2) They overlook wrongs.
We all have tendencies to get angry or may have gotten angry at one time or the other. *Sometimes, anger is a reaction to unfulfilled expectation from others or from ourselves*. The best way to curb or control our temper is never to expect much from people. Don't think you can't be offended. In fact expect offenses from those close to you even your spouse.



In marriage, spouses must be ready to accept the weaknesses of one another. *If you are the type that don't take nonsense, marriage is not for you*. Most of the "no nonsense" men end up beating their wives. The "Margaret Thatchers" often do not have stable marriages. In marriage, there's no guarantee that you won't offend each other. Knowing that such is a possibility will help you to live perpetually in the realm of self control and post-dated forgiveness. Until you are ready to tolerate "nonsense",  you are not yet ready for marriage. *Also, your ability to overlook wrongs and leave them in the past without referring to it or reacting is a huge sign of maturity that will bring peace to your marriage and relationship with people*. If you can imbibe these two attributes of sensible people, you'll have a sweet and lasting relationship with people and in your marriage.


PRAYER: Father, I receive grace to control my temper and overlook wrongs In Jesus name. Amen

*THE PURSUIT OF RIGHTEOUSNESS*


1Tim6:11 But you, Timothy, are a man of God; so run from all these evil things. Pursue righteousness and a godly life, along with faith, love, perseverance, and gentleness.  NLT


The justification of the believer is settled and complete. It is not something we should doubt or probe into. It is a done deal. The believer is justified and made righteous. Now we are the righteousness of God in Christ because the very righteousness of God is imputed to us. However, the teaching about the subject of righteousness needs proper explanation. There is 'the gift of righteousness' given to every believer at salvation. We didn't do anything to deserve it neither did we work for it because it is the gift of God given to us in Christ so that we can stand boldly in God's presence without feeling guilty or unworthy of God: *"For the sin of this one man, Adam, caused death to rule over many. But even greater is God’s wonderful grace and his gift of righteousness, for all who receive it will live in triumph over sin and death through this one man, Jesus Christ*"( Romans 5:17 NLT).


On the other hand, there is also 'the fruit of righteousness' which refers to the outward Christ-like character that is expected of the believer: *"May you always be filled with the fruit of your salvation—the righteous character produced in your life by Jesus Christ—for this will bring much glory and praise to God*"(Philippians 1:11 NLT). This is also known as the fruit of the Spirit. When a believer manifest the fruit of the spirit, he is more like Christ in his dealings with people. Paul advised Timothy to run from things that can spoil his Christian testimony and pursue righteousness. It is not enough to confess being the righteousness of God in Christ. The fruit of righteousness must be seen in our lives.


The gift of righteousness or we say righteousness was imputed to us at salvation so that we can live righteously: *"For the sin of this one man, Adam, caused death to rule over many. But even greater is God’s wonderful grace and his gift of righteousness, FOR ALL WHO RECEIVE IT WILL LIVE IN TRIUMPH OVER SIN AND DEATH through this one man, Jesus Christ*'(Romans 5:17 NLT). It is evident that the 'gift of righteousness' was given so that we can manifest or walk in the 'fruit of righteousness'. The pursuit of righteousness is an unending pursuit till Christ comes. The forces of unrighteousness are everywhere trying to drag believers into destruction but our focus must be single and we must be steadfast in our pursuit of righteousness. We were advised to seek first God's kingdom and its righteousness( referring to walking worthy of the Lord). What is your pursuit? Refrain from  pursuing other things.

PRAYER: Father, I receive grace to pursue righteousness In Jesus name. Amen

DEVOTIONAL 0479 | Source start line: 43066

4/12/22, 7:18 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: ProjectSave Devotional
Tuesday
12th April, 2022.

*THE BELIEVER'S SANCTIFICATION* Pt.1

1 Thessalonians 4:7 *For God has not called us for the purpose of impurity, but in sanctification*.

Another important theme in Christian doctrine that we must understand is sanctification. The teaching on justification won't be balanced if we stop there without considering what it is to be sanctified. The word "Sanctify" is synonymous with the word "Cleansing", "Holy" and "saint". What is the meaning of sanctification? In simple and unambiguous term, to sanctify is to set apart. Further, we can say that sanctification is a setting apart to and for a sacred purpose: *“Sanctify to Me every firstborn, the first offspring of every womb among the sons of Israel, both of man and beast; it belongs to Me*”( Exodus 13:2).




Sanctification carries another meaning which is very important. Because of the presence of evil, sin and wickedness in our world, sanctification is not only a separation TO God but separation FROM that which is evil, sinful and unclean. An illustration of this in the Old Testament is found in 2 Chronicles 29:5: *"and said unto them, Hear me, ye Levites, sanctify now yourselves, and sanctify the house of the LORD God of your fathers, and carry forth the filthiness out of the holy place*"( 2 Chronicles 29:5 KJV). Also, In the new testament, apostle Paul enjoined same: *"Having therefore these promises, dearly beloved, let us cleanse( sanctify) ourselves from all filthiness of the flesh and spirit, perfecting holiness in the fear of God*"( 2 Corinthians 7:1 KJV).



Justification is God's duty, you have no role in it other than your faith in the finished work of Christ. However, in sanctification, you have a great role to play because that's how you can live out or manifest  the Christ life. We cannot know that you're truly a child of God by claiming you're justified until we see the fruits and evidence of a sanctified life; a life set apart to God from sin and corruption of this world. Beware of preachers and teachers that tells you that how you live your life doesn't matter after you're justified. Now that you're justified, sanctification is God's will for you: *"It is God’s will that you should be sanctified: that you should avoid sexual immorality; that each of you should learn to control your own body in a way that is holy and honorable,*"(1Thessalonians 4:3-4 NIV).


*PRAYER*: Father, I receive the grace to live a sanctified life in Jesus name.


====================================================================================================
DEVOTIONAL 0480 | Source start line: 43213
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
4/13/22, 6:41 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: PROJECTSAVE DEVOTIONAL
Wednesday,
13th April, 2022.

*THE BELIEVER'S SANCTIFICATION* Pt.2

1 Thessalonians 4:7 *For God has not called us for the purpose of impurity, but in sanctification*.


*SANCTIFICATION IN TWO-FOLD*


In the new testament, there are two ways in which sanctification was presented to us. The first sense in which sanctification is presented to us is that every believer is already fully and eternally sanctified because of his faith in and his union with the Lord Jesus Christ: *"By the which will WE ARE SANCTIFIED through the offering of the body of Jesus Christ once for all*"(Hebrews 10:10 KJV). The tense is in the past. This is what bible scholars call "Positional Sanctification". By reason of your position in Christ as a believer, you're already sanctified. You didn't do anything to merit this other than the faith you put in the finished work of Christ: *"For by one offering he hath perfected for ever them that are sanctified*" (Hebrews 10:14 KJV). Hallelujah!!! I'm sanctified and blood washed. This is something to be thankful for: *"But we ought always to give thanks to God for you, brothers beloved by the Lord, because God chose you as the firstfruits to be saved, through sanctification by the Spirit and belief in the truth*"(2 The2:13).


The second sense in which sanctification is used in the new testament is that believers should be sanctified. It is presented as something to be done: *"It is God’s will that you should be sanctified: that you should avoid sexual immorality;*"(1 Thes 4:3 NIV). Did you see the phrase "Should be sanctified"? In the first sense, it was presented as something that is done or a fait accompli. Now, the scripture is saying again that we should be sanctified. Bible scholars call this "Progressive sanctification". For anyone to be used in God's service, this type of sanctification is required: *"Therefore, if anyone cleanses( sanctify)  himself from what is dishonorable, he will be a vessel for honorable use, set apart as holy, useful to the master of the house, ready for every good work*"(2Tim2:2, Emphasis mine).


Some of the new testament themes often have two sides to it. Just like sanctification; you have been sanctified but again you have to be sanctified. It is expected of you to live a sanctified life. *Often times, dwelling on the positional benefits of being in Christ( which doesn't have our input) and not considering the other side of the coin where our input is needed is what breeds libertinism.* Running with a side of the truth without considering the other side is much more dangerous than total ignorance of the truth. All believers are sanctified but experimentally we are not fully sanctified; this is a progressive work of God in our lives that will change us into the likeness of Christ until we see Him and are then (and only then) “conformed” to His image (Romans 8:29). On daily basis, your cry and mine should be "Sanctify me Lord". Our desire should be like that of Paul for the Galatian brethren: *"My dear children, for whom I am again in the pains of childbirth until Christ is formed in you*" (Galatians 4:19 NIV).


*ACTION POINT*: Father, let Christ be formed in me in Jesus name.

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
4/14/22, 1:51 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: PROJECTSAVE DEVOTIONAL
Thursday,
14th April,  2022.

*THE BELIEVER'S SANCTIFICATION* Pt.3

1 Thessalonians 4:7 *For God has not called us for the purpose of impurity, but in sanctification*.


*WORKING OUT YOUR SANTIFICATION*(A)


Previously, we affirmed from scriptures that we are sanctified by virtue of our position in Christ. It is a done deal because of the finished work of Christ on the cross not because of anything we did. God worked it in by Himself: *"For it is God which worketh in you both to will and to do of his good pleasure*"(Philippians 2:13 KJV). However, there's another side of the coin on thesame issue. In several portions of scripture, we were enjoined to live a sanctified life. That is, work "out" what God worked "in": *"Wherefore, my beloved, as ye have always obeyed, not as in my presence only, but now much more in my absence, work out your own salvation with fear and trembling*" (Philippians 2:12 KJV). This brings us to the practical side of the issue under consideration.


God has always played His part but we often fail to play our part as we ought to. Paul said "God is working IN us" and that we should work out what has been worked in. How can we work out sanctification or we put it straight, how can we live a sanctified life? The starting point is to be cleansed by the blood of the lamb and to be separated from filth: *"but if we walk in the light, as he is in the light, we have fellowship one with another, and the blood of Jesus Christ his Son cleanseth us from all sin*"(1 John 1:7 KJV). Also consider this: *"and said unto them, Hear me, ye Levites, sanctify now yourselves, and sanctify the house of the LORD God of your fathers, and carry forth the filthiness out of the holy place*"(2 Chronicles 29:5 KJV).


Paul gave a straight and clear step on how to live a sanctified life in his writings to Timothy: *"If a man therefore PURGE himself from these, he shall be a vessel unto honour, sanctified, and meet for the master's use, and prepared unto every good work*"(2 Timothy 2:21 KJV). The word 'purge' is from the Greek "Ekkathairo". It means to clean out or remove impurities. That's how it works. To live a sanctified life, you need to "remove" some friends, habits and attitudes from your life. Some books, music and movies should be removed from your library, phones and gadgets. *The practical side of sanctification will not be done by God but you*.  If you desire to please God, fish out those things the Spirit of God has been pointing your attention to and remove them. You have to be intentional about it, work out what God has worked in. His grace is available.


*PRAYER*: Father, I receive the grace to play my part in the deal. Lord, purge me till I'm totally clean in Jesus name.

4/15/22, 6:42 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: PROJECTSAVE DEVOTIONAL
Friday,
April 14th, 2022.


*THE BELIEVER'S SANCTIFICATION* Pt.4


1 Thessalonians 4:7 *For God has not called us for the purpose of impurity, but in sanctification*.


*WORKING OUT YOUR SANTIFICATION*(B)



The bible is complete. There is nothing God commands that He hasn't given the grace to accomplish. Apostle John says, *"Loving God means keeping his commandments, and his commandments are not burdensome*"(1 John5:4). God speaking through apostle Paul instructed us to do another thing so as to live a sanctified life. In Romans 6:13, he says, *"Neither yield ye your members as instruments of unrighteousness unto sin: but YIELD YOURSELVES UNTO GOD, as those that are alive from the dead, and your members as instruments of righteousness unto God*"( Romans6:13). Paul didn't say that we should pray to yield ourselves to God or scream to yield ourselves. He gave the instruction to the believer in Christ as something he should do. His responsibility.



What does it mean to yield anyway? The word yield is from the Greek "Paristemi". It means to present something. In context, Paul was saying we should present ourselves unto God. This expression is akin to what we read in Romans 12:1 saying, *"I beseech you therefore, brethren, by the mercies of God, that ye PRESENT your bodies a living sacrifice, holy, acceptable unto God, which is your reasonable service*". The secret of sanctification revealed here is that as long as we present our members to God in total surrender, we'll live above sin and the world. The intention of presenting a thing to another is not to take it back. Rather, it is to give that thing away. The receiver decides what to do with what he has received.


You have to present your life and body at the cross to live a sanctified life. The man that present himself at the cross is a dead man: *"I am crucified with Christ: nevertheless I live; yet not I, but Christ liveth in me: and the life which I now live in the flesh I live by the faith of the Son of God, who loved me, and gave himself for me*" (Galatians 2:20 KJV). When you stop living your life and you start living His, that's when the beauty of sanctification will be seen in your life. *The crucified life is a life that has been presented to God. It is a position or state of the heart whereby we don't allow the impulses of the flesh to rule us rather, we yield to the leading of the Spirit*. We present ourselves unto God by feeding the spirit and starving the flesh. That's how the flesh is crucified. It is a practical thing. *If you have struggles with your flesh sexually, check what feeds lust and cut off from it. That's how to crucify the flesh. You can pray from now till kingdom comes, until you do the needful, prayer remains a mere religious exercise*. A sanctified life is possible if we yield our total being unto God.



*PRAYER*: Father, I receive grace to yield my self to you in Jesus name.

DEVOTIONAL 0483 | Source start line: 43409

4/16/22, 6:59 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: ProjectSave Devotional
Saturday,
16th April, 2022.

*THE BELIEVER'S SANCTIFICATION* Pt.5


1 Thessalonians 4:7 *For God has not called us for the purpose of impurity, but in sanctification*.


*WORKING OUT YOUR SANTIFICATION*(C)


Most believers do not know the benefit of staying in fellowship with God in prayer and the word. Oftentimes, I do my best to teach and preach this because I know that living the Christian life is impossible without it. In matters of sanctification, a virile fellowship with God( In prayer and the word) is one of the ways to be sanctified and to live a sanctified life. Apostle John confirms this in 1John 1:7 saying, *"But if we are living in the light, as God is in the light, then we have FELLOWSHIP with each other, and the blood of Jesus, his Son, CLEANSES us from all sin*"(1 John 1:7 NLT). According to the scripture, "fellowship" is a precursor to cleansing(sanctification). As we are in fellowship with God, we become cleansed. That's something to grasp.


Another scripture that buttresses this truth is can be found in John 15:3, *"You are already clean because of the word I have spoken to you*"(John15:3 NIV). Jesus himself said this. The spoken word or the written word studied meditatively has a cleansing/sanctifying power.  In the old testament, the Psalmist said, *"Wherewithal shall a young man cleanse his way? by taking heed thereto according to thy word*"(Psalm119:9). There's a nexus between cleansing (sanctification) and the word of God likewise prayer. In fact, Paul spoke about sanctifying food with prayer: *"For every creature of God is good, and nothing to be refused, if it be received with thanksgiving: for it is sanctified by the word of God and prayer*"(1 Timothy 4:4‭-‬5 KJV).


Devote quality time to prayer and the word daily. As you do this, you will be cleansed. *The power released in prayer weakens the flesh and the cleansing power in the word makes clean*. Since sanctification is progressive, you have to do this on daily basis. Our transformation into Christ likeness won't happen in a day. It takes a life time. That's why Paul said: *"But we all, with unveiled face, beholding as in a mirror the glory of the Lord, are being transformed into the same image from glory to glory, just as by the Spirit of the Lord*"(2Cor3:28). We become like Jesus as we behold him in the place of fellowship. That's how to be cleansed.


*PRAYER*: Father, I receive the grace to fellowship with you on daily basis in Jesus name.

4/17/22, 6:11 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: ProjectSave Devotional
Sunday,

*THE BELIEVER'S SANCTIFICATION* Pt.6


1 Thessalonians 4:7 *For God has not called us for the purpose of impurity, but in sanctification*.


*WORKING OUT YOUR SANTIFICATION*(D)



There's a message some persons in our generation are trying to exclude from scriptures. If they could, they would have removed it but the eternal word of God stands. What is that message? It is the aspect of scriptures that talks about suffering. In our quest to live a sanctified life, God permits suffering, trials and vicissitudes of life to prune and cleanse us. While this is what we pray against often, in God's eternal plan and purpose for the believer, there's a purpose some of our unpalatable experiences serve which we must consider scripturally. God saw them coming, yet He permitted them because there's a purpose they serve in our lives. Speaking about Jesus, the writer of Hebrews says, *"In bringing many sons and daughters to glory, it was fitting that God, for whom and through whom everything exists, should make the pioneer of their salvation perfect through what he suffered*"( Hebrews2:10 NIV).
Jesus became the captain of our salvation through the things he suffered.




In fact, talking about the same Jesus, we were told He learnt Obedience through suffering: *"Even though Jesus was God’s Son, he learned obedience from the things he suffered*"( Hebrews5:8). God used suffering to perfect him for the assignment. If Christ learnt obedience through suffering, then we still have many lessons to take in the school of suffering. While we don't pray for suffering in life, there are challenges permitted by God for our purification and sanctification. Listen to Job: *“But he knows where I am going. And when he tests me, I will come out as pure as gold*"(Job 23:10 NLT). Still don't know how you and me can become like Jesus if we are not tried and pruned. Listen to Paul in Acts 14:22,  *"where they strengthened the believers. They encouraged them to continue in the faith, reminding them that we must suffer many hardships to enter the Kingdom of God*". These are the very words of the revered apostle. He suffered a lot for the Gospel. *Quickly, to put in the right perspective, the suffering implied in this teaching has to do with those things we suffer because of our resolve to live for Jesus*.



The beauty of sanctification will not be seen until you suffer for taking your stand for Christ in this depraved world. In 2 Timothy 3:12, Paul says, *"In fact, everyone who wants to live a godly life in Christ Jesus will be persecuted*". I don't know of any man who has walked with God getting His approval without facing the trials of life. Whatever you suffer for not compromising are part of those things God uses for your sanctification. Rejoice, it is for a purpose. Apostle Peter said, *'These trials will show that your faith is genuine. It is being tested as fire tests and purifies gold—though your faith is far more precious than mere gold. So when your faith remains strong through many trials, it will bring you much praise and glory and honor on the day when Jesus Christ is revealed to the whole world*"(1 Peter 1:7 NLT). Look beyond the present, consider what God intends to achieve through that trial.


*ACTION POINT*: *Consider it pure joy, my brothers and sisters, whenever you face trials of many kinds, because you know that the testing of your faith produces perseverance. Let perseverance finish its work so that you may be mature and complete, not lacking anything*(James 1:2‭-‬4 NIV).

*THE BELIEVER'S SANCTIFICATION* Pt.7

1 Thessalonians 4:7 *For God has not called us for the purpose of impurity, but in sanctification*.

*WORKING OUT YOUR SANTIFICATION*(E)


While growing up as a Christian( I'm still growing), I mean in my early years as a believer, the most common message on the pulpit then was majorly about the coming of Christ. After listening to those messages, it was always as though Christ would come after the service. Those messages kept us in check. We lived in expectation of His coming and it helped us to stay pure as apostle John said: *"Dear friends, we are already God’s children, but he has not yet shown us what we will be like when Christ appears. But we do know that we will be like him, for we will see him as he really is. And all who have this eager expectation will keep themselves pure, just as he is pure*"(1 John 3:2‭-‬3 NLT). The songs we sang those days as choruses were songs that emphasized His coming. The drama presentations focused on His coming. Some of the pictures or posters in the church told the same story.

One thing I'm very sure about is that Jesus Christ is coming. God hasn't changed his mind about that. He is coming for His own. The message about the coming of Jesus Christ is gradually waning or non existent in some circles. Little wonder why some believers live recklessly yet it is the consciousness of His coming that helps us to stay sanctified always: *"And now, dear children, remain in fellowship with Christ so that when he returns, you will be full of courage and not shrink back from him in shame. Since we know that Christ is righteous, we also know that all who do what is right are God’s children*" (1 John 2:28-29 NLT). In early church history, they have a tradition of reading the book of revelation in every service. They do that for two reasons: to remind themselves about the coming of Christ so as to be prepared and to also receive the blessings that comes with reading the book of revelation. Early church believers believe that there's a blessing that accrues when they read& listen to the book of revelation according to what is written in Chapter 1:3 saying, *"God blesses the one who reads the words of this prophecy to the church, and he blesses all who listen to its message and obey what it says, for the time is near*".


Are you still looking forward to His coming or you're just feeling cool and okay with the "Jamboree" and "prosperity Gospel" that has filled the air? Let me show you what one of the apostles of the early church said they were looking forward to: *"BUT WE ARE LOOKING FORWARD TO THE NEW HEAVENS AND NEW EARTH he has promised, a world filled with God’s righteousness. And so, dear friends, while you are waiting for these things to happen, MAKE EVERY EFFORT TO BE FOUND LIVING PEACEFUL LIVES THAT ARE PURE AND BLAMELESS  IN  HIS SIGHT*"(2 Peter 3:13‭-‬14 NLT). Those who look forward to His coming will  always "make every effort" to be pure and sanctified. Looking forward to His coming will translate into living a sanctified life.


*PRAYER*: Father, I receive the grace to look forward to the coming of Christ so as to live a sanctified life in Jesus name

*JUSTIFIED TO BE GLORIFIED*. Pt.1


Romans8: 29-30 *"For whom He foreknew, He also predestined to be conformed to the image of His Son, that He might be the firstborn among many brethren. Moreover whom He predestined, these He also called; whom He called, these He also justified; and whom He justified, these He also glorified*" NKJV


The opening text is a revelation of God's ultimate plan for those that are justified. *God's ultimate plan is the glorification of those that are justified*. In God's grand plan, experiencing the glories of the world to come and becoming ultimately like Jesus at His appearing is what God intends of His children: *"And when Christ, who is your life, is revealed to the whole world, you will share in all his glory*"(Colossians 3:4 NLT). Hallelujah!!! When Christ appears, we shall share in His glory. That's when we shall be glorified. For now, we are justified, sanctified ( positionally) but we are not yet glorified. Our glorification is in the future, we are still looking forward to it. This will happen at the appearing of Christ. Apostle Paul presented our glorification as though it is a fait accompli in the opening text.


If you're used to Pauline writings, you'll agree with me that often he writes as though all there is to be done is done and truly the Godward side is done but patient readers will also find out that there's another side to the same coin where responsibility is conferred on man. In Romans 8:29-30, he stated categorically that the justified is glorified yet in Titus 2:13, speaking about the glorification of the believer, he said: *"WHILE WE WAIT FOR THE BLESSED HOPE—the appearing of the glory of our great God and Savior, Jesus Christ*". Can you see that? We are still waiting for the "blessed hope", that is the appearing of Christ. It is until then we'll be glorified. Yes we are justified in the now as believers but we are yet to be glorified. God's desire is that we long for it.



As long as we are in Christ and Christ is in us, it is only then we have an assurance of sharing in His glory: *"For God wanted them to know that the riches and glory of Christ are for you Gentiles, too. And this is the secret: Christ lives in you. This gives you assurance of sharing his glory*"(Colossians 1:27 NLT). It is God's desire for you and me to enter into glory. Heaven is a place to be, it is a haven of rest for us after all our labours here on earth and sincerely, God want you and me to be there: *"God, for whom and through whom everything was made, chose to bring many children into glory. And it was only right that he should make Jesus, through his suffering, a perfect leader, fit to bring them into their salvation*" (Hebrews 2:10 NLT). Hallelujah!!! Knowing that a day is coming when Jesus will appear to take the saints home should help us to stay pure so that we can be partakers of that coming glory: *"Dear friends, we are already God’s children, but he has not yet shown us what we will be like when Christ appears. But we do know that we will be like him, for we will see him as he really is. And all who have this eager expectation will keep themselves pure, just as he is pure*"(1 John 3:2‭-‬3 NLT).


*PRAYER POINT*: Father, I receive the grace to look forward to that blessed hope eagerly and to live pure in readiness for your coming in Jesus name.

4/20/22, 6:49 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: PROJECTSAVE DEVOTIONAL
Wednesday,
20th April, 2022.

*JUSTIFIED TO BE GLORIFIED*. Pt.2

Psalms 17:15 As for me, I will see Your face in righteousness; I shall be satisfied when I awake in Your likeness. NKJV

*A COMPLETE LIFE CYCLE OF TRANSFORMATION*

In O'level biology, we were taught about life cycle of insects. The whole idea the teaching conveyed was how insect life developed from the egg stage to adulthood which is the final or perfect stage. For instance, the butterfly you see wasn't "born" as a butterfly. It went through a cycle of transformation from being an egg to larva to pupa and finally to becoming an adult butterfly that we cherish. The several stages it underwent is called metamorphosis. The believer in Christ is undergoing a metamorphosis. We're yet to complete our life cycle of transformation into Christ likeness: *"But we all, with open face beholding as in a glass the glory of the Lord, are changed into the same image from glory to glory, even as by the Spirit of the Lord*"( 2Cor3:18).



It is in our glorified state we shall be conformed fully to the image of Christ: *"Beloved, now we are children of God; and it has not yet been revealed what we shall be, but we know that when He is revealed, we shall be like Him, for we shall see Him as He is*" (I John 3:2 NKJV). Some are always asking if a believer can be perfect. I wish I can say "Yes" and "No" at the same time. You can only understand fully what it means to be perfect if you pay attention to what will happen in our glorification because our perfection is tied to it. For a believer, perfection is not a state rather it is a process with stages. The finality of that process shall be accomplished when we are glorified. Presently, we are not there yet. We are in the process, we're still metamorphosing daily into the image of Christ. Hallelujah!!!



Don't forget that Christ by the Spirit of God is still working in us daily to conform us: *"that He might present her to Himself a glorious church, not having spot or wrinkle or any such thing, but that she should be holy and without blemish*"(Ephesians 5:27 NKJV). The work of presenting you as a glorious church without spot or wrinkle is ongoing as you behold Him daily through His word. Christ won't stop this work until we see Him glory. Apostle Paul further said, *"who will transform our lowly body that it may be conformed to His glorious body, according to the working by which He is able even to subdue all things to Himself*"(Philippians 3:21 NKJV). The coming of Jesus is something we should long for daily. In His glorious appearing, we shall complete our life cycle of transformation into His very image, some day, we shall be like Him just as He is. Hallelujah!!!


*PRAYER*: Father, I thank you for your word. I receive the grace to submit myself to the process of transformation till I become fully like Jesus in glory.

*JUSTIFIED TO BE GLORIFIED*. Pt.3


Psalms 17:15 As for me, I will see Your face in righteousness; I shall be satisfied when I awake in Your likeness. NKJV


*A PERFECT PLACE TO BE*


Looking at the level of degradation in our world today, we may not understand Genesis 1:31 fully: *"Then God saw everything that He had made, and indeed it was very good. So the evening and the morning were the sixth day*". When God created the world, everything in it was good. There was perfect order and harmony. Adam and Eve lived in "paradise". We can't compare the glories of Eden with the most beautiful place on earth. Insecurity wasn't an issue before sin entered the world because God secured the earth. However, when sin entered through Adam, "paradise" was lost.  Today, terrorism is a global menace, wars and natural disasters has degraded our world. There are issues of environmental concerns that has warranted several conventions yet no solution.


Living in glory for the believer will mean living in a perfect world different from what obtains here on earth: *"There will be no more night. They will not need the light of a lamp or the light of the sun, for the Lord God will give them light. And they will reign for ever and ever*" (Revelation22:5). We all love beautiful and serene environments. The beauty of Paris is the reason people often say "See Paris And Die". Paris may be the most or one of the most beautiful places on the planet earth, yet it is subject to the corruption and decay that exist on this earth. Stop living for things that will fade away: *"And this world is fading away, along with everything that people crave. But anyone who does what pleases God will live forever*" ( 1John2:17 NLT).


Living to please Him and Him alone should be our desire. Here is not home. Heaven is the real home. That song writer said, "This world is not home, I'm just passing through, my treasures are laid up somewhere beyond the blues, the angels beckon me from heaven's open door and I can't feel at home in this world any more". When we leave here, we'll go home. We are not there yet. *Then I saw a new heaven and a new earth, for the old heaven and the old earth had disappeared. And the sea was also gone. And I saw the holy city, the new Jerusalem, coming down from God out of heaven like a bride beautifully dressed for her husband. I heard a loud shout from the throne, saying, “Look, God’s home is now among his people! He will live with them, and they will be his people. God himself will be with them. He will wipe every tear from their eyes, and there will be no more death or sorrow or crying or pain. All these things are gone forever*" (Revelation 21:1‭-‬4 NLT)


*PRAYER*: Father, I thank you for the promise of that blessed home in heaven. I receive grace to walk worthy of you in Jesus name

4/22/22, 7:39 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: PROJECTSAVE DEVOTIONAL
Friday,
April 22nd, 2022.



*JUSTIFIED TO BE GLORIFIED*. Pt.4



Psalms 17:15 As for me, I will see Your face in righteousness; I shall be satisfied when I awake in Your likeness. NKJV



*A PLACE OF FELLOWSHIP*


The word "Fellowship" is from the Greek "Koinonia". It means to be intimate. Fellowship is intimacy. Often, I illustrate intimacy using couples. I have seen couples who began to resemble each other after years of marriage. Initially, they looked differently but the moment they became intimately united to each, the journey to resemblance began. In fact, sometimes, their offsprings can have one or two things in their physical look that they picked from both of them. For instance, the shape of the head may look like that of the father but the face may look like that of the mother. That's the power of intimacy.




The kind of fellowship we have presently is just a foretaste of glory. When we enter into glory, we shall experience true fellowship. The intimacy will not be forced, it will be natural. It is until then we shall know all things because we'll be closely knitted with the Godhead: *"Now we see things imperfectly, like puzzling reflections in a mirror, but then we will see everything with perfect clarity. All that I know now is partial and incomplete, but then I will know everything completely, just as God now knows me completely*"(1 Corinthians 13:12 NLT). In that kingdom, our fellowship will not be with God only but with one another. We shall meet with saints of all ages: *"And people will come from all over the world—from east and west, north and south—to take their places in the Kingdom of God*"(Luke 13:29 NLT).




In glory, "Eden" will be restored. In that garden, God has fellowship with man but sin broke that fellowship. God longs for us more than we long for Him. The reason He gave us His Spirit is to fellowship with us because every other thing we need is embedded in fellowship. Little wonder why our benediction says, *"May the grace of the Lord Jesus Christ, the love of God, AND THE FELLOWSHIP OF THE HOLY SPIRIT BE WITH YOU ALL*"(2 Corinthians 13:14 NLT). No matter the depth of fellowship we have here and with one another, it is just a foretaste of glory and what is to come. The real fellowship will take place up there. Strive to be there.



*PRAYER*: Father, my heart longs to be with you, to fellowship with you and behold you just as you are. I receive grace to walk worthy of you in Jesus name.

*JUSTIFIED TO BE GLORIFIED*. Pt.5


Psalms 17:15 As for me, I will see Your face in righteousness; I shall be satisfied when I awake in Your likeness. NKJV


*WE SHALL REIGN AND RULE*


After the rapture of the saints, we shall spend a period of seven years in heaven where we shall stand before the "Bema" judgement and partake in the marriage supper of the lamb. In "Bema" judgement, that's where we shall receive the reward for our service here on earth( 2Cor5:10). After which we shall partake in the marriage supper of the lamb with our groom- Christ( Rev19:7-10) This will last for seven years while the earth will experience seven years of great tribulation and the reign of the anti Christ(Mt24:21). When seven years is over, Christ shall fight the anti Christ in a war known as Armageddon. He shall conquer him and then set up His millennial kingdom. Though this order of event is hotly debated but I believe in pre-tribulation rapture. That's where I stand.




All believers who will rapture with Christ shall return to reign and rule with Him in the millennia kingdom. They shall sit over cities as rulers and judges: *“‘Well done!’ the king exclaimed. ‘You are a good servant. You have been faithful with the little I entrusted to you, so you will be governor of ten cities as your reward*".(Luke 19:17 NLT). In fact, apostle Paul made it clear that we shall judge the world and angels: *"Don’t you realize that someday we believers will judge the world? And since you are going to judge the world, can’t you decide even these little things among yourselves? Don’t you realize that we will judge angels? So you should surely be able to resolve ordinary disputes in this life*"( 1 Corinthians 6:2‭-‬3 NLT)



In glory, we shall serve Christ and be actively involved in leadership. While we know that the atmosphere shall be filled with worship, we won't be excluded from governance as well. Luke 22:29-30 says, *"and just as My Father has granted Me a kingdom, I grant you that you may eat and drink at My table in My kingdom, and you will sit on thrones judging the twelve tribes of Israel*". Nothing compares with the glory ahead. There's so much about our glorification that our minds can't capture but we shall understand it better bye and bye. This hope should stir us to live a purified life: *"Dear friends, now we are children of God, and what we will be has not yet been made known. But we know that when Christ appears, we shall be like him, for we shall see him as he is. All who have this hope in him purify themselves, just as he is pure*"(1 John 3:2-3 NIV).


*PRAYER*: Father, I want to reign in your kingdom. Help me Lord.

DEVOTIONAL 0491 | Source start line: 43949
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
4/24/22, 6:21 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: ProjectSave Devotional
Sunday,
24th April 2022.


*JUSTIFIED TO BE GLORIFIED*. Pt.6


Psalms 17:15 As for me, I will see Your face in righteousness; I shall be satisfied when I awake in Your likeness. NKJV


*NO LIMITATIONS IN GLORY*


One day, I wanted to explain the concept of rapture to my boys and what will happen when the trumpet sounds. We were together in the living room watching cartoon when I saw one of the characters disappear and re appear in another location. From there, I started my teaching. I told them the day will come when we shall varnish into thin air and appear in heaven. There and then, the limitations on this body will no longer exist.  In this current body, there are limitations. Apostle Paul said, *"While we live in these earthly bodies, we groan and sigh, but it’s not that we want to die and get rid of these bodies that clothe us. Rather, we want to put on our new bodies so that these dying bodies will be swallowed up by life*" (2Cor5:4).



A writer said, "Down here sin limits us; we are freed from its guilt and penalty, but we possess a fallen nature and we live in a fallen body which is subject to sin’s power and Satan’s attacks. Down here our bodies give us a great deal of trouble, and we experience weakness, sickness, pain and the infirmities of age, and ultimately death itself – Romans 8:23 tells us this." Apostle Paul says, *"And we believers also groan, even though we have the Holy Spirit within us as a foretaste of future glory, for we long for our bodies to be released from sin and suffering. We, too, wait with eager hope for the day when God will give us our full rights as his adopted children, including the new bodies he has promised us*"(Romans 8:23 NLT).



In glory, there shall be no sickness, pain, tears or death: *"He will wipe every tear from their eyes. There will be no more death’ or mourning or crying or pain, for the old order of things has passed away*"( Rev21:4). Reading the same book of Revelation further, Chapter 22:3 says, *"No longer will there be any curse. The throne of God and of the Lamb will be in the city, and his servants will serve him*". After the fall of man, creation came under God's curse(Gen3:17-19). That brough a great limitation upon man. When we enter into glory, nothing shall be accursed.



Song of the day: https://youtu.be/2_1CyA7iDuY


Connect on social media:

IG: @Projectsave_ministries
@lanreoyeleke

Twitter: @Lanrezion01

Email: info@projectsaveng.org




*HAPPY SUNDAY*

*JUSTIFIED TO BE GLORIFIED*. Pt.7

Psalms 17:15 As for me, I will see Your face in righteousness; I shall be satisfied when I awake in Your likeness. NKJV

*AN ATMOSPHERE OF UNENDING WORSHIP*

Have you ever been bothered about your inability to sing according to tune? If yes, I have a goodnews for you. In glory, you'll not need voice training neither will you need to take music classes. Over there, you'll sing perfectly. I've been musically inclined from childhood. I know the way discordant tunes sound and when someone sings off key. I have seen zealous persons in the choir but without an iota of music in them. They give the most troubles in choir rehearsals because they often sing out of tune. It gives most of them concern because harsh choir directors may excuse them from the group. Don't be heart broken if you're in that category, when we enter into glory, we'll not need all the voice training. Our new bodies will be custom- made to worship God: *"And then I heard every creature in heaven and on earth and under the earth and in the sea. They sang: “Blessing and honor and glory and power belong to the one sitting on the throne and to the Lamb forever and ever*" (Revelation 5:13 NLT).



Worship is beyond music, making music is just part it. Worship is service and total surrender from a pure heart. The totality of all we shall be doing in heaven is "Worship" because our lives will be totally immersed in divinity. Presently, the reason God is sending out His sons to different unreached places on the earth is to ensure that there are souls from every tribes who will ultimately bring Him worship: And they sang a new song with these words: *“You are worthy to take the scroll and break its seals and open it. For you were slaughtered, and your blood has ransomed people for God from EVERY TRIBE AND LANGUAGE and people and nation. And you have caused them to become a Kingdom of priests for our God. And they will reign on the earth*"(Revelation 5:9-10 NLT). When we go from one tribe to the other doing mission work, the ultimate goal of God is to get people from every tribe and language of the world to bring Him worship.


The summary of all we shall be doing in glory is "Worship". Words cannot totally describe in details the glories of that heavenly home. I want to seize this opportunity to invite you to Jesus if you're yet to give your life to Him. Say this prayer out loud: *"Father, I know I'm a sinner. I confess Jesus Christ as my Lord and personal saviour. I believe He died and resurrected for my sake. Accept me in the beloved and write my name in the book of life. Thank you Jesus, in Jesus name. Amen*. Also, If your commitment to Christ is half hearted or may be your love for Christ has gone cold, the prayer below is for you: *"Father, I thank you because of the blessed hope. Lord, I need a revival of holiness and deep longing for that eternal home with you. Help me to retrace my steps back to the cross and keep me near the cross always. Let my heart burn for you always in Jesus name*".

Song of The Day: https://youtu.be/BRPGRdbGHSs


info@projectsaveng.org

DEVOTIONAL 0493 | Source start line: 44205

4/26/22, 6:39 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: ProjectSave Devotional
Tuesday
26th April, 2022.


*WISDOM FOR SINGLES*


Proverbs21:9 It’s better to live alone in the corner of an attic than with a quarrelsome wife in a lovely home. NLT


Sometimes ago, I was amazed at the outrage on social media over the teaching of a revered servant of God on marriage. He simply taught what is written in scriptures on love and submission in marriage. This man has been married for a over three decades without drame. A lot of those who criticized him may not be enjoying the marital bliss he's experiencing but they simply didn't fancy word-based instruction for making marriage work. We don't bend or twist God's word for popular opinion. The word is what it is. Nothing can change eternal principles. The Bible says, *"Your word, LORD, is eternal; it stands firm in the heavens*"(Psl119:89).



I often say that a believer doesn't need "Marriage seminar" rather what we need is a "Word Seminar". All that we need for life( marriage inclusive) is in the word. If we have the word taught clearly and we follow it, we'll have peaceful marriages. Before I got married, I learnt a lot from the ministry of Bishop David Oyedepo. I think it is wise we learn from men and women whose lives are producing result. He emphasized on reading books on marriage and studying the book of Proverbs Chapter 4-7 to understand more about the immoral woman and the kind of woman that a man shouldn't marry.  I read the book of proverbs especially those chapters 4-7 for weeks. As I read further, In Prov 21:9, the bible spoke about a quarrelsome wife. Immediately, I knew that kind of woman won't help my life. *Dear single, no matter how spiritual or beautiful a lady is, if she has tendencies for being quarrelsome, don't marry her. Also, if a man is a fighter, don't marry him*. That's a biblical recommendation.




Forget about seeing him/her in a vision or his charisma. Don't marry "anointing", check his/her values.  *A life that is not Christ-like is not worth uniting with in a sacred institution like marriage.* There are certain characters that can hardly be hidden in people if we pay attention to them. Your love shouldn't be blind. We've read about husbands killing wives and vice versa. It is better to live on the rooftop than to sleep on the same bed with a quarrelsome spouse. If you start receiving slaps in courtship and you're experiencing outburst of anger in a terrific manner, you should know the future spell doom. *It is better to be single trusting God to be married rightly than to be married praying to God to solve marital crisis.*


*PRAYER*: I wisdom to choose wisely in Jesus name, Amen.

4/27/22, 6:08 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: PROJECTSAVE DEVOTIONAL
Wednesday,
27th April, 2022.


*LIKE AHIMAAZ*?


2Samuel18: 22 But Ahimaaz continued to plead with Joab, “Whatever happens, please let me go, too.“Why should you go, my son?” Joab replied. “There will be no reward for your news.” 23 “Yes, but let me go anyway,” he begged. Joab finally said, “All right, go ahead.” So Ahimaaz took the less demanding route by way of the plain and ran to Mahanaim ahead of the Ethiopian.NLT


Do you know the man called Ahimaaz the son of Zadok? Let me refresh your memory about this old testament character. When Joab needed someone to deliver the news of Absalom's death to David, Ahimaaz volunteered to go but Joab was sensitive enough to know that he  won't deliver the message properly to David. He restrained him from going but this man pleaded. Reluctantly, Joab permitted him. Though Joab had sent an Ethiopian to deliver the message to David but Ahimaaz took a short route and outran the Ethiopian. When David asked him about Absalom his son, his response  didn't make much sense so he was told to stay aside. When the Ethiopian was asked the same question, his wise response hit the nail on the head and communicated the sad event to the heart of David. Ahimaaz was not sent, he didn't have the message for that hour. He may have delivered well at other times but that very assignment wasn't meant for him. Little wonder why his response didn't fulfill the intended purpose.


If I may ask, who sent you? That someone around is doing a program and pulling crowd doesn't mean you should go and do same. That you have the charisma that can hold people's attention is not tantamount to a call to pioneer a church. *Whatever God hasn't sent you won't make much sense to your listeners*. I see a generation of preachers, teachers, ministers and believers trying so hard to deliver a message God hasn't sent them. I listened to a preacher who preached the message I heard from another preacher. The original preacher of that message preached with so great anointing to the extent that at bus stops, music stores kept playing that particular message severally. This other preacher preached the same message without inspiration and anointing. He copied another man's message without having the grace and anointing that backed it. *One of the deceits of the enemy for those in ministry is to make you feel you can do what the person next door is doing without checking with God to know if it His will.* Ministers of the Gospel often fall into this trap. I once considered starting a church in the heart of Lagos years back because I saw many young men of my age doing so not until God stopped me.


*There are many echoes today but only few voices. I have decided to be a voice not an echo*. It is not about social media popularity or numerous posts on social media but by divine originality.  Some don't copy messages alone but even mannerisms. Today, we have so many 'thus saith the Lord' when God hasn't spoken? They are trying to deliver a message God didn't send them thereby attracting a "woe" into their lives.  Ezekiel 13:3 says, *"This is what the Sovereign LORD says: Woe to the foolish prophets who follow their own spirit and have seen nothing!*". I see you're trying hard to leave a 'spiritual impression' in the hearts of men when God hasn't sent you. This generation won't take you serious if your message isn't original. *God will only maintain what he ordain*. Are you sent?


PRAYER: Father, I receive the grace to stay where you put me and to run the errand you've sent me In Jesus name. Amen

DEVOTIONAL 0495 | Source start line: 44464

4/28/22, 6:29 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: PROJECTSAVE DEVOTIONAL
Thursday,
28th April,  2022.


*HOW YOUR LIFE SHOULD BE GUIDED*.


Psalms 37:23 The LORD directs the steps of the godly. He delights in every detail of their lives. NLT


I heard the story of a man who gave his life to Christ in a church meeting. On his way home, armed robbers waylaid him and others. He was asked in the usual manner armed robbers ask their victims, "Your life or your money"? His response was "I just gave my life to Christ in the church today so I have it no more with me. Also, I gave my money as offering, I have it no more with me." I cannot ascertain if the story is real or a fiction but therein lies deep truth about the life of a believer. *Right from the very day you gave your life to Christ, it ceased to be yours. Your life is no more with you.* Apostle Paul says, *"For you died to this life, and your real life is hidden with Christ in God*"(Colossians 3:3 NLT). The government of your life has changed power and handed over to a new master and Lord which is Jesus Christ. Any effort made to control your life is rebellion against the leadership of your life.



Apostle Paul said, *"He died for everyone so that those who receive his new life will no longer live for themselves. Instead, they will live for Christ, who died and was raised for them*(2 Corinthians 5:15 NLT). This is the truth about your life as a believer, your life is not your own. You are meant to live your life for Jesus and Him alone. It is a regimented life. You obey as He commands. Those who understand the Christian life from this perspective will never take any step without consulting the one who owns them. They won't go anywhere or change their location except their master say so. Neither will they negotiate instructions with Him. *The idea of negotiating instructions with God is alien to the culture of the scriptures*. It draws men into what looks like God's will but often they are off the track.




I once had a chat with someone about relocating abroad. In our conversation, I made it known to the person that the base of my assignment is here in Africa. I cannot move unless God says so because He alone direct the affairs of my life. The response I got was, "There are souls abroad too. You can do missions there as well"( paraphrase). While that may be true, the one whom I'm living for hasn't sent me there for now. What some are doing today is what I call "convenient living". Their belief is that whatever is "good and convenient" for them is God's will for their life so even if God calls them to do an assignment in a not so convenient terrain, they'll only accept to do it on their terms and not on His. It is possible to be in ministry but in the wrong location no matter how good the place looks. *Many have missed God because they don't trust Him enough to lead them aright*. That's the reason some singles ask funny questions like "What if God tells me to marry someone I don't like"? Some already believe that God may not give them their taste, they quickly come up with the idea of presenting their "candidate" to God for endorsement. *Those who live for Him seek to be guided by Him in all things. This is how your life should be guided*.


*PRAYER*: Father, I receive the grace to submit totally to the Lordship of Christ in Jesus name.

DEVOTIONAL 0496 | Source start line: 44672

4/29/22, 6:52 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: PROJECTSAVE DEVOTIONAL
Friday,
April 29th, 2022.


*DERAILING FROM PURPOSE*

Prov 27:8 A person who strays from home is like a bird that strays from its nest.


In the early days of our ministry, a senior minister I hold in high esteem told me that what I'm doing will still metamorphose into a church. He spoke like someone giving counsel not by prophecy. If he had spoken from a prophetic insight, I may have given it a second thought but I recognized the source of his counsel. He probably felt being in ministry automatically must translate into pioneering a church. Actually, I've seen this abberation over the years. Those who should be on the field or run an independent teaching ministry starting a church.  I met another person who pledged to support me if I start a church. He told me that young guys of my age that pioneered churches are making it and doing well. Probably, he looked at my condition and he felt my life will improve if I become a church founder. He couldn't wrap his head around seeing me going on evangelism to villages and bushes where I won't get fat offering.


If God has called you to do something for him, one great enemy you must fight is counsel that will distract and derail you from fulfilling your assignment. Often, such counsel come from trusted friends and families. Those who care about you. Actually, they always mean well when they present their counsel. However, their counsel may not align with God's purpose and will for your life. When Jesus came to the earth, they wanted to make him a king: *"Jesus, knowing that they intended to come and make him king by force, withdrew again to a mountain by himself*"( John6:15). The Jews thought to themselves that their liberator had come because they were under the rulership of Romans. Who knows if the elders of the land had consulted with Jesus about their intention to crown Him their king. They may have told him not to worry about his ministry as His kingly role won't affect it. He stuck to his guns. That's why you and I are beneficiaries of His atoning work.


Be sure of the pattern God gave to you for life and ministry. After receiving the pattern, stick to it: *"Be sure that you make everything according to the pattern I have shown you here on the mountain*"(Ex25:40). Once you derail from the pattern and instruction given to you, many will suffer from your disobedience. You will always fight the enemy call "Good Counsel". I am in no way saying you shouldn't listen take counsel or submit to mentors. The point here is that "good counsel" may not necessarily be God's counsel. What you need is "Right counsel" that aligns with God's purpose for your life.  Beware of counsel that derail from God's purpose.


*PRAYER*: I receive the grace to stick to the pattern of God for my life and ministry in Jesus name.

4/30/22, 9:12 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: ProjectSave Devotional
Saturday,
30th April, 2022.


*BEWARE OF ANTI BIBLE PHILOSOPHIES* Pt.1


Col2:8 *Don’t let anyone capture you with empty philosophies and high-sounding nonsense that come from human thinking and from the spiritual powers of this world, rather than from Christ*.


We are currently facing a dire situation this end time that is already consuming the ignorant and confusing the elect. I call it  "knowledge crisis". This is simply because there's increase in knowledge more than ever before. Social media is filled with several thoughts that has confused many and made thousands to stray. Unfortunately, some preachers or teachers push these heresies with subtlety but they don't even know the origin of what they're saying  That leaves us with mind boggling questions: "Is there an alignment between new found knowledge and the bible?" "What does the bible say about the several views being propounded"? Truly, we are in the end of times: *"But thou, O Daniel, shut up the words, and seal the book, even to the time of the end: many shall run to and fro, and knowledge shall be increased*"( Dan12:4). The increase in knowledge has its good sides but the negativity has caused many to stray simply because any knowledge that stands opposed to the knowledge of the risen Christ as revealed in the Bible is toxic and inimical to our walk with God.


Our generation is not the first to be confronted with with anti bible philosophies. It has been from time immemorial. It will surprise you to know that Zoroaster, the founder of Zoroastrianism lived in the time of Moses as suggested by researchers. Right from after the fall when man ate from the tree of knowledge of good and evil, the devil built his citadel of learning with men and raised teachers who will spread anti bible falsehood. This they do vigorously with all conviction. In the time of Paul, he lived in the age of philosophers most of whom have anti bible philosophical leanings. That's why he said: *Don’t let anyone capture you with empty philosophies and high-sounding nonsense that come from human thinking and from the spiritual powers of this world, rather than from Christ*( Col 2:8). What we are seeing or hearing today is not new.




The end time battle or warfare that we often deemphasize is "knowledge warfare". The enemy is using wrong knowledge to draw many into error. Unfortunately, there are many preachers who can't even identify these errors and refute it simply because they lack training. Paul saw it coming years ago and he said:  *"We use God’s mighty weapons, not worldly weapons, to knock down the STRONGHOLDS OF HUMAN REASONING AND TO DESTROY FALSE ARGUMENTS*"[4NLT], *We demolish ARGUMENTS( referring to anti bible philosophies) and every pretension THAT SETS ITSELF UP AGAINST THE KNOWLEDGE OF GOD, and we take captive every thought to make it obedient to Christ*( 2 Cor 10:4-5, emphasis mine). True warfare is knowledge warfare. The devil seeks to corrupt the truth of scriptures in order to deceive the elect of God. Only bible truth addicts will overcome in this warfare and make it home. The trick he( Satan) used in Eden is still the same: *"...“DID GOD REALLY SAY you must not eat the fruit from any of the trees in the garden?”(Genesis 3:1 NLT). It is time to know what God has said in scriptures and stick to it so that we won't be consumed by high sounding nonsense and empty philosophies of this age.


*PRAYER*: Father, I thank you for sending your word to me. Holy Spirit, reveal the truths in scriptures to me and help me stick to it in Jesus name.
