*BEWARE OF ANTI BIBLE PHILOSOPHIES* Pt.2


Col2:8 *Don’t let anyone capture you with empty philosophies and high-sounding nonsense that come from human thinking and from the spiritual powers of this world, rather than from Christ*.


*NEW AGE BELIEFS*


There's a man whose posts I read online. Reading his post is not deliberate but his topics and captions often draw my attention. A friend introduced me to his writings. The brother who introduced me to his wall once dubbed him 'a reformer' stylishly because this guy attacks established doctrines and teachings of the bible. His conclusions are somewhat derailing and it soothes the flesh. He's been able to convince those who want to indulge in sin to go ahead as long as it doesn't hurt their neighbours. Later, I realized he is a new ager. Unfortunately, he pastors a church and has some "theological training".


One anti bible philosophical
belief of this age that you and me must run from and filter out is known as "New Age Beliefs".
What exactly is the New Age? It is Impossible to narrow down. The New Age is actually a vast cocktail of beliefs and practices that is permissive of any action that is deemed good. The New Age beliefs and practices are drawn from Buddhism and Taoism, psychology, and psycho-therapy; paganism, clairvoyance, tarot and magic. New age beliefs is seasoned with liberal thoughts and philosophies that stand opposed to God's word. David Spangler is one of the voices that promulgated what we know today as "New Age".


New age belief is a have-it-your-way religion. It is a self-pleasing way of life. It seeks instant gratification. The new agers will tell you "As long as it is good for you and does not hurt the other person, then you can do it ". Hey! That sounds cool but have you ever considered the fact that something can be good for you and not hurt your neighbour yet not right in the sight of God? Proverbs 14:12 says, *"There is a way that appears to be right, but in the end it leads to death.*" Virtually almost all sinful acts are beneficial to man in one way or the other. God's standard remain the same regardless how we feel and He won't lower that standard for anyone. To know what God stands for on any issue, check His word.


*PRAYER*: I receive grace not to be deceived and to follow God's word in Jesus name.

5/2/22, 7:17 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: PROJECTSAVE DEVOTIONAL

2nd May, 2022.


*BEWARE OF ANTI BIBLE PHILOSOPHIES* Pt.3


Col2:8 *Don’t let anyone capture you with empty philosophies and high-sounding nonsense that come from human thinking and from the spiritual powers of this world, rather than from Christ*.


*RELIGIOUS PLURALISM*


A popular American preacher was on air years back. He is the  pastor of a mega church in the United States of America. He was asked if Jesus is the only way. He replied by saying "I don't know if Jesus is the only way".  Now, one would expect a Pastor of thousands to take a stand about Jesus publicly but he took a dangerous position that isn't expected of a new convert. The view he  expressed indirectly is that "Jesus could be or not". That response sounded like that of one who is in support of religious pluralism ideology. What is religious pluralism? Steve Cowan says, *"This is the view that all (religious) roads lead to God; that each religion is equally valid; that no one religion can or should claim to be the only legitimate way to God*.   John Hick, a pluralist, says, “There is not merely one way but a plurality of ways of salvation . . . taking place in different ways within the contexts of all the great religious traditions.”  Now this is a dangerous position or ideology. Some believers also take this position too.



Can we look at scriptures to see what God has to say about this? In John 14:6(AMP), Jesus Himself said, *"Jesus said to him, “I am the [only] Way [to God] and the [real] Truth and the [real] Life; no one comes to the Father but through Me*". JESUS IS THE ONLY WAY. Some critics call this assertion "Christian Particularism" while some call it "Religious Exclusivism". Whatever name they tag the bolded assertion, I know deep within my heart that Jesus is the only way to heaven. Anyone who doesn't accept Jesus Christ as Lord and saviour will not enter heaven: *"He who has the Son [by accepting Him as Lord and Savior] has the life [that is eternal]; he who does not have the Son of God [by personal faith] does not have the life*"( 1John5:12). Every religion claims to worship God but Christ is the missing link. Whoever want to see God, communicate or fellowship with God must go through Jesus Christ and no one else. Buddha, Mohammed, Confucius, and other religious leaders walked the surface of the earth but none of them was bold enough to lay claim to being the way to God only Jesus did.



In Acts 4:12,  Peter said, *“there is no other name under heaven given to men by which we must be saved*” (Acts 4:12). Now I feel like screaming "JESUS". No other name or personality is loaded  and capable of bringing a man to the father. *If you want to see God, look at Jesus*. About Him the Bible says,  *"Christ is the visible image of the invisible God. He existed before anything was created and is supreme over all creation*"( Col 1:15). If we understand Christ this way, we'll be eager to evangelize and reach the world for Him because He is the only way to God. Religious pluralism undermines the need for evangelism and missions. Holding on to the pluralist ideology makes one see evangelism as "fanaticism" or bigotry. Maybe you're not enthusiastic about Missions because you think every unsaved person will still make it to heaven anyway if they hold on to the tenets of their religion. That's not true. Even if they are committed to good works without putting their faith in Christ, they're going to hell. An unbeliever is someone to be pitied regardless of how nice they are. Don't get caught in the ideology of religious pluralism. JESUS IS THE ONLY WAY.


*PRAYER*: Father, I stand in the gap for every unsaved person I know right now, Lord may they see Christ as the only way to God. May they see the futility of religion and embrace Christ in Jesus name.

DEVOTIONAL 0500 | Source start line: 45004
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
5/3/22, 6:46 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: ProjectSave Devotional
Tuesday
3rd May,2022.

*BEWARE OF ANTI BIBLE PHILOSOPHIES* Pt.4


Col2:8 *Don’t let anyone capture you with empty philosophies and high-sounding nonsense that come from human thinking and from the spiritual powers of this world, rather than from Christ*.

*RELATIVISM*

There's an old philosophical ideology called "Relativism". Often, you have heard when people make statements like "Truth is relative." *Here, relativism mean that whether a particular statement is true or not is a matter of personal preference*. In essence, they're are saying what is true for "A" may not be true for "B*. If I say, "I believe in God" or "Jesus is the only way" a relativist will say "That's only true from my perspective/experience but not a general truth for all". *What relativism attacks is the universality of truth especially the truth of scriptures*. Relativist also believes that culture and world view colours our perspective. That is, "right" and "wrong" is  determined by our cultural perspectives so nothing is absolutely "right" or "wrong". All is based on our cultural perspectives. This philosophical ideology prevents us from judging anything as wrong because "Right" is relative. If we subject this theory to scriptural scrutiny, so many things are wrong with it.



*A philosophical ideology like this undermines the authority of the bible simply because  it places the truths of scriptures on the scale of relativism*. A relativist will quickly call the bible "Jewish book" or "Greco- Roman book" simply because he believes what is written in the Bible is coloured with Jewish laws and customs or the ancient Greco-Roman ideologies. A relativist will see the truths in the Bible as "Jewish truths" that doesn't apply to him. That's a dangerous position. The bible says,  *"Your word, LORD, is eternal; it stands firm in the heavens*" ( Psl 119:89). Biblical truths are not relative, they are universal. What is true about sin, righteousness, sanctification, rapture and other Bible themes in Africa is the same in Europe, America, Australia and other parts of the world. No difference. *What the bible DESCRIBES may be in the mould of the culture of that time but what the bible PRESCRIBES is universal, it breaks all moulds*.


Another evil of relativism especially in Africa is to make us feel Christianity is "White Man's Religion". This has made African Traditional Religion to thrive. The African relativist will tell you the truths in the bible is a "Western Construct" thereby making you feel he has another set of truths that he lives by. It is like saying "the white man is correct because that's what his culture permits, I am also correct because this what my forebears handed to me". The bible says, *"Jesus Christ is the same yesterday and today and forever*"( Heb13:8). Nothing is relative about Christ. He remains the saviour of the world. The life of Christ in us knows no race, color or culture. Don't subscribe to relativism in handing scriptural matters. This ideology has resurfaced. We can't handle scriptural truths with this ideology and still remain true to our faith. Beware!!!


*PRAYER*: Father, I receive the grace to stick to the truth and not place it on the scale of relativism in Jesus name.

DEVOTIONAL 0501 | Source start line: 45082
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
5/4/22, 5:59 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: PROJECTSAVE DEVOTIONAL
Wednesday,
4th May, 2022.


*BEWARE OF ANTI BIBLE PHILOSOPHIES* Pt.5


Col2:8 *Don’t let anyone capture you with empty philosophies and high-sounding nonsense that come from human thinking and from the spiritual powers of this world, rather than from Christ*.

*HISTORICAL JESUS*

Have you heard or read about "Historical Jesus Research"? If you haven't, I'll explain what this is all about. Those who hold on to this theory are saying that the Jesus we believe is different from the Jesus in history.  That is, the Jesus that Christians have believed in for 2000 years and who is described in the four New Testament Gospels is not the Jesus who really walked the roads of Palestine in the first century.  The picture presented to us by the gospels is said to be encrusted with traditions and myths created by the early church. This view present Jesus as a mythological figure that must be demythologized. Some even view Him as a philosopher. This historical Jesus view or theory is the reason some are saying the true Jesus that lived and walked the roads of Palestine married. Recently, I heard a preacher claimed that Jesus married eight wives. Some claimed he had a girl friend who bore him a child. All these assertions are an attack on the person of Jesus. *I believe in the Christ revealed in the Bible*.


The Jesus presented to us in the bible is the real Jesus not an historical Jesus: *"Jesus Christ is the same yesterday, today, and forever*" (Hebrews 13:8 NLT). He is not a philosopher like Plato, Aristotle, Cicero neither is he a mythological figure, He is real. Read what John the beloved said about Him: *"That which was from the beginning, which we have heard, which we have seen with our eyes, which we have looked upon, and our hands have handled, of the Word of life; (for the life was manifested, and we have seen it, and bear witness, and shew unto you that eternal life, which was with the Father, and was manifested unto us;)*"(1 John 1:1-2 KJV). Hallelujah!!! John presented to us a living Jesus that he saw, heard and touched. That's the Jesus of the bible that I believe in. He further made it clear that fellowship is possible with him: *"that which we have seen and heard declare we unto you, that ye also may have fellowship with us: and truly our fellowship is with the Father, and with his Son Jesus Christ*"(1 John 1:3 KJV). If Christ were to be a mythological figure, fellowship would have been impossible.


The Jesus of the bible is GOD: *"No one has ever seen God. But the unique One, WHO IS HIMSELF GOD, is near to the Father’s heart. He has revealed God to us*"(John 1:18 NLT). Let's check another scripture to confirm this truth: *“You must have the same attitude that Christ Jesus had. Though HE WAS GOD, he did not think of equality with God as something to cling to*” (Philippians 2:5-6). Paul using the past tense "Was" doesn't mean Jesus isn't God. He only used that tense to illustrate who Jesus was( and still is) when He wanted to take a subordinate role of a sufferer in His incarnation. Even old testament prophecy recognized Him as God: *"For to us a child is born, to us a son is given, and the government will be on his shoulders. And he will be called Wonderful Counselor, MIGHTY GOD, Everlasting Father, Prince of Peace*"( Isa9:6). The Jesus I serve is not a figure in history, He is God himself. To create discrepancies about the person of Jesus is to undermine His deity and who the bible truly say He is.


*CONFESSION*: Jesus Christ is my God. He is my saviour, my Lord, my deliverer. I'm glad He's in me and I have fellowship with Him. I'm in love with him and He's in love with me. Some day, I shall see Him and be conformed to His very image. Hallelujah!!!

N:B Join us to pray in the Spirit 9pm ( Nigerian time) every night on zoom:https://us02web.zoom.us/j/86794796510?pwd=VncrZjBCWE9yWFJaVStPOUZ2UC92dz09



info@projectsaveng.org

DEVOTIONAL 0502 | Source start line: 45151
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
5/5/22, 6:12 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: PROJECTSAVE DEVOTIONAL
Thursday,
5th May, 2022.



*BEWARE OF ANTI BIBLE PHILOSOPHIES* Pt.6



Col2:8 *Don’t let anyone capture you with empty philosophies and high-sounding nonsense that come from human thinking and from the spiritual powers of this world, rather than from Christ*.



*OPEN THEISM*.



Some may wonder why we have to learn anti biblical philosophies. The bible says, *"in order that Satan might not outwit us. For we are not unaware of his schemes*"(2Cor 2:11). In order not to be outsmarted by the devil, we must be fully aware of his schemes. Today, the enemy has outwitted many believers through deception. The spirit of seduction has seduced them with wrong knowledge about God, bible and the Christian life. One of the demons at work this end time is known as the spirit of seduction: *"The Spirit clearly says that in later times some will abandon the faith and follow deceiving spirits and things taught by demons*"( 1 Tim4:1). 2 Timothy 3:13 makes a frightening statement. It says, *“But evil men and seducers shall wax worse and worse, deceiving and being deceived*”. In the times we are living in, it should be obvious to any believer who is paying attention, that more and more people are being deceived in our churches and in life, generally through high sounding philosophical ideologies. Shouldn't we learn these things to avoid being lured into error? What makes some of these errors appealing sometimes is that it is coated with a little truth. This makes people swallow it hook line and sinker. *Nothing is as dangerous as errors mixed with truth*.




What is Open Theism all about? Open theists believe God's foreknowledge about the future is imperfect. According to this view, it is believed that if God knows the future perfectly, then it automatically mean man's action whether good or bad cannot be attributed to his free will or decision. That is the fore knowledge of God determines man's action, his decision or free will is not involved. Also, they hold on to scriptures or bible references where it was said that God "repented" or change His mind as a proof that the immutability of God is false. It  So the Open Theist prefer to believe that God's knowledge of the future is imperfect so as to accommodate man's imperfections or freedom of decision. It is another way of saying "God doesn't know all things" and that He changes like shifting shadows. This theory undermines the sovereignty, omniscience and immutability of God. Unfortunately, open theists are mostly liberal evangelicals.




The bible is our final authority on matters of doctrine and that's the only book or reference material we can turn to. What does the bible have to say about this? *“Our God is in heaven; he does whatever pleases him*"(Psalm 115:3). God's sovereignty is unquestionable. He does as He pleases, but know that God is not arbitrary. Paul said, *“Oh, the depth of the riches of the wisdom and knowledge of God! How unsearchable his judgments, and his paths beyond tracing out!”*( Rom11:33). Whatever ideology attacks the sovereignty of God is an affront and lack of respect for Him. Man's failure in the scheme of things doesn't mean God programmed man to fail, it is man's choice to fail because God never created man to be a robot who can't decide for himself. In order for His plans to be fulfilled, God always plan a way out for such human failures ahead. That's why the bible calls Jesus "The lamb that was slain before the foundation of the world"( Rev13:8). Meaning that long before Adam fell, God already made provision for redemption. That's a wise God whom nothing meets/ catches unaware. Man's failure didn't in any way reduce his omniscience or sovereignty, it only reveals His wisdom. *"And there is no creature hidden from His sight, but all things are open and laid bare to the eyes of Him with whom we have to do*" (Hebrews 4:13).



*PRAYER: Father, I thank you because of your sovereignty, omniscience and immutability. Thank you for deciding all things in my favour. Thank you Jesus.

DEVOTIONAL 0503 | Source start line: 45342
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
5/6/22, 6:29 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: PROJECTSAVE DEVOTIONAL
Friday,
6th May, 2022.


*BEWARE OF ANTI BIBLE PHILOSOPHIES* Pt.7



Col2:8 *Don’t let anyone capture you with empty philosophies and high-sounding nonsense that come from human thinking and from the spiritual powers of this world, rather than from Christ*.


*HEDONISM*


Aristippus of Cyrene was a student of philosophy under Socrates. He was the first to preach the philosophy of "Hedonism". What is Hedonism? This is the philosophy that supports the pursuit of pleasure and self- indulgence. Aristippus believed that the ultimate meaning of life is pleasure and, thus, pleasure should be man’s greatest pursuit. In the ancient world, what made this philosophy to gain wide acceptance was because Epicurus, his student, subscribed to hedonism. Epicurus taught that there is no afterlife and so one must enjoy pleasure as the ultimate end. In Acts 17:18, during the second missionary journey of Paul, he debated with the Epicureans. It was in the 18th century Hedenism re emerged and gained prominence through Jeremy Bentham as an ethical and psychological theory. Through Satan's subtlety, the philosophy of Hedonism has coloured modern preaching & teaching of some preachers resulting in flamboyance and pursuit of material things. Same has affected some Christians too. They may not know it is Hedonism but that's the philosophy their teaching and lifestyle portrays.



What does the bible have to say about Hedonism? King Solomon can give us an insight about this: *“I said to myself, ‘Come now, I will test you with pleasure to find out what is good.’ . . . I denied myself nothing my eyes desired; I refused my heart no pleasure*” (Ecclesiastes 2:1, 10). King Solomon was Hedonistic at a time but hear his conclusion: *“everything was meaningless, a chasing after the wind; nothing was gained under the sun*” (Ecclesiastes 2:11). That's worth pondering over. Those that are devoted to the pursuit of pleasure will not profit from the word of God. Jesus established this truth in his parable: *“And as for what fell among the thorns, they are those who hear, but as they go on their way they are choked by the cares and riches and pleasures of life, and their fruit does not mature*” (Luke 8:14). The pursuit of pleasure is not meant to be man’s ultimate goal or pursuit. The pop culture preaches and teaches Hedonism through music. That's why most of the songs are centred on worldly pleasure.



*No hedonistic man will make heaven*. God's ultimate purpose for our lives is not hedonistic pleasure but to please and glorify Him: *"Don’t copy the behavior and customs of this world, but let God transform you into a new person by changing the way you think. Then you will learn to know God’s will for you, which is good and pleasing and perfect*" (Romans 12:2 NLT). As God's children, we must learn to abide in God's presence to find true pleasure: *“In your presence there is fullness of joy; at your right hand are pleasures forevermore” ( Psl16:12). The "Gospel" that doesn't point to Christ, heaven, righteousness but is centred around the pursuit of wealth, breakthrough, and material things is hedonistic. Nothing corrupts true biblical teaching than the modern hedonistic philosophy. Jesus asked a very vital question: *“For what shall it profit a man, if he shall gain the whole world, but lose his soul?*"(Mark 8:36”). That's a question begging for answer from ages past. Do this: *“But seek first his kingdom and his righteousness, and all these things will be given to you as well*” (Matthew 6:33).



*PRAYER*: Father, may I not be conquered by hedonism in Jesus name.

DEVOTIONAL 0504 | Source start line: 45439
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
5/7/22, 7:29 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: ProjectSave Devotional
Saturday,
7th May, 2022.


*BEWARE OF ANTI BIBLE PHILOSOPHIES* Pt.8


Col2:8 *Don’t let anyone capture you with empty philosophies and high-sounding nonsense that come from human thinking and from the spiritual powers of this world, rather than from Christ*.


*EPICUREANISM*


Epicurus was a student of Aristippus. He built his philosophy on his teacher's philosophy of hedonism. While hedonism is the philosophy that backs the pursuit of pleasure, Epicurus added to it by saying there's no life after death. It is another way of saying, "Enjoy your life and its pleasure to the fullest because there's no other life after death". This philosophy was strong in the time of Paul. He met Epicureans on his second missionary journey in Acts17:18. It was the Epicureans that brought Paul to the Areopagus for questioning. Epicureanism taught that the highest state a person could attain was ataraxia—absolute peace—and aponia—complete absence of mental, emotional, and physical distress or need. The initial goal of Epicurus was to teach people to relax and enjoy life. Also, to let them know that the gods do not interfere in human affairs. Thus, no judgement in this life and after death. How wrong!


Though Epicureanism got somethings right especially teaching against worry which the bible also warns against in Matthew 6:25 and 1Peter 5:7 yet it erred in so many ways. The absolute peace( Ataraxia) Epicurus taught his disciples can only be gotten in CHRIST and in no other: *“I have said these things to you, that in me you may have peace. In the world you will have tribulation. But take heart; I have overcome the world*” (John 16:33). Epicureanism doesn't believe God is involved in human affairs but the bible teaches differently: *"This matter is by the decree of the watchers, and the demand by the word of the holy ones: to the intent that the living may know that the most High ruleth in the kingdom of men, and giveth it to whomsoever he will, and setteth up over it the basest of men*"( Dan4:17). God is interested in the affairs of man because man is very important to God. If God isn't interested in the affairs of man, He wouldn't have promised to direct man's ways. Proverbs 3:5-6 says, *"Trust in the LORD with all thine heart; And lean not unto thine own understanding. In all thy ways acknowledge him, And he shall direct thy paths*".


Epicurus belief about judgement, death and life after death is totally unbiblical. Brethren, there's life after death. The life after death is eternal.  Jesus said, “I am the resurrection and the life. Whoever believes in me, though he die, yet shall he live, and everyone who lives and believes in me shall never die*”(John 11:25b–26a). The writer of Hebrews(9:27) says, *"And as it is appointed unto men once to die, but after this the judgment*"(Hebrews 9:27 KJV). This is a fundamental bible truth that is incontrovertible. Accepting this truth will help us to keep eternity in-view. *Don't be entangled with needless, unprofitable and ungodly philosophies that will cause a set back in your walk with God*. At all times, stand with God's word and believe the written word.


*PRAYER*: Father, I receive the grace to believe your word and abide by it. I shun ungodly philosophies, I refuse to be manipulated by the cunningness of men in Jesus name.

DEVOTIONAL 0505 | Source start line: 45485
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
5/8/22, 4:41 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: ProjectSave Devotional
Sunday,
8th May, 2022.



*BEWARE OF ANTI BIBLE PHILOSOPHIES* Pt.9



Col2:8 *Don’t let anyone capture you with empty philosophies and high-sounding nonsense that come from human thinking and from the spiritual powers of this world, rather than from Christ*.


*STOICISM*



Stoicism was first taught by Zeno around 300 BC. Stoicism is one of many ancient Greek philosophies. Ancient Greece was the home of intellectuals in centuries past. Paul, in his missionary journey to Athens met with the stoics and engaged them in a debate in the Areopagus and later presented the Gospel to them in Mars Hill(Acts 17:18).  Stoicism emphasizes rationalism and logic. Stoicism believes in a universal force of reason that governed everything. That force is believed to make things happen on earth.  That force is known as "Logos" meaning "Word". It will interest you to know that the term "Logos" was used by Heraclitus some 500 years before Jesus was born. In the Gospel of John, Jesus is referred to as the "Logos" (Word), and the Word was made flesh and dwelt among us; he is the driving force sent by God( John1:1,14). Here, there's a similarity between Christianity and Stoicism because both believe in "Logos"- Word.




What is anti bible about Stoicism? Stoics believe "Logos" is an unknowable force. The idea of saying man cannot know God and presenting God as an impersonal force is totally anti scriptures. God want us to know Him that's why He sent Jesus: *“Now this is eternal life: that they may know you, the only true God, and Jesus Christ, whom you have sent*” (John 17:3). We can know God through a sound relationship with Christ:  *"Jesus told him, “I am the way, the truth, and the life. No one can come to the Father except through me. If you had really known me, you would know who my Father is. From now on, you do know him and have seen him!*”(John 14:6‭-‬7 NLT). The "Logos" that Stoics thought as unknowable and impersonal is revealed in the bible as the Christ who came in flesh and dwelled with men: *"In the beginning was the Word( Logos) , and the Word was with God, and the Word was God... And the Word was made flesh, and dwelt among us, (and we beheld his glory, the glory as of the only begotten of the Father,) full of grace and truth*"(John 1:1, 14 KJV). Hallelujah!!! Jesus Christ is the "Logos" that Stoics don't know. We know Him. He lives in us and we in Him.



Another teaching of Stoicism that is anti scriptures is that man should seek help from within. Whereas the bible teaches that we should seek help from God in times of trouble: *"I will lift up mine eyes unto the hills, From whence cometh my help. My help cometh from the LORD, Which made heaven and earth*" (Psalm 121:1-2 KJV). Man cannot help himself. The phrase “God helps those who help themselves” combines Stoicism and Christianity. They're immiscible. God's help is for the helpless. Finally, Stoicism doesn't have a firm belief about after life. For the Stoics, what matters isn’t so much what may or may not happen after death, but how we make best use of the time we have now. They've forgotten that life here on earth is a dress rehearsal for the life to come in eternity. We believe in resurrection of the dead and two eternal destinations for mankind- Heaven or hell: *"And just as it is appointed for man to die once, and after that comes judgment*" (Heb9:27). Revelations 20:6 says, *"Blessed and holy is the one who shares in the first resurrection! Over such the second death has no power, but they will be priests of God and of Christ, and they will reign with him for a thousand years*". Eternity is real, prepare to meet the Lord.


*PRAYER*: Father, I'll not be consumed by evil philosophies in Jesus name. My heart is open only for your word. Help me Lord.

DEVOTIONAL 0506 | Source start line: 45575
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
5/9/22, 7:06 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: PROJECTSAVE DEVOTIONAL
Monday,
9th May, 2022.


*BEWARE OF ANTI BIBLE PHILOSOPHIES* Pt.10


Col2:8 *Don’t let anyone capture you with empty philosophies and high-sounding nonsense that come from human thinking and from the spiritual powers of this world, rather than from Christ*.

*GNOSTICISM*

The most dangerous philosophical thought that threatened the early church was Gnosticism. Some bible scholars believe the epistle of 1 John was written to address Gnostic heresy that pervaded that time. Also, it is believed that some writings of Paul addressed the error of Gnosticism though another school of thought believed otherwise. However, the teachings of the bible is anti Gnosticism. This philosophy is influenced by Plato. What does Gnosticism stand for? What do Gnostics teach? First, Gnosticism believe that matter is inherently evil and spirit is good. This is called "Dualism". This presupposes that  anything done in the body, even the vilest sin, has no meaning because real life exists in the spirit realm only. In essence, you cannot help but sin because your body( matter) is inherently evil and any sin you commit with your body doesn't matter. Only your spirit is pure. That's totally heretic. Paul enjoined that we abstain from bodily and spirit pollution: *"Because we have these promises, dear friends, let us cleanse ourselves from everything that can defile our body or spirit. And let us work toward complete holiness because we fear God*"(2 Corinthians 7:1 NLT). Complete holiness is what bible teaches.


In the early days of Christianity, the libertines built a doctrine on this flawed premises thereby giving license to people to sin. Unfortunately, some in the "Hyper Grace" camp also teaches this Gnostic belief. I once read on the wall of someone who quoted a preacher who said "If a man dies while committing adultery, as long as he is born again, he will make it to heaven." I read another man who claimed that saved/ born again homosexuals will make heaven. Isn't that surprising? This is akin to saying  once saved you're forever saved regardless of what you do because Grace covers you. It seems similar views existed in the days of apostle Jude and he warned  saying: *"I say this because some ungodly people have wormed their way into your churches, saying that God’s marvelous grace allows us to live immoral lives. The condemnation of such people was recorded long ago, for they have denied our only Master and Lord, Jesus Christ*"(Jude 1:4 NLT). We live in the last days, the resurgence of "special knowledge" being peddled as revelation is leading many astray.


Also, Gnostics claim to possess special knowledge a “higher truth” known only to a certain few. This knowledge is not from the bible but acquired on some mystical plane of existence.  *Beware of spiritual knowledge that is not from the bible*. Gnostics believe salvation is gained through acquisition of special knowledge. This is not biblical. Salvation is by faith not through "special knowledge" : *“For it is by grace you have been saved, through faith—and this not from yourselves, it is the gift of God—not by works, so that no one can boast*” (Ephesians 2:8-9). Gnosticism denied the humanity of Jesus. They believe that Jesus’ physical body was not real, but only “seemed” to be physical, and that His spirit descended upon Him at His baptism, but left Him just before His crucifixion. This claim is known as "Docetism". The biblical view of Jesus affirms His complete humanity as well as His full deity. John 1:14 says, *"And the Word became flesh and dwelt among us, and we have seen his glory, glory as of the only Son from the Father, full of grace and truth*". God became a man to save sinful men. Apostle Paul confirmed his humanity: *"And being found in human form, he humbled himself by becoming obedient to the point of death, even death on a cross*"( Phil2:8). This is a fundamental bible truth. Do not subscribe to Gnostic heresies. It is damaging. All these philosophical heresies is a call to study Bible thoroughly and patiently. It is also a call to in-depth teaching of the bible. Don't love "jamboree" meetings, attend bible study meetings. The wave of false  doctrine is tossing people here and there and we must be on guard.

*PRAYER*: Father, help me not to be led astray by every wind of doctrine in Jesus name.

DEVOTIONAL 0507 | Source start line: 45676
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
5/10/22, 6:06 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: ProjectSave Devotional
Tuesday
10th May,2022.

*WHAT TO DO WHEN YOU DON'T KNOW WHAT TO DO* Pt.1

1Sam30:6 David was greatly distressed because the men were talking of stoning him; each one was bitter in spirit because of his sons and daughters. But David found strength in the LORD his God.


*RETREAT TO THE SECRET PLACE*


After I was told by the doctor that I tested positive to COVID, it was as though something jumped out of me. I felt lonely from within and confusion set in.  The matter became worse with my partner as well, because of the result. The afternoon we received the report that both of us tested positive, the house boiled and the atmosphere changed because of the conversation we had with his mother back in Nigeria. Immediately, I assumed i took the wrong step by traveling at that time even though it was the Lord who sent us. Sometimes, challenges makes us doubt God. Mentally, I calculated the distance between Nigeria and Ghana and I discovered I couldn't run home on foot. I didn't know what to do because my mind was deeply troubled. I looked at the hotel bills, feeding and other living expenses, it was as though someone arranged frustration for us. I just didn't know what to do. I was at my wits' end. I cannot pretend about how I felt. This is to let you know that I've been there even on several occasion. After all, I'm human and I don't like to pretend as though I'm super human. In His incarnation, Jesus got to that point where He said: *"...“My Father! If it is possible, let this cup of suffering be taken away from me. Yet I want your will to be done, not mine*”( Mt26:39).


Brethren, I've experienced confusion on several occasions and I came out of it. In my low moment, I cried. I wept like a baby whose doll was taken from him. I know you've been in that situation or condition several times. At such times, the enemy makes you feel God is distant and unresponsive. He makes you feel God has left you, at other times, he uses guilt to drive your spirit to a low point. He uses such times to point your errors and inadequacies to you, your dirty past and certain things you should have done that you didn't do. He comes to attack your faith making you feel God has left you alone. When you get to this point in anything you're doing, put everything on hold and retreat to the secret place: *"You called in [the time of] trouble and I rescued you; I answered you in the secret place of thunder; I tested you at the waters of Meribah. Selah*"(Psalms 81:7 AMP).



*The secret place is the place of light and direction in time of trouble*. At that very point when you don't know what to do, God is waiting for you to meet with Him in the secret place. That secret place is the place of fellowship. It is the place of "koinonia"( communion). You cannot come out of confusion and life troubles if you don't know how to plug yourself into the power that flows in the place of fellowship. *The secret of triumph can only be found in the secret place. Those that have a secret place culture will always come out with clarity of thought and direction because the secret of the Lord is with those that abide in the secret place*.



Don't let the enemy convince you to try out alternatives or take steps in a confused state. *Not knowing what to do is not uncommon to  man at certain times but doing just anything or taking just any step when you don't know what to do is the height of folly*. Calm down and retreat to meet the one who knows the right step to take. Until you receive clarity from the secret place on that issue confusing you, take no step.



*PRAYER*: Father, I receive the grace to be a secret place believer in Jesus name.

DEVOTIONAL 0508 | Source start line: 45762
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
5/11/22, 6:52 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: PROJECTSAVE DEVOTIONAL
Wednesday,
11th May, 2022.


*WHAT TO DO WHEN YOU DON'T KNOW WHAT TO DO* Pt.2

1Sam30:6 David was greatly distressed because the men were talking of stoning him; beeach one was bitter in spirit because of his sons and daughters. But David found strength in the LORD his God.

*BUILD INNER STRENGTH ON THE WORD*

The whole house boiled when my partner's mum called, we were down emotionally. The result punctured our confidence. We felt so ordinary at that moment. The outreach we embarked on was power filled with lots of testimonies. I remember an idolater gave her life to Christ and her shrine was pulled down, her totems were burnt in public. Another lady walking with stick was healed supernaturally. You know that feeling you have when people give testimonies of what the Lord has done through your ministry. Immediately we saw our COVID result, that feeling left. I felt ordinary. That's a proof that whatever exploit we do for the Lord is not by our power. *If the anointed is not careful, he may think he can handle every situation with his power. The challenges of life sometimes brings the anointed to the level of ordinary men. At that point, you'll need to seek God and build your faith to get result*. My own case was like of that of Elijah who ran away from Jezebel after calling down fire from heaven and  killing the prophets of Baal. One would have thought he would confront Jezebel with the same anointing but he fled. Why? He was discouraged and his faith was down. *After every exploit, the devil seeks to discourage and attack our faith by bringing strange challenges our way. At that moment, you may doubt God's power and the enemy may make you think all that God has done through you was a fluke or they just happened by chance*. At that moment, the confusion that sets in becomes indescribable.


David, the giant slayer ran from Saul and became a fugitive. To make matters worse, in the days when he was hiding and running from Saul, the Amalekites raided Negev and Ziklag where his family and that of his companions were. The bible says, *"When David and his men saw the ruins and realized what had happened to their families, they wept until they could weep no more*"(1 Samuel 30:3-4 NLT). Imagine warriors crying till they couldn't weep any more. That's a situation in life when wise and strong men becomes confused not knowing what to do. However, David did something that turned things around. The bible says, *"David was now in great danger because all his men were very bitter about losing their sons and daughters, and they began to talk of stoning him. BUT DAVID FOUND STRENGTH IN THE LORD HIS GOD*"(1 Samuel 30:6 NLT). The journey to triumph over the vicissitudes of life starts when you muster inner strength from within. This inner strength is not a psychological one but word based inner strength.


*You need a strong spirit to come out of that state of confusion and despair. You must find relevant scriptures that addresses your situation and stand on them until you are victorious. That's how to build inner strength- by the word*. The devil want you to remain down, hopeless and in a state of pity. He want you to "beg" for prayers at all times. He will point you to others who died on account of the same challenge. He will point bad and gory pictures for you. Don't subscribe to that. The strength of your spirit at such times will determine your victory. The enemy's goal is to attack your faith. Isaiah 7:9b says, *"If you do not stand firm in your faith, you will not stand at all*". In times when you don't know what to do, build inner strength through the word. Let's pray the Pauline prayers, make this confession:


*I pray that from his glorious, unlimited resources he will empower me with inner strength through his Spirit. Then Christ will make his home in my hearts as I trust in him. My roots will grow down into God’s love and keep me strong in Jesus name*. (Ephesians 3:16-17 NLT).



N:B Join us to pray in the Spirit 9pm ( Nigerian time) every night on zoom:https://us02web.zoom.us/j/86794796510?pwd=VncrZjBCWE9yWFJaVStPOUZ2UC92dz09



info@projectsaveng.org

DEVOTIONAL 0509 | Source start line: 45865
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
5/12/22, 12:19 PM - Lanre Oyeleke: PROJECTSAVE DEVOTIONAL
Thursday,
12th May, 2022.


*WHAT TO DO WHEN YOU DON'T KNOW WHAT TO DO* Pt.3


1Sam30:6 David was greatly distressed because the men were talking of stoning him; each one was bitter in spirit because of his sons and daughters. But David found strength in the LORD his God.


*FIND/CREATE  A FAITH BOOSTING ATMOSPHERE*

After we received the call that we won't be flying to Nigeria because of our result, we put a call through to the mother of the young man with me informing her about change in plans and the cause of our delay. Her words changed our atmosphere and the little faith that we were building crashed. For about thirty minutes, the whole house became hot and on fire. Though the situation wasn't as bad as she painted it. At that time, we were still in high spirits save the medical report. The fire of hopelessness, faithlessness, and despair burned in that atmosphere after that conversation. When our host heard the conversation and saw all that happened, he said, "I don't like this kind of atmosphere around my house". Immediately, i understood what he was trying to say. He was our coach and faith support system that period. The next thing we did was to pray and worship God. Immediately, the hope that we lost was restored.



There's another thing you must do when you find yourself in a situation where you don't know what to do. *Find a faith boosting atmosphere not an atmosphere that kills your faith*. If you can't find that atmosphere, create it by yourself. Sometimes, we are products of our atmosphere. *We soak into our spiritual system the juice that flows in our atmosphere*. When the twelve spies returned, ten of them gave a fearful report about the land. What they saw were giants. Immediately, the fear and hopelessness spread throughout the camp of Israel. That news changed the atmosphere and the people drank the juice of fear in that atmosphere: *"So they spread this bad report about the land among the Israelites: “The land we traveled through and explored will devour anyone who goes to live there. All the people we saw were huge*"(Numbers 13:32 NLT). Caleb understood this principle of creating the right atmosphere and they decided to change the narrative by "infecting" the atmosphere with the spirit of faith: *"But Caleb tried to quiet the people as they stood before Moses. “Let’s go at once to take the land,” he said. “We can certainly conquer it!*”(Numbers 13:30 NLT).


*In moments when you don't know what to, avoid toxic atmosphere and people who will hinder the flow of faith into your spiritual blood stream*. Don't fellowship with clueless people in your situation. Whatever flows in your atmosphere is powerful and it can get into you. If you can't create that atmosphere yourself, you need support systems that will help you out in this. *The enemy's trap in time of trouble is to look for a way to snuff out the little that remains in your faith and weaken your inner strength totally*. If he can keep you down in your mind and place you in an atmosphere of faithlessness, you will never be receptive to divine intervention. When you don't know what to do, create an atmosphere of faith. That's the way out.


*CONFESSION*: I refuse to soak in the toxicity in my atmosphere. I receive a fresh baptism of the spirit of faith. I will never remain down one more day. I refuse to be clueless and rudderless. I have a sound mind and will ever remain on top in Jesus name.


N:B You can be part of what God is set to do in Tanzania among the Maasai people. We covet your prayers and support. Contact: 07080100893.


info@projectsaveng.org

5/13/22, 6:40 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: PROJECTSAVE DEVOTIONAL
Friday,
13th May, 2022.



*WHAT TO DO WHEN YOU DON'T KNOW WHAT TO DO* Pt.4



1Sam30:6 David was greatly distressed because the men were talking of stoning him; each one was bitter in spirit because of his sons and daughters. But David found strength in the LORD his God.


*YOU NEED WISE COUNSEL*


I heard the story of a rich man who had only one child. He was very wealthy by the standard of his time. When he was about to die, he decided to write a will. In his will, he said his son is entitled to pick just one thing from his properties and the remaining should go to his oldest slave of many years. The son was confused he didn't know what to do. The oldest slave of decades was elated because he knew that only one thing belong to the son of his master after his demise. He dreamt of how the big mansions would become his as soon as the heir chooses whatever he wanted. Alas! The rich man died. As the time for choosing the choicest property came, the son of this wealthy man became confused. He didn't know what to do.



He decided to seek counsel from the elders. The elders saw his state of confusion and they decided to share a secret with him. They told him that  slaves are considered as property or assets in their culture and that slaves own nothing. They counseled him to choose the slave so that whatever the slave owns/ chooses will automatically belong to him. Upon hearing this wise counsel, he knew what to do. When the day came for him to make public his choice, the whole village gathered at the market square. The oldest slave who knew what was written in the will dressed like a king. He had praise singers following him. When he entered the market square, all eyes were on him. The time came for the son of this wealthy man to choose any of his father's properties. Most people expected him to choose a house or one of the farms owned by his father. He said "After seeking wise counsel, the only property I'll be choosing is my father's oldest slave." The moment the villagers heard that, they were baffled because they knew that choosing the slave gives him access and right of ownership to other properties owned by the slave.


*Seek wise counsel when you don't know what to do. Oftentimes, the confusion in our heart is simply because we lack wisdom on the matter confronting us*. In moments when you don't know what to do, seek wise counsel. *You might be one counsel away from solution*. In life, surround yourself with men who have depth in God and are in tune with God not foolish men like Jonadab.  You will always need counsel at one point or the other in life. God didn't create us to be lone rangers, you don't know it all. The bible says, *"Where there is no [wise, intelligent] guidance, the people fall [and go off course like a ship without a helm], But in the abundance of [wise and godly] counselors there is victory*"( Proverbs 11:14, AMP).


*PRAYER*: Father, I receive the grace to heed wise counsel in Jesus name.

DEVOTIONAL 0511 | Source start line: 45994
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
5/14/22, 7:00 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: ProjectSave Devotional
Saturday,
14th May, 2022.


*TRANSFER FROM YOUR BANK*


2Tim2:2 You have heard me teach things that have been confirmed by many reliable witnesses. Now teach these truths to other trustworthy people who will be able to pass them on to others.
NLT


Technology has made banking operations very easy. Years back in Nigeria, if you want to send money to someone, you'll have to take the cash with you to the recipient's bank and deposit it there. There was no alert system. The receiver will have to walk to the nearest branch of his bank to confirm deposit. Today, the story has changed. The transfer section of my bank is an exciting place to be. It amazes me that in seconds, you can transfer money from your account or even your phone to another person's account and the receiver would be alerted immediately. Today, you may not need to visit the bank for transfer because there are bank Apps to do whatever type of transfer you intend to do. So quick so easy.




*You have a spiritual knowledge bank*. Yes you do. The truths you have learnt from others about God and His word is stored in your knowledge bank. God's expectation of you is to spread or transfer that truth to others.  Apostle Paul said, *"You have heard me teach things that have been confirmed by many reliable witnesses. Now teach these truths to other trustworthy people who will be able to pass them on to others*"(2Tim2:2 NLT). As I teach daily through this devotional, you can transfer the truth from your phone and other devices to your friends. Don't hoard knowledge. Whatever truth you have learnt or would be learning from God's word, you are expected to imbibe them and teach others who would transfer the knowledge as well. Whenever I'm preaching or teaching God's word, I tell people to make notes and teach others what they have learnt. Nothing is new. Every preacher is a learner of truth and agent of truth transfer.



*The Gospel will not spread to the ends of the earth if we fail to transfer it*. Truth transfer should start from your home. Your spouse, children and those within your sphere of influence should make contact with the truth from you. *Don't be a selfish believer, don't hoard knowledge, spread the knowledge of God's word*. If we all decide to share or transfer the truth we know unto others, the gospel would spread throughout the world in no time. Use technology and all that is within your disposal to transfer the truth. God is counting on you to be a truth transfer agent this end time.


PRAYER: Father, I receive grace to be a truth transfer agent In Jesus name. Amen

DEVOTIONAL 0512 | Source start line: 46055
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
5/15/22, 1:33 PM - Lanre Oyeleke: ProjectSave Devotional
Sunday,
15th May, 2022.


*HE TAUGHT ME LEADERSHIP*


1Cor 11:1 Follow my example, as I follow the example of Christ.



On a certain day, as i was about leaving the house, my first son said he wanted to follow me out. Sarcastically, I told him to follow Jesus not me. He asked, "Are you not a Pastor"? I replied by saying "Yes." Then he further said, "A Pastor should follow Jesus so I will follow you"(paraphrase). That sank deep. Though I didn't want him to go with me that day and I twisted the narrative sarcastically to point his attention to following Jesus, he said he'll follow me because it is expected of me to follow Jesus. That's a great leadership lesson there. That young boy knew that a true Christian leader should be someone who is following Jesus and should be someone that people can follow as he follows Christ.




In the opening text, Paul emphatically told his readers to imitate him as he imitate Christ. True Christian leadership should be do as I do not do what I say. The basis for imitation should be the effulgence of a Christ-centred life that is visible to all. In another portion of scriptures, Paul said, *"Therefore I urge you to imitate me"*(1 Cor 4:16). Everyone that is saved is a leader in some respect. We should be bold to tell those that look up to us to follow Christ just as we are following Him. *Until our lives are Christ-centred, we are not fit for leadership in the kingdom*.



The leadership principle of this world is devoid of imitation. I have seen parents who don't want their children to live their kind of life. Deep within, they know something is wrong with their manner and conduct. *True Christian leadership must be modelled from home. The family is central to God's plan and purposes on the earth and that's where true Christian leadership can be modelled*. From there, the imitation of Christ-life will spread to the church and the society. *Fathers who will model Christ to their children are much needed in this generation. Mothers who will boldly tell their children to imitate and take after them are scarce and wanted as we speak*. Children learn more by imitation than through instruction. Whatever you instruct your children not to do shouldn't be found with and in you. *Whatever isn't good for your children isn't good for you. If you tell them not to watch a particular t.v.program then you shouldn't watch it as well. Nothing like adult movies. It should be "do as I do not do what I say"*.


*PRAYER*: Father, I receive the grace to model Christ to my children and to those around me in Jesus name.

DEVOTIONAL 0513 | Source start line: 46121
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
5/16/22, 7:17 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: PROJECTSAVE DEVOTIONAL
Monday,
16th May, 2022.

*I HAVE A COUNSEL FOR SINGLES*


Proverbs 2:11 Discretion will protect you, and understanding will guard you.


During my service year, I was privileged to serve God as a minister in Nigeria Christian Corpers' Fellowship. During that period, we were saddled with the responsibility of choosing new leaders in the fellowship. Other leaders at the zones also do same before handing over. In one of the zones, a lady was interviewed to take up one of the leadership roles  but she declined. When she was asked the reason for declining, her response was that she wanted to marry. Now, that's a good reason because we don't allow married people to lead in the fellowship. Also, if you haven't entered a relationship, you are not permitted to enter or accept a marriage proposal while  serving as a leader. However, in her case,  we discovered it was all a ruse. She wasn't in any relationship neither was Marriage in-view. We discovered this much later. In fact, she didn't marry until years after we finished NYSC. So what went wrong? We discovered she was so obsessed with the idea marrying so she doesn't want anything to hinder her desire because she knew that taking up leadership role could stop her from "achieving" marriage. It is understandable if she was in a relationship but she was not in any. Actually, she lost an opportunity to develop some leadership skills. So many sisters, most of whom weren't in any relationship, who served as leaders developed some leadership skills. Today, they're married.



What happened to this lady is common to so many singles I've seen in this generation. In a bid to marry, they suspend their growth in other areas of their life. In fact, some ladies have been told not to pursue further academic studies to post graduate level because if they do, men won't come. Have you heard this before? Most times, this counsel and other related counsels are given to ladies to make them feel if they rise as a single, men won't come for them. It sounds like wise counsel but they are filled with the insecurities of the adviser. Rather, the counsel should point such ladies towards living a humble and simple life not to stop their progress. *At any level you find yourself in life, there's a man for you. You'll always attract your kind*. The idea of being "left behind" is not only common to those who are developing themselves. Today, many ladies that aren't educated enough are single praying to be married. Don't let anyone guilt trip you because of your desire to make progress. It is painful that ladies are often at the receiving end of this counsel. Rarely will you hear a man told not to study further or pursue his dreams because of marriage. In fact, a lady once told her story on twitter about this issue. She got a scholarship admission for Masters&PhD in Canada. She lost her relationship and was persuaded to drop her academic pursuit for marriage. She continued though with pains. She cried at times seeing her friends marrying before her. Later God answered her prayers and gave her a good man. In her story, she appreciated God that she didn't drop the pursuit of her personal development for marriage because she's reaping the reward and her husband and children are beneficiaries as well.




Don't forget this: *"You prepare for marriage as you prepare for life*". If all you're preparing for as a single is marriage, you'll enter into marriage unformed, ill-prepared and as an immature person. *Don't pause or abandon your growth and life pursuit because you want to marry. Marriage is not an end in itself.* We should stop making singlehood look like a crime that must be avoided or exited at all cost. Marriage is not all there is to achieve in life. After all, there are many celibates who lived a fulfilled life. Your singleness is a gift from God that is rare. This phase of your life makes it easy for you to pursue your dreams and to work towards a future you've envisioned unhindered. *Marriage should only come into the picture to boost and catalyse your dreams, visions and pursuit*. The single that has nothing to live for has no business getting married. If a brother can't accept you because he feels you're far ahead simply know he is not for you. You can't afford to live under the same roof with an insecure man. If he's dangling options like "Choose between me and your academic pursuit/career progress". That's a manipulator. None of those things stop marriage if you pray and plan properly. *A man that is not ready to give you wings to fly should be avoided at all cost*.


*PRAYER*: Father, I receive wisdom to maximize my singleness in Jesus name.

DEVOTIONAL 0514 | Source start line: 46173
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
5/17/22, 7:02 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: ProjectSave Devotional
Tuesday
17th May,2022.


*CONSIDER VALUES*


Proverbs 2:11 Discretion will protect you, and understanding will guard you.


I had funny experiences as a single brother. One of such experience was that some sisters desired to have me as their fiancé. It wasn't a secret because some of them approached me telling me how God spoke to them. As I write this devotional, I can remember four sisters who did that. Others were indirect about it.  Another sister was fond of coming for counseling and asking for prayers as regards future partner. She told me how God spoke to her about a brother who wasn't forthcoming. So I joined in prayers that God should reveal the same to the brother and touch his heart to propose to this sister. One day, I was in Lagos National Stadium, I was prompted in my spirit to have a phone conversation with this sister asking who the brother was. When I called, she was reluctant to speak at first but later, she revealed the identity of the brother I've been praying about. Alas! I am the brother she saw in her illusionary vision that she got married to. I didn't know I've been praying for myself to see and hear God about this sister. I was naively sincere.



Looking back at those times, I wondered what the attraction was. For a long time, I couldn't figure it out but now I know. A number of them were attracted by "Charisma" or gifts. They saw a gifted young man. The manifestation of God's power was an aphrodisiac for some of them. I knew because some were ready to do just anything.  Those were days when someone must "fall under anointing" when I ministered. I was raw and unrefined in ministry. Now, I know better. I have a lot to tell singles especially young Christian leaders because the temptations and pressure from the opposite sex is real even in Christian circles. Sisters fight because of "bro".  Some sisters are thrilled by praying brothers or a brother that is gifted in power dimensions. They see such brothers as a form of "spiritual security". That's why Presidents and Prayer Coordinators of fellowships are hotcakes. It is not only sisters that get thrilled at gifted brothers. Some brothers love sisters that can sing especially brothers that are ministry inclined. They imagine having a wife that can sing like an angel while they preach powerfully. Truthfully, I was once attracted to a "spiritual" sister like that. I desired her for ministry. I dreamt of a future where they'll introduce us as "Pastor &Pastor Mrs". She fit the exact image of a Pastor Mrs or Mummy in the Lord. Don't laugh at me.



*As a Christian single, when you want to make the choice of a life partner, "Gifts" shouldn't be your  major consideration. Rather, you should consider the values of the person you're attracted to*. Values are standards and principles of behaviour that guide the day-to-day living of a person. *A man can be gifted but lacking in character. charisma without character will lead to moral catastrophic chaos*. When you marry, you won't live with the "gifts" of that brother or sister but his values. Often, gifts are for public ministration and they operate as the Spirit wills but one thing that will remain are the values of that man even when the gift ceases. Have you checked his values for purity, honesty and integrity? *Don't get carried away with public Charisma without checking for hidden character*. There are spouses of gifted  people that are living in regret. They can't come out publicly to tell us about the rotten character of the man with charisma that everyone so much admire. *Don't marry a man's gifts or ministry. His values, character, standards and principles  comes first before other things.*

*PRAYER*: Father, I receive wisdom to choose rightly in Jesus name

DEVOTIONAL 0515 | Source start line: 46243
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
5/18/22, 7:04 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: ProjectSave Devotional
Wednesday
18th May,2022.


*HER CHICANERY DID NOT WORK*

Proverbs 2:11 Discretion will protect you, and understanding will guard you.



During my service year, a sister from another zone of our fellowship in another town sent me a message telling me God told her to start praying for me till I finish my tenure as one of the fellowship leaders. I was happy God raised an intercessor for me. It was something I loved because I knew I needed God to succeed. Sometimes, this sister would send "dangerous" scriptures praying for me. Those scriptures were  great scriptures that will motivate anyone to pray. All our conversations were on the phone, we didn't meet physically.  As time went on, I noticed she began to ask personal questions. Her messages became more personal. On my birthday, she asked how old  I was but I declined. I insisted that her duty was to be my personal intercessor and nothing more. She dragged the matter with me to the point of vexation. I told her to tell me hers before telling her mine. She did  so I told her mine. She was four years older than me. The moment I did, she accused me of lying because I looked older than the age I claimed.  She said she saw my younger brother's picture on Facebook and he looks older than her how much more me. I never knew she had gone behind me to check my family. My younger brother has an intimidating stature but he's my younger brother.




Up to this point, all the conversations we had were via SMS. I didn't even know her facially. I was curious to know what she was up to. She said from the day she saw me, she knew I would be her husband. Ahhh!!!  She said she sat facing me in one of our conferences but I didn't notice her so she visited our lodge yet I didn't notice her. I didn't know someone was busy scheming to get me. I was busy serving God and enjoying myself. I told her I wasn't ready for marriage. Later, she told me she cried all night when I rejected her "proposal." When I heard all her tales, I knew her decision to intercede for me was all a ruse. She only used that to gain access to me thinking that will endear me to her. I saw desperation and trickery written all over her. So I asked why she had to do all that. Then she told me her story of previous heartbreak. I prayed with her and discharged her. In all these, I carried some of my associates along and I kept evidences of conversation. That was a form of security.


*Dear single, don't employ gimmicks in getting a spouse. You don't need it. You will never make the right choice if you're not clear headed and detached emotionally*. It is normal to be attracted to a beautiful lady or handsome brother but attraction isn't enough reason to marry. Other things must be considered. One of the lies the enemy sells to singles is "Time is going". To some, especially the ladies, he'll tell them "Your biological clock is ticking." All these put together makes some that are unstable to employ gimmicks in getting a mate. Some throw caution to the wind and they'll start cohabiting and acting as a wife that they're not. Some lower the standard of righteousness to get a mate. Some ladies have been told that if they don't marry by age thirty, they may not get a good husband. That's a big lie. *If you employ gimmicks to get a husband, you'll need to use a lot of gimmicks to keep him. If you lower the standard of righteousness to get him, you will comprise your faith to keep him.* To the single that is discouraged, God hasn't forgotten you. Live your life like every other normal person. *Singleness isn't a stigma neither is it a spiritual problem except in rare cases. Find purpose and live for a cause. As you walk that path, you'll find your mate. Purpose discovery leads to spouse discovery*.  No matter the number of Saturdays spent celebrating others, yours will come at the right time. Wait patiently on God, let Him match-make you.


*PRAYER*: Father, I receive wisdom and grace to wait on you patiently in Jesus name.

DEVOTIONAL 0516 | Source start line: 46413
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
5/19/22, 6:50 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: PROJECTSAVE DEVOTIONAL
Thursday,
19th May, 2022.


*STOP CONSULTING- HEAR HIM*


Proverbs 2:11 Discretion will protect you, and understanding will guard you.



Towards the end of my service year, on a certain day, my predecessor called to ask if there was any relationship between me and a particular sister in one of the zones. I told him there was nothing of such. I couldn't haven't done that because we had a rule that prohibited us from entering a relationship with the opposite sex. He asked if I've been talking to her but my response was 'No'. Now, the sister in question was a leader in her zone. If there was any discussion, it would have been on fellowship related matters but there was none because I related with her superior( Zonal Coordinator) not her. Having had funny experiences with some sisters, I asked my predecessor what the problem was. He told me this sister told him she took my name and others somewhere and she was told those other brothers have good morning time but bad evening time and  vice versa but when the "diviner" saw my name, he said morning, afternoon and evening will be good. When I heard that, I was jolted like a statue in the middle of an overflowing river. That sounded diabolical to me. Knowing that I regard my predecessor, probably she thought telling him would make him convince me about her. Sorry! I wasn't interested.




I couldn't believe a sister in our fellowship who had listened to sound doctrine could go to "seers" or "diviners" consulting on names. Not long after, she began to send me her pictures via email, several poses with several clothes. Immediately, I reported the situation to my associates. They were my security in such matters. I kept no secret on such matters. After she did all that without achieving success, she decided to visit our lodge known as "Family House". My self and associates were shocked to see her. My associates taunted me upon seeing her one evening. I ignored her politely till she left. I didn't hear anything from her again not until after service. One bright afternoon, I received a call from a man who introduced himself as a Pastor to this sister. I was shocked. In my mind, I thought everything ended in service. He said her spiritual daughter told him I was interested in her and that he's been praying about me. He called to confirm if I was truly interested. I told him point blank that I was never interested in her neither did I say anything that suggested such. I made him realize her spiritual daughter was the one who took my name somewhere and was talking to people to talk to me like he was doing. Quickly, he cut the call.



*Dear single, long before you reach the point of making a choice of whom to marry, develop the ability to hear from God and how to discern his voice rightly*. Only babies and mischievous people take names and pictures around for someone to help see visions. *I believe in bible-based counseling from mature Christians on the issue of marriage but I don't subscribe to the idea of getting someone to see visions for you on the choice of whom to marry*. God is your father, He wants to speak to you about the issues of your life. In His word, He promised to lead and direct us by himself: *"I will instruct thee and teach thee in the way which thou shalt go: I will guide thee with mine eye* (Psalm 32:8 KJV). Our earthly father won't communicate with us by proxy how much more our heavenly father. *Relationship with God should come first before thinking of relationship with man. You won't get it right with men until you get it right with God. No guess work in our walk*. The cordiality of your relationship with God will help you to discern the right relationship to enter with men without needing someone to consult for you. Beware of patronising "spiritual consultants" on the choice of whom to marry. *The ability to make informed decisions and stand by it and take responsibility is known as maturity*. We decide for babies and take responsibility for them to a large extent but if as an adult, someone else decide for you, you'll bear the consequences and take all the responsibility. *If you're not mature enough to hear from God by yourself on day-to-day decisions, don't bother getting married until that aspect is sorted*.


*PRAYER*: Father, I receive the grace to stay in fellowship with you, to discern your voice and to be led by the Spirit on all matters in Jesus name.

DEVOTIONAL 0517 | Source start line: 46502
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
5/20/22, 6:15 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: PROJECTSAVE DEVOTIONAL
Friday,
20th May, 2022.


*PATIENTLY INTERPRET REVELATIONS*


Proverbs 2:11 Discretion will protect you, and understanding will guard you.



There was this sister who became close to me after service.  She wasn't in my fellowship during my service year but she was a friend of the house. She attended some of our programs. During my service year, the only major contact I had with her that I can remember was when I visited her in her fellowship Corpers' lodge in company of other brethren when we were returning from an orientation camp meeting.  I heard she was sick and couldn't walk so we went there to pray. Years after, we became close as I was to others. Just friends. More so, she has a ministry, a very genuine one at that. I knew the hand of God was upon her to impact her generation and till now she's fulfilling her ministry in the Lord. Those days, we discussed ministry related issues and that got us closer the more. However, unknown to her, I was already in courtship with my wife. Both of them are close to some extent but i kept my relationship away from her and many others not until when we fixed a date for wedding.




In the process of time, I perceived unspoken intentions in her closeness with me but I gave her a benefit of doubt. I told my fiancee who is now my wife about it but she kept her calm. Later, she shared with her a revelation she had about me being her husband but I wasn't forthcoming.   All along, my wife(then my fiancee) played along without opening up to her that we were in a relationship. In fact, they prayed together about it. Recently, i asked my wife why she didn't react like some sisters will do. She said she left everything in God's hands trusting Him to perfect His will and plans. There's a lesson there: Don't fight for God, Whatever is truly yours can't be taken away from you. If my memory won't fail me, this sister told me the revelation she had but having known what she was driving at, I revealed the identity of my wife to her. She was shocked to know I was engaged with the same person she's been praying with.  I didn't take offence neither did i look down on her. Till date, I respect her. In fact, unlike other sisters who were utopian in their visions and carnal fantasies, I want to believe she had a revelation truly but she misinterpreted it. Whether her revelation was borne out of her emotions or by the Spirit, I can't say but I knew whatever it was she saw, she misinterpreted it. If she had waited a little longer, clarity would have come.



*Dear singles, don't be too quick to assume a literal interpretation or meaning to every revelation you see about marriage*. I know your heart is longing for that day, the devil knows but he can seize the opportunity of that anxiety to reveal things to you that aren't of God:  *"A dream comes when there are many cares, and many words mark the speech of a fool*"( Eccl5:3). On several occasions, I've had some brethren share revelations with me about whom they intend to marry. Often, I try not to be emotionally attached to those revelations because I've seen many back out of relationships they once claimed was of God. Did they see? Some truly had a revelation but they didn't discern the source and interpret such revelations properly. *Don't base your decision of whom to marry on revelations only. Other factors must be in place. Discern the source of that revelation rightly. Intimacy, admiration, emotional attachment with the opposite sex can make you see visions that aren't from the Spirit*. Whatever you see, patiently find the accurate interpretation. *Wearing a white gown and walking down the aisle with that brother in your dream may not necessarily mean God is saying he's your husband*. Also, it should be noted that conviction about whom to marry must be mutual. If you're seeing and hearing all the time and it is not mutual, stay on your lane and pray that God reveal same to the other person. Don't intimidate sisters with "thus saith the Lord", it is not necessary. Just declare your "manifesto" and trust God to convince her.


*PRAYER*: Father, I receive wisdom to handle matters crucial matters in Jesus name.
