DEVOTIONAL 0518 | Source start line: 46564
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
5/21/22, 8:04 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: ProjectSave Devotional
Saturday,
21st May, 2022.

*I DIDN'T SLEEP- I WAS WRONG*


Genesis 2:21 So the Lord God caused the man to fall into a deep sleep. While the man slept, the Lord God took out one of the man’s ribs and closed up the opening. NLT


I have shared my personal story several times. This story illustrates today's teaching. In my 300 Level in the university, towards the end of first semester,  I came under a self- induced pressure to enter a relationship. I just thought to myself that I was fit enough to also be in a relationship. I saw some brethren who were in a relationship and within me I felt I could  find myself a sister too. I saw it as "No big deal" affair because I was filled with the Spirit, laying hands on the sick and walking in the supernatural. I became over confident and anxious at the same time. What a bad combo. By the time all these thoughts ran through my mind, I was already close to a sister, my room mates and colleagues match- made us in their minds and they told me we were good together. "Cooler Ministration" started and this made my  room mates love her the more. They insisted we were good together. Beware of people who counsel you after they're induced. All these adulations and recommendations got the best of me and I felt I was making the right decision. Only one person cautioned me to be more patient before jumping into that relationship but I ignored her counsel thinking she was jealous because she wasn't in any relationship then.


I thought to myself that God was involved because the voice of men is the voice of God- "Vox Populi Vox Dei." I was wrong.  From the day I entered this relationship till the last day I called it off, I lost my peace. I knew something was wrong. The first time I heard something that sounded like an audible voice telling me to dissolve that relationship  was during that period of my confusion. Eventually, I did and peace was restored. That was the only serious pre marital relationship I had before I met my wife. There was something I failed to do which made me run into error.  I didn't do what Adam did when God wanted to give him a wife, I failed to sleep. In the opening text, Adam's sleep was a literal sleep but figuratively, every single that intend to get it right must "Sleep." God couldn't bring Adam a wife until he slept.  To "sleep" is to be free from pressure and anxieties of wanting to get married. Adam wasn't looking here and there for the sister that fit, God brought him a wife.


In the state of "Sleeping", anxiety and pressure will be dead and you won't be looking here and there for the sister that fits your spec. *As you "Sleep", you give God the opportunity to work on you before launching you into marriage because God's concern is not about you marrying fast but making you right before marrying right*. Some persons think age is what qualifies them to be married but as far as God is concerned, becoming "right"  for marriage precede finding "Mr/ Miss Right." *Some may remain single for a while until God is through with them.* God want to work on you and sharpen your life before certifying you fit. God saw Adam fit before bringing him a wife. It is in that state of "Sleeping" communion is enhanced and clarity is gotten. We have many wrong "Thus Saith The Lord" today among singles because they're fully awake looking and checking out several sisters. Some brothers are doing a trial and error kind of thing asking out several sisters at the same. Some sisters have two to three brothers they keep at the same time with the intention of marrying the one that fits their spec. You can't get it right like that. Allow God put you in a state of deep "Sleep". *It seems to me that those who got it right are those whose minds were not preoccupied with marriage but kingdom service and loving the Lord*. God himself has a way of bringing the right man or woman your way in places, times and moments you least expect. *Stop jumping from one singles deliverance meeting to the other, your problem isn't singleness.*  Can you focus your mind on God and His kingdom and live a normal life? Some singles are anxious to the point of depression and other health challenges. Why destroy yourself before the coming of your much awaited "Prince Charming?"  God is the best match-maker.  Anxiety is against the rules of this game. The journey starts with "Sleeping."


*PRAYER*: I receive wisdom in Jesus name

DEVOTIONAL 0519 | Source start line: 46647

5/22/22, 5:03 PM - Lanre Oyeleke: ProjectSave Devotional
Sunday,
22nd May, 2022.


*THE PARADOX*



1Tim 4:8 For bodily exercise profiteth little: but godliness is profitable unto all things, having promise of the life that now is, and of that which is to come. KJV



My parents have been married for over forty (40) years without taking a break for a day. Their union produced four wonderful children of whom I'm proud to be one. Both of them got saved in the 70s in a conservative evangelical church. The emphasis of that era was "Holiness". Those were the days when Christians were derogatorily called "SU". Godliness was the message and thorough Christian discipleship was their forte. I was told how matured believers discipled believers and held them accountable on almost all aspects of their lives. They went that far. Though there were certain extremes in their doctrines and dogma, at no point in history was any church movement perfect but that era stood for Christ and godliness and it paid off for their lives, marriage inclusive. Also, i looked at the  marriages of those who were my parent's contemporaries, those marriages( of over 40-50 years) are still standing because they drank from the same spiritual well and had the same values. That doesn't mean they had perfect marriages but separation and  divorce was uncommon among the brethren of that era.





Those days,  they had no privilege of listening to "Family Life" teachers and preachers like we have today. "Singles Conference" and "Marriage Seminars" weren't common likewise books and tapes. Knowledge and resource persons on the subject of marriage was limited unlike today where we have many preachers and teachers on the subject of marriage and family life. Our generation have numerous books and mesages on that subject.  *The paradox in all these is that the generation with plethora of resources and knowledge on marriage have had numerous cases of chaotic homes, separation and divorce while the generation with little or no resources had successful and stable marriages. This a paradox*. Chaotic homes, separation and divorce is rife today among Christians than it was yesteryears. I've not in anyway opined that older generation of believers didn't experience the marital maladies we're seeing today. They did but not as much as we have now.





For sometime, this observation of mine troubled me. I just wanted to know why these two generations had such differences on marital issues. In my meditation, the Holy Spirit dropped the  line of a  scripture on my heart: *"...but godliness is profitable unto all things,..*"(1Tim 4:8). Another version read thus: *"...but godliness (spiritual training) is of value in everything and in every way,...*"(AMP). Hey! My inner eyes opened. I saw that godliness is profitable for marriage too. The generation that pursued godliness( spiritual training) had good homes& stable marriages. *To think godliness is only profitable for eternity is to be short-sighted*. If the bible says godliness( spiritual training) is profitable for all things (marriage inclusive)then I believe that should be the pursuit of all. *A godly man will not cheat on his wife neither will he beat her. He will love her(unconditionally) just as Christ love the church. Nagging will not be the stock-in- trade of a godly woman neither will she make her home hellish for her husband. She will submit to her husband as unto the Lord*. Godly singles won't play pranks and use gimmicks to get a spouse, they'll wait on God till they get a clear direction from Him. They won't have problem discerning His voice because they are consistent with God. Sexual purity will not be compromised by godly singles. *Marital maladies are traceable to deficiency in godliness*. Nothing beats godliness (spiritual training). If singles and married train to be godly in every way, their marriages will glorify God. Our consecration has a direct impact on our marriage. Let's pursue godliness.



*PRAYER*: Father, I receive the grace to submit myself to spiritual training in Jesus name.

23rd May, 2022.

*FELT NEED VS REAL NEED*


1Tim 4:8 For bodily exercise profiteth little: but godliness is profitable unto all things, having promise of the life that now is, and of that which is to come. KJV



Several years ago, I received an invitation to preach in a program. After the program, my host told me to pray for a lady. I prayed for her and by God's grace, she got her miracle. Seeing the hand of God in her life, she introduced her friend to me for prayers. This lady wanted me to join her in prayers for a life partner. I agreed but I decided to probe into her life as led by the Spirit of God. I needed to know certain things about her before committing myself. In our discussions, I discovered that her values for sexual purity was totally anti bible. She was  sexually active in a relationship with a guy and she didn't see anything wrong about it. She wanted me to pray for her so that this guy will marry her.  In our discussions, she didn't see anything wrong with cohabitation as well. Anytime I try to talk her into what the scriptures says, she doesn't pay much attention. She wasn't interested. What she wanted was marriage and nothing more but I saw a need greater than marriage. She cut off from me.



Her felt need was marriage but by reason of insight, I knew her real need was godliness. Probably, she thought about her age and the proverbial biological clock that was ticking. Her desperation for marriage was totally ungodly. After sometime, I saw her heavily pregnant in her wedding picture on facebook. I wasn't surprised because she saw nothing wrong in that. She got what she wanted- marriage.  Most times, our felt needs are those needs that gives instant gratification but our real needs are those things that we need to be properly established in life and destiny. There are many singles who major on the minor and minor on the major. They think their need is a mate. Yes, it could be but there's something much needed than a mate yet undiscovered. What if God gives you your dream spouse today, do you have the character to sustain that marriage? Have you built a strong value system for fidelity in marriage? Do you have financial discipline to run and maintain a home?  *As a single, it won't cost God anything to grant your felt need, probably a spouse, but it will cost God time to process you for your real need because the actualization of your real need will sustain the realization of your felt need*.



Dearest "Mr&Mrs", you may not be enjoying your marriage. You may wonder why your home has become a fighting arena. You both may have gone to God in prayers to restore peace. Counsellors may have been consulted to mediate yet without result. Take your time to discover what your real need is. In scriptures, I discovered the real need that will profit us all for life that now is( marriage inclusive) and for the life to come. *Godliness is our real need*. If we get it right here, we'll get it right in everything. This is simply because the bible says in 1Tim4:8, *"... Godliness is profitable unto all things,...*" Often, we love to hear people teach us things like "Seven Tips For Getting The Right Spouse", "The ABC of How To Make Your Marriage Work". While those "tips" are good, there's something better.  It is called "Godliness". It is God's prescription for a life of peace and joy. It is God's eternal principle for profiting in life. *Man can run his life on methods but will never run life as intended by God without God's principle of Godliness in place*. In the kingdom, we rise through godliness, men will crash without it.


*PRAYER*: Father, help me to focus on my real need and take me to your processing room. Process and prepare me for profitable living in Jesus name.

DEVOTIONAL 0521 | Source start line: 46759

5/24/22, 7:02 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: ProjectSave Devotional
Tuesday
24th May,2022.


*THE PURSUIT OF OUR REAL NEED*


Galatians 4:19 Oh, my dear children! I feel as if I’m going through labor pains for you again, and they will continue until Christ is fully developed in your lives. NLT



Years back, a married woman poured her heart to me concerning the happenings in her marriage. She told me how her husband was messing around with young ladies and would abuse her physically. Surprisingly, she told me how prayerful he was during their years of courtship. He was fervent but a few months into their marriage, she saw his true colour. Lest I forget, they cohabited and had sex before marriage. At that time, all she wanted was a husband and she got it. She broke divine rules and compromised on godly standards to get her felt need. Probably, this man tick all her boxes but she missed one thing that was greater than other things she saw as marriageable qualities- "CHRIST FORMATION". Herself needed this just as her husband also needed Christ to be formed in him.


*Our real need is the formation of Christ in our lives*. This has become my utmost desire. A single brother whose life is conformed to the image of Christ will not pretend in any way to get a sister. Some brothers are pretenders. I've heard of brothers who became prayerful and fervent in church because they wanted to get a lady. I once knew a bad boy who began to attend church because of a sister she loved to marry. This sister fell for that show of piety and they got married. Let me tell you this simple but profound truth: *No one can pretend successfully  around you except you choose to overlook certain basic signals*. The man or woman in whose life Christ hasn't been formed cannot successfully pretend to be like Christ. A monkey can never roar like a lion. It is an abberation for a Christian single to be attracted to someone that has no iota of Christ in him or her. If such happens, something is definitely wrong somewhere. *It takes someone who is being transformed daily into the image into the image of Christ to discern and attract another man or woman who is on that same journey*. In the faith, we marry a co passenger on the journey to Christ formation. It is good to look out for good qualities in people you want to marry but please in all thy looking, look out for Christlikeness. *Don't confuse zeal, charisma and a show of piety for the real life of Christ*. That he can speak "capital letter" tongues doesn't mean he is marriageable. That she's prophesying and rolling on the floor, scattering our chairs in church doesn't mean she's manifesting the fruit of the Spirit. *The gift may attract but it is the fruit that will sustain your marriage*.


Dearest, if we want peace in our homes and lives in general, our prayer should be: "Lord, let Christ be formed in me". *If husbands and wives are in the same pursuit of Christ formation, homes will be peaceful. It doesn't mean there won't be issues but they'll quickly resolve it because they want to be in fellowship with their master and Lord*. Separation and divorce will be far from a marriage where Christ is being formed into the lives of those involved. A man ruled by Christ will say "I'm sorry" to his wife not because he's wrong but because he value peace in his relationship. Ego and pride will give way for humility. When Christ formation begins in the life of a woman, submission becomes natural. Respect for her husband won't be forced. She won't talk disrespectfully to her husband like some women do. Who says this is not the real need? No matter the number of books, seminars and resources on marriage you read/ attend, the things taught in those books won't work for you until CHRIST is formed in you. How I wish our singles &married meetings can also major on themes like: "Christian Consecration & Development of Christ- like character." Those are the real need. We must master the flesh and overcome it for the effulgence of Christ in our lives. To everyone, whether single or married, this I say: *"But put ye on the Lord Jesus Christ, and make not provision for the flesh, to fulfil the lusts thereof*"( Romans13:14).



*PRAYER*: Father, I receive the grace to put on Christ. I remove the garb of the flesh. Let Christ be fully formed in me daily in Jesus name.

DEVOTIONAL 0522 | Source start line: 46862
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
5/25/22, 5:56 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: ProjectSave Devotional
Wednesday
25th May,2022.


*LOSING BIRTHRIGHT*

Hebrews12:16 Lest there be any fornicator, or profane person, as Esau, who for one morsel of meat sold his birthright.


The concept of culture of "Birthright" is not easily understood by people with western orientation because it is not in their culture.  Though I have seen some cultures in Africa come close to it but there's a subtle resistance against it even up to the point of litigation because civilization is creeping in gradually. The Jewish culture is patriarchal and birthright is about inheritance. The first born male takes the larger share of his father's inheritance while the rest is shared among his siblings except the females. However, in a situation where there's no male child, the females can possess their father's property like the case of Zelophehad's daughters( Num27:1-11). Culturally, it was honourable for the first born male to inherit his father. That doesn't mean he is lazy or couldn't make something good out of life but by virtue of his position, that was his right. In fact, first born sons are prepared to inherit and sustain the family heritage. They train them to be responsible so that they can handle responsibility. Jacob knew the worth of what he wanted to collect from Esau when he asked him to sell his birthright to him(Genesis 25:29–33). The bible says: *"For ye know how that afterward, when he(Esau) would have inherited the blessing, he was rejected: for he found no place of repentance, though he sought it carefully with tears.*"(Heb12:17 Emphasis mine). The Jews don't joke with their "Birthright". They guard and protect it jealously. Don't forget the story of Naboth. He refused to sell the vineyard he inherited to Ahab because it was a legacy inheritance: *"Ahab said to Naboth, “Let me have your vineyard to use for a vegetable garden, since it is close to my palace. In exchange I will give you a better vineyard or, if you prefer, I will pay you whatever it is worth.”But Naboth replied, “The Lord forbid that I should give you the inheritance(C) of my ancestors*”(1 Kings21:2-3).


There's a deeper truth the bible is teaching us from the story of Esau and Jacob. There's more to birthright thatln meets the eye. In the opening text, Esau's action was regarded as profane and paralled with sexual immorality. So we can say that a sexually immoral person is like Esau who sold his birthright. *Every illegal sexual encounter doesn't leave you the same way you were before the encounter. There's a spiritual exchange that takes place.*  Virtues and glories are traded for demonic invasion and destabilization of destiny. *Sex outside marriage( pre marital or adultery) gives the devil a legal ground and open door to operate in peoples life without restraint.* Through sex, poison can be injected into a man's body and medical science won't be able to trace the cause of health breakdown. Some guys think it is smartness having sex with several ladies. They see it as a test of their manliness. Unfortunately, they've become walking corpses because they've been emptied of virtues and glories. In the glory bank of the enemy are great glories of men and women of destinies who could have changed the course of history in their families and generation but they carelessly "donated" their future for a few minutes of pleasure. There's something great about you and in you. The quickest way the enemy steals greatness is through sexual immorality. Have you noticed that when evil people want to bring a powerful man down, sex is the tool they use often. Why? Sex drains a man faster than any other sin. I've heard people say sin is sin. That's true but one kills faster. Just as there are fast action drugs, there are fast-action sins. Sexual immorality is the chiefest.



We live in a sex crazy world. Sex is almost becoming very cheap to get. Unfortunately, even the married aren't spared from this. The temptation comes to us all almost daily and you cannot be too sure about yourself on this. That pride of "I can't fall into sexual sin" is the beginning of the fall it self. That's why we must take caution lest we become a victim. Apostle Paul advised: *"For this is the will of God, your sanctification: that you should abstain from sexual immorality;that each of you should know how to possess his own vessel in sanctification and honor,*"(1Thes4:3-4). Paul was talking to believers in Thessalonica and I believe that admonition applies to us today. Each of us must learn to master our bodies so that we won't fall prey of the wave of sexual immorality in this generation.  The key to overcoming any form of sexual temptation is to abide in Christ and obey Romans 12:2 injunction: *"Do not conform to the pattern of this world, but be transformed by the renewing of your mind. Then you will be able to test and approve what God’s will is—his good, pleasing and perfect will*". The devil is hunting for destinies. You may look ordinary in your own eyes but Satan knows you have a great inheritance by virtue of your union with Christ. He want to collect that inheritance from you like Jacob did to Esau. *Condom may prevent infections and pregnancy but it can't prevent demons from draining you and taking valuables away from your life. Run from sexual immorality. That way, you'll save yourself and preserve your destiny.*

*PRAYER*: Father, I receive the grace not to sell my birthright on the altar of sexual immorality in Jesus name. Amen

5/26/22, 6:38 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: PROJECTSAVE DEVOTIONAL
Thursday,
26th May, 2022.


*GRANTING DESIRES*


Psalms 37: 4 Take delight in the LORD, and he will give you the desires of your heart.



Several times, I've heard some brethren quote the opening text. Often, they don't quote it in full. They emphasize on the last line of that verse "...and he will give you the desires of your heart". Most times, this scripture is used to support prayer requests that are often devoid of God's will for their lives. Sometimes, when we teach or preach to people about following the will of God for their lives, instead of asking the Lord what His will is for their lives, they go ahead and demand from God that thing which has been on their heart. Their belief is that as long as the desires are good, God will grant it. Hey! That sounds right but it is not scripturally right. *Somethings may be good but not in God's will for your life*. God is a perfect gentle man who doesn't impose His will on His children. He made man a free moral agent who can decide whatever he wants.



*It is not every good desire that is in line with God's will*. Oftentimes, we mean well for our lives but that doesn't mean we are right considering what God's will is. Sometimes, Satan can allow a believer have his desires met because he knows that having those things desired can sway him/her off from the perfect will of God. For example, some persons married without asking God if whom they married is God's will. They just fell in love with someone who ticked all their boxes and they got married. To such persons, whatever they want is what God must sanction. That they got what they wanted does not necessarily mean God has a hand in it.  The devil can allow God's children have "good" things so they forget to press in for the best of God. As they say, the enemy of the best is good. I don't want you to be naive about this. *God's desire is to grant only desires that aligns with His will for your life*. He is not an arbitrary God. He is a God whose actions and decisions are well planned and thought of.



The opening text starts this way: *"Take delight in the Lord*". What does this mean? It is love the Lord and lost in His will for your life. It is a dimension of continuous fellowship and communion with God whereby He becomes the Lord and driver of your life. At this point in your life, His desires become your desires. So when the Psalmist say "...And he will give you the desires of your heart". The "desire" implied here is that very thing God desire for our lives that He implanted in our hearts as a result of delighting in Him. There's a nexus between delighting in the Lord and granting of the resultant desires. It will be totally wrong to think God will just grant any carnal desire. *Whenever we have our carnal desires, just know that we are in a permissive terrain but God is not happy with it*. Israelites wanted a king, they got it but it wasn't God's plan for their nation. *Mature believers don't impose desires on God, they walk with God to know His desires and then they pursue that desire for their lives*. This is the difference between babies and sons. God's will for your life is the best, your duty is to seek and discover it. His will must become our desires.


*PRAYER*: Father, let your will become my desire in Jesus name.

DEVOTIONAL 0524 | Source start line: 47032

5/27/22, 7:18 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: PROJECTSAVE DEVOTIONAL
Friday,
27th May, 2022.

*POSITIONING FOR DIRECTION*. Pt.1


Proverbs 3:5 Trust in the Lord with all your heart; do not depend on your own understanding. NLT


*GOD CAN BE TRUSTED*


From my observation, some believers do not trust God enough to lead them in all areas of their lives. As a preacher/teacher of the Gospel, I've been asked several times by some singles: "What if God gives me a spouse I don't like"? As a matter of fact, the major fear of some persons from seeking the will of God is this. They have fears that God may not give them what they like. This is a distorted view of God that Satan has planted in the hearts of people. *God is committed to giving you His very best*. This may not be what you want but He'll surely give you what you need. This is what Jesus has to say on this matter: *"You parents—if your children ask for a loaf of bread, do you give them a stone instead? Or if they ask for a fish, do you give them a snake? Of course not! So if you sinful people know how to give good gifts to your children, how much more will your heavenly Father give good gifts to those who ask him*" (Matthew 7:9-11 NLT).


*To think God will lead you into something that is harmful or not beneficial is to bring God to a level lower than that of our earthly parents*. We serve a loving God that can be trusted. The day you understand the fatherhood of God, you will not have a hard time trusting Him for anything. In matters of divine direction, God is committed to ensuring you are in His will not yours. He's committed to leading you into what you need for your life not necessarily what you want. What we want might not be what we need. *When you pray for direction and it seems God is not tilting towards what you desire, it is obvious God is willing to give what you need. In the mind of God, He is mindful of His plans and purpose for your life. That's why God won't give you just anything or lead you anyhow*. You may not understand the rationale behind His leading sometimes but someday, it will make sense to you. The ways of God aren't our ways: *“For my thoughts are not your thoughts, nor are your ways my ways,” says the Lord. For as the heavens are higher than the earth, so are my ways higher than your ways, and my thoughts than your thoughts*" (Isaiah 55:8-9).



To enjoy divine direction, your trust in God must be a blind trust. Abram trusted God blindly by leaving his father's house for an unknown destination just because he heard God. If he were to be in our generation, most people will call him a fool for setting out on a journey without a definite destination. God's expectation is for you to trust Him that He is God enough to lead you aright and not amiss.  He want you to know that He has your best interest at heart. If you can settle this in your heart, you will not have a hard time hearing from God and obeying him. The latter part of that opening text says *"do not depend on your own understanding*". No matter how intelligent you are, you cannot lead yourself aright in life. *Divine direction is not for the "Intelligent" nor for men who lean on their understanding, it is for those who can trust God blindly*.


*PRAYER*: Father, I receive the grace to trust you totally for direction in Jesus name.

DEVOTIONAL 0525 | Source start line: 47206

5/28/22, 5:08 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: ProjectSave Devotional
Saturday,
28th May, 2022.


*POSITIONING FOR DIRECTION*. Pt.2


Proverbs 3:6 Seek his will in all you do, and he will show you which path to take.  NLT

*SEEK HIS WILL FIRST*


Severally, I've seen believers who took very important steps with the mindset of "If it is the Lord's will, it will work out". Some entered a relationship with this mindset as well hoping to find God's will in the midst of their decision. Most times, it is hard to decipher God's will in such situations especially when emotions is involved. After you're emotionally attached to a lady or brother with endearing words often used in communication, In such situations, you may find it hard to decipher God's will. Imagine a married man of years asking to know if His marriage is God's will. That's mischievous. You don't seek God's will in such circumstances, the marriage covenant is binding until death. Many  singles are in a relationship hoping to know "If" it is God's will. Some will say, "If it ends in marriage, then it is God's will but if it doesn't, then God is not involved."  *Trial and error is not God's method of speaking to His children*. I understand that God can use circumstances to align His children to His will but not when emotion is attached or when the heart is rigidly made up.



*The right order for finding direction is to seek God's will before making decisions not after the decision is made or in it*. Wise people don't start a journey before asking for direction rather they get details of the journey before embarking on it. We have an example in David. The Amalekites raided his camp in Ziklag. They burnt it down, took wives and children of his companions. The bible said they cried until they couldn't cry anymore. David didn't take any action Immediately. He sought God's face first to know the next step: *"Then David asked the Lord, “Should I chase after this band of raiders? Will I catch them?” And the Lord told him, “Yes, go after them. You will surely recover everything that was taken from you!” David and his men rushed in among them and slaughtered them throughout that night and the entire next day until evening. None of the Amalekites escaped except 400 young men who fled on camels*"( 1 Samuel 30:8, 17 NLT). Though he had military might to pursue those raiders but he didn't take that step until he received a clear direction from God. That was why he got the backing of God. That's the way to go.



God has promised to reveal His will to us: *"I will instruct thee and teach thee in the way which thou shalt go: I will guide thee with mine eye*",(Psalm 32:8 KJV). All you need do is to ask according to His word: *"Ask, and it shall be given you; seek, and ye shall find; knock, and it shall be opened unto you: for every one that asketh receiveth; and he that seeketh findeth; and to him that knocketh it shall be opened*"(Matthew 7:7-8 KJV). You don't need gimmicks to make decisions, just ask God. The Holy Spirit is your advantage. It is His duty to reveal God's mind to you concerning every matter. Jesus said, *"But when he, the Spirit of truth, comes, he will guide you into all the truth. He will not speak on his own; he will speak only what he hears, and he will tell you what is yet to come*"( John 16:3). Don't gamble with your life. At all times, seek God's will first before making decisions.


*PRAYER*: Holy Spirit, you know the mind of the father.  Reveal the will of the father to me concerning (...mention your case) in Jesus name.

5/29/22, 6:05 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: ProjectSave Devotional

*POSITIONING FOR DIRECTION*. Pt.3


Proverbs 3:6 Seek his will in all you do, and he will show you which path to take.  NLT



*DON'T BE SELECTIVE IN SEEKING*

Over the years, my observation among church folks is that we often hear alot of "God told me she's my wife" when singles want to marry. That period when brothers&sisters are in need of a spouse is the period they pray asking direction from God. In most churches or campus fellowship, it is common to hear cases of several brothers telling one sister she's the chosen one. In fact, some will paint a graphic picture of the revelation they had. This leaves alot of sisters confused because when two to three 'spiritual' brothers are seeing one sister in a vision, sisters in such shoes may be confused except they've heard God clearly. As a preacher& counselor, I've seen several brothers who told me God spoke to them about a sister, entered into a relationship and later broke the relationship because they felt God wasn't in the relationship. Hey! But they said God led them into it. If you ask the average Christian single in a relationship how God led them into that relationship, they'll always have something to say but when such relationships break, you'll wonder if it wasn't God they heard at first.





The problem lies in the fact most people are not familiar with God's leading prior to marriage. They start learning to hear God at the point of marriage. Very risky. This account for the reason we have many fake "thus saith the Lord, you'll be my wife". The only time some church folks ever prayed asking God for direction
was when they wanted to marry. When they wanted to make decisions about traveling abroad, where to live, career and all that, nothing was said of seeking God's face. They made those decisions on their own but when it was marriage O' Clock, they began to ask God to lead. That's what I call selective seeking. *You cannot hear God accurately on vital issue like marriage if you've not been hearing God on little things prior to marriage*.




Divine direction is no magic. *Only those that seek God in all things will be led in all things*. The opening text enjoins that we seek God in ALL things not some things. Don't always think some things can be decided on your own without involving God. You're limited in knowledge. To master the leading of the Spirit, you must have the habit of hearing God on little things before the "big" things. This is a call to a closer walk with God. *Those that have fellowship with God will not have problem hearing God*. Don't be a believer who comes to God only when in need of direction. You must be regular with God and in touch with heaven. Your heart must be so submissive to God's will in all things. I pray you won't miss God.



*PRAYER*: Father, I receive the grace to seek you in all things and not to be selective in Jesus name

DEVOTIONAL 0527 | Source start line: 47423

5/30/22, 6:12 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: PROJECTSAVE DEVOTIONAL
Monday,
30th May, 2022.

*SETTING SPIRITUAL LAWS IN MOTION* Pt.1

Mark 11:23 I tell you the truth, you can say to this mountain, ‘May you be lifted up and thrown into the sea,’ and it will happen. But you must really believe it will happen and have no doubt in your heart. NLT



*THE LAW OF CONFESSION*


Kenneth E. Hagin of blessed memory shared an experience in one of his sermons. He was called upon to pray for a man in coma. He spent sometime praying in tongues. After a while, he heard the Spirit say, "He has set spiritual laws in motion, release his spirit and let him go". Himself also repeated those words several times. He told the pastor of the young man what he heard. The pastor had a similar confirmation but both of them were taken aback on his matter because they wondered the kind of spiritual law the man set in motion. After a while, the brother of the man in coma showed up and said "my brother has always been saying he wouldn't live to see his 40th birthday". Immediately, Kenneth E. Hagin knew his confession set the spiritual law in motion and no one could reverse it except him. In agreement with the pastor, both of them released his spirit as instructed by the Spirit, the man in coma smiled and gave up thereafter.



Our words are not ordinary. Words are tools in the realm of the spirit used to set spiritual laws in motion. You can determine the course of your life through your words. The bible says, *"The tongue can bring death or life; those who love to talk will reap the consequences*"(Prov18:21). Words are powerful. I remember we set out for a village outreach years back. I joined the evangelistic team of my church. On our way, we got to a point where the road was flooded. Not so much a flood, we saw smaller vehicles passing and crossing the flood to the other side. Our driver said our vehicle won't cross to the other side. We told him to give a try since other vehicles were crossing. Those vehicles were smaller and close to the earth while ours was raised. As he tried to drive past the waters, the vehicle stopped right in the middle of that flood. We all blamed him for being negative in his confession. We couldn't go further until we got another vehicle. He set in motion a spiritual law that countered our intention. *Never allow negative people in your team*.


The writer of Proverbs says, *"From the fruit of their lips people are filled with good things, and the work of their hands brings them reward*"( Proverbs 12:14). This is profound. There are confessions I made several years ago and I'm seeing the manifestation today. There are confessions I'm making today that will shape my tomorrow. Some may sound too big, I don't care. I know I serve a big God who does big things. *"Your life will go in the direction of your words. Your words will shape your life*. What are you saying about your life? Some persons joke with negative utterances. They think it to mean something that doesn't matter. Their understanding is that those words are empty and meaningless. Your words are not empty, they determine your life. Never allow circumstances determine your words. Whatever you're going through will pass just maintain word based confession. Keep saying what God's word says about you and don't stop. *Very soon, your words will catch up with your life*.


*CONFESSION*: I will not fulfill the desires of the enemy. My life shall refelect God's glory and beauty. I walk in power, in favour and in the supernatural. I refuse to be a victim of the evil in my world. My lifitng shall be divine. I refuse to die of sickness, stray bullet or spiritual attack. I walk in divine health. The battles of life will not conquer me. My purpose I'll fulfill in Jesus name. I am triumphant.


N:B Pray and partner with us as we reach out to the Maasai tribe of Tanzania. You can send support to: 2023885279- First Bank of Nigeria. ProjectSave Int'l Ministry.


info@projectsaveng.org

DEVOTIONAL 0528 | Source start line: 47512
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
5/31/22, 6:38 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: ProjectSave Devotional
Tuesday
31st May,2022.


*SETTING SPIRITUAL LAWS IN MOTION* Pt.2


Mark 11:23 I tell you the truth, you can say to this mountain, ‘May you be lifted up and thrown into the sea,’ and it will happen. But you must really believe it will happen and have no doubt in your heart. NLT


*THE LAW OF FAITH*


When I was in secondary school, there was this big sister in our neighbourhood whom we talk often. She was years ahead of me in everything. If we were to be in thesame school, I would call her "Senior xxx" as the custom was when addressing senior students.  One day, during our conversation, she said her belief is that a student must spend minimum of two years at home before gaining admission into the university. Her belief was that entering higher institution immediately after secondary education was a misnomer. Eventually, I entered the university and was progressing steadily. This lady struggled to gain admission for years. She didn't gain admission until my third year in the university. Academically, she was sound but her delay in getting admission was due to her wrong mindset and beliefs. Eventually, when she got admission, I remembered what she said. It occurred to me that her belief played out in her life. She set in motion her delay with her belief.


You can set in motion what will happen in your life through what you believe in your heart. In life, it is "Be it unto you according to your faith". *Your good confessions will make no difference if it doesn't align with what you believe in your heart*. Word based confessions plus anti bible beliefs will yield no good result. The way it works is word based confession + right believing = Good result. You must radically work on what goes on inside of you. Many people say "It is well" when in their hearts, they're saying "Life is bad". *The content of your heart determines the happenings in your life*. Apostle Paul says, *"For it is by believing in your heart that you are made right with God, and it is by openly declaring your faith that you are saved*"(Romans 10:10 NLT). Though he was talking in context of salvation here but we can see that what we believe in our hearts is crucial. *If salvation is dependent on what we believe in our hearts, then other happenings in our lives will largely depend on what we believe. No magic about it. Those who believe one cannot be wealthy without cheating or cutting corners will always find a way to steal, cheat or cut corners. Their lives always gravitate towards their beliefs.


What do you believe? Consciously, you must work on the content of your heart using the word of God. *Let the word of penetrate your heart and let it be firmly rooted such that when negative thoughts come, it will meet with a stiff resistance.* Negative thoughts will always come, but you have a duty not to allow it gain root in your heart. The moment it becomes rooted in your heart, it becomes a belief system. Dwell on God's word enough, meditate on it and speak it always till it becomes a part of you. When the devil comes with negative thoughts, don't keep silent, talk back and keep saying what you believe in the written word of God until it is rooted in your heart. Paul says, *"Let your roots grow down into him, and let your lives be built on him. Then your faith will grow strong in the truth you were taught, and you will overflow with thankfulness*"(Col 2:7 NLT). This great apostle further said,  *"Let the Word of Christ dwell in you richly in all wisdom;...*"(Colossians 3:16). When God's word is rooted in your heart, your confessions will automatically produce result. What you say and what you believe must align. They  matter to God.


*CONFESSION*: The word of God is changing me. I am transformed daily and walking in the light of who God says I am. My heart is open to the word, it is gaining root right now. I will never live a defeated life in Jesus name.



N:B Join us to pray in the Spirit 9pm ( Nigerian time) every night on zoom:https://us02web.zoom.us/j/86794796510?pwd=VncrZjBCWE9yWFJaVStPOUZ2UC92dz09


info@projectsaveng.org

DEVOTIONAL 0529 | Source start line: 47686
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
6/1/22, 6:57 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: ProjectSave Devotional
Wednesday
1st June, 2022.



*SETTING SPIRITUAL LAWS IN MOTION* Pt.3



Joshua 21:43 Thus the Lord gave to Israel all the Land which he swore to give to their fathers; and having taken possession of it they settled there. And the Lord gave them rest on every side, just as he had sworn to their fathers; not one of all their enemies had withstood them, for the Lord had given all their enemies into their hands. Not one of all the good promises which the Lord had made to the house of Israel had failed; all came to pass.


*THE LAW OF CORRESPONDING ACTION*



Do you remember the story of the twelve spies? They were the men Moses sent to spy the land God promised. Ten gave a very bad report. They were faithless and discouraged. They saw giants they couldn't defeat and saw themselves as grasshoppers.  Their report made almost everyone in their camp to shiver for fear. In fact, they desired Egypt again because they felt Moses wanted to waste their lives in the wilderness. Only Caleb and Joshua gave a different report. The bible says, *"Then Caleb silenced the people before Moses and said, “We should go up and take possession of the land, for we can certainly do it*". Hallelujah!!! Caleb and Joshua set in motion the spiritual law that led to their conquest. The other guys who activated a negative spiritual law died only Caleb and Joshua made it to the promised land. Now, there's something I want us to learn from this. After Joshua and Caleb spoke faith filled words, they didn't sit down waiting for angels to come do battle for them. They took a corresponding action that was in line with their faith. And what was it they did? They fought: *"When the people heard the sound of the rams’ horns, they shouted as loud as they could. Suddenly, the walls of Jericho collapsed, and the Israelites charged straight into the town and captured it. They completely destroyed everything in it with their swords—men and women, young and old, cattle, sheep, goats, and donkeys*"(Joshua 6:20-21 NLT). Joshua and the Israelites wouldn't have taken the land if they hadn't fought. No matter the good report or faith they had, fighting was required to make their faith a reality.




Another example is David when he faced Goliath. Both of them exchanged words. David spoke glowingly about the God of Israel and he expressed his faith in God. Hey! He didn't stop there. The bible says, *"As Goliath moved closer to attack, David quickly ran out to meet him. Reaching into his shepherd’s bag and taking out a stone, he hurled it with his sling and hit the Philistine in the forehead. The stone sank in, and Goliath stumbled and fell face down on the ground*"(1 Samuel 17:48-49 NLT). Did you read that patiently? David didn't wait for Goliath to strike him first before taking action. The bible says he ran towards him and hurled his sling at him. That was how he conquered. *Your faith will amount to nothing when it is not backed by corresponding action*. We have many who know so much about the first two laws and they do them but they always fail in this third law. Some persons who are trusting God for a better life and has confessy it several times are lazy not ready to lift a finger to make their confessions come to pass. *There are many praying people but a few action takers*. If you're not not ready to take action, faith is not needed. *We exercise our faith by taking the right action*. If you confess health, live healthy and do the things healthy people do. Don't eat carelessly. The power of God is needed for divine healing but it takes wisdom to live in divine  health. That wisdom is shown through the actions you take on daily basis.



Sam O. Salau says, *Profound Positive Confessions that lacks Profound Serious Actions will leave you in a Profound Pitiable State on the day that matters most to your destiny!*. Men that build their castles in the air through positive confessions are not worth companying with. If you say God has called you into the ministry and has made great promises to you, that great promise God made will amount to nothing if you don't take the right action. You must wake up in the morning to pray, study well and set out to your office to do things that will make those promises a reality. Stop sleeping and eating all day. Ministry work is real work. It is hard work. *God will do nothing for men who are not ready to do anything. He is not a magician*. He's expecting you to take corresponding action. When Jesus healed in the bible, he always gave instructions to the healed to take action. For example to an invalid man, you'll hear words like "Take up your mat". If the invalid didn't make attempt to take up his mat, he'll remain like that. Faith works when backed by corresponding action. *Words are cheap, righteous action is the real deal. Bring to reality God's promises by taking the right action*.


*PRAYER*: Father, I receive the grace to take the right action backing my faith in Jesus name.

DEVOTIONAL 0530 | Source start line: 47796

6/2/22, 6:21 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: PROJECTSAVE DEVOTIONAL
Thursday,
2nd June, 2022.


*SETTING SPIRITUAL LAWS IN MOTION* Pt.4


Galatians 6:7 Don’t be misled—you cannot mock the justice of God. You will always harvest what you plant. NLT


*THE LAW OF SOWING& REAPING*


Bishop David Oyedepo told the story of how the Holy Ghost prompted him to sacrifice something precious to him. Afterwards, God said to him, “My son David, even if you don’t want to be rich, it is too late.” His sacrificial giving set in motion spiritual law that opened doors of continuous financial supply. You cannot achieve this with prayer if you've not done the right thing. Personally, I've experienced this over and over. When lockdown started in the month of March in the year 2020, my expected income from my personal venture for that month wasn't much but what I got was half of what I would have gotten. Yet, as prompted by the Lord, I gave a large part of it to support those in need. It was as though the world wanted to crash because there was an increase in the frequency of eating of my boys. How do I feed these soldiers? I later discovered that other families had the same experience with their children, because they weren't going to school, their frequency of eating increased. Before I started raising money for those in need and field missionaries, the giving started with me. One of those days, my wife bought pepper with the little we had. The girl that grinded the pepper poured it away. We looked at each other and we couldn't cry. We can't pick a fight with the girl to buy another pepper. Quietly, my wife entered the house, to your tent O' Israel.



After a while, supplies came for us in excess without asking those God used. In fact, I was afraid at a time because the frequency at which the supplies came was short and almost in succession. Still, we didn't stop giving those in need. Dearest, don't be deceived, the law of sowing and reaping is still in force. Nothing has changed it. It is an eternal principle that God set in motion for those that want to experience divine supply. *According to Paul in the opening text, It will be injustice on the part of God for men to sow and not reap*. Read what the bible says about this eternal law in Genesis 8:22, *"As long as the earth endures, seedtime and harvest,...will never cease*.” The bible says nothing can change the principle of sowing and reaping not even modernization. Often, what some people pray about only requires the right action of sowing. No matter how long a farmer prays on his farm, if he refuses to plant on his farm, nothing will grow except weeds. Same applies to our lives. *Seed sowing isn't only restricted to money. It includes actions and thoughts. If you sow love, understanding and respect in your marriage, you'll get it back. Some spouses aren't mad, they're only reacting to bad seeds sown*.



I've seen alot of believers quote Philippians 4:19 when they're in need of divine supply yet they fail to realize that Paul wasn't just praying there. Rather, it is a statement  of wish consequent upon the action Philippian church took by helping his ministry financially:   *"As you know, you Philippians were the only ones who gave me financial help when I first brought you the Good News and then traveled on from Macedonia. No other church did this. Even when I was in Thessalonica you sent help more than once.*"(Philippians 4:15-16 NLT). As a result of what the Philippian church did by sending him more than enough supplies, then he said: *"But my God shall supply all your need according to his riches in glory by Christ Jesus*"(Philippians 4:19 KJV). Simple! *You will not experience Philippians 4:19 if you didn't practice Philippians 4:15-16*. In this kingdom, there are operational laws that prayer& fasting cannot change. Everything has its own place and application. As you go about your legitimate businesses or jobs ( which is the platform for blessings), keep sowing into kingdom expansion projects, lives of ministers of the Gospel and the poor. It is just a matter of time,  you'll reap it fully according to the justice system of God.


*PRAYER*: Father, I receive the grace to sow the right seeds in Jesus name.

DEVOTIONAL 0531 | Source start line: 47872

6/3/22, 6:34 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: PROJECTSAVE DEVOTIONAL
Friday,
3rd June, 2022.


*BEFORE YOU EAT THAT FOOD*


2 Timothy 2:15 Work hard so you can present yourself to God and receive his approval. Be a good worker, one who does not need to be ashamed and who correctly explains the word of truth. NLT



While on campus, sometimes in my 100 level, I had a pot of vegetable stew in my cupboard. One night, after returning from a fellowship meeting, there was no light I rushed to the cupboard to eat my vegetable stew. Everywhere was dark but I knew my way around. Also, I didn't bother checking the pot because I kept it in the cupboard. As I took some vegetable stew and landed it in my mouth, I felt something meaty in my mouth. I didn't swallow immediately because I remembered there was no fish, meat or crayfish in the vegetable. I paused the chewing and vomitted what was in my mouth quickly. I knew something was wrong so I reached for a light source. I was curious to know how "meat" got into my pot and the kind of "meat" it was. Alas! I found cockroaches. It was disheartening to know I ate cockroach. My mistake was that I assumed my food was in the condition I left it, I didn't bother to check.




The word of God taught or preached can be likened to food. The house of God is the spiritual "eatery" where spiritual meals are served. The preacher or teacher serves the food. Today, there are other sources or outlets where spiritual food is served. Alot of messages are there on social media for our viewing and listening. People eat from various sources these days and these calls for caution. Before you accept a teaching as true and edifying, please check it well to avoid eating "cockroaches". The "cockroach" of heresy is very much around us in subtle manner. When these heresies are being served, it comes with high sounding words and human philosophy with the people shouting "Rhema" or "Ride on preacher". The unfortunate thing about some preachers and teachers of these errors is that they skillfully mix truth with lies. They start most of their messages on a note of truth, even fundamental bible truths. The moment they get people's attention, they infuse their heretical teachings in the middle and end it on a note of truth. So what we have is lies sandwiched between truths. Very dangerous.




Can you be like the Berean Christians for once? The man who taught them was the most revered apostle. A man with depth of revelation whose teachings formed the core of most New testament doctrine. A bible scholar per excellence, very versed in bible languages ( Greek&Hebrew). He was no pushover among the apostles. He wrote almost half of the new testament. His CV is very intimidating. Till date, I don't know anyone born of a woman who has broken his record and may ever break it yet the Bereans  didn't look at his CV to take his teachings hook line and sinker. They searched scriptures to confirm if what this bible scholar taught were true: *"And the people of Berea were more open-minded than those in Thessalonica, and they listened eagerly to Paul’s message. They searched the Scriptures day after day to see if Paul and Silas were teaching the truth*"(Acts 17:11 NLT). Today, we're experiencing a reverse. Some believers now believe and take whatever their "Papa" say more than the words of God. As long as it is spoken by their "G.O." or "Bishop", it is the truth. They quickly forget that they're humans susceptible to error. Years down the line, if these preachers discover their errors and recant, their followers who once defended them may be neck-deep in it such that coming out may be a challenge. A popular American preacher renounced the "Prosperity Gospel" he once preached and even asked for forgiveness. Surprisingly, some are still towing that dangerous path. The "Papa", "G.O." or "Bishop" you're defending today may find the truth tomorrow and repent. *Please, don't follow men to hell even if they're popular preachers. DON'T BE LOYAL TO ERROR IN THE NAME OF HONOUR*. The Christian walk is not built on what a preacher say but on what God said in His word. Beware cult-like following. It is dangerous to be under a man who force his ideas down the throat of people without giving room for questioning. Some churches&preachers are like that. That's a cult Don't eat "cockroach", check before eating.



*PRAYER*: Father, I receive the grace to be loyal to you and your word. Help me to check my spiritual food properly before eating in Jesus name.

DEVOTIONAL 0532 | Source start line: 47917

6/4/22, 10:05 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: ProjectSave Devotional
Saturday,
4th June, 2022.


*PREPARE FOR OPPORTUNITIES*


Eph 6:15 and your feet shod with the preparation of the gospel of peace; KJV


Years back, while in the seminary, I studied my bible hermeneutics( interpretation) manual after the exam.  I passed the exam so there was no need to read it like someone preparing for examination.  However, on a weekend,  I just picked it up casually and I read a portion where the "discrepancy" in the account of timing of Jesus' crucifixion was recorded in Mark (15:25) and John's Gospel(19:14-15) was explained. Though it is not a discrepancy, it is just the context of account as regards the timing that differs. That weekend, I could have done other things than reading but I have a reading culture that I've established over the  years. The following sunday, we were paired in church to go out for evangelism. Myself and my evangelism partner picked a corner of the street and we engaged a Muslim boy. I preached the Gospel to this young man and he paid rapt attention. After the message, I asked if he was ready to give his life to Christ. He replied by saying "I have a  question for you." I indulged him. I was surprised when he asked me why there's a descripancy in the record of the timing of Jesus'  crucifixion in Mark and John's Gospel. Immediately, I smiled and thanked God for preparing me ahead. I didn't know that the reading I did days before was in preparation for that evangelistic assignment. In a nutshell, I answered him convincingly and he was surprised meeting someone like me. He didn't ask that question because he wanted to learn but because he wanted to disgrace me and my partner with false knowledge. My partner told me she wouldn't have answered the way I did if she were alone because it was a bomb.



There is power in prior preparation. A wise man said, "*proper prior preparation prevent poor performance*". In life, opportunities will come. It could be an opportunity to share the Gospel with an unbeliever or an opportunity to showcase your skill in a particular area or field of expertise. *Failure  occurs when unpreparedness meets with opportunities but success happens when preparation meets with opportunity*. In the story of David's conquest over Goliath, I saw in that event preparation meeting with opportunity. David was a shepherd boy who mastered the use of sling and stone. He may have used it to hunt animals but it is obvious that he had a mastery of that uncommon weapon. Also, while tending his father's flock, he killed a lion and bear. His life in the wilderness was a preparation for the great opportunity that led to his enthronement. Goliath wasn't much of a threat to him though he was to other soldiers of high rank. While the army commanders saw a great threat in Goliath, David saw an opportunity too good to miss. He used the weapon he had mastered during his preparatory phase to fight and conquer. *God doesn't use unprepared men no matter how urgent the need may be*. The deliverance of Israelites from Egypt was important and urgent yet God couldn't use Moses until He was prepared for 40 years. Jesus waited 30 years in preparation for a ministry of three and half years. *In destiny, no man rises above his level of preparation*.



Some are praying for blessings and miracles they're not prepared for. If God gives you your dream spouse and settle you in marriage, are you prepared for the responsibilities that comes with marriage? What do you know about parenting? Have you built muscles to tolerate your spouse and children? You know as I always say, anyone who is not prepared to take "nonsense" is not ready for marriage. *God may not launch us into certain dimensions of power and glory until we're prepared*. Preparation is so crucial that it is demanded of us to prepare for the coming of the Lord(Mt.24:42-44).  For whatever God intend to do with your life, there are  different stages of preparation. God can take you through His own preparation and at other times, you can be led to take initiative in preparing yourself for a great task ahead. How many books have you read in that area of interest and expertise? Lack of preparation can limit you. *Adequate preparation positions you to feature on a bigger platform in destiny*. Are you prepared?


*PRAYER*: Father, I receive the grace to be prepared at all times in readiness for opportunities in Jesus name.

DEVOTIONAL 0533 | Source start line: 47941
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
6/5/22, 6:33 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: ProjectSave Devotional
Sunday,
5th June, 2022.



*GLADYS AYLWARD- AIWEH-DEH*



Romans 1:16  For I am not ashamed of the gospel of Christ: for it is the power of God unto salvation to every one that believeth; to the Jew first, and also to the Greek.



Gladys Aylward (1902-1970) worked as a missionary in China. When Gladys applied to the China Inland Mission she was denied because of language barrier and her young age  but she didn't give up.  In 1932, Gladys arrived in Yangcheng and began to work with an older missionary, Jennie. Gladys worked for the Chinese government as a “foot inspector”, enforcing the new laws against female foot binding.



She became a beloved Chinese citizen, affectionately called “Ai-Weh-Deh”. She took in many orphans and unwanted girls. When the region was invaded by the Japanese during WWII, Gladys took around 100 orphans over the mountains to safety.  Alyward later settled in Taiwan where she spent her remaining days running her orphanage and sharing Jesus with her many friends and the children for whom she cared. That's creative and strategic evangelism. The idea of evangelism some folks have today is not relevant with the times. The more we see the  decadence in our society, the greater the opportunities we have to evangelize. We only need to pray for wisdom, the right strategy and timing. *No place can be locked against the Gospel. The problem is that we haven't found the key to penetrate.* The first outreach we had in our ministry was to street urchins in Lagos. I've preached in that neighbourhood for years but I didn't have the privilege to get the attention of those area boys. As the Lord instructed, we organized an inter-street football competition and put a slot for evangelistic preaching into it. They came. The attendance we had should be over 200 from my estimation. That's strategic evangelism.





*The message of the Gospel remains the same but the strategy for propagating it can change from place to place*. Do you know that you can share Jesus through caring for people? There are street children in your neighbourhood that are less privileged. You can organize a meeting, cook for them and share the love of Christ with them. What about starting a bible club in your neighbourhood? Yes, you can start a bible club in your neighbourhood. You can celebrate your next birthday in the orphanage and share Christ's love with orphans. Gladys didn't have much money but she had a willing heart. Ask the Lord to give you a heart that is willing to share the Gospel and the right strategy to enter into places locked against the Gospel.




PRAYER: Father, I receive the grace to be compassionate about the lost In Jesus name.

DEVOTIONAL 0534 | Source start line: 48040

6/6/22, 6:19 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: PROJECTSAVE DEVOTIONAL
Monday,
6th June, 2022.


*FAMILY AFFAIRS* Pt.1


1 Peter 3:1 Likewise, wives, be subject to your own husbands, so that even if some do not obey the word, they may be won without a word by the conduct of their wives,


*HE NEEDS RESPECT*


My Senior Pastor told the story of a colleague in ministry whose wife disrespected him. The man in his life time was a full time church Evangelist mightily used of God in his days but he doesn't have money unlike his wife who was into business. Those days, most ministers were poorly paid. Till date, alot are still remunerated poorly.  He was a victim of poor welfarism.  Due to this,  his wife became arrogant and disrespectful all because she was the bread winner in the home. On a certain day, she soaked her undies in water and left home for her daily business. When she returned, she queried her husband why he refused to wash her undies. The husband was shocked because of her affront and disrespectful attitude. She took one of the wet undies to hit the man out of anger. This man didn't retaliate to avoid a fight but he was pained. Not long after, the woman was duped by fraudsters. The money she relied on varnished and she died a terrible death. Often, we speak against men abusing their wives but little has been said about women who abuse their husbands verbally, emotionally and physically. A lot of men are suffering in silence. Can we be fair enough to look at their issues too?





In recent times, the campaign against physical abuse against women has increased and there's greater awareness against this evil. Do you know that some men are also victims of abuse? We often look away when such happens to men but we must balance issues such as this so that we can have  wholesome and healthy families. *Aside sex, one of the needs of a man is respect*.  We live in an age where wives want to rub shoulders with their husbands. The campaign for equality in marriage is everywhere on the internet but i've read my bible from Genesis to Revelation severally yet I didn't see a place where equality between husband and wife is mentioned.  1 Peter 3:6(AMP) says, *"just as Sarah obeyed Abraham [following him and having regard for him as head of their house], calling him lord. And you have become her daughters if you do what is right without being frightened by any fear [that is, being respectful toward your husband but not giving in to intimidation, nor allowing yourself to be led into sin, nor to be harmed]*". Sarah, out of respect for her husband knowing he is the head of the family called her husband "Lord". That's deep.






Dear wives, there's a blessing that comes with respect. In my bible, I found a woman who disrespected and mocked her husband: *"As the ark of the LORD was entering the City of David, Michal daughter of Saul watched from a window. And when she saw King David leaping and dancing before the LORD, she despised him in her heart.*"(2 Sam6:16).  She confronted David on this and she was served her comeuppance: *"And Michal daughter of Saul had no children to the day of her death*"(2 Sam6:23). She died childless.  *Respecting your husband attracts a blessing*. Some women are having a hardlife today because they set in motion laws working against them simply because they didn't respect their husbands. You find alot of women in prayer meetings and vigils. Some of their problems could be solved through a simple change in attitude towards their husbands. It is common to see church women respect their Pastors, they kneel for them and do all sort but they don't do same to their husbands. Ministers wives aren't left out in this. *If your husband is called into the ministry, that's why you should respect him more. No woman should regard him more than you do*. Some ministers who fell into adultery did so because they found respect and regard in the hands of a strange woman. That's not a justification for adultery but do not give the devil a foothold in your marriage through your indiscretion.   Hear this, as far as God is concerned, your husband occupies a more pivotal leadership role in your life than your pastor. I know this is debatable but let's consider scriptures: *"For husbands, this means love your wives, just as Christ loved the church. He gave up his life for her*" ( Ephesians 5:25 NLT). The headship of the husband is compared with that of Christ over the church. In the family order, God didn't call that Pastor your head but your husband. Even if you earn more than him, older in age or more educated, you must respect your husband and regard him as your head. Stop tongue lashing him as though he is your son. He is not.


*PRAYER*: I receive wisdom to run and manage my home in Jesus name.

Tuesday
7th June, 2022.

*FAMILY AFFAIRS* Pt.2


Ephesians 5:25 Husbands, love your wives, just as Christ loved the church and gave himself up for her
*SHE NEEDS LOVE*


When I read the dailies, the family section often catches my attention. I see a lot of court cases involving families seeking to dissolve their marriages. The reasons most people give for wanting to dissolve their marriages shows that true love is lacking. The most misunderstood word in our generation is "Love." Musicians sing it in their songs, film producers produce love movies, authors write love stories and poets write love poems yet the world doesn't understand what true love is all about. If they had, most marriages wouldn't be what it is today. Almost all marriages if not all started on the note of "I love you." I don't think any man married his wife saying "babe, I hate you, will you marry me"? I doubt if any lady in her right senses will accept such proposal. Most men are at their best whenever they're wooing a lady simply because they want to show how much they are in love. "I love you" has been used to deceive many single sisters into marriage. Ladies in bad marriages are suffering the consequences of not being able to recognize true love because they perceived love wrongly. Women are emotional feelers and often, men use this weakness against them.




*In any marital relationship, the intrinsic need of all women is love*. How they interpret "Love" differs from person to person but the element of  love must reflect in how the man relates with them daily. Women don't have much problem loving because they're wired to do so. It ain't easy for a man to love that's why the command to love is given to the man. The common love most men have is "Chemical love". They interpret love is response to hormone triggers. Often, when a man is telling a woman "I love you", he has strong erection under his trousers ready for sex while the lady thinks differently. She wants that love to translate into attention and care. Sex comes last for a woman. In the opening text, God commanded husbands to love their wives just as Christ loved the church. To understand this kind of love, the question we should ask is this, "How did Christ love the Church"? Let's check Romans 5:8, *"But God showed his great love for us by sending Christ to die for us while we were still sinners*"( NLT). The love of Christ to the church is shown in His sacrifice for the church: *"Greater love hath no man than this, that a man lay down his life for his friends*"(John15:3). When we didn't love him, He loved us and still died for us. His love for us isn't because we're perfect, it is an act of the will on His part regardless of our disposition towards Him. Christ didn't stop there, he took it upon Himself to make us holy: *"to make her holy and clean, washed by the cleansing of God’s word*"(Eph 5:26 NLT). Now this is deep and getting more interesting. The love of Christ for the church mirror for the man how his love should be for his wife.



*Men must learn how to love. The only way to learn true love is to learn Christ*. Looking at Christ, His love was sacrificial and unconditional. He accepted the Church just as she is. You must learn to accept your wife as she is including her imperfections and looks. True love will not say, "She's old and has lost shape, let me go hunting". Hear this, no matter how hard some ladies try, their looks& shape will never remain the way it was when you married them. Some are genetically wired to evolve over time. That's why we emphasize that love shouldn't be built on physical features alone. Constantly, you need to always affirm to her that you love the way she looks and that you're still in love with what you see. That's not a lie because loving her despite her looks means accepting her the way she is. Men who commit adultery often say "Is it possible to eat beans and not be tired"? Wow! It is possible. You can transform beans into bean-cake (akara), bean-pudding( Moi Moi), eat beans with bread or cassava flakes, you can do wonders with beans. Same with your wife.  Another aspect of Christ's love for the church is that Christ made the church clean, holy and acceptable. In the family, this translates into making the woman better. *It is not enough to accept a woman as she is, love confers on the man responsibility to make his wife better*. Women get better with love. The failure of wives in any ramification is traceable to the failure of husbands. *A loving husband will cultivate his wife, mentor her and give her wings to fly*. This is true love. If after 10 years of marriage, your wife is the same spiritually, academically, emotionally, financially,etc, you have not invested true love into her. No male man is born to love this way because our definition of love is selfish. We want hot sex quickly and move on whereas, that's not all there is to love.  Until we learn of Christ, we can't love as expected. *Love is responsibility*.



*PRAYER* : Father, I receive the grace to learn true love from Christ and show same to my wife in Jesus name.
