*FAMILY AFFAIRS* Pt.3



1 Tim 5:8 But if any provide not for his own, and specially for those of his own house, he hath denied the faith, and is worse than an infidel.




*WHEN YOU PROVIDE FOR THE FAMILY*






One day, while discussing with a  woman in my office, she told me it is a "cultural" thing for women in her place to be hardworking while the men lazy around. I want to believe it is an unspoken or untaught aspect of her culture. She used her family as an example. She told me how her father made the girls do the hardest and most physically demanding work in the family. The girls pound yam and change deflated car tyres while the guys sit doing nothing. I have been privileged to witness what she said when I lived in her ancestral land. I saw women driving bikes, bicycle, backing babies while going to farm or market but most of their men sit doing nothing. They drink alcohol. If is truly a "cultural" thing, God's children shouldn't be entangled in that kind of culture. Over the years, I've met with several hard working wives who bear the burden of working very hard to provide for their families. They're not widows neither are their husbands sick or handicapped. The kind of business I'm into expose me to alot of women in this condition. I have seen men who lazy around, drink alcohol, they do nothing to make the welfare of the family better. They're good at having sex and getting their wives pregnant. Unfortunately, in the fold, we have such lazy men singing and shouting hallelujah with us. Alot of hardworking wives become bitter the moment they discover their lazy husbands still chase anything in skirt all around while they're at work trying to provide for the family. Often, this makes women look older than their age. This I know from numerous counseling cases. Some men wait for month end to "receive" their wives salaries and spend as they like. Some even control their wives account and deprive her the privilege of eating from the fruit of her labour. These I've seen and I can say loudly that it is abnormal. Men, I have a message for you.




*God didn't design a woman to be the major  provider for the family. It puts on them a stress they can't bear emotionally. It is like putting 50kg load on someone that weighs 30kg. The natural design of a man is to work and provide for his family*. Any culture or tradition that opposes this is anti bible. Let's consider the first man God created and his job description: *"The Lord God took the man and put him in the Garden of Eden to work it and take care of it*"(Genesis 2:15 NIV). This is divine order. God gave the first man a work or job to do before giving him a family. Some think "work" is a result of man's fall but the scripture above preceded the fall. So God created man to work before the fall. God is wise, if he had given Adam a wife before giving him a job, Eve would be worn out, emotionally distraught and over burdened. That's happening to many women today.  When God brought Eve, her assignment was to help Adam. That is, Adam was the man with the major responsibility while the wife only gave support where necessary. This order hasn't changed. *Even if your wife earn millions, by nature she's designed to still collect something from you no matter how little*. That "little" speaks volume to her about you. In her heart, she knows that you don't earn as much as she does but when you give, she'll know you're responsible. The worse is when men work and still doesn't provide for the family or when they can work but refused to work. Men, there's dignity in any legal labour that puts food on the table for your family even if it is driving.  Don't be ashamed to do whatever isn't against God's will to stand in your place. *As long as you're not sick, there's no excuse for not working even if it pays a little. That "little" speaks volumes to her that you're responsible*.






*Men, the moment your children especially your sons know that your wife is at the vanguard of carrying financial burdens in the home, you'll pass the baton of irresponsible living to them*. A woman once told me her husband said he doesn't know that it was his duty to provide for his family probably because he never saw his dad do that. That's one of the dangers of raising children without a responsible father figure. Some men are raised in polygamous homes where mothers fend for their children. Their fathers don't care. When sons see their father labour for the family, they won't be taught to do same when they grow up. It will flow naturally because we all learn by imitation. Even if you're called into full time ministry, wake up in the morning and resume to your office and return in the evening like other men. Don't sleep and wake up doing nothing under the pretext of "my office is in my house and my job is to pray and read the bible." Even as a full time minister, there are things you can do that won't take your time or attention from the work. From my personal study& observation, most Nigerian ministers( even of mega churches) doing fine have businesses that pay them more than what any church can pay them. That's wisdom.  It is unwise to put all your financial life and plan on what the church pays you. Plan your life and future independent of what comes from the church. I've seen church leaders who were neglected after they retired. They spent all their life depending on church allowance. In retirement, they felt sidelined. Their children aren't happy with the church system that impoverished their fathers and deprived them. Yours shouldn't be like that. Men, take your place and work. *Single ladies, beware of falling in love with men whose dreams and aspirations is on their mouth. They're doing nothing daily to achieve their dreams. Be sure he is working and not deceiving you with cliché like "I'm a contractor". Beware of such brothers. You need finance to sponsor romance*.  And to the wives, this message is not intended to make life hell for your husbands. Support where necessary and don't neglect him especially when he doesn't have enough. It is a team work. I've only used this teaching to call the attention of the team captain to his major role. I call you blessed.




*PRAYER*: Father, I receive the grace to stand in my place as the head of the family in Jesus name.

*FAMILY AFFAIRS* Pt.4


Proverbs 14:1 The wise woman builds her house, but with her own hands the foolish one tears hers down.


*APPRECIATE HIS EFFORT*


My wife has an attitude of thanking and praying for me whenever i give her  money for upkeep or for the children. Also, she instruct the children to do same whenever I give her money to buy some of the things they need. I can't count the number of times my boys have come prostrating saying "Daddy thank you." I didn't teach her to do this but deep within my heart, I feel good and motivated to do more as God provides because someone appreciates the little I'm doing. I'm not a perfect dad neither am I the richest, In fact I trust God for every need in the family to be met and I've never thought to myself that I have the ability to meet all needs. However, I prioritize the needs of my wife and children more than mine because it is my duty to provide and I'm motivated through her good attitude and response. *It is not enough to tell men to be hardworking, wives with hardworking husbands should show sincere appreciation. Responsible men are scarce.*



*One of the things that causes heart ache for men is to labour and not be appreciated for their labour*. Some wives are insatiable, unappreciative and wicked. There are wives who collect children school fees from their husbands and do not pay in school. Some use the money for contribution, to re stock their shop or spend it on clothes and jewelries. Such attitude breaks the heart of any hardworking man. Another evil I've seen under the sun is women who pitch their children against their fathers. They intentionally re orientate their children to hate their fathers. They make the children feel their father doesn't care about them and that they're the one making provision available for their school fees, upkeep and all, whereas, their father does. Children raised by these mothers grow up resenting their dad because their mom told them he didn't contribute to their growth and development. This account for the reason some children take care of mothers more in old age. *Mummy, it is an evil thing to make daddy look irresponsible in the eyes of your children*. Even if you've shouldered the responsibility more, never make it known to your children.



I give it to my wife. There are times i didn't have money yet she made all things ready and still tell the children to come thank me. I don't need to tell them "Your mum did" because we are one. *The family should run as a team even though we know there are individual roles and responsibilities but the system we operate in our homes should not undermine the principle of oneness in marriage*. The bitter truth you need to know is that no man will meet all needs at all times. Don't get angry with him whenever he tells you he doesn't have.  Things don't work out well for hardworking men at all times. Your duty is to help. That's the reason I don't support the idea of becoming a sit-at-home wife. *Often, responsible men give sacrificially not necessarily out of abundance*. All wives I know have more clothes than their husbands yet the man can manage the little he has not because he can't change colours like Chameleon just that priority differ.  You also have a significant role to play in the welfare of the home. *If you have a responsible husband, please, understand with him and appreciate him always. That's how to get more from him*.



*ACTION POINT*: If you have a hardworking and responsible husband, you need to be thankful to God and to show him sincere appreciation. Do that today.

*FAMILY AFFAIRS* Pt.5


Amos 3:3 Can two people walk together without agreeing on the direction? NLT


*WORK AS A TEAM*


On my wedding day, the preacher shared the story of his father and mother. He told us why his late father remained an under achiever all his life. He stressed on the fact that he was a man who didn't work with his wife to plan a better life for the family. Anytime his wife brings suggestions that will move the family forward, he would disregard it because he had a mindset that women aren't wise. Till date, there are men who do not plan with their wives. Their believe is that most women know nothing. Have you ever come around men who describe women's brain as fish brain? If you know any of such, give them a "Pentecostal" distance. Such people may influence you to run your family on a solo mode. God's intention for the family is to operate as a formidable team that will advance His purpose here on earth. *Oneness of the two should be the strongest unit on earth because the bonding should be spiritual, emotional, mental and physical*.


*If you don't want to live and die as an underachiever, work as a team with your spouse*. Every team runs with a vision and there are plans on how to achieve the vision. If I may ask, what plans do you have for your family and children? Do you have plans to build a house you can call your home? I know an old couple who lived in a particular rented apartment for over 40 years. They got married there and had all their children there. Their grandchildren visited them there as well. When landed property was cheap in their neighbourhood, the man was doing his thing while the wife was also doing her thing. No vision, no cooperation. After 40 years, the children of the landlord sent them packing. They were tired of seeing his face. Money was not the problem of this man at least in his youthful days. He had a good job, traveled abroad and enjoyed life but he spent a larger part of his married life( 40 years) as a tenant. That shouldn't be your story.



*As a man, your duty is to cast a vision for your family, interpret it correctly to your wife and let her know her role. That's leadership. You cannot know the value your wife will add to your life and family in general until you share your vision with her*. What women react to most times isn't lack of money but lack of vision. God created them to be led. When they come under a bad family leader, they react negatively because something in them want to be guided and led. Often, most single guys have inferiority complex problems when they come around a lady that has achieved. If only they know that such ladies crave true and genuine leadership, they won't run back if led. Even some husbands have problems when their wives earn more. That's a sign of visionless and insecure men. *If the family runs as a team, your leadership will channel resources in the direction of your vision.  In family team work, it is not "my money" neither is it "her money", it is "our money*." If a woman knows that her earnings would be spent on something profitable for the family, she won't hold back. By nature, women are natural givers. You can do little as an individual but you can do alot as a team. It is not always about abundance of resources but resourcefulness.



*Action Point*: As the man, you're the leadership. Sit with your wife and chart the course for a better life for your family, interpret your vision correctly and let her know the role she's to play.

*FAMILY AFFAIRS* Pt.6


Amos 3:3 Can two people walk together without agreeing on the direction? NLT


*AGREEMENT IS CRUCIAL*


I once heard about a couple that had recurring fight over the issue of vigil. The husband came from a church background where women are not permitted to attend vigils whereas the wife had a different background. This Christian wife hates the idea of staying at home on friday night while vigil is ongoing in church. That challenge resulted from their failure to reach a concrete agreement in courtship.  *Often, what people overlook either in courtship or in the early stages of marriage later becomes the bone of contention in marriage*. Most times, some of the challenges couples face in  marriages stems from lack of agreement on crucial matters. *One of the reasons we advocate for quality courtship is to reach agreement on issues that may threaten the life and joy of the marriage. Courtship time is talking time*. It shouldn't just be about "I love you." Though, there are issues you may not be able to agree on in courtship because those issues may not present itself. However, if you have a culture of proper communication  and reaching agreement on fundamental issues before marriage, you'll not have much problem in reaching agreement on other issues in marriage. Fundamental issues like faith, finance, family life and size, sex,in-laws, location, friends and others requires agreement. As "little" as some of these look, if there's no proper agreement on them, it may snuff out life and peace in the home.



I know a brother and sister who didn't marry because they couldn't agree on the subject of tithing. The sister believes  in tithing 'dogmatically' but the brother doesn't. They were wise enough to part ways in courtship. Today, both are doing fine in their marriages.  A man refused to pay the fees of one of his children because he told his wife that the child was not in their family size plan. Was he not the one who impregnated his wife? Hilarious you'll say but that's just how irrelevant issues cause significant problems. *Agreement helps in channeling energies and resources in a particular/ common direction*. If you're not yet married, you have time to agree and disagree. It is either you proceed further into marriage if you reach agreement or you part ways and build your life with someone else. However, for the married, there's no exit option. *You may need to disagree( respectfully) to agree. You can disagree without becoming disagreeable*.



*On any issue of concern, the way to reach agreement is to have a robust communication with your spouse*. This type of communication explore the length, breadth, depth and height of the matter before reaching agreement. If you're trying to get your spouse to reason with you on a matter, present your facts calmly and lovingly. *You don't need to raise your voice to get him/ her see reason with you. You must be deliberate, intentional and systematic in presenting your facts before each other*. Also, you may need to give room for wide consultation before reaching a common ground.  You're not trying to win an argument neither are you trying to show superiority of wisdom. The moment your partner ( especially the man) perceives you're coming from a standpoint of superiority, you'll always meet a brick wall. Your goal should be the common good of the family not self.



*ACTION POINT*:  Look at those issues causing problems in your home, prayerfully discuss them with your spouse and trust God to help you on out.

DEVOTIONAL 0540 | Source start line: 48859

6/12/22, 5:59 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: ProjectSave Devotional
Sunday,
12th June, 2022.


*FAMILY AFFAIRS* Pt.7

Amos 3:3 Can two people walk together without agreeing on the direction? NLT

*STICK TO WHAT WORKS*


I have listened to several teachings on the subject of marriage and read several books and articles. Oftentimes, some authors/ speakers mix principles with methods. *Principles for making marriage work is universal. A principle is an unchanging law that have universal application. However, the method of applying the principle may vary from person to person and place to place*. To make your marriage work, there are bible based principles that must be adhered to however, the method may vary from one family to another. I remember the days when some conservative preachers emphasized so much on couples maintaining only joint accounts without keeping any separate accounts. They had good intentions. They were trying to build on the principle of unity, oneness and transparency in the home but their method of insisting on keeping joint accounts will not work for every home. They got it right on the principle aspect but not so right in the method.


The idea of keeping a joint account may be working for them or for some families, but from what I know it cannot work in all situations at all times for every couple. Rather, I'll counsel that couples should be open in matters of finance. Husbands should let his wife know how much he earns and same goes to the wife. That's openness. Then both of them should agree on how those monies will be spent and apportioned on various family needs. Once there's understanding and agreement between couples on this, you don't need to enforce another. *Joint understanding on matters of finance is better than keeping a joint account*. I've only given that illustration to buttress my point, there could be other methods that works. Don't sacrifice the peace and joy of your marriage for a method that works for another person but not working for you. Have you considered the applicability of that method in your marriage? *Stick to biblical principles that makes marriage work and find a suitable method for applying those principles*.


I once listened to another preacher who insisted that all wives must kneel down for their husbands as a sign of respect. Are you kidding me? What about people from cultures where kneeling is counter cultural? That preacher was trying to impose his cultural expression for respect on his listeners. It won't work. Even myself, if my wife starts kneeling for me, I'll be shocked because I don't fancy it yet she's giving me the honour and respect I deserve in her own way. If I demand her to start kneeling for me, I'll make an hypocrite of her.   The principle he should have emphasized is that wives should respect, honour and submit to their husbands then leave each wife to express it in ways acceptable to both of them and probably to their culture. *You don't use another man's template to run your home. It is dangerous*. No two homes are exactly the same. *Stick to what works for you as long as it is not anti bible*. Counseling on marriage should emphasize and reinforce principles not methods. Doing the latter could create more problems than you're trying to solve.  Search the scriptures for biblical principles that makes marriage work and stick to them. I call you blessed.


*PRAYER*: Father, I receive wisdom on marital matters in Jesus name.

DEVOTIONAL 0541 | Source start line: 48931

6/13/22, 7:13 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: PROJECTSAVE DEVOTIONAL
Monday,
13th June, 2022.


*WHY I BELIEVE JESUS IS GOD* Pt.1


Hebrews 13:8 Jesus Christ is the same yesterday, today, and forever.NLT


I was teaching this subject recently, a man whom I know has been in the faith for decades openly declared that he doesn't believe Jesus is God. I have seen or read alot of opposing views about the deity of Jesus. If there's one issue that some bible scholars have argued for or against, it is the issue of Jesus' deity. I'm not writing this to argue. I simply want to state truths found in scriptures. Till date, Islam will never accept the deity of Jesus. They call it a 'Shiek', it is quite understandable if they do not accept this fundamental truth because they do not believe in the inspiration of scriptures. That's why I'm not so concerned about what they believe on this matter. For them to accept the deity of Jesus, they have to accept the bible as the authentic word of God. However, I'm more concerned about bible carriers who still argue with a truth plainly revealed in scriptures. *If Jesus is not God, do you think His blood could wash sins away? It takes the blood of deity to wash away the sins of humanity*. Hallelujah!!! I'm glad I'm blood washed, sanctified and saved eternally.



Let me give you the first scriptural evidence about the deity of Jesus from his very mouth. Unfortunately, some think Paul was the one who claimed deity for Jesus whereas, Jesus did by himself. Let's consider what He said in John 10:30(NLT), *"The Father and I are one.” Now this looks like a casual statement but the implication is deeper than you think. When Jesus made that statement about His oneness with the father, the Jews who listened to Him picked stones and wanted to stone Him. Why did they react negatively to that statement? Let's consider verse 33 of the same chapter: *"They replied, “We’re stoning you not for any good work, but for blasphemy! YOU, A MERE MAN, CLAIM TO BE GOD*.” Now you can see that the Jews understood what Jesus meant when He claimed to be one with the father. They knew that He was claiming to be God.  To them, He was a mere man claiming to be God but Jesus isn't a mere man neither is he another prophet, He is God. He claimed to be God and scriptures say so. Hallelujah!!! Jesus is God.




From the old testament, the deity of Jesus has been revealed. Prophet Isaiah revealed this truth but often we overlook it. Let's read Isaiah 9:6, a prophecy about the birth of Jesus,  *"For a child is born to us, a son is given to us. The government will rest on his shoulders. And he will be called: Wonderful Counselor, MIGHTY GOD, Everlasting Father, Prince of Peace.*"  Prophet Isaiah, by the inspiration of the Holy Spirit called Jesus "Mighty God". If He isn't mighty God, Isaiah wouldn't have called him so. Jesus is a mighty God. We sing it in our songs. It is sacrilegious to think Jesus to be a mere man. He is God who took the form of man to save man. He is called "Emmanuel" meaning "God with us". He is God that is with us. Hallelujah!!! I have Jesus living in me and abiding with me. God is with me, I'm conscious of His ever abiding presence.



*PRAYER*: Father, I thank you for revealing Jesus to me, help me to work in the consciousness of this scriptural reality in Jesus name.

*WHY I BELIEVE JESUS IS GOD* Pt.2

Hebrews 13:8 Jesus Christ is the same yesterday, today, and forever.NLT


The subject of Jesus' deity cannot be ignored because it is central and fundamental in the doctrine of the Christian faith. Those who see Jesus as a mere messenger or prophet will have a hard time accepting the reality of Jesus' deity. More so, those that do not believe in the bible as divinely inspired or those that see the bible as a book of Jewish scrap legend will totally rebuff this teaching. My aim is not to argue with anyone but set forth the counsel of God on this matter and show you from scriptures the proofs of Christ's deity. Jesus Himself claimed to be God. Before we consider what others said about His deity, it is good we hear from the master Himself.  In John 8:58(NLT), *"Jesus answered, “I tell you the truth, before Abraham was even born, I Am!*” Here, the use of "I Am" by Jesus is deliberate because this is a reference back to Exodus 3:14 when God revealed Himself to Moses as the "I Am."



Immediately he used that phrase "I Am" to describe Himself, in the next verse, the Jews who heard him picked stones to stone him for blasphemy: *"At that point they picked up stones to throw at him. But Jesus was hidden from them and left the Temple*". Their negative reaction to Jesus was because he claimed to be God. If he meant otherwise, the Jews would not have reacted the way they did. The testimony of Jesus about His deity is sufficient for us. However, God has a way of restating truths that are crucial and fundamental through His servants. John reiterate the concept of Jesus' deity: *"In the beginning the Word already existed. The Word was with God, and the Word was God*"(John 1:1 NLT). The "Word" in context is a person. How did we know? Reading downward in thesame chapter to verse 14, John said, *"And the Word was made flesh, and dwelt among us, (and we beheld his glory, the glory as of the only begotten of the Father,) full of grace and truth*". The Word that became flesh is Christ and John told us that very Word( that became flesh) was God in verse 1 of that chapter. This is so clear and unambiguous.


*If Jesus is not God, salvation would be impossible*. Only the blood of deity can save. If He were human just like you and me, His name and blood will not be significant. He is the King of glory that went to hell and took the keys of death and hell from Satan in His burial and resurrection. Only God could have done that. His name and blood command so much respect in heaven, on earth and in hell. That's why only His name is given to man for salvation: *"Jesus is “the stone you builders rejected, which has become the cornerstone.’Salvation is found in no one else, for there is no other name under heaven given to mankind by which we must be saved*”(Acts 4:11-12). Hallelujah!!! Jesus is God.


*PRAYER*: Holy Spirit, reveal Christ to me in Jesus name. Amen.

*WHY I BELIEVE JESUS IS GOD* Pt.3


Hebrews 13:8 Jesus Christ is the same yesterday, today, and forever.NLT



This subject is very interesting. It is a truth revealed by God to His children in the pages of scriptures and confirmed in  our hearts by the Spirit of God. Trying to analyse this logically or mentally will always lead to confusion. As I said previously, those that do not believe in the inspiration of the bible will have a hard time accepting this truth. If you believe the bible is God's inspired word, you shouldn't have a hard time believing what it says. God's word says Jesus is God and I believe it. That settles it. In the Bible, one of the major commandments in the decalogue is, *"Thou shalt have( worship) no other gods before me*"( Ex.20:3 KJV, emphasis mine).  Here, God doesn't want us to worship another god. He alone deserves worship.  In the book of Revelation(22:8-9), an angel rejected worship because angels are not to be worshipped except God: *"I, John, am the one who heard and saw all these things. And when I heard and saw them, I fell down to worship at the feet of the angel who showed them to me. But he said, “No, don’t worship me. I am a servant of God, just like you and your brothers the prophets, as well as all who obey what is written in this book. WORSHIP ONLY GOD*!”


However, I discovered that Jesus accepted worship. Yes, He accepted worship. Why? Because He is God. Let's consider a few scriptures: *"They entered the house and saw the child with his mother, Mary, and THEY BOWED DOWN AND WORSHIPPED HIM. Then they opened their treasure chests and gave him gifts of gold, frankincense, and myrrh*"( Mt. 2:11 NLT). Right from when He was a baby, he began to receive worship. If you say He was a baby whou couldn't have rejected worship, what about other times in His adulthood where he was worshiped and didn't reject it or forbid the people from worshipping him? In Matthew 14:33, *THEN THE DISCIPLES WORSHIPED HIM. “You really are the Son of God!” they exclaimed.*" Hallelujah!!! His disciples worshipped Him and even some women did after His resurrection: *"And as they went, Jesus met them and greeted them. And they ran to him, grasped his feet, and WORSHIPED HIM*"(Matthew 28:9 NLT). In the same chapter( Vs 16-17), the disciples worshiped him again: *"Then the eleven disciples left for Galilee, going to the mountain where Jesus had told them to go. When they saw him, they worshiped him—but some of them doubted!*" If you say the first worship was a mistake, is the second one a mistake too?


Do not forget that in all of these, Jesus didn't reject worship  neither did he rebuke those who did because He is God who is deserving of worship. The angel in Luke 19:10 rejected worship because only God should be worshiped: *"Then I fell down at his feet to worship him, but he said, “No, don’t worship me. I am a servant of God, just like you and your brothers and sisters who testify about their faith in Jesus. WORSHIP ONLY GOD. For the essence of prophecy is to give a clear witness for Jesus*".  In Luke 24:52, after His ascension, the bible says, *"So they worshiped him and then returned to Jerusalem filled with great joy*"( Luke 24:52 NLT). By revelation, they( disciples) knew Jesus is God and that's why they kept on giving Him worship. I will show you another person who wasn't among the twelve, yet it is recorded in the Bible that he worshiped Jesus. In John 9:38, we were told about a man that was born blind. After he received his miracle, he gave Jesus worship after acknowledging Him as the Son of God: *“Yes, Lord, I believe!” the man said. And he worshiped Jesus.*" These are sufficient proofs to show that Jesus is deity. Accepting this truth will deepen our walk with God and help us to serve Him better. This truth will make you realize why salvation how precious our salvation is because holy God became man to die for sinful men so that they might be made holy and righteous. Let me hear you say, "Jesus is God."


*PRAYER*: Holy Spirit, reveal Jesus to me from scriptures. Let Him be real in my heart and help me to worship, serve and love Him better in Jesus name.

*WHY I BELIEVE JESUS IS GOD* Pt.4


Hebrews 13:8 Jesus Christ is the same yesterday, today, and forever.NLT


Thomas, one of the disciples of Jesus Christ is popularly known as a doubter. If I ask most church folks about any other thing Thomas did or said, I'm sure 95% will tell me he doubted Jesus' resurrection( John 20:24-27). What if I told you that he affirmed the deity of Jesus Christ? Yes he did. When Jesus appeared to His disciples, He showed Thomas His hands that Roman soldiers pierced with nails. Upon seeing Jesus whom he doubted his resurrection, out of amazement, Thomas made a profound statement saying *"My Lord and my God!” Thomas exclaimed.*"(John 20:28 NLT). He referred to Jesus as his Lord and God. It wasn't a slip of tongue. He knew Jesus is God. The disciples of Jesus didn't have issues with this either neither did Jesus reject being called God. In fact, His earthly ministry proved that He is God. In Matthew 9, Jesus forgave a paralyzed man his sins: *"Some people brought to him a paralyzed man on a mat. Seeing their faith, Jesus said to the paralyzed man, “Be encouraged, my child! Your sins are forgiven*.”(Matthew 9:2 NLT). This didn't go well with the religious teachers(Vs 3), they said, *"...“That’s blasphemy! Does he think he’s God"?”



Truly, only God forgives. He's the one that has the power to forgive sins and the pronouncement of Jesus is another proof that He is God. If he isn't God, He wouldn't have forgiven that man: *"Jesus knew what they were thinking, so he asked them, “Why do you have such evil thoughts in your hearts? Is it easier to say ‘Your sins are forgiven,’ or ‘Stand up and walk’? So I will prove to you that the Son of Man has the authority on earth to forgive sins.” Then Jesus turned to the paralyzed man and said, “Stand up, pick up your mat, and go home!*”(Matthew 9:4-6 NLT). Hallelujah!!! Jesus has the authority to forgive sins because He is God. Even in His glorified state, the power to forgive sins didn't diminish because He purchased our redemption with His blood: *“So guard yourselves and God’s people. Feed and shepherd God’s flock—his church, purchased with his own blood—over which the Holy Spirit has appointed you as leaders*"(Acts 20:28 NLT). Hallelujah!!!



In the text above, Paul gave Pastoral counsel to some church leaders, he told them to feed God's flock whom God purchased with His blood. That's a veiled reference to Christ because it is Christ that purchased the church with His blood. "God" in context of Paul's statement is referring to Christ. That's another affirmation of His deity by a man whose was not directly among His twelve disciples. How did he know this? I want to believe it is by revelation. There's so much we need to know about Jesus. We may not grasp certain things logically except revealed to us by the Spirit. Teachings like this can only be grasped by the help of the Holy Spirit. The more you try to reason it out, the farther you go from the truth. Till we see Him in glory, our prayer should be like that of Paul who said: "That I may know Him*".  Ask the Lord to reveal Christ to you.



*PRAYER*: Holy Spirit, reveal Jesus to me from scriptures. Let Him be real in my heart and help me to worship, serve and love Him better in Jesus name.

*WHY I BELIEVE JESUS IS GOD* Pt.5


Hebrews 13:8 Jesus Christ is the same yesterday, today, and forever.NLT


I believe in the authority of scripture. I believe in the deity of Jesus Christ because God's word say so. Jesus is the second person in the Godhead. He is the son of God and He is God.  Another profound scriptures confirming His deity can be found in Hebrews 1:8 where God the father called the son God: *"But to the Son( Jesus) he( God the father,) says, “Your throne, O God, endures forever and ever. You rule with a scepter of justice*"( Emphasis mine). That's no mistake. It is deliberate. God the father addressed the son saying "Your throne, O God". He could have said, "Your throne, O mere mortal". Jesus is not a mere mortal, not a messenger nor another prophet, He is God. Often, people reject this truth because all they see is his incarnation and not his divinity. In one of the old testament prophecies, he was called God: *"For a child is born to us, a son is given to us. The government will rest on his shoulders. And he will be called: Wonderful Counselor, MIGHTY GOD, Everlasting Father, Prince of Peace*"(Isaiah 9:6 NLT). Jesus is a mighty God.


Let's consider what Paul said about His deity. Apostle Paul is the most hated of writers in the bible because his depth of revelation is uncommon. Bible critics don't hate Paul. Some accused him of bringing "Romanism" or "Hellenism" into his writings. Paul didn't dilute scriptures, he expounded on it better because the Holy Spirit revealed mysteries to him: *"Dear brothers and sisters, I want you to understand that the gospel message I preach is not based on mere human reasoning. I received my message from no human source, and no one taught me. Instead, I received it by direct revelation from Jesus Christ*"( Galatians 1:11-12 NLT). Pauline revelations are divine.  Now, about the deity of Jesus, Pauline writings confirmed it further: *"while we look forward with hope to that wonderful day when the glory of our GREAT GOD AND SAVIOR, JESUS CHRIST, will be revealed*"( Titus 2:13 NLT). In context, Jesus is referred to as "Great God and Saviour". He is the soon coming king. Hallelujah!!!


In Philippians 2:5-6, Paul confirmed the deity of Jesus and showed his subordination in the Godhead, lesson of submission and humility for all: *"You must have the same attitude that Christ Jesus had. Though he was God, he did not think of equality with God as something to cling to*"(Philippians 2:5-6 NLT). Here, Paul confirmed to us that Jesus was God even in his incarnation but he chose to be lowly and subordinate to God the father however, that didn't reduce Him or change his status as God. He is co equal, co eternal with the father yet he chose to submit to the father as the second person in the Godhead. If you doubt this coequality,co eternality with God the father, read this: *"For it pleased the Father for all the fullness [of deity—the sum total of His essence, all His perfection, powers, and attributes] to dwell [permanently] in Him (the Son),*"(Colossians 1:19 AMP). What other evidences do we need? The bible says He is God. I believe it. If Christ is not revealed to you in your heart by the Holy Spirit, you will keep arguing with this truth. Ask the Lord to reveal Christ to you.


*PRAYER*: Holy Spirit, reveal Jesus to me from scriptures. Let Him be real in my heart and help me to worship, serve and love Him better in Jesus name.

*WHY I BELIEVE JESUS IS GOD* Pt.6

Hebrews 13:8 Jesus Christ is the same yesterday, today, and forever.NLT


*THE SALVATION QUESTION*

Is salvation possible without the involvement of God? For me, this is the most important question we need to ask as touching the deity of Christ. When we talk about salvation, it is a very deep spiritual subject. Mankind in his fallen state is doomed for death ( spiritual and eternal death) because Romans 6:23 says, *"For the wages of sin is death,...*" The sin nature is not our fault, we inherited that fallen and sinful nature from Adam: *"When Adam sinned, sin entered the world. Adam’s sin brought death, so death spread to everyone, for everyone sinned*" (Romans 5:12 NLT). The unregenerated mankind is a sinner not because he sinned but by nature.  The penalty for the sin of Adam is death. We all inherited Spiritual death (separation from God) from Adam and doomed for eternal death (suffering in the lake of fire):  *"For the sin of this one man, Adam, caused death to rule over many...*" (Romans 5:17 NLT). The sin of Adam made man hopelessly doomed for eternal death not until Christ stepped in to change the narrative.



This despicable situation of mankind made God to send Christ to save us from the disaster Adam brought on us all: *"Yes, Adam’s one sin brings condemnation for everyone, but Christ’s one act of righteousness brings a right relationship with God and new life for everyone*"( Romans 5:18 NLT). Hallelujah!!! Spiritual death is the first disaster Adam brought on us. This is a total separation from God. It is a breakdown of fellowship and a state of insensitivity to divinity. This can be remedied in Christ. The second disaster Adam brought on mankind is eternal death- suffering in the lake of fire. It is also called second death. This is the destiny of all unregenerated man and it is the most painful of all deaths. It is eternal:  *“But cowards, unbelievers, the corrupt, murderers, the immoral, those who practice witchcraft, idol worshipers, and all liars—their fate is in the fiery lake of burning sulfur. This is the second death*”(Rev.21:8 NLT). Do you think the blood of man like you and me can save man from all these? This is what I want you to see in this. Can the sacrifice of a finite man appease the wrath of an infinite God? Think!



*Only the blood of God( Jesus) can save and rescue man from spiritual death and the wrath to come*. To achieve this, God the father sent God the son( the second person in the trinity) to take the form of man to die for sinful man so that man can be saved: *"For God made Christ, who never sinned, to be the offering for our sin, so that we could be made right with God through Christ*"(2 Corinthians 5:21 NLT). This is deep and it is a mystery. That a holy God took the form of man, came to die for me to deliver me from the wrath to come is worth thanksgiving all day: *"He himself is the sacrifice that atones for our sins—and not only our sins but the sins of all the world*" (1 John 2:2 NLT). Religious people are trying to use good works, animal sacrifice to achieve salvation yet we have it freely because we believe in the finished work of Christ for our salvation on the cross: *"He did not enter by means of the blood of goats and calves; but he entered the Most Holy Place once for all by his own blood, thus obtaining eternal redemption*"( Hebrews 9:12). Hallelujah!!! We have eternal salvation through Christ. *If Jesus Christ is not God, your salvation and mine wouldn't have been possible because the blood of a mere man like us isn't potent to restore man to fellowship with God and deliver mankind from the penalty of sin*. If you want to give your life to Christ and be saved, say this prayer:


*"Father, I know I'm a sinner. Forgive me of my sins. Wash me with the precious blood of Jesus. I believe  he died and resurrected for my redemption. I confess Jesus Christ as my Lord and personal saviour, in Jesus name. Amen.*"






N:B Feel free to broadcast our message to all on your contact. Our goal is to reach 20,000 people daily with God's word.


info@projectsaveng.org

*WHY I BELIEVE HOLY SPIRIT IS GOD*


Hebrews 13:8 Jesus Christ is the same yesterday, today, and forever.NLT


There's also a need to establish the deity of the Holy Spirit and this can only be done by studying the bible systematically. The Holy Spirit is the third person in the Godhead and He is fully God along with God the father and God the Son( Jesus Christ). He is not an impersonal force neither is he a source of power controlled by God. Some even think the Holy Spirit is another name for Jesus in spirit form, apart from His body. None of these ideas aligns with scriptures. The bible describes the Holy Spirit as a divine person who has been present with the Father and the Son since before time began: *"In the beginning God created the heavens and the earth. The earth was formless and empty, and darkness covered the deep waters. And the Spirit of God was hovering over the surface of the waters*"( Genesis 1:1-2 NLT). The revelation of His personality reverberates throughout the bible. He can speak( Acts 13:1-2), He has a will(1 Cor.12:4-7), He can be grieved( Eph4:30). All these scriptural evidences shows that He's not a mere force but a divine personality.


Aside being a person, He is also God as revealed to us in scriptures: *"Then Peter said, “Ananias, why have you let Satan fill your heart? You lied to the Holy Spirit, and you kept some of the money for yourself*"(Acts of the Apostles 5:3 NLT). Peter said clearly that Ananias lied to the Holy Spirit. In the verse after the one cited above, Peter said Ananias lied to God, referring to the Holy Spirit: *"The property was yours to sell or not sell, as you wished. And after selling it, the money was also yours to give away. How could you do a thing like this? You weren’t lying to us but to God*!”( Acts of the Apostles 5:4 NLT). Reading contextually, Peter couldn't have made the mistake of referring to two different persons. The Holy Spirit in verse 3 is the same person referred to as God in verse 4. Hallelujah!!! The Holy Spirit is God. Peter made no mistake about it.


This is a mystery revealed only to sons. In the trinitarian order, He is the third person in the Godhead. The subordination of Christ to God the father and the Spirit to both of them doesn't imply inferiority but orderliness and submission. They're co equal, coeternal. If the Holy Spirit is not God, His divinity would have been questionable. Only God can speak, lead, heal and revive men. All the great works we see in the body of Christ today are the works of the Holy Spirit. The bible that you read is called the word of God because the Holy Spirit inspired the prophets and the apostles to write or speak what is there: *"Above all, you must realize that no prophecy in Scripture ever came from the prophet’s own understanding, or from human initiative. No, those prophets were moved by the Holy Spirit, and they spoke from God*"(2 Peter 1:20-21 NLT).


*PRAYER*: Holy Spirit, reveal yourself to me from scriptures. Help me to love and fellowship with you better in Jesus name.

6/20/22, 11:49 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: PROJECTSAVE DEVOTIONAL
Monday,
20th June, 2022.


*WHY I BELIEVE IN TRINITY* Pt.1

2 Cor 13:14May the grace of the Lord Jesus Christ, and the love of God, and the fellowship of the Holy Spirit be with you all.

*WHAT DO WE MEAN*?


One of the most controversial bible doctrine is "Trinity". It is not controversial among those to whom it has been revealed neither is it controversial among those that have studied the bible along this line of truth. It is easy to find critics of this doctrine use history to refute this claim but no other source is credible except the bible. The word "Trinity" itself is not in the bible, we only use it to describe the relationship that exist in the Godhead. Even if you don't like that word, if you open your heart and allow God's word and Spirit in, you will agree there is God in three unique persons. What do we mean by "Trinity"? Are we saying God is three? To start with, let's establish a truth from scriptures: GOD IS ONE. In Deuteronomy 6:4, the bible says: *"Hear, O Israel: The LORD our God is one LORD:*". This declaration is what the Jews call Shema. It is a declaration that affirms that God is one. In Mark 12:29(ESV), *"Jesus reaffirm thesame truth saying: Jesus answered, “The most important is, ‘Hear, O Israel: The Lord our God, the Lord is one*". This is no mistake or fluke of saying. It is what it is. However, when we talk about trinity, what we are saying is that one God exist in three persons thereby making each of them coequal, coeternal and of thesame essence in all ramifications. The doctrine of trinity has never denied the oneness of God, rather it affirms it and establishes the existence of one God in three unique persons- God the father, God the son and God the Holy Spirit.



From the beginning(Gen1:26), when God said, *"Let US make man in our image, in our likeness, and let them rule over the fish of the sea and the birds of the air*". I want you to note the pronoun "us". Definitely, it refers to more than one person. You don't use "us" when talking to yourself or about yourself. It is obvious there were more than one person in the conversation. You will also notice if those God was talking to weren't in the very image and essence with Him, He wouldn't have said man should be made in their image. It would have been a selective creation in the image of just one person but we see God talking to another person with creative power and ability like Him. The same pronoun was used in Gen 3:22: *"And the LORD God said, Behold, the man is become as one of US, to know good and evil: and now, lest he put forth his hand, and take also of the tree of life, and eat, and live for ever:*". In the same book of Genesis (11:7), the use of the same pronoun "us" reflect in God's conversation. God wouldn't have been talking to angels when he said "Let us". In Isaiah 6:8, *"Then I heard the voice of the Lord saying, “Whom shall I send? And who will go for US?” And I said, “Here am I. Send me!*”. Now, it is established there's a "us" right from creation. The trinitarian doctrine didn't start with Paul as some haters of Paul have said. It started right from the beginning, the prophets confirmed it.


In the prophetic writing of Isaiah (48:16), speaking about the Son (Jesus Christ), the  Son is speaking while making reference to he Father and the Holy Spirit: *"Come near me and listen to this: "From the first announcement I have not spoken in secret; at the time it happens, I am there." And now the Sovereign LORD has sent me, with his Spirit*". The speaker mentioned "Sovereign LORD"( denoting God the father) and he further said the LORD has sent him with his "Spirit"( denoting the Holy Spirit). Obviously, it is the Son (Jesus Christ) that is talking here. Let's check  a new testament scripture that revealed the persons in trinity. In Matthew 3:16-17, at the baptism of Jesus, the trinity surfaced: *"As soon as Jesus baptized, he went up out of the water. At that moment, heaven was opened and he saw the Spirit of God descending like a dove and lighting on him, And a voice from heaven said, *"This is my Son, whom I love; with him I am well pleased*". In this passage, God the father spoke from heaven, God the Son was baptized and God the Spirit descended on the Son like  a dove. Are you still in doubt about the three persons of the trinity? It is only those that don't believe in the inspiration of the bible that doubt these things. How can we be made perfect in our faith if we spurn this truth? I believe in the holy trinity because it is an established truth in the scriptures both old and new testament.


*PRAYER*: Father, I thank you for the revelation of your word and counsel to me. Holy Spirit, make this truth real in my heart in Jesus name.

*WHY I BELIEVE IN TRINITY* Pt.2

2 Cor 13:14 May the grace of the Lord Jesus Christ, and the love of God, and the fellowship of the Holy Spirit be with you all.


The revelation of the holy trinity is right there on the pages of scriptures. often most people do not see it neither do they recognize it. The subject of trinity is one that is revealed progressively. Only those that believe in the inspiration of the bible will believe this teaching. If you subject it to mental analysis, you'll be confused because spiritual things can only be discerned spiritually. When Jesus gave the command we now know as The Great commission, there's something there that buttresses the trinity. He said, *"Therefore, go and make disciples of all the nations, baptizing them in the name of the Father and the Son and the Holy Spirit*"( Matthew 28:19 NLT). This is Jesus speaking here, he emphasized that baptism should be in the name of the Father, Son and the Holy Spirit. This is because they are inseparable beings that work in concert. Without these three unique personalities, your salvation would not have been made perfect. At benediction time in church, we reaffirm the holy trinity by saying: *"May the grace of the Lord JESUS CHRIST, and the love of GOD, and the fellowship of the HOLY SPIRIT be with you all*". If you've been confessing that after church service, you've been confessing the trinity.


Humanly, there's no perfect way to describe or illustrate the trinity. Any illustration we give only comes close but not perfect. Take a look at your egg. It has a shell, yolk and albumen( egg white). These three unique components make up the egg yet you don't go to the market saying you want to buy yolk or shell. What you buy is egg. It is left for you to do whatever you want with it. We can liken that to the trinitarian relationship. Though they are three unique persons, yet they are one. God exist in three persons. Paul, speaking about Jesus Christ said, *"You must have the same attitude that Christ Jesus had. THOUGH HE WAS GOD, he did not think of equality with God as something to cling to*"(Philippians 2:5-6 NLT). Jesus is God, the Holy Spirit is God( Acts 5:3-4).


In creation, God used man to buttress this tripartite relationship. Man is a tripartite being: spirit, soul and body. 1 Thesalonians 5:23 says, *"Now may the God of peace make you holy in every way, and may your whole spirit and soul and body be kept blameless until our Lord Jesus Christ comes again*"(1 Thessalonians 5:23 NLT). Man is three-in-one working together. You cannot separate man into different entities because the triune components of man work together. Though they're unique with separate functions yet they work together as one  For instance, you don't marry someone and say "I'm married to her body alone. I'm less concerned about others". Hey! When you married, you got married to the total being of the person. That's the way it is at salvation. When you accepted Christ, it was also an acceptance of the father, and the Holy Spirit. You cannot truly believe in Jesus and not believe in the Godhead. I believe in the holy trinity because it is revealed in scriptures.


*PRAYER*: Father, I thank you for the revelation of your word and counsel to me. Holy Spirit, make this truth real in my heart in Jesus name.

6/22/22, 5:52 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: ProjectSave Devotional

*WHY I BELIEVE IN TRINITY* Pt.3


2 Cor 13:14 May the grace of the Lord Jesus Christ, and the love of God, and the fellowship of the Holy Spirit be with you all.


*IS THE FATHER THESAME AS THE SON AND SON SAME AS THE SPIRIT*?


The critics of our trinitarian doctrine often refuse to accept that it is not only a new testament doctrine but a revelation that has been from the pages of old testament. The writings of the old testament prophets distinguished between the personalities in the trinity. Moses, the man who was entrusted with the law(Torah) began by revealing this truth in the book of Genesis.  In the old testament, "LORD" is distinguished from "Lord": *"Then the LORD rained down fire and burning sulfur from the sky on Sodom and Gomorrah*"(Genesis 19:24 NLT). Anytime you see "LORD" in capital letters, it is a reference to God the father. Consider the rendering of the same word in Psalms 2:7, *"I will declare the decree: the LORD hath said unto me, Thou art my Son; this day have I begotten thee*"( Psalm 2:7 KJV). In this scripture, it is clear that the LORD( God the father) has a Son referring to Christ. Those who doubt the Sonship of Christ can readily see that the relationship between God the father and God the Son is clearly shown here. Christ is God's only begotten son while we are adopted sons. The Spirit is distinguished from the "LORD": *"The LORD( God the father) replied, “Take Joshua son of Nun, who has the (Holy) Spirit in him, and lay your hands on him*"( Numbers 27:18 NLT, Emphasis mine). What did you observe? God the father(LORD) spoke to Moses about ordaining Joshua for leadership because he had the Holy Spirit in him. The personalities in the Godhead are distinguished from one another.



In the new testament, Jesus distinguished between the Father and the Spirit. In John 14:16-17, He said, *"And I will ask the FATHER, and he will give you another Advocate, who will never leave you. He is the HOLY SPIRIT, who leads into all truth. The world cannot receive him, because it isn’t looking for him and doesn’t recognize him. But you know him, because he lives with you now and later will be in you*"(John 14:16-17 NLT). Jesus is not the father neither is He the Spirit. All the times Jesus spoke to the Father in the Gospels, He wasn't speaking to Himself but to another person in the Trinity- the Father. The personalities in the holy Trinity are distinguished from another yet we have one God existing in the three of them. That's why that popular hymn says, *"God in three persons, blessed Trinity*". Hallelujah!!! The scriptures confirm this truth from the old testament to the new testament. The proof of this doctrine is to dwell on the sufficiency of scriptures that teaches it not on human or logical reasoning.



Don't forget the Shema, it is the declaration of Jews found in Deuteronomy 6:4 which says, *"Hear, O Israel: The LORD our God is one LORD:*". This declaration is what the Jews call Shema. It is a declaration that affirms that God is one. In Mark 12:29(ESV), Jesus reaffirmed thesame truth saying: *"Jesus answered, “The most important is, ‘Hear, O Israel: The Lord our God, the Lord is one*". We believe in one God in three persons and nothing is contradictory in this. The Father is God: *"But don’t be so concerned about perishable things like food. Spend your energy seeking the eternal life that the Son of Man can give you. For God the Father has given me the seal of his approval*”( John 6:27 NLT), the Son is also God: *"Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob are their ancestors, and Christ himself was an Israelite as far as his human nature is concerned. AND HE IS GOD, the one who rules over everything and is worthy of eternal praise! Amen*"( Romans 9:5 NLT), the Holy Spirit is also God: *"Then Peter said, “Ananias, why have you let Satan fill your heart? You lied to the Holy Spirit, and you kept some of the money for yourself. The property was yours to sell or not sell, as you wished. And after selling it, the money was also yours to give away. How could you do a thing like this? You weren’t lying to us but to God!*”(Acts of the Apostles 5:3‭-‬4 NLT).


*CONFESSION*: I believe in one God in three persons. I believe in the unity of the Godhead. I anchor my faith on the doctrine of Christ handed to the apostles and prophets. I refuse to buy into demonic logic and lies about the deity of Christ and the Holy Spirit. I stay on the revealed word of God for my profiting in Jesus name. Amen.


*N:B*: Subscribe to my YouTube channel to watch videos of our messages and outreaches:  https://www.youtube.com/channel/UC7yLo36cW8VIf0hG9Pusguw


info@projectsaveng.org

6/23/22, 6:42 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: PROJECTSAVE DEVOTIONAL
Thursday,
23rd June, 2022.


*WHY I BELIEVE IN TRINITY* Pt.4

2 Cor 13:14 May the grace of the Lord Jesus Christ, and the love of God, and the fellowship of the Holy Spirit be with you all.


*LEARNING SUBMISSION AND HUMILITY FROM THE TRINITY*


The holy Trinity is an embodiment of vital Christian lessons for us. Whenever we preach or teach humility, submission to authority and order, we need to draw our lessons from the holy Trinity. Some of the things that exist in Trinity is subordination, humility, submission and order yet they are coequal and coeternal. In John 14:28( NLT), Jesus said, *"Remember what I told you: I am going away, but I will come back to you again. If you really loved me, you would be happy that I am going to the Father, who is greater than I am.*" Jesus clearly revealed the father is greater than Him. In fact, He said in John 5:19(NLT), *"So Jesus explained, “I tell you the truth, the Son can do nothing by himself. He does only what he sees the Father doing. Whatever the Father does, the Son also does*". These scriptures shows that Jesus Christ is subordinate to the Father yet He is coeternal and coequal with the father. We know this because scriptures says, *"Though he(Christ) was God, he did not think of equality with God as something to cling to.*"( Philippians 2:6 NLT, Emphasis mine). That mean He is equal with the Father but because of his role in the Trinity, He didn't consider that equality.



Christ is God yet he's in full submission to the father. When Paul said "he did not think of equality with God", that's not a denial of his coequality with God.  In context, Paul was trying to teach humility and submission using Christ as an example. Look at verses 7&8: *"Instead, he gave up his divine privileges; he took the humble position of a slave and was born as a human being. When he appeared in human form, he humbled himself in obedience to God and died a criminal’s death on a cross*". Despite Christ being God, he humbled himself to the position of a slave and died the death of a criminal though He wasn't. That's the extent of His submission to the father's will yet He is God with full attributes of divinity. *If Christ wasn't subordinate to the Father, He wouldn't have been a saviour. He will prove points that God shouldn't become man to die for sins*. The Holy Spirit is also subordinate in the Trinitarian relationship: *"But when the Father sends the Advocate as my representative—that is, the Holy Spirit—he will teach you everything and will remind you of everything I have told you*"(John 14:26 NLT). It still beats the imagination of the carnal and unregenerated man that Christ is God yet He calls God the Father His father. *If the Holy Spirit isn't subordinate, He wouldn't be here with us today, leading and guiding us into all truths.*



Let's not try to unravel mysteries that are exclusive to God. Only the Godhead knows why their relationship is that way. Paul says, *"O the depth of the riches both of the wisdom and knowledge of God! how unsearchable are his judgments, and his ways past finding out!*"( Romans 11:33, KJV). Who are we to question God on why Christ and the Holy Spirit are subordinate to the father? Yet, their subordination doesn't reduce their coequality and coeternality. That's a lesson in humility and submission for us today. You don't humble yourself because you're in a lower state/ position than others. You do because it is consistent with the nature of a new creation in Christ. *Wives, God is not telling you to submit because you're inferior. After all, Christ is not inferior to the Father. Submission won't reduce you, it will only amplify your uniqueness. God's command for you to submit to your husband is to bring orderliness and peace in marriage and to make you play certain roles*. The true of measure of your humility will be shown when you have power, influence, money, influence, knowledge yet you submit to authority. In human relationships, let's recognize hierarchical order and submit to it. Imagine a world where everyone claims equality in power& authority, there'll be confusion and disorderliness. This is one of the greatest lessons from the Trinitarian relationship.



*PRAYER*: Father, I receive the grace to humble myself and to submit to hierarchical order in Jesus name.

*WHY I BELIEVE IN TRINITY* Pt.5

2 Cor 13:14 May the grace of the Lord Jesus Christ, and the love of God, and the fellowship of the Holy Spirit be with you all.


*DISTINCT ROLES OF THE GODHEAD*(A)


In previous teaching, it is clear that there's order, submission and subordination in the Trinity. Their subordination to the Father is not out of inferiority but orderliness. That's a great lesson for us. We must emulate the Son and the Spirit in this. In this kingdom, humility is a priceless virtue. *Wives should know that the command to submit to their husbands is not because of inferiority but because of their unique roles*. Each member of the Godhead have distinct roles they play because submission & subordination created an order. Revelation 1:1 revealed God the Father as the source of divine revelation: *"This is a revelation from Jesus Christ, which God gave him to show his servants the events that must soon take place. He sent an angel to present this revelation to his servant John,*"( Revelation 1:1 NLT). God determines the secrets or mysteries to be unveiled to man. Moses said, *"The LORD our God has secrets known to no one. We are not accountable for them, but we and our children are accountable forever for all that he has revealed to us, so that we may obey all the terms of these instructions.*"( Deuteronomy 29:29 NLT).



The miracles in the bible and the great supernatural display we read about are sourced from the Father: *"Believest thou not that I am in the Father, and the Father in me? the words that I speak unto you I speak not of myself: but the Father that dwelleth in me, he doeth the works.*"(John 14:10 KJV). It is the Father in Jesus that did those mighty works. He is the source. God the Father is always working in the best interest of man: *"But Jesus answered them, My Father worketh hitherto, and I work.*"(John 5:17 KJV). You're an expression of God's workmanship. Paul said, *"For we are his workmanship, created in Christ Jesus unto good works, which God hath before ordained that we should walk in them.*"(Ephesians 2:10 KJV). Hallelujah!!!



Another distinct role of the Father is the salvation of mankind. God the Father is the brain behind salvation. John 3:16( with emphasis) says, *"For God( the Father) so loved the world, that he gave his only begotten Son( Christ), that whosoever believeth in him should not perish, but have everlasting life.*" You and I couldn't have accepted Christ into our lives if God the Father hadn't sent Him to die for our sins. Our salvation is not an afterthought, it was planned by God the father before the foundation of the world: *"And all that dwell upon the earth shall worship him, whose names are not written in the Book of Life of the Lamb, slain from the foundation of the world.*"(Rev 13:8). Christ is the lamb of God that was slain for our salvation before the foundation of the world. God the Father didn't wait for Adam and Eve to mess up before looking for a way out. He had it all planned from before the beginning. Redemption is an expression of God's love: *"But God( the Father) commendeth his love toward us, in that, while we were yet sinners, Christ died for us.*"( Romans 5:8 KJV, Emphasis). I can say that God the Father is obsessed with mankind. That's why the Psalmist asked, *"What is man, that thou art mindful of him? and the son of man, that thou visitest him?*"(Psalm 8:4). God the father is working out your own good.


*CONFESSION*: I am the image of God. I am on the mind of the Father always. He is at work in me and through me to fulfill His eternal purpose on the earth. Henceforth, my life shall radiate the beauty of salvation and God's grand eternal plan for me in Jesus name.

*WHY I BELIEVE IN TRINITY* Pt.6

2 Cor 13:14 May the grace of the Lord Jesus Christ, and the love of God, and the fellowship of the Holy Spirit be with you all.


*DISTINCT ROLES OF THE GODHEAD*(B)


No member of the holy Trinity is without a vital role. The bible is clear on this. When the angel appeared to Joseph in the dream, he declared that salvation from sin is an exclusive role of Christ: *"And she will have a son, and you are to name him Jesus, for he will save his people from their sins*"(Mt 1:21 NLT). Salvation cannot be gotten from any religious leader or angel. The only person that offer eternal salvation is Christ: *"There is salvation in no one else! God has given no other name under heaven by which we must be saved*”( Acts 4:12 NLT). Religion is man's way of trying to reach God. Man invented rituals and rites as a way to reach the Father but Christ is the only way of getting to the Father: *"Jesus told him, “I am the way, the truth, and the life. No one can come to the Father except through me*"(John 14:6 NLT). Hallelujah!!! Christ is the way to salvation, the way to heaven, there's no other way.



What if I told you Christ also played a vital role in creation? Yes, he did. God the father created everything through Him: *"God created everything through him, and nothing was created except through him*"( John 1:3 NLT). Jesus is the agent or channel of creation because through Him, all things were made by God: *"But for us, There is one God, the Father, by whom all things were created, and for whom we live. And there is one Lord, Jesus Christ, through whom all things were created, and through whom we live*"(1 Cor 8:6 NLT). Christ was actively involved in creation because He is deity. Only God can create. The bible says, *"for through him God created everything in the heavenly realms and on earth. He made the things we can see and the things we can’t see— such as thrones, kingdoms, rulers, and authorities in the unseen world. Everything was created through him and for him. He existed before anything else, and he holds all creation together*"(Col 1:16-17 NLT). Glory!!!


These are salient truths revealed in scriptures about Christ. He is not only a saviour, He was also involved in creation and still is. Christ is also involved in revelation. He came to reveal the father to us: *“My Father has entrusted everything to me. No one truly knows the Son except the Father, and no one truly knows the Father except the Son and those to whom the Son chooses to reveal him*”(Mt 11:27 NLT). If you want to know God the father, look at Jesus. If you want to grasp the extent of God's love, look at Jesus. What God the father wouldn't do, Jesus didn't do. He is the perfect representation of God. Study Jesus in the Gospels to understand the Father perfectly. That's why the writer of Hebrews enjoined that we look up to Jesus: *"looking unto Jesus the author and finisher of our faith; who for the joy that was set before him endured the cross, despising the shame, and is set down at the right hand of the throne of God*"(Heb 12:2 KJV). Your walk with God cannot be perfected until you behold Christ in the word of God. Stay close to your bible to learn Christ.


*CONFESSION*: I receive the revelation of Christ by the word and the Spirit. My faith is strengthened and firmly rooted in Christ and I grow in the faith of the Son in Jesus name.

*WHY I BELIEVE IN TRINITY* Pt.7


2 Cor 13:14 May the grace of the Lord Jesus Christ, and the love of God, and the fellowship of the Holy Spirit be with you all.


*DISTINCT ROLES OF THE GODHEAD*(C)


There's no way you'll read the bible from the first book to the sixty-sixth book without coming to a conclusion in favour of the holy Trinity. Not that alone, if you read the scriptures with the Holy Spirit as your guide, you will clearly see the roles of each member of the Trinity. In creation, the Holy Spirit was involved: *"The earth was formless and empty, and darkness covered the deep waters. And the Spirit of God was hovering over the surface of the waters*"(Genesis 1:2 NLT). In the book of Job, it is clearly stated that the Holy Spirit made the heavens beautiful. It is obvious that He has a 'making' power, a creative power: *"His Spirit made the heavens beautiful, and his power pierced the gliding serpent*"( Job 26:13 NLT). Do not forget that in Genesis 1:26, God said, *"Let us make man in our image...*". The pronoun "us" shows that God was speaking to more than one person. The "us" in that text obviously refers to the person of Jesus Christ and the Holy Spirit. The Spirit of God was present and involved in creation.


Another work of the Spirit is divine revelation. He is the conveyor of God's mind to us on issues that pertains to us: *"When the Spirit of truth comes, he will guide you into all truth. He will not speak on his own but will tell you what he has heard. He will tell you about the future*"(John 16:13). The Spirit of God is a revealer of deep secrets and truths. If there's any issue that is unclear to you, ask Him. When you hear a true believer saying, "Thus saith the Lord", or may be "God said". You may wonder how they heard God. That's the Holy Spirit at work. He takes what's on the mind of the father and reveal to us. About  God's mysterious plans, Paul said, *"God did not reveal it to previous generations, but now by his Spirit he has revealed it to his holy apostles and prophets*"(Eph 3:5 NLT). All that the Apostles and prophets said and wrote were revealed by the Holy Spirit. That's the reason I treasure what is written in scriptures, it is innerant. Peter said, *"Above all, you must realize that no prophecy in Scripture ever came from the prophet’s own understanding, or from human initiative. No, those prophets were moved by the Holy Spirit, and they spoke from God*"(2 Peter 1:20-21 NLT). The scriptures is inspired by the Holy Spirit, don't trivialise your bible.


Jesus operated in the miraculous by the power and anointing of the Spirit: *"And you know that God anointed Jesus of Nazareth with the Holy Spirit and with power. Then Jesus went around doing good and healing all who were oppressed by the devil, for God was with him*"( Acts 10:38 NLT). The Spirit of God play a vital role in salvation. A believer cannot claim to have eternal life without the Holy Spirit: *"he saved us, not because of the righteous things we had done, but because of his mercy. He washed away our sins, giving us a new birth and new life through the Holy Spirit*"(Titus 3:5 NLT). The Spirit of God is the executor of God's program on earth, salvation inclusive. You have been made holy by the power of the Spirit. Peter said, *"God the Father knew you and chose you long ago, and his Spirit has made you holy. As a result, you have obeyed him and have been cleansed by the blood of Jesus Christ. May God give you more and more grace and peace.*"(1 Peter 1:2 NLT). Hallelujah!!! I'm made holy by the Spirit of God.


*CONFESSION*: The Spirit of God is in me, He is working to reveal God's mind to me. I have direction by the Spirit in Jesus name.

DEVOTIONAL 0555 | Source start line: 49953
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
6/27/22, 6:35 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: PROJECTSAVE DEVOTIONAL
Monday,
27th June, 2022.

*WHAT IS YOUR DECISION*?


Phil1:29 For unto you it is given in the behalf of Christ, not only to believe on him, but also to suffer for his sake;


Have you heard about Nokseng? The name may not ring a bell but his song remains one of the choicest evangelistic songs while preaching in crusades and evangelistic meetings all over the world. His song has been translated into several languages because it is a song of commitment. Nokseng was a Garo man from India. Along with his family, he decided to follow Jesus Christ in the middle of the 19th century through the efforts of an American Baptist missionary. He was persuaded to renounce his faith by the village chief, the convert declared, "I have decided to follow Jesus." His two children were killed and in response to threats to his wife, he continued, "Though no one join me, still I will follow." His wife was killed, and he was executed while singing, "The world behind me, The cross before me." This display of faith is reported to have led to the conversion of the chief and others in the village.


This is where the church got the song: 'I have decided to follow Jesus'.  We use it for evangelistic purposes but not many are ready to abide by the wordings of that song. It is easy to sing, and to preach but the test comes when we are faced with persecution. The surprising thing about Nokseng's story is that he was a new convert who hadn't spent enough time to hear deep mysteries through teaching but when he accepted Christ,  he decided to follow Him till the end regardless of whatever he may face. We are already in that time when our decision will be tested. Recently, I read a portion in the bible that struck me: *"For you will be expelled from the synagogues, and the time is coming when those who kill you will think they are doing a holy service for God*"( John 16:2 NLT). This is happening already. People kill Christians and they're happy thinking they're rendering a service to God.


Whoever is not ready to die for the sake of the Gospel is not ready to follow Christ. I'm not saying you should look for death. No, that's not what I'm saying. If you're presented with a decision to deny your faith or choose death, it is at that point your resolve to follow Jesus will be tested. Will you deny Christ? Paul said, *"But none of these things move me, neither count I my life dear unto myself, so that I might finish my course with joy, and the ministry, which I have received of the Lord Jesus, to testify the gospel of the grace of God*"( Acts 20:24). Are you ready to follow Jesus Christ under any circumstance? *Our generation is accustomed to the easy side of Christianity, no one is telling believers that persecution is part of the deal. When some believers face a little persecution, they'll quickly turn back or compromise*.  Hear the words of Paul: *"Yea, and all that will live godly in Christ Jesus shall suffer persecution*"(2 Tim3:12). Those of us that live in countries where there's tolerance towards practicing our faith,  we may not understand the subject of persecution well but we must have it at the back of our minds that someone somewhere is in jail,  has been killed and is about to be killed  for the sake of his faith in Jesus. Probably, someone is about to be killed for believing in Jesus under the threat of renouncing his faith. We have a duty to pray for such people and to prepare ourselves for persecution in any form it may come.


PRAYER: Father, I receive the grace to stand strong and firm in the face of persecution. We pray for persecuted believers all over the world,  may they not deny you In Jesus name. Amen

DEVOTIONAL 0556 | Source start line: 50046
