6/28/22, 6:49 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: ProjectSave Devotional
Tuesday
28th June, 2022.

*UNLEASH YOUR CHILD-LIKE FAITH*

Mark 10:14-15 Let the children come to me; do not hinder them, for to such belongs the kingdom of God. Truly, I say to you, whoever does not receive the kingdom of God like a child shall not enter it"


As a young father, I've learnt a lot observing my children. They ask for so many things believing they will get it. A sister said her son asked her saying, "Mummy, buy me a giant". I was like that when I was their age. When i was around 7 years of age, my younger sister and I used to pray for the restoration of electric power supply. We got answers. In fact, whenever there's power outage and our big sisters and brothers are in desperate need of electric power, they'll call on me and my younger sister to pray. We were young and our minds were devoid of impossibilities. We didn't know that certain factors led to power outage, all we wanted was power supply so that we can watch television. We normally go on our knees for power to be restored. Today, I doubt if I have that kind of faith because I'm full of information that fuels unbelief in that regard.


The mind of a child knows no impossibility. Can I still pray for power restoration like i use to? I doubt if I can.  Why? Now i know there's something called power generation, transmission and distribution. So in my mind, i'll try to find out what the problem could be and how it maybe impossible to get the power restored on time. Sometimes, knowing too much is an enemy of faith. When Jesus advocated for Child-likeness in kingdom matters, he knew perfectly that the innocence and child-like heart is the best way to relate with God. Yes it is. Children don't keep grudges. If they fight with one another, they become playmate again the next moment. Their faith flow freely unhindered because their mind isn't burdened with guilt and cares.


*It is possible to get to a point in our walk with God where it may be hard to believe God for miracles because of familiarity*. This is very common to those that have been in church for years. We may become too familiar with God, His servants and sacred things and they lose value in our eyes. You'll see new converts obey every instruction and they'll go home with answers but oldies analyse everything before acting and even when they do, their hearts are full of "If's". *Sometimes, the worst set of people to preach to and get them have faith for miracles are preachers especially theologians. They may be looking for exegetical and homiletical loopholes in your message and thereby losing out*. If you're in that category, please repent. Scientists can be worse too. Your attainment in God or science shouldn't block you from experiencing divine possibilities.  Children trust their parents for everything and they demand for costly things ignorantly. Their believe is that daddy is so rich and can buy anything for them. When will you start trusting God like that? When will you start asking for big things? Looking at the economy, you may ask God for "reasonable" miracle because your faith can't carry the "exceeding abundantly" dimension of God. God doesn't perform miracles using human parameters. Paul said: *"Now unto him that is able to do EXCEEDING ABUNDANTLY above all that we ask or think, according to the power that worketh in us*"(Ephesians 3:20 KJV). Your child-like faith is the key to operating in the realm of "exceeding abundantly".


ACTION POINT: Switch on that child-like faith in you and ask for whatever you want from God without doubting in your heart(Mark11:23-24). Ask Him for big things.

DEVOTIONAL 0557 | Source start line: 50126
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
6/29/22, 2:31 PM - Lanre Oyeleke: PROJECTSAVE DEVOTIONAL
Wednesday,
29th June, 2022.


*HOW I WON THE BATTLE AGAINST GUILT& CONDEMNATION*


Heb8:12 And I will forgive their wickedness, and I will never again remember their sins.” NLT


There was a season in my life I came under intense attack of guilt and condemnation. I can't find the exact words to explain what I went through. What made it worse was that I kept hearing voices always accusing me. I almost ran mad because I kept hearing accusing voices and I felt condemned and unworthy of God totally. I lost the conviction about the cleansing power in the blood of Jesus and my thoughts about God's forgiving power came under intense attack of doubt and fear. I was carrying too heavy a load around. I was weakened spiritually. The devil played videos of my past to me, brought memories of my past failures and almost snuffed out my joy and liberty in Christ. At a time, I felt so unworthy of God.



My liberation came through the word of God. I read the text above and I took it personal. God's word is saying to you and me that your sins will not be put to rememberance anymore. One of the things that kills faith is guilt and condemnation. The moment you set out to pray and ask God for the things you desire, the enemy has a way of bringing your past to your memory then you become weak and prayerless. No matter how bad your past is, the moment you come to Christ, your sins are washed away without a dot remaining: *"Come now, let’s settle this,” says the Lord. “Though your sins are like scarlet, I will make them as white as snow. Though they are red like crimson, I will make them as white as wool*"(Isaiah 1:18 NLT). Is the devil condemning you and taking you on a guilt trip based on your past? Simply let him know that you have been forgiven completely because God's promise is that He would forgive your shortcomings completely as long as genuine repentance is constant.



Your forgiveness is not an emotional thing neither is it dependent on how you feel but on God's word. The nature of God's forgiveness cannot be understood by the carnal mind: *"I, even I, am he who blots out your transgressions, for my own sake, and remembers your sins no more*"( Isa 43:25). God chooses to forgive and forget our sins for His sake not even for our sake. If you slip into guilt over your past, simply know the enemy is at work.  You just have to believe God's word for what it says. If God's word says you're forgiven, that's it nothing can change it.  When we don't believe God's word enough, the devil will make us doubt scriptures like this and use our emotional feelings to get us. Believing God's word about your forgiveness and righteous status doesn't depend on how you feel but about His word:  *“For ever, O Lord, thy word is settled in heaven*” (Psalm 119:89). No matter how bad your past was, a drop of Christ's blood has a strong cleansing power. Throw the feelings of guilt away and stick to your faith in the scripture above.




CONFESSING: I am the righteousness of God in Christ Jesus, I rise above condemnation, shame and guilt. I am in God's love camp and I refuse perpetual guilt and shame In Jesus name. Amen




N:B You can be part of what God is set to do in Tanzania among the Maasai people. We covet your prayers and support. Contact: 07080100893.


info@projectsaveng.org

DEVOTIONAL 0558 | Source start line: 50190
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
6/30/22, 9:57 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: PROJECTSAVE DEVOTIONAL
Thursday,
30th June, 2022.


*BEWARE OF LITTLE FOLLY*


Eccl10:1 As dead flies cause even a bottle of perfume to stink, so a little foolishness spoils great wisdom and honour. NLT


My dad told the story of a man who preached a wonderful message in the bus in one of his trips those days. His message was great and everyone in the bus knew that his message was divinely inspired by the Lord. However, this man did something funny when they got to the police check point. This preacher switched over into the flesh and he angrily threw something at the police man. The bus was stopped and he was dealt with by the police. Everyone wondered why he behaved that way after preaching a powerful sermon. Even if it was a mistake, he should have begged the police man but he didn't. The police man didn't care who he was, he dealt with him properly.



Folly lurked somewhere within to rubbish his wisdom and honour. In life, we must avoid little foolishness that can erode our honour and turn our much respected wisdom into what people are scorning. Sometimes, things like this are spiritual and they happen in moments when we are not discerning. When Jesus asked his disciples who men think he was, only Peter gave a Spirit led answer: *"Simon Peter answered, “You are the Messiah, the Son of the living God*.”( Mt.16:16). In the next verse(17), read the response of Jesus: *"Jesus replied, “Blessed are you, Simon son of Jonah, for this was not revealed to you by flesh and blood, but by my Father in heaven*". He was in tune with the Spirit and He knew who Jesus was by revelation. Later, Jesus spoke about his death and how he will suffer, the same Peter who knew about the sonship of Jesus by revelation spoke against the death of Christ. Read what Jesus said in verse 23, *"Jesus turned to Peter and said, “Get away from me, Satan! You are a dangerous trap to me. You are seeing things merely from a human point of view, not from God’s*”(Mt 16:23 NLT). Satan spoke through Peter and Jesus rebuked the devil that spoke through him.


It is possible to be in the Spirit this moment and in the flesh the next moment. That's what happened to Peter. Foolishness sets in the moment you switch from the Spirit into the flesh. Just imagine a man who preached a powerful sermon on holiness and was caught in adultery/ fornication the next moment. Isn't that foolishness? What about starting in the Spirit and ending in the flesh? Why will someone sing so powerfully and become something else the next moment? Paul said, *"So I say, walk by the Spirit, and you will not gratify the desires of the flesh*"( Gal 5:16). Walking in the Spirit always is the only way not to be foolish. Little foolishness can manifest in our words and actions. If you don't have the right word for a matter, it is wise not to talk. Trying to have a say in everything when you don't have anything meaningful to contribute would turn-on foolishness. Acting without thinking and poor planning would turn-on folly. Also, not being led by the Spirit of God is foolishness at its peak. Be wise!

PRAYER: Father, I expel foolishness in my life and I receive wisdom In Jesus name.

7/1/22, 2:48 PM - Lanre Oyeleke: PROJECTSAVE DEVOTIONAL
Friday,
July 1st, 2022.

*HOW TO SEE THE VISIONS OF GOD*

Ezekiel1:1 On July 31 of my thirtieth year, while I was with the Judean exiles beside the Kebar River in Babylon, the heavens were opened and I saw visions of God. NLT


Several years ago, I was a lover of anything that have to do with "motivational books". In all the motivational books and talks, I noticed one recurring theme, vision. However, they limit vision to a mental picture of the future. Most of those motivational talks comes with statements like: "If you can't see it, you can't get it" or "The vision you can't picture you will never feature". To some extent, these are truisms.  Till date, there is so much emphasis on 'vision'. Motivational preachers preach it and even unbelievers also talk about it. Virtually everyone has heard or read about having a vision for one's life and that's good however when vision is limited to a mental or psychological imaginative exercise, we distort so many things in God's plan for our lives. Unbelievers can imagine as well. There should be something more. *A believer shouldn't live by imagination alone but by insight into God's plans and purpose*.  In the opening text, I love the way Ezekiel puts it: *"...the heavens were opened and I saw visions of God.*"


*There are man- made vision and there are God-given visions. Man- made vision requires using your imaginative faculties to create a mental picture of what you desire but God-given vision is a divine insight into God's plans and purpose for one's life*. In the school of flesh and human psychology that some are preaching today, emphasis on seeing into God's mind and fulfilling His will for our lives is almost non-existent. Men of old that we read about didn't just create a mental picture of their desires. They saw into God's mind and received a blue print for their lives. Listen to Paul, *“So then, King Agrippa, I was not disobedient to the vision from heaven*"(Acts 26:19). Paul's life was driven by the heavenly vision he saw not self ambition. In most cases, what people call vision is self-concocted and ego driven not heavenly. I call that ambition because it was cooked-up in their minds. *When God reveal His mind to you about what he want you to do, then you are seeing the vision of God and not yours*. It is better to run with God's vision for your life. If you'll imagine anything at all, it should be centred on what the Spirit of God reveal.


How do you see God's vision for your life? Let's see how John the beloved connected with the realm of the divine: *"I WAS IN THE SPIRIT on the Lord's day, and heard behind me a great voice, as of a trumpet,*"(Rev 1:10 KJV). There's no other way to see the visions of God other than to be in the Spirit. The Holy Spirit is the revealer of God's mind. With Him, you can have insight into God's mind for the now and future. You don't need to be a prophet to see what God wants you to see, you only need to be in the Spirit. When walk in the Spirit, we'll have insights into spiritual things.  When we walk in the flesh, we can't see the things of God even from His word. A daily walk in the Spirit positions us to see beyond the realms of human.



PRAYER: Father, I receive grace to walk in the Spirit so that my eyes can see the visions of God In Jesus name. Amen

7/2/22, 9:35 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: ProjectSave Devotional

July 2nd, 2022.

*STAY AWAY- DON'T USE YOUR SIEVE*


Romans 16:17 And now I make one more appeal, my dear brothers and sisters. Watch out for people who cause divisions and upset people’s faith by teaching things contrary to what you have been taught. Stay away from them. NLT


In my few years in the faith, I have observed the nature of heresy. One thing I noticed about heresy is that it is infectious, highly infectious. It spreads like gangrene. It has a way of penetrating into people who get exposed to it long enough till they become attuned to it. I have heard alot of folks talk about "sieving" what they hear from the pulpit and weighing whatever they hear with the truth they already know. This presupposes that they'll continue to expose themselves to anything and they'll keep sieving out errors all the time. This sounds like common sense but it lacks bible sense in a way. I don't know how long you'll always have to "sieve" to get the right stuff. You see, when it comes to heresy, the bible didn't say you should sieve it, the opening text clearly states that you stay away from whatever is contrary to what you have been taught. Does that sound harsh? That's sanctified wisdom.


Oftentimes, we try to meander and find our way round these things. When we clearly know that the preacher is known for constant heresies, anti biblical practices or we know that a book contains so many spiritual toxins, instead of staying away as the bible enjoined,  many will still take their "sieve" with them. They forget so easily that it is not in one single exposure to error that one buys into it. *It takes constant and consistent  exposure to imbibe error*. We cannot be wiser than the scriptural admonition that tells us to stay away. There are meetings you should not attend, there are books you should not read. *Staying away from people who teach contrary to the truth is not hatred, it is the best form of love and care you can show to them and to your spiritual health*. Knowing the doctrinal stand of some ministers, I won't bother feasting on their teachings neither will they have a place to teach or preach for me. Inviting such persons will be a disservice to God's people. Sometimes, I've seen certain ministerial manipulations in the way some persons invite guests or organize programs. Why bring two to three different speakers with divergent doctrinal persuasions to teach in the same conference meeting? To attract crowd or what?  You'll leave God's people confused that way.


There's a spiritual dimension to the issue of heresy. Apostle Paul who enjoined that we stay away from such also made it clear there's a spirit behind it: *"Now the Holy Spirit tells us clearly that in the last times some will turn away from the true faith; they will follow deceptive spirits and teachings that come from demons*"(1 Tim 4:1 NLT). The reason heresy is infectious is simply because the spirit of deception is behind it. You don't expose yourself to teachings and ideas that are antibiblical and expect to manage it. *A spiritual force field is created in any atmosphere of learning where you find yourself. The sole aim is to influence you till you accept what is taught so that it becomes a rule for living*. Some persons wouldn't have been occultic and mystics if they hadn't exposed themselves to it. Same applies to homosexuals. That's why Satan keeps them in a community of closely knit likeminds so that they stay on the lies and deception of demons. Be wise.


*PRAYER*: Father, I receive the grace to stay away in Jesus name.

7/3/22, 11:22 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: ProjectSave Devotional
Sunday,
July 3rd, 2022.


*SHE LOVED HER ENEMIES*


Matthew5:43 “You have heard that it was said, ‘Love your neighbor[a] and hate your enemy.’ 44 But I tell you, love your enemies and pray for those who persecute you, 45 that you may be children of your Father in heaven...


Have you read or heard the story of Jim and Elizabeth Elliot? The names may not ring a bell but in the annals of world missions, they can't be forgotten. Jim and Elizabeth Elliot were missionaries sent to the Huaorani tribe in Ecuador also called the Aucas by the Quichua.  Jim and four other missionaries were later killed by the tribe they were trying to reach. After Jim's death, Elizabeth returned and spent two years ministering to the tribe that killed her husband. Elizabeth Elliot is an outstanding female missionary in the last few generations who was loved by all. Through her extensive speaking and many beautiful books, her legacy and inspiration will live on in the hearts of all who read them. She showed love and compassion to the people that killed her husband by preaching Christ to them. That was the exact kind of compassion Christ had for evil people.



The Christian faith is under attack by enemies of Christ. It is no news that churches and Christians have come under intense attack in recent times. Churches have been torched, Christians killed and priests abducted. In our generation, if we were in Elizabeth's shoe, many of us would have cursed and despised the killers of her husband. Today, most believers have resolved to curse these enemies instead of doing what the master commands. The stock-in-trade of some preachers is to curse when such calamities happen yet the curses hasn't reduced the spate of persecution and opposition against the Church.  Christ commanded us to pray for them, our pains shouldn't stop us from praying for their salvation and witnessing Christ to them if the opportunity present itself. We owe them the message of the gospel and a showing of love.



According to oral tradition of church history,  when emperor Nero wanted to kill some of the Christians who didn't deny the faith in his time, they were singing and rejoicing in the face of death. After they died and had turned to skeletons, Nero visited the place where their skulls were and he was surprised that their skulls looked like someone that is laughing. He became angry. Our forebears in the faith were men who prayed for their enemies at the point of matrydom. In Acts 7:60, it was recorded of Stephen, the first matyr: *"Then he fell on his knees and cried out, “Lord, do not hold this sin against them.” When he had said this, he fell asleep.*" Many saints of old died loving their enemies. They weren't merchants of curses. On the cross, Jesus didn't curse the thief that despised him. Despite the pain, his heart was full of love. *The power of love will propel us to pray the salvation of our persecutors and seek ways to win them over to Christ*. This is a very hard message however, it is the Gospel of Christ.



PRAYER:- Father, help me to love like Christ In Jesus name.

7/4/22, 7:02 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: PROJECTSAVE DEVOTIONAL

July 4th, 2022.

*LED BY LOVE OR FEAR*?

2 Corinthians 5:14 Either way, Christ’s love controls us.[a] Since we believe that Christ died for all, we also believe that we have all died to our old life.[b]NLT


I once shared the story of my experience while returning to Lagos from Ibadan years back. Oftentimes, I preach in the bus but on this particular trip, I didn't preach. Where I sat in the bus, I heard a voice telling me that if I don't preach in that bus, i won't get to Lagos. Now, that sounds ridiculous. That was a threat. The voice didn't say preach, what I heard came to me like a threat. I took my time to discern the voice i was hearing and i knew It wasn't the Holy Spirit. That was a religious spirit speaking. If I had obeyed that instruction of preaching( which seemed right), i would have done the right thing under fear( which was wrong). Also, it may open me up to being led by a religious demon rather than the gentle voice of the Spirit. Some are hearing religious demons speaking to them yet they think they are hearing God. Have you forgotten that Satan's temptations sounded a bit "religious"? *“If you are the Son of God, jump off! For the Scriptures say, ‘He will order his angels to protect you. And they will hold you up with their hands so you won’t even hurt your foot on a stone*."(Matthew 4:6 NLT). Satan quoted scriptures to back his temptation. That's how religious demons operate.


Religious demons do not necessarily tell people to do something evil. They can take from a little knowledge of scripture to bring their victims under control and later lead them into a perversion of scriptures. Some may hear the voice I heard and start preaching but is the instruction totally consistent with the word and nature of God? The answer is obviously 'No'. While it is right to preach, it is very wrong to preach under threat and fear. God doesn't lead that way. I didn't preach that day and here i am bouncing in the Lord. Still alive doing the work of the master. I know this experience may not go well with some persons. They may still see it as an act of disobedience but I'm glad I disobeyed a seemingly good instruction from a demon. I know when God speaks. Sorry, that was an exception. *Our act of service must be controlled by love not by fear. Religion's bait of control is fear and guilt. These combination are very dangerous*. Some preachers know how to put fear in the minds of people to get them do their bidding not the Lord's. The worst i've seen is in the area of finances. Some may threaten that if you don't part with certain amount of money, God won't hear you. The voice of fear and guilt is loudest during offering time in some pulpits. They have forgotten quickly that whatever is given out of coercion is not acceptable. Listen to Paul: *"Each one must give as he has decided in his heart, not reluctantly or under compulsion, for God loves a cheerful giver*"(2 Cor 9:7 ESV).



I don't want people to sow into our ministry out of fear but out of love. When love controls what a man is doing for God, he'll do it from a heart of thanksgiving and worship. When love is the driving force behind soul-winning, kingdom service and giving, you'll do it despite reward. The only motivation some persons have for working for God is because they've been told what they'll get from it. A preacher painted a picture of better life, cars, houses, job, good marriage, etc before their eyes. They presented a transactional system to them instead of helping them have a revelation of why they should do what ought to be done. *Stop transacting with God, start loving Him. People who enter into transactional service with God have a very high tendency of backsliding because when expectations aren't met, they feel deceived*. Have read several stories of people who asked for a refund of seed because what the Pastor promised didn't happen. Oh! I serve Him because I love him. The patriarchs of old did because they loved Him. In fact, the book of Hebrews 11 told us about men who had it rough and who received promises without experiencing it's fulfilment on this side of eternity yet died for the sake of whom they loved- God. *God is seeking for loving worshippers not fearful worshippers*. If at all there's any type of fear we should have for God, it should be a reverential fear for God(2Cor7:1) not a crippling or damaging fear. I see people doing things for God out of a crippling fear thinking God is leading them, that's not God. Be controlled by love.

PRAYER: Father, i receive grace to be motivated by love for you In all i do In Jesus name

7/5/22, 1:58 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: ProjectSave Devotional
Tuesday
July 5th, 2022.


*ON MY WAY TO MINNA*


1 John 4:1 Dear friends, do not believe every spirit, but test the spirits to see whether they are from God, because many false prophets have gone out into the world. NIV


Some years back, I was traveling to Minna from Lagos. My destination was a rural village in Niger state but Minna was a major  transit route for me to link up with other missionaries before proceeding to the mission field. When we arrived at Jebba, the driver parked the vehicle in a restaurant. We alighted from the vehicle to eat. I paid for my meal( water inclusive) and I finished all that was in my plate. However, I needed additional satchet water. I requested of the waitress to give me an additional satchet water which she did and left. As I took the water from her, a voice told me that if I don't pay for the satchet water, I'll return to Jebba to restitute the debt of five naira for the satchet water. Ha! Upon hearing that, I looked around for the lady that served me the additional satchet water. When I found her, I gave her the money for the additional satchet water but to my surprise, she declined. I asked to know the reason for her refusal, she said the initial payment I made for the food also covered the extra satchet water she served me. I became angry at the demon that spoke to me. I felt like punching the person behind that voice if he had a physical form. I also felt stupid for quickly coming under temporary guilt because of five naira satchet water. Initially, I thought that I was asking for something I needed to pay for not knowing my initial payment covered for it. Obviously, the voice I heard wasn't God's, it was a religious demon. It took me years before I understand this thing I'm teaching. I fell victim several times.


I'm always careful around people who claim to always "hear" and what they hear may sound "spiritual" but when properly discerned, most of those things aren't from God though they may sound like what God would say but it is not. *Not all voices that speak piously to you is  the voice of God even if they sound like what God would say*. I want to liberate someone with this simple but profound truth. If there's one thing Charismatics need to be careful about in this end time, I think one of it is indiscriminately saying "thus saith the Lord" or "the Lord said". Satan, knowing that most believers want to "hear" and are desirous of having an intimate relationship with God to the point of speaking and hearing from Him, he has also released religious demons that say what may sound like what God would say so that God's people can be subservient and ultimately led astray. The extreme of this is when some start hearing voices to the point of going insane. There are numerous persons in mental homes who speak in "tongues" (obviously not of God) and do alot of 'spiritual gymnastics' that church folks do. Psychiatric doctors can bear witness to this. I once ministered to a lady who lost total control of her mind to religious demons. She was acting "churchy", carrying her bible, preaching and doing all sorts but a demon was controlling her. People who crave the esoteric in an unhealthy manner often fall victims of this.


Dearest, your goal as a believer shouldn't be to "hear" but to "know." Let me explain further. *Knowing God through His word is a higher experience and level than  "hearing" a voice speak to you.* There's a level of knowledge about God that you'll possess and thus hear accurately because there will be an alignment between the written word( accurately interpreted) and what the Spirit says. As Kenneth E. Hagin of blessed memory says, 'the Spirit and the word will always agree.' Paul's prayer was not "That I may hear Him" rather it was "That I may know Him." *When you know Him as you should, you will hear Him as you should.* Oftentimes, those that are very lazy in private bible study want to build their intimacy with God on what they hear or see in a vision. They've forgotten that several voices speak from the realm of the spirit. The chiefest of them all that seeks to control believers that aren't grounded in the word are religious demons. Do not get me wrong, I haven't said God doesn't speak, He does but getting to discern His voice accurately requires some patience. Don't always be in a rush to act impulsively on every seemingly "spiritual" instruction you hear. Test that spirit with the word, prayer, patience, counsel and with time. *God is never in a hurry with some of the things we think we are under compulsion to do. He himself desire we come to know when He is speaking and when He is not*. Receive deliverance from religious demons speaking to your mind in Jesus name.


*PRAYER*: I receive total deliverance from religious demons speaking to my mind. I refuse confusing voices speaking to me, shut up in the name of Jesus Christ.

DEVOTIONAL 0564 | Source start line: 50551

7/6/22, 5:56 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: ProjectSave Devotional
Wednesday
July 6th, 2022.


*JESUS IS AT THE DOOR*

Rev 3:20 Here I am! I stand at the door and knock. If anyone hears my voice and opens the door, I will come in and eat with that person, and they with me. NIV

What's your understanding of Revelation 3:20? I have heard the opening text used severally during evangelistic sermons while trying to win souls. However, the context shows that Jesus was referring to a church( believers) in Asia Minor. He was not knocking at the door of unbelievers but believers who were lukewarm. Why was he knocking on the door of their hearts? He knocked on the door of their hearts because He wanted to eat with them. Does he eat human meals? In his incarnation, he ate food made by humans but now in his glorified state (resurrected body), he doesn't eat human meals. What food was implied in the text above? It is fellowship. This  word is not limited to what we do when we come together. It refers to  the depth of our closeness with the father. A communion.

Now that you're born again, it is possible that Jesus may be knocking on the door of your heart for deeper fellowship. That's His food. *Fellowship is all about intimacy.* Whenever I want to illustrate what fellowship is, I often use my paternal uncles as example. My uncles have similar voices. Most times, strangers find it hard to identify who is talking because almost  all of them sound alike. However, if they're all talking at once, I can identify my dad's voice and the identities of each of them. This is possible for me because over the years, I've fellowshipped with my dad to the point where I don't need to see his face to identify him. Once I hear his voice, that's all I need because I'm fully acquainted with His voice.  *"Your inability to identify God's voice/leading is a pointer to a weak fellowship with him".* Often, people have asked to know how to be sure of God's voice. They're looking for a quick formula to what they ought to learn by Fellowship. You cannot discern His voice if you're not intimately connected to Him.

The Christian journey should be progressive and not static. We are meant to be so close to God in a way that our lives would be enwrapped in him totally. There is a realm higher than where you're operating. Jesus is knocking on the door of your heart, saying: "I want to eat with you." You cannot exhaust the several dimensions in God, the more you know him, the more you'll discover so many things yet unknown about him. I think that was the reason why Paul said, *"That I may know him, and the power of his resurrection, and the fellowship of his sufferings, being made conformable unto his death;*"(Phil3:10). Paul was already a great Apostle with great depths of revelations, yet fellowship was his desire. This is not the time for casual Christianity; fellowship is not limited to what you do when you pray or study the bible. It is the consciousness of God that you carry around everywhere knowing that you can be in touch with him anytime. Our desire should be to know Him more.

*PRAYER*: Jesus, I am hungry, I want to dine with you. To know you more and to see you as you are.

7/7/22, 6:25 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: PROJECTSAVE DEVOTIONAL
Thursday,
July 7th, 2022.


*BIRTHING REVIVAL*


Acts 1:14 They all met together and were constantly united in prayer, along with Mary the mother of Jesus, several other women, and the brothers of Jesus. NLT


The famous Oke Oye revival in South West Nigeria was a product of intense prayer that started in 1918 at Ijebu Ode, South- west of Nigeria. In 1918, a group of believers in Ijebu Ode, South Western Nigeria started praying earnestly for revival.  They devoted themselves to serious united prayer for one purpose- revival. In their time, churches were syncretic and what they had was cold formal religion. They were persecuted by their denomination because they were different from others as they manifested the gifts of the Spirit.  The answer to their prayer came in 1930 when Apostle Ayo Babalola came on the scene. The popular Oke Oye revival was a notable revival in West Africa.  Young Babalola had been praying intensely since 1928 for revival. The Spirit of prayer was upon this humble servant of God. He could pray down a storm. When revival broke out in his time, lukewarm Christians became flaming hot for Christ and the lost found their way to the cross.  The revival swept communities and rid them of idols.


*The labour that birth revival is a silent labour but the fruit is seen by all. When revival comes, you don't need to announce it, people will see it. Revival announces itself.* A friend once told me we can't have such revival again. I told him we can. If individuals are revived, we can birth corporate revival if we pray. Though the pentecostal experience was promised but we were told that: *"They all met together and were constantly united in prayer, along with Mary the mother of Jesus, several other women, and the brothers of Jesus.*" They could have sat doing nothing because of the promised outpouring of the Spirit. Yet, these devout men and women connected to heaven in prayers as a united entity. The Spirit of revival came upon everyone of them in that upper room like cloven tongues of fire and they all  spoke with new tongues(Acts2:1-4). The impact of their prayers didn't stop there. We were told that on the same day, Peter preached and 3,000 were added to the church. It was not Peter that made altar call, it was the people that asked: *“Men and brethren, what shall we do?*” (Acts 2:37). They came under a deep conviction because the preaching of Peter was Spirit-inspired and baked in the oven of prayer. *One vital secret of life transforming preaching is prayer.*


United prayer was the key that the early church used to take territories. Acts4:31 says, *"After they prayed, the place where they were meeting was shaken. And they were all filled with the Holy Spirit and spoke the word of God boldly.*" Hallelujah!!! If their united prayers shook the meeting place, it must have dismantled the kingdom of darkness in their locality. After that prayer meeting, they declared the word of God boldly.  *When revived men join hands in prayers, impossibility gives way.*  oh! we need revived men to be united in the place of prayer. The Church that is willing to see revival will never joke with United prayer. The Christian that want to experience revival must not be far from his prayer room. The generation that want to experience revival must have prayer altars erected in the hearts of men. No prayer, no revival. Revival is birthed through the prayer labour of holy men.


*PRAYER*: Father, revive me.

DEVOTIONAL 0566 | Source start line: 50783

7/8/22, 7:50 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: PROJECTSAVE DEVOTIONAL
Friday,
July 8th, 2022.

*THE POWER OF HIS PRESENCE*

Matthew17:4 Peter said to Jesus, “Lord, it is good for us to be here. If you wish, I will put up three shelters—one for you, one for Moses and one for Elijah.”


Benny Hinn shared the story of his experience as a growing young man who encountered the Holy Spirit. While he was fellowshiping with the Holy Spirit in his room, the presence of God filled the room to the point where his mother was pushed backward when she opened the door.There is power available for every believer who can tarry in God's presence.  *We don't enter His presence because we want power, we enter His presence to fellowship and there His power rub on us*. On the mount of transfiguration, the face of Jesus shone brightly.  Three disciples(Peter, James&John) of Jesus who were part of his inner circle were on that mountain. What they saw made them tremble. After Peter saw the glorified face of Jesus and two Old testament prophets( Moses&Elijah), he also experienced the beauty and manifestation  of God's presence and he said: “Lord, it is good for us to be here...”


Many believers do not know the power that lies in abiding in God's presence. The anointing that many seek is in God's presence. The concept of rubbing with anointing oil started with the Jewish shepherd. They smear their sheep with oil as a form of protection against insects. *In God's presence, the anointing is always available to rub on only those that stay in God's presence*.  Everyone of us must learn to practice God's presence in our daily lives. Some get so preoccupied with asking for power but they neglect this principle of staying in God's presence. You need to visit the mountain to see those who are praying for power. Some have fasted for power to death. A man on e told us that some face the sun asking for power. They don't care about their eyesight. That's to the extreme. *The power of God is available only His presence. Those who cultivate and practice His presence every time will operate easily in His power*.


Years back, whenever I'm driving to the office, my vehicle was always smoky with God's presence. Those days, I'll drive two hours thirty minutes to the office from my residence. Anytime I alight from the vehicle, I was always filled with an unusual anointing. I wasn't praying for power, I was only enjoying sweet fellowship with the Holy Spirit. Learn to create an atmosphere where you can sense His presence and key into it at all times. Your kitchen can be filled with the "smoke" of His presence if only you can cultivate His presence there. I once read about a woman who became baptized in the Holy Spirit in her kitchen. You don't need to visit the mountain before coming into His glorious presence. You can live in the consciousness of his presence through constant fellowship, this is not limited to church or  worship session. We must learn to open our spirit man to fellowshipping with God even in our bathroom. Those that have this understanding need not wait to whip emotions or do a special 'waiting' to connect to the celestial. As we do this, the glory of God would descend and we'll begin to walk in the supernatural.


PRAYER: Father, i plug into your presence with an open heart and a ready spirit In Jesus name.

DEVOTIONAL 0567 | Source start line: 50854
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
7/9/22, 2:57 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: ProjectSave Devotional
Saturday,
July 9th, 2022.

*PRAYING HEAVEN'S AGENDA*


James 4:3 When you ask, you do not receive, because you ask with wrong motives, that you may spend what you get on your pleasures. NIV


In church history, there was a man, John Hyde by name. He prayed to the extent that he was nicknamed "Praying Hyde". He went to India on missions and he was also used by God in Shrewsbury revival. He was passionate about heaven's concern and he prayed, *"Give me souls or I die*". History will not forget John Knox. He was passionate about the things of the Lord. He saw the need for the salvation of many in his day. He was concerned about how multitudes were going to hell in numbers. As a revived man, he prayed, *"Lord, give me Scotland or I die*". These men could have asked for palatial mansions and the best cars money could afford in their time. However, the fire of revival that was burning in their soul pointed their attention to what was germane in the heart of God - Souls. *Revived men pray to take territories and capture hearts of men for the Lord.* John Wesley was used by God to spread revival fire around Britain and his ministry sent missionaries around the world. He was known to spend the first two hours of each day in his prayer room with a bible wide opened in his front. That room was called "Power House of Methodism."


Over hundred years after Wesley's death, someone wanted to buy liquor in a town where he once preached. He couldn't find liquor to buy. He asked why the shops weren't selling liquor. He was told that a man named John Wesley preached in that town and since then, no one sold liquor in that town. *We can pray about so many things but the prayers that touch the heavens and get their attention are prayers that reflect the agenda of heaven on humanity*. God is more concerned about seeing souls saved, baptized, healed and established in the faith than anything else. Those are the concerns of heaven. *If a revived man sees abnormal trends in the society or the church, he'll raise a prayer altar first before any other thing*. When you see prostitutes and fraudsters around you, what's your reaction? Do you pity them or you pray for them?


*When you hear about unreached peoples that have not heard about Christ most of whom you may never meet in your lifetime,  do you ever think of praying for them.*? These are the prayer concerns of revived men not "sudden breakthroughs". If Jesus were to attend our prayer meetings, will He be happy about the prayer points raised? Think about the prayer points that occupies our prayer meetings today. Is it kingdom-centred or self-centred?  If a Pastor is revived, he would lead his church to pray about the concerns of heaven. If an Evangelist is revived, he would point the body of Christ to what matters to God-Souls, and lead the church to win them whilst not forgetting the place of prayer. How many prayer altars have we raised for terrorists, prostitutes, and the unsaved? I'm not talking about cursing bad people from the altar but heart-felt prayer from a heart filled with love seeking the salvation of their souls. I am guilty of laxity in this as well and I've been trusting God to give me a heart that loves what he loves more so that my prayers can align with His will. *Our generation hasn't experienced revival because we are not praying the kind of prayers that birth revival.We need to go back into history to learn how the generation that experienced revival prayed.* Prior to the Azusa street revival, the prayer team prayed all nights for months sometimes with their heads in crates of egg asking to see God's glory. Truly, they saw the glory of the Lord. My heart is focused on these things and it is a growing burden in my heart. *Until you're revived, your prayer points would only focus on what satisfies self. God is looking for men that will pray down His glory*.


*PRAYER*: Father, let your holy fire burn in me. Stir in my heart the desire to pray about the things that matter to you and your kingdom in Jesus name.

DEVOTIONAL 0568 | Source start line: 50904
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
7/10/22, 7:06 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: ProjectSave Devotional
Sunday,
July 10th, 2022.


*NO SHORT CUT TO PERSONAL FELLOWSHIP*


Psalms42:1 As the deer pants for streams of water, so my soul pants for you, O God.


Our generation is a generation of several "fasts." We have fast food, fast lane, fast cash schemes, fast internet speed and a host of others. We're in a hurry to get things done. Getting things done fast has crept into our spiritual lives and walk with God unknowingly. We want a church service that'll be fast not minding whether the Spirit of God moves as He wills. In our prayer and word study time, we want it so quick so that we can catch up with the bus or train yet when we watch football or our favourite movies, we do not want to miss a single action. I laugh at what some persons call "Prayer life". Hooking up with a prayer program on t.v., radio, online or getting someone to pray for you regularly is not in anyway the definition of a virile prayer life. Those things can be an addition but not a substitute for getting personal with God.


These are times of great spiritual laziness and coldness where people have substituted deep fellowship with God with seeking after men of God instead of seeking the God of the man they are running after. Some have a weird idea that God can only be found on the prayer mountain. I use to have the mentality of looking out for the day one of the great men of God i admire would lay hands on me for great impartation but now i know better. You can't get all that God has endowed a man with through consistency in the place of private fellowship by simply getting his hands laid on you. No short cut to that. In the kingdom, we must climb the ladder of fellowship daily till we see God in glory. You may wait in vain if you're waiting for the day someone will impart you. It may never happen. God made it clear to me that He is always available for me if i am available for Him. That's profound. I am not against impartation but we must be careful not to neglect quality fellowship with God in search for men. What does it mean to be available for God? It simply means to have time to fellowship with Him.


To explain fellowship better, think about the intimacy that husband and wife share. If you meet happy couples, they must have related to a point where they can predict each other's action. In fact, some couples start resembling one another after years of fellowshiping together. What do you think will happen when the same happens between you and God? *So many desire to be like Jesus but do not want to spend quality time with Jesus*. It doesn't work like that. Stop looking for quick fix fellowship. Get personal with God and have time for Him. Your life of prayer and word study must be consistent. Cultivate and practice His presence. *Don't wait till you get to the kind of spiritual atmosphere that stirs you, create it anywhere you find yourself*. Your kitchen, bathroom, toilet, car, can be places where you'll enjoy sweet fellowship with the most high. Your heart must be filled with songs of praise and live with a consciousness of God and eternal realities all day. That is true fellowship.


PRAYER: Father, envelope me with the beauty of your presence as i fellowship with you In Jesus name.

DEVOTIONAL 0569 | Source start line: 50928

7/11/22, 10:09 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: PROJECTSAVE DEVOTIONAL
Monday,
July 11th, 2022.

*WAITING FOR MUMMY*

Psalms40:1 I waited patiently for the LORD; he turned to me and heard my cry.


In 1992, we relocated from Lagos to our hometown to join dad who was pastoring there at that time. Mum stayed back in Lagos to monitor the family business and was still involved in ministry within the same denomination that sent dad home for ministry. Relocating to our hometown for me, my younger and one of our cousins was fun but not funny because mummy was not living with us. Mum visited occasionally and sometimes would come with her evangelistic team for crusade. We always longed for her coming. Those days, mobile telecommunications was not existing so we had no means to reach her. However, dad had a way of knowing when she would come visiting. Any time we heard that mum was coming, our expectations was always high and we would become impatient in our waiting all of a sudden. If we hear the sound of a vehicle, we may rush out to check if it was mum. Waiting to see someone from Lagos those days was like waiting for someone to come from heaven.


In the first quarter of 1994, we were told mummy put to bed in Lagos and that she would be bringing the baby to our hometown for christening. My younger sister and I left the school before closing time because we were impatient in waiting to see mummy. We devised a scheme in school so that our school head could release us to go home. Alas! Our school head fell for our trick and he released us to go home. Those days, whenever we hear mum would be coming, we always lose our patience. *One of the hardest thing to do in life is to wait for the fulfillment of God's word*. Those who haven't waited on God for certain things that took years may not understand what waiting on God entails. What God has promised to do He would do, you need to cultivate the christian habit of waiting patiently on God. *To wait patiently on God is to stay expectant without using human means and method to find a way out.* Some became tired of waiting  patiently on God and they 'helped' themselves with alternatives.


*In God, there are no alternatives.* The Psalmist knew this and that was why his gaze was fixed on the LORD only. *You cannot be waiting on God and be looking at man at thesame time, it is impossible*. The Psalmist made it clear that after he waited  patiently on God, God heard him. God sees all you're going through but his word to you this season is that you should wait patiently. The writer of Hebrews said, *"We do not want you to become lazy, but to imitate those who through faith and patience inherit what has been promised.*"(Heb.6:12). Your waiting time is not a wasted time. God is working out somethings in you and for you during the waiting period. There is a timing for the fulfilment of your expectation and you can be sure that when God comes through for you, He'll give the best.


CONFESSION: I refuse to be impatient in my waiting season. I receive the grace to wait in love, faith and patience until i see result In Jesus name.


info@projectsaveng.org

DEVOTIONAL 0570 | Source start line: 50985
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
7/12/22, 7:30 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: ProjectSave Devotional
Tuesday
July 12th, 2022.

*ARE YOU MAD?*


Prov20:3 Avoiding a fight is a mark of honor; only fools insist on quarreling. NLT


I was in a bookshop to buy some items years back. The woman who owns the shop was on seat. I know her to be a "Christian." As we were there, she got angry and picked quarrel with someone. In anger, she said, "I'm mad and you'll know that Jesus is the author and finisher of our faith." When she said that, I marveled. My Jesus is not the author of "madness" rather He cures it. I've met people who claimed to have "madness" running through their veins. People who always lay claim to special "madness" often pick quarrels with others. They are short-fused and hot-tempered in character yet they are cool with it. Unfortunately, I've seen many Christians who claimed to be "mad." I still wonder how a Spirit-filled person can harbour the spirit of "madness" at the same time. The bible says, *"But the Holy Spirit produces this kind of fruit in our lives: love, joy, peace, patience, kindness, goodness, faithfulness, gentleness, and self-control. There is no law against these things!*"( Galatians 5:22-23 NLT).This is the portrait of a believer's life. "Madness" is not our thing.



If we'll be good students of the bible, you'll see that there are clear instructions against anger. The preacher says, *"Control your temper, for anger labels you a fool*"( Eccl.7:9). If you're prone to getting angry easily, the bible calls you a fool. In fact, the bible warns against making friends with hot-tempered people: *"Don’t befriend angry people or associate with hot-tempered people,*"( Prov.22:24). One of the things I tell singles to watchout for when choosing a mate is temper. If he/she has issues with temper, don't proceed to the next level. "Madness" has made some men beat their wives even to the point of death and vice versa. Why should a believer be proud of "madness" and even claim he/she has been to the mental home for cure? I know they've not been there physically but they're exaggerating their proclivity for causing trouble and glorifying their anger issues. Such people are not worth relating with. Run. Are you "mad"? If you are in this category of smoky- hot, short-fused people, let me introduce Jesus to you. He is the cure for your "madness".



*Don't take pride in what should make you cry to God in prayer*. To overcome anger, simply surrender your emotions, feelings to the Spirit and yield to Him: *"So I say, let the Holy Spirit guide your lives. Then you won’t be doing what your sinful nature craves*"( Gal.5:16). Don't agree with the devil that being "mad" is natural to you. Some have refused help for their "madness" because they've accepted it as part of the features of their temperament. That's the devil preaching to you. You can be a peaceful and loveable person. *The true mark of honour is in your ability to live at peace with others and maintain that peace*. One of the famous beattitude says "the peace makers shall be called true sons of God." A wise man said 'fools blow up in the face of anger'. There are things that will tempt you and I to become "mad" and to pick quarrel but we must build spiritual capacity to hold ourself in and be controlled. Anything or anyone that will tempt you into picking quarrels and displaying "madness" must be avoided. The mark of honour is to avoid a fight.

*CONFESSION*: I refuse to be mad. I submit my life and emotions to you my Lord. Henceforth, I'll live like Jesus, I receive grace to be orderly, calm and refined in character in Jesus name.


N:B Join us to pray in the Spirit 9pm ( Nigerian time) every night on zoom:https://us02web.zoom.us/j/86794796510?pwd=VncrZjBCWE9yWFJaVStPOUZ2UC92dz09


info@projectsaveng.org

July 13th, 2022.



*HOW CONCERNED ARE YOU ABOUT THE LOST*?


Acts 5:42 And every day, in the Temple and from house to house, they continued to teach and preach this message: “Jesus is the Messiah.” NLT



In a book, I read that one of those pseudo christian churches knock on 900,000 doors weekly all over the world. The purpose is to bring their message to the inhabitants of those homes. They're passionate about winning people to their sects.  In London alone, it was reported that the same Pseudo Christian sect has 1,000 people giving away literature - and they get through about 6,000 brochures, 20,000 books, and 100,000 magazines every month, just to win the hearts of men. How much more those of us that carry the authentic message of the gospel and eternal life. It pains God to see religions that do not promise her adherents eternal life spreading like wildfire yet His children who are carriers of His grace and life are keeping mute about the wonders of redemption. It is abnormal for a Christian not to share the gospel with at least one person in a year. It is a pointer to a serious spiritual malady.  *The fire of revival is the fire for soul-winning.* If the fire for soul-winning is dead in you, ask the Lord to rekindle it again.


A sister involved in evangelizing prostitutes told me about some of their converts. She told me how those ladies of easy virtues struggle to pay rent. They have financial target they must meet on weekly or monthly basis. I asked her about how they recruit girls into prostitution. She told me that most of the prostitutes "evangelize" other girls and one person can bring five girls into the business. That's how they perpetuate themselves in the trade. How many lost and unsaved persons have you brought into the faith? We cannot talk about revival without mentioning the early church in Acts of Apostles. That church grew immensely during persecution. Worst of all, it was religious folks that persecuted them the most at the early stage. Why? They felt threatened that they may win everyone over from Judaism to Christianity because their evangelism was aggressive. The fire and passion for soul winning that burned in them was irresistible. Their passion made them to go knocking on doors looking for unsaved people to win unto Christ( Acts 5:42).


Can we knock on doors like they did? Do we really care that some folks are going to hell even in our neighbourhood? Or do we feel too educated to go from house-to-house preaching the gospel?  *The passion for lost souls and how to get them over into the fold overwhelms a revived man. He is always obeying the "Go Ye" command and keeps finding ways to share the gospel by any means possible.* A revived man will not be comfortable around sinners without sharing the gospel. It is pitiful that sects we deem as heretics and modern cults are not ashamed to share their faith by knocking on our doors. When last did you preach to the lost? Are you concerned about the lost?

*PRAYER*: Father, rekindle my passion for lost souls in Jesus name.

*EVEN IN THE BATHROOM*


Isaiah50:4The Lord God hath given Me the tongue of the learned, that I should know how to speak a word in season to him that is weary. He wakeneth morning by morning; He wakeneth Mine ear to hear as the learned.


While in school, as I prepared for class in the morning, I had an inward witness in my spirit that i should be careful of getting offended that day. This happened in the bathroom. It was very clear and direct. The Spirit of God prepared me for the kind of temptation coming my way that day while i was in the bathroom. I took it calmly and was prepared  for whatever may come my way that day. As i stepped into the class, I met a revision going on. The person handling the revision took permission to leave so that i'll take over. As I got to the front of the class, my class leader demanded that I should tender an apology for a promise I didn't fulfil. The man who made the promise told everyone that he was the one who made a  promise not me yet the class leader caused a scene and accused me wrongly in front of everybody. I almost fumbled by flaring up in defense but the Holy Spirit prepared me ahead for the day in the bathroom.



I tendered an apology and I continued the revision session. There was a great calm in the class. Everyone knew I was right because the student who made a  promise openly cleared me yet I was accused wrongly. I knew deep within that the devil wanted me to be angry that day but God prepared me for the day ahead. Jesus won Satan lost. This is one of the benefits of fellowshiping with God daily. What you do in the morning when you wake up matters alot. You can get insight about the rest of the day in the morning as you wait on God. *The morning season is not just a time to ask for what you want but a time to receive from the Lord what you should do if you encounter the devil in the day.* Are you prepared? Some temptations that believers fell into could be avoided if they had listened to the Holy Spirit ahead of time. *In the place of sweet fellowship, the Spirit of God want to tell us what lies ahead, what to avoid and how to handle the unavoidable.*


Another vital thing I want you to note in my story is the place where the Holy Spirit spoke to my heart- the bathroom. In fact, I've said it several times that I often receive/ prepare some powerful messages I preach in the toilet while doing the thing. Your kitchen can be filled with the cloud of glory as you fellowship with God. While at work, your office space can be filled with the glory of His presence. Now, you need to understand that your fellowship with God shouldn't be a part-time thing but a full time activity which can take place anywhere and at anytime. Some persons only have a place and time where they spend quality time with God. While that's good but it is not enough. We are meant to cultivate the presence of God anywhere and at anytime as the Spirit of God enables. I read one of Kathryn Khulman's post where she said she doesn't want to give the Holy Spirit one hour so that the manifestations in her ministry won't be attributed to her one hour meeting with God. She decided that her fellowship with the Spirit would be a continuous thing. This habit we must cultivate consciously. As you drive, cook, wash, walk, fellowship with God. That way, you'll be sensitive at all times.


PRAYER: Father, i receive the grace to fellowship with you at all times. Holy Spirit, I want to be closer too you In Jesus name. Amen

DEVOTIONAL 0573 | Source start line: 51438
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
7/15/22, 5:33 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: PROJECTSAVE DEVOTIONAL
Friday,
July 15th, 2022.


*PASSING DIVINE TESTS*


Jeremiah 17:10 "I, the LORD, search the heart, I test the mind, Even to give to each man according to his ways, According to the results of his deeds.


Years back, I returned from a mission outreach we held outside Nigeria with a little left over of the ministry's money on me. That little money left on me was more than all that was in my personal account then.   After every outreach, i normally account for all expenses during the outreach in our own way and I'll return the remaining money into the ministry's purse. That I still do. When I got home, my mother-in-law who had been with us for weeks was prepared to return to her base. She had been of great help to us especially in taking care of the children. It is honourable for us to send her forth with substantial money and some gifts. That's not a payment for services rendered. She's a blessed woman already.  It is all about honour.  All I had was less than N7,000 in my personal account.  My wife asked what we would give to her. I was working on my laptop and thinking about an appropriate response but I was short of words. I just didn't know the right answer to give but i calmed her. Something pointed my attention to the ministry's money with me. I was tempted to give her something substantial from the left over money but I decided I won't. The money with me was more than enough to meet that pressing need. I looked away because it wasn't mine. It was hard. I didn't even borrow from it as some would have done. Long ago, I made it a policy that ministry money isn't available for such.


A few minutes after my wife left the room, I received a message from someone I know in Sokoto state. She asked me to send my personal account number. I sent it without thinking twice. The alert I got was a shocker. I gave my mother-in-law a substantial amount and we had a litle left on us.  I didn't tell this person I needed money neither did I solicit for her help. It was a miracle. However, deep within, I knew I passed a test and got the result immediately. *If you must rise in life, your integrity will be tested. God will not move you to the next level in life and ministry until you pass the exams in the school of faithfulness and integrity*. As a minister of the Gospel, don't mix ministry money or properties with yours, separate them. *When God bless your ministry with landed properties, don't take part of it to build your personal house neither should you claim the church's parsonage as yours. If you do, it shows you lack integrity*. You raised the fund for that project in the ministry's name not yours, why convert it to your personal property? Remember, ministry and ministry properties cannot be inherited so why handle it as though it is yours? Have a separate purse for yourself and family. The arrogance and lack of integrity of some ministers is the reason they say "God's money belongs to the man of God." Hear this, God's money belongs to God and not the man of God.


I'm not saying it is wrong for the ministry to remunerate or take care of ministers. I believe in having robust welfare package for God's servants. However, it must be done the right way, agreed upon by relevant authorities and documented to avoid arbitrary spending. It is wrong for a minister to dip his hands into the purse against order. Taking what is not yours by right is stealing. Money, women & power will test you. God rejected Eliab before he was chosen yet David was accepted by God before he was enthroned. Isn't that surprising? God must have tested Eliab at some point without him knowing. When he and others were paraded before Samuel, God rejected them for Kingship not out of hatred but because they weren't fit for the task. A shepherd boy in the wilderness passed God's test in the wilderness and was enthroned above men of military might and rank. Where Samson failed, Joseph succeeded, where Judas failed, Peter succeeded(Acts8:20). *Prayer alone is not enough to raise a man, there are test to be taken.* It is good to pray but it is also good to pass divine tests. Some ministers practically live on the mountain yet they are nothing short of a failure in life and ministry yet some have never been to any mountain before but keep growing in leaps and bounds as they passed divine tests. That's not to downplay the place of personal prayer retreat.  This may be your season of examination, be diligent to write your papers well.


PRAYER: Father,i receive the grace not to fail you In Jesus name.

DEVOTIONAL 0574 | Source start line: 51472

7/16/22, 7:24 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: ProjectSave Devotional
Saturday,
July 16th, 2022.


*GOD REPLACED THE VESPA RIDER*


1Sam15:28 So Samuel said to him, “The Lord has torn the kingdom of Israel from you today, and has given it to a neighbor of yours, who is better than you.


I was told the story of a man in a certain Gospel church whose financial contributions were key. He was the richest in the church. He had a vespa motorcycle. Others in the church had no vehicle not even a bicycle, including the Pastor.  Those of us that has been around for sometime would know Vespa motorcycle.  That man's tithe took care of so many needs in the church including the Pastor's allowances. In the course of time, this man became proud and arrogant. He began to make certain demands that were not proper probably because he was the major financier of that church.  The pastor stood his ground and didn't compromise. I love Pastors that are firm. The Pastor's refusal to compromise infuriated this man and he left the church. Afterwards, the church struggled financially, even the pastor felt it because his allowances were affected. The church began to pray for breakthrough; on a certain day, a man with a Peugeot 504 drove into the church premises. He was a humble naval officer that was on transfer to that town. He joined the church and his financial commitment to the church was more than that of the Vespa riding man. The Vespa riding man had a very bad ending. Proud and arrogant people don't end well.


*God still replaces men. Don't be deceived, if you mess up God's calling  and the assignment he has given you, you can be replaced.* In that church or ministry where your commitment is registered, don't think you're indispensable. *Never think without your money, the church cannot survive. The day you position yourself to think that way, you just signed your replacement form*. That Vespa rider signed his replacement form the moment he taught he was indispensable. In the church, some workers hold their Pastor to ransom probably because they're the numero uno in their department. They believe without them, that department cannot function well so they misbehave and do things as they like. This is common to gifted singers and music people in the church. Some want to turn themselves to "guest" ministers in their church. This is loathsome to God. If you're fond of doing this, please stop. Don't sign your replacement form. *Some persons killed a glorious ministry even before it started*.



Bear in mind that there's always a better neighbour somewhere. Ask Saul. *The mystery of replacement is that God replaces people with someone that is better.* Saul started well but he missed it along the line, and a better neighbour of his was chosen. Listen to what God told Saul through Prophet Samuel when he messed up: *"And Samuel said to him, “The LORD has torn the kingdom of Israel from you today and has given it to someone else—one who is better than you.*"( 1 Sam.15:28). Lord, may I not be replaced in destiny.  *The danger of replacement is that, when a man is replaced in God's agenda, he becomes empty afterwards.* God doesn't just replace a man anyhow, He always ensure that He uses someone that is better. Don't be consumed with pride, there's a better neighbour around the corner. Beware.



PRAYER: Father, may I not be replaced in destiny, I refuse to sign my replacement form In Jesus name.

DEVOTIONAL 0575 | Source start line: 51633
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
7/17/22, 5:38 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: ProjectSave Devotional
Sunday,
July 17th, 2022.


*DON'T LOOK DOWN ON PEOPLE*


2Kings5:2 Now bands of raiders from Aram had gone out and had taken captive a young girl from Israel, and she served Naaman’s wife. 3 She said to her mistress, “If only my master would see the prophet who is in Samaria! He would cure him of his leprosy.”


It is common for bosses to look down on gate men, drivers, maids and servants as persons without knowledge and ideas. Most of our movies in this part of the world also portray them as funny characters who knows nothing and without ideas whereas in real life, it is not so. Some persons just believe that anyone that is lower in status knows nothing and amount to nothing. Naaman almost missed his healing because of this attitude.
l'lI suggest the story of Naaman be read by every man or woman that is highly placed.  Naaman was leprous though he was a commander of the Aramean army. His house help suggested to him that he should go and see Prophet Elisha for healing. He must have struggled with that idea especially because of who suggested it to him but he obliged. Thank God he did. He may have died a leper if he hadn't listened to his house girl.



Elisha  instructed him to bathe in Jordan river seven time. This got him angry. He expected Elisha to pray and strike the place where the leprosy was for healing but Elisha didn't. There's another lesson for us there. *God may choose not to move in ways we expect. This account for one of the reasons we don't see or experience the supernatural like the days of old. While His principles remain unchanging, His method may change. Don't box God*.  Surprisingly, this army general consulted his house maid on the matter and she was instrumental to his healing. What if Naaman had looked down on her as ordinary? He may die a leprous army general. *Those we see as ordinary sometimes can be very useful as links to the top*. An ordinary clerk in an office could be pivotal to helping you win that contract you're bidding for. Stop acting like the landlord of the world. Be nice to people regardless of their status.


Some men haven't made much progress in life because they believe their wives know nothing. Some men believe women are blockheads who know nothing. Men in this category prefer to consult with beer guzzling friends for counsel. Many men would have made progress in life save for the fact that they refused to listen to wise counsel ffrom their wives. Haman looked down on Modecai as an ordinary gate keeper. He wanted Mr Modecai to bow but the table turned. The same gate keeper became so influential in the city and Haman publicized him around town. One of the test of our Christianity is to check how we handle our employees and junior staff or colleagues at work.  There is no rule that says your driver or cleaner at work cannot advise you. You need to be open for interraction especially with ordinary people. Treat them well. Even little children can supply the extra information you need to make you extra ordinary. Those you see as little matter alot if you will make progress in life.


PRAYER: Father, I receive the grace to esteem all men. Help me to see people through your eyes In Jesus name

7/18/22, 6:51 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: PROJECTSAVE DEVOTIONAL

July 18th, 2022.

*BEWARE OF COUNSEL ON THE STREET OF SOCIAL MEDIA*


Hebrews13:17 Obey your spiritual leaders, and do what they say. Their work is to *watch over your souls*, and they are accountable to God. Give them reason to do this with joy and not with sorrow. That would certainly not be for your benefit. NLT



There's a dangerous trend I've seen in recent times. I see people coming on social media to ask for counseling. Some even ask for  counsel on marital issues on social media platforms where there are people with diverse beliefs and values about marriage. On several occasions, some simple men have brought their family issues to the media asking for counsel from believer and unbelievers.  You need to see divergent and demonic views that aren't edifying. Some singles also ask for counsel on social media on how to choose a mate and how to handle their relationships. That's very immature. Most of the counsel they often get are anti biblical. Imagine someone who is keeping his home intact telling you to divorce your spouse because of little misunderstanding. The street of social media is very dangerous and we need to know our limits as God's children. I see this trend as an abuse of social media and a ploy of the devil to downplay the office of the Pastor. As a believer, if you have issues that are beyond what you can handle in your home, business or office, simply consult your pastor or spiritual leader. He is expected to be a neutral umpire.



For those that are blessed with credible and wise pastors, you stand to lose so many things if you don't tap into his wealth of wisdom and grace. Pastoral counseling is lacking in this generation because most people do not understand the role of their Pastor in their lives. A lot of marriages are in serious mess today because at the point of entering into it, they by-passed the Pastor's counsel, hid their spouses from the pastor and just presented him/her as an "angel". You make it hard for your Pastor to do proper scrutiny after you've proposed and fixed a wedding date without his knowledge. At that point, he has no option than to accept your choice. *Singles, beware of making marital choice without carrying your Pastor along*. From the very beginning, before you propose or before you accept any proposal, involve your Pastor. Social media counselors won't join you in prayer neither will they help scrutinize your choice. I know a sister who wanted to marry a man who already has a child old enough to write SSCE but he hid it from this sister. Through pastoral counseling, the sister discovered that her fiance had a child he hid from her. That was the end of their relationship because of deception. If she hadn't taken the step she took, she may live to regret her marriage. *Do not be afraid to hear a different opinion from your Pastor.* The fear most people have is that they're afraid of hearing something contrary so they hide their relationship from their Pastor. That's a sign that shows you're about to make the wrong choice.



*Beware of brothers/ sisters who do have Pastors they're genuinely submitted to*. If I'm about to make a choice and someone mature spiritually point my attention to something, it behooves on me to search my spirit, investigate and pray to be sure I'm on the right or wrong track. I don't need to run away from such a person even if I stand by my convictions. Some persons have tagged those who give them true counsel "Enemy of progress."  *Stop running from that Pastor who loves you to the point of giving sincere and unbiased counsel.* I understand that some have been betrayed and disappointed by some Pastors in the past, that doesn't nullify the Christian practice of seeking Pastoral counsel when needed. You only need to be under the right Pastor. *If you understand the role of a shepherd, you will prayerfully submit to a sound pastoral oversight. Stay where you can be reached, don't be lost in the crowd because you want to identify with a popular church.*   Stop disgracing yourself on social media by asking for counsel. Certain situations happen in life where you need to talk to someone you can see for counsel. God has set ministry gifts in place for special purposes and one of such that cares for the sheep, tend the sheep and feed is the office of the pastor. A sheep must have a shepherd or else it would be lost. Who is pastoring you?


PRAYER: I receive wisdom for living In Jesus name.

DEVOTIONAL 0577 | Source start line: 52050
