7/19/22, 6:57 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: ProjectSave Devotional
Tuesday
July 19th, 2022

*HE BADE GOD FAREWELL*

1 Samuel 16:14 Now the Spirit of the Lord had departed from Saul, and an evil[a] spirit from the Lord tormented him.

Before Billy Graham, there was Charles Templeton. It is even safe to say they ministered in the same era. In the 1940's, Charles Templeton was a popular Canadian Evangelist who ministered powerfully alongside Billy Graham. His ministerial prospect was so promising because he was a crowd puller. In his time, stadiums were full whenever he had his evangelistic meetings. However, Templeton ended up leaving the Christian faith, eventually becoming an atheist. He entered into crisis of belief about God, the bible and the Christian faith in general in 1956 and he finally left the Christian faith in 1957. In 1982, though still an atheist, he said of Billy Graham, *“There is no feigning in him: he believes what he believes with an invincible innocence. He is the only mass evangelist I would trust”*.   Templeton died in 2001 at the age of 86, shortly after he published one of the most heart-breaking books ever published: *Farewell to God*. In the book, he gave reasons why he became an atheist and abandoned the faith. He had a bad ending. Who knows the number of persons the book must have drawn into atheism. He bade God farewell.




This man backslid to the point of no return, was replaced by God and he died pitiably. If you still have doubts about the issue of God replacing men or a Christian backsliding to a point of no return, Charles Templeton is a recent contemporary example to study from books and internet resources if you're in doubt of Saul's story.  *The first thing that happens to a man replaced by God is disconnection from the Spirit.* A replaced man becomes empty, irrational and useless. He may be an intellectual yet he is foolish. He may know the bible and have sound seminary education yet he may not be different from a philosopher.  King Saul had occasional torment by a demon because God's Spirit left him. Benny Hinn and Kathryn Khulman said they valued the Holy Spirit so much to the extent that they are careful not to lose Him. *The entrance of the Spirit into a man's life comes with manifestations but His exit is silent but it spells the beginning of misery*.  The devil is not afraid of your crowd pulling anointing neither is he afraid of the height you've reached in the ministry. He is a resilient enemy seeking whom to deceive and devour: *" Stay alert! Watch out for your great enemy, the devil. He prowls around like a roaring lion, looking for someone to devour.*"(1 Peter 5:8 NLT). We need to be careful about backsliding gradually. This thing doesn't happen in one day.



Some believers still hold the view that it is impossible for a believer to backslide and go to hell. Now, I ask, the case of a man who was once on fire for God, pulled crowd in evangelistic meetings and preached the Gospel but delved into atheism, is heaven sure for such a person? To say he is eternally saved even if he cursed God because he was once saved is to be self deluded and untrue to  scriptures. Apostle Paul says, *"Now the Holy Spirit tells us clearly that in the last times some will turn away from the true faith; they will follow deceptive spirits and teachings that come from demons.*"(1 Timothy 4:1 NLT). Once a man departs from the faith, he cannot partake in the glorious inheritance of the saints and all that is reserved for sons. The writer of Hebrews also told us that it will be hard restoring those who were once enlightened about the faith( referring to those who were once in the faith) and had tasted of the power of the world to come If they fall away ( Hebrews 6:1-6). *Another thing you and I need to be careful about is the pursuit of wrong knowledge. Trouble starts when you begin to "investigate" God and bible.* You will encounter wrong knowledge and become Godless. We don't try to know God from a philosophical standpoint neither can we "reason" Him out. *Subjecting spiritual things to intellectualism will make you a philosophical atheist and you may bid God farewell*. God and His word can only be revealed to you by the Spirit. I will not bid God farewell. I don't know about you.


PRAYER: Father, help me. May I not bid you farewell In Jesus name.

*LOST IN THE PAST*

Job29:4 As I was in the days of my youth, when the secret of God was upon my tabernacle;

When I was about completing my first degree in the university, I spent a lot of time praying about the next phase of my life asking God to reveal what His plans were to me. Though this became major prayer point for me immediately after my 100 Level and I stayed on that prayer point till my final year and beyond. However, I increased the intensity in my final year. After writing my final paper, on a dark night, I did a prayer walk from my hostel to my fellowship venue. I climbed the gallery and was praying in the midnight. I was all alone in that vicinity or say i didn't see anyone in sight, most students had gone home. That period,  I was waiting to defend my project. From the dark, a young man walked to where I was, stopped my prayer and told me he was sent to give me a message in response to my requests. He told me about how an angel instructed him to reach out to urgently.



I was not moved by the "angelic message" but I was curious to know this person better. He was not a brother in any of the fellowship on campus but I know his face because I've seen him several times on campus. I decided to enter into a conversation with him to know him better. As we discussed, he began to say things like 'when I was on fire for God' and other statements that showed that he had a solid walk with God in the past. Actually, he had God's call upon him and I sensed it right there. Himself told me about God's assignment for his life but he became sexually immoral and lost his place. He spoke from the standpoint of a man with a bitter heart. In fact, from that conversation, I discovered he was actively involved in ministry in the past doing exploits but he strayed. He wasn't a student of our school but he had business on campus. He told me he was trusting God to get back into the ministry but I won't forget a statement he made, "When I was on fire for God." That statement pierced my heart deeply and since then one of my prayer points have always been "May I not become once upon a time in the faith".


*There are champions of yesterday still basking in the glory of the past. They are still walking, talking and may even be honouring invitations here and there but all that is left in their walk are stories of past exploits*. At some point in the past, they got lost and missed it but haven't been restored. In my little time on earth, I've seen this. I knew God permitted me to have that encounter so that I see the possibility of regression. *I also learnt that Satan doesn't respect height reached before bringing a man down, only men with depth will remain in this race*. From the scripture above, Job had the secret of God upon his tabernacle as a youth but he didn't tell us if he still had those secrets as an old man. I don't want to be a story telling christian at my old age. There are good stories to tell at old age but I don't want to tell those stories from the standpoint of a man lost in the past. I don't want to tell people 'I was a preacher'. God forbid! *To avoid being lost in the past, consistency in our daily walk with God will keep us on track till the end.* People miss it in their walk with God at the point of 'it doesn't matter' and from there, they dwell in past glory and miss out on God. We are not of them that draw back, our aim should be to finish strong.


*PRAYER*: Father, restore me to the right track anywhere I've missed it. May I not become a champion of yesterday in Jesus name. Amen

*JUST ONCE MORE*

Judges16:28 Then Samson prayed to the Lord, “Sovereign Lord, remember me. Please, God, strengthen me just once more, and let me with one blow get revenge on the Philistines for my two eyes.”


I am not unaware of some persons who have great assignments but failed God and probably are on God's replacement list. They sense greatly that God is already shopping for their replacement even when they're not dead. King Saul was in this category and to say such persons doesn't exist is to deny the obvious. I have a message of hope for people in that category. It is not over yet as long as you're alive. To be restored to your assignment, you need to do what Samson did. I discovered that when Saul failed God and stirred His anger, he didn't ask for another chance to to continue his assignment though he pleaded for forgiveness quite alright (1 Sam15:24-31).  He failed to take advantage of the mercy of God that was available. When Samuel accused him of sparing sheep, goats and king Agag, he gave excuses instead of him to beg for God's mercy. The happenings around his life made God to stand on his decision to replace him without changing his mind.


Samson was another man that failed God and was disgraced but he did something that Saul failed to do. In Judges16:28, he asked God to give him the last chance to fulfil destiny when he held the pillars of the hall where sport was made of him: *"Then Samson prayed to the Lord, “Sovereign Lord, remember me. Please, God, strengthen me JUST ONCE MORE, and let me with one blow get revenge on the Philistines for my two eyes*.” The import of that request has a direct impact on his yet unfulfilled assignment.  God granted that prayer and he killed more Philistines(an enemy nation) at once in his death than when he was alive. Though the road was rough for him but his assignment of judging Israel as a warrior who was to deal with the Philistines was fulfilled. He crossed the finish line albeit in a battered state though avoidable.


*God is not just the God of one more chance but He's the God of another chance, that chance could be one or more depending on His sovereignty*. I watched a film where a man asked for one more chance to right his wrongs and accept Jesus. He was in a dream. God in His mercy granted his request. God is merciful. I don't want you to lose sight of that. If today's devotional has ministered to you, it may be God giving you another chance to right your wrongs and re launch you into destiny. Saul failed to ask God for another chance like Samson did, he forgot that God is plenteous in mercy but Samson took  advantage of it. Just once more; God is reaching out to you.

PRAYER: Father, i realise you still love me and you're not willing to replace me in destiny. Show me mercy and give me another chance In Jesus name.

*HOLISTIC PREPARATION FOR LIFE& MARRIAGE* Pt.1


Colossians2:7 Let your roots grow down into him, and let your lives be built on him. Then your faith will grow strong in the truth you were taught, and you will overflow with thankfulness. NLT


*SPIRITUAL LIFE FIRST*


Most singles keep getting prepared for wedding at the exclusion of life and marriage. *Often, I tell people that you prepare for marriage as you prepare for life in general.* Some carve out the marital department of their lives and prepare so much for it but they neglect other aspects. For example, in this part of the world, there are ladies who abandoned a thriving career, business, and their academic pursuit because they wanted to marry. The society made them feel that ambitious and successful ladies rarely get husbands. Our society stigmatizes successful women who do not have husbands or those ones whose marriages have issues. Often, they attribute their marital crisis to their inability/ refusal to submit. In some cases, it may be and it may not. However, it is wrong to always assume that all high flying women are not marriageable because they won't be submissive. This misconception has caused many young and promising ladies to 'slow down' on their journey and focus on marriage thinking they'll pick up after marriage. Often, it doesn't work out that way. *You don't neglect vital preparations for life at the expense of marital preparation. Your life in its entirety is bigger than marriage. Marriage is part of life, just a fraction*.


*The first preparation you need in life is spiritual preparation. We don't have the opportunity to prepare spiritually for life before life happens. We only prepare for life in life on daily basis.* As a single who desires a great life and marriage, you prepare spiritually by giving yourself to    daily meditation on the word of God and being constant with God in the place of prayer. If you have not built this culture into your life as a single, it may become hard to develop this culture of word study and prayer in marriage because of pressures that comes in marriage. In my discussion with couples especially women, I noticed that they complain about meeting up with personal devotion especially studying of the bible because they get busy with the children, husband and house chores though that's not an excuse if they plan their time. Some of these women are those I can call devout and spiritual yet they're struggling to meet up because of domestic duties. What do you think will happen to a single sister that hasn't built a strong devotional life before marriage? Aside the benefits a strong devotional life has for marriage, it has immense benefits for your life in general. Paul's says, *"For bodily exercise profiteth little: but godliness is profitable unto all things, having promise of the life that now is, and of that which is to come*"(1 Tim 4:8).


When you're rooted in God's word, living a life of purity won't be an issue. David said, *"How can a young person stay on the path of purity? By living according to your word.*"(Psalm119:9). If you're struggling with certain impurities in your life as a single, the word of God is the cure. It is not wise to carry those excesses into marriage. They won't affect only your marriage but your life in general. *Some of the character flaws that break marriages also break good relationships and makes some persons lose their jobs.* That's why we must get rid of them because they're not beneficial to our lives in general. Those reoccuring personal struggles with the flesh can be cut off from your life as you grow root in the word of God: *"For the word of God is alive and powerful. It is sharper than the sharpest two-edged sword, cutting between soul and spirit, between joint and marrow. It exposes our innermost thoughts and desires.*"(Heb4:12). The word of God is potent to cut you off from lust, masturbation and inordinate desires. Do not marry because you want to  overcome lust, masturbation and inordinate desires. Some married folks still lust and masturbate. This is the time to start studying the bible as though your life depends on it. Truly it does. *Whatever you invest in your life spiritually will have a direct impact on your marital life. Invest in your spiritual life*. You need to be addicted to the word and prayer because it has so much benefit for your life- marriage inclusive. Your life first.


*PRAYER*: Father, I receive the grace to invest quality into my spiritual life in Jesus name

7/23/22, 9:04 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: ProjectSave Devotional
Saturday,
July 23rd, 2022.



*HOLISTIC PREPARATION FOR LIFE& MARRIAGE* Pt.2



2Pet1:5 For this very reason, make every effort to add to your faith goodness; and to goodness, knowledge; NIV


*CHARACTER FORMATION*


I once shared the story of a woman whose husband was guilty of battery. She reported her husband to her Pastor. He perceived that her husband was hot-tempered while she was insultive. Bad combination. The Pastor gave her a broomstick and told her to put the broomstick in her mouth always. He told her that whenever her husband was angry, she should not talk so that the broomstick won't fall. She obeyed her Pastor and the husband was surprised that his verbally abusive wife had changed. The wisdom of learning to keep silent in moments of anger saved their marriage. *Most times, character flaws that destroys most marriages are the negative characters that couples failed to deal with before marriage.* For example, a sexually immoral man who haven't mastered the flesh will continue his sexcapades in marriage likewise a woman. A play boy will graduate into becoming a play man.  Some may think marriage would cure that character flaw only to discover that marriage amplifies it. *You don't marry to change a bad character, you change before entering into marriage. Marriage is not a rehabilitation program.*




*Everyone of us must desire what I call Christ formation. When Christ is formed in us, our character will be properly formed.* This will help us in all areas of our lives. Paul prayed and said,  *"Oh, my dear children! I feel as if I’m going through labor pains for you again, and they will continue until Christ is fully developed( formed) in your lives*( Galatians 4:19 NLT). You need Christ formation for life and marriage. You need to develop the strength of godly character before entering into marriage. You need it for life and in marriage. One can tell those that will have a good home by merely observing their character. I have seen several situations where a mature adult misbehaves and those around said, "That's how they behave at home." An insultive woman in the market won't spare her husband at home.  *People don't behave differently in marriage from how they behave in their day-to-day living. A disrespectful lady will surely disrespect her  husband. A street fighter will surely beat his wife.* Aside marriage, we need godly character in all our endeavours.  Why some homes are battle grounds is because of the character flaws of those involved. *When two people come together to be joined as husband and wife, they are bringing their personality and character into the marriage*. Often, I counsel singles not to overlook certain character flaws while in courtship. They're good pointers to who he/she might be. If while in courtship, he/she disrespect adults and doesn't see anything wrong with that, that character flaws will be brought into your marriage. If she's not submissive to her parents, she might not submit to you.  Though you're praying for "Mr/Mrs" right to come your way, you need to add godly virtues to your own life so that you won't ruin your marriage with your hands. *The most important renovation project that must be done in your life as a single is you.*



*Let me ask you this sincere question, can you marry someone that is exactly like you in character?*  Don't be quick to give an answer. In the opening text, Apostle Peter admonished that we add certain godly virtues to our lives. Telling us to 'add' mean that we have a part to play in the development of those virtues. *As a single, you have to turn yourself in for a character development project. The character flaws you fail to deal with today may deal with you in marriage tomorrow.* Even though Apostle Peter admonished that we add those virtues to our lives for a smooth relationship with God. I'm sure those virtues will help singles and the married folks have  blissful homes. Beauty is good but it is fleeting, it is not bad to be tall dark and handsome. Painfully, physical appeal is not enough to sustain a marriage. As you invest in developing a godly character, you're securing your home.


*PRAYER*: Father, I need your help. I receive grace to develop godly character in Jesus name

*HOLISTIC PREPARATION FOR LIFE& MARRIAGE* Pt.3


Hosea 4:6 my people are destroyed from lack of knowledge. “Because you have rejected knowledge, I also reject you as my priests; because you have ignored the law of your God, I also will ignore your children. NIV


*BE ROUNDLY INFORMED*


Years back, there was a sister who would pick only books on marriage if she ever visited the library or bookshop. Due to this habit of picking only books on marriage, she earned the nick name "Life Partner." Reading books about marriage is good and it is one of the things we recommend to singles. *However, you don't prepare for marriage well by reading books on marriage only*. I said previously that we prepare for marriage as we prepare for life. *Marriage is not all there is to life. Don't be so obsessed about marriage and neglect other vital aspects of your life*. There are other important aspects of your life you need to be properly informed about to make your marriage work. What about reading books on finance and budgeting? Don't you know that finance is key to romance in marriage? Here we go. Alot of marriages are stormy because either of the spouses know little or nothing about money management. They know so much about love&sex but are ignorant about money little wonder they have money issues at all times. Acquiring simple money management skill through reading may have averted some of their issues.


One of my lecturers in the seminary said, "Know little thing about everything and know everything about little things." That has helped me in my personal life. As a single, invest in your library. Sometimes, not getting a mate may look like a spiritual problem to some but not in all cases.  The inability of some to enter a relationship or retain one may be tied to a skill/ knowledge they lack.  Do yourself a world of good to learn from books and mentors. *So much investment goes into acquiring a degree so that we can be established in a career but only few people study to prepare for life after school- marriage inclusive*. Have you read books on parenting? What about health& nutrition? Don't think you will need knowledge in these areas? I have observed that those who succeed in marriage are those who have rounded preparation for life. You can go to a marriage school and still fail in marriage. That's not to berate quality pre marital education. It is important if you can get one but not at the neglect of other aspects of your life. Our lives are in spheres. We must prepare for each sphere of life adequately because they will impact on our marital life in the long run.


In the corporate world, no company will employ an untrained fellow especially for professional roles. Marriage is a professional venture that requires adequate preparation.  God frowns at ignorance: *"Therefore my people are gone into captivity, because they have no knowledge: and their honourable men are famished, and their multitude dried up with thirst.*"(Isaiah5:13). When you read, your horizon becomes broad. As a single, read books that centre on the roles,  perspective and function of genders. This understanding of gender differences will help you to know how these things play out in marriage. When you're armed with that knowledge, you'll know how to weather certain storms. Further, you can learn about simple financial management, entrepreneurial skills, budgeting, real estate investment, health & nutrition, and parenting. Improve on your culinary skills too. Getting quality information in these areas and others will impact positively on your marriage. We live in a world of useful documented information. Be informed. *As you invest in your physical appearance, do not forget to invest in your brain.* Your library is a pointer to how serious you are about your life and marital future. Don't enter into marriage ignorantly.


*PRAYER*: Father, I receive grace to be a life long learner and to invest quality information into my life in Jesus name.

7/25/22, 8:20 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: PROJECTSAVE DEVOTIONAL

*HOLISTIC PREPARATION FOR LIFE& MARRIAGE* Pt.4


Hosea 4:6 my people are destroyed from lack of knowledge. “Because you have rejected knowledge, I also reject you as my priests; because you have ignored the law of your God, I also will ignore your children. NIV

*FINANCIAL PREPARATION*


Financial preparation is key to living a comfortable life. There's no dream or vision that can be achieved without it. The bible says, *"A feast is made for laughter, wine makes life merry, and money is the answer for everything*"( Eccl 10:19). Aside love, money runs marriage. As a single, as you prepare for marriage, you don't neglect your financial life. It may not matter to you now but in marriage, it will become a crucial matter. There are some young folks who so much love luxury. They know the latest cars in town and they like designers. Their desire for life is grandiose but most of them are not prepared financially neither are they taking steps to achieve their desire. Often, people in this category resort to ungodly ways of making money.  I have seen laziness at its peak in the lives of some especially men. How can someone wake up in the morning and be comfortable doing nothing all day other than to press phone and gist around in the neighbourhood? I've been around for sometime and I can tell that there are guys who want to live off people and rip them. They neither work nor make plans to learn a craft yet they love good life. Always looking for hardworking ladies to marry to secure their financial lot.  They're physically and mentally lazy. If you tell such persons to build their financial life gradually from the scratch by starting small, they may tell you they want to start with six figure salary.

Dearest, the starting point in financial preparation is work. The bible is clear on this: *"Even while we were with you, we gave you this command: “Those unwilling to work will not get to eat*”(2 These 3:10). If I may add, those unwilling to work should not marry. Stop praying for blessings when your hands are doing nothing. The promise of blessings is predicated upon the work of your hands. If you do nothing, God will bless nothing. I am of the opinion that no church should join a man that is not working to a sister in  marriage. Even if the earnings are little, it is better than doing nothing at all. That counsel may sound harsh especially to some ladies who feel time is no longer on their side and just want to marry quickly but the reality often dawn on them in marriage. Some of the marital challenges Pastors deal with are related to this aspect of life. The bible says, *"But if any provide not for his own, and specially for those of his own house, he hath denied the faith, and is worse than an infidel*"( 1 Tim 5:8). While in courtship, if you notice laziness in any form, it may be a red flag. This applies on both sides. Sisters, you don't need to marry a man that is "super rich", however the basic elements that points to financial readiness must be in place. This counsel is rare but I'm convinced you must hear it. That's why it is good you're under a pastor who is not emotionally attached to you to the point of hiding vital truths from you.



Getting financial education is another vital point to note in preparing for life financially. As a single, this is the time to invest in quality financial education. *Qualitative financial education will teach you  how to make money, multiply it and manage it for judicious use.* If you don't know these things, start learning them now. Invest in books and seminars that will be beneficial to you in this area.  This is the best time of your life to be enterprising and to lay the foundation for multiple streams of income. Develop the right attitude to money. Stop spending on impulse and be responsible with money. Some folks show gross financial irresponsibility by borrowing and not repaying while some spend on pleasure only at the expense of other important things. If your financial goal is to spend on things that don't add value on long term, you need to reset your goals. The journey to learning about money should start now so as to avoid future problems.

*PRAYER*: Father, I receive financial wisdom in Jesus name.

7/26/22, 7:25 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: ProjectSave Devotional
Tuesday

*HOLISTIC PREPARATION FOR LIFE& MARRIAGE* Pt.5


2Tim1:7 For God hath not given us the spirit of fear; but of power, and of love, and of a sound mind.

*EMOTIONAL DEVELOPMENT*


Our approach to life must be holistic. Scriptures teaches that God has given us all that pertains to life and godliness (2 Pet.1:3). As we grow, we must not neglect gaining mastery over our emotions. Emotion is the part of a person's character that consists of their feelings, as opposed to their thoughts. I love that definition. According to Collins dictionary, an emotion is a feeling such as happiness, love, fear, anger, or hatred, which can be caused by the situation that you are in or the people you are with. There's always a part of us that is emotional. There are adults, who still miss their parents just as children will miss their parents on a long trip. Many people have outbursts of emotion in difficult situations. They tend to become overwhelmed and face a lack of self- control. People who are more resilient in such situations are known as emotionally strong. Those that have not gained  mastery over their emotions may make permanent decisions in temporary situations and ruin much good for the future.


Singles willing to be married should know that marriage has a way of checking our emotional strength. Lack of self control is a sign of emotional weakness, inability to take initiative when needed is another sign of emotional dullness. *If you are not a master over feelings, your marriage may be boring*. Feelings are fickle and that's why you don't take major decisions on the basis of how you feel. The mature single is one who can separate reality from emotions. Some singles walk around carrying long faces as though all of life's problems are on them. Some are distraught about delay in marriage and heart breaks but failed to recover emotionally.  *The truth is that no one wants to marry an emotional wreck.*  Brighten up and find healing. *If you're wounded emotionally, heal before entering into marriage. Wounded singles may end up hurting their partners in marriage.* Some marriages are facing big storms today because one of the parties brought in emotional baggage of unhealed past either because of rape, molestation, torturous upbringing or heartbreaks. They live with someone that loves them but do not know how to receive love. A lot of couples are like this. *Marriage is for those that are whole emotionally*.


Sometime ago, it was reported that a father beat his child to death. Anger caused it. If you don't know how to apply brakes to your anger or feelings, you'll be a bad parent. Parenting requires emotional stability.  *The first investment into your emotional life is the word of God. Let it saturate your life and be your manual for living*. Colossians 3:16 says, *"Let the message about Christ, in all its richness, fill your lives. Teach and counsel each other with all the wisdom he gives. Sing psalms and hymns and spiritual songs to God with thankful hearts.*" You cannot be dosing heavily on the word of God and not experience a change. Most people are looking for quick fixes through counselors. They fail to realize that transformation takes a process. It is never sudden. Another thing you can do is to learn the art of patience before reacting to issues. Learn to pause or ask for a time out before reacting to issues. Don't let feelings rule over thought neither should it becloud your sense of judgement. Start living your life this way and let it be part of you. It has benefit for life and marriage.


*PRAYER*: Father, I receive grace to be strong emotionally in Jesus name.

Wednesday
July 27th, 2022.

*HOLISTIC PREPARATION FOR LIFE& MARRIAGE* Pt.6


Proverbs18:1 Unfriendly people care only about themselves; they lash out at common sense. NLT

*YOUR SOCIAL LIFE*


One of my house fellowship teachers those days told us about some believers from a particular denomination who do not relate cordially with neighbours in their multi tenanted houses. They took the matter of holiness to the extreme up to the point where they refused to bath in the general bathroom. Most of them bath in their rooms and pour the water outside. They believed sharing thesame bathroom with sinners will contaminate them. They wear a long look and carry pious faces around. Smiling is a problem. Their social life was poor. *Interpersonal relationship defines social life*. For a believer, our interactions with people should be within the confines of what is acceptable in scriptures. God's prescription for a good scoial life in Hebrews 12:14 is: *"Work at living in peace with everyone,..*". When we talk about social life, what comes to the mind of some  is involvement in entertainment and wild parties. However, the simplest way to understanding social life is by linking it to the actions that a human being carries out in contact with other people.


We live in the world though we are not of the world yet we cannot but have contact with people around us. *God expect us to have contact without contamination*. As a single willing to be married, you must develop the social aspect of your life in way that is biblical and beneficial to humanity. *Those who don't know how to get along with people may find it hard getting along with their spouses in marriage*. There are single folks that are known trouble makers in their social circle. I'm wary of people who always have problem with people. Everywhere they get to even in church, they're known for having issue with the brethren. Their behaviour is anti social. In their community, they don't greet neither do they show courtesy and respect. They don't have good friends because they're social persona non grata. Even their immediate family members have turned them to prayer projects. It is impossible for people with such antecedents to have a blissful marriage. People bring who they are into their marriage. *The marital breakthrough some are praying for is tied to a viable social life*. A sister whose reputation is that of a recluse, doesn't talk or greet people, poor dress sense and bad body odour or a brother who doesn't smile nor treat people well, anti social in behaviour,  such persons may keep praying for marital breakthrough.


You must invest in this part of your life before saying "Yes I do". You can improve your social life by realizing that you'll always need people at every stage of your life. Yes you will. *There's a Zulu proverb that says, "Umuntu Ngumuntu Ngabantu" meaning "We are people through other persons. It expresses humanity. Who we are at every point in time is a function of our relationship with others*. A healthy social life is a huge capital to achieving many things in life.  Even Jesus, the incarnate son of God couldn't fulfill his assignment on earth without humans. He had a social circle of 12 disciples.  In marriage, your social circle will widen to include your spouse, in-laws and friends. Some are having serious problems with their in-laws not because their in-laws are bad but because of their deficiency in social skills. You need to learn how to care, love and bear with people without comprising your faith.


*PRAYER*: Father, I receive grace to develop a healthy social life patterned after Christ in Jesus name.

*HOLISTIC PREPARATION FOR LIFE& MARRIAGE* Pt.7


Esther 2:3 Let the king appoint agents in each province to bring these beautiful young women into the royal harem at the fortress of Susa. Hegai, the king’s eunuch in charge of the harem, will see that they are all given beauty treatments. NLT


*YOUR HEALTH AND OUTLOOK*


Sometimes ago, we mobilized for an outreach in Taraba state among the Kona people. When the time came for me to travel, I didn't take good care of my health that period so I couldn't go. I sent a brother to represent me. I don't know if anything meaningful can be achieved in life without good health. When King Xerxes wanted to replace queen Vashti, they assembled virgins for care and beautification. They assigned someone to take care of their looks. After several months of special bodily care, a day was set aside for choosing a beautiful maiden. Esther, God's daughter, was chosen. She was good looking. Though God's favour was on her but she was subjected to bodily treatment so as to make her presentable. Her appearance was important because a queen must be good looking and healthy. As a single, as you're investing in your spiritual life and other areas of your life, do not abandon your health and outlook.  I am of the opinion that good health and outlook plays an important role before and after marriage.


In as much as we encourage people to be spiritual, sometimes, the first attraction comes from the outlook. Do not say, "Physical appearance doesn't matter, only my spiritual life does", and all you do is fast and pray for a spouse.  Except we want to live in denial of the truth, everyone loves beauty. We all love something good, however, beauty lies in the eyes of the beholder. Don't look like your grandmother in your 20s and 30s. I have seen some  grandmas and grandpas with good dress sense.  *Learn good grooming and proper colour combination so that you don't look like a clown.* You don't need to buy the most expensive dress to look good. Spirituality shouldn't be a cover up for bad dress sense and body odour. Some folks won't brush their  teeth during fasting thinking they're preserving the anointing. You can be decent in your dressing and outlook and still look good. Take that from me, i'm in no way promoting indecency. You can look good and be decent.


In the past few years, I've been close to nutritionists and those that know about wellness through nutrition. I've been learning from them. You need to develop good eating habits and watch your weight. This is crucial. As a single, you should not be mistaken for a mother of three or a father of four because of obesity. If you can't watch it now, it maybe hard in marriage. Every man loves to see his wife still maintain a kind of youthful outlook and women also love to see their husbands look like the young men that they were during courtship. Attraction is a great factor in marriage. It must be in place before and after marriage. *You're not being carnal by taking care of your body, it shows you treasure the gift God gave you.* Exercise regularly and register in the gym if you have the means. If you're conscious of your health and outlook as a single, it would help you in marriage.


*PRAYER*: Father, I receive the grace to take care of my health and outlook in Jesus name.

*MARRIAGE IS GOD'S IDEA NOT OURS*


Genesis 2:18  The Lord God said, “It is not good for the man to be alone. I will make a helper suitable for him.” NIV


Someone once asked which law governs the different types of marriages that we have. I fumbled in my answer because I approached the question from a standpoint different from what the bible says. I realized this much later after the person who asked had left. I was too liberal in my answer. *Now, whether customary, registry or marriage conducted in church, as long as there's proper consent between the two families, God recognizes such marriage and He expect such marriage to run on His terms not ours*. Marriage is God's idea regardless of the type of marriage. One of the greatest institutions in the world today is marriage. If I say it is the greatest institution, I will not be wrong. Marriage has drawn the attention of all within and outside the church. As a preacher, i noticed that I get more responses when i preach on marriage than other subjects of the bible. Though I still see it as a form of misplaced priority spiritually for people to focus on marriage only instead of developing a total life that will in turn affect your marriage positively. *What we need most either single/ married is a continuous thorough Christian discipleship experience that will in turn shape every aspect of our lives- marriage inclusive*.


Most people enter into marriage with ideas that are alien to the bible because they don't know who instituted marriage and why He instituted it. Marriage was not instituted by your village chiefs nor your government. *Marriage is a sacred and divine institution founded by God so that couples can fulfill His purposes here on earth. The origin and originator of a thing is important If we are to have a good grasp of that thing. The founder of every institution has a vision and purpose. *If we must succeed in marriage, there's a need to see marriage from God's perspective or else we'll fail*.  Most marriages are troubled and failing because there's a deviation from the laid down pattern of love, submission and understanding.  Some are running their marriages with a customary pattern while others are using a legal/ modern template instead of using a biblical template. *As long as our generation tries to run  marriage without following the pattern of the originator, most marriages will hit the rocks.*


*The best marriage manual still remains the bible.* That's where we can find God's pattern for a successful marriage. Today, most people want to hear messages like: ' Seven Keys To  Marital Bliss'. They want quick fixes but not the bible. On the pages of the scriptures, you'll see God's pattern for marriage. When I wanted to marry, I read alot about marriage and the strange woman from the book of Proverbs. I'm yet to see a book on marriage, fidelity, infidelity, that is explicit like the book of Proverbs. To learn about marriage, social media is not always the right medium of learning. Alot of anti biblical ideas are being peddled on several social media platforms. You'll meet some wannabe counselors on social media who may counsel that you divorce your spouse while they're keeping theirs yet the same challenge exist in their marriages but they're managing it. That is not to say you'll not find helpful tips on social media sometimes through godly counselors. However, the first place to turn for learning is the scriptures. It is our manual for living. Don't run your marriage according to the dictates of the society, public opinion or your culture, search the manual- the bible.


PRAYER: Father, I shall not fail maritally In Jesus name.

*MARRIAGE AND PURPOSE MUST ALIGN*


Genesis2:18 The LORD God said, “It is not good for the man to be alone. I will make a helper suitable for him.”NIV



One of the things we rarely hear people talk about when it is time for marriage is purpose. Often, adults encourage mature singles to marry because of age or the proverbial biological clock that is ticking.  Mothers often put pressure on their sons or daughters for marriage because they need grandchildren. It is uncommon to see someone who raises the question of purpose with singles when it comes to marriage. Myles Munroe said that when the purpose of a thing is not known, abuse is inevitable. There are couples that entered into marriage without knowing the purpose for which they are in marriage. They have abused their marriages and they've been abused as well. There are general/ common purposes for marriage. During wedding ceremonies, you'll hear the Pastor read out some of the purposes for marriage which include: companionship, to avoid immorality&procreation. These are the general purposes for marriage.



However, there's a specific purpose for the union of two in marriage. Everyone on the surface of the earth came to this world for a purpose.  *Your purpose is your God-ordained assignment.* It is the reason God is keeping you alive, your purpose is the reason you've gone through all the dealings( sweet or bitter). *Your purpose is the impact God has designed you to make. You can only leave your footprint in the sand of times when you discover and fulfill your purpose*. When the time to marry comes, God's grand design is to lead you to marry someone whose purpose in life will boost and catalyse yours and vice versa. *Your union is to amplify the assignment and purpose you both came to the world to fulfill and not to destroy it.* The assignment  of some folks halted or paused after marriage because they married wrongly. They got on the wrong ship and they had to drag along in life. Kenneth E. Hagin of blessed memory revealed that his wife's assignment in his life was to raise a godly generation of the Hagins even to the fourth generation. That's exactly what she did before she died. All the children she raised are in ministry today.  She wasn't dragging microphone with her husband. Her purpose in their marriage wasn't on the pulpit but to raise children who will have pulpit ministry. That's not as easy as you think. She literally trained ministers.



*One is meant to chase a thousand and two ten thousands. That's the power of a purposeful union.* Unfortunately, not all two's are chasing ten thousand. If one was chasing a thousand before marriage and two can barely chase hundred after marriage, there's a problem somewhere. *I tell singles that spouse discovery is easy once purpose is discovered*. There's nothing as sweet as having a spouse who support and catalyses your journey in life borne out of the understanding of God's purpose for your life. *When you pray for a spouse, what God gives is a purpose mate.* If the person refuses, God would shop for another person who can fit into your life's assignment. *As far as God is concerned, the assignment doesn't change but men& women who will fulfill the assignment can be changed*. That's how I answer singles who ask questions like "What if God's will for my life refuses to marry me" OR "Does God have one specific spouse for my life"? *If there's anything God guard jealously in our lives, it is our assignment here on earth.* Long before I got to the junction called marriage, I knew I was heading for ministry. Knowing I have the call of God upon my life streamlined my choice. Even though I didn't know whom I was to marry immediately after my discovery, however I knew whom I shouldn't marry. Knowing your purpose in life will help you out of certain confusions. Life is a journey. Once you know your destination, you'll know the right park to board the vehicle that leads to your destination.  As a child of God, you can't marry indiscriminately. Knowing God's purpose for your life will sieve some choices out of your life.  God's kingdom want to profit from our marriages. He wouldn't allow his children to marry anyone that would destroy his investment. *Discover your purpose in life first, then spouse discovery becomes easy. Purpose discovery should lead to spouse discovery*.


*ACTION POINT*: Singles, don't enter into marriage until you settle the question of life's purpose.

*DIVORCE IS NOT GOD'S ORIGINAL PLAN FOR MARRIAGE*


1Cor7:27 If you have a wife, do not seek to end the marriage. If you do not have a wife, do not seek to get married. 28 But if you do get married, it is not a sin. And if a young


We know of natural disasters and we think it is the worst disasters that ever happened to humanity. In my own view, divorce or marital disaster is the worst disaster on earth. One of the worst disaster the world is experiencing is not earthquake neither is it tsunami. It is the tsunami of divorce. Globally, it has assumed a new dimension. Some 2.2 million marriages and almost 1 million divorces took place in the EU in 2016 according to a report. According to the UN, the country with the highest divorce rate in the world is the Maldives with 10.97 divorces per 1,000 inhabitants per year. As at February 28, 2020, it was reported in one of the media outlets that between January and February, a total of 4, 000 divorce applications were reportedly filed before various courts in the Federal Capital Territory (FCT), Abuja- Nigeria. We may not have the divorce statistics of others African countries but we know the story is almost thesame everywhere.  A woman once told me to pray God so that her divorce process in court would hasten. That's how bad it has become in today's world.



I once saw on the internet ladies celebrating a divorce party with a friend. They celebrated a 'disaster' without shame. Men and women have sunk so low to disparage and desecrate this holy institution. *Some singles are afraid to marry because of what they've seen in the marriages of those they look up to. More so, some are entering into marriage with this idea of: 'If it doesn't work out, i'll walk out'. That's an anti-bible mentality*. Divorce is against the original plan of God for the marriage union.  Even Jesus made it clear to the Pharisees that divorce wasn't God's original plan from the beginning when the Pharisees asked if a man could put away his wife away for any cause: *"The Pharisees also came unto him, tempting him, and saying unto him, Is it lawful for a man to put away his wife for every cause?...He saith unto them, Moses because of the hardness of your hearts suffered you to put away your wives: BUT FROM THE BEGINNING IT WAS NOT SO.*"( Mt 19:3‭, ‬8 KJV). Divorce is a deviation from God's original plan for marriage.


*Marriage is not a western idea where people opt in and out any how. God instituted it and he expect that each union should last till death part the couples.* I understand that there are reasons people give or even adduce to scriptures as valid grounds for divorce but Christ teaches forgiveness and patience. The negative impact of divorce can be generational. I have seen this happen to children and even adults of over 40&50 years who are still bitter about their parent's divorce while they were young. The bitter memory lingers.  The kind of trauma and pain children from divorced parents go through is such that may not heal in a life time. This I know from children who are from divorced homes. Some scholars opined that children from divorced homes are likely to divorce their spouse. In reality, I've found this to be true in about 90% of broken marriages that I know. When children are raised in turbulent homes where the father or mother walks out at will, they may grow up thinking it is the right thing to do when things aren't working. A generation must break that negative trend. Are you considering a divorce? Be patient with God to resolve all issues threatening the peace of your home. Decide to stay in your marriage and make it work as long as your life is not under threat. There is no problem without a solution. God can restore the joy of your marriage.


PRAYER: Father, we pray that you restore broken marriages and help marriages on the brink of divorce In Jesus name.

*MARRIAGE IS BETWEEN ADAM AND EVE NOT ADAM AND STEVE*


1Cor6:9-10 Or do you not know that the unrighteous will not inherit the kingdom of God? Do not be deceived: neither the sexually immoral, nor idolaters, nor adulterers, nor men who practice homosexuality, nor thieves, nor the greedy, nor drunkards, nor revilers, nor swindlers will inherit the kingdom of God.


Years back, a sister told me that I should watch what will happen to the world in the coming years about the issue of recognition and acceptance of homosexual practice. She predicted that the enemy will sell the idea of accepting Gays&Lesbians through the media repeatedly until churches and society see nothing wrong in same-sex union. Gradually, not up to a decade after that discussion, nations and peoples that once saw same sex union as ungodly are beginning to accept it claiming some were created by God for homosexual union. The unfortunate phrase they use "sexual orientation." They assert that some persons are created that way. Unfortunately, some persons I know are now sharing this view. The devil is a smart devil. I was discussing with a brother sometime ago about this same issue. In our discussion, he sounded a little bit apologetic towards Lesbian Gay Bisexual Transgender& Queer rights. He said  there is an argument that most homosexuals are created by God to have such feelings so it is their natural frame/ sexual orientation. Quickly, I refuted that claim. God didn't create anyone with a sexual orientation for same-sex feelings or union. It is purely demonic. This is what the bible says, *“Do not practice homosexuality, having sex with another man as with a woman. It is a detestable sin*"(Leviticus 18:22 NLT). Reading further in Leviticus 20:13, homosexual union was punishable by death: *"If a man violates his father by having sex with one of his father’s wives, both the man and the woman must be put to death, for they are guilty of a capital offense. “If a man practices homosexuality, having sex with another man as with a woman, both men have committed a detestable act. They must both be put to death, for they are guilty of a capital offense*." This shows the extent to which God hates this sin.


Pauline revelation in the new testament confirms  homosexual union as a sin that made God abandon those practicing it. Romans 1:26&27, speaking about homosexuals say, *"THAT IS WHY GOD ABANDONED THEM TO THEIR SHAMEFUL DESIRES. Even the women turned against the natural way to have sex and instead indulged in sex with each other. And the men, instead of having normal sexual relations with women, burned with lust for each other. Men did shameful things with other men, and as a result of this sin, they suffered within themselves the penalty they deserved*." When we call an act a perversion, it means it is a deviation from the norm. Right from the old testament, God frowned at the practice of homosexuality and same sex marriage. In fact, it was an abomination before God and it still remain so,  Lev18:22,20:13. Going further into the new testament, Jude 7 expressly told us that one of the reasons why Sodom and Gomorrha was destroyed was because of this issue: *"And don’t forget Sodom and Gomorrah and their neighboring towns, which were filled with immorality and every kind of sexual perversion. Those cities were destroyed by fire and serve as a warning of the eternal fire of God’s judgment*." In Greek, the phrase translated as sexual perversion literally mean 'Unnatural sexual intercourse' like homosexual intercourse. Even in the ancient world, it was regarded as unnatural.


*If the generation before us did not receive the endorsement of God for same-sex marriage but a fierce judgement, do you think ours would be exempted*? I don't know many things about marriage but the little I know is from the scriptures. I didn't see homosexual couples/ gay christians in my bible neither did I see anyone under the old covenant who professed to be practicing homosexual and still partook in temple worship. It is paradoxical to be a Christian and homosexual at the same time. *God instituted marriage as a union between heterosexuals not homosexuals*. It must be between a man and woman; there's a high manipulation of scriptures going on among same-sex marriage apologists. They've twisted scriptures to support their perversion. They have their bible scholars and exegetes. I'm fully aware of this. However, the foundation of the Lord stands sure.  *When God wanted to give Adam a help mate, he would have given him 'Steve'( a male) rather he gave him 'Eve'( a female)*. No matter how homosexuals try to paint their perversion with rainbow colours, I stand with the bible on thIs matter. Liberals are saying we should promote a society where people are free to do as they like( same-sex marriage inclusive) as long as it doesn't hurt anyone. However, it hurts God deeply even when Christians see nothing wrong in it. He feels so bad about it. God is saying His judgement is not only for those that practice LGBTQ but also for those that support them: *"who knowing the judgment of God, that they which commit such things are worthy of death, not only do the same, but have pleasure in them that do them*"(Romans 1:32 KJV).


*ACTION POINT*: If you're having erotic feelings towards persons of the same sex, it is unnatural. You need to renew your mind with the word of God, undergo deliverance& thorough biblical counseling.

8/2/22, 7:58 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: ProjectSave Devotional
Tuesday
August 2nd, 2022.

*LOVE ATTRACTS BUT UNDERSTANDING SUSTAINS MARRIAGE*


1 Pet3:7 In the same way, you husbands, live with your wives in an understanding way [with great gentleness and tact, and with an intelligent regard for the marriage relationship].... AMP.


One of the surprises I've seen under heavens are divorced or separated couples who claim to love each other. It beats my imagination that couples claim to be in love yet their marriages are stormy. Anytime I read news either online or in print media, at least i'll see a case of divorce once in two weeks. I have taken my time to analyse critically what went wrong in most of those marriages. As an aside, i have observed a trend among some divorcees, whenever they ask them if they're still in love with their estranged spouses. Quite a number claimed they still loved their ex  but they can't live together because of irreconciliable differences. I know you've heard that statement too. If a man is professing to still love a woman he has divorced, what could be wrong? All over the world, irreconcilable differences seems to be the major reason for separation and divorce.


*I concluded that lack of understanding contributed to the failure of most marriages not lack of love. Love can attract but understanding sustains*. We have seen love birds who professed undying love for each other but ended their marriage bitterly. When you talk to some divorcees, they may speak bitterly about their estranged spouses but deep within, the affection is still there. In fact, some still see each other secretly or "accidentally" find themselves making love when alone by accident yet they find it hard to live together. Misunderstanding in marriage results from 'missing understanding' about your partner. At the root of every misunderstanding is a missing understanding. Once proper understanding is in place, the marriage remains. *Understanding your spouse and accepting that you can't change certain things will help you to avoid misunderstandings*. In marriage, you don't use the template of another couple to run your home neither do you relate with your spouse like you do to everyone. Understanding that there are individual differences and learning how to cope with the individual differences of your partner will help you against the evil conclusion called irreconcilable differences. *Communication is a major pathway to understanding. Couples who communicate rarely have misunderstanding. The more they communicate, the better they understand each other.


Sometimes, you may need to study or observe to understand your spouse. There are things mouth can't tell yet you can only learn by observation and adjusting accordingly. Husbands, do your best to understand your wife so that you can live in harmony with her. Same applies to wives. The way Mr 'A' is loving and living with his wife may not work for you because Mrs 'A' is different from your wife. *As you love, seek to understand.* Don't complain that your wife talks too much, generally women are wired to talk. Just be a good listener. Don't complain about your husband's love for sport, be part of it. If he's a Chelsea fan, be an Arsenal fan, watching football together can be romantic. Change the things you can change but don't be mad about those things you can't change. You are not a changer. Those who told you marriage is all about enjoyment from A-Z told you half truth. There's enjoyment and endurance in marriage. Don't forget that endurance is one of the fruit of the Spirit. It is a Christian virtue we must cultivate if our marriages will work. I'll only counsel against enduring a spouse when life is threatened. *Aside that, enjoy each other's perfections and celebrate it and endure the imperfections you can't change*. Let God handle that. Love will attract but understanding will keep your marriage intact.


PRAYER: Father, I receive the grace to love my spouse with understanding In Jesus name.

8/3/22, 5:37 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: ProjectSave Devotional
Wednesday
August 3rd, 2022.

*BE REALISTIC IN YOUR MARITAL EXPECTATIONS*


Amos3:3 Can two walk together, except they be agreed? KJV

One of my brothers in Christ told me about a lady he wanted to marry. In one of their discussions, the lady told him that her expectation in marriage was that her future husband should be able to have sex for fifteen hours.

When he heard this, he knew something was wrong somewhere. He probably thought this lady was joking but when he saw her seriousness, he perished the thought of marrying that lady.

No need to prove a point. Alot of people entered into marriage with weird and unrealistic marital expectations. Some single brothers think they'll have sex  with their wives for 365 days in a year till death part them. They think women are sperm toilets.  *Only lazy men sleep with their wives every night.*

Some ladies have read romantic novels where an unrealistic picture of marriage was painted. They fantasize and dream about their husbands acting like the man they read about. Often, reality proves them wrong and when proven wrong, conflict begins.

*What you read in romantic novels and most of the things you watch in films are make-believe, they are not real.* Some couples keep having bedroom problems not because of the unrealistic bedroom expectations.

For instance, If a woman has watched porn several times, one of the things she'll notice is how most of those porn actors last long on a woman but may fail to realize that most men that act porn are under the influence of drugs and that the film trick of replaying a scene as though it lasted for hours was employed by these porn film makers to make the man look strong whereas it is not so in reality. You didn't marry a porn and marriage isn't about sex only. Don't forget that.

Same applies to men who have unrealistic sexual expectations from their wives. As funny as it sound, foolish unrealistic expectations on bedroom matters has broken many homes.

No thanks to local aphrodisiac sellers in my country. Almost every local herb or drink is touted a sexual performance enhancer. The marketers will say all manners of sweet nonsense making you feel if you can't last one hour per round, you're lazy. Hey! That's abnormal.

As funny as it sounds, some are fighting marital battles triggered by this funny unrealistic expectations.

It is not only in bedroom matters people have unrealistic expectations. Some marriage counselors often paint unrealistic picture of marriage to singles making it look like a roller coaster.

For those that are experiencing challenges in marriage, they feel like a failure especially when they hear statements like: 'I have never had any disagreement with my spouse since we married'. For me, that's very unrealistic and if true, someone is pretending in that marriage.

*Listen to this, that you're having challenges agreeing on issues and understanding each other in some areas doesn't mean you've failed in marriage. Disagreement is normal in every human relationship. However, inability to resolve those challenges amicably and allowing it to fester for long is where you can be adjudged a failure.*


*If you enter into marriage with unrealistic assumptions, you'll be disappointed when certain realities hit you.*

That doesn't mean you should be expecting the worse but what i'm saying is that you should be realistic with your expectation as much as possible. It would help you to cope and adjust properly. Having realistic expectations doesn't connote negativity.

One of the reasons some marriages are boiling is because of unfulfilled expectations.

For example, thinking your spouse won't offend you at least once in a life time is very unrealistic. You did not marry an angel. Even if you do, because you're human, you'll need to learn how to cope with an angel in marriage because they have wings but you don't. The process of learning to cope will surely bring some issues.

*I make bold to say there's no perfect marriage but there can only be an ideal marriage.* A marriage where Christ is the Head through the man and where the wife is submissive with godly children inclusive.

*Conflict of ideas and opinion may arise in marriage but there's no need to engage in combat.* To make your marriage work, disagree without being disagreeable.


*ACTION POINT:* Look out for those unrealistic expectations and discard them. Step into reality and develop adaptation strategies.

August 4th, 2022.


*SOWING INTO YOUR MARRIAGE*


Gal6:7 Be not deceived; God is not mocked: for whatsoever a man soweth, that shall he also reap. KJV


Some men are wondering why their wives are always reacting and nagging. Some women are also concerned about the funny attitudes of their husbands.

While there's no justification for wrong doing whatsoever, some persons are only reaping the reward of their actions. There's a way you'll handle a woman and she'll give you her best and vice versa.

I have been to the prayer mountain several times and I can tell you that some men on the  prayer mountain are praying death for their wives because they feel she's a witch. This I know firsthand from interactions. When they return home, the wife they wished death for may even be the first to welcome them.

I've taken my time to examine this funny trend and I concluded that most of those women are not witches but these men pushed their wives to the wall through their uncaring and unloving attitude. It is natural for a woman to react funnily when her mind isn't at rest in her marriage.

The threshold of endurance only  differ from woman to woman regardless of how spiritual they are.  How I wish I'm privileged to preach to some of those men praying death for their wives, I'll tell them to go sow the seed of love into their marriages.


In computer programming, there's an acronym known as 'GIGO' which means garbage in garbage out. What they're saying is that a computer will only give back what has been programmed into it.

In agriculture, under normal circumstances, whatsoever you sow is what you'll reap. The principles shared applies to marriage. *You cannot get from your marriage what you have not invested into it.* Simple!

I keep hearing about making marriages work on the lips of almost every marriage counselor. How can we make marriage work? By sowing the right seeds into our marriages. Which seeds are we talking about here? Mango, corn, orange or apple seeds? Of course not. The bible specified the seeds to be sown.


To the husband, the bible commanded that you love your wife as Christ loved the church, Eph5:25. This love is unconditional. *Love is the primary need of a woman, if you give it to her without reservation, you'll get the very best from her*. Even when it seems the result is not forthcoming, keep sowing the seed of love. Don't stop. In due season, you will reap a good harvest.


To the woman, bible's prescription is submission ,( Eph 5:24). The word of God didn't attach condition to it. Feminism is trying to attach a condition to submission and sometimes they don't even regard it at all. Some don't even like to hear that word.

*From the beginning, God knew what will make marriage work because he founded the institution. No matter how we try,  the bible principle still work for all times and all ages*. Wives, sow the seed of submission and respect into your husband's lives without holding back. Keep doing it, don't stop.


That you're not getting result today doesn't mean you won't get result. I don't know of any farmer who plants a seed and expect harvest on the same day. A good farmer will continue to nurture, water and weed out unwanted plants for the seed he planted to grow. Same applies to marriage.

*Doing what the bible prescribes won't produce magical result. God is a God of process. Your duty is to keep at it until it produces result*.


CONFESSION: Father, I receive the grace to sow the right seeds into my marriage. My home is built on godly principles and my marriage is working by the power of the Spirit In Jesus name.

https://t.me/projectsaveministries

*DON'T PATRONIZE LIES AND LIARS*

1Jhn4:1-Beloved, believe not every spirit, but TRY the spirit whether they are of God...

Sometimes ago, someone narrated a revelation to me about a woman that claimed to have seen Jesus. She said Jesus told her that whenever couples are having sex, the HolySpirit will come out of them and stay away from them. This lie went viral.


She sold books and tapes of well formed lies and many bought into it. Later, I was told by a minister of the Gospel that her revelation caused a lot of problems in a church because some of the women don't want the Holy Spirit to "jump" out of them during sex so they began to deny their husbands.


We can't tell how many homes that evil revelation destroyed. That woman later recanted all she said and denied seeing a revelation. This happened years after many people have patronized her lies and bought her books and tapes.


You need to know the difference between Holy Spirit and familiar spirits. Demons also reveal things to people and it'll look real. That someone claimed to have seen Jesus or a vision of heaven/hell doesn't make him/her authentic.


Some are freaks of those spurious liars claiming to have seen what the ordinary eye can't see. I'm not saying God doesn't show people visions but running after visions and revelations isn't the right thing for a child of God to do. *Your desire should be to know more of God through the word of God. If there's anything I desire to know accurately, it is the written word*.


When someone comes telling you "thus saith the Lord", you have a biblical right not to accept what he/she is saying until it is adequately proven. Listen to John, *"Dear friends, do not believe everyone who claims to speak by the Spirit. You must test them to see if the spirit they have comes from God. For there are many false prophets in the world*".(1 John 4:1 NLT)


*The biblical litmus test for revelations is to check if it conforms to God's word and His principles. Better put, we need to check if it conforms to accurate interpretation of scriptures*.

A revelation may resemble something that is written in scriptures yet it doesn't conform properly to God's standard if interpreted accurately. A man said all of us will face the white throne judgement because he saw it in a revelation but I disagreed because the believer won't face the white throne judgement as revealed in scriptures. We'll face "BEMA" judgement.


Even though white throne judgement is found in scriptures and he saw it in his revelation, I stand with accurately interpreted scriptures on that issue.


Whatever message or revelation that leaves out the fundamentals of our faith which is salvation by grace isn't from God. Any revelation that brings us into legalistic bondage and rituals like it was in the Mosaic days isn't from God.


Christ has become the centre of our worship, whatever cannot be found in Christ and properly explained in the epistles is obviously not by the Spirit. Don't go around patronizing lies and liars.


PRAYER: I receive grace to know the accurate truth In Jesus Name.

*DON'T BOARD THE WRONG SHIP*


Jonah1:3 But Jonah got up and went in the opposite direction to get away from the LORD. He went down to the port of Joppa, where he found a ship leaving for Tarshish. He bought a ticket and went on board, hoping to escape from the LORD by sailing to Tarshish. NLT


There's a trap the enemy always set for every man on a mission to his generation. The trap is to make the person embark on a different journey/assignment from their God-given assignment. It is a trap we all must be careful about.


I met a young man after my final exams in the university. He told me about the call of God upon his life into the ministry and how he  strayed from God's call. One of the reasons he gave for not fulfilling his assignment was because he got into wrong relationships that made him stray into sin. He boarded the "ship" of relationship with the opposite sex and he got lost.

God sent Jonah to Nineveh to warn them about His impending judgement. It was an assignment aimed at forewarning so that the wise could take heed. Instead of going to Nineveh, Jonah boarded the wrong ship to Tarshish.

Some believers are still making the same mistake Jonah made. Jonah was sent to Nineveh but he boarded a ship to Tarshish. He went the opposite direction just like many have boarded the wrong ship today going in the opposite direction.

The ship of Jonah was a physical ship but in context of what is happening today, we can liken that ship to anything or opportunity that derail us from the plan and purpose of God for our lives.

The 'ship' could be relationship, scholarship, courtship, internship and others. Before you embark on any 'ship', you must be sure of God's direction for your life and board the 'ship' that will take you there.

There are hundreds of people today that have strayed. Obviously, they know they are in the wrong ship but they are finding it hard to pull out.

Dearest, there are relationships that will ruin your ministry and extinguish your fire. Pull out now.

*Failure to fulfill God's assignment for your life may lead to the destruction of those you've been sent to*.

Beware of opportunities that would stray you from purpose. *It is wise to know that all opportunities are not divine open doors. The moment you enter through some doors, you may not come out on the other side.*

They're destiny traps. It is better to disembark from that ship to save your life and the life of others.


*PRAYER*: Father, I receive grace not to board the wrong 'ship' in destiny In Jesus name.

*I BELIEVE IN MIRACLES*


Job37:5 God thundereth marvellously with his voice; great things doeth he, which we cannot comprehend.

Years back, a lady told me she hasn't visited the toilet for about two weeks. I didn't know she was a sickle cell warrior. At first, I prayed for her that she would pass faeces out of her body and God answered. Later, she told me she was a sickle cell warrior.


I told her my God can change her genotype. We agreed in prayers and I told her to go and check her genotype again. When she returned, her result read 'AA'. Miraculously, God changed her genotype from 'SS' to 'AA'. The last time I heard from her, she was doing her PhD in India and still bouncing in the Lord. I believe in miracles.


Upon hearing her testimony, another sister believed God for a change of her sister's genotype. We agreed in prayers and God  changed "SS" to "AS". That is purely an act of God that is beyond comprehension, medical science cannot explain how it happened. When the sovereignty of God is at work in a man's life, protocols are broken and rules are suspended.

Time will fail me to mention several cases of healing and miracles in our outreaches. Whatever God has done before, He can still do it again and do much more in your life.

*Now unto him that is able to do exceeding abundantly above all that we ask or think, according to the power that worketh in us, unto him be glory in the church by Christ Jesus throughout all ages, world without end. Amen* ( Ephesians 20KJV).


Your reliance should be more on God's grace and mercy to flow in the realm of 'beyond comprehension'. If you can explain it, it is not a miracle. God has designed your life to operate daily in the miraculous.


I speak into your life today, the miracles that are beyond comprehension, the acts of God that man cannot explain or understand shall happen to you this season.


PRAYER: Father, let my life be a reflection of miracles beyond human comprehension In Jesus name. Amen

*WHY DO YOU WANT TO MARRY HER*?


Isaiah48:17 This is what the LORD says—your Redeemer, the Holy One of Israel: “I am the LORD your God,who teaches you what is good for you and leads you along the paths you should follow. NLT

I've shared this experience of mine several times and I think it is still relevant for didactic purpose. When I was working on my final year research project, I was returning from the lab on a sunny day when I heard the Lord ask me a question: 'why do you want to marry sister 'X'?.

I was shocked because the question came unexpectedly. I couldn't utter a word of reply because I wanted to engage that sister because she was fair to behold, smooth skinned and I love her body structure and carriage. She's what we commonly call hour glass those days. There was no other reason for wanting to marry her other than her beauty.

Somehow, I couldn't respond because I knew where God was going on the matter so I killed the thought and stopped the idea. I couldn't reply by saying "I love her body shape". God was pointing my attention to purpose by asking that question but I couldn't define the purpose for wanting to marry her. Afterwards, God began to teach me somethings about patience and He began to show me the path that I must follow on the matter of marriage.

I thought she was good for me but God had other plans. My judgement was purely based on sight instead of insight. Scriptures says: *"There is a way which seemeth right unto a man, but the end thereof are the ways of death*"( Proverbs 14:12).


Dearest, why do you want to marry that sister or brother? Can you define the purpose of that move you're making about marriage? I won't be surprised If your judgement is based on culture, tribe, social status or looks alone. *It is dangerous to focus on container and overlook content*. The only thing some brothers see in that sister they're admiring for marriage is her body. Their judgement is based on sight not insight.


*Don't be wise in your own eyes, the day you stop playing pranks with God, you will hear Him clearly*.

*Trust in the LORD with all thine heart; And lean not unto thine own understanding. In all thy ways acknowledge him, And he shall direct thy paths*(Proverbs 3:5-6 KJV).

Get to that point where you're pliable in God's hands and don't be rigid. That decision you're about to make, let the Spirit of God teach you what is good for you and not the flesh or worldly ideas.

PRAYER: Father, I receive grace to stay sensitive to your voice and to obey In Jesus name. Amen

DEVOTIONAL 0598 | Source start line: 53948
