8/9/22, 7:37 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: ProjectSave Devotional
Tuesday
August 9th, 2022.

*WHAT IS YOUR DEFINITION OF GREATNESS*?

*Mt.20:26 Not so with you. Instead, whoever wants to become great among you must be your servant, NIV*


Several times, I've been in prayer meetings where the prayer points were centred on becoming great. In most of these prayer meetings, I noticed that most of the prayer leaders define greatness as being wealthy and popular. I've been in prayer meetings where they led us to pray to become the most prominent and wealthy in our families. Hogwash!


Coming to the ministry, I've related with much older ministers and contemporaries. In some ministers meetings, the prayer is not so different. Some ministers pray to be great in ministry from the standpoint of wealth and fame. They want to "blow" in ministry. This is one of the bye products of "propserity gospel".  It is better not to be in some circles because they'll influence you into ungodliness.


Among some young ministers, what I see is the crave to be recognized and seen as doing something worthwhile. That's why some young ministers are almost always on social media. They're trying to project themselves to their world because that's their definition of greatness.


In some cases, they exaggerate testimonies and numbers. They speak "evangelistically". They gather around themselves people who respond to the Gospel emotionally and sensationally. Not everyone who fell under their anointing was truly touched of God. Some are just emotional responses to the man of God.

You need to see some fellowship presidents on campuses having security and walking with entourage. I once had a colleague like that while on campus. In class, he was a regular guy but once we leave the classroom, seeing him in the hostel requires that you pass through protocols some of whom are ladies. Someone drives him round campus as they "paparize" him.

In almost all quarters, people are seeking greatness by creating an aura of importance around themselves. Some who don't receive up to 50 calls or counselees in a day are having P.A.just for the pride of having one.


In Matthew's Gospel, Jesus redefined greatness saying, *"Not so with you. Instead, whoever wants to become great among you must be your servant,*". The definition of Christ's greatness is not tied to fame or wealth but to service. Often, we don't see a village pastor or missionary as successful or great because he's obscure. When God calls people to minister in the village where there's no media coverage or money, we call it sacrifice but when a man's ministry is situated in the city or abroad with large crowd, wealth and media coverage, we call it "breakthrough in ministry". That shows we've been infected with the bug.


Another thing I've seen in how some preachers pursue greatness is to seek platforms through association. They want to be seen preaching or teaching in a popular program or church. They ensure they are in the clique of the who's who to get invitations. Some organize programs and invite "popular" preachers and guests God's people don't need because they want only the who's who on their platforms. These guests end up preaching conflicting doctrines leaving God's people confused.


Any minister who continues this way will corrupt his calling and compromise on doctrines. You don't force relationships, God has a way of making it come.

Early in ministry, I was like that. I wanted to link up with some big names so as to bring relevance to the work I'm doing. However, a mentor cautioned me. Thank God I heeded his counsel. When you climb your mountain and get to the top, others at the top have a way of linking with those that have reached the top.


*Our definition of greatness determines how we pursue it*. As far as God is concerned, wealth, fame, media presence and other human parameters doesn't define greatness. Faithfulness in service and a heart that is committed to Him defines greatness. Nothing more.


*PRAYER*: Father, I thank you for your word. I receive the grace to stay faithful in service to you in Jesus name.

DEVOTIONAL 0599 | Source start line: 54019

8/10/22, 7:28 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: ProjectSave Devotional
Wednesday
August 10th 2022.


*VALUE TEACHING*


Nehemiah8:8 They read from the Book of the Law of God and clearly explained the meaning of what was being read, helping the people understand each passage. NLT


Some believers disparage teaching and look down on bible teachers as boring people. They dislike when bible teachers engage in a systematic breakdown of bible doctrines. They just see it as unnecessary exercise yet some of these folks can't explain the "ABC" of the faith let alone deep concepts.


Many have ignorantly made statements like "Who exegesis help"? I don't know how a believer can grow without proper exegetical teaching of scriptures. *To preach is to proclaim but to teach is to explain. We win souls through preaching but we disciple believers through teaching( explanation).*


Unfortunately,  some pastors are in this mix. They believe their calling is enough. No need for learning under teachers where they'll get accurate explanation. Some think as long as they can preach a storm and get responses like "ride on Pastor", they don't need a structured system of learning. They talk down the idea of going to a sound seminary, training programs and exploring avenues for learning. Some won't sit down to learn except from a big name.

In my opinion, the hardest set of people to teach are preachers not church members. I've observed that Pastors that are committed to learning will raise members that are committed to doing same. Don't forget that Apollos was mighty in scriptures yet Aquila and Priscilla observed his inadequacies and they explained the scriptures to him more accurately: *"When Priscilla and Aquila heard him preaching boldly in the synagogue, they took him aside and explained the way of God even more accurately.*" ( Acts of the Apostles 18:26 NLT)

We preachers need the humility of Apollos to learn when we come across our own Aquila and Priscilla. The later weren't great preachers like Apollos neither were they mighty in scriptures like him yet they had deeper understanding. Apollos must have been a very humble preacher to learn under a couple without impressive CV.


As a bible teacher, you don't know all things. Seek to be taught. *Every teacher need a teacher. Learning from teachers should be a lifelong adventure not for a shortwhile*. The rate at which you learn should be more than the rate at which you dispense what you've learnt.


*If you want to grow, stay under anointed teachers of scriptures. Do not be a Christian that want to hear something sensational or a message that will stir your emotions*. Don't confuse bogus and ambiguous words with depth. That someone speaks rhyming words doesn't make him a teacher.

*Ability to explain scriptures and breakdown hard concepts using simple language and teaching you how to apply them to your daily Christian living is the hallmark of a bible teacher.*


Being under anointed teachers will impart your spirit-man directly and make your root grow deeper. You need a church where you'll be taught not where jamboree is exalted above the accurate explanation of God's word. As a person, I doubt If I can attend a church without Sunday school or other structure of Christian discipleship.

Subscribe to getting explanation for scriptures by sitting under the teachers God has chosen for you. Stop running around, sit down and learn. That's the only way you can grow.

*PRAYER:* Father, I receive grace to  learn scriptures In Jesus name.

*YOU NEED BROTHER JOSHUA*


*Numbers 11:17 I will come down and talk to you there. I will take some of the Spirit that is upon you, and I will put the Spirit upon them also. They will bear the burden of the people along with you, so you will not have to carry it alone*. NLT


I thank God I met brother Joshua. When I was on campus, brother Joshua, a medical student was one of the brothers I was very close to in my fellowship. He was fervent and devoted to the Lord. Till I left him in school, he was not chosen as a fellowship executive but he had a heart of service.


In my 200 level, brother Joshua decided that both of us would be praying one hour every night in front of his hostel somewhere beside the road. I did not have a sound prayer life prior to that time. I was lazy in prayer. However, brother was God's agent of revival sent to my life to stir something in me. At the beginning, I was reluctant and lazy about it because it was burdensome.


As time went on, the fire of prayer came upon me mightily. In fact, at a time, I was the one calling bro Joshua to prayers at night. On my own, I began to wake up around 4am in the morning to pray. It was as though a mantle of prayer transferred from him into my life. My hostel colleagues noticed the change because my flat vibrated as I prayed every morning.  My school father got the report and he cautioned I prayed silently. I didn't intend to disturb anyone but I got lost in prayers most times and I didn't know when I raised my voice.


Thank God I met brother Joshua Ojewunmi. Ever since, my prayer life hasn't remained the same. I can't claim to be the most prayerful man on earth but my relationship with Dr Joshua Ojewunmi brought about a shift in my prayer life. Till date, that seed is still growing. Before I left school, I engaged my immediate mentees in several prayer programs and passed the same spirit to others.


*Dearest, mentorship is about impartation. It has a lot to do with transference of mantle. It is not about dropping names like some do*. In the spirit realm, God can take the Spirit upon a man and transfer to another. This is not the same as the infamous "tapping the anointing" that many people are doing today. It has more to do with walking closely with someone who carries a mantle you desire. Certain anointings can be imparted through association.


If you're lazy in prayer, submit to someone whose prayer life is strong and follow his instructions. If your word study life is weak, prayerfully locate someone with strong and consistent bible study ethics and let the person help you build a culture of studying scriptures. Let him hold you accountable. Some of us learnt these things through people we interacted with. That's one of the offshoots of thorough discipleship.


Stop walking with folks that won't add anything to your spiritual life. Elisha received a double portion through association with Elijah: *"You have asked a difficult thing,” Elijah replied. “If you see me when I am taken from you, then you will get your request. But if not, then you won’t*.”( 2 Kings 2:10).


My own brother Joshua helped me in building a strong prayer life. Prayerfully locate your own "brother Joshua" and follow closely. *Who you follow determines what will follow you.*


*PRAYER*: Father, I receive the grace to walk with the right people in Jesus name.

DEVOTIONAL 0601 | Source start line: 54164
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
8/12/22, 7:57 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: PROJECTSAVE DEVOTIONAL
Friday,
August 12th, 2022.


*IT MUST BE FROM A WILLING HEART*


*Ex 25:2 “Speak to the children of Israel, that they bring Me an offering. FROM EVERYONE WHO GIVES IT WILLINGLY WITH HIS HEART YOU SHALL TAKE MY OFFERING*. NKJV


Kenneth E. Hagin of blessed memory wrote a powerful book titled "Midas Touch". He wrote that book to correct some of the common extremes of preachers in the Faith movement on the subject of prosperity. He himself was once a victim but he corrected himself and aligned with the word before he passed on. Till date, some of the extremes he corrected in that book are still in practice.


There are preachers who claim to have the anointing for raising millionaires and billionaires. The moment they get talking about their heroics in helping people out of poverty, the next thing that follows is fund raising. This they do by coercion and manipulation. *It is a slap on redemption for someone who claim to have millionaire and billionaires raising anointing yet cannot raise missionaries that will reach the unreached. I've read my bible from Genesis to Revelation, I didn't see anyone with that "dangerous" anointing.*


An elder shared his experience with me. He attended an evangelistic meeting where the evangelist told everyone of them in the meeting to empty their pocket. He was a young believer then, he took the last dime in his pocket and gave it as an offering under "evangelistic pressure". After the service, he trekked home because he had no transport fare on him again. Some of you have been there before. You have parted with gifts or monies that didn't come from your heart because someone put you under pressure instead of giving you biblical reasons why you should give and allowing the Holy Spirit do the work of conviction.


Even Abraham's attempt to offer Isaac as a sacrifice was willing, God saw it in Him and he was commended for his willingness. *God doesn't accept any offering that is not given willingly.* From the old testament to the new, the nature of God as regards the matter of giving is unchanging.



In Paul's  epistle to the Corinthians in 2Corinthians9:7,  Paul said, *"So let each one give as he purposes in his heart, not grudgingly or of necessity; for God loves a cheerful giver*". You may need to read that again patiently. The moment giving( any form of giving at all) comes with threats, curses, arm twisting and spurious promises of "special things" God will do when you give your money or substance, all of these are against the standard of God. *A new testament believer can never come under curse for not giving*.


*If we understand stewardship of money as new testament believers, what to give and the percentage to give will no longer be an issue to argue about*. We'll give out of understanding that we own nothing but God owns everything, we are just stewards. God doesn't accept unwilling offerings given under pressure or threat. There's no gift you want to give to God that is greater than what God gave- Christ.  Any other thing you give ranks 2nd. If giving under Moses(law) was to be done willingly, how much more under the dispensation of Christ(Grace).


As a preacher of the gospel, simply tell your congregants the purpose their giving will serve and teach them to love God, His kingdom and stewardship. If they understand these concepts, they will give willingly. *When believers grow in the knowledge of the word, giving becomes a life style because there's an understanding.  Coercion during giving  is not God's way*.


It is not on record that the apostles in the early church asked their listeners to empty their pocket or bank account neither did they resort to threat and curses. Giving was done willingly in the early church. They must have told them why they should give and the purpose it will serve.


In Acts 4:33-34, the bible says, *"With great power the apostles continued to testify to the resurrection of the Lord Jesus. And God’s grace was so powerfully at work in them all- that there were no needy persons among them. For from time to time those who owned land or houses sold them, brought the money from the sales*".


When you approach the offering plate, check your heart to be sure there's a willingness to give.


ACTION POINT: Don't give against your will, God won't accept it.

8/13/22, 7:30 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: ProjectSave Devotional

August 13th, 2022.


*CREDIT YOUR PRAYER ACCOUNT*


*Revelations 5:8 And when he took the scroll, the four living beings and the twenty-four elders fell down before the Lamb. Each one had a harp, and they held gold bowls filled with incense, which are the prayers of God’s people*. NLT


Recently, the Holy Spirit reminded me about the fulfillment of some prayers I and brother Joshua prayed back in 2006/2007.  It was a time of personal prayer revival for me.


God used brother Joshua to lead that personal revival in my life and till date, I'm still benefitting from  that revival. At that time, it seemed foolish to me that bro Joshua led me to pray about marriage, ministry, children and other issues that weren't pertinent at that time.


I expected our prayers to centre around academics and other immediate areas of concern. Though we prayed for spiritual growth, insight and other very important things too but then it sounded strange whenever he delved into issues that were in my future then. I can say that there were things we prayed about that I'm yet to see because I'm not in the season for those things yet.


However, in this present season, I can see the fulfillment of some of those prayers and I'm grateful to God that someone led me to credit my prayer account for a time I'll be needing them.


In all, I learnt a great lesson. We all have a prayer account with God. There's a prayer bank with an account that can be credited with prayers from time to time. When you save money in a bank, you're saving for the day you'll need it to meet a need. Same applies to prayer.


There are prayers that don't meet your immediate needs yet the Spirit of God might lead you to pray such prayers. You may be wondering the relevance of those prayer points  to your life in the immediate. What the Holy Spirit is doing is to help you credit your prayer bank awaiting the day you'll need it.


You can pray about the next season of your life long before you enter into it. It is not too late to pray for the next 20-30 years of your life. That's not anxiety, that's wisdom. Often, we miss God in certain areas of life because we haven't credited our prayer bank sufficiently.


*Some singles only pray about marriage a few years or months to marriage whereas they should have saturated that season of their life with prayers long before getting there*.


In the 17th chapter of John's Gospel, Jesus credited the prayer bank of the church: *"I pray that they will all be one, just as you and I are one—as you are in me, Father, and I am in you. And may they be in us so that the world will believe you sent me*"(John 17:21 NLT). You and I are beneficiaries of that prayer he prayed over 2000 thousand years ago.


Reading the annals of men that prayed, it was revealed that men like Apostle Ayo Babalola, Savonarola and the likes prayed for a time and season they knew they won't witness. They credited prayer bank of the church and the nation ahead for those that might need them. Some of us are enjoying the prayers of men who didn't know us.



*True prayer doesn't affect time only,  it affects eternity*. That's why believers of old often prayed about eternal issues and ending well in the race long before they got to the finish line. You can pray for your unborn children, your grandchildren, your old age, where you want to live, that ministry you've not started, and other issues.

*Prepare the next phase and season of your life with prayer*.


*PRAYER*: Father, I receive the grace to credit my prayer account in Jesus name. Amen

8/14/22, 6:41 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: ProjectSave Devotional
Sunday,
August 14th, 2022.


*LESSONS IN CHRISTIAN STEWARDSHIP*
PT.1


1 Corinthians 4:2 Moreover it is required in stewards, that a man be found faithful. KJV


*YOU ARE A STEWARD*


In my early days as a believer, one of the teachings we had in Believer's Class was "Stewardship". It didn't make much sense to me then but now I know better. If there's anything believers must understand about their position in Christ in this culture that makes man "The owner", I think the teaching on stewardship should be re emphasized over and over till we understand God's mind.


One of the things that has also caused argument in recent years is the subject of "Tithing". For me, it is not an issue to argue about If kingdom people understand their place as stewards. *What we give to God shouldn't be a subject of debate as long as our giving is scripturally done*. Kindly take note of "Scriptural Giving." Some give but not scripturally.


I understand there are extremes on the subject of giving just like other subjects but it is safe to stay in the middle of the lane on biblical matters. My concern is about stewardship and how it affects our approach to life.


Who is a steward? A steward is a manager of resources. Someone who holds a thing in trust for another person. *Redemption automatically makes us stewards of everything ( gifts, time, life, ministry, money, spouse, children, education, etc) God has given to us*. We own nothing, God owns everything.


*1Pet 4:10 Each of you should use whatever gift you have received to serve others, as FAITHFUL STEWARDS of God’s grace in its various forms.*


*The day you gave your life to Christ, you became a steward of your life*. You lost the right of ownership to everything because your life, money, time,etc ceases to be yours. Listen to Paul:  *"He died for everyone so that those who receive his new life will no longer live for themselves. Instead, they will live for Christ, who died and was raised for them.*"( 2 Corinthians 5:15 NLT). Did you read that?


According to brother Paul, those who receive the new life will no longer live for themselves. They'll no longer have the right of ownership. That's who we are as stewards. *We own nothing, God owns everything*.


Most of the teachings on New Creation Realities tells us so much about our supremacy over sin, sickness, Satan but only a few teaches on stewardship because this is where our commitment to God lies. Others are God's commitment to us by reason of our new life but stewardship is where our commitment to God is tested.

Let this mind be in you henceforth that you are only a steward of all that God has committed into your hands.


*PRAYER*: Father, I thank you for your word to me. I receive the grace to live as a kingdom steward in Jesus name.

8/15/22, 7:17 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: PROJECTSAVE DEVOTIONAL
Monday,
August 15th, 2022.


*LESSONS IN CHRISTIAN STEWARDSHIP* PT.2


*1 Corinthians 4:2 Moreover it is required in stewards, that a man be found faithful*. KJV


*GOD OWNS EVERYTHING YOU OWN NOTHING*.


To understand Christian stewardship, we need to see clearly from scriptures where God belongs so that we can put ourselves where we belong too. This is what the Psalmist says about God: *"THE EARTH IS THE LORD'S, AND EVERYTHING IN IT. The world and all its people belong to him*"(Psalms 24:1 NLT). God is not just a partial owner of the earth and EVERYTHING in it, He is the owner of all.


From the very beginning, God instituted the principle of stewardship by putting mankind in-charge of the earth and all that is in it. Mankind became a manager of God's creation including animals. He gave mankind dominion as a steward not as the owner. *God never relinquished the right of ownership to mankind. Never!* This eternal order is what the fall of man corrupted in the hearts of mankind.


One of the effects of the fall of man is that man wanted to be "like God"( the owner) with respect to status and ranking. *Though we are created in His image and likeness but it should be noted that equality doesn't exist between mankind& deity*. Satan deceived our progenitor in the garden saying: *“God knows that your eyes will be opened as soon as you eat it, and you will be like God, knowing both good and evil"*(Genesis 3:5 NLT) In an attempt to be "like God"( the owner of all things), mankind lost it.

Satan's attempt to usurp God's authority and power in heaven met with resistance that led to His eviction from heaven. He used Adam and Eve to further his agenda but he lost the battle again with God but he succeeded in bringing mankind under control. Now, he rules the earth indirectly through man.


According to the order laid by God, man was answerable to God as regards the stewardship of the earth and everything in it, but mankind chose a different leader, came under Satanic control and gave her report of stewardship to Satan.  Ever since, man's rebellion against his maker made him to think he owns everything. Man began to live for the very things he was put in-charge instead of living for the one who put Him(God) in- charge of those things. Satan instituted Mammon( god of wealth and material things) worship for mankind. Man lost it here.


Thank God for redemption. One of the beauties of redemption is its restorative work on mankind. In redemption, God still remains the owner, Christ is the Lord over all and man is subject to deity as stewards. A steward is answerable to his master and practically live for him. That's why we shouldn't throw away the admonition of Paul: *"He died for everyone so that those who receive his new life will no longer live for themselves. Instead, they will live for Christ, who died and was raised for them*"(2 Corinthians 5:15 NLT).


As believers, we are to live for our master who owns us and everything He put in our care. Paul says, *"But now you are free from the power of sin and have become SLAVES OF GOD.  Now you do those things that lead to holiness and result in eternal life*"(Romans 6:22 NLT). In bible times, slave owns nothing, their master owns everything. Being a "slave of God" simply connote loyalty and stewardship. It is surprising that we're not only sons of God but slaves of God.

Your time, money, intellect, children, spouse, attainments, etc aren't yours. God only put those things in your care as a steward. If we have this kingdom mentality, nothing will be hard for us to release or use when the Lord makes demand for them.



*PRAYER*: Father, give me the heart of a steward. Let my perspective about life and all that is in this world change and align with yours. Help me to see things through the length of Christian stewardship in the name of Jesus Christ.

DEVOTIONAL 0605 | Source start line: 54667

8/16/22, 7:03 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: ProjectSave Devotional
Tuesday
August 16th, 2022.

*LESSONS IN CHRISTIAN STEWARDSHIP* PT.3

*1 Corinthians 4:2 Moreover it is required in stewards, that a man be found faithful*. KJV

*STEWARDS ARE ACCOUNTABLE*.

Oftentimes, we read the parable of the talent as one of those parables of Jesus but we fail to realize that it is a parable illustrating stewardship.


In Matthew 25:14-30,  a master who was traveling entrusted valuables to his servants. According to their abilities, he gave one servant five talents, the second received two, and the third received only one. Talent in context wasn't referring to gifts or endowment. It was in the 13th century English speakers and readers interpreted the parable of talent to mean gifts or endowment.

In the New Testament, a talent was a value of money or coin. Just as dollar is a value of money in the U.S. This master gave them money to trade with. The ones that received five and two talents multiplied it but the other servant buried it. The day of accountability came. The first two were commended for multiplying their talents while the latter was rebuked and thrown into outer darkness.


The first lesson there which we have learnt previously is that there is a "Master"( God) who entrust things to his servants( we, children of God) for proper use.


*Stewards own nothing, God owns everything. God is our master, He owns all things (including silver and gold). His desire is that whatever He gives us, we use it as prescribed for His own glory.*


Secondly, the lesson of accountability is clear from that parable. *Stewards shall give account to God on how well they manage whatever is entrusted to their care*. As stewards, we shall give account to God how we live our very lives: *So then each of us will give an account of himself to God* (Romans 14:12).

There's a judgement seat of Christ where every one of us will receive the reward of our
stewardship. In 2 Corinthians 5:10, Paul says, *"For we must all appear before the judgment seat of Christ, so that each one may receive what is due for what he has done in the body, whether good or evil.*" Pay day is coming. All you're doing for the Lord and His Kingdom isn't going unnoticed, pay day will come.


One may think that Pastors and preachers are exempted from accountability. Preachers will give account to God on how well they run their ministries and discharge their duties over the flock. The writer of Hebrews(13:7) says, *"Obey your leaders and submit to them, for they are keeping watch over your souls, as those who will have to give an account. Let them do this with joy and not with groaning, for that would be of no advantage to you.*"


*As a pastor, that church is not your church, it is for Christ*. You're only a trustee who will give proper account to God. *The offerings and tithe is not yours. God's money is not for the man of God, it is for God. He determines how the man of God will use it for His kingdom*. If ministers understand this, greed will be far from them.

*Your children are not yours, they are God's.* What about your time, money and life itself? *Often times, we sing songs telling God we give our lives to him but we hold back other areas of the very life we promised to give God*. If only we realize that all aspects of our lives are under His watch awaiting the day of accountability, we'll live for Him fully.


*ACTION POINT*: And no creature is hidden from his sight, but all are naked and exposed to the eyes of him to whom we must give account (Hebrews 4:13).

DEVOTIONAL 0606 | Source start line: 54902
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
8/17/22, 9:35 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: ProjectSave Devotional
Wednesday
August 17th 2022.

*LESSONS IN CHRISTIAN STEWARDSHIP* PT.4

*1 Corinthians 4:2 Moreover it is required in stewards, that a man be found faithful*. KJV

*FAITHFULNESS IN STEWARDSHIP*.

To be faithful is to be loyal and steadfast. It is the quality of being honest and truthful. If there's one thing God requires from stewards, it is faithfulness.

*The reward of a steward won't be based on "success" but on how faithful he is to his master and to the work committed into his hands*.

In Matthew 25:21, Jesus told us about a master who commended his faithful servant:   *“His master replied, ‘Well done, good and faithful servant! You have been faithful with a few things; I will put you in charge of many things. Come and share your master’s happiness!*’

We live in a world where the drive for success and fame is on the increase. People want to "blow". Some persons don't believe you're working in the kingdom unless you're in the spotlight.

It is not that success is bad just that man's definition of success is different from God's definition of success.


God doesn't define success from the standpoint of popularity, wealth, large following ( online & offline), geographical spread and other human parameters. *Faithfulness in stewardship is God's definition of success*.

A minister of God that is called to labour in the corner of a village often is not deemed by many as having "brokenthrough" in the ministry yet the moment a preacher's face appears on the poster or billboard of a big conference, such is quickly defined as ministerial success or breakthrough.

You only need to know how many preachers are fasting and praying to travel abroad because they want to add "International" to their ministerial CV. They want to be acclaimed as having preached around the world. International open doors is not bad but it is not the focus of a faithful minister.

As a preacher, are you faithful to the doctrines of scriptures and defense of the faith? Are you feeding the flock or you're fleecing them? Hope you've not damaged the lives of sisters and women under you? What's your attitude to money? Hope you've not merchandise the gift of God in your life?

Read what Peter said, *"Each of you should use whatever gift you have received to serve others, as faithful stewards of God’s grace in its various forms.*"(1 Peter 4:10 NIV)

How often do you think  about that very day you'll stand before God to give account of all that has been entrusted into your care?

A Christian's stewardship is not temporal but for a lifetime. Our master determines how well we've discharged our duties using His parameters not ours. Let's work towards staying faithful.

*PRAYER*: Father, give me the grace to be faithful in Jesus name.

DEVOTIONAL 0607 | Source start line: 54986

8/18/22, 8:00 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: PROJECTSAVE DEVOTIONAL
Thursday,
August 18th, 2022.


*LESSONS IN CHRISTIAN STEWARDSHIP* PT.5


*1 Corinthians 4:2 Moreover it is required in stewards, that a man be found faithful*. KJV


*WHAT GOD ENTRUSTED TO US- LIFE *.


As stewards, we need to examine those things God entrusted to us. *The very first thing God entrusted to our care is our whole life.* Man has a material and immaterial part. The material part is the body while the immaterial part is known as soul or spirit.


Hebraic scholars believe man is essentially body and soul. Often, we see this expressed severally in the bible especially old testament and the Gospels. Paul futher separated the immaterial part of man to spirit and soul. Those who believe in the former and latter are saying the same thing. Man has a material and immaterial body.


In 1 Thessalonians 5:23, Paul revealed to us the tripartite nature of man saying, *"Now may the God of peace make you holy in every way, and may your whole spirit and soul and body be kept blameless until our Lord Jesus Christ comes again.*"(1 Thessalonians 5:23 NLT). Man is a spirit, with a soul residing in a body.


With our bodies, we make contact with the physical world around us. The bible calls our body the temple of the living: *"Do you not know that your bodies are temples of the Holy Spirit, who is in you, whom you have received from God? YOU ARE NOT YOUR OWN;*"(1 Corinthians 6:19). That body of yours is not yours, it is for the Lord. That's why fornication is sin against the body according to Paul.


We are stewards of our bodies and we shall give account to God on how we use it. God's expectation is that we use our bodies to do His will: *"Do not let any part of your body become an instrument of evil to serve sin...*"(Romans 6:13). Our bodies should be used as instruments of righteousness. In addition, we are meant to maintain it properly with good food, rest and exercise.


Our soul is the seat of our mind, will and emotions. This is where we think, reason and decide what to do. The body only execute what has been decided in the soulish realm. Our mind (a major component of the soul) should be subject to renewal daily through the word: *"And be not conformed to this world: but be ye transformed by the renewing of your mind, that ye may prove what is that good, and acceptable, and perfect, will of God*"(Romans 12:2).


Whatever we do with our soul shall be accounted to God because idle words which proceed from the thoughts in our minds shall be accounted for: *"And I tell you this, you must give an account on judgment day for every idle word you speak*"(Mt 12:36).


To use your soul as intended by God, adhere to this Pauline admonition: *"Let the message about Christ, in all its richness, fill your lives. Teach and counsel each other with all the wisdom he gives. Sing psalms and hymns and spiritual songs to God with thankful hearts*"( Colossians 3:16).


Our spirit is the link with divinity. When God speaks, He speaks to our spirits because He is spirit( John 4:24). We are to avoid whatever can contaminate our spirits: *"Having therefore these promises, dearly beloved, let us cleanse ourselves from all filthiness of the flesh and spirit, perfecting holiness in the fear of God*"( 2 Corinthians 7:1 KJV). Wrong knowledge especially along the lines of mysticism can corrupt the spirit.

Your whole life( spirit, soul and body) is the first thing God entrusted into your care as a steward. Take care of it because you'll account for it.


*PRAYER*: Father, I receive the grace to care for my whole life as intended as a steward in Jesus name.

*LESSONS IN CHRISTIAN STEWARDSHIP* PT6.


*1 Corinthians 4:2 Moreover it is required in stewards, that a man be found faithful*. KJV

*WHAT GOD ENTRUSTED TO US- INFLUENCE*.

A man entered my office to pick up some documents on behalf of his friend. He introduced himself and we got talking. I told him I don't know him though he was trying to describe himself properly to familiarise himself with me. He tried but I still made it clear to him that I didnt know him.


He said, "I know you. You are a public figure." I was startled. No one has ever told me I am a public figure not until that time. To a large extent, I still doubt I am. Truly, he knew so much about me than I thought anyone I don't know could ever know.


After he left, I learnt something from that experience. The number of those watching us that we don't know may be more than those we know. Also, as people watch us either closely or from afar,  we are influencing them one way or the other. We all influence thoughts and opinions.


You might not know but it is the truth. If you have children, you're already influencing them and helping them to lay the foundation for their lives. My sons mimic me sometimes even my tongues. That's influence at work.

*The way we talk, dress, react and behave is influencing someone. For those of us in ministry, we are influencers of people. Our influence is God-given. He will hold us responsible as to how we use it. We shall give an account of our stewardship in this regard someday.*


In most churches, most of the sisters dress like the senior Pastor's wife especially if she has a unique way of dressing. Some persons talk like their Pastors unconsciously and even dress like him because they've come under his influence. If they're not careful, they'll behave like him having observed him closely.


*"For we don’t live for ourselves or die for ourselves.*" (Romans 14:7 NLT)


The family is the greatest platform for influencing children and shaping young minds. The best way to do this is to be a godly model to children. Don't surrender this aspect to celebrities and worldly media which has negative influence on today's children.


If you're a school teacher, you're an influencer. Sometimes, children believe whatever their teachers tell them even if their teachers are wrong. Some of them will say, "My aunty said...".


*As a kingdom steward, use the power of influence positively to make people see what is right and to make them do it. Ultimately, use the power of influence to point people to Jesus Christ in every situation and when opportunities present itself.*


*PRAYER*: Father help me to influence people rightly in Jesus name.

*LESSONS IN CHRISTIAN STEWARDSHIP* PT.7


*1 Corinthians 4:2 Moreover it is required in stewards, that a man be found faithful*. KJV

*WHAT GOD ENTRUSTED TO US- CHILDREN*.


Sometime ago, I saw the video of a young mother who vibed to a music in the presence of her son. What I can't tell is if it was in a club or party. Her dressing and appearance wasn't modest. Her son of less than 5 years began to drink alcohol in her presence and she didn't do anything about it. She kept dancing.  This was on camera.


There are several cases of negligent parents who refused to inculcate the right values into their children. They themselves are bad models and they're raising another crook under their roof. Unfortunately, we have many adults that still need proper parenting. I've seen mothers who need good mothers and father who need good fathers.


Stewardship is not complete If we neglect the stewardship of our children. We shall give account to God because those children aren't ours, they are for the Lord: *"Children are a gift from the LORD; they are a reward from him.*" Psalm 127:3 NLT


You are not the owner of that child. The primary purpose in the heart of God for giving you that child or children is to raise, train and nurture them unto godliness: *"Didn’t the LORD make you one with your wife? In body and spirit you are his. AND WHAT DOES HE WANT? GODLY CHILDREN FROM YOUR UNION. So guard your heart; remain loyal to the wife of your youth*"(Malachi 2:15). Godly children- that's what God wants from our marriages.

We need to pay attention to the kind of children we are raising under our roofs. The mass media, music and entertainment industry is influencing them greatly and we must watch it.


*Don't surrender to mass media, environment, music and pop culture to raise your children. You must be intentional about raising your children in the way of the Lord.*


Godly seeds are raised through godly instructions. Abraham modeled this in the bible. Speaking about him, God said, *"For I know him, that he will command his children and his household after him, and they shall keep the way of the LORD, to do justice and judgment; that the LORD may bring upon Abraham that which he hath spoken of him*"(Genesis 18:19 KJV). Find time to teach them bible.


*Teaching them is not enough, you should also model what you teach. In my opinion, children learn more by observation than through words. They pattern their lives after the adult they see often. That's why they must be around the right set of people*.


Their opinion about life and its facet is not taught in school, they learn it at home by watching their parents. If you're a daddy that beats mummy, they may beat their wives in future. The tendency is high. If you're a nagging mummy, your daughters have very high tendency to do same when they're married. *Most times, what people do as adults are things they saw others( especially their parents) do in their formative years.* The wrong habit or character only manifested at an opportune time in adulthood.


What are you modeling for the heritage of the Lord in your care? If we want to raise wholesome children, we must live wholesome lives. Like begets like.

*PRAYER*: Father, I will not in my stewardship towards the children you've given to me in Jesus name.

*LESSONS IN CHRISTIAN STEWARDSHIP* PT.8


*1 Corinthians 4:2 Moreover it is required in stewards, that a man be found faithful*. KJV


*WHAT GOD ENTRUSTED TO US- GIFTS*.

I once received an invitation to preach several years ago. I was asked what my price was. I replied by telling the person who called that I don't charge to preach. I'm a steward of whatever grace or gift I carry. It is not something to be monetized but to be used for God's glory.

Many believers are walking on slippery grounds in matters of gifts, talents and endowment. Some are putting a price tag on what they received freely from God. Jesus said, *"Heal the sick, raise the dead, cure those with leprosy, and cast out demons. Give as freely as you have received!*"( Matthew 10:8).

Your gifts, talents, skills are tools for kingdom advancement. Bezaleel was endowed with wisdom in all crafts. God entrusted him with that special ability.

*“Look, I have specifically chosen Bezalel son of Uri, grandson of Hur, of the tribe of Judah. I have filled him with the Spirit of God, giving him great wisdom, ability, and expertise in all kinds of crafts. He is a master craftsman, expert in working with gold, silver, and bronze. He is skilled in engraving and mounting gemstones and in carving wood. He is a master at every craft!* Exodus 31:2‭-‬5 NLT

In subsequent verses, God made it clear to Moses that the purpose of that endowment upon Bezaleel and Oholiab was for the work( building) of the tabernacle, ark of covenant, utensils and other sacred items.

Why will you be so gifted and yet warm benches in church from Sunday to Sunday? It is a waste of endowments to look on while others are using theirs for God's glory. Don't let the devil hijack your gift for his use.

It is common knowledge that most entertainers started in church but left because they feel unappreciated or unrecognised. Flimsy excuse. *We are in kingdom service for an audience of one not for the audience of the Pastor or church people.*

*It is poor understanding of stewardship of gifts that makes people abandon the use of their gifts in God's kingdom and switch their allegiance.*

A steward will not seek the "appreciation" or recognition of Pastor or church folks. They're humans who will gift an account of their stewardship as well.  You're playing for an audience of one not man.

Fight pride and that feeling of "I've arrived" especially when people begin to compliment you for your gifts. If not careful, instead of using the gift of God for  kingdom service and to bring glory to God, it may become a tool for self glorification.

We are entrusted with gifts, talents and divine endowments. As trustees, we shall give God full account some day.


*PRAYER*: Father, I will not abuse the gifts you gave me in Jesus name

8/22/22, 7:19 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: PROJECTSAVE DEVOTIONAL

*LESSONS IN CHRISTIAN STEWARDSHIP* PT.9


*1 Corinthians 4:2 Moreover it is required in stewards, that a man be found faithful*. KJV

*WHAT GOD ENTRUSTED TO US- THE GOSPEL*.

Spreading the goodnews of the kingdom is a trust given to every child of God not a few. Often, we think only Evangelists and missionaries are saddled with the responsibility of preaching the Gospel whereas it is an assignment for all believers regardless of status, age, education and experience.


God didn't classify some of us as "Goers" because everyone should go to where sinners are. Though we are also meant to be "Givers" but not at the neglect of preaching to those around us and living the Gospel. As you give to support those who are going to places where you may never go to preach the Gospel, you have a duty to preach to those around you. It is a trust.


The "Go Ye" command was the last command of Christ in the bible. His last command should be our very first priority as believers and corporately as a church because God gave it to us as a trust.


Paul said: *"For we speak as messengers approved by God to be entrusted with the Good News. Our purpose is to please God, not people. He alone examines the motives of our hearts*" ( 1 Thessalonians 2:4 NLT).


*Thousands cross into Christless eternity on daily basis without hearing about Jesus*. All around us, sinners are crossing into eternity in droves. Some sinners who have crossed into dark side of eternity may blame us for not telling them they could be redeemed from eternal hell. *The message about eternal life is too valuable to be kept to ourselves. Don't keep your mouth shut, spread it.*


When I get to heaven, I want to see many souls who came to Christ on account of my obedience to the Great Commission task. You cannot afford to sleep while millions are going to hell. While on campus, as led by the Spirit,  I once preached in a hostel block where someone had less than 5 months to live. The young man fought me for preaching. He didn't give his life to Christ.  Painfully, he died, trapped in a burning house as I was told.

*If we know the terrible judgement that await sinners, we will preach the Gospel as though our very lives depend on it.*

Further, we are not only to preach the Gospel, we are meant to live it. A man may preach the Gospel by word of the mouth but may not live what he is preaching. One of the most effective ways to preach the Gospel to friends, families and neighbours is for them to see you living a life that is worthy of the kingdom.

Living out the Gospel is a trust. Someday, we shall give account to God.

*PRAYER*: Father, I receive the grace to preach the Gospel and to live it in Jesus name.


====================================================================================================
DEVOTIONAL 0612 | Source start line: 55547
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
8/23/22, 7:30 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: ProjectSave Devotional
Tuesday
August 23rd, 2022.

*LESSONS IN CHRISTIAN STEWARDSHIP* PT.10

*1 Corinthians 4:2 Moreover it is required in stewards, that a man be found faithful*. KJV

*WHAT GOD ENTRUSTED TO US- MONEY& POSSESSION*.


In light of Christian stewardship, we can't exclude the stewardship of money& possessions from the life of a steward. In Deuteronomy 8:18, the bible says, *"But thou shalt remember the Lord thy God; for it is He that giveth thee power to get wealth, that He may establish His covenant which He swore unto thy fathers, as it is this day.*"


In the text above, the "power" to get wealth refers to ability or ideas to make wealth. God is the one who gives the ability & ideas to get wealth. A steward knows this truth and will never assume the status of "it is my money." Stewards own nothing their master own everything.


If Christians understand the stewardship of money, they won't live for it. In 2 Corinthians 5:15, Paul said, *"He died for everyone so that those who receive his new life will no longer live for themselves. Instead, they will live for Christ, who died and was raised for them.*


*You can't live for Christ and live for money at the same time*. The one who owns your life knows what you need for life and He will generously supply.


*Stewards handle finances as a trust.* They use money to further the cause of the kingdom and they give generously. Stewards know it is their responsibility to support their local church, missions&missionaries, and other kingdom advancement projects. *That you don't support the kingdom with your finances shows you lack understanding about your role as a steward.*


A wise man said, *"Giving is the proof that you've conquered greed.*" Stewards are givers. They give to God because they know He is the true owner of their possession. *Greedy people want to get all they can and can all they get and sit on the can.*


*When we understand the stewardship of money, we won't argue about giving or whatever percentage to give. Percentage is not the issue, lack of understanding about stewardship of money and the usefulness of finances in the kingdom is the issue.*


True stewards will always give to their master's work as they receive His blessings. This they'll do bountifully, generously and cheerfully.


We run the risk of ending like that rich fool If we rely on worldly wealth and not rich towards God and His Kingdom.


Ponder on this Pauline admonition: *"Teach those who are rich in this world not to be proud and not to trust in their money, which is so unreliable. Their trust should be in God, who richly gives us all we need for our enjoyment. Tell them to use their money to do good. They should be rich in good works and generous to those in need, always being ready to share with others. By doing this they will be storing up their treasure as a good foundation for the future so that they may experience true life* (1 Timothy 6:17-19 NLT).


*PRAYER*: Father, I receive the grace to be a faithful steward of money and possessions in Jesus name.

DEVOTIONAL 0613 | Source start line: 55614
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
8/24/22, 6:57 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: ProjectSave Devotional
Wednesday
August 24th 2022.

*LESSONS IN CHRISTIAN STEWARDSHIP* PT.11

*1 Corinthians 4:2 Moreover it is required in stewards, that a man be found faithful*. KJV

*WHEN THE LORD DEMAND FOR HIS TRUST*

One of my mentors told us about his experience. He was invited to preach in a church. After his ministration, he was given  honorarium worth N200,000. He kept the money, he didn't spend it.

Days after, the Pastor who invited him called saying the Holy Spirit instructed him to add more to the money. He did and made it up to a million naira. Still, he kept the money. He began to ask the Lord what he should do with the money.


Now, I want you to take note of that attitude. He had personal projects&needs he could use the money for yet he asked the owner of the money what he should use it for. That's the attitude of a steward. Most people are afraid to live in this dimension because they don't trust God enough to meet their needs.


After a while, the Holy Spirit reminded him that he had once desired to bless the pastor that joined him on his wedding day. He was instructed to use the money to buy him a car. He added some money and got him a good car.

*When we understand the stewardship of money& possessions, we'll ask the owner how to spend the money. Not all monies are for spending, don't eat your seed with your harvest.*

I've had several experiences along this line but the one I can't forget in a hurry happened a few years back. I wanted to pay my tithe but the Spirit of God instructed me to keep it. I carried this money in my back pocket every where I went.


One day, I was in the market area. I saw an evangelist I knew from way back. A genuine and firebrand woman of God. She told me she had an invitation to preach in another town but was stranded at the market. The Holy Spirit told me I should give her the money I've been keeping.


Immediately I gave her, it was as though a burden rolled off my chest. An unusual joy filled my soul. She was very happy because the money was more than enough to pay her bills to and fro.  She said it was the Holy Spirit that led her to the market. She didn't have anything to do there. She prayed that God should supply her needs miraculously not knowing I was the answer to her prayer. This is one of the ways God distribute His resources to meet kingdom needs.

Dearest, the Lord is in the habit of demanding for His trust. In our hearts, we know when this happens but often we harden our hearts against His voice. Sometimes, due to biases, we look away from genuine kingdom needs and causes.


Some of us are too inquisitive and always suspicious of preachers when it comes to money yet we claim to be led by God. The Holy Spirit won't lead you to put your money in the hands of phoney people.


*Stewards don't hoard resources*. Today, let it sink deeply into your heart that God will demand for the resources He entrusted into your care. It is not yours, release it to the owner.


*PRAYER*: Father, I receive the grace to be a faithful steward of money and possessions in Jesus name.

DEVOTIONAL 0614 | Source start line: 55771
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
8/25/22, 7:45 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: PROJECTSAVE DEVOTIONAL
Thursday,
August 25th, 2022.


*SCRIPTURAL GIVING* PT.1


Speak unto the children of Israel, that they bring me an offering: of every man that giveth it *willingly* with his heart ye shall take my offering.Exodus 25:2 KJV


*GIVE WILLINGLY*.


One of the most misunderstood and abused themes in the bible is the subject of giving. The abuse of this very important  kingdom practice has made many to criticize pastors and churches as money loving and greedy.


"Prosperity Gospel" has done alot of damage in this regard by veering off the path of scriptures. *If giving is scriptural, there has to be biblical guidelines on how to give. We can only get bible result if we do things the bible way.*


What type of offering or giving does God accept? The offering God accept is a willing offering not one that is given grudgingly. From the opening text, it is clear that God's demand was not  overriding their will. God specifically told Moses that whatever the children of Israel wanted to give as offering should be given willingly.


In 1 Chronicles 29:6, when David raised offering for the building of the temple shortly before his death, the principle of willingness was not thrown to the sidelines as people gave: *"Then the leaders of families, the officers of the tribes of Israel, the commanders of thousands and commanders of hundreds, and the officials in charge of the king’s work GAVE WILLINGLY*".


The leaders were the first to give willingly and then the people of Israel. They rejoiced as they did: *"Then the people rejoiced, for that they offered WILLINGLY, because with perfect heart they offered WILLINGLY to the LORD: and David the king also rejoiced with great joy*" (1 Chronicles 29:9 KJV).


Apostle Paul emphasized the same thing about offerings in 2 Cor 8:12 saying, *"For if there be first a WILLING MIND, IT IS ACCEPTED according to that a man hath, and not according to that he hath not*".


I have been in meetings where people gave because they were forced to do so by the preacher. The preacher began to call names and he forcefully extracted a commitment from them. We didn't learn of Christ this way. *When preachers don't trust God enough to meet their needs, they resort to gimmicks.*


Your giving must come from your heart for God to accept it. Not all giving is acceptable to God because gifts/offerings given outside scriptural principles of giving is a mere exercise in futility that will yield nothing.


*ACTION POINT*: Consider today's principle whenever you want to give.

DEVOTIONAL 0615 | Source start line: 55874
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
8/26/22, 6:42 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: PROJECTSAVE DEVOTIONAL
Friday,
August 26th, 2022.


*SCRIPTURAL GIVING* PT.2


Galatians 6:7 Don’t be misled—you cannot mock the justice of God. You will always harvest what you plant. NLT.


*GIVE ACCORDING TO CAPACITY*


Several years ago, anti graft agency revealed that a man embezzled heavily from his office. When asked about the money, he said he sowed the money as seed in church. This man stole because he wanted to give beyond his capacity.


Another woman, a civil servant who stole heavily was caught. When the money she stole was traced, it was found in a church account. One thing I know is that her church didn't tell her to steal to give. However, she may have been influenced by extreme teachings on giving& sowing seed.


*God doesn't demand for what you don't have*. I stated this clearly because one of the common abuses around giving or sowing seed is when people are forced and manipulated to give beyond their capacity. How do you expect a man whose total annual income is not more than N3 million to sow a seed of N5million?


On this, speaking to the Corinthians, Paul said, *"Now you should finish what you started. Let the eagerness you showed in the beginning be matched now by your giving. *GIVE IN PROPORTION TO WHAT YOU HAVE* (2 Corinthians 8:11 NLT).


I have heard or seen preachers who tell people to borrow to sow seed. This is wrong and unscriptural. *From the scripture above, if a man has to borrow to sow seed, such giving is breaking the basic scriptural rule stated above.* Your giving is acceptable based on what you have not based on what you don't have.


That you're to borrowing to sow shows you don't have and God doesn't demand what you don't have. The tenor of scriptures revealed that to us. In the early churvh, people willingly gave what they had. It was not recorded that Peter told them to borrow to sow seed. Even under the old covenant, it wasn't recorded that people borrowed to give.


I've heard of students who sowed their school fees as a seed because a preacher promised them miracle money and grades. Often, most of them become stranded in the middle of the session trying to find solution to their indiscretion.  Hey! There's nothing like miracle money. I didn't see it anywhere in the bible where Jesus, Paul and other apostles preached or taught on miracle money.


The "Gospel of Mammon" is behind all the abuses surrounding money today.  If you leave a meeting and you become restless because you want to give something bigger than what you have because of spurious promises made by the preacher, you've been infected with Mammon's virus.


*Giving should be based on capacity and in proportion to what you have.* God won't demand for what you don't have. Some persons even steal to give in church these days, it is wrong. *The altar doesn't sanctify stolen money*.


*PRAYER*: Father, deliver me totally from the influence of Mammon in Jesus name.

DEVOTIONAL 0616 | Source start line: 56001
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
8/27/22, 8:14 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: ProjectSave Devotional
Saturday,
August 27th, 2022.


*SCRIPTURAL GIVING* PT.3


Galatians 6:7 Don’t be misled—you cannot mock the justice of God. You will always harvest what you plant. NLT.

*GIVE GENEROUSLY*


Pastor E.A. Adeboye told the story of a man in one Kenneth E.Hagin's conference who promised to give the total offerings everyone gave towards a project. The total everyone gave was $3.5million. This wealthy man gave $3.5 million singly.


When asked about the secret of his wealth, he said he reversed the order of tithing. He gave God 90% while he took 10%. He started his business with $500 and in five years, the business grew to worth $5 million. Now, that's a man who understand the principle of generous giving. No one forced it on him, he decided to give that way. I know a man who gives 70% and lives on 30%. This he's been doing for years.  That's generous giving.


Often, we read the bible but do not pay attention to details. Reading through the new testament starting from the early church, I noticed that the principles behind giving was the same and it was consistent.


*Generous giving is what the bible teaches in the new testament.* In the early church,  it was not recorded that the Apostles forced or mandated the people to give a certain amount or percentage. The brethren gave generously to the point of selling their possessions and giving all to the church.


For instance, in Acts of Apostles, this is what they told us about how the early church gave: *"There were no needy people among them, because those who owned land or houses would sell them and bring the money to the apostles to give to those in need*"(Acts of the Apostles 4:34‭-‬35 NLT).


If the Apostles had insisted on a fixed amount to be given, that church would always be running out of supplies because they'll be limiting those who had the capacity to give more. Thank God they didn't. In that church, money wasn't an issue neither were there arguments on percentage to give.


*As stewards of resources, they knew all they had belonged to God and were willing to give all. Truly, they gave all*. I wish the Lord can restore this spirit of giving back to the contemporary church.  A new testament believer should not be limited to a certain amount or percentage.


Read this Pauline admonition: *"Remember this: Whoever sows sparingly will also reap sparingly, and whoever sows generously will also reap generously*"( 2 Corinthians 9:6 NIV). Long time ago, I decided to be generous with my giving and not be limited to a percentage. There were times I gave all and I did joyfully. Your giving should be generous and not limited to a fixed amount. That's the new testament pattern.


*God expect us to be generous with our giving but should be based on our capacity.*  If you can give N10,000 conveniently and you're giving N1,000, that's not generous giving. You're undergiving. This is not heaven's standard for our giving as new testament believers.


Some can do more but they're doing less because they've put a peg on what should leave their purses. Sometimes, the Spirit of God tells us what to give and the exact amount in some cases but because most of us are not generous givers, we shut His voice out and do as we wish. That's not God's will for us.


*ACTION POINT*: Don't put a peg on what you should give to the Lord. Generous giving is the standard for the new testament believer.


====================================================================================================
DEVOTIONAL 0617 | Source start line: 56055
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
8/28/22, 5:37 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: ProjectSave Devotional
Sunday,
August 28th, 2022.


*SCRIPTURAL GIVING* PT.4

Galatians 6:7 Don’t be misled—you cannot mock the justice of God. You will always harvest what you plant. NLT.

*GIVE DECISIVELY NOT UNDER COERCION*


My first time on a prayer mountain was eventful. However, the way and manner one of the prayer leaders raised a particular offering and spoke to those who couldn't give left a sour taste in my mouth. He didn't just speak to them harshly, he made them feel worthless.


A sister told me that her father stopped attending church because the Pastor started laying curse on those who weren't responding financially. Though that's not enough reason to stop attending church and no reason can be good enough to quit fellowshiping with other believers.


However, the excesses of some preachers has chased many away from church and pierced the hearts of some because of the several unbiblical methods they employ in getting money from people. The worst is when manipulation and emotional blackmail is used.


If we'll be truthful to ourselves, the bible is clear on the way and manner you should give. Listen to Paul: *"Each of you should give what you have decided in your heart to give, not reluctantly or under compulsion, for God loves a cheerful giver*"(2 Corinthians 9:7 NIV).

Two things are noteworthy in this verse:

(a) *Decide what you want to give.* That is, no amount should be imposed on you. You are at liberty to decide. God honours your decision. No man/preacher should do that for you.  Do not also forget that the Holy Spirit can prompt you to give specific amount. However, no man under heaven has the right to do impose on you against your decision.


(b) *Don't give under compulsion/ reluctance.* This is akin to the previous point made. The yoke of compulsion in giving is not scriptural. Do not forget that giving must come from a willing heart. If it is from a heart that has been forcefully compelled, such giving has no reward.


*Don't let anyone tell you that your giving is tied to receiving a miracle. God doesn't sell miracles.*


The apostolic&prophetic foundation upon which our faith is built gives no room for manipulation. Never did we read about Peter or Paul promising "sudden breakthroughs" and coercing people to give to support their missionary endeavours. Needs were made known and the brethren gave in response to meet those needs. No sweet-talking, no manipulation.


As a pastor, if you still have to sweet-talk your people before giving, it shows they know little or nothing about the stewardship of money. You need to constantly expose them to scriptural truths on the subject until you get desired result.


*As stewards of God's resources, don't wait until you're sweet-talked before you give. We give to worship God and to meet needs in the kingdom*. It is manipulative to give heavily to a minister who makes spurious promises of sudden breakthroughs and miracle money yet your local church has needs unmet and your pastor or field missionaries are in need. So bad!


*ACTION POINT*: Study all the scriptures in this series and let the Holy Spirit minister to you.

DEVOTIONAL 0618 | Source start line: 56153

8/29/22, 7:44 PM - Lanre Oyeleke: PROJECTSAVE DEVOTIONAL
Monday,
August 29th, 2022.


*SCRIPTURAL GIVING* PT.5


Galatians 6:7 Don’t be misled—you cannot mock the justice of God. You will always harvest what you plant. NLT.


*GIVE CHEERFULLY*

Apostle Paul told us another important attitude that must be in place for our giving to be acceptable. In 2 Corinthians 9:7, Paul says, *"Each of you should give what you have decided in your heart to give, not reluctantly or under compulsion, for God loves a cheerful giver.*"


Giving must be done cheerfully. This speaks of a joyful attitude. The Christian attitude towards giving should not be with sadness. If all  other biblical principles of giving are followed, cheerful giving will result. Don't let anyone steal the joy that comes with giving through manipulation.

Giving grudgingly shows that one of the biblical principles of giving has been broken. There's no way you'll give scripturally and not be cheerful about it even if you're giving all. I know the pain that comes with sacrificial giving but even at that, the element of cheerfulness must be constant.

As a steward, even when God demand that you give all, it must be done with a cheerful attitude knowing that what you want to give is not yours but God's. These are the scriptural ways of giving.


*ACTION POINT*: Study all the scriptures in this series and let the Holy Spirit minister to you.

DEVOTIONAL 0619 | Source start line: 56219
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
8/30/22, 7:35 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: ProjectSave Devotional
Tuesday
August 30th, 2022.


*DO NOT SET YOURSELF UP*


Phil3:3 For it is we who are the circumcision, we who serve God by his Spirit, who boast in Christ Jesus, and who put no confidence in the flesh


Years back, I heard a sad story about a vibrant brother. He boasted in a service that he can't fall into immorality even if he sees the nakedness of a fair lady. The devil heard that and he arranged a temptation for him.

This brother received a distress call from a sister in church late in the night. Instead of asking the Lord whether to go or not, he dashed to the sister's house out of fleshly zeal. Upon getting there, he saw a fair looking lady in a seductive mood. Alas! He fell.

Confidence in the flesh kills. There's a type of confidence that sets a man on the path of destruction. Fleshly confidence in oneself is a rehearsal for falling flat in this race.

*If you listen to the music sin plays, it is just a matter of time, you'll dance to its tune and face the music later*. Don't toy with sin saying "I can handle it". You cannot handle it.


Most times, such confidence wears the garb of self-righteousness. When you meet self-righteous folks, they'll see the wrongs in others and boast to people why they can't fall into certain sins.


They act as though  they are immune to sin whereas the bible says, *"Dear brothers and sisters, If another believer is overcome by some sin, you who are godly should gently and humbly help that person back onto the right path. And be careful not to fall into the same temptation yourself.*"(Gal6:1NLT).


From  that scripture, it is clear that the one restoring a fallen brother can fall into the same sin if he is not careful let alone a self-righteous folk whose feet are set on the path of destruction.


*Don't make yourself vulnerable to things that can destroy you. Stop counseling ladies in a lonely place. Singles, don't stay alone with the opposite sex in a lonely room. Sexual passion doesn't respect your "sulphuric" tongues.*


Confidence in the flesh will prevent you from taking heed and being watchful. It takes grace to stand and to be sustained in this race. However, do not take grace for granted and neglect the fact that you're still a mortal that is prone to errors.


The thought of this should humble you and make you do what Hebrews 4:16 says, *"Let us therefore come boldly to the throne of grace, that we may obtain mercy and find grace to help in time of need.*"


*PRAYER*: Father, uphold me by your arms of grace and help me to be watchful in Jesus name.


====================================================================================================

DEVOTIONAL 0621 | Source start line: 56340
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
8/31/22, 7:04 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: ProjectSave Devotional
Wednesday
August 31st 2022.


*REASON TO REJOICE*


Luke10:20 However, do not rejoice that the spirits submit to you, but rejoice that your names are written in heaven.”NIV


Oftentimes,  preachers speak about how they cast demons out of people and how God used them to perform a miracle. They narrate such events with great joy. This is similar to the experience of the disciples in the opening text.


I have spoken to several believers who were so proud of a powerful preacher. Some would even exaggerate the miracles and healings. Invitations these days sound like "Come experience a miracle".

No thanks to social media. Some persons have created false image for some ministers. I have watched short video clips of impartation services. Some things I saw were okay with me but some looked staged.


You need to see the views those videos had and the numerous comments of praise. *We humans are freaked when we see the supernatural in display but God is not surprised a bit because what we call supernatural is natural to Him*.

Truly, there's a joy that comes with winning souls, casting out devils and healing the sick.  As a minister,  I know this by experience. Sometimes, when we prepare for services with great expectations of  miracles,  healings and deliverance and nothing happens, we feel bad. I am in this mix too.


Those meetings with great testimonies are a reference point in our preachings. We love to talk about it so that people can believe.The disciples of Jesus had that experience too and they came to Jesus telling him about those amazing wonders God did through them.


It was as though Jesus burst their bubbles. Jesus said they should not rejoice that the spirits submitted to them, but they should rejoice that their names are written in heaven. That's profound.


God is not surprised when people get healed and delivered. We publish that in our church bulletins and handbills to draw crowd but God is asking a question, is your name written in heaven?

As a preacher,  it is possible to preach a storm and go to hell. It is possible to sing like angel but not found in heaven's register. It is possible to build great cathedrals for your church,  give to missions and help the poor yet, heaven is saying,  'I don't know you'.

Jesus said on that day, some will come to him to advertize their exploits but he would tell them, 'depart from me you workers of iniquity'. *Supernatural manifestations makes no meaning to God if you're still living in sin*.

The man that is fit to rejoice is the man whose  name is written in the lamb's book of life. We should seek to be faithful to the one who has called us and to please Him not for the applause of men. *Miracles, healings and deliverance are good but they don't determine our status with God.*


PRAYER: father, I receive grace to please you In Jesus name.

DEVOTIONAL 0622 | Source start line: 56436
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
9/1/22, 3:58 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: PROJECTSAVE DEVOTIONAL
Thursday,
September 1st, 2022.


*GOD SHOWS NO FAVOURITISM*


*Acts10: 34 Then Peter replied, “I see very clearly that God shows no favoritism.35 In every nation he accepts those who fear him and do what is right.*


When some church folks share their testimonies, several times I've heard them make statements like: "I'm God's favourite." Even some ministers of God have said this statement before on the altar to their congregation.


I have always imagined a scenario where someone in the church is battling with rejection and condemnation and a preacher or testifier comes to the altar to say: "hey! amongst you all, God loves me more than you all." How do you think a depressed and condemned soul would feel in that service? Does God love anyone less?


*We might be tempted to think that the manifestations of God's power, grace and mercy in our lives shows we're in a "special" relationship with God that differentiates us from others who are not experiencing same whereas it is not true. That's just an act of God's mercy and grace*.


*There's something about the sovereignty of God we can't explain no matter how hard we try however, we should not think we're God's favourites above others when things work in our favour.* Let's stop making men doubt the love of God in their lives because of "happenings" in our lives. That's why we must believe what the word says about God's love for us regardless of "happenings" or feelings.



*That a man is experiencing the hand of God more than the other person doesn't mean such a person is God's favourite. That a man is in the wilderness phase of life doesn't mean he is loved less by God.* Until we strike a balance in matters like this, some believers won't stop having a distorted view about God.


God has no favourite because the bible said so. His love for us is equal and same regardless of our tribe, colour, race and tongue. *Your past is not a determinant of God's love for your life neither is your background a factor in this matter. If it were, God wouldn't love any man from Africa.*


*But God showed his great love for us by sending Christ to die for us while we were still sinners* ( Romans 5:8 NLT).


Apostle Peter said,  *"... In EVERY NATION, He accepts those who fear him and do what is right"*. His love is without ethnic or racial bias. We have one duty as believers, that is to fear Him. The fear of God simply mean to walk in reverential fear. That's what we owe God.


God's love for us has been shown to all through the death of Christ on the cross and through the grace that Christ brought to us. Nothing can be greater than God's love through Christ, His sacrifice and  what He wrought for us. Other benefits aside these are secondary and they don't mean we're loved specially from others whether we experience them or not.


*CONFESSIONS:* Father, thank you because you love me. I know I'm loved because Christ died for me. I'm grateful for your love, grace and mercy over my life in Jesus name.

https://t.me/projectsaveministries


N:B Happy New Month!
