DEVOTIONAL 0623 | Source start line: 56517
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
9/2/22, 6:33 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: PROJECTSAVE DEVOTIONAL
Friday,
September 2nd, 2022.


*WHEN GOD CALLS- OBEY*


Isaiah8:8 Then I heard the Lord asking, “Whom should I send as a messenger to this people? Who will go for us?”
I said, “Here I am. Send me.” NLT


Some persons have asked If God punishes those who refused to heed His call. This question stem from the fact most of them have heard some preachers who shared their sordid experience about what happened when they didn't answer God's call into the ministry.


In my opinion, I think it is the devil that punished them not God. Why did I say that, when we walk in disobedience, we give Satan the legal right to deal with us but when we walk in obedience, we are under God's protective covering. You have no genuine reason to live in disobedience, just obey.


Maria Woodworth Etter lost five of her children because she lived in disobedience to God's call upon her life. In her days, it was unpopular for women to preach.  Out of fear and timidity, she declined God's call severally.


Her disobedience opened the door to untimely death. At the age of forty, she said yes to the call of destiny. Her ministry was supernatural and unique during the revivals in U.S.A. In Pennsylvania, police reported that there were no criminals because of the impact of her ministry.


God is seeing the myriads of problems the human race is facing today, many are dying in sin daily, the sick are increasing, the destitutes and the hungry are increasing by the number, the homeless cannot be counted, economies in the world are melting down.


People are crying in pain and agony daily unto God but God is looking for a 'messiah' to send, someone filled with His Spirit and wisdom. Right from the days of Prophet Isaiah, God is still asking 'who will go for us'? Destiny has been calling but only few persons are answering.


*The call of destiny is that prompting in your heart you received by the Spirit to launch into a problem-solving assignment. It is the voice asking If you will go and solve a problem here on earth*. Answer the call of destiny on your life.

*Don't waste time, you'll soon be old and many may die on account of your disobdience and sluggishness.*


Stop giving excuses, there are 1001 reasons not to answer the call of destiny and those excuses would always be there. Answering the call of destiny is tied to someone's deliverance, please answer God now.


PRAYER: Father, I receive the grace to heed the call of destiny, I won't delay and I won't waste time In Jesus name. Amen

9/3/22, 5:58 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: ProjectSave Devotional

*NOT CONDEMNED- GO AND SIN NO MORE*


John8:10- "Jesus straightened up and asked her, "woman, where are they? Has no one condemned you?"...No one sir, she said. " Then neither do I condemn you," Jesus declared. "Go now and leave your life of sin."


I heard the story of a preacher who committed adultery. He didn't forgive himself and he lived under the wave of condemnation for sometime.


He had a dream where he was told that he was infected with HIV. When he woke up, his sorrows multiplied and he turned skin&bones over time. He stopped going to church because he felt unworthy of God's presence. Have you felt like that before?

The pastorate of his church came visiting. He told them about his sinful act and how someone told him in the dream that he was infected with HIV.


Someone amongst the pastors asked If he confirmed from the laboratory If he was truly infected. He said "No." His blood sample was taken. Alas! He was HIV negative. The spirit of condemnation tortured him not God. Religious folks would have agreed he was under a divine torment from heaven, no he was not.


Someone may say God punished him with those thoughts and dreams. That's not God.  That couldn't have been God.  *We know the nature of God and what He can do by looking at Christ. When we look unto Christ, we'll see God clearly*.


*CHRIST IS THE VISIBLE IMAGE OF THE INVISIBLE GOD. He existed before anything was created and is supreme over all creation,*(Colossians 1:15 NLT).


When you look at what Jesus did when they brought the  woman caught in the very act of adultery  to Him, his judgement was not condemnatory. He said, "... "woman, where are they? Has no one condemned you?"...No one sir, she said. " Then neither do I condemn you," Jesus declared. "Go now and leave your life of sin"* ( John 10:8).


What  He said was exactly what God the father would have said. God doesn't cast away. He is doing all to draw men to Himself. He is in the business of restoring backsliders and making them stand on their feet again.


*The purpose of Christ's coming is to restore and lead men to repentance. If you've erred, don't sit there and mourn your woes, repent and continue running the race diligently.*


The devil will always try to remind you about some of your mistakes, don't listen to his voice of condemnation. If you have fallen, stand up. Make amends and continue the journey till you cross the finish line.


PRAYER: I rebuke every spirit of condemnation torturing my heart and I receive mental&emotional healing In Jesus name.

DEVOTIONAL 0625 | Source start line: 56857
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
9/4/22, 5:33 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: ProjectSave Devotional
Sunday,
September 4th, 2022.


*WHAT SENSIBLE PEOPLE DO*


Proverbs19:11 Sensible people control their temper; they earn respect by overlooking wrongs. NLT


In one of the open heavens devotional that I read years back,  Pastor E.A. Adeboye told the story of how he got angry with his wife while on a journey several years ago. The wife was equally angry as well. Both of them had heated argument in the vehicle.


Since he was driving and he needed to concentrate, he parked his vehicle beside the road. He trekked to a nearby village to buy fruits. When he returned, he offered her fruit, then he was able to restore sanity. That little time spent on the road buying fruits calmed the whole situation.


That's practical wisdom on how to control one's temper. If he had continued the journey, he may have crashed the vehicle in anger. Some persons have died that way.


According to the scripture above, there are two things that sensible people do:
(1) They control their temper
(2) They overlook wrongs.


We all have tendencies to get angry or have gotten angry at one time or the other. *Sometimes, anger is our reaction to unfulfilled expectation from others or from ourselves, the best way to curb or control our temper is never to expect much from people.*


I read the experience of a revered minister online. Three months after marriage, he had serious misunderstanding with his wife. He thought in his mind, "doesn't she know I'm an Apostle"? It was God's wisdom that prevailed in his situation through his wife.


*Don't think you can't be offended. In fact, expect offence from those close to you even your spouse.* If you have unrealistic expectations about people, when they disappoint, you'll be angry. Keep your expectations real.


In marriage, spouses must be ready to accept the weaknesses of one another. *If you are the type that don't take nonsense, marriage is not for you. Until you are ready to tolerate "nonsense," you are not fit for marriage.*


*Also, your ability to overlook wrongs and leave them in the past without referring to it or reacting is a huge sign of maturity*. If you can imbibe these two attributes of sensible people, you'll have a sweet and lasting relationship with people, marriage inclusive.


PRAYER: Father, I receive grace to control my temper and overlook wrongs In Jesus name. Amen

9/5/22, 4:58 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: PROJECTSAVE DEVOTIONAL
Monday,
September 5th, 2022.

*DON'T BE DULL SPIRITUALLY*

John 21:4 Early in the morning, Jesus stood on the shore, but the disciples did not realize that it was Jesus.NIV


Spiritual dullness or insensitivity is a spiritual disease that is worse than HIV. At a time, the disciples of Jesus were spiritually dull. After the death of Jesus Christ,  some of his disciples joined Peter in fishing.  I perceive they must have been tired of all that happened and their hopes may have been dashed.


Their response to all the events that happened caused coldness of heart and lack of zeal to continue their pursuit of the ministry. Peter may have felt this way so he went fishing and others followed him. They were expecting an economic& political Messiah just like the Jews of their time.


What amazed me in the story was that they did not recognize Jesus on time when he came to the river where they were fishing. They were spiritually dull to know that Christ was staring at them in the eyes.

Having spent three and half years with him, they should have recognized his voice,  his physique and his mannerism but on this occasion, their perception was dull. This story describes the life of many believers today.

When Jesus comes around,  most believers don't recognize Him. If Jesus comes to our street announcing himself as Jesus,  not many will recognize Him still. Sometimes, when he comes to meet with us,  He doesn't make noise because He comes to meet us in places we least expect.


Jesus had come to some severally in meetings,  conferences, through teachings, counsel, worship sessions,daily devotionals and even on the pages of scriptures but they didn't recognize Him because of spiritual dullness.

*Some persons miss divine encounters because they expect a thunderous display& some other specific things to happen whereas, divine encounter may look casual on the outside but the in it contains the treasures our life need.*


Just like prayers& faith. Some persons don't believe in simple prayer. If it doesn't have some "acrobatic" display coupled with baritone voice plus capital letter tongues, they won't believe. This has made many miss God.


*The disciples failed to recognize Jesus because He appeared casually*. If an angel had announced Jesus in the skies coupled with thunder and lightning, they may have known that divinity had come to visit. Surprisingly, Jesus came casually.


In Ephesians1:17-18, apostle Paul knew that spiritual dullness is inimical to our spiritual health so he prayed that God will give us the Spirit of wisdom and revelation in the knowledge of Christ and that the eyes of our understanding be enlightened.


*We cannot encounter Christ and recognize Him until our spiritual eyes are open and our perception is active*.


That's something to pray about daily.  Some have been waiting on God for direction or a word from God for years. God has been speaking but they are not hearing because of spiritual dullness. *Oftentimes, one of the causes of spiritual dullness is carnality. A carnal believer is always dull and may never pick spiritual signals.*

May you never miss Jesus again.

PRAYER: Father, open the eyes of my understanding so that I can recognize Jesus. I don't want to miss divine encounters with divinity again In Jesus name.

DEVOTIONAL 0627 | Source start line: 57081
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
9/6/22, 5:42 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: ProjectSave Devotional
Tuesday
September 6th, 2022.


*TRADITION OF MEN AND DOGMA*


Luke 19:44 They will crush you into the ground, and your children with you. Your enemies will not leave a single stone in place, because you did not recognize it when God visited you. ”NLT


*Human tradition and dogma is another reason men miss God and divine encounters.*

The best example from scriptures that comes to mind on the issue of tradition and dogma are the Pharisees, Saducees, Scribes and religious Jews.


They were the custodians of the Mosaic law in their days. The length and breadth of the law was in their head. Scholars told us that  Pharisees knew all the 613 laws of Moses and have them stored in their memory.

However they missed Jesus because they were too rigid and dogmatic about it. They were legalistic. Legalism made them to miss an encounter with the Lord.

They disregarded the person of Jesus and failed to recognize his ministry as valid. They didn't understand that they were interfacing with the one who created Moses.

To them,  Moses was superior and no one could be greater. Jesus was not a conventional Rabbi, they despised his teachings.

*Today, there are Pharisaic brethren in the church. They miss divine encounters because they hold on to the tradition of men and church dogmas that are not necessarily biblical.*


Several examples abound. I was listening to a preacher on television years back, a brother was with us in the living room.

The message was powerful but this brother didn't even take time to hear what God was saying through this preacher.

All he saw were the ladies that used trinkets in the church but he missed out on that word.  How many times have we missed God because of trivial denominational dogma and traditions? You know those dogmas, don't you?

Some can hardly hear or listen to another preacher except  their Pastor. They  believe the truth can only be found on the lips of their pastor and others are heretic.  They are so rigid that they hardly believe God can speak through other people.

Sometimes, God may have tried getting their attention through other people but because their mind is fixed on dogmas,  they miss God. Denominationalism falls under this category as well.


*That encounter you desire from God may not be in places you expect and it may not be through the traditional method of your church neither must it align with dogmas. During  church services, stop trying to predict God.*


The only time some feel God speaks during a meeting is during sermon and prayers.  Your encounter may not be in the preaching and prayer, it could be during the announcement. Be sensitive at all times, shun human tradition and dogmas.


PRAYER: Father, I receive grace to  overcome the tradition of men In Jesus name.

Wednesday
September 7th 2022.

*AVOID IT AT ALL COST-  TRAP OF FAMILIARITY*


Mark6:3 Then they scoffed, “He’s just a carpenter, the son of Mary and the brother of James, Joseph, Judas, and Simon. And his sisters live right here among us.” They were deeply offended and refused to believe in him.


Pastor E. A. Adeboye  told the story of a man that insisted on seeing him despite being busy. He came on a day when he doesn't attend to any body because it was a day with God alone.

Despite his busyness, this man waited till he finished. Pastor Adeboye asked the man what he wanted. He said, "just a handshake". Wow!  Not even prayers?


The man got a good handshake and left but his faith connected strongly with "just a handshake".

Pastor Adeboye said that most of his Pastors shook his hands on his way to the office that day and every other day but do not benefit from it because they are used to it.

Someone else treasured that handshake and that was the encounter he needed for a turn around.

*The anointing you don't respect you will not attract*.


In my few years of ministering to people, I noticed that those closest to me hardly benefit from the grace of God upon my life.

Outsiders have shared amazing testimonies that may  sound unbelievable to those closest to me yet I try to give my best to them when preaching and praying but with only few result.


The same has happened to most Pastors. Infact, one of the reasons some pastors bring invited guests to grace their programmes is to ensure the people get blessed more and to achieve certain results they have been struggling to get from their people.

It is not because the invited guest is more anointed or can preach better, but people tend to down play the gift of God on a man they see every time. This is the problem with familiarity.

Surprisingly, the invited guest may be experiencing the same with his members but going to another church,  he looks like a super hero. Same also applies to the pastor that is invited to go preach elsewhere.

This syndrome may sound normal but I want to tell you that it is totally abnormal. *Familiarity with the vessel God has placed above you will make you miss God over and over again.*


Don't be like the Jews who missed their time of visitation because of  familiarity with Jesus' ancestry. Contemptuously, they called him, 'son of a carpenter' instead of admitting his messianic status.


God can be using your siblings or child mightily and yet not be a partaker of that grace because you see him as that "little boy" of yesterday. *Beware of looking down on vessels whose history you can tell*.

*If you allow what you know about the past of a man to come in-between you and being blessed by God through that man, you may remain on the same spot for a long time. Often, those God sent to us are already around us but we esteem them less and miss God repeatedly*.

Sometimes, when you know too much and have "seen" your pastor too much, the tendency to look down on him and not value him may set in.

The devil uses the trap of familiarity to keep people on the same spot making them to look for what is not lost.

Avoid the trap of familiarity.


PRAYER: Father, I receive grace not to fall into the trap of familiarity In Jesus name. Amen

DEVOTIONAL 0629 | Source start line: 57219

9/8/22, 5:31 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: PROJECTSAVE DEVOTIONAL
Thursday,
September 8th 2022.


*UNBELIEF- THE FRUIT OF FAMILIARITY*


*Mark6:5 And because of their unbelief, he couldn’t do any miracles among them except to place his hands on a few sick people and heal them.* NLT


The opening text shows us the negative impact of familiarity. The text didn't say "Jesus didn't do any miracles among them", rather it says "he couldn't do any miracles among them."


That implies that he tried to do some miracles among them but couldn't achieve His intentions. The reason for that is because of their unbelief. They didn't see Him as messiah. To them, He was the son of Joseph the carpenter.


*One bad thing familiarity breed is unbelief.* I noticed that new converts and those coming to church newly always encounter God quickly.


During testimony time,  they always have wonderful testimonies to give but some old church folks who have been under the pastor for years would be struggling to get a miracle. Don't tell me you haven't seen this happen.


I have heard some old church folks say things like: "when I gave my life to Christ newly,  I get answers to prayers easily". They thought God was doing a welcome party for them.


Some have taken it further by saying that miracles are not for believers. Their argument is that miracles are basically for evangelistic purposes. That's partly true because God use miracles as a bait to bring sinners to himself.


However, believers are also entitled to miracles in areas of need because it is part of their redemptive right. *The major reason  some believers are finding it hard to experience a miracle is because of unbelief produced by familiarity.*


It happened in the time of Jesus and it is still happening today. I've also discovered that ministers of the gospel are not left out in this. It is harder trying to minister healing to a fellow minister. *There's a deep-seated pride in some preachers that makes it hard for them to be blessed by other ministers.*


*Try to maintain healthy distance from your pastor and treasure access as golden. Unrestricted access can breed familiarity sometimes. Let your focus be on the grace of God upon his life and not his faults. Don't join his critics, pray for him daily.  That's how to kill familiarity. If you look at every man critically, you'll find something to critique.*


This is not an excuse to put up with a leader who has been consistently and unrepentantly sinful, evil and wicked. Learn to draw the lines.


If you want to encounter God differently from the previous, you must belief in God and also believe the man God is using for you at that hour is God sent even If he is your biological child. It is biblical to believe in God and also in his prophet, 2Chronicles20:20.


Today,  the prophet may be your pastor,  evangelist or anyone that is positioned to minister to you.  Stop missing out on the largesse of heaven because of trivial reasons.


PRAYER: Father,  I receive grace to overcome familiarity and unbelief in Jesus name.

9/9/22, 8:23 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: PROJECTSAVE DEVOTIONAL

*SPIRITUAL PRIDE- AN ENEMY OF DIVINE ENCOUNTER*


1Peter 5:5 In the same way, you who are younger, submit yourselves to your elders. All of you, clothe yourselves with humility toward one another, because, “God opposes the proud but shows favor to the humble.” NIV


I was raised as a conservative evangelical. In my early years as a believer, I was taught to look down on those churches we call "orthodox churches" in Nigeria and some denominations as worldly and  heretic.

It grew to become a spiritual stronghold that prevented me getting blessed whenever I meet someone from there. God began to deal with me on this starting from 2003. In my circle of friends, there was a firebrand sister raised from one of those churches we normally tag as "worldly."

Coming to know this sister closely and her depth of walk with God, spiritual pride was dealt a huge blow by God. At some point, I've attended and ministered in those churches we deemed worldly and heretic. I must say that I found God there.

*Most of those churches that thought God was exclusively theirs are now a shadow of their past. Silently, power leaked out, sound doctrine died and what is left is noise, old glory and an outward display of life but internally they're dead. I've been around for sometime and I know what I'm saying.*


According to an online dictionary, pride is defined as the feeling or deep pleasure or satisfaction derived from one's own achievements, the achievements of those with whom one is closely associated, or from qualities or possessions that are widely admired.

Pride is the feeling or mentality that makes you think you're better than others. *Pride makes a man look down on others as worthless and without value. If this extends into our walk with God,  we will miss God over and over because God oppose the proud but he gives grace only to the humble.*


I think spiritual pride was one of the problems Pharisees and Saducees had when Jesus came on the scene. You may not see it that way but in my personal opinion,  I think it is.


To be a Pharisee requires alot especially knowledge about the law of Moses. Saducees are picked from the wealthy families. They are aristocrats and they also possess a deep knowledge about the Mosaic law.


Here comes Jesus, the son of a carpenter who never attended any Rabbinic school yet he claimed to have knowledge about God and spiritual things.  How do you think a Pharisee or Saducee would be patient to listen to an ordinary Jewish carpenter teach?


Spiritual pride prevented them from encountering God through Jesus.

*Today,  spiritual pride comes in shades of many colours.  There are believers or ministers who would never sit to learn under anyone they perceive does not have their 'spiritual qualification'. So wrong*!


They may ask questions like: 'did he go to Bible school'? Does he run a mega church? *That someone pastor a small church doesn't mean he cannot be used by God  to minister to the Bishop of a large cathedral. God doesn't work like that*.


*God can use a teenager to meet the spiritual need of a General overseer only if he is humble.*  Spiritual pride robs us of many blessings. *Some will never take notes in church when their junior or someone they brought into the faith is preaching.*


I see spiritual pride often and God is warning us about it especially those of us in the ministry. *Don't sit like a father of spiritual sons in God's presence when God has chosen to speak to you through those you call sons. Sit and learn like a learner under the feet of the master*.


Do all you can to fight spiritual pride, it is an enemy of spiritual encounter.


PRAYER: Father, I receive the grace to mortify spiritual pride in my life In Jesus name.

*SIN SHIELDS FROM DIVINE ENCOUNTER*


Isaiah 59:1-3 Surely the arm of the LORD is not too short to save,  nor his ear too dull to hear. But your iniquities have separated  you from your God; your sins have hidden his face from you, so that he will not hear. For your hands are stained with blood,  your fingers with guilt. Your lips have spoken falsely, and your tongue mutters wicked things..NIV


Pastor E.A. Adeboye told the story of a lady who prayed that God should use her. She  spent time in prayers and her desire was to have a definite encounter with God.


On a certain day,  she went to the toilet to ease herself. The first toilet was messed up,  she opened the next one,  it was the same.

God spoke to her that her life has been dirty like those toilets.  That was why she had not gotten an answer to the request of her heart. God will not pour his power and glory into a dirty vessel.


Some may confuse the grace and mercy of God as being permissive of sin. No,  He is not.

His eyes are too pure to behold iniquity: *"But you are pure and cannot stand the sight of evil. Will you wink at their treachery? Should you be silent while the wicked swallow up people more righteous than they?*" (Habakkuk 1:13 NLT).


The reason divine encounter has eluded so many today is because their heart is filled with all manner of wickedness.

You see,  God knows us better than we know ourselves. For instance, a brother in the church may appear to be hungry for God and His power but the purpose of asking for power may be to outshine other ministers.


*Some persons stay long on the waiting line of divine encounter not because of big and bogus sins like adultery but because of hidden sins and impure motives*.

If you have been desiring a definite encounter with God,  please check your life again. Your prayer should be that the Holy Spirit should purge you of everything that can make you miss God.

*Often, we emphasize on prayer, faith, diligence and others as prerequisite for encountering God but we fail to realize that living in sin nullifies all other effort*.


*Holy living is a producer of power. It sets a man on a platform that makes divine encounter accessible.*


*You cannot be swimming in sin and still expect God to show up*. This message may seem outdated to this generation who believe grace covers for all but it is relevant for all ages even if it is not popular.


*We pray so much for revival but we permit carnality in different shades of colours. We ask for power but compromise seems not far from us. God is not arbitrary. His standard doesn't change. We either accept it or leave it*.

Paul says, *"Well then, should we keep on sinning so that God can show us more and more of his wonderful grace? Of course not! Since we have died to sin, how can we continue to live in it?*"( Romans 6:1-2 NLT).

Someone who lives in sin will not behold the glory of God.  Never.


*PRAYER:* Father,  purge me of anything that will prevent me from having a definite encounter with you In Jesus name.

9/11/22, 10:33 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: ProjectSave Devotional
Sunday,

*THE MAN GOD CANNOT USE* PT.1

Isaiah 6:8 *Then I heard the Lord asking, “Whom should I send as a messenger to this people? Who will go for us?” I said, “Here I am. Send me*.” NLT


*SELF-WILLED BELIEVER*


A popular bestselling book written years back titled "The Man God Uses" by Oswald J. Smith remains a must-read for every believer who intends to be used by God.


However,  it is common to hear people make statements like "God can use anybody." If what is meant is that God can use anybody regardless of race, colour, nationality, past failures, social status, education and income as long as they meet God's requirements for consecration, I totally agree.


Sadly, we also need to know that there are people God cannot use. I'm not saying there are folks God shouldn't use but cannot use. This mean  that no matter how hard God tries, He won't be able to use them because they are not the kind of vessels He uses.


The standard of God as regards kingdom service never changes. Paul said: *" Nevertheless the foundation of God standeth sure, having this seal, The Lord knoweth them that are his. And, Let every one that nameth the name of Christ depart from iniquity*"( 2 Tim 2:19).


*The self-willed believer cannot be used by God.* Who is self-willed believer? This is someone who has accepted Jesus as saviour but not as Lord. *A Lord always will always want to exercise the right of ownership and authority. That's the place Jesus want to take in our lives.*


However, when a believer is self-willed and not pliable in the hands of our Lord Jesus Christ, He cannot be used by God no matter how hard he tries. *You cannot desire to be used by God and still want to call the shots.* In this kingdom, there is one Lord with many subjects.


Our Lord want to exercise full control, send us to wherever He needs us. He is not looking for subjects who will negotiate terms with Him. Until you come to that point where you can say, "Not my will but yours be done" you can't be used by God.


*PRAYER*: Father, I submit to your Lordship in Jesus name.

DEVOTIONAL 0633 | Source start line: 57566

9/12/22, 7:34 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: PROJECTSAVE DEVOTIONAL

September 12th, 2022.


*THE MAN GOD CANNOT USE* PT.2


Isaiah 6:8 *Then I heard the Lord asking, “Whom should I send as a messenger to this people? Who will go for us?” I said, “Here I am. Send me*.” NLT


*THOSE WHO SHUN THE MAKING PROCESS*


The story of Moses is a good illustration that fit for this. From an early age, the hand of God was upon him. He had the mandate to liberate his people from slavery. While growing up, he may not know the exact assignment but he knew Leadership was entrusted to him.


On a certain day, he saw an Egyptian fighting an Israelite. He sided with the Jew and killed the Egyptian thinking no one saw him. On another day, he saw two Jews fighting. This time around, knowing that they were brothers who shared the same ancestry, he decided to resolve the issue amicably.

To his surprise, one of the aggrieved asked him, *"...Who made thee a prince and a judge over us? intendest thou to kill me, as thou killedst the Egyptian? And Moses feared, and said, Surely this thing is known*"( Ex 2:14). That was a very deep question Moses couldn't answer.

At that time, God hadn't made him the leader of his people. He hadn't passed through the Leadership Making School of God. When he realized he hadn't taken his prerequisite leadership courses and training in the school of divinity, he enrolled in the wilderness university where kingdom leaders are made.

For forty years in the wilderness, God taught Moses character and leadership using animals as practical. Why? God knew the people he would lead much later were stubborn and stiff-necked. He learnt patience and meekness in the wilderness.


*The man that is not formed cannot be used by God the way He desires*. Look at Paul's prayer for the Galatians: *"My little children, of whom I travail in birth again until Christ be formed in you,*"(Galatians 4:19 KJV).



Sometimes, we desire to be used by God in a certain way but we forget there's a price to pay. We forget there's a making process. God's focus is on the making process while man looks at the finished product. *The height some of us desire in the kingdom will be messed up If God doesn't help prepare us*.


*God is not an arbitrary God. He is detailed and painstaking in all He does.* God knew the Jews were suffering yet He didn't release Moses for the task of liberating them from the grip of Pharoah not until Moses was made ready.

*In God's making process for those He will use, two major things are Imbued in the process: Christlike character and leadership. He uses the crucible of adversity, life challenges and our day-to-day happenings to achieve this. Until heaven certifies a man fit for the task, He can't be deployed*. You may ask, "Can God ever stop working on us". The answer is "NO".

However, there's a stage He gets  with us before releasing us into His service and at every level, he work on us before moving us to the next level.

Jesus, though He knew He was the Messiah as a young boy yet He didn't start his assignment fully until His making period was full. In this kingdom, we don't deploy ourselves or else we'll hear a voice saying "Who made you a leader over us"?



*PRAYER*: Father, make me.


====================================================================================================
DEVOTIONAL 0634 | Source start line: 57721
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
9/13/22, 6:34 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: ProjectSave Devotional
Tuesday
September 13th, 2022.

*THE MAN GOD CANNOT USE* PT.3

Isaiah 6:8 *Then I heard the Lord asking, “Whom should I send as a messenger to this people? Who will go for us?” I said, “Here I am. Send me*.” NLT

*THE CRITIC, THE EMBITTERED AND ANGRY SOUL*

Our generation has so many embittered and angry people who vent their anger and vituperations against God and anything He is doing through His servants.

These people also desire to be used by God yet they take the position of a critic. They rarely see any good thing about those God is using. Social media has amplified their voices. Unfortunately, they are followed by the simple and undiscerning.

One would think they have a message for the body but they spew only galls. Unfortunately, they may have a large following but If you look through them, they are bitter and angry souls.

Hebrews 12:15 says, *"See to it that no one falls short of the grace of God and that no bitter root grows up to cause trouble and defile many*" (NIV).

*No matter how gifted and knowledgeable you are, bitterness and critical spirit will corrupt your endowments. God cannot use bitter souls*.

Miriam and Aaron, the sister of Moses were embittered, critical and angry at their brother when he married a cushite but deep within, their anger wasn't only because he married a cushite. They were envious and jealous about how God was using Moses.


How did I know? Read this: *"They said, “Has the Lord spoken only through Moses? Hasn’t he spoken through us, too?” But the Lord heard them*" (Numbers 12:2‭-‬3 NLT). This is not a statement of rebuke to Moses but a statement of anger, bitterness and envy. We all know how it ended.

*You cannot start a great thing for God from a critical, bitter and angry spirit especially against others whom God is using.  God doesn't raise police and detectives in His kingdom*.

Those who start out with the spirit of "I can also do what he is doing" will never go far.  This account for many breakaways and multiplicity of ministries yet without eternal impact.

Their voice may be heard for a while but with time, the volume reduces till we hear them no more.

Even if you're offended, do your best to reconcile. Don't carry bitterness around in your bosom. The devil finds expression through angry and bitter souls. May that not be your portion.


*PRAYER*: Father, rid me of bitterness and cleanse me totally in Jesus name.

DEVOTIONAL 0635 | Source start line: 58107
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
9/14/22, 6:50 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: ProjectSave Devotional
Wednesday
September 14th 2022.

*THE MAN GOD CANNOT USE* PT.4

Isaiah 6:8 *Then I heard the Lord asking, “Whom should I send as a messenger to this people? Who will go for us?” I said, “Here I am. Send me*.” NLT

*THE CARNAL BELIEVER*

The word “carnal” is translated from the Greek word sarkikos, which literally means “fleshly.” This is the word Paul used in describing some of the Corinthian brethren in 1 Corinthians 3:1-3.

*And I, brethren, could not speak unto you as unto spiritual, but as unto carnal, even as unto babes in Christ. I have fed you with milk, and not with meat: for hitherto ye were not able to bear it, neither yet now are ye able. For ye are yet carnal: for whereas there is among you envying, and strife, and divisions, are ye not carnal, and walk as men*?(1 Corinthians 3:1-3 KJV).

To be carnal is to be worldly. It is to give in to the cravings of the sinful nature and to fulfil its desires. Here, we are talking about those who live carnal lives perpetually. No repentance no change of heart. This our concern.

You may be wondering why some persons you know who live carnally are doing "mighty" things. Seeing people like this,  it is common to quickly adjudge them as being used by God but is it God that is using them?

In the book of Matthew 7, Jesus spoke about some persons who on the last day will lay claim to being used by God with exploits done in His name yet they are not known in heaven.

*"Not every one that saith unto me, Lord, Lord, shall enter into the kingdom of heaven; but he that doeth the will of my Father which is in heaven. Many will say to me in that day, Lord, Lord, have we not prophesied in thy name? and in thy name have cast out devils? and in thy name done many wonderful works? And then will I profess unto them, I never knew you: depart from me, ye that work iniquity*".(Matthew7:21-23 KJV).

The scripture above shows clearly that it is possible to do great things in the name of Jesus yet not known by Jesus. *One deception that comes with gift is to make the gifted think He is in tune with God when he is not*. The devil knows how to do this.

One of my trainers told me about an adulterous prophet who was warned about his lifestyle. He said, "After all, my prophecies still come to pass". That man was deceived by performance. He has forgotten that Satan can also give counterfeit gifts to those who do his bidding.

*Satan can use a carnal believer and give him counterfeit gifts*. To be truly used by God, your life must be attuned to His will. That standard hasn't changed.

James asked a question still begging for answer: *"Does a spring of water bubble out with both fresh water and bitter water? Does a fig tree produce olives, or a grapevine produce figs? No, and you can’t draw fresh water from a salty spring*" (James 3:11‭-‬12 NLT).

One source can't produce two things. Forget about those you know who speak in tongues, work miracles and still live impure lives. Don't let Satan deceive you into thinking God uses anyone who lives in impurity.

Apostle Paul says, *IF you keep yourself PURE,  you will be a special utensil for honorable use. YOUR LIFE WILL BE CLEAN, AND YOU WILL BE READY FOR THE MASTER TO USE YOU FOR EVERY GOOD WORK.*"(2 Timothy 2:21 NLT). This is the standard of God for anyone He want to use- purity.


*PRAYER*: Father, purge me.

*THE MAN GOD CANNOT USE* PT.5

Isaiah 6:8 *Then I heard the Lord asking, “Whom should I send as a messenger to this people? Who will go for us?” I said, “Here I am. Send me*.” NLT

*THE ANTI CHURCH*

It is common today to see people who hate the institution called "Church" and they do all that is within their power to launch campaign of hate against the church and the leaders of the church.

Quite a number of these people have pseudo ministries that propagate lies, attacks and invectives against the church. They lure people into an isolated spiritual wilderness and disconnect them from the gathering of the brethren where they can fellowship and draw spiritual nourishment.

*And let us not neglect our meeting together, as some people do, but encourage one another, especially now that the day of his return is drawing near*( Hebrews 10:25 NLT).

*The church is God's plan. He doesn't have an alternative plan.* Most of these church haters carved out a "ministry" for themselves against the church to lure people away from under sound pastoral leadership. They do this because of their painful experience in the church.

However, that's not a justification for taking sides with the devil against the church. *If you've grown to hate the church and all that God is doing through her, God can't use you*.

*Now I say to you that you are Peter (which means ‘rock’), and upon this rock I will build my church, and all the powers of hell will not conquer it*( Mt16:18).


*Every ministry and minister that is against the church will not stand*. Beware of those who pitch you against the church and church leaders. No matter how intellectually sound they are coupled with their high sounding "theologies", God cannot use them.


God won't scatter His own institution. That's not to say there are no pseudo churches here and there. However, the true church of God where God's word is taught shouldn't be a subject of hateful debate and attack.

*The church may not be perfect but Christ is the perfecter of the Church. He is washing His church and purging her of impurities. Why stand against God's ongoing project?*


Today, we have many Sauls who came out of the body to attack the church yet they fail to realize they are kicking against the pricks. Soon, they'll be hurting their feet.


I see a lot of church haters online and some of the persons who champion this cause sound like men with 'special ministries' but deep within me, I know they're fighting a battle they can't win. God doesn't use such persons. Don't be a disciple of such people if you desire God to use you.



*PRAYER*: Father, I will not be an enemy of the church in Jesus name.

*THE MAN GOD CANNOT USE* PT.6

Isaiah 6:8 *Then I heard the Lord asking, “Whom should I send as a messenger to this people? Who will go for us?” I said, “Here I am. Send me*.” NLT

*FOLLOWERS OF BALAAM*

In Numbers 22-24, the story of Balaam and Balak was told. Balaam was a prophet. King Balak of Moab offered a handsome reward/ wages to Balaam to curse God's people.

Though he didn't curse the Israelites because God restrained him from doing so but he was enticed by the gifts of Balak. In other to collect the gifts of Balak, Balaam advised the Moabites on how to entice the people of Israel with prostitutes and idolatry.

Balak followed Balaam’s advice, and Israel fell into sin, worshiping Baal of Peor and committing fornication with Midianite women. For this God plagued them, and 24,000 men died (Numbers 25:1–9; Deuteronomy 23:3–6).

Peter compares false teachers to Balaam, *“who loved the wages of wickedness*” (2 Peter 2:15). Listen to what Jude said about Balaam: *"What sorrow awaits them! For they follow in the footsteps of Cain, who killed his brother. Like Balaam, THEY DECEIVE PEOPLE FOR MONEY. And like Korah, they perish in their rebellion (Jude 1:11 NLT).

*The spirit of Balaam is still at work today. It is a spirit that makes people preach, teach and prophesy for greedy gain. A man that hasn't conquered greed cannot be used by God.* Greed corrupts gifts and callings.

*It is a sin to make merchandise of grace and gifts. You don't charge to pray, preach, heal or minister to God's people. It is totally unbiblical*. In the days of Christ here on earth, it wasn't recorded that he charged to pray for someone. The. apostles didn't do it.

It is not uncommon today to hear about how some ministers charge to be invited. Some charge to preach per hour. Some even charge for consultation like medical consultants do. Even though miracles and healings may be taking place in their ministry, I doubt the Holy Spirit is behind such manifestations.

For me, the moment you put a price tag on invitation, I close the chapter immediately. If every set man and pastor forbid their leaders from bringing such greedy people to their pulpit, they'll run out of business soon. Sometimes, we help perpetuate this evil by sending invitation to men who will drain us financially.

Many have failed God in this area. God is changing the narrative because revival is at our doorstep.  He is looking for men whose hearts are committed to His cause not those greedy for gain. If your heart is full of schemes for gain, another spirit will use you not the Spirit of God.


*PRAYER*: Father, purge me of all traces of  Balaam in my life in Jesus name.

*THE MAN GOD CANNOT USE* PT.7

Isaiah 6:8 *Then I heard the Lord asking, “Whom should I send as a messenger to this people? Who will go for us?” I said, “Here I am. Send me*.” NLT

*THE SELF SUFFICIENT&PROUD*

I was told about the attitude of some persons during their ordination into ministry in their denomination. They put up the attitude of "I deserve it". This attitude reeks of pride and the spirit of self sufficiency.

In the same denomination, some ordainees  would go to God in prayer and fasting asking for grace to function effectively and not to fail Him. Often, God uses the latter than the former.

The attitude of self sufficiency and pride doesn't make a man rely on God for effectiveness in life and ministry. Paul says, *"Not that we are sufficient of ourselves to think any thing as of ourselves; but our sufficiency is of God;*"( 2 Cor3:5).

That feeling of having acquired deep theological knowledge may make a man feel self sufficient and not want to rely on God for his ministry. Some may see no need to pray because they know what to preach.


Oratory and ability to command people's attention may make a man feel self sufficient. Some think being a good speaker translates into being a good preacher. How wrong!


The moment a man think himself qualified on the basis of certain parameters, pride may set in. When pride sets in, such persons either remain static or they go down.

This is what scriptures says about the proud: *"But he giveth more grace. Wherefore he saith, God resisteth the proud, but giveth grace unto the humble*"(James 4:6).

*Self sufficient and proud people don't pray. In fact, prayerlessness is a sign of pride. It is a way of telling God, "I can do it alone, I don't need you". The man whose attitude exclude God cannot be used by God.*


God cannot use a man who rely in himself, his qualifications, and status for spiritual assignment. The humble will continue to be a ready vessel for God's use anytime.


*PRAYER*: Father, I receive the grace to remain humble and to see Christ as my sufficiency in Jesus name.

*SHE STUMBLED*

*2Cor6:3 We live in such a way that no one will stumble because of us, and no one will find fault with our ministry.*


I heard a story about an insane lady. She was at the junction of a church in a large city here in Nigeria. That junction was her usual spot.


She was there for 11 years in her abnormal condition. On a certain day, someone who has a ministry to the insane went to her usual spot, picked her up and rehabilitated her. After the whole exercise, she became normal.


She was asked about what led to her insanity. The young girl said she became insane immediately after her pastor raped her.


The pastor that raped her always pass her bye for the 11years period of her insanity. He's the pastor of the church down the street. That girl stumbled into insanity because of the irreverent act of that pastor and his ministry was faulty.

I have seen and heard several similar cases of those whose lives were damaged by priest. It is not only in sexual matters. It is sad to say some pastors duped people and ran away with money.


As a minister of the gospel, do you know how many people have picked offense because of your ministry? Some have said, "If that Pastor is the only pastor in town, I won't go to church again."

As a child of God, even if you are not in five-fold ministry, your life shouldn't be offensive neither should it make people stumble.


Those around you should see Christ in you and be willing to serve the Lord because of your conduct. If people decide not to follow Christ because your life is not exemplary, they have stumbled and your ministry is faulty already.


You shall account for every soul that has stumbled because of your carelessness.


PRAYER: Father, I shall not be a stumbling block to the salvation of others In Jesus name. Amen

*STARVE IT TO DEATH*

Col3:5 Mortify therefore your members which are upon the earth; fornication, uncleanness, inordinate affection, evil concupiscence, and covetousness, which is idolatry:

I was once a victim of masturbation. Yes, I masturbated years after I became born again. I was preaching already and I was involved in ministry. This happened four times.

Surprisingly, it never happened to me when I was in the world. I was troubled about it and I began to examine myself to know where I got it wrong.

I discovered I was feeding the flesh with erotic pictures. When flesh became fat, it led me into masturbation. The moment I discovered the trigger, I cut off immediately and I was restored.

Oftentimes, when I counsel those addicted to masturbation, I always recommend that they starve flesh to death.


In your quest for victory over masturbation, there is a place of prayer and the place of right action. Prayer and right action must work together to produce the right result.

The scripture above clearly states that we should mortify fornication and other things. Another word that is akin to mortify is the word kill. When you see that word mortify, other words like mortuary, morgue, mortician comes to mind.  They are pointing to the same thing- death.

God's word to you today is that you should identify what triggers masturbation and kill it. Simple as ABC. I don't pray where bible says I should take action. So many Christians love to pray but they fail to do what is expected to complement the prayer. Prayer releases or connect us to the grace to do.

Masturbation just like any other addiction or perversion can be killed if the person is desirous of victory. But how can this be done? Simply starve your flesh of its object of lust.

*Whoever sows to please their flesh, from the flesh will reap destruction; whoever sows to please the Spirit, from the Spirit will reap eternal life*(Galatians 6:8).

Some believers are fond of going to God in tears anytime they fall into the sin of masturbation. *Penitence is good but you cannot be praying for victory over masturbation and still be watching pornography or watching nudes and reading erotics. It doesn't work that way.*

*As long as you know what ' Mr Flesh' delights in, starve the flesh of those things.* You don't do it once and stop, it must be done continuously until you gain mastery over it. That's how mortification is achieved.

*ACTION POINT*: What are the things that triggers masturbation that requires mortification? It could be t.v.programmes, certain magazines, pornography etc

Tuesday
September 20th, 2022.

*DON'T WAIT TILL YOU'RE BIG*

Ecclesiastes 11:4 He who observes the wind will not sow, And he who regards the clouds will not reap( NKJV)

Everyone aspire to be great. We all seem to have a vision for greatness and enlargement. When praying, we include in our prayers this desire.

Not only do we desire to be great, we also have an idea of the things we intend to do when we become great. If I ask, what will you do when you become a millionaire?

A number of persons will speak glowingly about great projects they intend doing that'll eventually benefit humanity. Almost everyone have a postponed desire for the future.

However, you don't need to be big or great before you start those things. *Often, we overlook the power of starting small and growing big with time.* Don't wait till you're big before your life benefit humanity.

*If you don't have a culture of doing the right thing when you're small, you may not do it when you become great*.

Oftentimes, I've had people tell me they wish to support missions but they're waiting for the time they'll have big money. Most people in this category never give a dime even if millions come their way because they didn't have a culture of giving when they had little.

They forget that little is much with God. *If you mean to give to support missions, give that little in your hands. God will accept what you have not what you anticipate to have*.

Over the years, in our ministry, those who give little consistently have grown over time to give bigger amounts but those that always wait to give when they're big have never given at all. We're still waiting.

*If you know the right thing to do to help someone or to salvage a situation, do it now. Don't wait till you're big*. Maybe you intend to help the poor, widow, downtrodden and less privileged, don't wait till you become a millionaire. Start with one and trust God to make it grow into a multi million charity organization.

*Whatever your hand finds to do, do it with all your might, for in the realm of the dead, where you are going, there is neither working nor planning nor knowledge nor wisdom.*( Ecclesiastes 9:10).

*The only people who don't grow are those who don't start small*.


*PRAYER*: Father, I receive the grace to start small and grow big in Jesus name.

*TEST IT IN GOD'S LABORATORY* Pt.1

1 John4:1 Beloved, do not believe every spirit, but test the spirits to see whether they are from God, for many false prophets have gone out into the world. ESV

In science, a laboratory is central to whatever scientists does. That's where tests and experiments are carried out using laboratory techniques and methods.

Also, God has commissioned us to carry out tests using His techniques and methods as revealed in scriptures. The opening text say so. In the kingdom, we don't accept prophecies and teachings hook line and sinker until properly tested.

When I stumbled on this scripture many years ago in my personal bible study, I didn't know what it meant like I do now.

I read it in passing and speed read like most of us do to scriptures that do not fascinate us. Much later, I discovered the treasure locked within it because it is more relevant in today's world of many 'thus saith the Lord'.


Increasingly at an alarming rate, we are seeing the rise of so many fake prophets than ever. Some herbalists have left their shrine to start churches and prayer houses to consult for people in a modern way.

They dress like real preachers without wearing their occultic regalia while some speak demonic tongues to confuse people. I have seen many people confused by some of these con men and women all in the name of 'thus saith the Lord'.

What about prophetic gimmicks just to raise money?  1John4:1 is relevant today than ever. When we buy clothes, shoes, cars, electronics and other gadgets, we are wise enough to test them to verify originality.


*God's word also says, we can test all Spirits to see whether they are of God. If it is not possible, God wouldn't have recommended we do it*.

As from today, cultivate the habit of subjecting spiritual things to spiritual tests before accepting them.

*PRAYER*: Father, give me a discerning heart In Jesus name.

DEVOTIONAL 0643 | Source start line: 58825

9/22/22, 6:00 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: PROJECTSAVE DEVOTIONAL
Thursday,
September 22nd 2022.


*TEST IT IN GOD'S LABORATORY* Pt.2


*2 Peter 1:19 We have also a more sure word of prophecy; whereunto ye do well that ye take heed, as unto a light that shineth in a dark place, until the day dawn, and the day star arise in your hearts:*


*THE LITMUS TEST FOR PROPHECIES- THE WORD OF GOD*.


Someone once told me he saw a vision about my ministry and how I will go round the world preaching the Gospel. Well, I don't know If I should call that a confirmation of what I know from scriptures or by prophecy.


The word of God said I should go into the world and preach the Gospel. Since I've laid my life on the altar of obedience, it is certain I will go round the world to preach. However, I didn't contest what he said not until he told me to do a ritual which he called prophetic action.


If my memory won't fail me, he said I should wake up in the midnight at a certain time and bath with  soap and sponge then throw it away thereafter. Immediately, something in me was alerted that this has moved from prophecy into rituals.


In Chemistry, when testing the acidity or alkalinity of a solution, a neutral purple litmus paper is used for verification. The colour changes would tell what the solution is.

*Since the word of God says we should test all spirits, there must be a spiritual litmus  test to know whether a prophecy, word of knowledge or a manifestation is from God.*


There are regulatory agencies in Nigeria that test products and goods just to ensure they're authentic.   The word of God is the litmus test for checking false prophets and prophecies because it has integrity.


*A preacher said, "God's word is settled in heaven, if it was settled on earth, men would have tampered with it".  I agree 100%. Anything you cannot see or confirm from scriptures should be discarded*.


Some may say that certain things like river bathing ( citing Naaman), burning of incense( referring to OT ritualistic practice) ,etc  are in the word of God. Yes I agree but you need to understand how God dealt with mankind in dispensations throughout scriptures and how and where what we read in scriptures apply to us today as individuals.


*Certain actions were permitted by God in the old testament because Christ, the perfect representation of God hadn't come.
Christ was veiled in the old testament and unveiled in the new testament. His one time sacrifice on the cross has taken care of those rituals. Hallelujah!!!*


*We don't base practice and doctrines on what is descriptive. Rather, as New testament believers, we base our practices on what is prescriptive. What the bible describes in certain places is not what it prescribes in all situations and circumstances.*


The Apostles and prophets of the new testament laid the doctrinal foundation of the New testament church and whatever we didn't see them do or teach requires critical examination. That's a safe ground.


I prefer to stick to God's word as the basis for my action rather than prophecies. I have lived my life this way and it has helped me greatly. The word of God is a more sure word of prophecy as said by Apostle Peter.


I have heard people do crazy things because a prophet told them to do so whereas those things have no root in scriptures. *A believer that doesn't know God's word is not qualified to hear from God.*

All over the internet, we see pictures of  prophets telling people to drink dettol, eat snakes, grasses and drink petrol.

Dearest, if a prophecy or manifestation doesn't fall in line with God's word with two or more supporting scriptures, discard it.

*Don't be deceived by the accuracy of a prophecy. If the prophet, prophecy, source and content fall short of God's word, ignore it.*

PRAYER: Father, I receive a discerning heart In Jesus name.

*TEST IT IN GOD'S LABORATORY* Pt.3

*Acts16:16 Once when we were going to the place of prayer, we were met by a slave girl who had a spirit by which she predicted the future. She earned a great deal of money for her owners by fortune-telling.*

*ACCURACY IS NOT ENOUGH PROOF*

There's a guy I often see in traffic on my way to church. He begs for money and whenever he comes begging, he says things that are accurate about those he beg from.

I know he is insane and he is not operating by the Spirit of God. Whatever insight he has about people is by the operation of familiar spirit. Yes, familiar spirits can reveal things that are accurate about people. Necromancers operate using familiar spirit.


I have seen people queueing behind a prophet because his prophecies are accurate. That may look harmless on the outside but there may be more to it.

One of my associates told me about a prophet who came to their house to see his clients most of whom were into internet fraud. This prophet predicted accurately yet the question mark on his prophetic ministry is that he's also a prophetic consultant for internet fraudsters.

If you're close to your bible, you will know that the spirit behind his accuracy isn't God. The Spirit of God doesn't aid and abbet fraudulent activities.

The story of the slave girl in Acts16  is a lesson for us all in that regard. We were told that this slave girl predicted the future for people and earned great money for her masters.

If her predictions weren't accurate, nobody would patronize her. Obviously, she was always spot on and that earned her money.

In fact, she was right with her proclamation about Paul&his companion being servants of God but when Paul discerned the spirit behind operation, he was angry and he cast it out.


That means a prophet may be accurate in prediction but still, such prophecies may not be from God. *God is not concerned with accuracy of prophecies only, He is concerned with the source as well*.


Apostle Paul was discerning to know  the slave girl wasn't connected to God but to familiar spirits.  *Brethren, I don't want you to be moved by 'thus saith the Lord' but what is written in the word of God*.


*The word of God is the accurate and infallible prophetic writing. That is why it is called 'a more sure word of prophecy',(2Pet1:19).*


If you want to know how God thinks or you want to know God's mind on an issue, look into God's word.

Be so addicted to God's word to the extent that God can speak to you on everything from the pages of scriptures.


PRAYER: Father, I receive grace to become a word addict In Jesus name

*TEST IT IN GOD'S LABORATORY* Pt.4


*Matthew7:16 Ye shall know them by their fruit...KJV


*CHARACTER TEST*

When I was in school of ministry training, one of my lecturers spoke along the line of character for ministers.

He told us about an immoral prophet who was confronted about his immoral lifestyle. His response was, "Afterall, my prophecies still come to pass."

He was deceived by accuracy and blinded against his character flaws.

*Satan can take over the life of a man and make him operate in pseudo gifts to deceive him into thinking that God is still on his side.*

It is quite unfortunate that some believers do not mind the character or reputation of the person who speaks or prophesy to them.

Those who seek for prophecies all over often fall victim of these con men whose lives are totally opposite from the life of Christ.

*Some will say, "His anointing is what I need not his life." They forget whoever speaks to them is also imparting into them what he carries, whether good or bad.*

It baffles me why some persons still submit to men who do sex deliverance, exorcism by kissing, flogging of demons and other strange practices. Maybe they're hypnotized but biblical illiteracy is a major factor.

If you know this about any man and you still put yourself under him, you'll have yourself to blame.

*Dearest, we can only follow men that are following Christ but if they deviate, we should stop following them but we should continue to love and pray for them*.

*TEST IT IN GOD'S LABORATORY* Pt.5


*Jude11 Woe unto them! for they have gone in the way of Cain, and ran greedily after the error of Balaam for reward, and perished in the gainsaying of Core*.


*BALAAM SYNDROME- MONEY TEST*

The spirit of Balaam should be checked when testing prophecies. The spirit of Balaam is a mercantile spirit that prophesy for money or use gimmicks to swindle people.

One common attribute of fake prophets almost everywhere is their greed and love for money. They're inseparable. They often use fear factor to make their victims feel they won't get result If they don't give.

I read about a prophet who showed the congregation cubes of sugar and he placed monetary value of N1,000 on it for anyone that wanted his life to be sweet like sugar.

The most painful aspect of it is that people always fall for such gimmicks.   What about those that charge for prayers? Haven't you heard about prayer contractors that fix charges to pray and fast for people?


So many horrible things are happening today that most believers have failed to test. People now prefer extra biblical practices like using Menorah lamp stand, prayer shawl, prayer mat, ark of covenant rather than what is written in the word.

I have read my bible from cover to cover severally and I didn't see where a genuine prophet or Jesus charged to prophesy, heal and deliver.

A prophet like Elisha even rejected gifts let alone demanded for it.   The error of prophet Balaam was that he was greedy for gain. Beware of those who use 'thus saith the Lord' to withdraw money from your pocket and demand a mandatory seed from you to be given to them so that prophesy or pray for you.


We now live in the end of end time. We must be circumspect and watchful of little things or practices that can make us stray from the faith that was once delivered unto the saints.

PRAYER: Father, I shall not be deceived In Jesus name.

9/26/22, 6:07 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: PROJECTSAVE DEVOTIONAL

*TEST IT IN GOD'S LABORATORY* Pt.6

1Cor4:15 For though ye have ten thousand instructors in Christ, yet ye have not many fathers; for in Christ Jesus I have begotten you through the Gospel.

*SPIRITUAL FATHERHOOD ANGLE*

Sometime ago, a very controversial prophet was questioned about his spiritual root. He emerged on the scene but no one could trace where he was coming from. Every man should have a traceable root.

The history of my Christian journey can be written and traced because there are people God used to raise me. I can easily point to the people God used to raise me over the years.

In ancient Israel, there was a register of prophets and their writings. Also, prophets knew each other and their roots. Prophets had known trainers and one could trace the trajectory of their rising except for a few.

Over the years, I discovered that one of the things that gives credibility to a man's ministry is who he is submitted to as his spiritual father.

Today, there are many preachers, teachers, pastors and prophets in town with bogus bill boards and titles yet they cannot point to anyone as their spiritual father. Beware of heeding to someone whose spiritual paternity is not traceable.

In these days where prophets emerge from nowhere, you can also test their genuiness by assessing them from the angle of spiritual fatherhood. Who raised him? Who taught him the fundamentals of the Christian faith?

While some do not have a traceable spiritual father, some have spiritual fathers that are phony. What kind of spiritual son do you think a greedy and occultic spiritual father would produce?

Somehow, people pattern their ministries after their spiritual father. Most of the criminals in town wearing cassock and 'prophelying' have a spiritual father that is exactly like them.  This matter is very crucial because of the times we are in.

Have you ever questioned the spiritual paternity of that prophet or pastor? It is clear that Timothy was the Spiritual son of Paul. Elisha's prophetic ministry received a boost because the people knew who his spiritual father was.

They knew that his spiritual father followed God so they saw a replica of Elijah in him. In addition to other prescribed litmus test, do not neglect the angle of verifying  spiritual paternity.

*PRAYER*: Father, I will not fall victim of  fake prophets and ministers In Jesus name.

*TEST IT IN GOD'S LABORATORY* Pt.7

*1 John4:1 Beloved, do not believe every spirit, but test the spirits to see whether they are from God, for many false prophets have gone out into the world*. ESV

*THE SALVATION QUESTION*

Another factor that must be considered when subjecting prophecies to test is salvation. It is not safe to follow what an unsaved man says in prophecy.

It is not a question of whether what they say is accurate or not rather it is a test of the spirit behind operation. After all, astrologers, crystal ball gazers and mystics predict accurately.

Years back, a controversial prophet was rejected by the umbrella body of  Pentecostals because they heard he claimed to have been born again from his mother's womb.

If truly he said that, it means he doesn't understand what salvation is all about. Nobody was born saved, we were all born into sin in need of a saviour.

The psalmist says, *"For I was born a sinner— yes, from the moment my mother conceived me.*"(Psalms 51:5 NLT).

To buttress this point further, apostle Paul says, *"For everyone has sinned; we all fall short of God’s glorious standard.*"(Romans 3:23 NLT)

At the right time when we've grown in understanding, we are meant to give our lives to Christ.

To many who listen to such a prophet, they would see him as a supernatural being who deserves their followership because of his acclaimed salvation from the womb.

Many of such ministers exists today, they demand worship and are taking the place of the Holy Spirit in the lives of their members.

*If a man doesn't have genuine salvation experience, it is not safe to be under such a fellow. Salvation experience should be the starting point of any man's journey before entering into ministry*.

Be intentional about trying spirits to ascertain whether they are genuine or not. A minister performing miracles without salvation experience is a time bomb waiting to explode.

PRAYER: I shall not be deceived In Jesus name.

9/28/22, 6:42 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: ProjectSave Devotional
Wednesday
September 28th 2022.

*INTERPRET REVELATIONS SCRIPTURALLY*

John 16:13 *But when he, the Spirit of truth, comes, he will guide you into all the truth. He will not speak on his own; he will speak only what he hears, and he will tell you what is yet to come.*


My mentor told the story of a church founder who had a revelation. In that revelation, God told him that he'll give him more wives.

He didn't bother to press further to know the meaning. This man married more wives. Later, he took ill and died.

On the day of his burial, the Spirit of the Lord came upon someone in the meeting saying, "Thus saith the Lord, truly I told my servant I'll give him more wives but I meant more churches not physical wives".

Now, this man truly heard God but he failed in his interpretation of what he heard. Scripturally, the church is the bride of Christ. If he had been patient to pray and search scriptures, he may have found the scriptural meaning of what the Lord told him.

*Husbands, love your wives, even as Christ also loved the church, and gave himself for it;*( Eph 5:25).

*For I am jealous over you with godly jealousy: for I have espoused you to one husband, that I may present you as a chaste virgin to Christ* ( 2 Corinthians 11:2).

As I heard through another person, he shared the experience of a pastor. This man saw himself walking barefooted in the dream. He prayed against it because he thought it had a negative connotation.


He had this dream repeatedly and he prayed. After sometime, he quitted "warfare" prayers and asked God what the meaning was. The Holy Spirit reminded him of Ephesians 6:15 which says, *"and your feet shod with the preparation of the gospel of peace;*"

He received a clear instruction from the Lord to go and evangelize. That was the meaning of that particular revelation he was having. *Revelations are meant to be interpreted scripturally not emotionally*.

Most of us buy and read dream interpretation books. A child of God shouldn't interpret revelation with another man's interpretation. Only babies do that.

God may show two persons the same thing yet he is speaking differently to them. That I saw egg in my dream and it meant trouble as the Spirit of God interpreted it to me doesn't mean anyone who sees egg should interpret it the same way. It could mean other things.

*If we want to understand dreams clearly, we must avoid stereotypes that people peddle around.* Spending money in the dream doesn't mean you'll be poor as some persons believe.

*Beware of dream interpretation experts*. They may not be attuned to the Spirit at all times. Seek to know God's mind for yourself in prayers and study. Don't be lazy.


*Take your time to pray and study the scriptures extensively and check for patterns that relates with what the Lord is showing you in your dreams before concluding on interpretation*.

That's the only safe ground.

*PRAYER*: Father, I receive the grace to patiently wait on you in prayers and in the place of study for proper interpretation of revelations in Jesus name.

DEVOTIONAL 0650 | Source start line: 59849
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
9/29/22, 6:24 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: PROJECTSAVE DEVOTIONAL
Thursday,
September 29th 2022.


*YOU ARE A SAINT*


Ephesians 1:1 *Paul, an apostle of Jesus Christ by the will of God, to the saints which are at Ephesus, and to the faithful in Christ Jesus:*


Religion has made sainthood look like a 'special' achievement or a status that must be conferred on a few outstanding persons. As religious as it sounds, that's not God's standard for sainthood.


Some persons believe saints can only be found in heaven. That's far from the truth. In several instances where believers were called "saints", they were on earth. That's not disputing the fact that glorified believers are saints. They are saints as much as those of us on earth are.


It is alien to scriptures to pray to saints,  venerate and worship saints. We didn't read in scriptures that any apostle or the early church did. This was imported into church tradition when the church became apostate. In the bible, saints are called to revere, worship, and pray to God alone.

In the opening text, Paul called Ephesian believers "saints". It wasn't a thing of status for just one outstanding person but a status for all believers in Christ.

The word “saint” comes from the Greek word hagios, which means “consecrated to God, holy, sacred, pious." The idea the word “saints” convey is a group of people set apart for the Lord and His kingdom.

To speak scripturally, the “saints” are the body of Christ, Christians, the church. All Christians are considered saints. All Christians are saints—and at the same time are called to be saints.

1 Corinthians 1:2 says: *“To the church of God in Corinth, to those sanctified in Christ Jesus and called to be holy*…” The words “sanctified” and “holy” come from the same Greek root as the word that is commonly translated “saints.”

It is common to read in scriptures where believers were called "saints". In the early church, no one conferred sainthood on anyone. Everyone who believed in Jesus and accepted him were all regarded as saints. Let's consider these scriptures.


In Acts 9:32, Peter referred to believers as saints, *"Now as Peter was traveling through all those regions, he came down also to the saints who lived at Lydda*".


When Ananias received from the Lord to go pray for Paul, he made reference to the saints as those Paul persecuted: *"…Lord, I have heard from many about this man, how much harm he did to Your saints at Jerusalem*" (Acts 9:13).


Even Paul spoke about how he persecuted believers. In his defense, he called them "saints": *"And this is just what I did in Jerusalem; not only did I lock up many of the saints in prisons* …“ (Acts 26:10).


*By reason of your connection with Christ, you are a saint. It is not because of your perfection. If perfection was the criteria, none that has ever been called a saint would qualify.*


*Sainthood was conferred on you because of the finished work of Christ on the cross and nothing more. Hallelujah*!!!

God's expectation is that you live as saint. Your conduct should portray that status of sainthood that Christ conferred on you when you put your faith in Him.

Romans 16:2 reveal that saints have acceptable manner: *"that you receive her in the Lord in a manner worthy of the saints*…" (Romans 16:2).

Ephesians 5:3 reveals what is proper among saints,  *"But immorality or any impurity or greed must not even be named among you, as is proper among saints*".

Don't doubt it again. You are a saint.

*CONFESSION*: Father, I thank you because I'm a saint. The finished work of Christ made me a saint. It is not by works but by grace. Henceforth, I live out the life that is expected of me as a saint. I walk in the in the newness of life in Jesus name. Amen

9/30/22, 6:04 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: PROJECTSAVE DEVOTIONAL
Friday,
September 30th, 2022.


*YOU CAN INSIST ON GOD'S WORD*


Jeremiah 1:12  *The Lord said to me, “You have seen correctly, for I am watching to see that my word is fulfilled*.” NIV


Most believers do not understand the power in the word of God. Some see it as mere writings or another religious literature.

A lot of people desire a change in their lives. They desire to turn situations around for good yet they do not realize that the instrument for change and turn around is the word of God.


God's word contains God's promises and a written plan of how your life should be. When things happen contrary to what God promised in His word, you can insist on the fulfilment of what is written in God's word.


The bible says, *"For ever, O LORD, thy word is settled in heaven*"(Psalms 119:89). *If God's word is settled on earth, then it becomes unreliable because men may tamper with it but you can rely on it because it is settled in heaven*. Hallelujah

If you receive a report that is contrary to what is written in God's word, simply insist on God's word. How can you do this? Look for minimum of two scriptures that promises your desire.

2 Corinthians 13:1 says, *"This will be my third visit to you. “Every matter must be established by the testimony of two or three witnesses*.”

When you go into the place of prayer, go with the scriptures you've found and let God know what He said in His word. Insist on the fulfilment of those scriptures. Don't waiver. God's word has integrity and he doesn't want to be seen as a liar because He cannot lie.

The bible says, *"God is not human, that he should lie, not a human being, that he should change his mind. Does he speak and then not act? Does he promise and not fulfill?*(Numbers 23:29).

Hallelujah!!! GOD cannot lie. Whatever he says can be taken to the bank. God's word has integrity. When situations go contrary, you can insist on the word in the place of prayer.

*Every negative situation can obey God's word If we insist.*

If the doctor has said you have few more days to live or things aren't going well in your finances or with your children, don't fret. Simply insist on what the word says in the place of prayer. Do this continuously until you see a change.

*I bow down toward Your holy temple and give thanks to Your name for Your loving devotion and Your faithfulness; You have exalted Your name and Your word above all else* ( Psalms 132:2).

God's word and name is exalted above all situations or report, insist on it.


*CONFESSION*: The word is working in me. I cannot be defeated. I have the living word of God in me. I insist on God's word which says, (...quote scriptures relevant to your request) and I turn this negative situation around in Jesus name.
