DEVOTIONAL 0652 | Source start line: 60212
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
10/1/22, 6:45 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: ProjectSave Devotional
Saturday,
October 1st, 2022.

*HIS REWARD OR THEIR AWARD*?

Hebrews 11:6 And without faith it is impossible to please God, because anyone who comes to him must believe that he exists and that he rewards those who earnestly seek him. NIV


The culture of awarding believers is relatively new to the body of Christ. Years back, it was rare to see special award ceremonies given to church workers.



It is not out of place to recognize outstanding performance and to encourage those that are lax.


In the bible, Paul commended a few brethren for being outstanding:  *"Greet Andronicus and Junia, my fellow Jews who have been in prison with me. They are outstanding among the apostles, and they were in Christ before I was*"( Romans 16:7).


However, God's reward supercede man's award. Sometimes ago,  I received some awards. I looked at the criteria set for the decision made. I discovered they judged based on what they saw.

It is human to applaud people based on visible performance. I looked through the scriptures especially Acts of apostles, I didn't see anyone who was given an award in church based on performance. Not even those that gave their all.


Everyone worked for the Lord because of love and they knew that God is the rewarder of those that diligently seek him. In fact,  they were ready to die If death was involved.

Paul was not looking forward to Peter's commendation but his eternal reward that was kept in heaven which the righteous judge will give to him.


2 Timothy 4:8 says, *"henceforth there is laid up for me a crown of righteousness, which the Lord, the righteous judge, shall give me at that day: and not to me only, but unto all them also that love his appearing.*"


*Are you labouring for award or reward?  What men will give to you as recognition for a job well done is an award not a reward.*


Human award can be a wooden plaque or something the eyes can see but prone to perishing. *Kingdom labour is something that should be done without thinking of who will say, 'thank you'.*

I know it hurts when you're so busy for the Lord without due recognition. I know how it feels when those that should say,  'well done' are your fiercest critics but If I may ask, 'are you working for them or for the one that has called you'?

*The moment you switch your focus away from the one that called you,  you'll find yourself labouring to be awarded by mere men. Your loyalty will be to men and not to God.*

I am not condemning award but God's reward is better. He will give it only to those that diligently seek Him. *In my opinion, silent labours that no one knows about attract eternal rewards. Everyone should have a silent labour in the kingdom that no one knows about*.


*There are many we have awarded but without reward in heaven.* Some are masters in eye-service. They know how to impress to be seen by Pastors or their leaders.

The Pastor may say,  "bro Joe deserves an award for what we see him doing in church"  but God may be saying "bro Stephen  deserves a great eternal reward for what he is doing that the church/pastor cannot see."


*When we get to heaven, earthly accolades like: 'he is the pastor of the fastest growing church or he pastors the church with the biggest cathedral' will cease.*


In heaven,  God's reward will humble many and we shall be surprised at how those we don't know or those we thought were doing nothing will be rewarded.

*Labour faithfully and for the applause of the one who sees what men cannot see. Labour for His "Thank you" not their "Thank you".*


*PRAYER*: Father, I receive the grace to work with my whole heart whether I'm seen or not, In Jesus name. Amen

DEVOTIONAL 0653 | Source start line: 60361
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
10/2/22, 2:14 PM - Lanre Oyeleke: ProjectSave Devotional
Sunday,
October 2nd, 2022.

*FRANCIS GAMBA*

Acts 5:41 So they went on their way from the presence of the Council, rejoicing that they had been considered worthy to suffer shame for His name.


In the year 1554, Francis Gamba, a Lombard of the Protestant persuasion, was apprehended and condemned to death by sentence in Milan.

At the place of execution, a monk presented a cross to him, to whom Gamba said, "My mind is so full of the real merits and goodness of Christ that I want not a piece of the senseless stick to put me in mind of him." For this statement, his tongue was bored through and was burnt.


The history of the church is the history bloodshed and persecution. It is filled with gory incidents of men and women who were murdered for their faith in Christ. Some were thrown to wild animals and others were crushed to death.

*The freedom of worship  we have today was paid for by the price of blood. The bible you carry in your hands is the product of some people's resilience and blood. As the church was persecuted so was the Holy Bible*.


When people hear about the  persecution of believers in history, what comes to mind is that they faced persecution from outsiders or unbelievers. Yes, that true but not the whole truth.

At the onset of the protestant reformation, the Romish church persecuted the protestants even to the point of mass execution.

In 1209, in the city of Beziers, one of the Romish church leaders told some men that they can escape purgatory and enter heaven straight If they kill the protestants. Sixty thousand protestants died as a result of that massacre.

We can go on and on telling stories of how the saints that has gone ahead of us shared in the suffering of Christ. They were convinced about their faith and were ready to give up anything including their lives for sake of what they believe.

Romans 8:17 says, *"and if children, heirs also, heirs of God and fellow heirs with Christ, if indeed we suffer with Him so that we may also be glorified with Him.*"

*Everyone of us in one way or the other will suffer certain things for the sake of our faith in Christ. Ours may not death or physical harm but sufferings may come in form of deprivation for not compromising*.

*I don't know any true believer who hasn't suffered some form of persecution for the sake of Christ. If there is, the faith of such a person is questionable.*

Suffering for Christ's sake is part and parce of our calling: *"For to you it has been granted for Christ’s sake, not only to believe in Him, but also to suffer for His sake,*"( Phillipians 1:29).

Paul wasn't talking to the Phillipians alone. That scripture applies to everyone who comes to Jesus. It is a matter of "When" not "If".

Let me ask you, when the day of suffering for the kingdom comes, what will you do? Will you escape through the door of compromise or take your stand?

*PRAYER*: Father, I receive the grace to stand for Christ  when the storms of persecution rage. I receive inner strength to stand by my convictions in Jesus name

DEVOTIONAL 0654 | Source start line: 60478

10/3/22, 7:06 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: PROJECTSAVE DEVOTIONAL
Monday,
October 3rd, 2022.

*WHAT ARE YOU DOING ABOUT THAT DIVINE VISION FROM HEAVEN*?

"Acts26:19 So then, King Agrippa, I was not disobedient to the vision from heaven*. NIV

Bro. Gbile Akanni shared the story of a man God called to do the work of an  evangelist in Africa. He received the call of God as a young man but he didn't obey promptly.


This man negotiated his calling with God by asking  for more time so that he could do certain things. Also, the man told the Lord that he needed to plant a church that'll serve as a support base for his evangelistic calling in Africa. Something that wasn't in God's plan for his life.


He started the church outside God's will but God still waited for him to obey His mandate. He wasted so much time and at old age, he finally accepted to come to Africa. However, this man was already rusty and couldn't do much at his age because of his delayed obedience to God's call.


Apostle Paul stood trial before king Agrippa.  He narrated his salvation encounter  to him and his audience. He made it clear to them that it was  during that encounter he received his life's assignment.

Paul didn't waste time in pursuit of his calling. We all read about the exploits of Paul and how he vehemently pursued the  assignment he received in the vision.

The pursuit of his calling landed him in trouble,  jail and court. Despite his trials, he stood before Agrippa telling him that he had lived in obedience to the vision he received from the Lord.

In the face of persecution and trouble, will you devote yourself to the pursuit of that God-given vision? I don't know of any man who is fully devoted as Paul was. Apostle Paul is a kingdom role model. An enigma whose life is worth studying.

Many years ago,  I spoke to a man who claimed he had the call of God into ministry. At the time we met,  he had backslid terribly and he had abandoned everything about ministry.

Evidently,  I still sensed the hand of God upon him but he was stale. On several occasions, I have met people like that or with similar stories.

Some may even tell you how God spoke to them in a dramatic way about their calling but they are yet to obey. *Delayed obedience is dangerous. It is equal to disobedience*.

*Your vision from heaven is that unique assignment God has impressed upon your heart to pursue or fulfill*.


What vision did God give to you?  Have you neglected it or you are pursuing it? Yours may not be a vision to be in pulpit ministry but you know it is a calling that would change lives yet you didn't act on it.

*One of the dangers of delayed obedience or disobedience is that it will put your life at risk*.  Maria Woodworth Etter lost five out of six children because of delayed obedience.

She was afraid of obeying God's call because of her gender but she forgot that that the opinion of people doesn't count when God gives instruction.

It was not God that killed her children but her disobedience opened the door for the devil to inflict pain and sorrow. Are you thinking about the cost of obedience? I think it is better to obey than to lose all that you've ever worked for.

Eventually when Woodworth Etter obeyed God's calling on her life, her ministry swept the whole of Pennsylvania clean of crime.  Police wrote a report that they had no crime cases to attend to anymore because of her ministry.

Literally, everyone became holy.  *The devil knows how impactful you will become if you obey.  Failure to act on what the Lord instructed you to do will make the life of some persons deteriorate even to the point of going hell. Should it be said that people went to hell because of your delayed obedience.*?


PRAYER: Father, I receive the grace to obey God's call upon my life In Jesus name.

DEVOTIONAL 0655 | Source start line: 60537
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
10/4/22, 5:41 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: ProjectSave Devotional
Tuesday
October 4th, 2022.

*UNDERSTANDING WHAT MATTERS TO GOD* Pt.1

Philippians 1:10 For I want you to understand what really matters, so that you may live pure and blameless lives until the day of Christ’s return. NLT

*THE FRUIT OF YOUR SALVATION*.

In Paul's prayer for the Philippians, he desired that they know what matters to God so that they can live a pure and blameless life until the day of Christ's return. What a desire!

It goes to show that If we don't understand the things that matter to God, we'll be trapped in impurity and vain life.

To know what matters to God, Paul prayed saying, *"May you always be filled with the fruit of your salvation—the righteous character produced in your life by Jesus Christ—for this will bring much glory and praise to God*"(Philippians 1:11 NLT).

Every farmer is concerned about getting a bountiful harvest from his/ her farmland. I don't know if there's any farmer whose desire is to labour and not get anything in return.

Farming is very hard. Even mechanized farming is not easy especially If a large expanse of land is to be cultivated. The goal of farming is fruitful harvest.

God's desire for every believer is fruitfulness. He want our lives to bear fruit: *"You did not choose me, but I chose you and appointed you so that you might go and bear fruit—fruit that will last—and so that whatever you ask in my name the Father will give you*" ( John 15:26).

*The fruit a believer should produce is the fruit of salvation, that righteous character that shows that we have encountered Jesus. Actually, that's the pointer to the fact that we are saved*.

A man might be gifted. His life and ministry may be filled with raw manifestations but when the fruit is lacking, he'll make a mess of the gift. *The absence of the fruit makes the gift loose value*.

I see people spend time fasting and praying for the gift. Some of those you'll find on the mountain are there looking for power. How many times have we tarried in God's presence because we want to bear fruit?

That fruit of salvation is what we know as the fruit of the Spirit( Galatians 5:22-23). *The man whose life bears fruit is the man that will be pleasing and blameless before God*. We need to submit our lives to be cultivated by God.

How can we bear fruit? How can our lives be fruitful and pleasing to the Lord? The answer to that question is simple. Christ is the vine, we are the branches. If any man abide in Christ, he will bear fruit.

*Abide in me, and I in you. As the branch cannot bear fruit of itself, except it abide in the vine; no more can ye, except ye abide in me. I am the vine, ye are the branches: He that abideth in me, and I in him, the same bringeth forth much fruit: for without me ye can do nothing*( John 15:4-5 KJV).


*PRAYER*: Father, I receive the grace to abide in Christ so that my life can bear fruit in Jesus name.

DEVOTIONAL 0656 | Source start line: 60604
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
10/5/22, 6:30 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: ProjectSave Devotional
Wednesday
October 5th 2022.

*UNDERSTANDING WHAT MATTERS TO GOD* Pt.2

Psalms 8: 4-5 *what are mere mortals that you should think about them, human beings that you should care for them? Yet you made them only a little lower than God and crowned them with glory and honor*. NLT

*THE REDEMPTION OF MANKIND*

Man matters to God. Among God's creation, man is the only specimen that was created in the very image and likeness of God.

*Then God said, “Let us make human beings in our image, to be like us. They will reign over the fish in the sea, the birds in the sky, the livestock, all the wild animals on the earth, and the small animals that scurry along the ground*”(Genesis 1:26 NLT).


Man was specially designed and crafted by God to function and play unique roles on earth.  *The only creation that has connection with divinity is man not animals.*


Even after man disobeyed God, because God's plan for mankind is unique, the plan for redemption was activated immediately because God doesn't want to lose man eternally.


Right there in the garden of Eden, God quickly killed an animal, made a leather material from it to cover man's nakedness: *"And the Lord God made clothing from animal skins for Adam and his wife*"(Genesis 3:21 NLT). The killing of an animal is symbolic of the death of Christ.

The concern God has for man is the reason He sent Christ to die for mankind so that man can obtain eternal salvation.

*“For this is how God loved the world: He gave his one and only Son, so that everyone who believes in him will not perish but have eternal life. God sent his Son into the world not to judge the world, but to save the world through him*" (John 3:16-17 NLT).

God is ensuring that we all end our race with Him in glory. Heaven is for mankind not for animals.

*Since God is so concerned about the redemption of mankind, it shouldn't be of a lesser concern to us. Our lives should reflect the concerns of God*.

*As men die daily without Christ or not hearing the Gospel should bother us and lead us to take righteous action of missions and evangelism*.

Some of us are concerned about our football clubs, favourite t.v.series, trend&fashion, career, business and so on but we care less about those whom Christ died for.


*The price of blood that Christ paid for mankind shows the extent of God's concern for mankind*. One of the ways we demonstrate that love is to tell them about the price that has been paid for their sins and who paid it.

*Your love for humanity is not truly reflective of how God loves mankind if all you do is charity without Jesus, humanitarian work without the Gospel in mind*.

Some think charity is the height of love expression for mankind. No it is not. Though charity is good and it is expected of us but telling someone about Jesus is the height of it.

That sinner needs something more than acts of charity.He needs Jesus and you show you love him If you can tell him about Jesus. Charity may be a vehicle that will open the door of his heart. No doubt. Your goal should be to introduce Christ to sinners.

Don't look on while God's most precious asset dies. Don't look on while God's investment( Mankind) is in ruin.



David must have had an insight into God's concern  for mankind before asking that question: *"What is man, that thou art mindful of him? And the son of man, that thou visitest him?*(Psalm 8:4 KJV).

Man is precious to God.

*PRAYER*: Father, now I know that mankind matters to you. I receive the grace to show this concern through evangelism and missions in Jesus name.

DEVOTIONAL 0657 | Source start line: 60805
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
10/6/22, 6:49 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: PROJECTSAVE DEVOTIONAL
Thursday,
October  6th 2022.

*UNDERSTANDING WHAT MATTERS TO GOD* Pt.3

Psalms 8: 4-5 *What are mere mortals that you should think about them, human beings that you should care for them? Yet you made them only a little lower than God and crowned them with glory and honor*. NLT

*THE WELFARE OF MANKIND*

Years back as an undergraduate, in the village where my school was situated, I discovered a school where parents couldn't pay N120 for their children examination fees. That's less than a dollar.

I wouldn't know if it is because of negligence or lack of value for education but I knew poverty was a contributing factor. Some of their parents may have the money but they thought of feeding first before any other thing.

Quickly, I mobilize the brethren to meet this need. I thank God we did because the head master of the school shared their struggles with us in that regard and how our support was a relief.

*After redemption, the welfare of mankind matters to God*. Yes, it is one of the topmost agenda of God for mankind. When God created mankind, He put him in a garden where all he'll ever need were provided for.

Throughout scriptures, we saw that it is not God's will for any man to lack or live  in abject poverty and sickness. Under the old covenant, God made provision for the poor by instructing the Jews to allow the poor eat whatever grows on their farmland in the Sabbath year.

*“Plant and harvest your crops for six years, but let the land be renewed and lie uncultivated during the seventh year. Then let the poor among you harvest whatever grows on its own. Leave the rest for wild animals to eat. The same applies to your vineyards and olive groves*(Ex 23:10-11 NLT)

Under that same covenant, God instituted a tithing system for the poor and less privileged. There were three types of tithe in the old covenant: Levitical/sacred tithe( Numbers 18:21,24 ), Feast tithe ( Deuteronomy 14:22-27 ) and Tithe for the poor( Deuteronomy 14:28-29).

The latter is given to the poor, orphans, widows and Levites once in three years. It is triennial.

*“At the end of every third year, bring the entire tithe of that year’s harvest and store it in the nearest town. Give it to the Levites, who will receive no allotment of land among you, as well as to the foreigners living among you, the orphans, and the widows in your towns, so they can eat and be satisfied. Then the Lord your God will bless you in all your work*(Deuteronomy 14:28-29 NLT).

This shows the extent of God's concern for the welfare of mankind. The bible says, *"Whoever is generous to the poor lends to the Lord, and he will repay him for his deed*( Proverbs 19:17). God takes it personal when you help the poor.

The early church was concerned about people's welfare. They weren't just speaking in tongues while looking away from the needs of other brethren. Welfare of the brethren took a larger share in their contributions.

Speaking about them, the bible says, *"And all the believers met together in one place and shared everything they had. They sold their property and possessions and shared the money with those in need*(Acts of the Apostles 2:44-45 NLT).

If this is the pattern in the early church, should ours be different? God will never look away from genuine needs of mankind. He is working to meet our needs in one way or the other. That's the extent of the father's love.

The writer of Hebrews says, *"Do not neglect to do good and to share what you have, for such sacrifices are pleasing to God*"(Hebrews 13:16).

*All that is needed to supply the needs of the poor is on earth already in the hands of some persons. Included in God's provision for your life is something to give the poor. You only need to be sensitive to know what is yours for consumption and what belongs to others in what the Lord has given to you.*

Don't look away from genuine needs you can meet. It is unlike Christ to tell needy people to go and pray when you can meet their needs on the spot: *"If you can help your neighbor now, don’t say, “Come back tomorrow, and then I’ll help you*”(Proverbs 3:28).

*The prayer of the poor is sincere and heartfelt. Raise prayer warriors from among them by meeting their needs.* It should be noted that we are not talking about helping lazy persons. A lazy man should find a job.

However, there are people whose work is not enough to feed them. The people in that category should be our focus. One day, while I was about alighting from the vehicle, a young boy who hawked  fruit came to me begging that I should buy his fruit because that was what he was living on.

I didn't intend buying the fruit but out of compassion, I bought some because the money he realized that day will provide for his meal. Moreso, he was too young to hawk.

God is not happy seeing men go to bed hungry at night. Do something about it. Your are His agent on earth to meeting such needs.


*ACTION POINT*: Don't be selfish. Prayerfully locate people that have genuine needs around you and lend a helping hand.

DEVOTIONAL 0658 | Source start line: 60925

10/7/22, 6:38 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: PROJECTSAVE DEVOTIONAL
Friday,
October 7th, 2022.


*UNDERSTANDING WHAT MATTERS TO GOD* Pt.4

Psalms 8: 4-5 *What are mere mortals that you should think about them, human beings that you should care for them? Yet you made them only a little lower than God and crowned them with glory and honor*. NLT

*THE WILL OF GOD*.


One book that helped me greatly is "Following God's Plan For Your Life" and "Plans, Purposes & Pursuit" by Kenneth E. Hagin of blessed memory. If you haven't read those books, get them and read them.

I will not be bold to talk on the subject of God's will without having read and followed the teachings in those books.


Talking and teaching about the will of God is a very sensitive issue especially at this point in time when most people want to figure things out with their brain.


*The will of God is simply the plan and purpose of God*. The popular Jeremiah 29:11 says, *"For I know the plans I have for you,” says the Lord. “They are plans for good and not for disaster, to give you a future and a hope*"(Jeremiah 29:11 NLT).

Believers need to know God's got a plan for their lives. This truth need to be emphasized and re emphasized over and over again. How can a good God who is loving and caring not have a plan for His creation?

*God's plan and purpose matters to Him more than our plans and schemes*. A lot of people figure their lives out using their brains because they think God's plan isn't as good as what they're planning in their mind. That's why many are living in regret today.

*God's commitment is not to your plans neither is he committed to your schemes but His commitment is to His plans for your life*. When you walk out of that plan, you walkaway from His coverage.

We must find God's will for several aspects of our lives, marriage, finances, career, country of residence, ministry, etc. Alot of  Nigerians are relocating out of Nigeria but only few are asking God If He has such in plan for them.

In the days of Isaac, there was economic hardship just as we have today. Isaac wanted to relocate to Egypt where majority went. I suppose that Egypt was economically stable yet the Lord stopped him and gave him direction on where to stay.

*A severe famine now struck the land, as had happened before in Abraham’s time. So Isaac moved to Gerar, where Abimelech, king of the Philistines, lived. The Lord appeared to Isaac and said, “Do not go down to Egypt, but do as I tell you*(Genesis 26:1‭-‬2 NLT).

It amazes me how Christians join the bandwagon without seeking God's face. Most people are victims of bandwagon effect. They do what everyone is doing. *What everyone is doing may be popular but it may not be the right thing for you*.


*The life a believer is meant to be a purposeful life not a common life. Common living is simply living for survival but purposeful living is living in the will of God.*

Beware of doing what is popular. Often, it takes people far away from the will of God for their lives.

*The best place to be in life is in the will of God*. The best career for you is the career that aligns with the will of God for your life likewise ministry. It is possible to be in ministry and not be in the will of God in ministry.  The best marriage is marriage contracted in the will of God.

*Since the day our generation started saying "God has no hand in the choice of whom to marry", that was when the foundation for most of the marital problems we see today began.*

Many who thought they could figure out marriage with their brain or because of what they learnt in books are crying today in their marriages. The proliferation of marriage teachers, seminars, books, conferences didn't reduce the numbers of failed marriages within the church.

*As long as the will of God and His principles are jettisoned, nothing works.*

Who do you think God is? An arbitrary man who created humans without a plan and purpose for them? *We belittle the omniscience of God when we do things our own way instead of His own way.*

Before you take any step, follow this counsel: *"Don’t act thoughtlessly, but understand what the Lord wants you to do*(Ephesians 5:17 NLT).

If you ask Him about what to do, He will speak to you: *"The Lord says, “I will guide you along the best pathway for your life. I will advise you and watch over you*"(Psalms 32:8 NLT).



*PRAYER*: Father, reveal your will to me as regards (...mention the areas in need of clarity) and help me to be at the centre of your will always in Jesus name.

DEVOTIONAL 0659 | Source start line: 61020

10/8/22, 8:31 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: PROJECTSAVE DEVOTIONAL
Saturday,
October 8th, 2022.


*UNDERSTANDING WHAT MATTERS TO GOD* Pt.5


Psalms 8: 4-5 *What are mere mortals that you should think about them, human beings that you should care for them? Yet you made them only a little lower than God and crowned them with glory and honor*. NLT

*THE HOLY SPIRIT*.

The indwelling and empowerment of the Holy Spirit matters to God. If not, Christ wouldn't have said: *"But the Comforter, which is the Holy Ghost, whom the Father will send in my name, he shall teach you all things, and bring all things to your remembrance, whatsoever I have said unto you*".

*Without the Holy Spirit, the believer's walk becomes boring and merely religious*.


At salvation, the Holy Spirit indwells the believer: *"He is the Holy Spirit, who leads into all truth. The world cannot receive him, because it isn’t looking for him and doesn’t recognize him. But you know him, because he lives with you now and later will be in you*"( John 14:17 NLT).

All believers have the Holy Spirit dwelling inside them. Apostle Paul wrote, *“Do you not know that your bodies are temples of the Holy Spirit, who is in you, whom you have received from God? You are not your own; you were bought at a price. Therefore honor God with your bodies*” (1 Corinthians 6:19–20).


Aside the indwelling of the Spirit, every believer is meant to have the Spirit of God upon him/her. This is what we know as baptism of the Spirit. The initial evidence is speaking in tongues.

Some still argue whether one can be filled with the Spirit without the initial evidence of speaking in tongues. I know alot of scholarly arguments abound here.


However, I prefer to stick to evidence of scriptures without engaging anyone in a debate. The very first time the Holy Spirit came upon the church, everyone spoke in new language.

*And everyone present was filled with the Holy Spirit and began speaking in other languages, as the Holy Spirit gave them this ability*( Acts of the Apostles 2:4 NLT).

In the house of Cornelius, as Peter preached, the Holy Spirit came upon his household: *"Even as Peter was saying these things, the Holy Spirit fell upon all who were listening to the message. For they heard them speaking in other tongues and praising God. Then Peter asked *"(Acts of the Apostles 10:44, 46 NLT).

In Acts 19:1-7, Paul ministered to some believers and they got baptized in the Holy Spirit with the evidence of speaking in tongues.

Jesus referred to this experience as a river that is flowing.



*On the last day, the climax of the festival, Jesus stood and shouted to the crowds, “Anyone who is thirsty may come to me! Anyone who believes in me may come and drink! For the Scriptures declare, ‘Rivers of living water will flow from his heart.’” (When he said “living water,” he was speaking of the Spirit, who would be given to everyone believing in him. But the Spirit had not yet been given, because Jesus had not yet entered into his glory*( John 7:37‭-‬39 NLT).

The Holy Spirit upon the believer is the empowerment of the believer for kingdom service especially on the field of souls: *"But you will receive power when the Holy Spirit comes upon you. And you will be my witnesses, telling people about me everywhere—in Jerusalem, throughout Judea, in Samaria, and to the ends of the earth*” ( Acts of the Apostles 1:8 NLT).

You cannot do exploit for the Lord without the empowerment of the Spirit. How do you think we can change our world in the absence of the Spirit?

The Holy Spirit is so important to the life of the believer that was why Jesus commanded the disciples to tarry for Him in Jerusalem: *"And, behold, I send the promise of my Father upon you: but tarry ye in the city of Jerusalem, until ye be endued with power from on high*"( Luke 24:49).


The empowerment of the Spirit is not optional for the believer. It is a must. Every child of must be baptized with the evidence of speaking in tongues.

It is one of the ways to enrich your spiritual life and have a flourishing walk with God.


*PRAYER*: Holy Spirit, fill me.

10/9/22, 4:42 PM - Lanre Oyeleke: ProjectSave Devotional

October 9th, 2022.


*UNDERSTANDING WHAT MATTERS TO GOD* Pt.6

Hebrews 10:25 *And let us not neglect our meeting together, as some people do, but encourage one another, especially now that the day of his return is drawing near*.

*FELLOWSHIP AND PASTORAL CARE*.

Sometimes ago, a lady reached out to me on facebook asking for counsel on a particular issue. I decided to ask about her pastor and her commitment in church.

I asked because she needed someone that can monitor her up closely. Not some social media counsel and counseling. Her issue is such that requires proper pastoral attention.

Her response to my question shows she wasn't under pastoral oversight and care. She talked it down in a way that is repulsive.  With my little experience, I know it is not so wise to counsel such a person remotely.

We live in an age where people are closing their hearts against physical fellowship with the brethren and submission to pastoral care. I will never be a party to modern ideologies that downplay the place of church and proper pastoring. Never!

*Your sad experience with a church or that pastor doesn't make all churches and pastors bad. If the driving of a wreckless driver won't stop you from boarding another vehicle, being under a pastor who acted unchristianly shouldn't stop you from submitting to another*.

Don't let the devil preach hate and disgust to you. Going to church and submitting to a Pastor is biblical. In fact, it is a command.

*And LET US NOT neglect our meeting together, as some people do, but encourage one another, especially now that the day of his return is drawing near*( Hebrews 10:25). Did you see "Let us Not"?

About submitting to Pastoral care, the writer of Hebrews said, *"OBEY your leaders and SUBMIT to them, for they are keeping watch over your souls, as those who will have to give an account. Let them do this with joy and not with groaning, for that would be of no advantage to you*"(Hebrews 13:17).

These words aren't mine but God's. Who am I to go against what is commanded by the most high? If fellowship and pastoral care doesn't matter to God, God wouldn't have commanded it.

*Online streaming of services cannot replace that atmosphere of physical services.* We need contact with the brethren. The value of being edified in a physical meeting is unquantifiable.

*True fellowship with one another doesn't really happen in online services. This can only happen when we meet to raise holy hands unto the Lord.*

The church is God's design to edify and build the saints. Imagine buying a car and not taking it to the service station for regular servicing? You know what will happen.

Don't let the voice of modernism and new age beliefs pitch you against the church and your Pastor. If you notice a church is deviating from the basics of the faith, you're free to switch to another fellowship centre where your spiritual needs will be met.

Another common anomaly is when people attend a church but have a different person pastoring them. I have experienced this with many folks. *If you don't trust your Pastor to lead and give the necessary nurturing, why are you still there?*

It is never God's design that your church is different from where you're getting pastoral care. *Your pastor should be in your church and your church should be where your pastor is.*

*And I will give you shepherds after my own heart, who will guide you with knowledge and understanding*( Jeremiah 3:15).

Don't attend a church because it is the popular church in town. *Your loyalty shouldn't be to the popularity of a church but to sound doctrine, Christlike pastoring and nurturing that you get there*. Don't be lost in the crowd because you want to identify with a church.


*ACTION POINT*: If you don't fellowship with a church, prayerfully ask the Lord to lead you to one. Also, submit to sound pastoral care and stop seeking for pastoring on social media. True disciples aren't raised that way.

10/10/22, 7:22 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: PROJECTSAVE DEVOTIONAL

*UNDERSTANDING WHAT MATTERS TO GOD* Pt.7

1 Thessalonians 4:16-17: *For the Lord himself will come down from heaven with a commanding shout, with the voice of the archangel, and with the trumpet call of God. First, the believers who have died will rise from their graves. Then, together with them, we who are still alive and remain on the earth will be caught up in the clouds to meet the Lord in the air. Then we will be with the Lord forever.* NLT

*RAPTURE OF THE SAINTS*

The world has heard about several wonders. We have read and heard about the 7th wonder of the world and other things that seem to draw attention of intellectuals.

There is an event that will happen which will surpass all the world's wonders ever seen. This event will be divinely orchestrated without human input. All the wonders in the world are purely human in design and input but the coming wonders shall be totally divine.

We call it the rapture of saints. That's the last world wonder. The saints of God shall disappear from the earth to reappear in heaven. Hallelujah!!! I can't wait to experience this glorious wonder.

When we rapture to heaven, we shall be with the Lord for seven years in heaven. Two major events shall happen up there: Bema judgement( 2 Corinthians 5:10, Romans 14:10)  and marriage supper of the lamb( Eph. 5: 27; Rev. 19: 1-14; Matt. 22: 1-14).

At Bema judgement, the believer shall receive the reward for work done while in the body. At marriage supper of the lamb, we shall feast with Christ our groom. It shall be a glorious union.

The message on rapture is lost in so many pulpits today. Some go to church in a year without hearing about the coming of Christ. Apostle Paul said, *"So encourage each other with these words*"( 1 Thessalonians 4:18 NLT).

The message about rapture should be an encouragement to every believer. There's so much sorrow, struggle and pain on earth. Some have lost their loved ones and things aren't going on well. However, knowing that we shall meet with Christ someday should encourage us all.

The message of rapture should make us prepare and expectant. The lines of one of my favourite hymn reads:

When Jesus comes to reward His servants,
Whether it be noon or night,
Faithful to Him will He find us watching,
With our lamps all trimmed and bright?

Are you prepared to meet the Lord?

*PRAYER*: I receive the grace to live ready in Jesus name

October 11th, 2022.

*TELL OTHERS ABOUT HIM*

2 Tim 1:8 So never be ashamed to tell others about our Lord... NLT

Years back, something unusual happened in a bus I boarded in Lagos. A man( an Islamic cleric) in that bus taught on the subject of marriage.

I was surprised the subject of marriage became a major discussion in the bus. It was a marriage seminar that held in that bus. People paid rapt attention.

He taught about the different types of people no one should marry. If my memory won't fail me, he sold books and offered to consult and counsel people in his office.

Indirectly, I knew he only used that message to gain the attention of his listeners because he was out there propagating Islam without fear or shame.

At other times, I have met traditionalists in the bus selling their charms and promoting the devil shamelessly. This is common in Lagos.

I'm sure they'll be promoting their beliefs when they meet people one-on-one not only when they're faced with a crowd. They're not ashamed to sell charms.

If traditionalists can be so bold to sell charms, why can't we share Christ with people publicly?

*The message of the gospel is the greatest message and that's the need of the hour globally*.  Jesus is the answer for the world and the myriads of problems the world is facing.

You should not be ashamed to tell your friends, families, neighbours and customers about the good news of our Lord Jesus Christ.

No one loves to break bad news. It takes tact to tell someone about a sad event but no-holds-barred when sharing about moving to a new house, buying a new car or announcing wedding plans.

Sharing such is delightsome. Who doesn't like to hear good news?

*The wonders of redemption is much more than those things. No other news beats the message of eternal life, justification, atonement and all that Christ did for us on the cross. Hallelujah!!! It is sweet to tell.*

I love that hymn titled "Wonderful Story of love." The first line of the song says, "Wonderful Story of love, tell it to me again." Everyone loves goodnews.

*Sinners know they are sinners. What they want to hear is the way out of their sins. The sinner must hear about the price that was paid for his redemption*.

Why are you ashamed to tell sinners about the very message that liberated you?

The Apostles of old preached this message everywhere without fear, they were jailed for it and most of them died for this gospel message because they were convinced about it as a message worth sharing.

I want to implore you not to be ashamed of sharing the goodnews of our Lord Jesus Christ.

Jesus said, *"For whosoever shall be ashamed of me and of my words, of him shall the Son of man be ashamed, when he shall come in his own glory, and in his Father's, and of the holy angels.*"( Luke 9:26).

*CONFESSION*: Father, I remove every toga of shame from my life. Henceforth,  I am a mobile evangelist, I receive the grace to share the gospel wherever I go in Jesus name.


*Kindly listen to this audio message by Bro Lanre*👇🏽👇🏽👇🏽

*UNDERSTANDING MINISTRY*

https://t.me/projectsaveministries/64

10/12/22, 6:40 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: ProjectSave Devotional
Wednesday
October 12th 2022.

*PLANNING IS NOT A SIN*

Proverbs24:27 *Do your planning and prepare your fields, before building your house*. NLT

God is a planner. A very detailed planner. He leaves nothing to chance. He planned our lives before we appeared on the scene.

The creation of the world and how He put everything in its place is a proof of detailed planning.

*Orderliness is a proof of adequate planning*. Orderliness is the plan of God for our lives and not the other way round.

Some persons see planning as helping God but they fail to realize that God has given us liberty to plan within His will for our lives.


Proper prior planning prevent poor performance. That is very true. *Good things dies in the hands of those who don't plan. Resources waste in the hands of non- planners.*

Another truism says "If you fail to plan, you have planned to fail."

God has given us the liberty to plan within His will for our lives. There are Christians that see proper planning as something carnal but when you look at the lives of such people, they don't have visible results.

*As Spirit-filled believers, all you need do is to receive the pattern from God and plan towards achieving what's on God's mind.*

Even Jesus stressed on the importance of planning when He was talking about counting the cost If a man want to build a tower.

*For which of you, intending to build a tower, sitteth not down first, and counteth the cost, whether he have sufficient to finish it? Lest haply, after he hath laid the foundation, and is not able to finish it, all that behold it begin to mock him, saying, This man began to build, and was not able to finish* ( Lk 14:28-30).

Have you seen a contractor/builder without a plan and budget? You don't start a building contract and request for money half-way because you under-budgeted, nobody would take such a contractor serious.

You have to live life consciously knowing fully that there is no time to play.

In fact, you need to plan your prayer time, study time, when to fast and so on. Devil is a deliberate planner and that is why he succeeds in his dirty business but God's people hardly plan.

Those in the world plan very well and they outwit God's children, so why shouldn't we plan? Terrorism is the plan of some people to propagate their ideology.

They have plans to take over military, commerce, politics and governance as a means of propagating their ideology and they're succeeding. We have greater power and wisdom on our side to take over our world.

Dearest, God is not against planning, please start planning and write them down.

PRAYER: Father, I receive grace to plan within your will In Jesus name.

DEVOTIONAL 0664 | Source start line: 61434

10/13/22, 6:36 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: PROJECTSAVE DEVOTIONAL
Thursday,
October 13th 2022.

*PROPER CONDUCT MATTERS*


Phil 1:27 *Whatever happens, conduct yourselves in a manner worthy of the gospel of Christ. Then, whether I come and see you or only hear about you in my absence, I will know that you stand firm in the one Spirit, striving together as one for the faith of the gospel* NIV

I watched a film sometimes ago. There was a young man who had an altercation with a woman in traffic. He abused this woman and called her unprintable names.

After sometime, this young man fell in love with a lady. Their relationship blossomed and they decided to marry each other. As the custom is, both of them decided to introduce each other to their families.

When this lady took her fiancé home to meet her mother, her mother recognized him as the man who insulted her in traffic. The woman concluded that her daughter won't marry that young man.

His public conduct stood against him where it matters most. She knew he wouldn't do better in marriage because the smoke of his character was visible.

Recently, I became so concerned about how believers conduct themselves in the public especially on social media. The words, the comments and expression of some persons drew my attention.

The privilege of internet access has made many to forsake the Christian virtue of respect and proper conduct. It is appalling to read posts filled with hate, disrespect and bile against elders and fathers in the faith from Christians.

Where the boldness to write such comes from is unexplainable but surely it is not from God. This untoward attitude is common among the young, believers inclusive.

*Social media shouldn't make you lose your virtues as a believer. Don't hide behind keypad to type comments or replies you wouldn't be bold to say If you are privileged to meet the person physically*.

You may not be able to say a word If you were to physically meet most of those whom you disrespect on social media. How you behave on the space called social media speaks volumes about you.

You may say it doesn't matter, but as they say, "Internet never forget." Sometimes, we can adjudge you as rude, uncouth and disrespectful by checking your posts and replies on social media.

That you don't share the same view or agree on an issue with someone doesn't mean you should use insulting words in responding to their views. Learn to read and ignore certain posts.

*Maturity is knowing you don't have to comment on everything online. Why should you be proud of cyber bullying and taking friends to the cleaners because you disagree on a few things?* Think about friends and opportunities you've lost because of this.

Some have lost great opportunities because of how they portray themselves publicly on social media. Dear sister, maybe your suitor lost interest because of your last post on Facebook. Watch it.

Your prospective employer may be searching your social media handles and account to see If there's anything that disqualify you. Be wise.

The biblical counsel which reads "Whatever happens, conduct yourselves in a manner worthy of the gospel of Christ" is not a counsel for onsite interaction, it cuts across.

*Don't be a good Christian when we see you physically but uncouth and  disrespectful when you're online. Access to internet bundle shouldn't make you jettison basic Christian ethics and values*.

*It pays to be Christlike onsite and online*.


*ACTION POINT*: Adjust where necessary.

DEVOTIONAL 0665 | Source start line: 61514

10/14/22, 5:56 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: PROJECTSAVE DEVOTIONAL
Friday,
October 14th, 2022.

*MY SHORT SERMON TO THE TEENAGE GIRL*

Luke 9:62 *But Jesus told him, “Anyone who puts a hand to the plow and then looks back is not fit for the Kingdom of God*.”


After preaching in a secondary school fellowship meeting, a young teenager spoke to me saying, "Sir, It seems it is hard following God as a teenager." I understood her perfectly. She spoke from the standpoint of teenage pressure she was experiencing.


I peeped into her mind with my inner eyes, I discovered she felt following God is easy for adults and not for teenagers. She felt adults have a special immunity against temptation.


Most young people feel this way because they think adulthood aligns people towards the genuine pursuit of God and righteousness automatically. I have found that to be totally untrue. An ungodly young man may grow to become an ungodly adult.


Every stage in life comes with its own temptations. While the young may struggle with sexual temptations and addictions, the old may have deep-seated struggle with financial impropriety. Why? They have bills to pay and the temptation to want to make money at all cost may set in.

Although in today's world of vanity, some youths can also do anything to make money. The young get tempted to make illegal money for show off but the old get tempted to make illegal money to pay bills. Same temptation or sin for different purposes.

There are sinful allures that comes with every developmental stage in life. In my discussion with her, I told her that maturity and growth( physical& spiritual) has not made me immune to temptations. In fact, the devil repackaged it for me.

She was surprised when I told her I also face the same challenge of reneging on my Christian commitments and consecration. However, the grace of God and my commitment to Christ has kept me.

*Commitment to follow Jesus is not conferred on people by age*. It is simply a decision one must make at any age one realizes its necessity. In fact, it is better to make that commitment as a young man or woman.

The bible says, *"Don’t let the excitement of youth cause you to forget your Creator. Honor him in your youth before you grow old and say, “Life is not pleasant anymore.” Remember him before the light of the sun, moon, and stars is dim to your old eyes, and rain clouds continually darken your sky*"(Ecclesiastes 12:1-2 NLT).

*As a youth, it is easier to build a strong spiritual life and culture in your youthful days than in the days when you grow grey hairs. If this is not true, the scripture above wouldn't have said so*.

There's a time in life when life itself won't be pleasant anymore. Only your spiritual discipline, consecration and culture that you imbibed from your youthful age will appeal to you.

*Don't postpone your commitment to God and His kingdom till a later time. There's never a convenient time for that in this ever-busy and fast changing world. The time is now*.

*Satan is after the young because he knows that's the stage of life where the foundation for the future is laid*. Once the foundation is wrong at an early age, it will take grace for such people to fulfill God's plan and purpose for their lives.

When I was much younger, I was privileged to attend a meeting of Pastors and some Christian leaders. One of the old men in that meeting said his regret in life was that he wasn't born again when he was my age. I knew that statement was laden with deep-seated regret, pains and wounds that may never heal on time.

Build your prayer life now. Make it a culture to pray and study the word daily. Start now. Your spiritual growth and commitment should not be at the mercy of new year resolution. Don't postpone evangelism till you're old. Now that you have the strength, do it.


Do things that contribute to your spiritual growth on daily basis. It is much easier now than later.


*PRAYER*: Father, I receive the grace to build a strong spiritual culture of prayer, word study, evangelism and Christian consecration starting from now in Jesus name.

10/15/22, 7:24 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: PROJECTSAVE DEVOTIONAL
Saturday,
October 15th, 2022.

*YOU ARE NOT FORGOTTEN*

Isaiah49:14 *But Zion said, The Lord hath forsaken me, and my Lord hath forgotten me. 15 Can a woman forget her sucking child, that she should not have compassion on the son of her womb? yea, they may forget, yet will I not forget thee*.

My senior Pastor told the story of a woman who forgot her child in the church. She attended one of the midweek services and left her child there in the church.

Though it wasn't intentional. Probably her mind was occupied with many cares. She realized her forgetfulness when she got home.

The opening text asked a question about whether a woman can forget her suckling child.

It is possible for a woman to forget her child. Jesus was left behind in the temple at the age of 12. His parents thought he was with them on the journey.

They realised he didn't journey with them on the third day. To forget is human but constant remembrance is exclusive to divinity.

As a reader of this devotional, men may forget you. Even your families may ignore you. Those who show they care about your condition may forget you totally but God cannot forget you.

I'm glad to inform you that God hasn't forgotten you. You are that Zion of God that is written on his palms and engraved on his wall.

*Your prayers may seem like they're bouncing back to you or not being heard at all,but how you feel doesn't matter but what God's word has said*. If God's word says you're not forgotten, that settles it.

*Often, we look at what is happening to others especially their testimonies and the dryness around us to  reach a conclusion*.

He said you are not forgotten and I'm sure your time will come soon. One thing we fail to understand is that there is an appointed time for individuals.

*The absence of major testimonies in your life isn't an indicator that you're forgotten. It simply mean your time hasn't come yet. Wait!*

*While waiting, He want us to know our real friends, understand Him better and cultivate a deeper fellowship with Him*.

I see your tests becoming testimonies and your mess becoming a message. Your deepest pains will be your gain and the glory of God shall be revealed after that story.

You are not forsaken nor forgotten, you are always on His mind and He will attend to your case at the appointed time.

*PRAYER*: Father, thank you for having me in mind. I receive grace to wait on you patiently because I know I'm not forgotten In Jesus name. Amen

DEVOTIONAL 0667 | Source start line: 61764
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
10/16/22, 7:05 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: ProjectSave Devotional
Sunday,
October 16th, 2022.

*USE YOUR AUTHORITY- DON'T BEG DEMONS*

Luke10:19 *Look, I have given you authority over all the power of the enemy, and you can walk among snakes and scorpions and crush them. Nothing will injure you.* NLT

I watched a film that didn't portray the victory of the believer in the light of scripture. In that movie, a pastor conducted deliverance and sent a demon out of a lady.

Later, he came under serious spiritual attack by demoniacal forces. When his condition didn't improve, they took him to a witch doctor. The witch doctor told him he was under attack because of the demon he cast out.

The witch doctor warned him to desist from casting out demons and he appeased the demon on his behalf. That is crass nonsense.

They belittled the power of the cross and made light the believer's authority. Jesus never told us to appease or beg demons. Some folks in the church still believe that whenever they're under attack, they need to appease or beg demons.

That's totally unbiblical. You have authority in the name of Jesus, use it. It is not a  strange thing to be attacked. We are in warfront. It is only strange If you don't stand against that attack with the word and prayer.

One of the most unbiblical practice I have seen some Christians exhibit in the place of prayer is to beg God to do what He has given them the power to deal with.

Some think they are sounding humble when they beg God to help cast out a demon. Religious folks don't like it when we talk like this but I'm only echoing what is written.


There's no where in my bible where people beg demons to leave. You need to hear the prayers of some people, you'll see why their prayers are not answered.


The opening text is clear and unambiguous. You have been given the power to trample on serpents and scorpions( referring to Satan and demons) and they shall not hurt you.

I take this scripture as my legal right to deal with devil. Why should I beg a man that my Jesus has defeated and disgraced?

*He canceled the record of the charges against us and took it away by nailing it to the cross. In this way, he disarmed the spiritual rulers and authorities. He shamed them publicly by his victory over them on the cross*(Colossians 2:14-15 NLT).


*The kind of 'respect and honour' some believers give to the devil raises a lot of questions about their salvation*. Some persons eulogize demons.   In Mark16:17, one of the things Jesus said a believer would do is to cast out demons.

*These miraculous signs will accompany those who believe: They will cast out demons in my name, and they will speak in new languages*(Mark 16:17 NLT).


I didn't see anywhere in my bible where Christ and the apostles begged a demon. Look at your situation in the face and issue a decree without apology.

*Your situation has ears. The devil is afraid of your bold stubborn audacious faith that is expressed through righteous decree.*

*CONFESSION*: I decree and declare, every situation that does not glorify God in my life, disappear and do not return again In Jesus name.

@LanreOyeleke

Twitter: @OyelekeBenjamin

Email: info@projectsaveng.org

*HAPPY SUNDAY*

DEVOTIONAL 0668 | Source start line: 61829
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
10/17/22, 6:26 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: PROJECTSAVE DEVOTIONAL
Monday,
October 17th, 2022.

*DO WHAT JOB DID*

Mark 11:25 “And when you stand praying, if you hold anything against anyone, forgive them, so that your Father in heaven may forgive you your sins.”


One of the challenges we face is forgiving those who offend us. Often, we find it hard to forgive the bible way because our human nature always want to take its pound of flesh.

Unforgiveness sits well with the flesh.and the flesh takes joy in counting scores with detractors. As believers, we shouldn't be trapped in the sin of unforgiveness because in itself, it is a sin and can hinder many things in our lives.

In the Lord's prayer, the benefit of forgiving others is clear: *"and forgive us our sins, as we have forgiven those who sin against us* (Matthew 6:12 NLT). You cannot receive forgiveness of sin If you don't forgive others.

God's expectation of us is to extend His mercy to others just as He has done to us. Several scriptures encouraged that we forgive others.

Colossians 3:13 says, *“Bear with each other and forgive one another if any of you has a grievance against someone. Forgive as the Lord forgave you*.”

Ephesians 4: 31-32 says, *“Get rid of all bitterness, rage and anger, brawling and slander, along with every form of malice. Be kind and compassionate to one another, forgiving each other, just as in Christ God forgave you*.”


Now to the issue of Job, during his ordeal, his friends hurt him deeply with their words. A man in physical pains had friends whose words pierced his soul and damaged his confidence. He must have taken offense. In fact, God rebuked them for their words.

However, we were told that Job did something we don't do often to those that offend us. Job 42:10 says, *"After Job had prayed for his friends, the Lord restored his fortunes and gave him twice as much as he had before*".

The easiest way to forgive people is not to say, 'I forgive you.' Sometimes, that statement brings momentary relief but later, you may find yourself reacting again whenever you remember the offense of the offender. Someone you thought you have forgiven, his thoughts will stir bitterness in you again. Have you experienced this?

*To truly forgive others, pray for them*. The healing you need is in the place of prayer. The proof that you've let go is when you pray for those who hurt you. *Anyone who can pray for his offenders has conquered unforgiveness.*

*Humanly, it is not easy to pray for someone you don't love. It takes love and clear-mindedness to pray for people. In our deepest pains, we may find ourselves praying the prayer of retribution*.

*If we can pray the prayer of love and mercy for our offenders, we'll receive divine infusion of grace to forgive*. The man you pray for often you'll rarely fight or quarrel with the person let alone live with the person in unforgiveness.

If you're struggling to forgive someone, do what Job did: start praying for the person. In no time, that unforgiveness will melt like wax.

*PRAYER*: Father, I receive the grace to forgive in Jesus name.

DEVOTIONAL 0669 | Source start line: 61993
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
10/18/22, 6:38 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: ProjectSave Devotional
Tuesday
October 18th, 2022.

*RESTORING RELATIONSHIPS*

Matthew 5:23-24, *“Therefore if thou bring thy gift to the altar, and there rememberest that thy brother hath ought against thee; Leave there thy gift before the altar, and go thy way; first be reconciled to thy brother, and then come and offer thy gift*.”


I value relationship alot. I've been in ministry for years now and I can boldly say that my life and ministry has benefited from flourishing relationships I nurtured from way back.

*No matter how pious and devoted a man is, If he's not skilled at nurturing relationships, he may suffer repeated setbacks in life*. The success of almost everything we do hinges on quality relationship.

However, relationships can experience some strain and it can break as well. This is highly possible because human interactions are without issues.

To forgive someone is not enough. After forgiving the person who offended you, the next stage is to restore the relationship.

In case you're the offender, you're expected to take steps to restore the relationship as well. If you have wronged somebody and it has caused them to have something against you, then doesn’t it seem right to go to that person and try make things right?

It is very easy to forgive people when they come to us but it is often uneasy to apologize to those we have wronged in one way or the other. Pride is the problem here.

What we want others to do to us we ought to do it unto others. In Matthew 7:12, Jesus says,  *“Therefore all things whatsoever ye would that men should do to you, do ye even so to them: for this is the law and the prophets*.”


*Those who value relationships don't wait for others to come, they always make the first move.* The same applies in marriage. Don't wait for your spouse to apologize before you forgive and restore your relationship. Be the first to do that for peace sake.

*Broken men apologize even when they're right simply because they value their relationship above all else*.

What we're learning is not human psychology, it is scriptural. Jesus taught us not to drop our offerings until we restore broken relationships.

He even commanded that we be the one to take the first step towards reconciliation and restoration.

Matthew 5:23-24, *“Therefore if thou bring thy gift to the altar, and there rememberest that thy brother hath ought against thee; Leave there thy gift before the altar, and go thy way; first be reconciled to thy brother, and then come and offer thy gift*.”

We are not saying that you  should go seeking everyone who has offended you and vice versa. Doing that will amount to putting a burden and yoke on your neck. It is practically impossible to do that.

That also doesn't mean you'll maintain cordial relationship with everyone who offended you after forgiving them. Being around some persons is harmful and inimical to your total being. Such, forgive and maintain a healthy distance.

When you know someone has tendencies to harm you, knowing what he/she has done before, it makes no sense to always hang around such persons. That's practical wisdom. Just forgive and move on.

However, there are people and persons the Spirit of God will lay on your heart to restore broken relationship with. Those whom their relationship with you is tied to God's plan and purpose for your life. When you sense that prompting, just obey.

Don't allow pride and ego rob you of the benefits that is hidden in such destiny relationships.

*ACTION*: Who has offended you? If you know any, please forgive. Who did you offend? If there's any, please go reconcile with the person and restore your relationship.


*Kindly listen to this audio message by Bro Lanre*👇🏽👇🏽👇🏽

*REASONS GOOD PEOPLE MARRY BAD PEOPLE*

https://t.me/projectsaveministries/45

DEVOTIONAL 0670 | Source start line: 62156
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
10/19/22, 6:58 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: ProjectSave Devotional
Wednesday
October 19th 2022.

*WHAT ABOUT YOU*?

1 John 1:9, *“If we confess our sins, he is faithful and just to forgive us our sins, and to cleanse us from all unrighteousness*”.

You might have heard many sermons about forgiving others and restoring relationship through reconciliation but only a few about forgiving yourself.

It is harder to forgive ourselves than to forgive others. I know this personally by experience.

*Those that have perfectionist tendencies and always want to be right at all times may find it hard to forgive themselves when they make mistakes*.

*Failure to forgive yourself is as sinful as when you refuse to forgive others*.


Another problem we have along this line is unbelief. How? *Those who fail to forgive themselves hardly believe they're forgiven by God*.

*It is impossible to believe in God's forgiving grace and still have problems forgiving oneself*. Those who believe in the power of God to forgive will never wallow in guilt.

Some are fond of asking God for forgiveness for sins committed decades ago. If they pray ten times in a day, they'll ask forgiveness over the same sin ten times.

An extreme I've heard people recommend is to tell others to fast and pray to be forgiven. Hogwash! You don't earn God's forgiveness through fasting and prayer.

*Forgiveness is God's act of mercy and grace. We receive it when we confess and forsake our sins.*

Unscriptural teachings about forgiveness makes people not to forgive themselves.

God Himself doesn’t even want to remember our sins (Isaiah 43:25), so why should we?

*I, even I, am he that blotteth out thy transgressions for mine own sake, and will not remember thy sins*(Isaiah 43:25 KJV).

Are we smarter then God to remember something He chooses to forget? Of course, we are not smarter than God.

*One of the ways the enemy weaken our faith and prayer life is to make us hate ourselves because of our mistakes*.

It is worse when we stay around those who constantly remind us about our gaffes.

*It is better to avoid environments and people that constantly remind you about your mistakes*. Some are working with Satan in the ministry of accusation.


Your walk with God won't flourish If you're haunted by the sins God has forgiven and forgotten.

God is rich in mercy. He is not out to look for your fault and count your sins against you If you've truly repented.

*For God was in Christ, reconciling the world to himself, NO LONGER COUNTING PEOPLE'S SINS AGAINST THEM. And he gave us this wonderful message of reconciliation* (2 Corinthians 5:19 NLT).

Don't join the devil in his ministry of accusation. Please, for your sake, forgive yourself.

*ACTION POINT*: Ponder on all scriptures cited in today's devotional and look for additional bibles verses on forgiveness.

DEVOTIONAL 0671 | Source start line: 62257

10/20/22, 6:17 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: PROJECTSAVE DEVOTIONAL
Thursday,
October 20th 2022.


*WHAT UNFORGIVENESS WILL DO TO YOU*


Matthew 18:34-35, *“And his lord was wroth, and delivered him to the tormentors, till he should pay all that was due unto him. So likewise shall my heavenly Father do also unto you, if ye from your hearts forgive not every one his brother their trespasses*.”


I was privileged to pray for a young teenager sometimes ago. She sent for me through her dad that she had some confessions to make and that I must be there.

Before I got there, she didn't say anything about what she intended to confess to her dad and her guardian whom she stayed with but as I got there, she began to talk. Actually, a demon spoke through her saying he wanted to destroy her.

She confessed how she was demonized and the harm she had done to her host and his family. Truly, the demon in her dealt with that family. All the years she was with them, everything was on standstill in that family.

As she confessed, she felt a strangling effect on her neck. With God's help, we silenced and sent the demon out of her. Though her deliverance was momentary because there were other issues that needed to be dealt with.

I was curious to know why a young girl of her stature and age became so demonized. In our discussion, I discovered she opened the door of her heart to demons through unforgiveness.

She was bitter towards her parents because she felt neglected from childhood. She thought her parents loved her siblings more than her. To make matters worse, she lived with her aunt from childhood so she felt unloved.

*Those that live in bitterness towards others and themselves end up harbouring demons.* Believers can open the door for demons to torment them If they don't walk in love.

Jesus warns us about this in Matthew 18:23-35. Some persons who live under demonic torment perpetually are harbouring bitterness in their hearts. *The moment unforgiveness is given a place, you have legalized demonic torment in your life.*

No matter the number of deliverance services you attend, your situation cannot change until you forgive. This is the price you'll pay If you refuse to let go.

Unforgiveness is also known as bitterness. The bible gave stern warnings about bitternes: *“Looking diligently lest any man fail of the grace of God; lest any root of bitterness springing up trouble you, and thereby many be defiled*"( Hebrews 12:15).


Bitterness is also known in the Bible as spiritual poison: *“For I perceive that thou art in the gall (poison) of bitterness, and in the bond of iniquity*"( Acts 8:23).

If you remain in unforgiveness, you prevents God from forgiving your own sins!
Unforgiveness puts up a wall in our relationship with our heavenly Father.

Jesus was very clear that if we are to be forgiven, we cannot be unforgiving towards others: *“But if ye forgive not men their trespasses, neither will your Father forgive your trespasses*”( Matthew 6:15)

The price of unforgiveness is high, you can't pay it. Endeavour to walk in love.

*ACTION POINT*: Don't dwell in unforgiveness. Forgive and let go.

DEVOTIONAL 0672 | Source start line: 62383

10/21/22, 6:58 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: PROJECTSAVE DEVOTIONAL
Friday,
October 21st, 2022.

*VICTORY OVER SATANIC ACCUSATION*

Rev 12:10 *And I heard a loud voice saying in heaven, Now is come salvation, and strength, and the kingdom of our God, and the power of his Christ: for the accuser of our brethren is cast down, which accused them before our God day and night*.

I was once a victim of satanic accusation. There was a time in my life I became guilt-ridden years after I gave my life to Christ. The devil played the videos of my forgiven past to me and I felt unsaved.

Feelings are fickle. We walk by faith not by feelings. That period of my life was the worst time ever in my life because I appeared good but guilt and condemnation was eating me up inside.


One of the things that compounded my problem then were wrong teachings and unbiblical books I exposed myself to. The demon of accusation latched on those exposure to torment my heart.

The straw that broke the camel's back was when a brother I respect so much told me that a believer must restitute all past sins. It was then I knew what they called spiritual yoke and burden of the Pharisees.

*For they bind heavy burdens and grievous to be borne, and lay them on men’s shoulders; but they themselves will not move them with one of their fingers* ( Matthew 23:4).

In the opening text, Satan is referred to as "Accuser of our brethren". One of his dirty duties is to bring accusation against the children of God.

He does this through a demon known as accusing spirit.  *The accusing spirit is an anti-Christ spirit, because it approaches people with no love, but a tone of condemnation*.

This demon speaks condemnation into our hearts. It constantly tells you how much of a failure you are. *This spirit tries to make believers feel unworthy of God by reminding them of their inadequacies*.


This spirit tears down rather than builds up.
The accusing spirit will manifest in a variety of ways. It can cause accuse a person directly, it can cause them to accuse others around them, or it can accuse God in a person’s mind.

*The accusing spirit is an anti-Christ spirit because it deliberately belittles the finished work of Christ on the cross and makes you feel the blood of Jesus isn't enough to cleanse you of your past.*

*What should you do to overcome*?

Believe the word of God about the  efficacy of the blood of Jesus to cleanse and wash your sins away once and for all.

*In him we have redemption through his blood, the forgiveness of sins, in accordance with the riches of God’s grace*( Eph 1:7).

*and from Jesus Christ, who is the faithful witness, the firstborn from the dead, and the ruler of the kings of the earth. To him who loves us and has freed us from our sins by his blood* ( Rev 1:5).

You must believe that your sins are not only forgiven but they're forgotten after you confessed and repented. So anytime those past sins come to your rememberance, it can't be God.

*“I, even I, am he who blots out your transgressions, for my own sake, and remembers your sins no more* ( Isaiah 43:25).

*And I will forgive their wickedness, and I will never again remember their sins*”(Hebrews 8:12 NLT).

Believing all these scriptures isn't enough. Whenever the enemy brings accusations against you, talk back to him and rebuke him in the name of Jesus Christ. Do this often.

*They triumphed over him by the blood of the Lamb and by the word of their testimony; they did not love their lives so much as to shrink from death* ( Revelation 12:11 NIV).

*But even the archangel Michael, when he was disputing with the devil about the body of Moses, did not himself dare to condemn him for slander but said, “The Lord rebuke you*!”(Jude 1:9 NIV).

Now you know what to do when the enemy comes with his accusations. The ball is in your court.


*ACTION POINT*: Meditate of the scriptures cited in today's devotional and confess them often.
