DEVOTIONAL 0740 | Source start line: 73948
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
1/16/23, 5:33 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: PROJECTSAVE DEVOTIONAL
Monday,
January 16th, 2023.

*INTENTIONAL LIVING* Pt.16

Gen13:14 *And the Lord said unto Abram, after that Lot was separated from him, Lift up now thine eyes, and look from the place where thou art northward, and southward, and eastward, and westward*. KJV

*BE INTENTIONAL ABOUT THE QUALITY& PURSUIT OF YOUR VISION*

My mentor told us about a wealthy man who decided to give $1,000, 000 to a worthy cause in the kingdom. He won a very big contract and he felt a worthy Gospel vision must benefit from the profit made.

He approached his Pastor and asked to know what he would do If the church receives a million dollar donation. His pastor replied by saying, "I'll fix the toilets".

The rich man was curious to know If that was all the Pastor had in mind to do with $1,000,000. The Pastor couldn't find a meaningful use for that huge donation.

This rich man asked to know what the cost of fixing the toilets was. He handed the token needed for fixing toilet to the Pastor.

This rich man kept his $1,000,000. He was driving round town one day, he over heard another Pastor talking about their plans to organize a city-wide crusade that will harvest souls and build another church auditorium with a larger capacity to contain both old and incoming members. Their vision was evangelistic and qualitative for what $1,000,000 can push.

The rich man saw that as a worthy cause worth supporting. He approached the Pastor of that church and handed over the $1,000,000 to the church for the crusade and new church auditorium. This is no fiction. It is a real event that happened.

I picked a few lessons from that story. *The quality of your vision determines the quality and quantity of resources that will flow towards you*.
*Be intentional about casting and pursuit of quality vision*. It is no vision If it is not bigger than you so that you'll keep trusting God for its fulfilment. A viable vision requires the help of God for its fulfilment.

*The man that had a toilet-size vision got the resources for toilet repairs. The man that had a city-wide vision got the resources for his vision*.

The quality of your vision determines the quality of resources that will flow towards it. This year, expand your vision. You can shape your year by re accessing your vision, the scope and coverage, and what is needed to actualize it.

*Per adventure you're done with a phase in your vision, feel free to catch another vision and run with it except you're done with your assignment*. In every vision there are visions. Those in ministry understand that very well.

Paul's vision to see the Gentile world saved was no small vision. He was intentional about the pursuit of that vision. Eventually, he reached the whole Asia minor.

*With God, no vision is small. The problem we have is with small minds who can't see farther. It is always a proposition of "As far as your eyes can see"*.

Even If God's vision for your life is to be a minister in a village, a qualitative vision will make eternal impact in that village and even city dwellers may come towards the brightness of your rising. Ask John the Baptist.

He was the voice of one crying in the wilderness but the bible says, *"People from Jerusalem and from all of Judea and all over the Jordan Valley went out to see and hear John*"( Mt 3:5).

Jesus said about him, *"He was a burning and a shining light: and ye were willing for a season to rejoice in his light*"( John 5:35).

Location is not the problem but the quality and intentionality engaged in the pursuit of the vision.

*ACTION POINT*: *Be intentional about the quality and pursuit of that vision*.

DEVOTIONAL 0741 | Source start line: 74023
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
1/17/23, 5:34 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: ProjectSave Devotional
Tuesday
January 17th, 2023.

*CONQUERING DESTRUCTIVE SEEDS* Pt.1

Judges 16:20 *And she said, The Philistines be upon thee, Samson. And he awoke out of his sleep, and said, I will go out as at other times before, and shake myself. And he wist not that the Lord was departed from him*. KJV

*WAS DELILAH THE PROBLEM*?

Anytime Samson is mentioned, Delilah comes to mind. Was Delilah the reason Samson fell? Before you answer that question, we may need to look deeper.

I noticed that Samson had been messing around with strange women before he met Delilah. Read Judges14. There was a destructive habit Samson had before he met Delilah.

His sexual escapades didn't start with that destructive lady. He had a track record of sexual impurity even before he met Delilah.

*I'm of the opinion that If he hadn't met Delilah, his destructive habit would still have brought him down. Delilah wasn't his problem, his destructive habit was*.

The devil knew how great his destiny was and the kind of anointing he carried. The devil x-rayed his life and found a destructive seed in him and that was the seed of lust and lack of self-control which expressed itself through immorality.

*What brings great men down grows with them as they journey to the top*.

It is not something that jumps on them all of a sudden.  The devil is always wise to spot that destructive seed in every believer.

He strive to arrange people, places and circumstances that will nurture that destructive seed to give it full expression. Paul says, *"and do not give the devil a foothold*" ( Eph 4:27).

*Surprisingly, the devil may wait for years to get a man down but while waiting for time and season of execution, he'll be busy planning and preparing*.

*No man falls suddenly*. Every fall is a product of habits, character or attitude that wasn't dealt with properly before greatness.

Anger dealt with Moses at a time he was almost getting to the promised land. If only we know the labour of Moses, we'll see his inability to enter Canaan as a great loss.

Long before he got to that point, that destructive seed of anger was in his life. It was nurtured over time till it brought him down.

The attitude that will scatter the marriage of some persons in the future is already growing with them but they don't know.

As a reader of this devotional, I know the Spirit of God is revealing those destructive seeds in you to you.

They may not stop your greatness but they want to bring you down from the ladder of greatness.

Deal with them now.

*PRAYER:* Father, let your holy fire destroy every ingredient and seed of hell in me In Jesus name.

DEVOTIONAL 0742 | Source start line: 74105
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
1/18/23, 5:54 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: ProjectSave Devotional
Wednesday
January 18th, 2023.

*CONQUERING DESTRUCTIVE SEED* Pt.2

Deut32:51 *For both of you betrayed me... You failed to demonstrate my holiness to the people of Israel there. So you will see the land from a distance, but you may not enter the land I am giving to the people of Israel*.

*THE FIRST MEGA CHURCH PASTOR*.

Moses in the bible was the first person to pastor a mega church. Pastoring a single congregation of over 3 million for almost four decades is no mean feat.

Moses was exceptional. In Christian leadership, after Christ, one can learn a lot of leadership lessons from Moses' life.

Anytime I study the life of Moses, I don't know of any Pastor whether dead or living that had the kind of shepherd's heart Moses had.

To lead over 3 million people out of Egypt on foot and ensure they are well fed, properly governed and protected is no small task. He pastored a whole nation.

I doubt If many of us could bear a little of what Moses bore without giving up. I don't have his patience, I must confess.

The people he led criticized and frustrated him even to a point where God wanted to destroy them all except Moses and his family.

Pastor Moses pleaded with God to spare them and God did (Ex32:9-14). That's a true Pastor. If I were him, I may have told God to kill them all so that I'll be free from their troubles but this wonderful pastor made no such request

Though he was the meekest man on earth, Moses did not make it to the promise. Why?

The seed of anger in him that manifested in Egypt and later in his ministry was what prevented him from entering the promise land.

In Egypt, he killed an Egyptian out of anger(Ex2:11-15), the devil saw it and he saw in Moses what he could use to bring him down but he didn't act on it immediately.

Again, when he returned from the mountain and he saw them worshipping a calf, out of anger, he broke the stone tablet (Ex32:19).

The devil said 'yes, this seed is growing'. Funnily, his anger seems justified considering the stress of fasting, climbing mountains and praying but God didn't see it that way.

His anger was not daily but occasional, yet it was his undoing.

The straw that broke the camel's back was when he hit the rock  out of anger instead of speaking to it (Num20:11). That was where he missed it.

From the first time he killed till he lost it at the rock, If I'm not wrong in my calculations, it took a space of almost 80 solid years.

*The seed of hell that bring men down from destiny and glory sometimes doesn't grow on time but the devil knows it is there waiting for it to manifest at the time that matters most*.

Jesus boldly declared that the prince of this world "x-rayed" him but couldn't find anything that belongs to hell in him: *"Hereafter I will not talk much with you: for the prince of this world cometh, and hath nothing in me*"( John 14:30 KJV).

Can you say the same thing about your life? Christians of old pray for purging because they understand this thing.

Your earnest desire should be for a thorough purging so that God can rid your life of destructive seeds.

*PRAYER*: Father, purge from my life every destructive seed and ingredients of hell In Jesus name.

DEVOTIONAL 0743 | Source start line: 74159
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
1/19/23, 5:17 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: PROJECTSAVE DEVOTIONAL
Thursday,
January 19th, 2023.

*CONQUERING DESTRUCTIVE SEED*Pt.3

2Sam12:10 *From this time on, your family will live by the sword because you have despised me by taking Uriah’s wife to be your own*.

*THE MINISTREL'S THIRD LEG*

King David was a ministrel. An anointed one at that. He conducted deliverance for Saul without prayer. He only played music and the anointing of God upon his life sent demons away.

*Whenever the spirit from God came on Saul, David would take up his lyre and play. Then relief would come to Saul; he would feel better, and the evil spirit would leave him*( 1 Sam 16:23 NIV).

There is an aspect of King David's  story that is not commonly preached. It is common to hear about his conquests over Goliath and other enemies.

Most times, you'll hear preachers talk about the sure mercies of David and how he was a man after God's heart. All of these are in order and they're worth telling.

However, there is another aspect of his life that put his family and unborn generation  in trouble. The destructive seed of sexual immorality found expression in him. His unborn generation partook in the curse his actions attracted.

We are always quick to rebuke Solomon for marrying 700 wives and having 300 concubines(1Kings11:3) but he learnt it from David his father and he took it to the next level.

At the time of Absalom's rebellion, it was recorded that David had 10 concubines (2Sam15:16) and in his conquests, he married more wives(2Sam3:2-5).

*And unto David were sons born in Hebron: and his firstborn was Amnon, of Ahinoam the Jezreelitess; and his second, Chileab, of Abigail the wife of Nabal the Carmelite; and the third, Absalom the son of Maacah the daughter of Talmai king of Geshur; and the fourth, Adonijah the son of Haggith; and the fifth, Shephatiah the son of Abital; and the sixth, Ithream, by Eglah David's wife. These were born to David in Hebron* (2 Samuel 3:2‭-‬5 KJV).

As people were hailing him for his conquests, he was marrying more wives. The devil saw that his third leg lacked control. He waited for an opportuned time to strike.

Satan arranged a day and time for him. He stood on the top of his house and beheld a beauty called Bathsheba. She was bathing. He did this sightseeing when kings were at war.

Unfortunately, he fell for Satan's set up and he fell into adultery (2Sam11). It happened as planned. We don't how long the enemy took to conceive and execute that plan but I'm sure it took him sometime after observing him over time.

The singular action of committing adultery made him progress into murder to cover up his heinous act. He attracted the curse of God upon his life and his family. In his lifetime, his own son played out a negative script because of the curse his actions attracted.

When he was about to die, those around him already knew that being around women revives him so they brought Abishag, a young girl to revive him(1Kings1&2).

That was how bad it was in the life of David. Thank God he had learnt his lessons, he didn't touch her. *Satan uses habits, attitudes and things people indulge in over time to rubbish them later*.

*A man can be anointed for conquest and territorial dominion yet what will eventually rubbish him may be growing with him unnoticed*.  Lord, what is that destructive seed growing with me as I journey to the top?

*Beware of things you do that has no immediate repercussion. It is a set up for a total downfall at a time that matters most*.

Stop nurturing what can kill you in the long run. The man God help is the man that is sincere to him in the place of prayer. If it is lust, take it to him now.

You and I need to be sincere with ourselves as we walk with God. We must get rid of those little foxes that spoils the vine. They may be little but they cause great harm.


*PRAYER*: Father, take me to your theatre room and operate upon my heart. Remove those destructive seeds I'm nurturing In Jesus name.

DEVOTIONAL 0744 | Source start line: 74263
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
1/20/23, 5:35 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: PROJECTSAVE DEVOTIONAL
Friday,
January 20th, 2023.

*CONQUERING DESTRUCTIVE SEED* Pt.4

2Kings5:26 *But Elisha said to him, “Was not my spirit with you when the man got down from his chariot to meet you? Is this the time to take money or to accept clothes—or olive groves and vineyards, or flocks and herds, or male and female slaves? 27 Naaman’s leprosy will cling to you and to your descendants forever*”

*THE SPIRIT OF GEHAZI*

There's a spirit I call the 'spirit of Gehazi'. It is the spirit of greed, avarice and love for Mammon. Sometimes, it ruins a glorious destiny before stepping into its fullness.

Kenneth E. Hagin of blessed memory told the story of a man that was operating powerfully in the supernatural. He had a unique healing anointing.

In one of his programmes, he began to raise money indiscriminately probably because people were thrilled to see healing miracles. I noticed that people can easily be cajoled to give after seeing raw power in demonstration. That's not God's way.

*We'll always need money to run our programs and outreaches. Also, ministers welfare is biblical and not something up for debate but God expect us to go about it the bible way.*

Papa Hagin said he saw the anointing lift from him. That great Evangelist lost the anointing because of greed and love for money.

In the bible, Gehazi was positioned to take over the mantle of prophetic leadership from Elisha. Don't forget Elisha got a double portion of anointing from Elijah to become a national prophet.

Their prophetic anointing was unique even amidst other prophets in town. Everyone conceded spiritual leadership to them because they were a different breed.

Those that knew Gehazi must have tipped him as a successor of the Elijah-Elisha prophetic order but he lost it because of greed and love of money. He collected the gifts his master rejected (2 kings 5).

*The spirit of Gehazi seeks to commercialize the anointing*.  Little wonder why Elisha's anointed staff didn't work in his hands when he was told to lay it on a dead lad(2Kings4:29-36).

If the boy had risen, brother Gehazi may charge them for resurrection service. Greed was a destructive seed that grew with him and later destroyed him when it mattered most.

There are powerful singers, preachers and anointed brethren who wouldn't minister until you negotiate a fee with them. *Those who put money or material gain above souls will have limited impact*.

Some preachers prefer to be called 'speakers' so that they can appear as  someone that want to render a consultancy service not as ministers/ preachers of the word of life.

To them, that's being branded for ministry but they are grounded without knowing. It is a thing of joy to be called a bible preacher. No need to call it another name not even life coach.

*It is not easy to be anointed but it is so easy to lose the anointing through greed*. I have seen a preacher cart away all offerings raised during his ministration without taking permission from his host. Those who do such don't last in ministry.

*The ministry of some would have been global but greed for gains made it local*. Are you greedy for gain? Is money and gains your motivation for doing ministry?

*Money is a good servant but a bad master. Kill greed before it kills you*.

*PRAYER*: I mortify all traces of greed in my life in Jesus name.


====================================================================================================
DEVOTIONAL 0745 | Source start line: 74344
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
1/21/23, 5:28 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: PROJECTSAVE DEVOTIONAL
Saturday,
January 21st, 2023.

*CONQUERING DESTRUCTIVE SEEDS* Pt.5

Romans 8:13  *For if ye live after the flesh, ye shall die: but if ye through the Spirit do mortify the deeds of the body, ye shall live*. (KJV)

*HOW TO CONQUER- STOP FEEDING THE PYTHON*

My pastor told the story of a man that kept a snakelet as  pet. The snakelet belonged to the boa constrictor family of snakes.

It was a python actually. He fed the python well not knowing that he was feeding a predator that would work against him.

As the python grew, it'll crawl into the living room and stretch to  its full length on the bodies of the man's children to measure its height against that of the children.

The python did this occasionally until it grew as tall as the eldest child of the man. The man travelled and left the python hungry.

As usual, the python crawled into the man's house but couldn't find anything to eat except the man's children. He swallowed one of them to satisfy his hunger.

Can you relate with that story? What  brings men down are things they've been feeding secretly.

We have examined several cases of men in the bible who missed it because they didn't deal with what they thought was little.

The little character flaw or habit eventually  led to their downfall. It didn't jump on them all of a sudden, it grew gradually because they fed it.

*What is the path to victory*?

*The first thing is to identify the destructive seed growing in you*.

*It is impossible to confront  and conquer what you haven't identified*.

Identification is not enough but knowing how to deal with it is what guarantees victory.

Internalize the word of God: *"I have hidden your word in my heart, that I might not sin against you*" ( Psalms 119:11 NLT).

When the word of God is strongly rooted in your spirit ( not just in your head), it has the power to mortify any evil seed. That's why we encourage daily study of God's word.

The second step is to be  watchful. 1Corinthians 10:12 says: *"If you think you are standing strong, be careful not to fall*" (1 Corinthians 10:12 NLT).

Further, avoid being in environment or around people that nurtures or trigger the growth of that destructive seed.

*Take the case of sexual immorality as an example, you can't stick to porn and be around those that do not value sexual purity and still remain pure*. Never! It won't work.

*You must consciously starve the destructive seed in you and cut off every source of its nourishment*.

*Do not forget that prayer is a tool that binds all others together. It is warfare*.

Through prayer,  grace is released for victory.  Under grace, sin shall no longer have dominion over you.

Rom6:14 says, *"Sin is no longer your master, for you no longer live under the requirements of the law. Instead, you live under the freedom of God’s grace*"(NLT).

*PRAYER*: Father, I receive grace to overcome the destructive seed growing in me  In Jesus name.

*CONQUERING EVIL TRADITIONS* Pt.1

Mark7:13 *Thus you nullify the word of God by your tradition that you have handed down. And you do many things like that*.” NIV.

According to Merriam Webster dictionary, a tradition is an inherited, established, or customary pattern of thought, action, or behavior (such as a religious practice or a social custom)

From bible days, there were man-made traditions of Jews. The Gentiles had theirs. Most Jewish traditions have a touch of religious legalism while that of Gentiles is antinomian and sensual.

For instance, the Pharisees and the scribes asked Jesus why his disciples did not wash their hands before eating *according to the tradition of the elders* ( Mark 7:5).

Jesus responded by saying, *"For laying aside the commandment of God, ye hold the tradition of men, as the washing of pots and cups: and many other such like things ye do* (Mark 7:8).

There's something I want us to examine in that text. They( Pharisees & scribes) laid aside God's commandments to fulfill the tradition of the elders thereby nullifying the word of God.

*The evil traditions of men is opposed to the will of God*. In our world, we have many traditions that aren't in God's will for mankind. Where tradition is in sync with God's principle, there's no problem about that.

I was privileged to talk to a man who told me it is normal for a man to cheat on his wife. However, he forbade such for his wife.

Later, I discovered that his opinion is popular among several men in the society. Cheating is seen as normal or as an adventure which is an exclusive reserve of men. That's an evil tradition that has the backing of many.

In fact, a pastor once counseled a woman not to mind the 'sexcapades' of her husband because it is 'normal' for men(Paraphrase). Normal? Why normalize what is abnormal with God?

If a woman is caught in adultery,  it would be announced from the roof top by men who themselves are guilty of the same but If a man is caught in the act, tradition of men  may exonerate him on the spot saying it is a man's world yet God doesn't excuse such sin for any reason regardless of gender.

*Nothing is wrong with the tradition of men as long as it doesn't conflict with the word of God but where there's a conflict, God frowns at it.

*The tradition of men is the tradition of compromise, fleshly gratification and lust. The believe is that as long as it doesn't affect anyone, it is all well and good*.

It is a tradition of "the end justifies the means" even if the means is ungodly. Beware of societal norms that are acceptable but stand in opposition to what God's word says.

Apostle Paul said,  *"And be not conformed to this world[tradition of men]: but be ye transformed by the renewing of your mind, that ye may prove what is that good, and acceptable, and perfect, will of God*", Romans 12:2 ( Emphasis mine).

*PRAYER*: Father, I receive the grace to break free from negative tradition of men in Jesus name

*CONQUERING EVIL TRADITIONS* Pt.2

Mark7:13 Thus you nullify the word of God by your tradition that you have handed down. And you do many things like that.” NIV

*HOPE SHE'S NOT FROM ASABA*?

During our courtship, we visited an elder prior to our wedding. My aim was to introduce my fiancée to him formally.

I expected him to ask about my convictions and other important questions. Also, to counsel us but to my chagrin, in her presence, he asked, "I hope she's not from Asaba"?

He asked because I spent over a year in Asaba so he thought I'll marry a wife from there.  It was obvious he wasn't in tune with inter tribal marriages for reasons best known to him.

I couldn't imagine what he may have done If I had affirmed his suspicion. Would he have said I shouldn't marry her If she were an indigene of Asaba? I don't want to think about what may have happened there.

Those that aren't from Africa or third world countries where tribal differences isn't a major concern may not understand the weight of this matter. Tribalism or say inter tribal hatred is an evil tradition bedeviling Africa. Unfortunately, it crept into the church and lives of some believers.

Most of the civil wars in Africa especially in the past were inter tribal.The Rwandan genocide was between the Hutus and Tutsis, Kanyarwandan War, Biafra war and other unrest in Africa were inter tribal.


*Evaluating fellow humans and even believers from human/tribal standpoint belittles God's creation and it is a sin.*

Apostle Paul said, *"So we have stopped evaluating others from a human point of view. At one time we thought of Christ merely from a human point of view. How differently we know him now!*"( 2 Cor 5:16 NLT). Hallelujah!!!

Tradition of men says, "all men from that tribe are the same, don't marry or do business with them." *To evaluate a believer and put him/her in a tribal class puts a question mark on your salvation*.

Paul said, *"There is no longer Jew or Gentile, slave or free, male and female. For you are all one in Christ Jesus*"(Gal 3:28 NLT).

Some human tradition is full of hate and disdain for people. In some cultures, some are classified as descendants of slaves. The lineage of free born are forbidden from marrying the descendants of slaves and some Christians still hold on to this evil tradition.

They inherited the hatred and dysfunctional world view of their progenitors. The Jews were like that in the days of Jesus. They do not want to have anything doing with Gentiles yet salvation is for both Jews and Gentiles.

God loves everyone equally without a bias for tribe. We're meant to be loving like our heavenly father. Our love for humans should be without tribal preference.

Break free from tribal limitations, it can limit you in destiny.

*PRAYER*: Father, deliver me from the evil traditions of men in Jesus name.

*CONQUERING EVIL TRADITIONS* Pt.3

*GREED& MAMMON WORSHIP*

Anyone that comes under the influence of Mammon can do anything to be rich. Our generation has made mammon worship a tradition.

Internet fraud is gradually receiving the nod of people who should speak against it.

Do not subscribe to this evil tradition of greed that doesn't care about how money is made. The way of Christ is hard work, godliness and contentment with whatever we have.

Paul said, *"But godliness with contentment is great gain*"(1Tim6:6). In verse 10 of the same chapter Paul said, *"For the love of money is a root of all kinds of evil. Some people, eager for money, have wandered from the faith and pierced themselves with many griefs*".

Run from greed and love of money.

*PRAYER*: Father, deliver me from the evil tradition of greed in Jesus name.

*EXPOSED TO SERPENT ATTACK*

Ecclesiastes 10:8 *He that diggeth a pit shall fall into it; and whoso breaketh an hedge, a serpent shall bite him* (KJV).

In the bible, Satan is often referred to as the serpent. In the Book of Revelation, he was called ancient serpent and the Dragon several times.

*This great dragon—the ancient serpent called the devil, or Satan, the one deceiving the whole world—was thrown down to the earth with all his angels* (Revelation 12:9 NLT).

I don't love snakes because of its lethality. I know most people don't as well. Those who live in serpent prone areas are conscious of being attacked by serpents.

The world is Satan's territory of operation( 2 Cor4:4) and we must be careful not to expose ourselves to his attack. *Living in sin is the quickest way we expose ourselves to his attack*.

Mount Zion Film Ministries produced a film titled,  "Abejoye". In that movie, there was a family that was living fine.

The head of the home began to have an affair with one of the sisters in the church. As this affair persisted,  the devil began to attack the family of this man.

His children had nightmares and there were strange happenings in their home. The sin of adultery opened the door for demons to torment the family.

Peace came when the man repented of his sin.

*Brethren, sin can make demonic attacks to be frequent in the life of a believer*.

*The devil is always looking for an open door through which he can come into the life,  business and ministry of a believer*.

Often,  sin opens the door for Satanic attack. Sin is a legal ground on which the devil can launch attacks.


*Even If you know your place in redemption and can take authority over the devil,  If the knowledge of your redemptive right is not balanced with holy living, you will be exposed*.

It is pure deception to think your believer's authority over Satan and his minions will be effective whilst living in sin.

Sin breaks the edge of protection. *After Saul disobeyed God, a demon tormented him because he sent an invitation to hell through his disobedience*.

When Jesus healed that man at the pool of Bethesda,  he instructed him not to return to his vomit of sin lest a worse thing happen to him(John5:14).

There's a nexus between sin and persistence of demonic attack.

Some have become regular at deliverance meetings. That is not the will of God for His children. Demons are not as powerful as we think.

However,  we can empower them through sinful lifestyle. The bible says, *"When the ways of a man is pleasing to God,  even his enemies would be at peace with him*" ( Proverbs 16:7).

It doesn't mean the enemy won't attack but there's an assurance of victory.

Examine your life. Be sure you're not living in sin.

*PRAYER*: Father, I receive the grace to live in purity in Jesus name.

*WATCH WHAT YOU SAY*

Proverbs 18:21 *The tongue has the power of life and death, and those who love it will eat its fruit*.

Ten out of the twelve spies brought a negative report but only two spoke differently. They latter entered the promised land while others perished.

*If after praying you say something that is contrary to the content of your prayer,  you have repudiated your prayers*. Speaking the word of God may not yield result immediately but it would produce life and joy in the long run.

Recently, I almost slipped into negativity because of the situation of this country. I found myself almost saying what everyone is saying but I remembered my words are powerful. I spoke life into my life despite the ongoing hardship.

Stop cursing yourself and do not curse others. Your words are legal grounds on which God or the devil need to act on.

*Your life will go in the direction of your words*. What do you say about yourself?

DEVOTIONAL 0751 | Source start line: 74745
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
1/27/23, 5:38 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: PROJECTSAVE DEVOTIONAL
Friday,
January 27th, 2023.

*BE CONVINCED ABOUT THE FAITH*

Heb11:37 *They were stoned, they were sawn asunder, were tempted, were slain with the sword: they wandered about in sheepskins and goatskins; being destitute, afflicted, tormented*

In church history, Martin Luther was the father of protestant reformation of doctrines. Martin Luther wrote 95 theses, a document listing various oppressive and unbiblical practices of the Roman Catholic Church.

Luther posted his theses on the door of the church at Wittenberg on October 31, 1517, now known as Reformation Day. The reformers popularized the Latin word 'Sola' meaning only.

Their theology hinged on five 'solas' and they are: *sola scriptura: “Scripture alone”, Sola fide: “faith alone”,  Sola gratia: “grace alone”, Solo Christo: “Christ alone” and Soli Deo gloria: “to the glory of God alone*”. All these distinguished the Roman Catholic theology from the Protestant's.

However, there was a reformer who lived before Martin Luther and John Calvin. His name is John Huss. He was a Czech priest who was burned at the stake for heresy against the doctrines of the Catholic Church.

Particularly he fought against the wrong doctrines of Ecclesiology and the Eucharist as taught by the Roman Catholic Church.

Also, he opposed the sale of indulgence.  An indulgence was a payment to the Catholic Church that purchased an exemption from punishment (penance) for some types of sins.

Huss was martyred on July 6, 1415. He refused to recant his position of the charges that were brought against him.

On the day he died he was said to have stated, *"God is my witness that the things charged against me I never preached. In the same truth of the Gospel which I have written, taught, and preached, drawing upon the sayings and positions of the holy doctors, I am ready to die today*".

*How convinced are you about your beliefs in Christ? You are not convinced about your faith in Jesus If you can't die for it*.

Those we call the heroes and heroines of our christian faith had something in common- they were ready to suffer for their faith in Christ Jesus even unto death. Today, there's a reversal of that attitude.

*We no longer hear messages that deepen our resolve to follow Christ even If it means dying. Little wonder why there are many compromising Christians today*.

I doubt If most of us can endure half of the persecutions the early Christians endured. We read about them as If their experiences were tales. No they are not.

I once heard a preacher berating missionaries who suffer for their calling. He insinuated that most of them were not sent. What an error!

*Absence of suffering is not a validation of our calling neither does it invalidate it*. Modern Christians don't like this type of message because scriptures that talk about suffering for the faith isn't in their bibles.


*For it is commendable If someone bears up under the pain of unjust suffering because they are conscious of God* ( 1 Peter 2:19 NIV).

Suffering for our conviction in Christ is part of our calling in Christ: *"Yes, and everyone who wants to live a godly life in Christ Jesus WILL suffer persecution*" ( 2 Timothy 3:12).

I present to you the whole counsel of God as it was, and is and shall continue to be.

*CONFESSION*: Father, I will follow you till the end even If it means suffering for your name.

Daily, we shall be praying in tongues for one hour between 9pm- 10pm.

DEVOTIONAL 0752 | Source start line: 74859

1/28/23, 5:05 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: ProjectSave Devotional
Saturday,
January 28th, 2023.

*YOU NEED THE GIFT OF MEN*

Ephesians 4:16  *He makes the whole body fit together perfectly. As each part does its own special work, it helps the other parts grow, so that the whole body is healthy and growing and full of love*. NLT

There are gifts that come directly from God. We know these gifts to be those spiritual graces that enables believers for kingdom service.

Another class or type of gifts is what we commonly call the gift of men. This refers to a situation where God brings you into relationships, circles and associations that will boost your vision.

It is God bringing useful men your way for life and destiny. We all need the gift of men.

Sometimes, I wonder why Christ, the messiah needed the company of 12 disciples in his ministry. As God himself, one would think that He'll come to the earth and evangelize the whole Isreal alone, die and return to heaven.

He discipled his disciples and sent them out to carry on his missionary mandate even before he died: *"The Lord now chose seventy-two other disciples and sent them ahead in pairs to all the towns and places he planned to visit*" ( Luke 10:1 NLT).

After he resurrected, he commissioned them officially to go into the world to continue his ministry(Matthew28:18-19). We are partakers of that mandate. Why didn't he do it alone? Why didn't he send angels?

Another surprising thing about the ministry of Jesus was that he had people ministering to his needs. The book of Luke 8:1-4 told us that there were some women who ministered to his needs from their substance.

*Joanna, the wife of Chuza, Herod’s business manager; Susanna; and many others who were CONTRIBUTING FROM THEIR OWN RESOURCES TO SUPPORT JESUS and his disciples* ( Luke 8:3 NLT).

In essence, they were ministry partners of Jesus who ensured that he had enough supplies and provisions for his crusades and for personal welfare. Why didn't he command money to fall from heaven and carry on?

Apostle Paul also had co labourers in his ministry(Col4:7-12) and it was on record that the Phillipian church supported him financially(Phil4:15-16).

*As you know, you Philippians were the only ones who gave me financial help when I first brought you the Good News and then traveled on from Macedonia. No other church did this. Even when I was in Thessalonica you sent help more than once* (Philippians 4:15‭-‬16 NLT)

Even Paul, the rugged apostle was not a lone ranger. Brethren in Phillipi supported his apostolic ministry from their resources. One would have thought he was all alone doing his thing alone by the anointing.

In the pursuit of their vision, they didn't run with it as a lone ranger. They were surrounded by useful men.

*We will never outgrow the need for resourceful men as long as we're on earth. The earth has no place for self-made men.*

It is not enough to pray for the gift of men. We must learn how to attract and nurture meaningful relationships by loving people genuinely and caring for them.

*Those that ride on the wings of arrogant self-sufficiency mentality don't go far but those that master the art of networking and partnership will always get to the finish line on time*.

When Adam was in his perfect state, God saw the need for a help meet(Gen2:18). It was not the fall of man that necessitated him needing help, it is God's grand design for anyone pursuing a great vision to be in need of help.

If you don't want to die unfulfilled, be open to receiving help from God and man. *Destiny fulfilment is not for lone rangers. You need the gift of men*.

Pray for it and nurture relationships

*PRAYER*: Father, I need the gift of men In Jesus name.

*BEWARE OF ANTI GOSPEL IDEOLOGIES*

Galatians 1:8 *Let God’s curse fall on anyone, including us or even an angel from heaven, who preaches a different kind of Good News(Gospel) than the one we preached to you* (NLT, Emphasis mine).

In fact, she preached to a point where she said that christianity and other faith are one as long as good works is constant. Hey! That's a very dangerous ground to tread. Total error.

Eph2:8-9 says, *"God saved you by his grace when you believed. And you can’t take credit for this; it is a gift from God. Salvation is not a reward for the good things we have done, so none of us can boast about it*" ( Ephesians 2:8-9 NLT).

*Good works saves no man*. If it does, heaven will be for philanthropists only. How much of philanthropy can you do to be saved? Salvation is in Christ alone only for those who believe in his redemptive work on the cross.

*We don't do good works to be saved rather, we do good works because we are saved*. Ephesians 2:10 says, *"For we are God’s masterpiece. He has created us anew in Christ Jesus, so we can do the good things he planned for us long ago*"( NLT).

If you don't understand the gospel, please don't preach it. Just sit under a teacher and learn properly so that you can preach rightly. It is good to have zeal but knowledge is key.

*Preaching the gospel without adequate understanding leads to creation of dangerous errors that can lead people off course*.

Jesus spoke about the doctrine of men in Matthew15:9. I have noticed  the doctrine or gospel of men is causing confusion all over the place today.

*It is a doctrine that takes the focus of people away from the cross and the finished works of Christ*. The message of the cross is the authentic message the world must hear. That's our boast.

Galatians 6:14 days, NIV: *May I never boast except in the cross of our Lord Jesus Christ, through which the world has been crucified to me, and I to the world*.

The doctrine of men is 'Christopagan' in nature. Anywhere you see a mixture of Christian practices and paganism, that's Christopaganism.

Pastors must teach their members the content of the gospel message in clear terms so  they can communicate it effectively to others.

*As a soul-winner, your duty is to present Christ and his finished work to the people*. Some are proclaiming the gospel of their denomination and 'papa' not that of Christ.

Your invitation to sinners should be to Christ first before inviting them to church. Christ-centred gospel is the authentic gospel.

*PRAYER*: Father, I receive grace to present the gospel of Christ to people accurately without mixing it with dogma and heresies In Jesus name.

*DYNAMICS OF FAITH* Pt.1

Romans 10:17 *So then faith cometh by hearing, and hearing by the word of God*. KJV

*WHAT IS FAITH*?

*I define faith as believing God's word, speaking God's word and acting on God's word in the face of contrary circumstances*.


These three things are essential components of faith. What some people have is mental assent and they call it faith.

*Faith is not faith until it is rooted in God's word. The word of God is the source of the faith that works*.

I have ministered severally to people trusting God for one thing or the other. Some received their desired results while others did not.

Same applies to cases of healing and miracles. I noticed that it is easier ministering to those who understand the principles of faith and its dynamics.

From my little experience, preachers should teach how faith works and how believers can receive from God with their word-based faith.

If you want to minister healing to people, preach faith along the lines of healing, you'll see results.

Virtually all that came to Jesus received their miracles or healing by faith. Jesus was fond of saying: *"Be it unto you according to your faith*" (Matt9:29).

If faith wasn't important, Jesus wouldn't have stressed it.

If you want to build your faith to become solid and strong, do not build it around 'things' or medium. When medium is absent, what would happen to you?

Do not build it around your 'papa' or 'G.O.' but only on God's infallible and innerant word.

The psalmist says, *"For ever, O LORD, Thy word Is settled in heaven*"(Psalm 119:89 KJV). God's word is settled in heaven forever, nothing on earth can unsettle it not even your situation or present circumstances.

The psalmist says, *"The words of the LORD are pure words: As silver tried in a furnace of earth, purified seven times*"( Psalms 12:6 KJV).

*God's word has eternal integrity* Apostle Peter said, *"We have also a more sure word of prophecy; whereunto ye do well that ye take heed, as unto a light that shineth in a dark place, until the day dawn, and the day star arise in your hearts*" (2 Peter 1:19 KJV).

No other prophetic declaration is as sure as is the written word of God. If anyone sees anything contrary to what is written in the word of God against your life, stand on the written word to cancel the fulfilment of negative prophecies. Why? The written word is a more sure word of prophecy.

*The word of God in your heart and mouth will put above life's challenges.*

When you're faced with the challenges of life, search out relevant scriptures and begin to meditate on them till faith is built up in you like an edifice.

*Let that word be your confession even in face of contrary circumstances*.

*CONFESSION*: God's word is my anchor not man or medium. The word is working for me and in me to produce results In Jesus name.

1/31/23, 5:10 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: ProjectSave Devotional

*DYNAMICS OF FAITH* Pt.2

John 20:29 *Then Jesus told him, “Because you have seen me, you have believed; blessed are those who have not seen and yet have believed*.” NIV

*BELIEVING PRECEDE SEEING*

You've heard several times that seeing is believing. That's the statement of those who live in the sense realm.

I was teaching about faith in sometime ago. I told my listeners the reason  it seems the prayers of some believers are not answered is because they have reversed the bible pattern of faith.

They hold on to the popular mantra of: Seeing is believing. *In the kingdom of God, it is what you believe you will see*. Now, that's not easy for the flesh.

*The human mind loves to see evidence but God's word is the only evidence that gives the assurance of answer to prayers*. You must believe God's word more than physical evidence.

Mark 11:24 says, *"I tell you, you can pray for anything, and if you believe that you’ve received it, it will be yours*"( Mark 11:24 NLT). It is the believing that precedes the having.

That is foolish to the human mind but that's God's wisdom in getting our request answered.

*God did not put our 'receiving' in the future, our receiving should be in the present and past even if we do not see it at the moment*.

Our receiving is not tied to how we feel or what we see but our believe and absolute trust in God's word. Halleluyah!!!

A believer should not find it hard to believe. The moment you start doubting God's word and his ability to do what He said he will do, you have wasted your prayer effort.

Sometimes, I see people who love to go round asking for prayers on the same issue. Several evangelists have laid hands on them yet there's no result.

They drop names of big preachers that have prayed for them on the same issue. Such persons I don't take serious because they may want to add me to the long list.

Many of them want instant manifestation before believing their prayers are answered instead of believing instantly that they have received answers before seeing manifestation.

*The absence of instant result or physical manifestation doesn't determine the efficacy of your faith or prayers*.

Your faith should stand on the truth revealed in God's word that you have received what you prayed for.

*This is how faith works*.

*CONFESSION*: I am a believer. I believe God's word and stand on it regardless of physical result in the name of Jesus Christ.

2/1/23, 5:14 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: ProjectSave Devotional
Wednesday

*DYNAMICS OF FAITH* Pt.3

Romans4:17 *(As it is written, I have made thee a father of many nations,) before him whom he believed, even God, who quickeneth the dead, and calleth those things which be not as though they were*. KJV

*INCUBATE YOUR FAITH*

Yongi Choo told the story of how he was poor when he started out in ministry.

He asked God to provide a bicycle, desk and chair for him. He specified  the exact thing he wanted. He testified in church that he had received all those things.

Some of his members decided to visit him to verify If his testimony was true. When they got home, they saw nothing.

He told them that he was pregnant of those things. They laughed and thought he was out of his mind. He told them that every pregnancy needed time before delivery.

He got the exact type of items he requested from God after some time.

*Sometimes, faith requires a gestation period for it to deliver physical result*.  The result faith produces is not instant at all times. Abraham, the faith waited for the result of his faith for decades.

*Faith is not another magic formula. Certain processes are involved before it produces result*. Learning to wait patiently on God in faith is one virtue we must cultivate.

Hebrews 6:12 says, *"We do not want you to become lazy, but to imitate those who through faith and patience inherit what has been promised*"( NIV).

*You must keep doing two things: calling those things which be not as though they were( consistent faith confession) and believing you received when you prayed coupled with thanksgiving. That's how to incubate your faith*.

*After you pray and believe you received, the confession of your mouth must be as though you have seen what you're asking for and the believe of your heart must be intact*.

The problem we have often is that sometimes, when we come out of a meeting where our faith is stirred, we don't know how to maintain that till we see result.

Sometimes, it is as If our faith jumped out after those faith lifting services. Next time, just keep incubating that faith with scriptures in your mouth.

*Your life will go in the direction of your words*. What is it you've asked God for?

Believe you have received them by faith and keep incubating them.

CONFESSION: *Father, i thank you because the finished work of Christ has delivered unto me all i need for life and godliness*. Halleluyah!!!

DEVOTIONAL 0757 | Source start line: 75201
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
2/2/23, 5:47 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: PROJECTSAVE DEVOTIONAL
Thursday,
February 2nd, 2023.

*THE MORNING STAR OF REFORMATION*

Matthew5:10 *Blessed are they which are persecuted for righteousness' sake: for theirs is the kingdom of heaven*.

*John Wycliff* was known as “The Morning Star of the Reformation".  He  was an outstanding theologian who lived in 14th century.

He was known for his prowess as a translator of scriptures. He believed the  Bible should be available to people in their common tongue. He translated the Latin Vulgate( Bible) into common English.

He was persecuted for his stand against Papal authority. While he was not burned at the stake as a martyr like Tyndale, his persecution extended beyond his death. His body was exhumed and burned along with many of his writings.

The Anti-Wycliffe Statute of 1401 brought persecution to his followers and specifically addressed the fact that there should not be any translation of Scripture into English.

*Virtually all that we are enjoying today in the body of Christ(especially in nations with low persecution index) were paid for with the blood and sacrifice of saints who sacrificed their time, comfort and life*.

*Anywhere you see people worshipping Jesus unhindered, you may need to dig into history. You'll discover that some paid the price of time, money, commitment and even life to make that happen*.

Anytime you read your English bible, remember John Wycliffe and William Tyndale. These men paid the price to get bible translated into English against the wish of the religious leaders of their time.

*It is important we remind ourselves about those that have made huge sacrifice(s) for us to be here. This should in turn make us resolute in our decision to serve the Lord come what may*.

Jesus said, *"For whosoever will save his life shall lose it: and whosoever will lose his life for my sake shall find it*" (Matthew 16:25 KJV).

*Christianity is no christianity without sacrifice*. Christ sacrificed his very life for us to receive eternal life.

Every generation must sacrifice somethings for the perpetuity of the Gospel. Ours may not necessarily be our life. It may be time, money, intellect, commitment, etc.

*The sacrifice of some people made the sending of missionaries to your tribe/country possible when it was still unreached*.

*Today, you've been reached with the Gospel. You're enjoying Jesus and worshipping freely in your paneled church, what are you sacrificing for the unreached in your time to hear the Gospel*?

God is calling you to a higher place of commitment where you need to make sacrifices for his kingdom. How far can you go with and for God?

*PRAYER*: Father, I receive grace to contribute my quota to kingdom advancement. I receive the grace to pay the price In Jesus name.

DEVOTIONAL 0758 | Source start line: 75288

2/3/23, 4:59 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: PROJECTSAVE DEVOTIONAL
Friday,
February 3rd, 2023.

*FUELLING YOUR PRAYER LIFE* Pt.1

Zech12:10 *"Next I'll deal with the family of David and those who live in Jerusalem. I'll pour a spirit of grace and prayer over them* ...MSG

*ENGAGE THE SPIRIT*

Have you ever been in a vehicle whose fuel got exhausted on the way? For those who might have experienced such, you'll agree with me how frustrating it could be when in such circumstance.

*The prayer life of some believers have run out of 'fuel'. The fire on their prayer altar is dead and their prayer time seems boring and dry. For those in this category, prayer is now a religious routine instead of a fellowship*.

Many believers desire to be mighty in prayer. I have seen the desire in many especially during counseling. Every now and then, people pray to become prayerful( though not bad) but they forget the source of a fruitful prayer life- HOLYSPIRIT.

*If prayer is a fleshy exercise, we wouldn't need the Holy Spirit but because of its spiritual nature, we cannot but do without the Spirit of God*.

Epaphras( Col4:12-13), Moses( Deut9:11-19) and Jesus Christ had the working of the Spirit of prayer in their lives.

*In history, every man or woman who had a consistent virile prayer life had the indwelling of the Spirit which enabled them to pray prevailingly*.

When you read the prayer exploits of men like Apostle Ayo Babalola who prayed for 72hours non-stop, do not think he did it in the flesh. Something stirred him to stay that long in God's presence and it seemed to him as though he had spent a few hours.

The Holy Spirit fuels a consistent, persistent and virile prayer life not the flesh. You can't wake up and say, 'I want to pray 72 hours non stop like Ayo Babalola did, you will wear out in no time'. You need that divine fuel.

Some of the father who prayed for days non-stop didn't set out to pray as long as they did. They surrendered to the Spirit and they were caught up in that realm. Those long hours seemed like a few minutes to them.

Paul enjoined that we pray in the Spirit: *"And pray in the Spirit on all occasions with all kinds of prayers and requests. With this in mind, be alert and always keep on praying for all the Lord’s people*" (Ephesians 6:18 NIV).

*Praying in the Spirit is simply praying in the power of the Spirit, by the leading of the Spirit, and according to His will. It is a situation  where the Holy Spirit fuels our prayer life*.

The Spirit of God is called the Spirit of grace and supplication(prayer). Trying to pray without the Spirit of prayer in your life will make you weary.

Many have become religious in their prayer life while some do not even have a prayer life at all. So bad.

*Until you submit your prayer life to the Spirit and receive a fresh baptism of prayer, your prayer life cannot be consistent and fruitful*.

Dearest, allow the Spirit of God take the wheel of your prayer life. He is a sweet gentle Spirit that wouldn't force himself on your life.

You need to invite him into your life right now. You cannot become mighty in prayer without the Spirit of prayer.


*PRAYER*: Holy Spirit, you're welcome into my life. Take the wheel of my prayer life and enable me to become strong and consistent in my prayer life. I receive a fresh baptism of the Spirit of prayer and supplication.

DEVOTIONAL 0759 | Source start line: 75348
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
2/4/23, 6:52 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: PROJECTSAVE DEVOTIONAL
Saturday,
February 4th, 2023.

*FUELLING YOUR PRAYER LIFE* Pt.2

Psalm5:3 *My voice shalt thou hear in the morning, O Lord; in the morning will I direct my prayer unto thee, and will look up*.

*DISCOVER YOURSELF*

God gave us a body. It is the vehicle through which we execute his agenda here on earth. Also, it plays a vital role in our fellowship with him.

There's a need to discover and understand the workings of our body for us to be effective in our prayer life.

When I was in school as an undergraduate, I don't read at midnight. I discovered that once I sleep at night, I won't wake up till the next morning.

Some people have the grace to sleep and wake up at midnight to read but I don't. If I try it, i'll walk around during the day like a drunk.

Having known that, I rescheduled my reading time and drew a plan that fit into my active hours. I could read from afternoon till midnight with my eyes wide open as long as there's no nap in between.

I discovered that my body requirement for sleep is minimum of 6 hours a day. If I want to wake up early, I'll time my sleep. Once I achieve 6 hours, I'll be fine.

Back in the days, I ensured all academic activities stopped early in the night for me to wake up early to pray. I developed a custom of waking up by 4am to pray having satisfied my body's demand for 6 hours sleep.

*For you to have a consistent prayer life, you must discover yourself especially your body's requirement for rest and your active time*.

Some are natural early risers while others are late risers. Many years ago, I tried praying between 1-3pm at night but it wasn't working for me because i'm active till about 12 midnight and i'm expected to wake up between 5-6am in the morning.

I was damaging my health because I wasn't sleeping well so I stopped. My prayer life became better when I discovered myself and struck a balance between work time,  resting time and waking time.

Also, you must take your time to know what your daily schedule is. No matter how busy you are, you can carve a  time for prayer.

*If you form the habit of  sleeping early, waking up early to pray shouldn't be a problem*. As they say in nursery school, "Early to bed early to rise".

*It is a nice thing to wake up early to pray. However, to achieve this, you must cultivate the discipline of sleeping early*.

What about those that return home late from work and want to leave home very early to work the next day? I know this is true for some especially those living in cosmopolitan cities with traffic issues.

Understand there's no excuse for not have a strong prayer life. While you may not be able to wake up early to pray because of the brevity of sleep, you still have to pray in the morning.

Also, you can take advantage of break time in the office or the hours before work starts to commune with your heavenly father.

I know a sister who leaves home as early as 5am for office so that she can beat traffic. Often, she gets to work on or before 6am. Official resumption is 8am. Between 6am-8am, she spends quality time with God in her office before the start of work.

How it works for Mr 'A' may be different from that of Mr 'B'. God understands the peculiarity of individual's situation and he's willing to fellowship with us If we're ready for him.

*Discover yourself. Reorder your priorities to give time for prayer. Prayer is so important*. If you'll need to cut down on t.v. time to sleep early and wake up early, the sacrifice is worth it.

*You must fight for your prayer life. If the devil  kill your prayer life, every other thing dies.*

*PRAYER*: Father, I cast out the spirit of prayerlessness in my life. Holy Spirit, baptize me afresh with the Spirit of prayer.

2/5/23, 5:22 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: ProjectSave Devotional
Sunday,

*FUELLING YOUR PRAYER LIFE* Pt.3

1Cor9:25 *And every man that striveth for the mastery is temperate in all things. Now they do it to obtain a corruptible crown; but we an incorruptible*.

*DISCIPLINE AND DENIAL*

I am a huge fan of Cristiano Ronaldo. Though age is catching up with him and he's not performing optimally, I still admire him.

Many athlete didn't play soccer above 35 years of age. At 38, he's still on the field aspiring for more. One thing that made him last long at the top is his discipline. To keep fit, he doesn't drink and eat anyhow.

He gets to training before his teammates and they leave him there after the training is over. That's discipline and denial at work.

*Discipline is living as demanded while denial involves sacrificing temporal pleasure to achieve something spectacular*.

You cannot build a strong prayer life If you lack discipline even if the most anointed preacher lay hands on you. It is impossible.

I listened to a man of God who said he formed the habit of waking up by 3am to pray and study on daily basis. He goes to bed by 9pm at night daily so that he can wake up early.

He denied himself of television programs at night because of what he want to achieve.

*Just as an athlete would deny himself of certain pleasures to gain mastery, same applies to anyone that desires to be consistent and mighty in prayer*.

*One of the reasons for prayerlessness is indiscipline*.

After discovering your body requirement for rest, be disciplined enough to sleep at the proper time so that you can wake up early to pray.

There are times you may need to avoid heavy foods at night so that you can be light to wake up early.

One of the things I recommend to people is that they should not eat heavy meals If they intend to do vigil or wake up to pray early.

*Sometimes, you need to engage in long fast to revive your prayer life If the fire of prayer is burning low*.

*Your prayer life must be carefully planned and given all it takes*. For those with very tight work schedule, be disciplined enough to use those spare hours or break time in the office to fellowship with God.

For sit at home moms, the hours of the day when your husband has gone to work and children are in school can be used judiciously. *Avoid unnecessary street gist*.

Do not forget that you would achieve more spiritually If you spend time with God than with men.

Leonard Ravenhill said: '*To be much for God, you have to be more with God*'. That requires some discipline and self denial.

*PRAYER*: Father, let the burden of prayer rest upon me in Jesus name.

2/6/23, 6:09 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: PROJECTSAVE DEVOTIONAL

*FUELLING YOUR  PRAYER LIFE* Pt.4

2Kings2:15 *And when the sons of the prophets which were to view at Jericho saw him, they said, The spirit of Elijah doth rest on Elisha. And they came to meet him, and bowed themselves to the ground before him*

*DISCIPLED TO PRAY*

God bless Dr. Ojewunmi Joshua. He discipled me to pray. My association with him transferred something into me.

As an undergraduate in the university, this brother  decided to engage me in prayer every night for a minimum of one hour.

We met by the roadside of his hostel at night for prayers. When we started, it was as if he was pestering me because the flesh wasn't willing to pray.

However, I took it upon myself to ensure that I join him at the prayer spot every night. I didn't know God was using him to disciple me along that line. I have seen some of the manifestations of those prayers we prayed then.

It got to a point where I became more interested in that prayer than he was. At the beginning, he was the one sending for me but later I began to remind him to join me on the spot where we normally meet. It was as if the grace to  pray robbed on me.

*If you associate with praying men closely, the mantle of prayer will fall on you*.

Whatever is in your atmosphere can be transferred to you easily. Saul prophesied not because he was a prophet but the atmosphere around him.was filled with the Spirit of prophecy. *You become whatever is in your atmosphere*. That's why it is good to be in the right place at all times.

If you're in a "Fireless" atmosphere and you relate with people whose hearts are lukewarm, same will be transferred to you.

In my early years as a believer, our church Evangelist would camp us somewhere for 2-3 days for prayer retreat. God used those experiences to transfer something into me. That's part of the benefits of discipleship.

*If you're struggling with your prayer life, look for someone whose prayer altar is on fire. Take the coal of fire from his altar to ignite yours*.

Apostle Ayo Babalola ignited the prayer altar of his associates by taking them to the mountain to pray regularly. After his demise, his disciples became prayer champions. Till date, he left behind a praying church.

*In the kingdom, we learn and grow by associating with the right persons and by being in the right place*. We can learn spiritual things and how to operate in them.

Look at your church, prayerfully spot a revived brother or sister, tell the person to find time for you so that you both can be praying together.

As you do that, something is adding to your prayer life. If you put your heart to it, you'll grow to start praying independently.

*PRAYER*: Father, I receive the grace to locate and associate with those that would boost my prayer life In Jesus name.

*FUELLING YOUR PRAYER LIFE* Pt.5

Colossians4:2 *Devote yourselves to prayer, being watchful and thankful*.NIV

*IDENTIFY YOUR PRAYER BOOSTERS*.

*A prayer booster is any good thing that stirs you to pray*. For me, anointed music does it. Once an anointed minstrel is singing, I can stay in the place of prayer for as long as I can.

In the morning when praying, often, I ensure quality Christian song is playing in the background. That's my prayer booster. Every song( good or bad) creates a type of atmosphere.

Any time I feel dry and weak to pray, I would listen to some of those selected songs. As an aside, one of Don Moen's songs titled "You Said" stirs in me more passion for missions.

I won't forget watching Benny Hinn's worship and healing meetings on my laptop. I'll sit on the floor flowing along with the worship and from there i'll take off into depth of fellowship.

Yours maybe different but you need to identify those things that serve as a booster for your prayer life.

It has helped many and it shall continue to help others. We were told by a trusted colleague years back that one of our fathers loves to see the image of a praying hand because it stirs him to pray.

Another prayer booster for me is being in an environment where people are praying.

Sometimes, when I go to prayer camps or mountains, seeing people praying normally push me to my knees.

*Don't surround yourself with prayer killers. Carnality can kill your prayer life*.

Just as anointed music stir some people to pray and boost their prayer lives, carnal music and fleshly attachment can ruin your prayer life.

*Carnality is the enemy of a virile prayer life*.

*God can do so much in our lives but He has chosen not to do much unless we give Him the express permission through our prayers*.

*The goal of identifying your prayer booster is to ensure that your prayer altar is red hot and flaming with the fire of the Holy Spirit*.

*PRAYER*: Father, rekindle the fire on my prayer altar In Jesus name.

DEVOTIONAL 0763 | Source start line: 75738

2/8/23, 5:00 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: ProjectSave Devotional
Wednesday
February 8th, 2023.

*THE PALESTINIAN SHEPHERD*

John 10:14 *“I am the good shepherd; I know my sheep and my sheep know me*" (John 10:14 NIV).

Recently, I heard the story of a Palestinian shepherd whose sheep was stolen. This shepherd was perturbed about how a live sheep was stolen under his care.

He decided to look for the sheep but his effort yielded no fruit. He decided he'll go to the market on the market day to continue his search.

When he got to the market where sheep were sold, it seemed an arduous task spotting his own sheep but he deviced a method. What was it?

He began to sing a song as he walked around the market. Not long after, his lost sheep ran towards him because the sheep recognised his voice and that song.

The shepherd normally sing that song for his sheep on daily basis. The sheep recognised the song and knew that only his shepherd could be singing that song.

This story underscores the importance of fellowship. *It is not possible to be close to God and not hear or recognize his voice*.

One challenge many believers have today is their inability to recognize God's voice and discern him. You'll find Christians of decades on this table.

Some even concluded man can't know if he's truly serving God. They believe it is only God that knows those that are serving him. That's inaccurate.

Jesus said, *"...I know my sheep and my sheep know me*"( John 10:14 NIV). A true sheep knows the shepherd.

If there's a sheep that doesn't know or recognize the shepherd, something is obviously wrong with their fellowship.

*One reason we emphasize daily fellowship with God is because the more you relate closely with the Lord, the more you discern his voice and actions*.

Recently, I was walking up the stairs to my flat. My children were with their home lesson teacher. My little daughter said, "that's my daddy coming".

She didn't see me, she only heard my footsteps and she predicted accurately it was me. Their teacher had to check if it was true. I was surprised seeing him at the door. He told me how my daughter said, "That's my daddy coming".

How did she know? She had fellowshiped with me to the point where she recognizes the sound of my footsteps. Those who fellowship with God can predict him accurately.

We can get to that point with God and in God.  A dimension where we can discern what God is doing without hearing him speak or what he is saying even in a whisper.

**True fellowship is intimacy*. This starts with the regular study of the word and consistency in the place of prayer.

*PRAYER*: Father, I receive grace to fellowship closely with you in Jesus name.

DEVOTIONAL 0764 | Source start line: 75804
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
2/9/23, 5:52 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: PROJECTSAVE DEVOTIONAL
Thursday,
February 9th, 2023.

*GOD WON'T MISMANAGE YOUR LIFE*

Jeremiah 31:3 *Long ago the LORD said to Israel: “I have loved you, my people, with an everlasting love. With unfailing love I have drawn you to myself*.

There was economic recession In a certain country. Every system and structure collapsed and life became hard for the people. Almost everyone migrated for greener pastures.

There was this man who stayed back with his family on God's instruction. Everyone around him tried to convince him against staying back. Of course, it was illogical to stay back at such times.

However, this man stood his ground. His response to those who thought he was insane was: "The all-knowing and all-wise God is the manager of my life. His instruction I follow per time because I know he won't mismanage my life".

Later, that man became wealthy during recession because he cultivated a large expanse of land to produce the staple of his people. He was the sole producer of that food. Government bought food from him alone because other farmers ran away.

After the recession when others returned, they had a hard time rebuilding their businesses because only one man dominated the business scene of the country. Aside farming, he invested in other lines of businesses. He dominated.

*I am glad to inform you today, "God won't mismanage your life*". Erase from your heart the fear about God not managing your life properly. *That feeling of not seeing God's instruction to us as the best stems from lack of trust*.

The writer of proverbs says, *"Trust in the LORD with ALL THINE HEART; and lean not unto thine own understanding*". So it is safe to say men lean on their own understanding because they don't trust God with all their heart.

Sometimes ago, I pondered on the reason some believers shun divine direction and scheme their way to run their lives. It occurred to me that most people think God can't manage their lives properly.

Some have been wrongly taught that God's will is unpleasant and may not be appealing. This is the reason some singles ask questions like: "What if God gives me a wife that is not beautiful"? "What if God tells me to marry someone that is not my speck"?

Matthew 7:9-11 says, *“Which of you, if your son asks for bread, will give him a stone? Or if he asks for a fish, will give him a snake? If you, then, though you are evil, know how to give good gifts to your children, how much more will your Father in heaven give good gifts to those who ask him*"! ( NIV). Ponder on this.

In ministry circle, it is often the fear of the unknown or tomorrow. A man God sent to the village for ministry may think God has sentenced him to a life of penury and pain so he may scheme his way into the city to plant a church to better his lot.

I was once like this because I had my fears about the future of being a missionary evangelist and teacher of the word without founding a church. No salary, nowhere to pass the offering bowl. You know what I mean?

It got me thinking why God assigned my peers to pastor in the cities with better prospect. Anytime I move round town, all I saw were adverts of young folks like me pastoring churches in posh areas in Lagos. I began to imagine their standard of living and quality of life. I spent productive time in unhealthy comparison.

I almost pulled out to do ministry my own way because I felt God was managing my life wrongly. No thanks to those who even thought I can't be in ministry without founding a church. The language is always, "you'll need a base". Now I know better that it pays to obey God when it seems he doesn't make "sense".

*At the root of every disobedience to divine instructions about our lives is that feeling of distrust thinking God doesn't mean well for us*.

*Hey! God won't mismanage your life*. The instruction may seem "foolish" now but in the end you will understand why God gave that instruction. *Often, God won't reveal everything to us about our lives at once*.

*He want us to obey him per time he gives instruction trusting that he can manage our lives properly*. When God told Abraham to leave his kindred to a land he will show him, it sounded "foolish" because God didn't tell him where he was taking him.

He obeyed by packing his bags and taking his wife with him not knowing where God was taking him to. That's a man who trust God enough to manage his life.

Have you heard God on a matter and you're beginning to think God doesn't mean well for you? I want you to find comfort in this scripture: *'The LORD says, “I will guide you along the BEST PATHWAY for your life. I will advise you and watch over you*'.

Don't settle for the good or the better pathway. The best pathway is the best for your life. God won't mismanage your life.

*CONFESSION*: I walk in God's perfect will for my life henceforth. He cannot mismanage my life. He who created me knows what is best for my life. I receive the grace to obey and walk right at the centre of his will in Jesus name.


*Kindly listen to this audio message by Bro Lanre*👇🏽👇🏽👇🏽

*PETERINE COUNSEL FOR HUSBANDS  Pt.4*

https://t.me/projectsaveministries/88

DEVOTIONAL 0765 | Source start line: 76172
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
2/10/23, 5:32 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: PROJECTSAVE DEVOTIONAL
Friday,
February 10th, 2023.

*FAITHFUL UNTIL DEATH OR FOR A WHILE?*

Revelation 2:10 ... *Be faithful until death, and I will give you the crown of life*. NKJV

In church history, there was a Bishop, Polycarp by name. He was a 2nd century Bishop of Smyrna. The church in Smyrna was one of the seven churches John wrote to in his revelation.

Polycarp was  a disciple of  Apostle John who wrote the books of the Gospel of John, the three Epistles of John and the book of Revelation.

He was prominent among the Apostolic fathers who may have been    responsible for compiling the New Testament of the Bible that we have today.

His refusal to burn incense to the Roman Emperor earned him a death sentence by burning at the stake. Tradition says the flames did not kill him so he was stabbed to death.

At the point of death, Polycarp said, *"Eighty and six years I have served Him, and He has done me no wrong",  how then can I blaspheme my King and Savior? You threaten me with a fire that burns for a season, and after a little while is quenched; but you are ignorant of the fire of everlasting punishment that is prepared for the wicked*".

Miraculously, his body did not burn, so he was pierced with a sharp object.  On his farewell, he said "I bless you Father for judging me worthy of this hour, so that in the company of the martyrs I may share the cup of Christ." Profound!

Anytime I read stories of martyrs, I weigh my walk with God on a scale and I'm often found wanting. Still can't fathom the depth of their conviction about Christ even to the point of death. Their faithfulness to Christ wasn't just for a time but till their dying day.

I heard the story of another preacher in our day who stood against the occult in his community. He preached against their evil works and everyone knew his stand. Eventually, he took ill.

After trying several options available for his healing, he resolved to be taken to the occult he spoke against for healing. They mocked him and he died in that condition.

If you compare this man with Polycarp, there's a whole lot of difference. One was faithful to Christ even if it meant dying while the other turned his back on Christ at the point of death because he wanted to escape death.

*Can you die for what you believe? Until we get to that point where we're convinced to the point of dying for what we believe, our resolve to follow Christ isn't genuine*.

Sometimes, I wonder If the same Gospel we are hearing today was what they heard in the Apostolic and sub apostolic era. I doubt it is the same.

*The Gospel that made some of them rejoice in the face of death and still thanked God for being counted worthy to die for him, I doubt it is the same Gospel. Ours is against suffering in any form, theirs was ready for suffering as long as it was for the sake of Christ*.

*With the numerous churches, seminaries, many teachers and programs, to a large extent, our generation is still lacking men of conviction and those that can take their stand for Christ without compromise*.

If Polycarp were to be some believers in this generation, he'll burn incense to the Roman emperor and claim he gave to Ceasar what belonged to Ceasar.

Most times, when you hear that statement, compromise has taken place. Bishop Polycarp was faithful to the cause of Christ till the end.

He would have escaped  gruesome death with a little compromise. The crown of life is only for those that can fight the good fight of faith and stay faithful to Christ to the end.

How are you running the race?

*PRAYER*:  Father, i receive the grace to stay faithful to the end In Jesus name.
