*RAISE THEM TO FULFILL DESTINY*

Judges13:12 *Manoah said, “Now let Your words come to pass! What will be the boy’s rule of life, and his work*?” NKJV

I heard the story of a great preacher in America who decided to send his son for training in a particular field different from what God intended of his son.

The young boy had the call of God into ministry but his father felt his family can'be all about preachers. He wanted something secular at least.

His son became frustrated and later committed suicide.  The young man was raised for something different from his destiny. This preacher regretted his action.

*It is not enough to know God's plan for our children. After knowing what God has in plan, we must raise them to fulfill it*.

When the angel appeared to Manoah telling him about the son  his wife would give birth to and his purpose for coming into the world,  he asked a profound question that struck me.

To paraphrase it, what he asked was: How shall we train this child to fulfil his destiny? That's very specific.

Manoah sensed that Samson had an uncommon destiny and as such, there must be a blue print for training him so that he can fulfil his uncommon destiny. The angel gave them the blue print on how to raise him.

Every child has come to the world to fulfill an uncommon destiny. Don't wait for a special revelation or angelic visitation before you believe that.

Your duty is to ask the Holy Spirit for the training blue print of each of your children. This teaching may sound unfamiliar but I was taught  by the Holy Spirit in my study.

*You see, parenting is spiritual, that's why you cannot do it in the flesh. Some parents trained their children out of destiny. They insisted their children must help fulfill their own failed dreams.*

Some gave their children the best of education in that regard but God had a different plan for those children.

*You're meant to train your child(ren) in line with God's purpose not just your desire*.

I won't forget my mum telling me: "You know you're a man of God". Now, she said that statement at a crucial time in my life when I would have abandoned God's assignment for my life and pursue other things.

She knew a little about God's mind for me and she did her best for me in that regard. She's one of the reasons I'm in ministry today.

Aside the inspiration of the Spirit, i'm writing devotionals today because she trained me to write lessons from my daily study of the word. She demanded we submit our notes for evaluation.

Till date, writing lessons from scriptures including sermon preparation is so easy for me. Someone raised me that way because it aligns with my assignment.

Parents, pay the price to know God's mind concerning the purpose of each of your seeds and train them in accordance to the revealed will of God for their lives.

*ACTION:* What do you think would have happened if Joseph&Mary forced Jesus to become a carpenter/farmer? Jesus may not fulfil His purpose for coming to the world. That's what will happen when children are ordered out of God's purpose for their lives.

*MAXIMIZING YOUR YOUTHFULNESS Pt.1*

Lam3:27. *It is good for someone to work hard [carry/bear the yoke] while he is young*( EXB).


During my service year, I was privileged to attend a meeting where some Christian elders were represented. I reprsented my fellowship.

We all introduced ourselves. Obviously, I was the youngest among them. One of the men who was present in the meeting told me he wished he knew Christ when he was a young man like me.

Seeing me in that meeting and knowing the body I represented as a youth, he lamented on how he wasted his youthfulness in the world.

When I studied the life of most achievers, there is a common trend I observed. One of my observation is that they set their foot on a definite path early in life.

If you look  at most inventors, scientist and even successful men in the ministry, they started in their youthful age, paid the price as a youth and at old age, they consolidated the efforts of their yester years.

On the other hand, those that troubled our world and destroyed lives also started as youths. Most terrorists are youths, in fact teenagers have joined the train.

*I noticed that whenever God want to do something remarkable in a generation, he looks for young men likewise the devil. That strength and zeal can do alot positively and negatively*.

If you look at the hip pop music world, the reigning guys are youths. That tells  how important youths are.

*Dear young man/woman, youthfulness is a privilege and a blessing from God. It is a foundation laying time for a glorious life. It is a time to pay the price for your rising in life*.

*I won't be wrong to say that you cannot rise farther than the price you paid as a youth, it is a seed sowing time, a time to catch God's vision for your life*.

*Don't do in your sixties what you ought to do in your thirties*. Every phase of life has its own task and pursuit. Strength doesn't increase with age, it reduces.

*There are yearning, learning and earning years in life*. It is good to bear the yoke early so that you'll rest in your earning years.

*Don't play when you're meant to be paying the price for your rising in life. Maximize your youthfulness, don't waste it*.

*PRAYER*: Father, i receive grace to maximize my youthfulness In Jesus name. Amen

*MAXIMIZING YOUR YOUTHFULNESS* Pt.2

Lam3:27. It is good for someone to work hard [carry/bear the yoke] while he is young. EXB

*SELF DEVELOPMENT*

The youthful age is the time to invest in learning. It is the yearning and learning time of life.

One of my lecturers in the seminary told us he finished primary school at the age of 20. He came to Lagos to work as a houseboy but he loved education.

With little help here and there, he finished his primary education at the age of 20. Did a self-study for O'levels and he made some papers.

Today, that man has four doctorate degrees. He invested so much in himself; in the field of theology, he is not a man you can push aside. He knows what he knows. He has committed himself to raising ministers.

I heard the story of a very young man who committed himself to self- development at the age of 17. By the time he clocked 22, he had read 700 books and was organizing seminar for professors in their field.

Those professors paid to attend  his seminar. That is the power of self- development.

The best time to invest in yourself is when you're young. That can be done through constant learning and reading.

*If you want to feature properly in your future, you must be a reader. An avid learner would surely emerge as a leader in his chosen endeavour*.
Don't be an outdated young man or woman. Learning is not restricted to the classroom only. There are other avenues you can explore.

Don't kill the vision God gave you through ignorance.

Your learning must go beyond the four walls of the classroom. Invest in your level of knowledge, seek to know more; how far you'll go in life is a function of your learning.

*True success is when you influence your world with the product of your personal development*.

*PRAYER:* Father, I receive grace to invest positively in myself In Jesus name.

*MAXIMIZING YOUR YOUTHFULNESS* Pt.3

Lam3:27. *It is good for someone to work hard [carry/bear the yoke] while he is young* (EXB)

*NEVER WALK ALONE*

I love the slogan of Liverpool FC, 'Never Walk Alone'. This slogan underscore the benefit of viable relationship.

During my National Youth Service Corps orientation programme(NYSC), we were made to go on a long walk called 'endurance trekking'.

We trekked on foot from one town to another. It was such a long journey on foot with soldiers and other paramilitary agencies as guides.

Have you gone on endurance walk alone before? If you have, you will notice that walking alone can sap energy quickly and journey seems so long and lonely.

If you walk the same distance with a group of friends going the same direction, by the time you get to your destination, you will not  know you've covered a long distance.

Endurance walk is very interesting with a group not when you're alone. That explains the power of relationship.

That time of life when you need to build destiny relationship is now that you are young. You have to be intentional about it.

If you look at men that climbed the ladder of greatness, they climbed with men and women of like minds.

*After discovering your purpose, you must consciously build relationship with men and women of like passion. It is a fuel that keeps you alive on the journey*.

Ecclesiastes 4:9-10 says, *"Two are better than one,because they have a good return for their labor: If either of them falls down,one can help the other up.But pity anyone who falls and has no one to help them up*".

*In life, you cannot journey alone. You need those that will motivate, edify and revive your vision*.

In your youthful days,  walk with the right people and nurture that relationship.

*PRAYER*: Father, I receive grace to build the right relationship In Jesus name. Amen

The relationship between mentor and mentee sometimes is a bitter-sweet relationship because a true mentor will not hide the truth from you even if it hurts.

The end result of this powerful relationship is greatness. At this crucial time of life that you're young, prayerfully find a mentor and submit to him.

*MAXIMIZING YOUR YOUTHFULNESS* Pt.5

I thank God I found my life's purpose early. From the very time I read "Plans, Purposes& Pursuit" by Kenneth E. Hagin, I spent a considerable amount of time asking the Lord to reveal his mind to me concerning why I am here.


I have seen men and women who discovered in their later years what they ought to have been doing from their youthful years. Often, this comes with regret and pains.


When an elder tells a younger person 'I wish I were like you in my youthful days', that's a statement that is laden with regret.


Satan doesn't joke with youths. He has plans and purposes for every youth. He knows that's the best time to make use of their energy for his dirty dozens.


Every crime ring, terrorist organization, drug cartel, cult groups and prostitution business are filled with youths majorly.


*As a youth, this is the time to discover and start pursuing your purpose. Don't wait until you've traveled a long distance on the wrong path before retracing your steps*.


There are general purposes that every child of God must fulfill. This includes: worship, evangelism and giving God pleasure.


However, there are specific purposes unique to every individual. That, you must discover in the place of prayer and fellowship.


*Don't pursue money when you should be pursuing the fulfilment of your existence*. Your purpose is the reason God created you. It can only be revealed to you by God.


God will judge and reward you in heaven using the fulfilment of your purpose as the metrics not just doing anything. Stop running another man's race, find your path and stay there.


*Satan's bait for the young is to make them pursue a better life at the expense of what will give them fulfilment*. We live in a world that is crazy about "better living".



*The campaign for "better life" is louder than the campaign for living a "fulfilled life". The former is about pursuing selfish ambitions that gratifies self, the latter is about the pursuit of our existence which impact others positively*.


*One can be wealthy and famous yet living a wasted life*. Purpose fulfilment isn't about wealth and fame but the value we are adding to lives here on earth.


*The easiest way to live a wasted life is to live a purposeless life. Avoid it*.


*PRAYER*: Father, reveal my purpose to me in Jesus name.

*DEAR MINISTER* Pt.1

Colossians 4:17 *And say to Archippus, “Be sure to carry out the ministry the Lord gave you*.” NLT

*FOCUS ON YOUR MINISTRY*

Recently, my eyes opened to certain things about ministry and the tactics the enemy is using to derail ministers from their assignment.

From time immemorial, the popular bait of the enemy has always been money, women and power. Virtually everyone in ministry has been warned about these at one time or the other.

Till date, the enemy is still using these old baits to catch men in ministry and to bring them down. However, I discover another subtle evil he uses to weaken ministers before making them fall. That tool is lack of focus.

If the enemy can divert  your attention  from your ministry( assignment), you may lose your ministry while still wearing the collar.

*Your ministry is that specific assignment God gave you.* It is your originality.

*Some are wearing the collar but they have lost their rightful place on the altar. They have the title but have lost their mantle because their focus is on something else except their ministry*.

Lack of focus on the assignment can make a minister abandon his calling and start fighting for a place in forbes list. *Lack of focus can make a man abandon fulfilment in ministry for fame and fortunes.*

*Such a person may turn the message of Christian growth and discipleship to business growth because it is the reigning message on cold pulpits without holy fire because It spins money*.

His assignment could be in the village but due to pressure for fame and fortunes, the minister who has lost focus will re direct his path to the city.

The minister who has lost focus will run after title and abandon his assignment.  Seeks ordination for an office he hasn't been called to occupy. Are you occupying your rightful office?

Have you wondered why we are having so many self-imposed apostles and prophets today but few evangelists? Lack of focus is the reason.

Some that are called into the evangelistic ministry are seeking to operate in the prophetic instead of combing villages, towns and cities in search for the lost.

The enemy will divert their attention from souls to start seeking how to say "thus saith the Lord" when the Lord hasn't said anything. Can't you see the multiplication of fake prophets everywhere?

The worst is when such persons decide to open a church thinking they'll finance their outreach ministry from the church purse. Some prefer a ministry where they can pass the offering bowl around.

*Church ministry is a big distraction to any minister that hasn't been called to start one*.

*When the focus of a minister is on achieving  human metrics for measuring sucess, he will find himself chasing after shadows and living a competitive life. His glory will be in superlative expressions like "biggest", "largest" but real impact will be lost.*

Paul saw these long ago that was why he counseled Archippus to focus on his ministry. Today, the counsel to Archippus in Colossians 4:17 is still relevant to you and me: *"And say to (...insert your name), “Be sure to carry out the ministry the Lord gave you*.”

*PRAYER*: Father, I receive the grace to focus on my ministry in Jesus name.

*DEAR MINISTER* Pt.2

Colossians 4:17 *And say to Archippus, “Be sure to carry out the ministry the Lord gave you*.” NLT.

*FOCUS ON TEACHING SOUND DOCTRINE*

I observed a trend recently, mostly online. I see preachers whose messages lack doctrinal originality. Their duty is to attack other preachers and call them out derogatorily.

Unfortunately, some young ministers of my age seem to focus on this in ministry more than teaching sound doctrine. They want to correct "fathers". They think they can build their ministry by tearing down another.

They seem not to have an original message except that which is drawn from the mistakes of the preacher next door. They spew bile and hatred pitching brethren against one another thereby causing division in the body. Something that is unlike Christ.

The justification for their error-hunting ministry is that Jesus spoke against the Pharisees so it wouldn't be a bad idea to confront those they deemed heretic. Funnily, they don't know the difference between error and heresy.

To them, anyone who doesn't agree with their interpretation of scriptures is heretic whereas, that's theologically wrong. Not all errors makes one heretic. Heresy is total deviation from basic fundamental doctrines.

There may be little error in our interpretation but that doesn't mean we are heretic. For example,  Calvinism differ in interpretation of scriptures from Arminism on certain issues yet both theological positions are held by God's children from centuries past till now.

On each side of the divide are wonderful and great believers. If you have to ring the heresy bell on everyone you don't agree with, those who do not agree with you can tag you heretic as well. Hope you'll accept that label in good faith?

So what should your focus be? *Your focus in ministry should be on the message the Lord has given you, the teaching of sound doctrines, properly exegeted*. Hunting for mistakes of the preacher next door is not a ministry. No one has been called to 'police' the church.

You will do well to focus on teaching sound biblical doctrines to God's people. By so doing, they will grow in discerment to decipher what is right or wrong. *Polemics should be about issues not persons*.

*"Hold on to the pattern of wholesome teaching you learned from me—a pattern shaped by the faith and love that you have in Christ Jesus*" (2 Timothy 1:13 NLT)

*Making another preacher the subject of your sermon is not a sign of ministerial focus. It is a sign of immaturity*. That's like peeping to another man's farm yard while he was cultivating his leaving yours uncultivated. Christ nor the apostles did not teach us ministry this way.

We all will not agree on certain biblical issues till Christ comes. However, the unity of the body is paramount. Your duty is to teach sound doctrine: *"But speak thou the things which become sound doctrine:*" (Titus 2:1 KJV).

Some might ask, "Does it mean we shouldn't stand against heresy and errors"? *You have to stand against heresy and correct errors by teaching the truth not by calling names and spewing bile*.

*ACTION POINT*: Use the pulpit to preach and teach sound doctrine. That should be your focus. Raise a people that are truth-loving, by so doing, they will grow to discern errors.

*DEAR MINISTER* Pt.3

Colossians 4:17 *And say to Archippus, “Be sure to carry out the ministry the Lord gave you*.” NLT.


*FOCUS ON CONTINUOUS TRAINING*

"Lanre, I think you need training" (paraphrase). Years back, that was the statement I heard during a conversation with someone I revere.

I did not know what he saw, but I know the stench of ignorance around me was so much that he had to voice  his concern and what he perceived was my major need.

Getting quality training for ministry is so vital and crucial. It is one thing to be called and anointed for ministry, it is another thing entirely to be trained for ministry. *A call into ministry doesn't automatically guarantee success in ministry*.

Donald Barnhouse said, *"If I had only  three years to serve the Lord, I would spend two of them studying and preparing*". This speaks volume about importance of training.

Jesus, the son of God used the first 30 years of life to prepare for a ministry that lasted about 3 years. Wayne Stiles said,  "He grew up with a hammer in one hand and a scroll in the other".

Jesus trained his disciples for years before leaving them. All of the disciples of Jesus attended "Jesus Christ Theological Seminary". They had hands-on training from Jesus himself.

Paul did not jump into the pulpit immediately after his conversion. He did not start special programmes neither did he register a bogus ministry name.

Rather, he retreated to Arabia (Galatians 1:17)  to study and realign his understanding of the Bible with the reality of Jesus as Messiah little wonder he gave us deep revelation.

Even in the old testament, the prophets placed premium on training. Prophets attended a school of prophet(1 Sam19:18-24). Before anyone could be called a "Rabbi" those days, he must have attended Rabbinical school and study up to post graduate level.

*Quality training makes you effective and saves time*. One of my lecturers in school of ministry said, "What an untrained minister will do in 10 years, a trained minister might do in one year".

*Ministers who do not have time for training are already wasting time. An investment in quality training will save you struggling time in ministry and give you speed*. Those who do not have time for training end up achieving nothing with time.

If you complain about what it will cost to get quality training, then you may need to count the cost of ignorance and check which is costlier. Quality training comes at a cost, it can't be cheap.

There are many bible illiterate pastoring and preaching. Men who know nothing about scriptures and doctrine. They are raising immature and confused people.

*The type of Christians we have today reflects the type of shepherd leading them- untrained.* Wishy-washy leaders produce wishy-washy followers.

Listen to God speaking through Jeremiah: *"Then I will give you shepherds after my own heart, who will lead you with knowledge and understanding*"( Jeremiah 3:15). How can a pastor fulfill this assignment without adequate training?

Knowledge is imparted in training. Do not let pride rob you of learning opportunities. It pays to learn.

Dear minister, you need to apply yourself to relevant trainings and it must be continuous. Keep ugrading your knowledge about ministry, don't stop.

*ACTION POINT*: Identify the type of training you need and go for it.

*DEAR MINISTER* Pt.4

Colossians 4:17 *And say to Archippus, “Be sure to carry out the ministry the Lord gave you*.” NLT.

*FOCUS ON YOUR SPIRITUAL LIFE*

A brother in ministry once said, "It is possible to be in ministry and be lost". That's a truism. One can be a backslider yet preaching and teaching.

There is a tendency to be preaching here and there, jumping from pulpit to pulpit with posters and billboards all over the place yet without a virile walk with the Lord.

Personally, I have experienced this. At a time, I decided between invitations and the strength of my personal walk with God. I chose the latter regardless of honorarium.

*You'll eventually lose your ministry If you focus on the ministry more than you focus on the correctness of your life and walk with God. Your life comes first before the ministry*.

*Your life must be correct for your ministry to be correct*.

*On judgment day many will say to me, ‘Lord! Lord! We prophesied in your name and cast out demons in your name and performed many miracles in your name.’ But I will reply, ‘I never knew you. Get away from me, you who break God’s laws*’(Matthew7:22-23 NLT).

As a preacher, there's a great tendency to be studying scriptures because you want to preach a powerful message not because you want to add value to your spiritual life.

That's a dangerous. A terrain where you pray and study only when you have preaching engagements. At that level, spiritual dryness and burnout is around the corner.

When that old Christian tradition of reading the bible and praying daily for personal growth is no longer there, you're on your way down. You may preach a storm and everywhere is on fire but your own spiritual life is ebbing away gradually.

In a book I read years back, the author said it was discovered that most pastors who had a scandal had abandoned private devotion altogether.

*Your spiritual life come first. It is the strength of your daily devotion and fellowship that will impart the people and value to them*.

Check the way you live your life. Ensure you're practicing what you preach and lead by example. Are you mortifying the flesh daily or you think you're now above temptation?

*Keep a close watch on how you live and on your teaching. Stay true to what is right for the sake of your own salvation and the salvation of those who hear you*( 1 Tim 4:16 NLT).

*ACTION POINT*: How is your spiritual life as a preacher? When last did you examine If you're still in the faith? What about your personal bible study&prayer life? Are there secret sins? Examine yourself.

Wednesday
April 19th 2023.

*DEAR MINISTER* Pt.5

Colossians 4:17 *And say to Archippus, “Be sure to carry out the ministry the Lord gave you*.” NLT.

*FOCUS ON YOUR MARRIAGE*

Heard the story about the funeral service of a preacher. Eulogies and encomiums poured in for him from different quarters.

However, the children and wife were surprised at the very things they heard about their dad because he doesn't embody any of those virtues in his life time.

He was a good preacher and pastor to the people but a bad husband to his wife and bad father to his children.

There are wives of ministers who wished they had never married a minister of the Gospel. They are unloved and neglected. Some preachers wives are lonely and depressed.

Some preachers children have vowed never to answer God's call into the ministry because of their dad's lifestyle and the way he's treating them.

*There's a way you'll handle your home that'll make your children love or hate the work of ministry. You can't afford to portray God and his work in bad light to your family*.

*True ministry starts from home. Your marriage is as important as your ministry*. Strive to succeed at home first. In the new testament, it is a major criteria for selection of leaders.

*This is a trustworthy saying: “If someone aspires to be a church leader, he desires an honorable position.” He must manage his own family well, having children who respect and obey him. For if a man cannot manage his own household, how can he take care of God’s church*? ( 1 Timothy3:1, 4-5 NLT).

Give your family their time. *Do not let your ministry take you away totally from your family. That is not God's design but man's indiscretion*.

It is better to be celibate and focus on the ministry than to be married and neglect the marriage.

Dear minister, your marriage need attention. A good marriage is a boost for your ministry but a bad home may ruin your labour in ministry.

*ACTION POINT*: Are you paying proper attention to your marriage? Do the needful.

4/20/23, 7:14 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: PROJECTSAVE DEVOTIONAL
Thursday,
April 20th, 2023.


*DEAR MINISTER* Pt.6

Colossians 4:17 *And say to Archippus, “Be sure to carry out the ministry the Lord gave you*.” NLT.


*BEWARE OF BITTERNESS& OFFENCE*


I watched a Christian movie years back. It was about a church leader, a General Overseer who became bitter about one of his associates who left him.


Out of anger, he cursed him. The other associate minister was arrogant as well. Their case was a mix of arrogance, pride, anger and bitterness mixed together.


Thank God they amended their ways and reconciled later after much damage had been done on both sides. Satan inflicted much pain on them because they weren't walking in love. There are lessons we can draw from that movie.


*First, as a minister of the Gospel, beware of offenses. They will come*. The things that will make you harbour bitterness and unforgiveness as you journey in ministry are bound to happen.


I doubt If there's anyone in ministry for decades who haven't been offended either by associates or members in the ministry. Sometimes, fathers in ministry offend sons. There are times of betrayals and backstabbings.


In Matthew 18:7, Jesus said,  *"Woe unto the world because of offences! for it must needs be that offences come; but woe to that man by whom the offence cometh!*"


My counsel is that you forgive and let go. It may be painful but you must rise above your hurts and move on with your ministry. *A call into ministry is a call to forgive*.


Do not curse associates or those leaving you. Pray and release them If they want to go. *We don't do ministry from the standpoint of bitterness*.


Secondly, If you're offended or hurt, especially as an associate, do not lead a rebellion. Your response to offense shouldn't be to stage a coup and break the church away.


I have seen this over the years. *Those who breakaway because of offenses do not maximize their potential in ministry. They remain stunted in growth because God doesn't build his work on a faulty foundation*.


*Your reasons for a breakaway may sound genuine to men but God will not sanction it*. Resigning honourably is much better than dividing the fold and running away with the flock. Some change the name of the church outrightly. That's wrong.


*If you know God has put in you the spirit of a pioneer, don't use bitterness and anger towards your principal to start up. To exit, follow the procedure. Get the blessing of the setman and go start your work in a new place where you don't have influence on the people*.


Do not start a new work in the neighbourhood of your former church. It is against the ethics of church planting.

Whatever you'll do in ministry, be guided by the word and Spirit not by offense and bitterness.


Hebrews 12:15 says, *"Looking diligently lest any man fail of the grace of God; lest any root of bitterness springing up trouble you, and thereby many be defiled;*".


*ACTION POINT*: Forgive those who have offended you. You may need to check your heart If you have offended others too. The right thing to do is to seek their forgiveness. Seek to correct your faulty foundation in ministry.

DEVOTIONAL 0835 | Source start line: 82671
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
4/21/23, 7:45 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: PROJECTSAVE DEVOTIONAL
Friday,
April 21st, 2023.

*DEAR MINISTER* Pt.7

Colossians 4:17 *And say to Archippus, “Be sure to carry out the ministry the Lord gave you*.” NLT.

*FINANCIAL PROBITY MATTERS*

Early in ministry, we weren't keeping record of income and expenditure. I felt it was not necessary since I was spending the money for God's work.

The mindset I had then was that God is the judge. He sees me. Since I did not take a dime from ministry money for personal use, I thought that was fine.

I discussed this with someone I revere in ministry. He counseled against it. He made it clear that accountability is first to God and then to men. He told me how important it is to have proper record and the benefit of accountability.

I took that counsel. Ever since, we have been accounting for every penny even to the point of allowing external auditors look into our books. Today, every ministry partner receives our yearly audit report of income and expenditure.

Not all ministers agree with this but I know it is right and proper to be open on money matters in ministry. For us, it has helped greatly in building the people's confidence about how we handle money.

Sometimes ago, we sent a letter soliciting for outreach support to a church in the U.S. We attached the most recent audit report to the letter just to let them know we shall account for monies spent.

They were surprised to see a ministry in Nigeria doing that. God used them to fund part of that outreach. Transparency and openness endeared us to them, especially the senior pastor.

Dear minister, in matters of finances, be transparent and accountable. Never assume the ministry money is yours. No it is not yours, it is for God.

Some dubious fellows say "God's money is for the man of God". That's not true, you are only a steward of it not the owner. *Separate your purse from that of the ministry. Don't use the ministry account as personal account and vice versa*.

Some might ask, "Are you saying the man of God is not entitled to welfare"? I have not insinuated such. *Scripturally, ministers of the Gospel are to be supported adequately by the people they minister to and the church( 1 Cor 9:1-14). That is incontrovertible. God ordained it*.

However, this must be done in an orderly manner. There must be a written policy on what goes to your welfare and agreed upon by the relevant authorities in your ministry. There's a scriptural precedence on this.

Under the old covenant, God specified what belongs to the Levites and priests from the offerings, gifts and sacrifices of the people. Everyone in Israel knew the tithe was for the Levites and priests. All that was due the priests were written and specified. They don't take things arbitrarily.

*In financial matters, you must be guided. Let a written policy document guide your expenses. You're not different from a thief If you take what has not been given to you. You must have a healthy approach to money matters in ministry*.

*In addition, do not beg or demand for honorarium. You're not a ministry merchant. Never preach for money or gain. Stop selecting invitation because of money. Your presence on pulpits should not be for the highest bidder*.

You will do well to hold adequate transport fare If invited to preach outside your domain. Do not depend on honorarium to foot your transport bills. You may be disappointed.

*ACTION POINT*: Have a healthy and bible-based mindset about money as a minister. It will help you.

*DEAR MINISTER* Pt.8

Colossians 4:17 *And say to Archippus, “Be sure to carry out the ministry the Lord gave you*.” NLT

*PERSONAL CONSECRATION AND RETREAT*

As a growing minister, one of the things I learnt early from some models in ministry is the art of developing personal consecration and constant retreats.

One of my models in ministry said there are days he earmarked to be alone with God. He doesn't counsel or see anyone until later in the day. That's a personal consecration he developed for effectiveness in ministry.

I read another minister whom God used to change the course of history in his country of residence. In his book,  he wrote on how he'll lock himself in the room for days until he get refilled.

Most of us love to quote Paul. Though he is long gone yet he's still preaching on various pulpits around the world. I doubt If anyone can preach a complete Gospel without referring to any of the Pauline revelation.

Paul didn't attain to that depth in one day. Listen to what he said, *"Dear brothers and sisters,... I received my message from no human source, and no one taught me. Instead, I received it by direct revelation from Jesus Christ. Nor did I go up to Jerusalem to consult with those who were apostles before I was. Instead, I went away into Arabia, and later I returned to the city of Damascus*"(Gal1:11-12,17 NLT).

Till date, there's a debate on what Paul did during his three years seclusion. I believe those were years of personal retreat that birthed the revelations in his epistles.

*When you see great men in ministry, check their personal consecration and retreat lifestyle*. While others are playing away their time, they are busy with God.

*It is not wise to dispense all the time without refuelling. Fight against that addiction to microphone. Rather, be addicted to his presence. That's where value can be added to your life and ministry*.

It is not the number of invitations you get that validate your calling neither is it a determinant of whether you're on fire or not. There may be a flicker but once it burn out, those inviting you may relegate you into the bin of forgetfulness.

*Your depth is measured by the strength of your intimacy with God in fellowship not the number of invitations you get. Conquer that microphone addiction*.

There are times you need to suspend preaching and teaching activities to be alone with God. Don't preach yourself dry to the point of suffering a burn-out.

*Going on personal retreat is not something you do once in a while, you must include it in your plans. You have to be intentional about it.  Find a place and time for it*.

If you want to remain fresh and green, If you want your words to be mixed with the power and fire of the Spirit, always go on personal retreats and develop personal consecration.

*ACTION POINT*: Develop a personal consecration and habit of going on personal retreats.

*DEAR MINISTER* Pt.9

Colossians 4:17 *And say to Archippus, “Be sure to carry out the ministry the Lord gave you*.” NLT.

*BE PATIENT WITH GOD FOR GROWTH*

Recently, I read about a pastor who decided to commit suicide because his church was not growing. Nothing seems to be working in his ministry so he decided to commit suicide.

This kind of feeling is what made some persons to cut corners in ministry because they have waited for long doing the right thing but nothing is happening.

No thanks to social media. The media has a way of amplifying what people are doing in an unrealistic manner. There is a way to snap pictures to make it look as If there's a crowd in church.

*The pastor of a small church who is consistent with God may think he is not doing enough seeing the growth of another church that started yesterday*.

This may cause a watering down and lowering of standards just to achieve rapid growth. Beware of that trap of "rapid" growth. *In ministry, whatever will last doesn't happen rapidly*.

You have to be patient with God to learn the ropes and be part of the process. *God uses time to process us in ministry to make us better, fit and prepared for his assignment*.

*Do not put yourself under pressure to populate and perform*. Follow the process steadily and do not look at another man's farmyard. If God blesses the work of others,thank God for them and rejoice with them but run your race steadily.

Do not yourself under pressure because of another man's progress. Pressure leads to competition. Once you start competing, you'll veer off into another man's race. Run your race patiently.

Having done all you need to do, please be patient with God for growth and increase. Your current phase does not necessarily mean you are lazy or not doing the right thing. It is just a phase.

*ACTION POINT*: Dear minister, please be patient with God

April 24th, 2023.

*DEAR MINISTER* Pt.10

Colossians 4:17 *And say to Archippus, “Be sure to carry out the ministry the Lord gave you*.” NLT.


*RUN FROM THE "ABC" TRAP*( A)

The "ABC" trap in ministry is a coinage for the obsessive desire for large Attendance, Building and Cash.

*A for attendance*. We are in the age of mega church. It is noteworthy that God has blessed the work of some ministers to the point where attendance in a single service is in tens and hundreds of thousands.

We cannot deny the move of God and his mighty power in drawing multitudes to himself. I am sure some ministers did not start out with the desire to be mega. They remained faithful to their assignment and God blessed their ministry.

*I have nothing against mega churches that God raised and properly pastored the bible way. However, as a minister of the Gospel, your obsession shouldn't be about running a "mega" church or ministry. Not everyone will have a mega church*.

I did not see it as a genuine ministry desire. From the standpoint of scriptures and apostolic doctrine, we never saw Jesus, Paul, Peter and other servants of God focus their teaching or preaching on how to be "mega" in ministry. Check the content of their teaching, they taught doctrines.

*The obsessive desire to be mega may lead to compromise and lowering of standards*. In a bid to increase attendance, some have gone diabolical or employed gimmicks so that crowds can come in.

Some mega churches did not go diabolical but they lowered standards for crowd to come in. *I am wary of mega church model that do not have sunday school discipleship structure for discipling their members*.

The desire is always to quickly finish service for the next to start. Quality is sacrificed for quantity and brevity. That's not how the church of Christ in centuries past was built strong and solid.

Let me quickly make a case for the restoration of sunday structure which has died in most mega and modern churches today. One of the reasons given by most churches who neglect sunday school is that they have other training structures for their members.

While that may be true, the question to ask is about the effectiveness of such training structures. Do people come/ attend those training classes? Are the training structures continuous like the old weekly sunday school?

In reality, every pastor knows that whatever training structure fixed during the week is limited in reach because those who attend church during the week are not up to 50% of the total membership. This may be due to work and traffic issues for those living in mega cities.

*The sunday school remains the most effective opportunity to train and raise disciples because sunday is the day where most people have time for corporate worship. You can reach more persons on sunday than during the week*.

A number of solid and properly trained believers today were raised through the sunday school system. Check.

*The New Testament Church is a discipleship centre. The desire to populate should not overide the desire to disciple believers to become more like Jesus Christ*.

Evangelism and discipleship should be your forte. Kill the obsessive desire for attendance before it kills your ministry.

*ACTION POINT*: Do not compromise or lower biblical standards to increase attendance. Disciple God's people.

*DEAR MINISTER* Pt.11

Colossians 4:17 *And say to Archippus, “Be sure to carry out the ministry the Lord gave you*.” NLT.

*RUN FROM THE "ABC" TRAP*( B)

One of the Popes in history took St. Thomas Aquinas around the magnificient church building. He said, "No longer can we say silver and gold have I none".

St. Thomas Aquinas replied saying "Neither can we say 'rise up and walk." The former was obsessed with the magnificience of the building but the latter pointed him to the loss of power in the church.

I heard the story of a preacher who was a cougar to high class society women. He will sniff cocaine to sleep with these women. He was doing threesome.

Why? Anywhere he wanted a church, these rich women will build one for him. That was the promise they made to him. It was a deal. He was obsessed with physical building at the expense of his soul.

*'B' for building. Is it bad for a church to build? No it is not. I am an advocate owning properties instead of renting. In fact, I counsel that churches start building early and not wait till they have problem of space*.

As a ministry, we are also building and trusting God for huge funds to finish it. I know God will finish what He started in Jesus name.

*However, it is a demonic trap to be so obsessed with physical building at the expense of building God's people. It is Satan's ploy to make ministers compete in building structures using it as one of the metrics of success*.

One of the fallouts of apostasy in church history is the focus on building magnificient structures. It became an obsession of the Bishops of those days till the church lost the fire of the Spirit.

Unfortunately, some of those magnificient structures have either been burnt down, demolished or taken over by the enemies of the Cross. Hagia Sophia cathedral is an example.

The Jihad of the Ottoman leader ensured Hagia Sophia turned to a mosque. Muslims worshipped there for over 400 years before the government of Tutkey turned it to a museum. That shows God doesn't dwell in buildings made by man but God indwells man.

Acts 7:48 says, *"However, the Most High doesn’t live in temples made by human hands. As the prophet says*".

Another place reads, *“He is the God who made the world and everything in it. Since he is Lord of heaven and earth, he doesn’t live in man-made temples*"( Acts 17:28).

*Dear minister, do not get carried away by building physical structures. While it is good for our comfort as a people, it should never be an obsessive pursuit or a thing of pride*.

God has not called us to build the biggest cathedral. It is not a calling neither is it a ministry. *It shouldn't even be mentioned that one of our metrics for success in ministry is building*.

Some monies are better invested in evangelism, missions and discipleship than in building our empire. The empire of man will always fall as that has been the order in history.

*ACTION POINT*: The moment Satan gets you obsessed with the "B" trap, you will lower the standard of righteousness to achieve it. Beware!

*DEAR MINISTER* Pt.12

Colossians 4:17 *And say to Archippus, “Be sure to carry out the ministry the Lord gave you*.” NLT.

*RUN FROM THE "ABC" TRAP* (C)

Recently, it was reported in the news that a preacher who came for a crusade in Nigeria swindled God's people after which he bolted.

He collected their phones, over 50 phones. He sold a bottle of water to them at the rate of N4,800. He said there was perfume inside the bottle water, and many people paid for the water.

C for cash. This is the commonest of all traps. From the onset, some are in ministry basically for the cash while the focus of some  changed from souls to getting cash.

In a bid to getting cash, they  lie prophetically using God's name to swindle the people and to make them part with their monies. They merchandise the gospel and rip the people off.

I am an advocate of good welfare for preachers. Yes, it is biblical to support a minister of the Gospel. God ordained it. Poverty slows a minister down. I hate it.

*That said, as a minister, you don't set your heart on using your privileged office to amass wealth. You will be tempted into doing wrong stuff*.

*But people who long to be rich fall into temptation and are trapped by many foolish and harmful desires that plunge them into ruin and destruction* (1 Timothy 6:9 NLT).

*Dear man of God, do not let the love of money overcome you. The love of God and souls should dominate your heart*.

Apostle Paul has good counsel for us here: *"For the love of money is the root of all kinds of evil. And some people, craving money, have wandered from the true faith and pierced themselves with many sorrows. But you, Timothy, are a man of God; so run from all these evil things. Pursue righteousness and a godly life, along with faith, love, perseverance, and gentleness*"(1 Timothy 6:10-11 NLT).

Dear minister, do not get trapped by the "C" syndrome. Be contented and trust God to supply your needs.

Paul said, *"Yet true godliness with contentment is itself great wealth. After all, we brought nothing with us when we came into the world, and we can’t take anything with us when we leave it*" ( 1 Timothy 6:6-7 NLT).

*ACTION POINT*: Don't put a price tag on your ministry neither should you monetize the grace of God. You are a Gospel messenger not a gospel merchant.

*TEACH ME TO DO YOUR WILL*


Psalms143:10 *Teach me to do your will, for you are my God; may your good Spirit lead me on level ground* (NIV).


I have read the book of Psalms severally and I must tell you that it has become a special devotional book for me.


Out of the several prayers prayed by David in Psalms, I found his prayer in Psalms 143:10 important especially when confused about what the perfect will of God is.


Every true child of God should have a desire to do God's will. The word of God is the will of God written for men in all ages.


However, there are certain things that are not directly written in the word. For example, the name of the country you should travel to, the job offer to accept, the name of the husband or wife that you should marry is not in the bible.

Though the bible contains principles and guidelines for the choices we may want to make. However, certain specifics are not there.


Yet, God doesn't want us to do things our way because the moment we begin to do things our way, we may veer off the path designed for us.


Sometimes, we want to do God's will but we do not know what it is exactly and how to go about it. That's where confusion sets in. I have been there severally.


You and I need to pray the Psalmist prayer often: *"Teach me to do your will, for you are my God*". Jesus promised that the Holy Spirit will guide us into all truths( John16:33).

*The Spirit of God knows the will of God and he is our empowerment to do God's will*.

Be rest assured that God is working in you to do his will and good pleasure daily( Phil2:13). Your duty is to surrender to the Holy Spirit.


*PRAYER*: Father, teach me to do your will today and for the rest of my life in Jesus name. Amen

*DEAL WITH FALSE GUILT*

1John1:9 If we confess our sins, he is faithful and just and will forgive us our sins and purify us from all unrighteousness.

*What is false guilt? This is the guilt that comes or remains after you've repented of your sins. Remaining in perpetual guilt is demonic*.

One of the tools the enemy uses to dampen our faith, prayer life and to make us feel unloved by God is false guilt. In itself, guilt is not bad if it leads us to genuine repentance.

Sometimes, conviction comes with guilt but  what the Holy Spirit does is to point us to the solution. However, there is a type of guilt that is destructive and mind wrecking.


Those days on campus, some medical students told me that a psychiatric patient spoke in tongues while another had mental issues because of guilt related feelings. It baffled some of them how a Christian can be in a mental home. Some are there because of false guilt.

One of the reasons  guilt feelings remain after repentance is because victims hardly believe that their sins are forgiven*. Yes! that's a major factor.

In other religion of the world, it takes doing a lot to be forgiven but here in Christianity, we receive forgiveness by faith because someone paid the price. Hallelujah!!!

*If you are having problems with reoccurring guilt, it shows that you didn't believe in the forgiving power of God through Christ*.

I have heard those who recommend fasting as a condition to be forgiven. As good as fasting is, it is not a criteria to be forgiven, faith in the finished work of Christ is.

When God forgives, he forget and he doesn't keep records of your failures:  *"And I will forgive their wickedness, and I will never again remember their sins*”( Hebrews 8:12 NLT).

*If you can believe what the bible says about God's forgiveness, you will overcome false guilt*.

*Let me say that we all feel guilty sometimes, but our feelings may not be right at all times. How we feel only points to our belief system. A faulty belief system about God's forgiveness will keep you in perpetual guilt*.

Dare to believe what God says in his word not how you feel. If you submit to feelings, you will always feel inadequate and unworthy of God. Demons use false guilt to talk and control people, don't listen.

*PRAYER*: Father thank you because you love me. I take authority over the demon of false guilt troubling my heart in Jesus name.

DEVOTIONAL 0843 | Source start line: 83573

4/29/23, 12:00 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: PROJECTSAVE DEVOTIONAL
Saturday,
April 29th, 2023.

*PROCESSED IN THE WILDERNESS* Pt.1

Ex13:17'...*God did not lead them along the main road that runs through Philistine territory, even though that was the shortest route to the Promised Land....18 So God led them in a roundabout way through the wilderness toward the Red Sea*(NLT).

The wilderness season of life is not a palatable season. It is the time God breaks and make men to become suitable for life and destiny.

Let's consider the journey of the Israelites. When the children of Israel left Egypt to the promised land, God decided not to lead them through a short-cut to the promised land because if they encounter war, they might return to Egypt.

Instead, God led them in a round about way through the wilderness. As a thoughtful reader, you should ask a simple question.

If God could help them deal with Pharoah by showing his mighty acts through the 10 plagues, why can't He( God) use the same power to deal with the enemies they may face on the shortest route to the promised?

After all, He is powerful and He's a man of war, why should He lead them through the wilderness to avoid enemies He can deal with?

Well, the answer is very simple. *God needed to lead them through the wilderness because He wanted to process them for the promised land*.

These are people who just got their independence from slavery, If they had entered the promised land in three days through the shortest route, they'll carry the Egypt mentality, character and culture into their new domain so God needed to spend a little time to process them.

*Egypt is symbolic of the world and her evil system, the wilderness experience represents the dealings of God in our lives, the challenges, the test and trials*.

Modern Christianity doesn't teach people about the dealings of God yet it is real. Christ couldn't become our high priest until he had his share of wilderness experience.

*Even though Jesus was God’s Son, he learned obedience from the things he suffered.In this way, God qualified him as a perfect High Priest, and he became the source of eternal salvation for all those who obey him* ( Heb 5:8-9).

*After you left your life of sin and you came to Christ, maybe you were told that once you come to Christ, no more challenges. That's not true about the Christian race*.

*Evangelism or soul winning activity built on false promises such as this will raise people who will not stand for Christ in the day of trouble*.

One way or the other, it is God's grand design that you and I should have the wilderness experience before entering into your 'promised land'.

Christ didn't promise us a life without trial but victory is what He promised: *"I have told you all this so that you may have peace in me. Here on earth you will have many trials and sorrows. But take heart, because I have overcome the world*”( Jn 16:33 NLT).

Sometimes, God leads us in a roundabout way of the wilderness instead of passing through short-cuts. Submit yourself to His dealings.

*PRAYER*: Father, I receive the grace to submit myself for your dealings In Jesus name.

DEVOTIONAL 0844 | Source start line: 83645

4/30/23, 12:53 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: ProjectSave Devotional

**PROCESSED IN THE WILDERNESS* Pt.2

Ex16:4 *Then the LORD said to Moses, “Look, I’m going to rain down foodl from heaven for you. Each day the people can go out and pick up as much food as they need for that day. I will test them in this to see whether or not they will follow my instructions*.

*TESTING PERIOD*

During my youth service, one of our senior friends, Pastor Tony Itsueli,  shared with us a revelation he had during his own service year.

In that vision, he saw a long line of corpers. He saw someone awarding marks to individuals as though they had written an examination. The fellow( presumably an angel) also showed him what he scored after his NYSC year. Never knew service year was a testing time until he shared that with us.

God test and score his children like an examiner. In fact, I won't be wrong to say God is an examiner: *“I the LORD search the heart and examine the mind, to reward each person according to their conduct, according to what their deeds deserve*"( Jer 17:10 NIV).

Have you ever wondered when and where God tested the brothers of David when he disqualified them for kingship in the presence of Samuel? They were living their normal lives not knowing God was seeking who to enthrone.

David was somewhere in the wilderness tending the flock of his father yet he did not know he was writing a test. He passed his test for kingship unknowingly and God took him from the wilderness to the throne.

In every citadel of learning, examination precedes promotion. I am yet to see a student that got promoted without writing exams. The same applies in the things of the Spirit.

God is a good examiner; the opening test made it clear that God tested the Israelites to be sure they would adhere to instructions.

*You cannot become a living proof until God proves you. The milk and honey of the promised land can only be entrusted into the hands of those that have successfully passed God's test*.

The reason believers are not on the same level of glory spiritually  may be because of success or failure in previous  divine examinations.

*Operating at some level of glory may not necessarily have to do with how long you can pray or knowledge possessed but how well you have passed God's test of faithfulness and integrity*.

An assignment where faithfulness and integrity is needed, God will never put the unfaithful there. Joseph is a prime example. God needed a faithful man to preserve a race.

Joseph proved himself faithful in Potiohar's house as a servant. The faithful and uncompromising servant later became the second in command who will distribute food resources equitably and with wisdom.

If he was an unfaithful man, hunger and famine may have exterminated the Jewish race. God tested and tried a man during his wilderness season, he passed and was lifted.

Speaking about Joseph, the bible made it clear that the word of the Lord tried him (Psalm105:19). He wouldn't have become a prime minister If he had failed his test.

God saw in Joseph a man that was faithful because he was tested faithful in little things. He managed his master's money, properties and  wife with purity. *If he had corrupted himself with money, God wouldn't have recommended him for the throne*.

*If he had numerous girl friends as a chief slave, he may become a professor of adultery as a prime minister. If you fail God, you peg your progress*.

In Matthew4:1, we were told that it was the Holy Spirit that led Jesus Christ into the wilderness to be tested. I know you have been praying that God should move you to the next level of glory, but are you ready to write exams?

*Your present level is a test, if you pass it well, no devil can stop you from moving to the next level*.

*PRAYER*: Father, may I not fail your test. I receive grace to pass life's test In Jesus name.

*PROCESSED IN THE WILDERNESS* Pt.3

Exodus15:24 *So the people grumbled against Moses, saying, "what are we to drink*? (NIV).

*CHARACTER REFINERY* (A)

Have you watched 'Roots'? If you haven't, please do. It has some some historical value. I was privileged to watch it years back.

It is the story of how slave merchants captured a teenage boy from Gambia to America. It is the true life story of Kinta Kunteh.

In the slave camp, murmuring, grumbling&rebellion was a common character among the slaves. The rebellious action of Kinta Kunteh led to the amputation of his feet by his slave masters.

He became bitter towards them and passed it on to generations after him. Till date, the black race especially the African Americans still have that subtle bitterness in them against the whites till date.

One of the reasons for the wilderness experience is to refine our character. The Israelites were slaves for many years in Egypt.

One character that is common to people who have had inhumane treatment is bitterness,  murmuring&rebellion. God knew they couldn't enter into the promised land with that character.

He took them through the wilderness so that He can deal with that character. In Exodus16:2, they murmured and we were told that the destroyer( devil) destroyed some of them(Israelites) because they murmured(1Cor10:10).

What God was trying to deal with in their lives ended up destroying them because they failed to submit to His dealings in their lives. *The wilderness season can only turn out well for you If you submit to God's dealings*.

*There are habits&characters that may bring you down when you climb the ladder of glory; the reason why God has permitted some delay in your life is to deal with those habits. If not dealt with by God, you may mess things up*.

*What Samson failed to deal with brought him down and destroyed him. Some singles  are praying for a life partner but what can destroy their marriage resides in them, yet they wonder why God hasn't settled them. God is not interested in failed marriages*.

Let God use this trying times to work on you and refine your character so that we can speak like Jesus who said: *"...for the prince of this world cometh, and hath nothing in me*"(John 14:30 KJV).

*PRAYER*: Father, take me to your refinery and refine my character through the word and by your Spirit In Jesus name.

*PROCESSED IN THE WILDERNESS* Pt.4

Exodus 2:14  *The man said, “Who made you ruler and judge over us? Are you thinking of killing me as you killed the Egyptian?” Then Moses was afraid and thought, “What I did must have become known*”(NIV).

*CHARACTER REFINERY* (B)

The life and times of Moses is worth studying by all. He was a great leader indeed. The Jews don't joke anything from or about Moses. Have you stidied the life of Moses?

His birth, his escape from death during babyhood, miraculous adoption by Pharaoh's daughter, his growth in the palace, his quest for the liberation of his people which led him to kill the Egyptian, how he fled into the wilderness, his encounter with God and return to Egypt?

You are familiar with his story but that's not all there is to his life. Moses sensed the purpose of God for His life while in Egypt, he knew he had a mandate to deliver his people from slavery.

However, he went about it the wrong way when he killed the Egyptian.

He thought no one knew until he was challenged: *'Who made you a ruler and judge over us*"?

When he saw that he was not yet 'made' as a leader, he fled into the wilderness to enrol for a 40 years duration character training course.

Moses' character flaw was anger and I believe he may be pompous. Why? He was raised in the house of Pharaoh as a prince.

Those days,  princes live large, servants are around to do almost everything. God saw the enormity of his assignment but his character doesn't match. Destiny must match with character but Moses' was different.

The only place where he could be refined in character was in the University of Wilderness. He studied for 40 years in the wilderness citadel of learning.

We didn't hear anything about his life not until he completed his wilderness course. Actually, the world doesn't see men in the wilderness season of life because they are in training.

God is concerned about the quality of your character before handing over great kingdom task to you. Oftentimes, character is formed during the wilderness season of life.

*There must be a 'making' before you feature in God's plan for your life*.

*The dealings of God in our lives give us depth. While men care about height God is concerned with depth*.

*With great depth, you can reach any height and remain there but with poor depth, you'll crash from any height reached. What do you want*?

*PRAYER*: Father, I submit myself to your dealings upon my life In Jesus name.

*PROCESSED IN THE WILDERNESS* Pt.5

Exodus 2:14  *The man said, “Who made you ruler and judge over us? Are you thinking of killing me as you killed the Egyptian?” Then Moses was afraid and thought, “What I did must have become known*”(NIV).

*CHARACTER REFINERY* (C)

Anytime I study Joseph, i'm intrigued. At a very young age, he had a dream that was prophetic in nature.

He told his parents and brothers about it but his brothers became bitter. They conspired against him and sold him into slavery.

We know how he ended his life by becoming the prime minister in Egypt. I have heard some preachers blame Joseph for telling his brothers about his dream.

They insinuated that if he had kept his dream to himself, he wouldn't have passed through that ordeal.

I use to believe that not until I came across Psalm105:19 where it was clearly stated: *"Until the time came to fulfill his dreams, the Lord tested Joseph’s character*"( NLT).

It is so clear that  God knew about his ordeal. In fact,  the  verse reads in KJV: *"Until the time that his word came, the word of the LORD tried him*".

*In contemporary English, that sentence could be rendered as: Before the fulfilment of his dream: God permitted him to pass through challenges*.

*It was not his brothers that orchestrated his ordeal, they were only instrumental to what has been designed. If they didn't sell him into Egypt, Joseph would still find himself there*.

Joseph himself acknowledged this truth in the presence of his brothers in Genesis 45:7-9.

So why did God allow Joseph pass through those challenges? I think it was to refine his character.

Mr Joseph was loved by his father, in other words, he was pampered. His mother  died when she gave birth to Benjamin(Gen35:16-18). His mum was the favourite wife of his father and he was the first for his mother.

Jacob felt all he could do to honour his late beloved was to pamper this boy, so he made a coat of many colours for him (Genesis37:3). His brothers saw that preferential treatment and they became angry.

His brothers were farmers but Joseph stayed at home.  As long as He was tied to his father's apron, he couldn't fulfil destiny.

God had to allow Mr Joseph pass through those painful experiences to refine his character, mature him and prepare him for the throne.

*Character development is more important to God than ascending the throne because He want you to last on the throne of your destiny*.

In your wilderness season, ask the Lord to show you the character he want you to drop and the ones He want to develop in you. It is crucial If you will move out of your wilderness season.

*PRAYER*: Father, I receive grace to learn what I need to learn during my wilderness experience In Jesus name.

**PROCESSED IN THE WILDERNESS* Pt.6

Hebrews 5:8  *Son though he was, he learned obedience from what he suffered* (NIV).


*CHARACTER REFINERY*(D)


Jesus Christ had his fair share of wilderness experience. Sometimes, we do not pay attention to the sufferings of the Christ as we should.


The idea of his suffering we have is the suffering on the cross but that's just the height of it but not all.


For Christ to leave His glory to take the form of a man is a form of suffering on its own. I am very sure most of us would have declined to come to the earth to redeem mankind if we had lived in heaven and tasted of her glory.


For a man like me that have seen two sides of life, i'll rather choose to stay in heaven than come to the earth let alone Nigeria.

Christ left His glory, He took the form of a man: *"Instead, he gave up his divine privileges; he took the humble position of a slave and was born as a human being. When he appeared in human form*" ( Philippians 2:7 NLT).


Can you imagine what His growing years were like? Probably, his mother treated him like a child, gave him instructions on where to play, where to go, what to eat and when to eat.


For a man like Jesus Christ who is God Himself, that's suffering yet the bible said He learnt obedience through what He suffered.

*Even though Jesus was God’s Son, he learned obedience from the things he suffered* ( Hebrews 5:8 NLT).


God's purpose for allowing the wilderness experience is not to kill you but to refine your character. Jesus couldn't offer eternal salvation not until he passed through the wilderness season.


Hebrews 2:10 says: *"God, for whom and through whom everything was made, chose to bring many children into glory. And it was only right that he should make Jesus, through his suffering, a perfect leader, fit to bring them into their salvation*" (NLT).

We need to be sensitive to the dealings of God and the seasons in which we find ourselves. What you are going through is permitted for a reason.

The earlier you learn your lessons and submit to the dealings of God, the quicker your exit from the university of wilderness.

You are coming out strong in Jesus name.


*PRAYER*: Father, make me the man you want me to be In Jesus name.

*PROCESSED IN THE WILDERNESS* Pt.7

Exodus19:5-6 *Now if you obey me fully and keep my covenant, then out of all nations you will be my treasured possession. Although the whole earth is mine, 6 you[a] will be for me a kingdom of priests and a holy nation.’ These are the words you are to speak to the Israelites*”

*MINDSET LAUNDRY*

Slavery affects the mind primarily because of its dehumanizing effect. Till date, countries and continents brutally enslaved in the past has traces of mindset damage passed from one generation to the next.

Even after independence, the generation of slaves still look up to their master's children for survival. That is freedom not independence. True independence is a state of the mind functioning rightly not just a proclamation.

This is one of the things God doesn't want for the Israelites. They were slaves for years in Egypt. Generations were born into slavery and thus their mindset was that of a slave.

If a Jewish couple in slavery gave birth to a child during those years in Egypt, the child wouldn't know what freedom is because from childhood to adulthood, servitude under harsh condition was the order of the day.

The thought of such a child would be warped and wired to think like a slave and not a free born.

*God's original intention for the nation of Israel was to make them a kingdom of priests and holy nation whose influence would be unmatched*.

*There was no way they would have fulfilled the desire of God If they hadn't passed through the wilderness. God seized the opportunity of their sojourn to do a mental laundry on them so they can see themselves the way He sees them*.

God knew that if they had passed through the shortest route to the promised land, He wouldn't have had the opportunity to change their mindset. It would be disastrous to enter the promised land with the mindset of a slave.

*You need to know who you are in Christ and exercise your rights and privileges in Him but it is impossible to manifest your believer's authority without a challenge demanding for the exercise of your authority in Christ*.

*There are things God permits so that we can learn how to exercise our faith, understand our royal status and take authority*.

*Slaves cannot command, only kings do but when a king thinks, talk and act like a slave, he needs a mental laundry*.

*The wilderness experience gives you the opportunity to renew your mind through God's word,  to reveal who you are in Christ and to take charge like a king that you are*.

PRAYER: Father, I receive grace to allow God's word transform my thinking In Jesus name

*PROCESSED IN THE WILDERNESS* Pt.8

Psalm 55:12-14 *If an enemy were insulting me, I could endure it; if a foe were rising against me, I could hide.(13) But it is you, a man like myself, my companion, my close friend,(14) with whom I once enjoyed sweet fellowship at the house of God,as we walked about among the worshipers*.

*TO KNOW YOUR TRUE FRIENDS*

*Dearest, you cannot know your true friends until you pass through the wilderness*.

One of the problems the children of Israel had were the mixed multitudes that journeyed with them from Egypt into the wilderness on their way to the promised land.

Exodus 12:38 says, *"A mixed multitude also went up with them, along with flocks and herds, a very large number of livestock*".

It is impossible to grow in life without good companions and associates. *Some persons are chaff, they must be separated from the wheat of your life*.

In the opening scriptures, David told us about his friends and companions that betrayed him.

*Before entering into the fullness  of God's plans and purposes for your life, some persons must be separated from your life*.

They are the chaff in the wheat of your life, they are the clog in your wheel yet you call them friends but God knows they are not.

The period of life you have the privilege of knowing your true friends, companions and associates is in your wilderness season.

At the time Jesus was about to be glorified, he needed to hand over the mandate to his 12 disciples, the son of perdition showed up in betrayal.That was a form of separation.

When Job passed through his ordeal, those he thought were his friends wrongly accused him of sinning against God not knowing it was God that permitted it.

In life, we cannot do without friends but God is trying to help us build solid relationship that would sustain us in the promised land.

*PRAYER:* Father, help me to separate the wheat from the chaff in my life In Jesus name.

*PROCESSED IN THE WILDERNESS * Pt.9

Exodus 17:15 *Moses built an altar and called it The Lord is my Banner*.

*FELLOWSHIP TIME*

The wilderness season affords us the opportunity to build an altar. An altar in the physical is used for sacrifice but in the spiritual, it denotes a place of fellowship.

*One of the reasons for the wilderness experience is that it is a time where an altar of fellowship can be built*.

While in Egypt, the Israelite couldn't build an altar for God. They were occupied with hard labour so they had no time to fellowship with their maker.

God's desire is  that we worship Him in Spirit&truth(John4:24). If they had passed through the shortest route to the promised land,Moses wouldn't have built an altar.

God was so concerned about the altar in Leviticus to the extent that he gave an instruction about it: *'The fire must be kept burning on the altar continuously; it must not go out*"(Lev6:13).

In your wilderness season,  God want to develop a relationship with you. That season is the time to be set on fire for the Lord.

*A virile private& personal fellowship is important to God. God want to talk with you and commune with you as friend. That is only possible when you have a virile fellowship with him*.

Your prayer life and word study life needs to be revived. You don't beg a man in the wilderness to be closer to God in the word and in prayers because there are so many battles to conquer before entering into the promised land.

*The earlier you had recognized that your wilderness season is for a purpose the quicker your exit would be*.

*PRAYER*: Father, i receive the grace for constant fellowship  with you In Jesus name.

*PROCESSED IN THE WILDERNESS* Pt.10

Psalm86:11 *Teach me Thy way, O Lord; I will walk in Thy truth; unite my heart to fear Thy name*(KJV).

*LEARN THE LESSONS QUICKLY*

Have you heard about Murphy and Peter's principle? If you haven't, I'll share with you. I find their postulations relevant to this teaching.

Murphy&Peter principle states that:
1- A lesson is repeated until it is learned
2-If you don't learn the easy lessons, they get harder.
3-You'll know you've learnt  a lesson when your action change.

*To buttress Murphy&Peter principle in context of our teaching, i'll say that In your wilderness season, the quicker you learn your lessons, the faster your exit*.

One major purpose for the wilderness experience in the life of the Israelites was that God wanted to school them using heaven's curriculum. They just left Egypt, a country that is symbolic of sin, idolatry and evil.

They had seen the Egyptian life and probably some imbibed their culture. God knew it was impossible for them to change except He takes them through kingdom lessons but they failed to learn, little wonder why most of them died in the wilderness without entering the promised land except Joshua& Caleb.

*If you don't want to stay long in your wilderness season, learn fast. Consider Murphy&Peter principle. Failure to learn the easy way would make your lessons harder*.

*The proof that you have learnt a lesson is the change we see in your action*.

Dearest, God is working on you. That unfavourable circumstance has a lesson embedded in it for you.  Stop prolonging your days in the wilderness like the Jews did.

God intended their wilderness journey to last 40 days but it stretched into 40 years because they didn't learn fast. The promised land is so precious to God and He wouldn't entrust it in the hands of those that have not taken full lessons.

*PRAYER*: Father, I receive grace to learn fast In Jesus name.

*PROCESSED IN THE WILDERNESS* Pt.11

Isaiah31:1 *Woe to them that go down to Egypt for help; and stay on horses, and trust in chariots, because they are many; and in horsemen, because they are very strong; but they look not unto the Holy One of Israel, neither seek the Lord*!

*DO NOT SEEK HELP FROM EGYPT*

*One of the temptations you might face in your wilderness season is to seek help from other sources. Ungodly alternatives will present itself to you*.

Sometime ago, I was inside a commercial bus in Lagos, a man began to advertise charms. He sweet-talked the passengers and boasted about the efficacy of  his charms to make them rich yet he looked poor and haggard.


Most of the people patronized him. The lady that sat beside me began to make jest of those buying the charms.

The man kept talking about the several cases or issues the charm could solve, immediately, this lady ordered for hers. I was shocked. I thought we were both against such. From what i saw, even those who professed to be 'Christians' patronized him.

The only help Egypt offers is demonic help. You cannot afford to run to Egypt charmers for help, it attracts God's curse.

There are many believers that still believe going the traditional way is a good way when all else fail.

The resurgence of African Traditional Religion is beginning to gain ground. They're now on radio and even television. Their common mantra is that traditional religion is older than Christianity so it is superior. They try to appeal to the reasoning of their listeners. That's Egyptian trick.

In your wilderness season many Egyptian options will present itself but please don't go there.

Psalm16:4 says: *"Their sorrows shall be multiplied that hasten after another god*".

The resultant effect of seeking help from Egypt is more sorrows. Stay with God and trust Him, your wilderness season will soon be over.

*PRAYER*: Father,I receive grace to trust you fully In Jesus name

*PROCESSED IN THE WILDERNESS* Pt.12

1Cor10:10 *Neither murmur ye, as some of them also murmured, and were destroyed of the destroyer*.

*DO NOT MURMUR*

*In your wilderness season, do not murmur. God hates murmuring*.

One of the things that got God angry towards the children of Israel was because they murmured. In fact, their murmuring opened the door for the destroyer to destroy them.

They were used to murmuring during slavery in Egypt. Their task masters stressed them and put unnecessary burden on them so murmuring was a normal reaction but they forgot that God is a loving father not a task master.

I have heard of people who would give God conditions like: 'If you don't do 'XYZ', i'll backslide'. Some think they can manipulate God into doing things for them by issuing threats.

Even the Israelites told Moses that Egypt was better than the wilderness because of foods like onion, garlic, etc. Murmuring drove them to saying those things that displeased God.

During your wilderness season, please don't murmur. Do not talk anyhow to God and accuse Him as If He's a criminal.

*Murmuring is an expression of faithlessness, it is another way of saying God does not know what He is doing*.

You must be conscious of God's mercy and love during your wilderness season. Know that His dealings with you during the wilderness is season is borne out of love not anger.

Satan will always try to paint God to you in bad light and make you feel He is wicked. He may suggest you try out ungodly alternatives because He doesn't care.

Please don't listen to Him. If you accept his suggestion,   he will drive you into destruction like he did to the Israelites in the wilderness.

*PRAYER*: Father, I rebuke the spirit of murmuring in my life In Jesus name.
