**PROCESSED IN THE WILDERNESS* Pt.13

Psalm 118:5 In my distress I prayed to the Lord, and the Lord answered me and set me free.

*DEVELOP A VIRILE PRAYER LIFE*

I have been through some wilderness seasons that transformed my prayer life. One of such seasons was in 2006 while on campus.

I almost lost my studentship on campus. I was in school for weeks without a bearing, floating and totally confused. It was a time of great depression for me and I hope to write all that happened during that phase of life in my autobiography as the Lord permit.

One thing that resulted from that period in my life was a virile prayer life. I fasted for days and I lost count. The battle was tough but I decided to seek God's face.

Anytime I look back at those times, i thank God for what He did in my life. I went through the furnace of stagnancy but a strong prayer culture resulted from that season.


I felt my prayers were wasted because I didn't get the desired result but I understood much later that God had a hand in what happened and it was God's way of realigning me for His purpose.

*The wilderness season of your life should produce a strong prayer life in your walk with God. That thing you're going through should make you run to Him in prayers and not to run away from Him.*

I once heard a father in the Lord say he had a challenge in his life, he prayed for 3 hours straight. Something he had not done before. A revolution occured in his prayer life.

I have noticed that mighty men in the school of prayer did not just get there in one day, challenges launched them into a deeper dimension of prayer.

Pastor E.A. Adeboye said the opposition against Him early in ministry when he became the General Overseer was what led him into 40 days fasting and prayer without food.  Something  he had not done before that time.

After that phase was over, 40 days fasting&prayer became a lifestyle and we can see the product of his prayer life every where.

There are battles you cannot win with sweet grammar. Those battle are fought and won on your knees. No one will pray your portion of prayers for you.

*In the wilderness season, God want to make you outgrow dependency on the prayer life of others. He want you to have a personal prayer altar and not rely on your pastor or mother's prayer life alone*.

David said, *"Blessed be the LORD my strength, which teacheth my hands to war, and my fingers to fight*"( Psalms 144:1).

*The wilderness season is heaven's war college*. That is where you learn the art and science of warfare. I laugh at those who scorn the reality of spiriitual warfare.

Some persons are now denying the reality of spiritual warfare. The denial of the enemy's work is not a guarantee of victory. The right thing to do is to acknowledge the victory of Christ on the cross and enforce it.

The enforcement of our purchased victory is the warfare I'm talking about. I see some believers in serious mess but they hang on to mere talks and confessions instead of addressing the root of their issues in prayers.

Before you enter the promised land, God want to strengthen your prayer life because it is a tool for warfare. There are enemies at the gate of your promised land,

*PRAYER*: Father, I receive grace to cultivate a strong& steady prayer life In Jesus name.

5/12/23, 6:03 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: PROJECTSAVE DEVOTIONAL

*PROCESSED IN THE WILDERNESS* Pt.14

Psalm144:1 Blessed be the LORD my strength, which teacheth my hands to war, and my fingers to fight. CSB

*LEARN FAITH WARFARE*

Spiritual warfare is real. There was a time I had an attack that almost made me lose my mind. For several months, I couldn't sleep soundly because I heard strange voices.

It was so terrible because If I had cause to wake up at night to ease myself, the voices I heard before catching sleep will continue their gibberish till day break.

During that period, I knew I had to face it because the enemy was driving me somewhere but I refused to follow him there.

During that period, I had a terrible dream where I saw myself in a coven with two old haggard women. One of them urinated in my mouth. Waking up, I knew all that was happening to me wasn't ordinary.

No one taught me to fight. Halleluyah! Christ won the battle. However, after that period or season, I stopped being simple about spiritual warfare issues. So many things are going in the spirit realm

There are different types of warfare but the toughest of them all is spiritual warfare. That's what Apostle Paul called 'The Good Fight of Faith'(1Tim6:12).

It is Faith warfare. We don't use bombs, grenade, guns and physical weapons like worldly military men do. There are two extremes I've seen on the matter of spiritual warfare.

The first are those that see the devil in everything even when it is glaring that common sense can be applied to solve the issue at hand. The second extreme are those who do not believe there's spiritual warfare going on.

The latter suffer in silence yet claiming the devil isn't going about his business.  Apostle James knew the devil won't stop doing his dirty jobs that was why he told us to resist him(James4:7).

*Resisting the devil is not something you do smiling. It takes having a good grasp of the word and knowing your rights and privileges in Christ. This is the real faith warfare we're talking about not just yelling at the devil and doing gymnastics*.

Getting to your promised land will come with lots of confrontations. Ask the children of Israel. They fought the Amalekites, Hittites, Perizites, and other nations before they possessed the promised land.

Your wilderness season is the time for you to master the art of faith warfare. *Our offensive weapon is the word and our defensive weapon is faith in the word*.

When believers sleep, the enemy is fully awake to perpetrate evil, launch attacks and destroy. *Fight your way out of the wilderness*.

*PRAYER*: Father, I receive grace to overcome the battles of life In Jesus name.

May 13th, 2023.

*THE POWER OF SOLVING PROBLEMS*

Deuteronomy 8:18: *And you shall remember the Lord your God, for it is He who gives you power to get wealth, that He may establish (confirm) His covenant which He swore to your fathers, as it is this day*.”

Henry Ford had a faulty equipment. He did all he could to make the machine work but was without success.

When Ford saw that no one  could repair the piece of badly needed machinery, Ford called in Steinmetz who fiddled around with it for about ten minutes and had it working again.

A few days later, Ford received a bill from Steinmetz for $10,000! He immediately called up his friend and complained about the huge bill.

He said, "Ten thousand dollars is a lot of money to pay someone for tinkering around for ten minutes."

Steinmetz calmly answered, "Well, ten dollars of that bill was for the ten minutes I spent tinkering; $9,990 was for knowing where to tinker."

Knowledge is not just power, it is also money. Having relevant knowledge can be translated into wealth.

At a time, I needed clarity on some issues. I asked around but the answers I got weren't satisfactory. Someone referred me to a guru in that field.

When I consulted him, I paid a token to him just to share knowledge with me on the issue.

There is a principle behind wealth that will work for anyone anywhere in the world. *As long as you're adding relevant value to humanity by solving problems, money will come to you*.

*Never disconnect value creation and addition from wealth creation*. This is the secret of the wealthy all over the world.

*The rich keeps getting richer because they are providing solution to common problems*. You're buying those household items because you're not producing them.

As you purchase what you need to solve certain problems in your house, office or even church, the producer of the product is getting richer.

*Whatever problem you cannot solve on your own, you'll have to pay someone who will solve it for you. That's how problem solvers make money*.

*No matter how bad the economy of a nation is, problem solvers will make money*. I want to challenge you to start solving problems.

No matter how long you pray for financial breakthrough, money won't fall from heaven like rain because there is no mint company over there.

Instead of thinking about how to defraud people, pray God to give you an idea that will solve problems.

*There cannot be pure wealth transfer into the pockets of kingdom people If we are not creative and adding value by solving problems*.

When some think about wealth transfer, they think God will automatically transfer the wealth of unbelievers to them. That's not true.

What God will do for you is to give you an idea and get you to work on it. If you need to acquire a relevant skill that will make you solve problems in exchange for money, it is not too late.

Start solving problems for prople. *Money is what you get in exchange for creating solution*.

*PRAYER*: Father, I receive the grace to become a problem solver  In Jesus name.

*A MIND THAT KNOWS NO  IMPOSSIBILITY*

Mark 11:23 *I tell you the truth, you can say to this mountain, ‘May you be lifted up and thrown into the sea,’ and it will happen. But you must really believe it will happen and have no doubt in your heart* (NLT).

One morning during our family devotion, my second son mentioned his prayer request. It is our practice to allow our children tell us their prayer requests.

That very morning, our second son raised a prayer point that made me and my wife look at each other in unbelief. You know how you look at someone after hearing what sounds impossible.

His request seemed so impossible to us but he was serious and focused in the way he asked God to do it. To be candid, If we had hope of getting money or support from anywhere, we could have believed easily.

In fact, he prayed and asked God to grant that request before the end of that particular month. His mind knew no impossibility ours saw the many obstacles but we couldn't stop his prayers not to discourage him.

Leaving that prayer session, God began to point my attention to how faith works. The young boy didn't see the obstacles in his mind, he didn't know the cost price of commodities in the market.

While myself and wife believed God can do all things but the many obstacles, state of economy and our financial condition put  great doubt in our hearts.

*Faith only works for a man whose mind does not accept or agree with impossibility*.

*True faith is daring the impossible despite obstacles. If you really want your faith to work, your focus should be on what the power of God can do not the limitations of man.*

Our faith should be rooted in God's word not in the happenings around us. As far as God is concerned, impossibility is nothing: *"And looking at them Jesus said to them, “With people this is impossible, but with God all things are possible*” ( Mt 19:26).

Find a scripture that promises what you desire. Believe it, keep your focus on it and not pn the circumstances and the impossibilities. Never stop confessing that scripture until you see the desired result.

*CONFESSION*: My faith is working for me, it is not dormant or useless. My faith rest in the power of God and His word not on man's limitations. I refuse to live in the reality of man's limitations. The reality of redemption becomes my reality in Jesus name.

*CLOSE THE DOOR AGAINST JEZEBEL*

Revelation 2:22 *“Therefore, I will throw her on a bed of suffering, and those who commit adultery with her will suffer greatly unless they repent and turn away from her evil deeds* ( NLT).

I was privileged to minister deliverance to a young lady, a teenager. She attended a program with her guardian and she began to manifest for days after the programme.

When I met her, she was acting funny as demon oppressed people do when under the power. Her guardian told me what happened. As I prayed for her in the name of Jesus Christ, she manifested more.

Often when I cast out demons, I don't bother asking for names and details because it is not a pattern to follow. Demons can lie when you ask them questions( Jn 8:44)  but on this occasion, I asked questions because I perceived I was dealing with a very strange spirit.

When I asked the demon what the name was, she replied saying "spirit of Jezebel". After, I asked what her mission was, she said 'to destroy her'. In all my years of ministry, praying for people, I don't engage demons but I believe God led me to ask questions on this occasion. At least, Jesus did when he ministered to the mad man at Gadara ( Mark 5).

The last straw that broke the camel's back was when I asked how she got into her. Guess what she said? The answer I got was simple, "through sex". That shook me. My mind raced far. I thought aboout many who are deeply engaged in sexual immorality. They have become a home for this demon.

Do you know Jezebel in the bible? Read about her in first Kings 18. Jezebel, the wife of Ahab was associated with idolatry, murder and sexual immorality.

In the new testament, she was also symbolic of high level sexual promiscuity and demonic operations. God had to warn the church in Thyatira against the influence of Jezebel.

*“But I have this complaint against you. You are permitting that woman—that Jezebel who calls herself a prophet—to lead my servants astray. She teaches them to commit sexual sin and to eat food offered to idols* ( Revelation 2:20 NLT). If you read further, God threatened to kill her children( Vs 23).

*If you're sexually immoral, you are hurting yourself greatly. There is a mystery about what happens during illicit sex that we may not know fully because it involves exchange of demon spirits*.

Some will say "but I used condom". Condom is just latex rubber, demons can pass through that thin rubber. If they can pass through thick walls, how much more a thin rubber.

*Through illicit sex, so many persons have become a home for all kinds of demons not only Jezebel spirit. If Satan want to deal with a man or woman quickly, illicit sex is his potent tool. That's why powerful men are rendered powerless quickly on the bed of immorality*.

*If Satan send a demon on assignment, it is very easy for that demon to operate unhindered in the life of a sexually immoral person because illicit sex is a door opener and virtue drainer*.

*Some persons are struggling through life and experiencing problems upon problems because because through illicit sex they opened the door for demons to torment and draw their lives backward*.

I know a married man who was flourishing and doing fine. He was not so wealthy but was very comfortable. He became sexually active with a young lady. His life turned worse not long after. His reversal of fortune was shocking.

The young lady confessed to a senior minister I know that any man who sleeps with her can never breakthrough in life. Immediately, I knew where the man's problems came from. Till date, he's still struggling and running in circles.

If you have opened the door for Jezebel through sexual immorality, please close it. First, you need genuine repentance. You must have a change of heart about illicit sex accepting that it is a sin and not fun. It is capable of sending of sending one to hell( Rev 21:8).

*Renew your mind with God's word daily( Romans 12:2). The greatest sexual organ is the mind*. If you can renew your mind with God's word daily, you break the hold and strength of sexual immorality in your life.

You need to break every ungodly relationship and distance yourself from whatever trigger sexual immorality. Porn, x-rated videos, sensual music, alcohol and all other things that can trigger sexual sin.l

Lastly, drive out demons that has entered your life through sexual immorality. No one engages in illicit sex and remains thesame thereafter. Knowing this, you should be intentional and deliberate about sending them out of your life.


*PRAYER*: Father, I receive total deliverance from the spirit of lust and sexual immorality in Jesus name. Let your holy fire purify my heart in Jesus name. I receive the grace to close the door against Jezebel spirit and I send packing demons that entered my life through sexual immorality in Jesus name.

May 16th, 2023.

*A CONVERSATION WITH GOD*

Eccl3:11 Yet God has made everything beautiful for its own time...

A man walked to the top of a hill to talk to God. He had a conversation with him. The man asked, “God, what’s a million years to you?” and God said, “A minute.”

Then the man asked, “Well, what’s a million dollars to you?” and God said, “A penny.”Then the man asked, “God…..can I have a penny?” and God said, “Sure…..in a minute.”

It means the man would have to wait a million years literally.

If you have not waited for anything before, you may not understand the subject of God's timing.

Waiting on God for anything is not so easy to the flesh because God's timing and table is different from ours.

Oftentimes, some of the things we call delay aren't delays as far as God is concerned because he determines the time not us.

You can only use that term 'delay' when the scheduled timing for a thing has passed. If you don't know the scheduled timing for the fulfilment of a promise, you cannot say it is delayed.

There's a popular mantra that says: God's time is the best. Why does God put us on the waiting line? Why is it that sometimes when it seems as if God has answered our prayers we still have to wait for months or years before we see the answer?

*The only answer I can give is that God is using our waiting time to process us. God doesn't waste our time*.

*Time is a tool in the hands of God to process us and make us who He want us to be*. I'm yet to see a good product that was not properly processed, good things take time.

*All those great promises from God will not happen except you pass through the preparation furnace of time*.

*It is not the promises God is processing but you. You are the one that needs proper divine processing to feature in the promise of God.*

*Character to handle greatness is what God is building in us with time*. God may not give you 'big' things except you are processed to handle 'big' things.

*True and lasting greatness takes time.* God want us to grow through the process and mature  so that we can handle things rightly.

Your waiting time is not a wasted time. Start learning the lessons and submit yourself to God's processing course.

*PRAYER*: Father, I receive the grace to be processed in your waiting room.

DEVOTIONAL 0861 | Source start line: 85294

5/17/23, 6:21 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: ProjectSave Devotional
Wednesday
May 17th 2023.

*NOT CONNECTED*

1 Corinthians 1:9 God is faithful, who has called you into fellowship with his Son, Jesus Christ our Lord.


Sometime ago, I was trying to do something on my phone. I had the intention of charging my phone alongside.

After using my phone for a while, I discovered it was not connected to the charger. All the while, I thought I was charging it. My assumption was wrong.

No matter how strong the battery of a phone is, the power of its battery life reduces with frequency of use. The only way to restore it is to charge it by connecting it to the power source.

*Just as my phone was not connected for charging, the spiritual batteries of many believers are  used up without proper recharging because they are not connected to the power source daily*.

They assume there is restrengthening but looking closely, there is no connection with the one that can re strengthen them.

It is very unfortunate that some ministers and believers do not have a private devotional life where they can draw strength.

They may preach a storm and do ministry but they suffer burn-out quickly because they are not connected to the one who can strengthen them.

Jesus taught us the power of retreat. In Luke 5:16, the bible said, *"But Jesus OFTEN withdrew to lonely places and prayed*". Very profound.

The son of God didn't take chances on that. He wasn't just jumping from pulpits to pulpits neither was he trying to be popular in town with his face on every poster.

He understood the power of staying connected to the power source. Little wonder why his preachings and teachings amazed the people.


*Some ministers have become career preachers not spiritual or anointed preachers. In these days of artificial intelligence, they can easily prepare a message and deliver using their oratory skills but devoid of life*.

One may be deceived into thinking that  having preaching engagements weekly is a sign of spiritual strength.

Yes, it could be a sign that God is doing something in your life but it should never be mistaken for quality of walk with God and spiritual strength.

I saw something written about Peter in Acts 10:8, *"...Peter went up upon the housetop to pray about the sixth hour:*". You see, this apostle was part of the very first outpouring of the Spirit upon the church yet he withdrew to the housetop to pray.

He didn't rely on his initial experience. He was hungry for more. His life wasn't about how many invitations he got but the quality of fellowship he had with God

Something tells me that was his devotional culture. He had a time and place to refuel and stay connected.

*We expend so much virtue when we minister and do ministry. That's why our spiritual batteries require constant charging. You do this by connecting with God in the place of fellowship*.

*As a minister, it is dangerous to study the bible only because you have a message to preach. Studying the bible should be for the benefit of your walk with God first before others*.

To the believer, attending prayer meetings online and offline is not tantamount to having a prayer life. That's not the true definition of prayer life.

*What we call "prayer life" is that constant and persistent prayer fellowship between you and God in your private closet all alone with God*.

To avoid suffering a burn-out and emptiness after doing ministry here and there, go on personal retreats and ensure you maintain a devotional culture of prayer and the word. That's where we draw strength from.


*PRAYER*: I receive grace to stay connected in the place of fellowship in Jesus name. Amen

5/18/23, 6:37 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: PROJECTSAVE DEVOTIONAL

*"MISTHOS"*


1Cor 3:8 *The one who plants and the one who waters work together with the same purpose. And both will be rewarded for their own hard work*( NLT).


The Greek "misthos" is translated as reward. Virtually everyone in the faith has some expectation of a reward from God.


However, there's a misconception on how God reward His people. It has made some persons to pursue the wrong things and ignore the things that are germane.



Further, he said that God will reward each man based on their work ( assignment, purpose). There's something I want you us to examine there.


*God doesn't reward just any work. His reward is for the work he has assigned to us not for doing just anything*.


Paul, a planter knew his reward would be based on the effectiveness of his planting work, he didn't cross from his lane into the watering ministry of Apollos.


Some persons ignore the "little" assignment God gave them because it is unpopular. They see the beauty in the assignment of others because of how it is presented in the media but  failed to check the eternal impact.


Some see themselves as disadvantaged because of their location and have been thinking of relocating to a city where people will see their works or to intensify on media hype of their exploits.


Another class of people abandoned their real assignment for what they perceive will put money in their pocket yet they may claim to be working for God.

I almost fell for this early in ministry. I almost abandoned field ministry to go start a church when God hasn't instructed because I felt having a standing congregation will better my lot.


*Dearest, what God reward is faithfulness, dedication and commitment to the very assignment he has committed into our hands even if it seems little( though there are no little assignments) or unpopular*.


Your duty is to seek God's face to know His mind on the exact thing He want you to do. I love Paul the apostle. He knew the assignment that will earn him his reward and he was faithful to it.

In 1Cor 1:17, he said, *"FOR CHRIST DIDN'T SEND ME TO BAPTIZE, BUT TO PREACH the Good News—and not with clever speech, for fear that the cross of Christ would lose its power*". Wow! That's profound.


Is it bad to baptize people? Of course, it is not bad yet Paul said God didn't send him to baptize people. He knew his reward is attached to his assignment of preaching and establishing a local body of believers where none existed.

*Your reward is attached to your purpose in life, discover and fulfill it*.


*PRAYER*: Father, I receive the grace to find my assignment, fulfill it faithfully till you come for me in Jesus name. Amen

*THERE IS POWER IN THE NAME OF JESUS*

Philippians 2:9 *Wherefore God also hath highly exalted him, and given him a name which is above every name*.

In one of the French countries where we had an outreach, we had healing and deliverance line. I love to pray for people by laying on of hands.

In this particular program, as people stepped forward, my interpreter asked what their challenge was. He interpreted to me whatever was said by the people either in French or their local language.

An adult lady steppped forward to me. She couldn't speak English but my interpreter asked her what her problem was. She told him that she was a bedwetter and she had other spiritual problems.

As I laid my hands on her calling the name of Jesus, her body shook. The ushers and those around held her. That was not a surprise to me because i've seen that happen severally.

What surprised me was how demons  oppressing a lady who didn't understand what I said in English bowed to the Lorship of Jesus at the mention of His name. She may not understand English but the demons in her recognized that name.

This lady couldn't communicate in English but my command in the name of Jesus spoken in English language sent the demons out.

*Anywhere on earth the name of Jesus is mentioned in any language, demons will tremble. The name has universal application and recognition. Demons know this truth*.

God exalted that name above all names. The name of Jesus is empowered so that all knees in heaven, on earth and umderneath the earth must bow.

*Wherefore God also hath highly exalted him, and given him a name which is above every name: that at the name of Jesus every knee should bow, of things in heaven, and things in earth, and things under the earth; and that every tongue should confess that Jesus Christ is Lord, to the glory of God the Father* ( Philippians 2:9-11 KJV)

The only name Satan and demons recognize is the name of Jesus Christ. Hell recognize that name too well not to obey. When confronted with life's challenges, your bailout is the name of Jesus Christ.

Some traditionalists prefer to call the name of ancestors in times of trouble while some pseudo Christian cults pray in the name of angels. The name of any angel wasn't empowered to save, heal and deliver not even Michael.

Angels are servants not saviour: *"But to which of the angels said he at any time, Sit on my right hand, Until I make thine enemies thy footstool? Are they not all ministering spirits, sent forth to minister for them who shall be heirs of salvation?*"(Hebrews 1:13-14 KJV).

Having fought and won the battle for our redemption, the name is empowered to save mankind from sin, sickness and Satan.

*Neither is there salvation in any other: for there is none other name under heaven given among men, whereby we must be saved* ( Acts 4:12).

Take advantage of the power in the name of Jesus Christ. Satan and his cohorts will run.

*PRAYER*: In the name of Jesus Christ(...insert your request here).

*WRONG MARITAL COUNSELING*

Jer7:24 *But they did not obey or incline their ear, but walked in their own counsels and the stubbornness of their evil hearts, and went backward and not forward* ( ESV).

Sometime ago, a man in church called me aside to counsel me on how to handle my wife. I can't remember If it was before my wedding or immediately after.

He told me never to open-up on my finances to her and ensure that I keep secrets from her. He told me how he had been dealing with his own wife in that regard.

He spoke fluently about it to an extent that we stood for several minutes without knowing time had gone.

At the end of the conversation, I can't remember saying anything but within me, I knew that his advice were counter-peace for marriage so I didn't take it. That counsel died right there.

I don't have regrets that my wife knows everything about my finances. That has helped us to plan and manage money prudently. She won't tell me to buy what we can't afford because she knows what we have.

*Beware of bad advisers. Those who will tell you to turn the heat on in your home but aren't doing same in theirs. Those who counsel you break your home but are keeping theirs. Such are bad counselors*.

I read about a woman that attended a meeting where she was told that women aren't cooks so they should stop cooking for their husbands.

She got home and told her loving husband she won't cook again. After sometime, the man sent her packing. She lost her home to bad counsel but her advisers may have theirs intact.

*It is not every counsel you take or listen to especially as regards marriage*. What works for Mr 'A' may not work for Mr 'B'. You should fashion out what works for you and stick to it as long as it is biblical.

When you need counsel, ask the Spirit of God first and dig out wisdom from the scriptures. If God want you to ask someone, He will direct you to someone that is spiritually sound who will tell you His mind.

*Not all counsel are for taking, run from bad advisers*.

*PRAYER*: Father, I receive wisdom In Jesus name. Amen

*MISDIRECTED ZEAL IS DANGEROUS*

Rom10: 2- *I know what enthusiasm they have for God, but it is misdirected zeal*.

Heard the story of a man that went to the zoo asking to be allowed into the cage where lions were kept. He wanted to replicate Daniel's story by coming out of the lion's den unhurt.

He was granted access into the cage of lions. Without much ado, the lions feasted on him.  He disgraced himself not God. Eventually, he lost his life from the injuries sustained.

I have heard and seen Christians that died foolishly out of zeal without accurate knowledge.

I was part of a prayer team years back. We went for a vigil and we met one of the brothers writhing in pain on the floor. He had hernia. Instead of taking him to the hospital, we prayed fervently.

The more prayed, the more he cried because he was in severe pains. Eventually, we lost him. None among us took the initiative of taking him to the hospital. We lost him due to misdirected zeal.

*knowledge brings balance to zeal. Zeal without knowledge can throw one off balance all in the guise of spirituality*.

Isaiah 33:6 says, *"And wisdom and knowledge shall be the stability of thy times, and strength of salvation: the fear of the LORD is his treasure*".

Zeal without knowledge is common to babes or new converts that is why every young believer needs a discipler to disciple him into maturity in the things of the Spirit.

*Therefore my people are gone into captivity, because they have no knowledge: and their honourable men are famished, and their multitude dried up with thirst* (Isaiah 5:13 KJV).

Sometimes, out of zeal, believers die of sickness because they heard their pastor say he hasn't used a drug for decades.

Some will try to do same forgetting the fact that the man is living in a clean environment, eating good food with supplements and, resting well.

*Applied knowledge is key to living a balanced Christian life*.

No matter how prayerful or pious you are, nothing can take the place of knowledge.

*ACTION*:  Zeal is good but knowledge is better. Dig deep into God's word to know his mind. Go for accurate knowledge and live by your convictions.

*THE SOURCE IS IMPORTANT- CHECK*

*EVERYONE IS DOING IT- THE ENEMY'S DECEPTION*

John8:44 *For you are the children of your father the devil, and you love to do the evil things he does...When he lies, it is consistent with his character; for he is a liar and the father of lies*( NLT).

The strength of the enemy lies in his ability to deceive people with convincing proofs. He makes people believe a lie.

*Right believing produces right living while wrong believing produces wrong living.* The latter is the goal of the enemy- to make you believe a lie so you can live a lie.

One of the lies Satan tell those that are sexually immoral is "everyone is doing it". People console themselves with that mentality.

Hey! Not everyone is doing it. I have met people who believe a man must have at least one vice.

It is either he's a womanizer, smoker, drunk or a gambler. That assertion doesn't make people embrace true freedom from sin.

Even If everyone is doing it, the bible said that we should not follow multitudes to do evil: *“You must not follow the crowd in doing wrong. When you are called to testify in a dispute, do not be swayed by the crowd to twist justice*"( Exodus 23:2 NLT).

*The multitudes may not be right always.* The path of righteousness has only few people walking on it.

*But the gateway to life is very narrow and the road is difficult, and only a few ever find it* ( Mt 7:14). The path of righteousness has never been popular. Only few walk in it.

*The easiest way to perish with the world is to join the majority*. The word of God should be your guide under all circumstances.

Some married men believe in what they call 'side chick'. The believe is that you cannot enjoy one woman all your life so there must be an assistant 'wife'. That's crass error.

Most times, you will notice that when you're tempted, the first picture the devil brings to you are those that are doing it and going scot-free.

Truly, when we look at sexual sin, it seems as if it is the norm today. There are believers that are cohabiting with their spouse-to-be.

That action alone sends the wrong signal to those that are trying to break free from sexual sin.

*Whatever God hates is what you should hate even if it has become a norm in the society*.

When Satan comes telling you "Everyone is doing it", simply tell him God has remnant few somewhere reserved and preserved in true holiness and righteousness and that's where you belong.

*PRAYER*: Father, I receive the grace to stand for the truth even If it is not common or popular In Jesus name.

May 24th 2023.

*DON'T BE IN THE MIX*

Romans12:2 *Don’t copy the behavior and customs of this world, but let God transform you into a new person by changing the way you think. Then you will learn to know God’s will for you, which is good and pleasing and perfect* (NLT).

The idiomatic expression  "If you can't beat them join them" means that, if someone is too strong for you to defeat, it is better to be on the same side as them.

In our day-to-day conversation and interaction with the outside world, this statement has come to connote "compromise".

It is suggestive of aligning with  unbiblical norms and practices in our society so as to have an edge.

For example, a student that  failed several times may join the bandwagon of those involved in examination malpractice just to escape the shame of yearly failure.

In some offices, some cut corners to make extra  cash. If you find yourself in that environment, you may discover that your colleagues at work are living fine while you go broke before month end.

The devil may  tempt you to join them if you can't beat them.The conformity to  the sinful standard in the world is anti bible regardless of the circumstance or situation in which we find ourselves.

Right from the old covenant, God warned against unholy alliance and joining multitude to do evil: *"Thou shalt not follow a multitude to do evil;...*", Ex.23:2.

*A practice may be popular and en vogue but may not be of God. Whenever we are in the crowd for too long, an irresistible  influence is exerted upon us*.

We should never look at what people are doing as our standard for daily living. Our calling is to be separated unto the Lord: *"Therefore, "Come out from them and be separate, says the Lord. Touch no unclean thing, and I will receive you*" (2Cor 6:17).

Every day, Satan is inventing new ways of getting us in the mix. He'll present compromise to us as harmless with a sense of "it doesn't matter, so many people are doing it".

Dearest, anytime the devil tells you that a thing doesn't matter, just know that it matters much.

.Majority may be doing it but that doesn't make it right. Don't join them.

They may be lying about their age to secure a job, inflate contracts and demand kick backs as a means of survival in this harsh economy. Don't join them.

The way to eternal life has always been narrow with only few persons walking in it: *"Because strait is the gate, and narrow is the way, which leadeth unto life, and few there be that find it*", (Matthew7:14).

Daily, the road is getting more narrow because only the few who have refused to" join them" remains on the path.

Today, God is telling you to separate from them if you can't beat them. Don't be found in the mix of compromise.

*PRAYER*: Father, I receive the grace to separate from them instead of joining them in Jesus name. Amen.

*HEARING GOD THROUGH UNUSUAL VESSELS*


Psalms 32:8 *I will instruct thee and teach thee in the way which thou shalt go: I will guide thee with mine eye*.


I heard the story of a man God called into the ministry but did not yield. You know how it is for some persons who have great reluctance about saying "Yes" to God.

This man delayed his obedience to God's call. One day, he alighted from the bus and a mad man walked towards him telling him to obey God's call. This happened in the public and those around saw it.


The man himself knew it was God speaking to him through the mad man because he had heard God on the same issue several times. God speaks through unusual vessels.

It will surprise you to know that I began to write daily devotionals after an encounter with a carnal believer. He drinks and womanizes yet God spoke through him to me.


Sometimes, God confirms to us what He's been telling us to do through unusual vessels. People we least expect can be God's mouth piece.


Sometimes, we may be praying for divine direction on certain things, we may have fasted and done all the spiritual exercises needed, yet, He didn't speak to us the way we expected.


*God may speak to us through a child or through someone we least expect. What matters is that we are  spiritually alert&sensitive to God's Spirit at all times so that whenever He's talking to us, we'll pick  it regardless of the vessel He uses*.


To hear God speak to us even if He chooses to speak to us through a mad man we need to position ourselves like John the beloved: *"I was in the Spirit on the Lord's day, and heard behind me a great voice, as of a trumpet*"(Revelation 1:10 KJV).


John heard those mysteries in the book of revelation because he was in the Spirit. *Often, we miss God  because we are in the flesh and have a preconceived notion about how we want Him to speak to us*.


I've been there several times. There were days I expected God to speak audibly or with a bold inscription on the wall. Unfortunately, God didn't show up that way.


Just like Elijah In 1 Kings19:11-13, when he sought to hear God, we were told that there was a wind, an earthquake and fire but God was not there. Finally, he heard God through the still small voice.


That divine direction you've been praying for may come through a little child, house help and from people you least expect*.

Just stay alert and be sensitive so that you can recognize God when He's speaking.


*PRAYER*: Father, I receive the grace to be sensitive at all times in Jesus name.

*TESTED AND CERTIFIED FIT*

1 Sam16:7 *But the LORD said to Samuel, "Do not consider his appearance or his height, for I have rejected him. The LORD does not look at the things people look at. People look at the outward appearance, but the LORD looks at the heart*( NIV).

In one of the Church Growth ministers conference I attended years back,  one of the officiating ministers gave a word of knowledge about one of the pastors in attendance.

As revealed to the minister by the Lord, he said there was a Pastor in that meeting who had 8 members all his years in ministry and was planning to quit because the church was not growing.

The minister of God that gave the word of knowledge said those years of stagnancy were his testing phase and God said that he has passed the test successfully. Wow!

That part of test and being successful caught my attention. That man thought God was looking away from his predicament not knowing God stood as an examiner watching how was taking his divine exams.

Another story that caught my attention in the bible was the story of David and his brothers. When God was shopping for a king,  he had a list containing only one successful candidate.

When the elder brothers of David came before Samuel, God told him that he had rejected them. This is my question, "Why did God reject them"? What criteria did God use in selection?

I perceive the brothers of David failed the qualifying exams that would have made them the choice candidate for the throne little wonder why God told Samuel they were rejected.

If someone hadn't failed a test, you can't just reject by mere looks. Obviously, there was a test they failed at one time or other. Unknowingly, they were tested but not certified fit.

They were living their lives normally not knowing everyday was an examination day. They heard their result on a day they least expect.

*Brethren, you are in God's examination hall without knowing. God is looking at your attitude daily to see if you qualify for the throne. God will announce your result very soon*.

Two Christians may not have the same result in God's score sheet. Don't be jealous of men who are climbing levels of glory. You don't know the test they wrote and their scores.

We have many praying men that are not breaking forth. They have prayed on mountains and in valleys, yet no result to show for it. They may wonder If God doesn't hear their cry.

*Some failed the test of God in simple matters like money, power, people management, etc thinking prayer will substitute for their failures. It doesn't work like that in God's school*.

*Life is a school, we are all in the classroom, there won't be promotion until we pass our exams. Be circumspect in the way you live,  you maybe writing exams unknowingly*.

*PRAYER*: Father,  I will not fail divine exams In Jesus name.

*DELIVERED FROM THE WICKED*

Psalms 71:4 My God, rescue me from the power of the wicked, from the clutches of cruel oppressors (NLT).

Though the medics came  to the rescue but it was God's miraculous power that delivered us. Imagine the attention we would have gotten from the press had we all died.

This lady is innocent in looks but she's wickedness personified. Why did she poison us? What was our offence? Why did she want to wipe everyone out? What will she gain by taking our lives?

I saw something in that scripture. Wicked men are unreasonable. Satan has filled the heart of some to do only evil.

What's God's promise to you? In Psalms 125:3, the bible says, *“For the rod of the wicked shall not rest upon the lot of the righteous; lest the righteous put forth their hands unto iniquity*”.

However, the righteous who doesn't know and appropriate his right and privileges in the word will be a victim of the wicked. I have seen this happen to God's people severally.

If you have been under the siege of the wicked, the Lord shall deliver you If you cry to him for deliverance.

*PRAYER*: Father, deliver me from wicked men and their wickedness in Jesus name.

*WHO SENT YOU*?

2Samuel18: 22 *But Ahimaaz continued to plead with Joab, “Whatever happens, please let me go, too.“Why should you go, my son?” Joab replied. “There will be no reward for your news.”...So Ahimaaz took the less demanding route by way of the plain and ran to Mahanaim ahead of the Ethiopian* (NLT).

There was an old testament character, Ahimaaz by name. He was the son of Zadok.

When Joab needed someone to deliver the news of Absalom's death to David, Ahimaaz volunteered to go but Joab restrained him because he won't deliver the message to David properly. Instead, he sent an Ethiopian.

When David asked him about Absalom his son, his response  didn't make much sense so he was told to stay aside. When the Ethiopian was asked the same question, his wise response hit the nail on the head and communicated the sad event to the heart of David.

Ahimaaz was not sent, he didn't have the message for that hour. He may have delivered well at other times but that very assignment wasn't meant for him. Little wonder why his response didn't fulfill the intended purpose.

If I may ask, who sent you? That someone else is doing a program and pulling crowd doesn't mean you should go and do same.

*That you have the charisma that can hold people's attention is not tantamount to pioneering a church or ministry*.

Some persons became emergency ministry setmen because they can't submit to leadership. Having caused problems for their leaders, they break out and start "their own" ministry. So wrong!

*Whatever God hasn't sent you won't make much sense to your listeners*. I see a generation of preachers, teachers, ministers and believers trying so hard to deliver a message God hasn't sent them.

I listened to a preacher who preached the message I heard from another preacher. The original preacher of that message preached with so great anointing to the extent that music stores at bus stops kept playing that particular message severally.

This other preacher preached the same message without inspiration and anointing. He copied another man's message without having the grace and anointing that backed it.

*One of the deceits of the enemy for those in ministry is to make you feel you can do what the person next door is doing without checking with God to know if it is His will.*

Ministers of the Gospel often fall into this trap. I once considered starting a church in the heart of Lagos years back because I saw many young men of my age doing so not until God stopped me.

*There are many echoes today but only few voices. I have decided to be a voice not an echo*.

*It is not about social media popularity or numerous posts on social media but by divine originality*.

Some don't copy messages alone but even mannerisms without having the anointing that backed those mannerisms.

Today, we have so many 'thus saith the Lord' when God hasn't spoken? They are trying to deliver a message God didn't send them thereby attracting a "woe" into their lives.

Ezekiel 13:3 says, *"This is what the Sovereign LORD says: Woe to the foolish prophets who follow their own spirit and have seen nothing!*".

I see you're trying hard to leave a 'spiritual impression' in the hearts of men when God hasn't sent you.

*God will only maintain what he ordain*.

Are you sent?

*PRAYER*: Father, I receive the grace to stay where you put me and to run the errand you've sent me In Jesus name. Amen

*CHARACTER THAT SUSTAINS POWER*

Prov16:32 *Better to be patient than powerful; better to have self-control than to conquer a city*.

Several years ago, I was power hungry to the extent that I would pray for several days alone in the room without food. That hunger is just unexplainable.

I read about several powerful preachers from God's Generals and other books. I asked God to give me a combination of their anointings. I wanted ancient mantles to fall upon me.

Those days, I mentioned the names of those preachers whose mantles I desired. Unfortunately,  I never desired to be like Christ but men made by Christ.  Our generation need to be warned against this.

Now I know why God didn't give me all the anointing of about 5-6 of them together. If He had, I would have killed many people with the power. Anger would have made me turn some persons to imbecile and mad whereas, God is love.

Jesus saved lives with the anointing and walked in love with his detractors. He controlled himself and represented God to the people. That is good use of power but I wasn't mature to handle what I was asking for.

Desiring power is not bad, I still do just that I have a better understanding now than in previous years when I wanted the power to gratify self.

We all need the power of God but there are two things I've found to be better than power, they are patience and self-control. These two things forms the very core of our Christian character.

Over the years, I have seen very anointed men of God who lacked these two virtues fall by the way side. Samson lacked self-control. His appetite for anything in skirt was his undoing.

For those that are power hungry, impatience can make them to seek shortcuts or ungodly alternatives when power is not flowing. That's why some preachers go diabolical.

On the other hand, lack of self-control can ruin the testimony powerful men. Once a man cannot control his sexual appetite, quest for fame and money, he is on his way down. It may take time but it will surely happen. Ask Samson.

*God can easily give his power. It is one of the things God can share with mankind but the purity of the vessel carrying the power and the character to sustain it is of paramount importance to God*.

When praying for power, just know in your heart that some virtues sustain power without which power will leak and rubbish the reputation of the carrier.

*PRAYER*: Father, I receive the grace to develop the virtues of patience and self-control In Jesus name.

*TELL HIM TO GET OUT*

Luke 10:19 *Behold, I give you the authority to trample on serpents and scorpions, and over all the power of the enemy, and nothing shall by any means hurt you*. NKJV

During one of our missionary outreaches in a French speaking West African country, we had a healing and deliverance line.

A lady in her twenties( I suppose) was on the healing and deliverance line. She told my interpreter that she bedwets among other problems she had.

I knew her problem was demonic. As I laid my hands on her, she began to manifest. We ended the program that night and left for the apartment where we lodged.

In the midnight, I had an attack experience. I began to urinate on my body from sleep so I jumped out of bed and rushed to the toilet to finish up there.

After urinating in the toilet, I remembered I prayed for a bedwetting lady the previous night so I knew what happened to me was a demonic attack.

From the toilet, I looked towards my bed and I sensed there was a  strange presence in my room. Immediately, I issued a decree in the name of Jesus and told Satan and his demons to get out of my room.

Truly, I sensed their exit and returned to bed. Had a sound sleep that night onwards till I left that country. The programmes we had were powerful with great testimonies.

Do not allow Satan mess with you. *One unchanging character of Satan and demons is to make a mess of peoples lives*. They want to put their dirty dozen on you, your children, your job, ministry and family.

You must resist him at all times: *"But resist him, be firm in your faith [against his attack—rooted, established, immovable], knowing that the same experiences of suffering are being experienced by your brothers and sisters throughout the world. [You do not suffer alone.]*"( 1Pet 5:9).

In the Greek, the tense in which the word "resist" is rendered is in the present indicative. That is a continous kind of action. Satan is stubborn and we must stubbornly resist him.

Do not fear him neither should you fear his minions. They are defeated already. On the cross, Christ made a mess of Satan and his cohorts defeating them eternally.

Colosssians 2:15 says, *"and having spoiled principalities and powers, he made a shew of them openly, triumphing over them in it*"(Colossians 2:15 KJV).

Satan can only deal successfully with those who do not know their rights and privileges in Christ. So many believers do not know their rights so they get threatened and fearful whenever Satan comes up with his tricks.

About every believer, the bible says, *"and hath raised us up together, and made us sit together in heavenly places in Christ Jesus:*"(Ephesians 2:6 KJV).

Halleluyah!!! We are seated with Christ in the place of power and authority. This is where you are whether you feel like it or not. It is a position higher than where Satan and demons dwell.

I want you to look at the dirty dozens of the enemy in your life, that sickness, demonic attack, backwardness and those things that do not represent redemption. Issue a decree telling him to get out of your life with his belongings.

*CONFESSION*: Satan, get out of my life and take(... Insert name of challenges) along with you as you go in Jesus name. I belong to Jesus. I am more than a conqueror.

*"E DON RED"- HANDLING STORMY SITUATIONS*

Mark4: 38 *Jesus was in the stern, sleeping on a cushion. The disciples woke him and said to him, “Teacher, don’t you care if we drown?” 39 He got up, rebuked the wind and said to the waves, “Quiet! Be still!” Then the wind died down and it was completely calm*.

Several years ago, I had an unforgettable experience traveling on the sea when I decided to visit some brethren on the Island.

Our journey started on the lagoon and we sailed on the Atlantic ocean for about 30 minutes before getting to our destination.

When we got on the Atlantic, we had a funny experience that brought fear into the hearts of everyone in the fibre boat.

The turbulence was great and the boat driver said in pidgin: "e don red" meaning things have become bad. We were told that the waves could break our boat apart leading to accident.

When I heard that, my faith flew into the sea. After a while, I began to pray in tongues and the situation became better.

It is easy to preach about the faithlessness of the disciples when they were in the storm but you never can tell what your reaction would be if you were in their shoes.

Storms represent life's challenges. Sometimes, we find ourselves in stormy situations that challenge our faith. At that moment, it may look as If our world is crashing with no one to help.

The intensity of the storm may be so much to the extent of forgetting  all faith lessons we have learnt. This is one thing you should never forget: *"Jesus is right there with you in the storm*".

The assurance of His presence is vital in stilling the storms of life.  The disciples didn't fail to recognize the presence of Jesus in the storm. They called on him to still the storm and He did.

Dearest, the master is right there with you in the storm of life. The devil will try to make you feel forgotten, deserted and guilty, know that Jesus is right there with you and he's ready to still the raging storm If you call on him.

*PRAYER*: Jesus, I thank you because I know you're with me in the storm of life. I rebuke the storm of adversity(..specify) in Jesus name.

*LIVING IN PLENTY DURING SCARCITY* Pt.1


Isaiah1:19 *If you will only obey me, you will have plenty to eat* ( NLT)


*OBEDIENCE TO INSTRUCTIONS*


The word of God came alive more to me during COVID-19 lockdown.

Severally, I have shared the story on how God provided for me and my family during that period. I entered the lockdown with less than N20,000 in my account.

From the little I had, the Spirit of God prompted me to give  people from that little I had on me. As instructed, I wrote an e-book, sold it and used the proceeds to give missionaries and the needy palliatives.

I obeyed. We realized almost a million naira yet I didn't take from it for personal use though we had pressing needs in the family especially feeding.

Miraculously, God sent adequate supplies all through that period and even beyond the lockdown. It was a miracle.

In my few years of walking with God, I discovered that everything has its own place and uses. *As good as prayer and fasting is, it cannot substitute for obedience to simple instructions*.

Many rush to programmes where they promise prosperity and cash flow after but not many experience those things.

Some have sown seeds so that the rain of abundance can fall on them yet nothing of such happened in their lives.

God work with principles, most of the things we desire are tied to obeying simple instructions, that's all.

*The secret of plenty is to live in constant obedience to divine instructions*.

Today, God is saying He has seen your tears in prayer, your fasting is legendary, your kingdom service is second to none but just obey that instruction that looks foolish.

There are people that have disobeyed God's call into ministry because they are afraid of hunger.

Despite the fact that they are working, the fruit of their labour isn't showing. They still run into huge debts because there is a pending instruction yet to be obeyed.


The fear of the outcome of your obedience to God shouldn't stop you from taking the step that will launch you into God's will for your life.

When God told Abraham to leave his kindred, he didn't know where God was taking him to but as an act of faith, he obeyed.


The secret of plenty (even in recession) is obedience. We all need to learn from the widow of Zarephath, her obedience to divine instruction from God's servant made the difference for throughout the famine (1Kings17:7-16).


*ACTION*: What are the pending instructions yet to be obeyed?


*HAPPY NEW MONTH*!

6/2/23, 5:29 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: PROJECTSAVE DEVOTIONAL
Friday,
June 2nd, 2023.

*LIVING IN PLENTY DURING SCARCITY* Pt.2

Isaiah1:19 *If you will only obey me, you will have plenty to eat* ( NLT)

*LOCATION IS IMPORTANT*

The diamond acre story illustrates this very important point. Some of us have read or heard this story before. There was a farmer who toiled on his farm but couldn't breakthrough.

He heard about another land where people mine diamond and make fortune from sales of diamond. He decided to sell his farmland in other to raise money for his trip and relocation. Eventually he did.

The new owner of his farmland cultivated that land and found a gemstone but he didn't know exactly what it was. He showed it round and they confirmed to him it was a diamond.

Overnight, he became a multi millionaire because his field had diamond. He became a diamond merchant. The old owner lost an opportunity by changing his location. What he sought for in a strange land was on his farmland.

Genesis 26 tells a similar story about Isaac.
During famine, there was a mass exodus of people to Egypt. Using modern metrics, it is safe to say there was economic recession then. People relocated for greener pastures.

However, God knew the "plenty" of Isaac was tied to staying in Gerar so He was instructed to stay back and he obeyed. The bible says: *"When Isaac planted his crops that year, he harvested a hundred times more grain than he planted, for the Lord blessed him. He became a very rich man, and his wealth continued to grow. He acquired so many flocks of sheep and goats, herds of cattle, and servants that the Philistines became jealous of him* (Genesis 26:12‭-‬14 NLT).

*Every provision is tied to a specific location. When you miss your divine location, you'll surely miss divine allocation*.

*The wisdom you need in times of scarcity is to ask God to show you the location where your provisions are*. Very important. Your meal is not anywhere. Sometimes, it is in the least expected place where people do not want to stay.

During famine, God instructed Elijah to relocate to brook Cherith because his provision was there to be delivered by a raven(1Kings 17:3). That sounds weird. Who goes to the brooks to get food during famine?

However, Elijah's location for divine provision was at the brook Cherith. If he had stayed where he was, he may have died of hunger.

*Can you ask God to show you the land of your provision? In scarcity, it can work bothways. It is either God tells you to stay like Isaac or he tells you to move like Elijah. In fact, Elijah changed location twice at God's behest*.

*Relocation is not bad If the order is from God. You can relocate to your God-ordained location so as to get your provision. Staying back is also not bad If God instruct*.

*Relocating to a place where He didn't send you is an invitation to fruitless toil and slavery. Staying where He didn't ordain is an invitation to hunger*.

To find your "plenty" in times of scarcity, you can either be like Elijah or Isaac. Find out from God.

*ACTION POINT*: Prayerfully find your divine location.


====================================================================================================
DEVOTIONAL 0878 | Source start line: 86835
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
6/3/23, 7:34 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: PROJECTSAVE DEVOTIONAL
Saturday,
June 3rd, 2023.

*LIVING IN PLENTY DURING SCARCITY* Pt.3

Galatians 6:7 *Don’t be misled—you cannot mock the justice of God. You will always harvest what you plant* ( NLT).

*GIVE YOUR WAY INTO PLENTY*

My experience before and during COVID lockdown will remain fresh in my memory forever. It looked like a time of hopelessness but obedience to the covenant rescued me.

Prior to the lockdown, my wife and I drew a cheque in favour of a woman of God we wanted to bless. When it was rumoured that there'll be a lockdown, the cheque was cleared. Our account read red.

Fear gripped our hearts. The little we had left, we gave people from it and used proceeds of my e-book for palliatives. Practically, we fed whole families and some depended on us for their meal almost all through that period.

At the onset of lockdown, things were slow but after awhile, God sent supplies monthly. Sometimes, twice or thrice monthly, we received divine salaries paid into our account through those God sent to us. We didn't beg or ask them. They did it willingly.

I have never seen anything like that before in my life. At a time, my mum asked where I got money from when she heard what I did by giving palliatives. God surprised me beyond my imagination.

The issue of giving remains controversial among those who want to sound politically right. This is because of the several abuses and extremes taught and practiced by some preachers.

*Child of God, no matter what the world or critics say about this covenant practice, it is valid biblically. I have tested and tried it. It works as God designed it. I am proudly a giver*.

*Giving is one of the "foolishness" of God that makes for increase*. I love this "foolishness" because it produces result. Even though my motive for giving is love( which should be for everyone) yet God has never failed in sending the harvest.

I love this quote by Bro Azeez Ogunkoya: *"Sow like a fool and reap as the wise*". The bible confirms this.

*Give freely and become more wealthy; be stingy and lose everything. The generous will prosper; those who refresh others will themselves be refreshed* ( Proverbs 11:24-25 NLT).

This is an incontrovertible truth in scripture. There are numerous scriptures that give backing to this covenant practice. That some persons abuse it or go to extremes on it shouldn't discourage you from doing it rightly.

*As long as the earth remains, there will be planting and harvest, cold and heat, summer and winter, day and night*.”( Genesis 8:22 NLT).

The law of seed-time and harvest remains valid till the world ends. God's design is that we sow to harvest. In every harvest, there is a seed.

ACTION POINT: *Those who eat their seed with their harvest will never have any harvest.* Those who hoard God's blessings end up having nothing. Let giving be your lifestyle.

DEVOTIONAL 0879 | Source start line: 86885
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
6/4/23, 5:58 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: ProjectSave Devotional
Sunday,
June 4th, 2023.

*LIVING IN PLENTY DURING SCARCITY* Pt.4

Galatians 6:7 *Don’t be misled—you cannot mock the justice of God. You will always harvest what you plant* ( NLT).

*ANOTHER DIMENSION TO GIVING*

After brook Cherith dried, God instructed  Elijah to go meet a widow in Zarephath because He had directed her to feed him: *“Go at once to Zarephath in the region of Sidon and stay there. I have directed a widow there to supply you with food*”( 1 Kings 17:9).

When Elijah met her, she was gathering stick to cook her last meal for her son and herself. This was during a severe famine. The man of God demanded of her to cook for him first. In 1 Kings 17:15-16, the bible says, *"She went away and did as Elijah had told her...*".

What was the reward of her obedience? The bible says, *"...So there was food every day for Elijah and for the woman and her family. 16 For the jar of flour was not used up and the jug of oil did not run dry, in keeping with the word of the Lord spoken by Elijah*"(1 Kings 17:15-16).

I love this story though I rarely teach from it. If Elijah were to be a prophet in our time, he would have earned himself a bad press for making such demand from a widow yet he acted within the confines of God's instruction.

*Giving to genuine men of God is a door opener for a flow of plenty into your life even during scarcity.* Anytime I have the opportunity to give to genuine servants of God, I do it quickly.

If that widow had neglected Elijah's need, God will still provide for Elijah anyway but she may have died of hunger.  Why? God doesn't joke with the welfare of those He called into the ministry. The Psalmist(35:27) says, *"...Let the Lord be magnified, Who has pleasure in the prosperity of His servant*”.

If God delight in the prosperity of his servant, who are you to wish His servants poverty and penury? My little experience with some church folks shows they love to see God's servant poor and beggarly. The moment he's prospering, they assume he's no longer faithful in ministry.

*Satan is teaching this generation to be stingy towards genuine men of God thereby robbing God's people of their blessings*. I refuse to be in this camp. Even as a preacher, I give to those in ministry. I respect and honour genuine servants of God.

The worst deceit is to think your spiritual leader or that servant of God is not worth your giving because he has money or living a better life. *We don't give to a man of God because he is poor or in need, we do it because God commanded it in scriptures*.

*Those who are taught the word of God( you and me)  SHOULD PROVIDE FOR THEIR TEACHERS, sharing all good things with them* (Galatians 6:6 NLT. Emphasis mine).

*Don't short-change that preacher that is praying, counseling and teaching you God's word. Provide for his need. When he blesses you from his heart, something will be activated on your behalf*.

This is another Pauline instruction: *"Elders( referrring to those in five-fold ministry) who do their work well should be respected and paid well, especially those who work hard at both preaching and teaching*"(1 Timothy 5:17 NLT. Emphasis mine).

You cannot follow God's principles and be denied of what accrues from  your obedience. If you truly desire a supernatural turn around in your life and finances, do as instructed in today's devotional.

*ACTION POINT*: Make it your habit to give to genuine( note my emphasis on this word) servants of God. It pays.

6/5/23, 6:28 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: PROJECTSAVE DEVOTIONAL
Monday,
June 5th, 2023.

*YOU NEED TO DEVELOP A PRAYER CULTURE*

Daniel6:10 *But when Daniel learned that the law had been signed, he went home and knelt down as usual in his upstairs room, with its windows open toward Jerusalem. He prayed three times a day, just as he had always done, giving thanks to his God*( NLT)

There was an outstanding government official in the bible. His name is Daniel.  He was hated for being godly.

His colleagues in government decided to set him up. They requested the king not to allow anyone pray in the name of any god except to the king. Whoever goes contrary to the rule would be cast into the lion's den. The king consented to this.

When Daniel heard about it, the scripture has this to say: *"...he went home and knelt down AS USUAL in his upstairs room, with its windows open toward Jerusalem. HE PRAYED three times a day, just as he had always done, giving thanks to his God*" ( Daniel 6:10 NLT).



I want you to take note of the phrases and words in caps, "As usual", prayer was a usual thing in his life not something done occasionally. He prayed thrice everyday.

Daniel had a prayer culture that cannot be broken or disturbed by any rule. As a government official who had many responsibilities and duties to discharge, he had a prayer culture that nothing could change.

*Busy schedule is not an excuse for prayerlessness. You are not busy like Daniel was*.As a believer, you must develop a culture of prayer.

*Your prayer life is that private time spent in the place of prayer alone with God. This is not thesame as joining a prayer meeting online and offline*.

*Never substitute a prayer meeting/programme for your private prayer time alone with God. Mighty men in the kingdom were baked in the oven of private prayer*.

Some persons do not pray in private anymore. They equate prayer meetings( online&offline) to having a prayer life. Saying "Amen" to prayer is not prayer life. If those praying for you were raised that way, they wouldn't be where they are today.

*True prayer life is a private life in our closet alone with God*.

*Prayer is a life line for a Christian, don't joke with your prayer life. Nothing should stop you from praying not even your job. If you're too busy to pray, then you're unnecessarily busy.*

June 6th, 2023.

*SATAN'S TOOL- ACCUSATION&GUILT*

Isa43:25, *"I, even I, am he that blotteth out thy transgressions for mine own sake, and will not remember thy sins*".

Recently in a small group teaching session I had, I told the brethren about Satan's trick of weakening the believer's faith.

Over the years, I realized accusation is his major weakening tool. He accuses us about our short-comings and brings to our rememberance what God has forgiven and forgotten.

The person under Satan's accusation will live in guilt and doubt God's redeeming power to save, forgive and forget. Sometimes, guilt feelings arising from Satanic accusations can lead to depression.

Psychiatry homes are filled with depressed people whose mental problem arose from guilt feelings and Satanic accusation.

Do you know that God forgets repented&blood washed sins? Yes, He does because the word said so.

*“Come now, let’s settle this,” says the Lord. “Though your sins are like scarlet, I will make them as white as snow. Though they are red like crimson, I will make them as white as wool* ( Isaiah 1:18 NLT).

*We commit to our memories past errors&mistakes because religion has taught us to do so. Sometimes, we  re-confess a sin that God has forgiven thinking He may choose to forgive one and hold on to the other*.

This is what God has to say in his word, *“I—yes, I alone—will blot out your sins for my own sake and will never think of them again*"(Isaiah 43:25 NLT).

If these scriptures shared are not convincing, read this: *"And I will forgive their wickedness, and I will never again remember their sins*" (Hebrews 8:12 NLT)

If God does not remember the sins he has forgiven why commit such to memories?

Many times, we are wrongly taught especially on issues about forgiveness. So many still swim in sin because someone told them they can't be forgiven anymore, so they decided to continue sinning because they feel mercy has eluded them.

Another reason hell will be so hot is because men decided not to take advantage of the loving side of God which is remission of sins.

Whether your sins are big or small, the blood of Jesus is potent to wash them. God is saying to you today that you should go and sin no more.

*PRAYER*: Father, thank you for the blood of Jesus Christ.

Wednesday
June 7th, 2023.

*WILL MEN PRAISE GOD BECAUSE OF YOU*?

Galatians1:24 *And they praised God because of me* (NLT).

I read the obituary of a woman in the U.S. Her children stated categorically that they won't miss her because the world would be a better place without her.

That is sad. She abandoned them at an early age to marry their dad's brother. That memory lingered for long.


Sometimes ago, I was in a meeting where the preacher said, "we miss Archbishop Benson Idahosa in Nigeria".

Many years ago, a former head of state died in one of the African countries, the news of his death made the people to start singing and dancing on the street. People were so happy as If it was the devil that died. He was a brutal leader.

In another African country, when their leader died, they mourned and the citizens gave glory to God for sending them a leader that was selfless and people-centred.

*When we remember you, two things are bound to happen, It is either we thank God because of you or we thank God for taking you away and not be missed. The choice is yours*.

I noticed that most people lie in funerals. When we visit bereaved families and they tell us to write or say something about the deceased, our words are adulating but not sincere at all times.

I once saw an inscription saying, *"Live a good life so that we won't lie in your funeral*".

When I go home to be with the Lord, I want men to sincerely praise God because of me.

*Your life is meant to be impactful and not to be a burden. You are meant to touch lives positively to a point where people would be praying for you not to die*.

If the world around you doesn't praise God and miss you after your demise, your stay on earth has many unanswered questions.

The only way to start leaving indelible marks in the heart of men is to find your purpose and start fulfilling it.

*Walking the path of purpose is what will make men thank God because of you. Failure to do so, you will not be missed*.

*PRAYER*: Father, let my life be impactful In Jesus name.

DEVOTIONAL 0883 | Source start line: 87263

6/8/23, 6:26 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: PROJECTSAVE DEVOTIONAL
Thursday,
June 8th, 2023.


*DO NOT KILL LOVE IN YOUR  MARRIAGE*


Proverbs 17:9 *Whoever would foster love covers over an offense, but whoever repeats the matter separates close friends*.


I once told the story of a couple who had a very stormy marriage. Both of them decided to visit a counselor to resolve their issues. The counselor told them to make a box for keeping records of offenses.


The counselor instructed them to write the nature of offense, time and date on a paper and drop it inside the box. This continued for a specified period of time.


After sometime, the box was opened. The wife's box was full of complaints while the box of the man contained only one piece of paper saying, "I love you". The man chose not to dwell on her nagging, faults and fault-finding attitude but the wife was a trophy winning fault-finder.


This singular action made her realize that love only prospers when couples don't dwell on each other's faults.


*I have discovered how to kill love in marriage. The easiest way to sap the joy, strength, love  and kill the love in marriage is to always dwell on offenses, pile them up and never forgive*.


*As you focus on the imperfections of your spouse daily, it magnifies till it injects hatred into the bloodstream of your marriage*.


*Often, we fail to realize that we're not married to angels. It will be very unrealistic to think that someone with flesh and blood won't offend us*.


*Love only prospers when we dwell more on the good side of our spouse*.


Stop trying to change what the Holy Spirit alone can change. If you do, you'll be frustrated always because you'll fail. Commit what you can't change to the ultimate changer- the Holy Spirit.


Focus on the strength of your spouse, those things that makes him/her standout. Adulate him/her for those things.


If you look for fault always, you'll find one. Fault finding is the reason some concluded "all women/ men are the same".


Often, fault-finding and failure to forgive is what leads to the conclusion: "irreconcilable differences". Some marriages would have survived their storm If the couples had followed this biblical counsel.


In fact, If you want to help your spouse out of his/her love-killing habits, focus on his/ her area of strength, give all your energy to it and create an environment where virtues and strength thrive.


Unconsciously, those things that get you upset may find the exit door. *Constant fault-finding changes no one*.


Wise husbands&wives who don't want to die before their time must learn to look away from certain things: *"Hatred stirs up strife but LOVE COVERS ALL SINS*" (Proverbs 10:12).


*PRAYER*: Father, I receive grace to stay in love and never to kill it in Jesus name.
