June 9th, 2023.

*CONQUERING THE JAMES 4:3 SYNDROME*

James 4:3 *And even when you ask, you don’t get it because your motives are all wrong—you want only what will give you pleasure* ( NLT).

I have been privileged to attend several prayer meetings. In each of those meetings, often I listen to prayer points raised to determine what to pray about.

On several occasions, I decided not to pray certain prayers because they are anti-bible. The motive behind some of them are selfish, ego-driven, vindictive and judgemental.

I have been in prayer meetings where the prayer leader told us to pray that God should bless us more than our siblings and family members so that our family members can call us 'daddy'. That's not biblical.

The focus in some of those prayers raised is that they( family) shouldn't be able to perform any family function without us. The motive behind this prayer is not pure. It is a prayer point that fuels competition and rivalry among siblings and niblings.

Well, most people love to pray such prayers because they want to prove a point to their families and some only need plenty money so that everyone would be subservient to them. To them, that's success.

Those impure motives are the reason God hasn't answered their prayers until now. *If you're praying to be better than others, you will always be below them. That's a fact! God doesn't want you to compete with others rather, he want the best for you*.

God is loving and merciful. His desire for all His children is to live well and be satisfied with the good things of life. *If you pray that God single you out so that others will be damaged and below you, your prayers won't be answered because it doesn't align with God's will*.

If God refuses to bless your siblings and families, you won't enjoy your money because you will have to bear all their financial burdens.

In fact, If they don't fear God, they may kill you so that they'll inherit your properties. In Africa such happens. Some.ministers are also guilty of this. When they're praying for their ministries, they run others down in prayers and lift theirs up.

I have heard of those who went fo the mountain to pray down other ministries and ministers. My dad experienced this in the early days of his pioneering ministry. Some folks went to the mountain praying that his ministry close down.

When they returned, they said he won't last more than 3 years. Today, he has lasted for 3 decades( still counting) minus a year at the time of this writing. God doesn't answer such prayers.

Little wonder why some have remained on the same spot for long. *If your desire for others is evil, no matter how long you pray, what you desire for others won't leave you*.

From today, eliminate selfishness and greed from your prayer life, you'll get better results that way.

*Learn to pray for others what you're praying for yourself*.

*The principle of watering others to be watered in return is still in effect. Water others in prayers and you'll remain ever-green*.

*PRAYER*: Father, I eliminate selfishness from my prayer life today and forever In Jesus name.

6/10/23, 6:23 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: PROJECTSAVE DEVOTIONAL
Saturday,
June 10th, 2023.

*WHAT THE PREACHER TOLD US ON OUR WEDDING DAY*

Amos 3:3 *Can two people walk together without agreeing on the direction*? (NLT)

The preacher who preached on our wedding day told us why his  father was an underachiever all his life time. He counseled us against it

His father didn't work together with his mother as a team. His father was too independent and he felt he doesn't need the input of his mother.

He ignored her suggestions and counsel because he had a mindset that women aren't wise. Till date, there are men who do not plan with their wives. Their believe is that most women know nothing but money.

Have you ever come around men who describe women's brain as fish brain? If you know any of such, give them a "Pentecostal" distance. Such people may influence you to run your family on a solo mode.

*God's intention for the family is to operate as a formidable team that will advance His purpose here on earth*. Marriages on solo mode cannot fulfill God's purpose.

*Oneness of the two should be the strongest unit on earth because the bonding should be spiritual, emotional, mental and physical*.

*If you don't want to live and die as an underachiever, work as a team with your spouse*.

Every team runs with a vision and there are plans on how to achieve the vision. If I may ask, what plans do you have for your family and children?

An old man spoke to me with a regret laden tone years back. He lived in thesame rented apartment for over 40 years. He got married there and had all his children there. His grandchildren visited him there as well.

When landed property was cheap in their neighbourhood, the man was doing his thing while the wife was also doing her thing. No vision, no cooperation. Instead of working as a team, they were living as competitors and rivals.

After 40 years, the children of the late landlord sent them packing. They were tired of seeing his face. Money to build was not his problem. In his youthful days, he had a good job even until his retirement. He traveled abroad and enjoyed life but he spent a larger part of his married life( 40 years) as a tenant in thesame house. That shouldn't be your story.

*As a man, your duty is to cast a vision for your family, interpret it correctly to your wife and let her know her role. That's leadership. You cannot estimate the value your wife will add to your life and family in general until you share your vision with her*.

*What women react to most times isn't lack of money but lack of vision. God created them to be led by their husbands. When they come under a bad family leader, they react negatively because something in them want to be guided and led*.

Often, most single guys have inferiority complex problems when they come around a lady that has achieved. If only they know that such ladies crave true and genuine leadership, they won't run back If led.

Even some husbands have problems when their wives earn more. That's a sign of visionlessness and insecurity.

*If the family runs as a team, your leadership will channel resources in the direction of your vision.  In family team work, it is not "my money" neither is it "her money", it is "our money*."

If a woman knows that her earnings would be spent on something profitable for the family, she won't hold back. By nature, women are givers naturally.

*You can do little as an individual but you can do alot as a team. It is not always about abundance of resources but resourcefulness*.

*ACTION POINT*: As the man, you're the leader. Sit with your wife and chart the course for a better life for your family, interpret your vision correctly and let her know the role she's to play.

DEVOTIONAL 0886 | Source start line: 87483

6/11/23, 7:33 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: ProjectSave Devotional
Sunday,
June 11th, 2023.

*HE MINISTERED TO ME IN THE BATHROOM*

Revelation 2:4 *“But I have this complaint against you. You don’t love me or each other as you did at first*!(NLT).

Sometime ago, I was preparing to take my bath when the Spirit of God ministered to me about the subject of 'backsliding'.

The Spirit of God made me realize that we often conclude a believer has backslid when we observe actions like: living in sin openly, erratic church attendance, and so on. However, those are the terminal signs of backsliding.

He made me realize that there's a subtle sign of backsliding that we often don't notice in our lives even as preachers and believers that are going to church regularly, speaking in tongues and full of church activities.

*That subtle sign of backsliding is when our priorities change from spiritual to self. When we prioritize the things that please us above what please God, that is a subtle but dangerous sign of backsliding*.

*Certain things that are pleasing to us may not be outrightly sinful but may not be in God's will either*.

*It is possible to be committed in church and punctual in all services because you want to be in the good books of the pastor. You don't want the visitation team to come checking on you because of your absence*.

You no longer attend church because you want to fellowship with God and the brethren. Before, you evangelize on your own without telling anyone but now, you prefer to pray or give money( though not bad) to support those preaching while you close your mouth without telling sinners about Jesus.

You assume your money and prayers will get the job done for you forgetting that the great commission is a task for everyone.

You changed your priority from preaching the gospel to giving and praying for those who are preaching the gospel whereas you are suppose to be preaching the gospel while not neglecting other things( prayer&support). That's subtle backsliding.

Before you became a pastor, you had time for sound fellowship with God but now, irrelevant meetings, invitations, counseling and "church office work" is your priority. You no longer study the bible for your own spiritual growth but you do because you're looking for what to preach.

You are committed to preaching nice sermons of homiletical exactitude but no commitment to personal spiritual growth. This is a case of being committed to the ministry more than the one who called us into the ministry. Very dangerous. It is just a matter of time, you'll burn out spiritually and stand in front of God's people as a performer.

*On many pulpits today, there are many performers today but few life dispensers.*

When believers backslide subtly, it is not noticeable because they're full of activities and performance but less of life. It is even worse when pastors mandate members to evangelize whereas they just sit back in church office attending to other things.  Check your priorities once again.

*The moment we don't prioritize the very things that matter to God and we place them at the lower rung of the ladder for what pleases self, it is a sure sign of backsliding*.

Hear the voice of the master: *"Look how far you have fallen! Turn back to me and do the works you did at first...*"Revelation 2:5 NLT.

*PRAYER*: Father, restore me.

DEVOTIONAL 0887 | Source start line: 87552

6/12/23, 7:02 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: PROJECTSAVE DEVOTIONAL
Monday,
June 12th, 2023.

*LOT IS BETTER THAN SLOT*

Psalm 16:5- *The LORD is the portion of mine inheritance and of my cup: thou maintainest my lot(Goral)*(Emphasis mine).

Back in the days when I was job-hunting, it was common to hear people talk about having job slots.

Some of the interviews I did were already concluded before we applied because job slots were given to certain principal officers who filled those vacancies with their candidates. I applied for a job where only two persons were needed to fill a vacancy.

Before the interview, one person had been employed. Out of the hundreds that applied, they needed just one. Even that single vacancy was lobbied for by the high and mighty. Eventually, a classmate whose father is very influential got a job in that establishment.

It became frustrating for me then because merit was not considered. The son of a nobody just couldn't get a decent job and be somebody.

I began to seek for slots through people even to the point of getting a letter from a politician. I thank God those efforts failed.

If those effort had succeeded, I would have gone off course in life. I wouldn't be in ministry today. Even though I knew God's calling was upon me those years but I wanted to do it my own way to prove a point. Thank God i'm on my lane.

If I had gotten those slots, I would have lost my lot in the divine scheme of things for my life. I'll be running a race that isn't for me. I discovered my lot and applied myself to it. It is paying off.

According to the opening scripture, the Hebrew word "Goral" is translated 'lot' which means 'portion' or 'destiny'. It is not determined by any man. It speaks of a divinely destined placement in life.

A slot can be denied,removed or given to another person for bureaucratic or political reasons but your divine lot can't be changed because it is permanent, it maybe delayed but it can't be denied.

Psalms 16:5 made it clear that your lot is secured under the watchful eyes of God. No one can take it from you because God is maintaining it for you. Halleluyah!!! Though, ignorance can make a man beg around for slots when he has a divine lot.

As we dig deeper into the word, we'll discover our lot. *All the promises of God are our lot*. The government can deny us basic amenities and essentials of life but God will freely give us what belongs to us.

Romans 8:32 says, *"Since he did not spare even his own Son but gave him up for us all, won’t he also give us everything else?*"( Romans 8:32 NLT).

We can stand on God's promises and the finished work of Christ to lay claim to what belongs to us. When slots are filled-up, never forget you have an unchanging lot.

There's something greater than connection called "GOD-NECTION". There is a leg greater than long-leg called "GOD-LEG". There is something better than a slot called LOT (destiny, portion,place).

I release you into the fullness of your lot today In Jesus name. When others say they have a slot, tell them you have a lot - a 'Goral'.

*CONFESSION*: Father, thank you for watching over my lot. I enter into the fullness of my lot today and forever in Jesus name.

DEVOTIONAL 0888 | Source start line: 87611
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
6/13/23, 6:44 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: ProjectSave Devotional
Tuesday
June 13th, 2023.

*ANOTHER PERSPECTIVE ABOUT WORSHIP*

Gen 14:20 *And blessed be God Most High, who has defeated your enemies for you.” Then Abram gave Melchizedek a tenth of all the goods he had recovered* (NLT).

Oftentimes, the subject of giving is taught mainly from the angle of getting something in return. It is rare to see or hear preachers preach about it from the standpoint of worship.

It is a foundational truth that  whatever a man sow, he will reap( Gal 6:7). However, the motivation behind giving should not be because we want to get something in return. What about giving as an expression of our worship to God?

*Primarily, God created us to worship Him. Our lives and all we do( giving inclusive) is meant to be an expression of His worship*.

Revelation 4:11 says, *"Thou art worthy, O Lord, to receive glory and honour and power: for thou hast created all things( including our substance), and for thy pleasure they are and were created*"( Emphasis mine).

*We pervert sacred acts when it serves only our purpose and desires instead of His purpose- worship*.

*If our only reason for giving is to get, we will come under manipulation of all sorts by con men who think material things can 'buy'  God*.

Those of us who don't promise people "high things" when we ask for support, we may not get quick responses because people are always asking "What's in it for me If i support your ministry"? We do things from a clearer understanding of scriptures not for self-gain.

Where materialism rules, motivation for evangelism, prayer, visitation, prayer, bible study,and so on would be because of what to get from God in return. In such cases, the heart will desire material things more.

It will be a trade-by-barter kind of thing. Some turned the offering box to a money vending machine. This is the bane of materialistic Gospel.

Some were disappointed after sowing heavy seeds under pressure without result. Why? Their understanding about giving was faulty.

*The only time some persons give is when the preacher promises something in return for giving. If this describes you, you're a spiritual babe. You need to grow*.

Let's learn from our father, Abraham. He gave tithe as an expression of worship to God not even because he wanted anything in return. He wasn't into any 'trade' with God neither was he coerced or put under pressure.

He was not promised a 'sudden breakthrough' in 24 hours. As a matter of fact, he was already wealthy. How do you think you can convince a man of his stature to sow seed so that he can breakthrough? He had everything money could buy in his days.

He understood the greatness of God, his awesomeness and magnificence. He knew God deserved the best of his substance so he gave as an act of worship. In my opinion, this is the highest form of giving.

*Just as we express our worship to God in songs, we can do same through our giving.*

*In the Old Testament, sacrifices to the Lord were designated as “gifts” (Numbers 18:11;  Hebrews 5:1) not because God is in need of anything but he demanded for those things as an expression of worship under that dispensation*.

When the wisemen from the east worshipped baby Jesus, their worship was expressed in the form of “gifts” (Matthew 2:11). They gave to baby Jesus as an act of worship.

*True worshippers are genuine givers who don't need to be coerced. They understand that money is a servant and not a master that should be worshipped. Rather, they give money in worship of the maker of all things*.

Apostle Paul confirmed this truth when he commended the Philipians for the financial support sent to him. He said it was “fragrant offering, a sacrifice acceptable and pleasing to God.”(Phil4:18 ESV). That's worship.

The amount was not important but the heart from which the offering came- a heart of worship.

I want you to adjust your motive and change your perspective on this subject. See your life and all you own as an instrument of worship. Then, giving will not be a burden but an act of worship.

*PRAYER*: Father, I receive the grace to worship you with my substance in Jesus name.


====================================================================================================
DEVOTIONAL 0889 | Source start line: 87692
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
6/14/23, 6:21 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: ProjectSave Devotional
Wednesday
June 14th, 2023.

*UPDATE YOUR KNOWLEDGE*

Hosea 4:6 *My people are destroyed for lack of knowledge: because thou hast rejected knowledge, I will also reject thee, that thou shalt be no priest to me: seeing thou hast forgotten the law of thy God, I will also forget thy children*(KJV).

There was a boat engine technician, Mr Jack by name. In his time, he was an expert in fixing old model boat engines.


A new model of cruise boat had technical issues. Someone recommended Mr Jack to the owner to fix the new boat engines but he got stuck with this new model.

He became confused because he wasn't getting it. He looked like a fool on the job he had been doing for years.

He had a hunch to request for the manual of the cruise boat. In the manual, he read how the manufacturer described what should be done when the engine develop the kind of fault he was trying to fix.

He followed the instruction and he got the job done in few minutes after trying for four days. Just by updating his knowledge through study, he had a breakthrough.

Sometimes, what you need do to get out of confusion is to get accurate information by updating your knowledge.

Mr Jack thought  the engine he wanted to repair was like other engines he had been fixing.

We are like that most times. We try to solve today's  problems with yesterday's method.

When we are not learning new ways of doing things, we become stagnant. That's the reason experts fail sometimes.

This applies in ministry as well. In ministry, you need to update yourself. You can't run ministry on stale knowledge.

When we run on stale knowledge, stagnancy sets in. In moments of confusion, after getting to that point where it seems all you know to do has failed, go for fresh information. This applies to marriage, ministry, business and career.

The Jews were in captivity but most of them may not know it had been prophesied that their captivity would last seventy years but Daniel got hold of the right information that made him pray the right prayers (Dan9:2).

The information you need may be in some of those books on your shelf or in the library.

The moment you know what to do and how to go about it, that is the end of confusion. There are times God speaks to us through anointed literature materials. I have benefitted so much from this.

Someone has written a book about some of the questions you're asking. Some of the things causing confusion has been solved and documented by someone. Start reading. Update your knowledge.

*PRAYER*: Father, I receive the grace to search for the right information in Jesus name.

*THE SECRET OF SPIRITUAL POWER*


Ex 34:34-35 *But whenever he went into the Tent of Meeting to speak with the Lord , he would remove the veil until he came out again. Then he would give the people whatever instructions the Lord had given him, and the people of Israel would see the radiant glow of his face. So he would put the veil over his face until he returned to speak with the Lord*( NLT).


I was on the mountain to pray years back. One of the ministers gave a word of caution about extreme things some people do when seeking for God's power.

He said some would pray looking up to the shinning  sun because they want power. That's extreme. It is good we understand how spiritual things work.

There is so much prayer going on among charismatics for power. Some go as far doing strenuous things to show how serious they are for God's power.


Having read the history of several men mightily used by God, I observed a number of them were men who loved to stay in the place of prayer for long. Their prayers wasn't about power as we may think. They were just fellowshiping.


*As a believer that is baptized in the Spirit, the power of God is in you already. However, that power will be released from within your regenerated spirit as you dwell in God's presence.*

*The more you stay in God's presence, the more the release of power from within your spirit*.


I learnt a vital lesson from Moses in that regard. We were told that whenever he enters God's presence, the glory of the Lord will envelope him and his face will become radiant.

He didn't go into God's presence to ask for a radiant face. He simply went to God to fellowship and commune with him.

Power is one of the additions you'll get as you stay in God's presence. *It is only a man that tarry at wine that gets intoxicated.*

If you stay long in a room where perfume is produced or sprayed constantly, when you step out of that room, the fragrance of that perfume will smell on your body and people will perceive it.


Your intention of entering the room might be to see how it is produced but coming into that atmosphere where the fragrance of the perfume is sprayed, the fragrance rubs on you.

That's the way it is with God's power. Do not forget that the power of God is in you already when you received the baptism of the Spirit(Acts1:8).

Actually, what happens as you stay in God's presence is that the power locked up in your spirit would be released.

*Your desire should be to dwell in his presence more. That's how to launch into the realm of the supernatural*.


*PRAYER*: Father, I receive the grace to dwell in your presence in Jesus name.

*HOW TO MAINTAIN YOUR HEALING AND MIRACLES* Part 1

John5:14 *But afterward Jesus found him in the Temple and told him, “Now you are well; so stop sinning, or something even worse may happen to you*( NLT).

Some persons are disturbed about why they lost their healing and miracles days after meetings. This has made some persons conclude healings and miracles we see were staged or just a temporary thing.

Let me share my personal experience. In 2005, I was healed of a sharp pain on my chest in an anointing service. I knew God healed me. Later, after some years, I felt the sharp pain again.

The devil made me feel my healing was a hoax but I shut him up. Ever since then, I have seen people who had similar experience. I have seen people who received divine healing and miracles experience a relapse.

Sometimes, people think that if healing occurs and the healed person experiences relapse, maybe the preacher is not genuine or probably the healing was not authentic but that's not the case often. Some were healed through prayer but they died after. That is not the will of God.

In John 5:14, after Jesus healed the man at the pool of Bethsaida, he told him: *"..., “Now you are well; so stop sinning, or something even worse may happen to you.*" The instruction was clear. Jesus told him to stop sinning so that the worse won't happen to him.

The tense used is a continuous tense- "sinning". That is, if he makes a practice of sinning, the devil would bring a worse calamity upon him. There is a lesson there for us all.

Jesus healed him and it was evident to all but the words of Jesus implied that there was a possibility of losing his healing if he continues in sin. This is plain and simple.

What you need to understand is that sin gives the enemy  entrance to torment the lives of people. If you pray or you're prayed for and God intervened, do not play with sin.


The devil always use every opportunity to strike back. He is not happy that you're living in health. He is not happy that the power of God prevailed over that health challenge.

The moment you step out of line and remain there, the latter attack may be worse than the former.

*PRAYER*: Father, I shall not lose my healing and miracles to carelessness in Jesus name.

*HOW TO MAINTAIN YOUR HEALING AND MIRACLES* Part 2

Mark 9:23  *"Jesus said to him, 'If you can believe? All things are possible for one who believes*".

*PETERINE SYNDROME*

Quite a number of us know the story of Peter walking on water. Apostle Peter operated in the miraculous by walking on water. His faith in Jesus' command made it possible.

However, he regressed into unbelief when he started looking at the wind. He took his gaze off Jesus and the result was that he sank gradually. Truthfully, he walked on water. That was a miracle but he sank gradually after the miracle(Matthew14:22-33).

*Another reason some believers lose their healing and miracle is because they stopped believing. They regressed into unbelief after getting their miracle*.

*The devil is tricky. If he cannot get you into unbelief before getting your healing and miracle, he may intensify his effort to draw you into unbelief after you've gotten your healing and miracle*.

His aim would be to make you lose your healing and miracle. He may shoot an arrow of pain into your body and he'll whisper words of  unbelief into your ears saying, "the healing you received was a hoax".

If you don't talk back to him authoritatively and put him where he belongs, he'll put the same sickness back on you in double fold.

The devil will always try to convince you that your healing was not authentic. He does that through the media today. Alot of junks are out there against the miraculous.

Some people believe all healings and miracles taking place are staged. Some folks no longer believe God for healing because of what they read and see in the media. It is worse when you listen to cessationist theologians.

*To keep your healing and miracle, you must continue to exercise the faith you had initially.  Faith is not something you use and dump. It must be exercised continuously*.

That you received your healing yesterday doesn't mean the devil won't fire his fiery darts of pain and sickness again. Faith is a shield. A warrior doesn't drop his shield at war because arrows can be fired at anytime.

Apostle Paul said, *"Above all, taking the shield of faith, wherewith ye shall be able to quench all the fiery darts of the wicked.*" (Eph6:16, KJV).

*PRAYER*: Father, I shall not lose my healing and miracle in Jesus name.

**HOW TO MAINTAIN YOUR HEALING AND MIRACLES* Part 3

Proverbs 18:21 *The tongue can bring death or life; those who love to talk will reap the consequences* (NLT).

*YOUR CONFESSION IS IMPORTANT*

Often, I say "Your life will go in the direction of your words". This is a fact that is scripturally proven. The tongue is a powerful tool that can kill and make alive.

*One of the reasons some believers lost their healing and miracles was because they took sides with the devil by confessing their doubts and unbelief*.

God doesn't joke with words spoken by men likewise the devil.

In Numbers 14:28, God told Moses to say these words: *"Now tell them this: ‘As surely as I live, declares the LORD, I will do to you the very things I heard you say.*" In Numbers 13, Moses sent twelve spies to Canaan. Ten returned with frightening report but Caleb and Joshua spoke faith-filled words. Out of the twelve, they( Joshua and Caleb) made it to the promised land.

Sometimes,  demonic attacks and oppression do resurface after an initial healing or deliverance.  In Luke 11:14-26, Jesus spoke about casting out demons from a person. He said it is possible for the demon to return with other wicked deomons to his former house if he finds the place empty.

The same principle applies to healing because sicknesses are caused by demons in some cases. *After receiving your healing and miracle, part of your maintenance duty is to hold on to the faith that birth the miracle and keep confessing God's word*.

*Do not use your mouth to repudiate your healing. The devil may shoot an arrow of pain again. That's to test where you truly stand. The moment you agree with him by confessing that the sickness is still there because of little pains,  he'll make your body his abode. The second attack may be worse than the first*.

What are you saying? The word of God says in Proverbs 18:20 *“A man's stomach shall be satisfied from the fruit of his mouth, from the produce of his lips he shall be filled.*”

*Do not use your mouth to put your life in the reverse gear*. Do not change your confession of faith.

Proverbs12:14 *"From the fruit of their lips people are filled with good things, and the work of their hands brings them reward.*"

*CONFESSION*: I am not going to lose my healing and miracle.I refuse to reverse my life to the old order of sickness, pains and misery in Jesus name.

*HOW TO MAINTAIN YOUR HEALING AND MIRACLES* Part 4

Proverbs 2:11 *Discretion will protect you, and understanding will guard you*( NIV).

*TAKE CAUTION*

I heard the story of a preacher who prayed for a cripple comes to mind.  Miraculously, the cripple walked and everyone saw the manifestation of God's power.

However, this preacher didn't take caution, he wanted everyone to see how genuine the miracle was. Instead of telling the man to walk, he told him to dance. Not long after, the recipient of the healing died.

He understood later that he shouldn't have instructed him to dance but to walk. He may have given that instruction thinking dancing was an action of faith but it was the right action at the wrong time. In cases like that, physiotherapists can tell what the next line of action should be.

That is why some healing ministers have medics in their team for counseling purposes. *That we operate in the supernatural is not an excuse to throw caution to the wind.* In life, we need to apply godly wisdom in all we do.

Another reason some believers lose their healing is because of failure to take wise precautions after receiving their healing and miracle. For instance, If God heals Mr John of diabetes and hypertension with clinical proofs, it is not an excuse to eat any how and live carelessly thereafter.

Some may say, "but if it is God that healed him, why shouldn't he eat whatever he likes"? Well, we should not forget that we still live in a mortal body that is prone to decay. *"Therefore we do not lose heart. Though outwardly we are wasting away, yet inwardly we are being renewed day by day.*" (2Cor 4:16).

*Do not neglect your body after receiving healing. The habits that brought sickness upon you should be discontinued. If you do not obey simple medical advice, it shows you don't love yourself*.

Doctors are not enemies of divine healing neither are they anti Christ. In fact, medical wisdom is from God. There are  believers who avoid doctors like plague because of what they heard from a preacher.

You maybe surprised that the same preacher checks his health regularly and follow a strict diet regime. The dietician is not an enemy of your nutrition rather he is there to help you put things in the right perspective so that you won't eat poison in the name of food. Be wise!

*PRAYER*: Father, I receive wisdom concerning these things in Jesus name.

*WE WON'T BE HERE FOREVER*

Matthew24:36  *“However, no one knows the day or hour when these things will happen, not even the angels in heaven or the Son himself. Only the Father knows* (NLT).

Sometime ago, I read two sermon illustrations about rapture. The first is about a mother who explained to her little girl, the death of her father.

The mother said: “God has sent for your father and will send for us, but I do not know just when.” Finally, the little girl said: *“If we do not know just when God is going to send for us, do you not think we had better pack up and get ready to go? God might send when we are not ready*.”

F.B. Meyer once asked D.L. Moody, “What is the secret of your success?”  Moody replied, “For many years I have never given an address without the consciousness that the Lord may come before I have finished.”

These stories illustrates what it mean to be ready. In the history of the early church, tradition has it that they lived under a very strong conviction that Christ would come in their time. Little wonder why they took the work of soul-winning serious.

A wise man said we should stop getting ready for the coming of the Lord but we should live ready for his coming. The process of getting ready is not the same as being in a state of readiness.

No true believer will feel at home here because *"...our citizenship is in heaven, from which we also eagerly wait for the Savior, the Lord Jesus Christ*",Phil 3:20.

*Jesus is coming soon*. We must live with the consciousness of His soon return every day. The signs are there for us to see. The message of His coming is becoming rare on pulpits today. The "theology" of some do not believe in the rapture of the saints.

Men live as though there's no where after here forgetting that the life after this life is eternal and never ending. Our goals and plans must factor in eternity. We won't be here forever.

*We are not designed to live here forever, there's a home after here*.

Knowing that Christ is coming will help us stay pure in readiness for His coming: *"And everyone who has this hope in Him purifies himself, just as He is pure*" (1John3:3).

*PRAYER*: Father, I receive grace to live ready on daily basis in Jesus name.

*TROUBLE IS PART OF THE PROCESS*.

Genesis 50:20  *You intended to harm me, but God intended it for good to accomplish what is now being done, the saving of many lives"(NIV).

One of our songs in church was changed or re composed by some to say, "...when success comes my way, I will praise you Lord".

Initially, the word 'trouble' was in the place where we now have 'success' in the song. The composer was right. Out of fear and lack of understanding, the word changed. Troubles and trials are part of the process.

The brothers of Joseph thought they could kill his dreams by selling him into slavery.

They conspired to silence  and send him into oblivion. Little did they know that they were part of the process that would fast track the fulfilment of his dreams.

They forgot that God was interested in making good His promises in the life of His children even when the process is rough.

Get this clear today, *God can delegate many things but not the fulfilment of your destiny*. He is the supervisor of that project even when the process is rough.

*Your destiny is a scripted destiny with details prepared before you were born*.

*The process that leads to the fulfilment of destiny is not always sweet but God has a hand in it because He makes all things work together for our good*.

Those frustrating you may think they are stopping you but i'm glad to tell you they are  tools in God's hands to process you.

Joseph knew that truth, that was why he didn't revenge when he saw his brothers.

Get out of the pity party and start celebrating the outcome of that unpalatable process.

The resultant effect of the process you're going through is that many shall be saved, receive hope and be set on course for destiny when you share your testimony.

*PRAYER*: Father, I receive grace to pass through the processes involved in fulfilling destiny In Jesus name.

*RUN TO THE MERCY SEAT*


Ex25:22 *"And there I will meet with thee, and I will commune with thee from above the Mercy Seat*..."

The Ark of covenant or testimony is a sacred object in ancient Israel. It was approximately 42 inches long and 27 inches wide.

The mercy seat covered the ark which contained the three emblems of man’s rebellion: the golden pot of manna—man’s rebellion against God’s provision, two tablets of stone on which God wrote the Ten Commandments—man’s rebellion against God’s standard, and Aaron’s rod—man’s rebellion against God’s authority.

Once a year on the Day of Atonement, the high priest would sprinkle the blood of the sacrificed animal on the mercy seat and so make propitiation for the sins of Israel.

Today, it is not the blood of animals that makes propitiation for our sins, but the holy blood of Christ.


Romans 3:24-25, *"being justified freely by his grace through the redemption that is in Christ Jesus: whom God hath set forth to be a propitiation through faith in his blood, to declare his righteousness for the remission of sins that are past, through the forbearance of God;*.


His blood speaks for us and put God on our side. God does not see our rebellion. He sees the blood of His Son and accepts us!


Now, we can run to the mercy seat: *"Let us therefore come boldly unto the throne of grace, that we may obtain mercy, and find grace to help in time of need*" (Hebrews 4:16 KJV)


*Do not allow  the devil drive you into your past.* If there is any battle I fought sternly as a believer, it is the battle of guilt and condemnation. Satan drove me into depression because I felt unworthy of God.

Thank God I won that battle by the word and the Spirit.


*No matter how horrible your life is( past&present), God's mercy is available for you If you repent*.


Religion will burden you with loads of guilt but Christianity will show you the way out of guilt. That's goodnews.

If God's word says you should come boldly(not out of religious piety)to the throne of grace to obtain mercy, that means mercy is available. Take advantage of it.


*CONFESSION*: My past is over in Christ. I receive cleansing for my sins and I appropriate the atoning work of Christ  In Jesus name. Amen

6/23/23, 8:09 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: PROJECTSAVE DEVOTIONAL

June 23rd, 2023.

*THE REHOBOAM SYNDROME*

2 Chron 12:1 *And it came to pass, when Rehoboam had established the kingdom, and had strengthened himself, he forsook the law of the Lord , and all Israel with him*( KJV).

I was discussing with a senior minister of the Gospel recently. I told him about a missionary who received a very huge support from an American church.

When I mentioned the amount, the senior minister was surprised and he said to me, 'If you receive that amount of money, you will not go to the mission field again'. Quickly, I responded saying 'No".

However, I had a rethink to check my heart. What will happen when I begin to swim in abundance? Will I still go around preaching in rural places? Am I sure comfort won't make me serve God in my own way instead of His own way?

It is possible  remodel ministry to suit oneself because of change in status. One will still appear to the public as 'minister of the Gospel' but as far as God is concerned, the register is reading 'backsliden'.

There are things I've observed over the years among some believers. I have seen cases of those who burned for the Lord when they were seeking for something but they backslid when they got it.

As a church boy, while growing up, I saw people who burned for the Lord especially singles but their fire burnt out after they married or got their desired jobs. Some persons carried a fire that could burn hell while on campus but are nowhere to be found in the divine scheme of things.

Our opening text described a king who forsook the ways of God after he was established on the throne. If he was not following the ways of God, the bible wouldn't have said he forsook God's ways.

It is plausible that he was zealous and vibrant when he was an ordinary man but he lost touch with heaven immediately he became comfortable. That is one of the dangers in time of peace.

Some believers are like Rehoboam who forsook the ways of God after their major breakthrough landed. Some prayed, fasted and had serious devotional life but the moment they got to their palace in life, they forsook God.

It is a dangerous thing to forsake God's ways because of comfort. The Psalmist says, *"The wicked shall be turned into hell, and all the nations that forget God.*"(Psalm9:17).

*Now that your status has changed and God has lifted you, this is not the time to relegate God and his ways to the dustbin. The trap of gradual backsliding is common to those with enviable growth and progress but it may not be visible  because the glitz and glamour of a new status may hinder us from seeing properly*.

*Now that you have a car, can you still join the brethren on door-to-door evangelism on foot? If the things you held dear in your walk with God are now a thing of the past because of your new status, you're no different from Rehoboam*.

God want us to always burn with the fire of our first love. Hear the words of Jesus, *"Nevertheless I have somewhat against thee, because thou hast left thy first love.   Remember therefore from whence thou art fallen, and repent, and do the first works; or else I will come unto thee quickly, and will remove thy candlestick out of his place, except thou repent*"( Rev2:4-5).

*PRAYER*: Father, I have started well, I receive the grace to finish well in Jesus name

DEVOTIONAL 0899 | Source start line: 88320

6/24/23, 5:54 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: PROJECTSAVE DEVOTIONAL
Saturday,
June 24th, 2023.

*CALLING IS NOT ENOUGH- ADD TRAINING*

1Chronicles 25:7 *Their number who were trained in singing to the Lord, with their relatives, all who were skillful, was 288*

Under the old covenant, the Levites were chosen by God to do the work of ministry in that dispensation.

During the reign of David, they began to assign priestly duties by lots and when they wanted to assign the work of music, they chose the descendants of Asaph.

What caught my attention in the opening text is the phrase, *"Their number who were TRAINED in singing to the Lord.*" You may not know why it caught my attention but I'll explain.

The Levites had only one purpose- priestly duties which included every aspect of temple service. It was their divine calling and ordination.

The question is, "Why were they  trained to execute a divine assignment entrusted into their hands. Can't they rely on anointing alone to do the work"?

There's a lesson there for us all. *The discovery of your God-ordained purpose does not make training less important*.

*Being called is not enough. In addition to your glorious calling, add continuous training*.

*The discovery of your purpose or calling in life is not an automatic license to give it expression without adequate training*.

*God is not arbitrary in His doings neither is He in a hurry to launch you out; He doesn't launch men out for an assignment without giving room for preparation*.

Moses spent the first 80 years of his life undergoing serious divine training. Joshua trained under Moses, Paul trained under Gamaliel in his pre conversion days and after his conversion, trained under Barnabas and spent 3 years in Arabia in preparation for his assignment.

The disciples didn't launch out in ministry not until after they were certified fit by Jesus, their trainer. They attended "Jesus Christ Theological Seminary". The best seminary in history.

*It is one thing to know what God want you to do in life and it is another thing to be adequately trained to do that which God has called you to do.*

What will take an untrained fellow to achieve in 10 years may take a trained man 2 years. An untrained man may have quantity of result but quality will be lacking. It takes thorough training to be fit for purpose.

*An untrained preacher may think preaching is all about elocution and alliterations in expression but a trained preacher knows the art of preaching is to expound scriptures accurately and grow the man in Christ to become mature, transformed and fit for the master's use*.

I'm yet to see a star athlete that didn't train his way into stardom. Don't make a mess of that assignment that God has given you even if it looks so 'little' though there's no little assignment in God. Get quality training.

*PRAYER*: Father, I receive the grace to submit to quality training in the name of Jesus Christ

6/25/23, 7:32 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: ProjectSave Devotional

*PURPOSE DISCOVERY DIMENSIONS* Pt.1

Acts 9:6 *And he trembling and astonished said, Lord, what wilt thou have me to do? And the Lord said unto him, Arise, and go into the city, and it shall be told thee what thou must do*.

*WHAT*

I started praying intensely in 2006 asking God about what my assignment in life would be. Much later, I read two Kenneth Hagin's books that fueled the intensity of my prayer in that regard.

The books: Following God's Plan For Your Life and Plans, Purposes&Pursuits helped me in no small measure. The craving to be right at the centre of God's will for my life increased having read how Kenneth E. Hagin of blessed memory narrated how he was out of God's will for the first twelve years in ministry before getting it right.

The major question I began to ask God for years were, "Father, what is your will for my life and ministry"? What is my assignment in life? I didn't get answers to these questions until years after when I started praying. My desire was to get it right in life.

*In your quest to fulfilling destiny, the question of "What" must be asked by you and answered by God. You don't decide "what" for yourself in life. God has a plan for you before your were born. Your duty is to discover that plan and fulfill it*.

Our ambitions are not thesame with His purpose for our lives. *The starting point of being at the centre of God's will in matters of destiny is to ask from God, "What is my purpose in life*"?

As you pray, trust God for answers.  He has promised us comprehensive direction in his word: *"The Lord says, “I will guide you along the best pathway for your life. I will advise you and watch over you*" (Psalms 32:8 NLT).

*PRAYER*: Father, reveal your purpose for my life to me in Jesus name.

DEVOTIONAL 0901 | Source start line: 88425
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
6/26/23, 6:33 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: PROJECTSAVE DEVOTIONAL
Monday,
June 26th, 2023.

*PURPOSE DISCOVERY DIMENSIONS* Pt.2

Jonah 1:1-2 *Now the word of the LORD came unto Jonah the son of Amittai, saying, Arise, go to Nineveh, that great city, and cry against it; for their wickedness is come up before me*

*"WHERE"- THE QUESTION OF LOCATION*

The story of Jonah illustrates today's devotional properly. God sent Jonah to Nineveh but he decided to go to Tarshish. The assignment of God for his life was to find full expression in Nineveh but he re routed to another location.

The question of "Where" is important after discovering the "What" of our purpose in life. While "What" is a question of assignment or purpose, "Where" is a question of location for the fulfilment of the assigment.

Jesus is the lamb of God that was  slain from the foundation of the world. He was destined to die for the sins of mankind. To achieve this purpose, he needed to come to the earth not planet mars or jupiter. If he had gone to another planet, he would have missed the location for his assignment.

Paul was an apostle sent to the Gentiles. If he had focused his apostolic ministry on the Jews, he may have missed out on the will of God for his life. He could have preached the right message based on the revelation he had but to the wrong people.

Paul said, *"For the same God who worked through Peter as the apostle to the Jews also worked through me as the apostle to the Gentiles*"(Galatians 2:8 NLT).

*Every divine assigment has a "Where" for its execution. Some have truly discovered the "What" of their assignment but they are in the wrong location*. Why some persons are ineffective is because they are doing the right thing in the wrong place.

*As a man that want to fulfill God's purpose on earth, your location is as important as the assignment itself. You don't pack your bags and relocate to where God has not sent you*.

*A man on purpose doesn't live with a hustler's mentality. A hustler is seeking for opportuinities here and there not minding if it is the will of God*. Some have lost their ministries and assignment to this indiscretion thinking they can execute their assignment from anywhere.

You can only execute your purpose where God has destined for you not anywhere on earth. *The man on purpose doesn't seek for the proverbial greener pastures at the expense of his assignment*.

*Those who understand the nature of God's purpose knows there is no other pasture that is greener than where God plant you*. It may be tough and rough but he has a place where your table is prepared.

Before packing your bags, check with God to be sure your next location is the land for the fulfillment of your destiny. While man thinks more about getting comfort, God thinks more about you being in His will then every other thing follows.

*ACTION POINT*: If you have discovered God's will for your life, then ask God to know If you are in the right location.

DEVOTIONAL 0902 | Source start line: 88473
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
6/27/23, 6:47 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: ProjectSave Devotional
Tuesday
June 27th, 2023.

*PURPOSE DISCOVERY DIMENSIONS* Pt.3

Galatians 4:4 *But when the right time came, God sent his Son, born of a woman, subject to the law* (NLT)

*"WHEN"- THE QUESTION OF TIMING*

In the opening text, we were told that at the right time, God sent his Son. We know what His son came to do, to redeem mankind.

Christ could have come immediately after Adam fell into sin so that he can quickly salvage the situation. He could have come in the dispensation of the law to rescue man from legalism.

During the 400 silent years where no prophet came or arose in Israel after Malachi until John the baptist, He could have showed up but he didn't until the time appointed by God. Why? God has a set time in his agenda for the fulfilment of every divine purpose.

*Every divine purpose is timed. God works with time. He is not arbitrary or haphazard. In matters of divinely ordained purpose, anytime is not God's time*.

When God reveals his mind to you about an assignment or say His purpose for your life, ask him to reveal the time for the execution of his plans and purpose.

*Some went ahead of God and hit the rocks. When you go ahead of God, you will not have his backing or support. When you move with the timing of God, all things needed shall be supplied*.

*Time is a tool in the hands of God to process us for the right time*. That is, when God reveals his mind to us about his purpose for our lives, the waiting time before the time of execution is the period of preparation.

If God has revealed his mind to you about his will, don't rush it. Ask him for the timing.

*PRAYER*: Father, I will not miss you in Jesus name.

DEVOTIONAL 0903 | Source start line: 88521
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
6/28/23, 7:21 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: ProjectSave Devotional
Wednesday
June 28th, 2023.

*PURPOSE DISCOVERY DIMENSIONS* Pt.4

Ex 25:40 *“Be sure that you make everything according to the pattern I have shown you here on the mountain* (NLT)

*"HOW"- THE QUESTION OF PATTERN*.

In the voice of healing days in America, Kenneth E. Hagin of blessed memory was counseled to use tent for his meetings.


Several persons told him they were instructed by the Lord to tell him to have a big tent for his meetings like some evangelists in town but he declined because that was not the pattern of ministry God gave him.

After settling the questions of "What", "Where" and "When" with God in the place of prayer, you need to ask Him the question of "How".

*"How" is the question of pattern to follow. It is the question of modus operandi and model. I have come to know God as a very detailed and precise person*.

When he instructed Moses to build a santuary in Exodus 25, God gave him detailed instructions on how to go about it. I was surprised that God gave him measurements and types of wood and materials to be used for the articles of the temple.

Others may use the materials that suit them and conventional dimensions or measurements but God instructed Moses to do it differently. He gave him a unique pattern.

*The execution of divine purposes and assignments must follow divine pattern*.

In the apostolic ministry of Paul to the Gentiles, his pattern was to look for a Jewish synagogue gathering in any Gentile community. He fellowshiped with them and used that platform to reach out to his target audience- the Gentiles.

*Two persons may be called to pastor yet their pastoring pattern may be different from each other*. I have seen Pastors with special pastoring patterns because of their target audience.

That assignment God gave you has a unique pattern. Have you discovered it?

*ACTION POINT*: Spend quality time in prayer asking for details about that assignment. He has promised to lead us( Psalms 32:8).

DEVOTIONAL 0904 | Source start line: 88581

6/29/23, 6:41 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: PROJECTSAVE DEVOTIONAL
Thursday,
June 29th, 2023.


*PURPOSE DISCOVERY DIMENSIONS* Pt.5

Luke 5:32 I have come to call not those who think they are righteous, but those who know they are sinners and need to repent.” NLT


*"TO WHOM"- QUESTION OF AUDIENCE*


I love Jesus. He knew His audience while on earth. Those who thought they were righteous didn't benefit from his ministry because He came for those who recognized themselves as sinners.


Two great leaders in our time comes to mind. Evangelist Reinhard Bonnke of blessed memory and Rev Dr. Bola Akin-John are examples of today's devotional.


Reinhard Bonnke, a German evangelist was sent to Africa. His major audience were in Africa. He could have staged his crusades in Europe or Asia but the Lord pointed him to the unsaved peoples in Africa.


On the other hand, Rev Dr Bola Akin-John is a trainer of pastors and ministry leaders for over three decades. He teaches biblical Church Growth&Health with great dexterity and anointing.


He has authored over 100 best-selling books for church leaders and published other resource materials. Anywhere church leaders are gathered, his ministry finds expression there.


He could have pioneered a church and run regular church meetings but the every day comer to church are not his audience. His audience are the leaders of those shepherd the sheep. I have benefited from his ministry immensely.


*In purpose fulfilment, you need to define your audience. The question of "To whom" must be answered the moment God gives you an assignment*.


*The bitter truth is that you can't reach everyone*. Knowing your potential audience will help focus your energy better on those to search for.

The principal audience of Paul were the Gentiles in his day. He didn't waste his energy traveling around Israel and writing epistles to the Jewish believers. He concentrated his effort on the Gentiles.


All over the world, God is giving His sons assignment to target groups. Surprisingly, God has not forgotten children and teenagers. God is raising people to disciple children and teenagers. If your audience are in that category, don't despise the assignment.


Who are your audience?


*ACTION POINT*: Ask the Lord to reveal your audience to you.

DEVOTIONAL 0905 | Source start line: 88640
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
6/30/23, 12:19 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: PROJECTSAVE DEVOTIONAL
Friday,
June 30th, 2023.

*IS PHILIPPIANS 2:12 ADVOCATING SALVATION BY WORKS?*

Phil2: 12 Therefore, my dear friends, as you have always obeyed—not only in my presence, but now much more in my absence—continue to work out your salvation with fear and trembling, NIV

Oftentimes, whenever we quote Philippians 2:12 as it relates to proper Christian conduct, some persons accuse that we are advocating salvation by works.

The opening text is very crucial to our understanding of the common salvation we share. It started with "Therefore". As a rule of thumb, whenever you see that word, you need to know what was there before.

Certain line of thoughts and admonition had gone before getting to that point so contextual reading will help us to interpret properly.

From Philipian 1:27, Paul began to give admonition about how to live the christian life: *"Whatever happens, conduct yourselves in a manner worthy of the gospel of Christ. Then, whether I come and see you or only hear about you in my absence, I will know that you stand firm in the one Spirit, striving together as one for the faith of the gospel*".

If you read downward into chapter 2, Paul stressed on living a Christ-like life( godliness), unity and imitating Christ's humility. That's the intention of the writer, Paul.

So when Paul enjoined that we  work out our salvation with fear and trembling, it is an admonition to produce the fruit of salvation( godliness, humility and unity) in the way and manner we live.

Whenever this scripture is quoted, some persons are quick to misinterpret our intention to mean, "they are promoting salvation by works." No we are not.

The import of what Paul meant was that we(saved believers) should "work out" what has been "worked into" us. It is not the same as saying "do these to be saved." It would not have been because he was talking to saved persons.

A believer is someone who have the life of God and imbued with the capacity for godliness: *"By his divine power, GOD HAS GIVEN US EVERYTHING WE NEED FOR LIVING A GODLY LIFE. We have received all of this by coming to know him, the one who called us to himself by means of his marvelous glory and excellence.*"(2 Peter 1:3 NLT).

God's deposit for godly living is in you already. You are a carrier of a different kind of life, eternal life. This is a life that is not patterned after the flesh and mundane affection. Paul is saying God has worked this life into you, just work it out.

The Christian life is not on auto pilot. God has played His part, it is your turn to play your own part. God didn't create man to be a robot that has to be controlled electronically.

There's a "doing" of righteousness expected of believers. The grace "to do" has been released already, take advantage of it: *"For the grace of God that bringeth salvation hath appeared to all men, TEACHING us that, DENYING UNGODLINESS and worldly lusts, we should live soberly, righteously, and godly, in this present world;*", Titus2:11-12.

*PRAYER*: Father, I receive the grace to work out what has been worked into me in Jesus name

DEVOTIONAL 0906 | Source start line: 88693

7/1/23, 8:37 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: PROJECTSAVE DEVOTIONAL
Saturday,
July 1st, 2023.

*BE A GOOD FRIEND TO THE HURTING*

Job17:17 *A friend loveth at all times, And a brother is born for adversity*(KJV).

The friends of Job were bad friends. It took me time before I discovered their errors.

Job faced serious trials in his life time. He experienced reversal of fortune. His friends who came to mourn with him made  unsavoury remarks about his integrity and purity.

His friends couldn't comprehend why such calamity befell him  because of their belief in divine retribution. To them, the righteous don't face challenges. As far they were concerned, he was paying for his sins. Very wrong mentality.

Those that were meant to bring him word of comfort added to his pains. They insinuated that his sins caused what befell him, (Job4:3-8, 8:20).

This is what the bible said about him, *"There was a man in the land of Uz, whose name was Job; and that man was PERFECT AND UPRIGHT, and one that feared God, and eschewed evil*"(KJV ).

The words of Job's friends didn't only grief Job, it griefed God as well.

In Job 42:7-8, the Lord saw their words as a display of  foolishness: *"After the Lord had spoken these words to Job, the Lord said to Eliphaz the Temanite: “My anger burns against you and against your two friends, for you have not spoken of me what is right, as my servant Job has. 8 Now therefore take seven bulls and seven rams and go to my servant Job and offer up a burnt offering for yourselves. And my servant Job shall pray for you, for I will accept his prayer not to deal with you according to your folly. For you have not spoken of me what is right, as my servant Job has*”.

*God is not happy when you don't speak the right words to those in pains. You misrepresent God to the hurting when you speak as though God is paying them back for their evil*.

*It is not everyone facing the vicissitudes of life that is reaping their evil and wickedness*.

*If you can't help the hurting to heal and find comfort, do not talk to them*.

*Sometimes, those who break your heart the most during life challenges are believers*. The moment some brethren see a child of God facing life's challenges, they switch over to the attitude of Job's friends.

They easily forget that no one is exempted from facing life's challenges.

Pastor Paul Ilo said, *"You're either on your way into a trial, in it or on your way out of it*".  That hurting brother/ sister needs succour not an accuser.

Often, we step into the ministry of Satan against our fellow brothers. We aid the condemnatory feelings and low self esteem people have during their trials instead of helping them out.

*If you want to be a good friend, help people to find inner healing for their pains and employ yourself as a personal intercessor*.

*PRAYER*: Father, I thank you for loving me. I receive grace to be a good friend in Jesus name.

DEVOTIONAL 0907 | Source start line: 88735
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
7/2/23, 12:11 PM - Lanre Oyeleke: ProjectSave Devotional
Sunday,
July 2nd, 2023.

*SPIRITUAL WARFARE REQUIRES SPIRITUAL INTELLIGENCE*

2 Cor 2:11 *So that Satan will not outsmart us. For we are familiar with his evil schemes*(NLT).

I have watched a few war films and read about real wars. I discovered that large armies may not win a war, sometimes it is not the army with the best ammunition that wins.

Every troop can only excel base on the level of war intelligence of the commanding officer.  In some of the war films I watched, I saw how smart soldiers under the command of an intelligent General subdued and decimated another army of soldiers at war.

War veterans know that tactics and intelligence is very vital at war, that's why they try to get enough information about their enemy before going to war. They don't learn about their enemy when an attack is launched.

They learn ahead so that they won't be caught unaware. In the game of football, a good coach would try to get tactical information about his opponent so that he can employ the right tactics.

In the opening text, apostle Paul made a bold statement that may not go well with some believers today. He said, *"For we are FAMILIAR with his evil schemes*". He was referring to the devil.

You can not be familiar with a thing or person if you have not studied or observed that thing closely. If I choose to preach a sermon about demonic operations and how they execute their plans, some may sneer at that message and ignore it because they may assume I'm glorifying the devil.

Such people may end up becoming casualty in spiritual warfare because they are not well-informed about the tactics of the enemy. Some even deny the operations of the enemy thinking it is an act of faith yet we see the havoc he is wrecking around us.

In warfare, Ignorance is costly and could be fatal regardless of the ammunitions in his arsenal. Brethren, we are on the battle field. The devil is a well-informed strategic tactician. He is our arch enemy(1Pet5:8),  not that woman or man in your village.

Thank God for the  victory Christ gave us and our redemptive rights. All we need to wage a good warfare is available but  if we are not familiar with the tactics of our enemy, we may be outsmarted.

You wonder why battles persist in the lives of believers who are praying night and day yet no result? There's something yet unknown about the tactics the enemy is using to fight them.

Kenneth E. Hagin of blessed memory said that sometimes, we need to trust God to give us a revelation so that we can deal with demons. Just paraphrasing.

If a battle has persisted for long and you've done all you know to do, start  researching on the tactics the enemy has employed to perpetuate evil in your life.

With the help of Holy Spirit, you may discover that it could be something as little as unforgiveness or unknown covenants  that opened the door for the devil and kept him there.

You need spiritual intelligence to overcome spiritual battles.

*PRAYER*: Father, may the devil not outsmart me, open my inner eyes to see the tactics of the enemy in Jesus name.

7/3/23, 5:50 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: PROJECTSAVE DEVOTIONAL
Monday,
July 3rd, 2023.

*HANDLING LIFE WHEN YOU DON'T HAVE A CLUE*

Psalms32:8 *I will instruct thee and teach thee in the way which thou shalt go: I will guide thee with mine own eyes*

I have been clueless severally. I can't forget the days I was seeking for scholarship to further my studies abroad.

I once wrote a Japanese scholarship exam to pursue a masters program. The exam was totally Japanese and I couldn't see any letter that resembles the English letter I was taught from nursery. If they had told us the language would be fully Japanese, I won't attempt it at all.

I was totally clueless despite the fact that I had options right in front of me but they were clueless options. Don't ask me what the result was. Ever since i've been sitting for exams in my life, that was the first I sat to write an exam whose instruction I couldn't read.

Sometimes in life, we are faced with certain situations where we are totally clueless and even a guess would mean error. It is not uncommon to find ourselves in situations where we don't know what to do.

*Trying to figure things out with your human mind when you're clueless is the quickest way to failure*.

*Never assume you know the way when you don't have the direction. Assumption is the lowest form of knowledge*.

The only reliable source at that time is the Holy Spirit. Jesus has promised us that He would teach us all things: *"But when the Father sends the Advocate as my representative—that is, the Holy Spirit—he will teach you everything and will remind you of everything I have told you*"( John 14:26).

Times of cluelessness will surely come, but the way out is by asking the person that knows the mind of God- the Holy Spirit.

He has promised us that He would show us the way, so key into Psalms 32:8 today.

*PRAYER*: Holy Spirit, I am on clueless on(...insert areas of life where direction is needed), speak to me and direct me in  Jesus Name.

DEVOTIONAL 0909 | Source start line: 88823

7/4/23, 6:30 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: ProjectSave Devotional
Tuesday
July 4th, 2023.

*DIETRICH BONHOEFFER'S DECISION*

John 9:4 *We must quickly carry out the tasks assigned us by the one who sent us. The night is coming, and then no one can work* (NLT).

A captivating story of Dietrich Bonhoeffer was told in "Our Daily Bread" daily devotional, November 10 1991.

At the height of World War II Dietrich Bonhoeffer was imprisoned for taking a stand against Hitler. Yet he continued to urge fellow believers to resist Nazi tyranny.

A group of Christians, believing that Hitler was the Antichrist, asked Bonhoeffer, “Why do you expose yourself to all this danger? Jesus will return any day, and all your work and suffering will be for nothing.”

Bonhoeffer replied, *“If Jesus returns tomorrow, then tomorrow I’ll rest from my labor. But today I have work to do. I must continue the struggle until it’s finished*”.

*There are a number of God's children with a heavenly mandate for their generation. Attached to their names are souls of men awaiting when they'll step out to rescue them*.

However, some persons in this category are foot-dragging and dilly-dallying. They have one excuse or the other why they are yet start out on what God has called them to do.

There is another group of persons who are doing the work God has called them to do but they aren't passionate about it. They are lazy, they see kingdom service as a second option or side job. The zeal for the work is lacking. They think they have all the time to do the work of God forgetting that every kingdom assignment is always within a specific time frame.

God intend to do great things through people in these categories but their attitude to kingdom has limited them.

We have a role model in Jesus and the apostles. In Corinthians 9:16, Paul said, *"Woe to me if I do not preach the gospel*!" Paul threw himself into the work and he stood out from among others.

*Dearest, do not be lazy about kingdom assignments. Don't use ministry as your retirement plan If God has called you*. The day time is work time. There'll always be a night time when no man work.

*Don't do at 60 what you ought to do at 30. Time is going, very soon, our time will be up and we'll all return to give account to our master.

We'll rest from our labours someday If we labour.  Apostle Paul enjoined us in Romans 12:11 saying, *"Not slothful in business; fervent in spirit; serving the Lord*;".

*PRAYER*: Father, I receive grace for fruitful labour in the kingdom in Jesus name.

DEVOTIONAL 0910 | Source start line: 89067

7/5/23, 5:40 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: ProjectSave Devotional
Wednesday
July 5th, 2023.

*BE HUNGRY FOR MORE*

Philippians 3:14 *I press on toward the goal to win the prize for which God has called me heavenward in Christ Jesus* ( NIV).


*Years back, I made a decision that my best in kingdom service, spiritual fervency and walk with God will never be in the past.*

This is simply because I have met men of yesterday whose exploits were in the past but nothing to show for it in the present.

It griefs my heart hearing some persons talk about when they use to evangelize, pray for the sick and get involved in the things of the kingdom.

Some have built a monument around certain experiences instead of moving on and desring more of God. What should be a movement has become a monument.

Peter and other disciples on the mount of transfiguration had this mentality. When they saw the glory of God, Peter said, *"...Lord, it is good for us to be here: if thou wilt, let us make here three tabernacles; one for thee, and one for Moses, and one for Elias*"(Mt 17:4).

Can you see that? Peter wanted to build a memorial, a monument. Jesus didn't honour that request. They could turn that spot to another shrine If Christ had allowed them.

While I'm an advocate of contentment in material things, when it comes to spiritual things, I am like Oliver Twist who always ask for more. Yesterday's experience with God is not enough.

*One reason amidst several others why we don't grow spiritually is because of complacency and satisfaction with our level of walk with God*.

There are people who were used by God and certain manifestations took place. They preached it over and over( though not bad) but in the present, we are not seeing anything.

*Once we start building monuments around the things of God, we'll start worshipping our past&previous achievements and stop desiring more of God*.

Apostle Paul was a rugged apostle who conquered many grounds for the Lord yet he knew he hadn't gotten to the height God desired of him.

At the time he said he was pressing on towards the mark of his high calling in Christ and leaving the past behind, he had already written nine epistles and he still wrote another four powerful epistle after that statement.

Paul could have built monuments around nine epistles and start celebrating them but he knew If he pressed deeper in his fellowship with God, he could receive more revelations.

Why should you be contented with winning just one soul when you can win millions? Why should you be contented with praying for one sick person when God can use you to empty hospitals? Why should you be contented with Pastoring 100 people when you can Pastor 1000 people?

Make a decision that you will never be a champion of yesterday. Be hungry for more.

*ACTION*: Being satisfied with your spiritual level will place a bar on your spiritual growth. To grow spiritually, don't be satisfied with yesterday, desire more of God.


info@projectsaveng.org

DEVOTIONAL 0911 | Source start line: 89128

7/6/23, 6:10 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: PROJECTSAVE DEVOTIONAL
Thursday,
July 6th, 2023.


*HUSBANDS, DON'T ABANDON YOUR PRIESTLY DUTIES*


Hebrews5:1 *For every high priest taken from among men is appointed on behalf of men in things pertaining to God, in order to offer both gifts and sacrifices for sins;*


Sometimes ago, I acted irrationally. On a certain night, when my wife and I retired to  bed I told her to pray. Often, after the general family prayers, we normally pray together alone before sleeping.


I lead  prayers often, let's say 99% of the time even during the general family devotion. On this terrible night, I told her to handle the family prayers but she refused.

I pushed the responsibility to her but she kept pushing it back to me. Funnily, there were times she led the prayers but on that terrible night, she refused. I became angry. Out of anger, I did not pray and we slept like that. I fumed.


The Holy Spirit rebuked me for not fulfilling my role and responsibility. Now, you wouldn't know how angry I was even when God's Spirit was rebuking me.


I felt I  have been doing all the teaching& praying in the house so it wouldn't be too much for her to pray that night.  Later, God made me realize that priests don't delegate their priestly duties.


It doesn't mean that women cannot pray for their families. In fact, most women do that more but it is the primary responsibility of  men to lead their families into God's presence because of their priestly role in the family.


If we call for prayer meetings in our churches, you can be so sure that more women would be in attendance than men. It shouldn't be.

A priest represent men to God. His duties include intercession, offering of sacrifices and teaching people the ways of the Lord. As a husband in the home, the afore mentioned are your responsibilities.


*God is not happy when men can't pray with and for their families. God is sad when fathers do not teach their families the ways of the Lord*.


Eli was a High priest in Shiloh, he performed his temple priestly duties well( to the best of our knowledge) but he failed in his family priestly duties. His failure in the latter affected his temple reputation.


*Men, step into your God-ordained priestly role and be the father God designed you to be*.


*PRAYER*: Father, I receive the grace to succeed in my family priestly duties In Jesus name.

*WHEN GOD'S SILENCE IS DANGEROUS*

1Sam28:6 *And when Saul enquired of the Lord, the Lord answered him not, neither by dreams, nor by Urim, nor by prophets*.

Pastor E.A.Adeboye told a story about a time in his life many years ago when he stopped hearing from God or we say God stopped speaking to him.

In his account, he felt miserable and helpless because he was disconnected from the direction of the Spirit.

What did he do? He despised another Pastor in his heart because he was called upon to interprete for him. That season of his life was miserable. He prayed to God and vowed never to repeat that mistake.

King Saul was chosen by God and he pioneered monarchy in Israel. When he disobeyed God, he no longer heard from God either through the Urim or prophets.

He ended up consulting a necromancer so that he can receive direction from Samuel. One of the dangerous seasons in the life of a Christian is that season when he is spiritually dull to hear from God.

The silence of God as regards receiving direction could portend danger. The man that no longer hear from God is in danger.

*If God has been speaking to you expressly or clearly on issues that pertains to your life before and you're no longer hearing from Him now, you're in a dangerous season of your life*.

The devil is ready to lead many into error and destruction when they no longer hear from God. *If you're dull spiritually, check your life to be very sure that you didn't unplug yourself from the source*.

Disobedience and carnality is the major source of disconnection. That is why Paul said: *For to be carnally minded is death but to be spiritually minded is life and peace.(Rom8:6)*.


*PRAYER*: Spirit of God plug me once again to your channel of direction, I repent of carnality In Jesus name.

DEVOTIONAL 0913 | Source start line: 89276
