7/8/23, 7:13 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: PROJECTSAVE DEVOTIONAL
Saturday,
July 8th, 2023.

*BEND ME, O GOD- THE PRAYER OF EVAN ROBERTS*

James4:3 *When you ask, you do not receive, because you ask with wrong motives, that you may spend what you get on your pleasures*(NIV)


Evan Roberts, a 26 years old Welsh was at the forefront of the historic revival in Wales. In six months, the revival in Wales caused 100,000 souls to come to the Lord.

However,  he often prayed, “Bend me, O God.” How does "bending" relate to revival? Why can't he pray for power and anointing? I may not be able to answer fully for him but one thing I know is that he was concerned about the well-being of his spiritual life so that God can make him a vessel for national revival.

*Do not forget, only revived men can bring revival to others.* A doctor that is fainting and almost collapsing cannot be called upon to resuscitate a patient that has fainted.

*Revived men prayed prayers that were life-transforming so that God can use them as agents of transformation*.

I have taken time to study about men that God used in revivals of yore, one notable aspect of their prayer life is their prayer point.

I have always known that God would not have used them the way we read about them If they had prayed the wrong prayers.

Sometime ago, I saw a derisive comment on prayers like: "Lord, break me and mould me". The brother said If he were God, he would break the person into pieces. I knew he lacked understanding about what the prayer meant.

You see, why God has not used us as we desire is because there's a resistance of the flesh. Something is still struggling with God.

In Galatians 5:17, Paul said, *"For the flesh desires what is contrary to the Spirit, and the Spirit what is contrary to the flesh. They are in conflict with each other, so that you are not to do whatever you want.*"

Don't tell me that you have not experienced this conflict since you gave your life to Christ. Men who desire revival always pray to overcome this conflict and they express it in several ways that's why Evan Roberts often prayed, "Bend me, O God."

*Until God prevails in you, He cannot prevail on men through you.*

*Men that desire revival are not using prayer as a tool to assassinate their enemies neither are they seeking earthly gains*.

The great Apostle Paul was an agent of revival and he was a revived man himself. Despite his depth and spiritual experience, he prayed so many kingdom prayers and one of such is: *"That I may know him, and the power of his resurrection, and the fellowship of his sufferings, being made conformable unto his death;*"( Philippians 3:10KJV).

What is your major prayer point?
Rev David Ogbueli says, *"We can know your age in the Spirit by your prayer points*".

*The content of your heart and your overriding desire will flow into your prayers. Enemy conscious people never cease to pray against enemies but kingdom conscious people pray kingdom-focused prayers*.

*PRAYER*: Father, I receive the grace to seek after you earnestly in the place of prayer. Not to seek for pecuniary gains but to seek your glory and to be overwhelmed by the mighty power of your presence in the name of Jesus.

DEVOTIONAL 0914 | Source start line: 89332
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
7/9/23, 7:31 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: ProjectSave Devotional
Sunday,
July 9th, 2023.

*WHEN REVIVED MEN PRAY*

James 4:3 *When you ask, you do not receive, because you ask with wrong motives, that you may spend what you get on your pleasures* (NIV).

John Knox was passionate  about the kingdom. He saw the need for the salvation of the lost and reformation among believers. As a revived man, he prayed, *"Lord, give me Scotland or I die*".

The Lord answered that prayer. One man put a whole nation in his pocket through prayers. It is on record that the Queen of Scots said, "I fear the prayer of John Knox more than all the assembled armies of Europe".

Another man in church history was John Hyde. He prayed to the extent that he was nicknamed "Praying Hyde". He went to India on missions and he was used by God in Shrewsbury revival. He was passionate about souls and he prayed, *"Give me souls or I die*".

These men could have asked for palatial mansions and the best cars money could afford in their time. However, the fire of revival that was burning in their soul pointed their attention to what was germane in the heart of God - Souls.

*Revived men pray to take land and capture hearts of men for the Lord.* John Wesley was used by God to spread revival fire around Britain and his ministry sent missionaries around the world. He was known to spend the first two hours of each day in his prayer room with a bible wide opened in his front. That room was called the power house of Methodism.

Over hundred years after his death, someone wanted to buy liquor in a town  he once preached. He couldn't find liquor to buy. He asked why the shops weren't selling liquor. He was told that a man named John Wesley preached in that town and since then, no one sold liquor in that town.

*We can pray about so many things but the prayers that touch the heavens and get their attention are prayers that reflect the agenda of heaven on humanity*. God is concerned about seeing souls saved, get baptized, healed and established in the faith than anything else.

If a revived man sees abnormal trends in the society or the church, he'll raise a prayer altar first before any other thing. When you see prostitutes and fraudsters around you, what's your reaction? Do you pity them or you pray for them?

*When you hear about unreached people groups that have not heard about Christ most of whom you may never meet in your lifetime,  do you ever think of praying for these people.*? These are the prayer concerns of revived men not "sudden breakthroughs".

If a Pastor is revived, he will lead his church to pray kingdom prayers. If an Evangelist is revived, he would point the body of Christ to what matters to God-Souls, and lead the church to win them whilst not forgetting the place of prayer.

One revived man the world will not forget is Nicklaus Zinzendorf. He committed the Moravians to a 24-hour prayer chain. The Moravians prayed for 24 hours for about 100 years non stop.  They prayed the will of heaven down on earth, sent out missionaries and through their prayers, souls came to the Lord.

I am guilty of laxity in this as well and I've been trusting God to give me a heart that loves what he loves more so that my prayers can align with His will. My heart is focused on these things and it is a growing burden in my heart.

*A generation prayed to see the glory of God on earth and they saw it but my generation is fervently praying for the world to see their glory yet no revival. What a pity!*

Until you're revived, your prayer points would only focus on what satisfies self. God is looking for men that will pray down His glory.

*PRAYER*: Father, let your holy fire burn in me.

*REKINDLE THE FIRE OF MY FIRST LOVE*

Revelation 2:4-5 *Yet I hold this against you: You have forsaken the love you had at first. Consider how far you have fallen! Repent and do the things you did at first. If you do not repent, I will come to you and remove your lampstand from its place* (NIV).

Tuesday
July 11th, 2023.

*DON'T EXPLORE ALTERNATIVES- WAIT*

Psalms 40:1 *I waited patiently for the LORD; he turned to me and heard my cry*.

In the early 90s, I spent part of my schooling years in my home town, Ileogbo- Osun state. We relocated from Lagos because dad left Lagos to pastor in our home town on transfer at that time.

Living in the village was fun but not so pleasant because mummy was not living with us. She stayed in Lagos to monitor her business.  Those days, mobile telecommunications was not existing so we had no means of reaching her.

However, dad had a way of knowing when she would come visiting. Any time we heard that mum was coming, our expectations were always high and we would become impatient in our waiting all of a sudden.

If we hear the sound of a vehicle, we may rush out to check if it was mum. Sometimes, we were disappointed.

I cannot forget when we were told that she put to bed and that she would be bringing the baby to the village for christening. My younger sister and I left the school before closing because we were impatient. We devised a scheme so they released us before closing.

What God has promised to do He would do, you need to cultivate the Christian habit of waiting patiently on God.

*To wait patiently on God is to stay expectant without using human means and method to find a way out*.

Some became tired of waiting  patiently on God and they 'helped' themselves with alternatives. *In God, there are no alternatives*.

The Psalmist knew this and that was why his gaze was fixed on the LORD only. *You cannot be waiting on God and be looking at man at thesame time, it is impossible*.

The Psalmist made it clear that after he waited  patiently on God, God heard him. God sees all you're going through but his word to you this season is that you should wait patiently.

Your waiting time is not a wasted time. God is working out somethings in you and for you during the waiting period.

There is a timing for the fulfilment of your expectation and you can be sure that when God comes through for you, He'll give the best.

*CONFESSION*: I refuse to be impatient in my waiting season. I receive the grace to wait in love, faith and patience until i see result In Jesus name.

info@projectsaveng.org

*THE SUPERNATURAL DOESN'T HAVE TO BE SPECTACULAR*

2Kings5:10 *But Elisha sent a messenger out to him with this message: “Go and wash yourself seven times in the Jordan River. Then your skin will be restored, and you will be healed of your leprosy.”11 But Naaman became angry and stalked away. “I thought he would certainly come out to meet me!” he said. “I expected him to wave his hand over the leprosy and call on the name of the LORD his God and heal me!*( NLT)

Heard about a man who traveled a long distance to see a man of God for prayers. His expectations were high thinking something spectacular will happen.

When he met the man of God, a simple prayer was said and the man of God discharged him to go. He was shocked that all the man did was just to say a simple prayer and let him go.

That's akin to what transpired between Naaman and Elisha. The former expected 'prophetic vibrations and gymnastics'. He was shocked to hear that all he needed to do was to go bath in Jordan.

Naaman could have died of leprosy If not for the intervention of his maid. He acted by faith and got his healing.

*The supernatural acts of God can come in ways that defy your spectacular expectation*.

There are people that expect something extra after a simple prayer of faith, they don't believe that miracles and healings can happen without 'special effect' like falling down and rolling on the floor.

I have prayed for people who expected me to tell them 'thus saith the Lord' but most of them didn't get any answer from me or from the Lord because God wasn't moving that way at that time.

*In most cases, our spectacular expectations robs us of the supernatural*.

There are preachers that don't believe healing or deliverance can happen except someone falls under the anointing. That's why some push people down while laying hands.

While standing, God can heal and deliver just as He would when someone falls under the anointing. *You don't dictate or determine the path of the Spirit and the supernatural, you can only align yourself to it and see God move the way He chooses*.

*As a preacher, the moment Satan knows you have spectacular expectations, he can arrange one for you*. Not all fallings and screamings during power services are by the Spirit.


Demons also speak their tongues. It takes discernment to know the spirt at work when people are manifesting because Satan mimic the move of the Spirit sometimes.

Your duty is to plug into the divine by faith. Leave God to determine how He want to move. Let him decide to manifest in thunder, fire, storm or as a gentle flowing but powerful breeze. Faith is your duty not the  spectacular.

*Don't miss the supernatural because of your desire for the spectacular*.

*PRAYER:* Father, I align myself to your move In Jesus name.

*WINNING BATTLES THROUGH OBEDIENCE TO INSTRUCTION*


Matthew 2:13 *When they had gone, an angel of the Lord appeared to Joseph in a dream. “Get up,” he said, “take the child and his mother and escape to Egypt. Stay there until I tell you, for Herod is going to search for the child to kill him*”(NIV).


I love to teach this subject using the events surrounding the birth of Jesus.


When Jesus was born, his life was under threat because of Herod's evil decree. An angel of God instructed his foster father in a dream to escape with the baby and mother to Egypt.


He gave further instruction that they should not exit until he received another instruction. That's something to note about God and how he leads.


You may be wondering why the omnipotent God would tell Joseph to run with baby Jesus because of Herod. Could it be that God couldn't protect Jesus( the Son of God) from Herod? Of course not.


God could have killed Herod and his soldiers in a millisecond but He chose not to do that. Rather, he gave instruction that they should escape. I love Joseph, he didn't fast and pray neither did he go to the mountain. He simply obeyed.


*Where most believers failed in their quest for victory over life's battles is that they failed to hear God on the way out of their battles while some that heard God felt the instruction they received from God doesn't make sense*.


*The only tool of spiritual warfare some people know is mountain fasting&prayer whereas listening to God for instruction and obeying Him could deliver them from so much troubles*.


In warfare, the direction of the commanding officer is more important than guns&bombs. It is possible to die in fasting&prayer while  problem persist.

Some have foolishly fallen into the hands of the devil because they didn't seek God for direction.


Dearest, when God speaks to you on the way out of your life's battles, that is not the time go to the prayer mountain, just obey.


*Obedience to divine instruction is a potent warfare strategy that helps in winning the battles of life*. Start asking God for the way out.


*PRAYER:* Father, I receive the grace to hear you clearly on the next line of action.

*HE PREPARED ME FOR THE DAY*

Isaiah50:4 *The Lord God hath given Me the tongue of the learned, that I should know how to speak a word in season to him that is weary. He wakeneth morning by morning; He wakeneth Mine ear to hear as the learned*.

Sometimes ago In the seminary, I was in the bathroom preparing for class when the Holy Spirit warned me through an inward witness that I should be careful of getting offended that day. It was so clear I knew it was the Spirit of God speaking to my heart.

I took it calmly and was prepared  for whatever may come my way that day but I didn't know the form and manner in which the offense will come.

As I stepped into the class, a revision was going on. The person handling the revision took permission to leave so that i'll take over. As I got to the front of the class, my class governor demanded that I should tender an apology for a promise I didn't fulfill.

The man who made the promise told everyone that he was the one who made a  promise not me yet the class leader caused a scene and accused me wrongly in front of everybody. I hate to be accused wrongly. I noticed I can't stomach wrong accusation and can get out of my way to prove my innocence.

I almost fumbled by flaring up in defense but the Holy Spirit prepared me ahead for the day in the bathroom. As soon as I remembered what the Spirit told me earlier in the morning,  I tendered an apology and I continued the revision session.

There was a great calm in the class. No one expected me to stomach that toxic and shameful accusation without putting up a strong defense but I put Satan to shame by apologizing for a wrong I did not commit.

Everyone knew I was right because the student who made a  promise openly cleared me yet my class governor insisted that I tender an apology. Shame on Satan. Recently, I learnt that trying to defend every accusation could be a sign of pride. There's a time to talk and a time to be silent.

*What you do in the morning when you wake up matters alot. You can get insight about the rest of the day in the morning as you wait on God*.

*The morning season is not just a time to ask for what you want but a time to receive from the Lord what you should do If you encounter Satanic tricks in the day.* Are you prepared for today?

*Some temptations believers fall into could be avoided If they had listened to the Holy Spirit ahead of time. In the place of sweet fellowship, the Spirit of God want to tell us what lies ahead, what to avoid and how to handle the unavoidable.*

Another vital thing I want you to note in my story is the place where the Holy Spirit spoke to my heart- the bathroom. In fact, I've said it several times that I often receive/ prepare some powerful messages I preach in the toilet while doing the thing.

*Your kitchen can be filled with the cloud of glory as you fellowship with God. While at work, your office space can be filled with the glory of His presence. Now, you need to understand that your fellowship with God shouldn't be a part-time thing but a full time activity which can take place anywhere and at anytime*.

Some persons only have a place and time where they spend quality time with God. While that's good but it is not enough. We are meant to cultivate the presence of God anywhere and at anytime as the Spirit of God enables.

I read one of Kathryn Khulman's post where she said she doesn't want to give the Holy Spirit one hour so that the manifestations in her ministry won't be attributed to her one hour meeting with God.

She decided that her fellowship with the Spirit would be a continuous thing. This habit we must cultivate consciously. As you drive, cook, wash, walk, fellowship with God. That way, you'll be sensitive at all times.

*PRAYER*: Father, i receive the grace to fellowship with you at all times. Holy Spirit, I want to be closer too you In Jesus name. Amen

*THE LITTLE I KNOW ABOUT MARRIAGE* Pt.1

Genesis 2:18  *The Lord God said, “It is not good for the man to be alone. I will make a helper suitable for him*” (NIV).

*MARRIAGE IS GOD'S IDEA*

The greatest institution under attack in the world today is marriage. I stand to be corrected on this. Satan is trying to make marriage look unappealing and frightening especially to singles.

Many singles are afraid to marry seeing what has become of many marriages including that of pastors. The fear is real.

Marriage has drawn the attention of all within and outside the church. As a preacher, I noticed that I get more responses when I preach on marriage than other subjects of the bible.

*Most people enter into marriage with ideas that are alien to the bible because they don't know who instituted marriage and why He instituted it*. This is the primary reason for the several marital failures we see today.

Marriage was not instituted by your village chiefs nor your government. *Marriage is a sacred and divine institution founded by God*.

*The origin and originator of a thing is important If we are to have a good grasp of that thing. The founder of every institution has a vision and purpose. Every manufacturer who manufactured a product has a manual of operation for his product. Same applies to marriage*.


*If we must succeed in marriage, there's a need to see marriage from God's perspective or else we'll fail.  Most marriages are troubled and failing because there's a deviation from the laid down pattern*.

*The best marriage manual still remains the bible. That's where we can find God's pattern for a successful marriage*.

Today, most people want to hear messages like: ' Seven Keys To  Marital Bliss'. They want quick fixes but not the bible. On the pages of the scriptures, you'll see God's pattern for successful marriage.

When I wanted to marry, I read alot about marriage and the strange woman from the book of Proverbs. I'm yet to see a book on marriage that gave  explicit practical wisdom on how to handle the strange woman and marriage like Proverbs and other books of the bible.

*It is impossible to have marital crisis If two persons in marriage are following the biblical pattern laid by God for marriage. Absolutely impossible*.

When I see believers in Christ having marital crisis and breakdown, it is plausible one or the two persons involved are flouting biblical injunction on marriage.

*Believers need thorough Christian discipleship than marriage seminars. Marriages of true disciples don't fail*.

Failure to follow God's manual for life and marriage will lead to serious marital breakdown. Don't run your marriage according to the dictates of the society, public opinion or your culture, get into the manual- the bible.

PRAYER: Father, i shall not fail maritally In Jesus name.

*THE LITTLE I KNOW ABOUT MARRIAGE* Pt.2

Genesis2:18 *The LORD God said, “It is not good for the man to be alone. I will make a helper suitable for him* (NIV).

*MARRIAGE IS FOR A PURPOSE *

Myles Munroe said that when the purpose of a thing is not known, abuse is inevitable. There are couples that entered into marriage without knowing the purpose for which marriage was instituted by God.

They have abused marriage as an institution and they've been abused as well. There is a general purpose for marriage. During wedding ceremonies, you'll hear the Pastor read out some of the purposes for marriage which include: companionship, to avoid immorality&procreation.

However, there's a more specific purpose for which marriage was created which differ from one person to another. Every one on the surface of the earth came to this world for a purpose.

*Your purpose is your God-ordained assignment*. It is the reason you're alive, your purpose is the reason you've gone through all the dealings( sweet or bitter). Your purpose is the impact God has designed you to make.

When the time to marry comes, God's grand design is to lead you to marry someone whose who will allow you fulfill your assignment here on earth not someone who will destroy it.

*Spouse discovery is easy once purpose is discovered*. There's nothing as sweet as having a spouse who support and catalyses your journey in life borne out of the understanding of God's purpose for your life.

*When we pray for a spouse, what God gives is a purpose mate not a bedmate*. If there's anything God guard jealously in our lives, it is our assignment here on earth. *God will not lead you to marry a man or woman who will truncate His assignment in your life*.

As a child of God, you can't marry indiscriminately. God's kingdom want to profit from our lives. He wouldn't allow his children to marry anyone that would destroy his investment.

If that relationship is not purpose-driven, it might be a waste of precious time.

*ACTION POINT*:  If you're married, enquire from God to know whether you are fulfilling His specific purpose. If you're single, settle with God what your life's assignment is.

*THE LITTLE I KNOW ABOUT MARRIAGE* Pt.3

1Cor7:27 *If you have a wife, do not seek to end the marriage...*

*DIVORCE IS ANTI GOD*

The issue of divorce cannot be overlooked in our world even in the church. It is growing daily and almost looking like a norm. While there are several excuses people give for this, it should be noted that God never intended divorce. It is not in his plan.

One of the worst disaster the world is experiencing is not earthquake neither is it tsunami. It is the tsunami of divorce. Globally, it has assumed a new dimension.

Some 2.2 million marriages and almost 1 million divorces took place in the EU in 2016 according to a report. We may not have the divorce statistics of Africa and other continents but we know it is happening around us.

The day I read about a woman hosting divorce party in the news, I knew Satan was ready to stop at nothing until marriages crash. People now wear it around like a badge.

Some singles are afraid to marry because of what they've seen in the marriages of those they look up to. Moreso, some are entering into marriage with this idea of: 'If it doesn't work out, i'll walk out'. That's an anti-bible mentality. Those who entered into marriage that way eventually walked out

*In plain and simple language, divorce is anti God. It is a deviation from the original plan of God for marriage*. Even Jesus made it clear to the Pharisees that divorce wasn't God's original plan from the beginning(Matthew19:4).

The negative impact of divorce can be generational If God doesn't intervene. I have seen this in my little stay here on earth. A research shows that most children raised in broken homes tend not to value the unity of marriage and are likely to repeat same in their marital life.

Divorce may be right in civil law but in God's law, it is totally unacceptable. The kind of trauma and pain children from divorced parents go through is such that may not heal in a life time.

*Singles, do not rush into a marriage where you'll be looking for the exit route. Be patient with God. It is easier to make the right decision than it is to want to opt out of a toxic marriage. Even after opting out, the scar remain for long*.

*For yourself, develop virtues that can make marriage work and marry someone with similar values. Clash of values is what break homes while compatibility of values is what sustains it. This is the wisdom you need*.

Are you considering a divorce? Be patient with God to resolve all issues threatening the peace of your home. There is no problem without a solution. Seek counsel and let the voice of reasoning and wisdom prevail.

*PRAYER*: Father, my home shall not break In Jesus name.

**THE LITTLE I KNOW ABOUT MARRIAGE*  Pt.4

1Cor6:9-10 *Or do you not know that the unrighteous will not inherit the kingdom of God? Do not be deceived: neither the sexually immoral,... nor men who practice homosexuality,... nor swindlers will inherit the kingdom of God*.

*MARRIAGE IS BETWEEN ADAM AND EVE NOT ADAM AND STEVE*

Sometime ago, while discussing with a brother, he sounded a little bit apologetic towards Lesbian Gay Bisexual Transgender Queer Intersex Asexual rights.

He said  there is an argument that most homosexuals are created by God to have such feelings so it is their natural frame. Quickly, I refuted that claim. If God created anyone that way, He wouldn't have condemned it.

The bible condemn same-sex marriage in strongest terms. In fact, those involved are termed "reprobates": *"And even as they did not like to retain God in their knowledge, God gave them over to a reprobate mind, to do those things which are not convenient*"(Romans 1:28 KJV).

The bible says that homosexual frame and practice is  unnatural and it is a perversion: *"For this cause God gave them up unto vile affections: for even their women did change the natural use into that which is against nature: and likewise also the men, leaving the natural use of the woman, burned in their lust one toward another; men with men working that which is unseemly, and receiving in themselves that recompence of their error which was meet*" (Romans 1:26-27 KJV)

*The truth of the bible is the truth not what someone feels or says*. An act of perversion is a deviation from the norm.

Right from the old testament, God frowned at the practice of homosexuality and same sex marriage.

In fact, it was an abomination before God and it still remain so: *"Do not practice homosexuality, having sex with another man as with a woman. It is a detestable sin*"(Leviticus 18:22 NLT)

Going further into the new testament, Jude 7 expressly told us that one of the reasons why Sodom and Gomorrha was destroyed was because of this issue. If that generation did not receive the endorsement of God for same-sex marriage but a fierce judgement, do you think ours would be exempted?

God created marriage to be heterosexual not homosexual. It   must be between a man and woman. There's a high manipulation of scriptures going on among same-sex marriage apologists.

They've twisted scriptures to support their perversion. When God wanted to give Adam a help mate, he would have given him 'Steve'( a male) rather he gave him 'Eve'( a female). No matter how homosexuals try to paint their evil with rainbow colours, I stand with the bible on the matter.

Liberals are saying we should promote a society where people are free to do as they like( same-sex marriage inclusive) as long as it doesn't hurt.

Hey! The bible says, *"who knowing the judgment of God, that they which commit such things are worthy of death, not only do the same, but have pleasure in them that do them*"(Romans 1:32 KJV).

*ACTION POINT*: If you're having erotic feelings towards persons of the same sex, it is unnatural. You need to renew your mind with the word of God. Go for deliverance& counseling. If you need help, contact us:

DEVOTIONAL 0924 | Source start line: 89970
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
7/19/23, 7:16 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: ProjectSave Devotional
Wednesday
July 19th, 2023.

*THE LITTLE I KNOW ABOUT MARRIAGE*  Pt.5

1 Pet3:7 *In the same way, you husbands, live with your wives in an understanding way [with great gentleness and tact, and with an intelligent regard for the marriage relationship]*.... AMP

*LOVE ATTRACTS BUT UNDERSTANDING SUSTAINS MARRIAGE*

The media is awash with stories of divorce cases. Some think it is only celebrity marriages alone that crash but some soft sells report similar cases among ordinary people.

These are people who once professed undying love for one another cutting ties. I have asked severally what went wrong in most of those marriages.

As an aside, I have observed another strange trend among some divorcees. When asked If they love their estranged spouses, quite a number claimed they still loved their exes  but they can't live together because of irreconciliable differences.

I know you've heard that statement too. If a man is professing to still love a woman he has divorced, what could be wrong?

My conclusion was that lack of understanding contributed to the failure of their marriage not lack of love. *Love can attract but understanding sustains*.

We have seen love birds who professed undying love for each other but ended their marriage bitterly. When you talk to divorcees, they may speak bitterly about their estranged spouses but deep within, the affection is still there.

Misunderstanding in marriage results from 'missing understanding' about your partner. When understanding is missing, love evaporates. Once proper understanding is in place, the marriage remains and love is sustained.

As a husband, you need  to study your wife so that you can understand her.Same applies to wives. *You love your spouse the way they want to be loved not your own way*.

The way Mr 'A' is loving and living with his wife may not work for you because Mrs 'A' is different from your wife. She has a different love language. That's what Gary Chapman taught in his book: Love Languages. The love of everyone has a unique language that must understood.

*As you love, seek to understand*. Don't complain that your wife talks too much. Generally women are wired to talk more than men. Just be a good listener. They need attention and may react when they're not getting enough of it.

There are intrinsic need of both genders in marriage that must be understood for love to flourish. Men want respect, reverence and sex while women want affection, attention and romance. Understanding these basic needs about your spouse and how to meet them will prosper your love life.

What is love without properly understanding what keeps love alive in your spouse?

Don't complain about your husband's love for sport, be part of his recreational activities. If he's a Chelsea fan, be an Arsenal fan. Watching football together can be romantic. Change the things you can change but don't be mad about those things you can't change.

*PRAYER*: Father, I receive the grace to love my spouse with understanding In Jesus name.

DEVOTIONAL 0925 | Source start line: 90028

7/20/23, 6:29 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: PROJECTSAVE DEVOTIONAL
Thursday,
July 20th, 2023.


*THE LITTLE I KNOW ABOUT MARRIAGE*  Pt.6

Amos3:3 *Can two walk together, except they be agreed?* KJV


*BEWARE OF UNREALISTIC MARITAL EXPECTATIONS*


It is unhealthy to enter marriage with unrealistic expectations. One funny but unhealthy expectation some brothers had before entering into marriage was that sex will be daily. Funny, isn't it? Unfortunately, they were disappointed and many with such unrealistic expectation will still be disappointed.


*While sex is basic in marriage and couples are encouraged not  to deny one another, yet it is unrealistic to think it can be done daily. If there is a man who have sex daily, it is a pointer to joblessness or a sexual dysfunction*.


The way and manner some preachers and marriage counselors present marriage to some is the reason some are in serious marital crisis today.


The idea of marriage some ladies have is akin to what they read in romantic novels. *Some characters only exist on paper not in reality. You don't build marriage on ideaologies from fictional work. Marriage is for real people*.


If you think the occasional disagreements you experience with your spouse is abnormal because you heard statements like: 'I have never had any disagreement with my spouse since we married', then you're simple hearted. You can believe monkeys drive cars and pilot planes.


*There is a difference between marital crisis and petty disagreements that are occasional. It is possible to have a crisis-free marriage but marriage without disagreement on issues at least once in a lifetime is impossible*.


The reason for this is because no two persons in marriage have exactly the same perspective on all issues. We handle our differences with maturity and grow into loving one another despite our different opinions.


In fact, love is strengthened as we learn to handle our differences with Christian maturity. Marriage is one institution I have come to know as the place where the state and level of your walk with God will be revealed to you.


If you're impatient, proud, intolerant, insensitive,etc marriage will reveal all these to you. All these will make a good believer go back to God for refining of character. We are projects in progress in God's hands.


*If you enter into marriage with unrealistic assumptions, you'll feel disappointed when certain realities hit you. That doesn't mean you should be expecting the worse but what I'm saying is that you should be realistic with your expectation as much as possible*.


*You did not marry an angel. Even If you do, because you're human, you'll need to learn how to cope with an angel in marriage because they have wings but you don't. Can you live with a winged being perfectly*?
*Listen, there's no perfect marriage here on earth but there can only be an ideal marriage*.


*The perfect marriage will be when Christ( our groom) comes for the church( his bride) but now, He is still washing us daily with the water of the word. In the glorious bye and bye, we'll be exactly like him*.


A marriage where Christ is the head through the man, where the wife is submissive with godly children is the ideal marriage we can have on this side of eternity.


Conflict of ideas and opinion may arise in marriage but it doesn't need to degenerate into crisis. *Disagreeing without being disagreeable is what makes marriage work*.


*ACTION POINT*: Check those unrealistic expectations and dump them where they belong- planet utopia. As couples, work towards agreeing on major areas of difference maturedly.

*THE LITTLE I KNOW ABOUT MARRIAGE* Pt.7

Gal 6:7 *Be not deceived; God is not mocked: for whatsoever a man soweth, that shall he also reap*( KJV).

*LOVE IS A LIVING THING- FEED IT*

Telling you love is a living thing that requires feeding is probably one of the funniest thing you might be hearing from me. Isn't it?

I heard same from a marriage counselor, Pastor Mrs Oshofowora and her explanation made much sense to me. The reality of the truth she shared dawned on me.

Imagine having a child that is malnourished and starved. The outlook, strength and health is predictable- horrible. Everyone is as healthy as what they eat. In these days of heightened nutritional awareness, we now know the importance of proper diet.

Same applies in marriage. *Figuratively, love is a living thing. It can grow, flourish and be healthy If properly fed. On the other hand, love can be weak, poor, unhealthy and eventually die when starved*.

*True marital wisdom is knowing the right love diet of your spouse and feeding him/her the love diet*. That's all. Your love diet may be different from that of your spouse. Couples in marriage must know what to feed their love for their marriage to remain healthy.

*You don't love your spouse the way you think he/she should be loved. You love based on how your spouse want to be loved. The onus of discovery lies on you both*.

This truth is what Gary Chapman taught in his famous book: Five Love Languages. Though, I believe love languages could be more than five because of individual differences and unique preferences.

You see, this truth and others i've taught so far is the secret behind marriages that are working. *A flourishing marriage is worked out systematically not wished. It requires feeding love with the appropriate diet for it to remain strong*.

*Principles and wisdom makes marriage work not miracles*. If miracles make marriages work, prayer warriors will have the best marriages in the world because they'll pray down miracles. There won't be divorce and separation among believers. Unfortunately, marriages don't work like this.

The love between couples cannot flourish beyond what they feed it. *You cannot get from your marriage what you have not invested into it. Simple!*

*CONFESSION:* Father, I receive the grace to feed my spouse love with the right diet in Jesus name. My home is built on godly principles and my marriage is working by the power of the Spirit In Jesus name.

*THE LITTLE I KNOW ABOUT MARRIAGE* Pt.8

Malachi 2:15  *Didn’t the LORD make you one with your wife? In body and spirit you are his. And what does he want? Godly children from your union. So guard your heart; remain loyal to the wife of your youth.*” (NLT).

*YOUR MARRIAGE IS A TEMPLATE FOR YOUR CHILDREN'S MARRIAGE*

Sometimes ago, I read a man online supporting domestic violence against women. He sounded weird to people because in a world where everyone is trying to protect women from physical assault, he thought otherwise.

I took my time to discern the rationale behind his ideology then I found out that his reference point was his parent's marriage. He claimed that his father beat his mother to correct her. Now, he's a grown man and he doesn't see anything wrong with that.

*Let's thank God for the several marriage seminars and conferences for singles&married but I think we need quality and consistent trainings for parents as well because how parents live their lives as couples will most likely become a pattern their children will follow in marriage*.

*A poorly parented male will likely become a bad husband likewise females. Some of the challenges or problems marriage counselors are trying to fix in marriages stems from the parental foundation of either in the marriage*.

I have seen well trained ladies who got married to husbands that were poorly raised. How did I know? When a father supports his son's stupidity, then you'll know he took after him.

While this may sound 'unspiritual' to some but I think one thing singles need to watch before saying 'yes' to anyone is the quality of parenting the person has received. This is my personal opinion.

What about If the person was raised in a dysfunctional home, does that mean singles should run from such persons? In cases like this, what the person need to check prayerfully is If the person has had a change of values different from where he/she is coming from.

If there's a change of value-system and Christ has worked on his/her heart, a 'Yes' is in order If the person is God's will. I hope I have cleared this.

*If parents can become good models for their children, we'll have less marital crisis in our society because people reproduce after their kind*.

*A father who is not a godly father- figure to his sons will likely reproduce himself in his sons. A nagging mother will likely reproduce herself in her daughters*.

Every marriage has a semblance of the family where those involved are coming from.

A woman once told my mum that the reason she causes trouble in her child's P.T.A.meetings is because her father did same when she was in school. She grew up thinking a P.T.A.meeting is a battle ground.

A research once conducted showed that children from divorced homes are likely to experience same in future because they have grown not to value the unity of marriage. What kind of marriage do you want your children to have?

*Run your marriage well and your children will use your marriage as a template for theirs. While theirs may not be exactly like yours, they'll build on it and live a better life*.

*Dear married couples, your home is the first marriage seminar and conference your children will ever attend in the first 20-25 years of their lives. They are watching how you're running your lives as couples. No other marriage seminar or conference will be that long. Please, run your home well.*

I don't know much about marriage but these are the little things I know about marriage. Hope this series blessed you?

*ACTION*: Fathers, show true love to your wives and let your sons see you do it. Indirectly, you're teaching them how to love their future wives. Wives, be a model of Christian submission, your daughters will pick that virtue from you. *Children are the greatest imitators*.

DEVOTIONAL 0928 | Source start line: 90366

7/23/23, 7:13 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: ProjectSave Devotional
Sunday,
July 23rd, 2023.

*REFINED IN THE FURNACE OF TRIALS*

1Pet4:12 *Dear friends, don’t be surprised at the fiery trials you are going through, as if something strange were happening to you.(13) Instead, be very glad—for these trials make you partners with Christ in his suffering, so that you will have the wonderful joy of seeing his glory when it is revealed to all the world*(NLT).

The subject of suffering, trials or challenges as we may choose to call it remains controversial among charismatics today. So controversial!

Several extremes abound on this subject. Some believe in perpetual suffering, that's an extreme. Another school of thought believe a believer should not go through life's challenges at all.

In fact, I heard a preacher who asserted that believers should not go through challenges and If they do, it is their fault. Bad theology there!

Should we say everyone going through a challenge in life has defaulted in one way or the other? If yes, someone has to explain clearly what the fault of Abraham was when he experienced delay in childbearing. I need to know what Jesus did that made him suffer like a criminal on the cross.

In fact, If we share this sentiment, then we can conclude that all the apostles of Jesus who suffered and were martyred( except John the beloved) were at fault. Can you see the weakness of that argument?

Recently, I read the interview of a preacher. When they asked him If he has experienced spiritual attack, he said "No". He said it is only insensitive and unprepared pastors that get attacked. He sounded cocky to me about not having experienced such before. That's not true in my opinion about him.

This is someone who once shared his marital challenges in the public. He told us  how his wife gave him serious problems that threatened his peace as a minister until God intervened. If that is not a spiritual attack, what is it?  To me, what he described was a spiritual attack on his marriage to discredit his ministry yet he didn't know life's challenges could come in that form.

*I love real people. I am not so happy when we preachers present ourselves as being above the experiences of humanity. Very anointed men go through challenges as well. It doesn't mean they have sinned or have become weak*.

Paul shared his challenges with the Corinthian church. Christ, the anointed one experienced the cross. We just have to be humble before God in matters like this. *No one prays for challenges to come yet at one time or the other in life, we face it including those denying it*.

*That you're going through real stuffs, I mean hard times, difficulties, challenges or suffering as we may choose to call it doesn't mean you defaulted somewhere. While I do believe certain problems are man-made yet I know some are not*.

In John 16:33, Christ himself said, *"I have told you all this so that you may have peace in me. Here on earth YOU WILL have many trials and sorrows. But take heart, because I have overcome the world*"( NLT)

*Living the faith life is not a guarantee of a problem-free life. Rather, it is a guarantee of victory over every of life's challenges*. To me, this is a balanced scriptural perspective.

*One of the things that trials do to us is that it refines us. We all have rough edges that God need to smoothen. While in the furnace of trials, God want to use that medium to separate the wheat from the chaff in your life and teach you patience*.

*While in the furnace of trials, God want to build a solid relationship with you in the secret place. While in that same furnace of trials, God want you to know who your real friends are*.

If you can approach your challenges in life from the standpoint of seeing the purpose it is intended for, you will come out quickly. *We cannot enter into destiny unrefined. The furnace of trial is our school of preparation before we emerge on the scene*.

Moses couldn't enter into God's plan for his life not until he was schooled in the university of wilderness. He came out of the wilderness a better leader. I see you coming out of your season of trials very soon in Jesus name.

*CONFESSION*: I walk in victory over life's challenges In the mighty name of Jesus Christ. Amen

*WHY YOU NEED A GENUINE PASTOR* Pt.1

Hebrews13:17 Obey your spiritual leaders, and do what they say. Their work is to *watch over your souls*, and they are accountable to God. Give them reason to do this with joy and not with sorrow. That would certainly not be for your benefit(NLT).

One of Satan's ploy this endtime is to undermine the ministry of genuine pastors in the lives of believers now that artificial intelligence is gaining prominence. Was surprised seeing a church where AI preacher preached.

*Don't be deceived to think AI can give you quality pastoring. God didn't design pastoring to be done remotely or through an artificial  person. Humans put into the office by Christ are to play that role*.

It is common to see people ask for counsel on issues on social media. A grown man( though immature) would bring his family issues to the media and ask for counsel from believer and unbelievers and you'll see divergent and demonic views that are unbiblical.

Some singles ask for counsel on social media groups/ forums on how to choose a mate and how to handle their relationship where there's a mix of believers and unbelievers. What kind of counsel do you think they'll get?

I see this trend as an abuse of social media and a ploy of Satan to downplay the office of the Pastor. *Social media is not designed to pastor you even If you stream services online. Being under a Pastor is not only in listening to his messages online. Other things are involved most of which cannot be done online*.

*In the days when you need counsel, your Pastor is your go to person. He is to nurture you. You stand to lose so many things If you don't bring your life under the umbrella of a sound, genuine and faithful pastor. You'll be tossed to and fro by every wind of doctrine*.

The argument some put forth against this teaching is: "Not all pastors are wise and credible". I agree but you don't have to be under such pastors.  *Enjoy the privilege of being under a good pastoral covering, harness it and stop disgracing yourself on social media*.

Certain situations happen in life where you need to talk to someone you can see for counsel. God has set ministry gifts in place for special purposes and one of such that cares and tend for the sheep is the office of a pastor.

*A sheep must have a shepherd or else it would be lost. Who is pastoring you*?

PRAYER: I receive wisdom for living In Jesus name.

DEVOTIONAL 0930 | Source start line: 90488

7/25/23, 7:06 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: ProjectSave Devotional

*WHY YOU NEED A GENUINE PASTOR* Pt.2

Hebrews13:17 Obey your spiritual leaders, and do what they say. Their work is to *watch over your souls*, and they eare accountable to God. Give them reason to do this with joy and not with sorrow. That would certainly not be for your benefit. NLT

*HE IS A WATCHMAN- ALLOW HIM*

In ancient towns and cities with walls, there were men who served as sentries or watchmen. They stay on a high tower to watch over the city. Often, they position themselves to look around for probable invasion.

Their duty is to alert the military formations about oncoming enemy and to get everyone ready for battle in order to protect the city's territory. So arduous a task.

In the opening text, the scripture described spiritual leaders as watchmen/ sentries who watch over souls of men. A genuine pastor does this in many ways but how will believers benefit If they are not under any pastoral covering?

Years back, a sister wanted to marry. She brought her fiance to her pastor for counselling. In the process of counselling both of them, the pastor began to ask probing questions from the man.

The Pastor discovered the man has had a child before without informing this sister. That was how God saved that sister from a deceitful man. I have also seen a brother rescued from a deceitful sister before marriage through the help of his pastor.

*Some of the challenges believers are facing today especially in marriage is because they bye-passed pastoral counsel and scrutiny at the entry border of marriage*.

Some took offense and left church because their pastor confronted them with the truth about their lives, relationship, job and marriage. Is that you?

*Your Pastor's assignment in your life can be likened to that of a security man who ensures that nothing is stolen- allow him perform his God-ordained duty*.

*The opening text says that your pastor must give account to God as a watchman. When he meets God, what will he tell God about you? That you're stubborn and disloyal*?

You see, true pastors know these things and they work hard to ensure they fulfill their core assignment. *I am wary of a generation that fail to recognize the place of spiritual leaders but prefer to seek counsel from google and social media platforms*.

As I said in the first teaching, If you have a pastor that possess true pastoral qualities, allow him to Pastor you and get some counsel from him on crucial matters. Humble yourself to learn from him and stop acting like you know it all.

*If you're looking for a church to attend, stop seeking to belong to a popular church. Seek where you can get proper pastoring. A place where you can proudly call the Shepherd "My Pastor"*.

Don't be an Island. The idea of taking your marital or life issues to the social media asking for advice is out of place. Take advantage of pastoral wisdom.

*PRAYER*: Father, we pray for genuine Pastors all over the world, father keep them and increase their wisdom. Help believers to remain loyal to their spiritual leaders In Jesus name. Amen

7/26/23, 6:45 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: ProjectSave Devotional
Wednesday
July 26th, 2023.

*WHY YOU NEED A GENUINE PASTOR* Pt.3

Jeremiah3:15 *And I will give you shepherds after my own heart, who will guide you with knowledge and understanding* (NLT).

*TO BE GUIDED WITH KNOWLEDGE*

Over the years, In my interaction with some persons who reached out to me for counseling, I observed a pastoring gap in their lives.

That gap stemmed from the fact that they lacked the knowledge a pastor would have equipped them with. These people fall into two categories.

The first category are not regular in their various churches so they missed out on what their pastors should have taught them. They are not submissive to the spiritual growth structure of the church yet they are complaining and looking for what is not lost.

The second category have a pastor over them and are committed to the growth structure of their churches but the quality of what they're being fed from the pulpit is not good enough. They are feeding on a diet that is not balanced. Spiritually, they are malnourished.

*One benefit you'll derive from proper pastoring is that you'll grow in knowledge of the word*. That is why genuine pastors don't joke with bible study and accurate teaching of scriptures.

*The quality of your spiritual diet determines the quality of your spiritual growth*.

Genuine pastors who knows their onions will teach you how to apply scriptures to your daily life. If you come under true shepherds, you will not be afraid to discuss issues with them except you want to remain that way.

*When people bypass their pastors and take their issues to the wrong places, it is either they don't know the role of their pastors or the pastor is not capable as perceived. That's why pastors must keep growing too*.

I have heard about pastors who scattered families with wrong counsel instead of helping them to build godly homes. This is not the portrait of a new testament pastor.

Does he place value on teaching the scriptures? What does he teach? How does he live his life? Can you imitate him just as he is imitating Christ? Does he have integrity? All these should be considered before submitting yourself for pastoring.

*It is unwise to stay under a pastoral care that doesn't care or doesn't have the requisite knowledge to lead you out of issues.  It has always been fatal and would always be fatal*.

*You don't use church membership to reward or please a pastor who is not feeding you properly neither do you attend a church because of its popularity when you know the pastoring you're getting there is poor*.

I see alot of this today. They know they're in the wrong place but they choose to stay because it is the happening church in town. Such persons don't value their spiritual growth.

God's promise to you and me is that he would give us pastors after His own heart (talking about those called genuinely) who will guide the sheep with knowledge and understanding.

*If you're under a genuine shepherd, submit yourself to the spiritual growth structure he has set and be fed properly. Stay there and stop jumping from church to church*.

*PRAYER*: Father, I receive grace to learn and grow In Jesus name.

*WHY YOU NEED A GENUINE PASTOR* Pt.4

Psalms 92:12 *The righteous shall flourish like the palm tree: He shall grow like a cedar in Lebanon. Those that be planted in the house of the LORD Shall flourish in the courts of our God* ( KJV)

*BE PLANTED AND FLOURISH*

Rev Brandon Bailey told the story of a woman who has been in a particular church for forty five (45) years. In those years, the church had three (3) pastors.

He was surprised to see someone who stayed in a church for that long. He asserted that one of the reason old time Christians have a strong walk with God is because they stayed in a church to be fed. They weren't jumping from one church to another seeking "new" experience.

It can't be that in forty five years, the woman wasn't offended. There's a possibility of offense and misunderstanding between her and a member or one of the pastors yet she didn't change her church. She resolved her issues and kept enjoying fellowship with the saints.

*We have so many teachings on pastoring today. Most ministers conferences are targeted at pastors and ministers but we have few that teaches people to be responsible members.*

One of the several excuses people give for changing church is offense. In ten years, some have changed church thrice looking for church of angels. They fail to realize that offense is common where humans are.

A number of believers are not growing spiritually because they are not planted in a local church. They eat different spiritual diets from different puplits and this has led to their stunted growth. They want to be part of a popular church in town yet they are not growing spiritually.

Mothers know that a child that eats around doesn't grow. Growth is for a child that stays with the mother for constant nourishment.

The psalmist says(vs 13), *"Those that be PLANTED in the house of the LORD Shall FLOURISH in the courts of our God*.

Stop looking for "special" churches all over the place. Some have been deceived to think that some churches have a "special" covenant with God that makes their ministries better than the other.

This deception is fueled by the unbiblical utterances made by some pastors to water down other ministries and elevate theirs. If you check those churches, they have members who are not different from those in other churches. They only share the good side with public.

*If you're looking for a church to attend, the criteria should simply be that which is stated in the bible- A new testament church which is the ground and pillar of truth headed by Christ.*

Paul says in 1Tim3:15 *"But if I tarry long, that thou mayest know how thou oughtest to behave thyself in the house of God, which is the CHURCH OF THE LIVING GOD, the PILLAR and GROUND of the TRUTH*"

*If the word of God is taught in truth and your church is a portrait of a new testament church where discipleship is important, please stay there even If it is not popular. Be planted and flourish*.


*ACTION POINT*: Am I planted in my local church or i'm truant? Before concluding that you're not growing in that church have you submitted to the growth structure( sunday school, discipleship class, house/ cell meetings, bible study, prayer retreats, conferences,etc) of that church? If yes, for how long?

DEVOTIONAL 0933 | Source start line: 90701

7/28/23, 7:56 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: PROJECTSAVE DEVOTIONAL
Friday,
July 28th, 2023.

*INVEST IN LEARNING*

Proverbs 23:23 *Buy the truth, and sell it not; Also wisdom, and instruction, and understanding* (KJV)

Sometimes ago, I needed an information badly. I asked around but persons whom I thought would have an answer did not know about my enquiry in details.

Someone referred me to a consultant. When I called him, he told me to pay any amount I could afford before sharing the information with me. He sent me his account details and I made a transfer of a token.

We spoke on phone for a few minutes. His explanation dispeled my ignorance on the subject matter. Till date, I speak like an authority on that subject but most people didn't know it is a product of my investment in learning. It cost me a token.

*Getting valuable knowledge comes at a cost even the knowledge of scriptures*. Some of us attended paid seminaries, school of ministries, attended conferences, bought books and tapes for us to have a better understanding of scriptures and ministry.

*True knowledge add value. When you add true value, you'll experience growth*. However, learning comes at a cost. *Unwillingness to pay the price of learning is the reason some have remained stagnant in life*.

It is very easy to identify non-learners. They always complain about "cost" of learning. When they buy clothes, shoes, bags and jewelries, they do it with joy and they take pride in the cost of mundane things in their possession but they complain about the cost of conference meetings and edifying materials.

*Non learners always want learning to be free yet If they get free books and materials, they won't read it*. It shows money is not their problem,  they just do not value learning. The opening text says, "Buy the truth". It means that learning certain truths comes at a cost.

Scripture is not against committing your resources to learning. In fact, it supports it.
Proverbs 4:7 says, *"The beginning of wisdom is this: Get wisdom. THOUGH IT COST ALL YOU HAVE, GET UNDERSTANDING*". Note the word "Cost".

Stop complaining about the cost of that book, school of ministry or conference meeting. Look at the value it will add to your life If you attend.

*Your willingness to grow in any area of life will be shown by how much you are willing to pay to learn*.

*ACTION POINT*: Stop looking at cost of learning start looking at the value learning will add to your life. True knowledge will come at a cost but you'll be better for it If you invest in it.

DEVOTIONAL 0934 | Source start line: 90754
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
7/29/23, 7:34 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: PROJECTSAVE DEVOTIONAL
Saturday,
July 29th, 2023.

*OPEN THE DOOR FOR HIM*

Rev 3:20 *Here I am! I stand at the door and knock. If anyone hears my voice and opens the door, I will come in and eat with that person, and they with me* ( NIV).

It is common to hear evangelists use the opening text when preaching to sinners that they might accept Christ. However, the context shows that Jesus was referring to a church( believers) in Asia minor not unbelievers.

Why was he knocking on the door of their hearts? He knocked on the door of their hearts because He wanted to eat with them. Does he eat human meals?

In his incarnation, he ate food made by humans but now in his glorified state (resurrected body), he doesn't eat human meals. What food was implied in the text above? It is fellowship.

This  word is not limited to what we do when we come together. It refers to  the depth of our closeness with the father. A communion.

Now that you're born again, it is possible that Jesus may be knocking on the door of your heart for deeper fellowship. That's his food. *Fellowship is all about intimacy.*

Often, I  use my paternal uncles as illustration for fellowship. My uncles sound alike with my dad. Most times, strangers find it hard to identify who is talking because they sound alike.

However, If they're all talking at once, I can identify my dad's voice and the identities of each of them. This is possible for me because over the years, I've fellowshipped with my dad to the point where I don't need to see his face to identify his voice.

Once I hear his voice, that's all I need because I'm fully acquainted with His voice.  *"Your inability to identify God's voice/leading is a pointer to a weak fellowship with him".*

The Christian journey should be progressive and not static. We are meant to be so close to God in a way that our lives would be enwrapped in him totally.

There is a realm higher than where you're operating. Jesus is knocking on the door of your heart, saying: "I want to eat with you." *You cannot exhaust the several dimensions in God, the more you know him, the more you'll discover so many things yet unknown about him*.

That was the reason why Paul said, *"That I may know him, and the power of his resurrection, and the fellowship of his sufferings, being made conformable unto his death;*"(Phil3:10). Paul was already a great Apostle with great depths of revelations, yet fellowship was his desire.

*This is not the time for casual Christianity*. Fellowship is not limited to what you do when you pray or study the bible. It is the consciousness of God that you carry around everywhere knowing that you can be in touch with him anytime.

*PRAYER*: Jesus, I am hungry, I want to dine with you. To know you more and to see you as you are.

DEVOTIONAL 0935 | Source start line: 90845

7/30/23, 6:09 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: ProjectSave Devotional
Sunday,
July 30th, 2023.

*REMEMBER TO THANK HIM ALWAYS*

Psalm 103:1-2 *Bless the Lord , O my soul: and all that is within me, bless his holy name. Bless the Lord , O my soul, and forget not all his benefits*:

In 1860, a ship went aground on the shore of Lake Michigan near Evanston, and Edward Spencer waded again and again into the frigid waters to rescue 17 passengers. He was a ministerial student in Evanston, Illinois, who was part of a life-saving squad.

In the process, his health was permanently damaged. Some years later at his funeral, it was noted that not one of the people he rescued ever thanked him according to Warren Wiersby.

Most times, we suffer a self-induced amnesia about God's goodness in our lives.  We forget God's interventions and miracles easily. Often, we only remember these things when they're fresh in our memories but with time, it fades.

The Psalmist said, *"Bless the Lord, O my soul, and forget not all his benefits*", Psalm102:2. The Psalmist didn't say we should forget some benefits and thank him for the seemingly big ones but he said we should not forget ALL his benefits no matter how insignificant they are.

I heard the story of a woman who frequently thanked God in church for stooling with ease. The Pastor became tired of her frequent thanksgiving. Later, he developed a health challenge that made it difficult for him to pass stool. It was during that experience he appreciated God for the woman's testimonies.

Every day, we should learn to count our blessings no matter how little they are and give thanks. *Though there are no 'little' blessings but what you call little blessings are great blessings when summed up.*"

*Do not take for granted the little interventions of God in your life*. That headache you call ordinary has killed many people. If you have ever fought the battle for survival and won, you'll appreciate God for life each passing day.

If only you know what it takes to go out and return, you'll never take such a miracle for granted especially If your outing has ever been threatened. While still waiting for that "big" miracle, you have every reason to be thankful. *Forget not all his benefits ( past&present).*

*CONFESSION*: Father, I thank you for all you've done for me.

IG: @Projectsave_ministries
@lanreoyeleke

Twitter: @Lanrezion01

Email: info@projectsaveng.org

*FROM US TO YOU* Pt.1

1 Thessalonians5:25 *Dear brothers and sisters, pray for us*

*PRAY FOR US ALWAYS* (A)

There are certain requests pastors and spiritual leaders wish to make from their followers but they are tongue-tied. Sometimes, it takes great courage and humility for leaders to request certain things from those they are leading. One of those request is prayer.

Growing up in the house of a pastor, I realized that spiritual leadership is the hardest job on earth. Humans are the hardest to lead even in the church. If you think i'm exaggerating on this, ask Moses. The very people he suffered for prevented him from the promised land.

By divine ordination, I'm also in the ministry. I know how it feels and I see that expectations are high yet it seems no one cares about me.  I have experienced situations where people expected me to pray for them even when I was overwhelmed with my own challenges.

Years back, I was on admission in the hospital. The nurses set lines for me and I laid on the bed in my full length fighting to live. Right there, I received a call from a sister asking for prayers. I obliged her request because I had the strength to pray.

She didn't ask how I was faring, my family and ministry. All she wanted was prayer. Later, I told her that I was on admission when she called for prayers. She couldn't believe it.  I expected her to ask about my welfare first but she didn't. All she wanted from me was prayers.

That's how most of us treat our spiritual leaders. We always want to "use" them and If it seems they don't have our time, we get angry or even switch camp to the next available pastor.  Such attitude could be a sign of spiritual immaturity or self-centredness.

I have read through the new testament severally. I can't remember a place where believers begged their Pastors or spiritual leaders for prayers. What I found was a great man of God( Apostle Paul)  asking the brethren to pray for him and those in the ministry. Today, reverse is the case.

Yea, he prayed for some churches but  he did that out of duty not because they requested to be prayed for. Ideally, every pastor prays for those he is leading on daily basis even to the point of  mentioning their cases to God.

Unfortunately, church folks want their pastors and spiritual leaders to be on their knees constantly praying for them yet, they are not willing to pray for their leaders. Some do not pray for their leaders at all. So bad!

*Your prayers are part of what we need to succeed in our assignment. There is no high flier in ministry without intercessors. Some persons are on their knees on his behalf*.

*We preachers are not  super human being without challenges neither are we free from the vicissitudes of life. You owe us a duty under God to pray for us daily. I don't mean once in a week but daily. Criticism is cheap but prayer is the real work*.

*Spiritual leaders often face challenges they hardly share with their followers except it becomes visible to all. Don't blame them, it is part of their training not to share such so that you won't be discouraged. Some preachers smile on the pulpit but they shed tears at home*.

How can you explain a preacher who has outstanding results praying for waiting couples yet he is trusting God for thesame miracle from decades past? He may not come out to share his pains when dedicating those children. He may console himself with several cliches but deep within, there is a struggle.

*As ministers, we are merchants of hope because we want your faith to be alive. This is the reason we hide our challenges from you even when we are feeling the heat.*

*Dear reader, whether things are fine with your spiritual leaders or not,  make it a point of duty to pray for them. Their anointing is not an immunity from life's challenges*.

*Don't wait till you hear that your spiritual leader is under attack or experienced a scandal before going on your knees. If church folks are faithfully praying daily for their spiritual leaders, we may not hear sad news about them. LET THE GOSSIP ABOUT US STOP, INTENSE PRAYER SHOULD START*.

If you're confused about the areas of our lives in need of prayers,  just pray for our spiritual lives, our health, our spouses, children, our ministry and pray against forces working assiduously to bring us down in life and ministry.

*Pray for us daily, we need it. This is what many preachers wish they tell their members*.

*ACTION POINT*:  If ProjectSave daily devotional has been a blessing to your life, pray for the writer( Bro Oyeleke Olanrewaju) daily.

*FROM US TO YOU* Pt.2

1 Thessalonians 5:25 *Dear brothers and sisters, pray for us*.

*PRAY FOR OUR FAMILIES* (B)

Dr D.K. Olukoya told the story of how witches fasted for over 200 days to scatter the homes of ministers in a particular country. The first major casualty of that evil plot was the pastor of one of the biggest churches in that country. His marriage scattered and married the friend of his wife.

Kenneth Hagin of blessed memory told the story of another pastor's wife who was deceived by a demon that she could use her beauty to make money as a model. Yea, she rebuked that demon but after sometime, the demon whispered the same words into her ears until she looked at herself in the mirror as a beautiful woman wasting away in a pastor's house. She left her husband and became a model.

The son of a very popular Christian leader in America committed suicide by shooting himself to death. He had Borderline Personality Disorder and major depression, according to his family.

CNN reported the incident that this Pastor's son died in a wave of despair. Another popular televangelist son shot himself in the heart and died. There are several stories of preacher's kid who died mysteriously or are wayward.

Negative happenings in the lives of preachers are not ordinary. Unfortunately, they make news headline faster than any other news from the religious world. *It appears as though people are always hunting for bad news about preachers and their families*.

As a preacher's kid,  in moments of errors or mistakes, people are quick to point you to your root and they say it derogatorily like this: *"And you call yourself a pastor's son*.

I have been there as a preacher's kid. Such statements are demoralizing. He wonder why his father's work is brought into every statement people make about him. He seems not to have a normal life like others.

If you want to correct the child of your pastor, don't bring his father's ministry into it. Just correct him/ her like you would other kids. I hope you'll take this counsel.

*The focus of this teaching is to point your attention to the prayer need of your spiritual leader. The devil is attacking homes of most ministers. He is doing it with all the strength he has because he wants to rubbish their ministries and calling*.

*Satan tries to make preacher's  children wayward and break their homes. Doing this, he knows their ministry will likely crumble. He tries to find an in-road into their families to wreck havoc*.

*Don't wait till it happens to your pastor or that spiritual leader you respect. Pray for him*. The devil may be planning worse for him. If all you  do daily is to present the marriage and children  of your pastor or that spiritual leader to God in prayers, you'll be doing a lot.

*If that spiritual leader is still single, please intensify in your prayers so that he won't marry an agent of the devil. Marriage is very crucial to success in ministry except for ministers that have chosen to be celibate*.

Some may argue against it but that's my opinion. This kind of teaching is for the mature not for those who are seeking to "use" spiritual leaders but for those who care about their lives and ministry.

*Stop the gossip about us. Please, pray for us*.

*PRAYER*: Father, protect the marriage of (....insert names) from breaking. Give him the wisdom on how to manage his home and rule his family.  Lord, let your fear rule and reign in the hearts of his children and make them a godly examples in Jesus name.

*FROM US TO YOU* Pt.3

1 Thessalonians 5:25 *Dear brothers and sisters, pray for us*.

*PRAY FOR OUR MINISTRY* (C)

*One of the targets of the enemy is to bring down God-ordained ministries*.

He is not happy when a man or woman called into the ministry is fulfilling his assignment. Satan will stop at nothing to ensure genuine ministry ceases to exist.

I use to know a very popular church that had a branch in my neighbourhood years back. The church was very popular in the mid 90s. Their senior Pastor was on television those days.

In the process of time, the church closed most of her branches. Till date, I haven't seen any of their branches because the church came under serious attack.

There are several ministries that ended suddenly without traces of prior existence. That's exactly what the enemy want to do to the ministry of any God-ordained minister.

In my few years in ministry and walk with the Lord, I have seen a situation where a church was sold to another denomination because they ceased to exist.

*To think  the devil is happy when souls are won, discipled and liberated from the shackles of hell is to be naive in spiritual matters*.

*The enemy's tactics has always been to strike the shepherd so that the sheep will scatter yet we are not discerning enough to know when the enemy is at work*.

Satan uses internal crisis,  disloyal associates, bad marriage, attack on health, poor decision, moral failure, persecution from within and without, discouragement, scandals, betrayal, false accusations, stagnation and other fiery darts to attack a ministry and bring it down.

*Some church folks spend hours gossiping about their pastors but won't go on their knees to intercede. That shouldn't be you*.

Listen to Paul in 1Thess 2:18, *"We wanted very much to come to you, and I, Paul, tried again and again, but Satan prevented us.*" It was Satan himself that prevented Paul from going to Thessalonica to fulfill his ministry.

If only God can permit you to see into the realm of the spirit and to hear about the enemy's plot for your spiritual leader,  you'll never joke with continuous intercession.

*Dearest, we need your prayers for our ministries*. That spiritual leader you respect and hold in high esteem needs serious intercession.

If truly you love what God is doing through him/ her, you should volunteer to be his/ her personal intercessor.

*When you see a successful minister, there are intercessors behind the scene praying night and day*.

Your pastor must not fail in his assignment neither should that brother in ministry fail in his. Get on your knees now and pray.

*Stop the gossip about us. Please, pray for us*.


*PRAYER*: Father, I crush powers that want ( ...insert name) to fail in ministry. I stop their maneouvers and activities and decree victory over every onslaught of the enemy in Jesus name
