8/27/23, 6:15 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: ProjectSave Devotional
Sunday,
August 27th, 2023.

*LESSONS ON FATHERHOOD* Pt.6

John 5:19 So Jesus explained, *“I tell you the truth, the Son can do nothing by himself. He does only what he sees the Father doing. Whatever the Father does, the Son also does* ( NLT)

*A MODEL TEACHER*.

While growing up, my dad took time to teach us scriptures. We learnt the fundamentals of the Christian faith from him. I wouldn't have known some of the things I know today If not for him.

He was my first bible lecturer and theological instructor. I inherited a library of quality resource materials from him. Today, some of those books are not available around except one order for them.

God the father is a teaching father. From the old testament, God gave his people statutes and he instructed his priests and prophets to teach them to the people.

Deuteronomy 4:5 says, *"See, I have taught you decrees and laws as the LORD my God commanded me, so that you may follow them in the land you are entering to take possession of it*".

In the ministry of Jesus, the father taught about the kingdom through Jesus. I believe Jesus was a teacher because his father instructed him. Just as he said in the opening scriptures: *“...I tell you the truth, the Son can do nothing by himself. He does only what he sees the Father doing. Whatever the Father does, the Son also does"*( John 5:19).

The ministry of teaching continued in the life of a believer through the Holy Spirit: *"But the Advocate, the Holy Spirit, whom the Father will send in my name, will teach you all things and will remind you of everything I have said to you*" ( Jn 14:26).

*A true father who has come to know God the father must be a teacher of God's oracles to his family. Fatherhood is incomplete without teaching*.


Abraham exemplified this to the point where God boasted about him: *"For I have known (chosen, acknowledged) him [as My own], so that he may TEACH and command his children and [the sons of] his household after him to keep the way of the LORD by doing what is righteous and just, so that the LORD may bring upon Abraham what He has promised him*" (Genesis 18:19 AMP).

Can God boast about you like he did to Abraham? What are you teaching your wife and children? God's expectation of you is that you sit as the tutor-general of your family not your wife. She's only there to help.

Don't be too busy trying to make money at the expense of your family's spiritual growth and development. Also, he who must teach must learn. The learning must be continuous.

In most churches, only few men attend bible study meetings and teaching programs. As a father, If you don't sit to learn under a teacher, what will you teach your family?

*ACTION POINT*: Have a teaching plan for your family. Also, as a father, be a learner.

DEVOTIONAL 0963 | Source start line: 92885
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
8/28/23, 7:15 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: PROJECTSAVE DEVOTIONAL
Monday,
August 28th, 2023.

*LESSONS ON FATHERHOOD* Pt.7

John 5:19 So Jesus explained, “I tell you the truth, the Son can do nothing by himself. He does only what he sees the Father doing. Whatever the Father does, the Son also does( NLT)

*A MODEL OF DISCIPLINE*

My father is a disciplinarian. Actually, he copied that from God the father. The bible says in Hebrews 12:6 *"because the Lord disciplines the one he loves, and he chastens everyone he accepts as his son*". When it comes to the issue of discipline, God is our model.

*The purpose of discipline is not to punish like a criminal but to correct*. According to scriptures, God's discipline is for those he loves so that his children won't end their lives in perdition. *Discipline is an expression of love*.

*Fathers, one of the ways to express or demonstrate love to your children is to discipline them*. It is scriptural. Proverbs 3:13 says *"because the LORD disciplines those he loves, as a father the son he delights in*".

Realizing that discipline is an expression of love will make you proactive in helping your family to stay on the right path. God did not instruct us to punish because the end goal of punishment will not lead to a behavioural change but discipline does.

Those days, when my parents want to discipline us, they will show us what we did wrong and that comes after several warning. That stubborness that makes us repeat the same error after several warnings is shown the way out with prayer and the rod. I thank God for what we have become today.

The world,  especially the west is trying to rewrite the bible on this subject.  In context of scriptures in this piece, where the bible uses "rod", it is what it is not a figurative word. Proverbs 23:13 says *"Do not withhold discipline from a child; if you punish them with the rod, they will not die*". I didn't write that, I found it in my bible. *Though, I agree that context can define discipline. As long as it corrects, it is still biblical.*

I understand that there are abuses and extremes but that doesn't invalidate what the scripture says. Every defence against the use of rod doesn't hold water. Just look at the generation that was spanked and disciplined correctly ( note the word "correctly") and compare them with those they tell we shouldn't spank. Any difference? Be the judge.

We need to look at the scriptural recommendation properly and apply it for result to be produced. The rod is for erring children not adults. That implies that the use of rod is within a time frame in the growth phase of a child. When done properly, it produces result.

Proverbs 22:15 says *"Foolishness is bound in the heart of a child; But the rod of correction shall drive it far from him*". The bible calls it "rod of correction" not rod of inflicting bruises and marks. Discipline must be age-appropriate. When done in love, it produces result.

The folly in the heart of a child the bible speaks about is the reason they repeat the same thing after several verbal warnings. However, that folly responds to discipline because children don't like anything that inflicts mild pain or discomfort.

Fathers, use the rod when it is useful. A generation is being raised wrongly because we want to look or appear woke and pander to a culture that is anti God. I pray we don't harvest shame in the nearest future.

Proverbs 29:17 says, *"Discipline your children, and they will give you peace; they will bring you the delights you desire*".

*ACTION POINT*: Discipline is corrective not punitive. The drive is love not hatred. Discipline must be age-appropriate.

DEVOTIONAL 0964 | Source start line: 92937
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
8/29/23, 8:02 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: ProjectSave Devotional
Tuesday
August 29th, 2023.

*LESSONS ON FATHERHOOD* Pt.8

John 5:19 So Jesus explained, “I tell you the truth, the Son can do nothing by himself. He does only what he sees the Father doing. Whatever the Father does, the Son also does( NLT)

*A MODEL PROVIDER*

On my way to catch up with an appointment, I listened to an interesting conversation in the bus. The conversation was between a man who called himself a pastor and some women.

This man opined that it is not his duty to make provision for his wife and children as long as his wife is working. From the view he expressed, he believed women do nothing with their money so men should abdicate their responsibilities and hand them over to their wives.

To me, he sounded like a man who is an expert at impregnating his wife but doesn't care about other responsibilities that follow.

I became attentive because I wanted to know how the whole thing will end. Some of the women who heard him opposed him with bitterness in their hearts against his callous and unkind statement. Most of them are market women struggling to help their families.

They wondered why an able-bodied man who can work and make life better for his wife and children will push all of that to his wife. That man is a shame to fatherhood.

Some men are only good at sleeping with their wives but are  never ready to take responsibility for making provisions for their seeds.

I have met women who are burdened with the yoke of providing for their household not because their husbands are dead or sick, those men are just lazy and irresponsible. Women married to such men age faster because they are never happy. Whenever I see this, i'm not happy.

Some men love life, fashion and pleasure. They invest in their looks and are nice to people on the streets but their reputation with their wives and children is akin to that of Satan.

God the father is a provider. Every other father must imitate him in this. In the Lord's prayer, while praying to the father, Jesus said, *"Give us this day our daily bread*"( Luke 11:3). Do you know that God is committed to providing for mankind? Yes, we all are sustained by him.

Paul says, *"But if any provide not for his own, and specially for those of his own house, he hath denied the faith, and is worse than an infidel*"( 1 Tim 5:8).

*Some women are not really after the amount you earn especially If they earn more. All they want to see is that you are committed to the welfare of the family by giving what is within the frame of your earning capacity*.

*Don't sit at home watching t.v. while other men are at work neither should you say you're waiting for the "big jobs" to come while you can do something to make life better for your wife and children*.

*As a man, If you are not sick, there is nothing stopping you from working to provide basic needs for your family. There is dignity in labour*.

*ACTION POINT*: Fathers who do not provide for their household may lose their respect in the long run. Respect is earned and not demanded. Ensure you do all that is legal to make provision for your family.

DEVOTIONAL 0965 | Source start line: 93038
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
8/30/23, 6:40 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: ProjectSave Devotional
Wednesday
August 30th, 2023.

*LESSONS ON FATHERHOOD* Pt.9

John 5:19 So Jesus explained, *“I tell you the truth, the Son can do nothing by himself. He does only what he sees the Father doing. Whatever the Father does, the Son also does* ( NLT)

*A MODEL OF HARD WORK*

I was privileged to reside in a part of this country years back. In as much as I love the people, I saw something I didn't agree with and it miffed me.

I observed a culture of laziness among their males. You'll see a grown man wake up in the morning drinking alcohol with wrapper tied on his body while his wife strap her child to her back, ride bicycle and go to the farm. I wasn't just pleased with all that.

I was privileged to speak to a lady from that side who confirmed to me that such habit exhibited by men seems to be cultural though not pronounced. She told me her brothers also took after their dad in that regard while the ladies do the hard jobs.

God the father is not lazy. He is a hardworker who has strong work ethic. John 5:17 says, *"But Jesus replied, “My Father is always working, and so am I*"( NLT).

Jesus confirmed to us that the reason he is working is because his father is always working. *In heaven, there is no room for laziness. If our heavenly father is working, why should earthly fathers be lazy*?

The bible condemns idleness in strong terms: *"Yet we hear that some of you are living idle lives, refusing to work and meddling in other people’s business. We command such people and urge them in the name of the Lord Jesus Christ to settle down and work to earn their own living*"(2 Thessalonians 3:11‭-‬12 NLT).

Unfortunately, I noticed that lazy people have high wishes and dreams. They know the name of good cars, clothes and luxury but are doing nothing to get their desire. Proverbs 13:4 says, *"Lazy people want much but get little, but those who work hard will prosper*"( NLT).

*As a father who loves and want the well-being of his family, there is no legal job that is belittling or below status. Making money doesn't answer to "status". It answers to those who can deploy legal means to get it from where it is hidden*.

One day, I told someone I can do uber to make extra cash If need be. The response was "that is below your status"( paraphrasing). Hey! This status you're talking about is not paying my bills, why should I wallow in lack because of "status"?

All those funny mentality is the reason so many graduates are poor and unemployed while the unschooled are landlords. A number of houses in Lagos are built by unschooled men while the schooled pay rent to those men. Can't you see a problem there?

*When you think you're bigger than a particular job, you lose the right to live as desired*. Why care so much about people's opinion especially those who aren't paying your bills?

Proverbs 14:23 says, *"Work brings profit, but mere talk leads to poverty*"(NLT). Poverty is man-made not God-sent, kick it out of your life.

*ACTION POINT*: I refuse to be lazy in the name of Jesus Christ. In my life, I cast out the spirit of laziness in the name of Jesus Christ.

*LESSONS ON FATHERHOOD* Pt.10

John 5:19 So Jesus explained, *“I tell you the truth, the Son can do nothing by himself. He does only what he sees the Father doing. Whatever the Father does, the Son also does* ( NLT)

*A MODEL OF CHRISTIAN DEVOTION*

On the day of my convocation, when my parents and famlies were about leaving the convocation ground, we all gathered beside the vehicle to pray. One of my room mates back in school was with us.

After they left, he said "In over twenty years of living with my parents, we have not prayed together as a family neither did I see them praying alone"( paraphrase). He was surprised to see us praying as a family unit. He had no model of Christian devotion and consecration though himself is a believer.

Today, I see myself Imitating my parents in certain things especially their devotional life. Those days, whenever we wake up, we met them praying privately. Today, when my children wake up, they meet me praying.

Oftentimes, I position myself to be seen by them because I am modeling something to them intentionally. I want them to value personal devotion. I emphasize to them the importance of having an altar. I am not just talking it, I am modeling it because *kingdom habits&routines are contagious*.


*We learn by imitation*. Imitation is not limitation If you are imitating the right thing. In Hebrews 6:12, the bible says, *"that you do not become sluggish, but imitate those who through faith and patience inherit the promises*".


*However, the next generation can only imitate the models they see. Fathers, we must be models of true christian devotion and consecration. Your children should see your dedication to the things of the kingdom and imitate you in that*.


*If truly you want your children to be devoted to the Lord, lead the way. Do not sit at home watching football while mid week service is going on in church*. Let them be part of your devotional life. Share with them from scriptures and let them share what they learn in their study. Take them to church and let them see how you value fellowship with other brethren.


*Do not grow spiritually in isolation, integrate them*. Talking about a generation of Israelites, when their fathers failed in modeling devotion, the bible says, *"After that generation died, another generation grew up who did not acknowledge the Lord or remember the mighty things he had done for Israel*" (Judges 2:10 NLT).


Fathers, we must be conscious of posterity in all we do. If we don't want our children to depart from the way of the Lord, we must be in the Lord and walk in his ways.


*ACTION POINT*: Let your family be part of your christian devotion and consecration. Show and teach them the importance of having a devotional life and integrate them. Do not live your spiritual life in isolation.

*LESSONS ON FATHERHOOD* Pt.11

John 5:19 So Jesus explained, *“I tell you the truth, the Son can do nothing by himself. He does only what he sees the Father doing. Whatever the Father does, the Son also does* ( NLT)

*WHEN THERE IS NO MODEL FATHER*

Recently, I was discussing with a brother who lost his father at a very young age. He is a minister of the Gospel. In our discussion, he told me how his pastor has played the role of a father in his life to the point where he calls him daddy as though he were his biological father.

In previous lessons, much emphasis was laid on modeling true fatherhood by earthly fathers who are christians. What about those who do not have a father or are from a background where Christ is not honoured or revered?

If you are in any of these category, I want you to know that you have hope. God the father is our father. He is a father to the fatherless.  You can learn true fatherhood from him by reading scriptures and allowing the Holy Spirit teach you more about his fatherhood.

Further, the local church is an expression of God's family here on earth where the pastor stands as the father figure for all representing God the father. It is to your advantage If you belong to a local church where Christ is modeled and taught by the pastor.

In Ephesians 2:19, Paul says, *"So now you Gentiles are no longer strangers and foreigners. You are citizens along with all of God’s holy people. You are members of God’s family*" ( NLT).

*Per adventure you have no one around you who is a model of godly fatherhood, your pastor should be able to play that role in your life provided you're under the right shepherd*.

*The local church is designed by God to raise disciples and transform people into the very image of the son whose pattern is akin to that of God the father*.  The impact of the local church in our lives( family life inclusive) is far-reaching.    Your christian walk is incomplete without being part of a local church.

*It is impossible to submit yourself to a genuine spiritual father and not have in him a true model father*. Being under true authority will shape you into the kind of father we have been discussing about in this series.

*You lose alot when you abandon the family of God headed by the pastor. You cannot run a solo race and be built*. If you do not belong to a church or you are not submitted to godly spiritual authority that can model true fatherhood to you, ask the Holy Spirit to lead you to the right place.

*PRAYER*: Father, I thank you for this series. As a father, I receive the grace to model godliness in Jesus name.

9/2/23, 6:50 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: PROJECTSAVE DEVOTIONAL
Saturday,
September 2nd, 2023.

*FORGIVE YOURSELF*

2Cor5:17 *Therefore if any man be in Christ, he is a new creature: old things are passed away; behold, all things are become new* (KJV).


So much has been said about forgiving people who trespass against us but the admonition to forgive oneself is rare.

Most times, we hear the phrase "forgive yourself" in passing but those who preach it don't dwell on it like they do on forgiving others.

It is very easy to tell others that their past  is gone because they are new creation in Christ but it is very hard to believe what we're preaching to others whenever the devil reminds us of our gaffes in the past.

I've been there even as a preacher. I have condemned myself and even felt guilty to the point of depression. I was fighting a battle within. The devil kept telling me forgiveness was not cheap and I almost took sides with him. It took me a long time before I could accept God's forgiveness that was available to me through Christ.

*When you fail to forgive yourself, it is a sure sign that you don't believe in the sufficiency of Christ's sacrifice to cleanse you of your sins and wipe your slate clean.*

A man said, "Failing to forgive yourself will put blinders on your spiritual eyesight quickly. It will cause you to see things through the eyes of guilt, shame and condemnation. It will ruin your faith, and cause you to go blind spiritually."

*Those who hardly forgive themselves hardly forgive others.* The devil will make you doubt God's forgiveness to weaken your faith and prayer life, he'll bring negative emotions your way and bring to remembrance all your wrongs in the past.

He'll play them to you like a video. He'll make you see yourself lesser than other brethren. Ultimately, he'll make you doubt God's word about forgiveness and the efficacy of Jesus' blood.


The writer of Hebrew said, *"Just think how much more the blood of Christ will purify our consciences from sinful deeds so that we can worship the living God. For by the power of the eternal Spirit, Christ offered himself to God as a perfect sacrifice for our sins.*"(Heb9:14).

God will forgive the vilest offender who believes in the cleansing power of the blood. Think about the vilest thing a man can do, God will surely forgive.  Prophet Isaiah said, *"“Come now, and let us reason together,” Says the LORD, “Though your sins are like scarlet, They shall be as white as snow; Though they are red like crimson, They shall be as wool.*"(Isa1:18).


After you're forgiven, He won't keep the record. Hebrew 8:12 says, *"And I will forgive their wickedness, and I will never again remember their sins.*” If God has promised not to remember your sins, why are you doing sin remembrance service?

The enemy will try  to remind you of your past and continue to beat you up over sins that were supposed to be nailed to the cross. Forgiveness is a gift from God. When the recipient of a gift receives it gladly and with joy, the giver is glorified.

*When you forgive yourself, God is glorified but if you dwell on your past and feel guilty and condemned, Satan is glorified. Hey! Forgive yourself*.

*CONFESSION*: Father, thank you for expressing your love to me by sending Christ to die for my sins. I believe in the efficacy of Christ's blood to forgive my sins. I accept the totality of His sacrifice for me on the cross. I refuse to feel guilty and condemned for my forgiven past in the name of Jesus Christ. Amen.

https://t.me/projectsaveministries

Sunday,

*THE TRIP THAT ENDS NOWHERE- GUILT TRIP*

Romans 4:7 *"Blessed are those whose transgressions are forgiven, whose sins are covered*".

There is a trip you must not embark on. It is called guilt trip. After you've forgiven yourself and accepted God's love through Christ for your sins, do not stay around people or in environments that will make you feel guilty and condemned again.

This is very crucial if you want to silence the voice of guilt and condemnation that makes you feel unforgiven. There's something called guilt trip.

Guy Winch describes it as a form of verbal or nonverbal communication in which a guilt inducer tries to induce guilty feelings in a target, in an effort to control their behavior. *As such, guilt trips are a clear form of psychological manipulation and coercion*.

*The devil specializes in taking people on a guilt trip.* The bible calls him the accuser of the brethren ( Rev12:10). Before accusing you, he'll take you on a trip to your past, he'll present the pictorial/visual details to you and then accuse you.

*Sometimes, those who know about your dirty past can guilt trip you to control and manipulate you, please don't allow them.* It is even worse when this is coming from the brethren because of what they know about you from your past.

The height of it is when they use your former lifestyle to preach right in your presence not knowing that you've been forgiven by God.

A Pastor once told a lady that If she leads song in church, he won't sing along. He said that in reaction to what she did. He didn't care to lead that lady back to the right track. He picked her wrongdoing and made a very hasty decision.

That lady will need special grace to grow spiritually under such a  Pastor and she may find it hard to forgive herself even after God has forgiven her because her Pastor has laid a burden of guilt on her.

If you love yourself, please stay away from those that always guilt trip you to downplay your new creation status or to disregard what God is doing through you.

Paul said, *"Who will bring any charge against those whom God has chosen? It is God who justifies.*"(Rom8:33). Hallelujah!!! Now that you're in Christ, you're justified.

*CONFESSION*: Father I thank you for the provisions made for my sins on the cross. Thank you for the vicarious substitution of Christ on the cross. Nothing in my past is strong enough to make me feel guilty and condemned one more day. I'm a new creation in Christ and I walk in the newness of life henceforth in Jesus name.

9/4/23, 6:39 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: PROJECTSAVE DEVOTIONAL
Monday,
September 4th, 2023.

*PAY ATTENTION TO YOUR HEALTH*

Eph5: 29 No one hates his own body but feeds and cares for it, just as Christ cares for the church. NLT

Heard about a young evangelist who died at the age of 29. On his death bed, he said, "God gave me a message and he gave me a horse. I damaged the horse and I couldn't deliver the message." The message he referred to was his ministry/ calling while the horse was his body. He died without fulfilling his ministry because he didn't take good care of his health.

In contemporary church history, some powerful healing ministers in America during the voice of healing days died very young.  Their death was not because they weren't anointed but it was due to their failure to take good care of their health. So many believers have died cheaply while some are living in sickness because they neglected their health.

In this part of the world, we are fond of tagging every sickness as demonic attack. Yes, sickness is not of God( Acts10:38), but lack of wisdom on living healthy can cause sickness in the life of a believer. That has nothing to do with demons.

Some believers see anything medicinal as anti faith. Some have been taught that orthodox medicine is not of God and that using such is a way of saying God cannot heal. Some faith preachers have caused confusion in this area because of imbalanced interpretation of scriptures. So many believers have paid dearly for this.

*Ignorance and foolishness has killed many more than demonic attack.* A minister intentionally refused to take medications on his death bed. He spewed them out because he felt medicine was anti faith. He died there.

Taking good care of your health is a sign that shows you love yourself. I know you're trying to love your neighbour but you can express that love better if you're in good health.

How do you take good care of your health? Eat right, exercise your body, rest well, maintain personal hygiene, check your health status often and listen to your doctor's advice. Do not wait till there are symptoms of sickness before checking your health. Do not disregard medical advice.

My mentor told us about a preacher that fell while preaching, they resuscitated him. Instead of taking heed to medical advice not to climb the pulpit for sometime, he continued preaching as usual. He passed on after a week.

Dearest, I see you have a great life ahead. Don't die unfulfilled because of avoidable health issues. Prevention is better than cure.

*PRAYER*: Father, I receive wisdom on how to handle my health in Jesus name.

DEVOTIONAL 0971 | Source start line: 93337

9/5/23, 5:37 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: ProjectSave Devotional
Tuesday
September 5th, 2023.

*BLESS YOUR FOOD BEFORE EATING*

1 Tim4:4 *"Since everything God created is good, we should not reject any of it but receive it with thanks. For we know it is made acceptable by the word of God and prayer*"( NLT)

Recently, I had more insight about why we need to bless our meals before eating them. While growing up, I thought blessing our meals was a christian ritual without significance but now I know better.

In my pursuit of knowledge and self development, I sat under an African Traditional Religion scholar who in pursuit of his Ph.D conducted a research on 'Traditional medicine and Magic among Farmers' in one of the towns in South west, Nigeria.

In his research, he visited a community where farmers use demon spirits to cultivate large expanse of land. Without mechanized farming or human workers on their farms,  their farm yield is bountiful. The ordinary eyes can't see farm workers yet plants are growing and weeds are cleared. This is no fiction, it is real.

In a bid to know the secret behind their unusual stride in farming, he met with the head of all traditionalist in that town. He was told to sit on a mortar as he made incantations and shared secrets with him from 1am midnight to 4am in the morning.

One thing that struck me was that farmers engaged in this act do not eat the produce from their farms neither their families as well. If they do, death is the result. They sell all their farm produce to markets in distant lands where they have no families.

You can't convince me that consuming such foods will be without effect on peoples lives. Satan can't cultivate farms without poisoning it. He is wicked and gives nothing good freely. The spiritual controls the physical and there are deep mysteries about the operations in the spirit realm we may not understand.

The psalmist says, *"Have respect unto the covenant: for the dark places of the earth are full of the habitations of cruelty*"( Psalm 74:20).

Do you know the farming method or style that was used to cultivate that food you're about to eat? I haven't said you should conduct deliverance service on meals. What i'm advocating for is that you do what we read about in the bible- bless your meals. That's all.

Under the old covenant, temple articles were sanctified: *"We have prepared and consecrated all the articles that King Ahaz removed in his unfaithfulness while he was king. They are now in front of the LORD’s altar"( 2 Chronicles 29:19).

If those under that covenant were smart enough to consecrate ordinary articles, why shouldn't we sanctify the food that goes into our body system?

I once watched a t.v.documentary on how electronics made in Japan were dedicated in  a shrine before they are sold into the market. Can you imagine that?

Don't be too woke or naive about issues such as this. In the opening text, Apostle Paul,  made it clear that we can make our food acceptable by the word and prayer. In essence, when we don't bless our foods with the word and prayer, it won't be accepted.

In Acts 27:35, Paul blessed bread before eating: *"Then he took some bread, gave thanks to God before them all, and broke off a piece and ate it*". He could as well eat it without blessing it but he knew the significance of blessing meals.

Since you and I do not know the source of the food we buy in the market, it is not too much to sanctify it with the word and prayer to make it acceptable.

*ACTION POINT*: A simple prayer before your meals isn't a bad idea. It won't cost you a dime.

*WHAT DWIGHT L. MOODY SAID THAT STRUCK ME*

Eccl7:1 *A good reputation is more valuable than costly perfume. And the day you die is better than the day you are born*( NLT).

Dwight L. Moody was an American evangelist and publisher. This particular quote of his struck me: *"If I take care of my character, my reputation will take care of me*".

Your character is what you do often but your reputation is what people know or see about you. If you take care of your character, your reputation will take care of itself.

I was part of a decision making committee sometimes ago, we decided to recommend someone we felt was good enough to handle a critical position.

After we recommended him, the decision to bring him on met with a strong resistance because the fellow has a reputation for immorality and financial recklessness. If he had taken care of his lustful and profligate character, he wouldn't have had a bad reputation with people.

One of my acquaintances attended a church but she left because the pastor made immoral advances at her. Her mum and other family members left as well. Till now, the Pastor has been pastoring an almost empty church.

His reputation for immorality is working against him. Even If he has repented it would take time before he can regain the trust of people. *Wise men take care of their character so their reputation can take care of itself*.

Don't live a life of 'I don't care what people say or feel about me'. That's absolutely wrong! In life, in as much as you need God to lift you up and make you great, you need the goodwill of men as well. Don't downplay this.

*When crucial decisions about you are to be made, you will not be there. That is where your reputation with men will speak for you*.

*If you have a reputation for integrity, men will commit great things into your hands. If you are given to accountability, it becomes easier for people to entrust valuables into your hands*.

*If you don't take care of your character, your reputation with men  may limit how far you can go in life*.

*PRAYER*: Father, I receive grace to take care of my character In Jesus name. Amen

DEVOTIONAL 0973 | Source start line: 93527
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
9/7/23, 7:52 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: PROJECTSAVE DEVOTIONAL
Thursday,
September 7th, 2023.


*HUNT FOR RELEVANT KNOWLEDGE*


Proverbs 15:14 *The discerning heart seeks knowledge, but the mouth of a fool feeds on folly*.


The story of Christopher Columbus is relevant to our discussion. He  was a voyager who made several voyages around the then known world. In one of his journeys, he had no food and his companions were hungry.


They got to an island where they saw people wining and dining. They begged  for food but they refused Christopher Columbus and his crew food. All entreaties made fell on deaf ears.


Christopher Columbus remembered reading  in a book that eclipse of the sun was to happen in days to come. He told the Islanders that he would cause darkness to cover the land. They ignored him.


After some days, darkness covered the whole land. The Islanders remembered what Columbus said about making darkness cover the whole land. They rushed to him begging for forgiveness and they gave him food that was sufficient for him and his crew.


Probably they thought he had special powers but what he had was just knowledge. He leveraged on his investment in getting relevant knowledge to get what he wanted.


*The world is designed to run as a knowledge economy. Sometimes, what differentiates the rich from the poor is knowledge. Two persons might be doing thesame business but the successful one likely knows something the other person doesn't know*.


There was a time I needed an information urgently. Someone recommended a man to me who could shed light on the matter. When I called this man on phone, he said, "I sell knowledge. So you'll have to pay me for the knowledge and my time." Brethren, I paid.


There are nations without mineral resources but their GDP is from the large pool of intellectuals who have used their knowledge to create values worth exporting to other parts of the world.  This is how life is designed. We all are trading value to get what we want.


*The man that has relevant value to add get the most. In life, you'll end up paying for what you don't know. However, you must also invest relevant knowledge in yourself so that you'll be paid for what others do not know that they need*.


Apostle Paul's writings and speeches showed that he invested relevant knowledge in himself. Little wonder why he stood before the intellectuals of his day at the Areopagus.


Theologians agree that Pauline corpus must be studied as a course and interpreted differently because his mastery of Greek language and writing style which differ from others. He was a scholar.


It is obvious that he was diligent and studious. He said, *"When you come, be sure to bring the coat I left with Carpus at Troas. Also bring my books, and especially my papers*"(2Tim4:13).


This is the time to invest in quality books and hunt for relevant information that can benefit your life. That's how to invest in knowledge. *The scope of your knowledge should not be limited to that course you studied in school. Read wide and broaden your horizon*.


The answer to some of the things you're praying about may be written inside a book. Ignorance has killed many and stagnated many lives than pandemic.

*The secret of great men is in what they know that many do not know*. Your  investment in getting knowledge will pay off some day. Don't cheat yourself, this is how the world is designed to run.


*PRAYER*: Father, I receive the grace to invest relevant knowledge into my life in Jesus name.

*LOVING WOUNDED SOLDIERS*

Galatians6:1 *Dear brothers and sisters, if another believer is overcome by some sin, you who are godly should gently and humbly help that person back onto the right path. And be careful not to fall into the same temptation yourself*( NLT)

From the library of an old reverend, I read a book titled: "I Was Wrong" by Jim Baker. A very big book but I finished it in six days. It is an autobiography of the author who was a highly rated pastor in the U.S. in the 70s and 80s before he went to prison.

He had access to the white house and flew the Airforce one with the president. His incarceration made the headlines and he was despised largely by the Christian community. Some of the pastors he raised or mentored in the ministry turned their back against him while some of his trusted friends joined the conspiracy that brought him down.

The straw that broke the Camel's back was when his wife of decades(31 years) divorced him in 1992 and got married to the contractor who built their ministry property.

He had a television network called PTL club that hosted great men of God including our revered Benson Idahosa of blessed memory.  In 1989, Jim Baker was sentenced to prison for wire fraud amid sexual scandal. In his book, he said: "I fell from a lofty height".

While in prison, he was washing the toilet and someone came telling him he had a visitor. He was reluctant to see who his visitor was. When he came out, he was surprised to see Billy Graham and his wife. Billy Graham hugged him tightly not minding the cloth he wore.

When he was released from prison in 1994, the Grahams loaned him a house and car for three years and Franklin Graham( Billy's son) bought him two brief cases in anticipation of his return to ministry.

Jim Baker said: *“He saw me coming back. At that time, I still didn’t plan to come back.*". When Billy Graham died, he visited to pay his last respect and he said: *“They represented Jesus Christ to somebody who the world said was fallen and would never preach again*". Now, that touched me deeply.

Hey! A fallen soldier can rise again If we pray and show him love. We often write people off and make them a subject of our ministerial gossip even to the extent of preaching against them on the altar. Very wrong.

*When you talk more than you pray for a fallen brother, you're taking sides with the devil because the devil doesn't like when we pray for such people but he'll rather have us gist about them.* We live in the days of smear campaign against preachers. The world is looking for the slightest error of preachers.

The faux pas of clerics from other faith don't make news like that of Christian preachers. Those behind such evil publicity are fellow believers who want to trend on social media. When we do that, we are not acting like Christ.

*The love of Christ is not only for those in his "good books", his love is for all including that brother or sister despised by the brethren.*  Looking deeply into his heart, his desire is for the fallen soldier to be restored not despised.

*ACTION POINT*:-  From today, ensure you start praying for the restoration of that brother or sister has fallen into sin. If you have access to such persons, greet, hug, fellowship with them and let them know how much you love them. That way, you may be helping someone back on his feet. The return of a wounded soldier can be deadly to the enemy. Ask Samson.

DEVOTIONAL 0975 | Source start line: 93690

9/9/23, 9:26 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: PROJECTSAVE DEVOTIONAL

*FIGHT SPIRITUAL PRIDE*

1Peter 5:5 *In the same way, you who are younger, submit yourselves to your elders. All of you, clothe yourselves with humility toward one another, because, “God opposes the proud but shows favor to the humble*”.

My very first time  in a white garment church, I went there with a sense of spiritual pride. Coming from a pentecostal background, I felt I knew God's word better and will dish out "Rhema" when I get there. Also, I felt were more sound and accurate than them.

You can't blame me. I  was raised as a conservative evangelical and in my growing years in the faith, we were made to believe we were better than people from there including the churches we call "Orthodox" in Nigeria though we are also orthodox, speaking theologically. We assumed all of them were  thesame. While I agree there are bad eggs but all are not thesame. Take that from me.

When I got this white garment church, two ministers from their fold preached before me. I couldn't believe what I heard. The sermons were sound and instructive. In fact, the youth preacher's message was a wake-up call to me. If he had made an altar call, I may be the first to step out because I saw my backsliden state in his sermon.

That day, I learnt to be humble in an unusual way. When I mounted the pulpit, I confessed my sin to the people and then I had a release to preach. I think I left that service more blessed than anyone else.

According to an online dictionary, pride is defined as the feeling or deep pleasure or satisfaction derived from one's own achievements, the achievements of those with whom one is closely associated, or from qualities or possessions that are widely admired.

*Pride makes a man look down on others as worthless and without value. If this extends into our walk with God,  we will miss God over and over because God oppose the proud but he gives grace only to the humble.*

*In my opinion, spiritual pride is far worse. It is an enemy of divine encounter. It makes one look down on people and places God has set for our blessing*. Why some are not blessed is simply because they look down on those God sent to them. This is common to those who have stayed long in church or senior ministers.

Spiritual pride was one of the problems Pharisees and Saducees had when Jesus came on the scene. You may not see it that way but in my  opinion,  I think it is.


To be a Pharisee requires alot especially knowledge about the law of Moses. Saducees are picked from the wealthy families. They are aristocrats and they also possess a deep knowledge about the Mosaic law.

Here comes Jesus, the son of a carpenter who never attended any Rabbinic school yet he claimed to have knowledge about God and spiritual things.  How do you think a Pharisee or Saducee would be patient to listen to an ordinary Jewish carpenter teach? Spiritual pride prevented them from encountering God through Jesus.

*Today,  spiritual pride comes in shades of many colours.  There are believers or ministers who would never sit to learn under anyone they perceive does not have their 'spiritual qualification'. So wrong*!

They may ask questions like: 'did he go to Bible school'? Does he run a mega church? *That someone pastor a small church doesn't mean he cannot be used by God  to minister to the Bishop of a large cathedral*. It is in my generation we use human parameters to determine who and who cannot minister to us.

*God can use a teenager to meet the spiritual need of a General overseer only If he is humble.*  Spiritual pride robs us of many blessings. *Some will never take notes in church when their junior or someone they brought into the faith is preaching.*

I see spiritual pride often and God is warning us about it especially those of us in the ministry. *Don't sit like a father of spiritual sons in God's presence when God has chosen to speak to you through those you call sons. Sit and learn like a learner under the feet of the master*.

Do all you can to fight spiritual pride, it is an enemy of spiritual encounter.

*PRAYER*: Father, I receive the grace to mortify spiritual pride in my life In Jesus name.


====================================================================================================
DEVOTIONAL 0976 | Source start line: 93773
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
9/10/23, 8:06 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: ProjectSave Devotional
Sunday,
September 10th, 2023.

*GUARD AGAINST LOW MOMENTS*

1Cor10:12 *So, if you think you are standing firm, be careful that you don't fall!*

There is a spiritual syndrome I want to describe. I call it the low moments. I used that expression because I'm lost for words but I believe the Holy Spirit will interpret it clearly in your heart.

What do I mean by low moments? This is simply the time in a man's spiritual walk  when there is laxity and lukewarmness in his fellowship with God. For some, they struggle to pray as they use to while others may not be interested in studying the word. Others experience both at the same time.

There were two occasions in the life of David that reflected he had a low moments spiritually. I deduced from his actions that he was not fully in tune with God as he should and that led him into error with repercussions beyond what he could handle.

The first was the case of adultery between him and the wife of Uriah and how he masterminded his death. The bible aptly describes where King David should be at the time he saw the wife of Uriah from the rooftop in her bathroom. The bible says: *"In the spring, at the time when kings go off to war,..But David remained in Jerusalem.*"(2Sam11:1).

Note,  he was at the right place at the wrong time. He was not where he should be physically as a king and spiritually as a man ordained to lead in the Lord's battles.

The second occasion that revealed his low moments was in 1Chron21:1 *"Satan rose up against Israel and caused David to take a census of the people of Israel.*" David became cold spiritually to the point where Satan influenced his actions.

How can a worshipper who was always ministering before God with psalms become so lax to the point of coming under the influence of Satan? Something must have gone wrong somewhere. If a believers lives in sin perpetually, something is wrong somewhere.

A preacher in his low moments may study the bible because he want to preach not for personal devotion. He may fast because there's a corporate declaration for fasting in the church not for his personal spiritual growth. In fact, he may attend church services because he is obliged to do so by reason of his office not because he love to.

Surprisingly, he may preach powerfully but his spiritual stamina  for a fruitful Christian life is low if not empty.  These and many more are the signs of low moments.

*The seemingly obvious fall of a man into sin is always preceded by low moments in prayer, bible study and watchfulness.The devil will not strike his deadly blow of moral failure on a believer until the low moments sets in.*

*The antidote to low moments is to stay in the place of consistent fellowship with God in the place of prayer, word and watchfulness. We cannot outgrow these essentials. Show me a sinning believer and I'll describe to you the temperature of his relationship with God*.

*When the devil want to deal with a believer, he makes such a person to become too busy to pray, makes bible study a boring burden thereby reducing the spiritual strength of such a person.*

*Spiritual manifestations in your meetings isn't a measure of your spiritual stamina. Samson was still performing exploits when he was playing around with strange women*. When Delilah dealt with him, he thought he could do like before not knowing the Spirit of God had left him. Low moments is the precursor to a fall.

*If you're in your low moments, you need to come out of it quickly by retreating from activities and staying alone with God until you sense the surge of God's revival in your spiritual life*.

If you're a preacher, you may need to turn down preaching invitations and go sort yourself with God till you're back on your feet on again. Don't jump on the altar with weak spiritual stamina and empty tank.

*PRAYER*: Father, I receive strength in my inner man. I need a fresh baptism of the Spirit of prayer, bible study and watchfulness today. Fill me Lord.


====================================================================================================
DEVOTIONAL 0977 | Source start line: 93894
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
9/12/23, 6:00 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: ProjectSave Devotional
Tuesday
September 12th, 2023.

*ON THIS DAY, IN THE YEAR 2020*

Psalm34:1 *I will bless the Lord at all times: his praise shall continually be in my mouth*.

September 12th, 2020 stands as one of the memorable days in my life. Though It was not the most memorable day because there were other days in-between that year that stood out because of notable events.

That day marked the beginning of a turbulent journey that shook my faith and those around me.  I traveled to one of the neighbouring West African states, (Ghana to be precise)for  evangelistic outreaches.

I took a young man along with me for media coverage. He has never been part of my team but he was recommended by my media assistant who couldn't make the journey.

Getting to the airport by 3pm, we were meant to fly to Accra by 8:45pm that day but we had a delay till the next day. We didn't fly until after 10am the next day. We were discouraged about the turn of events but I kept praying in the airport.

A number of persons decided to return home because they were tired of the failed promises from the airliners. For me, there was no going back because I've said good bye to friends and families.

When the airline staff told us the likelihood of canceling the flight, I walked to one of them telling her that a king sent me on errand. I told her that if I don't go, many souls will die and go to hell. I told her I was on a rescue mission. I looked strange to those airline staff.

I paced the floor of the airport in the midnight praying when almost everyone had slept. When it was daybreak, they told us to get ready because an aeroplane was coming from Accra to pick us. God answered my prayer and the young man with me was excited.

When we got to Ghana, we had wonderful meetings. Souls responded to the call for salvation and there were numerous miracles such that hasn't happened before in our meetings. A lady on crutches was healed miraculously, swellings disappeared, pains were healed instantly and in all Jesus was glorified.

After the whole program, the first thing that happened was the cancelation of our flight and we had to reschedule. In-between, we tested positive to COVID-19. That was a huge blow to our faith. I felt like Elijah who killed prophets of Baal but ran from Jezebel.

We spent weeks in Ghana more than scheduled to the point where we couldn't pay hotel bills not because we don't have money but because if we had continued, we wouldn't have anything left on us for our return.

The young man had very bad symptoms. He could barely walk. On one of the Sundays, I called for an ambulance to come pick him because his case was an emergency. The ambulance didn't come so I quickly got a taxi but I didn't tell the driver we both had covid.

This happened In a country where we don't have families and relatives, where you cannot easily access funds. Thank God for bro Walter Chuku, he was there for us. The mother of this young man became very disturbed emotionally. The devil kept telling me the young man will die and I will spend two years in jail when I return to Nigeria because the family will sue me. I rejected his insinuations.

If that young man had died, my ministry may have ended or tainted with a bad image forever because a number of persons may have concluded that I took him out of the country for rituals. *My intention is not to tell stories today but to thank the father of all mercies who kept me and saved me from shame*.

*It is good to thank God always and not forget His benefits. Whatever God has done in the past(big/small), please never forget it*. Our amnesia about God's greatness and deliverances shows that we do not value His interventions but for me, I value this and several other things the Lord has done for me: *"Bless the LORD, O my soul: And all that is within me, bless his holy name.*" Psalm 103:1 KJV

Praise the Lord with us!

Song of the day: https://youtu.be/-15v9iworAU?si=qeNfFSaKFj7kkC7W

====================================================================================================
DEVOTIONAL 0978 | Source start line: 93961
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
9/13/23, 12:25 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: ProjectSave Devotional
Wednesday
September 13th, 2023.

*'HE IS YOUR DADDY BUT HE IS MY SON'*.

2 Corinthians 6:18: *"And I will be a father to you, and you shall be sons and daughters to me, says the Lord Almighty*".

During one of my trips on mission assignment to a foreign mission field, my children stayed with my mom for a period of time.

They told her that they wanted to talk to me. She reached out to me on whatsapp telling me the children wanted to hear my voice. After delaying almost all day, I called. At the background, the children were eager to talk to me likewise my mom.

I perceived they were dragging the phone with her  because they wanted to talk to me first before her. They were excited I called. I heard my mom say, "He is your daddy but he is my son". That struck a chord in me.

I picked two major lessons from that scenario. First, no matter how grown a child is, he remains "my son" or "my daughter" to his parents even at 100 years of age. Let me relate this story to our relationship with God.

*God is our father and we are his sons. No matter the title people call us here on earth, we stand before him as sons not as daddies. As a preacher, you're only a 'papa' or 'daddy' on earth to your members. In God's sight, you are a son. We all stand equal as sons before God*.

*Second, you can't be too great or big for fellowship with your heavenly father*. In fact, God longs to fellowship with us more than we desire to fellowship with him. In that story, my mom longed to speak with me likewise my children.

*In our fellowship culture, God comes first before others. There are preachers who prioritise praying and counseling church members than staying alone with the person to whom they stand as son. Their voices are heard by men who call them "daddy" or "papa" but their father hasn't heard from them in a while*.

It is an anomaly to be busy with men either as a minister, business man or parent and not be available for fellowship with God. Do not let what people call you get your attention and drag you away from God.

*Pay attention to the one who calls you 'My son'. From him, you'll receive abundance of supply for those who call you 'daddy'*.

*PRAYER*: Father, I receive the grace to spend quality time with you in Jesus name. Amen

DEVOTIONAL 0979 | Source start line: 93989
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
9/14/23, 7:14 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: PROJECTSAVE DEVOTIONAL
Thursday,
September 14th, 2023.


*WHO IS USING A CARNAL BELIEVER, GOD OR SATAN*?


Isaiah 6:8 *Then I heard the Lord asking, “Whom should I send as a messenger to this people? Who will go for us?” I said, “Here I am. Send me*.” NLT


One of my trainers told me about an adulterous prophet who was warned about his lifestyle. He said, "After all, my prophecies still come to pass". That man was deceived by performance. He has forgotten that Satan can also give counterfeit gifts to those who do his bidding.


You may be wondering why some persons you know who live carnally are doing "mighty" things. Seeing people like this,  it is common to quickly adjudge them as being used by God but is it God that is using them?


The word 'carnal' is translated from the Greek word "sarkikos", which literally means “fleshly.” This is the word Paul used in describing some of the Corinthian brethren in 1 Corinthians 3:1-3.


*And I, brethren, could not speak unto you as unto spiritual, but as unto carnal, even as unto babes in Christ. I have fed you with milk, and not with meat: for hitherto ye were not able to bear it, neither yet now are ye able. For ye are yet carnal: for whereas there is among you envying, and strife, and divisions, are ye not carnal, and walk as men*?(1 Corinthians 3:1-3 KJV).


To be carnal is to be worldly. It is to give in to the cravings of the sinful nature and to fulfil its desires. Here, we are talking about those who live carnal lives perpetually. No repentance no change of heart. This our concern.


In the book of Matthew chapter 7, Jesus spoke about some persons who on the last day will lay claim to being used by God with exploits done in His name yet they are not known in heaven.


*"Not every one that saith unto me, Lord, Lord, shall enter into the kingdom of heaven; but he that doeth the will of my Father which is in heaven. Many will say to me in that day, Lord, Lord, have we not prophesied in thy name? and in thy name have cast out devils? and in thy name done many wonderful works? And then will I profess unto them, I never knew you: depart from me, ye that work iniquity*".(Matthew7:21-23 KJV).


The scripture above shows clearly that it is possible to do great things in the name of Jesus yet not known by Jesus. *One deception that comes with gift is to make the gifted think He is in tune with God when he is not*. The devil knows how to do this.


*Satan can use a carnal believer and give him counterfeit gifts*. To be truly used by God, your life must be attuned to His will. That standard hasn't changed.


James asked a question still begging for answer: *"Does a spring of water bubble out with both fresh water and bitter water? Does a fig tree produce olives, or a grapevine produce figs? No, and you can’t draw fresh water from a salty spring*" (James 3:11‭-‬12 NLT).


One source can't produce two things. Forget about those you know who speak in tongues, work miracles and still live impure lives. Don't let Satan deceive you into thinking God uses anyone who lives in impurity.


Apostle Paul says, *IF you keep yourself PURE,  you will be a special utensil for honorable use. YOUR LIFE WILL BE CLEAN, AND YOU WILL BE READY FOR THE MASTER TO USE YOU FOR EVERY GOOD WORK.*"(2 Timothy 2:21 NLT). This is the standard of God for anyone He want to use- purity.


*PRAYER*: Father, purge me.

DEVOTIONAL 0980 | Source start line: 94065

9/15/23, 12:20 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: PROJECTSAVE DEVOTIONAL
Friday,
September 15th, 2023.

*STOP HATING ON THE CHURCH*

Ephesians 5:29 For no one ever hated his own flesh, but nourishes and cherishes it, just as Christ does the church (ESV).

In recent times, there seems an increase in online posts by self-proclaimed 'special ministers' actively attacking Churches across various denominations worldwide. Deep within,  I know better than joining forces with them since I believe such actions lack divine backing whatsoever!

Out of hatred for the church, some persons attribute the failures of our political leaders to the church. I wonder how the church of Christ is responsible for unemployment, insecurity, bad infrastructures and other problems bedevilling our country.

Instead of holding politicians responsible for their inability to fulfill their promises to the electorate, these church-haters attack pastors. When they see a church embarking on a building project, they make statements like: "Why can't they use that money to build industries that will create jobs"? Hey! job creation is the work of industrialists and political leaders not the church or pastors.

I think these church critics should exercise some discretion. In all my adult life, I have not seen any advanced country where people point fingers at the church for unemployment, insecurity and other societal peoblems. Don't demand from your pastors or the church what you should demand from your political leaders.

Due to this wide spread animosity against the church from online bullies, we now have many out-of-church believers who prefer to 'worship' online with their new found preacher. Unfortunately, a significant number of these church-haters operate under pseudo-ministries, which espouse falsehoods, attacks, and diatribes against the church.

*They lure unsuspecting individuals into an isolated spiritual wilderness where they are disconnected from congregations that offer fellowship and spiritual sustenance. I am a stickler for proper pastoring and this cannot be done remotely*.

The scripture admonishes us not to forsake our meetings with one another, as some do but instead encourages us to support each other in preparation for His imminent return (Hebrews 10:25 NLT).


*The Church is God's divine plan; He has no alternative blueprint for salvation*. I am aware that painful and sad experiences of people with some pastors and churches made them turn their backs against the church.

However, this does not justify taking sides with Satan against God's chosen instrument - His Church. *If you have developed hatred towards the church or all that it represents regarding what God is doing through her, then you cannot be used by Him*.

Christ declared that upon
the rock, he would build his church such that even all powers of hell could not conquer it (Matthew 16:18).

Any ministry or minister contrary to the teachings of Christ's Church will eventually fail because we must beware of those who pit themselves against sound doctrine and leadership within churches.

No matter how intellectually gifted they may appear or how impressive their theological arguments might seem, God is against their actions. *God will not support someone or a ministry that aims to bulldoze what he is building*.

God never scatters His own institutions; although there may be pseudo-churches here and there globally, true followers should embrace any opportunity available in every local assembly where they can learn more about him without subjecting themselves unnecessarily to hateful debate and attacks on social media platforms.

*While acknowledging that no human organization is perfect per se, Christ remains committed as a perfecter of his Church by washing her continually while purging impurities thereof until she becomes spotless before Him (Ephesians 5:27)*.

Why stand up against what He is shaping? Today many like Saul attack churches indiscriminately without realizing they are fighting a lost battle ultimately destined for failure. Please, do not join them. In history, all enemies of the church failed.

I want you to love the church and what Christ is doing in and through her. No church on earth is perfect but our joy is that Christ is perfecting us daily.


*PRAYER*: Father,please help me stay faithful both inwardly &outwardly partaking fully in my local congregation trusting You always in Jesus name.

DEVOTIONAL 0981 | Source start line: 94116
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
9/16/23, 7:26 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: PROJECTSAVE DEVOTIONAL
Saturday,
September 16th, 2023.

*CALLED TO BE A DISCIPLE*

Luke 9:23 “Then he said to them all: “Whoever wants to be my disciple must deny themselves and take up their cross daily and follow me.”

We live in a title crazy world. Here in Africa, people value their titles. If you don't call them by their titles, they may take offense. Somehow, this crept into the church. Ministry offices/ assignments are now seen as titles that must preceed names.

When God called us into the fold, we were called to be 'disciples' of Jesus not carriers of titles and appelations.

The term 'disciple' is from the Koine Greek word "mathētḗs". It simply means "one who engages in learning through instruction from another, pupil, apprentice".

When Jesus called the twelve to be His disciples, they understood Him perfectly because the Jewish concept of discipleship is akin to apprenticeship.

It involves followership through learning and imitation. That is why the twelve abandoned all they were doing to follow Jesus and to learn from Him daily.

Central to our walk with Jesus is discipleship. *A common calling that we all have is the call to discipleship- a follower of Jesus Christ.*

In Matthew 28:19, Jesus said, *"Therefore, go and make disciples of all the nations, baptizing them in the name of the Father and the Son and the Holy Spirit.*"

As we follow Christ daily, our aim should be to make others a follower of Christ. *Discipleship is not a tea party, it is not an event neither is it a class, it is a life style that is committed to following one person(Christ), to become like Him*.

Today, some are beginning to turn this concept around to mean what it is not. Their understanding of discipleship is that which is centred around man instead of Christ.

Some preachers are proud to call those around them "my disciples" instead of disciples of Christ. That's similar to what the Pharisees were doing in the days of Jesus, they took the place of God in the hearts of men that was why Jesus said, *"And do not call anyone on earth ‘father,’ for you have one Father, and he is in heaven.*"(Matthew 23:9 NIV).

They had large followership to the point where people no longer see God as their father but the Pharisees. They were hypocrites and they made others children of hell like they were. That's why Jesus was at loggerheads with them. They misrepresented the whole concept of discipleship.

*In Christian discipleship, we follow Christ not man. God can use man to teach us Christ but we should bear in mind that the person we are following ultimately is Christ not that man.*

This is a kingdom value we must not jettison for anything. The faith of some believers becomes weak when their human model fails and falls. It shouldn't be.

The writer of Hebrews says, *"Looking unto Jesus the author and finisher of our faith; who for the joy that was set before him endured the cross, despising the shame, and is set down at the right hand of the throne of God.*"(Heb12:2).

Henceforth, you must make Christ your object of focus, learn of Him and walk in His footsteps.

*PRAYER*: Father, I receive grace to walk in the footsteps of Christ and to be His lifelong disciple in Jesus name.

DEVOTIONAL 0982 | Source start line: 94163
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
9/17/23, 7:12 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: ProjectSave Devotional
Sunday,
September 17th, 2023.

*BROTHER APOLLOS* Pt.1

Acts18:24 *Meanwhile a Jew named Apollos, a native of Alexandria, came to Ephesus. He was a learned man, with a thorough knowledge of the Scriptures*(NIV).

*SOUND SCRIPTURAL EDUCATION*

While studying the book of Acts of apostles, I saw something about Apollos in the eightenth chapter, from verse 24 downwards. The introduction of this humble brother was without flattery.

They told us who he was, a Jew.  No title,  no office,  no portfolio just a brother or we say a believer. We were not told If he headed a church. If he did,  the scriptures wouldn't be silent about it.

The writer did not stop there,  he went further to tell us about certain striking qualities that made this Christian brother to stand out from among the brethren.

First,  he was learned with a thorough knowledge of the scriptures. Every child of God must emulate Apollos in that regard.  It doesn't  matter whether you are a sanitation officer in church or a chorister.

During my service year,  my national leader in Jos, Plateau state, told us the story of his water baptism experience. He told us that the driver of the bus who drove them to where they did baptism explained the scriptures to them while he was driving them. They were surprised at his depth in the word even though he was the driver of the bus.

That's how every Christian should be. If Pastors can raise men and women to be like Apollos  in the church,  certain burden would be off their shoulders.

*Why some believers are confused and have become a burden to their Pastors in the counseling room is because they are not rooted properly in the word*.

Apollos must have taken his time to dig deep into scriptures and submit to learning before the Bible could say that he was learned in scriptures.  *Do not leave adequate study of the scriptures and learning to your pastors alone*.

*Be serious with the word of God as if you're preaching the next sermon*. I live close to an Islamic theology school. I noticed they have numerous students who enroled for a seven years training in Islamic theology not because they want to pioneer their mosques but because they want to represent their religion well by gaining depth of knowledge.

If you meet heretics or sects,  one thing that stand them out is their confidence and knowledge.  Jehovah witness is a good example. Their young members can confidently engage some senior pastors in debate,  why?  Because they have some depth.

*Who says you cannot be deep in the knowledge of God's word as a believer even without leadership title in church*? Be hungry to know as much as you can through learning.

*PRAYER*: Father, I refuse to be a biblical novice In Jesus name.

DEVOTIONAL 0983 | Source start line: 94200

9/18/23, 5:51 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: PROJECTSAVE DEVOTIONAL
Monday,
September 18th, 2023.

*BROTHER APOLLOS*  Part 2

Acts 18:25 *He had been instructed in the way of the Lord, and he spoke with great fervor and taught about Jesus accurately, though he knew only the baptism of John* ( NIV).

*HE TAUGHT ABOUT JESUS ACCURATELY*

Sometime ago, I discussed with someone about a common trend in the evangelistic method of some believers. During evangelism, it is common to hear some folks say things like: "Come and listen to my papa preach'.

When given the opportunity to preach,  they can hardly make a statement or two without saying,  'Papa said'.  They know so much about 'Papa' but they know little or nothing about Christ.  Another funny invite I got was to come hear "my prophet". I use to have a funny barber who did not preach Christ to me but his prophet.

Some teach and preach Papa's or prophet's 'theology' but not the theology of Christ. Men are popular on their lips more than Christ. Why?  They haven't met the Jesus of the Bible though they know their papa.

Brother Apollos was not teaching about another person save Christ. There's a hymn I love so much.  One of the stanzas read thus:
Jesus only is our message,
Jesus all our theme shall be;
We will lift up Jesus ever,
Jesus only will we see.

One of the offshoot of acquiring sound bible-based education as we were taught yesterday is that it would help you to present Christ and teach him accurately like Apollos did. We need to return to preaching Christ to our world.

*You cannot  accurately teach what you have not learnt*. The bane of preaching and teaching today is that we have many who want to or have assumed the position of preachers& teachers without being taught.

Jesus gave a learning invitation to all in Matthew 11:29 : "Take my yoke upon you, and *learn of me*, for I am meek and lowly in heart: and ye shall find rest unto your souls". Why would Jesus tell us to come learn of him?

The reason is because He is the central theme of the Bible from Genesis to revelation. He was concealed in the old testament but fully revealed in the new testament. The true gospel is focused on him and no one else.

Though brother Apollos knew only the baptism of John, yet, his knowledge about Christ was sound. Paul's desire was to know Christ more, Phil3:10.  Little wonder why he taught about Christ deeply and accurately. To teach Christ accurately, he must be revealed to you from his word and you have to learn of him.

*PRAYER*: Father,  reveal Christ to me more through your word and in my heart In Jesus name.

DEVOTIONAL 0984 | Source start line: 94234
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
9/19/23, 6:06 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: ProjectSave Devotional
Tuesday
September 19th, 2023.

*BROTHER APOLLOS*  Part 3

Acts18:26 *He began to speak boldly in the synagogue. When Priscilla and Aquila heard him, they invited him to their home and explained to him the way of God more adequately* (NIV).

*SUBMITTED HIMSELF AS A DISCIPLE*

The notion of discipleship is well-defined among the Jewish community, yet individuals with a Western perspective may struggle to comprehend its true essence. As Africans, however, we possess some level of understanding regarding this concept.

Within the Jewish culture, apprenticeship programs are deeply ingrained in nearly every trade or profession and require a significant investment of time before one can become a master. It is not merely a one-year commitment but rather a lifelong pursuit.

I acknowledge that, even after attaining mastery in a profession, the previous master's years of experience may continue to warrant their recognition as such by graduate apprentices. This is why Jesus stated: *"A servant cannot be greater than his master*"(John 15:20).

Brother Apollos humbly submitted himself to learning and discipleship under Aquila and Priscilla, despite possessing extensive scriptural knowledge and boldly preaching in the synagogue.  Although he may have been drawing crowds with his teachings, he recognized the value of receiving instruction from those who were more spiritually advanced than him.

*One of the bane of fame and popularity in ministry is the feeling of "I don't need discipler". In fact, the anointed and famous may not listen to those he perceived haven't attained his height whereas depth is qualifies people to become disciplers not height*.

*You may have the height in ministry but someone who may be unknown have the depth you need to sustain that height*. In such instances, it would be wise to seek out and learn from these individuals.

Apollos recognized the vital role of discipleship in achieving success and fulfillment in both his personal and spiritual pursuits. I pose this question to you: who is currently serving as your mentor or discipler?

Although some individuals may claim that the Holy Spirit fulfills this role, it remains true that even the most accomplished leaders within the kingdom have relied upon a strong support system comprised of trusted guides and mentors.

Despite Moses' close relationship, God did not teach him everything he needed to know. However, Jethro provided leadership and delegation guidance to Moses, even though his congregation was not as large as Moses'.

It is essential to remember that no matter how far one has progressed, sitting under a discipler and learning more is crucial. Regardless of how advanced you believe your knowledge to be, humbly submit yourself to a mentor and continue learning.

*PRAYER:* Heavenly Father, I beseech thee for the grace to fully embrace discipleship in the name of Jesus.

9/20/23, 6:38 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: ProjectSave Devotional
Wednesday
September 20th, 2023.

*YOUR COMPANY MATTERS*

Acts4:23 *And being let go, they went to their own company, and reported all that the chief priests and elders had said unto them*(KJV).

During my time on campus, I shared a living space with an individual who frequently engaged in the activity of clubbing alongside his companions. At the onset of each new academic term, this person exhibited extravagant and careless behaviour. When he possessed ample funds, he often displayed conceited and haughty tendencies. However, I observed that his demeanor shifted towards one of modesty whenever he found himself experiencing financial hardship. Sometime, this individual would even approach me humbly to request basic sustenance such as noodles.

On a certain day, I decided to question why his friends in club couldn't help him in seasons of hunger. He told me that even in severe hunger, his friends won't buy him a meal except alcohol. The cost of a bottle of beer could buy him a meal but his friends won't. They don't mind buying him bottles of beer but they never bought him a meal. Bad company!

In chapter three of Acts,  Peter and John healed the man that was lame from birth. That led to a stiff opposition from the religious leaders of their day.

They faced the council of religious lords because the miracle they performed by the power of the Spirit made news. Also, many people  converted to the Christian faith from Judaism. They decided to call Peter and John to order but they refused to heed their threat.

When they left, we were told that Peter and John went to their "own company". The word "company" in the opening text could mean colleagues,  friends or associates.

Consider this,  after facing a panel of big wigs in the Mosaic law, If they had returned home,  their faith may shake but they knew the right place to go,  they went to people of like minds who could fire up their faith for world evangelization.

It is probable that some of the brethren in their company told them not to worry about the threats of religious leaders. That encouragement was enough to fire them on.

We were told that after they reported all that happened to their company, they all prayed together and the place was shaken, Acts4:31.

In your down moments, do you have a company of people whose words can lift you? Who are the men in your company? Are they vision killers or vision boosters? Do you have friends that can challenge you to pray and be better spiritually?

*Your company is a determinant of what will accompany you*. It is not good If men in your company cannot give you wise counsel.

Stop surrounding yourself with 'Yes' men.  Feel free to evict those that do not believe in your vision from your company because they will always demotivate you and they may kill your vision in the long run.

*In your company,  include vision boosters, men that can stir you to wholesome thinking and fervent prayers so you can get back on your feet when you run to them in your down moments*.

*ACTION POINT*: You must be selective to be productive. The power to decide those that will be in your company is in your hands.
