9/21/23, 6:21 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: PROJECTSAVE DEVOTIONAL
Thursday,
September 21st, 2023.


*A CLEAR PERSPECTIVE ON FAITH*


Hebrews 11:1 *Faith shows the reality of what we hope for; it is the evidence of things we cannot see*( NLT).


Allow me to demonstrate the concept of faith using this lovely example. Imagine that the wealthiest individual on earth presents you with a cheque for $1 million dollars on a weekend when banks are closed, with the expectation that you will deposit it into your account once they reopen. Would you question the validity of this cheque?


I'm sure you will not because you know the person who gave you the cheque is worth more than the amount and is capable of giving more. Also, his signature on the cheque is authentic and that's enough to get the money out.


Even though you haven't gotten the cash physically in your hands or transferred the money to your bank account, in your mind, you believe without doubt that you are $1 million dollar richer.


In fact, you may start planning what to do with the money as though you have it. That is what faith in God is.


*When you strongly believe what you have not seen because someone superior with a proven track record of integrity promised you those things, that is simply faith.*


*God's word is the promissory note that forms the basis of our believing even when we haven't seen what is promised*.  Faith in God's word is of great value  to God. He is honoured when we trust Him and take Him at His word.


One line in our hymn says, "Tis so sweet to trust in Jesus, just to take Him at His word". *Faith is taking God at His word saying "according to your word I believe*".

*The integrity of God is tied to His word*. God doesn't deny His word. At every point in our lives, God want us to take Him at His word in prayer.


*It should be noted that you don't need to see what is promised before believing, rather you believe before seeing what is promised*. You act and talk as though you have it already even when your physical eyes haven't seen it. That's faith.

*Faith is believing God's word, talking God's word and acting on God's word.* Start believing God's word. God places value on a life of faith.

It takes faith to obey God. For example, God may be calling you to do great things for Him in the kingdom yet you may not have resources. Faith will launch out trusting God to bring needed resources for the task.

It is God's desire that faith becomes our lifestyle and not just a tool of solving problems when they arise. .

*CONFESSION:* My faith is activated by the Word. I live in the realm of possibilities. All things are working for me by Faith, I believe I have my anwers already in the name of Jesus.


info@projectsaveng.org

*WHY DECEPTION THRIVE*

Joshua 9:14-15 *So the Israelites examined their food, but they did not consult the Lord. Then Joshua made a peace treaty with them and guaranteed their safety, and the leaders of the community ratified their agreement with a binding oath* ( NLT)

Recently, I began to study the book of Joshua as part of my devotional reading of scriptures. When I got to the story on how the Gibeonites deceived Joshua and the Israelites, my eyes opened to a truth I have not seen for all the years i've been reading that portion of scripture.

The Gibeonites occupied one of the lands the Israelites were meant to conquer and take as their possession. Out of fear of being overun by the armies of Israel, they devised a scheme to deceive Joshua into making a peace treaty with them. Joshua fell for it. Later, he realized they were deceived but it was too late to rescind his decision because he made an oath with them in God's presence.

I saw the reason Joshua and the elders of Israel were deceived in chapter 9 verse 14: *"So the Israelites examined their food, BUT THEY DID NOT CONSULT THE LORD*".

Any time we fail to seek God's face concerning pending choices and decisions, we open ourselves to deception. With my little experience, there are phony, fake and deceptive people around who will always want to take advantage of our kindness to their selfish advantage.

Several stories abound among God's people about how they were deceived into marriages, businesses and other life endeavours. Long ago, I heard the story of a committed church sister who was deceived into marriage by a muslim who pretended as a christian brother. On the night of their wedding, he declared his identity and mandated her to start praying the muslim way.

I am very sure it is not the will of God for anyone who has fallen victim of deceit to be deceived. *Oftentimes, God's people bypass spiritual order and fall headlong into the pool of deception*.

To think God is pleased when his children are deceived is to have a very wrong view of Him. He gave us an assurance of comprehensive guidance in his word: *"The Lord says, “I will guide you along the best pathway for your life. I will advise you and watch over you*"( Psalms 32:8 NLT). Reading this text of scripture, do you think God is pleased If we are deceived?

If we fall prey of deception in any form, it is traceable to our failure in seeking God's face and waiting on him patiently for answer. *It is one thing to seek God's face, it is another thing to wait on him patiently for answers*.

*Don't run ahead of God. Never be in haste to make crucial decisions that can make or mar your life. Haste is the undoing of fools*. Before making that decision, what is God saying?

*PRAYER*: Father, I receive the grace to wait on you patiently and never to be in haste until you give a go ahead in Jesus name.

*PURPOSE OF MIRACLES*. Pt.1

Acts 9:32-35 As Peter traveled about the country, he went to visit the Lord’s people... There he found a man named Aeneas, who was paralyzed and had been bedridden for eight years. “Aeneas,” Peter said to him, “Jesus Christ heals you. Get up and roll up your mat.” Immediately Aeneas got up. *All those who lived in Lydda and Sharon saw him and turned to the Lord*( NIV).

*FOR SOUL-WINING*

There are many christians or ministers today desiring to see miracles happen in their meetings but nothing is happening. The desire to see miracles happen at all cost has led some to seek diabolic powers.


Myles Munroe said,  'If the purpose of a thing is not known, abuse is inevitable'. God has a purpose for the miraculous. It is not for show-off or to make us look special in the eyes of the people.

The story of Apostle Peter and Aeneas revealed to us one of the purposes of miracles. The man Aeneas had been bedridden for eight years.

One of the things He has in mind is to bring many to the saving knowledge of Christ through the display of supernatural. *If you don't have soul-winning in mind,  you will not operate in the miraculous*.

Lost souls are very important to God.

*PRAYER*: Father, I receive grace to align my thoughts to yours In Jesus name.

*PURPOSE OF MIRACLES* Pt. 2

John9:3 “Neither this man nor his parents sinned,” said Jesus, “but this happened so that the works of God might be displayed in him

People began to die and strange things began to happen. The Chief priest wanted the people to come bowing down to his idols again so that Satan can be worshipped.

We were told in Genesis 1:31 that when God looked upon all that he had created,  they were very good. God couldn't have  made him blind, it was the devil.  He seeks to destroy all that God has made and to pervert it. He is behind the woes and pains of men today.

So why would God allow a miracle to take place where the devil has wrecked havoc?  The answer is obvious.  Anywhere the devil has taken glory through his dirty works of destruction, God may perform miracles to take the glory in such cases.

The devil is attacking you because he want you to compromise your faith and  give him worship so that he can take the glory but God will not share his glory with any man.

The miraculous happens so that only Him can take the glory by providing solution. God takes the glory in your wellness not in your sickness and pain. He takes the glory in your restoration not in frustration.

*PRAYER*:  Father, perform a great miracle in my life so that you can take the glory, in Jesus name.

*PURPOSE OF MIRACLES* Pt.3

Acts4:4 *But many who heard the message believed; so the number of men who believed grew to about five thousand* (NIV).

*BREAKING DEMONIC SOUL-TIES*

1 For 6:16 *Or do you not know that he who is joined to a prostitute becomes one body with her? For, as it is written, “The two will become one flesh”* (ESV).

The late Reverend Ezekiel Atang recounted the tale of a lady whose boyfriend endeavoured to remove her eyes during an altercation. Miraculously, she eluded his grasp and many assumed their romantic liaison had terminated. Yet, before long they reunited, much to the astonishment of all who witnessed it.

One anecdote that has remained etched in my memory is the account of a young lady who confided in me about her abusive partner. Despite urging her to leave him, she opined that he only hit her out of love. Her words left me speechless.
What transpired in both narratives is the potency of soul ties - an unbreakable bond formed between two individuals through sexual intimacy.

What constitutes a soul tie? *A soul tie denotes the intangible bond that links two or more individuals on a spiritual level, frequently established through sexual activity*.

The Bible doesn’t use the word soul tie, but it speaks of  souls being knit together, becoming one flesh, etc. Soul ties are not necessarily bad.

The bonds of the soul that exist between married partners attract them to one another like magnets. However, when it comes to those who engage in adultery and fornication, these same ties can lead a woman who has been mistreated and harmed by her partner to seek him out despite his lack of affection towards her and abusive treatment. In such cases, she may find herself inexplicably drawn to a man whom she would ordinarily despise and avoid at all costs in the natural world.

You might have seen something like this but do not understand what it is. This is where the danger of sexual immorality lies.

*When soul tie is formed on the on altar of sexual immorality, a single lady or brother will become blind to the killer flaws of his/ her partner only to realize how dangerous those flaws are in marriage." That's one of the reasons we preach against sex before marriage.

If soul tie is not broken between erstwhile lovers who are married to separate spouses, they can engage in extra marital affair with each other If given the opportunity. That's what some commonly call "sparking Old flames".

Ladies hardly break free from their first boy friend especially the man that deflowered them. That's why we teach total separation between people who were once sexually active.

*In the demonic world, unholy soul ties can serve as bridges between two people to pass demonic garbage( curses, retrogression, sickness,etc) through. It is the means of demonic spirit transfer.*

Many people have become demonized unknowingly through sex. They fight spiritual battles they inherited through sex. There are sexually transmitted demons. This is a deep spiritual truth many struggle to understand.

Soul tie is used by witches and those in the occult to manipulate their victims. As long as there is sexual intercourse between them and their victim, they take advantage of the soul tie formed  to manipulate their victims. Illicit sex is a tool of witches to manipulate people.

Some men are under heavy manipulation from ladies they've slept with and their wives would be wondering what is wrong with their  husbands.

I bring to you a message of deliverance. No matter how long the enemy has dealt with you through soul ties, Jesus can set you free.

*"Shall the prey be taken from the mighty, or the lawful captive delivered? But thus saith the Lord, Even the captives of the mighty shall be taken away, and the prey of the terrible shall be delivered: for I will contend with him that contendeth with thee, and I will save thy children.*"( Isaiah 49:24-25).

To be free, you need to renounce all ungodly relationship, sever ties, repent and engage in fervent prayers.


*PRAYER*: Father, I break every soul tie formed on the altar of sexual immorality in Jesus name. I receive total deliverance from the garbage brought into my life through soul ties.

*TESTS PRECEDING PROMOTION*. Pt. 1

Gen 39:9 *There is none greater in this house than I, neither hath he kept back any thing from me but thee, because thou art his wife. How then can I do this great wickedness, and sin against God*?”

*THE FEMALE TEST*

Many believers engage in prayer and fasting with the aim of securing a promotion, yet they often overlook the fact that certain tests must be successfully completed prior to attaining such an advancement. This is akin to the academic setting where promotion is contingent upon passing prerequisite courses.

On his journey to greatness, Joseph met with one of the greatest test that many men failed- female test. The story of Samson and Reuben comes to mind also.

Samson's destiny was divinely declared to his parents before he came to the world. His kind of anointing was rare. Unfortunately, when he sat for female test, he failed. A bright destiny became bleak.

Reuben would have inherited more than half of his father's estate as the first son but the sin of sexual immorality reduced him to a mere man among his brothers. In my opinion, Judah took the shine off him.

*I am not sure there is a male  that will not face the female test especially If they are going somewhere in life. Every great man in the kingdom faced this test, some passed some failed*.

In my view, the Almighty does not arbitrarily elevate individuals without subjecting them to rigorous assessments. While supplicating for promotion is commendable, it cannot be secured solely through prayer or even by receiving anointment with 'promotion oil'.

*If you doubt divine tests, why did God reject the brothers of David for Kingship as though they sat for a test and chose David as though he passed a test. Have you considered that scenario? God observed their daily lives and was marking their scripts. They didn't know they were in a divine examination hall.*

I perceive God must have observed certain things in the life of Joseph over time before recommending him for the throne. It may surprise you that God had told him earlier in his dream what would become of him, so why the test?

Well, the dream he had was a revelation of God's mind about his life but the tests he must pass to feature in that plan is another thing. The good plan of God for us doesn't change but we may not feature in that plan If we fail divine test. In some cases when men fail, God finds a replacement to fulfill that plan.

If Joseph had agreed to the demands of Mrs Potiphar, he would have remained the chief of staff of slaves in that house because of free sex. He wouldn't have had the opportunity to go to prison and consequently the palace.

God saw in him a man who would not take advantage of women through influence. In the fullness of time,  God placed him on the throne.

*God is shopping for quality men as I speak*. He is watching your relationships with women in the office, church and those sisters in the fellowship whom you have influence over.

*I perceive that some campus youth pastors and excos of yesteryears would have become great today but they cut their journey to greatness short because of how they handled sisters- they failed God*.

*God is tired of priests damaging His daughters. Men with great discipline and value for sexual purity are in high demand to occupy great and strategic places, will God find you worthy?*

*PRAYER*: Father, I will not fail the female test,  give me grace to pass excellently In Jesus name.

*TESTS PRECEDING PROMOTION*. Pt. 2

Matthew 5:5 *“Blessed are the meek, for they shall inherit the earth*


*THE TEST OF MEEKNESS- ABILITY TO CONTROL POWER*.


Sometime ago, I was at a 'suya' (roasted beef) spot. I stood at the extreme end of the line of those waiting to buy 'suya'. The fruit seller beside the 'suya' seller shouted at me not to block her. I moved though I wasn't blocking her.


The man beside me became visibly angry with her because she was fond of shouting at people who come to buy 'suya'. The man told me he would organize an evening raid that will send every trader in that spot packing except the suya seller.


He boasted he will prove how powerful he was to the woman so that she would learn how to talk.  I perceived he was a senior police officer or military man so I told him not to show how powerful he was. That's a lesson in the school of meekness for us all.


*What is meekness from biblical perspective? According to New testament Greek etymology of the word, 'meek or meekness' is 'strength/ power under control'*.


A common illustration is that of a powerful war horse under the control of his master. The horse is powerful but his power is under control. That's the bible way to describe meekness.

*Meekness is the ability to control and use power properly. We can't know you are meek until God gives you power and we see how you use it*.


One of the tests every child of God aspiring to climb the ladder of glory must take is the test of meekness or test of power. God want to see what you will do with power so he'll allow you have a little taste of it.


So many glory climbers have failed the test of meekness. There are those who use anointing to curse or to take advantage of people financially.

*Why God may not give some persons the power they desire is because he has seen their tendencies to abuse power when he gave them little power. The anointing in the life of some persons might become commercialized*.


I remember the story of a corpers' fellowship president who had sisters that undressed him and read Bible into his hearing while he sleeps on the bed like a don. Just a little taste of power, he messed it up.


I was an active student leader as well and I saw several anomalies all in the name respecting the office of the 'president'. I have asked severally, "where are some of those campus pastors of yesteryears that lived like Lords"?


We thought some would leave school and become great preachers but some are nowhere to be found. Some returned to their home churches and couldn't serve in units and departments because they tasted little power and became corrupted.


Heaven is saying that some failed the meekness test.  We saw the glamour of their office, how their mates were compelled to wash and iron their clothes, cook for them and even carry their bibles but God's score sheet is reading ten over hundred.


Some would have become greater than we thought but they failed the test of meekness when they were given little power.


*Every man desiring a new level of glory in God must pass the test of meekness before moving to the next level. That office is a test*.

*Don't use your power to take advantage of people or to show how powerful you are to those that offend you*.


*PRAYER*: Father,  may I not fail the test of meekness In Jesus name

*TESTS PRECEDING PROMOTION* Pt.3

1 Timothy 6:10 *For the love of money is the root of all evil: which while some coveted after, they have erred from the faith, and pierced themselves through with many sorrows*(KJV).

*THE MONEY TEST*

I have an experience along this long which I have shared several times. Years back,  I returned from a mission trip with a few thousands left from the traveling fare given to me by the ministry. As it is our custom, every left over fund must be returned into the ministry's account.

On this fateful day, when I returned to my base in Nigeria, I met everyone at home including my mother-in-law whom I left at home before embarking on my mission trip.  She delayed her return till after my trip.

Upon my return, the ideal thing was to honour her with gifts especially money having spent time with us. Unfortunately,  what was left in my personal account was not up to N10,000 but the ministry's cash in my hand was much more.

My wife asked what we would give her,  I told her God would provide. In my heart, the temptation to take from the ministry's money arose but I made a hard decision not to touch the money. It was really hard because there was a battle within either to take from it or not.

Some minutes after talking to my wife,  a brethren residing in the north requested for my account details.  I did not tell this person I needed money neither did I make any subtle request.  God saw my heart.

Shortly, after I sent my account details,  I received an alert that settled the needs. When I thought about the whole scenario and how it played out, I realized It was a test. I passed the money test.

If I had taken that money, nobody would know including my wife because it is what I declare that would be recorded but I would have lost credibility with God. Some (ministers inclusive) have lost credibility with God little wonder great finances is not flowing in their direction.

*As you climb the ladder of glory,  the money test will come. Can we keep money in your care and still have it intact upon request without hearing spurious excuses on "legitimate" needs you met with money entrusted in your care*?

*As a preacher, you may be tempted to start preaching and praying for money because of the anointing*. When you start striking a monetary deal or you bargain with people before ministering to them,  you have failed money test.  Your anointing may be flowing and the people may be getting results but you're no longer communicating life.

Judas Iscariot failed the money test. He stole from the ministry purse of Jesus( John12:6). He lost his Apostleship because of greed for gain. Painfully, some ministers steal from ministry purse. They take what is not given them in the name of borrowing yet they don't refund it.

*Before God will promote you to that level you desire,  you must pass the test of faithfulness in handling money*. How faithful are you in that little office where you work?  Do you steal your boss'money or falsify figures to embezzle money?

*If the greed for gain has conquered your heart,  you may remain on that level for life. God is looking for men He can entrust true wealth into their hands.  Money should be a servant to you not a master*.

*PRAYER:* Father, I receive grace to pass the money  test In Jesus name.  Amen

DEVOTIONAL 0995 | Source start line: 95033

9/30/23, 6:04 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: PROJECTSAVE DEVOTIONAL
Saturday,
September 30th, 2023.

*RESHAPED BY THE POTTER*

Jeremiah 18:4 *But the pot he was shaping from the clay was marred in his hands; so the potter formed it into another pot, shaping it as seemed best to him* ( NIV).

The prophet Jeremiah was dispatched to the potter's house to witness the skilled artisan's manipulation of clay. This occurred during a period when his ministry was at its lowest ebb, as Judah had abandoned their devotion to the Almighty and instead engaged in idolatry despite his preaching.

While observing the potter in his workshop, he witnessed a peculiar occurrence. As the potter worked with the clay, shaping it to his will, an unexpected event transpired - the clay became marred due to impurities within.

However, undeterred by this setback and with unwavering determination, the skilled craftsman continued to hold onto the flawed material and set out to mold it into a new form.

The Divine Creator assumes the role of a potter, while we mere mortals are akin to clay. Our shaping and molding is achieved via life's circumstances and hardships, which serve as the wheel in this grand design.


Nevertheless, like clay, one may encounter difficulties in conforming to the will of God. Upon perusing the text, I pondered over why the clay had become flawed in the hands of its potter.

Should the clay (representing oneself) resist being shaped by its potter (God), it may become marred. The presence of impurities within said clay may also result in marring; however, a silver lining exists - so long as the clay remains within its potter's hands, it can be remolded.

Despite any previous mistakes or struggles with the will of God, there is hope. Although you may have missed opportunities to connect with Him in the past and faced hardship, your current state does not define your potential for transformation.

As long as you remain under His guidance, you can be molded into a form that aligns with His desires.To achieve this transformation, it is essential to surrender yourself completely to God's plan and allow Him to work through you according to His own wisdom.

Embracing this mindset means relinquishing control over your life and trusting in divine guidance. It is wise to reach a point where resistance against God ceases entirely so that He can lead you towards becoming the person He intended for you to be.

Surrender yourself to God and be reshaped.

*PRAYER*: Father, I am in your hands, make me and mould me into what you desire In Jesus name.

DEVOTIONAL 0996 | Source start line: 95066
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
10/1/23, 6:22 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: ProjectSave Devotional
Sunday,
October 1st, 2023.

*HOW AMBITION ALMOST RUINED MY MINISTRY*

Acts 26:19 “And so, King Agrippa, I obeyed that vision from heaven( NLT).

When I sensed the call of God into ministry, my decision was that I'll be the sole or major financier of the ministry. I devised two ways of achieving that. First, I decided i'll go into academics so I could have time for ministry but I discovered that my income may not be enough to fund the ministry so I thought about oil and gas.

I did a post graduate program that has application in oil and gas sector where there was money at that time. I applied to several oil and gas firms. Also, I thought about acquiring some skills that could fetch me money from that sector.

It took time for me to realize my desire was a cocktail of pride and ambition mixed together. I wanted to stand before all to say, "I funded this ministry without collecting a dime from anyone". I rehearsed my pride in my heart and God resisted it.

James 4:6 says, *"And he gives grace generously. As the Scriptures say, “God opposes the proud but gives grace to the humble*".

My ambition to work in oil and gas sector and fund ministry alone could have limited the reach of my ministry. I wanted everything to revolve around me. *Personal income can fund ambition but God is the only one who fund his vision through his people*.

Also, I failed to realize that my personal income, no matter how huge it is, can limit the reach of my vision. That could mean  the ministry will never be richer than my personal income. If I earn N1million, the ministry income is limited to N1million. That way, a limit is placed on the ministry's finances and her operations. God doesn't operate like that.

*When God gives a vision, he want us to trust him for everything. He want us to recognize him as the source not a means.  He( the source) supplies our needs through his people( the means). That is how God fund his work*.

*This is the reason I don't see anyone as a sponsor of our ministry. God is the sponsor, men are just partners who sees what God is doing and has decided to partner with him. We don't have sponsors, we have partners*.

*I am yet to see any man with a God-given vision who supplies all his needs and his vision flourish. The ambition to be the alpha et omega of that divine assignment can ruin your God-given vision*.

Till date, some persons called into full time ministry are still trying to gather enough money through work to start-up. They are delaying the execution of the vision the Lord gave them because they want to take the glory in that they provided the ministry's needs. They forgot God is the source.

*If the Lord has placed a burden on your heart or has given you an assignment, do not try to do it your own way*. It may not be in the area if finance like mine. It could be that God sent you to a village but you're trying to situate your ministry in the city centre to be relevant.

Do not forget that John the baptist ministry was located in the wilderness yet city people went there to meet him. His light shone and the glory of God upon his ministry could not be resisted.

It took time for me to realize some young ministers also choose wives on the basis of profession because they want a spouse whose income can help their ministry and home. Doctors, nurses,  bankers,oil and gas workers are the target here. They will never marry a school teacher even If God leads in that direction.

Quit self tactics and human wisdom. Let God have his way. Just ask him how he want you to go about that assignment.

*PRAYER*: Father, I quit self tactics and human wisdom. I rely on you to help me in the assignment you have given to me in Jesus name.

*DON'T COMPANY WITH ENEMIES OF THE CHURCH*

Ephesians 4:29 *Do not let any unwholesome talk come out of your mouths, but only what is helpful for building others up according to their needs, that it may benefit those who listen*( NIV).

Recently, I read the post of a supposed believer who claimed she stopped supporting her former local church because her senior pastors were living large on church money. She spoke ill of them on social media and others supported her. She said she'll give missionaries her money instead. Very wrong reason to support a missionary.

People who talk like that don't even give missionaries anything because they don't honour any minister be it their pastors or missionaries. They are stingy entities. *When people are stingy, they always look for every reason not to give*. If their pastor drives a good car which may be a gift, they suspect he is using church money. If he lives in a good house and his children attend good schools, they cry foul.

Have you not read in the bible where it says, *"...Yea, let them say continually, Let the LORD be magnified, which hath pleasure in the prosperity of his servant*"( Psalm 35:27 KJV). While God hates greed and materialism among his ministers, he takes pleasure in their well-being and prosperity. Why should you hate what God loves for his servants?

*In fact, I won't encourage anyone to support missionaries because they hate the style of their local church pastors.  Giving should be done out of love and understanding of our stewardship of money not because you want to prove a point*.

Unknown to that lady, I know a little about her former pastors. They have a thriving side business and from there they are making some money to live their lives. I am not holding brief for them but I know her claims are malicious and defamatory.

The demonic entity, which harbours disdain towards ministers of God, has propagated a culture amongst youths to disrespect their elders under the guise of being "woke". This insidious force was unleashed upon our world approximately four years ago and regrettably, many believers have succumbed to its influence.

I am seeing a trend, a negative one that may cut-short the life span of so many young people in the immediate future. In the decalogue,  *"The fifth commandment says, “Honor your father and your mother, that your days may be long in the land that the Lord your God is giving you*” (Exodus 20:12).

Our kingdom figures, who are also parents, should be held to the same standard. Though I acknowledge that there may be flaws within our community that must be addressed in order to prevent negative behaviours from being mimicked by younger generations, I firmly believe that the most effective approach is one grounded in love and a reaffirmation of sound doctrinal teachings. *Publicly shaming God's servants on social media and resorting to name-calling is not an appropriate means of achieving this goal*.

The Almighty is summoning a battalion of youthful individuals who will conquer territories on His behalf. I am privileged to be counted among them. Regrettably, the Evil One is also enlisting those who lack wisdom into his ranks from within the church - both men and women who harbour no positive sentiments towards the institution, scrutinize every aspect of it with disdain, bear animosity towards preachers and are stingy when it comes to supporting Christian missions. It behooves you not to align yourself with their misguided agenda.

*ACTION POINT*: The street of social media is becoming toxic daily, be careful of what gets into your mind from it.

*DO NOT BE DISCOURAGED*

Phil3:13 *No, dear brothers and sisters, I have not achieved it,t but I focus on this one thing: Forgetting the past and looking forward to what lies ahead* (NLT ).

I read the story of three frogs in a pit. They tried jumping out from the pit but all to no avail. Two frogs gave up but the third one kept trying.

The two frogs down the pit were screaming and yelling at their colleague to stop trying but this frog kept trying. Eventually, he succeeded in jumping out of the pit and the two frogs down the pit were surprised.

When he made it up, the two frogs down the pit asked if he heard them. He said 'I was deaf to your yelling and if I heard anything, i thought you were encouraging me to jump harder'.  The other two remained in the pit but this frog became free.

In life, when you attempt to break the status quo and stand out, so many voices would yell and scream at you to stop. Please don't listen, be focused. Those screaming at you today would end up identifying with your success.

I want you to develop a thick skin against discouragement. The road to success in life, career, marriage and ministry is never easy. Sometimes, you may look stupid to people but please keep on. As long as your vision is genuine and your pursuit is legal, you will surely reach the top.

Try out that business idea, don't listen to those that failed. Rather, listen to those that have faced the same challenge and ask for the way out.

*ACTION POINT*: Do not be discouraged. Keep on.

October 4th, 2023.

*FRUIT IS IMPORTANT*

Philippians 1:10 *For I want you to understand what really matters, so that you may live pure and blameless lives until the day of Christ’s return* (NLT).

In Paul's prayer for the Philippians, he desired that they know what matters to God so that they can live a pure and blameless life until the day of Christ's return. What a desire!

It goes to show that If we don't understand the things that matter to God, we'll be trapped in impurity and vain life.

To know what matters to God, Paul prayed saying, *"May you always be filled with the fruit of your salvation—the righteous character produced in your life by Jesus Christ—for this will bring much glory and praise to God*"(Philippians 1:11 NLT).

Every farmer is concerned about getting a bountiful harvest from his/ her farmland. I don't know if there's any farmer whose desire is to labour and not get anything in return.

Farming is very hard. Even mechanized farming is not easy especially If a large expanse of land is to be cultivated. The goal of farming is fruitful harvest.

God's desire for every believer is fruitfulness. He want our lives to bear fruit: *"You did not choose me, but I chose you and appointed you so that you might go and bear fruit—fruit that will last—and so that whatever you ask in my name the Father will give you*" ( John 15:26).

*The fruit a believer should produce is the fruit of salvation, that righteous character that shows that we have encountered Jesus. Actually, that's the pointer to the fact that we are saved*.

A man might be gifted. His life and ministry may be filled with raw manifestations but when the fruit is lacking, he'll make a mess of the gift. *The absence of the fruit makes the gift loose value*.

*I see people spend time fasting and praying for the gift. Some of those you'll find on the mountain are there looking for power. How many times have we tarried in God's presence because we want to bear fruit*?

That fruit of salvation is what we know as the fruit of the Spirit( Galatians 5:22-23). *The man whose life bears fruit is the man that will be pleasing and blameless before God*. We need to submit our lives to be cultivated by God.

How can we bear fruit? How can our lives be fruitful and pleasing to the Lord? The answer to that question is simple. Christ is the vine, we are the branches. If any man abide in Christ, he will bear fruit.

*Abide in me, and I in you. As the branch cannot bear fruit of itself, except it abide in the vine; no more can ye, except ye abide in me. I am the vine, ye are the branches: He that abideth in me, and I in him, the same bringeth forth much fruit: for without me ye can do nothing*( John 15:4-5 KJV).

*PRAYER*: Father, I receive the grace to abide in Christ so that my life can bear fruit in Jesus name.

They didn't pray to 'blow', their prayers focused majorly on kingdom expansion.


Apostle Paul prayed for Philemon that he maybe active in sharing the gospel so that he would have a full understanding of every good thing we have in Christ.  That's a kingdom-centred prayer!


Paul knew that If Philemon was active in sharing his faith in Jesus to sinners, souls would be saved and more people would be added to the kingdom. As he does that, the gospel becomes clearer to him.

As a christian, you need to always pray this type of prayer for yourself and others in the fold. Any pastor whose members are struggling to evangelize, this is the right prayer point for your members. It is a precursor to church growth and kingdom expansion.


Can you imagine having a congregation of 50 active soul-winners whose target is to to win lost souls and bring unchurched people into the church monthly, won't that be massive?

*SEXUAL PURITY- NOT BY HUMAN STRENGTH*

Isaiah40:31, *“But those who wait on the Lord shall renew their strength. They shall mount up with wings like eagles, they shall run and not be weary, they shall walk and not faint*” (Isaiah 40:31 NKJV).

Heard the tale of a man who proudly proclaimed on the pulpit that he could not be swayed into sexual immorality by fair-skinned women. Satan, being an opportunist, took note and set a trap for him without delay.


That very night, a female member of the congregation reached out to him in distress and requested his presence to offer prayers. Without hesitation, he foolishly grabbed his Bible and made his way to her home unaccompanied - lacking any form of chaperone or accountability.

When he got there, this sister was alone and she pretended to be sick. The whole prayer session ended on the bed. He fell into sexual sin with a fair sister who seduced him.

As a believer, the strength to stay sexually pure in this immoral and depraved world is beyond what human energy can muster. *We don't boast about our ability to stay pure from the standpoint of the flesh.*

The bible says, *"He will guard the feet of his faithful servants, but the wicked will be silenced in the place of darkness. “It is not by strength that one prevails;*"(1Sam2:9). To prevail daily over the forces of immorality, you need a different strength that is purely  divine not mental,physical or strength of will power alone.

Over-confidence in the flesh is the reason many fall flat. Why take risk such as cohabiting with a lady you're not married to thinking you will never fall? You see, it may not happen in the first six months. Satan can permit that to reinforce your confidence in the flesh but the plan to mess you up is set in motion already.

One day, it will happen suddenly after Satan has messed with your mind over time. *Sexual sin is worth running from, you don't wait for it to come close thinking you have the strength to overcome it*.

Living a sexually pure life is not a mystical feat, but rather, it is founded upon principles derived from scripture that have stood the test of time. While knowledge and implementation of various practices may aid in this pursuit, as believers we must recognize that the grace and fortitude necessary to maintain sexual purity can only be attained through continuous communion with the Holy Spirit. Indeed, He serves as our enabler for leading a life marked by chastity.

One cannot remain morally upright amidst the depravity of this current era solely through their own fortitude. The fleshly desires are all too eager to seize any opportunity for expression, despite our efforts to resist them. This task is particularly arduous given the perversity that pervades our society today. Hence, only a celestial force can endow us with the strength to deny these temptations and utter a resolute "No."

Apostle Paul said, *"For it is we who are the circumcision, we who serve God by his Spirit, who boast in Christ Jesus, and who put no confidence in the flesh*"(Phil3:3). That's why no man should boast in his strength or ability. We are strengthened to stand by the most high.

Plug into the source of strength for purity in the place of deep fellowship. *The man that pants after God sincerely will not pant after the things of  flesh especially sexual immorality*. Walking with God one day at a time makes sin irritating; no man ever get to that point without a solid altar.

*PRAYER*: I receive strength to stand in purity. Father, as I fellowship with you on daily basis, let there be an infusion of strength and grace to walk with you daily in Jesus name.

*THE LAST WORLD WONDER*

1 Thessalonians 4:16-17: *For the Lord himself will come down from heaven with a commanding shout, with the voice of the archangel, and with the trumpet call of God. First, the believers who have died will rise from their graves. Then, together with them, we who are still alive and remain on the earth will be caught up in the clouds to meet the Lord in the air. Then we will be with the Lord forever.* NLT


The world has heard about several wonders. We have read and heard about the 7th wonder of the world and other things that seem to draw attention of intellectuals.

There is an event that will happen which will surpass all the world's wonders ever seen. This event will be divinely orchestrated without human input. All the wonders in the world are purely human in design and input but the coming wonders shall be totally divine.

We call it the rapture of saints. That's the last world wonder. The saints of God shall disappear from the earth to reappear in heaven. Hallelujah!!! I can't wait to experience this glorious wonder.

When we rapture to heaven, we shall be with the Lord for seven years in heaven. Two major events shall happen up there: Bema judgement( 2 Corinthians 5:10, Romans 14:10)  and marriage supper of the lamb( Eph. 5: 27; Rev. 19: 1-14; Matt. 22: 1-14).

At Bema judgement, the believer shall receive the reward for work done while in the body. At marriage supper of the lamb, we shall feast with Christ our groom. It shall be a glorious union.

The message on rapture is lost in so many pulpits today. Some go to church in a year without hearing about the coming of Christ. Apostle Paul said, *"So encourage each other with these words*"( 1 Thessalonians 4:18 NLT).

The message about rapture should be an encouragement to every believer. There's so much sorrow, struggle and pain on earth. Some have lost their loved ones and things aren't going on well. However, knowing that we shall meet with Christ someday should encourage us all.

The message of rapture should make us prepare and expectant. The lines of one of my favourite hymn reads:

When Jesus comes to reward His servants,
Whether it be noon or night,
Faithful to Him will He find us watching,
With our lamps all trimmed and bright?

Are you prepared to meet the Lord?

*PRAYER*: I receive the grace to live ready in Jesus name

*COMMON LIES PEOPLE BELIEVE* Pt.1

Romans12:2 *Don’t copy the behavior and customs of this world, but let God transform you into a new person by changing the way you think. Then you will learn to know God’s will for you, which is good and pleasing and perfect*(NLT).

*HEAVEN HELP THOSE WHO HELP THEMSELF*

Severally, I've heard people say, "heaven help those who help themself". Often, they allude this statement to scriptures. I have studied my bible from cover to cover, yet, I haven't found this statement in the bible. Not even a semblance of it. Often, the context in which most people make this statement is at the point where compromise seems to be the immediate answer to a lingering problem.

For example, a student who has failed examination severally and has not compromised his faith by engaging in malpractice or a female student that failed by refusing to yield to the sexual advances of her lecturer may be advised by friends to compromise. The statement that  comes with such evil counsel may be, "Heaven help those who help themself". They're trying to use 'heaven'( the throne of God) to support compromise. This is one of the common lies Satan made many believe.

*I make bold to say that Heaven's help is for those who cannot help themselves.*

*The voice of compromise comes to us in the form of 'wise' counsel but heeding to such will lead to eternal doom*. The same voice of deception was heard in the garden of Eden and Eve fell for it. That voice is reverberating in the hearts and ears of many today all over the world.

The way out is to stick to the revealed truth in the word of God stubbornly even if it means death. The three Hebrews were compelled to bow to the golden statue but were resolute not to bow even if God chooses not to rescue them. They didn't help themselves out by bowing to the statue.

The golden statue is always before us waiting for us to bow in compromise.  *Compromise will come wearing the garb of "heaven help those who help themself " or "It doesn't matter, everyone is doing it"*. You need to come to that point in your walk where nothing but the truth in God's word is what you'll stick to.

*PRAYER*: Father, I receive the grace not to heed these common lies. I refuse to compromise and I receive grace to stand in Jesus name.

*COMMON LIES PEOPLE BELIEVE* Pt.2

Romans12:2 *Don’t copy the behavior and customs of this world, but let God transform you into a new person by changing the way you think. Then you will learn to know God’s will for you, which is good and pleasing and perfect*( NLT).

*MEN ARE POLYGAMOUS BY NATURE- IT IS NORMAL TO CHEAT*.

Recently, I watched the video of a preacher in Ghana who claimed that men are polygamous by nature. Openly, he supported adultery but he redefined it to suit his mischief.

A man walked into my office years back. He had separated from his wife. As a lover of peace, I tried to mend the broken relationship but this man told me I shouldn't bother because he had made up his mind to move on.

The wife accused him of battery, gross irresponsibility and infidelity. He told me there's no big deal about cheating because it is normal for men to do that. I could remember vividly that while he was proud about cheating, he was bitterly suspicious of his wife's fidelity.

In my heart, I felt he shouldn't be pained If his wife pay him back in his own coin. Whoever & whatever makes it "normal" for men to cheat on their wives shouldn't outlaw cheating for wives too. After all, all cheating men do it with a woman( either single/ married). Cheating is incomplete until both sexes are involved.

*The society has alot of sinful androcentric stereotypes that has damaged the lives of men, ruined their destinies and scattered their homes. The devil has sold a free lie to men that they cannot be faithful to a woman all their life because they aren't wired for one woman. Big lie!*

What men should be shameful about has become a thing of pride with a feeling of conquest. In Proverbs 6:32, king Solomon, a professor of women affairs said: *"But the man who commits adultery is an utter fool, for he destroys himself*". No one knows the negative impact of adultery better than Solomon. He said every adulterer is a fool heading for destruction.

If animals like Beavers, Gibbons,Parakeet,
Yellow Crested Penguin, Swan, French Angelfish, Owl and a host of others are faithful to their mates in a monogamous relationship till death, how much more humans especially believers who live under the grace of God that has taught us to say 'No' to ungodliness (Titus2:11-12).

*God didn't create men to be polygamous by nature. It is just a carnal mindset most men accept as norm over time*.

Talking about marital fidelity, in Proverbs 5:15-17, the bible says, *"Drink water from your own well— share your love only with your wife. Why spill the water of your springs in the streets, having sex with just anyone? You should reserve it for yourselves. Never share it with strangers*".

The bible describes that man/woman you're having an affair with as a stranger. Run from strangers. Do not accept this lie. With God's help, you can be faithful to one woman all your life.

*PRAYER*: Father, I receive the grace to stay faithful to my spouse in Jesus name. Amen

*COMMON LIES PEOPLE BELIEVE* Pt.3

Romans12:2 *Don’t copy the behavior and customs of this world, but let God transform you into a new person by changing the way you think. Then you will learn to know God’s will for you, which is good and pleasing and perfect* ( NLT).

*TEST FOR SEXUAL COMPATIBILITY BEFORE MARRIAGE*

I use to know a sister who said "No" to a Christian brother because he refused to have sex with her. She wanted to test this brother's sexual prowess. Satan planted an unbeliever around her who had sex with her and even got her pregnant.

After getting pregnant, she did a traditional marriage and moved in with the guy. The last I heard about her union wasn't pleasant. Now, she's facing problems that sexual compatibility cannot solve.

Several times, I've heard and read some counselors say nothing is bad with premarital sex as long as marriage is in-view. Later, I realized some among the brethren see nothing wrong with it.

Some brothers are on a testing spree. What they are after is just to check sexual compatibility before marriage. Some sisters are actively involved in this as well.  This is one of the reasons we have so many broken relationships.

*A fornicator today is a potential adulterer in marriage tomorrow. The ability to delay gratification is a sign of maturity*.

You need to marry someone you can trust even when you're not available for sex because you can't be available everyday. *No serious married man/ woman is available for sex at all times except they're jobless*.

Talking about sexual immorality, the writer of Hebrews liken sexually immoral people to Esau: *"See that no one is sexually immoral, or is godless like Esau, who for a single meal sold his inheritance rights as the oldest son*"(Heb12:16).

On the altar of premarital sex, destinies have been wasted and birthrights sold out. Do this when tempted: *"Run from sexual sin!...*", 1Cor 6:18, NLT.

DEVOTIONAL 1006 | Source start line: 96018
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
10/12/23, 6:13 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: PROJECTSAVE DEVOTIONAL
Thursday,
October 12th, 2023.


*COMMON LIES PEOPLE BELIEVE* Pt.4


Romans 3:7 *“But,” someone might still argue, “how can God condemn me as a sinner if my dishonesty highlights his truthfulness and brings him more glory*?”


*THE END JUSTIFY THE MEANS*


The quote is very popular. It has been attributed to Niccolò Machiavelli, the famous Italian political philosopher. It is a sly way of saying that what matters is result even if the means/ process of getting that result is bad.


This idea has several names: "Situation Ethics," "Moral Relativity," "practicality," and other terms. One man called it "using the devil's tools to do God's work." Those who imbibe this unbiblical ideology aren't after the purity/ rightness of process, the concern is just about getting the desired result.


*Our God is not a God of "the end justifies the means". The means is very important to Him just as the end is*. There are plethora of scriptures that proves that the way and manner( means) we go about a thing matters to God. They don't go unnoticed.


In 1 Sam15:3, 10-23, God instructed Saul to destroy all the Amalekites and their flocks, but Saul kept the best animals to offer sacrifice to God. Offering sacrifice to God was "a good work," but it did not justify disregarding God's command.


In verse 11, God declared that Saul did not obey His commandments. "To obey is better than sacrifice" (v22). *God loves sacrifice but not at the expense of the right means- obedience to His commands*. The end did not justify a different means from what God had specified.


*You can't be stealing and defrauding people yet think God will accept your tithe and offering. In as much as we encourage people to honour God with their substance, it should not be at the expense of corruption. God doesn't sanctify stolen money*.


What about Nadab and Abihu? Their goal was to offer incense to worship God but they used a strange fire different from what God specified. Their action resulted in death( Lev10:1-2). They learned too late that the end did not justify using a different means.


Further, in 1 Chronicles 13:6,7,9,10 - David and Israel thought it was "good" to bring the ark of the covenant back among them (vv 2,4). The end or goal was "good." But while transporting it by ox cart, Uzzah touched it to keep it from falling. God killed him. Why? In 1 Chronicles 15:2,12-15 - God had appointed that the ark should be carried on poles by priests and Levites. No one else could touch it [Num. 4:15-20].


God killed Uzzah because they "did not consult Him about the proper order." Moving the ark was good, but God had specified the "proper order" to use. The end did not justify using a means different from what God said.


Jesus did not mince words in  Matthew 7:21-23 when he said that many will come with great testimonies of their good exploits( healing, deliverance, miracles, etc) in ministry on that day but He will say, *"depart from me, ye workers of iniquity*".

Their goals ( to operate in the miraculous & do good works) are good but God is concerned about the purity life of the person behind those manifestations and the rightness of means used in achieving those goals.


*The life a believer should not be hinged on the end justifying the means, God is concerned with every step and process involved*. Don't think that paying tithe of stolen money will make God change His mind about His hatred for stealing.


That testimony of academic success, promotion or employment gotten through malpractice and sexual immorality may motivate people in the church to pray more and expect a miracle but it doesn't move God a bit because you got the right thing the wrong way.


God's standard won't change because of anyone.  Paul says, *"Nevertheless, the foundation of God standeth sure, having this seal, The Lord knoweth them that are his. And, Let every one that nameth the name of Christ depart from iniquity*"(2Tim2:19).


*PRAYER*: Father, I receive the grace to do things right and to pursue my goals rightly in Jesus name.

DEVOTIONAL 1007 | Source start line: 96139
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
10/13/23, 5:04 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: PROJECTSAVE DEVOTIONAL
Friday,
October 13th, 2023.

*COMMON LIES PEOPLE BELIEVE* Pt.5

Romans12:2 *Don’t copy the behavior and customs of this world, but let God transform you into a new person by changing the way you think. Then you will learn to know God’s will for you, which is good and pleasing and perfect*( NLT).

*IF YOU CAN'T BEAT THEM JOIN THEM*

The statement above mean that if someone is too strong for you, just team up with them and be on their side. In our day-to- day conversation and interaction with the outside world, this statement has come to connote "compromise".

It is suggestive of aligning with  unbiblical norms and practices in our society so as to have an edge. For example, a student that has failed several times may join the bandwagon of those involved in examination malpractice just to escape the shame of yearly failure.

In some offices, some cut corners to make extra  cash. If you find yourself in that environment, you may discover that your colleagues at work are living fine while you go broke before month end. The devil may  tempt you to join them if you can't beat them.

The conformity to  the sinful standard in the world is anti bible regardless of the circumstance or situation in which we find ourselves.

From the old covenant, God warned against unholy alliance and joining multitude to do evil: *"Thou shalt not follow a multitude to do evil;...*"(Ex.23:2).  A practice may be popular and en vogue but may not be of God. Whenever we are in the crowd for too long, an irresistible  influence is exerted upon us.

We should never look at what people are doing as our standard for daily living. Our calling is to be separated unto the Lord: *"Therefore, "Come out from them and be separate, says the Lord. Touch no unclean thing, and I will receive you*"(2Cor 6:17).

Every day, the devil is inventing new ways of getting us in the mix. He'll present compromise to us as harmless with a sense of "it doesn't matter, so many people are doing it". Dearest, anytime the devil tells you that a thing doesn't matter, just know that it matters much.

Sometimes ago, someone told me he can help find a way to adjust my prepaid meter to reduce the billing. He presented it to me as though it is what everyone is doing and it doesn't matter. As harmless as it is, it is a way of "joining them" to do what is not right.

Majority may be doing it but that doesn't make it right. Don't join them. They may be lying about their age to secure a job, inflate contracts and demand kick backs as a means of survival in this harsh economy. Don't join them.

The way to eternal life has always been narrow with only few persons walking in it: *"Because strait is the gate, and narrow is the way, which leadeth unto life, and few there be that find it*" (Matthew7:14).

Daily, the road is getting more narrow because only the few who have refused to " join them" remains on the path. Today, God is telling you to separate from them If you can't beat them.

*PRAYER*: Father, I receive the grace to separate from them instead of joining them in Jesus name. Amen.

DEVOTIONAL 1008 | Source start line: 96201
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
10/14/23, 7:08 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: PROJECTSAVE DEVOTIONAL
Saturday,
October 14th, 2023.

*TIMING AND VISION*

Hab2:3 *For the vision is yet for an appointed time, but at the end it shall speak, and not lie: though it tarry, wait for it; because it will surely come, it will not tarry*( KJV).

Kenneth E. Hagin of blessed memory was a man I loved so much because of his  understanding about following God's perfect plan in life and ministry. It is not only faith I learnt from him. One of his secret was following God per time.

I read in an article that he didn't start his training school until he was around 57 years of age. He  knew he would be training ministers in a school long before that time but he waited for the right time even though age seemed not on his side.

Moses thought his ministry as a deliverer would start at forty years of age not knowing he had another forty years to the commencement of that vision.

*Sometimes, in-between when God revealed his plan to you and when you will start is the process of preparation*. God knew Moses was too raw to lead at forty so God led him into the wilderness to prepare him for the real assignment.

*God in His sovereignty sometimes can reveal what lies ahead but He may reveal it for our knowledge not for immediate action*.

The opening scripture reveals a vital component of vision which is timing. In context,  the opening scripture was talking about Christ.

The vision of Habakkuk was about the coming of Christ. We can learn a lesson or two about the nature of what God says or reveal to His children which is timing.

*Launching a vision before the appropriate timing can ruin the vision faster than anything*.

The men of Issachar were men who understood times and seasons(1Chron12:32). You must be sensitive to the timing God want you to launch out. *Not every revelation is given for immediate action*.

*When God tells you or gives you a glimpse of the future, he want you to start preparing*. When Jesus came to the world to save sinners,  he didn't start on the day he was born,  he waited for the appointed time before commencing.

*Don't be too quick to act on every vision God gives you. Ask the Holy Spirit If such revelation or burden is for immediate action or for the future*.

*PRAYER*: Father, I receive the grace to be sensitive to divine timing In Jesus name.

DEVOTIONAL 1009 | Source start line: 96240
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
10/15/23, 5:48 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: ProjectSave Devotional
Sunday,
October 15th, 2023.

*PREPARE FOR YOUR ASSIGNMENT*

Ezekiel 38:7 *"Be prepared, and prepare yourself, you and all your companies that are assembled about you, and be a guard for them*.

Apostle Paul is our example in the school of preparation. When he got converted,  he had a clear call from God to bear the light of the Gospel among the Gentile nations. That was a rare calling at that time but what surprised me was his depth of preparation.

It is fascinating that Luke, the author of Acts, spends only 16 verses (Acts 9:19-30; 11:25-26, 30; 12:25) describing the first 14 years of Paul’s work in ministry after his conversion and then spends 16 chapters detailing the next 10 years of his life starting with the first missionary journey!

Before Paul started his first missionary journey after conversion, bible scholars agreed that  it took him 14 solid years of divine drilling and dealing. The first three years were spent in Arabia,Gal1:7.

It is surprising that from his first missionary journey to the very last,  he did so much exploits in less than 10-12 years. His epistles were divinely unique. It is hard to preach a complete gospel without making reference to the Pauline corpus.

How did Paul make full proof of his ministry and the vision God gave him?  How come he exceled other apostles that he met in the faith?  The answer is preparation. GOD is not in a hurry.

Most times,  we are the ones hurrying to fulfill the vision we have received from God. If some of us had the Damascus experience, we would have started a ministry right there named: *'Damascus Experience Ministries'*. Paul did not do that. He recognized that the vision is for an appointed time and he waited patiently preparing for that time. If you emerge before your time you will fizzle out before the start date.

*Proper prior preparation prevents poor performance. So many visions are dying because those that received the vision are not prepared to carry the weight of the vision. Certain visions require studying further and becoming a student. In fact,  most vision requires that. All vision requires proper baptism of prayer and fasting*.

Even though Jesus is the saviour of the world,  he could not start until after he had fasted and prayed for forty days, coupled with the fact that he had spent thirty years observing, learning and preparing. Sometimes, challenges of life and circumstances can be our preparation ground.

God is not in a hurry, please don't be. Prepare for that assignment.

*PRAYER*: Father, I receive grace to be prepared adequately for my God-given  vision  In Jesus name.

DEVOTIONAL 1010 | Source start line: 96268
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
10/16/23, 5:53 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: PROJECTSAVE DEVOTIONAL
Monday,
October 16th 2023.

*VICTORIOUS OVER DEMONS*

Luke10:19 *Look, I have given you authority over all the power of the enemy, and you can walk among snakes and scorpions and crush them. Nothing will injure you.* NLT

I watched a live drama where a young man was beaten by demons in his sleep. This continued several times. When he discovered his authority as a believer, he stood against them in the name of Jesus Christ and got his victory.

Some believers suffer perpetual demonic problems because they don't take authority over demons. Some don't know they have such power. They live in fear of demons perpetually.

I watched a nollywood film that didn't portray the victory of the believer in the light of scripture. In that movie, a pastor conducted deliverance and sent a demon out of a lady.

Later, he came under serious spiritual attack by demoniacal forces. When his condition didn't improve, they took him to a witch doctor. The witch doctor told him he was under attack because of the demon he cast out.

The witch doctor warned him to desist from casting out demons and he appeased the demon on his behalf. That is crass nonsense.

They belittled the power of the cross and made light the believer's authority. Jesus never told us to appease or beg demons. Some folks in the church still believe that whenever they're under attack, they need to appease or beg demons.

That's totally unbiblical. You have authority in the name of Jesus, use it. It is not a  strange thing to be attacked. We are in warfront. It is only strange If you don't stand against that attack with the word and prayer.

One of the most unbiblical practice I have seen some Christians exhibit in the place of prayer is to beg God to do what He has given them the power to deal with.

Some think they are sounding humble when they beg God to help cast out a demon. Religious folks don't like it when we talk like this but I'm only echoing what is written.

There's no where in my bible where people beg demons to leave. You need to hear the prayers of some people, you'll see why their prayers are not answered.

The opening text is clear and unambiguous. You have been given the power to trample on serpents and scorpions( referring to Satan and demons) and they shall not hurt you.

I take this scripture as my legal right to deal with devil. Why should I beg a man that my Jesus has defeated and disgraced?

*He canceled the record of the charges against us and took it away by nailing it to the cross. In this way, he disarmed the spiritual rulers and authorities. He shamed them publicly by his victory over them on the cross* (Colossians 2:14-15 NLT).

*The kind of 'respect and honour' some believers give to the devil raises a lot of questions about their salvation*. Some persons eulogize demons.   In Mark16:17, one of the things Jesus said a believer would do is to cast out demons.

*These miraculous signs will accompany those who believe: They will cast out demons in my name, and they will speak in new languages*(Mark 16:17 NLT).

I didn't see anywhere in my bible where Christ and the apostles begged a demon. Look at your situation in the face and issue a decree without apology.

*Your situation has ears. The devil is afraid of your bold stubborn audacious faith that is expressed through righteous decree.*

*CONFESSION*: I decree and declare, every situation that does not glorify God in my life, disappear and do not return again In Jesus name.

DEVOTIONAL 1011 | Source start line: 96335
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
10/17/23, 6:15 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: ProjectSave Devotional
Tuesday
October 17th, 2023.

*POWER OF VISUAL APPEAL: HOW SATAN RECEIVES WORSHIP*

Matthew 4:8-9 *Next the devil took him to the peak of a very high mountain and showed him all the kingdoms of the world and their glory. “I will give it all to you,” he said, “if you will kneel down and worship me*”( NLT).

Recently, I was teaching some adolescents and teenagers about the temptations of Christ. I have read and taught this popular story severally but I had a deeper insight while teaching in that class.

The third temptation Satan brought before Christ was a very subtle and smart one. He showed Christ the kingdoms of this world and their glories. At that point, he was trying to stir in Christ the admiration for those things that appeal to the eyes. He employed the power of visual appeal.

We didn't not know the number of minutes/hours this particular temptation lasted. It is obvious Christ saw the very things Satan showed to him by looking and he was tempted. If he wasn't tempted, he wouldn't have rebuked Satan thereafter. Moreso, the bible says Satan tempted him. He demanded worship from Christ but he rebuked him.

*It isn't easy to fix our gaze on the beauties of this world, the materials in it and the things that gives pleasure and pride without being tempted. Whenever Satan want to distract us from that narrow path, he employs the power of visual appeal to make us focus steadily on what he wants us to see and then he will demand worship before releasing those things. This is the trap he sets for anyone who doesn't care what it takes to have worldly things*.

The lust for material things may be supported with several scriptures to back it up yet God sees it as giving worship to Satan. In Luke 12:15, the bible says, *"Be on your guard against all kinds of greed; life does not consist in an abundance of possessions*”( NIV).

*Satan may not know our hearts but he knows our disposition to worldly and material things. The moment he perceives that inordinate desire for the glories and beauties of this world, he will demand for worship*.

Today, people worship Satan not necesarily in a shrine but right in their hearts because they want to have the things he can offer. Daily, they sacrifice to Satan in their hearts and keep pledging allegiance to him just to have the glories of this world. That is the reason for many compromise we see around today.

Stop beholding the glories and beauties of this world. Don't fix your gaze on them lest you give worship to Satan. The bible says, *"Since you have been raised to new life with Christ, set your sights on the realities of heaven, where Christ sits in the place of honor at God’s right hand. Think about the things of heaven, not the things of earth*"(Colossians 3:1-2 NLT).

*PRAYER*: Father, open my eyes to see the glories of heaven and help me to fix my gaze continually on you not on worldly things in Jesus name.

DEVOTIONAL 1012 | Source start line: 96390

10/18/23, 6:36 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: ProjectSave Devotional
Wednesday
October 18th, 2023.

*FIGHT THE SPIRIT OF AMBITION*

Acts26:19 *Whereupon, O king Agrippa, I was not disobedient unto the heavenly vision*:(KJV).

Pastor E. A. Adeboye told the story of how he became a full time minister.  He said in his days,  being a pastor was synonymous with poverty.

He wanted to become one of the youngest vice chancellors. That was his ambition but God's vision for his life is totally different.

His sisters told him he was not in his right senses because they were looking up to him as the glory of the family having had a university education. He disappointed the spirit of ambition through obedience to God's will.

Don't tell me it is easy to yield to that divine vision, it is not always easy. There is an inner struggle and battle that must be fought and won. That's the battle of ambition- what you desire.

In a school of ministry,  my lecturer told us two things that always compete with every God-given vision and one of them is ambition.

Here,  we define ambition to mean what you desire to do while your God-given vision is God's desire and assignment for you.

*Ambition is always in constant battle with every God-given vision. There's no man that has received an assignment from God that didn't fight the battle of ambition*.

Paul boldly declared that he obeyed the heavenly vision he received. His focus was on his assignment. That was how he silenced the voice of ambition.

Sometimes, people around us help fuel the embers of ambition. I remember someone telling me many years ago that my mates were in the UK doing masters. He was insinuating that I was wasting my time doing ministry in Nigeria especially in villages.

Further, he said to me, "If you become a preacher,  you'll be respected and popular but you won't be rich". Hearing a statement like this can make some persons retreat to the mountain to fast and pray asking God to prove a point while some may abandon ministry totally.

At several points in my life,  the idea of quitting ministry came so that I can pursue my inner most ambition but God didn't  let me off the hook. Thank God I am in.

*The day you hear from God what your life assignment is, those things you had wanted to become will show up from nowhere to wage war against God's plan for your life*.

To overcome, just surrender to God's will and stay focused. Ask the Lord for grace to conquer the enemy of God's plan for your life- spirit of ambition.

*PRAYER*: Father, I receive grace to conquer the battle of ambition In Jesus name.

DEVOTIONAL 1013 | Source start line: 96451
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
10/19/23, 6:43 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: PROJECTSAVE DEVOTIONAL
Thursday,
October 19th, 2023.


*BUGATTI IN OUR BIBLE STUDY*


Luke‬ 12:15 *Then he said, “Beware! Guard against every kind of greed. Life is not measured by how much you own*.”( NLT).


Recently, in a bible study meeting, our teacher taught on proper christian conduct. She touched the subject of money and how believers should live a moderate  life devoid of ostentation.

A brother asked a question saying, "If a christian has suffered so much in life and later experience a financial breakthrough, can he buy a Bugatti since he is now blessed"( paraphrase). Now, the bible study leader hadn't heard "bugatti" until that time.


Quickly, I checked the price of a bugatti on google. My findings showed a price range of N500million naira to N800 million naira. What!!! In my mind, I couldn't calculate the number of outreaches, tracts, Gospel literature, missionaries,church buildings and other projects of kingdom interest this amount will fund.


*The christian who purchase a bugatti should not be seen as a sinner If his money is clean but we need to judge his decision using certain biblical parameters and he need to answer certain questions sincerely*.


How much is he contributing to the spread of the Gospel among the unreached peoples? How many missionaries has he sent to the mission field? How many poor people has he lifted from poverty and empowered? Is he buying the vehicle to compensate for his years of suffering? What does he need a bugatti for? Are there no moderate vehicles of moderate prices that can still serve?


The issue is not whether a Christian can buy a bugatti or not if affordable. For me, the issue is about moderation and kingdom priority. How will you feel driving a bugatti around town? Paul says, *"Let your moderation be known unto all men. The Lord is at hand*" ( Phil 4:5).


Do we need good cars? Yes, we need them. I need vehicles that will navigate rural terrains during our mission outreaches. I need a vehicle that will be comfortable for my family on our trips. A strong and durable one at that but not a Bugatti, please.


Someone may ask, "How is owning a Bugatti different from owning a private jet. Are they not in thesame class"? They are different in many ways. For a business man who has to make many business trips, a private jet is a necessity. For a preacher who has to preach in many countries without subjecting himself to the hassles of commercial flight operations like delay, cancellation and other time-wasting processes, a private jet is necessary. It aids the work of ministry. For me, I'm yet to see what a Bugatti will do for me that a good four wheeler won't do. It is just luxury not a necessity.


I read the story of Bishop Oyedepo that buttresses this point. In company of a friend, they visited a store in Canada. His friend saw an item worth $10,000 and asked If he would buy it. He refused and told him that amount would plant many rural churches. He had the money to buy that item but kingdom expansion was his priority. That was what guided his spending.


*Why should a Christian take pride in wearing a million dollar wrist-watch? Will it tell the time of rapture*? Why many are not blessed by God is because their heart is not positioned towards kingdom expansion. God knows that blessing them will not profit his work here on earth.


If we go by the teaching of scriptures, our spending must reflect kingdom priorities not our selfish and materialistic cravings. *God is not pleased when we abandon his work and spend on our materialistic and self-centred cravings to show we have arrived*.


*Sometimes, luxurious lifestyle is not necessarily a sign of wealth but unhealthy stewardship of money and unwise living*. You'll be surprised to know that the top wealthiest guys in the world live moderately. They are not so big on buying the latests.


Money-wise, buying a luxury car is not an investment but a liability. That is why some of the wealthiest won't buy certain vehicle. The cost of maintaining some vehicles can transform many lives If spent on humanity. I know celebrities who earn very high love these things and are in a rat race to keep up with the Joneses but that is not suppose to be the lifestyle of a believer.


In Haggai 1:4, the Lord says, “*Why are you living in luxurious houses while my house lies in ruins*"?  If it were to be in our day, that scripture may read thus: *"Why are you buying a bugatti while there are many missionaries to be sent on missions among the unreached*?


This is the command of God to us: *"Now go up into the hills, bring down timber, and rebuild my house. Then I will take pleasure in it and be honored, says the Lord*"( Haggai 1:8).


*In this piece, I have shared my personal opinion from a scriptural perspective and I hinged it on moderation and kingdom priority. Your may have a different opinion but as a believer, when God blesses, will you buy a bugatti*?


*PRAYER*: Father, deliver me from worldliness and the demon of materialism in Jesus name.
