DEVOTIONAL 1014 | Source start line: 96560
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
10/20/23, 6:54 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: PROJECTSAVE DEVOTIONAL
Friday,
October 20th, 2023.

*WHY SOME BELIEVERS ARE STINGY*

1 Corinthians 4:2 *Moreover, it is required of stewards that they be found faithful* ( ESV).

Sometime ago, a pastor lamented about one of his members who doesn't support their local church. He is wealthy and everyone knows he is because he is highly placed in the society. He tells his pastor how he bought new houses and landed properties in choice places but he doesnt put more than N500 naira in his tithe card.

I know how pastors feel when they have such parishioners. I have discussed with pastors who lamented on paying bills in the church especially rent when they have members who are not committed to anything in the church. Surprisingly, their problem is not poverty because this same people love luxury and will stop at nothing acquiring what they don't need to impress people who don't exist.

There are believers who are stingy towards God. Yes, I have seen this. They come to church quite alright but their offering is not commensurate with God's blessings in their lives. Some even pretend as though they have nothing but they prefer to buy expensive clothes and blings at the expense of kingdom advancement.

I have asked to know why some believers are stingy. My discovery shows the reason for that attitude is their lack of understanding about STEWARDSHIP. One subject believers should be taught in this self-centred and materialistic world is the subject of stewardship.

Stewardship started in Genesis(2:15) when God put man in-charge of Eden not as owner but as a steward: *"Then the Lord God took the man and put him into the garden of Eden to cultivate it and keep it*".

From the biblical perspective, who is a steward? *A steward is someone chosen or appointed to manage and supervise another person's property or finances*. It mean there is an original owner of the very property or money the steward is supervising or managing. It is obvious the owner is superior to the steward. The owner can demand of his steward anytime.

*As believers, we own nothing, God owns everything we have. We are just stewards of God's blessings and resources in our care and he can demand for it anytime*. Man doesn't have the right of entitlement to what another person own but God is entitled to everything in our care because he is the owner including our very life.

The moment we understand this, stinginess, selfishness and self-centred will die naturally. The early church did not have problem with getting the needed resources for the propagation of the Gospel and helping the poor because the people understood their roles as stewards. There was no emphasis on giving any percentage because they understood God owns 100% and they gave all in many instances.

*Tithing wouldn't be a thing to debate in that era because they gave more than 10%. Only stingy people debate and fight tithing because they are looking for excuses not to give.*

Also, the apostles did not sit over God's resources as Lords of empires, they saw administration of God's people and resources as a trust. This they did with utmost reverence and fear of God, not lording it over anyone. I wish we revisit this apostolic practice and make it the norm in our circles.

*You can only be stingy with what is yours. You cannot be stingy with another man's property especially when he is demanding for it*. If all believers everywhere understand their position as stewards, we won't debate on what to give God. We won't struggle with the Lord when he tells us to release what is in our hands because he gave it to us.

Don't live like that rich fool who thought all the abundance he had was by his power and the only thing left was for him to merry and enjoy. He was not rich towards God and his kingdom, his life was taken from him. Why? Heaven is not profiting from his life.

That which you are witholding is not yours, when God comes asking for it, release it. That is when he can trust you with more: *"Give freely and become more wealthy; be stingy and lose everything*"( Prov 11:24).

*PRAYER*: Father, I thank you for your word. Now I know i'm a steward of everything in my care. Lord, help me to become a good steward and let all traces of selfishness be eliminated in my life in Jesus name.


====================================================================================================
DEVOTIONAL 1015 | Source start line: 96619
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
10/21/23, 6:42 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: PROJECTSAVE DEVOTIONAL
Saturday,
October 21st, 2023.

*EXPERIENCES IN STEWARDSHIP OF RESOURCES*

1 Corinthians 4:2 *Moreover, it is required of stewards that they be found faithful* ( ESV).

When my late mentor taught us on stewardship of money, he told us a story of what happened after he received honorarium of N200,000 from his host after one of his ministrations.

The Holy Spirit instructed him not to spend the money. He kept the money and waited for the next order. After sometime, his host called telling him God instructed him to add to the honorarium he gave him previously and make it up to a million naira.

After receiving the additional honorarium, the Holy Spirit reminded him that he once desired to buy a vehicle for the pastor that conducted his wedding because he lacked mobility. He was instructed to get him a good car. Quickly, he added to the honorarium he received and got his pastor a good car.

I also have a similar experience in this regard. Sometime ago, I had good amount of money with me to give as tithe. The Holy Spirit instructed I keep it. The money was with me in cash and went everywhere with me.

On a certain day, while in the office, I had a leading to visit the bank. I went to the bank for no reason. As I stepped out of the banking hall, I had barely walked a kilometre, I met a lady evangelist I have known from way back. We greeted and the Holy Spirit instructed I give her the tithe he instructed me to keep. I obeyed gladly.

When she received the money, she told me what happened was a miracle because a church invited her for ministration but she had no transport fare. She was stranded at that point. The Holy Spirit instructed her to come to the market area where the bank was.

She left home without any hope of where to get transport fare but she knew the Spirit of God instructed she come to the location where I was. She prayed for me and left for her ministration. I had a strange peace of mind after giving that money to her. That is just one out of the several experiences i've had as regards stewardship of money.

*Dearest, you own nothing, God owns everything and he determines how to use the resources he has given to you*. He owns your life and that makes him the owner of every other thing: talent, money, skill, degree, etc

As new creation, we have lost the right to live for ourselves because our lives are already given: *"He died for everyone so that those who receive his new life will no longer live for themselves. Instead, they will live for Christ, who died and was raised for them*"(2 Cor 5:15).

*The spirit and pride of materialism is what makes us think like owners instead of stewards. A steward cannot own, he can only keep, manage and supervise*. We need to detach from worldliness and see ourselves the way God sees us.

If we attach ourselves to worldly things, it doesn't cost God anything to take us home so he can have us. *It is not a sin to have worldly things but it is a sin when worldly thihgs have us*.

That is why Paul says, *"After all, we brought nothing with us when we came into the world, and we can’t take anything with us when we leave it*"( 1 Tim 6:7).


*PRAYER*: Father, I thank you for your word. Now I know i'm a steward of everything in my care. Lord, help me to become a good steward and let all traces of selfishness be eliminated in my life in Jesus name.

DEVOTIONAL 1016 | Source start line: 96669
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
10/22/23, 4:21 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: ProjectSave Devotional
Sunday,
October 22nd, 2023.

*THERE IS A PROCESS INVOLVED*

Proverbs 13:12  *Hope you deferred makes the heart sick, But when the desire comes, it is a tree of life*( NKJV).

Years back, I discussed with a pastor who wanted to start a church planting work in a city. He told me how the church will gather thousands in about 3 years. His dreams were grandiose but unrealistic.


The number of years he projected passed but those things he expected didn't happen. I'm not doubting that God may have made such promises to Him but it was clear that he failed to understand the processes involved in the fulfilment of God's promises. He spoke as though it will happen automatically.


*For anyone who has very high expectations, to avoid being discouraged, you need to understand there are processes involved.*


*God uses time to process us for the promise. The reason for discouragement often is because we set sight on the promises alone but we do not submit to the process neither do we recognize that length of time is a tool in the fulfilment of God's promises*.

When God told Sarah and Abraham that they would give birth to a son, God has the power to make Sarah conceive immediately and give birth within 24 hours. However, God subjected Sarah to the normal gestation period of nine months common to every woman. *God doesn't boycott process*.

God may have told you very sweet and wonderful things about your life. Wonderful prophetic words may have come to you in time past but everything around you may seem like your journey is on a standstill. Do not be discouraged.

How do you think Joseph felt when he was sold to slavery, found himself in the house of Potiphar and promoted to the prison despite having great dreams?  Put yourself in his shoes. I'm pretty sure you'll be discouraged. If I were in his shoes, I may doubt if God truly spoke to me.

However, the scriptures has taught us to exercise patience and add the value of faith to it so we can obtain the promise: *"We do not want you to become lazy, but to imitate those who through faith and patience inherit what has been promised.*"(Heb 6:12).

*Dearest, God is not a magician. He will take you through phases and stages of maturation before launching you into the fullness of His promise*.

You must realize that each milestone achieved gets you closer to the ultimate fulfilment of the promise. Don't be discouraged, submit to the process.

*PRAYER*: Father, I receive the inner strength to withstand discouragement. Lord, I'll go for you wherever you send me, anywhere, any day anytime, in Jesus name. Amen

DEVOTIONAL 1017 | Source start line: 96702
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
10/23/23, 6:39 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: PROJECTSAVE DEVOTIONAL
Monday,
October 23rd 2023.

*MARKET PLACE PROFESSIONALS ARE RELEVANT*

Col4:14 *Luke, the beloved doctor, sends his greetings, and so does Demas* ( NLT).

I am not one of those who believe in the idea of a "secular" life for Christians. A christian has only one life with many facets. That life is a spiritual one where the will of God should be done. Classifying the believer's life into spiritual&secular is the reason for compromise at times.

*Some believers are only holy in the church. When they get to work, they play by the rules of the evil games people play there*. You'll hear statements like: "When you're in Rome, behave like Romans". This mindset has impeded the spread of Christianity in a way because there is a dichotomy between our consecration and the work we do. It shouldn't be.

*Our professional platforms should be an extension to show the world about Christ in the way we go about our duties and to share the gospel to those we come across. Who says a doctor cannot preach the Gospel to his patients*?

One may ask why the early church recorded so much success in evangelizing their world. One out of the many reasons I perceive was responsible for that feat was that they harnessed the platform provided for them in the market place.

The Market place does not mean the market where we buy things but a generic term for where people do their businesses and practice their professions. It could be in the banking, oil&gas, health, academic and other sectors.

*The early christians were not all full time evangelists and missionaries who did not have a secular job. We read that Luke was a doctor, Aquilla and Priscilla were tent-makers even Apostle Paul, Acts18:1-4. They must have used their professional platform to preach the gospel*.

If they waited till when Apostle Peter called for evangelism like we do today, they wouldn't have reached the whole Asia with the gospel in two years, Acts19:10. It is good we get it clear that those in the five-fold ministry can't do it alone.

*There are certain classes of people a pastor or an evangelist may never get to meet in years of ministry but doctors, lawyers, engineers, university lecturers and artisans meet such people daily. After discharging your professional duty, it won't be bad to share your faith with your client*.

*A school teacher or university lecturer whose years in service spans 35 years can win more souls from the classroom than some evangelist If the platform is harnessed properly*. The time we spend at work meeting people from various walks of life is more than the time spent by preachers with people in church*.

If every believer can harness professional opportunities especially among unreached people groups of the world, we'll cover grounds faster. Traditional missionaries and evangelists have tried but they are limited to an extent.

*Your training, skill and profession is a tool for world evangelization*.

*PRAYER*: Father, I will not fail to use my tool to preach about your grace in Jesus name.

DEVOTIONAL 1018 | Source start line: 96770
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
10/24/23, 6:12 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: ProjectSave Devotional
Tuesday
October 24th, 2023.

*COMMON SENSE FOR FINANCIAL MANAGEMENT*

Proverbs 21:20 *The wise have wealth and luxury, but fools spend whatever they get*(NLT)

While In the university, 300 level to be precise, one of my room mates was profligate. At the beginning of every semester, he will live large, go clubbing and spend money recklessly. Midway into the semester, he'll go broke and sometimes will come begging us for indomie.

This is someone who doesn't care about us when he was living large. I can't remember If he ever gave us a ride in his car.   The painful part of his ordeal was that his friends will never buy him food except beer.

Myself and other room mates weren't high earners in school but we managed the little we had and we never had any cause to beg anyone while on campus.

There are so many programmes about prosperity and wealth today. Some are on the mountain asking God for open heaven and breakthrough while some are busy casting out the demon of poverty. One would wonder why that demon isn't attacking wealthy unbelievers.

Could it be that some are immune to this demon while others are not? I have always told people that there are common sense rules guiding wealth creation and management. If an unbeliever apply them rightly, he/ she will get result.

I'm not denying the fact that devil can and do attack the finances of believers sometimes but more often than not, unwise financial actions lead many into lack and poverty.

How will you explain the case of someone looking for money to set up a business or to buy a property yet he/she is using a phone that can settle all those bills? Some ladies are carrying a plot of land on their head in the name of bone straight or Brazillian hair yet they are indebted to their landlord.

The bible is full of wise counsel on how to manage and multiply our finances. The opening text clearly says that fools spend whatever they get. That is,  reckless spending. Those who don't live on a budget or buy on impulse will always go broke and poor.

*Sometimes, people are broke and poor not because they aren't making money but because they're not good managers of the little they have*.

The craze for "Aso Ebi"( party uniform) has left many broke and poor. *If your investment in wardrobe and trinkets is higher than your investment in business, real estate, family and personal development, you'll live in perpetual poverty*.

It is not bad to tell those pestering you to buy "Aso Ebi" that you don't have the capacity for it. *Don't live a life you cannot afford to impress those that aren't looking at you*.

Send your children to school you can afford and live in a house whose rent you won't struggle to pay for. In Phillipians 4:11, Apostle Paul said, *"Not that I was ever in need, for I have learned how to be content with whatever I have.*" Stop spending to keep up with the Joneses.

*PRAYER*: Father, I receive wisdom on how to manage my finances in Jesus name.

DEVOTIONAL 1019 | Source start line: 96818

*WE HAVE AN ADVOCATE*

1John2:1 *My dear children,  I am writing this to you so that you will not sin.  But if anyone does sin,  we have an advocate who pleads our case before the Father.  He is Jesus Christ,  the one who is truly righteous*( NLT).

Many have read this portion of the scripture to mean that John gave license for believers to sin.  Another school of thought see it differently.  It is good we see it in context of what John was saying.

He started by saying that he wrote to them so that they would not sin, that mean his intention of writing to them was not to encourage sin.  He probably perceived some believers may come up with that.

He went further to state that IF any believer does sin, we have an advocate  or attorney who pleads our case with the father.  Hallelujah!

A believer does not sin intentionally with bold face and act as If nothing has happened.  If you see someone who calls himself a child of God and he enjoys sinning,  such a fellow is not born again.

The nature of Christ in us is not permissive to sin and would not lead us into sin.  Paul admonished that we put on the new man that is created to be like God in true righteousness and holiness(Eph4:24).

Actually,  the new man there refers to the new nature received at new birth. Apostle Paul say, that we should let the new nature of Christ in us manifest.

Though we have  the nature of Christ in us,  we fail God sometimes (1John1:7-9). That is why Christ has decided to stand as our advocate with the father.

When the devil bring accusations  against  us,  Christ is our defence. Glory!  You are not without an advocate in the court of heaven.

Do not give in to that feeling of guilt.

*PRAYER*: Father,  I thank you for the advocacy of Christ.

DEVOTIONAL 1020 | Source start line: 96866
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
10/26/23, 6:13 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: PROJECTSAVE DEVOTIONAL
Thursday,
October 26th, 2023.


*IN THE PLACE OF POWER AND AUTHORITY*


Ephesians 2:6, *And hath raised us up together, and made us sit together in heavenly places in Christ Jesus*.


One of our prayer coordinators while in service shared a true story to buttress Ephesians 2:6. He told us about a deliverance session where a demon spoke through a girl whom some brethren were trying to cast out. The demon asked: "where are you seated"? None of them could give answer.


A brother that had light observed the whole scenario. He approached the scene and the demon asked the same question. The brother answered: "I am seated with Christ in heavenly places." Immediately, he dislodged the demon and the lady was set free.


*In redemption, every believer is seated with Christ in heavenly places above principalities and power. That's the place of power and authority*.


Some have misinterpreted this to mean that the believer is already in heaven. That's not true. You're still on earth. *To be seated with Christ in heavenly places depicts a position of enormous power and authority*.


*The power available to the believer is enormous. However, power is not useful until delegated. Delegated power is what we call authority.* When a demand is placed on power by putting it to use, then it becomes authority.


Demons are no longer superior because where you are  is higher than where they are. Jesus didn't say we'll beg demons.


I bleed within when I see Christians who "respect" Satan and demons more than they exercise their authority over him. It is a big shame.


This is what Jesus said about you and me: *"These miraculous signs will accompany those who believe: THEY WILL CAST OUT DEMONS IN MY NAME, and they will speak in new languages*" (Mark 16:17 NLT).


In the new testament, we weren't told that someone was tagged a "deliverance minister". Everyone who knew their place of authority in the early church sent demons packing.


Our generation has given primacy to a selected few we call "deliverance minister". I haven't said God does not anoint people specially for exorcism. He does and I acknowledge this to be true, however, that doesn't mean believers cannot put Satan to flight.


A child of God should be able to cast out demons without seeking for the help and support of a deliverance minister here and there. *The authority to deal with demons has been given to us all. Hallelujah!!!*


Positionally,  we are seated in a place of authority over all sickness, diseases and demons.

*Look, I have given you authority over all the power of the enemy, and you can walk among snakes and scorpions and crush them. Nothing will injure you*( Luke 10:19 NLT).

You can look at the devil in the face and say "Get lost". *Believers linger in demonic oppression because of ignorance not because Satan is so powerful*.

Satan knows this redemptive provision is available to every child of God but he'll ensure to downplay these truths so that we can remain in bondage.


*In Christ, you have authority to deal with Satan and his cohorts. He is a defeated foe trying to appear as the all-powerful.* On the cross, his backbone was broken and Christ got the victory for us.

*In this way, he disarmed the spiritual rulers and authorities. He shamed them publicly by his victory over them on the cross*( Colossians 2:15 NLT).


When he arose, he said, *"...All power is given unto me in heaven and in earth*"(Matthew 28:18 KJV). We are partakers of that power. It is available to us.


Paul, in his prayer for the Ephesian believers said: *"the eyes of your understanding being enlightened; that ye may know what is the hope of his calling, and what the riches of the glory of his inheritance in the saints, and what is the EXCEEDING GREATNESS OF HIS POWER TO US-WARD WHO BELIEVE, according to the working of his mighty power, which he wrought in Christ, when he raised him from the dead, and set him at his own right hand in the heavenly places*"(Ephesians 1:18‭-‬20 KJV).


The power available to us as believers is exceedingly great. Use your authority in Christ.


*PRAYER*: You demonic forces tormenting my life( mention areas of concern) I send you out of my life, health, marriage, business, family, ministry in Jesus name.

DEVOTIONAL 1021 | Source start line: 96935

10/27/23, 6:27 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: PROJECTSAVE DEVOTIONAL
Friday,
October 27th, 2023.


*DEAL WITH ENTITLEMENT MENTALITY*


Galatians 6:7 *Be not deceived; God is not mocked: for whatsoever a man soweth, that shall he also reap* ( KJV).


I have come across persons with toxic entitlement mentality. They have a view that they must reap where they sow. For instance, when they help people, they do with the hope of getting same favour in return from the very person they help.


When this expectation is not met, they become bitter and angry. In sadness, such persons mention people whom they have helped, their sacrifice on people they have rendered some assistance in the past and how those persons couldn't come to their rescue in their low moments. I know it hurts but we must not fail to realize that God is our helper not men.


Preachers are not exempted from this. I have seen preachers lament about how they sacrificed so much for their members but such members couldn't reciprocate their gestures. They forgot them. Truly, some ministers are victims of ungrateful people but that shouldn't stop us from discharging the duties of our calling.


This sad experience has made some persons to become transactional whenever they want to render assistance including some preachers while some have decided not to help again. These are totally unbiblical. We are commanded to do good at all times.


*Long ago, I made a decision that I will never look back on my acts of kindness in expectation of a return from those I help. It has saved me alot of heartaches and disappointments. When I render help, I simply do it because it is the right thing to do and for God's sake*.

You know why? Hebrews 11:6 says, *"But without faith it is impossible to please Him. For he that cometh to God must believe that He is, and that HE IS A REWARDER of those who diligently seek Him*". My reward comes from God not man.


*No matter what the situation is, according to the opening text, the bible did not say we'll reap where we sow. The scripture only promises that we'll reap what we sow not necessarily from where we sow it*.


*When you sow seeds of help, prayer, money, love and kindness into the lives of people, just expect the harvest from wherever God wills but not necessarily from the persons you sow into their lives*.


Your duty is to sow. Be rest assured that harvest will come. It is God that determines where your harvest will come from. Keep sowing, do not be tired. At the right time, God will send the harvest from wherever He wills. Keep sowing.


*ACTION POINT*: Quit entitlement thinking. Keeping sowing good seeds and trust God for the harvest from wherever He wills.

DEVOTIONAL 1022 | Source start line: 97059
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
11/5/23, 4:57 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: ProjectSave Devotional
Sunday,
November 5th, 2023.

*IS THE OLD TESTAMENT USELESS*?

2 Tim 3:16-17 *All Scripture is inspired by God and is useful to teach us what is true and to make us realize what is wrong in our lives. It corrects us when we are wrong and teaches us to do what is right. God uses it to prepare and equip his people to do every good work*( NLT).

There are several unbiblical statements flying around today. Some say, "old testament shouldn't be in the bible"? Another extreme position taken by some is that believers shouldn't study the old testament because it is not useful for a new testament believer.

Similar to the previous is another presupposition, "God in the old testament is not the same God in the new testament". Those who hold this view assert that God of the old testament was wicked, harsh and cruel but the God presented to us in the new testament is loving, caring and merciful.

These distinctions present God as two different persons.  Somehow, this last assertion is one of the fuels for agnosticism in our day. All these campaigns against the bible and God has been for centuries. The originator was Marcion of Sinope ( 144 A.D). The church called him 'heretic Marcion'. Recently, these evil presuppositions resurfaced and has gained wide acceptance among those who undermine the authenticity&authority of scriptures.

They took this heresy to another dimension when they said the words and teachings of Jesus doesn't apply to us today and certain portions of new testament that present God as harsh( I believe the book of Revelation is implied here).

Is the old testament useless and not important to us? Let's go back to Paul, the man whom most anti old testament people quote and believe. Speaking by the Holy Spirit, Paul said, *"ALL SCRIPTURE( including old testament scripture) IS INSPIRED BY GOD and is useful to teach us what is true and to make us realize what is wrong in our lives. It corrects us when we are wrong and teaches us to do what is right. God uses it to prepare and equip his people to do every good work (2 Tim 3:16-17 NLT).

It will interest you to know that the new testament was not fully written and compiled at the time Paul made the statement above. Obviously, the scripture he referred to as inspired by God was the old testament. The only scripture that was available to the early church from the days of Acts of Apostles until the end of 14th century was the old testament. The early church  preached, taught and meditated on the old testament because they accepted it as the valid word of God.

In fact, on the day of Pentecost, Peter preached from the old testament and 3000 were added to the church not shrine. That was the only scripture available to Paul in his day. Could he be wrong to say what he said? Think.

Paul didn't stop there. He said "All scriptures"( referring to the old testament in context) are useful for teaching what is true. It means that there are truths in the old testament that is useful for us in this dispensation despite being new testament believers.

In fact, he said it is useful for doctrine and for training God's people for every good work( ministry inclusive). If this is true for Paul, why is it different for us in this generation? You can't be more Pauline than Paul.

*While I agree that adherence to the law is not the way to salvation and that justification is by faith yet inherent in the writings of the old testament is the unchanging nature of God and His principles that cut across dispensations*.

Selective study and cherry picking of scriptures is the bane of teaching and preaching today. Don't let the devil deceive you into thinking God is not speaking to you through the pages of scriptures (old testament), it is hell's way of robbing believers from all that God has for them. Beware!

*PRAYER*: Father, I receive the grace to cherish your word( old and new) and not look down on what the holy scriptures teaches. I receive grace to become a well rounded believer, growing unto maturity daily in Jesus name.

DEVOTIONAL 1023 | Source start line: 97091
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
11/6/23, 5:07 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: PROJECTSAVE DEVOTIONAL
Monday,
November 6th, 2023.

*ANY DISTINCTION BETWEEN CHRIST AND PAUL'S TEACHINGS?*

Luke4:18 *“The Spirit of the Lord is upon me, for he has anointed me to bring Good News to the poor. He has sent me to proclaim that captives will be released, that the blind will see, that the oppressed will be set free*.

Some teachers always draw a distinction between Jesus and Paul. They claim to be Pauline in teaching and doctrine. They counsel their listeners to focus on the writings of Paul more than the writings of Jesus because Paul preached the Gospel.

Sometimes, they go as far as saying the audience of Jesus were Jewish and He preached under the law so His words aren't relevant to us today. That's a dangerous conclusion. If Paul preached the gospel of the Kingdom, then I ask, 'what did Jesus preach'?

In the opening text, Jesus claimed that he was anointed to preach the good news( Gospel) to the poor. In other portions of scriptures, we read that Jesus proclaimed the good news( Gospel). See Luke4:43-43,8:1,20:1.

In 1Cor1:17,  Paul said, *"For Christ didn’t send me to baptize, but to PREACH THE GOOD NEWS—and not with clever speech, for fear that the cross of Christ would lose its power*". The Greek word "evangelizo" is translated as "preaching the gospel/good news". This is the same word used when the scriptures talked about Jesus preaching the good news and same was used all through the book of Acts.

*There's no distinction or difference in the  good news Jesus and Paul preached*. That is what the bible says. It is a dangerous conclusion to say that Jesus preached the law but Paul preached grace. How can a man that is grace personified preach a different message?

Speaking about Jesus Christ,  John 1:16 says, *"And of his fulness have all we received, and grace for grace*". Hallelujah!!! We didn't receive law for law in Christ but grace for grace. I wonder If some of those who are trying to draw distinction between Paul and Jesus read scriptures such as this.

*The reason some persons do not regard the teachings of Jesus as gospel/good news is because his teachings always rebuke them. They see themselves as they are whenever they read Jesus, they don't like the correction in it and the harsh tone of Jesus*.

The truths that Jesus taught confront them always, instead of repenting, they hide under certain Pauline scriptures. *The words of Jesus are so important that's why they're printed in red in some bible translations*. That a scripture rebukes you doesn't mean it is not good news.

*The good news can excite you and make you sober to straighten you up*. The flesh doesn't like the latter.  Those in the camp that opposes Jesus do not like to read the book of Revelation where Jesus rebuked some of  the seven churches. They don't see that book as part of the new testament because it doesn't suit their theology.

How I wish they read Pauline words such as this: *"But them that are without God judgeth. Therefore put away from among yourselves that wicked person*" (1Cor 5:13).

In fact, Paul went further to say something like this about the sexually immoral man: *"hand this man over to Satan for the destruction of the flesh, so that his spirit may be saved on the day of the Lord*". This is a big bang.

Dearest, we are in the end time. Paul said, *"For the time will come when people will not put up with sound doctrine. Instead, to suit their own desires, they will gather around them a great number of teachers to say what their itching ears want to hear*",2 Tim4:3. We now live in the age Paul anticipated long ago.

*Trying to distinguish between what Paul and Jesus preached is needless. Stay true to the teachings of the bible whether by Paul, Peter, John, Luke, or Jesus and other books of the bible. The book is one and the central theme is Christ*.

*PRAYER*: Father, I will not be conquered by the deception of this age in the name of Jesus Christ.

DEVOTIONAL 1024 | Source start line: 97527
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
11/7/23, 5:51 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: ProjectSave Devotional
Tuesday
November 7th, 2023.

*A DANGEROUS LIE- BEWARE*

Jude 1:3-4, *"...But now I find that I must write about something else, urging you to defend the faith that God has entrusted once for all time to his holy people. I say this because some ungodly people have wormed their way into your churches, saying that God’s marvelous grace allows us to live immoral lives. The condemnation of such people was recorded long ago, for they have denied our only Master and Lord, Jesus Christ*( NLT).

Sometime ago, I read an article online where the writer insinuated that gays will enter heaven as long they are born again. Then I ask, "Is it possible to be a gay and still be born again"? It is antithetical.

At other times, I read on Facebook where someone raised the issue of adultery, insinuating that it is only the body that is involved not the soul or spirit so If a Christian dies in the act, he will still enter heaven. I guess he was trying to say it is the body that committed the sin not his soul/ spirit. That's funny right?

I doubt a studious believer who has read the bible from cover to cover will ever swallow that junk. In fact, if that's the case, may be no one will go to hell.  Unfortunately, alot of young and vibrant folks are buying into this teaching all in the name of grace.

They shun reasoning with scriptures all because they're hearing what suits them from their teachers. Teachings like this have a semblance with gnosticism but quite a number don't know because it is "Christianized".

The word gnostics comes from the Greek word "gnosis" meaning 'for knowledge'.  Gnostics believe the material world is evil. They claimed that the God of the old testament was a lesser god, an inferior being who emanated from Yahweh, called the Demiurge.

Gnostics sees sin in a way that is extreme. They believe that they're incapable of committing real sins, though their bodies could engage in sinful lifestyle. What this implies is that if they commit a sin, it is only their bodies that is involved not their soul or spirit, so God won't count sins committed in the body against them because their spirit is perfect but the body( which belongs to the evil material world) is inherently sinful.

This is not far from what some are teaching& preaching on the altar today telling believers that if they sin, it is only their bodies that is affected but God still sees them as perfect and unblemished. They are the modern day "Christian" Gnostics who have twisted the message of God's marvelous grace to mean what it is not.

*Whenever God is addressing sin, he doesn't separate the act from man's sinful flesh. He addresses the total being*.

Consider how Jesus addressed a new testament church in the book of revelation: *“I know all the things you do. I have seen your love, your faith, your service, and your patient endurance. And I can see your constant improvement in all these things. “But I have this complaint against you. You are permitting that woman—that Jezebel who calls herself a prophet—to lead my servants astray. She teaches them to commit sexual sin and to eat food offered to idols*"( Rev 2:19-20 NLT).

Did you notice that Jesus didn't make any distinction between the flesh and spirit when addressing the sin of the church in Thyatira? He addressed them in their totality. He didn't say, "I know your body sinned with Jezebel but I'm exonerating your spirit or mind". If he had said that, He would have validated the Gnostic belief.

The way to press a self destruct button is to believe these junks peddled around by people who coat lies with a little truth. The devil knows that many won't swallow error as it appears so he has found a way to coat it with a little truth so that it can be swallowed.

Dearest, the time has come for you to know God for yourself through the study of the word or else you'll be misled into perdition. This is the age of systematic deception, beware.

*PRAYER*: Father, I will not be conquered by the deception of this age in the name of Jesus Christ.

DEVOTIONAL 1025 | Source start line: 97713
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
11/8/23, 5:22 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: ProjectSave Devotional
Wednesday
November 8th, 2023.

*THE MIRROR OF THE WORD*

James1:23-24 *For if you listen to the word and don’t obey, it is like glancing at your face in a mirror. You see yourself, walk away, and forget what you look like*( NLT)

I hardly use the mirror but my wife does almost all the time. I noticed that most ladies have a small mirror in their bags for outdoor use.

Often, they use the big mirror at home before going out and will still use the small mirror before entering the venue for an occasion. This is very common to the women folk. God bless our sisters.They do this to be sure  their outlook is intact especially their facial make-up.

Some will laugh and be sure their expression in laughter is fine. Whatever isn't in order in their make up will be adjusted as they look at the mirror.  This is how far people go with facial looks and beauty.

*The word of God can be likened to a mirror. It has the power and potential to reveal to us who we are and how we look spiritually*. Just as most of our sisters won't step out without looking into the mirror, we should not neglect this all important mirror daily.

It shows to us our clear imagery. If you want to see yourself the way you are, get a bible and start studying it daily. The word of God has an all-round application to all facets of our lives. You can assess your marriage, career, business, academics and ministry from the mirror of the word.

Some persons do not have time for the word, this neglect is the reason why we have many baby Christians today. They go to church every Sunday and are actively involved in church activities but do not have time for personal study of the word and prayer.

From observation, people in that category are often tossed to and fro by every wind of doctrine. It is not enough to look into the mirror, after seeing yourself the way you are, make adjustment.

Whenever you study the word, always ask yourself, 'is there any command/ injunction to obey'? If you study a portion of scriptures and you see certain things that aren't right with your life, simply adjust.

Studying God's word makes no sense until it transforms us. The transformation comes as we obey what is written in the word. Don't study the bible religiously or to mark reading attendance. Obey what is written therein and experience transformation.

*PRAYER*: Father, I receive the grace to look into the mirror of God's word daily and to make adjustment where necessary in Jesus name.

DEVOTIONAL 1026 | Source start line: 97787
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
11/9/23, 5:58 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: PROJECTSAVE DEVOTIONAL
Thursday,
November 9th, 2023.


*DO NOT BUILD YOUR MINISTRY ON LIES AND EXAGGERATION*.


Proverbs 12:22 — *“The Lord detests lying lips, but he delights in people who are trustworthy*”


Dr Bola Akin- John of blessed memory wrote a book titled: "Evangelistic Ministry". In the book, he warned against some of the evangelistic abuses common today. One that stands out is lies.


He cited an example of an evangelist who claimed he had 1.4 million persons in attendance in his crusade at Ibadan, South west,Nigeria and that 1.2 million persons were saved. After diligent enquiry, he discovered the venue could not conveniently contain 400, 000 people.


Another common evangelistic lies is to exaggerate miracles and healings in a way and manner that will catch people's attention. Some even arrange these miracles.


Why do some evangelist& missionaries lie about attendance, souls won and miracles? Probably to raise more funds from partners. This is the drive most times. In a bid to increase partnership base, some employ lies and gimmicks.


One of the traps that is common to ministers especially those in the evangelistic ministry today is the trap of exaggeration. There's something called "Evangelistic speaking". That is nothing short of lies.

As a missionary evangelist, this is one of our common pitfalls. Our ministry comes with a Satanic pressure to impress and to perform. Rarely will you find evangelists that want to preach in a village of less that 300 persons because the sight may not look appealing on social media. Unfortunately, we have many in the body who do not believe you are doing something meaningful without seeing pictures of crowds with hands up.


Billy Graham, one of the finest Christians in our time, an evangelist who is believed to have seen more converts than Paul decided against this in his Modesto manifesto.


One of the things he decided with his friends in evangelistic ministry was that they will not exaggerate their reports. It was and is common for evangelists to create untrue publicity about numbers, attendance, souls won and miracles.


Today, this devil is still around especially with  those mounting the pulpit. Social media has made it worse. The tricks of camera can be applied to make a small crowd appear larger than it is in the real sense just to create a false impression in the hearts of people.


*The moment you seek to build social media fans as a minister, you'll fall into this evil trap of exaggeration and showmanship*.  Psalm101:7 *"I will not allow deceivers to serve in my house, and liars will not stay in my presence*".


*To my dear colleagues in ministry, exaggeration will not grow your ministry. Stop lying about numbers either of souls won or churches planted as the case may be for church planters and missionaries*.


Lying may add a few bucks to your pocket but with time, the cookies will crumble. True and lasting ministry cannot be built on lies. It won't survive.


It is time to start building your life on truth even If it won't impress anyone. You're not in ministry for the multitude but for one. Anytime I'm out on the field to preach, I always tell myself that I'm stepping out to go and preach because of "one".


God can make us spend millions just because He wants one person saved. No need to feel bad about "low numbers". Don't tell the world your congregation is 5000 when in actual sense you're not up to 500. That's not faith but unfaithfulness.


Don't start multiple services by mandating  same set of people to come just because you want to create an impression of large numbers. Let God's hand rest upon you as you build your ministry on truth.


*PRAYER*: Father, I receive the grace to build my life and ministry on truth in Jesus name. Amen

DEVOTIONAL 1027 | Source start line: 97889
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
11/10/23, 5:21 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: PROJECTSAVE DEVOTIONAL
Friday,
November 10th, 2023.


*MY FIRST MARRIAGE SEMINAR*


1Cor11:1 *Follow my example, as I follow the example of Christ*.


When my parents celebrated their over four decades anniversary in marriage, I put up a post online saying their marriage was the first marriage 'seminar' I attended.


That meant alot to me because there was no other way I could have learnt what a true Christian marriage is practically without them. They modeled it to me. Marriage conferences, seminars and books are good but it os much better to learn Christian marriage practically from those who model it.

One way or the other, children learn marriage consciously or unconsciously from their parents. The question is: "What marital lessons are most children learning from their parents"? Constant battery, nagging or forgiveness and love?


I read the post of a wife beater online. He had an altercation with his wife which made him abuse his wife physically. He called the slap "correctional slap" in his post. The wife reported to the police and they arrested him.


His toxic outburst on social media caught my attention. He said "we don't have wife materials again. My dad slapped my mum and she never reported him to the police"( paraphrase). Now, his statement made me think deeply about his childhood and where it all went wrong with him.


He didn't become a wife beater because he read it in a book or was lectured about it, he lived with a father figure who modeled battery to him and he found himself doing same at adulthood.


He may have heard in seminars and teachings that it is wrong to beat a woman and mentally assent to it but battery has been registered in his subconscious from childhood so he'll find himself acting it out.

*Research shows that children learn marriage firsthand from their parents by observing how their parents treat each other*. Also, they handle their marriages the way they see their parents handle theirs. If they're from a broken home, theirs is likely to break if they don't learn how to handle crisis. This is a rule with just a few exception.


A young boy who has seen daddy beat mummy on several occasion will register the act in his subconscious as what a man should do to a woman that misbehaves. A girl whose mother verbally abuse her father or walk away from her marriage will likely do same to her husband if Jesus doesn't intervene quickly.


*Quite a number of anomalies in homes today are products of wrong modeling from childhood*.


What type of marriage do you desire for your children? Do you want it to be exactly like yours?
It is time to break the cycle if you've been modeling the wrong stuff to them.

*As a parent, you have to be intentional about what you say and do in the presence of your children*. Don't say "they're small, they don't know anything".

They know a lot because they have watched you over time. *Children learn quickly from what they see you do than what they hear you say*. Let them see Jesus in your life as you relate as husband and wife.


The bible says 'husbands should love their wives; daddies please love mummies the way Christ loved the church. Junior can't learn that kind of love in school or from book, you have to model it to him. Most men who don't care for their wives are products of poor/ wrong modeling in that regard.


Mummies, submit to daddy; the message of feminism is what the world will teach Jane. There's no other place where she can learn submission at its best except you model it to her.


*Your marriage is the first and longest marriage seminar your children will attend in life, please, model it well*.

*PRAYER*: Father, I receive the grace to model biblical marriage to my children in Jesus name.

DEVOTIONAL 1028 | Source start line: 98000
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
11/11/23, 4:50 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: PROJECTSAVE DEVOTIONAL
Saturday,
November 11th, 2023.

*GREATNESS- NOT FOR SALE, PAY THE PRICE*

1Tim13-14 *"The grace of our Lord was poured out on me abundantly, along with the faith and love that are in Christ Jesus*” (NIV).

There's a common fad in the church about "tapping grace" from someone that is deemed to be anointed or someone who operates in a special dimension. This is common in charismatic circles. You'll hear something like this: "I sow this seed to tap into your grace".

There are preachers who encourage their listeners to tap into "their grace" by sowing a seed or by touching them. I've seen this over and over again.

Sincerely, I've preached and practiced this before not until I realize that it is purely unscriptural. The scripture I held on to was Philippians 1:7(KJV) where Paul used the phrase "My Grace". I concluded that every man of God has a special/unique grace that could be tapped into with money or touch.

I saw men as dispenser of grace instead of Jesus. How wrong! I've collected&sown seed on basis of this faulty teaching but I've repented long ago. That's not to say that I don't believe in sowing seeds/ giving but I do it scripturally.

From other versions of the bible that are closer to the Greek rendition of Phillipians1:7, I noticed  Paul wasn't referring to a personal possessive grace he had but the grace of God that he shared with the Phillipian church and saints. Wow!

There's no doubt that God has specially given certain graces and abilities to certain people in the body. No doubt about that. However, it is not something to tap into by sowing of seed. We didn't see such example in the bible where grace was transferred by tapping.

I'm fully aware of almost all scriptures that some folks may want to use to support this ministry of tapping. Those scriptures fall short of proper interpretation of scriptures.

God is the giver of grace not man that's why Paul said: *"But by the GRACE OF GOD I am what I am..."*(1Cor 15:10). No man, no matter how highly placed in the ministry or anointed he is has the ability to dispense grace. *Grace is exclusively of God given to men out of His rich kindness.*

*When next you see a man operating in a dimension that you desire, check out the price he paid to operate in that level and map out plans to pay same or higher price*.

The height some men have reached in God cannot be tapped into overnight by giving money. *Spiritual heights & dimensions will become too cheap and easy as long as price of money can be used to tap into it*.

Dearest, you need to climb the same ladder that man climbed to the top. What he got through years of fasting, prayer, personal consecration and diligence cannot be gotten in a jiffy with a fat cheque. God doesn't accept bribes.

Even though God's grace was available to Paul for exploit, read what he said in 1Cor15:10: *"But by the grace of God I am what I am, and his grace to me was not without effect. NO, I WORKED HARDER THAN ALL OF THEM—yet not I, but the grace of God that was with me.*".

Grace made him a hard worker; results doesn't fall on any man's lap like ripe cherries. You pay the price on your way to result, you don't tap into it.

*CONFESSION*: I refuse to be lazy. I receive the grace to pay the price for greatness in Jesus name.


N:B: Download our audio and video messages on telegram. Connect by clicking the link below:
https://t.me/projectsaveministries

info@projectsaveng.org

DEVOTIONAL 1029 | Source start line: 98042
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
11/12/23, 4:41 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: ProjectSave Devotional
Sunday,
November 12th, 2023.

*LET'S CONSIDER JOB'S LIFE* Pt.1

Job1: 22 *In spite of everything, Job did not sin or accuse God of doing wrong*( CEV)

*A MAN COMMENDED BY GOD*

Some believers don't like to hear about the suffering of Job. I am yet to meet people, even preachers who see Job as a model. We get to hear more message about Abraham, David, Moses, et al but only very few sermons about Job.

A number of theories have been put forth about Job's suffering. First, a school of thought believed Job was the cause of his suffering because of what he said in Job 3:25 *"What I feared has come upon me; what I dreaded has happened to me*". They concluded that fear was the reason behind his calamities.

Another school of thought believed that it was his failure to tithe that brought calamity upon him. Is it true in light of scriptures? Abraham was the first person who tithed (Genesis14:20). He tithed as a personal decision not because he was taught or commanded by anyone. Tithing became a binding law/ command centuries  after Abraham tithed not before his existence. So anyone who didn't tithe before Mosaic law didn't commit any sin.

Historically, Job lived before Abraham( the first man who tithed); so his failure to tithe wouldn't have been  the reason for his calamity. He knew nothing about it and God didn't rebuke him for that because the law of tithing hadn't been given in his time. That the book of Job was placed after several events starting from Genesis to Esther doesn't mean the books were arranged by order of event. For example, Mark was written before Matthew but the latter was placed before the former.

If we'll be truthful in our exposition about Job's ordeal, we shouldn't neglect the conversation between God and Satan, that's where it all started: *"Then the Lord said to Satan, “Have you considered my servant Job?There is no one on earth like him; he is blameless and upright, a man who fears God and shuns evil*”(Job1:8).

God started the conversation that led to his ordeal. When Satan complained about the edge of protection around Job, God said: *"All right, you may test him,” the Lord said to Satan. “Do whatever you want with everything he possesses, but don’t harm him physically.” So Satan left the Lord’s presence*".(Job 1:12 NLT).

Whether Job had fears about the future or not, God gave Satan an express permission to go ahead with his dirty dozens and there was nothing and no one that would have stopped Satan except God. This is very clear in scripture. Now, all I've said so far is to lay a foundation for this teaching, that's not my major concern but the life of Job.

The first lesson I picked from the life of Job was his testimony of uprightness and integrity. The introductory part of Job chapter 1(NLT) says, *"There once was a man named Job who lived in the land of Uz. HE WAS BLAMELESS—A MAN OF COMPLETE INTEGRITY. HE FEARED GOD AND STAYED AWAY FROM EVIL.*" What a wonderful commendation.

Not many of Job's critics have this report. This is what God saw that made God to be proud of this humble patriarch of faith. Can God say the same about you? Can God boast about you in front of the hordes of hell?

I've tried to peep into Job's life though we are millenniums apart, all I saw was a man who had nothing but the fear of God in his heart. What mattered to God was not his wealth but his walk with him little wonder the bible introduced Job the upright and blameless man to us before we were told about his wealth. What will God say about you?

Don't try to reverse it, your Christian testimony about the correctness of your life and how it aligns with the will of God matter more to God first than whatever you've acquired. Stop the wrong pursuit.

*Who we are and have become is more important to God than what we have*.

*PRAYER*: Father, I thank you for your word that has come to me. I receive the grace to live a life that is pleasing unto you. I want to be that man you will be proud of, help me father, in Jesus name.

Monday,

**LET'S CONSIDER JOB'S LIFE* Pt.2

Job1: 22 In spite of everything, Job did not sin or accuse God of doing wrong.  CEV

*HIS ATTITUDE OF PRAISE IN TIMES OF TRIALS*

There are lessons to learn from the life of Job. Whenever you study bible characters especially men & women with good report such as this, it is wise to pay attention to little details about their life and imitate where possible.

The writer of Hebrew says: *"We do not want you to become lazy, but to imitate those who through faith and patience inherit what has been promised*"(Hebrews6:12). The bible is God's wisdom in written form for all ages.

Whatever we read about in scriptures is included by God as examples for us, the life of Job is not an exception: *"For everything that was written in the past was written to teach us, so that through the endurance taught in the Scriptures and the encouragement they provide we might have hope*"( Romans15:4). This is the word of the Lord.

I saw something that amazed me about Job. When the messenger told him about his loses. Job said, *“I came naked from my mother’s womb, and I will be naked when I leave. The Lord gave me what I had, and the Lord has taken it away. PRAISE THE NAME OF THE LORD!*”(Job 1:21 NLT).

Will you praise God in times of trouble? How can a man say "praise the name of the LORD" after losing all he had? Does it make sense? Just think about it.  I can't be sure I'll say the same thing If I was in his shoes. Maybe I will whine a little, complain and do plenty of crying. The heaviness in my heart will reflect all over me.

If i remember to pray at all, i'll start binding and casting demons and raining fire and brimstone on them. I'm just being truthful. Despite Job's troubles, he maintained an attitude of praise. That's a lesson for us there.

*If the devil can steal your song he will steal your praise*. As a child of God, you're meant to have an attitude of praise and joy regardless of happenings. *Happiness is dependent on happenings but Joy is one of  the fruit of the Spirit that is not dependent on any event*.

The only time some brethren will dance and sing is when a miracle has happened. You can tell when some persons have a testimony to share through their looks. *In our faith walk, we praise God on our way to testimonies not until we have one*.

Faith for change works well when we have an attitude of  praise. Stop looking&dressing your troubles. Do not let people discern your challenges from your looks. Dress well, wear a good perfume, witness the Gospel to someone joyfully, share with the brethren and stop disturbing people around for prayers.

Sometimes, faithlessness is the reason why people are always looking for who will pray for them. Disgrace the enemy with your praise. Be of good cheer, Christ has overcome the world. Hallelujah!!!

*PRAYER*: Father I thank you because my challenges and troubles are momentary. They won't last forever. I wear the garment of praise, I refuse the spirit of heaviness, sadness and gloom. I triumph by faith through the finished work of the son of God in Jesus name.

DEVOTIONAL 1031 | Source start line: 98130

11/14/23, 5:07 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: ProjectSave Devotional

*LET'S CONSIDER JOB'S LIFE* Pt.3

Job1: 22 *In spite of everything, Job did not sin or accuse God of doing wrong* (CEV).

*HIS ATTITUDE TOWARDS GOD*.

The book of Job is a theodicy. Often, theodocies raises alot of question from atheists and agnostics because they can't understand why evil exist in a world created by God.  One of the fuel for atheism and agnosticism is pain or evil. Atheists say 'there's no God', agnostics have repeatedly ask if there's God.

They keep asking this question because they see how some who serve God go through hard times, suffering and even those who claim to have faith in God yet suffer pain.

They see how the earth is ravaged by calamities, wars, famine, natural disasters yet God didn't do anything to stop it. This made atheist conclude there's no God because there wouldn't have been a God and evil will be in the world.

The agnostics say, 'If there's God, then He must be a wicked and uncaring God' but they aren't sure there's God. Unfortunately, there are Christians who are beginning to have this attitude because of life's challenges.

We may not understand all these fully on this side of eternity until we enter glory. We have seen situations similar to that of Job where calamity befell a righteous man or someone who prayed and it was as though God didn't answer. I know you've seen situations like this happen to anointed men.

It is in situations like this we become humble in thoughts and we soberly submit to God but some have seized such occasions to talk to God in a manner that is not glorifying. Some even turned their backs on God totally but Job was different.

The bible says, *"In spite of everything, Job did not sin or accuse God of doing wrong*" ( Job1:22). Just a little pain, some have made themselves a judge and they passed their verdict on God as unfaithful and uncaring. They may not say it by word of the mouth but their action says it all. They may stop serving in church or stop going to church outrightly. That's the danger of serving God for gain instead of love. Those who serve God for gain at some point may be disappointed.

Despite all that happened to Job, he adjudged God as faithful neither did he accuse God and pass on Him a damning verdict. Seeing the enormity of Job's calamity, his wife told him to curse God and die but Job said, *“...You are talking like a foolish woman. Shall we accept good from God, and not trouble?” In all this, JOB DID NOT SIN IN WHAT HE SAID*" (Job 2:10). What do you say about God in times of trouble and trials?

*Dearest, you must come to a point where you need to be convinced that God remains faithful, loving and caring even in unfavourable times and situations*. Happenings around you should not change this conviction.

You must learn to see God's purpose in everything even when it seems the enemy is having a field day. *"And we know that ALL THINGS(Good, bad&ugly) work together for good to them that love God, to them who are the called according to his purpose*" ( Rom8:28, Emphasis mine).

His sovereignty permits certain unfavourable things we can't explain to happen. Our theology or personal philosophy can't explain the reason for this but we shall understand better bye and bye. In all, at all times and in every situation, adjudge Him faithful. This should be your attitude towards God in trying times.

*PRAYER*: Father, you're faithful at all times even in times of  pain. I receive the grace to have the right attitude towards you at all times in Jesus name.

DEVOTIONAL 1032 | Source start line: 98188

11/15/23, 5:40 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: ProjectSave Devotional
Wednesday
November 15th, 2023.

*LET'S CONSIDER JOB'S LIFE* Pt.4

Job1: 22 *In spite of everything, Job did not sin or accuse God of doing wrong*( CEV).

*HIS ATTITUDE IN WAITING*.

*One of the hardest thing to do in our walk with God is to wait for the fulfilment of His promises. Waiting is not easy especially when there are short cuts and alternatives*.

At 75, God spoke to Abram about being the father of nations and led him out from among his people to a promised land. The pictures God painted seemed like something that will happen quickly.  He waited but in the process of waiting, he found an alternative in Hagar and gave birth to trouble- Ishmael. God told him Ishmael wasn't the promised child. The generation of Ishmael are the troublers of Israel till date.

*If you step out of God's waiting line into alternative line, you attract trouble that may last a life time or even outlive you*. Abraham waited more till he was 100 years before the promised child came. Sincerely, that was not easy.

*What makes waiting hard are the ungodly alternatives that present itself to us as the easy way out*.

Job could have sought for ungodly alternatives during his ordeal, he could have cursed God and take sides with the devil but he said:  *"If a man die, shall he live again? ALL THE DAYS OF MY APPOINTED TIME WILL I WAIT, till my change come*"(Job14:14). This is profound.

Job knew there was an appointed time for his change, he had a sense of timing so he concluded he would wait.

*Your troubles days have an appointed time for expiry. Are you truly waiting or wandering*? Some have misconstrued the faith message to mean getting instant result.

*That you don't get instant result doesn't mean you lack faith*. If it is so, then Abraham lacked faith but we know that it is not true. In fact, in all the years of his  waiting, he waited in faith.

Faith and patience in waiting work together: *"that ye be not slothful, but followers of those who through faith and patience inherit the promises*" (Heb6:12). True faith will wait for the promise.

*While waiting, learn the lessons God want you to learn. Your waiting time should not be a wasted time*. You must possess the attitude of a learner during your waiting period.

*The crucible of suffering is a tool for preparing us for greater task and glory but only those who learn during suffering will partake in the glory that will come after*.

As you wait, don't be bitter with those who hurt you or those who possess your desired promise or testimony. For example, if you're waiting on God for the fruit of the womb, be happy with every woman who deliver of a baby, buy them gifts and be part of the ceremony. Don't stop attending weddings because you're not married. The devil often uses such attitude to make singles bitter through the back door.

*Bitterness will prolong your waiting days in the wilderness of suffering. Shake it off*.

*PRAYER*: Father I thank you for teaching me to have the right attitude in and through life's challenges. I receive grace to wait for my appointed time of change in Jesus name.

*LET'S CONSIDER JOB'S LIFE* Pt.5

Job1: 22 *In spite of everything, Job did not sin or accuse God of doing wrong* (CEV).

*FORGIVENESS*.


Years back, a couple were trusting God for the fruit of the womb. God blessed them with the good things of life but they had  no child for several years. A brother whom I thought would be reasonable in reasoning told me he knew their wealth was impure and that may be the reason for their delay.


In fact, he attributed their delay to the dark secret behind their wealth. This is someone who is related to the brother by blood. I cringed with fear because I never expected such from him.

I made a personal decision to avoid any discussion that touched on that couple with him neither did I inform the expectant family what I heard. I trusted God with that family. To God's glory, they have a child now. Hallelujah!!!

*Be careful what you say about those passing through life's challenges so that you won't be seen as their enemy when their testimony comes*. In my walk of faith, I've learnt how brethren can be hurting and unkind in words to those passing through life's challenges. I know this by experience.


Job's friends were unkind in their remarks towards Him. If you take your time to study Job, you will notice some of his friends attributed his ordeal to the sins of his youth or to some evil he did.

This was a man whom the bible described as perfect, a man that was pleasing to God but men are saying he was suffering for his sins. I'm sure those words hurt Job deep into his marrows but he did something worthy of emulation- he forgave his friends: *"After Job had prayed for his friends, the LORD restored his fortunes and gave him twice as much as he had before*" (Job42:10).


*Your restoration is hinged upon forgiving those who hurt you in time of trouble, those who spoke unkind words to you attributing your challenges to your sin, prayerlessness, unkind attitude and so on. Please forgive them, pray for them and let go*.


*Sometimes, you really can't tell what people have in mind about you until you find yourself in the murky waters of life's trouble*. I am sure some of those who benefited from the ministry of Jesus were among those who shouted "Crucify Him".


When Jesus hung on the cross looking helpless, he was mocked by His own people but listen to what He said in Luke 23:34, *"Jesus said, “Father, forgive them, for they do not know what they are doing.” And they divided up his clothes by casting lots*".


*When life deal a deadly blow on you and men turn their backs on you speaking hurtful words instead of encouraging you, don't let it bother you. It is the devil's ploy to prolong your stay in the wilderness of life through bitterness and unforgiveness*. Emulate Job and Jesus in this.

I see God turning your life around soon in Jesus name.

*CONFESSION*: My change is coming. The dark days will soon be over and I shall walk into my testimony in Jesus name. I refuse to bear grudge, I forgive every unkind word spoken to me and about my challenges. I walk in love in Jesus name.

*LET'S CONSIDER JOB'S LIFE* Pt.6

Job 42:12 *So the Lord blessed Job in the second half of his life even more than in the beginning. For now he had 14,000 sheep, 6,000 camels, 1,000 teams of oxen, and 1,000 female donkeys* (NLT).

*A BETTER ENDING*

I love the account of Job's life even though preachers rarely tap into the didactic juice from his story. Reading Job's life gives hope and assurance. If a child of God pass through the fire and waters of trouble, the latter end shall be better. The latter part of Job's life was better than the beginning.

I love the way New Living Translation puts it, *"So the Lord blessed Job in the second half of his life even more than in the beginning...*". There's a second half of victory, breakthrough, revival, testimony, answered prayers and success.

Life is in phases. This present moment may be your own trouble phase, waiting phase, or mockery phase. Don't be bothered because it shall pass away.

Apostle Paul call thesr phases "momentary", that is it won't last long: *"For our light and momentary troubles are achieving for us an eternal glory that far outweighs them all*"(2Cor4:17).

When a thing is momentary, it won't last long. Abraham's barrenness was momentary. Eventually, he became a father and Sarah carried her baby. That will be your testimony. You cannot die like this, no you can't.

There's a power that transforms and lift a man out of the dungeon of trouble and settle him in the place of peace. That power is the power of God. It is working in my life already.

I have seen it change the story of those whom the world never thought will amount anything. I've seen this power break the yoke of delay in marriage and settle people in their homes.

Irrespective of what your challenge is, I decree, that power will bring about a change in your life in Jesus name.

Review all you've learnt in the life of Job and practice them. Live a life of praise, forgive those that mock you and walk worthy of God. Don't waste this precious insight that you have learnt. I see you testifying soon in Jesus name.

There's a second half of testimony, victory, breakthrough, success and triumph. Now is your appointed time, you're in that season already. Enter into the reality of it Jesus name.

*PRAYER*: Father, change my story in Jesus name.

DEVOTIONAL 1035 | Source start line: 98342

*EXEMPTED*

Ex.8:23 *I will make a clear distinction between my people and your people. This miraculous sign will happen tomorrow*’”( NLT).

When my wife wanted to deliver of our first child, she was placed on compulsory bed rest prior to the day of delivery. It was not a funny experience for first timers like us especially her health challenge during that particular pregnancy.

While in the hospital, she was in a ward where all the women had one or two issues. Some lost their babies, some were detained because they couldn't pay hospital bills despite losing their babies. I remember the case of a woman who had contraction at 5-6 months of pregnancy. That ward was a "dark room" in that hospital. I pitied those who couldn't pay their bills. They were treated harshly.

At a time, I blamed the doctors for putting her in such a ward because she was the only pregnant woman there. Others already lost their babies. Staying there could affect her mind before delivery. It was as though everyone was waiting to hear a bad news from her. Halleluyah! God exempted us.

After my wife put to bed without ceasarean section( doctors were contemplating a CS), she looked different and unique in the eyes of other women in her ward. A muslim woman became close to us after delivery because everything about our case was a miracle.

That season, we placed a demand on God's word and we got our desired result despite the prevailing circumstance in that ward. We left there alive, mother and child.

Dearest, God has promised to put a mark of distinction on his children. Something must show you are different from others. *Don't use faithlessness to stop the fulfilment of God's promise to you. It is not God's desire that you partake in the evil in town*.

*It doesn't glorify God that you experience what everyone is experiencing*. That is why your confession must be different. What disturbs everyone and kills many shouldn't kill you. You're God's ambassador here on earth with heavenly protection. Your life is hidden with Christ in God( Col 3:3). This is the biblical basis for exemption.

From today, whenever people talk about the common woes of sickness, failure, depression, suffering, poverty, etc, tell Satan: "My case is different. I have a divine exemption". The word of God is the basis for your exemption.

*Learn to talk your way out of unfavourable circumstances by showing the enemy your exemption letter-the word of God. Our victory is tied to believing and confessing God's word*.

*CONFESSION*: I am exempted from sickness, untimely death, poverty, depression, satanic oppression and other demonic gifts of hell. My case is different because Christ paid the price for my exemption. I walk in total victory in the name of Jesus Christ.

*"I THOUGHT YOU WERE STRUGGLING"*

2Cor 9:7 *Each one must give as he has decided in his heart, not reluctantly or under compulsion, for God loves a cheerful giver* (ESV).

Years back, someone abroad reached out to me on social media. She saw the pictures of our outreaches and that caught her attention. She offered to partner with us. That made me glad because we needed more hands to partner with us in spreading the Gospel and we still do.

I told her I'll like her to be part of the social media group we created for partners so she could get regular updates about programs & events. She accepted to join but she exited the group same day.

When I enquired to know why she did that, she said "I thought you were struggling". I asked to know what she meant. She told me that she thought we were struggling financially. That sounded rude to me.I replied saying, "God is our provider".

*Partnering with God for his work should not be from the standpoint of trying to help a "struggling God". You should be a co labourer with God working with him to expand his kingdom either by giving your time or money. That's the right attitude to giving*.

Knowing she was coming with foreign currency, I could have have resorted to begging and admitting that we are struggling financially so she could send plenty money but I knew in my spirit that her attitude was not right. We may not have all that we need but we are not a struggling ministry.

God is providing for his work without manipulation or pressure on people. Glory be to him alone. I refused to enlist our ministry in the camp of strugglers even though we needed money and would continue to need money but I don't want anyone to give to us out of pity and sympathy.

I could have told her about our projects on and off the field, the several missionaries we want to support, the churches to be planted on the mission fields and so on but I didn't do that simply because she wanted to give to a "struggling ministry" not to the kingdom vision we are running with.

*No man can lift a ministry out of struggle, only God can because He is the owner of the ministry not us*. When you want to give to a ministry or minister, you need to do so out of conviction about the genuineness of the value the ministry/ minister is adding to kingdom expansion.

*Your giving should support a worthy kingdom vision regardless of whether they have plenty or little. That's the attitude you must have when giving*. Those who want to help a "struggling ministry" may stop giving the day they perceive there's plenty money in the ministry coffers instead of supporting the vision.

*Besides, no genuine ministry can have enough to finish the Great Commission so why would someone think that his/her giving is so big to lift a ministry out of the struggle zone*?

*Ministers, beware of those who may want use money to Lord over your life and ministry. You're not in ministry for the highest/ biggest donor. No man should take God's glory in your life and ministry*.

*If you stop giving to that ministry/ minister because they don't look like they're struggling anymore, your prior giving wasn't done with the right attitude*. Some may say, "I can't give any pastor riding a car or jet my money". That's a misguided statement.

I noticed some church folks love to see their pastors and his family look beggarly, tattered and hungry. That's their conviction for giving. The moment he rides a car and his children attend a good school, their giving stops. They'll say "he's well fed". That's wrong. The command to support ministries and ministers has nothing to do with how much they have or do not have.

*Dearest, you need to have the right heart when extending your finances to a ministry or minister, check the vision of the ministry*. Are they involved in the Great commission or they're already in Great omission? Is their vision a kingdom vision or a self serving vision? Are they accountable to God and ministry partners?

*Conclusively, give as an extension of your worship to God and love for His kingdom. That's the attitude you must put on*.

*PRAYER*: Father, I receive the grace to put on the right attitude in matters of giving in Jesus name.
