*SNUFF OUT THE TICKING TIME BOMB* Pt.3

2 Sam12:10 *From this time on, your family will live by the sword because you have despised me by taking Uriah’s wife to be your own*.

*THE THIRD LEG OF DAVID*

I love king David. Anytime I read about him, I see a man raised by God's mercies and endowed with gifts. Can we talk about great men in our Kingdom without mentioning his name?

However, there is an aspect of king David's  story that is not commonly preached. It is common to hear about his conquests over Goliath and other enemies. Most times, we preach about the sure mercies of David and how he was a man after God's heart. All of these are in order and they're worth telling.

However, there is another aspect of his life that put his family and unborn generation  in trouble. He carried in him the ticking time bomb of sexual immorality. It found expression in him and it led to the murder of Uriah.

Often, we are quick to rebuke Solomon for marrying 700 wives and having 300 concubines(1Kings11:3) but he learnt it from David his father and he took it to the next level. Yes, what manifested in small measure in David's life gained full expression in gargantuan proportion in Solomon's life.

At the time of Absalom's rebellion, it was recorded that David had 10 concubines (2Sam15:16) and in his conquests, he married more wives(2Sam3:2-5). As people were hailing him, he was marrying more wives.

Satan saw his weakness for women. He waited for an opportuned time to strike. He set up a day and a time for him when he would behold a beauty called Bathsheba. It happened as planned. He fell into adultery (2 Sam11).

The singular action of committing adultery made him to progress into murder and eventually, he attracted the curse of God upon his life and his family.

When he was about to die, those around him already knew that being around women revives him so they brought Abishag to revive him(1Kings1&2). That was how bad it was in the life of David. Thank God he had learnt his lessons,he didn't touch her.

*A man can be anointed for conquest and territorial dominion yet, what would eventually rubbish him may be growing with him unnoticed*.  We have numerous examples to draw from. The scripture is a book of examples.

1 Corinthians 10:11-13 says, *"These things happened to them as examples and were written down as warnings for us, on whom the culmination of the ages has come. So, if you think you are standing firm, be careful that you don't fall!*"( NIV)

We need to be sincere with ourselves as we walk with God. We must get rid of those little foxes that spoil the vine.

*PRAYER*: Father, take me to your theatre room and operate upon my heart. Remove those destructive seeds i'm nurturing In Jesus name.

1/25/24, 2:33 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: PROJECTSAVE DEVOTIONAL

*SNUFF OUT THE TICKING TIME BOMB* Pt.4


2Kings5:26 *But Elisha said to him, “Was not my spirit with you when the man got down from his chariot to meet you? Is this the time to take money or to accept clothes—or olive groves and vineyards, or flocks and herds, or male and female slaves? 27 Naaman’s leprosy will cling to you and to your descendants forever*”...

*THE SPIRIT OF GEHAZI*

Kenneth E. Hagin of blessed memory told the story of a man that was operating powerfully in the supernatural. He had a unique healing anointing. In one of his programmes, he began to raise funds indiscriminately probably because people were thrilled to see healing miracles. Hagin said he saw the anointing lifted from him. That great evangelist lost the anointing because of greed and love for money.

As a preacher who operates in the power gifts, I know from experience that people can be manipulated easily to part with their monies and materials after they see power in display. That is not right because we did not learn of Christ that way. We did not see or read about Christ telling anyone to sow into 'his' anointing.

Gehazi was positioned to take over the mantle of prophetic leadership from Elisha. Don't forget Elisha got a double portion of anointing from Elijah to become a national prophet. Their prophetic anointing was unique even amidst other prophets in town so everyone conceded spiritual leadership to them. Those who knew Gehazi must have tipped him as a successor of Elijah-Elisha prophetic order but he lost it because of greed and love of money.

*The spirit of Gehazi seeks to commercialize the anointing, little wonder why Elisha's anointed staff didn't work in his hands when he was told to lay it on a dead lad(2Kings4:29-36). If the boy had risen, brother Gehazi may charge them for resurrection service. Greed was a destructive seed that grew with him and later destroyed him when it mattered most*.

*The spirit of Gehazi is the force behind vanity even from the pulpit. When the voice of vanity is loud especially from the pulpit, the very essence of ministry will be eroded*.

There are powerful singers, preachers and anointed brethren who wouldn't minister until you negotiate on a fee with them. Some preachers now prefer to be called 'speakers' so they can appear as someone that want to render a service for money not as ministers of the word of life. To them, that's being branded for ministry but they are grounded without knowing.

It is not easy to be anointed but it is so easy to lose the anointing. The ministry of some would have been global but greed for gains made it local. Are you greedy? Is money and gains your motivation for doing ministry?Money is a good servant but a bad master. Kill greed before it kills you.

*PRAYER*: I mortify traces of greed in my life in Jesus name.

January 26th, 2024


*SNUFF OUT THE TICKING TIME BOMB* Pt.5

Romans 8:13  *For if ye live after the flesh, ye shall die: but if ye through the Spirit do mortify the deeds of the body, ye shall live*. (KJV)


*STOP FEEDING THE PYTHON*


My pastor told the story about a man that kept a small python as  pet. He fed the python well not knowing that he was feeding a predator that would work against him. As the python grew, he'll crawl into the living room and stretch to  its full length on the bodies of the man's children.


The python did this occasionally until it grew as tall as the eldest child of the man. The man travelled and left the python hungry. As usual, the python crawled into the man's house but couldn't find anything to eat except the man's children. He swallowed one of them to satisfy himself.


Can you relate with that story? What  brings men down are things they've been feeding secretly. Some are feeding lust, greed, pride, ego and anger.


We have examined several cases of men in the bible who missed it because they didn't deal with what they thought was little. The little character flaw or habit eventually  led to their downfall.

What is the path to victory? The first thing is to identify the ticking time bomb in your life. It is impossible to confront  and conquer what you haven't identified. Identification is not enough but knowing how to deal with it is what guarantees victory.

Internalizing the word of God(Psalm119:11) and being watchful(1Cor10:12) is a must if we must overcome the ticking time bomb within.

Further, avoid being in environment or around people that nurtures or trigger the growth of that destructive seed. Take the case of sexual immorality as an example, you can't stick to porn and be around those that do not value sexual purity and still remain pure. Never! It won't work.


Consciously, you must starve the flesh in you and cut off every source of its nourishment. Do not forget that prayer is a tool in this warfare. Through prayer,  grace is released for victory.  Under grace, sin shall no longer have dominion over you(Rom6:14).


*PRAYER*:  Father, I receive grace to overcome destructive seed In Jesus name.

*DO NOT MIX DOGMA WITH GOSPEL*

Galatians 1:8 *Let God’s curse fall on anyone, including us or even an angel from heaven, who preaches a different kind of Good News(Gospel) than the one we preached to you*.NLT( Emphasis mine).

Years back, I was in a bus where a woman preached. She started with the message of repentance but ended with personal dogma and anti- gospel ideas. In fact, she preached to a point where she said christianity and other faith are one as long as good works is constant. Hey! That's a very dangerous ground to thread.

Ephesians 2:8-9 made it clear that our salvation is by grace through faith not through works. If philanthropy and being nice is the key to being saved, heaven will be full but salvation is in Christ alone. Hallelujah!

*The nicest and most generous unbeliever will go to hell because he is not saved through Christ. We are not saved by good works but we are saved to do good works*.

She messed the whole thing up when she began to talk about women adornment& hairdo. In fact, that elicited negative reactions from everyone in the  bus. If you don't understand the gospel, please don't preach it. Trying to preach the gospel without adequate understanding leads to creation of dangerous errors that can lead people off course.

Jesus spoke  about the doctrine of men (Matthew15:9). I have noticed that the doctrine of men is causing confusion all over the place. It is a doctrine that takes the focus of people away from the cross and the finished work of Christ on the cross.

Paul says, *"For I decided that while I was with you I would forget everything except Jesus Christ, the one who was crucified*"(1 Cor 2:2).

On the other hand, the doctrine of men is 'Christopagan' in nature. Anywhere you see a mixture of Christian practices and paganism, that's Christopaganism. Whatever we do as a form of ritual or routine as an addition to the name of Jesus Christ brings us close to Christopaganism.

This is common in some prayer houses where they believe in 'prophetic work'.The name of Jesus is enough and is empowered because of Christ's death, burial and resurrection. We need not add to it. If we do not believe God unless we use tokens, we do not believe at all.

Preachers must teach their listeners the content of the gospel message in clear terms so they can communicate it effectively to others.

As a soul-winner, your duty is to present Christ and his finished work to the people. Some are proclaiming the gospel of their denomination and 'papa' not that of Christ.  Your invitation to sinners should be to Christ first before inviting them to church. Christ-centred gospel is the authentic gospel.

*PRAYER*: Father, I receive grace to present the gospel of Christ to people accurately without mixing it with dogma and heresies In Jesus name.

*WHAT YOU SHOULD KNOW ABOUT FAITH* Pt.1

Romans 10:17 *So then faith cometh by hearing, and hearing by the word of God*( KJV)

*WHAT IS FAITH*?

Faith is believing God's word, speaking God's word and acting on God's word in the face of contrary circumstances. These three things are essential components of faith. What some people have is mental assent and they call it faith.

I have ministered severally to people who were trusting God for one thing or the other. Some received their desired results while others did not. Same applies to cases of healing and miracles.

I noticed it is easier ministering to those who understand the principles of faith and its dynamics. From my little experience, I think preachers should teach how faith works and how believers can receive from God with their word-based faith.

If you want to minister healing to people, preach faith along the lines of healing, you'll see results. As preachers, preach on what you want to see in the lives of your audience. That is how to stir faith in them.

*Faith is not faith until it is rooted in God's word. The word of God is the source of the faith that works*.

Virtually all that came to Jesus received their miracles or healing by faith. Jesus was fond of saying: "Be it unto you according to your faith" (Matt9:29). If faith wasn't important, Jesus wouldn't have stressed on it.

If you want to build your faith to become solid and strong, do not build it around 'things', people or mediums like water, mantles and anointing oil. When those mediums are absent, what will happen to you? Do not build it around your 'papa' or 'G.O.' but only on God's infallible and innerant word.

The psalmist says, *"For ever, O LORD, Thy word Is settled in heaven*" (Psalms119:89). The psalmist further says, *"The words of the LORD are pure words: As silver tried in a furnace of earth, purified seven times*" (Psalms12:6). In essence, it means God's word has integrity. We can bank on God's word believing that God will perform whatever he says.
Apostle Peter says, *"We have also a more sure word of prophecy; whereunto ye do well that ye take heed, as unto a light that shineth in a dark place, until the day dawn, and the day star arise in your hearts*"( 2 Pe.1.19.KJV). The word of God is a more sure word of prophecy that can strike out other negative prophecies concerning your life.

*When you're faced with the challenges of life, search out relevant scriptures and begin to meditate on them till faith is built up in you like an edifice. Let those scriptures be your confession till it becomes your reality*.

*CONFESSION*: God's word is my anchor not man or medium. The 'Logos' is my reality. The word is working for me and in me to produce results In Jesus name.

*WHAT YOU SHOULD KNOW ABOUT FAITH* Pt.2

John 20:29 *Then Jesus told him, “Because you have seen me, you have believed; blessed are those who have not seen and yet have believed*” (NIV).

*BELIEVING PRECEDE SEEING*

Sometime ago, I taught on the subject of faith in church. I told my listeners the reason the prayers of some believers are not answered.
One reason among several others is because they have reversed the bible pattern of faith. They hold on to the popular mantra of: Seeing is believing.

In the kingdom of God, it is what you believe you will see not the other way round. Now, that's not easy for the flesh. The human mind loves to see evidence(s) but in the kingdom, believing the written word of God is what guarantees what we'll see.

Mark11:24 says, *"Therefore I say unto you, What things soever ye desire, when ye pray, believe that ye receive them, and ye shall have them*".

Now, this scripture doesn't make sense to the human mind but that's God's wisdom in getting our request answered.

*God did not put our 'receiving' in the future, our receiving should be in the present and past even If we do not see it at the moment. Our receiving is not tied to how we feel or what we see but our believe and absolute trust in God's word*. Halleluyah!!!

*We receive the answers to our prayers by faith before we see the answer. That is simply what bible faith is*.

A believer should not find it hard to believe. The moment you start doubting God's word and his ability to do what He said he would do, you have wasted your prayer effort.

Sometimes, I see people who love to go round asking for prayers on the same issue. Several evangelists have laid hands on them yet there's no result. Many of them want instant manifestation before believing their prayers are answered instead of believing instantly that they have received answers before seeing manifestation.

*The absence of instant result or physical manifestation doesn't determine the efficacy of your faith or prayers*.

Your faith should stand on the truth revealed in God's word that you have received what you prayed for. It is until after you receive the answers by faith that you will see it.

*CONFESSION*: I am a believer. I believe God's word and stand on it regardless of physical result in the name of Jesus Christ.

*WHAT YOU SHOULD KNOW ABOUT FAITH* Pt.3

Romans4:17  *(As it is written, I have made thee a father of many nations,) before him whom he believed, even God, who quickeneth the dead, and calleth those things which be not as though they were* (KJV)

*INCUBATING YOUR FAITH*

Yongi Choo told the story of how he was poor when he started ministry. He asked God to provide a bicycle, desk and chair for him. He specified  the exact thing he wanted. He testified in church that he had received all those things.

Some of his members paid a visit to verify if his testimony was true. When they got home, they saw nothing. He told them that he was pregnant of those things. They laughed and thought he was mad. He told them that every pregnancy needed time before delivery. After some time, he got the exact types of items he requested from God.

Most times, faith requires a gestation period for it to deliver physical result but you must keep doing two things: calling those things which be not as though they were( consistent faith confession) and believing you had received when you prayed coupled with thanksgiving. That's how to incubate your faith.

*After you pray and believe you received, the confession of your mouth must be as though you have seen what you're asking for and the believe of your heart must be intact*.

Often, when we come out of a meeting where our faith is stirred, we don't know how to maintain that faith stirring till we see result. Sometimes, it is as if our faith jumped out after those faith lifting services.Next time, just keep incubating that faith with scriptures in your mouth.

*Your life will go in the direction of your words. Our kingdom is a talking kingdom. We say it to see it*.

If you confess hardship, failure, sickness, poverty, doom, gloom, shame and pain, that's what you'll get.

What is it you've asked God for? Believe you have received them by faith and keep incubating them.

*CONFESSION*: Father, I thank you because the finished work of Christ has delivered unto me all I need for life and godliness. Halleluyah!!!

*THE POWER OF GOD IS AT WORK IN YOU- RELEASE IT*


Eph 3:20 *Now unto him that is able to do exceeding abundantly above all that we ask or think, according to the power that worketh in us*( Ephesians 3:20 KJV)


Sometime ago when I returned home, my second son rushed in and said, "Daddy, my anointing is working". I inquired further to know to know what he meant.

He said, "I commanded a lizard to go and he ran. And I commanded the hanger to fall and it fell*. It was hilarious but  I decided not to discourage him because I don't know how much of the anointing he desired and how he's been praying about it in his own little way.

Obviously, he's been desirous of demonstrating God's power and that occasion meant a lot to him. Though I believe you don't need anointing to make a lizard run because lizards don't wait at the sight of humans especially at a close range. I can't explain the fallen hanger but I don't know how anointing will make hanger fall.

If he had healed the sick or raised the dead, I wouldn't have raised doubts about the quality of the anointing working thtough him.However, I learnt something that day.

The young boy was conscious of God's power. He knew there is a super human power from above that can make things work beyond human effort.

Paul told the Ephesian believers about this power and he brought them to its consciousness saying: *"Now unto him that is able to do exceeding abundantly above all that we ask or think, according to the power that worketh in us,*" (Ephesians 3:20).


He told them there's a power( Dunamis) at work in them already. It is also at work in us. Hallelujah!!!


That power is the reason God will do exceedingly abundantly above all that we ask or imagine. That phrase "Exceeding abundantly" is from the Greek "Uperekperissou" meaning extreme.

*The power of God in us can do extreme things beyond our imagination.  Hallelujah!!!*

Reading further, the power in that text is known as "Dunamis". It is a self starting power. Inherent super human ability. That's the power Jesus promised we'll have when the Holy Spirit comes on us. Hey! If you're baptized in the Holy Spirit, you have that power already. Hallelujah!!!

*To operate in the various dimensions that power can afford, you need to be conscious of it and release it to work for you by faith as you speak*.

Stop thinking and talking like a powerless and defeated fellow. Be conscious of 'Dunamis', that inherent ability to operate in the miraculous and make such happen for others.

Don't be afraid to pray for the sick, be bold to tell the devil to come out and don't ever beg demons. We do not need 'feelings' to release and operate in power. What we need is faith that is expressed through our words.

Sometimes, when I step on the podium to preach, I may not feel anything but I am conscious of that power working on the inside of me. This knowledge I have is the reason I speak God's word boldly, cast out demons and issue decrees. The power is there. It is in you.


*The power that operates in you puts you on a plane of "exceeding abundantly" where your wildest imagination cannot capture*.

Learn to spend time praying in the Holy Ghost to stir this power and pray for boldness to release it when needed. That's one of the secret of operating in the length and breadth of "Dunamis".


*CONFESSION*: Father, I thank you for your power that is at work in me. It can do exceeding abundantly above all that we can ever ask or imagine. Henceforth, I walk in the consciousness of that power. I refuse to beg demons and to be fearful. I walk in authority and power in Jesus name.

2/3/24, 6:19 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: PROJECTSAVE DEVOTIONAL
Saturday,
February 3rd, 2024

*THE MORNING STAR OF THE REFORMATION*

Matthew5:10 *Blessed are they which are persecuted for righteousness' sake: for theirs is the kingdom of heaven*

John Wycliffe was known as “The Morning Star of the Reformation,".  He  was an outstanding theologian who lived in 14th century. He was known for his prowess as a translator of scriptures. He believed that the Bible should be available to the people in their common tongue.

He translated the Latin Vulgate into common English. He was persecuted for his stand against Papal authority. While he was not burned at the stake as a martyr, his persecution extended beyond his death. His body was exhumed and burned along with many of his writings.

The Anti-Wycliffe Statute of 1401 brought persecution to his followers and specifically addressed the fact that there should not be any translation of Scripture into English.

Virtually all that we are enjoying today in the body of Christ(especially in nations with low persecution index) were paid for with the blood and sacrifices of saints who sacrificed their time, comfort and life.

Anytime you read your English bible, remember John Wycliffe and William Tyndale. Their efforts and sacrifice made it possible.

It is important that we remind ourselves about those that have made huge sacrifice(s) for us to be here. This should in turn make us resolute in our decision to serve the Lord come what may.

Matthew16:25 says, *"If you try to hang on to your life, you will lose it. But if you give up your life for my sake, you will save it*".

*Christianity is worthless without sacrifice*.

Christ sacrificed his very life for us to receive eternal life. God is calling you to a higher place of commitment where you need to make sacrifices for his kingdom.
*PRAYER*: Father, I receive grace to contribute my quota to kingdom advancement In Jesus name.

*THE POWER OF INTENSE PRAYER*

1Thessalonians5:17- *Pray without ceasing*

One of my trainers in school of ministry told the story of what he saw while going to church years back. He saw a crowd at a junction and there was a woman there who confessed she was a witch. She was sent on assignment to Lagos after they concluded their meeting at Ijebu- Ode, Ogun state, South West Nigeria.

On her way, she flew over a particular church that was praying intensely so she missed her route. She fell from the sky and began to confess. That's what intense, soul hot and fervent prayer can do. In all her years of practicing witchcraft, I want to assume she has flown over churches and houses where Christians were but nothing happened until she flew over that church that prayed intensely. Can your prayer life make demons uncomfortable in your domain?

The church of today is yet to tap into this dimension of prayer like saints of old did. The early church prayed and where they met shook: *"After they prayed, the place where they were meeting was shaken*"( Acts 4:31 NLT). The intensity of their prayers from within reverberated in the physical. Earthly elements felt it.

Samuel Chadwick said, *"The one concern  of the devil is to keep the saints from praying. He fears nothing from prayerless studies, work and Christian activities. He laughs at our toil, mocks our wisdom, but trembles when we pray"*. That is a profound statement.

*I have been in cold and dry prayer meetings but i've also been in intense atmosphere of prayer. The difference is clear. If we would bring divinity to intervene in the affairs of humanity, I know no other means than prayer. The devil loves to attack our prayer life with too many activities because he's afraid anytime we pray*.

Thank God for the increase in knowledge and great awareness about other aspects of our christian faith. *Knowledge glows when it is borne out of the womb of prayer*.

*A supernatural ministry is impossible without a virile prayer life. The saints of old didn't joke with prayer because it was their life-line*.

Good dreams and vision are good, possessing a great knowledge of scriptures is a must but what binds it all is prayer. The devil doesn't respect theologians but he fears a 'kneologian'. Anyone can be a theologian, even unbelievers but not anyone can be a 'kneeologian'. It is exclusive to those that are blood-washed and sanctified

The man whose hearts kneel in prayers can rise up to face the hordes of hell, set the captives free and raise the banner of Christ. Intense prayer is a must for victorious Christian living.

*PRAYER*: Holy Spirit, set my prayer life on fire. I cast out every spirit of prayerlessness in my life In Jesus name.

*IGNITING THE FLAMES OF YOUR PRAYER LIFE* Pt.1

Zech12:10 "Next I'll deal with the family of David and those who live in Jerusalem. I'll pour a spirit of grace and prayer over them...MSG

*NO PRAYER LIFE WITHOUT THE HOLY SPIRIT*

Years back, I read a book titled: "Seven Great Prayer Warriors". The book is a documented history of some Christians with a virile prayer life. Some of them prayed till the glory of God filled their lives.

I won't forget a man that prayed till his body got swollen as If he was pumped like a tyre. When his body couldn't contain the weight of glory, he said: "Take thy hands off thy servant because your servant can burst". The glory of God in and around him was too much for his mortal body to carry.

Many believers desire to be mighty in prayer like these men.  I have seen the desire in many especially during counseling. Every now and then, people desire to become prayerful( though not bad) but they forget the source of a fruitful prayer life- HOLYSPIRIT. Those men wouldn't have had a strong prayer life without the Holy Spirit. The  Holy Spirit is the Spirit of prayer.

*If prayer is a fleshy exercise, we wouldn't need the Holy Spirit but because of its spiritual nature, we cannot but do without the Spirit of God. The flesh does not want to pray*.

Epaphras( Col4:12-13), Moses( Deut9:11-19) and Jesus Christ had the working of the Spirit of prayer in their lives.

*In history, every man or woman who had a consistent virile prayer life had the indwelling of the Spirit which enabled them to pray prevailingly*.

When you read the prayer exploits of men like Apostle Ayo Babalola who prayed for 72hours non-stop, do not think he did it in the flesh. Flesh cannot pray that long. Something stirred him to stay that long in God's presence and it seemed to him as though he had spent a few hours.

*The Spirit of God is called the Spirit of grace and supplication(prayer). Trying to pray without the Spirit of prayer in your life will make you become tired very soon*.

*Many have become religious in their prayer life while some do not even have a prayer life at all. So bad. Until you submit your prayer life to the Spirit and receive a fresh baptism of prayer, your prayer life cannot be consistent and fruitful*.

Dearest, allow the Spirit of God take the wheel of your prayer life. He is a sweet gentle Spirit that will not force himself over your life. You need to invite him into your life right now.

*You cannot become mighty in prayer without the Spirit of prayer*.

*PRAYER*: Holy Spirit, you're welcome into my life. Take the wheel of my prayer life and enable me to become strong and consistent in my prayer life. I receive a fresh baptism of the Spirit of prayer and supplication.

DEVOTIONAL 1111 | Source start line: 104030

2/6/24, 6:09 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: ProjectSave Devotional
Tuesday
February 6th, 2024.

*IGNITING THE FLAMES OF YOUR PRAYER LIFE* Pt.2

Psalm5:3 *My voice shalt thou hear in the morning, O Lord; in the morning will I direct my prayer unto thee, and will look up*

*DISCOVER YOURSELF*

When I was in school as an undergraduate, I don't read at midnight. I discovered that once I sleep at night, I don't wake up till the next morning. Some people have the grace to sleep and wake up at midnight to read but I don't. If I try it, i'll walk around all day like a drunk.

Having known that, I rescheduled my reading time and drew a plan for my active hours. I could read from afternoon till midnight as long as there's no nap in between. I discovered my body's requirement for sleep is minimum of 6 hours a day.

You may be wondering how this story relates to prayer. Before you can have a consistent prayer life, you must discover yourself especially your body's requirement for rest and your active time. Some are natural early risers while others are late risers.

Many years ago, I tried praying between 1-3pm at night but it wasn't working for me because i'm active till about 12 midnight and i'm expected to wake up between 5-6am in the morning.  I was damaging my health because I wasn't getting enough sleep so I stopped. My prayer life became better when I discovered myself and struck a balance between work time,  resting time and waking time.

Also, you must take your time to know what your daily schedule is. No matter how busy you are, there's a time that can be carved out for prayer. If you form the habit of  sleeping early, waking early to pray will not be a problem.

However, for those that return home very late at night and leave home early in the morning time, it may not be possible to pray well in the morning but you can take advantage of break time in the office or the hours before work starts to commune with your heavenly father.

*What matters most is not the length of hours spent in prayer but the quality of fellowship and consistency*.

PRAYER: Father, I cast out the spirit of prayerlessness in my life. Holy Spirit, baptize me afresh with the Spirit of prayer.

2/7/24, 4:13 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: ProjectSave Devotional

*IGNITING THE FLAMES OF YOUR PRAYER LIFE* Pt.3

1Cor9:25 *And every man that striveth for the mastery is temperate in all things. Now they do it to obtain a corruptible crown; but we an incorruptible*.

*DISCIPLINE AND DENIAL*

I once listened to a man of God who said he formed the habit of waking up by 3am to pray and study on daily basis. He goes to bed by 9pm at night daily so he can wake up early. He denied himself of television programs at night because of what he want to achieve. That is discipline and denial at work.

*Discipline is living as demanded while denial involves sacrificing temporal pleasure to achieve something spectacular*.

Just as an athlete would deny himself of certain pleasures to gain mastery, same applies to anyone that desires to be consistent and mighty in prayer.

*One of the reasons for prayerlessness is indiscipline. Men and women that are mighty in prayer are people with strict routines that makes room for prayer*.

After discovering your body requirement for rest, be disciplined enough to sleep at the proper time so you can wake up early to pray. You may avoid heavy meals at night so you can be light to wake up early.

One of the things I recommend to people is that they should avoid eating heavy meals If they intend to do vigil or wake up to pray early.

*Sometimes, you need to engage in long fast to revive your prayer life If the fire of prayer is burning low*.

Your prayer life must be carefully planned and given all it takes. For those with very tight work schedule, be disciplined enough to use those spare hours or break time in the office to fellowship with God in prayer.

For sit at home moms, the hours of the day when your husband has gone to work and children are in school can be used judiciously. Avoid unnecessary street gist. Do not forget that you would achieve more spiritually If you spend time with God than with men.

Leonard Ravenhill said: '*To be much for God, you have to be more with God*'. That requires some discipline and self denial.

*PRAYER*: Father, let the burden of prayer rest upon me.

*IGNITING THE FLAMES OF YOUR PRAYER LIFE* Pt.4

2Kings2:15 *And when the sons of the prophets which were to view at Jericho saw him, they said, The spirit of Elijah doth rest on Elisha. And they came to meet him, and bowed themselves to the ground before him*.

*DISCIPLED TO PRAY*

Severally, I have recounted how God use Bro Joshua Ojewunmi to fan into flames the spirit of prayer in my life. It was an experience I will never forget in my life.

While in the university, 200 level to be precise, brother Joshua Ojewunmi decided to engage me in prayer every night for a minimum of one hour. We met by the roadside in front of his hostel at night for prayers.

When we started, it was as If he was pestering me because the flesh wasn't willing to pray. However, I took it upon myself to ensure that I join him at the prayer spot every night.

I didn't know God was using him to disciple me along that line. I have seen some of the manifestations of those prayers we prayed then. Those prayer sessions were deep. We prayed about our present and future then. Those years, we sought to know God's mind for our lives and ministry.

It got to a point where I became more interested in that prayer than he was. At the beginning, he was the one sending for me but later I began to remind him to join me on the spot where we normally meet. A special grace to  pray extensively rubbed on me.

*If you associate with praying men closely, the mantle of prayer will fall on you*. In my early years as a believer, our church evangelist would camp us somewhere for 2-3 days for prayer retreat. God used those experiences to transfer something into me. That's part of the benefits of discipleship.

*If you're struggling with your prayer life, look for someone whose prayer altar is on fire. Take the coal of fire from his altar to ignite yours*.

Stop walking with people that are lukewarm. It can rub on you. It is not a bad idea to be so close to that person you know can help ignite the flames that is about to quench. Who you follow determines what will follow you.

Apostle Ayo Babalola ignited the prayer altar of his associates by taking them to the mountain to pray regularly. After his demise, his disciples became prayer champions. Some of them prayer down the raw power of God.

In the kingdom, we learn and grow by associating with the right persons. Look around, prayerfully spot a revived brother or sister, tell the person to find time for you so that you both can be praying together. As you do that, the flames of your prayer life will be ignited. If you put your heart to it, you'll grow to start praying independently.

*PRAYER*: Father, I receive the grace to locate and associate with those that would boost my prayer life In Jesus name.

**IGNITING THE FLAMES OF YOUR PRAYER LIFE* Pt.5

Colossians4:2 *Devote yourselves to prayer, being watchful and thankful* (NIV).


*IDENTIFY YOUR PRAYER BOOSTERS*


There was a time in my life when the anointed music of Don Moen and some other gospel music ministers would stir me to pray and it does till date. My phone was filled with songs that connected me with the Holy Spirit.


Any time I feel dry and weak to pray, I would listen to some of those selected songs. I won't forget watching Benny Hinn's worship and healing meetings on my laptop. I'll sit on the floor flowing along with the worship and from there i'll take off into depth of fellowship.


Yours maybe different but you need to identify those things that serve as a booster for your prayer life. It has helped many and it shall continue to help others.

We were told by a trusted colleague years back that one of my role models loves to see the image of a praying hand because it stirs him to pray.


Another thing that has helped me is being in an environment where people are praying. Sometimes, when I go to prayer camps or mountains, seeing people praying normally push me to my knees.

*Don't surround yourself with prayer killers. Carnality can kill your prayer life. Just as anointed music stir some people to pray and boost their prayer lives*.

carnal music and fleshly attachment can ruin your prayer life. God can do so much in our lives but He has chosen not to do much unless we give Him the express permission through our prayers.

The goal of identifying your prayer booster is to ensure that your prayer altar is red hot and flaming with the fire of the Holy Spirit.

*PRAYER*: Father, rekindle the fire on my prayer altar In Jesus name.

DEVOTIONAL 1115 | Source start line: 104290
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
2/10/24, 5:45 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: PROJECTSAVE DEVOTIONAL
Saturday,
February 10th, 2024.

*THE MATYRDOM OF POLYCARP*

Revelation 2:10 ... *Be faithful until death, and I will give you the crown of life* (NKJV).

Polycarp was a 2nd century Bishop of Smyrna. Polycarp was  a disciple of  Apostle John who wrote the gospel of John, the three Epistles of John and the book of Revelation.

He is prominent among the sub apostolic fathers who may have been    responsible for compiling the New Testament Bible that we have today.

Because of his refusal to burn incense to the Roman Emperor he was sentenced to burning at the stake. Tradition says that the flames did not kill him so he was stabbed to death.

At the point of death, Polycarp said, "Eighty and six years I have served Him, and He has done me no wrong",  how then can I blaspheme my King and Savior? You threaten me with a fire that burns for a season, and after a little while is quenched; but you are ignorant of the fire of everlasting punishment that is prepared for the wicked."

Miraculously, his body did not burn, so he was pierced with a sharp object.  On his farewell, he said "I bless you Father for judging me worthy of this hour, so that in the company of the martyrs I may share the cup of Christ."
Tradition says a dove flew out of his body when he was stabbed. Some still think the dove was somewhere around but ancient witnesses believed the dove flew out of his body because many onlookers saw it from a close range.

Can you die for what you believe? If it were to be many believers in this generation, they'll burn the incense to Roman emperor and claim that they gave to Ceasar what belonged to Ceasar. Most times, when you hear that statement, compromise has taken place.

When I visited the field recently, a pastor told me about the subtle persecution one of the local pastors was facing. In the market where he plied his trade, he was asked to pay for a fetish traditional rites but he refused. Other christians paid but he refused.

The paramount chief and his goons decided they'll send him out of the village. He stood his ground not minding what the result will be. He stood his ground like Bishop Polycarp.

Bishop Polycarp was faithful to the cause of Christ till the end. He would have escaped  gruesome death with a little compromise.

*The crown of life is only for those that can fight the good fight of faith and stay faithful to Christ to the end*.

*PRAYER:* Father, i receive the grace to stay faithful to the end In Jesus name.

Sunday,
February 11th, 2024.

*BANQUET IN THE ENEMY'S CAMP*

Psalms 23:5   *Thou preparest a table before me in the presence of mine enemies*: ... KJV

Reinhard Bonnke of blessed memory told the story of how some witch doctors surrounded the hotel  he lodged during one of his evangelistic campaigns in one of the countries in Africa. They were busy doing their thing but he was busy sleeping.

His sleep was so sound that he cared less about what was going on outside. What he ignored is what others would have given all their attention to.

The table God prepared in the presence of David's enemies was a feasting table. In a banquet, tables are prepared with several delicacies amidst friends and well wishers but David's case was different. He was in the enemy's camp.

Can you imagine going to a party where there are armed robbers, kidnappers and ritualist? Imagine that your table is right at the centre of that hall surrounded by such evil people. I am pretty sure you wouldn't want to stay there if you know.

The world is full of wickedness and wicked people. Psalms 74:20 says, *"Have respect unto the covenant: for the dark places of the earth are full of the habitations of cruelty*". David acknowledged this world is full of cruel people.

Sometimes, we wonder why God doesn't kill all the wicked  people and rid them off the earth. Well,  God is  loving and merciful, he may not do that often because he gives room for the wicked to repent. That's why it is a waste of time praying death over enemies in most cases.

*If all your enemies die, they may not know the greatness and power of the God you serve*.

What does the great shepherd do to the sheep in such cases? He will disarm the enemies, protect the sheep and prepare a feast for the sheep while the enemy keep looking. Why are you afraid of the enemy?

The chief enemy has been fought and won on the cross(Col2:14-15). His minions are just trying to fight a battle that has been won for you.

All you need do is to stand your ground in the victory of the cross and declare, *"The Lord is my light and my salvation; whom shall I fear? the Lord is the strength of my life; of whom shall I be afraid?  When the wicked, even mine enemies and my foes, came upon me to eat up my flesh, they stumbled and fell* (Psalms27:1-2).

*Dear, don't be afraid of the enemy. God won't kill them all because he want them to watch while you eat from the delicacies of salvation prepared for you. Go to the banquet table and eat*.

*CONFESSION:* I am eating from God's banquet table in the presence of mine enemies. I refuse to live in fear of what the enemy is doing or may be doing because the victory of the cross is real and sure for me. I walk in the realities of Psalms 23 in the name of Jesus.

IG: @Projectsave_ministries
@LanreOyeleke

Twitter: @OyelekeBenjamin

Email: info@projectsaveng.org

DEVOTIONAL 1117 | Source start line: 104421

2/12/24, 5:41 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: PROJECTSAVE DEVOTIONAL
Monday,
February 12th 2024.

*DO NOT QUENCH THE SPIRIT*

1Thess5:19 Do not quench (suppress or subdue) the [ Holy] Spirit; AMP

Years back, I was in a vigil with some brethren where we were having a nice time in the Holy Ghost. The flow was interesting and powerful but we switched over into the flesh when vulgar words and comedy was introduced into the service.

It was as If something left us in that meeting. I got hold of the microphone to tell the brethren where we missed it. We repented of our insensitivity that night.

*Some churches have shut out the Holy Spirit in their services. They oscillate between the flesh and the spirit, holy and unholy. Often times, this happens because we mix entertainment with worship*.

There's a common saying that liturgy leads to lethargy. Liturgy refers to our order of services. Whenever we are rigid about our order of services and do not allow the Holy Spirit to move, we maybe quenching Him.

Sometimes, being too time conscious may break certain flow of the Spirit. Sometimes in our church meetings, there could be an outburst of holy laughter, tongues and interpretation, prophecies, joyful singing and crying. All these may happen beyond the preacher's control. That's not the time to ring the closing bell.

I love the move of the Holy Ghost. We become more anointed and strengthened spiritually If only we can key into the flow of the Spirit and not interrupt it.

Apostle Paul knew that it was so easy to quench the Spirit but not always easy to get in the flow that was why he voiced his concern to the Thessalonian church. The same voice is speaking to the church today.
PRAYER: Father, I receive the grace to sensitive to the Spirit In Jesus name.

DEVOTIONAL 1118 | Source start line: 104448
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
2/13/24, 5:22 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: ProjectSave Devotional
Tuesday
February 13th, 2024.

*WHEN STEWARDS ARE TESTED*

1Kings 17:7 *Some time later the brook dried up because there had been no rain in the land. 8 Then the word of the Lord came to him: 9 “Go at once to Zarephath in the region of Sidon and stay there. I have directed a widow there to supply you with food*.” NIV
A devout christian woman shared her experience with me on the price she and her husband paid for disobeying God's instruction he demanded for their money. Yes, you heard me well. God can make a demand for whatever he gives to you because you are not the owner. He is.

In the late 80s, she had an acquaintance whom God instructed to tell her to be part of their church building project. Instead of her to pray and ask God If truly he gave the message, she rejected the idea outrightly and went ahead with their building project in the village.

Herself and her husband had the money but they felt their building project should come first instead of putting the money into the church's building project as instructed by the Lord.

After that period of disobedience to God's instruction, it took them 33 years to complete their house in the village. It was a great struggle. In fact, part of the building collapsed in the course of construction. She told me that if they had known, they would have given the money to support that church building project back in the 80s.

*Refusal to give that little can hinder much.  There are times God makes demand for that very thing you hold dear. Not because he wants to deprive you but such demands are tests from God to know if we have become attached to the things of this world or whether we still trust him to supply our needs even if he decides to take all*.

*The moment you find it hard to release what is in your hands to God, it shows your heart is affixed on worldly things. God owns everything, we own nothing. We are just stewards managing God's property with us*.

It wasn't easy on the part of the widow of Zarephath to give her last meal to the prophet of God during famine. If Elijah were to be a prophet in our time, he'll have gotten bad press. The news headline may read thus: "Prophets That Exploit Widows".

However, the ways of God are totally different from the ways of man. That singular act of obedience opened her up for continuous supply of provision during famine.

1Kings 17:15 says, *"She went away and did as Elijah had told her. So there was food every day for Elijah and for the woman and her family. (16) For the jar of flour was not used up and the jug of oil did not run dry, in keeping with the word of the Lord spoken by Elijah*".

A wise man said, *"Giving is the proof that you've conquered greed*".  When God is asking for that little in your hands, it is because greater things are coming. Obedience in matters of giving open doors. Why are you witholding what the Lord is telling you to release?

*PRAYER*: Father, I receive the grace to be a sacrificial giver in Jesus name


====================================================================================================
DEVOTIONAL 1119 | Source start line: 104518
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
2/14/24, 5:35 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: ProjectSave Devotional
Wednesday
February 14th, 2024.

*OUR FATHER IS A BAKER*

Mt6:11 *Give us today our daily bread*

Sometime ago, some persons popularized a different perspective about the portion of Lord's prayer where Jesus prayed saying, "Give us this day our daily bread". They said, "Why ask for daily bread when you can own a bakery"?

This idea corrupt the very meaning of what Jesus intended in that prayer. It is like saying, "Jesus, you didn't pray well. You should have prayed that aspect this way". Could Jesus be wrong to have taught his disciples to pray asking for daily bread? Of course He wasn't.

While I agree the Lord's prayer is not cast-in-stone for believers, however, it reveals to  us a pattern or a model we can adopt in our prayer lives. It should be noted that "Daily Bread" is a  figurative phrase denoting daily provisions and divine supplies.

Why did Jesus teach his disciples to ask for daily bread and not ask for bakery?  The answer is simple.

*God want to be our source of sustenance and provision. He want to own the bakery while we receive the bread. The day we own the bakery that supplies our bread, we'll stop trusting Him for divine supplies. That doesn't mean we won't work but our jobs, businesses and career expressions are meant to be means of supply but not the source*.

Man has tendency to think he is sufficient to meet his needs. The pride of "I can do it on my own" can easily set in and we'll think we don't need God to meet our needs. It is possible to be working hard and still not have your needs met especially in this economy.

*The moment we get to the point of self sufficiency, we'll miss out on God's largesse for our lives because we'll be full of ourselves and we'll be disconnected from His abundant supply*.

He is the abundant source that never runs dry. The Psalmist says in Psalm145:15-16, *"The eyes of all look to You, And You give them their food in due time. You open Your hand And satisfy the desire of every living thing*". Hallelujah!!!

The owner of our bakery( God) is never tired of supplying our daily bread.

*The day you start baking your own bread with your effort, you'll run out of flour and your hands will become weak.*

Our bakery owner is rich in all and can supply generously without being tired. I learnt this by experience during the lockdown of 2020. I've never seen God in that dimension before.

Paul knew this truth about God that was why he said: *"But my God shall supply all your need according to his riches in glory by Christ Jesus*", Philippians 4:19 KJV. Hallelujah!!! He is not going to supply some but all our needs.

He is committed to doing that If we trust Him. God is my supplier, I'm the receiver. He is my baker and i'm the eater of bread.  I don't need to give myself headache about trying to produce my bread.

Why go through the stress of baking bread when I can just ask for it and receive it on time? Trust God for your needs and quit using human effort.

*PRAYER*: Father, I trust you for my daily bread. I don't need a bakery because I can't maintain it. Just do the baking and I'll receive the supply. Thank you for answering my prayer, in Jesus name.

DEVOTIONAL 1120 | Source start line: 104584
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
2/15/24, 7:12 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: PROJECTSAVE DEVOTIONAL
Thursday,
February 15th, 2024

*BE UPDATED ALWAYS*

Genesis22:1 *Some time later God tested Abraham. He said to him, “Abraham!” “Here I am,” he replied. 2 Then God said, “Take your son, your only son, whom you love—Isaac—and go to the region of Moriah. Sacrifice him there as a burnt offering on a mountain I will show you*”

In computer science, there is something called 'operating system'. Every computer device have an operating system to run efficiently. However, the operating system in each device needs to be updated from time to time. Once it is outdated, you may not enjoy your device. In our walk with God, we also need to get heaven's update on divine instructions given.

There is something for us all to learn in the story of Abraham and his attempt to sacrifice Isaac. What we commonly accept as the major lesson is that God tested Abraham and he passed the test. Some have also taken that scenario to preach on sacrificial giving.  All that said, there's another vital lesson we all must draw from that story.

It was God that told Abraham to take his son to mount Moriah for sacrifice. I'm sure Abraham heard God clearly on that because the bible say so. However, that instruction had a time lag. While he was about sacrificing his son, the instruction changed, God told him to stop. The old instruction to sacrifice  his son expired the moment God gave a counter instruction even though the intention of the former instruction was to test his heart.  If Abraham was insensitive to God, he would have killed his son citing the first instruction whereas God has moved on.

*Walking with God demands that we are current with him.*

There are believers who live on stale or expired  instruction. God has moved but they remained on the previous spot. The instruction to move from Egypt to wilderness was meant to be a temporary one, Canaan was the goal yet most of those who left Egypt couldn't make it to Canaan.

*You must be sensitive to differentiate between the journey and the destination.* There are certain instructions that are but for a moment. If you build a monument around it, you'll be walking outside God's program for your life. If you're still running a conference or program that was meant for a season, you'll be feeding people with stale food. Some programs have outlived its usefulness. Take time to know God's mind for the now so that you won't sacrifice your Isaac out of insensitivity.

*PRAYER*: Father, I receive grace to be current with you in Jesus name.

DEVOTIONAL 1121 | Source start line: 104608

2/16/24, 6:18 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: PROJECTSAVE DEVOTIONAL
Friday,
February 16th, 2024.


*HAVE YOU ASKED HIM*?


Job5:8 *“If I were you, I would go to God and present my case to him*."

Sometime ago, I needed a certain amount of money to pay someone who rendered a service to me. It is not my habit to buy or get things done for me on credit.

However, I needed to get that thing done at that point in time so I promised to pay before the end of the following month. Sincerely, I did not have an expectation on where the money would come from. I was troubled in my heart.

I told my wife about it but I failed to tell God. That was where I got it wrong and this is a common trait to most believers.  We have pressing needs and issues bothering us, we tell everyone but we fail to tell the man that can give solution.

On a fateful day, the Spirit of God reminded me that I have not asked God to supply my need for that money I needed to pay the person who did a job for me. Reluctantly, I asked God to supply my need. The prayer wasn't as serious as it should but I asked anyway.

A day or two after, a sister sent a message asking that I send my personal account number. I hesitated a bit because that was her first time of asking. I almost told her not to bother because she was nursing a baby.


Reluctantly, I sent my account details to her. Lo and behold! She sent the exact amount I needed to pay the man I was owing. Hallelujah!!! My mind went back to the time I prayed about it.


Sometimes, we trivialize the effect of simple prayers and overlook issues instead of telling God about it. No matter how small it seems, God is interested in that matter.

If I were you, I will tell it to God the way it is and leave it there in his presence. God is just a call away. He is interested in minute details about our lives.

*You must cultivate the habit of talking to God first before talking to men*. Learn to present every case to him, He is ready to answer.


*PRAYER*: Father, intervene in this matter(...mention the exact issue) and glorify yourself In Jesus name.

*SUCCESS REVEALED HIS FOLLY*

Luke 12: 16 *And he told them this parable: “The ground of a certain rich man yielded an abundant harvest. 17 He thought to himself, ‘What shall I do? I have no place to store my crops*.’

The parable of the rich fool is a good illustration that buttresses this topic. The ground of this man yielded so much harvest to a point where he contemplated  where to store the harvest.  That's normal for any man that experiences a boom.

However, the disposition of his heart is where he missed it. In verses 18&19, *"And he said, This will I do: I will pull down my barns, and build greater; and there will I bestow all my fruits and my goods. And I will say to my soul, Soul, thou hast much goods laid up for many years; take thine ease, eat, drink, and be merry*".

His life's security was in material prosperity alone not in God. In verse 20, God said: *"But God said unto him, Thou fool, this night thy soul shall be required of thee: then whose shall those things be, which thou hast provided*"?

How did he get to the point where his success in farming made a fool of him? When he was toiling and praying to be successful, why didn't God call him a fool?

In my opinion, he was seduced by the traps that beset successful people. He felt he had arrived and that was his undoing.

*Some persons stopped praying, fasting, studying the bible and their devotional life went on the downward spiral after they achieved little success*.

They stopped doing the very things that got them to the top. On getting to the top, they just couldn't manage success.

*For some ministers of the gospel, they fell into the trap of pride and over compensation. They over compensate themselves with exhorbitant material things under the guise of having suffered for the ministry in the past*.

*As a preacher, If your emphasis during preaching shift from the kingdom's message to material things even If it is your church edifice and fat bank account, success may be making a fool out of you*.

We all must learn how to manage success so that we won't end like the rich fool.

*The time to be more humble, more prayerful and closer to God is when our ground yields a bountiful harvest*.


*PRAYER*: Father, I receive the grace to manage success and not to fall into the trap that comes with success in Jesus name.

*HE IS WILLING TO GIVE YOU ALL THINGS*

Romans 8:32 *He that spared not his own Son, but delivered him up for us all, how shall he not with him also freely give us all things*?

There is a profound truth in Romans 8:32. The more I read it, the better my understanding. From that scripture, I understand that Christ is the most treasured possession of God the father.

He gave Christ to us to die for our sins. It was not an easy decision for God but he made it. If a man can give his only son to die, do you think there's any other thing he won't be able to give?

Apostle Paul is saying that there is nothing God will withold from us since He had given His most treasured possession- Christ. Hallelujah!!!

The way you see God is important and crucial in your walk with Him. There are people whose mentality about God tend towards cruelty and that of a task master. They never see the goodness of God.

When Satan throws his dirty dozens at them, they simply accept it and move on instead of confronting it with the name of Jesus and God's word. *God is not the one behind sickness, wars, terrorism, famine and pestilence. Satan is*.

God is ready and willing to give you all things. It cost God nothing to grant you the desires of your heart because Christ has been given already. *Satan may make you feel your needs are too much and big but it is not bigger than Jesus Christ*.

Psalms 84:11 says, *"For the LORD God is our sun and our shield. He gives us grace and glory. The LORD will withhold no good thing from those who do what is right*". If this is true under the old covenant,  how much more those that have been blood washed, saved and sanctified by Christ.

When next you pray, make your request with the understanding that God is able to grant your request.

*CONFESSION*: Father, I thank you for your love. I know you will not withold good things from me. Hallelujah!!!

2/19/24, 4:19 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: PROJECTSAVE DEVOTIONAL
Monday,

*YOUR WALK* Pt.1

Ephesians 5:8 *For ye were sometimes darkness, but now are ye light in the Lord: walk as children of light*: KJV

*WALK AS CHILDREN OF LIGHT*

I spent part of my adolescent years in my home town.  Our house had no electric power supply for sometime though some houses had power connection.  As a young boy from Lagos, life in my home town was boring and there was fear at night because we were surrounded by bushes and trees. We did not watch t.v.,the only thing we listened to was my dad's battery powered radio.

We heard several animal sounds at night. Darkness painted images in my heart so I was scared to move out at night. My dad ensured  our house got the needed connection to power.

It was a day of joy seeing power pole erected and cables running into our country home. Light came and darkness was dispeled. If you have not lived in darkness, you may not appreciate light.

Jesus is the light.  If we walk in Christ, the darkness of this world cannot overcome us. John1:4-5 says, *"In him was life; and the life was the light of men.  And the light shineth in darkness; and the darkness comprehended it not*". Hallelujah!!!

Darkness is synonymous with sin and evil. Jesus came to dispel darkness. The man in Christ  is in the light already. In fact, by virtue of being in the light, he has become  the light. *"Ye are the light of the world. A city that is set on an hill cannot be hid.*".(Matthew 5:14).

Your duty is to shine the light of Jesus in you anywhere you find yourself. Sin and evil should not be associated with you. In your office, school and neighbourhood, shine the light through your godly character. *Godliness is the true mark of living in the light*.

Paul says, *"And have no fellowship with the unfruitful works of darkness, but rather reprove them.  For it is a shame even to speak of those things which are done of them in secret.  But all things that are reproved are made manifest by the light: for whatsoever doth make manifest is light*".( Ephesians 5:11-13).

*CONFESSION*: I walk in the light of Jesus. I shine the light of Jesus. I shun evil and I embrace light in Jesus name.

DEVOTIONAL 1125 | Source start line: 104820

2/20/24, 5:00 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: ProjectSave Devotional
Tuesday
February 20th, 2024.

*YOUR WALK* Pt.2

3 John1: 4 *I have no greater joy than to hear that my children walk in truth*.

*WALKING IN THE TRUTH*

Severally, I have heard and read about church sisters who had sex with fake preachers to be delivered of a demon or to get solution to certain problems. I am always sad hearing that people fall for those demonic tricks.

My late mentor told us about a church where the senior pastor commanded everyone in his church to exchange their wives and have sex. He took the wife of his deputy and vice versa. Others in the church followed in that evil.

We live in a world of lies and deception. It seems lies sell faster than the truth but it doesn't last for long. When I thought I had seen it all, I saw another man in the print media who demanded money to help check people's names in the book of life. Still, I wonder if their 'customers' were hypnotized or they deliberately fell for those lies.

*The reason people fall for those tricks and extrabiblical practices is simply because they have not encountered the truth in God's word. It is only those that know the truth that will walk in it. You cannot walk in a truth you don't know. Impossible*.

To walk in the truth is to order one's life according to the Word of God. That's all. Jesus said, *"Sanctify them in the truth; your word is truth*"( John17:17). When your life is in tune with the rightly divided word of God, that is only when you can walk in the truth.

There's a dearth of sound and expository bible teachings today. People are walking in deception because they have been fed with lies either from the pulpit or from the media. That is why believers need to prayerfully connect with a truth teaching and preaching church.

*The believer's walk must be in tune with the properly dissected word of God without prejudice to dogma or private interpretation of scriptures. Start searching for the truth. It is right there in the bible*.

Jesus says, *"And ye shall know the truth, and the truth shall make you free*".(John 8:32). The truth liberates If you know and walk in it.

God's expectation of you is to be a lover of the truth written in God's word. In your walk, the bible, accurately interpreted must guide your life and decisions. That is how to walk in the truth.  Neglecting scriptural injunctions, instructions and verdicts will take you farther away from the truth.

*CONFESSION*: I walk in the truth of God's word. I walk in the light. I walk in Christ and I live the Christ life in Jesus name.

*YOUR WALK* Pt.3

Eph 5:1-2 *Be ye therefore followers of God, as dear children;  And walk in love, as Christ also hath loved us, and hath given himself for us an offering and a sacrifice to God for a sweet smelling savour* (KJV).

*WALKING IN LOVE*

The subject of love is a re occuring emphasis on our pulpits. In my former church, a drama was staged on the supremacy of  love over faith and hope. The scenes are still vivid in my imagination like yesterday.

The word 'love' in context of the scripture above refers to 'Agape' love. That is  sacrificial love. That is a type of love that is not self-centred.

Agape love is the type of love God has towards us that made him to send Christ to die for our sins: *"But God showed his great love for us by sending Christ to die for us while we were still sinners*"( Romans 5:8).

Apostle Paul admonished  Ephesian believers to follow God. He further stated how they are to follow God by telling them to walk in love as Christ hath loved us.

How did Christ demonstrate that love? He gave himself to us as an offering and a sacrifice to God. Hallelujah!!! The key word there is sacrifice.

In 1Cor 13, Apostle Paul took time to explain to us in details what love is all about. It is very easy to sing about loving God in an emotional way even to the point of crying yet the evidence of a love walk may not be there.

Walking in love towards God is about sacrifice and obedience. Jesus said, *“If you love Me, keep My commandments*” (John 14:15).

Another way to understand this is to say that, "those that walk in love with God are those that obey and please him".  *It is impossible to walk with God and not walk in love with men*.

*When we walk in love with men (believers&unbelievers), we bear with them and see how we can make the best of our relationship. Our love for people shouldn't be biased*.

The God-kind of love was shown to us when we were sinners that is why God didn't forsake us in our sinful state. We are expected to do same unto others.

*CONFESSION*: I grow in love of the father, I walk in love with men. The seed of love is in me. As I study the word and devote myself to prayers, love shall grow and I shall mature fully in love In Jesus name.

*YOUR WALK* Pt.4

Eph 5:15 *See then that ye walk circumspectly, not as fools, but as wise*.

*WALK CIRCUMSPECTLY*

The word 'circumspect' is a derivative of the Latin "circumspicere" meaning to be cautious. The basic meaning of Latin circumspicere is "to look around." Near synonyms are prudent and cautious.

As believers, the word of God is saying we must be cautious in our daily walk because there is great evil in our world today. There are several areas of life where we need to be circumspect especially in the choices we make, even where we live.

In our relationships with people, we must be circumspect. One of the reasons Jesus sent the Holy Spirit to us is because he want us to be properly guided in all areas of our life- relationship inclusive.

As  believers, how should we relate with others? Who should be in our company? Answering these questions and many more will guide us in living a circumspect life.

In 2Cor6:14, Apostle Paul says, *"Don’t team up with those who are unbelievers. How can righteousness be a partner with wickedness? How can light live with darkness*"?
*Your best friend and adviser should not be an unbeliever. Towing that path is a sign that you lack basic bible wisdom*.

*In marriage, you should not be united with an unbeliever. Don't be deceived by the niceness and show affection of that unbelieving suitor. He/she could be a ticking time-bomb. You cannot predict a man or woman without Christ*.

In our day-to-day living,  it is wise to pursue peace with men(Heb12:14) without compromising our stand on righteousness.

*CONFESSION*: I walk circumspectly in the name of Jesus.

DEVOTIONAL 1128 | Source start line: 105111

2/23/24, 5:59 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: PROJECTSAVE DEVOTIONAL
Friday,
February 23rd, 2024.


*YOUR WALK* Pt.5


Galatians 5:1 *Stand fast therefore in the liberty wherewith Christ hath made us free, and be not entangled again with the yoke of bondage*( KJV).


*WALK IN LIBERTY*


Apostle Paul wrote the epistle to the Galatian believers. He preached the gospel of Christ to them( Galatians 1:11). The central theme of his message was faith in Jesus' death for the forgiveness of their sins on the cross.

He taught that forgiveness can only be obtained by exercising faith in Christ without the rituals of the law. They believed (Galatians 1:6). His message was clear and simple.


As time progressed, a group of Jewish religious leaders from Jerusalem showed up to teach a different gospel. These men said that it was good to believe in Jesus but men must be circumcised and all must follow the law of Moses to truly be included in the family of God (Galatians 2:4). Without the works of the law, nobody could be saved. That was their teaching.


Paul made it clear that the rituals of the law(of Moses) cannot save any man because the law reveals our sinfulness but it cannot provide a way out(Galatians3:22-24). It was impossible to keep 613 laws of Moses.


Man was doomed to fail under the system of the law: *"For the law never made anything perfect. But now we have confidence in a better hope, through which we draw near to God*" (Hebrews 7:19). Jesus came to show us the way of grace. That's the easy way out.


Through Jesus, the righteousness the old testament saints were trying to achieve has been given to us on the platter of grace (2Cor 5:17-21). You cannot do anything to be saved except by believing in Jesus. Stop trying to mix a life of grace with rituals. *Jesus is enough*.


Like the Galatian believers, some believers are not sure about their salvation. They try to use the arms of the flesh to do something 'special' to please God. There are Mosaic Law driven believers and churches.


The implication of this is that people under this category will be under perpetual guilt and may never feel forgiven for their past. They'll always try but their effort will never be enough. Such people always have heavy burden of guilt they carry around.


This is one bondage the law brings. The writer of Hebrew have this to say about the law: *"The former regulation is set aside because it was weak and useless [19] (for the law made nothing perfect), and a better hope is introduced, by which we draw near to God*"( Hebrews 7:18-19).


Christ didn't come to put you into bondage to the rituals and ceremonies of the law. Stand and walk in that liberty of the cross. To enjoy this new life of grace, learn about the newness of this life from the epistles and walk in the Spirit always.


*CONFESSION*: I walk in the liberty of the cross. I stand firm in the liberty Christ has wrought for me on the cross.

https://youtu.be/KNCsL9foOGQ?si=ws5c8yyfKvoQR28o

Saturday,
February 24th, 2024.

*YOUR WALK* Pt.6

1 John 2:6 *He that saith he abideth in him ought himself also so to walk, even as he walked*( KJV)

*WALKING AS HE WALKED*

I did a study on 1 John chapter 2 so that I can understand the context of Apostle John's statement in verse 6.

I discovered he hit the nail on the head in verses 10 and 11 saying:  *"He that loveth his brother abideth in the light, and there is none occasion of stumbling in him. But he that hateth his brother is in darkness, and walketh in darkness, and knoweth not whither he goeth, because that darkness hath blinded his eyes*".

The 'he' being referred to in that chapter is Jesus. So how did Jesus walk? He walked in love. That was how he walked. The message of love cannot be overemphasized.


You cannot say you love God and hate your brother. On this side of eternity, we cannot see God but we can see our fellow humans created in the likeness and image of God.

The opposite of love is hatred. What is hatred? It is to have ill-feeling towards someone or something. It is to abhor or dislike. We try to paint hatred with the word 'dislike' but they are synonyms. Jesus didn't hate us even in our state of horrible sins(Romans5:8).

He hates sin( the act and state of transgression) but he loves sinners( the person). The test of love would come when you're hated and despised or when you are offended.

Nations have gone to war against one another, brothers have betrayed one another simply because of hatred. I see people seeking deliverance from all forms of demonic bondages but I'm yet to see someone seeking to be delivered from that demon of hatred because they think it is inconsequential whereas it can take people to hell.


Jesus was hated and despised yet he responded with love and compassion. At the point of arrest, he fixed the ear of Malchus the high priest's servant(Luke 22:51, John 18:10). If it were you, would you do that?

It was easy for the anointing to flow through Jesus because everything about him was done in love. That was how he walked.
*PRAYER*: Father, uproot every root of bitterness and hatred in me today in Jesus name.

2/28/24, 5:37 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: ProjectSave Devotional
Wednesday
February 28th, 2024.

*SATAN'S AGENDA FOR THE NEXT GENERATION* pt.4

1Cor 11:1 *Imitate me, just as I also imitate Christ*.

*ABSENCE OF GODLY MODEL*

In Egypt, there were no godly models the Jewish children could pattern their life after. Even If there were, probably Moses and Aaron. The models in Egypt were the magicians, sorcerers, witches, prostitutes and wicked Pharoah himself.


However, these men were on a mission to liberate the Israelites from the yoke of slavery so they were occupied with that assignment. God knew it was impossible for only Moses to model godly character to them that was why he wanted them separated from the Egyptians so their focus can be single.

Pharoah knew that if the children and women were allowed to go, they would see godly models to pattern their lives after and be formed differently.

*Egypt is symbolic of the world's system. The models this perverse system is projecting to our children are perverse hip-hop artists whose lyrics are vulgar, those that promote gangsterism, internet fraud, sexual immorality, improper dressing and all sort of vices*.

These things are no longer hidden unlike before where these things were covert. *The corruption of this world is being advertised on the internet, television and on all social media platforms. It takes grace to raise children that are saved and sanctified in this generation. Why? They are surrounded by wrong models. No thanks to the media*.

*Now we see programmes that promote nudity and immorality and they get paid heavily for these yet intelligence and creativity is not rewarded. All of these are the agenda of Satan for the next generation*.

However, there is hope. Where sin increased, grace abounded the more (Romans5:20). In the absence of godly models, Parents should be the first and major model of godliness to their children.

If there's any major challenge I see here, I think it is that of parents who have never been discipled and parents who grew up in an environment or home where godliness was not modeled. Those kinds of parents may find it hard modeling righteousness to their children.

Often, I tell people that children rarely do what we say sometimes but they do what they see us doing. If your home is hot and smoky, when your children grow up, they may pattern their homes after yours.

A research outcome says that when children grow into adults, they are likely to repeat the pattern of their of parent's marriage. That's why you should do your best to make your marriage to work. Every adult is a product of influence from our childhood.

As a parent, you're the only hope of godly modeling to your children. You can't afford to leave that to the media or people around. At least, for the sake of the next generation, start living a godly life and don't be pretentious about it.

If you make godliness a lifestyle, it will influence your children because they learn mostly by imitation.

*PRAYER*: Father, I receive the grace to be a godly model to my children in Jesus name.
