God is keeping them so they can hear and decide their eternal destination finally. You must yield to the promptings of the Holy Spirit any time he is telling you to preach. You never can tell what will happen to those God has sent you to preach to. Don't joke with the promptings to share the gospel.

PRAYER: Father, I receive the grace to preach the gospel In Jesus name. Amen

4/5/24, 7:05 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: PROJECTSAVE DEVOTIONAL

*ARE YOU IN DOUBT ABOUT HIS RESURRECTION?*


1 Cor 15:14 *And if Christ has not been raised, then all our preaching is useless, and your faith is useless*?

In years past, several theories were propounded by enemies of the cross to debunk the bodily resurrection of Jesus Christ. One of such demonic theories is the SWOON THEORY.


Swoon theory believed that Jesus fainted from exhaustion and was revived in the coolness of the tomb, arose and departed. They forgot that his executioners were professionals who knew how to crucify a criminal to the point of death. He was confirmed dead by his executioners.


Another theory is "HALLUCINATION THEORY". They believe that all of Christ's post resurrection appearances were not real, people who claimed they saw him only had mere hallucinations. This is laughable, isn't it?

He was buried, and he was raised from the dead on the third day, just as the Scriptures said. He was seen by Peter and then by the Twelve. After that, he was seen by more than 500 of his followers at one time, most of whom are still alive, though some have died,( 1 Cor 15:4-6). *Can 500 persons hallucinate all at once*?


The devil didn't stop there. Through his minions, he came up with another theory called "IMPERSONATION THEORY". This is the view that the appearances were not really Christ at all, but someone impersonating Him.

This, the opponents say, is evident because in some cases they did not recognize Him at first (or at all). They fail to realizes that his disciples saw his glorified body( John20:17).


SPIRITUAL RESURRECTION THEORY says Christ’s resurrection was not a real physical resurrection. Proponents of this theory assert that Christ’s body remained in the grave and His real resurrection was spiritual in nature. It was only told this way to illustrate the truth of spiritual resurrection. Big lie! Have they forgotten that the grave became empty as it was recorded in John 20:1-31?

THEFT THEORY is popular among the Jews. They believe his disciples stole his body and claimed that He rose from the dead. This theory was propounded by the religious leaders of his day who paid soldiers to spread the news that his body was stolen, Matthew28:13.


Lastly, some believed his tomb is unknown. How can that be? Didn't they read where the bible says that Joseph of Arimathea requested for Jesus' body to be buried in an unused tomb? Read John19:38-42.

*Satan knows If he can make you believe these lies, your faith in Christ is useless. There wouldn't have been a hope of resurrection for the dead in Christ and that could also mean that there wouldn't be hope of heaven for anyone.  Hallelujah!!!*


Christ arose. He arose  a victor from the domain of darkness because death could not hold him down, the power of resurrection and the glory was too much for the grave to handle.

The earth practically vomited him: *"But God raised him from the dead, freeing him from the agony of death, because it was impossible for death to keep its hold on him*" (Acts2:24). No other religious leader rose from the dead. They're dead, buried and rotten in the grave.

*Christ is not a religious leader, he's a life giver. He carries the power of an indestructible life*. When he arose, he came out triumphant.

All of the powers of hell and Satan their master were disarmed: *"And having disarmed the powers and authorities, he made a public spectacle of them, triumphing over them by the cross*" (Col 2:15 NIV).


Whatever you're facing must bow to the risen Christ.  The power of resurrection is available, tap into it.


*CONFESSION*: I unleash the resurrection power of Christ on (...mention name of your challenge). I reject it and send it back to hell. It is not mine. I refuse to bow to the whims and caprices of the enemy. I no longer belong to the kingdom of darkness. The power of God is at work in my mortal body to quicken me and make me fit spirit, soul and body in Jesus name.

*ARE YOU THIRSTY*?

Isaiah55:1 *“Is anyone thirsty?
Come and drink— even if you have no money!Come, take your choice of wine or milk— it’s all free* (NLT).

DEVOTIONAL 1163 | Source start line: 107833
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
4/7/24, 3:39 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: ProjectSave Devotional
Sunday,
April 7th, 2024.


*PRACTICING GALATIANS 6:6*

Gal6:6 *Those who are taught the word of God should provide for their teachers, sharing all good things with them* (NLT).


There are numerous instructions in God's word for every believer. One out of the numerous instructions many believers shy from are those that enjoined that we support preachers materially.

The moment mention is made of this very important subject, an alert system rings in the heart of many thinking someone is about to manipulate them. I am not doubting that some have taken this subject to the extreme but that should not make us blind to what the bible say.

Regardless of whatever the world says or the sordid experience of people in the hands of manipulators, we should stick to the bible on this subject to avoid extremes. Also, if we believe in preaching and teaching the total counsel of God, then we must emphasize this aspect of the gospel in a balanced manner.

Does the bible teach us to support the welfare( financially & materially) of those that minister to us? The answer is  YES. Let's examine the opening text: *"Those who are taught the word of God should provide for their teachers, sharing all good things with them*" (Galatians 6:6 NLT).

In Greek language, this text is in the imperative mood. That is, a command that must be obeyed. It is not a suggestion neither is it an advice. Apostle Paul says that If you're taught the scriptures by a teacher of God's word as I teach daily through our devotionals,  it is your duty to give back to your teacher, including me If my teaching ministry has been a blessing to you.


Another scripture that is akin to this is 1 Tim5:17: *"The elders who direct the affairs of the church well are worthy of double honor, especially those whose work is preaching and teaching*". The phrase 'double honour' is from the Greek "Diplous Timé". It speaks of giving monetary value to a thing.

In clear terms, Paul was commanding that preachers&teachers be paid handsomely. Note the word "double". They should be paid well.

If you draw spiritual nourishment from a preacher or teacher of the Gospel and your spiritual life has taken a turn for the positive, without being told, giving to such a preacher is your duty.

*You don't give to a minister of the gospel only because he has needs. You give because it is your God-assigned duty to his life whether he has or not*. Don't join the bandwagon of those who believe a minister must be poor to serve effectively.

Psalms 35:27 says *"May those who delight in my vindication shout for joy and gladness; may they always say, “The LORD be exalted, who delights in the well-being ( Prosperity) of his servant*" (NIV, Emphasis mine).

*Lack and poverty doesn't glorify God in the life of his servants. God is happy when the needs of his servants are met and their bills are paid. Comfort makes a genuine minister effective in the discharge of his duties*.

Also, do not say, 'He is doing fine, he doesn't need my token'. Hey! That attitude is very wrong. Scriptures did not enjoin we support ministers because of lack. It is simply our duty to them whether they're in lack or plenty. Do not wait for preachers to be in need before giving to them.

Another set of people often ask preachers to know if they are doing fine. That's not bad but I have observed that some are asking this question to determine whether he is deserving of their substance at that moment or not. If he says 'fine', they assume he's not in need. If he says otherwise, they'll give out of sympathy. Bad!

Having biblical revelation on this subject will change your attitude totally. I give to my teachers out of a biblical revelation that it is my duty not because they are in need. It is an instruction i'm bound to obey in and out of season.


*You must be sensitive enough to know how to come in to be of support to that preacher/ teacher that has blessed you. 'Thank you' doesn't pay his bills, give something. Even if it is little, a true servant of God will accept it and pray for you*.

I was raised by a Pastor and I am also in ministry now. I can tell you authoritatively that most (genuine) pastors are responsible for the welfare of some members and people in one way or the other. They deprive themselves of certain comfort just to make others happy.

If you support that preacher with money or material things, you may be surprised that what he'll spend on himself&family may not be up to 50% because he'll share with others. I know this to be true of missionaries. Yet, there are missionaries struggling to eat three square meal in a day while some are struggling to pay their children's school fees.


Stop waiting till december when your church will organize pastor appreciation service before giving to your pastor. If the church have to organize a special program for such purpose before you give to your pastors, it shows you lack understanding.

Don't wait till december when Projectsave will raise support for missionaries before supporting a missionary. This is God's word to you today, practice it and you'll see the reward.


*ACTION POINT*: Be intentional about blessing a preacher/teacher of God's word labouring over your life in prayers and the word.   It doesn't have to be plenty but that token given consistently will go a long way.

DEVOTIONAL 1164 | Source start line: 108306

4/14/24, 7:11 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: ProjectSave Devotional
Sunday,
April 14th, 2024.


*THE BLESSEDNESS OF BROTHERLY KINDNESS*


2 Pet 1:7 *And to godliness brotherly kindness; and to brotherly kindness charity* (KJV).


Years back, a woman told me about a man who wouldn't help people except they are members of his denomination. That man's definition of brotherly kindness is partial and unbiblical. It skewed in favour of his denomination not the body of Christ.

After I left the university, a friend told me to join a church on the Island so the Pastor can help me get a job. He told me to be active in the workforce. That sounded like a good idea for a job seeker but the underlining fact was I won't be helped if I don't switch denomination.

*God doesn't segregate neither does he select people to be favoured because of denomination. His love is extravagant and boundless.God's family is large and it extend beyond races, tribes, denomination and human boundaries. Our denominations are only units in the family*.

As far as God is concerned, our brothers are members of the household of faith. The household of faith is not our denomination alone but believers who belong to God's family.

Paul says,  *"As we have therefore opportunity, let us do good unto all men, especially unto them who are of the household of faith*" (Gal 6:10). The early church showed to us what brotherly kindness is.

When the church at Antioch heard the church in Jerusalem would soon be suffering from a famine, they gave sacrificially to help relieve their brothers’ and sisters’ financial burden (Acts 11:27–30). Churches in Macedonia and Achaia also contributed to the poor in Jerusalem. These were acts of brotherly kindness.

Brotherly kindness is a major theme of the New Testament. Jesus said, *“A new commandment I give to you, that you love one another: just as I have loved you, you also are to love one another*” (John 13:34).

Do not create denominational boundaries around your show of brotherly love. The blood of Jesus that binds us is thicker and stronger than denominational ties.

*PRAYER*: Father, I receive the grace to show brotherly kindness in Jesus name.

*WINNING FAITH IS A TALKING FAITH*

2Cor10:4-5 (*For the weapons of our warfare are not carnal, but mighty through God to the pulling down of strong holds;)  Casting down imaginations, and every high thing that exalteth itself against the knowledge of God, and bringing into captivity every thought to the obedience of Christ*;( KJV).

Satan told me I'll go to jail. He specifically told me I'll spend two years in jail on arrival in Nigeria. Why? The life of a young man who traveled with me on a mission trip hung on my neck. Both of took ill, we had COVID but his was worse.

This day will the Lord deliver thee into mine hand; and I will smite thee, and take thine head from thee; and I will give the carcases of the host of the Philistines this day unto the fowls of the air, and to the wild beasts of the earth; that all the earth may know that there is a God in Israel.  And all this assembly shall know that the Lord saveth not with sword and spear: for the battle is the Lord's , and he will give you into our hands*",(1 Samuel 17:45-47 KJV).

What reply did you give to the devil the last time he spoke to you?When you notice your imagination is beginning to  run wild over that challenge, talk back. Don't sink into your chair or retire to bed crying, talk back.

Walk around your house or room replying the devil and rebuking him. Be bold to tell him he's a liar. He doesn't have a track record of fulfilled promises.

He may tell you that you'll die poor, wretched, beggarly without a child. Don't let those imaginations and words of his go like that, talk back. Goliath won't stop talking, but the only way to silence him is to talk back.

*Action point*: Talk back before you stop reading this devotional.

*YOU HAVE TEMPORARY RESIDENT PERMIT*

1 Peter 2:11  *Dear friends, I warn you as “temporary residents and foreigners” to keep away from worldly desires that wage war against your very souls*.


While crossing one of the West African borders into another country, the immigration officer who stamped my passport told me that my stay was valid for one month. The stamp on my passport showed a month validity.

It dawned on me that all I wanted to do in that country must not exceed a month or else i'll run into trouble with the law of the land. The resident permit I had there was only for one month not permament.

As children of God, our divine permit to live in this world is temporary not permanent. Why? We are not from here. This is not our place of origin. We are on a crucial assignment temporarily. When our days are over, we'll return home.

Apostle Paul captured this thought saying, *"But we are citizens of heaven, where the Lord Jesus Christ lives. And we are eagerly waiting for him to return as our Savior*"( Philippians 3:20 NLT). Did you see that? Our citizenship is not here but in heaven. Glory!

Any citizenship card we carry in this world is temporary. Very soon, when our assigned days are over, we'll go to where we came from. Halleluyah!!!

Knowing we do not belong here, apostle Peter enjoined that we keep away from worldly desires that wage war against our soul because one can be lost in a strange land. The pleasures and allures of strange land can make the heart drift. When you are in a strange land, you must be conscious of the fact that your liberty is restricted and monitored.

As it is my custom, while i'm outside my earthly country, I am always extra careful not to be found breaking the law or getting into issues that will make the law enforcement agencies come for me. If I ever get into their trouble, I'll be a bad ambassador of my home country and may be punished by their laws.

Same is expected of every child of God on this temporary earth. Live circumspectly knowing you represent a country up above whose image is known for purity and uprightness.

Those in the world may do as they like, dress the way they want, talk anyhow, drink anything and live anyhow but you shouldn't. This is because you are expected to live on this temporary earth the very life  your country of permanent residence is known for.

*PRAYER*: Father, I receive the grace to live right on this temporary earth and to be a good ambassador of my heavenly country in Jesus name.

*SECRET OF ABUNDANCE DURING ECONOMIC HARDSHIP* Pt.1

Genesis 26:1-2 *A severe famine now struck the land, as had happened before in Abraham’s time. So Isaac moved to Gerar, where Abimelech, king of the Philistines, lived. The Lord appeared to Isaac and said, “Do not go down to Egypt, but do as I tell you* (NLT)

*WHAT IS GOD SAYING?*

Sometimes ago, a man shared with me how he spent 12 solid years in U.S.and Canada combined. In all the 12 years spent, they were years of misery, sorrow, confusion and distress. He traveled with the mentality of seeking the proverbial green pastures but it all turned out awry for him. After so many years of fruitlessness, he returned to Nigeria.

Some persons thought he was under the influence of some diabolical powers but it was based on divine arrangement. On getting to Nigeria, good things began to happen to him in succession and he's fulfilled. The very country where people are running from is now his Canaan.

The world is not new to economic recession, scarcity and cash crunch. In the time of Isaac, there was scarcity and people were migrating to Egypt. It is plausible that there was surplus in Egypt and they had the proverbial greener pasture. Probably, he contemplated joining the bandwagon of migrants but God spoke to him expressly not to go.

He obeyed that instruction which may have sounded foolish then considering the harsh economic realities of that day. What was the result? The bible says, *"When Isaac planted his crops that year, he harvested a hundred times more grain than he planted, for the Lord blessed him. HE BECAME A VERY RICH MAN, AND HIS WEALTH CONTINUED TO GROW*", Gen 26:12‭-‬13 NLT. The very land people were running from became the land where he enjoyed super abundance.

The first key to living in plenty during scarcity is to find the relevant instruction for the hour. You need to enquire from God whether to stay or to go. For some, going could be a displacement from God's will while for others, it may be a movement into destiny. However, you cannot tell what it is for you exactly until you're divinely instructed.

*It is only a life lived by divine instruction that will end in distinction*.

The natural instinct of man will want to exit any area where things seems not to be working to places where it is perceived things are better. However, whatever God says is very crucial to avoid regret. If you will live in plenty during scarcity, find relevant instruction for the hour.

*PRAYER*: Father, should I go or stay where I am? I'm open to your leading, Lord speak to me in Jesus name.

DEVOTIONAL 1169 | Source start line: 109310
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
5/10/24, 6:05 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: PROJECTSAVE DEVOTIONAL
Friday,
May 10th, 2024.


*SECRET OF ABUNDANCE DURING ECONOMIC HARDSHIP* Pt.2

1 King 17:3-4 *“Go to the east and hide by Kerith Brook, near where it enters the Jordan River. Drink from the brook and eat what the ravens bring you, for I have commanded them to bring you food*” (NLT).


*LOCATION DETERMINES ALLOCATION*


The nation of Israel experienced famine and drought in the days of Elijah after he decreed that rain or dew won't fall (1Kings17:1). When drought happens, the resultant effect is famine. Vegetations will cease to grow or may become stunted. Farmers groan when rain do not fall in season. Obviously, the nation will experience food crisis and shortage.

God didn't want his servant to suffer so he instructed Elijah to go to brook Cherith. Elijah obeyed God and relocated to brook Cherith where he drank from the river and was fed by ravens: *"So Elijah did as the Lord told him and camped beside Kerith Brook, east of the Jordan. The ravens brought him bread and meat each morning and evening, and he drank from the brook*" (1 Kings 17:5-6 NLT).

That's supernatural provision in a strange place because a raven is a bird not a human. If that happens in today's world, some will doubt it.

Further, I  observed that Elijah moved from brook Cherith to Zarephath after the river dried. God instructed him to relocate to Zarephath: *"Go and live in the village of Zarephath, near the city of Sidon. I have instructed a widow there to feed you*" (1 Kings 17:9 NLT). Elijah ate from this widow's table till the famine ended. We can see clearly how God instructed him to move from one place to the other on two occasions.

*In times of famine, relocation is not bad if led by the Spirit of God*. Similar to what was shared previously about Isaac, Elijah received a clear instruction on where to go for supernatural provision in time of famine.


*The same God that instructed Isaac to stay back and not go to Egypt is the same God that instructed Elijah to relocate to where he'll get abundance of supply during famine. What matters is hearing clearly from God on where to be per time*.

More often than not, your location determines your allocation. *Egypt will never yield for you what has been deposited in Canaan no matter how hard you try*.

Quit using Canaan principles in Egypt, it will end slavery. Just relocate. God brought Jacob and his descendants to Egypt so  their needs could be met but after sometime, their time expired there and they became slaves for overstaying their welcome.

Your location has a lot to do with the quality and quantity of supplies you'll get. Don't be dogmatic about staying where you are presently, your time may be up there. 2 Kings 7:3 told us about four lepers, *'And there were four leprous men at the entering in of the gate: and they said one to another, Why sit we here until we die?*". The lepers at the gate of Samaria were sensitive to know that sometimes, you need to move on to where there is abundance of supplies. They plundered Samaria and even returned to Israel to inform their kinsmen about where they could get abundance of supplies.

You may be flourishing in a location yet God want you to move on. Don't be deceived by little success or comfort. Sometimes, famine could also be a sign or God's way of drawing your attention for a change of location. Whether to stay or to relocate, what matters is that you hear clearly from God.

*A spiritually sensitive man will never partake in famine*.

*PRAYER*: Father, I receive the grace to be sensitive at all times in Jesus name.


====================================================================================================
DEVOTIONAL 1170 | Source start line: 109396
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
5/11/24, 8:09 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: PROJECTSAVE DEVOTIONAL
Saturday,
May 11th, 2024

*SECRET OF ABUNDANCE DURING ECONOMIC HARDSHIP* Pt.3

Proverbs 11:25 *The generous will prosper; those who refresh others will themselves be refreshed* (NLT)

*GIVING OUT OF OBEDIENCE*

My experience during COVID-19 lockdown is a proof that the covenant work. At the onset of lockdown, what I had on me was meagre.  My income was meagre because of the abrupt closure.

I wrote a cheque of a substantial amount 3 months before the lockdown. It was a seed to a woman of God I revere. I expected the cheque to be cleared on time but they did some days to lockdown, the huge amount was debited from my account.  I couldn't wrap my head around the fact that everything would go on standstill.

My children began to eat more than I expected. Maybe idleness caused it. One of the days at the onset of lockdown, my wife went to grind pepper. After grinding, the girl who operated the grinding machine mistakenly poured everything on the floor. She returned with nothing. It was a serious issue. From the little we had, my wife and I began to give people in distress. Though it wasn't easy but we did it anyway.

Later, I was prompted to raise money for brethren and missionaries in need. In obedience, we started a fund raising for COVID-19 palliative and we raked in almost a million naira but I didn't take a dime from it though I needed money to feed my family as well.

I sold one of my books for a period of time to raise money for palliative and I faithfully gave all proceeds to those in need and I sent evidence of disbursement to almost everyone who supported the cause. In all of this, we had great needs in the family.

Those who supported the palliative initiative didn't even bother to ask for my account details and I became worried because it was as If I was forgotten.  I didn't know it was a test. I told those who asked about my welfare that I was fine. I just didn't want to  take advantage of their benevolence at that time and divert funds to myself.

Not long after, someone requested for my account details. l received a "dangerous" alert. Quickly I paid my tithe and I supported brethren in need from the gift. I thought I'll manage the remaining till the end of lock down. Surprisingly, the bank alert of that miraculous supply I received became a monthly thing up until some months after the lockdown.

At the beginning of every new month, before the end of the first five days, dangerous supplies poured in. Miraculously, God paid my school fees( in the seminary) for two straight sessions during the lockdown.

*Sacrificial giving is a time tested method of opening the heavens for supernatural supply during scarcity. Those that don't know how to give are not ready to live in plenty during scarcity*.

Stop waiting for when you'll have plenty before giving. Giving when you don't have plenty is the way to know if truly you're a giver.

*Give freely and become more wealthy; be stingy and lose everything* (Proverbs 11:24 NLT).

*PRAYER*: Father, I receive the grace to excel in giving in Jesus name.

May 14th, 2024.

*RESPOND TO GOD'S LOVE*

Romans5:5 *And this hope will not lead to disappointment. For we know how dearly God loves us, because he has given us the Holy Spirit to fill our hearts with his love* ( NLT).

Most times, we preach about God's love to unbelievers and present the gospel to them with simplicity  but once they become born again, we present God differently. The fear is that if we dwell on God's love, they may be wreckless. That can only happen if we preach an imbalanced message of love ( Rom6:1,John14:15).

Some believers have the challenge of accepting God's love. When you see someone who has not forgiven himself or herself, failure to accept God's love as revealed in the scriptures is responsible for such. If  you understand the love of God, you'll stop hating yourself.

God's love is deep. That was why Paul prayed this prayer for the Ephesian believers: *"And may you have the power to understand, as all God’s people should, how wide, how long, how high, and how deep his love is*" (Eph3:18). He prayed this prayer for believers not unbelievers.

His prayer  for Ephesian believers should be our prayer as well. *Those that are struggling to accept God's love always struggle to love others*.

There are many believers struggling to accept God's love because they always feel inadequate and unworthy of God every time. This is the tactics of the devil to weaken their faith.

*Have you noticed that thoughts of guilt becomes stronger when you want to pray especially when you want to deal a deadly blow on the onslaught of the enemy*?

The devil knows that you can wreck havoc on his kingdom If you pray that's why he's always firing the darts of condemnation into your heart.

God is for you, he is not against you.  He is ensuring that you walk in his perfect will. Your duty is to cooperate with him( Phil2:12-13). You may have failed and fallen, but you should not remain in your mess because he wants to clean you up.

*“Come now, let’s settle this,” says the LORD. “Though your sins are like scarlet, I will make them as white as snow. Though they are red like crimson, I will make them as white as wool*"( Isa1:18 NLT).

The point of failure is where the devil is always waiting to tell you God doesn't love you anymore. At this point, you may find yourself asking for forgiveness for the same shortcoming over and over because you feel unloved and unaccepted by God.

*How you feel about yourself maybe totally wrong when compared with how God sees you. We don't walk with God by feelings but by faith*. The reason why God is quick to forgive and restore  is because of his love for us.

It is the devil that desires to keep you on the spot of self pity, self hatred and false guilt. Open your heart to the Holy Spirit, he'll fill your heart with God's love and make you see yourself from God's perspective.

*PRAYER*: Father, I thank you for loving me. Spirit of God, fill my heart with God's love and help me accept his love in Jesus name.

May 15th, 2024.

*THE TRAP OF BEING PERFORMANCE-DRIVEN*

1 John 4:1 *Beloved, do not believe every spirit, but test the spirits to see whether they are from God, for many false prophets have gone out into the world*.

A preacher told another pastor friend of his that "people want proofs and you must be able to tell them 'thus saith the Lord' because that is what they want to hear". This pastor told his friend there are prophets at the beach side that would give them such power. He was performance-driven.

All he wanted was to minister with proofs but not minding the processes involved whether it is right or wrong. It is not bad to desire proofs neither is it a sin to be performance-driven. Where it poses problem is when the processes involved is anti scriptures.

We live in a performance- driven world where employers want employees to produce results. That idea has been transported into the spiritual terrain as well. Banks give deposit targets to their staff not minding If the ladies get customers through immorality. What they want is result.

There's a common mantra that says "only fools doubt proofs", but in the light of scriptures, God and spiritual men can doubt proofs especially If the proofs are not in tandem with the teachings of the scriptures.

God doubted and questioned 'proof' in the bible when Moses struck the rock instead of speaking to it as instructed by God. When the people saw water gushing out, they were happy to have gotten what they wanted but God was sad. Why? The result did not follow the laid down process.

*God is not concerned about proofs and result as much as He is with the process involved and alignment with scriptures*.

Apostle Paul knew that proofs can be produced and results can be displayed without having its root from the accuracy of scriptures that was why he  said we should "test all spirits whether they are of God".

Alot of anti biblical practices are gaining popularity today. If you come on social media, you'll see prophets teaching people how to use elements from nature and mentioning the names of certain angels to get result. There is no precedence for such in scriptures.

In matters of the kingdom, the word of God is our final arbiter. When practices don't align with scriptures properly whether it is producing result or not, don't be involved. The devil loves to back unscriptural practices with results to deceive the people. Beware!

*PRAYER*: Father, I receive the grace to follow your word accurately In Jesus name.

June 2nd, 2024.


*WAIT UNTIL YOU ARE SENT*


Luke 1:80 *As John grew up, God’s Spirit gave him great power. John lived in the desert until the time he was sent to the people of Israel* (CEV).


I heard about a minister who had the call of God upon his life. He was in training under a senior pastor. This young minister demanded to leave before the set time of his training. His senior counseled against taking such hasty decision because it wasn't time yet but he was adamant.


Not long after, he returned as a casualty of one of his ministrations. He went somewhere to preach, as he ministered to someone under demonic influence, the lady spat on his eyes, that led to visual impairment. Thank God his sight was restored after his senior pastor prayed for him. Launching out at the wrong time can expose a man to demonic attacks.


Sometimes, we read certain verses of the bible speedily without taking into cognizance some words and phrases used. John the baptist was a child born as a result of prophecy, his future was declared by prophets of the old testament.

Prophecy pointed to him as the fore-runner of Jesus Christ yet the bible said he lived in the desert until he was sent. That's incredible.

How can a man whose destiny was declared by prophecy wait in the wilderness to be sent to fulfil the assignment he was destined to fulfil? Why should he wait? Could it be because he was not old enough to start his life's assignment?

So many questions but the answer is right there in the verse. It was clear that he was not sent because his time had not come. God uses the waiting time to prepare the called.

In Luke 3, we were told that God's word came to him in the wilderness and after that time, he began his public ministry.


*Do not start a spiritual assignment until you receive a go ahead from God*.

There are times God reveals or gives us a clue about what He would have us do but the time to launch out may not be immediate. There's always a waiting time of preparation before the time of manifestation.

Even Jesus who had divinity running in his blood couldn't start his ministry until the set time because He needed to prepare.

That revelation you had about God's vision for your life requires some waiting time of preparation before launching out.

Launching out  against God's timing is akin to sending yourself and you'll pay dearly for it.

*ACTION POINT*: Be sensitive to God's timing.

DEVOTIONAL 1174 | Source start line: 111871
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
7/17/24, 1:58 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: ProjectSave Devotional
Wednesday,
July 17th, 2024.

*GOD HAS NOTHING FOR SALE* Pt.1

Acts 8:20  *But Peter said unto him, Thy money perish with thee, because thou hast thought that the gift of God may be purchased with money* ( KJV).

*VENDORS OF TOKENS*

Recently, I saw a poster advertising a miracle soap. The seller of the soap is a self-styled prophet who claimed the soap will make buyers billionaires. That wouldn't have been an issue for me because i've seen similar gimmicks severally. What irked me about the poster was the fact that he claimed the soap was approved by Jesus.

How people sell tokens in the name of God and people still buy it because God's name is involved amazes me. We need to understand the nature of anything God gives so we can identify phony people who use the name of Christ to drive their merchandise. Where in the bible did Jesus and his apostles sell materials to the people so they can get a miracle?

The spirit of mercantilism has eaten deep into the fabric of some preachers. They've placed a price tag on every of God's gift. They have turned marketers and sellers of miracles thereby presenting God wrongly. I am here to tell you that whatever God gives or will be giving is free and cannot be bought with money because Christ paid it all. God has nothing for sale.

Romans 12:32 says, *"He that spared not his own Son, but delivered him up for us all, how shall he not with him also FREELY GIVE US ALL THINGS*? ( KJV). This scripture reveals to us the nature of God as regards gift. He doesn't sell it neither does he place a monetary price tag on it. He gives all freely.

If God starts selling his miracles through tokens like anointing oil, prayer water, special mantle, soap, candle, honey and other tokens, only the rich would buy it because the poor won't be able to afford it. I am sure you can't afford it because it will be too expensive.

Apostle Peter's response to Simon Magus in Acts 8:20 shows that miracles, healings, breakthrough and blessings cannot be bought with money:  *"But Peter replied, “May your money be destroyed with you for thinking God’s gift can be bought* ( NLT).

Only fake prophets and preachers sell tokens at a special price because it promises miracles and blessings. Now, don't tell me their token works. Satan also makes items work miracles so he can deceive people. That a thing or practice works is not the determinant of its genuineness. Satan knows how to make things work. You need to grow your discernment through the word so you can know when Satan is in operation.

Run from vendors of special prayer water, miracle soap, healing oil, anointed honey, deliverance padlock, cleansing broom, miracle mantle and other items people sell that promises miracles. *God is not a miracle merchant. He is not selling to his children what Christ has paid for*.

To receive anything from God, all you need is faith in the name and finished work of Christ. No buying no selling.

*PRAYER*: Father, I receive the grace to put my faith in the power inherent in the name of Jesus and his finished work on the cross in Jesus name. Amen

*EIGHT TRUTHS ABOUT MARRIAGE* Pt.1

Genesis 2:18  *The Lord God said, “It is not good for the man to be alone. I will make a helper suitable for him*” (NIV)

*MARRIAGE IS GOD'S IDEA NOT HUMAN TRADITION*

In recent times, Satan has been working to distort a fundamental truth about marriage. He is presenting marriage as a cultural or traditional idea founded upon customary laws. A number of persons embraced this untruth and ran with it.

Oftentimes, when you see people canvass for polygamy, they do so from the cultural standpoint with a few misunderstood biblical references. Those in this category tell unverified tales about how the marriages of our polygamous forefathers lasted and how they lived long despite keeping a harem.

They failed to ask if the women in those marriages were happy. They didn't consider the several spiritual and physical attacks on children from polygamous homes and the psychological impact growing in such a hostile environment. What about wastage of destinies, toxicity and manipulation of glories in polygamous homes?

Marriage was not instituted by your village chiefs nor your government. *Marriage is a sacred and divine institution founded by God*.

*The origin and originator of a thing is important If we are to have a good grasp of that thing. The founder of every institution has a vision and purpose. Every manufacturer has a manual of operation for his product. Same applies to marriage*.

*If we must succeed in marriage, we must see marriage from God's perspective or else we'll fail.  Most marriages are troubled and failing because there's a deviation from the laid down pattern*.

*The best marriage manual still remains the bible. That's where we can find God's pattern for a successful marriage*.

The greatest institution under attack in the world today is marriage. I stand to be corrected on this. Satan is trying to make marriage look unappealing and frightening especially to singles. Many singles are afraid of settling down in marriage seeing what has become of many marriages including that of pastors. The fear is real.

*Most people enter into marriage with ideas that are alien to the bible because they don't know who instituted marriage and why He instituted it*. This is the primary reason for the several marital failures we see today.

The moment you know the founder of marriage, the purpose he had in mind and the principles he set in motion for it to work, you will have a blissful marriage.

Today, most people want to hear messages like: ' Seven Keys To  Marital Bliss'. They want quick fixes but not the bible. On the pages of the scriptures, you'll see God's pattern and principles for a successful marriage.

When I wanted to marry, I read alot about marriage and the strange woman from the book of Proverbs. I'm yet to see a book on marriage that gave  explicit practical wisdom on how to handle the strange woman and marriage like Proverbs and other books of the bible.

*It is impossible to have marital crisis ( not conflict) If two persons in marriage are following the biblical pattern laid by God for marriage. Absolutely impossible*.

When I see believers in Christ having marital crisis and breakdown, it is plausible one or the two persons involved are flouting biblical injunction on marriage. True christian living by the two in marriage is all that is needed to make any marriage work.

*Believers need thorough Christian discipleship than marriage seminars. Marriages of true disciples don't fail*.

Failure to follow God's manual for life and marriage will lead to serious marital breakdown. Don't run your marriage according to the dictates of the society, public opinion or your culture, get into the manual- the bible.

PRAYER: Father, I shall not fail maritally In Jesus name.

*EIGHT TRUTHS ABOUT MARRIAGE* Pt.5

1 Pet3:7 *In the same way, you husbands, live with your wives in an understanding way [with great gentleness and tact, and with an intelligent regard for the marriage relationship]*.... AMP

*LOVE ATTRACTS BUT UNDERSTANDING SUSTAINS MARRIAGE*

The media is awash with stories of divorce cases. Some think it is only celebrity marriages that crash but some soft sells report similar cases among ordinary people.

These are people who once professed undying love for one another before severing ties. I have asked severally what went wrong in most of those marriages.

As an aside, I have observed another strange trend among some divorcees. When asked If they love their estranged spouses, quite a number claimed they still loved their exes  but they can't live together because of irreconciliable differences.

I know you've heard that statement too. If a man is professing to still love a woman he has divorced, what could be wrong?

My conclusion was that lack of understanding contributed to the failure of their marriage not lack of love. *Love can attract but understanding sustains*.

We have seen love birds who professed undying love for each other but ended their marriage bitterly. When you talk to divorcees, they may speak bitterly about their estranged spouses but deep within, the affection is still there.

Misunderstanding in marriage results from 'missing understanding' about your partner. When understanding is missing, love evaporates. Once proper understanding is in place, the marriage remains and love is sustained.

As a husband, you need  to study your wife so you can understand her.Same applies to wives. *You love your spouse the way they want to be loved not your own way*.

The way Mr 'A' is loving and living with his wife may not work for you because Mrs 'A' is different from your wife. She has a different love language. That's what Gary Chapman taught in his book: Love Languages. The love of everyone has a unique language that must understood.

*As you love, seek to understand*.

Don't complain that your wife talks too much. Generally women are wired to talk more than men. Just be a good listener. They need attention and may react when they're not getting enough of it.

There are intrinsic need of both genders in marriage that must be understood for love to flourish. Men want respect, reverence and sex while women want affection, attention and romance. Understanding these basic needs about your spouse and how to meet them will prosper your love life.

What is love without  understanding what keeps love alive in your spouse?

Don't complain about your husband's love for sport, be part of his recreational activities. If he's a Chelsea fan, be an Arsenal fan. Watching football together can be romantic. Change the things you can change but don't be mad about those things you can't change.

*PRAYER*: Father, I receive the grace to love my spouse with understanding In Jesus name.

*THE DEPTH OF GOD'S FORGIVENESS*

Psalm 130:3-4 *If you, Lord, kept a record of sins, Lord, who could stand? But with you there is forgiveness, so that we can, with reverence, serve you* (NLT)

Recently, I read the opening text during a bible reading session in the church and my heart was set on fire about the depth of God's forgiveness to sinful mankind. I have read this portion of scriptures severally but I didn't see the light in that text until recently.

The Psalmist made it clear that forgiveness is with God. He went further to state the reason for God's forgiveness: "...so that we can, with reverence, serve you( God)" This is profound. God forgives so you and I can serve him with reverence.

Our reverence for God should come from the understanding that he is able to unleash his wrath on sinners and send us to the hottest part of hell without an option of seeking forgiveness yet he didn't do that. Instead, he made provision available for our cleansing and forgiveness.

If God had decided not to make provision for forgiveness, you and I will not survive. That's why the Psalmist says in vs 3, *"If you, Lord, kept a record of sins, Lord, who could stand*"? The answer is that no one could stand. His wrath would have consumed us all.

In Christ, God has lavished his forgiveness on us all because of the atoning sacrifice of Christ. Paul says, *"In him we have redemption through his blood, the forgiveness of sins, in accordance with the riches of God’s grace*" Ephesians 1:7 (NIV)

*Whenever God remembers the blood of Jesus, it is a reminder of his willingness to forgive our sins*.
He doesn't stop there. When he forgives, he forgets. Religion tells you otherwise but my bible tells me your sins are forgiven and forgotten by God after repentance.

*"I, even I, am he who blots out your transgressions, for my own sake, and remembers your sins no more*". - Isaiah 43:25 (NIV)

The writer of Hebrews re echoed this truth: *"And I will forgive their wickedness, and I will never again remember their sins*" ( Hebrews 8:12, NLT).

Under the New Covenant that God made with us through the shedding of Christ's blood on the cross, our sins are forgiven and forgotten. What we have in Christianity is what many are struggling to obtain through religion.

*When someone who can unleash his wrath decides to forgive and forget, serve him with great reverence*.

*CONFESSION*: Father, I thank you because my sins are forgiven and forgotten. Thank you for erasing the record that stood against me. Thank you for wiping it clean with the blood of Jesus Christ. I receive grace to extend this grace of forgiveness to others and to serve you with reverential fear in Jesus name. Amen
This book is a must-read for singles: *9 REASONS GOOD PEOPLE MARRY BAD PEOPLE*.

Selar: https://selar.co/m/lanre-oyekele1

amazon: https://a.co/d/ceaCVab

*SCREEN IT WITH THE WORD*

1Jhn4:1-Beloved, believe not every spirit, but TRY the spirit whether they are of God...

Sometimes ago, someone narrated a revelation to me about a woman that claimed to have seen Jesus. She said Jesus told her that whenever couples are having sex, the HolySpirit will come out of them and stay away from them. This lie went viral.

She sold books and tapes of well formed lies and many bought into it. Later, I was told by a minister of the Gospel that her revelation caused a lot of problems in a church because some of the women don't want the Holy Spirit to "jump" out of them during sex so they began to deny their husbands.

We can't tell how many homes that evil revelation destroyed. That woman later recanted all she said and denied seeing a revelation. This happened years after many people have patronized her lies and bought her books and tapes.

You need to know the difference between Holy Spirit and familiar spirits. Demons also reveal things to people and it'll look real. That someone claimed to have seen Jesus or a vision of heaven/hell doesn't make him/her authentic.

Some are freaks of those spurious liars claiming to have seen what the ordinary eye can't see. I'm not saying God doesn't show people visions but running after visions and revelations isn't the right thing for a child of God to do.

*Your desire should be to know more of God through the word of God. If there's anything I desire to know accurately, it is the written word*.

*When someone comes telling you "thus saith the Lord", you have a biblical right not to accept what he/she is saying until it is adequately proven*.

Listen to John, *"Dear friends, do not believe everyone who claims to speak by the Spirit. You must test them to see if the spirit they have comes from God. For there are many false prophets in the world*".(1 John 4:1 NLT)

*The biblical litmus test for revelations is to check if it conforms to God's word and His principles. Better put, we need to check if it conforms to accurate interpretation of scriptures*.

A revelation may resemble something that is written in scriptures yet it doesn't conform properly to God's standard if interpreted accurately. A man said all of us will face the white throne judgement because he saw it in a revelation but I disagreed because the believer won't face the white throne judgement as revealed in scriptures. We'll face "BEMA" judgement.
Even though white throne judgement is found in scriptures and he saw it in his revelation, I stand with accurately interpreted scriptures on that issue.

Whatever message or revelation that leaves out the fundamentals of our faith which is salvation by grace isn't from God. Any revelation that brings us into legalistic bondage and rituals like it was in the Mosaic days isn't from God.

Christ has become the centre of our worship. Whatever cannot be found in Christ and properly explained in the epistles is obviously not by the Spirit. Don't go around patronizing lies and liars.

*PRAYER*: I receive grace to know the accurate truth In Jesus Name.

*ARE YOU SENT BY GOD*?

John1:6 God sent a man, John the Baptist, NLT

I heard about a man who went to the mountain to pray. He was heard saying, "Lord, if you don't call me, I'll call myself".  It is possible to pursue a self imposed vision and preach a self composed message without being sent. There are many 'unsent' people in ministry today delivering a copied message.

After Absalom died, Joab sent a Cushite to deliver the message of Absalom's death to David. Ahimaaz, the son of Zadok who was not sent, outran the Cushite to deliver the message but when David heard him, he couldn't make any sense out of what he said. David told him to step aside.

When you preach a message that is not given to you, you'll end up confusing your listeners.  When the Cushite, the man who was sent, delivered his message, David understood him properly and he was filled with emotions (2Sam18:20-33).

The old prophet delivered a message that was not given to him to the young prophet. The result of that was the death of a young promising prophet who had a bright future in ministry. That's why the lives and destinies of some people have been damaged under hirelings.

*Sent men preach life but unsent men damage destinies.*

*True ministry begins with a sense of calling by God and the progression of your ministry must be done based on how you were sent.*

Did God really send you or you sent yourself? What about that program you're doing? Are you sure you're not trying to use it to prove a point? Some persons do programs to prove they can, some pioneer church out of 'I can do it'. Little wonder there are many dry and cold pulpits today.

*There are many echoes in ministry today but few voices.*

There are those who copy the programs and concepts of another ministry thinking if they do same, they'll "blow". They copy to the point of copying the name of another ministry and adding theirs to it.

Some are always scavenging for the message of another preacher, no originality about their message. They no longer spend time in prayers and study. Such people end up preaching cold messages to cold people who may keep shouting, "ride on Pastor".

John the Baptist was a man sent by God. He had a divine mandate and message. His message was short but impactful. Devoid of theatrics and manipulation. We  can summarize it as: "Repent, for the kingdom of God is at hand".

Due to this, we were told that: *"John was like a burning and shining lamp,...*"(John5:35 NLT). Knowing your calling and staying true to the message given to you is a good way to make full proof of your calling. Stop copying others.

*PRAYER*: Father, I receive grace to do ministry according to the pattern in Jesus name.

DEVOTIONAL 1180 | Source start line: 112953

8/22/24, 6:37 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: PROJECTSAVE DEVOTIONAL
Thursday,
August 22nd, 2024.

*YOUR MINISTRY IS IMPORTANT- TREASURE IT*

Romans 11:13 I am talking to you Gentiles. Inasmuch as I am the apostle to the Gentiles, I take pride in my ministry. NIV

When I started out in field ministry, I was tempted to see it as a lesser ministry than others. There were people who spoke as though there was no big deal in what I was doing. Some counselled that with time, the ministry would metamorphose into a church.

I will not forget someone who told me that my mates were planting churches in town and they were doing well. It beats the imagination of people why I decided to comb bushes and forests in the name of soul-winning. It is over ten years now and i'm glad to be a 'bush evangelist'.

I once took a brother along to the mission field years back, on our way, he asked to know why I was passionate about a rugged place like where we were going. When he saw the road and the people, he couldn't hide his feelings. He forgot that the famous John the Baptist had his ministry headquarters in the wilderness not the city.

At a point in time,  I felt like quitting. Anytime I move round Lagos, I see churches springing up with young  pastors. In my heart, I felt my assignment was little.  Also, those were times of great financial distress and I felt pastoring would better my lot. At that time, the devil began to bring suggestions and I began contemplating starting a church in a posh area of Lagos.

I called the man that God used to bring me into missions. I narrated everything to him. He asked a simple question that knocked out my plans: *"what did God tell you in the beginning*"? I couldn't give an answer because *in this current phase of assignment,*(ministry could be in phases sometimes) I remembered God warned me not to deviate from the call. Ever before I was tempted to abandon field ministry, God warned me through people I don't know that I should not deviate.

Dear minister of the most high God, don't look down on your assignment as insignificant. I perceive strongly in my spirit that someone reading this piece is about quitting his mainline of ministry to pursue another office that he is not ordained for. It may be disastrous.

*God does not reward us for doing just anything, he rewards us for staying faithful to what He has called us to do even If men see it as insignificant.*

If children ministry is your calling, do it with all your heart and never allow anyone talk you down. *Run from suggestions and idea that will tilt you from your calling.*

We live in a generation where most ministers want to stand in the office of a prophet.  Some abandoned the core work of pastoring and they became a self acclaimed prophet because that's the reigning office, little wonder why there are so many Balaam.

Some evangelist felt field ministry is not profitable so they opened a church so that they can have a financial base by passing the offering bowl every week around but they lost it. Whatever your calling is, guard it jealously.

Paul said, *"...I take pride in my ministry*'( Romans 11:13 NIV). I want you to take pride in your ministry. Do not let anyone talk you out it neither should you lookdown on yourself.

*It is better to be a global voice in the ministry right from the wilderness than to be an echo in the city.*

*PRAYER*: Father, I receive the grace to be faithful to my calling in Jesus name.

8/23/24, 12:26 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: PROJECTSAVE DEVOTIONAL
Friday,
August 23rd, 2024.

*WHY DO YOU WANT TO MARRY HER*?

Isaiah48:17 *This is what the LORD says—
your Redeemer, the Holy One of Israel:
“I am the LORD your God,who teaches you what is good for you and leads you along the paths you should follow*( NLT).

During my final year research project in the university, I was returning from the lab on a sunny day when I heard the Lord ask me a question: 'why do you want to marry sister 'X'? I was jolted like a statue in the middle of an overflowing river. The voice was clear. I knew I heard God.

Proverbs 16:2 says, *“All a person’s ways seem pure to them, but motives are weighed by the LORD*”. God already weighed my motives and they were not in tandem with his.

I couldn't utter a word of reply because I wanted to engage that sister simply because she was fair to behold, smooth skinned and I love her body structure and carriage. She's had hour glass shape and stunning physique. That was all, nothing else.

I lost sight of destiny and focused on the container without checking If the content was good for my journey. I saw the present at that time but didn't peep into my future with the eye of the Spirit to check If we were fit. It was all for my carnal desires.

Somehow, I couldn't respond to that question because I knew where God was driving at. I killed the thought and perished the idea. God began to teach me somethings about patience. He showed me the path that I must follow on the matter.

In my eyes, I knew what was good for me but God said it wasn't good for me and I accepted his verdict. Can God interrupt your plans and desires? After weighing your motives and you're found wanting, can you surrender to him and forsake your plans?

*One reason most believers do not listen or obey God's voice on crucial matters is because they don't trust God enough to manage their lives*.

Some think their choice is better than God's choice for them because they fear he might give them what is bad. Christians who are wired to think this way do not know God neither do they know his ways.

In revealing the true nature of God to us, Jesus said, *“You parents—if your children ask for a loaf of bread, do you give them a stone instead? [10] Or if they ask for a fish, do you give them a snake? Of course not! [11] So if you sinful people know how to give good gifts to your children, how much more will your heavenly Father give good gifts to those who ask him*"( Mt 7:9-11).

God is working in your best interest. He cannot give you what is bad. Don't be wise in your own eyes.  He is a giver of good gifts according to the scripture cited above.

*The day you stop playing pranks with God and you are ready to obey him totally, you will hear Him clearly*.

Get to that point where you're pliable in God's hands and don't be rigid. That decision you're about to make, let the Spirit of God teach you what is good for you and not the flesh or worldly ideas.

*Why do you want to take that step*?

*PRAYER*: Father, I receive grace to stay sensitive to your voice and to obey In Jesus name. Amen

DEVOTIONAL 1182 | Source start line: 113040
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
8/24/24, 1:15 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: PROJECTSAVE DEVOTIONAL
Saturday,
August 24th, 2024.

*BIBLE TRUTH SUPERCEDES TRENDS*

John 8:32 *And you will know the truth, and the truth will set you free*.” NLT

There are common trends inevery generation. Those trends could manifest  in the area of fashion, music, dance, slang, commerce, trade and religion. In the body of Christ, there are trends that has come and gone.

Funnily, there was a time some pastors barbed punk hair style and posed with it. Young ministers posed for the camera with their punk and it seemed to be the common trend among preachers of that day. Images of posters in that era remain etched in my memory.

Several years ago, almost every preacher wanted to be called "Evangelist" because of the outbreak of God's power through the evangelistic ministry at that time but after a while, the craze for that title died.

As I speak, the trend is to be called a prophet or an apostle.  Even those who started ministry yesterday are now apostles teaching 'mysterious' things beyond scriptures. The use of superfluous words and esoteric registers is common to them.

There was a time when success motivation was the common trend on pulpits. Conferences and seminars were organized for motivating people not for bible study. That trend died and we are in the era of another.

While all those may not matter much, the painful thing is when preachers preach trending messages that aren't necessarily biblical so as to attract people.

When 'prosperity gospel' landed in our clime, what you'll hear often from most charismatic altars were prosperity messages that weren't biblical. Those messages ended with, "sow a seed to prosper".Unfortunately, I once preached it. It was a trend and probably it is still in practice in some circles but may not be so common as it was.

When hyper grace movement began to trend, some preachers abandoned the truth and began to preach trending hypergrace messages. In the process they presented God's grace wrongly. Why? They preached it because it was the trending message not because they understood what the grace of God meant from scriptures.

*In a bid to attract crowd and remain popular in ministry, some deliberately abandoned the truth for trends*.

*That something is trending doesn't validate it as the right thing. Sin trends. A message may trend but it may lack the substance of truth in it*.

*One of the temptations you'll face in the ministry is to abandon the truth for trends.*

I have discovered that trends don't last, they come as a wave and fizzle out with time.

*The truth in God's word is ageless, timeless and eternal. Biblical truth is the same everywhere, every time and at all times.*

Stop copying trends, search for the truth, know it, live it and preach it. We live in a generation where abnormal is assumed to be normal. Trends get so much publicity and attention.

In the days of Jesus and John the baptist, Pharisaic hypocrisy trended but when those two appeared on the scene, the message of repentance filled the air. There was a restoration of timeless and ageless truth- the message of the kingdom.

This warning is not limited to those in ministry. As a child of God, seek the truth and practice it. That trending fashion may be ungodly, that trending music or movie may put out the fire of the Spirit in you.

*Beware of erroneous trends that people have accepted as true. Never sacrifice the truth for trends*.

*PRAYER* Father, I receive the grace to stand on and for the truth at all times.

*THE SIMPLICITY IN CHRIST*

2 Corinthians 11:3 *But I fear, lest by any means, as the serpent beguiled Eve through his subtilty, so your minds should be corrupted from the simplicity that is in Christ*

Several years ago, I had a bad dream. In that dream, some persons attempted to set me ablaze. When I woke up, I was terrified so I told a brother. He told me to take a prophetic action of stabbing the edge of my bed. Due to my low level of knowledge then, I took a knife and stabbed the edge of my bed severally.

I wish I had known the power in the name of Jesus and my authority as a believer, I wouldn't have stabbed the bed. There is an easy way out in Christ becauase what ignorance and religion made complex, Christ made easy.

Our walk in Christ is meant to be a faith walk. A walk that believes only in the finished work of Christ on the cross. A walk where we obtain our victory, healing and answered prayers on the merit of sacrifice and not on rituals and sacrifices prescribed by religion.

Some persons prescribe 'rituals' for obtaining forgiveness and salvation. Something Christ paid for already with his precious blood. Whenever I hear some preachers tell people to spend days in fasting and prayers before obtaining forgiveness, I know the spirit of legalism is at work.

How long does it take God to forgive, 2, 3, 4, 5,6,7 days? 1 John 1:9 KJV says, *"If we confess our sins, he is faithful and just to forgive us our sins, and to cleanse us from all unrighteousness*". The very moment you repent and confess your sins, you are forgiven.

I have been to prayer meetings where the prayer leader asked that we confess sins of our childhood. So I thought to myself, how long will it take God to forgive the sins of my childhood? Does he keep records of sins I have confessed?

Isaiah 43:25 *“I, even I, am he who blots out your transgressions, for my own sake, and remembers your sins no more*".

God doesn't keep record of forgiven sins.

Religion attempts to make hard what Christ has made easy. For instance, some go to church with canes to beat their enemies, some go with cutlass to kill their enemies. Some have imported African Tradition rituals into Christianity thinking it is the way out as long as the name of Jesus Christ is called into it.

There are doctrines of demons everywhere. 1 Timothy 4:1 says, *“The Spirit explicitly says that in later times some will fall away from the faith, paying attention to deceitful spirits and doctrines of demons*" (NASB).

*Anything that takes the shine off Christ and his finished work on the cross is demon inspired*.

*CONFESSION*: Father I thank you for the cross. I thank you for redemption, I thank you for the blood that you shed for me on calvary. I thank you for my victory over sin, satan and demons.

Readers are leaders. Purchase any of my Spirit-inspired books on line.

IG: @Projectsave_ministries
@LanreOyeleke

Twitter: @OyelekeBenjamin

Email: info@projectsaveng.org

*HAPPY SUNDAY*

DEVOTIONAL 1184 | Source start line: 113252
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
8/27/24, 7:04 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: ProjectSave Devotional
Tuesday
August 27th, 2024.

*OVERCOMING WORLDLY SYSTEMS*  Pt.1

1John2: 15 *Love not the world, neither the things that are in the world. If any man love the world, the love of the Father is not in him*

*LOVE NOT THE WORLD*

I read a fictional story on the internet about a man who just bought a brand-new BMW car. As he opened the door to get out of the car, another car sped by and tore off the door of the car. He got out and he was starring at his car.

A bystander came and said, 'Sir, don't look at your car. Look at your arm.' He asks, 'What's wrong with my arm?' 'It's torn off!' And the man then looks at his arm on the floor and says, 'Oh no, my Rolex watch!'.

From the same online source, I read that when a man falls down with an iPhone 6 in his pocket and he hears a 'crack' sound, he will pray, 'Lord, let it be my leg.' (laughter).

This story is a testament to the fact that humans can love the 'things' of the world so much at the expense of their well being because the world has a lure for everyone of us even Christians.

When we talk about the world, we are not referring to the people in the world because God so loved the world ( referring to humans in John3:16) to the extent of sending His only begotten son to die  neither are we referring to the physical planet earth. 'The world' in context of the opening text refers to the system that governs the affairs of this world.

It is the system of practices associated with the standards of the society without reference to any demands or requirements of God. It also refers to the influence the system of the world exerts on us.
The world is a world of seductive material, things& an ungodly system or pattern of living. When God warned us not to love the world through Apostle John, He wasn't saying having a Ferrari or iPhone 7 is bad rather he was referring to the influence the devil exerts on us through those things.

Often, I tell people that material things aren't really the problem but how those things influence us and take us away from God. The world uses the things in it to seduce us and take us away from God.
*The greatest threat to our walk with God is not persecution from the world, but seduction by the world( material things&ungodly pattern of living)*.

It is impossible to love the world and love God at the same time. Apostle James corroborated this in James 4:4 saying, *"You adulterers! Don’t you realize that friendship with the world makes you an enemy of God? I say it again: If you want to be a friend of the world, you make yourself an enemy of God*".

Satan knew the world has a strong alluring and seductive power. When he tempted Jesus the third time, he presented the kingdom of the world and its glories to Him on a platter of gold but Christ refused it.

'The   world' Jesus rejected is what some are praying and doing vigils to have. They go to prayer meetings to ask for 'the world'. I remember I was once caught in that trap after school, pre NYSC. I went to camp to pray about getting a car in less than 3 months into NYSC because I wanted to enjoy life my own way.

Then, I told God how i'll drive around doing evangelism but was that really my motive? I rolled on the floor praying instead of asking God to place me right at the centre of His will for my NYSC. In His mercies, He put me where He wanted me to be - full time ministry.

Surprisingly, what I desired came months after I left service when I didn't even pray or fast for it. When the heart of a believer is captured by the world, he/she will find scriptures and reasons to back it up.

*Your love for God is not certain and sure until the throne of 'the world' is removed from your heart. Do you love God or the world*?

*PRAYER*: Father, purge from my heart all the traces and deposits of worldliness in Jesus name.
