DEVOTIONAL 1185 | Source start line: 113304
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
8/28/24, 5:20 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: ProjectSave Devotional
Wednesday,
August 28th 2024.

*OVERCOMING WORLDLY SYSTEMS*  Pt.2

1John2:16 *For all that is in the world, the lust of the flesh, and the lust of the eyes, and the pride of life, is not of the Father, but is of the world*.

*THE DRIVERS- LUST OF THE FLESH* (a)

There are three major things that drive those that are trapped in the worldly system and they are: the lust of the flesh, lust of the eyes and pride of life. All sins are  categorized under these three drivers. We shall examine the lust of the flesh first. What is it? What does it really mean? We need to understand what lust is first.

Lust is simply inordinate desire. Often, we associate it with sex but it is more than that. Inordinate desires are not limited to sex though it is an integral part of it. One can lust after material things.

What is "flesh"? Flesh refers to human nature that is corrupted by sin. When we talk about " flesh" in christian circles, I normally see some persons touching their bodies referring to it as flesh. No, that's not what the bible is saying. Flesh is just a sinful nature, it refers to all of human tendencies to misbehave and go against God's will.

Therefore, the lust of the flesh refers to the inordinate desires of the human nature that are in contrast to the will of God.

The world is engineered to stir in us fleshly passions and lust. It is a very dangerous thing to still have ties with the world after salvation because the allures of the world ignites sinful flames in the flesh. Sometimes, have you ever wondered why you fall into a sin you thought you have conquered? The simple reason that is happening is because you undermine the power of the flesh and gave room for its expression.

Apostle Paul knew this to be true that is why he said:  *"But put on the Lord Jesus Christ, and make no provision for the flesh, to fulfill its lusts*" ( Rom13:14). Abstinence from anything that can trigger fleshly lust is the first key to victory.

In 1 Peter 2:11, apostle Peter said: *"Dearly beloved, I beseech you as strangers and pilgrims, abstain from fleshly lusts, which war against the soul*". Don't stay around anything or anyone that triggers inordinate desires in you. If you struggle to keep your sanity anytime you shake hands or have body contacts with the opposite sex, simply abstain.

Apostle Paul also told us to "mortify" the flesh. Romans 8:13 says,  *"For if ye live after the flesh, ye shall die: but if ye through the Spirit do mortify the deeds of the body, ye shall live*". The word mortify means “to kill or subdue.” In biblical context, to mortify is to subdue the flesh. We get our English words mortuary and mortician from the same Latin root that gives us mortify.

Therefore, mortification of sin / the flesh is the “killing” of sin and the flesh through discipline and self denial. This requires a level of consciousness by saying "no" to whatever the flesh wants. The flesh loves pleasure, sex( illicit fantasies and adventures), food( gluttony), sleep( in excess), social activities( that aren't edifying and glorifying God). You can mortify the flesh through deprivation.

Lastly, we can overcome the lust of the flesh by crucifying it:  *"I am crucified with Christ: nevertheless I live; yet not I, but Christ liveth in*"( Gal2:20). This connotes living like a disciple of Christ- a follower, an imitator.

As long as we are alive, these are the things we have to continually do to live in victory over the flesh. Victory yesterday may not sustain you today, we must deal with the flesh daily.

*PRAYER*: I receive grace to live in victory over the flesh.


====================================================================================================
DEVOTIONAL 1186 | Source start line: 113340
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
8/29/24, 7:17 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: PROJECTSAVE DEVOTIONAL
Thursday,
August 29th, 2024.

*OVERCOMING WORLDLY SYSTEMS*  Pt.3

1John2:16 *For all that is in the world, the lust of the flesh, and the lust of the eyes, and the pride of life, is not of the Father, but is of the world*.

*THE DRIVERS- LUST OF THE EYES* (b)

My senior pastor told the story of a man who looked at a lady that was seductively dressed while walking on the road. He looked on not knowing there was an electric pole ahead. He didn't stop looking until he collided with the pole. That's a simple example of how lust of the eyes operates.

We have said in previous teaching that lust is inordinate desire. Uncontrolled looking can stir lust in the heart.  Lust of the eyes is the inordinate desire to possess what we see or to have those things which have visual appeal.

We live in a world of visual appeal. The adverts you see on t.v., trending fashion and designs, are designed to stir lust in you so that you can run after those things and make them your prime pursuit. When ladies dress to show their cleavages and reveal their shape, they are doing it to appeal to your visuals. It is intentional not accidental.

The first sin lust of the eyes trigger in us is covetousness. The devil makes us to focus on the world and the things in it so that we can covet them. The moment covetousness sets in, desperation to possess follows.

The foolishness of covetousness is that it desires things that doesn't last, things that fade off and become irrelevant with time. Some of  those who lived in the 60s&70s used the Volkswagen beetles car. They were the big boys of that time. Some stole money and defrauded people to buy that car. Today, if anyone takes pride in owning that brand and model of Volkswagen, they'll see him/her as backward because there are modern cars with better functions.

Unfortunately, what you call "modern, new model and trendy" today shall fade off and become unappealing tomorrow yet that is what the devil is making many to labour, steal and kill for. People want to be trendy and they do anything to keep up with trends that are fading away.

In the decalogue, God addressed the lust of the eyes in its prohibition against coveting. Exodus 20:17 commands, “You shall not covet your neighbor’s house; you shall not covet your neighbor’s wife, or his male servant, or his female servant, or his ox, or his donkey, or anything that is your neighbor’s.”

Coveting can include a desire to have people, possessions, or status. The tactic of Satan is to use a visual image to trap us. He did it to Eve and she fell. Eve looked at the forbidden fruit and saw that it was “pleasing to the eye” (Genesis 3:6).

He tried the same tactic on Jesus, he “showed him all the kingdoms of the world and their splendor” (Matthew 4:8). He then promised to give them to Jesus—for a price. To possess what your eyes lust after, it often come with a price. Satan won't stop using this trick to get believers.

Those whose hearts are not set on things above will always be trapped, they'll find themselves in endless pursuit of whatever they see. The antidote is to shift focus: *"Set your minds on things above, not on earthly things*". ( Col3:2).

*ACTION POINT*: Fix your gaze on Jesus and you'll never be trapped: *"Looking unto Jesus the author and finisher of our faith; who for the joy that was set before him endured the cross, despising the shame, and is set down at the right hand of the throne of God*" ( Heb12:2).


====================================================================================================
DEVOTIONAL 1187 | Source start line: 113372
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
8/30/24, 9:18 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: PROJECTSAVE DEVOTIONAL
Friday,
August 30th, 2024.

*OVERCOMING WORLDLY SYSTEMS*  Pt.4
1John2:16 *For all that is in the world, the lust of the flesh, and the lust of the eyes, and the pride of life, is not of the Father, but is of the world*.

*THE DRIVERS- PRIDE OF LIFE* (c)

I read the story of Muhammad Ali about what happened between him and an air hostess. Once he was on an airplane and he often said, “I'm the greatest.” The air hostess looked at him and saw he didn't have his seatbelt fastened.

She said to him, “Mr. Ali, you'll have to fasten your seatbelt.” Ali responded, “Superman don't need seatbelt.” The hostess then replied, “Superman don't need airplane either.”

A short study on the phrase "pride of life" in the Greek shows that apostle John was referring to the pride that results from having acquired possession, property and livelihood. This is the outcome of "lust of the eyes".

A man that has lived to satisfy the lust of his eyes by pursuing and acquiring earthly things will end up being proud of his "achievements".

The Greek "Bios" is the word translated life and it implies the biological life. Whereas, this is what Jesus said about this "life": *"And he said unto them, Take heed and beware of all covetousness; for a man's life consists not in the abundance of the things which he possesses*" (Luke 12:15 KJV).

The man that is possessed by the pride of life will only be living for this "life" and not eternal life. Unfortunately, there are many believers that are trapped by this "life" and nothing more. Some ministers aren't excluded.

The acquisition of earthly possessions and properties mean so much to them. It is a thing of "joy" to them as this reflects often in their testimony.  In ministry, the moment we begin to speak comparatively in an emphatic manner about being the "biggest", "largest" repeatedly without restraint, it shows we're trapped in the rat race of this "life" to outdo others.  Having achieve the goal, pride enters and we blow our trumpet. Isn't it happening?

The triumvirate evil apostle John told us in the opening text is a tactic that has been working from time immemorial and will continue to work because the devil has no new trick. He only modifies it.

The temptations of Jesus Christ is an example of each. When the devil told Jesus to jump from the top of the building so that angels can catch him IF truly He was the son of God, the devil was tempting to see if pride of life was in Jesus. How? He wanted him to prove a point about his sonship.

*Anytime you're tempted to prove a point to people about your status and what you have achieved, you're already close to being trapped by the pride of life*.

We all need to submit the whole of our life to God in its entirety so that these triune evil( lust of the flesh,  lust of the eyes and pride of life) won't find expression in us.

*PRAYER*: Lord, search my heart and rid me of these evil in Jesus name. I refuse to submit to the dictates and principles of these triune forces in Jesus name. Amen


====================================================================================================
DEVOTIONAL 1188 | Source start line: 113441
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
9/2/24, 7:11 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: PROJECTSAVE DEVOTIONAL
Monday,
September 2nd, 2024.

*NO SPIRITUAL FREEBIES*

Proverbs 23:23 *Buy the truth, and sell it not; also wisdom, and instruction, and understanding*.

Back in the days when my Christian journey started, we were motivated to empty our pocket into our heads. That golden counsel became part of me and has helped my spiritual life immensely. Those days, I practically starved myself to buy tapes and books because I desired growth.

I attended my first ministers conference at the of 17 using the little money I had on me to transport myself to and fro. I didn't know what my ministry was at then but I knew God's hands was on me. On one of the nights, on my way home, the Lord delivered me from robbers who attempted to rob. The major asset they found on me were books, jotter and bible.

Till date, that culture of buying books, paying to attend seminars and investing financially in spiritual growth is still part of me. I have more books than clothes. The impact of my investment is paying off greatly. It is one of the reasons you read me daily. I decided to invest value in myself so I can be valuable. I made that decision as a teenager.   I am still on that journey and I won't stop.

Fast- forward to this present time, I am privileged to have met believers especially young people who desire to grow but aren't willing to invest their money and time into profitable things that makes for growth. If they see a seminar or training that will help their growth, they'll be asking If it is free or looking for how to get discount.

If the seminar is free, they'll ask If there'll be free food and transportation. That attitude is not the attitude of one willing to grow. If you get anything freely, just know that someone paid for it. Nothing is free even in Freetown.

*Unfortunately, some of those seeking free seminars and training saved money to buy iphones. They didn't ask for discount at the iphone store, they paid and started displaying their phones*.

*Some sisters use wigs that can buy books that will help their destiny and shape them for their future. Painfully, nothing is upstairs. They wear expensive wigs on empty heads. Sad to say*.

*I don't know any person of value who has more clothes, shoes, wigs, than books. I am yet to meet one*.

*If you mean to grow spiritually and become someone of worth in this kingdom, you must conscioulsy invest in yourself. It will come at a cost.There is a price to pay for every rising*.

Stop looking for freebies all around. Change that attitude and you'll see yourself rise steadily. The truth you need comes at a cost, buy it and do not sell it. Place a great value on growth and act on it.

Read slowly and thoughtfully: *"BUY THE TRUTH, and sell it not; also wisdom, and instruction, and understanding*" (Prov 23:23).

*ACTION POINT*: Make it a habit to invest your pocket into your head.

DEVOTIONAL 1189 | Source start line: 113473
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
9/3/24, 5:18 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: ProjectSave Devotional
Tuesday
September 3rd, 2024.

*ESSENTIAL HABITS YOU MUST CULTIVATE* Pt.1

Philippians 4:9 *What you have learned and received and heard and seen in me—practice these things, and the God of peace will be with you*.  ESV

*POWER OF HABITS*

According to online Oxford dictionary,  a habit is a settled or regular tendency or practice, especially one that is hard to give up. Going by this definition, we can call something a habit when it is hard to give up. Every man on the surface of the earth have cultivated one or two habits that defines them.

Habits can either be good or bad. Our focus is not on bad habits but cultivating good habits that will strengthen our walk of faith. No one amount to anything great in life without cultivating habits that makes for greatness.

If you desire to be spiritual like Billy Graham or any other father in the faith, check their habits and imitate them. Their secret is not in what you see them doing in the public but what they do consistently in the secret over the years. It takes time for a practice or action to become habitual.

Dostoyevsky says, *"The second half of a man's life is made up of the habits he acquired in the first half*".

Jesus Christ had certain habits he cultivated while on earth. Luke 4:16 says, *"When he came to the village of Nazareth, his boyhood home, he went AS USUAL to the synagogue on the Sabbath and stood up to read the Scriptures*"( NLT). It was customary for him to attend synagogue meetings.

In another place, the habit of Jesus was revealed. John 18: 1-2 says, *"After saying these things, Jesus crossed the Kidron Valley with his disciples and entered a grove of olive trees. 2 Judas, the betrayer, knew this place, because JESUS HAD OFTEN GONE THERE WITH HIS DISCIPLES*"( NLT). We can't tell exactly what they did there but it is plausible that was their meeting point for fellowship

Speaking about Jesus, Mark 1:35 says, *"Very early in the morning, while it was still dark, Jesus got up, left the house and went off to a solitary place, where he prayed*". This a known habit of Christ.

*Your growth begins the day you start cultivating healthy habits that makes for growth. Start now*.

*ACTION POINT*: Run a check on your life to be sure you have healthy habits that will make you grow.


====================================================================================================
DEVOTIONAL 1190 | Source start line: 113538
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
9/4/24, 7:09 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: ProjectSave Devotional
Wednesday
September 4th, 2024.

*ESSENTIAL HABITS YOU MUST CULTIVATE* Pt.2

Philippians 4:9 *What you have learned and received and heard and seen in me—practice these things, and the God of peace will be with you*.  ESV

*QUIET TIME*( A)

While growing up under my mom, she emphasized to us the importance of observing quiet time. She bought bibles for us all and a jotter. She told us to jot lessons from our daily bible reading during our quiet time.

As a trained teacher, she took it upon herself to mark our jotters like school pupils submit their notes for marking. Then, we didn't know the importance of quiet time but as we grew in the Lord, the importance became glaring to us all. It is the reason I have been writing daily devotions for our global audience for over a decade.

What is quiet time? It is that time you spend alone with God. It is not the general family prayer time or a group prayer meeting online or onsite. Quiet time is you and God alone without distraction. It is the time you meet with God to draw strength daily.

*The lost art of quiet time is the reason we have weak Christians today*.

The emphasis on prayer meetings either online or onsite where people will be saying "Amen" ( though not bad) without having a personal time alone with God has produced more weaklings than men of strength. Our fathers didn't grow unto maturity that way.


I am yet to meet a strong believer who doesn't have a quiet time. *Your quiet time is the time you meet with God in the day before devil meets you*. It is that time we get answers to the questions that might come our way in the day. That is why it is preferable in the morning.

Speaking about Jesus, Mark 1:35 says, *"Very early in the morning, while it was still dark, Jesus got up, left the house and went off to a solitary place, where he prayed*". Jesus had a habit of waking up early to pray. Little wonder why he was full of power and wisdom.

The Psalmist says, *"O God, thou art My God; early will I seek thee: My soul thirsteth for thee, my flesh longeth for thee In a dry and thirsty land, where no water is*" ( Psalm 63:1 KJV).

You must cultivate the habit of spending time alone with God on daily basis for an infusion of strength and wisdom. The basic things we do in quiet time is worship, prayer and bible study whilst not forgetting listening to divine instructions. This is the habit of strong Christians.

Start doing this If you desire to be strong in the Lord.

*ACTION POINT*: Cultivate the habit of waking up early to pray and study the word. That way, you will be laying a solid foundation for your walk with the Lord.

DEVOTIONAL 1191 | Source start line: 114788
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
10/2/24, 6:07 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: ProjectSave Devotional
Wednesday,
October 2nd 2024.

*CROSSING ANCIENT LINES* Pt.2

1 Cor 1:27 *Instead, God chose things the world considers foolish in order to shame those who think they are wise. And he chose things that are powerless to shame those who are powerful* ( NLT).

*A CULTURE OF SELF SUFFICIENCY WITHOUT GOD*

I audited my sermon, teachings and perspectives from which I taught certain things, I discovered that I once tilted towards the ideas of  secular humanism partly. I had one leg in Christian orthodoxy and another in secular humanism. When I discovered this, I repented.

What is secular humanism all about anyway? Secular humanism believe in man's ability to self-govern and live through life without needing the supernatural. Secular humanism doesn't believe in the supernatural or its intervention in human affairs. It posits that man is autonomous and doesn't need God.

On the surface, this may sound logically true but when you study what this philosophical stance is all about, it is not biblically true. You have heard secular humanistic ideas from some pulpits. Some of those ideas sound like: "You don't need God to be rich",  "Use your brain to make good choices and stop disturbing God". Most motivational speakers are guilty of this.

Statements such as these makes people feel self-reliant without God. It removes God from the equation of our lives whereas God want us to depend on Him totally. Paul says, *"Not that we are sufficient of ourselves to think any thing as of ourselves; but our sufficiency is of God*"( 2 Cor 3:5).

In John 15:5, Jesus says, *"I am the vine, ye are the branches: He that abideth in me, and I in him, the same bringeth forth much fruit: FOR WITHOUT ME YE CAN DO NOTHING*". This is an absolute statement from our Lord Jesus Christ. As a child of God, your enabler is God not your senses.

Whatever you have achieved and all your strides, you have them because God enabled you. Paul says, *"For who makes you different from anyone else? WHAT DO YOU HAVE THAT YOU DID NOT RECEIVE? And if you did receive it, why do you boast as though you did not*"?(1 Cor 4:7). Permit me to say that even unbelievers who achieve certain things legally were enabled by God because He is rich towards all.

Old timers rely on God to the point where they pray asking for his intervention on all things including praying on meals, praying before driving a car, etc.

*The moment we remove dependence on God from a man's life in any way, we make man a god to himself*.

God is very jealous when we don't give him his glory. This is one ancient line we must never cross neither should we sacrifice it for philosophical intellectualism.

*PRAYER*: Father, I repent for not giving you your glory. I repent from thinking my wisdom and intellect has helped me thus far. From today, I shall return all the glory to you In Jesus name.

*IT IS ABOUT VALUE*


2 Samuel 24:24 *But the king replied to Araunah, “No, I insist on buying it, for I will not present burnt offerings to the LORD my God that have cost me nothing.” So David paid him fifty pieces of silver for the threshing floor and the oxen* ( NLT).


A church treasurer once told me about a wealthy man in his church whose tithe does not exceed two hundred naira every sunday. This is someone who got huge contracts but he just decided to be tight fisted towards God and his people.


People may find excuses to support his meagre giving towards the church but it was discovered that he spends heavily on socializing. About two top worldly musicians sang and praised him in their album. This is not cheap. From what we know, he will pay them heavily to get his name mentioned.


It is about value. That man did not value the things of God and his kingdom, hence, he reduced the flow of his giving towards church. He valued social life, hence, he lavished his money there. Nobody on earth is stingy in every sense of the word. Those who are frugal towards you spend lavishly elsewhere.


*People spend/give in the direction of what they value*.

Some don't mind spending any amount to book a VIP table in a comedy show but same person will ignore the call to support missions, missionaries, church planting in rural areas. He is liberal towards comedy shows but stingy towards kingdom expansion.

Leonard Ravenhill said, *"Today Christians Spend More Money On Dog Food Than On Missions*".


God want you and me to value His kingdom. The moment our heart aligns with kingdom priorities, our time, money, intellect, skill, training and giftings will flow in the same direction.

Matthew 6:33 says, *"Seek the Kingdom of God above all else, and live righteously, and he will give you everything you need*"( NLT).


*PRAYER*: Father, give me a heart that values your kingdom and help my heart to align accordingly in Jesus name.

DEVOTIONAL 1193 | Source start line: 115522

10/19/24, 5:52 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: PROJECTSAVE DEVOTIONAL
Saturday,
October 19th, 2024.

*WHAT I LEARNT IN A ROMAN CATHOLIC ORDINATION SERVICE*.

1 Thess5:12-13 *Dear brothers and sisters, honor those who are your leaders in the Lord’s work. They work hard among you and give you spiritual guidance. *Show them great respect and wholehearted love* because of their work. And live peacefully with each other* (NLT).


Years back, I was privileged to attend the priestly ordination of some Roman Catholic reverend fathers who graduated from their seminary. As a pentecostal preacher, I decided to learn lessons worth learning and shun whatever might not align with my theology.

I saw handsome young men who dedicated their lives to serve God by taking oath of celibacy, poverty and chastity. They spent ten solid years in the seminary. Some of them are university graduates who enrolled in the seminary after their first degree for another ten years training.

I heard the visiting Bishop cautioning families and friends not to disturb them for money because their new status is not a money making status but for service. He cautioned that they should not disturb them for family contributions. I was amazed to see  the joy on the faces of their parents& families. In my heart, I asked why they were happy knowing their sons won't marry and give them grandchildren?

Not only that, these young handsome men won't be rich like their counterparts in the business world and may not have the financial muscle to care for them like their colleagues in banking or oil& gas sector, why the joy?

I compared that scenario with what obtains in the evangelical/pentecostal circle. Sometimes, when a young man perceive God's call into the ministry especially full time, some parents, siblings and families may not give full support. The first line of rejection comes from immediate families.

This is unlike what I saw and heard about most Roman Catholic families where they accept such as a privilege. In fact, some Roman Catholic families pray to have a reverend father or sister in their families. That's how much they value those called into the ministry.

In our circle, everyone starts thinking about how poor those called into ministry will be. Sometimes, families despise them because they don't have money and are seen as lazy and visionless. I read a preacher who spoke about how his father despised him because he abandoned the practice of medicine for ministry.

Some parents may think of how much they have spent on their son and how they dreamt that he'll repay their efforts in a big way. Now that he says he's called into full time ministry, their hopes are dashed. I have met Christian parents who vowed not to allow their children accept God's call into the ministry.

Dear Christian parent, why do you celebrate and publicize your joy so much when your child graduate from law, medical or engineering school but keeps mum or becomes bitter If one of your child chooses to obey God's call into the ministry? It shows where your heart is.

The Holy Spirit taught me that what I saw in that Catholic gathering should be our attitude when one of us is called to serve in the ministry. Our focus should be on how God has given him the privilege to serve and impact lives for eternity. Our perspective should be eternal.

That child whom you are spending millions to train in medical school may abandon that degree for God's call. That should not surprise you. When the master calls, the called must obey. Don't be their opposition, support them to fulfill God's eternal purpose.

*ACTION POINT*: From today, I want us to have a change of attitude about those called into the ministry. If you have them as siblings, value them and revere them. I call you blessed.

DEVOTIONAL 1194 | Source start line: 115631

10/20/24, 6:55 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: ProjectSave Devotional
Sunday,
October 20th, 2024.


*KEEP PRAYING*

Psalm 65:2 O thou that hearest prayer, unto thee shall all flesh come ( KJV)


George Muller was one of the greatest intercessors in contemporary times. He was resilient in the place of prayer and he never gave up. History have it on record that he prayed for the salvation of some persons for decades.


For a certain man, George prayed for his salvation daily for over fifty years. He faithfully prayed daily until this man gave his life to Christ. Five decades plus is a long time yet this man committed himself to continuous intercession.


Another Christian woman prayed for the salvation of her wayward son for years. This woman died without witnessing her desired testimony. After he demise, her son had an accident. While unconscious, he saw a vision of hell. As he was about to be thrown into hell, an angel stopped the demon.


The angel opened his eyes to see how his late mum prayed for his salvation. On the strength of her prayers, he was shown mercy. This man came back to life and gave his life to Christ. God answered his late mom's prayer. Her investment in prayers paid off eventually.


I want to encourage you not to stop praying. God hears all prayers and he sends answers at the right time. Sometimes, our prayers today are seeds sown awaiting harvest tomorrow. Don't think you have wasted your time.


The seeds of prayer sown in years is what you are harvesting today. Sometimes, we experience certain miraculous happenings and we wonder why they happened without asking. Well, God mercies gives us what we don't deserve or ask for but at other times, such miraculous happenings are products of prayers of yester years.


If you have been praying for a particular thing or person, don't stop. The day of harvest is coming. Keep at it. If George Muller had quitted, he may not have those answers. Please, don't quit.


*PRAYER*: Father, help me not to relent in the place of prayer in Jesus name.

DEVOTIONAL 1195 | Source start line: 115774
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
10/22/24, 5:57 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: ProjectSave Devotional
Tuesday
October 22nd, 2024.

*SACRIFICING FOR SOULS*

2Tim2:10 *So I am willing to endure anything if it will bring salvation and eternal glory in Christ Jesus to those God has chosen* ( NLT)

Albert Schweitzer was a German missionary to Africa. He was a theologian, musicologist and physician. He was trained in theology and music at first.

One day, he saw an advert by a Missionary Society about the need for missionaries in Africa with expertise in medicine. He decided to dump all he was doing to enrol in medical school because he wanted to reach out to some primitive people in some parts of Africa.

This decision was not accepted by families and friends but he went ahead to study medicine because he wanted to be a medical missionary. To him, medicine was beyond a money- making profession but a tool for kingdom expansion.

In 1912, he arrived at Lambaréné on the Ogooué river, in what is now Gabon, in Africa (then a French colony). He moved to Africa at a time when Africa was dubbed 'white man's grave'.

It wasn't an easy decision in the flesh to leave the comfort of Europe for Africa at a time when there were no modern day comfort. He did this just for the sake of souls to be won to the Lord.

Need I tell you about David Livingstone whose heart was buried in Africa because his heart was totally devoted to his missionary calling to the part of Africa where he served. He died because of malaria and dysentery.

He could have chosen the ease of Europe but he decided to go all out for the sake of souls.  What can you endure for the sake of the gospel? What can you endure for the sake of one soul?

Apostle Paul said he can endure anything as long as it will bring salvation and eternal glory in Christ. I ask myself if I can boldly say the same in God's presence.

In our little way, we need to start growing spiritual muscles to be able to endure anything for the sake of lost souls if it will bring salvation and eternal glory. This is because, many souls are on their way to hell on a very fast lane. If we do not rescue them, they may be lost eternally.

*Our mission is not to pity souls but to rescue them*.

A rescue operation is not easy most times but as the Lord gives grace, be involved.

*PRAYER*: Father, I receive grace on this matter in Jesus name.

DEVOTIONAL 1196 | Source start line: 115846
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
10/23/24, 5:49 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: ProjectSave Devotional
Wednesday,
October 23rd 2024.

*CHANGING BAD NARRATIVES THROUGH PRAYERS*

James 5:16 *"...The earnest prayer of a righteous person has great power and produces wonderful results*".

In my final year, I wrote a very tough exam. Two lecturers taught the course, one was nice and considerate but the other don was frugal with marks. Moreso, it was known to students and staff that he was corrupt. I can't bribe for marks. In his test, he marked me down despite writing well.

The chances of scoring an "A" was so slim. When the exam question was served me, the instruction was that we answer four questions out of five. I answered all questions because I wanted them to record questions where I scored the highest. After the exam, I was unsettled in my spirit so I rushed to the hostel.

When I got to the hostel, I didn't bother eating, I launched myself into deep prayers immediately. I prayed in tongues setting my heart on the examination I wrote because I've made straight A's from my 300L and I don't want anything to change it. The presence of God filled my heart in that prayer.

Later, I left for an evening meeting in the fellowship. I was asked to say the closing prayer. With the fire of prayer burning in my heart, I led everyone to pray fervently. They didn't know why an ordinary closing prayer became "fireful" but within me, I knew I needed a change in the examination I wrote.

To the glory of God, I scored an "A" in that course. It was a slim "A" but it was a distinction anyway. An insider among staff told me that this corrupt don marked me down but the aspect of the other professor pushed me into distinction. I can't explain how it happened because the corrupt lecturer was the major handler of that course so he had upper hand in alloting marks.

*Prayer can change bad narratives*.

10/24/24, 6:38 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: PROJECTSAVE DEVOTIONAL

*AFTER PRAYING IN TONGUES, WHAT NEXT?* Pt.1

Jude 1:20 *But you, dear friends, must build each other up in your most holy faith, pray in the power of the Holy Spirit*( NLT)

I lead a prayer group where we pray in tongues every night for an hour. After each prayer session, we spend some minutes making bold declarations. I learnt a prayer secret about what is next after praying in the Spirit and that very secret is what we practice after praying in tongues.

You see, when you pray in tongues extensively, you generate power. You activate the anointing of God upon your life. However, what is the essence of generating power and not using it? Power is meant to be channeled in the right direction for our profiting.

I see a lot of persons who pray in tongues for hours but do not know how to maximize that moment. They generate spiritual energy but leave it unused.

1 Corinthians 14:4 (NLT) says, *"A person who speaks in tongues is STRENGTHENED PERSONALLY...*".

I want you to note the words in caps. Strength is meant for a purpose. Why generate so much personal strength and leave it unused?

After praying in tongues extensively, If you don't have an interpretation of the tongues spoken, you can go ahead and make declarations to change the situation you might be facing. Use your spiritual strength to change the bad narratives in your life.

After praying in tongues extensively, don't leave the prayer room without saying something about that problem. Don't beg that sickness or negative situation. Speak boldly against it and command it to leave your life. Put the power you have generated to use.

At that moment, your life will go in the direction of your declarations because a supernatural power is available to back it.

*ACTION POINT*: Every day of your life, spend quality time praying in the tongues and spend another quality making declarations in the direction of what you want to see in your life.

DEVOTIONAL 1198 | Source start line: 116040

10/25/24, 12:02 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: PROJECTSAVE DEVOTIONAL
Friday,
October 25th, 2024.

*AFTER PRAYING IN TONGUES, WHAT NEXT?* Pt.2

1 Corinthians 14:13 *So anyone who speaks in tongues should pray also for the ability to interpret what has been said. [14] For if I pray in tongues, my spirit is praying, but I don’t understand what I am saying*. NLT

In the opening text, apostle Paul makes it clear that anyone who speaks in tongues should pray for the ability to interpret what has been said. Meaning that everyone who speaks in tongues can interpret the mystery spoken in another language If they ask the Lord for interpretation ability. Also, you must understand that tongue speaking in the context of this chapter is a devotional gift not for public use.

In verse 14, Paul made it clear that when praying in tongues, it is the human spirit that is praying but the mind doesn't understand anything said. If we spin that round, it would mean that when we pray in the spirit and the candle of our mind is lightened supernaturally with an unusual understanding, then we have the interpretation of our tongues.

I want to boldly tell you that you can interpret your tongues. Yes, you can by the help of the Spirit. However, you need to understand how it works. Have you prayed in tongues to a point where you have unusual understanding about a thing or situation or you knew something about someone? I mean something you did not think about before entering into prayers?

If your answer is yes, then, you have received interpretations to the tongues you have spoken but you did not know what it was probably because you are not trained along that line. You see, spiritual things can be taught. That is, the operations of the Spirit.

1 Corinthians 12:1 says, *"Now concerning spiritual gifts, brethren, I would not have you ignorant*".

Paul told the Corinthian believers not to be ignorant about the gifts of the Spirit, it shows one can learn about it. *The expression of the gifts of the Spirit cannot be taught but the dynamics of its operations can be taught*.

Learn this today: after praying in tongues extensively with full concentration, don't ignore the deep impressions you might have on your heart. It could be about you, someone or something. Pray more in tongues. If It comes stronger again, that is the likely interpretation of the tongues spoken. Sometimes, it could comes like a flash vision in your heart.

I have received interpretations to tongues by revelations and insight. This is how it occurs and same can be your experience. God is not willing to keep you in the dark about issues of your life. The ability to pray in tongues and interpret is for your profiting. Take advantage of it today.

*ACTION POINT*: After praying in tongues, take note of the strong impressions you have in your heart. That is the likely interpretation of tongues spoken.  If there is none, go ahead and make declarations about your situation believing God for a change.

10/26/24, 6:10 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: PROJECTSAVE DEVOTIONAL

October 26th, 2024.

*OUR ADVOCATE IN THE COURT OF HEAVEN*

1John2:1 *My dear children,  I am writing this to you so that you will not sin.  But if anyone does sin,  we have an advocate who pleads our case before the Father.  He is Jesus Christ,  the one who is truly righteous*.  NLT

Some have read the scripture  above to mean that John gave license for believers to sin.  Another school of thought see it differently.  It is good we see it in context of what John is saying.

He started by saying that he wrote to them so they would not sin. It is obvious that his intention of writing to them was not to encourage sin.
Probably, he perceived some believers may come up with that.

He went further to state that IF ( a conditional word) any believer does sin, we have an advocate  or attorney who pleads our case with the father.  Hallelujah!

A believer does not sin intentionally with bold face and act as if nothing has happened.  If you see someone who calls himself a believer and he enjoys sinning,  such a fellow is not born again.  The nature of Christ in us is not permissive to sin and would not lead us into sin.

Paul admonished that we put on the new man that is created to be like God in true righteousness and holiness (Eph4:24). Actually,  the new man in that text refers to the new nature received at new birth so apostle Paul is  saying that we should let the new nature of Christ in us manifest.

Though we have the nature of Christ in us,  we fail God sometimes (1 John1:7-9). That is why Christ has decided to stand as our advocate with the father.

When the devil bring accusations  against  us,  Christ is our defence. Glory!  You are not without an advocate in the court of heaven.

Do not give in to that feeling of guilt.  Learn to refer Satan to your advocate.

*PRAYER*: Father,  I thank you for the advocacy of Christ.

DEVOTIONAL 1200 | Source start line: 116216

10/27/24, 7:37 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: ProjectSave Devotional

October 27th, 2024.

*WHAT COMES TO YOUR MIND WHEN YOU HEAR THE WORD 'SUCCESS'?*

Heb10:9 *Then he said, "Here I am, I have come to do your will." He sets aside the first to establish the second*.

I was addicted to success motivation books back in the days. I just love that feeling of 'I can make it'. At a time, American motivational speakers and writers made huge sales and penetrated the hearts of young minds desiring to be successful.Their high sounding rhyming words were fascinating too.

They told stories of men that became successful against all odds. Hearing those stories ignited in me a drive to succeed. Most motivational authors taught us to set goals, write them down, imagine the gains of success and pursue it vigorously. I did all these but I felt empty in all my pursuit.

My view about success was one-sided because I saw success from their own standpoint not God's. It was about ephemerals not the eternal. The endpoint is woven around material increase and fame. What comes to your mind when you hear thr word 'Success'?

So many desire success but only few people are pursuing success from the right perspective. Those guys involved in armed robbery, kidnapping, internet fraud and ritual killing want to be successful. Behind those heinous acts is the drive to succeed. Politicians want to succeed by winning election at all cost not minding the process.

However,  God sees success differently. If Jesus is  on the earth today,  by our standard, we may call him a failure. Why?  He may not have cars,  houses and properties. Don't forget that during his days on earth,  he said he doesn't have a place to lay his head (Luke9:58).

He didn't have a royal donkey,  he borrowed the one he rode into Jerusalem. What about Paul?  I doubt if that man had designers in his wardrobe. He was always on the move yet they were successful according to God's definotion of success.

*What is success from God's standpoint?  Success is the progressive actualization of God's plan for your life. That's how God define success*.

What matters to God is for you to be right at the centre of His will for your life. You can venture into a thing and be successful but it may not be in God's perfect plan for your life.

Jesus came to the earth on a divine mission,  he pursued it, finished and left.  Paul knew his assignment here on earth, he pursued it and left likewise the Apostles and other kingdom heroes.

God wouldn't lower his standard for success and define it our own way. Find God's perfect will for your life and pursue it. That's where true success lies.

*PRAYER*: Father, I receive the grace to discover your perfect will and fulfil it In Jesus name. Amen

DEVOTIONAL 1201 | Source start line: 116340
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
10/30/24, 5:05 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: PROJECTSAVE DEVOTIONAL
Friday,
October 30th, 2024.

*PUT TO DEATH YOUR EARTHLY NATURE*.

Colossians 3: 5--6 *Put to death, therefore, whatever belongs to your earthly nature: sexual immorality, impurity, lust, evil desires and greed, which is idolatry. 6 Because of these, the wrath of God is coming*

A believer is a new creation( 2 Corinthians 5:17). This is an incontrovertible truth. In Christ, the believer is not a refurbished being neither is he a rehabilitated being. He is a regenerated and recreated being. He had a form before meeting Christ but in Christ, he's made new. This is one of the wonders of God's marvelous grace.

Apostle Paul says, *"We know that our old self was crucified with him in order that the body of sin might be brought to nothing, so that we would no longer be enslaved to sin*"(Rom6:6 ESV).

Now, we have a new self that is created after the likeness of God: *"And to put on the new self, created after the likeness of God in true righteousness and holiness*" (Eph4:24). A believer is a new man.

While we know from Pauline revelation that the believer is new and created after the likeness of God in true righteousness and holiness, Paul said, *"PUT TO DEATH, therefore, whatever belongs to your EARTHLY NATURE: sexual immorality, impurity, lust, evil desires and greed, which is idolatry. 6 Because of these, the wrath of God is coming*" (Col3:5-6).

Paul was talking to believers, new creations who had the nature of God, he is saying they should put to death their earthly nature. If the earthly nature is absent or gone totally at salvation, he wouldn't have instructed they put it to death yet these were new creations being addressed here. We need to know what this earthly nature is and be sure there's no mix up somewhere.

Man is a tripartite being made up of spirit, soul and body (1Thes5:23). When you came to Christ, it is your spirit man that became new and regenerated not your soul( mind, will and emotions)or body. The information (good or bad) in your mind weren't formated.

Obviously, the earthly nature that must be put to death are the carnal tendencies that exist in your mind not your spirit. Apostle Paul knowing this enjoined Roman believers saying, *"Do not conform to the pattern of this world, but be transformed by the RENEWING OF YOUR MIND. Then you will be able to test and approve what God’s will is—his good, pleasing and perfect will*" (Romans 12:2 NIV).

You don't say "I'm a new creation so I'm not concerned about what goes into my mind". While your spirit is new, your mind requires daily renewal through the word. That way, you begin the process of putting to death the earthly nature. Don't neglect that earthly nature in you, put it to death.

*PRAYER*: Father, I receive the grace to put my earthly nature to death in Jesus name.


====================================================================================================
DEVOTIONAL 1202 | Source start line: 116388
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
10/31/24, 5:07 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: PROJECTSAVE DEVOTIONAL
Thursday,
October 31st, 2024.

*JESUS PAID IT ALL*

2 Corinthians 5:21 *For God made Christ, who never sinned, to be the offering for our sin, so that we could be made right with God through Christ* (NLT)

A man boarded a ship for a journey that spanned for weeks on the high sea. He got on board loaded with bread and all he felt he needed for the journey.

After some weeks, his bread became stale. He became hungry but he didn't know what to do to get quality meal. He saw others in the ship eating, drinking and partying.

One day, he summoned courage to beg for food. The person whom he begged from was surprised to see a fellow passenger begging. He couldn't imagine that someone who paid the same fare could be begging.

He asked this man If he paid the price for his fare. He answered affirmatively. The man showed him where it was written that the price he paid covered for all his feeding for the period of his journey.

Religion will never show or tell you that the price of sins has been paid. Rather, religion will make you live on rituals and rites that cannot cleanse your sins. Religion doesn't believe man can be saved.

I'm glad to tell the world the price of sins has been paid fully by the blood: *"For you know that God paid a ransom to save you from the empty life you inherited from your ancestors. And it was not paid with mere gold or silver, which lose their value. It was the precious blood of Christ, the sinless, spotless Lamb of God*"(1 Peter 1:18‭-‬19 NLT).

There's no other price a man can pay for his sins that will earn him forgiveness or salvation. Salvation cannot be earned, it is gift from God.

*God saved you by his grace when you believed. And you can’t take credit for this; it is a gift from God. Salvation is not a reward for the good things we have done, so none of us can boast about it*( Ephesians 2:8‭-‬9 NLT).

*He is so rich in kindness and grace that he purchased our freedom with the blood of his Son and forgave our sins*(Ephesians 1:7 NLT).

The death of Christ alone is enough. The bible says, *"But our High Priest offered himself to God as a single sacrifice for sins, good for all time. Then he sat down in the place of honor at God’s right hand*"( Hebrews 10:12 NLT).

Hallelujah!!! Now that Christ has offered himself for the sins of mankind, no need for any form of sacrifice: *"And when sins have been forgiven, there is no need to offer any more sacrifices*"(Hebrews 10:18 NLT).

The world must hear about this saving grace of our Lord Jesus Christ. The more they try to use man-made methods, the more they'll feel condemned.

*And since we have been made right in God’s sight by the blood of Christ, he will certainly save us from God’s condemnation*( Romans 5:9 NLT).

The saving grace of God is available for the sinner: *"We believe that we are all saved the same way, by the undeserved grace of the Lord Jesus*" (Acts of the Apostles 15:11 NLT).

As a believer, let the world know that Christ has made provision for their forgiveness and salvation.

I shall preach these truths all around the world because It is the word of God and I believe it. I'm fully convinced about this Gospel.

*ACTION POINT*: Study this short devotional sermon and check all scriptures. Meditate on them and start writing your action plan on how you'll tell the world about this wonderful provision.

DEVOTIONAL 1203 | Source start line: 116503
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
11/2/24, 5:27 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: PROJECTSAVE DEVOTIONAL
Saturday,
November 2nd, 2024.

*DISCERN BETWEEN YOUR SEED AND HARVEST*

2 Corinthians 9:10 *For God is the one who provides seed for the farmer and then bread to eat. In the same way, he will provide and increase your resources and then produce a great harvest of generosity in you* ( NLT).

I read something about a woman on facebook that caught my attention. This woman gave three cars to a pastor and began to walk on foot for a long time. Not only that, she experienced a reversal of fortune for worse yet she remained faithful. The story didn't tell us about her husband whether he gave his consent or not.

I can't tell whether the story is real or adapted from the real life experience of someone. Regardless, I know such exist somewhere. I have heard of people who gave their school fees as seed on campus fellowship and then became stranded. Some looked back in regret.

The problem I see here is that these people do not understand the difference between a seed and harvest. According to the opening scripture, God gives a seed for sowing while He gives harvest for eating. God in his wisdom separate for us what to sow and what to eat.

*You run into trouble when you sow your harvest and eat your seed*.

Much emphasis has been on seed sowing but only few on what to eat, that is the harvest. Oftentimes, the motivation behind giving without discerning a seed to be given and a harvest to be eaten comes from imbalanced messages and coercion which is totally unscriptural.

I have heard of preachers who compel people to borrow to sow seed. That's extreme.  If they borrow to sow seed, how will they pay back? I'll rather teach you to invest borrowed money in profitable business for it to yield a harvest that will afford you to repay your loan and still have some extra to give and do other things. We live in days where we must guide God's people on how to make& multiply money as we ask them to give.

I have rejected money or gifts before. I am wise enough to know what to accept and what to reject. Preachers, when people bring their harvest, return it, accept seeds only. Accepting some seeds will put you in trouble. Be discerning.

Students, don't sow your school fees as seed. Don't borrow money to sow seed. You'll be putting yourself in double trouble when your creditors come asking for their money and you can't repay. There is widom for all things. Your seed should come from your harvest not borrowed or stolen.

God, in his wisdom has taught us to separate seed from harvest. You need wisdom to do same. In as much as we encourage giving, we must not break divine protocols in doing this.

*ACTION POINT*: Beware of being manipulated or coerced to give to the point of giving all your harvest. Ask God to give you wisdom on how to discern between your seed and harvest.

DEVOTIONAL 1204 | Source start line: 116582
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
11/3/24, 5:29 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: ProjectSave Devotional
Sunday,
November 3rd, 2024.

*SHUN MODERN "CHRISTIANIZED" GNOSTICISM*

Jude 1:3-4, *"...But now I find that I must write about something else, urging you to defend the faith that God has entrusted once for all time to his holy people. I say this because some ungodly people have wormed their way into your churches, saying that God’s marvelous grace allows us to live immoral lives. The condemnation of such people was recorded long ago, for they have denied our only Master and Lord, Jesus Christ*. NLT

The word "Gnostics" comes from the Greek word 'gnosis' meaning "for knowledge".  Gnostics believe that the material world is evil. They claimed that the God of the old testament was a lesser god, an inferior being who emanated from Yahweh, called the Demiurge.

Gnostics sees sin in a way that is extreme. They believe they're incapable of committing real sins, though their bodies could engage in sinful lifestyle. What this implies is that if they commit a sin, it is only their bodies that is involved not their soul or spirit, so God won't count sins committed in the body against them because their spirit is perfect but the body( which belongs to the evil material world) is inherently sinful.

This is not far from what some are teaching& preaching on the altar today telling believers that if they sin, it is only their bodies that is affected but God still sees them as perfect and unblemished. They are the modern day "Christian" Gnostics who have twisted the message of God's marvelous grace to mean what it is not.


I once read an article online where the writer insinuated that gays will enter heaven as long they are born again. Then I ask, "Is it possible to be a gay and still be born again"?

At other times, I read on Facebook where someone raised the issue of adultery, insinuating that it is only the body that is involved not the soul or spirit so if a Christian dies in the act, he will still enter heaven. I guess he was trying to say it is the body that committed the sin not his soul/ spirit. That's funny right?

I doubt if a studious believer who has read the bible from cover to cover will ever swallow that junk. In fact, if that's the case, may be no one will go to hell.  Unfortunately, alot of young and vibrant folks are buying into this teaching all in the name of grace.

They shun reasoning with scriptures all because they're hearing what suits them from their teachers. Teachings like this have a semblance with Gnosticism but quite a number don't know because it is "Christianized".

*Whenever God is addressing sin, he doesn't separate the act from man's sinful flesh. He addresses the total being*.

Consider how Jesus addressed a new testament church in the book of revelation: *“I know all the things you do. I have seen your love, your faith, your service, and your patient endurance. And I can see your constant improvement in all these things. “But I have this complaint against you. You are permitting that woman—that Jezebel who calls herself a prophet—to lead my servants astray. She teaches them to commit sexual sin and to eat food offered to idols*" (Rev 2:19-20 NLT).

Did you notice Jesus didn't make any distinction between the flesh and spirit when addressing the sin of the church in Thyatira? He addressed them in their totality.

He didn't say, "I know your body sinned with Jezebel buy I'm exonerating your spirit or mind". If he had said that, He would have validated the Gnostic belief.

The way to press a self destruct button is to believe these junks peddled around by people who coat lies with a little truth. The devil knows that many won't swallow error as it appears so he has found a way to coat it with a little truth so that it can be swallowed.

Dearest, the time has come for you to know God for yourself through the study of the word or else you'll be misled into perdition. This is the age of systematic deception, beware.

*PRAYER*: Father, I will not be conquered by the deception of this age in the name of Jesus Christ.

DEVOTIONAL 1205 | Source start line: 116648
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
11/4/24, 4:47 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: PROJECTSAVE DEVOTIONAL
Monday,
November 4th, 2024.

*THE PRAYER OF MATURE BELIEVERS*

Psalms 105:19 *Until the time came to fulfill his dreams, the LORD tested Joseph’s character* ( NLT).

The opening text is about Joseph. The previous verses before verse 19 is about his ordeal. I need not go into details about his story because it is a popular bible story even children can narrate. There is something I want you to learn from his life.

The trials of Joseph had God's hands in it though the vehicle were his brothers. What his brothers meant for evil, God turned it around for good to bring to fulfilment what he had in plan for him.  Joseph said: *"You intended to harm me, but God intended it all for good. He brought me to this position so I could save the lives of many people*" (Gen 50:20 NLT).

I want you to examine Joseph's attitude. It was never recorded that he prayed against his brothers. He acknowledged that God used their evil intention to fullfill his dreams. Don't tell me God cannot do that again. God can still turn evil intentions around for good.

What kind of prayer do you think Joseph was praying during his ordeal? Even though there is no known prayer recorded but I am sure he wasn't asking God to kill his brothers as some of us could have done. Probably, the tone of his prayers was: Lord, let your will be done in my life. That's a better way to pray.

Contemporary believers of today in his shoes may have asked for the rain of sulphur and brimstone to fall on their enemies, whereas, some enemies are allowed in our lives as vehicles that will transport us to destiny.

If Joseph had prayed away his brothers and released divine brutality on them, he may not get to his place of destiny. Why? Because they were part of the script  It is mysterious how God uses what appears to be unfavourable to us to fulfill his will in our lives.

Sometimes, when you are faced with difficult situations mediated by wicked people, having prayed and fasted without getting result, simply ask the Lord's will to be done through what seems unfavourable. God can use wicked people as vehicles to fulfill his plan in your life.

Sometimes, you need opposition. God permits them to facilitate certain processes. Sometimes, you need people who will bad mouth you so you can be announced to the world. The more they talk about you, the more you are announced. God will use them to do free advertorials for you. Why pray that such people die? If you have divine insight into why God permits such, you will always thank him and ask that his will be done.

*ACTION POINT*: Not all prayers should be imprecatory. Simply ask that God's will be done. That's how mature believers pray.

DEVOTIONAL 1206 | Source start line: 116764

11/5/24, 5:19 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: ProjectSave Devotional
Tuesday
November 5th, 2024.

*HOW TO KNOW THAT YOU LOVE GOD*

2 John 1:6 *Love means doing what God has commanded us, and he has commanded us to love one another, juest as you heard from the beginning*. NLT

Often, we sing songs about loving God in our church meetings. Sometimes, we sing these songs to the point of shedding tears. The music and combination of sounds stirs the emotion and everywhere is charged with a divine presence as we express our love to God. Have you been in such atmosphere before?

I've been privileged to meet people living in sin yet claiming to love God. If you get on the streets asking about those who love the Lord, you'll be surprised to hear almost everyone answer in the affirmative. Then, one will wonder if loving God is akin to loving the opposite sex.

Reading the book of 2 John 1:6, it is clear what loving God truly mean from a scriptural perspective. *Obedience to God's commandments is the proof of our love to him not those songs that we sing while shedding tears*.

*Loving God is not an abstract thing. It requires the action of obedience. The man who loves God will make obedience to our master his priority. It is not about songs. Singing about our love for is cheap. Anyone can sing but not everyone is obeying God*.

Loving God starts with obeying what is written in scriptures. The bible is filled with numerous commandments for mankind. Those commandments are for our good.

1 John 5:3 says, *"For this is the love of God, that we keep his commandments: and his commandments are not grievous*" (KJV).

You proof your love to him in obedience not in mere talks or songs. Anyone can compose songs.  Obedience must be total not partial.

*ACTION POINT*: What are those instructions God gave you? Are there pending obedience to divine instructions?

DEVOTIONAL 1207 | Source start line: 116848

11/7/24, 5:18 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: PROJECTSAVE DEVOTIONAL
Thursday,
November 7th, 2024.

*WHAT IT MEANS TO FEAR GOD*

2 Cor 7:1 *Because we have these promises, dear friends, let us cleanse ourselves from everything that can defile our body or spirit. And let us work toward complete holiness because we fear God* NLT

I heard someone say God didn't want us to fear Him but rather, He want us to love Him. Another preacher said that the fear of God is not the beginning of wisdom but the love of God is the beginning of wisdom. That's a gross misunderstanding and misinterpretation of the scriptures.

If the bible says the fear of the Lord is the beginning of wisdom, that's exactly what the bible meant and nothing more. I don't subscribe to twisting what is clearly written.

The problem here is that those who hold this view  do not understand what it means to fear God in context of those texts where we were enjoined to fear God.

The fear implied here is reverential fear not a crippling fear. When you exhibit a crippling fear,  it can paralyse your relationship and affection for someone. At that,  we can say that you're afraid of that fellow but in this matter,  the fear of God refers to total reverence and honour for the Lord.

God doesn't want us to be afraid of Him but He want us to fear (reverence) him. Whether in the old or new testament, we must continue to reverence the Lord.

In the new testament, Apostle Paul in 2Cor 7:1 said we should perfect holiness in the fear of God. Jesus drove out money exchangers out of the temple because of their irreverent act. Don't be deceived, the fear of the Lord is still the beginning of wisdom. That is what shows that you're on the path of wisdom.

Your love for God would drive you to fear(reverence) Him without looking back. The proof of reverential fear for God is love for God and the proof of loving God is in purity and Christlikeness.

*PRAYER*: Father, I receive grace to fear you  In Jesus name

DEVOTIONAL 1208 | Source start line: 116872

11/8/24, 3:04 PM - Lanre Oyeleke: PROJECTSAVE DEVOTIONAL

November 8th, 2024.

*THE BATTLE FOR SEXUAL PURITY*

Eph5:3 *Let there be no sexual immorality, impurity, or greed among you. Such sins have no place among God’s people*. NLT

The recent news making the rounds about a man who slept with over 400 high profile women came as a shock to most people. Inclusive in the number is a pastor's wife and some pregnant women most of whom did not need his money because they were rich. I wasn't too surprised because Solomon had 300 concubines and 700 wives.

The shame that came with the revelation will never leave affected homes the same. I heard some of the participants (not victims because it was consensual) are going suicidal. Untamed passion and fantasies simply led to that nonsense that made news. They acted what was on their minds.  I see people making excuses for those women.

While excuses made for them may be true for some (though sin has no excuse), it cannot apply to all, over 400 women. Among those women, not all of them can be neglected emotionally by their husbands. I can't accept that. Untamed flesh, fantasies and passion is the reason for that rubbish. Even to the point of being filmed in the act?

There are demons sent out this endtime to pollute the minds of people and wreck their lives on the altar of sexual immorality. There's a hunt for great destinies from hell and anyone who is not careful can fall into it. No special immunity for anyone except those who are on guard. It is a battle we all must fight and win.

One may wonder why sexual immorality is hunting after great destinies. The answer is not far-fetched. Sexual immorality is a potent weapon in the hands of the enemy to ruin a great future. Nothing is too extreme to avoid falling.

Solomon, the wisest man in the old testament said, *"For by means of a whorish woman a man is brought to a piece of bread: and the adulteress will hunt for the precious life.*"(Prov6:26).

King Solomon had a Ph.D in women studies. Whenever he is talking, it is good we listen to him. He must have seen how great men were brought down and how their virtues were drained on the altar of immorality.


*Sexual sin is not just an amorous exercise, it is a covenant engagement capable of sucking out virtues and destroying all a man has worked for in one round of sex.* Ask Samson. I call it "fast action" sin that destroys investment of years.

Every vision carrier must abstain from sexual immorality. Paul said, *"For this is the will of God, even your sanctification, that ye should abstain from fornication:*"(1Thes4:3).

The bible didn't say you should speak in tongues when tempted sexually rather, it says "abstain" or "flee". 1Cor6:18 says, *"Flee fornication...*", you cannot be too careful in this matter.

*Do not let your erection determine your direction.*

Control what goes into your mind through your eyes and ears, those two organs are the gates into your mind.

*The greatest sexual organ is your mind. Guard it jealously.*

Do whatever it takes to get your mind sexually pure. Avoid dangerous body contacts that can spark lustful desires with the opposite sex. I respect those who refuse handshake with the opposite sex because it sparks lustful thoughts in them. Draw healthy boundaries and you'll be far from falling.

*Never allow the devil make you feel you can handle compromising situations. Over confidence brought Samson down. It is better to be safe than to be sorry.*


*PRAYER*: Father, I receive the grace to stay sexually pure in Jesus name.

DEVOTIONAL 1209 | Source start line: 116919
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
11/9/24, 7:48 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: PROJECTSAVE DEVOTIONAL
Saturday,
November 9th, 2024.

*THE TRAP OF EXAGGERATION- EVANGELISTIC LIES*

Proverbs 12:22 — *“The Lord detests lying lips, but he delights in people who are trustworthy*”

Dr Bola Akin- John of blessed memory wrote a book titled: "Evangelistic Ministry". In the book, he warned against some of the evangelistic abuses common today. One that stands out is lies.

He cited an example of an evangelist who claimed he had 1.4 million persons in attendance in his crusade at Ibadan, South west,Nigeria and that 1.2 million persons were saved. After diligent enquiry, he discovered the venue could not conveniently contain 400, 000 people.

Another common evangelistic lies is to exaggerate miracles and healings in a way and manner that will catch people's attention. Some even arrange these miracles.

Why do some evangelist& missionaries lie about attendance, souls won and miracles? Probably to raise more funds from partners. This is the drive most times. In a bid to increase partnership base, some employ lies and gimmicks.

One of the traps that is common to ministers especially those in the evangelistic ministry today is the trap of exaggeration. There's something called "Evangelistic speaking". That is nothing short of lies.

As a missionary evangelist, this is one of our common pitfalls. Our ministry comes with a Satanic pressure to impress and to perform. Rarely will you find evangelists that want to preach in a village of less that 300 persons because the sight may not look appealing on social media. Unfortunately, we have many in the body who do not believe you are doing something meaningful without seeing pictures of crowds with hands up.

Billy Graham, one of the finest Christians in our time, an evangelist who is believed to have seen more converts than Paul decided against this in his Modesto manifesto.

One of the things he decided with his friends in evangelistic ministry was that they will not exaggerate their reports. It was and is common for evangelists to create untrue publicity about numbers, attendance, souls won and miracles.

Today, this devil is still around especially with  those mounting the pulpit. Social media has made it worse. The tricks of camera can be applied to make a small crowd appear larger than it is in the real sense just to create a false impression in the hearts of people.

*The moment you seek to build social media fans as a minister, you'll fall into this evil trap of exaggeration and showmanship*.  Psalm101:7 *"I will not allow deceivers to serve in my house, and liars will not stay in my presence*".

*To my dear colleagues in ministry, exaggeration will not grow your ministry. Stop lying about numbers either of souls won or churches planted as the case may be for church planters and missionaries*.

Lying may add a few bucks to your pocket but with time, the cookies will crumble. True and lasting ministry cannot be built on lies. It won't survive.

It is time to start building your life on truth even If it won't impress anyone. You're not in ministry for the multitude but for one. Anytime I'm out on the field to preach, I always tell myself that I'm stepping out to go and preach because of "one".

God can make us spend millions just because He wants one person saved. No need to feel bad about "low numbers". Don't tell the world your congregation is 5000 when in actual sense you're not up to 500. That's not faith but unfaithfulness.

Don't start multiple services by mandating  same set of people to come just because you want to create an impression of large numbers. Let God's hand rest upon you as you build your ministry on truth.

*PRAYER*: Father, I receive the grace to build my life and ministry on truth in Jesus name. Amen

DEVOTIONAL 1210 | Source start line: 116967

November 10th, 2024.

*UNBELIEF: THE ENEMY OF YOUR TESTIMONY*

James1:6 *But let him ask in faith, nothing wavering. For he that wavereth is like a wave of the sea driven with the wind and tossed.7 For let not that man think that he shall receive any thing of the Lord* KJV


My dad told the story about a couple trusting God for the fruit of the womb many years ago. After they were prayed for, they took a step of faith, went to the market to buy baby stuffs. Months after, the woman took in and gave birth.

Some may say ' but I've been acting out my faith but no result'. I would refer you to the story of father Abraham, he exercised faith for 25 solid years without wavering. It was until after then he got the promise child.  Faith works with patience, it is not a magic wand, Heb6:12.

Unbelief is the principal enemy of your testimony. Satan is our arch enemy, I agree but the bible made it clear that he was defeated on the cross (Col2:14-15). In fact, our spiritual position in Christ is far above the devil's( Eph1:6).

The devil is no longer a major hindering factor in the battle for our testimony but unbelief. It may be impossible to enjoy the benefits that accrues to the new creation if unbelief is not dealt with.

If  you find it hard to believe God's word, it would be impossible to share a faith testimony and ultimately impossible to see the manifestation of your testimony. The enemy of your testimony is unbelief.

Unbelief would preach the opposite of God's word to your mind, just cast the demon of unbelief out of your mind and stand on God's word without wavering. That's how to conquer.

*PRAYER:* I cast out the demon of unbelief from my heart and I receive grace to stand on God's eternal word for a manifestation of my testimonies In Jesus name.
