DEVOTIONAL 1211 | Source start line: 117019
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
11/11/24, 5:05 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: PROJECTSAVE DEVOTIONAL
Monday,
November 11th, 2024.

*FINDING AND KNOWING CHRIST*

John 1:45 *Philip found Nathanael and told him, “We have found the one Moses wrote about in the Law, and about whom the prophets also wrote—Jesus of Nazareth, the son of Joseph*.” NIV

Something happened to Philip, he must have read and heard the law of Moses and the prophets severally before he told Nathaniel about his discovery as we read in the opening text.  The central theme of the law and all that was written in the prophets was centred on one person- Christ.

All the books of the bible was written to point us to the wonderful Jesus that died on the cross of Calvary. Glory!!!  He was concealed in the old testament canon but revealed in the new testament.

Philip had been a member of the synagogue, probably he was pious and devoted. He knew what the law and the prophets said about the coming messiah yet he didn't find him not until the day God appointed.

When he found him,  he recognized quickly that Jesus was the person the law and the prophets wrote about and he said: "We have found the one Moses wrote about in the Law,  and about whom the prophets also wrote-Jesus of Nazareth,  the son of Joseph". That must have been a wonderful experience for Philip.

In the days of Philip, many saw Jesus but they didn't recognize him as the one Moses and the prophets wrote about. They derided him,  mocked and accused him of operating by demonic spirit. Though they longed for him  but they didn't recognize him when he came.

A man can be in the church and yet may not know the head of the church-Christ. It is possible to be devoted in church attendance and activities without a personal fellowship with Christ. It is possible to be  a theologian and preacher of many years,  Pastor of many branches  without having met Christ.

Can you say like Philip: "I have found Christ"? Hearing about Christ is not the same as knowing Christ. There's a need for you and me to study the Jesus Christ from the bible and pray that the Spirit illuminate our hearts about who he really is  not just relying on who a preacher say he is.

For sure,  you cannot meet the Jesus of the Bible and your life would remain the same. When he comes into our lives,  he  brings transformation and as we submit to him,  there's a gradual conformation to His image. After a miraculous conversion on the road to Damascus and several years of Apostolic ministry,  apostle Paul  knew how important Christ was and he exclaimed: "That I may know Him"(Phil3:10). Sincerely, that's whom we all need to know and nothing more.

In him dwell all treasures of wisdom and knowledge(Col2:3). At salvation, you met Him and he came into your heart but that's not enough, it is the beginning of a life-long walk with Christ.

Your  earnest heart-cry should be: "Jesus,  I want to meet you again and again". The quest to know more of him should be a daily thing.

*PRAYER*: Father, I have heard your word. I want to meet with Christ and be deeper in the knowledge of Him daily in Jesus name.


====================================================================================================
DEVOTIONAL 1212 | Source start line: 117162
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
11/12/24, 5:14 AM - Lanre Oyeleke: ProjectSave Devotional
Tuesday
November 12th, 2024.

*BE ROOTED IN SOUND DOCTRINE*

1 Tim 4:6 *In pointing out these things to the brethren, you will be a good servant of Christ Jesus, constantly nourished on the words of the faith and of the sound doctrine which you have been following*.

I have been privileged to study and observe what is taught by various churches and the life of the people. I concluded that the doctrine of a people determines their life style and character to a large extent. I know a church where polygamy is not frowned against. There, it is common to see polygamy from the pulpit to the pew.

Also, the doctrine of an institution determines the spirits that will operate their. For instance, in a place where people are taught that God can say anything that cannot be substantiated in scriptures, wrong spirits prophesy in such places.

I heard about a woman who was prophesied to that she must have a man friend. She took it as a word from God and Satan got her one. She accepted that ungodly path because the doctrine she has listened to doesn't negate it.

What is doctrine?  A doctrine is that which is taught. It is a belief or set of beliefs that are fundamental.True bible doctrine is fundamental and universal. It cuts across denominational lines.

Institutions and government of nations have  doctrines they expect their subjects to live by.

In God's kingdom, we have bible doctrines which are fundamental to our faith in Christ. Those doctrines are to shape the life of everyone in God's kingdom.

Paul charges Titus,  *“You must teach what is in accord with sound doctrine”* (Titus 2:1). Why is sound doctrine so important?

Your walk with God is based on the accurate understanding of sound doctrine. What you believe affects what you do. Your doctrine determines your character. It determines the kinds of spirits you'll listen to.

Oftentimes, I see people bothered about why we have proliferation of churches without a marked increase in godliness among the people that profess our faith.

We only need to check what is  taught in most church centres. True doctrines of the bible properly taught and explained always lead to godliness.

*If any man teach otherwise, and consent not to wholesome words, even the words of our Lord Jesus Christ, and to the doctrine which is according to godliness;* (1 Timothy 6:3 KJV).

New Living Translation of that portion of scripture reads this: *"Some people may contradict our teaching, but these are the wholesome TEACHINGS OF THE LORD JESUS CHRIST. THESE TEACHINGS( doctrines)  PROMOTE A GODLY LIFE* (1 Timothy 6:3 NLT Emphasis mine)

The central message of the bible is Christ. As apostle Paul said, the teachings( doctrines) of the Lord Jesus Christ promote godly life.

*Where there's dearth of godliness, it is obvious there's dearth of sound doctrine*.

*Knowing sound biblical doctrine is to your spiritual life what balanced diet is to your physical body.*

*The moment you start learning sound bible doctrines, your life will begin to conform to the image of Christ. That's the goal of doctrine*.

If you're often confused about the truths of scriptures and tossed to and fro by every wind of doctrine, that may be a pointer to the lack of sound doctrine in your life.

*Knowing sound doctrine is the only way to ascertain truth in a world of falsehood.*

Wrong doctrines comes as a wave that toss people to and fro but sound doctrines establishes and stabilizes. To get a good supply of sound doctrine, stay under teachers that value the teaching of scriptures from the pulpit.

Do not seek after sensational and motivational sermons that blend human philosophy and psychology with scriptures. True bible sermon is not another "feel good" speech. It is meant to transform us into the image of Christ.

The new testament church is God's training school where bible doctrines should be taught. Seek to learn doctrine so you can get a rich supply of what is missing in your faith.

*PRAYER*: Father, I receive the grace to learn sound doctrine in Jesus name
